eng: Watch out for people—because they will hand you over to councils and they will beat you in their synagogues.
local: Á taá cʋ á tɩtɩ, sɩ nɔɔna wʋ́ ja abam ba vu ba saŋɩ sarɩya-dirǝ nakwa bam tee nɩ, yɩ ba daarɩ ba magɩ abam ba Wɛ-di sɩm wʋnɩ.

eng: No one pours new wine into old wineskins. If they did, the wineskins would burst, the wine would spill, and the wineskins would be ruined. Instead, people pour new wine into new wineskins so that both are kept safe.
local: Nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ dɩ nan bá sɛ o kwe sa-dʋŋa o lo o kɩ lɔ-doŋo wʋnɩ. O na kɩ kʋntʋ yɩ sana kam na keeri, kaá pa lɔɔ kʋm bagɩ mʋ. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ sana kam wʋ́ lo, yɩ lɔɔ kʋm dɩ cɔgɩ. Nɔɔnʋ nan maŋɩ sɩ o kwe sa-dʋŋa o kɩ lɔ-dʋŋʋ wʋnɩ mʋ, sɩ tɩ maama maŋɩ daanɩ.».

eng: Not everybody who says to me, Lord, Lord, will get into the kingdom of heaven. Only those who do the will of my Father who is in heaven will enter.
local: Kʋ daɩ nɩ balʋ maama na bǝ amʋ nɩ ‹Yuutu, Yuutu› tɩn mʋ wʋ́ tɔgɩ ba zʋ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋnɩ. Kʋ nan yɩ balʋ na sɩɩnɩ ba tɔgɩ amʋ Ko Wɛ wʋbʋŋa tɩn mʋ.

eng: Right away, they left their nets and followed him.
local: O na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma zaŋɩ lɩla ba yagɩ ba bʋrɩ sɩm, yɩ ba vu ba tɔgɩ o kwaga.

eng: so that what was spoken through Isaiah the prophet might be fulfilled.
local: Zezi na kɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ yɩ sɩ kʋ pa Wɛ nijoŋnu Ezayi na maŋɩ o ta kʋlʋ faŋa faŋa tɩn mʋ sɩɩnɩ kʋ kɩ. O dɛɛn tagɩ o wɩ:.

eng: The ax is already at the root of the trees. Therefore, every tree that doesnt produce good fruit will be chopped down and tossed into the fire.
local: Nɔɔnʋ nan maŋɩ o jɩgɩ dooru o jɩŋa nɩ, sɩ o ta maa goni tweeru tɩm o dɩ tɩga nɩ. Tiu kʋlʋ maama na ba lǝri bu-ŋʋna tɩn, o maŋɩ sɩ o go-kʋ mʋ o dɩ mini wʋnɩ sɩ kʋ di. Mʋ Wɛ dɩ na lagɩ DƖ kɩ nɔɔna balʋ na wʋ lǝni ba wʋrʋ ba yagɩ kǝm-balwaarʋ te tɩn.

eng: A legal expert came and said to him, Teacher, Ill follow you wherever you go.
local: Ba daa ta na wʋ kɛ tɩn, Wɛ cullu karanyɩna tu wʋdoŋ ma vu o te, yɩ o ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Karanyɩna, nmʋ na lagɩ n vu je sɩlʋ maama tɩn, amʋ wʋ́ tɔgɩ a vu.».

eng: While Jesus was speaking to them, a ruler came and knelt in front of him, saying, My daughter has just died. But come and place your hand on her, and shell live.
local: Zezi dɛɛn na wʋra o ŋɔɔnɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, Zwifǝ yigǝ tu wʋdoŋ ma ba o te. O kuni doonǝ o yigǝ nɩ yɩ o wɩ: «Amʋ bukɔ mʋ tɩga lele kʋntʋ. Nan ba sɩ n ma n jɩŋa n dwe-o, sɩ o joori o na ŋwɩa.».

eng: They will haul you in front of governors and even kings because of me so that you may give your testimony to them and to the Gentiles.
local: Abam na tɔgɩ amʋ tɩn ŋwaanɩ, baá ja abam ba vu ba saŋɩ dɩdɛɛra dɩ pwa tee nɩ. Kʋntʋ mʋ á wʋ́ wanɩ á brɩ-ba Wɛ cɩga kam, yɩ á daarɩ á brɩ dwi-gɛ tiinǝ bam dɩ.

eng: On the Judgment Day, many people will say to me, Lord, Lord, didnt we prophesy in your name and expel demons in your name and do lots of miracles in your name.
local: Sarɩya dɛ dɩm na yiǝ, nɔɔna zanzan wʋ́ ta dɩ amʋ ba wɩ: ‹Dɩ́ Yuutu, dɩbam dɛɛn tɔgɩ nmʋ yɩrɩ dɩm ŋwaanɩ mʋ dɩ́ ŋɔɔnɩ Wɛ yiyiu-ŋwɛ, yɩ dɩ́ ma nmʋ yɩrɩ dɩm dɩ́ zǝli ciciri nɔɔna yɩra nɩ, yɩ dɩ́ daarɩ dɩ́ ma dɩ́ kɩ wo-kɩnkagɩla zanzan.›.

eng: Continuing on, he saw another set of brothers, James the son of Zebedee and his brother John. They were in a boat with Zebedee their father repairing their nets. Jesus called them and.
local: Zezi na ve o maa kɛ tɩn, o daa ma na nɔɔna bale, ba dɩ yɩ cʋrʋ mʋ. Ba ko yɩrɩ mʋ Zebede. Ba dɩdʋa yɩrɩ mʋ Zakɩ. Wʋdoŋ wʋm maa yɩ o nyaanɩ, o yɩrɩ mʋ Zan. Bantʋ bale dɩ ba ko Zebede maa wʋ naboro wʋnɩ ba kwɛ ba bʋrɩ, sɩ ba ma ja kale. O ma bǝŋi biǝ bam sɩ ba ba ba taa tɔg-o.

eng: Look, my Servant whom I chose, the one I love, in whom I find great pleasure . Ill put my Spirit upon him, and hell announce judgment to the Gentiles.
local: «Nii, wʋntʋ mʋ yɩ amʋ Wɛ tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋlʋ a na kuri tɩn. Wʋntʋ mʋ yɩ wʋlʋ a na so-o yɩ o yǝni o paɩ a wʋ poli zanzan tɩn. Aá pa a Joro kʋm taa wʋ o tee nɩ. O ma wʋ́ pa dwi maama tiinǝ ba ba lwarɩ amʋ cɩga kam na yɩ te tɩn.

eng: I baptize with water those of you who have changed your hearts and lives. The one who is coming after me is stronger than I am. Im not worthy to carry his sandals. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and with fire.
local: Amʋ nan miisi abam na wʋnɩ mʋ, sɩ kʋ brɩ nɩ abam lǝni á wʋrʋ á yagɩ kǝm-balwaarʋ tɩm. Kʋ daarɩ wʋlʋ na lagɩ o saŋɩ amʋ kwaga o ba tɩn tiini o dwe amʋ. A wʋ maŋɩ sɩ a zeeri o nɛ natra dɩ. Wʋntʋ nan wʋ́ miisi abam Wɛ Joro dɩ mini wʋnɩ mʋ.

eng: Jesus replied, Foxes have dens, and the birds in the sky have nests, but the Human One has no place to lay his head.
local: Zezi ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «Kʋ na yɩ yirǝ, bantʋ jɩgɩ bɔɔna ba tigi da. Zunǝ dɩ jɩgɩ pwǝru ba tigi da. Kʋ daarɩ amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm ba jɩgɩ jǝgǝ sɩ a pǝni da a sin.».

eng: So Jesus and his disciples got up and went with him.
local: Zezi ma zaŋɩ o tɔg-o, dɩ o karabiǝ bam maama.

eng: Whenever they hand you over, dont worry about how to speak or what you will say, because what you can say will be given to you at that moment.
local: Ba nan na jaanɩ abam ba vu ba saŋɩ, sɩ á yɩ taá liǝ dɩ á na wʋ́ ta kʋlʋ tɩn naa á na wʋ́ ta te tɩn. Á na wʋ́ taá zɩgɩ ba yigǝ nɩ maŋa kalʋ tɩn, Wɛ wʋ́ pa á lwarɩ á na wʋ́ ta kʋlʋ tɩn.

eng: Then Ill tell them, Ive never known you. Get away from me, you people who do wrong.
local: A laan wʋ́ ta dɩ ba a wɩ: ‹A maŋɩ a yǝri abam. Ve-na daa nɩ, abam kǝm-balwaarʋ tiinǝ-ba.› ».

eng: immediately they left the boat and their father and followed him.
local: O na bǝŋi-ba kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma zaŋɩ lɩla ba yagɩ ba ko wʋm dɩ naboro kʋm, yɩ ba vu ba tɔg-o.

eng: He wont argue or shout, and nobody will hear his voice in the streets.
local: Wʋntʋ nan bá ta magɩ kantɔgɔ naa o taa kɩ sɔɔ. O nan bá ta paɩ o kwǝrǝ zaŋa cwe jeerǝ nɩ.

eng: The shovel he uses to sift the wheat from the husks is in his hands. He will clean out his threshing area and bring the wheat into his barn. But he will burn the husks with a fire that cant be put out.
local: Wʋntʋ nan ze zʋŋ-kɔgɔ mʋ sɩ o ma caarɩ o mɩna o pɛ o kɩ o tulǝ nɩ, sɩ o daarɩ mɩn-swaanʋ tɩm o zwɛ dɩ mini dɩlʋ na ba di dɩ dwe tɩn. Mʋ Wɛ na lagɩ DƖ pɔɔrɩ nɔɔna daanɩ DƖ kɩ-ba te tɩn.».

eng: Another man, one of his disciples, said to him, Lord, first let me go and bury my father.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ dɩ na yɩ o karabiǝ bam wʋ nɔɔnʋ dɩdʋa tɩn ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Amʋ tu, yagɩ sɩ a vu a kɩ a ko luǝ, sɩ a laan ba a tɔgɩ nmʋ.».

eng: Then a woman who had been bleeding for twelve years came up behind Jesus and touched the hem of his clothes.
local: Ba na maa kɛ kʋntʋ tɩn, kaanɩ wʋdoŋ dɩ ya tɔgɩ o wʋra, o na tiini o wɔɛ ka-wʋ taan bɩna fugǝ-yale. O maa bʋŋɩ o wʋnɩ o wɩ: «A na maŋɩ a dwe o gɔrɔ kʋm yɩranɩ dɩ, aá na yazurǝ.» O ma kɛ Zezi kwaga o dwe o gɔrɔ kʋm ni.

eng: You arent doing the talking, but the Spirit of my Father is doing the talking through you.
local: Kʋ daɩ abam tɩtɩ mʋ wʋ́ ŋɔɔnɩ, kʋ yɩ á Ko Wɛ Joro kʋm mʋ wʋ́ pa abam taanɩ dɩlʋ á na wʋ́ ŋɔɔnɩ tɩn.

eng: Everybody who hears these words of mine and puts them into practice is like a wise builder who built a house on bedrock.
local: «Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ, wʋlʋ maama na cǝgi a taanɩ dɩm yɩ o kɩ a na tagɩ kʋlʋ tɩn, kʋntʋ tu nyɩ dɩ wʋbʋŋa tu wʋlʋ na cwi o sɔŋɔ kuri pulɔrɔ baŋa nɩ yɩ o laan lɔ-kʋ tɩn mʋ.

eng: Jesus traveled throughout Galilee, teaching in their synagogues. He announced the good news of the kingdom and healed every disease and sickness among the people.
local: Zezi dɛɛn maa tʋlɩ Galile tɩɩnɩ dɩm maama. O maa yǝni o ve Zwifǝ bam Wɛ-di sɩm sɩ o taa brɩ nɔɔna Wɛ cɩga kam. O maa tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam dɩ Wɛ na lagɩ DƖ di paarɩ nɔɔna tɩtarɩ nɩ te tɩn. O maa kwǝri o pa yawɩɩna na yazurǝ dɩ yawɩɩrʋ tɩlʋ dwi maama na jɩgɩ-ba tɩn.

eng: He wont break a bent stalk, and he wont snuff out a smoldering wick, until he makes justice win.
local: Wʋntʋ bá cɔgɩ nɔɔna balʋ na tiini ba bwǝnǝ tɩn, oó ta jɩgɩ ba ŋwaŋa, nɩnɛɛnɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na bá bwǝri miu kʋlʋ na tiiri tɩn, yɩ o bá dwe kanɩa kalʋ mini na ba dana tɩn. Oó ta kɩ kʋntʋ mʋ, sɩ kʋ vu kʋ yi maŋa kalʋ o na wʋ́ pa amʋ Wɛ cɩga kam ba ka taa jɩgɩ dam nɔɔna tɩtarɩ nɩ, yɩ lanyɩranɩ wanɩ balɔrɔ.

eng: At that time Jesus came from Galilee to the Jordan River so that John would baptize him.
local: Zan dɛɛn na wʋ Zʋrdɛn bugǝ kam ni nɩ tɩn, Zezi ma nuŋi Galile tɩʋ nɩ o vu o te sɩ o miis-o na wʋnɩ.

eng: But Jesus said to him, Follow me, and let the dead bury their own dead.
local: Zezi ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «Ta n tɔgɩ-nɩ, sɩ n yagɩ sɩ balʋ na ba jɩgɩ ŋwɩa Wɛ tee nɩ tɩn taa kɩ ba lwi.».

eng: When Jesus turned and saw her, he said, Be encouraged, daughter. Your faith has healed you. And the woman was healed from that time on.
local: O na dwe-o kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma pipiri o nii-o yɩ o wɩ: «A bukɔ, kɩ pu-dɩa. Nmʋ na kɩ n wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ amʋ te tɩn mʋ pɛ n na yazurǝ.» Maŋa kam kʋntʋ nɩ nɔ́ɔ mʋ kaanɩ wʋm sɩɩnɩ o na yazurǝ.

eng: Brothers and sisters will hand each other over to be executed. A father will turn his child in. Children will defy their parents and have them executed.
local: Nɔɔna badonnǝ wʋ́ ja ba tɩtɩ cʋrrʋ ba kɩ nɔɔna jɩa nɩ sɩ ba gʋ-ba. Kwǝ dɩ wʋ́ kɩ ba biǝ kʋntʋ doŋ. Biǝ dɩ wʋ́ zaŋɩ ba pa ba ja ba niinǝ dɩ ba kwǝ ba gʋ.

eng: The rain fell, the floods came, and the wind blew and beat against that house. It didnt fall because it was firmly set on bedrock.
local: Dʋa ma ba ka nɩ ka pa bwi su sɩ pɛɛlɩ. Vu-dɩʋ dɩ ma ba kʋ magɩ sɔŋɔ kʋm, yɩ kʋ wʋ tʋ, kʋ kuri na cwi pulɔrɔ kʋm baŋa nɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: News about him spread throughout Syria. People brought to him all those who had various kinds of diseases, those in pain, those possessed by demons, those with epilepsy, and those who were paralyzed, and he healed them.
local: Nɔɔna ma ba ba ni o ŋwa je sɩm kʋntʋ nɩ, dɩ Siiri tɩʋ kʋm maama wʋnɩ. Ba maa yǝni ba jaanɩ yawɩɩna dwi dwi ba tu o te, balʋ yɩra na tiini ya wɔɛ tɩn, kʋ wǝli dɩ balʋ ciciri na yaarɩ-ba tɩn, dɩ balʋ na saɩ kɔɔrʋ tɩn, dɩ kwaarʋ. Ba na tu Zezi te kʋntʋ tɩn, o maa yǝni o pa ba na yazurǝ.

eng: And the Gentiles will put their hope in his name.
local: Wʋntʋ yɩrɩ ŋwaanɩ mʋ dwi maama wʋ́ ta jɩgɩ tɩɩna amʋ Wɛ tee nɩ.».

eng: John tried to stop him and said, I need to be baptized by you, yet you come to me.
local: Zan dɛɛn ya wʋ sɛ, yɩ o ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Amʋ mʋ maŋɩ sɩ a ba nmʋ te sɩ n miisi-nɩ na wʋnɩ, sɩ kʋ daɩ nmʋ mʋ wʋ́ ba amʋ te sɩ a miisi-m na wʋnɩ.».

eng: When Jesus got into a boat, his disciples followed him.
local: Zezi dɩ o karabiǝ bam laan ma vu ba zʋ naboro wʋ sɩ ba kɛ.

eng: When Jesus went into the rulers house, he saw the flute players and the distressed crowd.
local: Zezi laan ma vu o zʋ Zwifǝ yigǝ tu wʋm sɔŋɔ. O ma zʋ o na balʋ na maŋɩ ba wu wi yɩ ba kwe nɔɔna ba kɩ tɩn na ti ba yigǝ sɩ ba kɩ-o, dɩ nɔɔna zanzan na keeri yɩ ba frɩ ba tɩtɩ.

eng: Everyone will hate you on account of my name. But whoever stands firm until the end will be saved.
local: Nɔɔna maama wʋ́ ta culi abam, á na yɩ amʋ nɔɔna tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Wʋlʋ maama nan na tiini o zɩgɩ kǝŋkǝŋ sɩ kʋ vu kʋ yi yaara yam tiim tɩn, Wɛ wʋ́ vrɩ kʋntʋ tu ŋwɩa.

eng: But everybody who hears these words of mine and doesnt put them into practice will be like a fool who built a house on sand.
local: Kʋ daarɩ wʋlʋ maama nan na cǝgi a taanɩ dɩm yɩ o ba kɩ a na tagɩ kʋlʋ tɩn nyɩ dɩ nɔn-joro na lɔgɩ kʋ sɔŋɔ kasʋlʋ baŋa nɩ te tɩn mʋ.

eng: Large crowds followed him from Galilee, the Decapolis, Jerusalem, Judea, and from the areas beyond the Jordan River.
local: Nɔn-kɔgɔ zanzan dɛɛn ma nuŋi Galile tɩɩnɩ dɩm, dɩ je sɩlʋ ba na bǝ nɩ Tɩɩnɩ Fugǝ tɩn, dɩ Zeruzalɛm, dɩ Zude maama, dɩ je sɩlʋ na wʋ Zʋrdɛn bugǝ kam bubɛ dɩdoŋ dɩm nɩ tɩn ba tɔgɩ dɩd-o.

eng: They brought to Jesus a demon-possessed man who was blind and unable to speak. Jesus healed him so that he could both speak and see.
local: Nɔɔna badonnǝ dɛɛn ma ja lilwe, yɩ o kwǝri o yɩ mɩmaa ba ba Zezi te. Bɛŋwaanɩ cicirǝ dɛɛn mʋ jɩg-o ka yaara. Zezi ma pa nɔɔnʋ wʋm na yazurǝ. O yiǝ ma joori ya naɩ, yɩ o daarɩ o waɩ o ŋɔɔna.

eng: Jesus answered, Allow me to be baptized now. This is necessary to fulfill all righteousness. So John agreed to baptize Jesus.
local: Zezi ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «Yagɩ sɩ kʋ kɩ kʋntʋ lele, sɩ kʋ maŋɩ sɩ dɩ́ kɩ kʋntʋ mʋ, sɩ dɩ́ wanɩ dɩ́ kɩ kʋlʋ maama na yɩ Wɛ wʋbʋŋa tɩn.» O laan ma sɛ.

eng: A huge storm arose on the lake so that waves were sloshing over the boat. But Jesus was asleep.
local: Vu-fɔrɔ ma da kʋ zaŋɩ kʋ magɩ boro kʋm, yɩ kʋ wʋra kʋ miisi na bam kuri nɩ, dɩ Zezi ya tigi o dɔa.

eng: He said, Go away, because the little girl isnt dead but is asleep; but they laughed at him.
local: O ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Zaŋɩ-na á nuŋi, sɩ bu wʋm wʋ tɩgɩ, o tigi o dɔ mʋ.» O na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, nɔɔna bam maa jɩg-o ba mwana.

eng: Whenever they harass you in one city, escape to the next, because I assure that you will not go through all the cities of Israel before the Human One comes.
local: Ba nan na bɛɛsɩ abam tɩʋ kʋlʋ nɩ, sɩ á duri á vu kʋdoŋ. A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, abam bá wanɩ á tʋlɩ tɩɩnɩ dɩlʋ maama na wʋ Yisɩrayɛlɩ nɩ tɩn á ti, yɩ amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm daa ta wʋ joori a ba.

eng: The rain fell, the floods came, and the wind blew and beat against that house. It fell and was completely destroyed.
local: Dʋa ma ba ka nɩ ka pa bwi su sɩ pɛɛlɩ, yɩ vu-dɩʋ dɩ magɩ sɔŋɔ kʋm, kʋ pa kʋ tiini kʋ tʋ tɩga nɩ buri buri.».

eng: All the crowds were amazed and said, This man couldnt be the Son of David, could he.
local: Kʋ ma sʋ nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm maama, yɩ ba tɛ ba wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ mʋ yɩ Pɛ Davidi dwi tu wʋlʋ Wɛ na lagɩ DƖ tʋŋɩ tɩn na?».

eng: When Jesus was baptized, he immediately came up out of the water. Heaven was opened to him, and he saw the Spirit of God coming down like a dove and resting on him.
local: Zezi na miisi na kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma da o nuŋi na bam wʋnɩ. O laan ma na wɛyuu na pʋrɩ o baŋa nɩ, yɩ o na Wɛ Joro kʋm na dɛ da kʋ tu o baŋa, yɩ kʋ nyɩ dɩ kunkwǝŋǝ te.

eng: After he had sent the crowd away, Jesus went in and touched her hand, and the little girl rose up.
local: Ba na pɛ kɔgɔ kʋm maama nuŋi tɩn, Zezi laan ma vu o zʋ digǝ kam bu wʋm na wʋra tɩn. O ma ja o jɩŋa o pa o bi o zaŋɩ wɛɛnɩ.

eng: When Jesus finished these words, the crowds were amazed at his teaching.
local: Zezi dɛɛn na tagɩ wǝǝnu tɩntʋ maama o ti tɩn, nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋlʋ na wʋra tɩn ma tiini ba yɩ yǝǝu dɩ o zaasɩm dɩm.

eng: Disciples arent greater than their teacher, and slaves arent greater than their master.
local: Karabu nan tǝrǝ o na dwe o karanyɩna. Tɩntʋŋnʋ dɩ nan tǝrǝ o na dwe o yuutu.

eng: When the Pharisees heard, they said, This man throws out demons only by the authority of Beelzebul, the ruler of the demons.
local: Farizɩan tiinǝ bam na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma ta ba wɩ: «Wʋntʋ na waɩ o zǝli sʋtaana tɩn, kʋ yɩ sʋtaana-pɛ Belizebuli dam mʋ o ma o zǝli-ya.».

eng: They came and woke him, saying, Lord, rescue us! Were going to drown.
local: O karabiǝ bam ma vu ba zaŋ-o ba wɩ: «Dɩ́ Yuutu, vrɩ dɩbam n yagɩ, sɩ dɩ́ lagɩ dɩ́ tɩ.».

eng: A voice from heaven said, This is my Son whom I dearly love; I find happiness in him.
local: Kwǝrǝ laan ma ŋɔɔnɩ wɛyuu nɩ ka wɩ: «Wʋntʋ mʋ yɩ amʋ bu-dʋa a na so-o dɩ a wʋ maama. O ma yǝni o pa a wʋ poli zanzan.».

eng: News about this spread throughout that whole region.
local: O kǝm dɩm kʋntʋ ma jagɩ dɩ yi tɩʋ kʋm je sɩm maama.

eng: because he was teaching them like someone with authority and not like their legal experts.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ o ni-taanɩ dɩm jɩgɩ dam dɩ dwe ba tɩtɩ cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ bam taanɩ dɩm.

eng: Its enough for disciples to be like their teacher and slaves like their master. If they have called the head of the house Beelzebul, its certain that they will call the members of his household by even worse names.
local: Karabu nan na tu o yɩ nɩ o karanyɩna te, kʋ maŋɩ kʋntʋ. Tɩntʋŋnʋ dɩ nan na tu o yɩ nɩ o yuutu te, kʋ maŋɩ kʋntʋ. Ba na jɩgɩ sɔŋɔ tu ba bǝ ba wɩ ‹Sʋtaana-pɛ Belizebuli› tɩn, ba laan wʋ́ tiini ba twɩ o sɔŋɔ tiinǝ bam sɩ kʋ dwǝni kʋntʋ.

eng: Because Jesus knew what they were thinking, he replied, Every kingdom involved in civil war becomes a wasteland. Every city or house torn apart by divisions will collapse.
local: Zezi na lwarɩ ba wʋbʋŋa na yɩ te tɩn, o ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Tɩʋ kʋlʋ paarɩ na pɔɔrɩ kuni bɩle yɩ ba jɩgɩ daanɩ tɩn, kʋ bá daanɩ kʋ cɔgɩ. Tɩʋ kʋlʋ maama naa sɔŋɔ kʋlʋ maama na pɔɔrɩ kʋ tɩtɩ wʋnɩ kuni bɩle tɩn, kʋʋ́ tʋ mʋ.

eng: He said to them, Why are you afraid, you people of weak faith? Then he got up and gave orders to the winds and the lake, and there was a great calm.
local: O ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Bɛŋwaanɩ mʋ yɩ fʋʋnɩ jɩgɩ abam? Abam ba jɩgɩ wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ amʋ na?» O laan ma zaŋɩ wɛɛnɩ, yɩ o bagɩ viu kʋm dɩ na bam, yɩ tɩ maŋɩ da cɩmm.

eng: As Jesus departed, two blind men followed him, crying out, Show us mercy, Son of David.
local: Zezi dɛɛn ma zaŋɩ o maa kɛa. O na maa ve tɩn, lilwǝ bale ma zaŋɩ ba tɔgɩ o kwaga, yɩ ba tɛ dɩ kwǝr-dɩa ba wɩ: «Pɛ Davidi dwi tu! Duri dɩbam yibwǝnǝ!».

eng: Therefore, dont be afraid of those people because nothing is hidden that wont be revealed, and nothing secret that wont be brought out into the open.
local: Á nan yɩ taá fʋna dɩ nabiinǝ. Woŋo kʋlʋ maama na sǝgi nɔɔna tee nɩ tɩn jwa wʋ́ ba kʋ lwarɩ jaja. Woŋo kʋlʋ na pugi tɩn jwa wʋ́ ba kʋ puri.

eng: If Satan throws out Satan, he is at war with himself. How then can his kingdom endure?.
local: Kʋ ya na sɩɩnɩ kʋ yɩ cɩga nɩ a maɩ sʋtaana-pɛ dam mʋ a ma a zǝli sʋtaana nɔɔna tee nɩ, kʋntʋ ya wʋ́ brɩ nɩ sʋtaana-pɛ dam dɩm pɔɔrɩ kuni bɩle mʋ, yɩ o paarɩ dɩm bá daanɩ dɩ cɔgɩ.

eng: The people were amazed and said, What kind of person is this? Even the winds and the lake obey him.
local: Kʋ ma tiini kʋ kɩ nɔɔna bam yǝǝu, yɩ ba tɛ ba wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ yɩ wɔɔ mʋ, yɩ viu dɩ na dɩ ta sɛ o ni?».

eng: What I say to you in the darkness, tell in the light; and what you hear whispered, announce from the rooftops.
local: A na sǝgi a brɩ abam cɩga kalʋ maama tɩn, á maŋɩ sɩ á pa ka nuŋi pooni mʋ. A na waasɩ kʋlʋ dɩ abam tɩn, á maŋɩ sɩ á di nayuu mʋ á tɔɔlɩ-kʋ.

eng: When he came into the house, the blind men approached him. Jesus said to them, Do you believe I can do this? Yes, Lord, they replied.
local: O ma vu o zʋ sɔŋɔ kʋdoŋ. Lilwǝ bale bam ma tɔgɩ ba zʋ da. O laan ma bwe-ba o wɩ: «Abam bʋŋɩ sɩ aá wanɩ a kɩ á na lagɩ kʋlʋ tɩn a pa abam na?» Ba ma lǝr-o ba wɩ: «Dɩbam tu, ɛɛn, nń wanɩ n kɩ.».

eng: When Jesus arrived on the other side of the lake in the country of the Gadarenes, two men who were demon-possessed came from among the tombs to meet him. They were so violent that nobody could travel on that road.
local: Ba laan ma vu ba bɛ nɩnɩʋ kʋm ni dɩdoŋ dɩm, mɛ Gadara tiinǝ na zʋʋrɩ da tɩn. Nɔɔna bale maa wʋra ciciri na jɩgɩ-ba sɩ yaara. Bantʋ tigi yibeelǝ je nɩ mʋ, yɩ ba jɩgɩ fʋʋnɩ, kʋ paɩ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ warɩ o tɔgɩ cwǝŋǝ kam kʋntʋ o kɛ. Ba laan ma nuŋi yibeelǝ je sɩm nɩ ba vu ba jeeri Zezi.

eng: And if I throw out demons by the authority of Beelzebul, then by whose authority do your followers throw them out? Therefore, they will be your judges.
local: Kʋ na sɩɩnɩ kʋ yɩ amʋ jɩgɩ Belizebuli dam mʋ a ma a zǝli sʋtaana, abam nɔɔna bam dɩ nan maɩ dam dɩm kʋntʋ mʋ ba maa zǝli-ya na? Abam tɩtɩ nɔɔna bam na kɩ te tɩn mʋ brɩ nɩ cɩga tǝri á tee nɩ.

eng: Dont be afraid of those who kill the body but cant kill the soul. Instead, be afraid of the one who can destroy both body and soul in hell.
local: Abam yɩ taá kwarɩ fʋʋnɩ dɩ nɔɔna balʋ na wʋ́ wanɩ ba gʋ á yɩra yam yɩ ba bá wanɩ á jwǝǝru ba gʋ tɩn. Á nan maŋɩ sɩ á taá kwarɩ Wɛ mʋ, bɛŋwaanɩ dɩntʋ mʋ wʋ́ wanɩ DƖ pa á yɩra dɩ á jwǝǝru maama cɔgɩ min-tɩʋ nɩ.

eng: Then Jesus touched their eyes and said, It will happen for you just as you have believed.
local: O laan ma dwe ba yiǝ yam yɩ o wɩ: «Abam na kɩ á wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ amʋ sɩ a kɩ kʋlʋ a pa abam tɩn, Wɛ wʋ́ pa kʋ kɩ.».

eng: They cried out, What are you going to do with us, Son of God? Have you come to torture us before the time of judgment.
local: Ba ma kaasɩ baŋa baŋa ba wɩ: «Baŋa-Wɛ Bu, nmʋ tu dɩbam te sɩ n kɩ bɛɛ mʋ? Nmʋ tu sɩ n yaarɩ dɩbam yɩ Wɛ sarɩya dɛ dɩm ta wʋ yi mʋ na?».

eng: But if I throw out demons by the power of Gods Spirit, then Gods kingdom has already overtaken you.
local: Kʋ nan yɩ Wɛ Joro kʋm dam mʋ a ma a zǝli sʋtaana. Kʋntʋ maa brɩ nɩ Wɛ maŋɩ DƖ ba abam te, sɩ DƖ di paarɩ nɔɔna tɩtarɩ nɩ.

eng: Arent two sparrows sold for a small coin? But not one of them will fall to the ground without your Father knowing about it already.
local: Kʋ na yɩ kabǝmbǝli, sɩntʋ yǝlo yɩ mwalɩ mwalɩ mʋ. Á Ko Wɛ nan nii sɩ baŋa nɩ, yɩ sɩ dɩdʋa bá wanɩ ka tʋ tɩga nɩ ka tɩ dɩ Wɛ na wʋ sɛ.

eng: Their eyes were opened. Then Jesus sternly warned them, Make sure nobody knows about this.
local: O na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, ba yiǝ yam ma sɩɩnɩ ya joori ya puri. O ma kaanɩ-ba sɩ ba yɩ pa nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ lwarɩ kʋ ni nɩ.

eng: Far off in the distance a large herd of pigs was feeding.
local: Je sɩm kʋntʋ nɩ tǝrǝ zanzan mʋ ya wʋra ba beeri ba di.

eng: Can people go into a house that belongs to a strong man and steal his possessions, unless they first tie up the strong man? Then they can rob his house.
local: Sʋtaana-pɛ nan nyɩ dɩ nɔn-dɩdɛɛrʋ na yɩrɩ o sɔŋɔ te tɩn mʋ. Nɔɔnʋ na lagɩ sɩ o vu nɔn-dɩdɛɛrʋ wʋm sɔŋɔ o ŋɔ, o maŋɩ sɩ o da yigǝ o ja nɔn-dɩdɛɛrʋ wʋm o vɔ mʋ. O na vɔg-o o ti tɩn, mʋ o laan wʋ́ wanɩ o wǝǝnu tɩm maama o kwalɩmɩ.

eng: Even the hairs of your head are all counted.
local: Kʋ nan na yɩ abam, Wɛ ye abam yuuywe sɩm ni na maɩ te tɩn.

eng: But they went out and spread the word about him throughout that whole region.
local: O na kaanɩ-ba kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ta zaŋɩ ba karɩ ba ŋɔɔnɩ o taanɩ je sɩm maama nɩ.

eng: The demons pleaded with him, If you throw us out, send us into the herd of pigs.
local: Ciciri sɩm ma loori Zezi sɩ wɩ: «Nmʋ na lagɩ n zǝli dɩbam, sɩ n pa dɩ́ vu dɩ́ zʋ tǝrǝ bantʋ.».

eng: Whoever isnt with me is against me, and whoever doesnt gather with me scatters.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ maama nan na daɩ a nɔɔnʋ tɩn, o yɩ a dʋm mʋ. Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ maama nan na ba tɔgɩ o laɩ amʋ nɔɔna o kɩ jǝgǝ dɩdʋa tɩn, o jagɩ-ba mʋ.

eng: Dont be afraid. You are worth more than many sparrows.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ, á yɩ taá kwarɩ fʋʋnɩ, sɩ abam dɩdʋa dɩdʋa tiini á jɩgɩ kuri á dwe kabǝmbǝli zanzan.

eng: As they were leaving, people brought to him a man who was demon-possessed and unable to speak.
local: Maŋa kalʋ ba na maa kɛ tɩn, nɔɔna badonnǝ ma ja mɩmaa ba ba Zezi te. Kʋ yɩ cicirǝ mʋ tɔg-o ka pa o warɩ o ŋɔɔnɩ.

eng: Then he said to the demons, Go away, and they came out and went into the pigs. The whole herd rushed down the cliff into the lake and drowned.
local: O ma ta o wɩ, sɩ ve. Sɩ ma sɩɩnɩ sɩ nuŋi nɔɔna bam yɩra nɩ sɩ vu sɩ zʋ tǝrǝ bam. Tǝrǝ bam maama ma duri ba tu ba tʋ nɩnɩʋ kʋm wʋnɩ ba li na ba tɩ.

eng: Therefore, I tell you that people will be forgiven for every sin and insult to God. But insulting the Holy Spirit wont be forgiven.
local: Á taá ye nɩ, kǝm-balwaarʋ tɩlʋ maama nɔɔna na kɩ tɩn, naa ta-balwaarʋ tɩlʋ maama ba na ŋɔɔnɩ tɩn, Wɛ waɩ DƖ yagɩ-tɩ DƖ ma cɛ-ba. Kʋ daarɩ, nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ nan na ŋɔɔnɩ o twɩ Wɛ Joro kʋm tɩn, Wɛ bá sɛ sɩ DƖ ma cɛ kʋntʋ tu.

eng: Therefore, everyone who acknowledges me before people, I also will acknowledge before my Father who is in heaven.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ maama na zɩgɩ nabiinǝ yigǝ nɩ o brɩ jaja nɩ wʋntʋ yɩ amʋ nɔɔnʋ tɩn, amʋ dɩ nan wʋ́ zɩgɩ kʋntʋ tu kwaga nɩ a brɩ nɩ o yɩ a nɔɔnʋ a Ko Wɛ yigǝ nɩ.

eng: When Jesus had thrown out the demon, the man who couldnt speak began to talk. The crowds were amazed and said, Nothing like this has ever been seen in Israel.
local: Zezi ma zǝli cicirǝ kam o yɩra nɩ, yɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋm laan waɩ o ŋɔɔna. Kʋ ma sʋ nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm maama, yɩ ba tɛ ba wɩ: «Dɩbam maŋɩ dɩ́ wʋ nɛ wo-kɩnkagɩlɩ dɩntʋ doŋ Yisɩrayɛlɩ tɩʋ nɩ.».

eng: Those who tended the pigs ran into the city and told everything that had happened to the demon-possessed men.
local: Nɔɔna balʋ ya na nii tǝrǝ bam tɩn ma duri ba vu tɩʋ kʋm wʋ. Ba ma ta kʋlʋ maama na kɩ tɩn, dɩ Zezi na zǝli ciciri sɩm nɔɔna bale bam yɩra nɩ te tɩn.

eng: And whoever speaks a word against the Human One will be forgiven. But whoever speaks against the Holy Spirit wont be forgiven, not in this age or in the age that is coming.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na ŋɔɔnɩ ta-balwaarʋ o pa amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm tɩn, Wɛ wʋ́ sɛ sɩ DƖ ma cɛ kʋ tu. Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ nan na ŋɔɔnɩ o twɩ Wɛ Joro kʋm tɩn, Wɛ bá sɛ sɩ DƖ ma cɛ kʋntʋ tu maŋa dɩ maŋa.

eng: But everyone who denies me before people, I also will deny before my Father who is in heaven.
local: Kʋ daarɩ, nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ maama na vɩn amʋ nabiinǝ yigǝ nɩ tɩn, amʋ dɩ wʋ́ vɩn kʋntʋ tu a Ko Wɛ yigǝ nɩ.

eng: But the Pharisees said, He throws out demons with the authority of the ruler of demons.
local: Kʋ daarɩ Farizɩan tiinǝ bam dɛɛn wʋ sɛ, yɩ ba wɩ: «Wʋntʋ na waɩ o zǝli sʋtaana tɩn, kʋ yɩ sʋtaana-pɛ dam ŋwaanɩ mʋ.».

eng: Then the whole city came out and met Jesus. When they saw him, they pleaded with him to leave their region.
local: Kʋ ma pa tɩʋ kʋm nɔɔna bam maama nuŋi ba vu sɩ ba jeeri Zezi. Ba na nɛ-o tɩn, ba ma loor-o sɩ o nuŋi ba tɩʋ kʋm wʋnɩ o viiri.

eng: Either consider the tree good and its fruit good, or consider the tree rotten and its fruit rotten. A tree is known by its fruit.
local: Á na wɩ, tiu yɩ tu-ŋʋŋʋ, kʋ biǝ dɩ yɩ bu-ŋʋna mʋ. Á na wɩ, tiu yɩ tu-balɔrɔ, kʋ biǝ dɩ yɩ bu-balwaarʋ mʋ. Ba lwarɩ tiu kʋ biǝ lǝrim nɩ mʋ. Kʋntʋ tɩn, abam na sɛ nɩ amʋ kǝm dɩm lana, á maŋɩ sɩ á sɛ nɩ amʋ dam dɩm nuŋi Wɛ Joro kʋm te mʋ.

eng: Dont think that Ive come to bring peace to the earth. I havent come to bring peace but a sword.
local: A na tu lʋgʋ baŋa tɩn, á yɩ taá bʋŋɩ nɩ, kʋ yɩ sɩ a pa nɔɔna mʋ na ywǝǝni daanɩ. A nan tu sɩ a pa nabiinǝ mʋ pɔɔrɩ daanɩ.

eng: Jesus traveled among all the cities and villages, teaching in their synagogues, announcing the good news of the kingdom, and healing every disease and every sickness.
local: Zezi dɛɛn maa kaagɩ tɩ-kamunǝ dɩ tɩ-balɛ sɩm maama wʋnɩ. O maa yǝni o zʋʋrɩ Zwifǝ bam Wɛ-di sɩm o brɩ-ba Wɛ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam, dɩ Wɛ na lagɩ DƖ di paarɩ nɔɔna tɩtarɩ nɩ te tɩn. O maa paɩ nɔɔna na yazurǝ dɩ ba yawɩɩrʋ dwi dwi.

eng: Children of snakes! How can you speak good things while you are evil? What fills the heart comes out of the mouth.
local: Abam nyɩ dɩ bɩsankwɩ-dwǝ mʋ. Abam na yɩ nɔn-balwaarʋ tɩn, á bá wanɩ á ŋɔɔnɩ wo-ŋʋnnʋ. Woŋo kʋlʋ maama na wʋ nɔɔnʋ wʋbʋŋa nɩ tɩn mʋ tɔgɩ o ni kʋ nuŋǝ.

eng: Ive come to turn a man against his father, a daughter against her mother, and a daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law .
local: A tu sɩ a pa nɔɔna dɩ ba kwǝ mʋ pɔɔrɩ daanɩ, sɩ a daarɩ a pa bukwa dɩ ba niinǝ dɩ pɔɔrɩ daanɩ, kʋ daarɩ sɩ kaana dɩ ba banna niinǝ dɩ pɔɔrɩ daanɩ.

eng: Now when Jesus saw the crowds, he had compassion for them because they were troubled and helpless, like sheep without a shepherd.
local: O dɛɛn na nɛ nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm yaara yam na yɩ te tɩn, ba ŋwaŋa ma zʋ-o lanyɩranɩ, bɛŋwaanɩ ba tiini ba bwǝni yɩ ba dam dɩ ti. Ba nyɩ dɩ peeni sɩlʋ na ba jɩgɩ nayɩrʋ sɩ o taa nii sɩ baŋa nɩ te tɩn.

eng: Good people bring out good things from their good treasure. But evil people bring out evil things from their evil treasure.
local: Nɔn-ŋʋm ŋɔɔnɩ wo-ŋʋnnʋ tɩlʋ na wʋ o wʋbʋŋa nɩ tɩn mʋ, yɩ nɔn-balɔrɔ dɩ ŋɔɔnɩ wo-balwaarʋ tɩlʋ na wʋ o wʋbʋŋa nɩ tɩn.

eng: Peoples enemies are members of their own households .
local: Kʋ ma wʋ́ pa nɔɔnʋ dɩ o tɩtɩ sɔŋɔ tiinǝ taa culi daanɩ.

eng: Then he said to his disciples, The size of the harvest is bigger than you can imagine, but there are few workers.
local: O laan ma ta dɩ o karabiǝ bam o wɩ: «Kʋ nyɩ dɩ faa na kɩ lanyɩranɩ yɩ tɩntʋŋna fɩnfɩɩn yɩranɩ mʋ wʋra sɩ ba kɩ-kʋ te tɩn.

eng: I tell you that people will have to answer on Judgment Day for every useless word they speak.
local: Á taá ye nɩ, Wɛ sarɩya dɛ dɩm nɩ DƖ wʋ́ bwe nɔɔnʋ maama sɩ o ma o ni o ta bɩtar-yɔɔrʋ tɩlʋ maama o na tagɩ tɩn.

eng: Those who love father or mother more than me arent worthy of me. Those who love son or daughter more than me arent worthy of me.
local: Wʋlʋ na soe o nu dɩ o ko o dwe amʋ tɩn, kʋntʋ tu wʋ maŋɩ sɩ o taa yɩ a nɔɔnʋ. Wʋlʋ maama na soe o biǝ o dwe amʋ tɩn, kʋntʋ tu wʋ maŋɩ sɩ o taa yɩ a nɔɔnʋ.

eng: Therefore, plead with the Lord of the harvest to send out workers for his harvest.
local: Nan loori-na faa kʋm tu sɩ o tʋŋɩ nɔɔna zanzan, sɩ ba vu ba kɩ o faa kʋm ba pa-o.».

eng: By your words you will be either judged innocent or condemned as guilty.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ abam tɩtɩ ni-taanɩ mʋ wʋ́ brɩ nɩ á jɩgɩ bʋra naa á ba jɩgɩ bʋra.».

eng: Those who dont pick up their crosses and follow me arent worthy of me.
local: Wʋlʋ maama na wʋ sɛ sɩ o tɔgɩ amʋ kwaga, sɩ o na yaara nɩ wʋlʋ na zɩŋɩ tʋʋn-dagara o maa ve o tʋʋnɩ jǝgǝ te tɩn, kʋntʋ tu wʋ maŋɩ sɩ o taa yɩ amʋ nɔɔnʋ.

eng: At that time some of the legal experts and the Pharisees requested of Jesus, Teacher, we would like to see a sign from you.
local: Wɛ cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ dɩ Farizɩan tiinǝ badonnǝ dɛɛn ma zaŋɩ ba ta dɩ Zezi ba wɩ: «Karanyɩna, dɩbam lagɩ sɩ n kɩ wo-kɩnkagɩlɩ mʋ, sɩ kʋ brɩ dɩbam nɩ n nuŋi Wɛ te mʋ.».

eng: Those who find their lives will lose them, and those who lose their lives because of me will find them.
local: Wʋlʋ maama nan na lagɩ sɩ o yɩrɩ o tɩtɩ ŋwɩa tɩn, oó ga-ka mʋ. Kʋ daarɩ, wʋlʋ na sɛ sɩ o ga o ŋwɩa amʋ ŋwaanɩ tɩn, oó na ŋwɩa Wɛ tee nɩ.

eng: But he replied, An evil and unfaithful generation searches for a sign, but it wont receive any sign except Jonahs sign.
local: O ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Zɩm nɔɔna bantʋ yɩ nɔn-balwaarʋ mʋ, ba ba tɔgɩ Wɛ dɩ cɩga, yɩ ba lagɩ sɩ a kɩ wo-kɩnkagɩlɩ a brɩ-ba. Ba nan bá na, kʋ na daɩ wo-kɩnkagɩlɩ dɩlʋ na maŋɩ dɩ kɩ dɩ Wɛ nijoŋnu Zonasɩ faŋa faŋa tɩn.

eng: Those who receive you are also receiving me, and those who receive me are receiving the one who sent me.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na jeeri abam lanyɩranɩ tɩn, kʋ tu jeeri amʋ mʋ. Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na jeeri amʋ lanyɩranɩ tɩn, kʋ tu jeeri Baŋa-Wɛ dɩlʋ na tʋŋɩ-nɩ tɩn mʋ.

eng: Just as Jonah was in the whales belly for three days and three nights , so the Human One will be in the heart of the earth for three days and three nights.
local: Wʋntʋ dɛɛn kɩ da yatɔ mʋ wɩa dɩ tɩtɩɩ maama kalǝŋ-kamunu wʋnɩ. Kʋ nan maŋɩ sɩ kʋ kɩ amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm kʋntʋ doŋ mʋ, amʋ dɩ wʋ́ kɩ da yatɔ mʋ wɩa dɩ tɩtɩɩ maama yibeeli wʋnɩ.

eng: Those who receive a prophet as a prophet will receive a prophets reward. Those who receive a righteous person as a righteous person will receive a righteous persons reward.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ maama na jeeri Wɛ nijoŋnu lanyɩranɩ tɩn, dɩ Wɛ na tʋŋ-o tɩn ŋwaanɩ, Wɛ wʋ́ pa kʋntʋ tu dɩ tɔgɩ o na nyɔɔrɩ nɩ DƖ nijoŋnu wʋm te. Kʋ daarɩ, nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na jeeri nɔn-ŋʋm lanyɩranɩ o nɔn-ŋʋnɩm dɩm ŋwaanɩ tɩn, Wɛ wʋ́ pa kʋntʋ tu dɩ tɔgɩ o na nyɔɔrɩ dɩlʋ DƖ na wʋ́ pa nɔn-ŋʋm wʋm tɩn.

eng: The citizens of Nineveh will stand up at the judgment with this generation and condemn it as guilty, because they changed their hearts and lives in response to Jonahs preaching. And look, someone greater than Jonah is here.
local: Zonasɩ dɛɛn na ve Niniivi tiinǝ te o tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝrǝ kam dɩ ba tɩn, bantʋ lǝni ba wʋrʋ ba yagɩ kǝm-balwaarʋ. Wʋlʋ na dwe Zonasɩ tɩn nan wʋ abam tee nɩ yɩ á wʋ sɛ kʋ tu. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ, Wɛ sarɩya dɛ dɩm na yiǝ, Niniivi tiinǝ laan wʋ́ zaŋɩ ba brɩ nɩ abam ba jɩgɩ bʋra.

eng: I assure you that everybody who gives even a cup of cold water to these little ones because they are my disciples will certainly be rewarded.
local: A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na maŋɩ o kɩ na-zurǝ fɩnfɩɩn dɩ o pa amʋ nɔɔna bam wʋ dɩdʋa, o na yɩ a karabu tɩn ŋwaanɩ, kʋ tu wʋ́ na nyɔɔrɩ Wɛ tee nɩ.».

eng: The queen of the South will be raised up by God at the judgment with this generation and condemn it because she came from a distant land to hear Solomons wisdom. And look, someone greater than Solomon is here.
local: Pa-kana kalʋ dɛɛn na te Saba tɩʋ tɩn dɩ wʋ́ zaŋɩ o di abam sarɩya, yɩ o vɩn abam bʋra. Bɛŋwaanɩ wʋntʋ dɛɛn zaŋɩ o tɩtɩ tɩʋ kʋlʋ na tiini kʋ yɩ yigǝ yigǝ tɩn nɩ, o ba pɛ Salɔmɔn te sɩ o cǝgi o swan taanɩ dɩm. Wʋlʋ nan na dwe Salɔmɔn tɩn mʋ wʋ abam tee nɩ, yɩ á ba lagɩ sɩ á cǝg-o.».

eng: When an unclean spirit leaves a person, it wanders through dry places looking for a place to rest. But it doesnt find any.
local: «Cicirǝ na nuŋi nɔɔnʋ yɩra nɩ, ka laan wʋ́ vu ka beeri kapwǝrǝ yigǝ mʋ sɩ ka lagɩ jǝgǝ ka sin da. Ka na beeri ka ga tɩn.

eng: Then it says, Ill go back to the house I left. When it arrives, it finds the place vacant, cleaned up, and decorated.
local: ka laan maa wɩ: ‹Aá joori a vu a na nuŋi mɛ tɩn mʋ a taa wʋra.› Ka ma joori ka vu nɔɔnʋ wʋm te, yɩ ka na nɩ ka jǝgǝ kam fɔgɩ ka kwɛ lanyɩranɩ, yɩ kʋlʋkʋlʋ wʋ zʋ da.

eng: Then it goes and brings with it seven other spirits more evil than itself. They go in and make their home there. That person is worse off at the end than at the beginning. This is the way it will be also for this evil generation.
local: Ka laan ma joori ka vu ka lagɩ ciciri sɩrpɛ sɩlʋ na tiini sɩ yɩ balwaarʋ sɩ dwe ka tɩtɩ tɩn. Sɩ ma tɔgɩ daanɩ sɩ vu sɩ zʋʋrɩ da. Kʋntʋ, nɔɔnʋ wʋm laan wʋ́ tiini o na cam kʋ dwǝni faŋa. Mʋ kʋ na lagɩ kʋ kɩ zɩm nɔn-balwaarʋ tɩm dɩ te.».

eng: While Jesus was speaking to the crowds, his mother and brothers stood outside trying to speak with him.
local: Zezi dɛɛn ta na wʋra o ŋɔɔnɩ dɩ nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm kʋntʋ tɩn, o nu dɩ o nyaana ma ba ba zɩgɩ sɔŋɔ kʋm mancoŋo nɩ, yɩ ba lagɩ sɩ ba ŋɔɔnɩ dɩd-o.

eng: Someone said to him, Look, your mother and brothers are outside wanting to speak with you.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ ma ta dɩ Zezi o wɩ: «N nu dɩ n nyaana tu ba wʋ cicwǝŋǝ nɩ, yɩ ba lagɩ sɩ ba na-m.».

eng: Jesus replied, Who is my mother? Who are my brothers.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋm o wɩ: «Wɔɔ mʋ yɩ a nu, bran yɩ a nyaana?».

eng: He stretched out his hand toward his disciples and said, Look, here are my mother and my brothers.
local: O laan ma nii o karabiǝ bam seeni yɩ o wɩ: «A nu dɩ a nyaana mʋ tɩntʋ.

eng: Whoever does the will of my Father who is in heaven is my brother, sister, and mother.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ wʋlʋ maama na kɩ a Ko Wɛ wʋbʋŋa na lagɩ te tɩn mʋ yɩ a nyaanɩ dɩ a nakɔ dɩ a nu.».

eng: That day Jesus went out of the house and sat down beside the lake.
local: Dɛ dɩm kʋntʋ nɩ Zezi ma nuŋi sɔŋɔ kʋm nɩ o vu nɩnɩʋ kʋm ni. O ma jǝni tɩga nɩ sɩ o brɩ nɔɔna.

eng: Such large crowds gathered around him that he climbed into a boat and sat down. The whole crowd was standing on the shore.
local: Nɔn-kɔgɔ ma ba kʋ gilim-o. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ o ma zaŋɩ o vu o zʋ naboro wʋ o je da, yɩ kɔgɔ kʋm laan zɩgɩ na bam ni nɩ sɩ ba taa cǝgi o taanɩ dɩm.

eng: He said many things to them in parables: A farmer went out to scatter seed.
local: O ma maŋɩ mɩmaŋa o brɩ-ba wǝǝnu zanzan o wɩ: «Valʋ dɛɛn mʋ wʋra. O ma zaŋɩ o vu kara sɩ o du.

eng: As he was scattering seed, some fell on the path, and birds came and ate it.
local: O ma karɩ o dʋlɩ o wo-duuru tɩm. Tɩdonnǝ ma tʋ cwǝŋǝ ni nɩ. Zunǝ ma ba ba twɛ-tɩ ba di.

eng: Other seed fell on rocky ground where the soil was shallow. They sprouted immediately because the soil wasnt deep.
local: Tɩdonnǝ ma tʋ kandwa jǝgǝ nɩ valɩm na ba daga mɛ tɩn. Tɩ ma wʋ daanɩ tɩ puli, valɩm dɩm na ba lirǝ tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: But when the sun came up, it scorched the plants, and they dried up because they had no roots.
local: Wɩa ma ba ka baanɩ tɩ maama. Tɩ bʋnnʋ na wʋ zʋ tɩga tɩn, tɩ ma tɩ.

eng: Other seed fell among thorny plants. The thorny plants grew and choked them.
local: Kʋ daarɩ wo-duuru tɩdonnǝ ma tʋ sabarɩ wʋnɩ. Tɩ na puli tɩn, sabarɩ sɩm laan ma zaŋɩ sɩ li-tɩ sɩ gʋ.

eng: Other seed fell on good soil and bore fruit, in one case a yield of one hundred to one, in another case a yield of sixty to one, and in another case a yield of thirty to one.
local: Kʋ daarɩ tɩdonnǝ ma tʋ tɩ-ywǝŋǝ jǝgǝ nɩ. Tɩ ma nuŋi tɩ kɩ biǝ lanyɩranɩ. Tɩdaara kɩ nɩnɛɛnɩ biǝ bi bi. Tɩdaara ma kɩ nɩnɛɛnɩ biǝ fusɩrdʋ fusɩrdʋ. Tɩdaara dɩ ma kɩ nɩnɛɛnɩ biǝ fiintɔ fiintɔ.

eng: Everyone who has ears should pay attention.
local: Abam nan fɔgɩ á cǝgi a taanɩ dɩm lanyɩranɩ.».

eng: Jesus disciples came and said to him, Why do you use parables when you speak to the crowds.
local: Zezi na maŋɩ mɩmaŋɩ dɩm kʋntʋ o ti tɩn, o karabiǝ bam laan ma vu o te, yɩ ba bwe-o ba wɩ: «Bɛɛ mʋ yɩ nmʋ yǝni n maŋɩ mɩmaŋa n brɩ nɔɔna bam?».

eng: Jesus replied, Because they havent received the secrets of the kingdom of heaven, but you have.
local: O ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Wɛ paarɩ dɩm kuri ya sǝgi mʋ. Kʋ nan na yɩ abam, Wɛ maŋɩ DƖ pa á lwarɩ DƖ paarɩ dɩm kuri na yɩ te tɩn. Kʋ daarɩ nɔɔna badaara bam, Wɛ wʋ pɛ ba lwarɩ DƖ paarɩ dɩm kuri.

eng: For those who have will receive more and they will have more than enough. But as for those who dont have, even the little they have will be taken away from them.
local: Mɩmaŋa yam na maŋɩ sɩ ya zǝni nɔɔna wʋbʋŋa te tɩn nyɩ dɩ nɔɔnʋ na maŋɩ o jɩgɩ woŋo, o ta wʋ́ joŋi kʋdoŋ o wǝli da sɩ kʋ taa daga. Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ nan na maŋɩ o ba jɩgɩ kʋlʋkʋlʋ tɩn laan wʋ́ ba o ga kʋlʋ fɩnfɩɩn o ya na jɩgɩ tɩn mʋ.

eng: This is why I speak to the crowds in parables: although they see, they dont really see; and although they hear, they dont really hear or understand.
local: Kʋntʋ, a maɩ mɩmaŋa mʋ a brɩ-ba, bɛŋwaanɩ bantʋ yiǝ mʋ naɩ a na kɩ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ tɩn, yɩ ba ba ni tɩ kuri. Ba ta maa ni a zaasɩm dɩm, yɩ ba ba lwarɩ dɩ kuri.

eng: What Isaiah prophesied has become completely true for them: You will hear, to be sure, but never understand; and you will certainly see but never recognize what you are seeing .
local: Kʋntʋ mʋ brɩ nɩ Wɛ nijoŋnu Ezayi dɛɛn na tagɩ kʋlʋ faŋa faŋa tɩn sɩɩnɩ kʋ kɩ bantʋ tee nɩ. O dɛɛn tagɩ o wɩ: ‹Abam wʋ́ fɔgɩ á cǝgi Wɛ taanɩ dɩm dɩ á zwa, yɩ á bá ni dɩ kuri. Abam wʋ́ fɔgɩ á nii dɩ á yiǝ, yɩ á bá na.

eng: For this peoples senses have become calloused, and theyve become hard of hearing, and theyve shut their eyes so that they wont see with their eyes or hear with their ears or understand with their minds, and change their hearts and lives that I may heal them .
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ nɔɔna bam kʋntʋ wʋbʋŋa tɩgɩ mʋ. Ba zwa dɩ ma kwarɩmɩ, yɩ ba daarɩ ba pu ba yiǝ, sɩ ba yiǝ yɩ zaŋɩ ya na, sɩ ba zwa yɩ zaŋɩ ya ni, sɩ ba wʋbʋŋa yɩ zaŋɩ ya lwarɩ Wɛ taanɩ dɩm kuri. Kʋ na daɩ kʋntʋ, ba ya wʋ́ pipiri ba sɛ amʋ Wɛ, yɩ aá pa-ba yazurǝ.› ».

eng: Happy are your eyes because they see. Happy are your ears because they hear.
local: Zezi laan ma ta dɩ o karabiǝ bam o wɩ: «Kʋ nan na yɩ abam, á jɩgɩ yu-yoŋo, bɛŋwaanɩ abam yiǝ naɩ yɩ á zwa dɩ ni.

eng: I assure you that many prophets and righteous people wanted to see what you see and hear what you hear, but they didnt.
local: A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, faŋa faŋa Wɛ nijoŋnǝ bam dɩ nɔn-ŋʋna zanzan dɛɛn jɩgɩ tɩɩna sɩ ba ya na kʋlʋ abam na naɩ lele tɩn, yɩ ba wʋ wanɩ ba na. Ba dɛɛn ta jɩgɩ tɩɩna sɩ ba ya ni Wɛ taanɩ dɩlʋ abam na ni tɩn, yɩ ba wʋ wanɩ ba ni-dɩ.

eng: Consider then the parable of the farmer.
local: Nan cǝgi-na, sɩ a maŋɩ valʋ wʋm mɩmaŋɩ dɩm kuri a brɩ abam.

eng: Whenever people hear the word about the kingdom and dont understand it, the evil one comes and carries off what was planted in their hearts. This is the seed that was sown on the path.
local: Wo-duuru tɩlʋ na tʋ cwǝŋǝ kam ni nɩ tɩn nyɩ dɩ nɔɔna balʋ na ni Wɛ paarɩ dɩm taanɩ yɩ ba wʋ lwarɩ dɩ kuri tɩn. Wo-lwaanʋ tu sʋtaanɩ laan ma ba o lɩ-dɩ, yɩ dɩ wʋ zʋ ba wʋbʋŋa.

eng: As for the seed that was spread on rocky ground, this refers to people who hear the word and immediately receive it joyfully.
local: Wo-duuru tɩlʋ na tʋ kandwa jǝgǝ nɩ tɩn dɩ maa nyɩ dɩ nɔɔna balʋ na ni Wɛ taanɩ dɩm yɩ ba joŋi-dɩ lɩla dɩ wʋpolo.

eng: Because they have no roots, they last for only a little while. When they experience distress or abuse because of the word, they immediately fall away.
local: Taanɩ dɩm ma wʋ tiini dɩ zʋ ba wʋbʋŋa, ba ma wʋ zɩgɩ kǝŋkǝŋ. Yaara na yi-ba yɩ ba na bɛɛsɩ-ba Wɛ taanɩ dɩm ŋwaanɩ, baá da ba kwe ba yagɩ mʋ lɩla.

eng: As for the seed that was spread among thorny plants, this refers to those who hear the word, but the worries of this life and the false appeal of wealth choke the word, and it bears no fruit.
local: Wo-duuru tɩlʋ na tʋ sabarɩ sɩm wʋnɩ tɩn maa nyɩ dɩ nɔɔna balʋ na ni Wɛ taanɩ dɩm yɩ ba ta jɩgɩ liǝ dɩ lʋgʋ baŋa wǝǝnu tɩn. Sǝbu-lagɩ ma cɔgɩ ba wʋbʋŋa, nɩ sabarɩ sɩm na ligi wo-duuru tɩm te tɩn. Kʋ ma pa ba kwe Wɛ taanɩ dɩm ba yagɩ, yɩ dɩ wʋ jaanɩ kuri dɩ pa-ba.

eng: As for what was planted on good soil, this refers to those who hear and understand, and bear fruit and produce—in one case a yield of one hundred to one, in another case a yield of sixty to one, and in another case a yield of thirty to one.
local: Kʋ daarɩ wo-duuru tɩlʋ na tʋ tɩ-ywǝŋǝ jǝgǝ kam nɩ tɩn maa nyɩ dɩ balʋ na ni Wɛ taanɩ dɩm yɩ ba pa dɩ tiini dɩ zʋ ba wʋbʋŋa tɩn. Kʋntʋ ma pa Wɛ taanɩ dɩm ja kuri dɩ pa-ba, nɩnɛɛnɩ wo-duuru na kɩ biǝ lanyɩranɩ te tɩn. Tɩdaara kɩ biǝ bi bi, tɩdonnǝ ma kɩ biǝ fusɩrdʋ fusɩrdʋ, tɩtɩm ma kɩ biǝ fiintɔ fiintɔ.».

eng: Jesus told them another parable: The kingdom of heaven is like someone who planted good seed in his field.
local: Zezi dɛɛn ma maŋɩ mɩmaŋɩ dɩdoŋ o brɩ-ba o wɩ: «A lagɩ a brɩ abam Wɛ paarɩ dɩm na wʋ nɔɔna tɩtarɩ nɩ te tɩn mʋ. Kʋ nyɩ dɩ valʋ na kwe mɩn-ŋʋna o ja vu o kara o du.

eng: While people were sleeping, an enemy came and planted weeds among the wheat and went away.
local: O na dugi-ya kʋntʋ tɩn, o dʋm ma zaŋɩ pǝn-dʋa maŋa nɩ o ja mɩn-puurǝ o vu o du o gwaanɩ valʋ wʋm kara mɩn-ŋʋna yam wʋnɩ, yɩ o daarɩ o joori o vu sɔŋɔ.

eng: When the stalks sprouted and bore grain, then the weeds also appeared.
local: Mɩn-ŋʋna yam ma ba nuŋi ya ji mɩn-kwɩɩrʋ. O dʋm wʋm na dugi yalʋ tɩn ma nuŋi ya ji mɩn-kayalɩ.

eng: The servants of the landowner came and said to him, Master, didnt you plant good seed in your field? Then how is it that it has weeds.
local: Sɔŋɔ tu wʋm tɩntʋŋna laan ma vu ba ta dɩd-o ba wɩ: ‹Dɩbam yuutu, nmʋ na dugi n kara kam tɩn, kʋ daɩ mɩn-ŋʋna yɩranɩ mʋ n dugi na? Bɛɛ mʋ yɩ mɩn-kayalɩ laan nuŋi sɩ gwaanɩ da?›.

eng: An enemy has done this, he answered. The servants said to him, Do you want us to go and gather them.
local: O ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: ‹Kʋ yɩ a dʋm mʋ kɩ kʋntʋ.› Ba laan ma bwe-o ba wɩ: ‹Dɩ́ nan ve dɩ́ gɔ mɩn-kayalɩ sɩm dɩ́ yagɩ na?›.

eng: But the landowner said, No, because if you gather the weeds, youll pull up the wheat along with them.
local: O ma wʋ sɛ, yɩ o ta dɩ ba o wɩ: ‹Abam na kɩ kʋntʋ, á wʋ́ kɛ á gɔ mɩn-ŋʋna yam dɩ mʋ á wǝli da.

eng: Let both grow side by side until the harvest. And at harvesttime Ill say to the harvesters,First gather the weeds and tie them together in bundles to be burned. But bring the wheat into my barn.
local: Nan yagɩ-na sɩ ya maama taa wʋra sɩ kʋ ba kʋ yi zagɩm maŋa. Kantʋ maŋa kam nɩ, a laan wʋ́ ta dɩ mɩn-zagɩna bam, sɩ ba da yigǝ ba pɔɔrɩ mɩn-kayalɩ sɩm ba zagɩ ba kɩ daanɩ ba zwɛ, sɩ ba daarɩ ba zagɩ mɩn-ŋʋna yam ba pɛ ba kɩ a tulǝ nɩ.› ».

eng: He told another parable to them: The kingdom of heaven is like a mustard seed that someone took and planted in his field.
local: Zezi daa ta ma maŋɩ mɩmaŋɩ dɩdoŋ o brɩ-ba o wɩ: «Wɛ paarɩ dɩm nyɩ dɩ nɔɔnʋ na kwe wo-duu na yɩ mɩmɩɩnʋ o vu o du o kara nɩ.

eng: Its the smallest of all seeds. But when its grown, its the largest of all vegetable plants. It becomes a tree so that the birds in the sky come and nest in its branches.
local: Wo-duu kʋm kʋntʋ mʋ tiini kʋ yɩ mɩmɩɩnʋ wo-dwǝ maama wʋnɩ. Kʋ ma nuŋi, yɩ kʋ ji kamunu kʋ dwǝni dwǝ balʋ maama na wʋ o kara kam nɩ tɩn. Kʋ laan maa ji tu-kamunu, yɩ zunǝ tui ba sɔ ba pwǝru da.».

eng: He told them another parable: The kingdom of heaven is like yeast, which a woman took and hid in a bushel of wheat flour until the yeast had worked its way through all the dough.
local: Zezi daa ta ma maŋɩ mɩmaŋɩ dɩdoŋ o brɩ-ba o wɩ: «Wɛ paarɩ dɩm nyɩ dɩ kaanɩ na kwe dabɩlɩ o gwaanɩ dɩ dɩpɛ muni zwɩ-zɛ sɩtɔ te tɩn mʋ. Dɩ laan ma pa dɩpɛ muni dɩm maama tiini dɩ fʋlɩ.».

eng: Jesus said all these things to the crowds in parables, and he spoke to them only in parables.
local: Wǝǝnu tɩm kʋntʋ maama Zezi dɛɛn na brɩ nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm tɩn yɩ mɩmaŋa yɩranɩ mʋ o ma brɩ-ba. O wʋ ŋɔɔnɩ wo-woŋo dɩ ba yɩ kʋ wʋ dɛ dɩ mɩmaŋa.

eng: This was to fulfill what the prophet spoke: Ill speak in parables; Ill declare what has been hidden since the beginning of the world.
local: O nan kɩ kʋntʋ, sɩ kʋ pa Wɛ nijoŋnu wʋdoŋ taanɩ mʋ sɩɩnɩ dɩ kɩ. O dɛɛn tagɩ o wɩ: «Amʋ na lagɩ a ŋɔɔnɩ a brɩ-ba, aá ta maɩ mɩmaŋa mʋ a ma brɩ-ba. Aá pa ba lwarɩ Wɛ cɩga kalʋ na sǝgi lʋgʋ pulim maŋa nɩ sɩ kʋ ba kʋ yi zɩm tɩn.».

eng: Jesus left the crowds and went into the house. His disciples came to him and said, Explain to us the parable of the weeds in the field.
local: Zezi dɛɛn ma zaŋɩ o yagɩ nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm, yɩ o daarɩ o joori o vu sɔŋɔ. O karabiǝ bam ma ba o te yɩ ba wɩ: «Brɩ dɩbam mɩn-kayalɩ sɩm mɩmaŋɩ dɩm kuri.».

eng: Jesus replied, The one who plants the good seed is the Human One.
local: O ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na dugi mɩn-ŋʋna yam tɩn brɩ amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm na kɩ te tɩn mʋ.

eng: The field is the world. And the good seeds are the followers of the kingdom. But the weeds are the followers of the evil one.
local: Kara kam maa nyɩ dɩ lʋgʋ baŋa. Mɩn-ŋʋna yam maa nyɩ dɩ nɔɔna balʋ na yɩ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm nɔɔna tɩn. Kʋ daarɩ mɩn-kayalɩ sɩm maa nyɩ dɩ balʋ na yɩ sʋtaanɩ nɔɔna tɩn.

eng: The enemy who planted them is the devil. The harvest is the end of the present age. The harvesters are the angels.
local: Dʋm wʋlʋ na dugi mɩn-kayalɩ sɩm tɩn maa nyɩ dɩ sʋtaanɩ dɩm tɩtɩ. Zagɩm maŋa kam maa nyɩ dɩ lʋgʋ na wʋ́ ba kʋ ti te tɩn. Mɩn-zagɩna bam maa nyɩ dɩ Wɛ malɛsɩ sɩm.

eng: Just as people gather weeds and burn them in the fire, so it will be at the end of the present age.
local: Nɩ mɩn-zagɩna bam na zagɩ mɩn-kayalɩ sɩm ba zwɛ te tɩn, kʋntʋ doŋ mʋ wʋ́ kɩ lʋgʋ tiim maŋa nɩ.

eng: The Human One will send his angels, and they will gather out of his kingdom all things that cause people to fall away and all people who sin.
local: Kʋ na wʋ́ kɩ te tɩn, amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm wʋ́ tʋŋɩ a malɛsɩ tiinǝ bam sɩ ba vu ba lɩ kʋlʋ maama na paɩ nɔɔna tusi tɩn dɩ wo-lwaan-kǝrǝ bam maama Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋnɩ.

eng: He will throw them into a burning furnace. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: Ba laan wʋ́ zǝli-ba ba yagɩ min-lɔŋɔ wʋnɩ sɩ dɩ di-ba. Dáanɩ mʋ ba laan wʋ́ ta wʋra ba keerǝ yɩ ba dʋnɩ ba nwa.

eng: Then the righteous will shine like the sun in their Fathers kingdom. Those who have ears should hear.
local: Kʋ daarɩ balʋ maama na sɛ Wɛ yɩ ba kɩ lanyɩranɩ tɩn laan wʋ́ ta tiini ba nyɩɩna ba Ko Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋnɩ, nɩ wɩa na nyɩ te tɩn. Fɔgɩ-na á cǝgi a taanɩ dɩm lanyɩranɩ.».

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a treasure that somebody hid in a field, which someone else found and covered up. Full of joy, the finder sold everything and bought that field.
local: «Wɛ paarɩ dɩm daa ta nyɩ dɩ wo-ŋʋnnʋ tɩlʋ na yɩ lʋnnɩ nyɩm tɩn na sǝgi kara wʋnɩ. Nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ laan ma na-tɩ. O ma joori o fɔgɩ o sǝgi-tɩ. O wʋ na tiini kʋ poli tɩn, o ma vu o kwe o wǝǝnu maama o yǝgi sǝbu, sɩ o vu o yǝgi kara kantʋ, sɩ wo-ŋʋnnʋ tɩm taa yɩ o nyɩm.».

eng: Again, the kingdom of heaven is like a merchant in search of fine pearls.
local: «Wɛ paarɩ dɩm daa ta nyɩ dɩ pipimpiinu na beeri o lagɩ kandwa-ŋʋna sɩ o yǝgi te tɩn.

eng: When he found one very precious pearl, he went and sold all that he owned and bought it.
local: O ma ba o na kandwɛ dɩlʋ na tiini dɩ jɩgɩ lam yɩ dɩ sǝbu daga tɩn. O laan ma joori o vu o kwe o wǝǝnu maama o yǝgi, o ma yǝgi kandwɛ dɩm kʋntʋ yɩranɩ.».

eng: Again, the kingdom of heaven is like a net that people threw into the lake and gathered all kinds of fish.
local: «Wɛ paarɩ dɩm daa ta nyɩ dɩ nɔɔna na kwe bʋra ba dɩ bugǝ nɩ sɩ ba ma ja kale te tɩn. Ba na dɩ-ka da tɩn, ba ma ja kale dwi dwi.

eng: When it was full, they pulled it to the shore, where they sat down and put the good fish together into containers. But the bad fish they threw away.
local: Ka ma ba ka su lanyɩranɩ. Nɔɔna bam laan ma vaŋɩ-ka ba tuuri ba ja bɛ. Ba laan ma jǝni tɩga nɩ ba kuri kalǝŋ-ŋwɩ sɩm ba kɩ ba tampwarʋ wʋnɩ. Ba ma daarɩ ba kwe sɩlʋ na ba lana tɩn ba dʋlɩ ba yagɩ.

eng: Thats the way it will be at the end of the present age. The angels will go out and separate the evil people from the righteous people.
local: Kʋ nan wʋ́ ba kɩ kʋntʋ mʋ lʋgʋ tiim maŋa nɩ. Wɛ malɛsɩ wʋ́ ba sɩ pɔɔrɩ nɔn-balwaarʋ dɩ balʋ na sɛ Wɛ yɩ ba kɩ lanyɩranɩ tɩn daanɩ.

eng: and will throw the evil ones into a burning furnace. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: Sɩ laan wʋ́ daarɩ sɩ zǝli nɔn-balwaarʋ tɩm sɩ yagɩ min-lɔŋɔ kʋm wʋnɩ sɩ dɩ di-ba. Dáanɩ mʋ ba laan wʋ́ ta wʋra ba keerǝ yɩ ba dʋnɩ ba nwa.».

eng: Have you understood all these things?Jesus asked. They said to him, Yes.
local: Zezi na ŋɔɔnɩ o ti tɩn, o laan ma bwe o karabiǝ bam o wɩ: «Abam ni wǝǝnu tɩm maama kuri na?» Ba ma lǝr-o ba wɩ: «Ɛɛn, dɩ́ niǝ.».

eng: Then he said to them, Therefore, every legal expert who has been trained as a disciple for the kingdom of heaven is like the head of a household who brings old and new things out of their treasure chest.
local: O ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Kʋntʋ tɩn, Wɛ cullu karanyɩna wʋlʋ maama na tu o zaasɩ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm cɩga kam tɩn nyɩ dɩ sɔŋɔ tu wʋlʋ na lɩ wo-dʋnnʋ dɩ wo-dwǝǝnu o jɩjɩgɩrʋ wʋnɩ o paɩ nɔɔna te tɩn mʋ. Kʋntʋ tu laan waɩ o lɩ zaasɩm-dʋʋrɩ o wǝli dɩ zaasɩm-doori dɩm sɩ o ma brɩ o nɔɔna bam.».

eng: When Jesus finished these parables, he departed.
local: Zezi dɛɛn na maŋɩ mɩmaŋa yam kʋntʋ o ti tɩn, o laan ma nuŋi jǝgǝ kam kʋntʋ nɩ.

eng: When he came to his hometown, he taught the people in their synagogue. They were surprised and said, Where did he get this wisdom? Where did he get the power to work miracles?.
local: O ma joori o vu o tɩtɩ tɩʋ o na bɩgɩ mɛ tɩn. O ma zʋ Zwifǝ Wɛ-digǝ kam sɩ o brɩ-ba. O na brɩ-ba te tɩn, kʋ dɛɛn sʋ-ba lanyɩranɩ. Ba ma ta ba wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ kɩ ta mʋ o jɩgɩ swan yam kʋntʋ doŋ? O nan kɩ ta mʋ o na dam o ma kɩ wo-kɩnkagɩla yantʋ?.

eng: Isnt he the carpenters son? Isnt his mother named Mary? Arent James, Joseph, Simon, and Judas his brothers?.
local: Wʋm mʋ daɩ da-lwarʋ wʋm bu wʋm na? O nu mʋ daɩ Mari na? Kʋ daɩ o nyaana mʋ yɩ Zakɩ dɩ Zʋzɛfʋ dɩ Simɔn dɩ Zudasɩ na?.

eng: And his sisters, arent they here with us? Where did this man get all this.
local: Kʋ daɩ o nakwa bam maama mʋ wʋra dɩdaanɩ dɩbam tɩn na? Wʋntʋ nan nɛ dam dɩm kʋntʋ maama yǝn mʋ?».

eng: They were repulsed by him and fell into sin. But Jesus said to them, Prophets are honored everywhere except in their own hometowns and in their own households.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ ba dɛɛn wʋ sɛ nɩ Zezi sɩɩnɩ o nuŋi Wɛ te. Zezi ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ na yɩ Wɛ nijoŋnu, nɔɔna pa-o zulǝ lanyɩranɩ, kʋ daarɩ o tɩtɩ tɩʋ tiinǝ dɩ o sɔŋɔ tiinǝ nan bá nɩg-o.».

eng: He was unable to do many miracles there because of their disbelief.
local: Ba dɛɛn na wʋ kɩ ba wʋ-dɩdʋa sɩ ba sɛ-o tɩn mʋ pɛ o wʋ tiini o kɩ wo-kɩnkagɩla ba tee nɩ.

eng: Six days later Jesus took Peter, James, and John his brother, and brought them to the top of a very high mountain.
local: Da yardʋ na kɛ tɩn, Zezi laan ma ja Pɩyɛɛrɩ dɩ Zakɩ dɩ o nyaanɩ Zan, ba tɔgɩ daanɩ ba vu ba di pu-kamunu yuu, ba wʋra ba yɩranɩ.

eng: He was transformed in front of them. His face shone like the sun, and his clothes became as white as light.
local: Ba na wʋra kʋntʋ tɩn, Zezi yibiyǝ ma lǝni ba yigǝ nɩ. O yibiyǝ kam maa nyɩ dɩ wɩa na nyɩɩnɩ te tɩn. O gwaarʋ tɩm ma ji napwǝǝnu nɩ pooni te.

eng: Moses and Elijah appeared to them, talking with Jesus.
local: Yɩ ba laan na Moyisi dɩ Eli na wʋ Zezi tee nɩ, yɩ ba ŋɔɔnɩ dɩd-o.

eng: Peter reacted to all of this by saying to Jesus, Lord, its good that were here. If you want, Ill make three shrines: one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ ma ta dɩ Zezi o wɩ: «A Yuutu, dɩ́ na wʋ yo seeni tɩn, kʋ tiini kʋ lana kʋ pa dɩbam. Nmʋ nan na sɛ, sɩ n pa a pu vwǝ yatɔ yo, sɩ nmʋ dɩ Moyisi dɩ Eli taa zʋʋrɩ da.».

eng: While he was still speaking, look, a bright cloud overshadowed them. A voice from the cloud said, This is my Son whom I dearly love. I am very pleased with him. Listen to him.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ na wʋra o ŋɔɔnɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, kunkwǝǝnu tɩlʋ na nyɩɩnɩ tɩn ma ba tɩ kwǝli-ba. Ba laan ma ni kwǝrǝ na ŋɔɔnɩ tɩ wʋnɩ ka wɩ: «Wʋntʋ mʋ yɩ amʋ bu-dʋa a na soe dɩ a wʋ maama, yɩ o yǝni o pa a jɩgɩ wʋpolo. Á nan taá cǝgi o taanɩ lanyɩranɩ.».

eng: Hearing this, the disciples fell on their faces, filled with awe.
local: Zezi karabiǝ bam na ni kwǝrǝ kam kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma vi ba yigǝ tɩga nɩ, fʋʋnɩ na tiini dɩ zʋ-ba tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: But Jesus came and touched them. Get up, he said. Dont be afraid.
local: Zezi laan ma vu ba te o dwe-ba yɩ o wɩ: «Zaŋɩ-na, sɩ á yɩ pa fʋʋnɩ taa jɩgɩ abam.».

eng: When they looked up, they saw no one except Jesus.
local: Ba laan ma kwǝni ba yum wɛɛnɩ, yɩ ba daa wʋ nɛ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ, kʋ na daɩ Zezi yɩranɩ.

eng: As they were coming down the mountain, Jesus commanded them, Dont tell anybody about the vision until the Human One is raised from the dead.
local: Ba ma zaŋɩ piu kʋm yuu nɩ ba maa tuǝ. Zezi laan ma kaanɩ-ba o wɩ: «Yɩ pa-na nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ lwarɩ á na nɛ kʋlʋ tɩn, sɩ kʋ taa ve maŋa kalʋ amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm na wʋ́ ba a bi a yagɩ tʋʋnɩ tɩn.».

eng: The disciples asked, Then why do the legal experts say that Elijah must first come.
local: O karabiǝ bam laan ma bwe-o ba wɩ: «Wɛ cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ bam yǝni ba tɛ ba wɩ, Eli mʋ maŋɩ sɩ o da yigǝ o ba, sɩ wʋlʋ Wɛ na tʋŋɩ tɩn laan da kwaga. Kʋ yɩ cɩga naa vwan mʋ?».

eng: Jesus responded, Elijah does come first and will restore all things.
local: Zezi ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Kʋ yɩ cɩga mʋ. Eli maŋɩ sɩ o da yigǝ o ba o kwɛ woŋo maama, sɩ wʋlʋ Wɛ na tʋŋɩ tɩn laan ba.

eng: In fact, I tell you that Elijah has already come, and they didnt know him. But they did to him whatever they wanted. In the same way the Human One is also going to suffer at their hands.
local: Á nan taá ye nɩ, Eli maŋɩ o ba yɩ nɔɔna wʋ lwar-o. Ba ma tiini ba bɛɛs-o nɩ ba na lagɩ te tɩn. Kʋ nan na yɩ amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm, ba daa ta wʋ́ bɛɛsɩ amʋ dɩ kʋntʋ mʋ.».

eng: Then the disciples realized he was telling them about John the Baptist.
local: O karabiǝ bam laan ma lwarɩ nɩ, kʋ yɩ Zan wʋlʋ na miisi nɔɔna na wʋnɩ tɩn taanɩ mʋ o ŋɔɔna.

eng: When they came to the crowd, a man met Jesus. He knelt before him.
local: Zezi dɩ o karabiǝ batɔ bam laan ma joori ba vu ba yi nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm te. Nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ ma vu o kuni doonǝ Zezi yigǝ nɩ o ta dɩd-o o wɩ:.

eng: saying, Lord, show mercy to my son. He is epileptic and suffers terribly, for he often falls into the fire or the water.
local: «Amʋ tu, duri a bu yibwǝnǝ. O saɩ kɔɔrʋ mʋ yɩ kʋ tiini kʋ cana o yɩra nɩ, yɩ o yǝni o tʋɩ mini dɩ na wʋnɩ.

eng: I brought him to your disciples, but they couldnt heal him.
local: Amʋ nan jaan-o a ba nmʋ karabiǝ bam te mʋ, sɩ ba pa o na yazurǝ, yɩ ba wʋ wanɩ.».

eng: Jesus answered, You faithless and crooked generation, how long will I be with you? How long will I put up with you? Bring the boy here to me.
local: Zezi na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma ta o wɩ: «Abam zɩm nɔɔna bam brɩ á ba jɩgɩ wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ amʋ, yɩ á ba kɩ lanyɩranɩ. Amʋ wʋ́ daanɩ abam tɩtarɩ nɩ sɩ kʋ taa ve maŋa kɔɔ? Amʋ wʋ́ kɩ wʋ-zuru dɩ abam sɩ kʋ taa ve maŋa kɔɔ? Ja-na bu wʋm á ba yo seeni.».

eng: Then Jesus spoke harshly to the demon. And it came out of the child, who was healed from that time on.
local: Zezi laan ma bagɩ cicirǝ kalʋ na jɩgɩ bu wʋm tɩn, o pa ka nuŋi ka yag-o. O ma da o na yazurǝ maŋa kam kʋntʋ nɩ nɔ́ɔ.

eng: Then the disciples came to Jesus in private and said, Why couldnt we throw the demon out.
local: Nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm dɛɛn na viiri maama tɩn, Zezi karabiǝ bam laan ma vu o te ba bwe-o ba wɩ: «Bɛɛ mʋ yɩ dɩbam warɩ cicirǝ kam dɩ́ zǝli?».

eng: Because you have little faith, he said. I assure you that if you have faith the size of a mustard seed, you could say to this mountain, Go from here to there, and it will go. There will be nothing that you cant do.
local: O ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Kʋ yɩ abam na ba tiini á jɩgɩ wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ amʋ tɩn ŋwaanɩ mʋ. A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, abam na jɩgɩ wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ amʋ, yɩ kʋ na maŋɩ kʋ yɩ fɩnfɩɩn yɩranɩ nɩ nyasaŋ-bu na yɩ mɩmɩɩnʋ te tɩn, á wʋ́ wanɩ á ta dɩ pu-kamunu kʋntʋ sɩ kʋ zaŋɩ kʋ vu kʋ jǝni je-gɛ, yɩ kʋʋ́ sɩɩnɩ kʋ kɩ. Abam na jɩgɩ wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ amʋ, kʋlʋkʋlʋ tǝrǝ á na bá wanɩ á kɩ. .

eng: When the disciples came together in Galilee, Jesus said to them, The Human One is about to be delivered over into human hands.
local: Zezi karabiǝ bam dɛɛn ma ba ba wʋ daanɩ Galile nɩ. Zezi ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Baá ja amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm ba kɩ nɔɔna jɩa nɩ.

eng: They will kill him. But he will be raised on the third day. And they were heartbroken.
local: yɩ ba laan wʋ́ gʋ-nɩ. Da yatɔ dɛ nɩ a nan wʋ́ joori a bi a yagɩ tʋʋnɩ.» O taanɩ dɩm kʋntʋ ma tiini dɩ cɔgɩ ba wʋrʋ.

eng: When they came to Capernaum, the people who collected the half-shekel temple tax came to Peter and said, Doesnt your teacher pay the temple tax.
local: Ba dɛɛn ma vu ba yi Kapɛrnawum. Nɔɔna balʋ na yǝni ba joŋi lampoo Zwifǝ bam Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm ŋwaanɩ tɩn ma vu Pɩyɛɛrɩ te, yɩ ba bwe-o ba wɩ: «Abam karanyɩna ba ŋwɩ lampoo na?».

eng: Yes, he said. But when they came into the house, Jesus spoke to Peter first.What do you think, Simon? From whom do earthly kings collect taxes, from their children or from strangers.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ ma lǝri o wɩ: «O ŋwɩa.» O laan ma joori o vu o zʋ sɔŋɔ kʋlʋ ba na wʋra tɩn. Zezi ma da yigǝ o bwe-o o wɩ: «Simɔn, nmʋ wʋbʋŋa yɩ tɩta mʋ? Lʋgʋ baŋa pwa bam yǝni ba joŋi lampoo nɔɔna bra tee nɩ mʋ? Ba yǝni ba joŋi-kʋ ba tɩtɩ tɩʋ biǝ tee nɩ mʋ naa nɔɔna badonnǝ tee nɩ mʋ?».

eng: From strangers, he said. Jesus said to him, Then the children dont have to pay.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «Kʋ yɩ nɔɔna badonnǝ tee nɩ mʋ.» Zezi laan ma ta o wɩ: «Kʋ nan brɩ nɩ, dɩ́ na yɩ Wɛ biǝ tɩn, dɩ́ wʋ maŋɩ sɩ dɩ́ ŋwɩ.

eng: But just so we dont offend them, go to the lake, throw out a fishing line and hook, and take the first fish you catch. When you open its mouth, you will find a shekel coin. Take it and pay the tax for both of us.
local: Dɩ́ nan ba lagɩ sɩ dɩ́ pa lampo-joŋnǝ bam wʋbʋŋa cɔgɩ. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ, zaŋɩ n vu nɩnɩʋ kʋm ni, sɩ n dɩ gwǝlǝ n ma n ja kalǝŋǝ. Nmʋ na wʋ́ da yigǝ n ja kalǝŋǝ kalʋ tɩn, sɩ n kǝŋi ka ni, sɩ n na sǝbu-dalɩ da. Sǝbu-dalɩ dɩm kʋntʋ wʋ́ wanɩ dɩ yi Wɛ-digǝ kam lampoo kʋm kuni bɩle ŋwɩɩm. N na lɩ-dɩ, sɩ n ja n ba n ma n ŋwɩ n pa amʋ dɩ n tɩtɩ.».

eng: At that time the disciples came to Jesus and asked, Who is the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
local: Kantʋ maŋa kam nɩ Zezi karabiǝ bam ma vu o te, yɩ ba bwe-o ba wɩ: «Wɔɔ mʋ wʋ́ ta yɩ nɔn-kamunu o dwe maama Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋnɩ?».

eng: Then he called a little child over to sit among the disciples.
local: Zezi laan ma bǝŋi bu-balaŋa o pa o ba o zɩgɩ ba maama tɩtarɩ nɩ.

eng: and said, I assure you that if you dont turn your lives around and become like this little child, you will definitely not enter the kingdom of heaven.
local: O ma ta o wɩ: «A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, abam na wʋ lǝni á wʋbʋŋa á ji nɩ bu-balwa te, á bá na cwǝŋǝ sɩ á zʋ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋ.

eng: Those who humble themselves like this little child will be the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
local: Wʋlʋ na dwe maama Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋnɩ tɩn mʋ yɩ wʋlʋ na tu o tɩtɩ o ji nɩnɛɛnɩ bu-balaŋa kantʋ doŋ te tɩn.

eng: Whoever welcomes one such child in my name welcomes me.
local: Kʋlʋ na wǝli da tɩn, wʋlʋ maama na nɩgɩ bu wʋntʋ doŋ lanyɩranɩ amʋ yɩrɩ ŋwaanɩ tɩn, kʋntʋ tu nɩgɩ amʋ mʋ.

eng: As for whoever causes these little ones who believe in me to trip and fall into sin, it would be better for them to have a huge stone hung around their necks and be drowned in the bottom of the lake.
local: Balʋ na kɩ ba wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ amʋ tɩn, ba na maŋɩ ba muri nɩ bu-balwa te dɩ, nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na pɛ ba dɩdʋa yagɩ Wɛ cwǝŋǝ tɔgɩm tɩn, ba na lǝgi kandwɛ kamunu mʋ kʋntʋ tu ba nɩ ba dɩ-o nɩnɩʋ wʋnɩ yɩ o tɩ, kʋʋ́ ta garɩ cam dɩlʋ o na wʋ́ ba na Wɛ tee nɩ tɩn.

eng: How terrible it is for the world because of the things that cause people to trip and fall into sin! Such things have to happen, but how terrible it is for the person who causes those things to happen!.
local: Kʋ yɩ lɛɛrʋ lʋgʋ baŋa nɩ dɩ nabiinǝ balʋ na ganɩ nɔɔna sɩ ba yagɩ Wɛ cwǝŋǝ tɔgɩm tɩn. Gana yam kʋntʋ warɩ sɩ ya yɩ ba. Kʋ nan na yɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ maama na ganɩ nɔɔna sɩ ba yagɩ Wɛ cwǝŋǝ tɔgɩm tɩn, lɛɛrʋ wʋ o yuu nɩ.

eng: If your hand or your foot causes you to fall into sin, chop it off and throw it away. Its better to enter into life crippled or lame than to be thrown into the eternal fire with two hands or two feet.
local: Nmʋ jɩŋa naa n naga na wʋ́ pa n yagɩ Wɛ cwǝŋǝ tɔgɩm, kʋ maŋɩ sɩ n go-ka mʋ n yagɩ. N na gɛ n jɩŋa naa n naga, yɩ n daarɩ n na ŋwɩa Wɛ tee nɩ, kʋntʋ garɩ n na jɩgɩ n yɩra yam maama, yɩ ba laan dɩ-m ba yagɩ mini dɩlʋ na bá ti tɩn wʋnɩ.

eng: If your eye causes you to fall into sin, tear it out and throw it away. Its better to enter into life with one eye than to be cast into a burning hell with two eyes.
local: Nmʋ yi nan na wʋ́ pa n yagɩ Wɛ cwǝŋǝ tɔgɩm, kʋ maŋɩ sɩ n lɩ-dɩ n dʋlɩ n yagɩ. N na jɩgɩ yi dɩdʋa yɩ n na Wɛ ŋwɩa kam, kʋ garɩ n na jɩgɩ yiǝ yale, yɩ ba laan dɩ-m ba yagɩ mini dɩlʋ na bá dwe tɩn wʋnɩ.».

eng: Be careful that you dont look down on one of these little ones. I say to you that their angels in heaven are always looking into the face of my Father who is in heaven.
local: «Á taá ye sɩ á yɩ gooni wʋlʋ maama na muri amʋ nɔɔna bam wʋnɩ tɩn. Nan lwarɩ-na nɩ, Wɛ malɛsɩ sɩlʋ na nii bantʋ baŋa nɩ tɩn zɩgɩ amʋ Ko Wɛ tee nɩ DƖ sɔŋɔ nɩ mʋ maŋa maama. .

eng: What do you think? If someone had one hundred sheep and one of them wandered off, wouldnt he leave the ninety-nine on the hillsides and go in search for the one that wandered off?.
local: Kʋntʋ tɩn, nɔɔnʋ na jɩgɩ peeni bi , yɩ piǝ dɩdʋa na je, abam bʋŋɩ nɩ oó kɩ ta mʋ? O nan maŋɩ sɩ o yagɩ peeni funʋgʋ nʋgʋ kʋm kaporo nɩ mʋ piu yuu nɩ, sɩ o daarɩ o vu o beeri pi-jiǝ kam.

eng: If he finds it, I assure you that he is happier about having that one sheep than about the ninety-nine who didnt wander off.
local: A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, o nan na beeri o na-ka, o laan wʋ́ na wʋpolo zanzan kantʋ ŋwaanɩ kʋ dwǝni peeni sɩlʋ na daarɩ yɩ sɩ wʋ je tɩn.

eng: In the same way, my Father who is in heaven doesnt want to lose one of these little ones.
local: Kʋ nan yɩ bɩdwɩ mʋ dɩ abam Ko Wɛ na ba lagɩ sɩ DƖ nɔɔna balʋ na muri tɩn dɩdʋa dɩ je.».

eng: If your brother or sister sins against you, go and correct them when you are alone together. If they listen to you, then youve won over your brother or sister.
local: «Nmʋ ko-bu na kɩ-m lwarɩm, sɩ n vu n kaan-o dɩ o na kɩ kʋlʋ tɩn, sɩ kʋ taa yɩ abam yɩranɩ taanɩ, sɩ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ ta tɔgɩ o tǝrǝ. O nan na sɛ nmʋ na tagɩ kʋlʋ tɩn, kʋ brɩ nɩ n joori n dɩ-o Wɛ cwǝŋǝ kam wʋnɩ mʋ.

eng: But if they wont listen, take with you one or two others so that every word may be established by the mouth of two or three witnesses .
local: O nan na wʋ sɛ sɩ o cǝgi nmʋ taanɩ dɩm, sɩ n lagɩ nɔɔnʋ dɩdʋa naa nɔɔna bale sɩ á tɔgɩ daanɩ á vu o te, sɩ ba bwe-o ba nii, sɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm tagɩ kʋ wɩ: ‹Nɔɔnʋ na jɩgɩ taanɩ dɩ o doŋ, kʋ maŋɩ sɩ nɔɔna bale naa batɔ mʋ taa yɩ o maana tiinǝ.›.

eng: But if they still wont pay attention, report it to the church. If they wont pay attention even to the church, treat them as you would a Gentile and tax collector.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋm nan ta na wʋ sɛ sɩ o cǝgi bantʋ dɩ, sɩ n laan vu n ta dɩ Wɛ-digǝ kɔgɔ kʋm kʋ na yɩ te tɩn. O nan daa ta na wʋ sɛ sɩ o cǝgi á Wɛ-digǝ kɔgɔ kʋm taanɩ dɩm, sɩ n laan ta n nii-o nɩnɛɛnɩ o yɩ nɔn-balɔrɔ kʋlʋ na maŋɩ kʋ yǝri Wɛ tɩn mʋ.

eng: I assure you that whatever you fasten on earth will be fastened in heaven. And whatever you loosen on earth will be loosened in heaven.
local: A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ maama abam na pɛ ni sɩ o zʋ Wɛ kɔgɔ kʋm wʋnɩ tɩn, Wɛ wʋ́ zɩgɩ abam kwaga nɩ dɩ kʋntʋ. Yɩ abam na wɩ o bá zʋ, Wɛ daa ta wʋ́ zɩgɩ abam kwaga nɩ dɩ kʋntʋ.

eng: Again I assure you that if two of you agree on earth about anything you ask, then my Father who is in heaven will do it for you.
local: Kʋ wǝli da, abam nɔɔna bale na kɩ ni daanɩ lʋgʋ baŋa nɩ, sɩ á loori woŋo kʋlʋ maama amʋ Ko Wɛ tee nɩ, dɩntʋ wʋ́ kɩ DƖ pa abam.

eng: For where two or three are gathered in my name, Im there with them.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ, jǝgǝ kalʋ maama nɔɔna bale naa batɔ na lagɩ daanɩ amʋ yɩrɩ ŋwaanɩ tɩn, amʋ wʋ ba tɩtarɩ nɩ.».

eng: Then Peter said to Jesus, Lord, how many times should I forgive my brother or sister who sins against me? Should I forgive as many as seven times.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ laan ma vu Zezi te, yɩ o bwe-o o wɩ: «A Yuutu, amʋ ko-bu na kɩ-nɩ lwarɩm, kuni bɩgra mʋ a maŋɩ sɩ a yagɩ a ma cɛ-o? Kʋ maŋɩ sɩ kʋ yi kuni bɩrpɛ mʋ naa?».

eng: Jesus said, Not just seven times, but rather as many as seventy-seven times.
local: Zezi ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «Awo, kʋ daɩ kuni bɩrpɛ yɩranɩ, kʋ maŋɩ sɩ kʋ taa yɩ fusɩrpɛ kuni bɩrpɛ mʋ.

eng: Therefore, the kingdom of heaven is like a king who wanted to settle accounts with his servants.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm nyɩ nɩnɛɛnɩ pɛ wʋdoŋ na lagɩ o jeeli o sǝbu o tɩntʋŋna tee nɩ o nii te tɩn mʋ.

eng: When he began to settle accounts, they brought to him a servant who owed him ten thousand bags of gold.
local: O na puli sɩ o jeeli-kʋ tɩn, ba laan ma ja o tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋdoŋ na jɩgɩ o jɩnɩ zanzan nɩnɛɛnɩ sǝbu-dala mʋrr-fugǝ te tɩn ba ba o te.

eng: Because the servant didnt have enough to pay it back, the master ordered that he should be sold, along with his wife and children and everything he had, and that the proceeds should be used as payment.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋm kʋntʋ maa warɩ o ŋwɩ o jɩnɩ dɩm. Pɛ wʋm laan ma pa ni sɩ ba ja nɔɔnʋ wʋm dɩ o kaanɩ dɩ o biǝ ba yǝgi, kʋ wǝli dɩ o wǝǝnu maama, sɩ ba ma sǝbu kʋm kʋntʋ ba ŋwɩ jɩnɩ dɩm.

eng: But the servant fell down, kneeled before him, and said, Please, be patient with me, and Ill pay you back.
local: Tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋm na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma vu o kuni doonǝ pɛ wʋm yigǝ nɩ o loor-o o wɩ: ‹Amʋ tu, popo ja n banɩ dɩ amʋ, sɩ amʋ wʋ́ ŋwɩ n jɩnɩ dɩm maama a pa-m.›.

eng: The master had compassion on that servant, released him, and forgave the loan.
local: Pɛ wʋm ma duri o ŋwaŋa yɩ o yagɩ jɩnɩ dɩm o ma cɛ-o, yɩ o daa wʋ kɩ-o kʋlʋkʋlʋ.

eng: When that servant went out, he found one of his fellow servants who owed him one hundred coins. He grabbed him around the throat and said, Pay me back what you owe me.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋm kʋntʋ laan ma nuŋi o vu o jeeri tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋdoŋ na jɩgɩ o jɩnɩ fɩnfɩɩn nɩnɛɛnɩ sǝbu-dalɩ dɩdʋa te tɩn. O laan ma sɔ nɔɔnʋ wʋm ba, yɩ o fɩ-o sɩ o ŋwɩ o jɩnɩ dɩm.

eng: Then his fellow servant fell down and begged him, Be patient with me, and Ill pay you back.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋm ma kuni doonǝ o doŋ tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋm yigǝ nɩ o loor-o o wɩ: ‹Popo ja n banɩ dɩ amʋ, sɩ amʋ wʋ́ ŋwɩ n jɩnɩ dɩm a pa-m.›.

eng: But he refused. Instead, he threw him into prison until he paid back his debt.
local: O ma wʋ sɛ, yɩ o ja nɔɔnʋ wʋm o vu o kɩ pɩɩna digǝ nɩ, sɩ kʋ vu kʋ yi maŋa kam o na wʋ́ ŋwɩ o jɩnɩ dɩm tɩn.

eng: When his fellow servants saw what happened, they were deeply offended. They came and told their master all that happened.
local: Pɛ wʋm tɩntʋŋna badonnǝ na nɛ kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ ma pa ba wʋrʋ tiini tɩ cɔgɩ. Ba laan ma vu ba ta ba brɩ pɛ wʋm kʋlʋ maama na kɩ tɩn.

eng: His master called the first servant and said, You wicked servant! I forgave you all that debt because you appealed to me.
local: Pɛ wʋm laan ma bǝŋi o tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋm kʋntʋ o ta dɩd-o o wɩ: ‹Nmʋ yɩ nɔn-balɔrɔ mʋ. Amʋ yagɩ nmʋ jɩ-farɩ dɩm maama mʋ a ma cɛ-m, nmʋ na loori-nɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: Shouldnt you also have mercy on your fellow servant, just as I had mercy on you.
local: Kʋ ya maŋɩ sɩ nmʋ dɩ duri n doŋ wʋm ŋwaŋa, nɩnɛɛnɩ a na duri nmʋ ŋwaŋa te tɩn.›.

eng: His master was furious and handed him over to the guard responsible for punishing prisoners, until he had paid the whole debt.
local: Pɛ wʋm banɩ ma tiini dɩ zaŋɩ zanzan dɩ o tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋm. O ma kɩ-o o tɩntʋŋna bam jɩŋa nɩ, sɩ ba pa o na cam, sɩ kʋ vu kʋ yi maŋa kalʋ o na wʋ́ ŋwɩ o jɩnɩ dɩm maama tɩn.

eng: My heavenly Father will also do the same to you if you dont forgive your brother or sister from your heart.
local: Kʋntʋ tɩn, abam wʋlʋ maama na wʋ yagɩ o ma cɛ o ko-bu dɩ o wʋ maama tɩn, amʋ Ko Wɛ dɩ wʋ́ pa abam na cam kʋntʋ doŋ mʋ.».

eng: When they approached Jerusalem and came to Bethphage on the Mount of Olives, Jesus gave two disciples a task.
local: Zezi dɩ o karabiǝ bam dɛɛn ma vu ba twɛ Zeruzalɛm. Ba ma vu ba yi tɩʋ kʋdoŋ, kʋ yɩrɩ mʋ Betifaazɩ. Kʋ wʋ Olivi piu kʋm saŋa nɩ mʋ.

eng: He said to them, Go into the village over there. As soon as you enter, you will find a donkey tied up and a colt with it. Untie them and bring them to me.
local: Zezi laan ma lɩ o karabiǝ bale yɩ o wɩ: «Ve-na á vu á zʋ tɩʋ kʋlʋ na wʋ abam yigǝ nɩ tɩn. Á na yi da, á wʋ́ na bɩnaga na lǝgi da, yɩ bɩna-pɔla dɩ tɔgɩ ka wʋra. Á na nɛa, sɩ á bwǝli-sɩ á ja ba á pa-nɩ.

eng: If anyone says anything to you, say that their master needs them. He sent them off right away.
local: Nɔɔnʋ na bwe abam sɩ woŋo, sɩ á ta dɩd-o nɩ, kʋ yɩ dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm mʋ lagɩ-sɩ. Kʋ tu wʋ́ sɛ o yagɩ o pa abam, yɩ á ja á ba.».

eng: Now this happened to fulfill what the prophet said.
local: Kʋ kɩ kʋntʋ, sɩ kʋ pa Wɛ nijoŋnu wʋdoŋ dɛɛn na tagɩ kʋlʋ faŋa faŋa tɩn mʋ sɩɩnɩ kʋ kɩ. O dɛɛn tagɩ o wɩ:.

eng: Say to Daughter Zion, Look, your king is coming to you, humble and riding on a donkey, and on a colt the donkeys offspring. .
local: «Ve-na á ta dɩ Siyɔn tiinǝ bam nɩ: ‹Nii-na á pɛ wʋm, o maa bɩɩnɩ á te. Wʋntʋ tu o tɩtɩ mʋ, yɩ o di bɩnaga o maa bɩɩna. Ka yɩ bɩna-pɔla mʋ o diinǝ, yɩ ka nu mɩ zɩla zɩŋɩm.› ».

eng: The disciples went and did just as Jesus had ordered them.
local: Zezi karabiǝ bale bam ma sɩɩnɩ ba tɔgɩ yigǝ, ba vu ba kɩ kʋlʋ o na tagɩ sɩ ba kɩ tɩn.

eng: They brought the donkey and the colt and laid their clothes on them. Then he sat on them.
local: Ba ma ja bɩna-kwɩʋn kʋm dɩ bɩna-pɔla kam ba ba, yɩ ba lɩ ba gwaarʋ ba daŋɩ sɩ baŋa nɩ. Zezi laan ma di bɩna-pɔla kam, yɩ ba ve ba maa kɛa.

eng: Now a large crowd spread their clothes on the road. Others cut palm branches off the trees and spread them on the road.
local: Ba na maa kɛ tɩn, kɔ-fɔrɔ maa tɔgɩ-ba, yɩ ba zanzan lɩ ba gwaarʋ ba lwarɩ cwǝŋǝ kam yuu nɩ, sɩ Zezi tɔgɩ tɩ baŋa o kɛ. Badaara dɩ ma goni vɔɔrʋ ba dɩ cwǝŋǝ kam yuu nɩ, sɩ o tɔgɩ tɩ baŋa o kɛ.

eng: The crowds in front of him and behind him shouted, Hosanna to the Son of David! Blessings on the one who comes in the name of the Lord! Hosanna in the highest.
local: Nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋlʋ na wʋ o yigǝ nɩ dɩ o kwaga nɩ ba ve tɩn maa kaasa, yɩ ba tɛ ba wɩ: «Ozaana! Zulǝ taa wʋ Pɛ Davidi dwi tu wʋm tee nɩ! Zuli-na wʋlʋ na tu dɩ́ Yuutu Wɛ yɩrɩ ŋwaanɩ tɩn. Ozaana! Zulǝ taa wʋ Baŋa-Wɛ tee nɩ!».

eng: And when Jesus entered Jerusalem, the whole city was stirred up. Who is this? they asked.
local: Ba dɛɛn ma vu ba maa yi Zeruzalɛm tɩʋ kʋm ni. Tɩʋ kʋm tiinǝ bam maama ma zaŋɩ ba yɩ zulimaa ba sirigi je sɩm maama. Ba maa bwe ba wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ wɔɔ mʋ tɩntʋ?».

eng: The crowds answered, Its the prophet Jesus from Nazareth in Galilee.
local: Nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋlʋ na tɔgɩ Zezi tɩn maa tɛ ba wɩ: «Wʋntʋ yɩ Wɛ nijoŋnu Zezi mʋ, o na nuŋi Galile tɩʋ Nazarɛtɩ tɩn.».

eng: Then Jesus went into the temple and threw out all those who were selling and buying there. He pushed over the tables used for currency exchange and the chairs of those who sold doves.
local: Zezi dɛɛn ma vu o zʋ Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm wʋ. O ma na nɔɔna na wʋ ka kʋnkɔlɔ kʋm nɩ ba pipiǝ. O ma zǝli-ba o pa ba nuŋi. O ma vu o ja sǝbu-lǝnnǝ bam taabʋllʋ tɩm o pipiri o pu tɩga nɩ, o pa ba sǝbiǝ bam jagɩ. O ma daarɩ o yigi yitunnu tɩlʋ nɔɔna badonnǝ na je da ba yǝgi kunkwǝn-pulǝ tɩn o dɩ tɩga nɩ.

eng: He said to them, Its written, My house will be called a house of prayer. But youve made it a hideout for crooks.
local: O na wʋra o zǝli-ba kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Kʋ pʋpʋnɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ, nɩ Wɛ mʋ wɩ: ‹Amʋ digǝ kantʋ maŋɩ sɩ ka taa yɩ jǝgǝ kalʋ nɔɔna na wʋ́ ta tui ba warɩ amʋ da tɩn mʋ.› Abam nan pɛ kʋ ji ŋwɩɩna joro je.».

eng: People who were blind and lame came to Jesus in the temple, and he healed them.
local: Lilwǝ dɩ kwaarʋ dɛɛn ma ba Zezi te Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm wʋnɩ, yɩ o pa ba na yazurǝ.

eng: But when the chief priests and legal experts saw the amazing things he was doing and the children shouting in the temple, Hosanna to the Son of David! they were angry.
local: Zwifǝ kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam dɩ ba cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ bam ma na wo-kɩnkagɩla yalʋ o na kɩ tɩn, yɩ ba daarɩ ba na biǝ na zɩgɩ Wɛ-digǝ kam kʋnkɔlɔ kʋm nɩ ba kaasɩ ba wɩ: «Ozaana! Zulǝ taa wʋ Pɛ Davidi dwi tu wʋm tee nɩ!» Ba na nɛ kʋntʋ tɩn, ba bana ma zaŋɩ zanzan.

eng: They said to Jesus, Do you hear what these children are saying? Yes, he answered. Havent you ever read, From the mouths of babies and infants youve arranged praise for yourself? .
local: Ba ma ta dɩ Zezi ba wɩ: «Nmʋ ba ni biǝ bam na tɛ nmʋ taanɩ te? N bá cɩ-ba sɩ ba yɩ taa tɛ kʋntʋ na?» Zezi ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «A ye ba na tɛ kʋlʋ tɩn. Kʋ nan pʋpʋnɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ kʋ wɩ: ‹Nmʋ Wɛ mʋ pɛ biǝ dɩ bu-sɩsɩn zuli nmʋ dɩ ba niǝ.› Abam maŋɩ á wʋ karɩmɩ kʋntʋ na?».

eng: Then he left them and went out of the city to Bethany and spent the night there.
local: O laan ma zaŋɩ ba tee nɩ o viiri. O ma nuŋi tɩʋ kʋm nɩ o vu Betani, yɩ o pǝni dáanɩ.

eng: Early in the morning as Jesus was returning to the city, he was hungry.
local: Tɩga na pʋʋrɩ tɩtɩɩtɩ tɩn, o ma zaŋɩ sɩ o joori o vu Zeruzalɛm. O na wʋ cwǝŋǝ nɩ tɩn, o kana maa wɔɛ.

eng: He saw a fig tree along the road, but when he came to it, he found nothing except leaves. Then he said to it, Youll never again bear fruit! The fig tree dried up at once.
local: O ma na kapʋrʋ na zɩgɩ cwǝŋǝ kam daa nɩ. O ma vu sɩ o nii, yɩ o piini dɩ vɔɔrʋ yɩranɩ mʋ wʋra, yɩ mɔɔla tǝrǝ sɩ o di. O ma ta dɩ kapʋrʋ kʋm o wɩ: «N daa n bá joori n lǝ biǝ maŋa dɩ maŋa.» O na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, kapʋrʋ kʋm ma da kʋ kwaarɩ bɩdwɩ baŋa nɩ.

eng: When the disciples saw it, they were amazed. How did the fig tree dry up so fast? they asked.
local: Zezi karabiǝ bam na nɛ kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ ma sʋ-ba. Ba ma bwɛ ba wɩ: «Kʋ kɩ ta mʋ yɩ kapʋrʋ kʋm da kʋ kwaarɩ lɩla kʋntʋ?».

eng: Jesus responded, I assure you that if you have faith and dont doubt, you will not only do what was done to the fig tree. You will even say to this mountain, Be lifted up and thrown into the lake. And it will happen.
local: Zezi ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, á na kɩ á wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ amʋ yɩ á ba jɩgɩ bʋbwɛa, abam dɩ wʋ́ wanɩ á kɩ kʋlʋ na kɩ kapʋrʋ kʋm tɩn doŋ. Kʋ nan daɩ kʋntʋ yɩranɩ má. Á wʋ́ wanɩ á ta dɩ pu-kamunu kʋntʋ nɩ, kʋ gɔ kʋ na wʋ mɛ tɩn kʋ vu kʋ tʋ nɩnɩʋ kʋm wʋnɩ, yɩ Wɛ nan wʋ́ pa kʋ kɩ.

eng: If you have faith, you will receive whatever you pray for.
local: Kʋlʋ na wǝli da tɩn, abam na loori woŋo kʋlʋ maama Wɛ tee nɩ, yɩ á na kɩ wʋ-dɩdʋa nɩ á wʋ́ na, Wɛ wʋ́ pa á sɩɩnɩ á na-kʋ.».

eng: When Jesus entered the temple, the chief priests and elders of the people came to him as he was teaching. They asked, What kind of authority do you have for doing these things? Who gave you this authority.
local: Zezi dɛɛn ma joori o vu o zʋ Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm wʋ. O maa wʋra o brɩ nɔɔna bam. Zwifǝ nakwa bam dɩ ba kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam ma vu o te ba bwe-o ba wɩ: «Wɔɔ dam mʋ n ma n kɩ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ maama n na kɩ tɩn? Wɔɔ mʋ pɛ nmʋ ni sɩ n ta n kɩ kʋntʋ?».

eng: Jesus replied, I have a question for you. If you tell me the answer, Ill tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: O ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «A dɩ jɩgɩ bwiǝ a lagɩ a bwe abam. Á na lǝri á pa-nɩ, sɩ a laan brɩ abam wʋlʋ na pɛ amʋ ni sɩ a tʋŋɩ tɩn.

eng: Where did John get his authority to baptize? Did he get it from heaven or from humans? They argued among themselves, If we say from heaven, hell say to us, Then why didnt you believe him.
local: A lagɩ a bwe abam a nii, Zan na miisi nɔɔna na wʋnɩ tɩn, kʋ yɩ Wɛ mʋ pɛ-o cwǝŋǝ naa nabiinǝ mʋ?» O na bwe-ba kʋntʋ tɩn, ba maa wʋra ba bwɛ daanɩ sɩ ba nii, ba na wʋ́ lǝr-o te tɩn. Ba maa wɩ: «Dɩbam na lǝri dɩ́ wɩ, kʋ yɩ Wɛ mʋ, o laan wʋ́ bwe dɩbam o nii, bɛɛ mʋ yɩ dɩ́ nan wʋ sɛ Zan taanɩ dɩm?.

eng: But we cant say from humans because were afraid of the crowd, since everyone thinks John was a prophet.
local: Kʋ daarɩ, dɩ́ na wɩ kʋ yɩ nabiinǝ mʋ pɛ-o cwǝŋǝ, nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm wʋ́ ja dɩ dɩbam, dɩ ba maama na paɩ Zan yɩ Wɛ nijoŋnu tɩn ŋwaanɩ.».

eng: Then they replied, We dont know. Jesus also said to them, Neither will I tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Ba na banɩ daanɩ ba ga kʋlʋ ba na wʋ́ ta tɩn, ba ma lǝri Zezi ba wɩ: «Dɩ́ yǝri wʋlʋ na pɛ Zan cwǝŋǝ tɩn.» Zezi dɩ ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Kʋ na yɩ kʋntʋ, amʋ dɩ nan bá ta wʋlʋ na pɛ amʋ ni sɩ a taa tʋŋɩ tɩn.».

eng: What do you think? A man had two sons. Now he came to the first and said, Son, go and work in the vineyard today.
local: Zezi laan ma ta o wɩ: «A lagɩ a maŋɩ mɩmaŋɩ dɩdoŋ a brɩ abam, sɩ á bʋŋɩ á nii. Nɔɔnʋ mʋ wʋra dɩ o biǝ bale. O ma ta dɩ bu-kwɩan kam o wɩ: ‹Ve n tʋŋɩ a kara nɩ zɩm dɛ dɩm nɩ.›.

eng: No, I dont want to, he replied. But later he changed his mind and went.
local: Bu wʋm ma lǝr-o o wɩ: ‹A bá vu.› Kʋ na kɩ fɩɩn tɩn, o ma joori o lǝni o wʋbʋŋa yɩ o vu o tʋŋɩ.

eng: The father said the same thing to the other son, who replied, Yes, sir. But he didnt go.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋm daa ma ta dɩ o bu wʋdoŋ wʋm sɩ o dɩ vu o kara kam o tʋŋɩ. Wʋntʋ ma lǝri o wɩ: ‹A ko, aá vu.› Yɩ o laan daarɩ o wʋ ve.

eng: Which one of these two did his fathers will? They said, The first one. Jesus said to them, I assure you that tax collectors and prostitutes are entering Gods kingdom ahead of you.
local: Kʋntʋ tɩn, biǝ bam kʋntʋ bale wʋnɩ, wɔɔ mʋ sɩɩnɩ o kɩ ba ko wʋm na lagɩ kʋlʋ tɩn?» Ba ma lǝr-o ba wɩ: «Kʋ yɩ bu nakwɩ wʋm mʋ.» Ba na lǝri kʋntʋ tɩn, Zezi laan ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, nɔn-cɩcɔgɩrʋ, kʋ na yɩ lampo-joŋnǝ dɩ ka-bwǝǝru, wʋ́ zʋ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋnɩ ba daarɩ abam.

eng: For John came to you on the righteous road, and you didnt believe him. But tax collectors and prostitutes believed him. Yet even after you saw this, you didnt change your hearts and lives and you didnt believe him.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ, Zan na tu abam te tɩn, o dɛɛn brɩ abam cwǝŋǝ kalʋ á na wʋ́ da sɩ á na cɩga Wɛ tee nɩ tɩn, yɩ á wʋ sɛ o taanɩ dɩm. Nɔn-cɩcɔgɩrʋ tɩm nan sɛ-o yɩ tɩ lǝni tɩ wʋrʋ tɩ yagɩ kǝm-balwaarʋ tɩm. Abam nan nɛ bantʋ na kɩ te tɩn, yɩ á daa ta wʋ sɛ sɩ á lǝni á wʋbʋŋa sɩ á sɛ o taanɩ dɩm.».

eng: Listen to another parable. There was a landowner who planted a vineyard. He put a fence around it, dug a winepress in it, and built a tower. Then he rented it to tenant farmers and took a trip.
local: Zezi daa ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Cǝgi-na, sɩ a daa maŋɩ mɩmaŋɩ dɩdoŋ a brɩ abam. Sɔŋɔ tu wʋdoŋ mʋ wʋra. O ma zaŋɩ o cɩ gaarɩ, yɩ o daarɩ o jǝri tweeru tɩlʋ yɩrɩ na yɩ vinyǝ tɩn dɩ wʋnɩ. O ma daarɩ o kʋ goŋo tɩ-dɩa je nɩ, sɩ o na gwǝri tweeru tɩm biǝ bam, sɩ o kɩ dáanɩ o nɔ o ka tɩ na bam. O ma daarɩ o cwi dapala sɩ nɔɔnʋ taa je da o yɩrɩ gaarɩ dɩm. O na fɔgɩ o cɩ-dɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, o laan ma kwe nɔɔna o yagɩ da sɩ ba taa tʋŋa, yɩ o daarɩ o beeri o vu tɩʋ kʋdoŋ.

eng: When it was time for harvest, he sent his servants to the tenant farmers to collect his fruit.
local: Kʋ na yi tweeru tɩm biǝ bam gwǝrim maŋa tɩn, o ma tʋŋɩ o tɩntʋŋna, sɩ ba vu nɔɔna balʋ na nii o gaarɩ dɩm baŋa nɩ tɩn te, sɩ ba joŋi tweeru tɩm biǝ bam ba ja ba ba pa-o.

eng: But the tenant farmers grabbed his servants. They beat some of them, and some of them they killed. Some of them they stoned to death.
local: O tɩntʋŋna bam na tu tɩn, nɔɔna bam ma ja-ba, ba magɩ dɩdʋa lanyɩranɩ, ba gʋ wʋdoŋ, yɩ ba daarɩ ba dʋlɩ dɩdʋa dɩ kandwa ba gʋ.

eng: Again he sent other servants, more than the first group. They treated them in the same way.
local: Gaarɩ dɩm tu wʋm ta ma joori o tʋŋɩ o tɩntʋŋna badonnǝ nɔɔna bam tee nɩ. Bantʋ kɔgɔ dwe dayigǝ nɔɔna bam. Nɔɔna bam daa ta ma kɩ bantʋ dɩ kʋntʋ doŋ.

eng: Finally he sent his son to them. They will respect my son, he said.
local: Kʋ kweelim nɩ tɩn, o laan ma tʋŋɩ o tɩtɩ bu nɔɔna bam tee nɩ, yɩ o bʋŋɩ nɩ, baá nɩgɩ wʋntʋ.

eng: But when the tenant farmers saw the son, they said to each other, This is the heir. Come on, lets kill him and well have his inheritance.
