local: O bu wʋm na maa ve yɩ nɔɔna bam na-o tɩn, ba maa ŋɔɔnɩ daanɩ ba wɩ: ‹Mʋ gaarɩ dɩm tu bu, wʋntʋ mʋ lagɩ o ba o taa te o ko wʋm wǝǝnu tɩm. Nan pa-na dɩ́ gʋ-o, sɩ dɩ́ daarɩ dɩ́ taa te o wǝǝnu tɩm.›.

eng: They grabbed him, threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him.
local: Ba laan ma ja-o ba vaŋɩ ba ja nuŋi gaarɩ dɩm wʋnɩ, ba mag-o ba gʋ.

eng: When the owner of the vineyard comes, what will he do to those tenant farmers.
local: Kʋntʋ tɩn, gaarɩ dɩm tu wʋm laan na tuǝ, oó kɩ nɔɔna bam kʋntʋ tɩta mʋ?».

eng: They said, He will totally destroy those wicked farmers and rent the vineyard to other tenant farmers who will give him the fruit when its ready.
local: Zezi na bwe-ba kʋntʋ tɩn, ba laan ma lǝri ba wɩ: «O maŋɩ sɩ o gʋ nɔn-balwaarʋ tɩm kʋntʋ mʋ, sɩ o daarɩ o kwe gaarɩ dɩm o pa nɔɔna badonnǝ sɩ ba taa nii-dɩ, sɩ tweeru tɩm biǝ bam gwǝrim maŋa na yiǝ, sɩ ba taa pa-o o tweeru tɩm biǝ bam.».

eng: Jesus said to them, Havent you ever read in the scriptures, The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone. The Lord has done this, and its amazing in our eyes?.
local: Zezi daa ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Kʋ pʋpʋnɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ kʋ wɩ: ‹Kandwɛ dɩlʋ lwara na vɩn tɩn laan mʋ joori dɩ ba dɩ ji kandwɛ dɩlʋ na paɩ sɔŋɔ kʋm dana tɩn. Kʋntʋ yɩ Baŋa-Wɛ mʋ kɩa, yɩ kʋ yɩ wo-kamunu kʋ pa dɩbam.› Abam maŋɩ á wʋ karɩmɩ kʋntʋ na?.

eng: Therefore, I tell you that Gods kingdom will be taken away from you and will be given to a people who produce its fruit.
local: Kʋ brɩ nɩ Wɛ laan wʋ́ joŋi DƖ paarɩ dɩm abam tee nɩ, yɩ DƖ daarɩ DƖ pa balʋ na sɛ DƖ ni tɩn na cwǝŋǝ ba zʋ DƖ paarɩ dɩm wʋ. .

eng: Whoever falls on this stone will be crushed. And the stone will crush the person it falls on.
local: Kʋ na yɩ kandwɛ dɩlʋ Wɛ na kuri tɩn, nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ maama na tʋ dɩntʋ baŋa nɩ tɩn, kʋntʋ tu wʋ́ tiini o cʋcʋgɩ o tɩtɩ mʋ. Kʋ daarɩ, wʋlʋ maama kandwɛ dɩm na tʋ o baŋa nɩ tɩn, dɩ wʋ́ mɩɩrɩ kʋntʋ tu mʋ.».

eng: Now when the chief priests and the Pharisees heard the parable, they knew Jesus was talking about them.
local: Zezi dɛɛn na maŋɩ mɩmaŋa yam kʋntʋ maama tɩn, kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam dɩ Farizɩan tiinǝ bam ma lwarɩ nɩ kʋ yɩ bantʋ taanɩ mʋ o tɛa.

eng: They were trying to arrest him, but they feared the crowds, who thought he was a prophet.
local: Ba maa kwaanɩ ba lagɩ cwǝŋǝ sɩ ba ja-o, yɩ ba kwarɩ fʋʋnɩ dɩ nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm, bantʋ na pɛ o yɩ Wɛ nijoŋnu tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: Early in the morning all the chief priests and the elders of the people reached the decision to have Jesus put to death.
local: Tɩga na tu ka pʋʋrɩ tɩtɩɩtɩ tɩn, Zwifǝ kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam dɩ ba tɩʋ kʋm nakwa bam maama ma kɩ ni daanɩ, sɩ ba pa ba gʋ Zezi.

eng: They bound him, led him away, and turned him over to Pilate the governor.
local: Ba ma ja-o ba vɔ, yɩ ba ja-o ba vu ba kɩ gʋvɩrma tu Pilatɩ jɩŋa nɩ.

eng: When Judas, who betrayed Jesus, saw that Jesus was condemned to die, he felt deep regret. He returned the thirty pieces of silver to the chief priests and elders, and.
local: Zudasɩ wʋlʋ na yǝgi Zezi o pa nɔɔna bam tɩn na lwarɩ nɩ ba di o sarɩya yɩ ba lagɩ ba gʋ-o tɩn, o wʋ laan ma cɔgɩ dɩ o na kɩ kʋlʋ tɩn, yɩ o joori o kwe sǝbu-dala fiintɔ kʋm, sɩ o pa kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam dɩ nakwa bam.

eng: said, I did wrong because I betrayed an innocent man. But they said, What is that to us? Thats your problem.
local: O ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Amʋ kɩ wo-balɔrɔ, dɩ a na yǝgi nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na wʋ kɩ kʋlʋkʋlʋ tɩn a pa abam yɩ ba lagɩ ba gʋ-o tɩn.» Ba ma lǝr-o ba wɩ: «Dɩbam yigǝ tǝrǝ, kʋ tɔgɩ dɩ n tɩtɩ.».

eng: Judas threw the silver pieces into the temple and left. Then he went and hanged himself.
local: O laan ma kwe sǝbu kʋm o vu o dɩ o yagɩ Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm wʋnɩ, yɩ o daarɩ o vu o kuuri ŋʋna o tɩ.

eng: The chief priests picked up the silver pieces and said, According to the Law its not right to put this money in the treasury. Since it was used to pay for someones life, its unclean.
local: Kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam dɛɛn ma kwe sǝbu kʋm yɩ ba wɩ: «Sǝbu kʋntʋ yɩ jana nyɩm mʋ, kʋ culǝ sɩ dɩ́ gwaanɩ-kʋ Wɛ-digǝ sǝbiǝ bam wʋnɩ.».

eng: So they decided to use it to buy the potters field where strangers could be buried.
local: Ba ma bwɛ daanɩ, yɩ ba ma-kʋ ba yǝgi tɩga kadoŋ, ka yɩrɩ mʋ Kamɔ-mɔrʋ tɩga, yɩ ba ma kɩ vǝrǝ yibeelǝ jǝgǝ.

eng: Thats why that field is called Field of Blood to this very day.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ nɔɔna jɩgɩ jǝgǝ kam kʋntʋ ba ma bǝ nɩ Jana tɩga, sɩ kʋ ba kʋ yi zɩm.

eng: This fulfilled the words of Jeremiah the prophet: And I took the thirty pieces of silver, the price for the one whose price had been set by some of the Israelites.
local: Kʋntʋ ma pa Wɛ nijoŋnu Zeremi dɛɛn na tagɩ kʋlʋ faŋa faŋa tɩn ba sɩɩnɩ kʋ kɩ. O dɛɛn tagɩ o wɩ: «Ba kwe sǝbu-dala fiintɔ mʋ, ba ma yǝgi kamɔ-mɔrʋ tɩga kam, nɩ Baŋa-Wɛ dɛɛn na pɛ-nɩ ni, sɩ a ta te tɩn. Kʋ maa yɩ sǝbu kʋlʋ Yisɩrayɛlɩ dwi tiinǝ bam na sɛ sɩ ba ma yǝgi nɔɔnʋ wʋm tɩn mʋ.».

eng: Jesus was brought before the governor. The governor said, Are you the king of the Jews? Jesus replied, Thats what you say.
local: Zezi ma zaŋɩ o zɩgɩ gʋvɩrma tu Pilatɩ yigǝ nɩ. Gʋvɩrma tu wʋm ma bwe-o o wɩ: «Nmʋ mʋ yɩ Zwifǝ bam pɛ wʋm na?» Zezi ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «Mʋ ka da n taga.».

eng: But he didnt answer when the chief priests and elders accused him.
local: Kʋ daarɩ Zwifǝ kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam dɩ ba nakwa bam na ŋɔɔnɩ taanɩ dɩlʋ maama ba pa-o tɩn, o wʋ lǝri kʋlʋkʋlʋ.

eng: Then Pilate said, Dont you hear the testimony they bring against you.
local: Pilatɩ ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Nmʋ wʋ ni ba na ŋɔɔnɩ kʋlʋ maama ba pa nmʋ tɩn na?».

eng: But he didnt answer, not even a single word. So the governor was greatly amazed.
local: Zezi ma wʋ sɛ sɩ o lǝri ba taanɩ dɩm dɩlʋdɩlʋ. Kʋ ma pa gʋvɩrma tu wʋm tiini o yɩ yǝǝu dɩd-o.

eng: It was customary during the festival for the governor to release to the crowd one prisoner, whomever they might choose.
local: Bɩm maama Pakɩ candiǝ kam maŋa na yǝni ka yi, gʋvɩrma tu wʋm yǝni o lɩ pɩɩnʋ dɩdʋa mʋ o pa nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm, nɩnɛɛnɩ bantʋ na lagɩ wʋlʋ tɩn.

eng: At that time there was a well-known prisoner named Jesus Barabbas.
local: Kantʋ maŋa kam nɩ pɩɩnʋ wʋdoŋ maa wʋra, o yɩrɩ mʋ Barabasɩ. Nɔɔna maama dɛɛn ye o kǝm-balwaarʋ tɩm ni nɩ.

eng: When the crowd had come together, Pilate asked them, Whom would you like me to release to you, Jesus Barabbas or Jesus who is called Christ.
local: Nɔɔna bam na tu ba la daanɩ tɩn, Pilatɩ ma bwe-ba o wɩ: «Abam lagɩ sɩ a yagɩ bantʋ wɔɔ mʋ a pa abam? Kʋ yɩ Barabasɩ mʋ naa Zezi wʋlʋ ba na bǝ-o nɩ Krisi tɩn mʋ?».

eng: He knew that the leaders of the people had handed him over because of jealousy.
local: O dɛɛn bwe-ba kʋntʋ, bɛŋwaanɩ o ye nɩ, kʋ yɩ wʋ-gʋrʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ ba jaanɩ Zezi ba kɩ o jɩŋa nɩ.

eng: While he was serving as judge, his wife sent this message to him, Leave that righteous man alone. Ive suffered much today in a dream because of him.
local: Pilatɩ na je sarɩya dim jǝgǝ kam nɩ tɩn, o kaanɩ ma tʋŋɩ ni o pa-o o wɩ: «Yɩ zaŋɩ n kɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋm kʋntʋ kʋlʋkʋlʋ, o na jɩgɩ cɩga tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Bɛŋwaanɩ tɩtɩɩ dɩntʋ nɩ amʋ tiini a na yaara zanzan dɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋm kʋntʋ dɩndwɩa wʋnɩ.».

eng: But the chief priests and the elders persuaded the crowds to ask for Barabbas and kill Jesus.
local: Zwifǝ kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam dɩ ba nakwa bam ma sʋgɩ nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm, sɩ ba ta dɩ Pilatɩ nɩ, o yagɩ Barabasɩ o pa-ba, sɩ o daarɩ o pa ba gʋ Zezi.

eng: The governor said, Which of the two do you want me to release to you? Barabbas, they replied.
local: Pilatɩ daa ma joori o bwe-ba o wɩ: «Nɔɔna bantʋ bale wʋnɩ, abam lagɩ sɩ a yagɩ wɔɔ mʋ a pa abam?» Ba ma lǝr-o ba wɩ: «Barabasɩ.».

eng: Pilate said, Then what should I do with Jesus who is called Christ? They all said, Crucify him.
local: Pilatɩ laan ma bwe-ba o wɩ: «Sɩ kʋ na yɩ Zezi wʋlʋ ba na bǝ-o nɩ Krisi tɩn, abam wɩ a kɩ-o ta mʋ?» Ba maama ma ta dɩd-o ba wɩ: «Pa-o tʋʋn-dagara baŋa nɩ n gʋ.».

eng: But he said, Why? What wrong has he done? They shouted even louder, Crucify him.
local: O ma joori o bwe-ba o wɩ: «Wʋntʋ kɩ bɛɛ mʋ o cɔgɩ?» Ba laan ma tiini ba kaasɩ baŋa baŋa ba wɩ: «Pa-o tʋʋn-dagara baŋa nɩ n gʋ.».

eng: Pilate saw that he was getting nowhere and that a riot was starting. So he took water and washed his hands in front of the crowd. Im innocent of this mans blood, he said. Its your problem.
local: Pilatɩ ma lwarɩ nɩ kʋ ba jɩgɩ kuri sɩ o taa ŋɔɔnɩ taan taan, kʋ na lagɩ kʋ ja bʋbwaka kʋ ba tɩn ŋwaanɩ. O ma joŋi na o ma san o jɩa nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm maama yigǝ nɩ, yɩ o wɩ: «A yigǝ tɔgɩ ka tǝri nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ tʋʋnɩ dɩm wʋnɩ. Kʋ yɩ abam yigǝ mʋ.».

eng: All the people replied, Let his blood be on us and on our children.
local: Nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm maama ma lǝr-o ba wɩ: «Yagɩ sɩ o tʋʋnɩ dɩm caa taa tɔgɩ dɩbam dɩ dɩ́ biǝ.».

eng: Then he released Barabbas to them. He had Jesus whipped, then handed him over to be crucified.
local: O laan ma yagɩ Barabasɩ o pa-ba. O ma daarɩ o pa ba magɩ Zezi, yɩ o pa-ba cwǝŋǝ sɩ ba ja-o ba vu ba pa tʋʋn-dagara baŋa nɩ ba gʋ.

eng: The governors soldiers took Jesus into the governors house, and they gathered the whole company of soldiers around him.
local: Gʋvɩrma tu wʋm pamaŋna bam laan ma ja Zezi ba vu ba zʋ o sɔŋɔ kʋm kʋnkɔlɔ. Pamaŋna kɔgɔ kʋm maama ma gilim-o da.

eng: They stripped him and put a red military coat on him.
local: Ba ma lɩ o gwaarʋ tɩm, yɩ ba daarɩ ba zʋ-o gwar-sʋŋʋ.

eng: They twisted together a crown of thorns and put it on his head. They put a stick in his right hand. Then they bowed down in front of him and mocked him, saying, Hey! King of the Jews.
local: Ba ma sɔ sabarɩ yipugǝ ba pu o yuu nɩ. Ba ma kwe miu ba ma ji nacǝgǝ ba kɩ o jazɩm nɩ. Ba laan ma yáal-o ba mwana, yɩ ba ve ba kuni doonǝ o yigǝ nɩ, yɩ ba tɛ ba wɩ: «Zwifǝ bam pɛ, dɩnle.».

eng: After they spit on him, they took the stick and struck his head again and again.
local: Ba ma daarɩ ba twɩ lileeru ba yagɩ o yɩra nɩ, yɩ ba jɩgɩ miu kʋm ba lɔɛ o yuu nɩ.

eng: When they finished mocking him, they stripped him of the military coat and put his own clothes back on him. They led him away to crucify him.
local: Ba na mwan-o ba ti tɩn, ba laan ma lɩ gwar-sʋŋʋ kʋm, yɩ ba daarɩ ba joori ba zʋ-o o tɩtɩ gwaarʋ tɩm. Ba laan ma ja-o ba nuŋi ba vu sɩ ba pa-o tʋʋn-dagara baŋa nɩ.

eng: As they were going out, they found Simon, a man from Cyrene. They forced him to carry his cross.
local: Ba na jɩg-o ba maa kɛ tɩn, ba ma jeeri nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ, o yɩrɩ mʋ Simɔn o nuŋi Sirɛɛnɩ mʋ. Pamaŋna bam ma fɩ-o ba pa o joŋi tʋʋn-dagara kam o zɩŋɩ sɩ ba vu.

eng: When they came to a place called Golgotha, which means Skull Place.
local: Ba maama ma vu ba yi jǝgǝ kadoŋ ba na bǝ sɩ Goligota tɩn. Yɩrɩ dɩm kʋntʋ kuri mʋ Yukol-pʋa piu.

eng: they gave Jesus wine mixed with vinegar to drink. But after tasting it, he didnt want to drink it.
local: Ba na yi da tɩn, ba ma kɩ sana ba gwaanɩ dɩ liri sɩlʋ ni na cana tɩn ba pa Zezi sɩ o nyɔ. O ma laŋɩ o nii, yɩ o wʋ sɛ sɩ o nyɔ.

eng: After they crucified him, they divided up his clothes among them by drawing lots.
local: Ba laan ma ja-o ba pa tʋʋn-dagara kam baŋa nɩ. Ba na pag-o kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma kwe o gwaarʋ tɩm sɩ ba maŋɩ-tɩ ba pa daanɩ. Ba ma ta jɔrɔ sɩ ba nii, wɔɔ mʋ wʋ́ twǝri kʋlʋ o na lagɩ tɩn.

eng: They sat there, guarding him.
local: Ba laan ma je da ba yɩr-o.

eng: They placed above his head the charge against him. It read, This is Jesus, the king of the Jews.
local: Ba dɛɛn ma tanɩ tɔnɔ dagara kam yɩra nɩ Zezi yuu baŋa nɩ, sɩ kʋ brɩ kʋlʋ o na kɩ o cɔgɩ yɩ ba gʋ-o tɩn. Ba dɛɛn pʋpʋnɩ ba wɩ: «Wʋntʋ yɩ Zezi wʋlʋ na yɩ Zwifǝ bam pɛ tɩn mʋ.».

eng: They crucified with him two outlaws, one on his right side and one on his left.
local: Ba ma daarɩ ba ja ŋwɩɩna bale ba pa dagarɩ sɩdonnǝ baŋa nɩ. Ba ma cwi dɩdʋa Zezi jazɩm nɩ. Wʋwʋm maa wʋ o jagwiǝ nɩ.

eng: Those who were walking by insulted Jesus, shaking their heads.
local: Nɔɔna balʋ na tɔgɩ da ba kɛ tɩn maa niǝ, yɩ ba gɔgɩsɩ ba yuni yɩ ba twɩ-o. Ba maa yáal-o.

eng: and saying, So you were going to destroy the temple and rebuild it in three days, were you? Save yourself! If you are Gods Son, come down from the cross.
local: yɩ ba wɩ: «Nmʋ ya na bʋŋɩ sɩ n cɔgɩ Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm n dɩ tɩga nɩ sɩ n daarɩ n ma da yatɔ n joori n fɔgɩ n lɔ-ka tɩn, nmʋ na sɩɩnɩ n yɩ Baŋa-Wɛ Bu, sɩ n laan vrɩ n tɩtɩ, n tu dagara kam yuu nɩ sɩ dɩ́ nii.».

eng: In the same way, the chief priests, along with the legal experts and the elders, were making fun of him, saying.
local: Zwifǝ nakwa bam dɩ ba kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ dɩ ba cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ bam dɩ maa twɩ-o ba mwana, yɩ ba tɛ daanɩ ba wɩ:.

eng: He saved others, but he cant save himself. Hes the king of Israel, so let him come down from the cross now. Then well believe in him.
local: «O yǝni o vrɩ nɔɔna badonnǝ ŋwɩa, yɩ o laan warɩ o tɩtɩ ŋwɩa o vrɩ. O maa brɩ nɩ o yɩ Yisɩrayɛlɩ dwi tiinǝ pɛ mʋ. O laan nan tu dagara kam yuu nɩ lele sɩ dɩ́ nii, sɩ dɩ́ wʋ́ ba dɩ́ sɩɩnɩ dɩ́ sɛ-o.

eng: He trusts in God, so let God deliver him now if he wants to. He said, Im Gods Son.
local: O ta brɩ nɩ o tɩɩgɩ Wɛ, yɩ o tɛ sɩ o yɩ Wɛ Bu mʋ. Kʋntʋ, Wɛ na sɩɩnɩ DƖ lag-o, sɩ DƖ laan joŋ-o DƖ yagɩ.».

eng: The outlaws who were crucified with him insulted him in the same way.
local: Ŋwɩɩna balʋ ba na pagɩ dagarɩ sɩm baŋa nɩ ba wǝli da tɩn dɩ maa jɩg-o ba twɩ kʋntʋ doŋ.

eng: From noon until three in the afternoon the whole earth was dark.
local: Kʋ ma vu kʋ yi maŋa kam wɛ na tu dɩ yi yuparɩ baŋa nɩ tɩn. Lʋgʋ kʋm maama ma ba kʋ ji lim kʋ vu kʋ yi wɛ-gunim maŋa nɩ.

eng: At about three Jesus cried out with a loud shout, Eli, Eli, lama sabachthani , which means, My God, my God, why have you left me.
local: kʋ kɩ nɩnɛɛnɩ luu batɔ mʋ te. Maŋa kam kʋntʋ nɩ Zezi laan ma kaasɩ baŋa baŋa dɩ kwǝr-dɩa o wɩ: «Eli, Eli, lɛma sabakɩtani?» Taanɩ dɩntʋ kuri mʋ: «Amʋ Wɛ, amʋ Wɛ, bɛɛ mʋ kɩ yɩ n yagɩ amʋ?».

eng: After hearing him, some standing there said, Hes calling Elijah.
local: Nɔɔna balʋ na zɩgɩ da yɩ ba ni taanɩ dɩm tɩn ma bʋŋɩ nɩ, o bǝ Eli mʋ sɩ o ba.

eng: One of them ran over, took a sponge full of vinegar, and put it on a pole. He offered it to Jesus to drink.
local: Ba dɩdʋa ma kɩ lɩla o duri o vu o kwe sɩpɔɔ. O ma dɩ-kʋ sa-nywana wʋnɩ, yɩ o daarɩ o kɩ-kʋ miu ni nɩ, o vu o te o pa Zezi sɩ o nyɔ.

eng: But the rest of them said, Lets see if Elijah will come and save him.
local: Nɔɔna badaara bam na nɛ kʋntʋ tɩn, ba maa wɩ: «Yagɩ sɩ dɩ́ cǝgi dɩ́ nii, Eli wʋ́ ba o joŋ-o o yagɩ na?».

eng: Again Jesus cried out with a loud shout. Then he died.
local: Zezi daa ma joori o kaasɩ dɩ kwǝr-dɩa baŋa baŋa. O laan ma pa o siun ti.

eng: Look, the curtain of the sanctuary was torn in two from top to bottom. The earth shook, the rocks split.
local: Zezi na tɩgɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, gɔrɔ kʋlʋ ba na lǝgi ba ma cɩ Wɛ-di-laa kʋm tɩn ma kaarɩ bɩle, kʋ zɩgɩ baŋa nɩ kʋ tu kʋ kwaarɩ kʋ kuri. Tɩga kam maa sisiŋǝ, yɩ pulwaarʋ bagɩ tɩ yarɩ.

eng: and the bodies of many holy people who had died were raised.
local: Yibeelǝ yam ma puri. Kʋ daarɩ Wɛ nɔn-ŋʋna balʋ ya na tɩgɩ tɩn zanzan ma joori ba bi ba yagɩ tʋʋnɩ.

eng: After Jesus resurrection they came out of their graves and went into the holy city where they appeared to many people.
local: yɩ ba nuŋi ba yibeelǝ wʋnɩ, maŋa kalʋ Zezi tɩtɩ na tu o bi o yagɩ tʋʋnɩ tɩn kwaga nɩ. Bantʋ laan ma vu ba zʋ Wɛ tɩtɩ tɩʋ Zeruzalɛm wʋ ba pa nɔɔna zanzan na-ba.

eng: When the centurion and those with him who were guarding Jesus saw the earthquake and what had just happened, they were filled with awe and said, This was certainly Gods Son.
local: Pamaŋna bam yigǝ tu wʋm dɩ o nɔɔna balʋ na wʋra ba yɩrɩ Zezi tɩn ma na tɩga kam sisiŋim dɩm, dɩ kʋlʋ maama na kɩ tɩn. Fʋʋnɩ ma tiini dɩ zʋ-ba, yɩ ba tɛ ba wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ ya sɩɩnɩ o yɩ Baŋa-Wɛ Bu mʋ.».

eng: Many women were watching from a distance. They had followed Jesus from Galilee to serve him.
local: Kaana zanzan dɩ tɔgɩ ba wʋra, ba ma zɩgɩ yigǝ yigǝ yɩ ba niǝ. Bantʋ dɛɛn ya tɔgɩ dɩ Zezi mʋ ba nuŋi Galile ba ba, yɩ ba yǝni ba tɔgɩ ba zǝn-o.

eng: Among them were Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James and Joseph, and the mother of Zebedees sons.
local: Mari wʋlʋ na nuŋi Magɩdala tɩn dɛɛn tɔgɩ o wʋ kaana bam kʋntʋ wʋnɩ, dɩdaanɩ Mari wʋlʋ na yɩ Zakɩ dɩ Zʋzɛfʋ nu tɩn, kʋ wǝli dɩ Zebede biǝ bam nu.

eng: That evening a man named Joseph came. He was a rich man from Arimathea who had become a disciple of Jesus.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ dɛɛn mʋ wʋra, o yɩrɩ mʋ Zʋzɛfʋ, o na nuŋi Arimatɩ, yɩ o kwǝri o yɩ nadum. Wʋntʋ dɩ dɛɛn yɩ Zezi karabu mʋ. Tɩga dɛɛn na lagɩ ka yi tɩn.

eng: He came to Pilate and asked for Jesus body. Pilate gave him permission to take it.
local: o ma zaŋɩ o vu Pilatɩ te, sɩ o loor-o cwǝŋǝ sɩ o vu o kwe Zezi yɩra yam o kɩ. Pilatɩ ma sɛ, yɩ o pa ni sɩ ba kwe Zezi yɩra yam ba pa-o.

eng: Joseph took the body, wrapped it in a clean linen cloth.
local: Zʋzɛfʋ laan ma vu o kwe Zezi yɩra yam o pipiri-ya dɩ gar-jalɩ dɩlʋ na lana tɩn.

eng: and laid it in his own new tomb, which he had carved out of the rock. After he rolled a large stone at the door of the tomb, he went away.
local: Ba ma daarɩ ba kwe-ya ba ja vu ba tiŋi piu bɔɔnɩ wʋnɩ. Dɩntʋ maŋɩ dɩ yɩ o tɩtɩ nyɩm mʋ, ba dɛɛn lugi-dɩ piu yɩra nɩ mʋ. Dɩ yɩ bɔn-dʋʋrɩ mʋ. Ba na tiŋi Zezi yɩra yam da tɩn, ba laan ma bibili kandwɛ-pulɔgɔ kamunu ba ma pɩ bɔɔnɩ dɩm ni, yɩ ba daarɩ ba viiri.

eng: Mary Magdalene and the other Mary were there, sitting in front of the tomb.
local: Ba na wʋra ba kɩ-o kʋntʋ tɩn, Mari wʋlʋ na nuŋi Magɩdala tɩn dɩ Mari wʋdoŋ wʋm maa je yibeeli dɩm yigǝ nɩ ba niǝ.

eng: The next day, which was the day after Preparation Day, the chief priests and the Pharisees gathered before Pilate.
local: Dɛ dɩm Zwifǝ bam na yǝni ba ti ba yigǝ sɩ ba siun dɛ dɩm laan yi tɩn ma kɛ. Tɩga na pʋʋrɩ yɩ kʋ yɩ ba siun dɛ dɩm tɩn, Zwifǝ kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam dɩ Farizɩan tiinǝ bam laan ma zaŋɩ ba vu Pilatɩ te.

eng: They said, Sir, we remember that while that deceiver was still alive he said, After three days I will arise.
local: Ba ma ta dɩd-o ba wɩ: «Dɩbam tu, dɩ́ guli nɩ, vwan nyɩm wʋm ya na wʋra maŋa kalʋ tɩn, o dɛɛn tagɩ o wɩ, wʋm wʋ́ joori o bi da yatɔ dɛ nɩ.

eng: Therefore, order the grave to be sealed until the third day. Otherwise, his disciples may come and steal the body and tell the people, Hes been raised from the dead. This last deception will be worse than the first.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ, nan pa ni sɩ nɔɔna wuuri ba vu ba fǝri yibeeli dɩm ni, sɩ ba fɔgɩ ba taa yɩrɩ-dɩ sɩ kʋ vu kʋ yi da yatɔ yam. Kʋ na daɩ kʋntʋ, o karabiǝ bam wʋ́ wanɩ ba vu ba kwe-o ba ja viiri, yɩ ba daarɩ ba ta dɩ nɔɔna ba wɩ, o bi o yagɩ tʋʋnɩ mʋ. Kʋntʋ ma wʋ́ pa ba vwan yam laan tiini ya ji cɩga kʋ dwǝni pulim dɩm nɩ.».

eng: Pilate replied, You have soldiers for guard duty. Go and make it as secure as you know how.
local: Pilatɩ ma sɛ yɩ o wɩ: «Abam jɩgɩ nɔɔna. Nan pa-na ba vu sɩ ba kwaanɩ ba taa yɩrɩ-dɩ, sɩ kʋ maŋɩ dɩ abam na lagɩ te tɩn.».

eng: Then they went and secured the tomb by sealing the stone and posting the guard.
local: Ba ma zaŋɩ ba vu yibeeli dɩm jǝgǝ kam, ba fǝri dɩ ni kandwɛ kamunu kʋm na pɩ tɩn. Ba ma daarɩ ba yagɩ nɔɔna da sɩ ba taa yɩrɩ-dɩ.

eng: When Jesus finished saying these things, he left Galilee and came to the area of Judea on the east side of the Jordan.
local: Zezi dɛɛn na brɩ-ba wǝǝnu tɩm kʋntʋ maama o ti tɩn, o laan ma zaŋɩ Galile nɩ o vu o yi Zude tɩ-niǝ yam, Zʋrdɛn bugǝ kam ni dɩdoŋ dɩm nɩ.

eng: Large crowds followed him, and he healed them.
local: Nɔn-kɔgɔ ma tɔg-o kʋ vu, yɩ o pa ba yawɩɩna na yazurǝ je sɩm kʋntʋ nɩ.

eng: Some Pharisees came to him. In order to test him, they said, Does the Law allow a man to divorce his wife for just any reason.
local: Farizɩan tiinǝ badaara dɛɛn ma zaŋɩ ba vu Zezi te. Ba maa lagɩ sɩ ba maŋ-o ba nii, yɩ ba bwe-o ba wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ jɩgɩ cwǝŋǝ sɩ o vɩn o kaanɩ, kʋ na maŋɩ kʋ yɩ kʋlʋ maama ŋwaanɩ na?».

eng: Jesus answered, Havent you read that at the beginning the creator made them male and female?.
local: Zezi ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Kʋ pʋpʋnɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ kʋ wɩ: ‹Pulim nɩ, Wɛ na kɩ nabiinǝ tɩn, DƖ kɩ-ba sɩ ba taa yɩ baarʋ dɩ kaanɩ mʋ.› Abam nan ta wʋ karɩmɩ kʋntʋ na?.

eng: And God said, Because of this a man should leave his father and mother and be joined together with his wife, and the two will be one flesh .
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ, nɔɔnʋ na di kaanɩ, o maŋɩ sɩ o pɔɔrɩ o tɩtɩ o nu dɩ o ko tee nɩ, sɩ o daarɩ o pa o dɩ o kaanɩ wʋm jǝni daanɩ. Kʋntʋ wʋ́ pa ba laan ji nɔɔnʋ dɩdʋa.

eng: So they are no longer two but one flesh. Therefore, humans must not pull apart what God has put together.
local: Kʋntʋ tɩn, ba daa daɩ nɔɔna bale, ba laan yɩ nɔɔnʋ dɩdʋa mʋ. Wɛ na kɩ nɔɔna balʋ daanɩ tɩn, nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ daa wʋ maŋɩ sɩ o pɔɔrɩ-ba daanɩ.».

eng: The Pharisees said to him, Then why did Moses command us to give a divorce certificate and divorce her? .
local: Farizɩan tiinǝ bam daa ma bwe-o ba wɩ: «Bɛɛ nan mʋ yɩ Moyisi dɛɛn pa ni, nɩ nɔɔnʋ jɩgɩ cwǝŋǝ sɩ o pʋpʋnɩ tɔnɔ o pa o kaanɩ, sɩ kʋ brɩ nɩ o vɩ-o?».

eng: Jesus replied, Moses allowed you to divorce your wives because your hearts are unyielding. But it wasnt that way from the beginning.
local: Zezi ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Abam wʋrʋ na dana tɩn ŋwaanɩ mʋ pɛ Moyisi dɩ sɛ sɩ á vɩn á kaana. Kʋ na zɩgɩ pulim maŋa nɩ tɩn, kʋ ya wʋ maŋɩ sɩ nɔɔna vɩn ba kaana.

eng: I say to you that whoever divorces his wife, except for sexual unfaithfulness, and marries another woman commits adultery.
local: A nan lagɩ a ta abam sɩ, nɔɔnʋ na vɩn o kaanɩ, yɩ kʋ na daɩ nɩ o cɔgɩ dɩ baara, yɩ o daarɩ o di kaanɩ wʋdoŋ, kʋntʋ tu kǝm dɩm yɩ balɔrɔ nɩnɛɛnɩ o na pǝni dɩ o doŋ kaanɩ te tɩn mʋ.».

eng: His disciples said to him, If thats the way things are between a man and his wife, then its better not to marry.
local: Zezi na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, o karabiǝ bam ma ta dɩd-o ba wɩ: «Kʋ na sɩɩnɩ kʋ yɩ kʋntʋ mʋ baarʋ dɩ o kaanɩ laŋa nɩ, kʋ ya lamma dɩ nɔɔnʋ na maŋɩ o wʋ di kaanɩ.».

eng: He replied, Not everybody can accept this teaching, but only those who have received the ability to accept it.
local: O ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Kʋ nan daɩ nɔɔna maama mʋ wʋ́ wanɩ ba ni taanɩ dɩntʋ kuri, kʋ yɩ balʋ yɩranɩ Wɛ na zǝni-ba sɩ ba wanɩ ba joŋi-dɩ tɩn mʋ.

eng: For there are eunuchs who have been eunuchs from birth. And there are eunuchs who have been made eunuchs by other people. And there are eunuchs who have made themselves eunuchs because of the kingdom of heaven. Those who can accept it should accept it.
local: Nɔɔna badaara wʋra ba na ba bʋŋɩ sɩ ba di kaana, bɛŋwaanɩ ba maŋɩ ba lʋ bantʋ bakanɩ mʋ. Badaara dɩ maa wʋra ba na ba bʋŋɩ sɩ ba di kaana, yɩ kʋ yɩ nɔɔna mʋ jaanɩ-ba ba lwɩ. Kʋ daarɩ badaara wʋra ba tɩtɩ na vɩn sɩ ba di kaana, yɩ kʋ yɩ ba na lagɩ sɩ ba tʋŋɩ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm tɩtʋŋa yam tɩn ŋwaanɩ mʋ. Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na wʋ́ wanɩ o ni zaasɩm dɩntʋ kuri tɩn, sɩ o sɛ.».

eng: Some people brought children to Jesus so that he would place his hands on them and pray. But the disciples scolded them.
local: Nɔɔna badonnǝ dɛɛn ma ja bu-balwa ba vu Zezi te, sɩ o daŋɩ o jɩa ba yuu nɩ sɩ o warɩ Wɛ o pa-ba. O karabiǝ bam ma zaŋɩ ba kaanɩ-ba sɩ ba yɩ taa kɩ kʋntʋ.

eng: Allow the children to come to me, Jesus said. Dont forbid them, because the kingdom of heaven belongs to people like these children.
local: Zezi laan ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Yagɩ-na sɩ biǝ bam taa tui a te, sɩ á yɩ zaŋɩ á cɩ-ba. Bɛŋwaanɩ balʋ na nyɩ nɩ bu-balwa te tɩn mʋ maŋɩ sɩ ba zʋ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋnɩ.».

eng: Then he blessed the children and went away from there.
local: O na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, o laan ma daŋɩ o jɩa ba yuu nɩ o warɩ Wɛ o pa-ba, yɩ o daarɩ o viiri.

eng: A man approached him and said, Teacher, what good thing must I do to have eternal life.
local: Dɛ dɩdwɩ nɔn-dʋŋʋ kʋdoŋ dɛɛn ma zaŋɩ o vu Zezi te. O ma bwe-o o wɩ: «Karanyɩna, kǝm-laa kɔɔ mʋ a maŋɩ sɩ a kɩ, sɩ a ma na ŋwɩa kalʋ na ba ti tɩn Wɛ tee nɩ?».

eng: Jesus said, Why do you ask me about what is good? Theres only one who is good. If you want to enter eternal life, keep the commandments.
local: Zezi ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «Kʋ yɩ ta mʋ yɩ n bwe amʋ sɩ n lwarɩ woŋo kʋlʋ na lana tɩn sɩ n kɩ? Wɛ yɩranɩ mʋ lana. Nmʋ na lagɩ sɩ n na ŋwɩa DƖ tee nɩ, kʋ maŋɩ sɩ n ta n tɔgɩ DƖ cullu tɩm mʋ.».

eng: The man said, Which ones? Then Jesus said, Dont commit murder. Dont commit adultery. Dont steal. Dont give false testimony .
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋm ma bwe-o o wɩ: «DƖ cullu tɔɔ mʋ kʋntʋ?» Zezi ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «Yɩ zaŋɩ n gʋ nɔɔnʋ. Yɩ zaŋɩ n pǝni dɩ n doŋ kaanɩ. Yɩ zaŋɩ n ŋɔ. Yɩ fɔ vwan n pa n doŋ.

eng: Honor your father and mother, and love your neighbor as you love yourself .
local: Ta n zuli n nu dɩ n ko. Ta n soe n doŋ nɩ n tɩtɩ te.».

eng: The young man replied, Ive kept all these. What am I still missing.
local: Nɔn-dʋŋʋ kʋm laan ma ta o wɩ: «A maŋɩ a tɔgɩ cullu tɩntʋ maama. Kʋ nan ta gɛ bɛɛ mʋ sɩ a kɩ?».

eng: Jesus said, If you want to be complete, go, sell what you own, and give the money to the poor. Then you will have treasure in heaven. And come follow me.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Nmʋ na lagɩ sɩ n maŋɩ dɩ Wɛ cwǝŋǝ kam fasɩ, kʋ maŋɩ sɩ n kwe kʋlʋ maama n na jɩgɩ tɩn n yǝgi, sɩ n laan ma tɩ sǝbu kʋm n ma n zǝni yinigǝ tiinǝ. Kʋntʋ wʋ́ pa n ta n jɩgɩ jɩjɩgɩrʋ Wɛ-sɔŋɔ nɩ. Nmʋ na kɩ kʋntʋ, sɩ n laan ba n ta n tɔgɩ-nɩ.».

eng: But when the young man heard this, he went away saddened, because he had many possessions.
local: Nɔn-dʋŋʋ kʋm na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ ma pa o wʋ cɔgɩ zanzan, o jɩjɩgɩrʋ tɩm na tiini tɩ daga tɩn ŋwaanɩ. O ma viiri Zezi tee nɩ.

eng: Then Jesus said to his disciples, I assure you that it will be very hard for a rich person to enter the kingdom of heaven.
local: Zezi laan ma ta dɩ o karabiǝ bam o wɩ: «A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, nadum na lagɩ sɩ o zʋ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋnɩ, kʋʋ́ ta tiini kʋ cana kʋ pa-o.

eng: In fact, its easier for a camel to squeeze through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter Gods kingdom.
local: A nan ta lagɩ a ta dɩ abam nɩ, kʋʋ́ ta yɩ mwalɩ sɩ yogondi da gar-lwe bɔɔnɩ dɩ nuŋi kʋ dwe nadum na lagɩ sɩ o zʋ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋ.».

eng: When his disciples heard this, they were stunned. Then who can be saved? they asked.
local: O karabiǝ bam na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ ma tiini kʋ sʋ-ba. Ba maa tɛ ba wɩ: «Kʋ nan na yɩ kʋntʋ, wɔɔ mʋ wʋ́ wanɩ o na ŋwɩa Wɛ tee nɩ?».

eng: Jesus looked at them carefully and said, Its impossible for human beings. But all things are possible for God.
local: Zezi ma nii-ba yɩ o wɩ: «Nabiinǝ mʋ bá wanɩ ba kɩ, sɩ Wɛ waɩ woŋo maama DƖ kɩa.».

eng: Then Peter replied, Look, weve left everything and followed you. What will we have.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ laan ma ta o wɩ: «Nii. Dɩbam yagɩ dɩ́ woŋo maama mʋ, yɩ dɩ́ daarɩ dɩ́ tɔgɩ nmʋ. Bɛɛ mʋ dɩbam lagɩ dɩ́ na Wɛ tee nɩ?».

eng: Jesus said to them, I assure you who have followed me that, when everything is made new, when the Human One sits on his magnificent throne, you also will sit on twelve thrones overseeing the twelve tribes of Israel.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, abam balʋ na tɔgɩ amʋ tɩn wʋ́ ba á tɔgɩ á di paarɩ, maŋa kalʋ Wɛ na wʋ́ kɩ wǝǝnu maama sɩ tɩ taa yɩ nadʋnnʋ tɩn. Maŋa kam kʋntʋ nɩ amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm wʋ́ ba a jǝni a paarɩ jangɔŋɔ kʋm baŋa nɩ, yɩ abam dɩ wʋ́ jǝni paarɩ jangwaanʋ fugǝ-tɩle baŋa nɩ, sɩ á tɔgɩ á di Yisɩrayɛlɩ dwi tiinǝ kuri fugǝ-bɩle dɩm taanɩ.

eng: And all who have left houses, brothers, sisters, father, mother, children, or farms because of my name will receive one hundred times more and will inherit eternal life.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ maama na yagɩ o sɔŋɔ dɩ o nu-biǝ dɩ o nu dɩ o ko dɩ o biǝ dɩ o karɩ amʋ yɩrɩ dɩm ŋwaanɩ tɩn, kʋntʋ tu wʋ́ ba o na kʋlʋ maama o na yagɩ tɩn kuni bi mʋ, yɩ o daarɩ o na ŋwɩa kalʋ na ba ti tɩn Wɛ tee nɩ.

eng: But many who are first will be last. And many who are last will be first.
local: Balʋ na maŋɩ ba yɩ yigǝ tiinǝ tɩn zanzan wʋ́ joori ba ji kwaga tiinǝ. Kʋ daarɩ balʋ dɩ na maŋɩ ba yɩ kwaga tiinǝ tɩn zanzan wʋ́ ba ba ji yigǝ tiinǝ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋnɩ.».

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a landowner who went out early in the morning to hire workers for his vineyard.
local: Zezi daa ma daarɩ o wɩ: «Wɛ paarɩ dɩm nyɩ dɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ na jɩgɩ o kara. O ma zaŋɩ o nuŋi zɩzɩŋa nɩ o vu sɩ o lagɩ nɔɔna balʋ na varɩ ba joŋi sǝbu tɩn, sɩ ba vu ba tʋŋɩ o kara kam nɩ.

eng: After he agreed with the workers to pay them a denarion, he sent them into his vineyard.
local: O ma sɛ sɩ o ŋwɩ-ba sǝbu kʋlʋ ba na maŋɩ sɩ ba joŋi dɛ wʋnɩ tɩn. Kʋ yɩ sǝbu-dalɩ dɩdʋa mʋ. Ba na ŋɔɔnɩ ba maŋɩ daanɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma tʋŋɩ-ba sɩ ba vu o kara kam ba taa tʋŋa.

eng: Then he went out around nine in the morning and saw others standing around the marketplace doing nothing.
local: Wɩa na bɩgɩ fɩnfɩɩn nɩnɛɛnɩ luu nʋgʋ maŋa nɩ te tɩn, o daa ma vu yaga o na nɔɔna badonnǝ na wʋra, yɩ ba ba jɩgɩ tɩtʋŋa.

eng: He said to them, You also go into the vineyard, and Ill pay you whatever is right.
local: O ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: ‹Abam dɩ ba á tɔgɩ á tʋŋɩ a kara kam nɩ, sɩ amʋ wʋ́ ŋwɩ abam te na maŋɩ tɩn.›.

eng: And they went. Again around noon and then at three in the afternoon, he did the same thing.
local: Ba ma sɛ ba vu. Wɩa tɩtarɩ nɩ o daa ma joori o vu o lagɩ nɔɔna badonnǝ o wǝli da. Wɛ-gunim maŋa nɩ o daa ta ma vu o lagɩ nɔɔna badonnǝ dɩ o wǝli da.

eng: Around five in the afternoon he went and found others standing around, and he said to them, Why are you just standing around here doing nothing all day long.
local: Dɩdaan-ni nɩ o daa ma vu yaga o na nɔɔna badonnǝ ta na zɩgɩ da. O ma bwe-ba o wɩ: ‹Bɛɛ mʋ yɩ abam zɩgɩ yo seeni dɛ dɩm wʋnɩ maama, yɩ á ba kɩ kʋlʋkʋlʋ?›.

eng: Because nobody has hired us, they replied. He responded, You also go into the vineyard.
local: Ba ma lǝr-o ba wɩ: ‹Nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ wʋ kwe dɩbam sɩ dɩ́ tʋŋɩ dɩ́ pa-o.› O ma ta dɩ bantʋ dɩ, sɩ ba vu ba tɔgɩ ba tʋŋɩ o kara kam nɩ.

eng: When evening came, the owner of the vineyard said to his manager, Call the workers and give them their wages, beginning with the last ones hired and moving on finally to the first.
local: Tɩga na lagɩ ka yi tɩn, o laan ma ta dɩ o tɩtʋŋa yigǝ tu wʋm sɩ o ta dɩ tɩntʋŋna bam, sɩ ba zaŋɩ ba ba ba joŋi ba ŋwɩɩrʋ. O ma ta dɩd-o sɩ o puli o ŋwɩ tɩntʋŋna balʋ na tu ba kweeli tɩn.

eng: When those who were hired at five in the afternoon came, each one received a denarion.
local: Nɔɔna balʋ na tu ba sɩŋɩ tɩtʋŋa dɩdaan-ni nɩ tɩn ma ba sɩ ba joŋi ba ŋwɩɩrʋ. Ba ma ŋwɩ-ba sǝbu-dalɩ dɩdʋa dɩdʋa.

eng: Now when those hired first came, they thought they would receive more. But each of them also received a denarion.
local: Kʋ daarɩ balʋ na puli ba tʋŋɩ tɩtɩɩtɩ tɩn dɩ laan ma ba sɩ ba joŋi ba ŋwɩɩrʋ. Ba maa bʋŋɩ nɩ baá joŋi ŋwɩɩrʋ kʋ dwe bantʋ nyɩm tɩm. Ba dɩ nan joŋi sǝbu-dalɩ dɩdʋa dɩdʋa mʋ.

eng: When they received it, they grumbled against the landowner.
local: Ba ma joŋi ba sǝbu, yɩ ba daarɩ ba pʋʋna dɩ kara kam tu wʋm ba wɩ:.

eng: These who were hired last worked one hour, and they received the same pay as we did even though we had to work the whole day in the hot sun.
local: ‹Balʋ na tu kwaga tɩn tʋŋɩ fɩnfɩɩn yɩranɩ mʋ, nɩnɛɛnɩ luu dɩdʋa te. Dɩbam nan tu dɩ́ tʋŋɩ tɩtɩɩtɩ mʋ, kʋ cɛ dɩ́ yɩra wɩa yigǝ nɩ dɛ dɩm maama wʋnɩ, yɩ nmʋ pa bantʋ ŋwɩʋ maɩ dɩ dɩbam na joŋi te tɩn.›.

eng: But he replied to one of them, Friend, I did you no wrong. Didnt I agree to pay you a denarion?.
local: Kara kam tu wʋm ma lǝri ba dɩdʋa o wɩ: ‹A ciloŋ, nii yo. A wʋ kɩ nmʋ kampinǝ, bɛŋwaanɩ nmʋ sɛ sɩ n tʋŋɩ n joŋi sǝbu-dalɩ dɩdʋa mʋ dɛ wʋnɩ.

eng: Take what belongs to you and go. I want to give to this one who was hired last the same as I give to you.
local: Nan ja n sǝbu kʋm n viiri, sɩ taanɩ tǝrǝ. Amʋ laga, yɩ a ŋwɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na tu kwaga o tʋŋɩ tɩn, sɩ kʋ maŋɩ dɩ nmʋ na joŋi te tɩn.

eng: Dont I have the right to do what I want with what belongs to me? Or are you resentful because Im generous.
local: A nan ba jɩgɩ cwǝŋǝ sɩ a ma a sǝbu kʋm a kɩ kʋlʋ a na lagɩ tɩn na? Nmʋ nan jɩgɩ wʋ-gʋʋ dɩ a na jɩgɩ wʋ-yoŋo tɩn mʋ na?›.

eng: So those who are last will be first. And those who are first will be last.
local: Mʋ kʋ kuri, balʋ na wʋ kwaga nɩ tɩn wʋ́ ba ba ji yigǝ tiinǝ, yɩ balʋ na wʋ yigǝ nɩ tɩn wʋ́ joori ba ji kwaga tiinǝ.».

eng: As Jesus was going up to Jerusalem, he took the Twelve aside by themselves on the road. He told them.
local: Zezi dɩ o karabiǝ dɛɛn ma zaŋɩ ba maa kɛ Zeruzalɛm. Ba na wʋ cwǝŋǝ nɩ tɩn, o ma pɔɔrɩ o karabiǝ fugǝ-bale bam kɔgɔ kʋm wʋnɩ o pa ba tɔgɩ dɩd-o ba veǝ.

eng: Look, we are going up to Jerusalem. The Human One will be handed over to the chief priests and legal experts. They will condemn him to death.
local: O ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Taá ye-na, dɩ́ na maa ve Zeruzalɛm tɩn, dáanɩ mʋ ba laan wʋ́ ja amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm ba kɩ kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam dɩ cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ bam jɩŋa nɩ. Nɔɔna bam kʋntʋ wʋ́ di a taanɩ sɩ kʋ brɩ nɩ a maŋɩ sɩ a tɩ mʋ.

eng: They will hand him over to the Gentiles to be ridiculed, tortured, and crucified. But he will be raised on the third day.
local: Ba laan wʋ́ daarɩ ba kɩ-nɩ dwi-gɛ tiinǝ bam jɩŋa nɩ. Bantʋ dɩ wʋ́ mwanɩ amʋ, yɩ ba daarɩ ba magɩ-nɩ dɩ balaara. Ba laan wʋ́ ja-nɩ ba pa tʋʋn-dagara baŋa nɩ ba gʋ. Da yatɔ dɛ nɩ, a laan wʋ́ joori a bi a yagɩ tʋʋnɩ.».

eng: Then the mother of Zebedees sons came to Jesus along with her sons. Bowing before him, she asked a favor of him.
local: Zebede biǝ bam nu dɛɛn ma vu Zezi te dɩ o biǝ bale bam. O ma kuni doonǝ o yigǝ nɩ sɩ o loor-o.

eng: What do you want? he asked. She responded, Say that these two sons of mine will sit, one on your right hand and one on your left, in your kingdom.
local: Zezi ma bwe-o o wɩ: «Bɛɛ mʋ n laga?» O ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «Popo, pa ni sɩ n na tu n di n paarɩ dɩm, sɩ n pa a biǝ bale bantʋ jǝni nmʋ tee nɩ, n jazɩm nɩ dɩ n jagwiǝ nɩ.».

eng: Jesus replied, You dont know what youre asking! Can you drink from the cup that Im about to drink from? They said to him, We can.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Abam tɩtɩ yǝri á na loori kʋlʋ tɩn. Abam wʋ́ wanɩ á tɔgɩ á yaarɩ nɩ a na lagɩ a ba a na yaara yalʋ tɩn na?» Ba ma lǝr-o ba wɩ: «Ɛɛn, dɩ́ wʋ́ wanɩ.».

eng: He said to them, You will drink from my cup, but to sit at my right or left hand isnt mine to give. It belongs to those for whom my Father prepared it.
local: Zezi laan ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Abam wʋ́ sɩɩnɩ á tɔgɩ á yaarɩ amʋ yaara yam doŋ. Kʋ daarɩ wʋlʋ na wʋ́ jǝni a jazɩm nɩ naa a jagwiǝ nɩ amʋ paarɩ dɩm wʋnɩ tɩn, a ba jɩgɩ cwǝŋǝ sɩ a kuri nɔɔna sɩ ba jǝni da. A Ko Wɛ mʋ maŋɩ o tiŋi je sɩm kʋntʋ, sɩ o pa balʋ na wʋ́ ba ba jǝni da tɩn.».

eng: Now when the other ten disciples heard about this, they became angry with the two brothers.
local: O karabiǝ fugǝ kam na lwarɩ ba donnǝ bale bam na loori kʋlʋ Zezi tee nɩ tɩn, ba bana ma tiini ya zaŋɩ dɩ ba.

eng: But Jesus called them over and said, You know that those who rule the Gentiles show off their authority over them and their high-ranking officials order them around.
local: Zezi laan ma bǝŋi ba maama, yɩ o ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Abam maŋɩ á ye lʋgʋ baŋa pwa na yǝni ba jɩgɩ ba nɔɔna ba bɛɛsɩ te tɩn, yɩ ba dɩdɛɛra mʋ di dam ba baŋa nɩ.

eng: But thats not the way it will be with you. Whoever wants to be great among you will be your servant.
local: Kʋ nan na yɩ abam, kʋ wʋ maŋɩ sɩ kʋ taa yɩ kʋntʋ doŋ abam tɩtarɩ nɩ. Bɛŋwaanɩ, abam wʋlʋ na lagɩ sɩ o ji nɔn-kamunu á wʋnɩ tɩn, kʋntʋ tu maŋɩ sɩ o ji abam maama tɩntʋŋnʋ mʋ.

eng: Whoever wants to be first among you will be your slave.
local: Wʋlʋ na lagɩ sɩ o ji abam yigǝ tu tɩn, kʋntʋ tu maŋɩ sɩ o ji á gambaa mʋ.

eng: just as the Human One didnt come to be served but rather to serve and to give his life to liberate many people.
local: Kʋ yɩ kʋntʋ doŋ mʋ, amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm wʋ tu sɩ nɔɔna tʋŋɩ ba pa-nɩ. Amʋ nan tu sɩ a tʋŋɩ a pa nabiinǝ mʋ, sɩ a daarɩ a kwe a ŋwɩa a ma lǝni nɔɔna zanzan ŋwɩa Wɛ tee nɩ.».

eng: As Jesus and his disciples were going out of Jericho a large crowd followed him.
local: Zezi dɩ o karabiǝ bam dɛɛn ma nuŋi Zeriko nɩ ba maa kɛa. Ba na maa kɛ tɩn, nɔn-kɔgɔ maa tɔgɩ-ba.

eng: When two blind men sitting along the road heard that Jesus was passing by, they shouted, Show us mercy, Lord, Son of David.
local: Lilwǝ bale dɛɛn ma je cwǝŋǝ kam ni nɩ. Ba na lwarɩ nɩ Zezi mʋ tɔgɩ dáanɩ o maa kɛ tɩn, ba laan ma kaasɩ baŋa baŋa dɩ kwǝr-dɩa ba wɩ: «Dɩ́ Yuutu! Pɛ Davidi dwi tu! Duri dɩbam yibwǝnǝ!».

eng: Now the crowd scolded them and told them to be quiet. But they shouted even louder, Show us mercy, Lord, Son of David.
local: Nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm ma kaanɩ-ba sɩ ba pu ba niǝ. Ba laan ma tiini ba kaasɩ ba wɩ: «Dɩbam Yuutu! Pɛ Davidi dwi tu! Duri dɩbam yibwǝnǝ!».

eng: Jesus stopped in his tracks and called to them. What do you want me to do for you? he asked.
local: Zezi na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma zɩgɩ yɩ o bǝŋi-ba o bwe o wɩ: «Abam lagɩ sɩ a kɩ bɛɛ mʋ a pa abam?».

eng: Lord, we want to see, they replied.
local: Ba ma lǝri ba wɩ: «Dɩ́ Yuutu, dɩbam lagɩ sɩ n pa dɩ́ yiǝ mʋ joori ya taa naɩ.».

eng: Jesus had compassion on them and touched their eyes. Immediately they were able to see, and they followed him.
local: Zezi ma duri ba ŋwaŋa. O ma twɩ o jɩŋa o dwe ba yiǝ yam, yɩ ya joori ya naɩ bɩdwɩ baŋa nɩ. Ba laan ma zaŋɩ ba tɔgɩ o kwaga.

eng: Then Jesus spoke to the crowds and his disciples.
local: Zezi laan ma ŋɔɔnɩ dɩ o karabiǝ bam dɩ nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm maama.

eng: The legal experts and the Pharisees sit on Moses seat.
local: O ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Kʋ na yɩ Farizɩan tiinǝ bam dɩ Wɛ cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ bam, bantʋ mʋ tu ba lǝni Moyisi yuu nɩ, sɩ ba pa nɔɔna lwarɩ o zaasɩm dɩm na yɩ te tɩn.

eng: Therefore, you must take care to do everything they say. But dont do what they do.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ á maŋɩ sɩ á taá tɔgɩ kʋlʋ maama ba na brɩ abam tɩn. Á nan yɩ zaŋɩ á lwǝni ba na kɩ kʋlʋ maama tɩn, bɛŋwaanɩ ba tɩtɩ ba tɔgɩ kʋlʋ ba na brɩ nɔɔna tɩn.

eng: For they tie together heavy packs that are impossible to carry. They put them on the shoulders of others, but are unwilling to lift a finger to move them.
local: Ba na kɩ te tɩn, ba yǝni ba yaarɩ nɔɔna mʋ dɩ cul-dɛɛrʋ sɩ ba taa tɔgɩ-tɩ. Kʋ maa nyɩ dɩ ba na fɩ-ba sɩ ba zɩŋɩ zɩla yalʋ duuni na dwe ba zɩŋɩm tɩn, yɩ ba tɩtɩ yigǝ tǝrǝ sɩ ba twǝri zɩla yam fɩnfɩɩn dɩ ba zɩŋɩ.

eng: Everything they do, they do to be noticed by others. They make extra-wide prayer bands for their arms and long tassels for their clothes.
local: Kʋlʋ na wǝli da tɩn, ba yǝni ba brɩ ba kǝm-laarʋ tɩm jaja mʋ sɩ nɔɔna maama taa naɩ. Ba yǝni ba brɩ ba tɩtɩ nɔɔna yigǝ nɩ, nɩ ba jɩgɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm sono, dɩ ba na tiini ba vɔ-tɩ ba yɩra nɩ jaja tɩn. Ba ma kɩ zala zanzan ba gwaarʋ yɩra nɩ, sɩ kʋ pa nɔɔna taa zuli-ba.

eng: They love to sit in places of honor at banquets and in the synagogues.
local: Ba na yǝni ba vu nɔɔnʋ sɔŋɔ sɩ ba di candiǝ, kʋ yɩ je-ŋʋŋa je nɩ mʋ ba lagɩ sɩ ba taa je da. Ba na zʋ ba Wɛ-di sɩm, ba daa ta lagɩ je-kamunnu mʋ sɩ ba taa je yigǝ nɩ.

eng: They love to be greeted with honor in the markets and to be addressed as Rabbi.
local: Ba na ve yaga, ba lagɩ sɩ nɔɔna maama taa tui ba zuli-ba mʋ, yɩ ba ta lagɩ sɩ nɔɔna taa bǝ-ba nɩ ‹Karanyɩna›.

eng: But you shouldnt be called Rabbi , because you have one teacher, and all of you are brothers and sisters.
local: Kʋ nan na yɩ abam, á yɩ taá lagɩ sɩ ba taa bǝ abam nɩ ‹Karanyɩna›, bɛŋwaanɩ á maama yɩ nu-biǝ mʋ daanɩ, yɩ á jɩgɩ Karanyɩna dɩdʋa mʋ.

eng: Dont call anybody on earth your father, because you have one Father, who is heavenly.
local: Á yɩ zaŋɩ á ma nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ á ma bǝŋi nɩ á ‹Ko› lʋgʋ baŋa yo, Wɛ cwǝŋǝ kam tɔgɩm ŋwaanɩ. Bɛŋwaanɩ abam maama jɩgɩ Ko dɩdʋa mʋ, wʋntʋ mʋ yɩ Baŋa-Wɛ.

eng: Dont be called teacher , because Christ is your one teacher.
local: Kʋlʋ na wǝli da tɩn, á nan yɩ taá lagɩ sɩ nɔɔna taa bǝ abam nɩ ‹Yigǝ tiinǝ›, bɛŋwaanɩ á Yigǝ Tu yɩ dɩdʋa mʋ. Wʋntʋ mʋ yɩ Krisi wʋm Wɛ na tʋŋɩ tɩn.

eng: But the one who is greatest among you will be your servant.
local: Abam nɔn-kamunu mʋ yɩ wʋlʋ na yɩ tɩntʋŋnʋ á wʋnɩ tɩn.

eng: All who lift themselves up will be brought low. But all who make themselves low will be lifted up.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ, wʋlʋ na zǝŋi o tɩtɩ tɩn, Wɛ wʋ́ tu kʋntʋ tu. Wʋlʋ nan na tu o tɩtɩ tɩn, Wɛ wʋ́ zǝŋi kʋntʋ tu.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You shut people out of the kingdom of heaven. You dont enter yourselves, and you wont allow those who want to enter to do so.
local: Wɛ cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ-ba dɩ Farizɩan tiinǝ-ba, lɛɛrʋ wʋ á yuu nɩ. Abam yɩ pipiri-nyɩna mʋ. Á yǝni á cɩ cwǝŋǝ á pa nɔɔna warɩ sɩ ba zʋ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋnɩ. Abam tɩtɩ ba lagɩ sɩ á zʋ, yɩ á daarɩ á cɩ balʋ maama na lagɩ sɩ ba zʋ tɩn cwǝŋǝ. .

eng: How terrible it will be for you, legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You travel over sea and land to make one convert. But when theyve been converted, they become twice the child of hell you are.
local: Wɛ cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ-ba dɩ Farizɩan tiinǝ-ba, lɛɛrʋ wʋ á yuu nɩ. Abam yɩ pipiri-nyɩna mʋ. Á yǝni á beeri lʋgʋ baŋa je maama mʋ sɩ á nii, á wʋ́ wanɩ á pa dwi-gɛ tu dɩdʋa ba o tɔgɩ cullu tɩlʋ Wɛ na pɛ dɩbam Zwifǝ bam tɩn na. Bantʋ dɩdʋa nan na tu o sɛ Wɛ, á laan wʋ́ brɩ-o á tɩtɩ cullu mʋ, sɩ á pa o tiini o ji min-tɩʋ nɔɔnʋ kʋ dwǝni abam tɩtɩ na yɩ te tɩn.

eng: How terrible it will be for you blind guides who say, If people swear by the temple, its nothing. But if people swear by the gold in the temple, they are obligated to do what they swore.
local: Lɛɛrʋ wʋ á yuu nɩ. Abam nyɩ dɩ lilwǝ na vaŋɩ ba donnǝ lilwǝ te tɩn mʋ. Abam yǝni á brɩ nɔɔna á wɩ, nɔɔnʋ na dugi durǝ dɩ Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm, kʋntʋ daɩ kʋlʋkʋlʋ. Á ma daarɩ á brɩ á wɩ, nɔɔnʋ nan na dugi dɩ sǝbu-sɩŋa kalʋ na wʋ Wɛ-digǝ kam wʋnɩ tɩn, kʋ yɩ fɩfɩʋn mʋ sɩ kʋntʋ tu kɩ kʋlʋ o na dugi dɩ kʋ tɩn.

eng: You foolish and blind people! Which is greater, the gold or the temple that makes the gold holy?.
local: Abam yɩ jwǝǝru yɩ á kwǝri á nyɩ dɩ lilwǝ mʋ. Kʋ yɩ digǝ kam na yɩ Wɛ nyɩm tɩn ŋwaanɩ mʋ pɛ sǝbu-sɩŋa kam dɩ yɩ Wɛ nyɩm. Kʋntʋ tɩn, tɩntʋ wʋnɩ kɔɔ mʋ jɩgɩ kuri kʋ dwe kʋdoŋ?.

eng: You say, If people swear by the altar, its nothing. But if they swear by the gift on the altar, they are obligated to do what they swore.
local: Abam daa ta brɩ nɩ, nɔɔnʋ na dugi dɩ Wɛ kaanɩm bimbim dɩm, kʋntʋ daɩ kʋlʋkʋlʋ. Á ma daarɩ á brɩ nɩ, nɔɔnʋ na dugi dɩ kaanɩm wǝǝnu tɩlʋ maama na tigi bimbim dɩm baŋa nɩ tɩn, kʋ yɩ fɩfɩʋn mʋ sɩ kʋntʋ tu kɩ kʋlʋ o na dugi dɩ kʋ tɩn.

eng: You blind people! Which is greater, the gift or the altar that makes the gift holy?.
local: Abam nyɩ dɩ lilwǝ mʋ yɩ á ba naɩ kʋlʋ na yɩ cɩga tɩn. Kʋ yɩ bimbim dɩm na yɩ Wɛ nyɩm tɩn mʋ pɛ kaanɩm wǝǝnu tɩm dɩ yɩ Wɛ nyɩm. Kʋntʋ tɩn, tɩntʋ wʋnɩ kɔɔ mʋ jɩgɩ kuri kʋ dwe kʋdoŋ?.

eng: Therefore, those who swear by the altar swear by it and by everything thats on it.
local: Kʋntʋ, nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na dugi dɩ kaanɩm bimbim dɩm tɩn, kʋ brɩ nɩ kʋntʋ tu dugi dɩ bimbim dɩm tɩtɩ kʋ wǝli dɩ woŋo kʋlʋ maama na tigi dɩ baŋa nɩ tɩn mʋ.

eng: Those who swear by the temple swear by it and by everything thats part of it.
local: Wʋlʋ dɩ na dugi dɩ Wɛ-digǝ kam tɩn, kʋ brɩ nɩ kʋntʋ tu dugi dɩ Wɛ mʋ, Wɛ na wʋ ka wʋnɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: Those who swear by heaven swear by Gods throne and by the one who sits on it.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ dɩ nan na dugi dɩ Wɛ-sɔŋɔ kʋm tɩn, kʋ brɩ nɩ kʋntʋ tu dugi dɩ Wɛ paarɩ jangɔŋɔ kʋm mʋ, kʋ brɩ nɩ kʋntʋ tu dugi dɩ Baŋa-Wɛ mʋ.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You give to God a tenth of mint, dill, and cumin, but you forget about the more important matters of the Law: justice, peace, and faith. You ought to give a tenth but without forgetting about those more important matters.
local: Wɛ cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ-ba dɩ Farizɩan tiinǝ-ba, lɛɛrʋ wʋ á yuu nɩ. Abam yɩ pipiri-nyɩna mʋ. Kʋ na maŋɩ kʋ yɩ dwǝ dwi maama, ba zɩ ba daga dɩ, á yǝni á lɩ Wɛ tǝri á pa-DƖ. Kʋ lana. Kʋ daarɩ á yigǝ tǝrǝ dɩ Wɛ niǝ yadaara yalʋ na tiini ya yɩ kamunǝ tɩn, nɩnɛɛnɩ cɩga tɔgɩm dɩ yibwǝn-durǝ dɩ wʋ-dɩdʋa kǝm. Kʋ ya maŋɩ sɩ á taá kɩ wǝǝnu tɩm kʋntʋ mʋ sɩ á yɩ yagɩ Wɛ niǝ yadaara yam.

eng: At that time the kingdom of heaven will be like ten young bridesmaids who took their lamps and went out to meet the groom.
local: «Kantʋ maŋa kam nɩ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋ́ ta nyɩ dɩ bukwa fugǝ na zaŋɩ ba vu sɩ ba jeeri kan-bar-dʋʋrʋ te tɩn. Ba ma kwe ba min-zwǝǝnu ba ja wǝli da ba vu.

eng: You blind guides! You filter out an ant but swallow a camel.
local: Abam sɩɩnɩ á nyɩ dɩ lilwǝ balʋ na vaɩ ba donnǝ lilwǝ tɩn mʋ. Abam nyɩ dɩ nɔɔna balʋ na lagɩ ba nyɔ na, yɩ ba yǝni ba leeri-ba, sɩ kalisi na wʋra sɩ ba lɩ-dɩ ba yagɩ, yɩ ba yigǝ nan tǝrǝ dɩ wo-kamunu kʋlʋ ba na liri tɩn, kʋ na maŋɩ kʋ ma dɩ yogondi dɩ.

eng: Now five of them were wise, and the other five were foolish.
local: Bukwa bam banu maa yɩ jwǝǝru, yɩ banu bam yɩ swan tiinǝ.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You clean the outside of the cup and plate, but inside they are full of violence and pleasure seeking.
local: Wɛ cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ-ba dɩ Farizɩan tiinǝ-ba, lɛɛrʋ wʋ á yuu nɩ. Abam yɩ pipiri-nyɩna mʋ. Abam ŋwɩa kam nyɩ dɩ ba na sɩn zwɩ dɩ kabǝli kwɛ sɩ kʋ tɔgɩ cullu na brɩ te yɩ sɩ wʋ su dɩ digiru te tɩn mʋ. Abam wʋrʋ su dɩ kampinǝ dɩ pu-sɩŋa mʋ.

eng: The foolish ones took their lamps but didnt bring oil for them.
local: Balʋ na ba jɩgɩ swan tɩn ma wʋ kɩ nugǝ ba wǝli da, sɩ ba taa kɩ ba min-zwǝǝnu tɩm nɩ.

eng: Blind Pharisee! First clean the inside of the cup so that the outside of the cup will be clean too.
local: Farizɩan tu, nmʋ nyɩ dɩ lilwe mʋ te. Nmʋ maŋɩ sɩ n da yigǝ n sɩn n zwɩ wʋ mʋ, sɩ sɩ kwɛ dɩ daa yɩ taa jɩgɩ digiru.

eng: But the wise ones took their lamps and also brought containers of oil.
local: Kʋ daarɩ swan tiinǝ bam ma kɩ nugǝ wǝǝnu wʋnɩ ba ziǝ.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You are like whitewashed tombs. They look beautiful on the outside. But inside they are full of dead bones and all kinds of filth.
local: Wɛ cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ-ba dɩ Farizɩan tiinǝ-ba, lɛɛrʋ wʋ á yuu nɩ. Abam yɩ pipiri-nyɩna mʋ. Abam nyɩ dɩ yibeelǝ yalʋ ba na fɔgɩ ya kwaga sɩ ya taa lana nɔɔna yibiyǝ nɩ tɩn mʋ. Yibeelǝ yam kʋntʋ wʋ ma su dɩ twa kwi, yɩ ya tiini ya yɩ digiru yɩranɩ.

eng: When the groom was late in coming, they all became drowsy and went to sleep.
local: Kan-barʋ wʋm na tiini o daanɩ yɩ o ba tui tɩn, kʋ ma pa ba maama tigi ba dɔa.

eng: In the same way you look righteous to people. But inside you are full of pretense and rebellion.
local: Abam dɩ nan yɩ kʋntʋ mʋ. Á paɩ á jɩgɩ cɩga mʋ nɔɔna yibiyǝ nɩ, yɩ á wʋrʋ laan su dɩ lwarɩm yɩranɩ, dɩdaanɩ pipirǝ kikiǝ.

eng: But at midnight there was a cry, Look, the groom! Come out to meet him.
local: Tɩga na tu ka kwɩɩrɩ tɩn, nɔɔna maa tɔɔlɩ ba wɩ: ‹Kan-barʋ wʋm maa bɩɩna! Zaŋɩ-na á nuŋi á jeer-o.›.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You build tombs for the prophets and decorate the graves of the righteous.
local: Wɛ cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ-ba dɩ Farizɩan tiinǝ-ba, lɛɛrʋ wʋ á yuu nɩ. Abam yɩ pipiri-nyɩna mʋ. Abam yǝni á fɔgɩ á kɩ faŋa faŋa Wɛ nijoŋnǝ bam yibeelǝ, yɩ á daarɩ á kwɛ Wɛ nɔn-ŋʋna bam yibeelǝ lanyɩranɩ.

eng: Then all those bridesmaids got up and prepared their lamps.
local: Bukwa bam ma zaŋɩ ba tarɩgɩ ba min-zwǝǝnu tɩm.

eng: You say, If we had lived in our ancestors days, we wouldnt have joined them in killing the prophets.
local: Á ma daarɩ á tɛ á wɩ, abam dɛɛn ya na wʋra á kwǝ nakwa bam maŋa kam nɩ, á ya bá sɛ á wǝli dɩ ba á gʋ Wɛ nijoŋnǝ bam.

eng: But the foolish bridesmaids said to the wise ones, Give us some of your oil, because our lamps have gone out.
local: Bukwa balʋ na ba jɩgɩ swan tɩn min-zwǝǝnu tɩm maa lagɩ tɩ dwe. Ba ma ta dɩ ba donnǝ swan tiinǝ bam sɩ ba kɩ nugǝ ba pa-ba, sɩ ba min-zwǝǝnu tɩm lagɩ tɩ dwe.

eng: You testify against yourselves that you are children of those who murdered the prophets.
local: Abam tɩtɩ ni-taanɩ nan mʋ brɩ nɩ á yɩ balʋ na gʋ Wɛ nijoŋnǝ bam tɩn biǝ.

eng: But the wise bridesmaids replied, No, because if we share with you, there wont be enough for our lamps and yours. We have a better idea. You go to those who sell oil and buy some for yourselves.
local: Swan tiinǝ bam ma lǝri ba wɩ: ‹Aye, nugǝ kam ba daga sɩ ka yi dɩbam maama. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ, abam dɩ ve balʋ na jɩgɩ nugǝ ba yǝgi tɩn te á yǝgi.›.

eng: Go ahead, complete what your ancestors did.
local: Kʋntʋ, nan kwaanɩ-na á tʋŋɩ á kwǝ nakwa bam na kɩ wo-lwaanʋ tɩlʋ ba daarɩ tɩn sɩ kʋ vu kʋ ti.

eng: But while they were gone to buy oil, the groom came. Those who were ready went with him into the wedding. Then the door was shut.
local: Ba laan ma kɛ sɩ ba yǝgi. Ba na kɛ ba daarɩ tɩn, mʋ kan-barʋ wʋm yiǝ. Badaara bam na maŋɩ ba ti ba yigǝ tɩn, ba ma tɔgɩ dɩ kan-barʋ wʋm ba vu ba zʋ kadiri dim sɔŋɔ kʋm. Ba ma pɩ mancoŋo kʋm ni.

eng: You snakes! You children of snakes! How will you be able to escape the judgment of hell?.
local: Abam dɩ á kwǝ bam maama nyɩ dɩ bɩsankwɩ-dwǝ mʋ. Á brɩ á bá lu min-tɩʋ cam dɩm wʋnɩ.

eng: Later the other bridesmaids came and said, Lord, lord, open the door for us.
local: Kʋ na kɩ fɩnfɩɩn tɩn, mʋ bukwa banu balʋ na ba jɩgɩ swan tɩn laan tuǝ, yɩ ba bǝŋi kan-barʋ wʋm ba wɩ: ‹Dɩ́ yuutu, dɩ́ yuutu, pʋrɩ sɩ dɩ́ zʋ!›.

eng: Therefore, look, Im sending you prophets, wise people, and legal experts. Some of them you will kill and crucify. And some you will beat in your synagogues and chase from city to city.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ a lagɩ a tʋŋɩ a nijoŋnǝ abam tee nɩ, dɩ yiyiu tiinǝ dɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ karanyɩna tiinǝ dɩ. Abam wʋ́ ja badaara á gʋ, yɩ á daarɩ á pa badaara tʋʋn-dagara baŋa nɩ á gʋ. Á ma wʋ́ ja badaara á Wɛ-di sɩm wʋnɩ á magɩ, yɩ á daarɩ á taá tɔgɩ ba nɛ tɩʋ dɩ tɩʋ á bɛɛsɩ-ba.

eng: But he replied, I tell you the truth, I dont know you.
local: O ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: ‹A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, a yǝri abam.›.

eng: Therefore, upon you will come all the righteous blood that has been poured out on the earth, from the blood of that righteous man Abel to the blood of Zechariah the son of Barachiah, whom you killed between the temple and the altar.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ Wɛ wʋ́ pa abam na cam nɩnɛɛnɩ kʋ yɩ abam tɩtɩ mʋ gʋ nɔn-ŋʋna balʋ maama ba na maŋɩ ba gʋ lʋgʋ pulim nɩ sɩ kʋ ba kʋ yi zɩm tɩn. Kʋ na puli dɩ Abɛlɩ wʋlʋ na yɩ nɔn-ŋʋm tɩn, sɩ kʋ ba kʋ kweeli dɩ Barasɩ bu Zakari wʋlʋ abam kwǝ na gʋ Wɛ di-laa kʋm dɩ Wɛ kaanɩm bimbim dɩm tɩtarɩ nɩ tɩn.

eng: Therefore, keep alert, because you dont know the day or the hour.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ á maŋɩ sɩ á taá cʋ á tɩtɩ, bɛŋwaanɩ á yǝri dɛ dɩlʋ naa maŋa kalʋ amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm na wʋ́ joori a ba tɩn.».

eng: I assure you that all these things will come upon this generation.
local: A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, nɔɔna bantʋ maama caa wʋ́ ta tɔgɩ zɩm nɔɔna bam.».

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a man who was leaving on a trip. He called his servants and handed his possessions over to them.
local: «Wɛ paarɩ dɩm daa ta nyɩ dɩ sɔŋɔ tu wʋdoŋ na lagɩ o nuŋi o vu cwǝŋǝ. O ma bǝŋi o tɩntʋŋna, yɩ o kwe o wǝǝnu o pa-ba sɩ ba taa niǝ.

eng: Jerusalem, Jerusalem! You who kill the prophets and stone those who were sent to you. How often I wanted to gather your people together, just as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you didnt want that.
local: «Hǝii! Zeruzalɛm tiinǝ-ba, á yǝni á gʋɩ Wɛ nijoŋnǝ, yɩ á dʋlɩ nɔɔna balʋ Wɛ na tʋŋɩ abam tee nɩ tɩn dɩ kandwa. Kuni zanzan mʋ a ya lagɩ sɩ a la abam nɔn-biǝ a kɩ daanɩ, sɩ a taa nii á baŋa nɩ, nɩnɛɛnɩ coro na kukwǝri kʋ biǝ kʋ kɩ kʋ vwana kuri nɩ te tɩn. Abam maa ba sɛa.

eng: To one he gave five valuable coins, and to another he gave two, and to another he gave one. He gave to each servant according to that servants ability. Then he left on his journey.
local: O ma cɛ sǝbu-dala mʋrr-tɩnu o pa ba dɩdʋa, yɩ o daarɩ o pa dɩdʋa sǝbu-dala mʋrr-tɩle . O ma pa dɩdʋa kam dɩ sǝbu-dala mʋrʋ dɩdʋa . O nan pɛ-ba sǝbu kʋm, sɩ kʋ maŋɩ ba dɩdʋa dɩdʋa dam na maɩ te tɩn mʋ. O laan ma daarɩ o viiri.

eng: Look, your house is left to you deserted.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ abam tɩʋ kʋm laan wʋ́ ba kʋ ji di-dwǝǝnu.

eng: After the man left, the servant who had five valuable coins took them and went to work doing business with them. He gained five more.
local: Tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋlʋ na joŋi sǝbu-dala mʋrr-tɩnu tɩm tɩn ma kɩ lɩla o kwe sǝbu kʋm o ma kɩ pipiu, yɩ o na sǝbu-dala mʋrr-tɩnu nyɔɔrɩ o wǝli da.

eng: I tell you, you wont see me until you say, Blessings on the one who comes in the Lords name .
local: Nan taá ye-na nɩ, á daa bá na-nɩ sɩ kʋ taa ve maŋa kalʋ á na wʋ́ ta á wɩ: ‹Zuli-na wʋlʋ na bɩɩnɩ dɩ́ Yuutu Wɛ yɩrɩ ŋwaanɩ tɩn.› ».

eng: In the same way, the one who had two valuable coins gained two more.
local: Sǝbu-dala mʋrr-tɩle tu wʋm dɩ ma na sǝbu-dala mʋrr-tɩle nyɔɔrɩ o wǝli da.

eng: After the Sabbath, at dawn on the first day of the week, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary came to look at the tomb.
local: Ba siun dɛ dɩm na kɛ tɩn, tɩ-bra-pʋra nɩ Mari wʋlʋ na nuŋi Magɩdala tɩn dɩ Mari wʋdoŋ wʋm ma zaŋɩ ba vu yibeeli dɩm jǝgǝ kam sɩ ba nii.

eng: But the servant who had received the one valuable coin dug a hole in the ground and buried his masters money.
local: Kʋ daarɩ sǝbu-dala mʋrʋ dɩdʋa kam tu wʋm ma kwe sǝbu kʋlʋ o tu wʋm na pɛ-o tɩn o kʋ goŋo o suli da.

eng: Look, there was a great earthquake, for an angel from the Lord came down from heaven. Coming to the stone, he rolled it away and sat on it.
local: Tɩga ma da ka sisiŋi zanzan. Baŋa-Wɛ malɛka ma nuŋi wɛyuu nɩ, ka tu ka ba ka bibili kandwɛ kamunu kʋm ka yagɩ daa nɩ, yɩ ka daarɩ ka jǝni dɩ baŋa nɩ.

eng: Now after a long time the master of those servants returned and settled accounts with them.
local: Kʋ na daanɩ tɩn, sɔŋɔ tu wʋm laan ma joori. O ma bǝŋi o tɩntʋŋna bam sɩ ba ba ba jeeli o sǝbu kʋm ba nii.

eng: Now his face was like lightning and his clothes as white as snow.
local: Ka yibiyǝ maa tiini ka nyɩɩna nɩ dʋa na pɩpɩlɩ te tɩn, yɩ ka gwaarʋ tɩm dɩ tiini tɩ pɩɩrɩ parɩ parɩ.

eng: The one who had received five valuable coins came forward with five additional coins. He said, Master, you gave me five valuable coins. Look, Ive gained five more.
local: Sǝbu-dala mʋrr-tɩnu tɩm tu wʋm ma ja sǝbu-dala mʋrr-tɩnu nyɔɔrɩ dɩlʋ o na nɛ tɩn o wǝli da o vu o pa-o. O ma ta dɩ o tu wʋm o wɩ: ‹Amʋ yuutu, nii. Nmʋ ya n pɛ-nɩ sǝbu-dala mʋrr-tɩnu mʋ, yɩ a laan kɩ pipiu a na sǝbu-dala mʋrr-tɩnu nyɔɔrɩ a wǝli da.›.

eng: The guards were so terrified of him that they shook with fear and became like dead men.
local: Nɔɔna balʋ na wʋra ba yɩrɩ yibeeli dɩm tɩn ma bǝri dɩ fʋʋnɩ nɩnɛɛnɩ ba tɩgɩ mʋ te.

eng: His master replied, Excellent! You are a good and faithful servant! Youve been faithful over a little. Ill put you in charge of much. Come, celebrate with me.
local: O tu wʋm ma lǝr-o o wɩ: ‹Nmʋ kɩ cɩga, n jɩgɩ yawala, n yɩ tɩntʋŋ-ŋʋm. Nmʋ sɩɩnɩ n kɩ cɩga dɩ n na nii kʋlʋ fɩnfɩɩn a na kɩ n jɩŋa nɩ tɩn baŋa nɩ. Kʋntʋ, a laan wʋ́ pa n ta n nii a wǝǝnu zanzan baŋa nɩ. Nan ba sɩ dɩ́ kɩ wʋpolo daanɩ.›.

eng: But the angel said to the women, Dont be afraid. I know that you are looking for Jesus who was crucified.
local: Malɛka kam laan ma ta dɩ kaana bam ka wɩ: «Á yɩ taá kwarɩ fʋʋnɩ. Amʋ ye nɩ abam lagɩ sɩ á na Zezi wʋlʋ ba na jaanɩ ba pa tʋʋn-dagara kam baŋa nɩ ba gʋ tɩn mʋ.

eng: The second servant also came forward and said, Master, you gave me two valuable coins. Look, Ive gained two more.
local: Sǝbu-dala mʋrr-tɩle tu wʋm dɩ ma vu o te, yɩ o ta dɩd-o o wɩ: ‹A yuutu, nmʋ ya n pɛ-nɩ sǝbu-dala mʋrr-tɩle mʋ, yɩ a laan kɩ pipiu a na sǝbu-dala mʋrr-tɩle a wǝli da.›.

eng: He isnt here, because hes been raised from the dead, just as he said. Come, see the place where they laid him.
local: O nan tǝri yo. O joori o bi o yagɩ tʋʋnɩ, nɩnɛɛnɩ o ya na maŋɩ o ta kʋlʋ dɩ abam tɩn. Nan ba-na á nii o yɩra yam ya na tigi mɛ tɩn.

eng: His master replied, Well done! You are a good and faithful servant. Youve been faithful over a little. Ill put you in charge of much. Come, celebrate with me.
local: O tu wʋm ma lǝr-o o wɩ: ‹Nmʋ kɩ cɩga, n jɩgɩ yawala, n yɩ tɩntʋŋ-ŋʋm. Nmʋ sɩɩnɩ n kɩ cɩga dɩ n na nii kʋlʋ fɩnfɩɩn a na kɩ n jɩŋa nɩ tɩn baŋa nɩ. Kʋntʋ, a laan wʋ́ pa n ta n nii a wǝǝnu zanzan baŋa nɩ. Nan ba sɩ dɩ́ kɩ wʋpolo daanɩ.›.

eng: Now hurry, go and tell his disciples, Hes been raised from the dead. Hes going on ahead of you to Galilee. You will see him there. Ive given the message to you.
local: Abam nan kɩ lɩla á vu á ta dɩ o karabiǝ bam nɩ, Wɛ pɛ o joori o bi o yagɩ tʋʋnɩ, yɩ o laan wʋ́ da yigǝ o vu o taa cǝgi abam Galile nɩ. Dáanɩ mʋ á wʋ́ vu á na-o. Mʋ kʋlʋ a na lagɩ a ta a brɩ abam tɩn.».

eng: Now the one who had received one valuable coin came and said, Master, I knew that you are a hard man. You harvest grain where you havent sown. You gather crops where you havent spread seed.
local: Sǝbu-dala mʋrʋ dɩdʋa kam tu wʋm laan ma vu o ta dɩ o tu wʋm o wɩ: ‹Amʋ yuutu, a ye nɩ nmʋ laŋa dana. N yǝni n zagɩ mɩna yalʋ n tɩtɩ na wʋ dugi tɩn, yɩ n daarɩ n joŋi nyɔɔrɩ dɩlʋ na daɩ nmʋ tɩtʋŋa nyɩm tɩn.

eng: With great fear and excitement, they hurried away from the tomb and ran to tell his disciples.
local: Malɛka kam na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, kaana bam ma kɩ lɩla ba nuŋi yibeeli dɩm jǝgǝ kam nɩ ba maa kɛa, yɩ fʋʋnɩ dɩ wʋpolo kamunu jɩgɩ-ba. Ba ma duri ba vu sɩ ba ta dɩ Zezi karabiǝ bam kʋlʋ na kɩ tɩn.

eng: So I was afraid. And I hid my valuable coin in the ground. Here, you have whats yours.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ a kwarɩ fʋʋnɩ dɩ nmʋ, yɩ a kwe nmʋ sǝbu kʋm a vu a suli tɩga nɩ. Nan joori n joŋi n sǝbu kʋm.›.

eng: But Jesus met them and greeted them. They came and grabbed his feet and worshipped him.
local: Ba na maa kɛ tɩn, Zezi ma da o jeeri-ba, yɩ o kɩ-ba le. Ba ma vu ba yi o te, ba kuni doonǝ ba ja o nɛ yɩ ba zul-o.

eng: His master replied, You evil and lazy servant! You knew that I harvest grain where I havent sown and that I gather crops where I havent spread seed?.
local: O tu wʋm ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: ‹Nmʋ yɩ tɩntʋŋ-balɔrɔ mʋ, yɩ n jɩgɩ yawɔrɔ zanzan. Nmʋ na ye nɩ amʋ laŋa dana, yɩ a yǝni a zagɩ mɩna yalʋ a tɩtɩ na wʋ dugi tɩn, yɩ a lagɩ nyɔɔrɩ dɩlʋ na daɩ a tɩtʋŋa nyɩm tɩn.

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Dont be afraid. Go and tell my brothers that I am going into Galilee. They will see me there.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Á yɩ taá kwarɩ fʋʋnɩ. Ve-na a ko-biǝ bam te, sɩ á ta dɩ ba sɩ ba vu Galile, sɩ dáanɩ mʋ baá na amʋ.».

eng: In that case, you should have turned my money over to the bankers so that when I returned, you could give me what belonged to me with interest.
local: kʋ ya maŋɩ sɩ nmʋ kwe a sǝbu kʋm n tiŋi bankɩ nɩ. Nmʋ ya na kɩ kʋntʋ, a ya wʋ́ ba a joŋi-kʋ dɩ nyɔɔrɩ.

eng: Now as the women were on their way, some of the guards came into the city and told the chief priests everything that had happened.
local: Kaana bam na nuŋi yibeeli dɩm jǝgǝ kam nɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, nɔɔna balʋ na wʋra ba yɩrɩ-dɩ tɩn badaara ma zaŋɩ ba joori ba vu tɩʋ kʋm wʋ, yɩ ba ta kʋlʋ maama na kɩ tɩn ba brɩ Zwifǝ kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam.

eng: Therefore, take from him the valuable coin and give it to the one who has ten coins.
local: Kʋntʋ tɩn, joŋi-na sǝbu-dala mʋrʋ dɩdʋa kam o tee nɩ á pa wʋlʋ na jɩgɩ sǝbu-dala mʋrr-fugǝ kam tɩn.

eng: They met with the elders and decided to give a large sum of money to the soldiers.
local: Kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam laan ma vu ba ŋɔɔnɩ dɩ tɩʋ kʋm nakwa bam. Ba ma kɩ ni daanɩ yɩ ba kwe sǝbu-fɔrɔ ba pa pamaŋna bam.

eng: Those who have much will receive more, and they will have more than they need. But as for those who dont have much, even the little bit they have will be taken away from them.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ, nɔɔnʋ na maŋɩ o jɩgɩ woŋo, o ta wʋ́ joŋi kʋdoŋ o wǝli da sɩ kʋ tiini kʋ taa daga. Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ nan na maŋɩ o ba jɩgɩ kʋlʋkʋlʋ tɩn laan wʋ́ ba o ga kʋlʋ fɩnfɩɩn o ya na jɩgɩ tɩn dɩ mʋ.

eng: They told them, Say that Jesus disciples came at night and stole his body while you were sleeping.
local: yɩ ba wɩ: «Abam maŋɩ sɩ á ta nɩ, Zezi karabiǝ bam mʋ tu tɩtɩɩ nɩ, ba ŋɔ o yɩra yam ba kwe ba ja viiri, yɩ abam tigi á dɔa.

eng: Now take the worthless servant and throw him out into the farthest darkness. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: Kʋ nan na yɩ tɩntʋŋ-kayaa kantʋ, ja-o-na á nuŋi á dɩ á yagɩ lim jǝgǝ nɩ, mɛ yaara na tiini ya wʋra yɩ nɔɔna keerǝ yɩ ba dʋnɩ ba nwa tɩn.› ».

eng: And if the governor hears about this, we will take care of it with him so you will have nothing to worry about.
local: Gʋvɩrma tu wʋm nan na tu o lwarɩ kʋ ni nɩ, yɩ kʋ na jɩgɩ taanɩ, dɩbam wʋ́ ŋɔɔnɩ dɩ́ joŋi abam dɩ́ yagɩ, sɩ liǝ yɩ taa jɩgɩ abam.».

eng: Now when the Human One comes in his majesty and all his angels are with him, he will sit on his majestic throne.
local: «Nabiin-bu wʋm nan na joori o ba dɩ o paarɩ-zulǝ yam, yɩ Wɛ malɛsɩ sɩm maama tɔgɩ sɩ wʋ o tee nɩ, o laan wʋ́ ba o jǝni o paarɩ jangɔŋɔ kʋm baŋa nɩ.

eng: The beginning of the good news about Jesus Christ, Gods Son.
local: Baŋa-Wɛ Bu Zezi Krisi kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam na puli te tɩn mʋ tɩntʋ:.

eng: So the soldiers took the money and did as they were told. And this report has spread throughout all Judea to this very day.
local: Pamaŋna bam ma joŋi sǝbu kʋm, yɩ ba sɩɩnɩ ba kɩ kʋlʋ ba na tagɩ-ba sɩ ba kɩ tɩn. Mʋ taanɩ dɩntʋ mʋ Zwifǝ bam jɩgɩ ba tɛa, kʋ ba kʋ yi zɩm maama.

eng: All the nations will be gathered in front of him. He will separate them from each other, just as a shepherd separates the sheep from the goats.
local: Lʋgʋ baŋa dwi tiinǝ maama wʋ́ ba ba la daanɩ o yigǝ nɩ. O laan wʋ́ pɔɔrɩ-ba kuni bɩle, nɩ nayɩrʋ na pɔɔrɩ o peeni dɩ o bʋnɩ daanɩ te tɩn.

eng: happened just as it was written about in the prophecy of Isaiah: Look, I am sending my messenger before you . He will prepare your way.
local: Kʋ kɩ nɩ Wɛ nijoŋnu Ezayi na maŋɩ o pʋpʋnɩ kʋlʋ faŋa faŋa yɩ o tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝrǝ o wɩ: «Nii, amʋ Wɛ lagɩ a tʋŋɩ a nɔɔnʋ mʋ, sɩ o da nmʋ yigǝ o vu o kwɛ cwǝŋǝ kalʋ nmʋ na wʋ́ ba n tɔgɩ da tɩn.».

eng: Now the eleven disciples went to Galilee, to the mountain where Jesus told them to go.
local: Zezi karabiǝ fugǝ-dɩdʋa kam laan ma zaŋɩ ba vu Galile, piu kʋlʋ yuu nɩ o na maŋɩ o ta dɩ ba sɩ ba vu tɩn.

eng: He will put the sheep on his right side. But the goats he will put on his left.
local: Oó pɔɔrɩ peeni sɩm o pa sɩ taa wʋ o jazɩm nɩ, yɩ o daarɩ o yagɩ bʋnɩ dɩm o jagwiǝ nɩ.

eng: a voice shouting in the wilderness: Prepare the way for the Lord; make his paths straight .
local: «Nɔɔnʋ mʋ wʋ kagʋa wʋnɩ o tɔɔlɩ kwǝrǝ o wɩ: ‹Zaŋɩ-na á fɔgɩ á kwɛ dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm cwǝŋǝ kam, o na lagɩ o ba o tɔgɩ da tɩn. Pa-na cwe sɩlʋ o na lagɩ o ba o tɔgɩ tɩn fɔgɩ sɩ yɔɔrɩ lanyɩranɩ.› ».

eng: When they saw him, they worshipped him, but some doubted.
local: Ba ma sɩɩnɩ ba vu ba na-o, yɩ ba kuni doonǝ ba zul-o. Badaara maa ta jɩgɩ bʋbwɛa dɩd-o.

eng: Then the king will say to those on his right, Come, you who will receive good things from my Father. Inherit the kingdom that was prepared for you before the world began.
local: Nabiin-bu wʋm na yɩ pɛ kʋntʋ tɩn, o laan wʋ́ ta dɩ balʋ na wʋ o jazɩm nɩ tɩn o wɩ: ‹Abam balʋ a Ko Wɛ na kɩ lanyɩranɩ tɩn, á ba-na á tɔgɩ á di paarɩ dɩlʋ DƖ na maŋɩ DƖ tiŋi DƖ pa abam, kʋ na sɩŋɩ lʋgʋ pulim nɩ tɩn.

eng: John the Baptist was in the wilderness calling for people to be baptized to show that they were changing their hearts and lives and wanted God to forgive their sins.
local: Kʋ dɛɛn sɩɩnɩ kʋ kɩ kʋntʋ doŋ mʋ. Nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ dɛɛn mʋ wʋra, o yɩrɩ mʋ Zan. O maa wʋ kagʋa wʋnɩ, yɩ o miisi nɔɔna na wʋnɩ Wɛ ŋwaanɩ. O maa yǝni o tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝrǝ, o brɩ nɔɔna nɩ ba maŋɩ sɩ ba lǝni ba wʋrʋ ba yagɩ kǝm-balwaarʋ, sɩ ba daarɩ ba pa o miisi-ba na wʋnɩ, sɩ Wɛ laan wʋ́ yagɩ ba lwarɩm DƖ ma cɛ-ba.

eng: Jesus came near and spoke to them, Ive received all authority in heaven and on earth.
local: Zezi laan ma vu o yi ba te, yɩ o ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Wɛ pɛ-nɩ dam sɩ a taa te wɛyuu dɩ tɩga baŋa wǝǝnu maama.

eng: When Jesus finished speaking all these words, he said to his disciples.
local: Zezi dɛɛn na tagɩ wǝǝnu tɩm kʋntʋ maama o ti tɩn, o laan ma ta dɩ o karabiǝ bam o wɩ:.

eng: I was hungry and you gave me food to eat. I was thirsty and you gave me a drink. I was a stranger and you welcomed me.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ, kana dɛɛn jaanɩ amʋ, yɩ á pa-nɩ wʋdiu yɩ a di. Na-nyɔm dɛɛn jaanɩ-nɩ, yɩ á pa-nɩ na yɩ a nyɔ. A dɛɛn tu abam sam, yɩ á kɩ-nɩ voro lanyɩranɩ.

eng: Everyone in Judea and all the people of Jerusalem went out to the Jordan River and were being baptized by John as they confessed their sins.
local: Nɔɔna zanzan maa yǝni ba nuŋi Zude tɩ-biǝ bam maama dɩ Zeruzalɛm ba ve o te. Ba na ve o te kʋntʋ tɩn, ba maa tɛ ba brɩ kʋlʋ ba na kɩ ba cɔgɩ tɩn. O laan maa paɩ ba tu Zʋrdɛn bugǝ kam wʋnɩ, yɩ o miisi-ba na wʋnɩ Wɛ ŋwaanɩ.

eng: Therefore, go and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ, nan ve-na lʋgʋ baŋa dwi tiinǝ maama te, sɩ á pa ba ji amʋ karabiǝ, sɩ á daarɩ á miisi-ba na wʋnɩ dɩ amʋ Ko Wɛ yɩrɩ, dɩ amʋ na yɩ DƖ Bu tɩn yɩrɩ, dɩ DƖ Joro kʋm yɩrɩ ŋwaanɩ.

eng: You know that the Passover is two days from now. And the Human One will be handed over to be crucified.
local: «Abam ye sɩ kʋ daarɩ da yale mʋ sɩ Pakɩ candiǝ kam yi, yɩ ba laan wʋ́ ja amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm ba kɩ dɩdɛɛra jɩa nɩ, sɩ ba pa-nɩ tʋʋn-dagara baŋa nɩ ba gʋ.».

eng: I was naked and you gave me clothes to wear. I was sick and you took care of me. I was in prison and you visited me.
local: A dɛɛn ba jɩgɩ gɔrɔ sɩ a zʋ, yɩ á pa-nɩ gɔrɔ. A dɛɛn ba jɩgɩ yazurǝ, yɩ á ba á nii-nɩ. Ba dɛɛn kɩ-nɩ pɩɩna digǝ nɩ, yɩ á ba á nii-nɩ.›.

eng: John wore clothes made of camels hair, with a leather belt around his waist. He ate locusts and wild honey.
local: Zan dɛɛn zʋʋrɩ gɔrɔ kʋlʋ ba na sɔgɩ dɩ yogondi kʋrʋ tɩn mʋ. O maa maɩ kilǝ o ja o tǝŋǝ. O wʋdiu mʋ yɩ kayɩra dɩ tʋʋrʋ.

eng: teaching them to obey everything that Ive commanded you. Look, I myself will be with you every day until the end of this present age.
local: sɩ á daarɩ á brɩ-ba sɩ ba sɛ woŋo kʋlʋ maama amʋ na pɛ abam ni sɩ á kɩ tɩn. Nan taá ye-na nɩ, amʋ wʋ́ ta wʋra dɩ abam maŋa maama, sɩ kʋ vu kʋ yi lʋgʋ tiim maŋa.».

eng: Then the chief priests and elders of the people gathered in the courtyard of Caiaphas the high priest.
local: Zwifǝ nakwa bam dɩ ba kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam ma kikili daanɩ Kayifu sɔŋɔ nɩ. Wʋntʋ mʋ yɩ ba kaanɩm yuutu.

eng: Then those who are righteous will reply to him, Lord, when did we see you hungry and feed you, or thirsty and give you a drink?.
local: Wɛ nɔn-ŋʋna bam laan wʋ́ zaŋɩ ba bwe-o ba nii: ‹Dɩ́ Yuutu, maŋa kɔɔ mʋ dɩbam nɛ kana jɩgɩ nmʋ yɩ dɩ́ pa-m wʋdiu yɩ n di, naa na-nyɔm jɩgɩ-m yɩ dɩ́ pa-m na yɩ n nyɔ?.

eng: He announced, One stronger than I am is coming after me. Im not even worthy to bend over and loosen the strap of his sandals.
local: O maa tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝrǝ dɩ nɔɔna bam o wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ wʋ́ saŋɩ amʋ kwaga o ba, wʋntʋ tiini o dwe amʋ. A wʋ maŋɩ sɩ a tiiri a lɩ o nɛ natra a pa-o.

eng: They were plotting to arrest Jesus by cunning tricks and to kill him.
local: Ba ma banɩ daanɩ sɩ ba nii, ba na wʋ́ kɩ swan yalʋ ba ma ja Zezi ba gʋ tɩn.

eng: When did we see you as a stranger and welcome you, or naked and give you clothes to wear?.
local: Maŋa kɔɔ mʋ nmʋ tu dɩbam sam yɩ dɩ́ kɩ nmʋ voro, naa maŋa kɔɔ mʋ n ba jɩgɩ gɔrɔ yɩ dɩ́ pa-m gɔrɔ.

eng: I baptize you with water, but he will baptize you with the Holy Spirit.
local: Amʋ miisi abam na wʋnɩ mʋ. Wʋntʋ nan na tuǝ, oó miisi abam Wɛ Joro kʋm wʋnɩ.».

eng: But they agreed that it shouldnt happen during the feast so there wouldnt be an uproar among the people.
local: Ba na wʋra ba bwɛ kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma ta ba wɩ: «Dɩ́ nan wʋ maŋɩ sɩ dɩ́ ja-o candiǝ kam maŋa nɩ, sɩ kʋʋ́ pa vuvugǝ jǝni nɔɔna bam tɩtarɩ nɩ.».

eng: When did we see you sick or in prison and visit you.
local: naa maŋa kɔɔ mʋ nmʋ ba jɩgɩ yazurǝ naa n wʋ pɩɩna digǝ nɩ yɩ dɩ́ ba dɩ́ nii-m?›.

eng: About that time, Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee, and John baptized him in the Jordan River.
local: Maŋa kam kʋntʋ nɩ, Zezi dɛɛn ma nuŋi Galile tɩʋ kʋdoŋ, kʋ yɩrɩ mʋ Nazarɛtɩ. O ma vu o yi Zan te, yɩ o tu Zʋrdɛn bugǝ kam wʋ yɩ Zan miis-o na wʋnɩ.

eng: When Jesus was at Bethany visiting the house of Simon, who had a skin disease.
local: Zezi dɛɛn maa wʋ Betani nɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ sɔŋɔ nɩ, o yɩrɩ mʋ Simɔn. Wʋntʋ dɛɛn ya yɩ nanyɔŋɔ mʋ.

eng: Then the king will reply to them, I assure you that when you have done it for one of the least of these brothers and sisters of mine, you have done it for me.
local: Pɛ wʋm laan wʋ́ lǝri o ta dɩ ba o wɩ: ‹A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, abam nan na kɩ kʋntʋ doŋ á ma á wǝli a nɔɔna bam tɩn, kʋ na maŋɩ kʋ yɩ wʋlʋ na tiini o muri ba maama wʋnɩ tɩn dɩ, kʋ yɩ amʋ mʋ á kɩ kʋntʋ.›.

eng: While he was coming up out of the water, Jesus saw heaven splitting open and the Spirit, like a dove, coming down on him.
local: Zezi na maa nuŋi na bam wʋnɩ tɩn, o ma na wɛyuu na pʋrɩ yɩ Wɛ Joro kʋm tɔgɩ da kʋ tu o baŋa, yɩ kʋ nyɩ dɩ kunkwǝŋǝ te.

eng: a woman came to him with a vase made of alabaster containing very expensive perfume. She poured it on Jesus head while he was sitting at dinner.
local: Zezi na wʋra o di wʋdiu maŋa kalʋ tɩn, mʋ kaanɩ wʋdoŋ tu o te. O maa jɩgɩ kunkwǝlǝ ka na su dɩ tralɩ nugǝ kalʋ sǝbu na daga tɩn. O ma vu o lo nugǝ kam Zezi yuu nɩ sɩ o ma zul-o.

eng: Then he will say to those on his left, Get away from me, you who will receive terrible things. Go into the unending fire that has been prepared for the devil and his angels.
local: O laan ma wʋ́ daarɩ o ta dɩ balʋ na wʋ o jagwiǝ nɩ tɩn o wɩ: ‹Á ve daa a tee nɩ, sɩ abam mʋ yɩ balʋ Wɛ sɔɔla na wʋ á yuu nɩ tɩn. Kɛ-na á vu á zʋ mini dɩlʋ na ba dwe tɩn wʋ, Wɛ na maŋɩ DƖ tiŋi sɩ DƖ pa sʋtaanɩ dɩ dɩ malɛsɩ sɩm tɩn.

eng: And there was a voice from heaven: You are my Son, whom I dearly love; in you I find happiness.
local: O ma ni kwǝrǝ laan na ŋɔɔnɩ wɛyuu nɩ ka wɩ: «Nmʋ mʋ yɩ amʋ bu-dʋa a na soe dɩ a wʋ maama. A tiini a jɩgɩ wʋpolo zanzan dɩ nmʋ.».

eng: Now when the disciples saw it they were angry and said, Why this waste?.
local: Zezi karabiǝ bam na nɛ kʋntʋ tɩn, ba maa ba jɩgɩ wʋpolo. Ba maa tɛ ba wɩ: «Bɛɛ mʋ yɩ o ja nugǝ kam o cɔgɩ tɩntʋ?.

eng: I was hungry and you didnt give me food to eat. I was thirsty and you didnt give me anything to drink.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ, kana dɛɛn jaanɩ amʋ, yɩ á wʋ pɛ-nɩ wʋdiu sɩ a di. Na-nyɔm dɩ ma ja-nɩ, yɩ á wʋ pɛ-nɩ na sɩ a nyɔ.

eng: At once the Spirit forced Jesus out into the wilderness.
local: Kʋ na-niǝ da mʋ Wɛ Joro kʋm dɛ kʋ pa o vu kagʋa wʋ.

eng: This perfume could have been sold for a lot of money and given to the poor.
local: Dɩ́ ya wʋ́ wanɩ dɩ́ kwe-ka dɩ́ yǝgi sǝbu zanzan dɩ́ ma wǝli yinigǝ tiinǝ.».

eng: I was a stranger and you didnt welcome me. I was naked and you didnt give me clothes to wear. I was sick and in prison, and you didnt visit me.
local: A dɛɛn tu abam sam, yɩ á wʋ kɩ-nɩ voro. A dɛɛn ba jɩgɩ gɔrɔ, yɩ á wʋ pɛ-nɩ gɔrɔ sɩ a zʋ. A dɛɛn ba jɩgɩ yazurǝ, yɩ ba ta kwǝri ba kɩ-nɩ pɩɩna digǝ nɩ, yɩ á wʋ tu sɩ á nii-nɩ.›.

eng: He was in the wilderness for forty days, tempted by Satan. He was among the wild animals, and the angels took care of him.
local: O maa wʋ kagʋa kam wʋnɩ taan da fiinna. O na wʋra tɩn, sʋtaanɩ laan ma tui o te, sɩ dɩ gan-o sɩ o ya kɩ o cɔgɩ. O maa zʋʋrɩ dɩ kagʋ-vara, yɩ Wɛ malɛsɩ dɩ tui o te sɩ zǝn-o.

eng: But Jesus knew what they were thinking. He said, Why do you make trouble for the woman? Shes done a good thing for me.
local: Zezi ma lwarɩ ba na tɛ kʋlʋ tɩn, yɩ o ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Bɛɛ mʋ yɩ á jɩgɩ kaanɩ wʋm á yaara? O na kɩ-nɩ zulǝ yalʋ tɩn yɩ kǝm-laa mʋ o kɩ-nɩ.

eng: Jesus responded by speaking again in parables.
local: Zezi dɛɛn ma maŋɩ mɩmaŋɩ dɩdoŋ o brɩ nɔɔna bam o wɩ:.

eng: Then they will reply, Lord, when did we see you hungry or thirsty or a stranger or naked or sick or in prison and didnt do anything to help you.
local: Ba ma wʋ́ lǝri ba bwe-o ba wɩ: ‹Dɩ́ Yuutu, maŋa kɔɔ mʋ dɩbam kɩ nmʋ kʋntʋ? Maŋa kɔɔ mʋ dɩ́ nɛ kana dɩ na-nyɔm na jɩgɩ nmʋ, naa n na yɩ vǝru, naa n ba jɩgɩ gɔrɔ, naa n ba jɩgɩ yazurǝ, naa ba kɩ-m pɩɩna digǝ nɩ, yɩ dɩbam wʋ zǝni-m?›.

eng: After John was arrested, Jesus came into Galilee announcing Gods good news.
local: Ba dɛɛn ma ja Zan ba kɩ pɩɩna digǝ nɩ. Kʋntʋ kwaga nɩ Zezi ma joori o vu Galile. O maa wʋra o tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam o brɩ nɔɔna.

eng: You always have the poor with you, but you wont always have me.
local: Kʋ nan na yɩ yinigǝ tiinǝ, bantʋ wʋra mʋ dɩ abam maŋa maama sɩ á taá zǝni-ba. Kʋ daarɩ amʋ nan bá ta wʋra dɩ abam maŋa maama.

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a king who prepared a wedding party for his son.
local: «Wɛ paarɩ dɩm nyɩ dɩ pɛ wʋdoŋ na lagɩ sɩ o kɩ kadiri candiǝ o pa o bu te tɩn.

eng: Then he will answer, I assure you that when you havent done it for one of the least of these, you havent done it for me.
local: O ma wʋ́ ta dɩ ba o wɩ: ‹A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, abam na vɩn sɩ á wǝli amʋ nɔɔna bam tɩn, kʋ na maŋɩ kʋ yɩ wʋlʋ na tiini o muri ba maama wʋnɩ tɩn dɩ, kʋ yɩ amʋ mʋ á vɩn sɩ á wǝli.›.

eng: saying, Now is the time! Here comes Gods kingdom! Change your hearts and lives, and trust this good news.
local: yɩ o wɩ: «Maŋa kam laan yiǝ, Wɛ paarɩ dɩm na lagɩ dɩ ba nɔɔna tɩtarɩ nɩ tɩn. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ, lǝni-na á wʋrʋ á yagɩ kǝm-balwaarʋ tɩm, sɩ á daarɩ á sɛ Wɛ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam.».

eng: By pouring this perfume over my body shes prepared me to be buried.
local: Kaanɩ wʋm na logi nugǝ kam a yɩra nɩ tɩn, kʋ brɩ nɩ o wuuri o turi a yɩra yam mʋ, sɩ ba laan kwe-nɩ ba kɩ.

eng: He sent his servants to call those invited to the wedding party. But they didnt want to come.
local: Pɛ wʋm ya tagɩ dɩ nɔɔna badonnǝ mʋ sɩ ba ba ba tɔgɩ ba di kadiri candiǝ kam. Dɛ dɩm na tu dɩ yi tɩn, o ma tʋŋɩ o tɩntʋŋna sɩ ba vu ba bǝŋi nɔɔna bam kʋntʋ sɩ ba ba. Ba ma wʋ sɛ sɩ ba ba.

eng: And they will go away into eternal punishment. But the righteous ones will go into eternal life.
local: Bantʋ laan wʋ́ kɛ ba vu cam dɩlʋ na bá fɔgɩ dɩ ti tɩn wʋnɩ. Kʋ daarɩ Wɛ nɔn-ŋʋna bam ma wʋ́ zʋ DƖ te ba na ŋwɩa kalʋ na ba ti tɩn.».

eng: As Jesus passed alongside the Galilee Sea, he saw two brothers, Simon and Andrew, throwing fishing nets into the sea, for they were fishermen.
local: Zezi dɛɛn maa wʋ Galile nɩnɩʋ kʋm ni nɩ o veǝ. O ma na nɔɔna bale, ba na yɩ cʋrʋ. Ba dɩdʋa yɩrɩ mʋ Simɔn. O nyaanɩ yɩrɩ mʋ Andre. Ba yɩ kalǝŋ-jara mʋ. Ba maa wʋ na bam wʋnɩ ba dʋlɩ bʋrɩ sɩ ba ma ja kale.

eng: I tell you the truth that wherever in the whole world this good news is announced, what shes done will also be told in memory of her.
local: A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, ba na wʋ́ tɔɔlɩ amʋ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam lʋgʋ baŋa je maama nɩ tɩn, baá ta kaanɩ wʋm kʋntʋ dɩ taanɩ sɩ ba taa guli o gulǝ.».

eng: Again he sent other servants and said to them, Tell those who have been invited,Look, the meal is all prepared. Ive butchered the oxen and the fattened cattle. Now everythings ready. Come to the wedding party.
local: O laan ma joori o tʋŋɩ o tɩntʋŋna badonnǝ, sɩ ba vu nɔɔna bam te ba ta dɩ ba nɩ: ‹Ba-na, sɩ amʋ ti woŋo maama yɩ a pa ba gʋ na-paala dɩ varɩ-nunuguru. Nan ba-na sɩ dɩ́ di candiǝ kam.›.

eng: Come, follow me, he said, and Ill show you how to fish for people.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Zaŋɩ-na á ba á taá tɔgɩ amʋ. Aá pa á taá beeri nɔɔna mʋ á pa-nɩ, sɩ ba taa tɔgɩ-nɩ, nɩ á na yǝni á jaanɩ kale te tɩn.».

eng: Then one of the Twelve, who was called Judas Iscariot, went to the chief priests.
local: Kʋntʋ kwaga nɩ, Zudasɩ Yiskarɩyo wʋlʋ na yɩ Zezi karabiǝ fugǝ-bale bam wʋ dɩdʋa tɩn laan ma zaŋɩ o vu Zwifǝ bam kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam te.

eng: But they paid no attention and went away—some to their fields, others to their businesses.
local: Nɔɔna balʋ ba na bǝŋi kʋntʋ tɩn ma zaŋɩ ba vu ba wʋbʋŋa je, ba na ba nɩgɩ o beeri dɩm tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Ba dɩdʋa ve o kara mʋ. Dɩdʋa ma vu sɩ o kɩ o pipiu.

eng: Right away, they left their nets and followed him.
local: O na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma zaŋɩ lɩla ba yagɩ ba bʋrɩ sɩm, yɩ ba vu ba tɔgɩ o kwaga.

eng: and said, What will you give me if I turn Jesus over to you? They paid him thirty pieces of silver.
local: O ma bwe-ba o wɩ: «Abam wʋ́ pa amʋ bɛɛ mʋ, sɩ a brɩ abam cwǝŋǝ sɩ á ja Zezi?» Ba na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma sɛ sɩ ba pa-o sǝbu-dala fiintɔ.

eng: The rest of them grabbed his servants, abused them, and killed them.
local: Nɔɔna balʋ na daarɩ tɩn ma ja pɛ wʋm tɩntʋŋna bam, ba bɛɛsɩ-ba ba gʋ.

eng: After going a little farther, he saw James and John, Zebedees sons, in their boat repairing the fishing nets.
local: Zezi na ve yigǝ fɩnfɩɩn tɩn, o daa ma na nɔɔna bale. Ba dɩdʋa yɩrɩ mʋ Zakɩ. O nyaanɩ dɩ maa wʋra, o yɩrɩ mʋ Zan. Ba ko yɩrɩ mʋ Zebede. Ba maa wʋ naboro wʋnɩ ba kwɛ ba bʋrɩ.

eng: From that time on he was looking for an opportunity to turn him in.
local: Kʋ na puli maŋa kam kʋntʋ nɩ tɩn, o laan ma kwaanɩ o beeri cwǝŋǝ sɩ o ja Zezi o kɩ ba jɩa nɩ.

eng: The king was angry. He sent his soldiers to destroy those murderers and set their city on fire.
local: Pɛ wʋm banɩ ma tiini dɩ zaŋɩ. O ma tʋŋɩ o pamaŋna sɩ ba vu ba gʋ nɔn-gʋra bam kʋntʋ, sɩ ba daarɩ ba zwɛ ba tɩʋ kʋm maama.

eng: On the first day of the Festival of Unleavened Bread, the disciples came to Jesus and said, Where do you want us to prepare for you to eat the Passover meal.
local: Kʋ ma ba kʋ yi dɛ dɩlʋ ba na puli sɩ ba di dɩpɛ dɩlʋ na ba jɩgɩ dabɩlɩ dɩ wʋnɩ tɩn. Zezi karabiǝ bam ma vu ba bwe-o ba wɩ: «Nmʋ lagɩ sɩ dɩ́ di Pakɩ candiǝ kam jǝgǝ kɔɔ nɩ mʋ, sɩ dɩ́ vu dɩ́ ti woŋo maama yigǝ dɩ́ pa-m?».

eng: At that very moment he called them. They followed him, leaving their father Zebedee in the boat with the hired workers.
local: O laan ma bǝŋi-ba. O na bǝŋi-ba kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma zaŋɩ lɩla ba yagɩ ba ko wʋm dɩ o tɩntʋŋna bam naboro kʋm wʋnɩ, yɩ ba vu ba tɔg-o.

eng: He replied, Go into the city, to a certain man, and say, The teacher says,My time is near. Im going to celebrate the Passover with my disciples at your house.
local: O ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Zaŋɩ-na á vu tɩʋ kʋm wʋ nɔɔnʋ doŋ te, sɩ á ta dɩd-o nɩ: ‹Karanyɩna mʋ wɩ, o maŋa kam yiǝ. O nan lagɩ o tɔgɩ dɩ o karabiǝ bam o ba nmʋ sɔŋɔ mʋ, sɩ ba di Pakɩ candiǝ kam wʋdiu da.› ».

eng: Then he said to his servants, The wedding party is prepared, but those who were invited werent worthy.
local: O laan ma ta dɩ o tɩntʋŋna bam o wɩ: ‹Kadiri dɩm woŋo maama ti kʋ yigǝ sɩ dɩ́ di. A nan na bǝŋi balʋ sɩ dɩ́ di candiǝ kam tɩn ya wʋ maŋɩ dɩ ka.

eng: Jesus and his followers went into Capernaum. Immediately on the Sabbath Jesus entered the synagogue and started teaching.
local: Zezi dɩ o karabiǝ ma tɔgɩ daanɩ ba vu ba zʋ tɩʋ kʋdoŋ wʋ, kʋ yɩrɩ mʋ Kapɛrnawum. Zwifǝ bam siun dɛ na yi tɩn, Zezi ma vu o zʋ ba Wɛ-digǝ kam sɩ o brɩ-ba.

eng: The disciples did just as Jesus instructed them. They prepared the Passover.
local: O karabiǝ bam ma vu ba kɩ o na tagɩ kʋlʋ tɩn, yɩ ba kwɛ candiǝ kam wǝǝnu tɩm ba ti.

eng: Therefore, go to the roads on the edge of town and invite everyone you find to the wedding party.
local: Nan nuŋi-na á vu tɩʋ kʋm je sɩm maama nɩ, á nii cwe sɩm niǝ nɩ. Á na nɛ nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ maama tɩn, sɩ á bǝŋ-o sɩ o ba o tɔgɩ o di.›.

eng: The people were amazed by his teaching, for he was teaching them with authority, not like the legal experts.
local: O na brɩ-ba tɩn, nɔɔna balʋ na wʋra tɩn maa tiini ba yɩ yǝǝu dɩ o zaasɩm dɩm, bɛŋwaanɩ o ni-taanɩ dɩm jɩgɩ dam dɩ dwe ba tɩtɩ cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ bam taanɩ dɩm.

eng: That evening he took his place at the table with the twelve disciples.
local: Tɩga ma ba ka yi. Zezi dɩ o karabiǝ fugǝ-bale bam laan ma jǝni sɩ ba di wʋdiu kʋm.

eng: Then those servants went to the roads and gathered everyone they found, both evil and good. The wedding party was full of guests.
local: O tɩntʋŋna bam ma nuŋi ba beeri tɩʋ kʋm maama, ba la nɔɔna balʋ maama ba na nɛ tɩn, kʋ na yɩ nɔn-ŋʋna dɩ nɔn-balwaarʋ dɩ. Pɛ wʋm sɔŋɔ kʋm ma tiini kʋ su dɩ nɔɔna.

eng: Suddenly, there in the synagogue, a person with an evil spirit screamed.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ dɛɛn maa wʋ ba Wɛ-digǝ kam wʋnɩ, yɩ cicirǝ jɩg-o. O ma kaasɩ dɩ kwǝr-dɩa o wɩ:.

eng: As they were eating he said, I assure you that one of you will betray me.
local: Ba na wʋra ba di tɩn, Zezi laan ma ta o wɩ: «A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, abam wʋ nɔɔnʋ dɩdʋa mʋ lagɩ o yǝgi amʋ o pa nɔɔna.».

eng: Now when the king came in and saw the guests, he spotted a man who wasnt wearing wedding clothes.
local: Pɛ wʋm laan ma nuŋi sɩ o zaanɩ nɔɔna bam. O ma na nɔɔnʋ dɩdʋa na wʋra o na wʋ zʋ gwar-ŋʋŋʋ kʋlʋ na maŋɩ dɩ kadiri dim tɩn.

eng: What have you to do with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are. You are the holy one from God.
