local: «Zezi, Nazarɛtɩ tu, bɛɛ mʋ n lagɩ dɩbam tee nɩ? Nmʋ tu sɩ n cɔgɩ dɩbam dwi mʋ na? A ye nmʋ na yɩ wʋlʋ. Nmʋ yɩ Wʋ-poŋo Tu wʋlʋ Wɛ na tʋŋɩ tɩn mʋ.».

eng: He said to him, Friend, how did you get in here without wedding clothes? But he was speechless.
local: Pɛ wʋm ma bwe-o o wɩ: ‹A ciloŋ, nmʋ kɩ ta mʋ n tɔgɩ n zʋ yo seeni, yɩ nmʋ wʋ zʋ gɔrɔ kʋlʋ na lana tɩn?› Nɔɔnʋ wʋm ma ga ka lǝrǝ je.

eng: Deeply saddened, each one said to him, Im not the one, am I, Lord.
local: Ba na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, ba wʋrʋ ma tiini tɩ cɔgɩ zanzan, yɩ ba puli sɩ ba taa bwe-o dɩdʋa dɩdʋa ba wɩ: «Amʋ Yuutu, kʋ yɩ amʋ mʋ na?».

eng: Silence! Jesus said, speaking harshly to the demon. Come out of him.
local: Zezi ma kaanɩ cicirǝ kam o wɩ: «Cǝgi sɔɔ, sɩ n nuŋi n daarɩ baarʋ wʋm.».

eng: Then the king said to his servants, Tie his hands and feet and throw him out into the farthest darkness. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: Pɛ wʋm laan ma ta dɩ o tɩntʋŋna bam o wɩ: ‹Ja-na nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ á vɔ o jɩa dɩ o nɛ, sɩ á ja-o á nuŋi á dɩ lim wʋnɩ, yaara na wʋ mɛ yɩ nɔɔna keerǝ yɩ ba dʋnɩ ba nwa tɩn.›.

eng: He replied, The one who will betray me is the one who dips his hand with me into this bowl.
local: O ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na tɔgɩ dɩ amʋ o lu zʋŋa dɩdʋa wʋnɩ tɩn mʋ lagɩ o yǝgi amʋ o pa nɔɔna.

eng: Many people are invited, but few people are chosen.
local: Kʋntʋ brɩ nɩ, Wɛ bǝŋi nɔɔna zanzan mʋ, yɩ DƖ daarɩ DƖ kuri ba fɩnfɩɩn yɩranɩ.».

eng: The unclean spirit shook him and screamed, then it came out.
local: O na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, cicirǝ kam ma tiini ka ja nɔɔnʋ wʋm ka su. Ka ma kaasɩ dɩ kwǝr-dɩa yɩ ka nuŋi ka daarɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋm.

eng: The Human One goes to his death just as it is written about him. But how terrible it is for that person who betrays the Human One! It would have been better for him if he had never been born.
local: Kʋ nan maŋɩ sɩ amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm vu a tɩ, nɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm na maŋɩ kʋ brɩ a taanɩ te tɩn. Kʋ nan za wʋ́ ta yɩ lɛɛrʋ mʋ kʋ pa nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na wʋ́ yǝgi amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm o pa tɩn. Ba ya na maŋɩ ba wʋ lʋgɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋm kʋntʋ, kʋ ya wʋ́ ta gara.».

eng: Then the Pharisees met together to find a way to trap Jesus in his words.
local: Farizɩan tiinǝ bam laan ma zaŋɩ ba vu sɩ ba banɩ ba nii, ba na wʋ́ kɩ te ba pa Zezi ŋɔɔnɩ o tusi sɩ ba ja-o tɩn.

eng: Everyone was shaken and questioned among themselves, Whats this? A new teaching with authority! He even commands unclean spirits and they obey him.
local: Nɔɔna bam na nɛ kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ ma sʋ ba maama lanyɩranɩ, yɩ ba bwe daanɩ ba wɩ: «Bɛɛ mʋ kʋntʋ? O brɩ cwǝ-dʋŋa mʋ yɩ o zaasɩm dɩm jɩgɩ dam. Nii, o na tɛ ciciri nɩ sɩ viiri, yɩ sɩ sɛ o ni.».

eng: Now Judas, who would betray him, replied, Its not me, is it, Rabbi? Jesus answered, You said it.
local: Zudasɩ wʋlʋ na lagɩ o yǝg-o o pa tɩn laan ma bwe-o o wɩ: «Karanyɩna, kʋ yɩ amʋ mʋ na?» Zezi ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «Mʋ ka da n taga.».

eng: They sent their disciples, along with the supporters of Herod, to him. Teacher, they said, we know that you are genuine and that you teach Gods way as it really is. We know that you are not swayed by peoples opinions, because you dont show favoritism.
local: Ba ma lɩ ba karabiǝ badonnǝ, sɩ ba tɔgɩ dɩ Pɛ Erɔdɩ kwaga nɔɔna badonnǝ ba vu Zezi te. Ba ma vu ba ta dɩd-o ba wɩ: «Karanyɩna, dɩbam ye nɩ nmʋ yɩ cɩga tu mʋ, yɩ n yǝni n brɩ nɔɔna Wɛ cwǝŋǝ kam cɩga. Nmʋ yigǝ tǝrǝ dɩ nɔɔna na bʋŋɩ kʋlʋ ba pa nmʋ tɩn, nmʋ nan ba kuri nɔɔna daanɩ.

eng: Right away the news about him spread throughout the entire region of Galilee.
local: Nɔɔna balʋ maama na zʋʋrɩ Galile je sɩm nɩ tɩn dɛɛn ma ni Zezi ŋwa lɩla.

eng: While they were eating, Jesus took bread, blessed it, broke it, and gave it to the disciples and said, Take and eat. This is my body.
local: Ba na wʋra ba di maŋa kalʋ tɩn, Zezi ma kwe dɩpɛ yɩ o kɩ Wɛ le dɩ ŋwaanɩ. O ma daarɩ o fɔ-dɩ o pa o karabiǝ bam yɩ o wɩ: «Joŋi-na á di, sɩ dɩntʋ yɩ a yɩra mʋ.».

eng: So tell us what you think: Does the Law allow people to pay taxes to Caesar or not.
local: Dɩ́ nan lagɩ sɩ dɩ́ bwe nmʋ dɩ́ nii, nmʋ wʋbʋŋa yɩ tɩta mʋ? Dɩbam Zwifǝ bam cullu pɛ ni sɩ dɩ́ ŋwɩ lampoo dɩ́ pa lʋgʋ kʋm pa-farʋ wʋm na? Mʋ na kʋ wʋ maŋɩ?».

eng: After leaving the synagogue, Jesus, James, and John went home with Simon and Andrew.
local: Zezi dɩ o nɔɔna bam ma nuŋi Wɛ-digǝ kam nɩ, ba tɔgɩ dɩ Zakɩ dɩ Zan ba vu ba yi Simɔn dɩ Andre-ba sɔŋɔ.

eng: He took a cup, gave thanks, and gave it to them, saying, Drink from this, all of you.
local: O daa ma kwe tweeru biǝ sana dɩ zʋŋa, yɩ o kɩ Wɛ le tɩ ŋwaanɩ. O ma kwe o pa-ba yɩ o wɩ: «Abam maama joŋi-na á nyɔ.

eng: Knowing their evil motives, Jesus replied, Why do you test me, you hypocrites?.
local: Zezi na lwarɩ ba na jɩgɩ wʋbʋŋ-balwaarʋ tɩlʋ tɩn, o ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Abam yɩ pipiri-nyɩna mʋ. Bɛɛ nan mʋ yɩ á lagɩ sɩ á maŋɩ-nɩ á nii?.

eng: This is my blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many so that their sins may be forgiven.
local: Wo-nyɔɔrʋ tɩntʋ mʋ yɩ a jana kalʋ na lagɩ ka nuŋi ka duri sɩ ka ma vrɩ nɔɔna zanzan ba lwarɩm wʋnɩ tɩn. Kʋ maa brɩ nɩ Wɛ goni ni sɩ DƖ yagɩ nɔɔna lwarɩm DƖ ma cɛ-ba amʋ jana kam ŋwaanɩ.

eng: Simons mother-in-law was in bed, sick with a fever, and they told Jesus about her at once.
local: Paa maa jɩgɩ Simɔn tɩnkaanɩ yɩ o tigǝ. Ba ma ta dɩ Zezi kʋ na kɩ-o te tɩn.

eng: Show me the coin used to pay the tax. And they brought him a denarion.
local: Nan brɩ-na-nɩ sǝbu-dalɩ dɩlʋ dɩ́ na yǝni dɩ́ ma ŋwɩ lampoo tɩn.» Ba ma lɩ sǝbu-dalɩ dɩm ba brɩ-o.

eng: I tell you, I wont drink wine again until that day when I drink it in a new way with you in my Fathers kingdom.
local: Taá ye-na nɩ, a daa bá nyɔ wo-nyɔɔrʋ tɩntʋ, sɩ kʋ taa ve maŋa kam abam na wʋ́ tɔgɩ dɩ amʋ á jǝni amʋ Ko Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋnɩ, sɩ dɩ́ daa nyɔ daanɩ.».

eng: He went to her, took her by the hand, and raised her up. The fever left her, and she served them.
local: O ma vu kaanɩ wʋm te, o ja o jɩŋa o pa o zaŋɩ wɛɛnɩ. Paa kam ma da ka yag-o. O ma zaŋɩ o kɩ wʋdiu o pa-ba.

eng: Whose image and inscription is this? he asked.
local: O ma bwe-ba o wɩ: «Wɔɔ nyɩnyʋgʋ dɩ o yɩrɩ mʋ pʋpʋnɩ dɩ baŋa nɩ?».

eng: Then, after singing songs of praise, they went to the Mount of Olives.
local: Ba laan ma leeni ba zuli Wɛ, yɩ ba daarɩ ba nuŋi ba vu Olivi piu yuu.

eng: That evening, at sunset, people brought to Jesus those who were sick or demon-possessed.
local: Wɛ na zʋ yɩ tɩga wʋra ka yi tɩn, nɔɔna maa jaanɩ balʋ maama na yɩ yawɩɩna dɩ ciciri na jɩgɩ balʋ maama tɩn ba tui Zezi te.

eng: Caesars, they replied. Then he said, Give to Caesar what belongs to Caesar and to God what belongs to God.
local: Ba ma lǝr-o ba wɩ: «Kʋ yɩ pa-farʋ wʋm mʋ.» O laan ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Kʋ lamma. Nan taá ŋwɩ-na kʋlʋ na yɩ pa-farʋ wʋm nyɩm tɩn á pa-o, sɩ á daarɩ á taá paɩ Wɛ kʋlʋ dɩ na yɩ DƖ nyɩm tɩn.».

eng: Then Jesus said to his disciples, Tonight you will all fall away because of me. This is because it is written, I will hit the shepherd, and the sheep of the flock will go off in all directions .
local: Zezi laan ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Tɩtɩɩ dɩntʋ tɩtɩ nɔ́ɔ mʋ abam maama wʋ́ joori amʋ kwaga nɩ, nɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm na maŋɩ kʋ brɩ te tɩn. Wɛ dɛɛn tagɩ DƖ wɩ: ‹Aá pa ba gʋ nayɩrʋ wʋm, yɩ o peeni sɩm maama jagɩ.›.

eng: The whole town gathered near the door.
local: Tɩʋ kʋm nɔɔna bam maama ma vu ba gilimi Simɔn-ba sɔŋɔ ni nɩ.

eng: When they heard this they were astonished, and they departed.
local: O na lǝri-ba kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ ma sʋ-ba zanzan. Ba laan ma yag-o yɩ ba viiri.

eng: But after Im raised up, Ill go before you to Galilee.
local: Kʋ daarɩ, Wɛ na joori DƖ bi-nɩ, aá da yigǝ a vu a taa cǝgi abam Galile nɩ.».

eng: He healed many who were sick with all kinds of diseases, and he threw out many demons. But he didnt let the demons speak, because they recognized him.
local: Zezi ma pa nɔɔna zanzan na yazurǝ dɩ ba yawɩɩrʋ dwi dwi, yɩ o daarɩ o zǝli ciciri zanzan nɔɔna yɩra nɩ. O ma wʋ sɛ sɩ ciciri sɩm ŋɔɔnɩ kʋlʋkʋlʋ, sɩ na maŋɩ sɩ ye o na yɩ wʋlʋ tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: That same day Sadducees, who deny that there is a resurrection, came to Jesus.
local: Dɛ dɩm kʋntʋ nɩ, Sadusɩan tiinǝ badonnǝ dɛɛn ma zaŋɩ ba vu Zezi te. Bantʋ mʋ brɩ nɩ, nɔɔnʋ na tɩga o daa bá joori o bi tɩn.

eng: Peter replied, If everyone else stumbles because of you, Ill never stumble.
local: Zezi na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, Pɩyɛɛrɩ ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Nɔɔna bam maama na maŋɩ ba joori nmʋ kwaga nɩ, amʋ bá fɔgɩ a joori nmʋ kwaga nɩ.».

eng: Early in the morning, well before sunrise, Jesus rose and went to a deserted place where he could be alone in prayer.
local: Tɩga na pʋʋrɩ tɩtɩɩtɩ yɩ wɛ daa ta wʋ puli tɩn, Zezi ma zaŋɩ o nuŋi o vu jǝgǝ kalʋ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ na tǝrǝ tɩn. O wʋra o warɩ Wɛ o yɩranɩ.

eng: They asked, Teacher, Moses said, If a man who doesnt have children dies, his brother must marry his wife and produce children for his brother.
local: Ba ma vu ba ta dɩ Zezi ba wɩ: «Karanyɩna, Moyisi dɛɛn brɩ nɩ, nɔɔnʋ na tɩga, yɩ o dɩ o kaanɩ na ba jɩgɩ biǝ daanɩ, o nu-bu mʋ maŋɩ sɩ o di kadǝm wʋm sɩ ba lʋ biǝ o cʋrʋ kʋlʋ na tɩgɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: Jesus said to him, I assure you that, before the rooster crows tonight, you will deny me three times.
local: Zezi ma ta lǝr-o o wɩ: «A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ nmʋ sɩ, tɩtɩɩ dɩntʋ tɩtɩ nɔ́ɔ mʋ, kʋ na wʋ́ loori sɩ cɩbwɩʋ keeri tɩn, nmʋ wʋ́ fʋ kuni bɩtɔ nɩ n yǝri amʋ.».

eng: Simon and those with him tracked him down.
local: Simɔn-ba laan ma zaŋɩ ba nuŋi sɩ ba beeri o jǝgǝ.

eng: Now there were seven brothers among us. The first one married, then died. Because he had no children he left his widow to his brother.
local: Kʋntʋ, nu-pugǝ-biǝ barpɛ mʋ ya wʋ dɩbam tɩtarɩ nɩ. Ba dɩdʋa ma di kaanɩ yɩ o tɩ o daar-o, yɩ o wʋ lʋgɩ bu dɩd-o. Wʋlʋ na saŋɩ tɩn dɩ daa ma lǝri o di kaanɩ wʋm.

eng: Peter said, Even if I must die alongside you, I wont deny you. All the disciples said the same thing.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ laan ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «A brɩ a bá fʋ nɩ a yǝri nmʋ, kʋ na maŋɩ kʋ yɩ ba na lagɩ ba gʋ amʋ dɩ nmʋ maama dɩ.» Zezi karabiǝ bam maama dɩ ma ta kʋntʋ doŋ.

eng: When they found him, they told him, Everyones looking for you.
local: Ba na nɛ-o tɩn, ba ma ta dɩd-o ba wɩ: «Nɔɔna bam maama lagɩ sɩ ba na nmʋ.».

eng: The same thing happened with the second brother and the third, and in fact with all seven brothers.
local: O dɩ ma tɩ o daar-o. Batɔ tu wʋm dɩ daa ma di-o. Kʋ kɩ kʋntʋ doŋ mʋ dɩ ba maama barpɛ, yɩ ba wʋ lʋgɩ bu dɩ kaanɩ wʋm.

eng: Then Jesus went with his disciples to a place called Gethsemane. He said to the disciples, Stay here while I go and pray over there.
local: Zezi laan ma zaŋɩ o tɔgɩ dɩ o karabiǝ bam o vu jǝgǝ kadoŋ, gaarɩ na wʋra yɩ ba bǝ-ka nɩ Jesemanɩ tɩn. O ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Á taá je yo, sɩ a vu daa a warɩ Wɛ sɩ a ba.».

eng: He replied, Lets head in the other direction, to the nearby villages, so that I can preach there too. Thats why Ive come.
local: Zezi ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Zaŋɩ-na sɩ dɩ́ kɛ dɩ́ vu je sɩdonnǝ. A maŋɩ sɩ a vu tɩ-balwa balʋ na wʋ yoba seeni tɩn a tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝrǝ kam. Kʋ yɩ kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ a nuŋi Wɛ te a ba.».

eng: Finally, the woman died.
local: Ba maama na tɩgɩ tɩn, kaanɩ wʋm dɩ laan ma tɩ.

eng: When he took Peter and Zebedees two sons, he began to feel sad and anxious.
local: O ma pa Pɩyɛɛrɩ dɩ Zebede biǝ bale bam tɔg-o ba vu. O wʋ ma tiini kʋ cɔgɩ yɩ o bɩcarɩ di.

eng: At the resurrection, which of the seven brothers will be her husband? They were all married to her.
local: Dɩ́ nan lagɩ dɩ́ bwe dɩ́ nii, ba maama na di kaanɩ wʋm tɩn, ba laan na tu ba bi ba yagɩ tʋʋnɩ, bantʋ wɔɔ mʋ wʋ́ ta te kaanɩ wʋm?».

eng: He traveled throughout Galilee, preaching in their synagogues and throwing out demons.
local: O laan ma beeri o tɔgɩ Galile wʋ maama. O maa yǝni o zʋʋrɩ Zwifǝ bam Wɛ-di sɩm. O maa brɩ nɔɔna Wɛ cɩga kam, yɩ o zǝli ciciri nɔɔna yɩra nɩ.

eng: Then he said to them, Im very sad. Its as if Im dying. Stay here and keep alert with me.
local: O ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «A wʋ mʋ tiini kʋ cɔgɩ sɩ kʋ yi a tʋʋnɩ. Á nan zɩgɩ-na yo, á taá kogili-nɩ sɩ á warɩ Wɛ, sɩ á yɩ zaŋɩ á dɔ.».

eng: Jesus responded, You are wrong because you dont know either the scriptures or Gods power.
local: Zezi ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Abam tiini á tusi mʋ. Abam nan wʋ ni Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm zaasɩm dɩm kuri dɩ, yɩ á yǝri Wɛ dam dɩm na yɩ te tɩn.

eng: A man with a skin disease approached Jesus, fell to his knees, and begged, If you want, you can make me clean.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ dɛɛn mʋ wʋra, o na yɩ nanyɔŋɔ. O ma zaŋɩ o vu Zezi te, o kuni doonǝ o yigǝ nɩ o loor-o o wɩ: «Nmʋ na laga, nń wanɩ n pa a na yazurǝ a ji lanyɩranɩ.».

eng: Then he went a short distance farther and fell on his face and prayed, My Father, if its possible, take this cup of suffering away from me. However—not what I want but what you want.
local: O laan ma fʋfɔ o vu daa. O ma vi o yigǝ tɩga nɩ o wʋra o loori Wɛ yɩ o wɩ: «Amʋ Ko, kʋ na waɩ, sɩ n pa cam dɩlʋ na lagɩ dɩ ba amʋ baŋa nɩ tɩn kɛ. Nmʋ nan kɩ te na yi nmʋ wʋbʋŋa tɩn, sɩ n yɩ kɩ amʋ wʋbʋŋa na lagɩ kʋlʋ tɩn.».

eng: At the resurrection people wont marry nor will they be given in marriage. Instead, they will be like angels from God.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ, nɔɔna na tu ba bi ba yagɩ tʋʋnɩ, baá ta nyɩ dɩ Wɛ malɛsɩ sɩm na yɩ te tɩn mʋ, baara daa bá di kaana, kaana dɩ nan daa bá zʋ banna dɩ.

eng: Incensed, Jesus reached out his hand, touched him, and said, I do want to. Be clean.
local: O ŋwaŋa ma zʋ Zezi. O ma twɩ o jɩŋa o dwe-o, yɩ o wɩ: «Ɛɛn. A laga. Zaŋɩ dɩ yazurǝ sɩ n ji lanyɩranɩ.».

eng: He came back to the disciples and found them sleeping. He said to Peter, Couldnt you stay alert one hour with me?.
local: O na loori Wɛ o ti tɩn, o ma joori o vu o karabiǝ batɔ bam te. O ma na nɩ ba tigi ba dɔa. O ma zaŋɩ-ba, yɩ o ta dɩ Pɩyɛɛrɩ o wɩ: «Bɛɛ mʋ yɩ abam warɩ sɩ á kogili amʋ, sɩ á yɩ zaŋɩ á dɔ sɩ kʋ yi mɩmaŋa fɩnfɩɩn dɩ?.

eng: As for the resurrection of the dead, havent you read what God told you.
local: Kʋ nan na yɩ twa biim dɩm dɩ, abam maŋɩ á wʋ karɩmɩ kʋlʋ Wɛ na tagɩ DƖ brɩ abam yɩ kʋ pʋpʋnɩ DƖ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ tɩn na?.

eng: Instantly, the skin disease left him, and he was clean.
local: O na tagɩ kʋntʋ bɩdwɩ baŋa nɩ tɩn, mʋ yawɩʋ kʋm yagɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋm, yɩ o yɩra yam ji lanyɩranɩ.

eng: Stay alert and pray so that you wont give in to temptation. The spirit is eager, but the flesh is weak.
local: Fɔgɩ-na á yɩrɩ á tɩtɩ sɩ á taá loori Wɛ, sɩ á yɩ tʋ maŋɩm dɩm maŋa nɩ. Bɛŋwaanɩ nanzwǝnǝ wʋbʋŋa lagɩ sɩ ya sɛ Baŋa-Wɛ, yɩ o yɩra laan bwǝnǝ.».

eng: Im the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob? He isnt the God of the dead but of the living.
local: DƖ tagɩ DƖ wɩ: ‹Amʋ yɩ Abraham Wɛ, dɩ Yizakɩ Wɛ, dɩ Zakɔbɩ Wɛ mʋ.› Kʋ brɩ nɩ bantʋ daa ta jɩgɩ ŋwɩa mʋ DƖ tee nɩ, bɛŋwaanɩ Baŋa-Wɛ yɩ naŋʋna Wɛ mʋ sɩ DƖ daɩ twa Wɛ.».

eng: Sternly, Jesus sent him away.
local: Zezi ma daa ta dɩd-o o wɩ, o ve, yɩ o daarɩ o kaan-o o wɩ:.

eng: A second time he went away and prayed, My Father, if its not possible that this cup be taken away unless I drink it, then let it be what you want.
local: Zezi daa ma joori o vu daa, yɩ o loori Wɛ o wɩ: «Amʋ Ko, cam dɩntʋ na bá wanɩ dɩ lɩ amʋ baŋa nɩ dɩ yagɩ, yɩ a na maŋɩ sɩ a zʋ dɩ wʋ mʋ, amʋ ta sɛ sɩ kʋ kɩ nmʋ wʋbʋŋa na lagɩ te tɩn.».

eng: Now when the crowd heard this, they were astonished at his teaching.
local: Nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm na ni Zezi zaasɩm dɩm na yɩ te tɩn, kʋ ma tiini kʋ sʋ-ba.

eng: saying, Dont say anything to anyone. Instead, go and show yourself to the priest and offer the sacrifice for your cleansing that Moses commanded. This will be a testimony to them.
local: «Yɩ zaŋɩ n ta kʋlʋ na kɩ tɩn n brɩ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ. Nan yɔɔrɩ n vu Wɛ kaanɩm tu wʋm te, sɩ n pa o nii n yɩra yam, sɩ n daarɩ n kwe wǝǝnu tɩlʋ Moyisi cullu tɩm na brɩ nɩ tɩ maŋɩ tɩn n pa-o, sɩ o ma o kaanɩ Baŋa-Wɛ. Kʋntʋ wʋ́ brɩ nɔɔna maama nɩ n yawɩʋ kʋm sɩɩnɩ kʋ je.».

eng: Again he came and found them sleeping. Their eyes were heavy with sleep.
local: O daa ma joori o vu o na nɩ ba ta tigi ba dɔa, dɔɔm na tiini dɩ waɩ-ba tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: When the Pharisees heard that Jesus had left the Sadducees speechless, they met together.
local: Farizɩan tiinǝ bam dɛɛn ma lwarɩ Zezi na lǝri Sadusɩan tiinǝ bam te tɩn, kʋ pa ba daa warɩ taanɩ ba ta. Ba laan ma kikili daanɩ sɩ ba lagɩ cwǝŋǝ ba ma ja-o.

eng: But he left them and again went and prayed the same words for the third time.
local: O ma yagɩ-ba, yɩ o joori o vu daa o daa ta loori Wɛ o kɩ kuni bɩtɔ, nɩ o ya na maŋɩ o ta kʋlʋ tɩn.

eng: Instead, he went out and started talking freely and spreading the news so that Jesus wasnt able to enter a town openly. He remained outside in deserted places, but people came to him from everywhere.
local: Zezi na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, nɔɔnʋ wʋm ma nuŋi yɩ o karɩ o ŋɔɔnɩ kʋlʋ na kɩ tɩn o pa nɔɔna maama lwarɩ kʋ ni nɩ. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ Zezi daa warɩ o zʋ tɩʋ dɩ tɩʋ wʋ yɩ o wʋ sǝgi o tɩtɩ. O ma lɛɛrɩ o maŋɩ gaa wʋnɩ nɔɔna na tǝrǝ mɛ tɩn. Nɔɔna maa yǝni ba nuŋi je maama ba ve o te.

eng: Then he came to his disciples and said to them, Will you sleep and rest all night? Look, the time has come for the Human One to be betrayed into the hands of sinners.
local: O daa ma joori o vu o karabiǝ bam te, yɩ o ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Á ta tigi á dɔ mʋ na? Nii-na! Maŋa kam yiǝ, sɩ ba ja amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm ba kɩ nɔn-balwaarʋ jɩa nɩ.

eng: One of them, a legal expert, tested him.
local: Ba wʋ dɩdʋa na yɩ Wɛ cullu tɩm karanyɩna tɩn ma zaŋɩ sɩ o maŋ-o o nii o swan na yɩ te tɩn, o ma bwe-o o wɩ:.

eng: Get up. Lets go. Look, here comes my betrayer.
local: Zaŋɩ-na sɩ dɩ́ vu. Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na lagɩ o yǝgi-nɩ o pa tɩn maa bɩɩna.».

eng: Teacher, what is the greatest commandment in the Law.
local: «Karanyɩna, Wɛ niǝ yam maama wʋnɩ dɔɔ mʋ yɩ kamunu dɩ dwe?».

eng: While Jesus was still speaking, Judas, one of the Twelve, came. With him was a large crowd carrying swords and clubs. They had been sent by the chief priests and elders of the people.
local: Zezi ta na wʋra o ŋɔɔnɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, mʋ Zudasɩ wʋlʋ na yɩ o karabiǝ fugǝ-bale bam wʋ dɩdʋa tɩn tu o yi o te, yɩ nɔn-kɔgɔ zanzan tɔgɩ dɩd-o kʋ ba, yɩ ba ze sʋ-lwaanʋ dɩ da-lwaanʋ. Kʋ yɩ Zwifǝ nakwa bam dɩ ba kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam mʋ tʋŋɩ-ba.

eng: He replied, You must love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your being, and with all your mind.
local: Zezi ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «Ta n soe n Yuutu Baŋa-Wɛ dɩ n bɩcarɩ maama, dɩ n wʋ maama, dɩ n wʋbʋŋa maama.

eng: His betrayer had given them a sign: Arrest the man I kiss.
local: Zudasɩ ya maŋɩ o pa kɔgɔ kʋm lwarɩ nɩ, o na ve o kukwǝri nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ tɩn, sɩ ba ja kʋntʋ tu.

eng: This is the first and greatest commandment.
local: Dɩntʋ nan mʋ yɩ Wɛ dayigǝ ni yɩ dɩ yɩ kamunu.

eng: Just then he came to Jesus and said, Hello, Rabbi. Then he kissed him.
local: O laan ma yɔɔrɩ o yi Zezi te o jɔɔn-o yɩ o wɩ: «Karanyɩna, dɩnle», yɩ o daarɩ o kukwǝr-o.

eng: And the second is like it: You must love your neighbor as you love yourself .
local: Dɩlʋ na saŋɩ tɩn dɩ nyɩ dɩ dɩntʋ mʋ, dɩ tagɩ dɩ wɩ, ta n soe n doŋ nɩ n tɩtɩ te.

eng: But Jesus said to him, Friend, do what you came to do. Then they came and grabbed Jesus and arrested him.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Amʋ ciloŋ, nmʋ na tu sɩ n kɩ kʋlʋ tɩn, kɩ-kʋ lɩla.» Nɔɔna bam laan ma yi ba ja Zezi.

eng: All the Law and the Prophets depend on these two commands.
local: Moyisi zaasɩm dɩm dɩ faŋa faŋa Wɛ nijoŋnǝ bam zaasɩm dɩm maama yɔɔrɩ dɩ puli niǝ yantʋ yale baŋa nɩ mʋ.».

eng: One of those with Jesus reached for his sword. Striking the high priests slave, he cut off his ear.
local: Nɔɔna balʋ na maŋɩ ba wʋ Zezi tee nɩ tɩn dɩdʋa ma lɩ o sʋgʋ o ma zagɩ kaanɩm yuutu wʋm tɩntʋŋnʋ zwɛ o go.

eng: Now as the Pharisees were gathering, Jesus asked them.
local: Farizɩan tiinǝ badonnǝ dɛɛn ma ba ba kikili daanɩ. Zezi ma zaŋɩ o bwe-ba o wɩ:.

eng: Then Jesus said to him, Put the sword back into its place. All those who use the sword will die by the sword.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Joori n sɩn n sʋgʋ kʋm kʋ polo nɩ, sɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ maama na soe jara dɩ sʋgʋ tɩn, sʋgʋ mʋ wʋ́ gʋ kʋ tu.

eng: What do you think about the Christ? Whose son is he? Davids son, they replied.
local: «Kʋ na yɩ Krisi wʋm Wɛ na lagɩ DƖ tʋŋɩ tɩn, abam bʋŋɩ nɩ oó ta yɩ wɔɔ dwi tu mʋ?» Ba ma lǝr-o ba wɩ: «Oó ta yɩ Pɛ Davidi dwi tu mʋ.».

eng: Or do you think that Im not able to ask my Father and he will send to me more than twelve battle groups of angels right away?.
local: Nmʋ yǝri nɩ, a ya na lagɩ zǝnǝ, a ya wʋ́ ta dɩ a Ko Wɛ, yɩ o da o tʋŋɩ malɛsɩ kɔgɔ kuni fugǝ-bɩle, sɩ sɩ ba sɩ wǝli-nɩ na?.

eng: He said, Then how is it that David, inspired by the Holy Spirit, called him Lord when he said.
local: Zezi laan ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Kʋ nan wʋ́ kɩ ta mʋ kʋ taa yɩ kʋntʋ? Wɛ Joro kʋm dɛɛn pɛ Davidi ŋɔɔnɩ o woŋo, yɩ o bǝŋ-o nɩ o Yuutu. Faŋa faŋa tɩn Davidi tagɩ o wɩ:.

eng: But if I did that, how would the scriptures be fulfilled that say this must happen.
local: Amʋ nan ya na kɩ kʋntʋ, Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm na brɩ nɩ kʋ maŋɩ sɩ kʋ kɩ amʋ te tɩn daa wʋ́ wanɩ kʋ kɩ na?».

eng: The Lord said to my lord, Sit at my right side until I turn your enemies into your footstool?.
local: ‹Yuutu Baŋa-Wɛ tagɩ dɩ a Yuutu DƖ wɩ: Ba n jǝni a jazɩm nɩ, sɩ aá kɩ nmʋ dʋna maama n nɛ kuri nɩ.›.

eng: Then Jesus said to the crowds, Have you come with swords and clubs to arrest me, like a thief? Day after day, I sat in the temple teaching, but you didnt arrest me.
local: Zezi laan ma bwe kɔgɔ kʋm o wɩ: «Abam na kwe sʋ-lwaanʋ dɩ da-lwaanʋ á ba sɩ á ja amʋ tɩn, a yɩ ŋwɩɩnʋ mʋ na? Dɛ maama a yǝni a je Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm wʋnɩ a brɩ nɔɔna, yɩ á wʋ jaanɩ-nɩ.

eng: If David calls him Lord, how can he be Davids son.
local: Davidi nan na sɩɩnɩ o bǝŋi Krisi wʋm o wɩ o Yuutu tɩn, o nan daa wʋ́ kɩ ta mʋ o taa yɩ Davidi naa daga?».

eng: But all this has happened so that what the prophets said in the scriptures might be fulfilled. Then all the disciples left Jesus and ran away.
local: Kʋntʋ maama nan kɩ, sɩ kʋ pa faŋa faŋa Wɛ nijoŋnǝ bam na pʋpʋnɩ kʋlʋ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ tɩn mʋ ba kʋ sɩɩnɩ kʋ kɩ.» Zezi karabiǝ bam na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma duri ba viiri ba daar-o.

eng: Nobody was able to answer him. And from that day forward nobody dared to ask him anything.
local: Zezi na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ daa maa warɩ sɩ o lǝri kʋlʋkʋlʋ. Kʋ na zɩgɩ dɛ dɩm kʋntʋ nɩ tɩn, nɔɔna bam maama maa kwarɩ sɩ ba daa bwe-o taanɩ.

eng: Those who arrested Jesus led him to Caiaphas the high priest. The legal experts and the elders had gathered there.
local: Nɔɔna balʋ na tu ba ja Zezi tɩn ma ja-o ba vu ba kaanɩm yuutu Kayifu sɔŋɔ, mɛ Zwifǝ cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ dɩ ba nakwa bam na maŋɩ ba kikili da tɩn.

eng: Peter followed him from a distance until he came to the high priests courtyard. He entered that area and sat outside with the officers to see how it would turn out.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ ma dalɩ o tɔgɩ ba kwaga, o da o vu o yi kaanɩm yuutu wʋm sɔŋɔ. O ma zʋ o jǝni sɔŋɔ kʋm tɩntʋŋna bam tee nɩ, sɩ o nii kʋlʋ na lagɩ kʋ kɩ Zezi tɩn.

eng: The chief priests and the whole council were looking for false testimony against Jesus so that they could put him to death.
local: Zwifǝ kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam dɩ ba sarɩya-dirǝ nakwa bam maama maa kwaanɩ sɩ ba na nɔɔna balʋ na wʋ́ fɔ vwan ba pa Zezi tɩn, sɩ kʋ brɩ nɩ kʋ maŋɩ sɩ ba gʋ-o.

eng: They didnt find anything they could use from the many false witnesses who were willing to come forward. But finally they found two.
local: Nɔɔna zanzan ma zaŋɩ ba fɔ vwan ba pa-o, yɩ ba daa ta wʋ wanɩ ba na kʋlʋ na brɩ nɩ kʋ maŋɩ sɩ ba gʋ-o tɩn. Kʋ kwaga seeni mʋ nɔɔna bale zaŋɩ ba ba, yɩ ba ta ba wɩ:.

eng: who said, This man said, I can destroy Gods temple and rebuild it in three days.
local: «Nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ tagɩ o wɩ, wʋm wʋ́ wanɩ o magɩ dɩbam Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm maama o dɩ tɩga nɩ, yɩ o joori o ma da yatɔ o lɔ-ka.».

eng: Now Jesus left the temple and was going away. His disciples came to point out to him the temple buildings.
local: Zezi laan ma nuŋi Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm nɩ sɩ o viiri. O na maa kɛ tɩn, o karabiǝ bam ma ba o te, sɩ ba pa o maanɩ Wɛ-digǝ kam lɔɔm dɩm na lana te.

eng: Then the high priest stood and said to Jesus, Arent you going to respond to the testimony these people have brought against you.
local: Kaanɩm yuutu wʋm na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, o laan ma zaŋɩ o bwe Zezi o wɩ: «Nɔɔna bantʋ na tagɩ kʋlʋ ba pa nmʋ tɩn, nmʋ ba jɩgɩ taanɩ sɩ n lǝri na?».

eng: He responded, Do you see all these things? I assure that no stone will be left on another. Everything will be demolished.
local: O ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Abam wʋ nɛ ka na yɩ te tɩn na? A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, ka maama wʋ́ ba ka tʋ tɩga nɩ ka cɔgɩ maama. Kandwɛ daa bá daŋɩ dɩ doŋ baŋa nɩ.».

eng: But Jesus was silent. The high priest said, By the living God, I demand that you tell us whether you are the Christ, Gods Son.
local: Zezi maa cɩm. Kaanɩm yuutu wʋm laan ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Kwarɩ Ŋwɩa Tu Baŋa-Wɛ fʋʋnɩ, sɩ n ta n brɩ dɩbam, nmʋ yɩ Wɛ Bu Krisi wʋm DƖ na tʋŋɩ tɩn mʋ na?».

eng: Now while Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives, the disciples came to him privately and said, Tell us, when will these things happen? What will be the sign of your coming and the end of the age.
local: Zezi dɛɛn ma vu o jǝni Olivi piu kʋm yuu nɩ. O karabiǝ bam yɩranɩ ma vu o te, yɩ ba bwe-o ba wɩ: «Maŋa kɔɔ mʋ Wɛ-digǝ kam wʋ́ ba ka tʋ kʋntʋ? Bɛɛ mʋ lagɩ kʋ kɩ maana kʋ brɩ dɩbam nɩ maŋa kam yi sɩ nmʋ joori n ba lʋgʋ baŋa, sɩ lʋgʋ tiim mɩmaŋa laan yi?».

eng: You said it, Jesus replied. But I say to you that from now on youll see the Human One sitting on the right side of the Almighty and coming on the heavenly clouds .
local: Zezi ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «Mʋ ka da n taga. Amʋ nan lagɩ a ta dɩ abam nɩ, abam wʋ́ ba á na Nabiin-bu wʋm o na je Dam-fɔrɔ Tu Baŋa-Wɛ jazɩm nɩ, yɩ á ta wʋ́ daarɩ á na-o o na nuŋi Wɛ-sɔŋɔ nɩ o maa bɩɩnɩ kunkwǝǝnu wʋnɩ.».

eng: Jesus replied, Watch out that no one deceives you.
local: Zezi ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Á fɔgɩ á cɩ á tɩtɩ, sɩ á yɩ pa nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ ganɩ abam.

eng: Then the high priest tore his clothes and said, Hes insulting God! Why do we need any more witnesses? Look, youve heard his insult against God.
local: Kaanɩm yuutu wʋm na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma zaŋɩ o kaarɩ o tɩtɩ gɔrɔ, sɩ kʋ brɩ nɩ o wʋ tiini kʋ cɔgɩ dɩ Zezi taanɩ dɩm. O ma ta o wɩ: «Nii-na, o ŋɔɔnɩ o twɩ Wɛ mʋ, o paɩ o dɩ Wɛ ma daanɩ mʋ. Dɩ́ daa ba lagɩ maana tiinǝ sɩ ba ŋɔɔnɩ o taanɩ dɩm. Abam maama maŋɩ á ni, o na ŋɔɔnɩ o twɩ Wɛ te.

eng: Many will come in my name, saying, Im the Christ. They will deceive many people.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ nɔɔna zanzan wʋ́ ba abam te, yɩ baá ma amʋ yɩrɩ ba ma ganɩ abam ba wɩ, bam dɩdʋa dɩdʋa mʋ yɩ Krisi wʋm Wɛ na tʋŋɩ tɩn. Kʋntʋ doŋ mʋ baá ganɩ nɔɔna zanzan.

eng: What do you think? And they answered, He deserves to die.
local: Kʋntʋ tɩn, abam wɩ bɛɛ mʋ?» Ba ma lǝr-o ba wɩ: «O maŋɩ sɩ ba gʋ-o mʋ.».

eng: You will hear about wars and reports of wars. Dont be alarmed. These things must happen, but this isnt the end yet.
local: Á wʋ́ ba á na tigurǝ na wʋ abam je sɩm nɩ, yɩ á kwǝri á ni ka ŋwa tɩɩnɩ dɩlʋ na baŋwɛ tɩn nɩ. Nan yɩ pa-na fʋʋnɩ zʋ abam, bɛŋwaanɩ wǝǝnu tɩm kʋntʋ maama maŋɩ sɩ tɩ kɩ mʋ, yɩ kʋ ta daɩ lʋgʋ tiim maŋa.

eng: Then they spit in his face and beat him. They hit him.
local: Ba laan ma twɩ lileeru ba yagɩ o yiǝ nɩ, yɩ ba mag-o lanyɩranɩ. Badaara ma lɔ o pʋpwaanʋ.

eng: Nations and kingdoms will fight against each other, and there will be famines and earthquakes in all sorts of places.
local: Dwi wʋ́ zaŋɩ dɩ kɩ jara dɩ dwi dɩdoŋ, yɩ tɩʋ dɩ kʋ doŋ wʋ́ zaŋɩ tɩ magɩ jara. Kana dɩ wʋ́ ba ka taa wʋ je zanzan nɩ, yɩ tɩga dɩ wʋ́ sisiŋi je sɩdonnǝ nɩ.

eng: and said, Prophesy for us, Christ! Who hit you.
local: yɩ ba wɩ: «Nmʋ na yɩ Krisi wʋm Wɛ na tʋŋɩ tɩn, sɩ n lwarɩ n brɩ dɩbam wʋlʋ na magɩ-m tɩn.».

eng: But all these things are just the beginning of the sufferings associated with the end.
local: Wǝǝnu tɩm kʋntʋ maama nan nyɩ dɩ kaanɩ na sɩŋɩ o vri pugǝ yɩ ka wɔɛ te tɩn mʋ, yɩ cam daa ta wʋ kwaga nɩ.

eng: Meanwhile, Peter was sitting outside in the courtyard. A servant woman came and said to him, You were also with Jesus the Galilean.
local: Kantʋ maŋa kam nɩ Pɩyɛɛrɩ maa je kʋnkɔlɔ kʋm wʋnɩ. Kaanɩm yuutu wʋm sɔŋɔ tɩntʋŋ-kana ma vu o te, yɩ o ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Nmʋ dɩ tɔgɩ dɩ Zezi, Galile tu wʋm mʋ.».

eng: They will arrest you, abuse you, and they will kill you. All nations will hate you on account of my name.
local: Kantʋ maŋa kam nɩ nɔɔna wʋ́ ja abam ba kɩ dɩdɛɛra jɩa nɩ sɩ ba bɛɛsɩ abam, yɩ ba daarɩ ba gʋ abam. Abam na yɩ amʋ nɔɔna tɩn ŋwaanɩ, lʋgʋ baŋa nɔɔna maama wʋ́ ta culi abam.

eng: But he denied it in front of all of them, saying, I dont know what you are talking about.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ ma fʋ ba maama yigǝ nɩ o wɩ: «A yǝri woŋo kʋlʋ nmʋ na tɛ tɩn.».

eng: At that time many will fall away. They will betray each other and hate each other.
local: Kantʋ maŋa nɩ nɔɔna zanzan wʋ́ ba ba yagɩ amʋ kwaga tɔgɩm. Nɔɔna badaara wʋ́ kʋ da-kurǝ ba brɩ daanɩ, yɩ badonnǝ dɩ wʋ́ ta culi daanɩ.

eng: When he went over to the gate, another woman saw him and said to those who were there, This man was with Jesus, the man from Nazareth.
local: O ma zaŋɩ o nuŋi mancoŋo ni. Tɩntʋŋ-kana kadoŋ dɩ daa ma na-o, yɩ o ta dɩ nɔɔna balʋ na wʋra tɩn o wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ tɔgɩ dɩ Nazarɛtɩ tu Zezi mʋ.».

eng: Many false prophets will appear and deceive many people.
local: Nɔɔna zanzan wʋ́ ba ba brɩ nɩ ba yɩ Wɛ nijoŋnǝ mʋ yɩ ba daɩ, yɩ baá ganɩ nɔɔna zanzan.

eng: With a solemn pledge, he denied it again, saying, I dont know the man.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ ma du durǝ, yɩ o ta fʋ o wɩ: «A brɩ a yǝri nɔɔnʋ wʋm kʋntʋ.».

eng: Because disobedience will expand, the love of many will grow cold.
local: Wo-balwaarʋ tɩm na wʋ́ ba tɩ tiini tɩ gaalɩ tɩn, kʋʋ́ pa nɔɔna zanzan sono ti.

eng: A short time later those standing there came and said to Peter, You must be one of them. The way you talk gives you away.
local: Kʋ na kɩ fɩɩn tɩn, nɔɔna badonnǝ ma vu ba ta dɩ Pɩyɛɛrɩ ba wɩ: «Nmʋ sɩɩnɩ n yɩ ba kɔgɔ kʋm wʋ nɔɔnʋ dɩdʋa mʋ. Nmʋ ŋwaŋa kam mʋ brɩ nɩ n sɩɩnɩ n nuŋi dáanɩ mʋ.».

eng: But the one who endures to the end will be delivered.
local: Wʋlʋ maama nan na kɩ pu-dɩa o zɩgɩ kǝŋkǝŋ sɩ kʋ vu kʋ yi kweelim tɩn, Wɛ wʋ́ vrɩ kʋntʋ tu ŋwɩa.

eng: Then he cursed and swore, I dont know the man! At that very moment the rooster crowed.
local: O laan ma sɔɔlɩ o pa o tɩtɩ, sɩ kʋ brɩ nɩ o taanɩ dɩm yɩ cɩga, yɩ o wɩ: «A brɩ a yǝri nɔɔnʋ wʋm kʋntʋ.» O na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, cɩbwɩʋ laan ma da kʋ keeri.

eng: This gospel of the kingdom will be proclaimed throughout the world as a testimony to all the nations. Then the end will come.
local: Nɔɔna dɩ wʋ́ beeri lʋgʋ baŋa je maama ba tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam nɩ Wɛ lagɩ DƖ di paarɩ nɔɔna tɩtarɩ nɩ, sɩ kʋ taa yɩ maana nabiinǝ maama tee nɩ, sɩ lʋgʋ tiim laan yi.

eng: Peter remembered Jesus words, Before the rooster crows you will deny me three times.And Peter went out and cried uncontrollably.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ laan ma guli Zezi ya na tagɩ kʋlʋ dɩd-o o wɩ: «Kʋ na wʋ́ loori sɩ cɩbwɩʋ keeri tɩn, nmʋ wʋ́ fʋ kuni bɩtɔ nɩ n yǝri amʋ.» O na guli kʋntʋ tɩn, o laan ma nuŋi, yɩ o tiini o keeri zanzan.

eng: When you see the disgusting and destructive thing that Daniel talked about standing in the holy place.
local: Abam nan wʋ́ ba á na wo-lɔŋɔ kʋlʋ na yɩ cɔgɩnʋ tɩn na wʋ́ ba kʋ zʋ kʋ zɩgɩ Wɛ tɩtɩ di-kamunu kʋm wʋnɩ kʋ cɔgɩ-ka. Wɛ nijoŋnu Daniyɛlɩ maŋɩ o ta wo-lɔŋɔ kʋm kʋntʋ taanɩ.

eng: then those in Judea must escape to the mountains.
local: Abam na nɛ wo-lɔŋɔ kʋm kʋntʋ, sɩ abam balʋ na zʋʋrɩ Zude nɩ tɩn kɩ lɩla á nuŋi á duri á vu pweeru yuu á sǝgi da.

eng: Those on the roof shouldnt come down to grab things from their houses.
local: Nɔɔnʋ nan na wʋ nayuu nɩ, o daa yɩ tu o zʋ digǝ sɩ o kwe kʋlʋkʋlʋ.

eng: Those in the field shouldnt come back to grab their clothes.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ nan na wʋ kara nɩ tɩn, o dɩ yɩ joori sɔŋɔ sɩ o kwe o gɔrɔ o zʋ.

eng: How terrible it will be at that time for women who are pregnant and for women who are nursing their children.
local: Kantʋ maŋa kam nɩ kʋʋ́ tiini kʋ taa yɩ lɛɛrʋ dɩ kaana balʋ na jɩgɩ pwi tɩn, dɩ balʋ na jɩgɩ biǝ yɩ ba ŋɔgɩ yɩlɩ tɩn.

eng: Pray that it doesnt happen in winter or on the Sabbath day.
local: Nan taá loori-na Wɛ, sɩ cam durim dɩm kʋntʋ yɩ zaŋɩ dɩ ba taa yɩ waarʋ mɩmaŋa nɩ naa siun dɛ dɩm nɩ.

eng: There will be great suffering such as the world has never before seen and will never again see.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ yaara yalʋ na lagɩ ya ba maŋa kam kʋntʋ nɩ tɩn wʋ́ ta tiini ya camma ya dwe yalʋ na maŋɩ ya kɩ lʋgʋ pulim maŋa nɩ sɩ kʋ ba kʋ yi zɩm tɩn. Yaara yantʋ nan na kɛ, yaara yadonnǝ daa bá ba nɩnɛɛnɩ yantʋ te.

eng: If that time werent shortened, nobody would be rescued. But for the sake of the ones whom God chose, that time will be cut short.
local: Baŋa-Wɛ na wʋ pɛ yaara yam da yam tri, nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ bá wǝri. Kʋ nan yɩ Wɛ tɩtɩ nɔɔna bam DƖ na kuri tɩn ŋwaanɩ mʋ DƖ wʋ́ pa yaara yam da yam tri.

eng: Then if somebody says to you, Look, heres the Christ, or Hes over here, dont believe it.
local: Kantʋ maŋa kam nɩ, nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ maama na tagɩ dɩ abam o wɩ: ‹Nii-na Krisi wʋm Wɛ na tʋŋɩ tɩn wʋ yo seeni› naa: ‹O wʋ jǝgǝ kadoŋ nɩ›, sɩ á yɩ sɛ kʋntʋ tu.

eng: False christs and false prophets will appear, and they will offer great signs and wonders in order to deceive, if possible, even those whom God has chosen.
local: Nɔɔna badaara wʋ́ zaŋɩ ba brɩ nɩ bam dɩdʋa dɩdʋa yɩ Krisi wʋm Wɛ na tʋŋɩ tɩn mʋ, yɩ ba yagɩ ba daɩ. Badonnǝ dɩ wʋ́ brɩ nɩ bantʋ yɩ Wɛ nijoŋnǝ mʋ, yɩ ba yagɩ ba daɩ. Ba ma wʋ́ kɩ wo-kɩnkagɩla yalʋ na wʋ́ sʋ nɔɔna tɩn, sɩ ba ya na waɩ, sɩ ba sʋgɩ Wɛ tɩtɩ nɔɔna balʋ DƖ na kuri tɩn.

eng: Look, Ive told you ahead of time.
local: Nii-na, amʋ nan wuuri a ta a brɩ abam mʋ, yɩ ka maŋa kam ta wʋ yi.

eng: So if they say to you, Look, hes in the desert, dont go out. And if they say, Look, hes in the rooms deep inside the house, dont believe it.
local: Woŋo kʋlʋ na wǝli da tɩn, nɔɔna na tagɩ dɩ abam ba wɩ: ‹Nii-na Krisi tu o wʋ kagʋa wʋnɩ›, sɩ á yɩ zaŋɩ á vu da. Naa ba na tagɩ ba wɩ: ‹Nii-na, sɩ o wʋ sɔŋɔ kʋntʋ nɩ›, sɩ á yɩ sɛ.

eng: Just as the lightning flashes from the east to the west, so it will be with the coming of the Human One.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ, dɛ dɩm amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm na lagɩ a ba lʋgʋ baŋa tɩn na yiǝ, kʋʋ́ ta nyɩ dɩ dʋa na pɩpɩlɩ wɛyuu nɩ ka zɩgɩ bri-kwaga nɩ ka vu ka yi mancoŋo te tɩn mʋ.

eng: The vultures gather wherever theres a dead body.
local: nɩ bɩtar-dɩndɩa na tagɩ ka wɩ: ‹Wo-tʋʋ na wʋ mɛ tɩn, duurǝ dɩ gilimi da mʋ.› ».

eng: Now immediately after the suffering of that time the sun will become dark, and the moon wont give its light. The stars will fall from the sky and the planets and other heavenly bodies will be shaken.
local: «Yaara yam kʋntʋ maŋa kam na tu ka kɛ, kʋ bá daanɩ, yɩ wɩa kam laan ji nazono tim tim. Cana dɩ daa bá fuŋi sɩ pooni taa wʋra. Wɛyuu wʋ́ sisiŋi yɩ calɩcwɩ sɩm wʋ́ kwǝri sɩ tʋ tɩga nɩ, yɩ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ maama na wʋ wɛyuu nɩ tɩn wʋ́ vugimi daanɩ.

eng: Then the sign of the Human One will appear in the sky. At that time all the tribes of the earth will be full of sadness, and they will see the Human One coming in the heavenly clouds with power and great splendor.
local: Nɔɔna laan wʋ́ na wo-kɩnkagɩlɩ wɛyuu nɩ, sɩ kʋ brɩ nɩ Nabiin-bu wʋm maa bɩɩna. Lʋgʋ baŋa dwi tiinǝ maama wʋ́ ta keerǝ, dɩ ba na nɛ Nabiin-bu wʋm na maa bɩɩnɩ tɩn. Oó ta wʋ wɛyuu nɩ kunkwǝǝnu baŋa nɩ o bɩɩna dɩ dam, yɩ Wɛ paarɩ-zulǝ zanzan wʋ o tee nɩ.

eng: He will send his angels with the sound of a great trumpet, and they will gather his chosen ones from the four corners of the earth, from one end of the sky to the other.
local: Nabɔn-zɔŋɔ wʋ́ tiini kʋ taa wu wɛyuu nɩ, yɩ Nabiin-bu wʋm laan wʋ́ pa o malɛsɩ sɩm nuŋi sɩ jagɩ sɩ vu lʋgʋ baŋa je maama, kʋ na yɩ wa-puli dɩ wa-zʋʋrɩ, jazɩm dɩ jagwiǝ, sɩ la o nɔɔna balʋ Wɛ na kuri tɩn je maama sɩ kɩ daanɩ.».

eng: Learn this parable from the fig tree. After its branch becomes tender and it sprouts new leaves, you know that summer is near.
local: «Nan maanɩ-na á nii kapʋrʋ na yɩ te tɩn. Kʋntʋ na yǝni kʋ wʋra kʋ kwi vɔɔrʋ, á yǝni á lwarɩ nɩ kʋ twɛ yadɛ.

eng: In the same way, when you see all these things, you know that the Human One is near, at the door.
local: Kʋ nan yɩ bɩdwɩ mʋ dɩ á na nɛ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ maama a na tagɩ a brɩ abam tɩn na wʋra tɩ kɩa, sɩ á lwarɩ nɩ Nabiin-bu wʋm tum dɩm laan twɛ dɩ ti mʋ.

eng: I assure you that this generation wont pass away until all these things happen.
local: A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, wǝǝnu tɩm kʋntʋ maama wʋ́ ba tɩ wʋra tɩ kɩa, yɩ zɩm nɔɔna bam ta wʋ tɩgɩ ba ti.

eng: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will certainly not pass away.
local: Wɛyuu dɩ tɩga baŋa maama wʋ́ ba tɩ kɛ, yɩ amʋ taanɩ dɩm bá kɛ maŋa dɩ maŋa.».

eng: But nobody knows when that day or hour will come, not the heavenly angels and not the Son. Only the Father knows.
local: «Nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ yǝri dɛ dɩlʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm na lagɩ o joori o ba tɩn, nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ yǝri dɛ dɩm wʋnɩ maŋa kam o na wʋ́ ba tɩn, kʋ na maŋɩ kʋ yɩ Wɛ malɛsɩ sɩm dɩ DƖ Bu wʋm tɩtɩ dɩ. Kʋ nan yɩ a Ko Baŋa-Wɛ yɩranɩ mʋ ye.

eng: As it was in the time of Noah, so it will be at the coming of the Human One.
local: Nabiin-bu wʋm na wʋ́ joori o ba maŋa kalʋ tɩn, kʋʋ́ ta nyɩ nɩnɛɛnɩ kʋ dɛɛn na kɩ Nowe maŋa kam nɩ te tɩn.

eng: In those days before the flood, people were eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage, until the day Noah entered the ark.
local: Kʋ na wʋ́ loori dʋ-fara kam nɩnɩm tɩn, nɔɔna dɛɛn di ba wʋdiiru, yɩ ba daarɩ ba di kaana, yɩ kaana dɩ zʋʋrɩ banna, sɩ kʋ vu kʋ yi dɛ dɩm Nowe na zʋ naboro kamunu kʋm wʋ tɩn.

eng: They didnt know what was happening until the flood came and swept them all away. The coming of the Human One will be like that.
local: Ba dɛɛn wʋ lwarɩ woŋo kʋlʋ na lagɩ kʋ ba kʋ kɩ tɩn, sɩ kʋ vu kʋ yi maŋa kalʋ dʋ-fara kam na bam na tu ba li nɔɔna bam maama tɩn. Nabiin-bu wʋm dɩ lagɩ o ba kʋntʋ doŋ mʋ.

eng: At that time there will be two men in the field. One will be taken and the other left.
local: Kantʋ maŋa kam nɩ, nɔɔna bale na wʋ kara nɩ ba tʋŋa, Wɛ wʋ́ lɩ ba dɩdʋa DƖ daarɩ wʋdoŋ wʋm.

eng: Two women will be grinding at the mill. One will be taken and the other left.
local: Kaana bale dɩ na wʋra ba cʋɩ muni nɔŋɔ nɩ, Wɛ wʋ́ lɩ ba dɩdʋa DƖ daarɩ wʋdoŋ wʋm.

eng: Therefore, stay alert! You dont know what day the Lord is coming.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ, á fɔgɩ á cɩ á tɩtɩ, sɩ á yǝri dɛ dɩm á Yuutu wʋm na wʋ́ joori o ba tɩn.

eng: But you understand that if the head of the house knew at what time the thief would come, he would keep alert and wouldnt allow the thief to break into his house.
local: Lwarɩ-na sɩ, sɔŋɔ tu ya na lwarɩ maŋa kalʋ ŋwɩɩnʋ na lagɩ o ba tɩtɩɩ nɩ o ŋɔ tɩn, o ya wʋ́ yɩrɩ o sɔŋɔ kʋm, sɩ ŋwɩɩnʋ wʋm yɩ na cwǝŋǝ o zʋ o ŋɔ.

eng: Therefore, you also should be prepared, because the Human One will come at a time you dont know.
local: Kʋntʋ tɩn, abam maŋɩ sɩ á cɩ á tɩtɩ mʋ, bɛŋwaanɩ amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm lagɩ a ba maŋa kalʋ abam na ba bʋŋɩ nɩ aá ba tɩn mʋ.».

eng: Who then are the faithful and wise servants whom their master puts in charge of giving food at the right time to those who live in his house?.
local: «Wɔɔ mʋ yɩ tɩntʋŋ-ŋʋm wʋlʋ na jɩgɩ wʋbʋŋa tɩn? O nyɩ nɩnɛɛnɩ tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋlʋ o yuutu na yagɩ o tɩtʋŋa o pa-o yɩ o daarɩ o vu cwǝŋǝ tɩn. O yuutu wʋm ma lɩ-o sɩ o taa nii o donnǝ tɩntʋŋna bam baŋa nɩ, sɩ o daarɩ o taa pa-ba ba ni-wʋdiu maŋa maama.

eng: Happy are those servants whom the master finds fulfilling their responsibilities when he comes.
local: O yuutu wʋm na joori o ba, yɩ o piini dɩ o kɩ kʋlʋ maama o na wɩ o kɩ tɩn, tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋm kʋntʋ laan wʋ́ na yu-yoŋo.

eng: I assure you that he will put them in charge of all his possessions.
local: A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, o yuutu wʋm laan wʋ́ pa o taa nii o jɩjɩgɩrʋ tɩm maama baŋa nɩ.

eng: But suppose those bad servants should say to themselves, My master wont come until later.
local: O nan na yɩ tɩntʋŋ-balɔrɔ yɩ o bʋŋɩ nɩ, o yuutu wʋm daanɩ zanzan yɩ o ba tui.

eng: And suppose they began to beat their fellow servants and to eat and drink with the drunks?.
local: yɩ o na jɩgɩ o donnǝ tɩntʋŋna bam o maga, yɩ o daarɩ o tɔgɩ dɩ sa-nyɔra o di yɩ o nyɔ sana dɩ ba.

eng: The master of those servants will come on a day when they are not expecting him, at a time they couldnt predict.
local: tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋm kʋntʋ yuutu wʋm laan wʋ́ joori o ba dɛ dɩm tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋm na ba bʋŋɩ nɩ oó ba tɩn, dɩ maŋa kalʋ o na wʋ́ ba o zʋ tɩn.

eng: He will cut them in pieces and put them in a place with the hypocrites. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: O yuutu wʋm nan na tu o pɩɩr-o kʋntʋ, o laan wʋ́ pa o na cam, yɩ o daarɩ o pa o tɔgɩ dɩ pipiri-nyɩna o na yaara zanzan, jǝgǝ kalʋ nɩ ba na wʋ́ ta keerǝ yɩ ba dʋnɩ ba nwa tɩn.».

eng: When Jesus finished teaching his twelve disciples, he went on from there to teach and preach in their cities.
local: Zezi dɛɛn na brɩ o karabiǝ fugǝ-bale bam ba na maŋɩ sɩ ba kɩ te o ti tɩn, o laan ma kɛ o vu je sɩm kʋntʋ tɩɩnɩ dɩm wʋnɩ sɩ o taa brɩ nɔɔna, yɩ o tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝrǝ kam dɩ ba.

eng: Now when John heard in prison about the things the Christ was doing, he sent word by his disciples to Jesus, asking.
local: Zan wʋlʋ na miisi nɔɔna na wʋnɩ tɩn dɛɛn maa wʋ pɩɩna digǝ nɩ. O ma ni wǝǝnu tɩlʋ Zezi Krisi na kɩ tɩn. O ma tʋŋɩ o karabiǝ badonnǝ sɩ ba vu o te.

eng: Are you the one who is to come, or should we look for another.
local: sɩ ba bwe-o ba nii: «Nmʋ mʋ yɩ wʋlʋ Wɛ na tagɩ dɩ dɩbam nɩ nń ba tɩn na, naa dɩ́ ta wʋ́ ta cǝgi nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ mʋ?».

eng: Jesus responded, Go, report to John what you hear and see.
local: Ba na bwe Zezi kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma lǝri-ba o wɩ, ba ve ba ta dɩ Zan kʋlʋ maama ba na niǝ yɩ ba kwǝri ba na tɩn.

eng: Those who were blind are able to see . Those who were crippled are walking. People with skin diseases are cleansed. Those who were deaf now hear. Those who were dead are raised up. The poor have good news proclaimed to them .
local: «Á taá brɩ-o nɩ lilwǝ yiǝ puri, kwaarʋ dɩ waɩ tɩ veǝ, nanywaanʋ yɩra dɩ joori ya kwɛ lanyɩranɩ, zwa-kwarʋ dɩ zwa ni, twa dɩ joori ba bi. Kʋlʋ na wǝli da tɩn, yinigǝ tiinǝ dɩ maa ni Wɛ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam.

eng: Happy are those who dont stumble and fall because of me.
local: Wʋlʋ maama na ba joori amʋ kwaga nɩ tɩn mʋ yɩ yu-yoŋo tu.».

eng: When Johns disciples had gone, Jesus spoke to the crowds about John: What did you go out to the wilderness to see? A stalk blowing in the wind?.
local: Zan karabiǝ bam na ma viiri tɩn, Zezi laan ma ta Zan taanɩ o brɩ nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm o wɩ: «Abam dɛɛn na ve Zan te kagʋa wʋnɩ tɩn, á bʋŋɩ nɩ á wʋ́ na bɛɛ mʋ? Á ve sɩ á nii miu kʋlʋ viu na jɩgɩ kʋ zuuli tɩn mʋ na?.

eng: What did you go out to see? A man dressed up in refined clothes? Look, those who wear refined clothes are in royal palaces.
local: Aye, kʋ daɩ kʋntʋ. Sɩ bɛɛ mʋ? Á ve sɩ á nii nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na zʋ gwar-ŋʋnnʋ tɩn mʋ na? Aye, kʋ daɩ kʋntʋ. Nɔɔna balʋ na zʋ gwar-ŋʋnnʋ tɩn zʋʋrɩ sɔ-ŋʋnnʋ wʋnɩ mʋ, sɩ kʋ daɩ kagʋa wʋnɩ.

eng: What did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.
local: Bɛɛ nan mʋ á ve sɩ á nii? Á ve sɩ á na Wɛ nijoŋnu mʋ na? Ɛɛn, kʋ yɩ kʋntʋ mʋ. Á nan na nɛ wʋlʋ tɩn tiini o dwe faŋa faŋa Wɛ nijoŋnǝ bam na yɩ te tɩn.

eng: He is the one of whom it is written: Look, Im sending my messenger before you, who will prepare your way before you .
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm maŋɩ kʋ ta wʋntʋ taanɩ kʋ wɩ: ‹Nii, amʋ Wɛ lagɩ a tʋŋɩ a nijoŋnu mʋ, sɩ o da nmʋ yigǝ o vu o kwɛ cwǝŋǝ kalʋ nmʋ na wʋ́ ba n tɔgɩ da tɩn.›.

eng: I assure you that no one who has ever been born is greater than John the Baptist. Yet whoever is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he.
local: A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, nabiinǝ balʋ maama ba na maŋɩ ba lʋ tɩn wʋnɩ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ tǝrǝ o na dwe Zan. Kʋ daarɩ lele kʋntʋ, nɔn-nabwǝm wʋlʋ maama na zʋ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋnɩ tɩn laan tu o dwe Zan.

eng: From the days of John the Baptist until now the kingdom of heaven is violently attacked as violent people seize it.
local: Kʋ na zɩgɩ maŋa kalʋ Zan na miisi nɔɔna na wʋnɩ sɩ kʋ ba kʋ yi zɩm tɩn, Wɛ paarɩ dɩm laan jagɩ dɩ vu yigǝ dɩ dam, yɩ nɔn-balwaarʋ dɩ kwaanɩ sɩ ba cɩ-dɩ dɩ dam.

eng: All the Prophets and the Law prophesied until John came.
local: Faŋa faŋa Wɛ nijoŋnǝ bam dɩ Moyisi cullu tɩm dɛɛn maŋɩ tɩ brɩ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm cɩga kam, sɩ kʋ ba kʋ yi maŋa kalʋ Zan laan na tu tɩn.

eng: If you are willing to accept it, he is Elijah who is to come.
local: Abam na sɛ á joŋi ba na brɩ cɩga kalʋ tɩn, á wʋ́ lwarɩ nɩ Zan na tu o wʋra tɩn, wʋntʋ mʋ yɩ Eli wʋlʋ ba dɛɛn na tagɩ o taanɩ nɩ oó ba tɩn.

eng: Let the person who has ears, hear.
local: Abam nan fɔgɩ á cǝgi amʋ taanɩ dɩm.

eng: To what will I compare this generation? It is like a child sitting in the marketplaces calling out to others.
local: A ma bɛɛ mʋ a maŋɩ zɩm nɔɔna bam kǝm na yɩ te tɩn? Ba nyɩ dɩ biǝ na je yaga kaporo nɩ ba kwɛɛra te tɩn mʋ. Ba maa tɛ dɩ ba yuu donnǝ ba wɩ:.

eng: We played the flute for you and you didnt dance. We sang a funeral song and you didnt mourn.
local: ‹Dɩ́ magɩ gullu mʋ sɩ á sa, yɩ á wʋ sagɩ. Dɩ́ ma daarɩ dɩ́ leeni luǝ le, yɩ á yigǝ wʋ nywanɩ.›.

eng: For John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say, He has a demon.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ Zan na tu tɩn, o pɩɩnɩ o kana mʋ yɩ o ba nyɔ sana. Nɔɔna na nɛ-o tɩn, ba maa jɩg-o ba yáala ba wɩ, kʋ yɩ cicirǝ mʋ tɔg-o.

eng: Yet the Human One came eating and drinking, and they say, Look, a glutton and a drunk, a friend of tax collectors and sinners. But wisdom is proved to be right by her works.
local: Kʋ daarɩ, amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm nan na tu tɩn, a wʋ vɔgɩ ni. Nɔɔna daa ta maa yáalɩ amʋ dɩ, yɩ ba wɩ, a yɩ nɔn-kolo mʋ, yɩ a yɩ sa-nyɔrʋ dɩ. Ba daa ta maa wɩ, a yɩ lampo-joŋnǝ dɩ nɔn-cɩcɔgɩrʋ ciloŋ mʋ. ‹Wʋbʋŋa tu tɩtʋŋa mʋ brɩ nɩ o jɩgɩ wʋbʋŋa.› ».

eng: Then he began to scold the cities where he had done his greatest miracles because they didnt change their hearts and lives.
local: Zezi na tagɩ kʋntʋ o ti tɩn, o laan maa jɩgɩ balʋ na nɛ o wo-kɩnkagɩla yam yɩ ba ta wʋ sɛ sɩ ba lǝni ba wʋrʋ tɩn taanɩ o ŋɔɔna. O ma kaanɩ balʋ na zʋʋrɩ tɩɩnɩ dɩlʋ o na kɩ wo-kɩnkagɩla zanzan da tɩn o wɩ:.

eng: How terrible it will be for you, Chorazin! How terrible it will be for you, Bethsaida! For if the miracles done among you had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have changed their hearts and lives and put on funeral clothes and ashes a long time ago.
local: «Abam Korazin tiinǝ, á wʋ́ na lɛɛrʋ! Kʋ wǝli dɩ abam Betisayida tiinǝ dɩ wʋ́ na lɛɛrʋ! Bɛŋwaanɩ, wo-kɩnkagɩla yalʋ maama a na kɩ abam tee nɩ tɩn, yadonnǝ ya na kɩ Tiiri dɩ Sidɔn tiinǝ tee nɩ, bantʋ ya wʋ́ kɩ lɩla ba lǝni ba wʋrʋ ba yagɩ kǝm-balwaarʋ, yɩ ba ya wʋ́ zʋ gwar-zɩnzwara ba lo puru ba yuu nɩ, sɩ kʋ brɩ nɩ ba wʋrʋ mʋ tiini tɩ cɔgɩ zanzan.

eng: But I say to you that Tyre and Sidon will be better off on Judgment Day than you.
local: Taá ye-na nɩ, Wɛ sarɩya dɛ dɩm na yiǝ, Tiiri dɩ Sidɔn tiinǝ bá tiini ba na lɛɛrʋ nɩ abam na wʋ́ na lɛɛrʋ te tɩn.

eng: And you, Capernaum, will you be honored by being raised up to heaven? No, you will be thrown down to the place of the dead. After all, if the miracles that were done among you had been done in Sodom, it would still be here today.
local: Abam Kapɛrnawum tiinǝ, á ga bʋŋɩ nɩ Wɛ lagɩ DƖ zǝŋi abam wɛyuu nɩ mʋ na? Aye. Abam nan lagɩ á tʋ tɩga nɩ mʋ, á tu á kwaarɩ curu goŋ-luŋu kʋm kuri. Bɛŋwaanɩ, wo-kɩnkagɩla yalʋ maama a na kɩ abam tee nɩ tɩn, yadonnǝ ya na kɩ Sodɔm tiinǝ tee nɩ, ba ya wʋ́ lǝni ba wʋrʋ yɩ Wɛ ya daa bá cɔgɩ ba tɩʋ kʋm.

eng: But I say to you that it will be better for the land of Sodom on the Judgment Day than it will be for you.
local: Á taá ye nɩ, Wɛ sarɩya dɛ dɩm na yiǝ, Sodɔm tiinǝ bá tiini ba na lɛɛrʋ nɩ abam na wʋ́ na lɛɛrʋ te tɩn.».

eng: At that time Jesus said, I praise you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because youve hidden these things from the wise and intelligent and have shown them to babies.
local: Maŋa kantʋ nɩ, Zezi laan ma kɩ o Ko Baŋa-Wɛ le yɩ o wɩ: «A Ko, nmʋ yɩ wʋlʋ na te wɛyuu dɩ tɩga baŋa maama tɩn. Nmʋ nan mʋ pɛ n cɩga kam sǝgi swan tiinǝ dɩ yiyiu tiinǝ tee nɩ, yɩ n daarɩ n pa balʋ na nyɩ dɩ bu-balɛ te tɩn lwarɩ ka kuri.

eng: Indeed, Father, this brings you happiness.
local: A Ko, a kɩ nmʋ le. Nmʋ na kɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ yɩ n wʋbʋŋa na lagɩ kʋlʋ tɩn ŋwaanɩ mʋ.».

eng: My Father has handed all things over to me. No one knows the Son except the Father. And nobody knows the Father except the Son and anyone to whom the Son wants to reveal him.
local: O laan ma ŋɔɔnɩ dɩ nɔɔna bam o wɩ: «A Ko Wɛ nan kwe woŋo maama o kɩ a jɩŋa nɩ. Nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ bá wanɩ o lwarɩ Wɛ Bu wʋm, kʋ na daɩ o Ko yɩranɩ. Nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ dɩ nan bá wanɩ o lwarɩ a Ko Wɛ, kʋ na daɩ amʋ wʋlʋ na yɩ o Bu tɩn yɩranɩ, kʋ wǝli dɩ amʋ na lagɩ sɩ a pa nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ maama lwar-o tɩn.

eng: Come to me, all you who are struggling hard and carrying heavy loads, and I will give you rest.
local: Á ba-na amʋ te, abam balʋ bam maama á na bwǝni yɩ á zɩŋɩ zɩla yalʋ na dunǝ tɩn, sɩ amʋ wʋ́ pa á na siun.

eng: Put on my yoke, and learn from me. Im gentle and humble. And you will find rest for yourselves.
local: Á sɛ-na sɩ á tʋŋɩ á pa amʋ, sɩ á zaasɩ kʋlʋ a na lagɩ a brɩ abam tɩn. Bɛŋwaanɩ a yɩ wʋ-bono tu mʋ yɩ a yɩ tu-n-tɩtɩ tu. Aá pa á wʋbʋŋa taa tigi jǝgǝ dɩdʋa.

eng: My yoke is easy to bear, and my burden is light.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ amʋ tɩtʋŋa yam ba cana, yɩ a na lagɩ sɩ á kɩ kʋlʋ tɩn yɩ mwalɩ mwalɩ mʋ.».

eng: After a few days, Jesus went back to Capernaum, and people heard that he was at home.
local: Da fɩnfɩɩn na kɛ tɩn, Zezi ma joori o vu Kapɛrnawum. Tɩʋ kʋm nɔɔna bam ma lwarɩ nɩ o tu o wʋ sɔŋɔ nɩ.

eng: So many gathered that there was no longer space, not even near the door. Jesus was speaking the word to them.
local: Ba zanzan ma ba ba la daanɩ sɔŋɔ kʋm nɩ, yɩ laŋa daa tǝrǝ kʋ ni nɩ dɩ fɩnfɩɩn dɩ. O maa wʋra o ŋɔɔnɩ Wɛ taanɩ o brɩ-ba.

eng: Some people arrived, and four of them were bringing to him a man who was paralyzed.
local: O na wʋra o brɩ-ba kʋntʋ tɩn, nɔɔna badonnǝ ya jɩgɩ kɔrɔ ba bɩɩnɩ o te, yɩ nɔɔna bana zɩŋ-o dɩ gungolo.

eng: They couldnt carry him through the crowd, so they tore off part of the roof above where Jesus was. When they had made an opening, they lowered the mat on which the paralyzed man was lying.
local: Ba na yi jǝgǝ kam tɩn, ba maa warɩ ba ja-o ba yi Zezi te, kɔgɔ kʋm na daga tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Ba ma ja-o ba di digǝ kalʋ Zezi na wʋ ka wʋnɩ tɩn nayuu, ba zagɩ ba puri bɔɔnɩ ba maŋɩ dɩ Zezi na wʋ mɛ tɩn. Ba ma daarɩ ba pa gungolo kʋm kɔrɔ kʋm na tigi kʋ baŋa nɩ tɩn tɔgɩ da kʋ tu digǝ kam wʋ.

eng: When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralytic, Child, your sins are forgiven.
local: Zezi ma lwarɩ nɩ ba kɩ ba wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩd-o. O ma ta dɩ kɔrɔ kʋm o wɩ: «A bu, nmʋ lwarɩm dɩm ti.».

eng: Some legal experts were sitting there, muttering among themselves.
local: Wɛ cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ badaara ya tɔgɩ ba wʋra. Ba maa je ba bwɛ ba wʋnɩ ba wɩ:.

eng: Why does he speak this way? Hes insulting God. Only the one God can forgive sins.
local: «Bɛɛ mʋ yɩ o ŋɔɔnɩ kʋntʋ doŋ? O paɩ o dɩ Wɛ yɩ bɩdwɩ mʋ. O ŋɔɔnɩ o twɩ Wɛ mʋ. Nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ bá wanɩ o yagɩ nɔɔnʋ lwarɩm o ma cɛ-o, kʋ na daɩ Wɛ DƖ yɩranɩ.».

eng: Jesus immediately recognized what they were discussing, and he said to them, Why do you fill your minds with these questions?.
local: Zezi ma lwarɩ ba na jɩgɩ wʋbʋŋa yalʋ ba wʋnɩ tɩn. O ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Bɛŋwaanɩ mʋ á bʋŋɩ kʋntʋ doŋ?.

eng: Which is easier—to say to a paralyzed person, Your sins are forgiven, or to say, Get up, take up your bed, and walk?.
local: Kɔɔ mʋ yɩ mwalɩ kʋ dwe, a na tagɩ dɩ kɔrɔ kʋm a wɩ: ‹Nmʋ lwarɩm dɩm ti› mʋ yɩ mwalɩ, naa a na tagɩ a wɩ: ‹Zaŋɩ wɛɛnɩ n kwe n gungolo kʋm n ta n veǝ› mʋ yɩ mwalɩ?.

eng: But so you will know that the Human One has authority on the earth to forgive sins—he said to the man who was paralyzed.
local: A nan wʋ́ pa abam lwarɩ nɩ, amʋ wʋlʋ na yɩ Nabiin-bu wʋm tɩn jɩgɩ dam lʋgʋ baŋa nɩ, sɩ a yagɩ nɔɔna lwarɩm a ma cɛ-ba.» O laan ma ta dɩ kɔrɔ kʋm o wɩ:.

eng: Get up, take your mat, and go home.
local: «A lagɩ a ta dɩ nmʋ, zaŋɩ wɛɛnɩ sɩ n kwe n gungolo kʋm n ja n vu sɔŋɔ.».

eng: Jesus raised him up, and right away he picked up his mat and walked out in front of everybody. They were all amazed and praised God, saying, Weve never seen anything like this.
local: Zezi na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, bɩdwɩ baŋa nɩ mʋ nɔɔnʋ wʋm zaŋɩ ba maama yigǝ nɩ o kwe o gungolo kʋm o nuŋi o viiri. Ba na nɛ kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ ma kɩ ba maama yǝǝu. Ba ma zuli Wɛ yɩ ba wɩ: «Dɩ́ daa ta wʋ fɔgɩ dɩ́ na kǝm dɩntʋ doŋ.».

eng: Jesus went out beside the lake again. The whole crowd came to him, and he began to teach them.
local: Zezi daa ma nuŋi o vu o wʋ Galile nɩnɩʋ kʋm ni nɩ. Kɔgɔ zanzan ma ba o te kʋ gilim-o. O maa wʋra o brɩ-ba Wɛ cɩga kam.

eng: As he continued along, he saw Levi, Alphaeus son, sitting at a kiosk for collecting taxes. Jesus said to him, Follow me. Levi got up and followed him.
local: O daa ma zaŋɩ o maa kɛa. O na maa kɛ tɩn, o ma na nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ, o yɩrɩ mʋ Leevi. O ko yɩrɩ mʋ Alɩfɩ. O dɛɛn je mɛ seeni o na yǝni o je o joŋi lampoo tɩn mʋ. Zezi ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Zaŋɩ n ta n tɔgɩ-nɩ.» O ma sɩɩnɩ o zaŋɩ o tɔgɩ o kwaga.

eng: Jesus sat down to eat at Levis house. Many tax collectors and sinners were eating with Jesus and his disciples. Indeed, many of them had become his followers.
local: Zezi dɛɛn ma vu Leevi sɔŋɔ sɩ o di wʋdiu dɩ o karabiǝ bam. Lampo-joŋnǝ dɩ nɔn-cɩcɔgɩrʋ zanzan dɩ maa tɔgɩ ba wʋra ba di wʋdiu kʋm dɩ ba, bɛŋwaanɩ balʋ dɛɛn na tɔg-o tɩn daga.

eng: When some of the legal experts from among the Pharisees saw that he was eating with sinners and tax collectors, they asked his disciples, Why is he eating with sinners and tax collectors.
local: Wɛ cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ badaara na nuŋi Farizɩan tiinǝ kɔgɔ kʋm wʋ tɩn maa tɔgɩ ba wʋra. Ba na nɛ Zezi na je o di wʋdiu dɩ nɔn-cɩcɔgɩrʋ dɩ lampo-joŋnǝ kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma bwe o karabiǝ bam ba wɩ: «Bɛɛ mʋ kɩ yɩ o di wʋdiu dɩdaanɩ lampo-joŋnǝ dɩ nɔn-cɩcɔgɩrʋ tɩm kʋntʋ?».

eng: When Jesus heard it, he said to them, Healthy people dont need a doctor, but sick people do. I didnt come to call righteous people, but sinners.
local: Zezi ma ni ba na tagɩ kʋlʋ tɩn. O laan ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Kʋ na yɩ balʋ na jɩgɩ yazurǝ tɩn, bantʋ wʋ maŋɩ sɩ ba vu dɔgɩta tu te sɩ o sooni-ba. Kʋ daarɩ balʋ na ba jɩgɩ yazurǝ tɩn mʋ maŋɩ sɩ ba vu sɩ ba na soonim. A na tu lʋgʋ baŋa tɩn, kʋ daɩ sɩ a bǝŋi balʋ na maŋɩ ba yɩ nɔn-ŋʋna tɩn, amʋ nan tuǝ, sɩ a bǝŋi nɔn-balwaarʋ mʋ sɩ ba ba amʋ te.».

eng: Johns disciples and the Pharisees had a habit of fasting. Some people asked Jesus, Why do Johns disciples and the Pharisees disciples fast, but yours dont.
local: Zan karabiǝ bam dɩ Farizɩan tiinǝ bam dɛɛn yǝni ba vɔ ni. Nɔɔna badonnǝ ma vu Zezi te ba bwe-o ba wɩ: «Bɛŋwaanɩ mʋ Zan karabiǝ bam dɩ Farizɩan tiinǝ bam yǝni ba vɔ ni, yɩ nmʋ karabiǝ bam ba vɔa?».

eng: Jesus said, The wedding guests cant fast while the groom is with them, can they? As long as they have the groom with them, they cant fast.
local: Zezi ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ na di kaanɩ yɩ o kɩ candiǝ, yɩ o cilonnǝ na wʋra sɩ ba tɔgɩ ba di, bantʋ wʋ́ wanɩ ba vɔ ni yɩ kan-barʋ wʋm ta wʋ ba tee nɩ na? Ba bá sɛ ba vɔ ni maŋa kalʋ kan-barʋ wʋm ta na wʋ ba tee nɩ tɩn.

eng: But the days will come when the groom will be taken away from them, and then they will fast.
local: Kʋ nan wʋ́ ba kʋ yi maŋa kam ba na wʋ́ ja kan-barʋ wʋm ba viiri tɩn. Maŋa kam kʋntʋ nɩ o daa na tǝri ba tee nɩ tɩn, mʋ ba laan wʋ́ vɔ ni.

eng: No one sews a piece of new, unshrunk cloth on old clothes; otherwise, the patch tears away from it, the new from the old, and makes a worse tear.
local: Nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ bá sɛ o kwe garyi-dʋʋrɩ o ma fǝri gwar-doŋo kʋlʋ na kaarɩ tɩn. O nan na kɩ kʋntʋ, garyi-dʋʋrɩ dɩm laan wʋ́ vuuri gwar-doŋo kʋm dɩ pa kʋ tiini kʋ cɔgɩ zanzan.

eng: No one pours new wine into old leather wineskins; otherwise, the wine would burst the wineskins and the wine would be lost and the wineskins destroyed. But new wine is for new wineskins.
local: Nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ dɩ nan bá sɛ o kwe sa-dʋŋa o lo o kɩ lɔ-doŋo wʋnɩ. O na kɩ kʋntʋ, yɩ sana kam na keeri, kaá pa lɔɔ kʋm bagɩ mʋ. Lɔɔ kʋm wʋ́ cɔgɩ yɩ sana kam dɩ lo. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ nɔɔnʋ maŋɩ sɩ o kwe sa-dʋŋa o kɩ lɔ-dʋŋʋ wʋnɩ mʋ.».

eng: Jesus went through the wheat fields on the Sabbath. As the disciples made their way, they were picking the heads of wheat.
local: Zwifǝ bam siun dɛ dɩdoŋ na yi tɩn, Zezi dɛɛn ma tɔgɩ karɩ wʋ o maa kɛa. O karabiǝ bam dɩ maa tɔgɩ ba veǝ, yɩ ba bwǝri mɩna ba yu ba dʋna.

eng: The Pharisees said to Jesus, Look! Why are they breaking the Sabbath law.
local: Farizɩan tiinǝ bam na nɛ ba na kɩ te tɩn, ba ma ta dɩ Zezi ba wɩ: «Nii ba na kɩ te tɩn. Bɛɛ mʋ yɩ ba tʋŋɩ tɩtʋŋɩ dɩlʋ na cɔgɩ siun dɛ cullu tɩm tɩn?».

eng: He said to them, Havent you ever read what David did when he was in need, when he and those with him were hungry?.
local: Zezi ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Abam ta wʋ fɔgɩ á karɩmɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm na brɩ Pɛ Davidi taanɩ te tɩn na? Kʋ brɩ nɩ maŋa kalʋ Abɩata dɛɛn na yɩ Wɛ kaanɩm yuutu tɩn, kana dɛɛn jɩgɩ Davidi dɩ o nɔɔna bam, yɩ ba ba jɩgɩ wʋdiu sɩ ba di.

eng: During the time when Abiathar was high priest, David went into Gods house and ate the bread of the presence, which only the priests were allowed to eat. He also gave bread to those who were with him.
local: O ma vu o zʋ Wɛ digǝ, yɩ o kwe dɩpwa yalʋ na tigi da Wɛ ŋwaanɩ tɩn o di. Dɩpɛ dɩm kʋntʋ culǝ sɩ nɔn-gaa di-dɩ, kʋ na daɩ Wɛ kaanɩm tiinǝ yɩranɩ. Davidi dɛɛn di dɩpɛ dɩm, yɩ o daarɩ o pa o nɔɔna bam dɩ tɔgɩ ba di.».

eng: Then he said, The Sabbath was created for humans; humans werent created for the Sabbath.
local: O daa ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Wɛ na kɩ nabiinǝ DƖ yagɩ lʋgʋ baŋa nɩ tɩn, DƖ tiŋi siun dɛ da yarpɛ maama wʋnɩ, sɩ kʋ taa jɩgɩ zǝnǝ mʋ kʋ pa-ba, sɩ kʋ daɩ nɩ Wɛ kɩ nabiinǝ siun dɛ cullu tɩm tɔgɩm ŋwaanɩ mʋ.

eng: This is why the Human One is Lord even over the Sabbath.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm jɩgɩ ni siun dɛ cullu tɩm baŋa nɩ.».

eng: Jesus returned to the synagogue. A man with a withered hand was there.
local: Zezi daa ma joori o vu o zʋ ba Wɛ-digǝ kam. Nɔɔnʋ maa wʋra o jɩŋa dɩdʋa na tɩga.

eng: Wanting to bring charges against Jesus, they were watching Jesus closely to see if he would heal on the Sabbath.
local: Farizɩan tiinǝ badonnǝ maa lagɩ cwǝŋǝ sɩ ba brɩ nɩ Zezi kɩ o cɔgɩ. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ ba ma je ba yɩrɩ Zezi sɩ ba nii, oó sɛ o pa nɔɔnʋ wʋm na yazurǝ siun dɛ dɩm nɩ naa o bá sɛ.

eng: He said to the man with the withered hand, Step up where people can see you.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋm o wɩ: «Zaŋɩ n zɩgɩ nɔɔna bam maama tɩtarɩ nɩ.».

eng: Then he said to them, Is it legal on the Sabbath to do good or to do evil, to save life or to kill? But they said nothing.
local: O laan ma bwe ba maama o wɩ: «Culu brɩ bɛɛ mʋ? Kɔɔ mʋ maŋɩ dɩ culu kʋm, nɔɔnʋ na kɩ o doŋ lanyɩranɩ siun dɛ nɩ mʋ maŋɩ naa o na kɩ-o lwarɩm mʋ gara? Nɔɔnʋ na vrɩ o doŋ ŋwɩa siun dɛ nɩ mʋ maŋɩ naa o na gʋ-o mʋ gara?» O na bwe-ba kʋntʋ tɩn, ba maa cɩm.

eng: Looking around at them with anger, deeply grieved at their unyielding hearts, he said to the man, Stretch out your hand. So he did, and his hand was made healthy.
local: O ma nii ba maama yɩ o banɩ zaŋɩ. O wʋ ma cɔgɩ zanzan ba wʋbʋŋa na tɩgɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ. O laan ma ta dɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋm o wɩ: «Twɩ n jɩŋa kam.» O ma twɩ-ka, yɩ ka joori ka ji lanyɩranɩ.

eng: At that, the Pharisees got together with the supporters of Herod to plan how to destroy Jesus.
local: Farizɩan tiinǝ bam na nɛ o na kɩ te tɩn, ba ma nuŋi ba vu ba banɩ dɩ Pɛ Erɔdɩ kwaga nɔɔna bam, sɩ ba lagɩ cwǝŋǝ ba gʋ Zezi.

eng: Jesus left with his disciples and went to the lake. A large crowd followed him because they had heard what he was doing. They were from Galilee.
local: Zezi dɩ o karabiǝ bam ma zaŋɩ ba kɛ. Ba ma vu ba yi nɩnɩʋ kʋm ni. Kɔgɔ zanzan ma nuŋi Galile je sɩm nɩ ba tɔgɩ o kwaga.

eng: Judea, Jerusalem, Idumea, beyond the Jordan, and the area surrounding Tyre and Sidon.
local: Nɔɔna badonnǝ dɩ ma nuŋi Zeruzalɛm dɩ Zude maama, dɩ Yidume je sɩm nɩ. Badaara dɩ ma nuŋi je sɩlʋ na wʋ Zʋrdɛn bugǝ kam bubɛ dɩdoŋ dɩm nɩ tɩn, dɩ Tiiri dɩ Sidɔn tɩ-niǝ yam maama wʋnɩ. Kɔ-fɔrɔ zanzan ma vu Zezi te, ba na lwarɩ o na kɩ kʋlʋ maama tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: Jesus told his disciples to get a small boat ready for him so the crowd wouldnt crush him.
local: Ba kɔgɔ kʋm na daga yɩ ba tiini ba pɩɩn-o zanzan tɩn, o ma ta dɩ o karabiǝ bam sɩ ba ja naboro ba ba ba zɩgɩ da, sɩ o wanɩ o zʋ kʋ wʋ.

eng: He had healed so many people that everyone who was sick pushed forward so that they could touch him.
local: O na maŋɩ o pa nɔɔna zanzan na yazurǝ tɩn, kʋ ma pa yawɩɩna bam maama yigi ba pɩɩn-o, yɩ ba kwaanɩ sɩ ba dwe o yɩra.

eng: Whenever the evil spirits saw him, they fell down at his feet and shouted, You are Gods Son.
local: Maŋa kalʋ maama ciciri sɩm na yǝni sɩ na-o, sɩ wʋ́ tʋ o yigǝ nɩ mʋ, yɩ sɩ kaasɩ sɩ wɩ: «Nmʋ mʋ yɩ Wɛ Bu wʋm.».

eng: But he strictly ordered them not to reveal who he was.
local: Ciciri sɩm na yǝni sɩ tɛ kʋntʋ doŋ, o yǝni o kaanɩ-sɩ mʋ, sɩ sɩ yɩ pa nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ lwarɩ o na yɩ wʋlʋ tɩn.

eng: Jesus went up on a mountain and called those he wanted, and they came to him.
local: Zezi laan ma zaŋɩ o vu o di piu kʋdoŋ yuu. O ma bǝŋi balʋ o na lagɩ tɩn sɩ ba ba o te.

eng: He appointed twelve and called them apostles. He appointed them to be with him, to be sent out to preach.
local: Ba na tu tɩn, o ma kuri nɔɔna fugǝ-bale sɩ ba taa wʋ o tee nɩ, sɩ o taa tʋŋɩ-ba sɩ ba taa ve ba tɔɔla Wɛ kwǝrǝ kam dɩ nɔɔna.

eng: and to have authority to throw out demons.
local: O ma daarɩ o pa-ba dam sɩ ba wanɩ ciciri ba zǝli nɔɔna yɩra nɩ.

eng: He appointed twelve: Peter, a name he gave Simon.
local: Nɔɔna fugǝ-bale bam o na kuri tɩn yɩra mʋ tɩntʋ: Simɔn wʋlʋ Zezi na pɛ o yɩrɩ nɩ Pɩyɛɛrɩ tɩn.

eng: James and John, Zebedees sons, whom he nicknamed Boanerges, which means sons of Thunder.
local: dɩ Zakɩ wʋlʋ na yɩ Zebede bu tɩn, dɩ o nyaanɩ Zan. Zezi ma pa bantʋ yɩrɩ nɩ Bʋanɛrgɛsɩ, kʋ kuri mʋ Dʋ-baka biǝ.

eng: and Andrew; Philip; Bartholomew; Matthew; Thomas; James, Alphaeus son; Thaddaeus; Simon the Cananaean.
local: Dɩ Andre, dɩ Filipi, dɩ Batelemi, dɩ Matiyu, dɩ Toma, dɩ Zakɩ wʋlʋ na yɩ Alɩfɩ bu tɩn, dɩ Tadɩ, dɩ Simɔn wʋlʋ na yɩ Zelɔtɩ nɔɔna bam wʋ nɔɔnʋ tɩn.

eng: and Judas Iscariot, who betrayed Jesus.
local: dɩdaanɩ Zudasɩ Yiskarɩyo wʋlʋ na wʋ́ ba o yǝgi Zezi o pa nɔɔna tɩn.

eng: Jesus entered a house. A crowd gathered again so that it was impossible for him and his followers even to eat.
local: Zezi ma joori o vu sɔŋɔ. Kɔgɔ zanzan daa ta ma ba kʋ gilimi sɔŋɔ kʋm. Kʋ ma pa Zezi dɩ o karabiǝ bam daa warɩ ba na laŋa sɩ ba di wʋdiu dɩ.

eng: When his family heard what was happening, they came to take control of him. They were saying, Hes out of his mind.
local: O sɔŋɔ tiinǝ na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma zaŋɩ ba nuŋi sɩ ba vu ba ja-o ba viiri, bɛŋwaanɩ nɔɔna tɛ ba wɩ, o co mʋ.

eng: The legal experts came down from Jerusalem. Over and over they charged, Hes possessed by Beelzebul. He throws out demons with the authority of the ruler of demons.
local: Wɛ cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ ya nuŋi Zeruzalɛm ba ba sɩ ba nii-o. Ba maa tɛ ba wɩ: «Sʋtaana-pɛ Belizebuli mʋ jɩg-o. Kʋ yɩ wʋntʋ dam mʋ o ma o zǝli sʋtaana.».

eng: When Jesus called them together he spoke to them in a parable: How can Satan throw Satan out?.
local: Zezi laan ma bǝŋi-ba sɩ ba ba o te, yɩ o daarɩ o maŋɩ mɩmaŋa o brɩ-ba o wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ wʋ́ wanɩ o ma sʋtaana-pɛ dam o ma zǝli sʋtaana na?.

eng: A kingdom involved in civil war will collapse.
local: Tɩʋ kʋlʋ paarɩ na pɔɔrɩ kuni bɩle yɩ ba jɩgɩ daanɩ tɩn, kʋʋ́ tʋ mʋ.

eng: And a house torn apart by divisions will collapse.
local: Sɔŋɔ kʋlʋ maama na pɔɔrɩ kʋ tɩtɩ wʋnɩ tɩn, kʋ dɩ wʋ́ tʋ mʋ.

eng: If Satan rebels against himself and is divided, then he cant endure. Hes done for.
local: Sʋtaana-pɛ na joori o jɩgɩ dɩ o tɩtɩ, kʋ brɩ nɩ o paarɩ dɩm pɔɔrɩ bɩle mʋ yɩ o dam ti. Oó tʋ mʋ.

eng: No one gets into the house of a strong person and steals anything without first tying up the strong person. Only then can the house be burglarized.
local: Kʋ nan na yɩ te tɩn, sʋtaana-pɛ nyɩ dɩ nɔn-dɩdɛɛrʋ na yɩrɩ o sɔŋɔ te tɩn mʋ. Nɔɔnʋ na lagɩ sɩ o zʋ dɩdɛɛrʋ wʋm sɔŋɔ o ŋɔ, o maŋɩ sɩ o da yigǝ o ja dɩdɛɛrʋ wʋm o vɔ mʋ. O na dɛ yigǝ o vɔ-o, o laan wʋ́ wanɩ o zʋ o kwalɩmɩ o jɩjɩgɩrʋ tɩm maama o ja viiri.

eng: I assure you that human beings will be forgiven for everything, for all sins and insults of every kind.
local: A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, kǝm-balwaarʋ tɩlʋ maama nɔɔna na kɩ tɩn, naa ta-balwaarʋ tɩlʋ ba na ŋɔɔnɩ tɩn, Wɛ waɩ DƖ yagɩ-tɩ DƖ ma cɛ-ba.

eng: But whoever insults the Holy Spirit will never be forgiven. That person is guilty of a sin with consequences that last forever.
local: Kʋ daarɩ, wʋlʋ maama na ŋɔɔnɩ o twɩ Wɛ Joro kʋm tɩn, Wɛ bá sɛ sɩ DƖ ma cɛ kʋntʋ tu maŋa dɩ maŋa. Lwarɩm dɩm kʋntʋ wʋ́ ta tɔgɩ kʋntʋ tu sɩ kʋ taa ve maŋa kalʋ na ba ti tɩn.».

eng: He said this because the legal experts were saying, Hes possessed by an evil spirit.
local: Ba na tagɩ ba wɩ, cicirǝ mʋ jɩg-o tɩn, mʋ o tagɩ kʋntʋ dɩ ba.

eng: His mother and brothers arrived. They stood outside and sent word to him, calling for him.
local: Zezi nu dɩ o nyaana ma ba o na wʋ mɛ tɩn, ba zɩgɩ sɔŋɔ kʋm mancoŋo nɩ, yɩ ba pa ni sɩ ba bǝŋ-o.

eng: A crowd was seated around him, and those sent to him said, Look, your mother, brothers, and sisters are outside looking for you.
local: Kɔgɔ zanzan maa je ba gilim-o. Ba ma ta dɩd-o ba wɩ: «N nu dɩ n nyaana tu ba wʋ mancoŋo nɩ, yɩ ba lagɩ-m.».

eng: He replied, Who is my mother? Who are my brothers.
local: O ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Wɔɔ mʋ yɩ a nu, bran yɩ a nyaana?».

eng: Looking around at those seated around him in a circle, he said, Look, here are my mother and my brothers.
local: O laan ma nii balʋ na je ba gilim-o tɩn seeni yɩ o wɩ: «Amʋ nu dɩ a nyaana mʋ tɩntʋ.

eng: Whoever does Gods will is my brother, sister, and mother.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ wʋlʋ maama na kɩ Wɛ wʋbʋŋa na lagɩ te tɩn mʋ yɩ a nyaanɩ dɩ a nakɔ dɩ a nu.».

eng: Jesus began to teach beside the lake again. Such a large crowd gathered that he climbed into a boat there on the lake. He sat in the boat while the whole crowd was nearby on the shore.
local: Zezi dɛɛn maa wʋ nɩnɩʋ kʋm ni nɩ o brɩ nɔɔna bam. Nɔɔna zanzan ma ba ba gilim-o. Ba kɔgɔ kʋm na daga tɩn, o ma zaŋɩ o di naboro kʋ na wʋ na bam wʋnɩ tɩn, yɩ o jǝni sɩ o brɩ-ba. Nɔɔna bam maama ma maŋɩ na bam ni nɩ, sɩ ba taa cǝgi o taanɩ dɩm.

eng: He said many things to them in parables. While teaching them, he said.
local: O maa maɩ mɩmaŋa o brɩ-ba wǝǝnu zanzan, yɩ o zaasɩ-ba o wɩ:.

eng: Listen to this! A farmer went out to scatter seed.
local: «Cǝgi-na. Valʋ dɛɛn mʋ wʋra. O ma zaŋɩ o vu o kara sɩ o du.

eng: As he was scattering seed, some fell on the path; and the birds came and ate it.
local: O ma karɩ o dʋlɩ o wo-duuru tɩm. Tɩdonnǝ ma tʋ cwǝŋǝ ni nɩ. Zunǝ ma ba ba twɛ-tɩ ba di.

eng: Other seed fell on rocky ground where the soil was shallow. They sprouted immediately because the soil wasnt deep.
local: Tɩdonnǝ ma tʋ kandwa jǝgǝ nɩ valɩm na ba daga mɛ tɩn. Tɩ ma wʋ daanɩ tɩ puli, valɩm dɩm na ba lirǝ tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: When the sun came up, it scorched the plants; and they dried up because they had no roots.
local: Wɩa ma ba ka baanɩ tɩ maama. Tɩ bʋnnʋ na wʋ zʋ tɩga tɩn, tɩ ma tɩ.

eng: Other seed fell among thorny plants. The thorny plants grew and choked the seeds, and they produced nothing.
local: Kʋ daarɩ wo-duuru tɩdonnǝ ma tʋ sabarɩ wʋnɩ. Tɩ na puli tɩn, sabarɩ sɩm laan ma zaŋɩ sɩ li-tɩ sɩ gʋ, yɩ tɩ daa wʋ kɩ biǝ.

eng: Other seed fell into good soil and bore fruit. Upon growing and increasing, the seed produced in one case a yield of thirty to one, in another case a yield of sixty to one, and in another case a yield of one hundred to one.
local: Kʋ daarɩ tɩdonnǝ ma tʋ tɩ-ywǝŋǝ jǝgǝ nɩ, tɩ ma puli tɩ nuŋi tɩ kɩ biǝ lanyɩranɩ. Tɩdonnǝ kɩ nɩnɛɛnɩ biǝ fiintɔ fiintɔ. Tɩdaara kɩ nɩnɛɛnɩ biǝ fusɩrdʋ fusɩrdʋ. Tɩtɩm dɩ maa kɩ nɩnɛɛnɩ biǝ bi bi.».

eng: He said, Whoever has ears to listen should pay attention.
local: Zezi laan ma ta o wɩ: «Á nan fɔgɩ á cǝgi a taanɩ dɩm lanyɩranɩ.».

eng: When they were alone, the people around Jesus, along with the Twelve, asked him about the parables.
local: Kɔgɔ kʋm na viiri kʋ daarɩ Zezi o yɩranɩ tɩn, nɔɔna balʋ ya na wʋ o tee nɩ tɩn badaara ma tɔgɩ dɩ o karabiǝ fugǝ-bale bam, ba vu ba bwe-o mɩmaŋa yam kuri.

eng: He said to them, The secret of Gods kingdom has been given to you, but to those who are outside everything comes in parables.
local: O ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Kʋ na yɩ abam, Wɛ maŋɩ DƖ pa á lwarɩ DƖ paarɩ dɩm kuri na yɩ te tɩn, yɩ cɩga kam kʋntʋ ya maŋɩ ka sǝgi mʋ. Kʋ daarɩ, balʋ na daɩ amʋ kɔgɔ kʋm wʋ nɔɔna tɩn, a maɩ mɩmaŋa yɩranɩ mʋ a brɩ-ba Wɛ cɩga kam.

eng: This is so that they can look and see but have no insight, and they can hear but not understand. Otherwise, they might turn their lives around and be forgiven.
local: sɩ ‹ba na niǝ, sɩ ba yɩ taa naɩ lanyɩranɩ, ba na cǝgǝ, sɩ ba yɩ taa ni ka kuri. Kʋ na daɩ kʋntʋ, ba ya wʋ́ pipiri ba sɛ Wɛ, yɩ DƖ wʋ́ yagɩ ba lwarɩm DƖ ma cɛ-ba.› ».

eng: Dont you understand this parable? Then how will you understand all the parables?.
local: Zezi laan ma bwe-ba o wɩ: «Á wʋ ni mɩmaŋɩ dɩntʋ kuri na? Kʋntʋ tɩn, á nan wʋ́ kɩ ta mʋ á ni mɩmaŋa yadonnǝ yam kuri?.

eng: The farmer scatters the word.
local: Mɩmaŋɩ dɩm kuri mʋ tɩntʋ: Wo-duuru tɩlʋ valʋ wʋm na jɩgɩ o du tɩn mʋ brɩ Wɛ taanɩ dɩm na yɩ te tɩn.

eng: This is the meaning of the seed that fell on the path: When the word is scattered and people hear it, right away Satan comes and steals the word that was planted in them.
local: Nɔɔna badonnǝ nyɩ dɩ cwǝŋǝ kam ni wo-duuru tɩm na tʋ mɛ tɩn mʋ. Bantʋ na ni Wɛ taanɩ dɩm tɩn, sʋtaanɩ ma da dɩ ba dɩ lɩ-dɩ, yɩ dɩ wʋ zʋ ba wʋbʋŋa.

eng: Heres the meaning of the seed that fell on rocky ground: When people hear the word, they immediately receive it joyfully.
local: Nɔɔna badonnǝ nyɩ dɩ kandwa jǝgǝ kalʋ wo-duuru tɩm na tʋ da tɩn mʋ. Bantʋ na ni Wɛ taanɩ dɩm tɩn, ba joŋi-dɩ lɩla dɩ wʋpolo.

eng: Because they have no roots, they last for only a little while. When they experience distress or abuse because of the word, they immediately fall away.
local: Taanɩ dɩm ma wʋ tiini dɩ zʋ ba wʋbʋŋa. Yaara na yi-ba, yɩ ba na bɛɛsɩ-ba Wɛ taanɩ dɩm ŋwaanɩ, baá da ba kwe-dɩ ba yagɩ mʋ lɩla.

eng: Others are like the seed scattered among the thorny plants. These are the ones who have heard the word.
local: Nɔɔna badaara nyɩ dɩ sabarɩ sɩm jǝgǝ kam wo-duuru tɩm na tʋ da tɩn mʋ. Bantʋ mʋ yɩ balʋ na ni Wɛ taanɩ dɩm.

eng: but the worries of this life, the false appeal of wealth, and the desire for more things break in and choke the word, and it bears no fruit.
local: yɩ ba ta jɩgɩ liǝ dɩ lʋgʋ baŋa wǝǝnu tɩm. Sǝbu-lagɩ ma cɔgɩ ba wʋbʋŋa yɩ wo-yɔɔrʋ fra jɩgɩ-ba, kʋ pa ba kwe Wɛ taanɩ dɩm ba yagɩ, yɩ dɩ wʋ jaanɩ kuri dɩ pa-ba.

eng: The seed scattered on good soil are those who hear the word and embrace it. They bear fruit, in one case a yield of thirty to one, in another case sixty to one, and in another case one hundred to one.
local: Kʋ daarɩ nɔɔna badaara nyɩ dɩ tɩ-ywǝŋǝ jǝgǝ kam wo-duuru tɩm na tʋ da tɩn mʋ. Bantʋ mʋ yɩ balʋ na ni Wɛ taanɩ dɩm yɩ ba sɛ-dɩ lanyɩranɩ tɩn. Kʋntʋ ma pa Wɛ taanɩ dɩm ja kuri dɩ pa-ba, nɩnɛɛnɩ wo-duuru na kɩ biǝ lanyɩranɩ, sɩ kʋ maŋɩ dɩ biǝ fiintɔ fiintɔ, yɩ tɩdaara dɩ kɩ biǝ fusɩrdʋ fusɩrdʋ, yɩ tɩdonnǝ dɩ kɩ biǝ bi bi tɩn.».

eng: Jesus said to them, Does anyone bring in a lamp in order to put it under a basket or a bed? Shouldnt it be placed on a lampstand?.
local: Zezi daa ma maŋɩ o brɩ-ba o wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ na tarɩgɩ kanɩa, oó sɛ o kwe-ka o pu tɩtɔgɔ kuri nɩ, naa o zɩgɩ-ka gadogo kuri nɩ na? Aye. Oó kwe-ka o palɩ wɛɛnɩ mʋ.

eng: Everything hidden will be revealed, and everything secret will come out into the open.
local: Kʋlʋ maama na sǝgi zɩm nɔɔna tee nɩ tɩn wʋ́ ba kʋ kwɩn. Kʋlʋ maama na pugi zɩm tɩn wʋ́ ba kʋ puri sɩ nɔɔna maama lwarɩ-kʋ.

eng: Whoever has ears to listen should pay attention.
local: Á nan fɔgɩ á cǝgi a taanɩ dɩm lanyɩranɩ.».

eng: He said to them, Listen carefully! God will evaluate you with the same standard you use to evaluate others. Indeed, you will receive even more.
local: O daa ma ta o wɩ: «Kwaanɩ-na á pa á na ni kʋlʋ tɩn zʋ á wʋbʋŋa. Abam na maɩ bwaŋa kalʋ á maŋɩ á pa á donnǝ tɩn, Wɛ dɩ wʋ́ ma bwaŋa kantʋ mʋ DƖ maŋɩ DƖ pɛɛlɩ DƖ pa abam.

eng: Those who have will receive more, but as for those who dont have, even what they dont have will be taken away from them.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ, nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na maŋɩ o jɩgɩ woŋo tɩn, o ta wʋ́ joŋi kʋdoŋ o wǝli da, yɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ nan na maŋɩ o ba jɩgɩ kʋlʋkʋlʋ tɩn wʋ́ ba o ga kʋlʋ fɩnfɩɩn o ya na jɩgɩ tɩn dɩ.».

eng: Then Jesus said, This is what Gods kingdom is like. Its as though someone scatters seed on the ground.
local: Zezi daa ma ta o wɩ: «Wɛ paarɩ dɩm nyɩ dɩ nɔɔnʋ na kwe wo-duuru o du o kara wʋnɩ te tɩn mʋ.

eng: then sleeps and wakes night and day. The seed sprouts and grows, but the farmer doesnt know how.
local: Tɩtɩɩ nɩ o maa tigi o dɔa. Wɩa nɩ o maa yǝni o zaŋɩ. Wɩa dɩ tɩtɩɩ maama wo-vaalʋ tɩm maa pulǝ yɩ tɩ bɩra, yɩ valʋ wʋm yǝri tɩ na kɩ te tɩ puli.

eng: The earth produces crops all by itself, first the stalk, then the head, then the full head of grain.
local: Tɩga kam nɩ mʋ wǝǝnu tɩm tɩtɩ yǝni tɩ puli tɩ nuŋi tɩ da yigǝ tɩ kɩ vɔɔrʋ. Tɩ laan ja pugǝ yɩ tɩ daarɩ tɩ kɩ biǝ.

eng: Whenever the crop is ready, the farmer goes out to cut the grain because its harvesttime.
local: Wo-vaalʋ tɩm na bɩgɩ tɩ ti, valʋ wʋm laan wʋ́ kwe o sʋgʋ o vu o zagɩ-tɩ, faa maŋa kam na yi tɩn ŋwaanɩ.».

eng: He continued, Whats a good image for Gods kingdom? What parable can I use to explain it?.
local: Zezi daa ta ma ta o wɩ: «Amʋ wʋ́ ma bɛɛ mʋ a maa maŋɩ a brɩ abam Wɛ paarɩ dɩm na nyɩ te tɩn? Aá ma mɩmaŋa mʋ a brɩ abam sɩ á lwarɩ lanyɩranɩ.

eng: Consider a mustard seed. When scattered on the ground, its the smallest of all the seeds on the earth.
local: Wɛ paarɩ dɩm nyɩ dɩ nɔɔnʋ na kwe wo-duu na yɩ mɩmɩɩnʋ o du tɩga nɩ, yɩ wo-duu kʋm kʋntʋ mʋ tiini kʋ yɩ mɩmɩɩnʋ wo-dwǝ maama wʋnɩ.

eng: but when its planted, it grows and becomes the largest of all vegetable plants. It produces such large branches that the birds in the sky are able to nest in its shade.
local: O na dugi-kʋ tɩn, kʋ laan ma nuŋi kʋ ji kamunu kʋ dwǝni dwǝ balʋ maama na wʋ o kara kam nɩ tɩn. Kʋ ma ta nɛ kamunǝ kamunǝ, yɩ zunǝ tui ba sɔ ba pwǝru kʋ yuu nɩ, kʋ na jɩgɩ woro tɩn ŋwaanɩ.».

eng: With many such parables he continued to give them the word, as much as they were able to hear.
local: Zezi maa maɩ mɩmaŋa yantʋ dwi zanzan mʋ o maŋɩ Wɛ taanɩ dɩm o brɩ nɔɔna bam. O maa yǝni o brɩ-ba nɩnɛɛnɩ ba na wʋ́ wanɩ ba ni dɩ kuri te tɩn.

eng: He spoke to them only in parables, then explained everything to his disciples when he was alone with them.
local: O maa maɩ mɩmaŋa yɩranɩ mʋ o brɩ nɔɔna bam. Kʋ na yǝni kʋ yɩ wʋntʋ dɩ o karabiǝ bam yɩranɩ, o yǝni o tɛ wǝǝnu tɩm maama kuri o brɩ-ba.

eng: Later that day, when evening came, Jesus said to them, Lets cross over to the other side of the lake.
local: Dɛ dɩm kʋntʋ wa-zʋʋrɩ maŋa nɩ, Zezi ma ta dɩ o karabiǝ bam o wɩ: «Pa-na dɩ́ kwe naboro dɩ́ vu dɩ́ bɛ bubɛ dɩdoŋ dɩm.».

eng: They left the crowd and took him in the boat just as he was. Other boats followed along.
local: O na je naboro kʋm wʋnɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma kwe-o ba ja viiri yɩ ba daarɩ kɔgɔ kʋm. Nabwǝǝru tɩdonnǝ dɩ maa tɔgɩ-ba tɩ veǝ.

eng: Gale-force winds arose, and waves crashed against the boat so that the boat was swamped.
local: Vu-fɔrɔ laan ma zaŋɩ kʋ magɩ boro kʋm. Na bam ma pɔgɩlɩ zanzan ba wʋra ba sui boro kʋm, yɩ kʋ lagɩ kʋ miisi na bam kuri nɩ.

eng: But Jesus was in the rear of the boat, sleeping on a pillow. They woke him up and said, Teacher, dont you care that were drowning.
local: Zezi maa wʋ naboro kʋm kwaga kam nɩ o tigi pɔlɔ baŋa nɩ o dɔa. O karabiǝ bam ma vu ba zaŋ-o, yɩ ba ta dɩd-o ba wɩ: «Karanyɩna, dɩ́ na lagɩ dɩ́ tɩ tɩn, kʋ daɩ kʋlʋkʋlʋ dɩ nmʋ na?».

eng: He got up and gave orders to the wind, and he said to the lake, Silence! Be still! The wind settled down and there was a great calm.
local: O laan ma zaŋɩ wɛɛnɩ, yɩ o bagɩ vu-fɔrɔ kʋm o wɩ, kʋ kwǝri, yɩ o daarɩ o ta dɩ na bam o wɩ, ba yagɩ sɔɔ! O na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, viu kʋm ma sɩɩnɩ kʋ ja, yɩ na bam dɩ maŋɩ da cɩmm.

eng: Jesus asked them, Why are you frightened? Dont you have faith yet.
local: O ma ta dɩ o karabiǝ bam o wɩ: «Bɛɛ mʋ yɩ á fʋmma kʋntʋ? Abam ta wʋ kɩ á wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ amʋ na?».

eng: Overcome with awe, they said to each other, Who then is this? Even the wind and the sea obey him.
local: Fʋʋnɩ ma tiini dɩ ja-ba. Ba ma bwe daanɩ ba wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ yɩ wɔɔ mʋ, yɩ viu dɩ na dɩ ta sɛ o ni?».

eng: Jesus and his disciples came to the other side of the lake, to the region of the Gerasenes.
local: Ba ma vu ba bɛ nɩnɩʋ kʋm ni dɩdoŋ dɩm mɛ Zeraaza tiinǝ na zʋʋrɩ da tɩn.

eng: As soon as Jesus got out of the boat, a man possessed by an evil spirit came out of the tombs.
local: Zezi ma nuŋi naboro kʋm wʋnɩ. Nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ ma da o nuŋi yibeelǝ je nɩ o ba o jeer-o. Cicirǝ mʋ jɩg-o ka yaara.

eng: This man lived among the tombs, and no one was ever strong enough to restrain him, even with a chain.
local: O dɛɛn tigi yibeelǝ je nɩ mʋ. Kuni zanzan ba yǝni ba maɩ capʋnnʋ mʋ ba ma vɔ o jɩa dɩ o nɛ. O maa yǝni o vaŋɩ capʋnnʋ tɩlʋ na wʋ o jɩa nɩ tɩn o kɔ. O ma daarɩ o magɩ luguru tɩlʋ ba na kɩ o nɛ sɩm nɩ tɩn o cɔgɩ. O na tiini o dana tɩn, nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ daa warɩ o ja-o o vɔ, naa o ja-o o pa o jǝni cɩm.

eng: Night and day in the tombs and the hills, he would howl and cut himself with stones.
local: Maŋa maama wɩa dɩ tɩtɩɩ o beeri yibeelǝ je sɩm nɩ dɩ pweeru yuu nɩ mʋ, yɩ o kaasa. O maa yǝni o magɩ o tɩtɩ dɩ kandwa o fwǝlǝ.

eng: When he saw Jesus from far away, he ran and knelt before him.
local: O na tʋlɩ o na Zezi yigǝ yigǝ tɩn, o ma nuŋi yibeelǝ yam wʋnɩ o duri o vu o jeer-o. O na yi o te tɩn, o ma kuni doonǝ o yigǝ nɩ.

eng: shouting, What have you to do with me, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? Swear to God that you wont torture me.
local: yɩ o kaasɩ dɩ kwǝr-dɩa o wɩ: «Zezi, Yuutu Baŋa-Wɛ Bu, n tu amʋ te sɩ n kɩ bɛɛ mʋ? A loori-m dɩ Wɛ yɩrɩ, sɩ n yɩ zaŋɩ n yaarɩ-nɩ.».

eng: He said this because Jesus had already commanded him, Unclean spirit, come out of the man.
local: O tagɩ kʋntʋ, bɛŋwaanɩ Zezi maŋɩ o wʋra o tɛ dɩ cicirǝ kam sɩ ka nuŋi o yɩra nɩ.

eng: Jesus asked him, What is your name? He responded, Legion is my name, because we are many.
local: Zezi laan ma bwe-o o wɩ: «N yɩrɩ mʋ bɛɛ?» O ma lǝri o wɩ: «A yɩrɩ mʋ Kɔgɔ, bɛŋwaanɩ dɩ́ yɩ ciciri zanzan mʋ.».

eng: They pleaded with Jesus not to send them out of that region.
local: Ciciri sɩm ma pa nɔɔnʋ wʋm tiini o loori Zezi, sɩ o yɩ pa sɩ nuŋi je sɩntʋ nɩ sɩ viiri.

eng: A large herd of pigs was feeding on the hillside.
local: Tǝrǝ zanzan dɛɛn ma wʋ je sɩm kʋntʋ nɩ ba beeri piu kʋm tǝŋǝ nɩ ba di.

eng: Send us into the pigs! they begged. Let us go into the pigs.
local: Ciciri sɩm ma loori Zezi sɩ wɩ: «Pa dɩbam cwǝŋǝ sɩ dɩ́ vu dɩ́ zʋ tǝrǝ bam.».

eng: Jesus gave them permission, so the unclean spirits left the man and went into the pigs. Then the herd of about two thousand pigs rushed down the cliff into the lake and drowned.
local: O ma sɛ. Ciciri sɩm laan ma yagɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋm yɩra yɩ sɩ zʋ tǝrǝ bam. Tǝrǝ bam kɔgɔ kʋm maama laan ma duri ba tu ba tʋ bugǝ kam wʋnɩ ba li na ba tɩ. Ba dɛɛn yɩ nɩnɛɛnɩ tǝrǝ mʋrr-tɩle mʋ te.

eng: Those who tended the pigs ran away and told the story in the city and in the countryside. People came to see what had happened.
local: Nɔɔna balʋ ya na nii tǝrǝ bam baŋa nɩ tɩn ma duri ba vu tɩʋ kʋm wʋ dɩ dɛ sɩm maama nɩ, ba ta dɩ nɔɔna bam kʋlʋ na kɩ tɩn. Nɔɔna bam ma nuŋi ba vu sɩ ba nii kʋ na kɩ te tɩn.

eng: They came to Jesus and saw the man who used to be demon-possessed. They saw the very man who had been filled with many demons sitting there fully dressed and completely sane, and they were filled with awe.
local: Ba ma vu ba yi Zezi te. Ba ma na nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ ciciri kɔgɔ kʋm ya na jɩg-o tɩn. O maa je yɩ o wʋbʋŋa joori ya ba o tɩtɩ yɩ o zʋ gwaarʋ. Ba na nɛ-o kʋntʋ tɩn, fʋʋnɩ ma zʋ-ba.

eng: Those who had actually seen what had happened to the demon-possessed man told the others about the pigs.
local: Balʋ yiǝ na nɛ kǝm dɩm tɩn ma ta ba brɩ nɔɔna bam kʋ na kɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋm te tɩn, dɩ kʋlʋ na kɩ tǝrǝ bam dɩ tɩn.

eng: Then they pleaded with Jesus to leave their region.
local: Tɩʋ kʋm nɔɔna bam laan ma loori Zezi sɩ o nuŋi ba tɩʋ kʋm nɩ o viiri.
