eng: While he was climbing into the boat, the one who had been demon-possessed pleaded with Jesus to let him come along as one of his disciples.
local: Zezi ma vu sɩ o joori o zʋ naboro kʋm wʋ. Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ ciciri sɩm ya na jɩgɩ sɩ yaarɩ tɩn ma loor-o, sɩ o na sɛ, sɩ o taa tɔg-o.

eng: But Jesus wouldnt allow it. Go home to your own people, Jesus said, and tell them what the Lord has done for you and how he has shown you mercy.
local: O ma wʋ sɛ. O ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Ve sɔŋɔ, sɩ n ta dɩ n sɔŋɔ tiinǝ n Yuutu Baŋa-Wɛ na duri nmʋ ŋwaŋa dɩ DƖ na kɩ woŋo kʋlʋ maama DƖ pa-m tɩn.».

eng: The man went away and began to proclaim in the Ten Cities all that Jesus had done for him, and everyone was amazed.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋm laan ma kɛ o vu je sɩlʋ ba na bǝ nɩ Tɩɩnɩ Fugǝ tɩn, yɩ o tɛ o brɩ nɔɔna bam Zezi na kɩ kʋlʋ o pa-o tɩn. Balʋ maama na ni o taanɩ dɩm tɩn, kʋ ma sʋ-ba.

eng: Jesus crossed the lake again, and on the other side a large crowd gathered around him on the shore.
local: Zezi dɛɛn ma joori o da nɩnɩʋ kʋm wʋ dɩ naboro o bɛ bubɛ dɩdoŋ dɩm. O na zɩgɩ nɩnɩʋ kʋm ni nɩ tɩn, nɔn-kɔgɔ ma ba o te ba gilim-o.

eng: Jairus, one of the synagogue leaders, came forward. When he saw Jesus, he fell at his feet.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ maa tɔgɩ o wʋra, o yɩrɩ mʋ Zayirusi. O yɩ Wɛ-digǝ kam yigǝ tiinǝ wʋ dɩdʋa mʋ. O na nɛ Zezi tɩn, o ma kuni doonǝ o yigǝ nɩ.

eng: and pleaded with him, My daughter is about to die. Please, come and place your hands on her so that she can be healed and live.
local: yɩ o tiini o loor-o o wɩ: «Amʋ bukɔ balaŋa mʋ lagɩ o tɩ. Popo, a loori-m sɩ n ba n daŋɩ n jɩa o baŋa nɩ, sɩ kʋʋ́ pa o yawɩʋ kʋm je sɩ o joori o na ŋwɩa.».

eng: So Jesus went with him. A swarm of people were following Jesus, crowding in on him.
local: Zezi ma sɛ yɩ o tɔgɩ dɩd-o o ma ve. Kɔ-fɔrɔ dɩ maa tɔg-o, yɩ ba pɩɩn-o zanzan.

eng: A woman was there who had been bleeding for twelve years.
local: Kaanɩ wʋdoŋ maa tɔgɩ o wʋra, o na wɔɛ ka-wʋ taan bɩna fugǝ-yale.

eng: She had suffered a lot under the care of many doctors, and had spent everything she had without getting any better. In fact, she had gotten worse.
local: O dɛɛn tiini o yaarɩ dɔgɩta tiinǝ zanzan tee nɩ. O dɛɛn cɔgɩ o sǝbu maama o yawɩʋ kʋm baŋa nɩ, yɩ kʋ ta ba garɩ kʋ pa-o, kʋ fɔgɩ kʋ jiri lɛɛrʋ mʋ o yɩra nɩ.

eng: Because she had heard about Jesus, she came up behind him in the crowd and touched his clothes.
local: O laan na ni Zezi ŋwa tɩn, o ma tɔgɩ kɔgɔ kʋm wʋ o kɛ o kwaga o vu o dwe o gɔrɔ.

eng: She was thinking, If I can just touch his clothes, Ill be healed.
local: O maa bʋŋɩ o wɩ: «A na maŋɩ a dwe o gɔrɔ kʋm má dɩ, aá na yazurǝ.».

eng: Her bleeding stopped immediately, and she sensed in her body that her illness had been healed.
local: O na dwe-o kʋntʋ tɩn, jana kam ma da ka kɔ. O ma lwarɩ nɩ o yawɩʋ kʋm sɩɩnɩ kʋ je o yɩra nɩ.

eng: At that very moment, Jesus recognized that power had gone out from him. He turned around in the crowd and said, Who touched my clothes.
local: Bɩdwɩ baŋa nɩ mʋ Zezi maanɩ nɩ dam nuŋi o yɩra nɩ. O maa zɩgɩ kɔgɔ kʋm wʋnɩ o pipiri o nii, yɩ o bwe o wɩ: «Wɔɔ mʋ dwe a gɔrɔ?».

eng: His disciples said to him, Dont you see the crowd pressing against you? Yet you ask, Who touched me.
local: O karabiǝ bam ma ta dɩd-o ba wɩ: «Nmʋ wʋ nɛ nɔɔna bam maama na yigi ba pɩɩnɩ nmʋ te tɩn na? Bɛɛ mʋ yɩ n bwe nɩ, wɔɔ mʋ dwe-m?».

eng: But Jesus looked around carefully to see who had done it.
local: O daa ta maa fɔgɩ o nii kɔgɔ kʋm wʋnɩ sɩ o lwarɩ wʋlʋ na dwe-o tɩn.

eng: The woman, full of fear and trembling, came forward. Knowing what had happened to her, she fell down in front of Jesus and told him the whole truth.
local: Fʋʋnɩ laan maa jɩgɩ kaanɩ wʋm yɩ o yɩra saɩ, o na ye kʋlʋ na kɩ-o yɩ o na yazurǝ tɩn ŋwaanɩ. O ma vu Zezi te o kuni doonǝ o yigǝ nɩ, yɩ o ta o cɩga maama dɩd-o.

eng: He responded, Daughter, your faith has healed you; go in peace, healed from your disease.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «A bukɔ, nmʋ na kɩ n wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ amʋ te tɩn mʋ pɛ n na yazurǝ. Ve sɩ Wɛ wʋ́ wǝli-m, sɩ n sin dɩ n yawɩʋ kʋm.».

eng: While Jesus was still speaking with her, messengers came from the synagogue leaders house, saying to Jairus, Your daughter has died. Why bother the teacher any longer.
local: Zezi ta na wʋra o ŋɔɔnɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, nɔɔna badonnǝ ma nuŋi Zayirusi sɔŋɔ ba ba, ba ta dɩd-o ba wɩ: «N bukɔ wʋm tɩga. Daa n yɩ ta n jɩgɩ karanyɩna kam n yaara.».

eng: But Jesus overheard their report and said to the synagogue leader, Dont be afraid; just keep trusting.
local: Zezi maa cɩm, yɩ o ta dɩ Zayirusi o wɩ: «Yɩ pa fʋʋnɩ ja-m. Za n weeni n kɩ n wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ amʋ.».

eng: He didnt allow anyone to follow him except Peter, James, and John, James brother.
local: Zezi ma wʋ sɛ sɩ nɔɔna tɔgɩ dɩd-o ba vu Zayirusi sɔŋɔ kʋm, kʋ na daɩ Pɩyɛɛrɩ dɩ Zakɩ dɩ o nyaanɩ Zan yɩranɩ.

eng: They came to the synagogue leaders house, and he saw a commotion, with people crying and wailing loudly.
local: Ba ma vu ba yi sɔŋɔ kʋm. Zezi ma ni nɩ sɔɔ yɩranɩ mʋ wʋra bɔgɩ bɔgɩ, yɩ nɔɔna keerǝ yɩ ba coosǝ.

eng: He went in and said to them, Whats all this commotion and crying about? The child isnt dead. Shes only sleeping.
local: O ma zʋ sɔŋɔ kʋm, yɩ o ta dɩ nɔɔna bam o wɩ: «Bɛŋwaanɩ mʋ yɩ á kɩ sɔɔ yɩ á keerǝ? Bu wʋm wʋ tɩgɩ, o dɔ mʋ.».

eng: They laughed at him, but he threw them all out. Then, taking the childs parents and his disciples with him, he went to the room where the child was.
local: O na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma mwan-o. O ma pa ba maama nuŋi. O ma daarɩ o ja bu wʋm nu dɩ o ko dɩ o tɩtɩ karabiǝ batɔ bam o vu o zʋ digǝ kalʋ bu wʋm na tigi da tɩn.

eng: Taking her hand, he said to her, Talitha koum , which means, Young woman, get up.
local: O ma ja bu wʋm jɩŋa, yɩ o ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Talita kumi.» Taanɩ dɩntʋ kuri mʋ: «Bɩsankana, a lagɩ a ta nmʋ, zaŋɩ wɛɛnɩ.».

eng: Suddenly the young woman got up and began to walk around. She was years old. They were shocked!.
local: O na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, bɩsankana kam ma da ka zaŋɩ wɛɛnɩ ka veǝ. O bɩna dɛɛn yɩ fugǝ-yale mʋ. Ba na nɛ kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ ma sʋ-ba lanyɩranɩ.

eng: He gave them strict orders that no one should know what had happened. Then he told them to give her something to eat.
local: Zezi laan ma kaanɩ-ba o wɩ, ba yɩ zaŋɩ ba pa nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ lwarɩ kʋlʋ na kɩ tɩn. O ma daarɩ o ta dɩ ba o wɩ, ba kɩ wʋdiu ba pa bu wʋm sɩ o di.

eng: Now when Jesus saw the crowds, he went up a mountain. He sat down and his disciples came to him.
local: Zezi dɛɛn na nɛ nɔn-kɔgɔ na tɔg-o tɩn, o ma vu o di piu kʋdoŋ yuu o jǝni da. O na je tɩn, o karabiǝ bam ma ba o te.

eng: He taught them, saying.
local: O laan ma puli o brɩ-ba o wɩ:.

eng: Happy are people who are hopeless, because the kingdom of heaven is theirs.
local: «Balʋ na ye nɩ ba yɩ yinigǝ tiinǝ Wɛ yigǝ nɩ tɩn mʋ jɩgɩ yu-yoŋo, ba na wʋ́ tɔgɩ ba di Wɛ paarɩ dɩm tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: Happy are people who grieve, because they will be made glad.
local: Balʋ wʋrʋ na cɔgɩ tɩn mʋ jɩgɩ yu-yoŋo, Wɛ na wʋ́ pa-ba wʋ-zuru tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: Happy are people who are humble, because they will inherit the earth.
local: Balʋ na jɩgɩ ban-pʋnʋ tɩn mʋ jɩgɩ yu-yoŋo, Wɛ na wʋ́ pa ba taa te lʋgʋ tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: Happy are people who are hungry and thirsty for righteousness, because they will be fed until they are full.
local: Balʋ na paɩ Wɛ cɩga kam tɔgɩm jɩgɩ fra dɩ ba tɩn mʋ jɩgɩ yu-yoŋo, Wɛ na wʋ́ pa ba bɩcara pǝni tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: Happy are people who show mercy, because they will receive mercy.
local: Balʋ na jɩgɩ nɔɔna ŋwaŋa tɩn mʋ jɩgɩ yu-yoŋo, Wɛ na wʋ́ duri ba dɩ ŋwaŋa tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: Happy are people who have pure hearts, because they will see God.
local: Balʋ na yɩ wʋ-poŋo tiinǝ tɩn mʋ jɩgɩ yu-yoŋo, ba na wʋ́ ba ba na Wɛ tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: Happy are people who make peace, because they will be called Gods children.
local: Balʋ na paɩ nɔɔna jɩgɩ ywǝǝni daanɩ tɩn mʋ jɩgɩ yu-yoŋo, Wɛ na wʋ́ ta bǝ-ba nɩ DƖ biǝ tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: Happy are people whose lives are harassed because they are righteous, because the kingdom of heaven is theirs.
local: Balʋ na tɔgɩ Wɛ cɩga kam yɩ nɔɔna bɛɛsɩ-ba tɩn mʋ jɩgɩ yu-yoŋo, ba na wʋ́ tɔgɩ ba di Wɛ paarɩ dɩm tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: Happy are you when people insult you and harass you and speak all kinds of bad and false things about you, all because of me.
local: Nɔɔna na twɩ abam yɩ ba bɛɛsɩ abam, yɩ ba fɔ vwan ba tɛ wo-balwaarʋ dwi maama ba pa abam amʋ yɩrɩ ŋwaanɩ tɩn, á jɩgɩ yu-yoŋo.

eng: Be full of joy and be glad, because you have a great reward in heaven. In the same way, people harassed the prophets who came before you.
local: Á taá jɩgɩ wʋpolo lanyɩranɩ sɩ Wɛ wʋ́ pa á na nyɔɔrɩ zanzan DƖ tee nɩ. Kʋ na yɩ Wɛ nijoŋnǝ balʋ dɛɛn na loori abam yigǝ ba ba tɩn, nɔɔna dɛɛn maŋɩ ba bɛɛsɩ-ba kʋntʋ mʋ.

eng: You are the salt of the earth. But if salt loses its saltiness, how will it become salty again? Its good for nothing except to be thrown away and trampled under peoples feet.
local: Yɛ na paɩ wʋdiu ywǝnǝ te tɩn, abam dɩ nan maŋɩ sɩ á taá paɩ ywǝǝni taa wʋ nabiinǝ tee nɩ. Yɛ ywǝǝni nan na ti, n daa n bá wanɩ n pa dɩ joori dɩ ja ywǝǝni. Dɩ daa ba jɩgɩ kuri. Baá dɩ-dɩ ba yagɩ mʋ, sɩ nɔɔna taa nwanɩ dɩ baŋa nɩ.

eng: You are the light of the world. A city on top of a hill cant be hidden.
local: Abam nyɩ dɩ lʋgʋ baŋa pooni mʋ nabiinǝ tɩtarɩ nɩ. Tɩʋ kʋlʋ ba na lɔgɩ piu yuu nɩ tɩn bá wanɩ kʋ sǝgi, kʋ naɩ mʋ jaja.

eng: Neither do people light a lamp and put it under a basket. Instead, they put it on top of a lampstand, and it shines on all who are in the house.
local: Nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ bá sɛ o tarɩgɩ kanɩa o pu tɩtɔgɔ kuri nɩ. Oó kwe-ka o palɩ wɛɛnɩ mʋ, sɩ ka pooni dɩm taa naɩ dɩ paɩ nɔɔna balʋ maama na wʋ digǝ kam wʋnɩ tɩn.

eng: In the same way, let your light shine before people, so they can see the good things you do and praise your Father who is in heaven.
local: Abam dɩ nan maŋɩ sɩ á zǝŋi á pooni dɩm kʋntʋ mʋ nɔɔna tɩtarɩ nɩ. Ba laan wʋ́ na wo-laarʋ tɩlʋ á na kɩ tɩn, yɩ ba zuli á Ko Wɛ wʋlʋ na wʋ wɛyuu nɩ tɩn.».

eng: Dont even begin to think that I have come to do away with the Law and the Prophets. I havent come to do away with them but to fulfill them.
local: «Abam nan yɩ taá bʋŋɩ nɩ amʋ tu sɩ a pa Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm na brɩ kʋlʋ tɩn ji kafɛ, kʋ na yɩ Wɛ cullu tɩlʋ DƖ dɛɛn na pɛ Moyisi, dɩ DƖ nijoŋnǝ bam na brɩ kʋlʋ faŋa faŋa tɩn dɩ. Amʋ tu sɩ a pa tɩ tiini tɩ taa jɩgɩ kuri mʋ lanyɩranɩ, sɩ kʋ daɩ nɩ a lagɩ a cɔgɩ-tɩ mʋ.

eng: I say to you very seriously that as long as heaven and earth exist, neither the smallest letter nor even the smallest stroke of a pen will be erased from the Law until everything there becomes a reality.
local: A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, lʋgʋ baŋa dɩ wɛyuu na wʋ́ daanɩ sɩ kʋ taa ve maŋa kalʋ tɩn, kʋlʋkʋlʋ bá wanɩ kʋ lɩ Wɛ cullu tɩm wʋnɩ dɩ fɩnfɩɩn dɩ. Tɩ nan wʋ́ ta wʋra kʋntʋ mʋ taan, sɩ kʋ ba kʋ yi maŋa kalʋ Wɛ na wʋ́ pa woŋo maama kɩ kʋ ti tɩn.

eng: Therefore, whoever ignores one of the least of these commands and teaches others to do the same will be called the lowest in the kingdom of heaven. But whoever keeps these commands and teaches people to keep them will be called great in the kingdom of heaven.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ maama na wʋ sɛ Wɛ niǝ yam dɩdʋa yɩ o daarɩ o brɩ o donnǝ sɩ ba dɩ yɩ sɛ-dɩ tɩn, dɩ na maŋɩ dɩ yɩ mɩmɩɩ dɩ, kʋntʋ tu wʋ́ ba ji balaŋa Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋnɩ. Kʋ daarɩ, wʋlʋ maama na sɛ Wɛ niǝ yam yɩ o daarɩ o brɩ o donnǝ sɩ ba dɩ sɛ tɩn, kʋntʋ tu wʋ́ ba ji kamunu Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋnɩ.

eng: I say to you that unless your righteousness is greater than the righteousness of the legal experts and the Pharisees, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven.
local: Á nan taá ye nɩ, abam na wʋ sɛ Wɛ cɩga kam á dwǝni á cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ bam dɩ Farizɩan tiinǝ bam, á bá wanɩ á na cwǝŋǝ sɩ á tɔgɩ á zʋ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋ.».

eng: You have heard that it was said to those who lived long ago, Dont commit murder, and all who commit murder will be in danger of judgment.
local: «Abam ye nɩ ba dɛɛn brɩ dɩbam kwǝ bam ba wɩ: ‹Yɩ zaŋɩ n gʋ nɔɔnʋ. Wʋlʋ nan na gʋ nɔɔnʋ, ba maŋɩ sɩ ba di o taanɩ mʋ.›.

eng: But I say to you that everyone who is angry with their brother or sister will be in danger of judgment. If they say to their brother or sister, You idiot, they will be in danger of being condemned by the governing council. And if they say, You fool, they will be in danger of fiery hell.
local: Kʋ daarɩ, a nan lagɩ a ta dɩ abam nɩ, nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na jɩgɩ ban-zɔŋɔ dɩ o ko-bu, kʋ maŋɩ sɩ ba di kʋntʋ tu dɩ taanɩ mʋ. Wʋlʋ na tagɩ dɩ o ko-bu o wɩ, o ba jɩgɩ wʋbʋŋa, kʋ maŋɩ sɩ dɩbam sarɩya-dirǝ nakwa bam mʋ di kʋntʋ tu taanɩ. Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ dɩ na tagɩ dɩ o ko-bu o wɩ, o yɩ joro, kʋ maŋɩ sɩ kʋntʋ tu zʋ min-tɩʋ mʋ.

eng: Therefore, if you bring your gift to the altar and there remember that your brother or sister has something against you.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ, nmʋ na lagɩ n kwe n pɛɛrɩ n pa Wɛ, yɩ n laan na guli nɩ n ko-bu jɩgɩ n ban-zɔŋɔ.

eng: leave your gift at the altar and go. First make things right with your brother or sister and then come back and offer your gift.
local: sɩ n tiŋi n pɛɛrɩ dɩm, sɩ n joori n vu n ko-bu wʋm te sɩ á fɔgɩ daanɩ. Nmʋ na kɩ kʋntʋ, sɩ n laan joori n ba n kwe n pɛɛrɩ dɩm n pa Wɛ.

eng: Be sure to make friends quickly with your opponents while you are with them on the way to court. Otherwise, they will haul you before the judge, the judge will turn you over to the officer of the court, and you will be thrown into prison.
local: Kʋ daarɩ, nɔɔnʋ nan na jaanɩ-m o vu o saŋɩ, sɩ n kɩ lɩla n pa á fɔgɩ daanɩ yɩ á daa ta wʋ yi sarɩya yam dim jǝgǝ kam. Kʋ na daɩ kʋntʋ, o laan wʋ́ kɩ nmʋ sarɩya-diru wʋm jɩŋa nɩ, sɩ o pa n na cam. Wʋntʋ dɩ laan wʋ́ kɩ-m o tɩntʋŋnʋ jɩŋa nɩ, sɩ o ja-m o vu o kɩ pɩɩna digǝ nɩ.

eng: I say to you in all seriousness that you wont get out of there until youve paid the very last penny.
local: A lagɩ a ta cɩga dɩ nmʋ sɩ, nń ta n wʋra taan. Nmʋ na wʋ ŋwɩ jɩnɩ dɩm maama n ti, ba bá yagɩ-m.».

eng: You have heard that it was said, Dont commit adultery.
local: «Abam nan ye nɩ ba maŋɩ ba ta ba wɩ: ‹Yɩ zaŋɩ n pǝni dɩ n doŋ kaanɩ.›.

eng: But I say to you that every man who looks at a woman lustfully has already committed adultery in his heart.
local: Amʋ nan lagɩ a ta abam nɩ, nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na nii kaanɩ dɩ fra tɩn, kʋ nyɩ dɩ o maŋɩ o pǝni dɩd-o mʋ o wʋbʋŋa na lag-o tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: And if your right eye causes you to fall into sin, tear it out and throw it away. Its better that you lose a part of your body than that your whole body be thrown into hell.
local: Kʋntʋ tɩn, nmʋ jazɩm yi na paɩ n kɩ lwarɩm, sɩ n lɩ-dɩ n yagɩ. Nmʋ yɩra daa dɩdʋa na cɔgɩ, kʋʋ́ ta lana kʋ pa-m kʋ dwe n yɩra yam maama na wʋra yɩ ba dɩ-ya min-tɩʋ nɩ.

eng: And if your right hand causes you to fall into sin, chop it off and throw it away. Its better that you lose a part of your body than that your whole body go into hell.
local: Nmʋ jazɩm mʋ na paɩ n kɩ lwarɩm, sɩ n go-dɩ n yagɩ. Nmʋ yɩra daa dɩdʋa na cɔgɩ, kʋʋ́ ta lana kʋ pa-m kʋ dwe n yɩra yam maama na zʋ min-tɩʋ.».

eng: It was said, Whoever divorces his wife must give her a divorce certificate.
local: «Ba maŋɩ ba ta ba wɩ: ‹Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na yagɩ o kaanɩ, kʋntʋ tu maŋɩ sɩ o pa-o tɔnɔ kʋlʋ na wʋ́ brɩ nɩ ba yagɩ daanɩ tɩn.›.

eng: But I say to you that whoever divorces his wife except for sexual unfaithfulness forces her to commit adultery. And whoever marries a divorced woman commits adultery.
local: Amʋ nan lagɩ a ta abam nɩ, nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na yagɩ o kaanɩ yɩ kʋ daɩ nɩ o cɔgɩ dɩ baarʋ tɩn, kʋntʋ tu pɛ kaanɩ wʋm kɩ bwǝŋǝ mʋ, dɩ o daa na zʋ barʋ wʋdoŋ tɩn. Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ maama nan na di kaanɩ wʋm kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋntʋ tu dɩ kɩ bwǝŋǝ mʋ.».

eng: Again you have heard that it was said to those who lived long ago: Dont make a false solemn pledge, but you should follow through on what you have pledged to the Lord.
local: «Abam maŋɩ á ye nɩ ba dɛɛn brɩ dɩbam kwǝ bam ba wɩ: ‹Yɩ zaŋɩ n kwanɩ n ni, dɩ nmʋ na dugǝ n wɩ nń kɩ kʋlʋ n pa n Yuutu Baŋa-Wɛ tɩn.›.

eng: But I say to you that you must not pledge at all. You must not pledge by heaven, because its Gods throne.
local: Amʋ nan lagɩ a ta a brɩ abam sɩ á yɩ zaŋɩ á du dɩ kʋlʋkʋlʋ. Yɩ zaŋɩ á du durǝ dɩ Wɛ-sɔŋɔ, bɛŋwaanɩ dáanɩ mʋ yɩ Wɛ jǝŋǝ je.

eng: You must not pledge by the earth, because its Gods footstool. You must not pledge by Jerusalem, because its the city of the great king.
local: Yɩ du-na dɩ tɩga baŋa dɩ, ka na yɩ Wɛ nɛ cwiim jǝgǝ tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Nan yɩ du-na dɩ Zeruzalɛm dɩ, sɩ kʋntʋ yɩ Wɛ pa-farʋ wʋm tɩʋ mʋ.

eng: And you must not pledge by your head, because you cant turn one hair white or black.
local: Nan yɩ zaŋɩ á du dɩ á tɩtɩ yuu dɩ, bɛŋwaanɩ á bá wanɩ á pa á yuuyuǝ dɩdʋa tɩtɩ lǝni ka ji napoŋo naa nazono.

eng: Let your yes mean yes, and your no mean no. Anything more than this comes from the evil one.
local: Nan pa-na abam ‹ɛɛn› ji ‹ɛɛn›, sɩ abam ‹awo› ji ‹awo›, sɩ á daa yɩ wǝli kʋlʋkʋlʋ da. Woŋo na wǝli da, kʋntʋ yɩ sʋtaanɩ nyɩm mʋ.».

eng: You have heard that it was said, An eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth .
local: «Abam maŋɩ á ni sɩ ba tagɩ ba wɩ: ‹Nɔɔnʋ na pi n yi, sɩ ba pi kʋntʋ tu dɩ yi. Nɔɔnʋ nan na guri n yǝli, sɩ ba guri kʋntʋ tu dɩ yǝli.›.

eng: But I say to you that you must not oppose those who want to hurt you. If people slap you on your right cheek, you must turn the left cheek to them as well.
local: Amʋ nan lagɩ a ta a brɩ abam nɩ, nɔɔnʋ na kɩ-m lwarɩm, sɩ n dɩ daa yɩ kɩ-o lwarɩm n ma n ŋwɩ. Nɔɔnʋ nan na lɔgɩ n jazɩm pʋpɔŋɔ, sɩ n guni kʋdoŋ kʋm dɩ, sɩ o lɔ o wǝli da.

eng: When they wish to haul you to court and take your shirt, let them have your coat too.
local: Nɔɔnʋ nan na saŋɩ-m sɩ o joŋi n gwar-bu o jɩnɩ ŋwaanɩ, sɩ n pa o kwe n gwar-bwǝrǝ dɩ o wǝli da.

eng: When they force you to go one mile, go with them two.
local: Dɩdɛɛrʋ nan na fɩ-m sɩ n zɩŋɩ o zɩla n vu nabaa yi mʋrʋ, sɩ n zɩŋɩ n vu nabaa yi mʋrr-tɩle.

eng: The Pharisees and some legal experts from Jerusalem gathered around Jesus.
local: Farizɩan tiinǝ badaara dɩ Wɛ cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ badonnǝ dɛɛn ya nuŋi Zeruzalɛm ba ba, ba gilimi Zezi tee nɩ.

eng: Give to those who ask, and dont refuse those who wish to borrow from you.
local: Nɔɔnʋ nan na loori-m woŋo, sɩ n kwaanɩ n pa-o-kʋ. Nɔɔnʋ nan na lagɩ sɩ o jɩnɩ woŋo n tee nɩ, sɩ n yɩ van-o.».

eng: They saw some of his disciples eating food with unclean hands. They were eating without first ritually purifying their hands through washing.
local: Ba ma na o karabiǝ badonnǝ na je ba di wʋdiu, yɩ ba wʋ sanɩ ba jɩa sɩ kʋ tɔgɩ bantʋ cullu tɩm cwǝŋǝ.

eng: You have heard that it was said, You must love your neighbor and hate your enemy.
local: «Abam maŋɩ á ni sɩ ba tagɩ ba wɩ: ‹N maŋɩ sɩ n ta n soe n donnǝ mʋ, sɩ n daarɩ n ta n culi n dʋna.›.

eng: The Pharisees and all the Jews dont eat without first washing their hands carefully. This is a way of observing the rules handed down by the elders.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ Farizɩan tiinǝ bam dɩ Zwifǝ bam maama ba fɔgɩ ba di wʋdiu, yɩ ba wʋ sanɩ ba jɩa sɩ kʋ tɔgɩ cullu tɩlʋ ba nabaara bam na pɛ-ba tɩn.

eng: But I say to you, love your enemies and pray for those who harass you.
local: Amʋ nan lagɩ a ta abam nɩ, á taá soe á dʋna, sɩ á daarɩ á taá loori Wɛ á pa balʋ na jɩgɩ abam ba bɛɛsɩ tɩn.

eng: Upon returning from the marketplace, they dont eat without first immersing themselves. They observe many other rules that have been handed down, such as the washing of cups, jugs, pans, and sleeping mats.
local: Ba na yǝni ba zɩgɩ yaga nɩ ba ba sɔŋɔ, baá kɩ na mʋ ba ma zarɩ ba tɩtɩ sɩ ba ji lanyɩranɩ, yɩ ba laan daarɩ ba di wʋdiu. Ba ta jɩgɩ cullu tɩdonnǝ zanzan ba na tɔgɩ-tɩ ba wǝli da. Ba yǝni ba fɔgɩ ba sɩn ba zwɩ dɩ ba kɔɔra dɩ ba luu-kambi, yɩ ba laan daarɩ ba kɩ wʋdiu da.

eng: so that you will be acting as children of your Father who is in heaven. He makes the sun rise on both the evil and the good and sends rain on both the righteous and the unrighteous.
local: Á na kɩ kʋntʋ doŋ, kʋʋ́ brɩ nɩ abam yɩ Baŋa-Wɛ biǝ mʋ. Bɛŋwaanɩ dɩntʋ ba pɔɔrɩ nɔɔna daanɩ, DƖ paɩ wɩa nyɩ nɔn-balwaarʋ dɩ nɔn-ŋʋna maama baŋa nɩ. DƖ ta kwǝri DƖ paɩ dʋa nɩ ka paɩ balʋ na kɩ lanyɩranɩ dɩ balʋ na kɩ balɔrɔ tɩn maama.

eng: So the Pharisees and legal experts asked Jesus, Why are your disciples not living according to the rules handed down by the elders but instead eat food with ritually unclean hands.
local: Kʋ yɩ kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ Farizɩan tiinǝ bam dɩ Wɛ cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ bam dɛɛn bwe Zezi ba wɩ: «Bɛɛ mʋ yɩ n karabiǝ bam yǝni ba di wʋdiu, yɩ ba wʋ sanɩ ba jɩa nɩ dɩbam nabaara bam na pɛ dɩbam cullu tɩlʋ tɩn?».

eng: If you love only those who love you, what reward do you have? Dont even the tax collectors do the same?.
local: Abam nan na soe balʋ dɩ na soe abam tɩn yɩranɩ, bɛɛ nyɔɔrɩ mʋ á wʋ́ na Wɛ tee nɩ? Lampo-joŋnǝ dɩ yǝni ba kɩ kʋntʋ doŋ mʋ.

eng: He replied, Isaiah really knew what he was talking about when he prophesied about you hypocrites. He wrote, This people honors me with their lips, but their hearts are far away from me.
local: O ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Abam yɩ pipiri-nyɩna mʋ. Wɛ nijoŋnu Ezayi dɛɛn jɩgɩ bʋra dɩ o na maŋɩ o ta abam taanɩ faŋa faŋa te tɩn. Kʋ pʋpʋnɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ kʋ wɩ: ‹Nɔɔna bantʋ zuli amʋ Wɛ dɩ ba niǝ mʋ, yɩ ba wʋbʋŋa baŋwɛ dɩ amʋ.

eng: And if you greet only your brothers and sisters, what more are you doing? Dont even the Gentiles do the same?.
local: Abam nan na zuli á cʋrrʋ yɩranɩ mʋ, abam kɩ á dwe á donnǝ bam na? Balʋ na yǝri Wɛ tɩn dɩ nan yǝni ba kɩ kʋntʋ doŋ mʋ.

eng: Their worship of me is empty since they teach instructions that are human words.
local: Ba na paɩ amʋ zulǝ yalʋ maama tɩn yɩ kafɛ mʋ. Ba yǝni ba brɩ nabiinǝ wʋbʋŋa cullu, yɩ ba paɩ tɩ yɩ amʋ zaasɩm dɩm mʋ.›.

eng: Therefore, just as your heavenly Father is complete in showing love to everyone, so also you must be complete.
local: Abam nan maŋɩ sɩ á taá yɩ lanyɩranɩ dɩ cɩga tiinǝ, nɩ á Ko Baŋa-Wɛ na yɩ lanyɩranɩ dɩ cɩga tu te tɩn.».

eng: You ignore Gods commandment while holding on to rules created by humans and handed down to you.
local: Kʋntʋ, abam kwe Wɛ niǝ yam na brɩ te tɩn á yagɩ mʋ, yɩ á daarɩ á tɔgɩ nabiinǝ cullu.».

eng: Jesus continued, Clearly, you are experts at rejecting Gods commandment in order to establish these rules.
local: O daa ta ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Abam yǝni á vɩn Wɛ niǝ yam, yɩ á daarɩ á jɩgɩ á tɩtɩ wʋbʋŋa cullu mʋ á tɔga.

eng: Moses said, Honor your father and your mother, and The person who speaks against father or mother will certainly be put to death .
local: Faŋa faŋa tɩn, Moyisi dɛɛn brɩ Wɛ ni dɩm o wɩ, nɔɔnʋ maŋɩ sɩ o taa zuli o nu dɩ o ko. O daa ta brɩ nɩ, nɔɔnʋ na ŋɔɔnɩ ta-balɔrɔ dɩ o nu naa o ko, kʋntʋ tu maŋɩ sɩ ba gʋ-o mʋ.

eng: But you say, If you tell your father or mother,Everything Im expected to contribute to you is corban .
local: Kʋ daarɩ abam nan yǝni á ja Wɛ taanɩ dɩm á gugwǝli mʋ yɩ á wɩ, nɔɔnʋ na jɩgɩ woŋo kʋlʋ o ya na wʋ́ ma o zǝni o nu dɩ o ko tɩn, yɩ o na tagɩ dɩ ba o wɩ, woŋo kʋm kʋntʋ yɩ ‹Kɔrɩban› mʋ .

eng: then you are no longer required to care for your father or mother.
local: kʋ brɩ nɩ kʋntʋ tu daa wʋ maŋɩ sɩ o kwe woŋo kʋm o ma zǝni o nu dɩ o ko.

eng: In this way you do away with Gods word in favor of the rules handed down to you, which you pass on to others. And you do a lot of other things just like that.
local: Abam na brɩ nɔɔna sɩ ba taa tɔgɩ á tɩtɩ cullu tɩn paɩ Wɛ taanɩ dɩm daa ba jɩgɩ kuri abam tee nɩ. Abam nan ta yǝni á kɩ wo-balwaarʋ tɩntʋ donnǝ zanzan á wǝli da.».

eng: Then Jesus called the crowd again and said, Listen to me, all of you, and understand.
local: Zezi daa ta ma bǝŋi nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm sɩ ba ba o te, yɩ o ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Abam maama cǝgi lanyɩranɩ sɩ á ni a taanɩ dɩm kuri.

eng: Nothing outside of a person can enter and contaminate a person in Gods sight; rather, the things that come out of a person contaminate the person.
local: Kʋ daɩ woŋo kʋlʋ na tɔgɩ nɔɔnʋ ni kʋ zʋ o wʋ tɩn mʋ wʋ́ wanɩ kʋ cɔg-o. Kʋ yɩ woŋo kʋlʋ na tɔgɩ nɔɔnʋ ni kʋ nuŋi tɩn mʋ wʋ́ cɔg-o. .

eng: After leaving the crowd, he entered a house where his disciples asked him about that riddle.
local: O laan ma vu o zʋ sɔŋɔ o daarɩ nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm pooni nɩ. O karabiǝ bam ma bwe-o taanɩ dɩm kuri.

eng: He said to them, Dont you understand either? Dont you know that nothing from the outside that enters a person has the power to contaminate?.
local: O ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Abam dɩ ba jɩgɩ wʋbʋŋa na? Á maŋɩ sɩ á taá ye nɩ, woŋo kʋlʋ maama nɔɔnʋ na wʋ́ kwe o pa kʋ tɔgɩ o ni kʋ zʋ o wʋ tɩn bá wanɩ kʋ cɔg-o.

eng: Thats because it doesnt enter into the heart but into the stomach, and it goes out into the sewer. By saying this, Jesus declared that no food could contaminate a person in Gods sight.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ kʋ bá wanɩ kʋ zʋ o wʋbʋŋa. Kʋ nan wʋ́ tu o kana yɩranɩ mʋ, yɩ o laan daarɩ o tɔ-kʋ o yagɩ.» Zezi dɛɛn na tagɩ cɩga kam kʋntʋ tɩn, o brɩ nɩ wʋdiiru maama maŋɩ sɩ nɔɔna taa di-tɩ.

eng: Its what comes out of a person that contaminates someone in Gods sight, he said.
local: O daa ta ma ta o wɩ: «Kʋ yɩ kʋlʋ na maŋɩ kʋ wʋ nɔɔnʋ wʋnɩ yɩ kʋ laan nuŋi tɩn mʋ cɔg-o.

eng: Its from the inside, from the human heart, that evil thoughts come: sexual sins, thefts, murders.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ kʋ yɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋbʋŋa mʋ paɩ o lagɩ wo-balwaarʋ kǝm, kʋ na yɩ boorim wo-zɔɔna dwi maama, dɩ ŋwɩɩnɩ, dɩ nɔn-gʋra.

eng: adultery, greed, evil actions, deceit, unrestrained immorality, envy, insults, arrogance, and foolishness.
local: dɩ wo-swǝnǝ zanzan, dɩ pu-sɩŋa, dɩ kampinǝ, dɩ wo-digiru kikiǝ, dɩ wʋ-gʋrʋ, dɩ bɩbara dɩ nɔn-tʋra, dɩ kamunni, dɩ jwǝrim.

eng: All these evil things come from the inside and contaminate a person in Gods sight.
local: Wo-balwaarʋ tɩntʋ maama nuŋi nɔɔnʋ wʋnɩ mʋ yɩ tɩ cɔg-o.».

eng: Jesus left that place and went into the region of Tyre. He didnt want anyone to know that he had entered a house, but he couldnt hide.
local: Zezi dɛɛn ma nuŋi je sɩm kʋntʋ nɩ o vu Tiiri nawurǝ yam seeni. O maa vu o zʋ sɔŋɔ kʋdoŋ. O ma ba lagɩ sɩ nɔɔna lwarɩ nɩ o wʋra, yɩ o wʋ wanɩ o sǝgi o tɩtɩ dɩ nɔɔna bam.

eng: In fact, a woman whose young daughter was possessed by an unclean spirit heard about him right away. She came and fell at his feet.
local: Kaanɩ wʋdoŋ maa wʋra, o na yɩ dwi-gɛ tu. Ba ya lʋg-o Siiri nɩ mʋ, je sɩlʋ yɩrɩ na yɩ Fenisi tɩn. Cicirǝ maa jɩgɩ o bukɔ ka yaara. O maa na ni Zezi ŋwa tɩn, o ma kɩ lɩla o vu o te, yɩ o kuni doonǝ o yigǝ nɩ o loor-o, sɩ o zǝli cicirǝ kam o pa ka yagɩ o bukɔ wʋm yɩra.

eng: He responded, The children have to be fed first. It isnt right to take the childrens bread and toss it to the dogs.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Biǝ bam mʋ maŋɩ sɩ ba da yigǝ ba di ba daarɩ. Kʋ wʋ maŋɩ sɩ nɔɔnʋ joŋi o biǝ wʋdiu o dɩ kakuri yigǝ nɩ.».

eng: But she answered, Lord, even the dogs under the table eat the childrens crumbs.
local: Kaanɩ wʋm maa lǝri o wɩ: «Amʋ tu, n tagɩ cɩga. Kakuri sɩlʋ nan na tigi taabʋlʋ kuri nɩ tɩn waɩ sɩ twɛ wʋdiu kʋlʋ biǝ bam na di yɩ kʋ siiri tɩga nɩ tɩn sɩ di.».

eng: Good answer! he said. Go on home. The demon has already left your daughter.
local: O laan ma ta dɩ kaanɩ wʋm o wɩ: «Nmʋ lǝrǝ yam na lana tɩn ŋwaanɩ, a sɛ n loro kʋm. Joori n vu sɔŋɔ. Cicirǝ kam maŋɩ ka yagɩ n bukɔ wʋm yɩra.».

eng: When she returned to her house, she found the child lying on the bed and the demon gone.
local: Kaanɩ wʋm ma sɩɩnɩ o joori o vu sɔŋɔ. O ma na o bukɔ wʋm o tigi o tigǝ je nɩ, dɩ cicirǝ kam nuŋi ka daar-o.

eng: After leaving the region of Tyre, Jesus went through Sidon toward the Galilee Sea through the region of the Ten Cities.
local: Zezi laan ma zaŋɩ o nuŋi Tiiri nawurǝ yam nɩ o da Sidɔn o kɛ. O laan ma tɔgɩ je sɩlʋ ba na bǝ nɩ Tɩɩnɩ Fugǝ tɩn o vu o yi Galile nɩnɩʋ kʋm je sɩm.

eng: Some people brought to him a man who was deaf and could hardly speak, and they begged him to place his hand on the man for healing.
local: Nɔɔnʋ ma wʋra o na yɩ zwa-kɔgɔ yɩ o warɩ o ŋɔɔnɩ lanyɩranɩ. Ba ma ja-o ba vu Zezi te. Ba ma loor-o sɩ o kwe o jɩŋa o daŋɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋm baŋa nɩ sɩ o pa o na yazurǝ.

eng: Jesus took him away from the crowd by himself and put his fingers in the mans ears. Then he spit and touched the mans tongue.
local: Zezi ma ja-o o vu daa o yɩranɩ o daarɩ kɔgɔ kʋm. O ma kwe o nwa o tʋrɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋm zwa nɩ, yɩ o daarɩ o twɩ lileeru o kɩ o nʋa nɩ o ma dwe o dindǝlimi.

eng: Looking into heaven, Jesus sighed deeply and said, Ephphatha, which means, Open up.
local: O ma kwǝni o yuu o nii wɛɛnɩ, yɩ o suuri. O ma ta dɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋm o wɩ: «Efata», kʋ kuri mʋ Pʋrɩ!.

eng: At once, his ears opened, his twisted tongue was released, and he began to speak clearly.
local: O na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, o zwa yam ma sɩɩnɩ ya pʋrɩ, yɩ o dindǝlimi dɩ waɩ dɩ pipirǝ. O maa waɩ o ŋɔɔnɩ lanyɩranɩ.

eng: Jesus gave the people strict orders not to tell anyone. But the more he tried to silence them, the more eagerly they shared the news.
local: Zezi ma kaanɩ nɔɔna bam maama, sɩ ba yɩ ta kʋlʋ na kɩ tɩn ba brɩ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ. O na kaanɩ-ba kʋntʋ tɩn, mʋ ba laan fɔgɩ ba tɛ dɩ nɔɔna bam.

eng: People were overcome with wonder, saying, He does everything well! He even makes the deaf to hear and gives speech to those who cant speak.
local: Kʋ ma tiini kʋ sʋ balʋ maama na ni tɩn zanzan. Ba maa wɩ: «Woŋo kʋlʋ maama o na kɩ tɩn yɩ lanyɩranɩ mʋ. O paɩ zwa-kwarʋ joori tɩ ni, yɩ mɩmaarʋ dɩ waɩ tɩ ŋɔɔna.».

eng: In those days there was another large crowd with nothing to eat. Jesus called his disciples and told them.
local: Kantʋ maŋa kam nɩ kɔgɔ zanzan daa ma joori kʋ gilimi daanɩ Zezi tee nɩ. Ba maa ba jɩgɩ kʋlʋkʋlʋ sɩ ba di. Zezi ma bǝŋi o karabiǝ bam yɩ ba ba o te. O ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ:.

eng: I feel sorry for the crowd because they have been with me for three days and have nothing to eat.
local: «Nɔɔna bantʋ ŋwaŋa jɩgɩ-nɩ, dɩ ba na tu ba wʋ a tee nɩ zɩm da yatɔ yɩ ba daa ba jɩgɩ wʋdiu sɩ ba di tɩn.

eng: If I send them away hungry to their homes, they wont have enough strength to travel, for some have come a long distance.
local: A na pɛ ba viiri yɩ ba wʋ di kʋlʋkʋlʋ, kana wʋ́ pa ba bǝri cwǝŋǝ nɩ, bɛŋwaanɩ ba badaara nuŋi yigǝ yigǝ mʋ ba ba amʋ te.».

eng: His disciples responded, How can anyone get enough food in this wilderness to satisfy these people.
local: O karabiǝ bam ma lǝr-o ba wɩ: «Dɩ́ nan wʋ́ kɩ ta mʋ dɩ́ na wʋdiu gaa kʋntʋ wʋnɩ sɩ kʋ yi ba maama dim?».

eng: Jesus asked, How much bread do you have? They said, Seven loaves.
local: O ma bwe-ba o wɩ: «Abam jɩgɩ dɩpwa yagra mʋ?» Ba ma lǝri ba wɩ: «Ya yɩ yarpɛ mʋ.».

eng: He told the crowd to sit on the ground. He took the seven loaves, gave thanks, broke them apart, and gave them to his disciples to distribute; and they gave the bread to the crowd.
local: Zezi laan ma ta dɩ kɔgɔ kʋm sɩ ba maama jǝni tɩga nɩ. O ma kwe dɩpwa yarpɛ yam yɩ o kɩ Wɛ le ya ŋwaanɩ. O ma ja-ya o fɔ fɔ o pa o karabiǝ bam, yɩ bantʋ dɩ laan joŋi ba ja vu ba pa nɔɔna bam.

eng: They also had a few fish. He said a blessing over them, then gave them to the disciples to hand out also.
local: Ba dɛɛn jɩgɩ kalǝŋ-balɛ fɩnfɩɩn ba wǝli da. Zezi ma kwe-sɩ o kɩ Wɛ le, yɩ o daarɩ o ta dɩ o karabiǝ bam sɩ ba kwe sɩntʋ dɩ ba maŋɩ ba pa nɔɔna bam maama.

eng: They ate until they were full. They collected seven baskets full of leftovers.
local: Ba maama ma di ba su ba daarɩ. Ba laan ma twɛ cicwǝǝru tɩlʋ na daarɩ tɩn ba su tɩtwarʋ tɩrpɛ.

eng: This was a crowd of about four thousand people! Jesus sent them away.
local: Balʋ dɛɛn na di kʋntʋ tɩn yɩ baara mʋrr-tɩna mʋ , kʋ wǝli dɩ kaana dɩ biǝ dɩ. Zezi laan ma pa kɔgɔ kʋm viiri.

eng: then got into a boat with his disciples and went over to the region of Dalmanutha.
local: O ma daarɩ o vu o zʋ naboro dɩ o karabiǝ bam. Ba ma vu ba yi jǝgǝ kalʋ yɩrɩ na yɩ Dalɩmanuta tɩn.

eng: The Pharisees showed up and began to argue with Jesus. To test him, they asked for a sign from heaven.
local: Farizɩan tiinǝ badonnǝ dɛɛn ma ba Zezi te ba ma puli ba magɩ kantɔgɔ dɩd-o, yɩ ba lagɩ sɩ ba maŋ-o ba nii. Ba ma ta dɩd-o, sɩ o kɩ wo-kɩnkagɩlɩ dɩlʋ na wʋ́ brɩ-ba nɩ o dam dɩm nuŋi Wɛ tee nɩ tɩn.

eng: With an impatient sigh, Jesus said, Why does this generation look for a sign? I assure you that no sign will be given to it.
local: O ma tiini o suuri o wʋnɩ, yɩ o ta o wɩ: «Bɛɛ mʋ yɩ zɩm nɔɔna bam lagɩ sɩ a kɩ wo-kɩnkagɩlɩ a brɩ-ba? A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, a bá kɩ wo-kɩnkagɩlɩ a brɩ-ba.».

eng: Leaving them, he got back in the boat and crossed to the other side of the lake.
local: O na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma yagɩ ba te, yɩ o joori o vu o zʋ naboro kʋm sɩ ba vu ba bɛ bubɛ dɩdɩm dɩm.

eng: Jesus disciples had forgotten to bring any bread, so they had only one loaf with them in the boat.
local: Ba dɛɛn na wʋ naboro kʋm wʋnɩ ba maa ve ba bɛ tɩn, Zezi karabiǝ bam ya swe sɩ ba ja dɩpɛ ba wǝli da, dɩpɛ dɩdʋa yɩranɩ mʋ ba ya jɩga.

eng: He gave them strict orders: Watch out and be on your guard for the yeast of the Pharisees as well as the yeast of Herod.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Fɔgɩ-na á cɩ á tɩtɩ sɩ á yɩ pa Farizɩan tiinǝ bam dɩ Pɛ Erɔdɩ dɩ abam ba dabɩlɩ dɩm.».

eng: The disciples discussed this among themselves, He said this because we have no bread.
local: O na tagɩ dɩ ba kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma ŋɔɔnɩ daanɩ ba wɩ: «Kʋ yɩ dɩpɛ taanɩ mʋ o tɛa, dɩ́ na ba jɩgɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ.».

eng: Jesus knew what they were discussing and said, Why are you talking about the fact that you dont have any bread? Dont you grasp what has happened? Dont you understand? Are your hearts so resistant to what God is doing?.
local: Zezi ma lwarɩ ba na jɩgɩ kʋlʋ ba ŋɔɔnɩ tɩn, yɩ o wɩ: «Bɛɛ mʋ yɩ á ŋɔɔnɩ daanɩ á wɩ, á na ba jɩgɩ dɩpɛ tɩn mʋ te a ŋɔɔnɩ kʋntʋ? Abam ta wʋ lwarɩ Wɛ na jɩgɩ dam te tɩn na? Á wʋbʋŋa tɩgɩ mʋ na?.

eng: Dont you have eyes? Why cant you see? Dont you have ears? Why cant you hear? Dont you remember?.
local: Abam jɩgɩ yiǝ yɩ ya ba naɩ Wɛ cɩga kam. Á jɩgɩ zwa yɩ á ba ni Wɛ taanɩ dɩm kuri.

eng: When I broke five loaves of bread for those five thousand people, how many baskets full of leftovers did you gather? They answered, Twelve.
local: Abam maŋɩ á swe a dɛɛn na kwe dɩpwa yanu mʋ a fɔ fɔ a pa baara mʋrr-tɩnu tɩm yɩ ba di na? Ba na di ba ti tɩn, abam twɛ cicwǝǝru tɩm á su tɩtwarʋ tɩgra mʋ?» Ba ma lǝr-o ba wɩ: «Tɩtwarʋ fugǝ-tɩle.».

eng: And when I broke seven loaves of bread for those four thousand people, how many baskets full of leftovers did you gather? They answered, Seven.
local: O daa ma bwe-ba o wɩ: «A daa ta na kwe dɩpwa yarpɛ yam a fɔ fɔ a pa baara mʋrr-tɩna tɩm yɩ ba di tɩn, á twɛ cicwǝǝru tɩm á su tɩtwarʋ tɩgra mʋ?» Ba ma lǝr-o ba wɩ: «Tɩtwarʋ tɩrpɛ.».

eng: Jesus said to them, And you still dont understand.
local: O laan ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Abam nan daa ta ba ni kʋ kuri mʋ na?».

eng: Jesus and his disciples came to Bethsaida. Some people brought a blind man to Jesus and begged him to touch and heal him.
local: Ba dɛɛn ma vu ba yi Betisayida. Nɔɔna badonnǝ ma ja lilwe ba ja vu Zezi te, yɩ ba loor-o sɩ o ma o jɩŋa o dwe-o, sɩ o na yazurǝ.

eng: Taking the blind mans hand, Jesus led him out of the village. After spitting on his eyes and laying his hands on the man, he asked him, Do you see anything.
local: O ma ja lilwe wʋm jɩŋa o vaŋɩ o ja nuŋi tɩʋ kʋm daa. O ma twɩ lileeru o kɩ o yiǝ nɩ, yɩ o daŋɩ o jɩa lilwe wʋm baŋa nɩ. O laan ma bwe-o o wɩ: «Nmʋ naɩ woŋo na?».

eng: The man looked up and said, I see people. They look like trees, only they are walking around.
local: Lilwe wʋm ma puri o yiǝ o nii, yɩ o wɩ: «A naɩ nɔɔna mʋ. Ba nan nyɩ dɩ tweeru mʋ te yɩ ba veǝ.».

eng: Then Jesus placed his hands on the mans eyes again. He looked with his eyes wide open, his sight was restored, and he could see everything clearly.
local: Zezi daa ma daŋɩ o jɩa nɔɔnʋ wʋm yiǝ nɩ. O laan ma fin o yiǝ yam, yɩ ya joori ya naɩ. O maa waɩ o naɩ woŋo maama lanyɩranɩ.

eng: Then Jesus sent him home, saying, Dont go into the village.
local: Zezi laan ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ, o yɔɔrɩ o vu sɔŋɔ, sɩ o daa yɩ joori o zʋ tɩʋ kʋm wʋ.

eng: Jesus and his disciples went into the villages near Caesarea Philippi. On the way he asked his disciples, Who do people say that I am.
local: Zezi dɩ o karabiǝ bam daa ma kɛ, ba vu ba yi Sezaarɩ kʋlʋ na yɩ Filipi tɩʋ tɩn. Ba na maa ve tɩn, o ma bwe-ba o wɩ: «Nɔɔna yǝni ba tɛ ba wɩ, amʋ yɩ wɔɔ mʋ?».

eng: They told him, Some say John the Baptist, others Elijah, and still others one of the prophets.
local: Ba ma lǝri ba wɩ: «Badaara tɛ ba wɩ, nmʋ yɩ Zan wʋlʋ dɛɛn na miisi nɔɔna na wʋnɩ tɩn mʋ. Babam dɩ maa tɛ ba wɩ, nmʋ yɩ Wɛ nijoŋnu Eli mʋ. Badonnǝ dɩ maa tɛ ba wɩ, nmʋ yɩ faŋa faŋa Wɛ nijoŋnǝ bam dɩdʋa mʋ.».

eng: He asked them, And what about you? Who do you say that I am? Peter answered, You are the Christ.
local: O laan ma bwe-ba o wɩ: «Kʋ nan na yɩ abam, abam bʋŋɩ sɩ amʋ yɩ wɔɔ mʋ?» Pɩyɛɛrɩ ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «Nmʋ yɩ Krisi wʋm Wɛ na tʋŋɩ tɩn mʋ.».

eng: Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone about him.
local: Zezi laan ma kaanɩ-ba sɩ ba yɩ ta kʋntʋ ba brɩ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ.

eng: Then Jesus began to teach his disciples: The Human One must suffer many things and be rejected by the elders, chief priests, and the legal experts, and be killed, and then, after three days, rise from the dead.
local: Kʋ na zɩgɩ kantʋ maŋa kam nɩ, Zezi ma ŋɔɔnɩ o brɩ-ba o wɩ: «Amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm maŋɩ sɩ a na yaara zanzan. Zwifǝ nakwa bam dɩ ba kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam dɩ ba cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ bam mʋ wʋ́ culi amʋ. Baá gʋ-nɩ, yɩ da yatɔ dɛ nɩ a laan wʋ́ joori a bi.».

eng: He said this plainly. But Peter took hold of Jesus and, scolding him, began to correct him.
local: O maa ŋɔɔnɩ kʋntʋ jaja o brɩ-ba. Pɩyɛɛrɩ ma ja-o o vu daa, yɩ o kaan-o o wɩ, o yɩ taa ŋɔɔnɩ kʋntʋ doŋ.

eng: Jesus turned and looked at his disciples, then sternly corrected Peter: Get behind me, Satan. You are not thinking Gods thoughts but human thoughts.
local: Zezi ma guni o nii o karabiǝ bam seeni, yɩ o bagɩ Pɩyɛɛrɩ o wɩ: «Sʋtaanɩ, ve daa a tee nɩ! Nmʋ wʋbʋŋa yam yɩ nabiinǝ nyɩm mʋ, sɩ ya ba tɔgɩ Wɛ na lagɩ te tɩn.».

eng: After calling the crowd together with his disciples, Jesus said to them, All who want to come after me must say no to themselves, take up their cross, and follow me.
local: Zezi laan ma bǝŋi nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm maama dɩ o karabiǝ bam sɩ ba ba o te. O ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ maama na lagɩ sɩ o taa tɔgɩ amʋ kwaga tɩn, kʋntʋ tu maŋɩ sɩ o vɩn o tɩtɩ wʋbʋŋa sɩ o sɛ yaara nɩ wʋlʋ na zɩŋɩ tʋʋn-dagara o maa ve o tʋʋnɩ jǝgǝ te tɩn, sɩ o laan taa tɔgɩ-nɩ.

eng: All who want to save their lives will lose them. But all who lose their lives because of me and because of the good news will save them.
local: Wʋlʋ maama nan na lagɩ sɩ o yɩrɩ o ŋwɩa tɩn, kʋntʋ tu wʋ́ ga-ka mʋ. Kʋ daarɩ, wʋlʋ maama na sɛ sɩ o ga o ŋwɩa amʋ ŋwaanɩ dɩ Wɛ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam ŋwaanɩ, kʋntʋ tu wʋ́ na ŋwɩa Wɛ tee nɩ.

eng: Why would people gain the whole world but lose their lives?.
local: Nɔɔnʋ nan na nɛ lʋgʋ baŋa wǝǝnu tɩm maama, yɩ o laan na gɛ ŋwɩa Wɛ tee nɩ, bɛɛ nyɔɔrɩ mʋ o nɛa?.

eng: What will people give in exchange for their lives?.
local: Kʋlʋkʋlʋ nan tǝrǝ nɔɔnʋ na wʋ́ wanɩ o ma lǝni o ŋwɩa Wɛ tee nɩ.

eng: Whoever is ashamed of me and my words in this unfaithful and sinful generation, the Human One will be ashamed of that person when he comes in the Fathers glory with the holy angels.
local: Kʋntʋ tɩn, nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ maama na kwarɩ cavɩɩra amʋ ŋwaanɩ dɩ amʋ taanɩ dɩm ŋwaanɩ zɩm nɔɔna bam tɩtarɩ nɩ, ba na yɩ nɔn-balwaarʋ yɩ ba yigǝ tǝrǝ dɩ Wɛ tɩn, amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm dɩ wʋ́ kwarɩ kʋntʋ tu cavɩɩra, maŋa kam a na wʋ́ joori a ba lʋgʋ baŋa dɩ a Ko Wɛ paarɩ-zulǝ dɩ DƖ malɛsɩ sɩm tɩn.».

eng: Jesus left that place and came to his hometown. His disciples followed him.
local: Zezi dɛɛn ma nuŋi jǝgǝ kam kʋntʋ nɩ, yɩ o joori o vu o tɩtɩ tɩʋ. O karabiǝ bam dɩ tɔg-o ba vu.

eng: On the Sabbath, he began to teach in the synagogue. Many who heard him were surprised. Where did this man get all this? Whats this wisdom hes been given? What about the powerful acts accomplished through him?.
local: Zwifǝ bam siun dɛ na yi tɩn, o ma vu o zʋ ba Wɛ-digǝ kam yɩ o puli sɩ o brɩ-ba Wɛ taanɩ. Nɔɔna zanzan maa wʋra yɩ ba cǝgi o zaasɩm dɩm. Ba na ni tɩn, kʋ ma sʋ-ba lanyɩranɩ. Ba maa tɛ ba wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ kɩ ta mʋ o lwarɩ wǝǝnu tɩntʋ doŋ? O kɩ ta mʋ o jɩgɩ swan tɩntʋ, yɩ o jɩgɩ dam o ma kɩ wo-kɩnkagɩla yantʋ?.

eng: Isnt this the carpenter? Isnt he Marys son and the brother of James, Joses, Judas, and Simon? Arent his sisters here with us? They were repulsed by him and fell into sin.
local: Kʋ daɩ wʋm mʋ yɩ da-lwarʋ wʋm na? Wʋm mʋ daɩ Mari bu wʋm na? Kʋ daɩ wʋm nyaana mʋ yɩ Zakɩ dɩ Zʋzɛfʋ dɩ Zudasɩ dɩ Simɔn na? Kʋ daɩ wʋm nakwa mʋ wʋ dɩbam tɩtarɩ nɩ tɩn na?» Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ ba dɛɛn wʋ sɛ nɩ Zezi sɩɩnɩ o nuŋi Wɛ te.

eng: Jesus said to them, Prophets are honored everywhere except in their own hometowns, among their relatives, and in their own households.
local: O laan ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ na yɩ Wɛ nijoŋnu, nɔɔna yǝni ba pa-o zulǝ lanyɩranɩ, kʋ daarɩ o tɩtɩ tɩʋ tiinǝ dɩ o sɔŋɔ tiinǝ dɩ o cʋrrʋ nan bá nɩg-o.».

eng: He was unable to do any miracles there, except that he placed his hands on a few sick people and healed them.
local: O dɛɛn ma wʋ kɩ wo-kɩnkagɩla ba tee nɩ. Kʋ na daɩ o na kwe o jɩŋa o daŋɩ yawɩɩna fɩnfɩɩn baŋa nɩ o pa ba na yazurǝ tɩn.

eng: He was appalled by their disbelief. Then Jesus traveled through the surrounding villages teaching.
local: Kʋ ma sʋ Zezi dɩ ba na wʋ kɩ ba wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩd-o tɩn. Zezi dɛɛn maa tʋlɩ tɩ-balwa bam o karɩ o brɩ nɔɔna Wɛ cɩga kam.

eng: He called for the Twelve and sent them out in pairs. He gave them authority over unclean spirits.
local: O laan ma bǝŋi o karabiǝ fugǝ-bale bam sɩ ba ba o te. O ma lɩ-ba bale bale sɩ o tʋŋɩ-ba, yɩ o pa-ba dam sɩ ba wanɩ ba zǝli ciciri nɔɔna yɩra nɩ.

eng: He instructed them to take nothing for the journey except a walking stick—no bread, no bags, and no money in their belts.
local: O ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Á na maa ve, á yɩ zaŋɩ á kwe kʋlʋkʋlʋ á wǝli da á ja á vu, kʋ na daɩ nacǝgǝ yɩranɩ. Á nan yɩ kwe wʋdiu naa tampɔgɔ. Á yɩ kwe sǝbu á kɩ gwar-fwɛɛla nɩ á wǝli da.

eng: He told them to wear sandals but not to put on two shirts.
local: Zʋ-na natra, sɩ á yɩ kwe gwaarʋ tɩle tɩle.

eng: He said, Whatever house you enter, remain there until you leave that place.
local: Á na ve á zʋ sɔŋɔ kʋlʋ á pǝni da, sɩ á taá zʋʋrɩ daanɩ sɩ kʋ ba kʋ yi á viirim maŋa.

eng: If a place doesnt welcome you or listen to you, as you leave, shake the dust off your feet as a witness against them.
local: Kʋ daarɩ, á na yi tɩʋ kʋlʋ yɩ ba na wʋ sɛ sɩ ba jeeri abam lanyɩranɩ, naa ba na wʋ cǝgi abam taanɩ, sɩ á nuŋi tɩʋ kʋm kʋntʋ nɩ. Á na maa viirǝ, sɩ á pɩɩsɩ á nɛ fogo á yagɩ da, sɩ kʋ pa nɔɔna bam lwarɩ nɩ ba wʋ kɩ lanyɩranɩ.».

eng: So they went out and proclaimed that people should change their hearts and lives.
local: O na tagɩ kʋntʋ dɩ ba o ti tɩn, ba laan ma nuŋi ba vu ba tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝrǝ, ba brɩ nɔɔna nɩ ba maŋɩ sɩ ba lǝni ba wʋrʋ ba yagɩ kǝm-balwaarʋ tɩm.

eng: They cast out many demons, and they anointed many sick people with olive oil and healed them.
local: Ba ma zǝli ciciri zanzan nɔɔna yɩra nɩ. Ba ma kwǝri ba kwe nugǝ ba ma dwe yawɩɩna zanzan, yɩ ba na yazurǝ.

eng: Herod the king heard about these things, because the name of Jesus had become well-known. Some were saying, John the Baptist has been raised from the dead, and this is why miraculous powers are at work through him.
local: Pɛ wʋdoŋ dɛɛn mʋ wʋra, o yɩrɩ mʋ Erɔdɩ. O ma ba o ni Zezi ŋwa, nɔɔna zanzan na jɩgɩ o taanɩ ba ŋɔɔnɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Nɔɔna badonnǝ maa tɛ ba wɩ: «Kʋ yɩ Zan wʋlʋ na miisi nɔɔna na wʋnɩ tɩn mʋ joori o bi o yagɩ tʋʋnɩ. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ o jɩgɩ dam yɩ o waɩ wo-kɩnkagɩla o kɩa.».

eng: Others were saying, He is Elijah. Still others were saying, He is a prophet like one of the ancient prophets.
local: Nɔɔna badaara maa wɩ: «Kʋ yɩ Wɛ nijoŋnu Eli mʋ tuǝ.» Badaara dɩ ta maa wɩ: «O yɩ Wɛ nijoŋnu mʋ, nɩnɛɛnɩ balʋ na tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝrǝ faŋa faŋa tɩn.».

eng: But when Herod heard these rumors, he said, John, whom I beheaded, has been raised to life.
local: Kʋ daarɩ, Pɛ Erɔdɩ na ni Zezi ŋwa tɩn, o ma ta o wɩ: «Kʋʋ́ ta yɩ Zan wʋlʋ amʋ na pɛ ba go o yuu tɩn mʋ joori o bi o yagɩ tʋʋnɩ.».

eng: He said this because Herod himself had arranged to have John arrested and put in prison because of Herodias, the wife of Herods brother Philip. Herod had married her.
local: O dɛɛn tagɩ kʋntʋ, bɛŋwaanɩ o ya wʋ kɩ Zan lanyɩranɩ. O pɛ ba ja Zan ba vɔ ba kɩ pɩɩna digǝ nɩ. Kʋ dɛɛn kɩ tɩntʋ doŋ mʋ: Erɔdɩ ya di kaanɩ wʋdoŋ, o yɩrɩ mʋ Erɔdɩadɩ. Kaanɩ wʋm dɛɛn yɩ o zɩmbaarʋ Filipi kaanɩ mʋ.

eng: but John told Herod, Its against the law for you to marry your brothers wife.
local: Zan dɛɛn ma ta dɩd-o nɩ, kʋ culǝ sɩ o di o zɩmbaarʋ kaanɩ yɩ o ta ŋwɩ.

eng: So Herodias had it in for John. She wanted to kill him, but she couldnt.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ Erɔdɩadɩ culi Zan. O maa lagɩ sɩ ba gʋ-o, yɩ o wʋ nɛ cwǝŋǝ.

eng: This was because Herod respected John. He regarded him as a righteous and holy person, so he protected him. Johns words greatly confused Herod, yet he enjoyed listening to him.
local: bɛŋwaanɩ Erɔdɩ dɛɛn kwarɩ Zan. O maa ye nɩ Zan yɩ wʋ-poŋo tu yɩ o tɛ cɩga. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ Erɔdɩ ma fɔg-o o nii lanyɩranɩ, sɩ kʋlʋkʋlʋ yɩ kɩ-o. O maa yǝni o cǝgi o taanɩ dɩ wʋpolo, yɩ kʋ pa o wʋbʋŋa vugimi.

eng: Finally, the time was right. It was on one of Herods birthdays, when he had prepared a feast for his high-ranking officials and military officers and Galilees leading residents.
local: Erɔdɩadɩ dɛɛn laan ma ba o na cwǝŋǝ sɩ ba gʋ Zan. Pɛ Erɔdɩ lʋra maŋa na tu ka yi tɩn, o ma kɩ candiǝ yɩ o bǝŋi o tɩʋ kʋm dɩdɛɛra bam dɩ pamaŋna yigǝ tiinǝ, dɩ nadunǝ balʋ na zʋʋrɩ Galile nɩ tɩn, sɩ ba tɔgɩ ba di candiǝ kam.

eng: Herods daughter Herodias came in and danced, thrilling Herod and his dinner guests. The king said to the young woman, Ask me whatever you wish, and I will give it to you.
local: Ba na wʋra ba di candiǝ kʋntʋ tɩn, Erɔdɩadɩ bukɔ laan ma zaŋɩ o zʋ o sa o brɩ vǝrǝ bam. Kʋ ma pa pɛ wʋm dɩ o vǝrǝ bam wʋ poli lanyɩranɩ. Pɛ wʋm ma ta dɩ bukɔ wʋm o wɩ: «Woŋo kʋlʋ maama nmʋ na lagɩ tɩn, amʋ wʋ́ pa-m.».

eng: Then he swore to her, Whatever you ask I will give to you, even as much as half of my kingdom.
local: O ta ma ta o du o wɩ: «Aá pa-m woŋo kʋlʋ maama n na wʋ́ loori a tee nɩ tɩn. Kʋ na maŋɩ kʋ yɩ a tɩʋ kʋm cicoro dɩ, a ta wʋ́ pa-m.».

eng: She left the banquet hall and said to her mother, What should I ask for? John the Baptists head, Herodias replied.
local: Bukɔ wʋm na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma nuŋi o vu o nu te o bwe-o o wɩ: «Bɛɛ woŋo mʋ amʋ wʋ́ loori?» O ma lǝri o wɩ: «Ve n ta nɩ n lagɩ sɩ o go Zan yuu mʋ o pa-m.».

eng: Hurrying back to the ruler, she made her request: I want you to give me John the Baptists head on a plate, right this minute.
local: Bukɔ wʋm ma kɩ lɩla o joori o zʋ pɛ wʋm te, yɩ o ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «A lagɩ sɩ n go Zan yuu mʋ n kɩ zʋŋ-tanlaŋa wʋnɩ n pa-nɩ lele.».

eng: Although the king was upset, because of his solemn pledge and his guests, he didnt want to refuse her.
local: Pɛ Erɔdɩ na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, o wʋ ma tiini kʋ cɔgɩ zanzan. O nan na tagɩ o du vǝrǝ bam yigǝ nɩ tɩn, o maa ba lagɩ sɩ o vɩn.

eng: So he ordered a guard to bring Johns head. The guard went to the prison, cut off Johns head.
local: O ma kɩ lɩla o tʋŋɩ o pamaŋnʋ dɩdʋa sɩ o vu o go Zan yuu kʋm o ja ba. Nɔɔnʋ wʋm ma vu pɩɩna digǝ kam o go Zan yuu kʋm.

eng: brought his head on a plate, and gave it to the young woman, and she gave it to her mother.
local: O ma kwe-kʋ o kɩ zʋŋ-tanlaŋa wʋnɩ o ja ba o pa bukɔ wʋm. O ma joŋi o ja vu o pa o nu.

eng: When Johns disciples heard what had happened, they came and took his dead body and laid it in a tomb.
local: Zan karabiǝ bam na lwarɩ kʋlʋ na kɩ tɩn, ba ma zaŋɩ ba vu ba kwe o yɩra yam ba ja vu ba kɩ.

eng: The apostles returned to Jesus and told him everything they had done and taught.
local: Zezi karabiǝ fugǝ-bale bam ma joori ba ba o te. Ba ma ta ba brɩ-o kʋlʋ maama ba na kɩa, dɩ ba na brɩ nɔɔna Wɛ cɩga kam te tɩn.

eng: Many people were coming and going, so there was no time to eat. He said to the apostles, Come by yourselves to a secluded place and rest for a while.
local: Nɔɔna zanzan maa yǝni ba tui Zezi te, yɩ ba joori ba viirǝ. Ba na daga kʋntʋ tɩn, Zezi dɩ o karabiǝ bam daa warɩ ba na pwǝlǝ sɩ ba di wʋdiu dɩ. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ o ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Abam nan ba á yɩranɩ, sɩ dɩ́ vu gaa wʋ sɩ á wanɩ á sin fɩnfɩɩn.».

eng: They departed in a boat by themselves for a deserted place.
local: Ba laan ma vu ba zʋ naboro, sɩ ba vu je sɩlʋ na yɩ gaa wʋ tɩn ba yɩranɩ.

eng: Many people saw them leaving and recognized them, so they ran ahead from all the cities and arrived before them.
local: Ba na maa kɛ tɩn, nɔɔna zanzan ma na-ba yɩ ba lwarɩ-ba. Ba ma zaŋɩ ba nuŋi ba tɩɩnɩ dɩm maama nɩ, ba duri dɩ nɛ ba vu ba loori-ba jǝgǝ kalʋ ba na maa ve tɩn nɩ.

eng: When Jesus arrived and saw a large crowd, he had compassion on them because they were like sheep without a shepherd. Then he began to teach them many things.
local: Zezi ma nuŋi naboro kʋm wʋnɩ. O laan ma na nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm na tu kʋ wʋra. Ba ŋwaŋa ma zʋ-o lanyɩranɩ, bɛŋwaanɩ ba nyɩ dɩ peeni sɩlʋ na ba jɩgɩ nayɩrʋ sɩ o taa nii sɩ baŋa nɩ tɩn. O laan ma puli o wʋra o brɩ-ba wǝǝnu zanzan.

eng: Late in the day, his disciples came to him and said, This is an isolated place, and its already late in the day.
local: Kʋ na tiini kʋ daanɩ yɩ wɛ ba wʋra dɩ zʋʋrɩ tɩn, o karabiǝ bam ma vu ba ta dɩd-o ba wɩ: «Maŋa kam wʋra ka kɛa, yɩ jǝgǝ kalʋ dɩbam na wʋra tɩn yɩ gaa wʋ mʋ. Wʋdiu tǝri yo.

eng: Send them away so that they can go to the surrounding countryside and villages and buy something to eat for themselves.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ, pa nɔɔna bam vu sam wʋnɩ ba yǝgi wʋdiu ba di.».

eng: He replied, You give them something to eat. But they said to him, Should we go off and buy bread worth almost eight months pay and give it to them to eat.
local: O ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Abam tɩtɩ pa-ba wʋdiu sɩ ba di.» Ba ma ta ba wɩ: «Dɩ́ na lagɩ dɩ́ yǝgi dɩpɛ sɩ dɩ yi ba maama dim, kʋʋ́ yi nɩnɛɛnɩ sǝbu-dala biǝ yale mʋ . Dɩ́ wʋ́ wanɩ dɩ́ yǝgi kʋntʋ na?».

eng: He said to them, How much bread do you have? Take a look. After checking, they said, Five loaves of bread and two fish.
local: O laan ma bwe-ba o wɩ: «Abam jɩgɩ dɩpwa yagra mʋ? Ve-na á nii.» Ba na ve ba nii tɩn, ba ma joori ba ba, ba ta dɩd-o ba wɩ: «Ya yɩ dɩpwa yanu dɩ kale sɩle yɩranɩ mʋ.».

eng: Jesus left that place and went beyond the Jordan and into the region of Judea. Crowds gathered around him again and, as usual, he taught them.
local: Zezi laan ma zaŋɩ o nuŋi je sɩm kʋntʋ nɩ o vu o da Zude tɩ-niǝ yam. O ma vu o bɛ Zʋrdɛn bugǝ kam ni dɩdoŋ dɩm nɩ. Nɔn-kɔgɔ zanzan daa ma ba ba gilim-o. O ma ta brɩ-ba Wɛ cɩga kam, nɩ o na yǝni o kɩ te tɩn.

eng: He directed the disciples to seat all the people in groups as though they were having a banquet on the green grass.
local: Zezi laan ma pa o karabiǝ bam ni, sɩ ba pa nɔɔna bam pɔɔrɩ kɔgɔ kɔgɔ ba jǝni tɩga nɩ ga-lɩʋ kʋm baŋa nɩ.

eng: Some Pharisees came and, trying to test him, they asked, Does the Law allow a man to divorce his wife.
local: Farizɩan tiinǝ badaara dɛɛn ma zaŋɩ ba vu o te sɩ ba maŋ-o ba nii. Ba ma bwe-o ba wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ jɩgɩ cwǝŋǝ sɩ o vɩn o kaanɩ na?».

eng: They sat down in groups of hundreds and fifties.
local: Ba maama ma pɔɔrɩ yɩ ba jǝni, badaara kɔgɔ bi bi, badaara kɔgɔ fiinnu fiinnu.

eng: Jesus answered, What did Moses command you.
local: Zezi ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Moyisi dɛɛn pɛ abam culu kʋm o wɩ bɛɛ?».

eng: He took the five loaves and the two fish, looked up to heaven, blessed them, broke the loaves into pieces, and gave them to his disciples to set before the people. He also divided the two fish among them all.
local: Zezi laan ma kwe dɩpwa yanu yam dɩ kale sɩle sɩm, yɩ o kwǝni o yuu wɛɛnɩ o kɩ Wɛ le tɩ ŋwaanɩ. O ma fɔ fɔ dɩpwa yam o maŋɩ o pa o karabiǝ bam, sɩ ba laan joŋi-ya ba ja vu ba maŋɩ ba pa nɔɔna bam. O ma daarɩ o maŋɩ kale sɩle sɩm dɩ o pa ba maama sɩ ba di.

eng: They said, Moses allowed a man to write a divorce certificate and to divorce his wife.
local: Ba ma lǝr-o ba wɩ: «Moyisi pɛ ni, nɩ nɔɔnʋ jɩgɩ cwǝŋǝ sɩ o pʋpʋnɩ tɔnɔ o pa o kaanɩ, sɩ kʋ brɩ nɩ o vɩ-o mʋ.».

eng: Everyone ate until they were full.
local: Nɔɔna bam maama ma di ba su ba daarɩ.

eng: Jesus said to them, He wrote this commandment for you because of your unyielding hearts.
local: Zezi ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Moyisi dɛɛn pʋpʋnɩ kʋntʋ o pa abam, á wʋrʋ na dana tɩn ŋwaanɩ mʋ.

eng: They filled twelve baskets with the leftover pieces of bread and fish.
local: Zezi karabiǝ bam ma pɛ dɩpwa dɩ kale cicwǝǝru tɩlʋ na daarɩ tɩn ba su tɩtwarʋ fugǝ-tɩle.

eng: At the beginning of creation, God made them male and female .
local: Kʋ daarɩ, pulim nɩ Wɛ na kɩ nabiinǝ tɩn, DƖ kɩ-ba sɩ ba taa yɩ baarʋ dɩ kaanɩ mʋ.

eng: About five thousand had eaten.
local: Nɔɔna balʋ dɛɛn na di kʋntʋ tɩn yɩ baara mʋrr-tɩnu mʋ , kʋ wǝli dɩ kaana dɩ biǝ dɩ.

eng: Because of this, a man should leave his father and mother and be joined together with his wife.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ, nɔɔnʋ na di kaanɩ, o maŋɩ sɩ o pɔɔrɩ o tɩtɩ o nu dɩ o ko tee nɩ, sɩ o daarɩ o pa o dɩ o kaanɩ wʋm jǝni daanɩ.

eng: Right then, Jesus made his disciples get into a boat and go ahead to the other side of the lake, toward Bethsaida, while he dismissed the crowd.
local: Kʋntʋ kwaga nɩ Zezi ma da o pa o karabiǝ bam vu ba zʋ naboro kʋm, sɩ ba da yigǝ ba bɛ nɩnɩʋ kʋm ba vu ba yi Betisayida. Ba na maa kɛ tɩn, o ma pa kɔgɔ kʋm viiri.

eng: and the two will be one flesh. So they are no longer two but one flesh.
local: Kʋntʋ wʋ́ pa ba laan ji nɔɔnʋ dɩdʋa. Ba daa daɩ nɔɔna bale, ba laan yɩ nɔɔnʋ dɩdʋa mʋ.

eng: After saying good-bye to them, Jesus went up onto a mountain to pray.
local: O na pɛ ba viiri tɩn, o ma vu o di piu yuu sɩ o warɩ Wɛ.

eng: Therefore, humans must not pull apart what God has put together.
local: Wɛ na kɩ nɔɔna balʋ daanɩ tɩn, nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ daa wʋ maŋɩ sɩ o pɔɔrɩ-ba daanɩ.».

eng: Evening came and the boat was in the middle of the lake, but he was alone on the land.
local: Tɩga ma ba ka yi. Naboro kʋm maa wʋ nɩnɩʋ kʋm tɩtarɩ nɩ, yɩ Zezi ta wʋ buburu kʋm nɩ o yɩranɩ.

eng: Inside the house, the disciples asked him again about this.
local: Zezi dɩ o karabiǝ bam laan ma joori ba vu sɔŋɔ. Ba na zʋ tɩn, ba ma bwe-o o taanɩ dɩm kuri.

eng: He saw his disciples struggling. They were trying to row forward, but the wind was blowing against them. Very early in the morning, he came to them, walking on the lake. He intended to pass by them.
local: O ma na o karabiǝ bam na tiini ba kwaanɩ ba yɛ yɩ kʋ cɛ ba yɩra nɩ, vu-fɔrɔ na zaŋɩ kʋ jeeri-ba tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Tɩga na wʋra ka pʋʋrɩ tɩn, Zezi ma zaŋɩ o ve na bam baŋa nɩ o maa ve ba te. O ma vu o lagɩ o gaalɩ-ba o kɛ.

eng: He said to them, Whoever divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery against her.
local: O ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ na vɩn o kaanɩ yɩ o daarɩ o di kaanɩ wʋdoŋ, kʋntʋ tu kǝm dɩm dɩ ka-bwǝŋǝ yɩ bɩdwɩ mʋ.

eng: When they saw him walking on the lake, they thought he was a ghost and they screamed.
local: Ba na nɛ-o o ve na bam baŋa nɩ tɩn, ba ya bʋŋɩ nɩ kʋ yɩ ciru mʋ. Ba ma kaasɩ baŋa baŋa.

eng: and if a wife divorces her husband and marries another, she commits adultery.
local: Kaanɩ dɩ na vɩn o barʋ yɩ o vu o zʋ barʋ wʋdoŋ, o dɩ kǝm dɩm dɩ bwǝŋǝ yɩ bɩdwɩ mʋ.».

eng: Seeing him was terrifying to all of them. Just then he spoke to them, Be encouraged! Its me. Dont be afraid.
local: bɛŋwaanɩ ba maama nɛ-o yɩ fʋʋnɩ zʋ-ba zanzan. Zezi laan ma ŋɔɔnɩ dɩ ba o wɩ: «Pa-na á wʋrʋ kɩ dam, sɩ kʋ yɩ amʋ mʋ. Á yɩ pa fʋʋnɩ taa jɩgɩ abam.».

eng: People were bringing children to Jesus so that he would bless them. But the disciples scolded them.
local: Nɔɔna badonnǝ maa jaanɩ biǝ ba tui Zezi te sɩ o daŋɩ o jɩa ba baŋa nɩ. O karabiǝ bam ma zaŋɩ ba bagɩ-ba sɩ ba yɩ taa kɩ kʋntʋ.

eng: He got into the boat, and the wind settled down. His disciples were so baffled they were beside themselves.
local: O laan ma vu o zʋ naboro kʋm wʋ. O na yi ba te kʋntʋ tɩn, viu kʋm ma da kʋ kwǝri. Kʋ ma tiini kʋ sʋ-ba.

eng: When Jesus saw this, he grew angry and said to them, Allow the children to come to me. Dont forbid them, because Gods kingdom belongs to people like these children.
local: Zezi na nɛ ba na kɩ te tɩn, o banɩ ma zaŋɩ yɩ o ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Yagɩ-na sɩ biǝ bam taa tui amʋ te, sɩ á yɩ zaŋɩ á taá cʋ-ba. Bɛŋwaanɩ balʋ na nyɩ nɩ bu-balwa te tɩn mʋ maŋɩ sɩ ba zʋ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋ.

eng: Thats because they hadnt understood about the loaves. Their minds had been closed so that they resisted Gods ways.
local: bɛŋwaanɩ Zezi na maŋɩ dɩpɛ dɩm o pa nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm yɩ ba di tɩn, ba wʋ ni wo-kɩnkagɩlɩ dɩm kʋntʋ kuri, Wɛ cɩga kam na wʋ wanɩ ka zʋ ba wʋbʋŋa tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: I assure you that whoever doesnt welcome Gods kingdom like a child will never enter it.
local: A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, nɔɔnʋ na wʋ sɛ sɩ Wɛ taa te-o nɩ bu-balaŋa te, o bá na cwǝŋǝ o zʋ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋ.».

eng: When Jesus and his disciples had crossed the lake, they landed at Gennesaret, anchored the boat.
local: Ba ma vu ba bɛ ba yi bubɛ dɩdɩm dɩm. Ba ma ja naboro kʋm ba ja nuŋi ba zɩgɩ da. Ba na yi je sɩlʋ tɩn yɩrɩ mʋ Zenezarɛtɩ.

eng: Then he hugged the children and blessed them.
local: Zezi na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, o laan ma ja ja biǝ bam o kɩ o ja-kukoro nɩ. O ma daŋɩ o jɩa ba baŋa nɩ o warɩ Wɛ o pa-ba.

eng: and came ashore. People immediately recognized Jesus.
local: Ba na nuŋi naboro kʋm wʋnɩ tɩn, nɔɔna bam ma lwarɩ Zezi.

eng: As Jesus continued down the road, a man ran up, knelt before him, and asked, Good Teacher, what must I do to obtain eternal life.
local: Zezi ma zaŋɩ o maa kɛa. Nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ ma duri o vu o yi o te. O ma kuni doonǝ o yigǝ nɩ yɩ o wɩ: «Karanyɩna, n yɩ nɔn-ŋʋm. Nan brɩ-nɩ kʋlʋ a na wʋ́ kɩ, sɩ a wanɩ a na ŋwɩa kalʋ na ba ti tɩn Wɛ tee nɩ.».

eng: and ran around that whole region bringing sick people on their mats to wherever they heard he was.
local: Ba laan ma duri je sɩm kʋntʋ maama nɩ, ba vu ba ta ba pa nɔɔna lwarɩ. Mɛ maama ba na lwarɩ nɩ o tu da tɩn, ba ma kwe balʋ na ba jɩgɩ yazurǝ tɩn ba zɩŋɩ gungwǝǝlu wʋnɩ ba ja vu o te.

eng: Jesus replied, Why do you call me good? No one is good except the one God.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Bɛŋwaanɩ mʋ n bǝŋi-nɩ nɩ nɔn-ŋʋm? Nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ tǝrǝ o na yɩ nɔn-ŋʋm, kʋ na daɩ Wɛ DƖ yɩranɩ.

eng: Wherever he went—villages, cities, or farming communities—they would place the sick in the marketplaces and beg him to allow them to touch even the hem of his clothing. Everyone who touched him was healed.
local: O na yǝni o ve je sɩlʋ maama tɩn, kʋ na yɩ tɩ-balɛ naa tɩ-kamunǝ, nɔɔna yǝni ba ja yawɩɩna mʋ ba nuŋi ba jeer-o yaga ni nɩ, yɩ ba loor-o sɩ o pa-ba cwǝŋǝ sɩ ba dwe o gɔrɔ kʋm ni má. Balʋ maama na dwe o gɔrɔ kʋm kʋntʋ tɩn dɛɛn nɛ yazurǝ.

eng: You know the commandments: Dont commit murder. Dont commit adultery. Dont steal. Dont give false testimony . Dont cheat. Honor your father and mother .
local: Nmʋ maŋɩ n ye Wɛ cullu tɩm na brɩ kʋlʋ tɩn. Yɩ zaŋɩ n gʋ nɔɔnʋ. Yɩ zaŋɩ n pǝni dɩ n doŋ kaanɩ. Yɩ ŋɔnɩ. Yɩ zaŋɩ n fɔ vwan n pa n doŋ. Yɩ pini n doŋ. Ta n zuli n nu dɩ n ko.».

eng: Teacher, he responded, Ive kept all of these things since I was a boy.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋm ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Karanyɩna, kʋ na sɩŋɩ a biini nɩ tɩn, a maŋɩ a sɛ cullu tɩntʋ maama.».

eng: Jesus looked at him carefully and loved him. He said, You are lacking one thing. Go, sell what you own, and give the money to the poor. Then you will have treasure in heaven. And come, follow me.
local: Zezi ma nii-o dɩ sono, yɩ o ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Kʋ nan ta gɛ woŋo dɩdʋa mʋ sɩ n kɩ. Ve sɔŋɔ n kwe woŋo kʋlʋ maama n na jɩgɩ tɩn n yǝgi, sɩ n laan kwe tɩ sǝbu kʋm n ma n wǝli yinigǝ tiinǝ. Kʋntʋ wʋ́ pa n ta n jɩgɩ jɩjɩgɩrʋ zanzan Wɛ-sɔŋɔ nɩ. Nmʋ na kɩ kʋntʋ, sɩ n laan ba n ta n tɔgɩ-nɩ.».

eng: But the man was dismayed at this statement and went away saddened, because he had many possessions.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋm na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, o wʋ ma cɔgɩ zanzan yɩ o yigǝ nywanɩ, o jɩjɩgɩrʋ tɩm na tiini tɩ daga tɩn ŋwaanɩ. O ma yagɩ Zezi yɩ o viiri.

eng: Looking around, Jesus said to his disciples, It will be very hard for the wealthy to enter Gods kingdom.
local: Zezi laan ma pipiri o nii o karabiǝ bam seeni, yɩ o ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Nadunǝ na lagɩ sɩ ba zʋ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋ, kʋʋ́ ta tiini kʋ cana kʋ pa-ba.».

eng: His words startled the disciples, so Jesus told them again, Children, its difficult to enter Gods kingdom!.
local: O karabiǝ bam na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ ma sʋ-ba. Zezi daa ta ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «A biǝ-ba, nɔɔna na lagɩ sɩ ba zʋ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋ, kʋʋ́ ta tiini kʋ cana kʋ pa-ba.

eng: Its easier for a camel to squeeze through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter Gods kingdom.
local: Kʋʋ́ ta yɩ mwalɩ sɩ yogondi da gar-lwe bɔɔnɩ dɩ nuŋi kʋ dwe nadum na lagɩ sɩ o zʋ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋ.».

eng: They were shocked even more and said to each other, Then who can be saved.
local: Zezi na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ ma tiini kʋ sʋ o karabiǝ bam zanzan, yɩ ba tɛ daanɩ ba wɩ: «Kʋ nan na yɩ kʋntʋ, wɔɔ mʋ wʋ́ wanɩ o na ŋwɩa Wɛ tee nɩ?».

eng: Jesus looked at them carefully and said, Its impossible with human beings, but not with God. All things are possible for God.
local: Zezi ma nii-ba yɩ o wɩ: «Nabiinǝ mʋ bá wanɩ ba kɩ, sɩ Wɛ waɩ DƖ kɩa. Bɛŋwaanɩ Wɛ waɩ woŋo maama DƖ kɩa.».

eng: Peter said to him, Look, weve left everything and followed you.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ laan ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Nii. Dɩbam yagɩ dɩ́ woŋo maama mʋ, yɩ dɩ́ daarɩ dɩ́ tɔgɩ nmʋ.».

eng: Jesus said, I assure you that anyone who has left house, brothers, sisters, mother, father, children, or farms because of me and because of the good news.
local: Zezi ma lǝri o wɩ: «A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ maama na tagɩ amʋ ŋwaanɩ dɩ Wɛ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam ŋwaanɩ, o yagɩ o sɔŋɔ naa o nu-biǝ naa o nu dɩ o ko naa o biǝ naa o karɩ dɩ tɩn.

eng: will receive one hundred times as much now in this life—houses, brothers, sisters, mothers, children, and farms —and in the coming age, eternal life.
local: kʋntʋ tu wʋ́ ba o na kʋlʋ maama o na yagɩ tɩn kuni bi mʋ lʋgʋ kʋntʋ baŋa nɩ. Oó na sam dɩ nu-biǝ dɩ niinǝ dɩ biǝ dɩ karɩ, yɩ oó na yaara dɩ o wǝli da. Kʋ daarɩ maŋa kalʋ na wʋ́ ba tɩn, kʋntʋ tu wʋ́ na ŋwɩa kalʋ na ba ti tɩn Wɛ tee nɩ.

eng: But many who are first will be last. And many who are last will be first.
local: Balʋ na maŋɩ ba yɩ yigǝ tiinǝ tɩn zanzan wʋ́ joori ba ji kwaga tiinǝ. Kʋ daarɩ balʋ dɩ na maŋɩ ba yɩ kwaga tiinǝ tɩn zanzan wʋ́ ba ba ji yigǝ tiinǝ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋnɩ.».

eng: Jesus and his disciples were on the road, going up to Jerusalem, with Jesus in the lead. The disciples were amazed while the others following behind were afraid. Taking the Twelve aside again, he told them what was about to happen to him.
local: Zezi dɩ nɔɔna zanzan maa zaŋɩ ba wʋ cwǝŋǝ nɩ ba maa ve Zeruzalɛm. Zezi maa wʋ ba yigǝ nɩ o veǝ. O karabiǝ bam maa yɩ yǝǝu. Nɔɔna balʋ dɩ na tɔgɩ ba kwaga tɩn maa fʋmma. Zezi ma bǝŋi o karabiǝ fugǝ-bale bam daa nɩ, yɩ o ta o brɩ-ba kʋlʋ na lagɩ kʋ kɩ-o Zeruzalɛm nɩ tɩn.

eng: Look! he said. Were going up to Jerusalem. The Human One will be handed over to the chief priests and the legal experts. They will condemn him to death and hand him over to the Gentiles.
local: O maa ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Taá ye-na, dɩ́ na maa ve Zeruzalɛm tɩn, dáanɩ mʋ ba laan wʋ́ ja amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm ba kɩ Zwifǝ kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam dɩ ba cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ bam jɩŋa nɩ. Nɔɔna bam kʋntʋ wʋ́ di a taanɩ ba wɩ, kʋ maŋɩ sɩ a tɩ mʋ, yɩ ba daarɩ ba kɩ-nɩ dwi-gɛ tiinǝ bam jɩŋa nɩ.

eng: They will ridicule him, spit on him, torture him, and kill him. After three days, he will rise up.
local: Baá yáalɩ-nɩ ba mwanɩ, yɩ ba daarɩ ba twɩ lileeru ba yagɩ a yɩra nɩ. Baá magɩ-nɩ dɩ balaara, yɩ ba laan daarɩ ba gʋ-nɩ. Da yatɔ dɛ nɩ a nan wʋ́ joori a bi a yagɩ tʋʋnɩ.».

eng: James and John, Zebedees sons, came to Jesus and said, Teacher, we want you to do for us whatever we ask.
local: Zakɩ dɩ Zan balʋ na yɩ Zebede biǝ tɩn dɛɛn ma vu Zezi te ba ta dɩd-o ba wɩ: «Karanyɩna, popo, kɩ woŋo kʋlʋ dɩ́ na lagɩ tɩn n pa dɩbam.».

eng: What do you want me to do for you? he asked.
local: Zezi ma bwe-ba o wɩ: «Á lagɩ sɩ a kɩ bɛɛ mʋ a pa abam?».

eng: They said, Allow one of us to sit on your right and the other on your left when you enter your glory.
local: Ba ma lǝr-o ba wɩ: «Nmʋ na tu n ba n di n paarɩ dɩm, sɩ n pa dɩbam dɩdʋa jǝni n jazɩm nɩ sɩ dɩdʋa jǝni n jagwiǝ nɩ.».

eng: Jesus replied, You dont know what youre asking! Can you drink the cup I drink or receive the baptism I receive.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Abam tɩtɩ yǝri á na loori kʋlʋ tɩn. Abam wʋ́ wanɩ á tɔgɩ á yaarɩ, nɩ amʋ na lagɩ a ba a na yaara yalʋ tɩn na? Á wʋ́ wanɩ á yaarɩ á tɩ, nɩ a na lagɩ a yaarɩ a tɩ te tɩn na?».

eng: We can, they answered. Jesus said, You will drink the cup I drink and receive the baptism I receive.
local: Ba ma lǝr-o ba wɩ: «Ɛɛn, dɩ́ wʋ́ wanɩ.» Zezi laan ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Kʋ yɩ cɩga, abam nan wʋ́ sɩɩnɩ á tɔgɩ á yaarɩ amʋ yaara yam doŋ.

eng: but to sit at my right or left hand isnt mine to give. It belongs to those for whom it has been prepared.
local: Kʋ nan daɩ amʋ mʋ maŋɩ sɩ a kuri wʋlʋ na wʋ́ jǝni a jazɩm nɩ naa a jagwiǝ nɩ tɩn. Kʋ yɩ Baŋa-Wɛ mʋ maŋɩ DƖ tiŋi je sɩm kʋntʋ, sɩ DƖ pa balʋ na wʋ́ jǝni da tɩn.».

eng: Now when the other ten disciples heard about this, they became angry with James and John.
local: O karabiǝ fugǝ kam na tu ba lwarɩ ba donnǝ bale bam na loori kʋlʋ Zezi tee nɩ tɩn, ba bana ma tiini ya zaŋɩ dɩ Zakɩ dɩ Zan.

eng: Jesus called them over and said, You know that the ones who are considered the rulers by the Gentiles show off their authority over them and their high-ranking officials order them around.
local: Zezi laan ma bǝŋi ba maama, yɩ o ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Abam nan maŋɩ á ye nɩ lʋgʋ baŋa pwa kɩ dam mʋ dɩ ba nɔɔna, yɩ ba dɩdɛɛra di dam ba baŋa nɩ.

eng: But thats not the way it will be with you. Whoever wants to be great among you will be your servant.
local: Kʋ nan na yɩ abam, kʋ wʋ maŋɩ sɩ kʋ taa yɩ kʋntʋ doŋ abam tɩtarɩ nɩ. Bɛŋwaanɩ, abam wʋlʋ na lagɩ sɩ o ji nɔn-kamunu á wʋnɩ tɩn, kʋntʋ tu maŋɩ sɩ o ji abam maama tɩntʋŋnʋ mʋ.

eng: Whoever wants to be first among you will be the slave of all.
local: Wʋlʋ na lagɩ sɩ o ji abam yigǝ tu tɩn, kʋntʋ tu maŋɩ sɩ o ji abam maama gambaa.

eng: for the Human One didnt come to be served but rather to serve and to give his life to liberate many people.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm wʋ tu sɩ nɔɔna tʋŋɩ ba pa-nɩ. Amʋ nan tu sɩ a tʋŋɩ a pa nɔɔna mʋ, sɩ a daarɩ a kwe a ŋwɩa a ma lǝni nɔɔna zanzan ŋwɩa Wɛ tee nɩ.».

eng: Jesus and his followers came into Jericho. As Jesus was leaving Jericho, together with his disciples and a sizable crowd, a blind beggar named Bartimaeus, Timaeus son, was sitting beside the road.
local: Zezi dɩ o karabiǝ bam ma vu ba yi tɩʋ kʋdoŋ, kʋ yɩrɩ mʋ Zeriko. Ba ma tɔgɩ da ba nuŋi ba maa kɛa. Ba na maa kɛ tɩn, nɔn-kɔgɔ maa tɔgɩ-ba. Nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ maa wʋra o na yɩ lilwe. O yɩrɩ mʋ Batime, o ko mʋ yɩ Time. O maa je cwǝŋǝ kam ni nɩ yɩ o loorǝ.

eng: When he heard that Jesus of Nazareth was there, he began to shout, Jesus, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: O ma lwarɩ nɩ kʋ yɩ Nazarɛtɩ tu Zezi mʋ tɔgɩ dáanɩ o maa kɛa. O laan ma puli o kaasɩ baŋa baŋa o wɩ: «Zezi, Pɛ Davidi dwi tu, duri a yibwǝnǝ!».

eng: Many scolded him, telling him to be quiet, but he shouted even louder, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: Nɔɔna zanzan ma bag-o sɩ o pu o ni. O laan ma tiini o kaasɩ zanzan yɩ o wɩ: «Pɛ Davidi dwi tu, duri a yibwǝnǝ!».

eng: Jesus stopped and said, Call him forward. They called the blind man, Be encouraged! Get up! Hes calling you.
local: Zezi laan ma zɩgɩ yɩ o ta o wɩ, ba bǝŋ-o sɩ o ba. Ba ma bǝŋ-o, yɩ ba ta dɩd-o ba wɩ: «Kɩ wʋpolo, sɩ n zaŋɩ wɛɛnɩ, sɩ o bǝ-m o wɩ n ba.».

eng: Throwing his coat to the side, he jumped up and came to Jesus.
local: Lilwe wʋm ma da pɩɩsɩ o gɔrɔ o yagɩ, yɩ o zaŋɩ lɩla o vu o yi Zezi te.

eng: Jesus asked him, What do you want me to do for you? The blind man said, Teacher, I want to see.
local: Zezi ma bwe-o o wɩ: «Nmʋ lagɩ sɩ a kɩ bɛɛ mʋ a pa-m?» O ma lǝri o wɩ: «Karanyɩna, a lagɩ sɩ a joori a taa naɩ mʋ.».

eng: Jesus said, Go, your faith has healed you. At once he was able to see, and he began to follow Jesus on the way.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Nan ve, nmʋ na kɩ n wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ amʋ te tɩn mʋ pɛ n na yazurǝ.» Zezi na tagɩ kʋntʋ bɩdwɩ baŋa nɩ tɩn, mʋ lilwe wʋm yiǝ joori ya naɩ. O maa tɔgɩ Zezi kwaga cwǝŋǝ kam nɩ.

eng: As Jesus left the temple, one of his disciples said to him, Teacher, look! What awesome stones and buildings.
local: Zezi laan ma zaŋɩ o nuŋi Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm wʋnɩ. O na maa kɛ tɩn, o karabiǝ bam dɩdʋa ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Karanyɩna, nii kandwa yantʋ ba na mɛ ba lɔ digǝ kam tɩn na lana te. Nii ka lɔɔm dɩm na dana te.».

eng: Jesus responded, Do you see these enormous buildings? Not even one stone will be left upon another. All will be demolished.
local: Zezi ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «Nmʋ wʋ nɛ ka na daga te tɩn na? Ka maama nan wʋ́ ba ka tʋ tɩga nɩ ka cɔgɩ. Kandwɛ daa bá daŋɩ dɩ doŋ baŋa nɩ.».

eng: Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives across from the temple. Peter, James, John, and Andrew asked him privately.
local: Zezi dɛɛn ma vu o jǝni Olivi piu kʋm yuu nɩ, yɩ o tʋlɩ o na Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm o yigǝ nɩ. O na je kʋntʋ tɩn, Pɩyɛɛrɩ dɩ Zakɩ dɩ Zan dɩ Andre yɩranɩ ma vu o te, yɩ ba bwe-o ba wɩ:.

eng: Tell us, when will these things happen? What sign will show that all these things are about to come to an end.
local: «Maŋa kɔɔ mʋ Wɛ-digǝ kam wʋ́ ba ka cɔgɩ kʋntʋ? Bɛɛ mʋ lagɩ kʋ kɩ maana kʋ brɩ dɩbam, sɩ dɩ́ lwarɩ nɩ wǝǝnu tɩntʋ maama lagɩ tɩ kɩ?».

eng: Jesus said, Watch out that no one deceives you.
local: Zezi ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Á fɔgɩ á cɩ á tɩtɩ, sɩ á yɩ pa nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ ganɩ abam.

eng: Many people will come in my name, saying, Im the one! They will deceive many people.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ nɔɔna zanzan wʋ́ ba abam te, yɩ baá ma amʋ yɩrɩ ba ma ganɩ abam ba wɩ, bantʋ dɩdʋa dɩdʋa mʋ yɩ Krisi wʋm Wɛ na tʋŋɩ tɩn, yɩ baá ganɩ nɔɔna zanzan.

eng: When you hear of wars and reports of wars, dont be alarmed. These things must happen, but this isnt the end yet.
local: Á wʋ́ ba á na tigurǝ na wʋ abam tɩɩnɩ dɩm nɩ, yɩ á kwǝri á ni ka ŋwa tɩɩnɩ dɩlʋ na baŋwɛ tɩn nɩ. Á nan na ni kʋntʋ, sɩ á yɩ pa fʋʋnɩ zʋ abam. Wǝǝnu tɩm kʋntʋ maŋɩ sɩ tɩ kɩ mʋ, yɩ kʋ ta daɩ lʋgʋ tiim maŋa.

eng: Nations and kingdoms will fight against each other, and there will be earthquakes and famines in all sorts of places. These things are just the beginning of the sufferings associated with the end.
local: Dwi wʋ́ zaŋɩ dɩ kɩ jara dɩ dwi dɩdoŋ, yɩ tɩʋ dɩ kʋ doŋ wʋ́ zaŋɩ tɩ magɩ jara. Tɩga dɩ wʋ́ sisiŋi je sɩdonnǝ nɩ, yɩ kana dɩ wʋ́ ba ka taa wʋ je zanzan nɩ. Yaara yam kʋntʋ maama nan nyɩ dɩ kaanɩ na sɩŋɩ o vri pugǝ yɩ ka wɔɛ yɩ cam daa ta wʋ kwaga nɩ te tɩn mʋ.

eng: Watch out for yourselves. People will hand you over to the councils. You will be beaten in the synagogues. You will stand before governors and kings because of me so that you can testify before them.
local: Á fɔgɩ á cɩ á tɩtɩ, sɩ nɔɔna wʋ́ ja abam ba vu ba saŋɩ sarɩya-dirǝ nakwa tee nɩ. Ba ta wʋ́ ja abam ba magɩ ba Wɛ-di sɩm wʋnɩ. Baá ja abam ba vu ba saŋɩ dɩdɛɛra dɩ pwa tee nɩ, abam na tɔgɩ amʋ tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Kʋntʋ mʋ wʋ́ pa á wanɩ á brɩ-ba Wɛ cɩga kam.

eng: First, the good news must be proclaimed to all the nations.
local: Kʋ nan fɩn mʋ sɩ ba da yigǝ ba tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam ba brɩ dwi maama tiinǝ, sɩ woŋo maama tiim laan yi.

eng: When they haul you in and hand you over, dont worry ahead of time about what to answer or say. Instead, say whatever is given to you at that moment, for you arent doing the speaking but the Holy Spirit is.
local: Ba na jaanɩ abam ba vu ba saŋɩ, sɩ á yɩ taá liǝ dɩ á na wʋ́ ta kʋlʋ tɩn. Á na wʋ́ taá zɩgɩ ba yigǝ nɩ maŋa kalʋ tɩn, sɩ á laan ŋɔɔnɩ kʋlʋ Wɛ na wʋ́ pa abam tɩn. Kʋ daɩ abam tɩtɩ mʋ wʋ́ ŋɔɔnɩ, kʋ yɩ Wɛ Joro kʋm mʋ wʋ́ pa abam kʋlʋ á na wʋ́ ŋɔɔnɩ tɩn.

eng: Brothers and sisters will hand each other over to death. A father will turn in his children. Children will rise up against their parents and have them executed.
local: Nɔɔna badonnǝ wʋ́ ja ba tɩtɩ cʋrrʋ ba kɩ nɔɔna jɩa nɩ sɩ ba gʋ-ba. Kwǝ dɩ wʋ́ kɩ ba biǝ kʋntʋ doŋ. Biǝ dɩ wʋ́ zaŋɩ ba pa ba ja ba niinǝ dɩ ba kwǝ ba gʋ.

eng: Everyone will hate you because of my name. But whoever stands firm until the end will be saved.
local: Nɔɔna maama wʋ́ ta culi abam, á na yɩ a nɔɔna tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Wʋlʋ maama nan na kɩ pu-dɩa o zɩgɩ kǝŋkǝŋ sɩ kʋ vu kʋ yi kweelim tɩn, Wɛ wʋ́ vrɩ kʋntʋ tu ŋwɩa.

eng: When you see the disgusting and destructive thing standing where it shouldnt be , then those in Judea must escape to the mountains.
local: Abam nan wʋ́ ba á na wo-lɔŋɔ kʋlʋ na yɩ cɔgɩnʋ tɩn na wʋ́ ba kʋ zɩgɩ jǝgǝ kalʋ na wʋ maŋɩ sɩ kʋ taa zɩgɩ da tɩn.» «Abam na nɛ wo-lɔŋɔ kʋm kʋntʋ, sɩ abam balʋ na zʋʋrɩ Zude nɩ tɩn kɩ lɩla á nuŋi á duri á vu pweeru yum á sǝgi da.

eng: Those on the roof shouldnt come down or enter their houses to grab anything.
local: Nɔɔnʋ nan na wʋ nayuu nɩ, o daa yɩ tu o zʋ digǝ sɩ o kwe kʋlʋkʋlʋ.

eng: Those in the field shouldnt come back to grab their clothes.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ nan na wʋ kara nɩ tɩn, o daa yɩ joori sɔŋɔ sɩ o kwe o gɔrɔ o zʋ.

eng: How terrible it will be at that time for women who are pregnant and for women who are nursing their children.
local: Kantʋ maŋa kam nɩ kʋʋ́ ta tiini kʋ yɩ lɛɛrʋ dɩ kaana balʋ na jɩgɩ pwi tɩn, dɩ balʋ na jɩgɩ biǝ yɩ ba ŋɔgɩ yɩlɩ tɩn.

eng: Pray that it doesnt happen in winter.
local: Nan taá loori-na Wɛ, sɩ cam dɩm kʋntʋ yɩ ba waarʋ mɩmaŋa nɩ.

eng: In those days there will be great suffering such as the world has never before seen and will never again see.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ yaara yalʋ na lagɩ ya ba da yam kʋntʋ nɩ tɩn wʋ́ ta tiini ya camma ya dwe yalʋ na maŋɩ ya kɩ pulim nɩ Wɛ na kɩ lʋgʋ sɩ kʋ ba kʋ yi zɩm tɩn. Yaara yantʋ nan na kɛ, yadonnǝ daa bá ba nɩnɛɛnɩ yantʋ te.

eng: If the Lord hadnt shortened that time, no one would be rescued. But for the sake of the chosen ones, the ones whom God chose, he has cut short the time.
local: Baŋa-Wɛ nan na wʋ pɛ yaara yam da yam tri, nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ bá wǝri. Kʋ nan yɩ Wɛ tɩtɩ nɔɔna balʋ DƖ na kuri tɩn ŋwaanɩ mʋ, DƖ wʋ́ pa yaara yam da yam tri.

eng: Then if someone says to you, Look, heres the Christ, or There he is, dont believe it.
local: Kantʋ maŋa kam nɩ, nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ maama na tagɩ dɩ abam o wɩ: ‹Nii Krisi wʋm Wɛ na tʋŋɩ tɩn, o wʋ yo seeni› naa ‹o wʋ jǝgǝ kantʋ nɩ›, sɩ á yɩ sɛ kʋntʋ tu.

eng: False christs and false prophets will appear, and they will offer signs and wonders in order to deceive, if possible, those whom God has chosen.
local: Nɔɔna badaara wʋ́ zaŋɩ ba brɩ nɩ bantʋ dɩdʋa dɩdʋa mʋ yɩ Krisi wʋm, yɩ ba yagɩ ba daɩ. Badonnǝ dɩ wʋ́ brɩ nɩ bantʋ mʋ yɩ Wɛ nijoŋnǝ, yɩ ba yagɩ ba daɩ. Baá kɩ wo-kɩnkagɩla yalʋ na wʋ́ sʋ nɔɔna tɩn, sɩ ba ya na waɩ, sɩ ba sʋgɩ Wɛ tɩtɩ nɔɔna balʋ DƖ na kuri tɩn.

eng: But you, watch out! Ive told you everything ahead of time.
local: Á taá cʋ á tɩtɩ, sɩ a wuuri a ta a brɩ abam mʋ yɩ wǝǝnu tɩm maŋa daa ta wʋ yi.».

eng: In those days, after the suffering of that time, the sun will become dark, and the moon wont give its light.
local: «Yaara yam kʋntʋ maŋa na tu ka kɛ, da yantʋ wʋnɩ ‹wɩa kam laan wʋ́ ji nazono tim tim, cana dɩ daa bá fuŋi sɩ pooni taa wʋra.

eng: The stars will fall from the sky, and the planets and other heavenly bodies will be shaken.
local: Wɛyuu wʋ́ sisiŋi yɩ calɩcwɩ sɩm kwǝri sɩ tʋ tɩga nɩ, yɩ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ maama na wʋ wɛyuu nɩ tɩn wʋ́ vugimi daanɩ.›.

eng: Then they will see the Human One coming in the clouds with great power and splendor.
local: Maŋa kam kʋntʋ nɩ nɔɔna wʋ́ na Nabiin-bu wʋm na maa bɩɩnɩ lʋgʋ baŋa. Oó ta wʋ kunkwǝǝnu wʋnɩ o bɩɩnɩ dɩ dam, yɩ Wɛ paarɩ-zulǝ wʋ o tee nɩ zanzan.

eng: Then he will send the angels and gather together his chosen people from the four corners of the earth, from the end of the earth to the end of heaven.
local: O laan wʋ́ pa o malɛsɩ sɩm nuŋi sɩ jagɩ sɩ vu lʋgʋ baŋa je maama, kʋ na yɩ wa-puli dɩ wa-zʋʋrɩ, jazɩm dɩ jagwiǝ, sɩ la o nɔɔna balʋ Wɛ na kuri tɩn je maama sɩ kɩ daanɩ.

eng: Learn this parable from the fig tree. After its branch becomes tender and it sprouts new leaves, you know that summer is near.
local: Nan maanɩ-na á nii kapʋrʋ na yɩ te tɩn. Kʋntʋ na yǝni kʋ wʋra kʋ kwi vɔɔrʋ, á yǝni á lwarɩ nɩ kʋ twɛ yadɛ.

eng: In the same way, when you see these things happening, you know that hes near, at the door.
local: Kʋ nan yɩ bɩdwɩ mʋ dɩ á na tu á na wǝǝnu tɩlʋ a na tagɩ a brɩ abam tɩn na wʋra tɩ kɩa, sɩ á lwarɩ nɩ maŋa kam laan twɛ ka ti.

eng: I assure you that this generation wont pass away until all these things happen.
local: A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, wǝǝnu tɩm kʋntʋ maama wʋ́ ba tɩ wʋra tɩ kɩa, yɩ zɩm nɔɔna bam ta wʋ tɩgɩ ba ti.

eng: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will certainly not pass away.
local: Wɛyuu dɩ tɩga baŋa maama wʋ́ ba tɩ kɛ, yɩ amʋ taanɩ dɩm bá kɛ maŋa dɩ maŋa.

eng: But nobody knows when that day or hour will come, not the angels in heaven and not the Son. Only the Father knows.
local: Nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ nan yǝri dɛ dɩlʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm na lagɩ o joori o ba tɩn, yɩ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ yǝri dɛ dɩm wʋnɩ maŋa kam o na wʋ́ ba tɩn, kʋ na maŋɩ kʋ yɩ Wɛ malɛsɩ sɩm naa DƖ Bu wʋm tɩtɩ dɩ. Kʋ yɩ a Ko Baŋa-Wɛ yɩranɩ mʋ ye.

eng: Watch out! Stay alert! You dont know when the time is coming.
local: Á taá fɔgɩ á cʋ á tɩtɩ, sɩ á yǝri maŋa kam o na wʋ́ joori o ba tɩn.

eng: It is as if someone took a trip, left the household behind, and put the servants in charge, giving each one a job to do, and told the doorkeeper to stay alert.
local: Kʋʋ́ ta nyɩ dɩ sɔŋɔ tu na nuŋi sɩ o beeri o vu yigǝ yigǝ te tɩn. O ma pa o tɩntʋŋna bam ni sɩ ba taa nii o sɔŋɔ kʋm baŋa nɩ. O ma pa ba nɔɔnʋ maama o tɩtʋŋɩ. O ma ta dɩ ba dɩdʋa, sɩ o taa yɩrɩ o mancoŋo kʋm lanyɩranɩ.

eng: Therefore, stay alert! You dont know when the head of the household will come, whether in the evening or at midnight, or when the rooster crows in the early morning or at daybreak.
local: Abam dɩ maŋɩ sɩ á fɔgɩ á taá yɩrɩ kʋntʋ doŋ mʋ. Bɛŋwaanɩ á yǝri maŋa kalʋ sɔŋɔ tu wʋm na wʋ́ joori o ba tɩn. O waɩ o tui dɩdaan-ni nɩ naa tɩtɩɩ kʋnkʋrʋ nɩ, naa tɩ-bra-pʋra nɩ naa wa-puli maŋa nɩ.

eng: Dont let him show up when you werent expecting and find you sleeping.
local: Kʋntʋ tɩn, o na tu bɩdwɩ baŋa nɩ, sɩ o yɩ darɩ abam dɩ á tigi á dɔa.

eng: What I say to you, I say to all: Stay alert.
local: A lagɩ a ta dɩ abam dɩ nɔɔna bam maama nɩ, á maŋɩ sɩ á taá cʋ á tɩtɩ mʋ.».

eng: Jesus continued, I assure you that some standing here wont die before they see Gods kingdom arrive in power.
local: O daa ta ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, nɔɔna balʋ zɩm na wʋ yo seeni tɩn badaara bá tɩ, yɩ baá na Wɛ paarɩ dɩm na wʋ́ ba nɔɔna tɩtarɩ nɩ dɩ dam te tɩn.».

eng: Six days later Jesus took Peter, James, and John, and brought them to the top of a very high mountain where they were alone. He was transformed in front of them.
local: Da yardʋ dɛɛn na kɛ tɩn, Zezi laan ma ja Pɩyɛɛrɩ dɩ Zakɩ dɩ Zan o pa ba tɔgɩ daanɩ ba vu ba di pu-kamunu yuu. Ba wʋra ba yɩranɩ. Ba na wʋra kʋntʋ tɩn, Zezi yibiyǝ ma lǝni ba yigǝ nɩ.

eng: and his clothes were amazingly bright, brighter than if they had been bleached white.
local: O gwaarʋ tɩm ma tiini tɩ pɩɩrɩ parɩ parɩ yɩ tɩ nyɩɩna. Tɩ pɩɩrɩ tɩ gaalɩ nabiinu na wʋ́ wanɩ o zarɩ o gwaarʋ o pa tɩ pɩɩrɩ te tɩn.

eng: Elijah and Moses appeared and were talking with Jesus.
local: Eli dɩ Moyisi ma da ba nuŋi ba yigǝ nɩ, yɩ ba ŋɔɔnɩ dɩ Zezi.

eng: Peter reacted to all of this by saying to Jesus, Rabbi, its good that were here. Lets make three shrines—one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ laan ma ta dɩ Zezi o wɩ: «Karanyɩna, dɩ́ na wʋ yo seeni tɩn, kʋ tiini kʋ lana kʋ pa dɩbam. Nan pa dɩ́ pu vwǝ yatɔ yo seeni, sɩ nmʋ dɩ Moyisi dɩ Eli taa zʋʋrɩ da.».

eng: He said this because he didnt know how to respond, for the three of them were terrified.
local: Fʋʋnɩ dɛɛn zʋ-ba zanzan. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ o garɩ o ŋɔɔnɩ kʋntʋ doŋ.

eng: Then a cloud overshadowed them, and a voice spoke from the cloud, This is my Son, whom I dearly love. Listen to him.
local: Kunkwǝǝnu laan ma ba tɩ kwǝli-ba. Ba ma ni kwǝrǝ na ŋɔɔnɩ tɩ wʋnɩ ka wɩ: «Wʋntʋ mʋ yɩ amʋ bu-dʋa a na so-o dɩ a wʋ maama. Á nan taá cǝgi o taanɩ lanyɩranɩ.».

eng: Suddenly, looking around, they no longer saw anyone with them except Jesus.
