local: Ba ma da kwǝni ba nii jǝgǝ maama, yɩ ba daa wʋ nɛ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ, kʋ na daɩ Zezi yɩranɩ.

eng: As they were coming down the mountain, he ordered them not to tell anyone what they had seen until after the Human One had risen from the dead.
local: Ba ma zaŋɩ piu kʋm yuu nɩ ba maa tuǝ. Zezi laan ma kaanɩ-ba o wɩ: «Yɩ pa-na nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ lwarɩ á na nɛ kʋlʋ tɩn, sɩ kʋ taa ve maŋa kalʋ amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm na wʋ́ ba a bi a yagɩ tʋʋnɩ tɩn.».

eng: So they kept it to themselves, wondering, Whats this rising from the dead.
local: Ba ma sɛ yɩ ba ŋɔɔnɩ ba yɩranɩ ba bwɛ ba nii, ka bi ka yagɩ tʋʋnɩ taanɩ dɩm kuri mʋ bɛɛ.

eng: They asked Jesus, Why do the legal experts say that Elijah must come first.
local: Ba laan ma bwe-o ba wɩ: «Wɛ cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ bam yǝni ba tɛ ba wɩ: Eli mʋ maŋɩ sɩ o da yigǝ o ba, sɩ Krisi wʋm laan da kwaga. Kʋ yɩ cɩga mʋ naa vwan mʋ?».

eng: He answered, Elijah does come first to restore all things. Why was it written that the Human One would suffer many things and be rejected?.
local: Zezi ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Kʋ yɩ cɩga mʋ. Eli maŋɩ sɩ o da yigǝ o ba o kwɛ woŋo maama, sɩ Krisi wʋm laan ba. Bɛɛ nan mʋ yɩ kʋ pʋpʋnɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ kʋ wɩ, Nabiin-bu wʋm maŋɩ sɩ o yaarɩ zanzan sɩ nɔɔna cul-o?.

eng: In fact, I tell you that Elijah has come, but they did to him whatever they wanted, just as it was written about him.
local: Á nan taá ye-na nɩ, Eli maŋɩ o ba, yɩ nɔɔna tiini ba bɛɛs-o nɩ ba wʋbʋŋa na lagɩ te tɩn, nɩ kʋ na maŋɩ kʋ pʋpʋnɩ o woŋo Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ te tɩn.».

eng: When Jesus, Peter, James, and John approached the other disciples, they saw a large crowd surrounding them and legal experts arguing with them.
local: Zezi dɩ o karabiǝ batɔ bam laan ma joori ba vu ba yi o karabiǝ babam bam te. Ba ma na kɔgɔ zanzan na gilimi-ba. Wɛ cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ badonnǝ maa wʋra ba magɩ kantɔgɔ dɩdaanɩ ba.

eng: Suddenly the whole crowd caught sight of Jesus. They ran to greet him, overcome with excitement.
local: Kɔgɔ kʋm na nɛ Zezi tɩn, ba ma tiini ba yɩ yǝǝu, yɩ ba duri ba vu o te ba jɔɔn-o.

eng: Jesus asked them, What are you arguing about.
local: O ma bwe-ba o wɩ: «Bɛɛ ŋwaanɩ mʋ á zɩgɩ á magɩ kantɔgɔ dɩ ba?».

eng: Someone from the crowd responded, Teacher, I brought my son to you, since he has a spirit that doesnt allow him to speak.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ maa wʋ kɔgɔ kʋm wʋnɩ, o ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «Karanyɩna, a ya jaanɩ a bu mʋ a ba nmʋ te, sɩ n pa o na yazurǝ. Cicirǝ mʋ jɩg-o ka yaara, ka pa o warɩ o ŋɔɔnɩ.

eng: Wherever it overpowers him, it throws him into a fit. He foams at the mouth, grinds his teeth, and stiffens up. So I spoke to your disciples to see if they could throw it out, but they couldnt.
local: Maŋa kalʋ ka na yǝni ka ja-o tɩn, ka yǝni ka dʋl-o ka dɩ tɩga nɩ mʋ. Lile-puŋǝ ma nuŋi o ni nɩ yɩ o dʋnɩ o yǝlǝ. O yɩra yam maama maa yǝni ya kʋ. A ma loori nmʋ karabiǝ bam sɩ ba zǝli cicirǝ kam sɩ ka yag-o, yɩ ba wʋ wanɩ.».

eng: Jesus answered them, You faithless generation, how long will I be with you? How long will I put up with you? Bring him to me.
local: Zezi na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma ta o wɩ: «Abam zɩm nɔɔna bam brɩ á ba jɩgɩ wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ amʋ. Amʋ wʋ́ daanɩ abam tɩtarɩ nɩ a kɩ wʋ-zuru dɩ abam sɩ kʋ taa ve maŋa kɔɔ? Ja-na bu wʋm á ba.».

eng: They brought him. When the spirit saw Jesus, it immediately threw the boy into a fit. He fell on the ground and rolled around, foaming at the mouth.
local: Ba ma ja-o ba vu o te. Cicirǝ kam na nɛ Zezi tɩn, ka ma su bu wʋm ka pa o yɩra saɩ pɔgɩ pɔgɩ. O ma tʋ tɩga nɩ o bibilǝ. Lile-puŋǝ maa nuŋi o ni nɩ.

eng: Jesus asked his father, How long has this been going on? He said, Since he was a child.
local: Zezi ma bwe bu wʋm ko o wɩ: «Maŋa kɔɔ mʋ kʋ puli kʋ kɩ-o kʋntʋ?» O ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «O biini nɩ.

eng: It has often thrown him into a fire or into water trying to kill him. If you can do anything, help us! Show us compassion.
local: Kuni zanzan cicirǝ kam yǝni ka dʋl-o ka dɩ mini wʋnɩ dɩ na wʋnɩ mʋ, sɩ ka gʋ-o. Nmʋ nan na wʋ́ wanɩ, sɩ n duri dɩbam yibwǝnǝ n zǝni dɩbam.».

eng: Jesus said to him, If you can do anything? All things are possible for the one who has faith.
local: Zezi ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «Nmʋ tagɩ n wɩ, amʋ na wʋ́ wanɩ mʋ. Wʋlʋ maama nan na kɩ o wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ amʋ tɩn, kʋlʋkʋlʋ tǝrǝ Wɛ na bá wanɩ DƖ kɩ DƖ pa kʋntʋ tu.».

eng: At that the boys father cried out, I have faith; help my lack of faith.
local: Bu wʋm ko laan ma kaasɩ o wɩ: «A sɩɩnɩ a jɩgɩ wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ nmʋ. Nan wǝli-nɩ sɩ a taa fɔgɩ a jɩga.».

eng: Noticing that the crowd had surged together, Jesus spoke harshly to the unclean spirit, Mute and deaf spirit, I command you to come out of him and never enter him again.
local: Zezi ma na nɔn-kɔgɔ zanzan na durǝ kʋ maa bɩɩnɩ ba te. O laan ma bagɩ cicirǝ kam, yɩ o ta dɩ ka o wɩ: «Nmʋ wʋlʋ na paɩ nɔɔna jiri mɩmaarʋ dɩ zwa-kwarʋ tɩn, a tɛ dɩ nmʋ sɩ n nuŋi bu wʋm yɩra nɩ n viiri, sɩ n daa yɩ joori n yaar-o.».

eng: After screaming and shaking the boy horribly, the spirit came out. The boy seemed to be dead; in fact, several people said that he had died.
local: Zezi na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, cicirǝ kam ma kaasɩ, yɩ ka tiini ka su bu wʋm ka pa o yɩra saɩ pɔgɩ pɔgɩ, yɩ ka nuŋi ka daar-o. Ka na yag-o tɩn, o ma bǝri. Nɔɔna zanzan maa tɛ ba wɩ: «O tɩgɩ mʋ.».

eng: But Jesus took his hand, lifted him up, and he arose.
local: Zezi laan ma ja o jɩŋa o pa o zaŋɩ wɛɛnɩ o zɩgɩ.

eng: After Jesus went into a house, his disciples asked him privately, Why couldnt we throw this spirit out.
local: Zezi na joori o zʋ digǝ tɩn, o karabiǝ bam ma vu o te ba yɩranɩ, yɩ ba bwe-o ba wɩ: «Bɛŋwaanɩ mʋ dɩbam wʋ wanɩ dɩ́ zǝli cicirǝ kam?».

eng: Jesus answered, Throwing this kind of spirit out requires prayer.
local: O ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Cicirǝ kantʋ doŋ zǝlim cana. Cicirǝ kantʋ dwi warɩ ka zǝli kʋ na daɩ dɩ Wɛ-loro dam.».

eng: From there Jesus and his followers went through Galilee, but he didnt want anyone to know it.
local: Ba dɛɛn ma nuŋi jǝgǝ kam kʋntʋ nɩ ba vu ba tɔgɩ Galile wʋ ba kɛ. Zezi maa ba lagɩ sɩ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ lwarɩ ba je.

eng: This was because he was teaching his disciples, The Human One will be delivered into human hands. They will kill him. Three days after he is killed he will rise up.
local: o na jɩgɩ o karabiǝ bam o brɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ. O maa brɩ-ba o wɩ: «Baá ja amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm ba kɩ nɔɔna jɩŋa nɩ yɩ ba gʋ-nɩ. Ba nan na gʋ-nɩ, da yatɔ dɛ nɩ a laan wʋ́ joori a bi a yagɩ tʋʋnɩ.».

eng: But they didnt understand this kind of talk, and they were afraid to ask him.
local: O karabiǝ bam ma wʋ ni o taanɩ dɩm kuri, yɩ ba kwarɩ sɩ ba bwe-o ba nii.

eng: They entered Capernaum. When they had come into a house, he asked them, What were you arguing about during the journey.
local: Ba ma vu ba yi Kapɛrnawum. Ba ma zʋ sɔŋɔ. Zezi ma bwe o karabiǝ bam o wɩ: «Dɩ́ na wʋ cwǝŋǝ nɩ dɩ́ maa bɩɩnɩ tɩn, á ya magɩ kantɔgɔ bɛɛ ŋwaanɩ mʋ?».

eng: They didnt respond, since on the way they had been debating with each other about who was the greatest.
local: Ba maa cɩm. Bɛŋwaanɩ, ba ya na maa bɩɩnɩ tɩn, ba ya magɩ kantɔgɔ daanɩ sɩ ba nii, bantʋ wʋnɩ wɔɔ mʋ yɩ kamunu o dwe ba maama.

eng: He sat down, called the Twelve, and said to them, Whoever wants to be first must be least of all and the servant of all.
local: Zezi ma jǝni tɩga nɩ. O ma bǝŋi o karabiǝ fugǝ-bale bam sɩ ba ba o te. O ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Wʋlʋ na lagɩ sɩ o taa yɩ yigǝ tu tɩn, kʋntʋ tu maŋɩ sɩ o ji ba maama kwaga tu mʋ, sɩ o taa tʋŋɩ o pa ba maama.».

eng: Jesus reached for a little child, placed him among the Twelve, and embraced him. Then he said.
local: O ma ja bu balaŋa o pa o zɩgɩ ba maama tɩtarɩ nɩ. O ma kwe-o o kɩ o ja-kukoro nɩ, yɩ o ta dɩ ba o wɩ:.

eng: Whoever welcomes one of these children in my name welcomes me; and whoever welcomes me isnt actually welcoming me but rather the one who sent me.
local: «Wʋlʋ maama na nɩgɩ bu balaŋa kantʋ doŋ lanyɩranɩ amʋ yɩrɩ ŋwaanɩ tɩn, kʋntʋ tu nɩgɩ amʋ mʋ. Wʋlʋ maama nan na nɩgɩ amʋ tɩn, kʋ daɩ amʋ yɩranɩ mʋ o nɩga, kʋ yɩ o nɩgɩ Baŋa-Wɛ dɩlʋ na tʋŋɩ-nɩ tɩn dɩ mʋ.».

eng: John said to Jesus, Teacher, we saw someone throwing demons out in your name, and we tried to stop him because he wasnt following us.
local: Zan ma ta dɩ Zezi o wɩ: «Karanyɩna, dɩ́ ya nɛ nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ na zǝli ciciri nɔɔna yɩra nɩ dɩ nmʋ yɩrɩ. O nan na daɩ dɩbam kɔgɔ kʋm wʋ nɔɔnʋ tɩn, dɩ́ ma kwaanɩ sɩ dɩ́ cɩ-o, sɩ o daa yɩ taa kɩ kʋntʋ.».

eng: Jesus replied, Dont stop him. No one who does powerful acts in my name can quickly turn around and curse me.
local: Zezi ma lǝri o wɩ: «Á ya wʋ maŋɩ sɩ á cɩ-o. Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ maama na tɔgɩ amʋ yɩrɩ ŋwaanɩ o kɩ wo-kɩnkagɩlɩ tɩn bá wanɩ o pipiri lɩla o ŋɔɔnɩ ta-balɔrɔ o pa-nɩ.

eng: Whoever isnt against us is for us.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ wʋlʋ maama na ba culi dɩbam tɩn yɩ dɩbam nɔɔnʋ mʋ.

eng: I assure you that whoever gives you a cup of water to drink because you belong to Christ will certainly be rewarded.
local: A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na kwe na o pa abam sɩ á nyɔ, á na yɩ amʋ Zezi Krisi nɔɔna tɩn ŋwaanɩ, kʋntʋ tu wʋ́ na pɛɛrɩ Wɛ tee nɩ.

eng: As for whoever causes these little ones who believe in me to trip and fall into sin, it would be better for them to have a huge stone hung around their necks and to be thrown into the lake.
local: Kʋ na yɩ balʋ na kɩ ba wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ amʋ tɩn, ba na maŋɩ ba muri nɩ bu-balwa te dɩ, nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na pɛ ba dɩdʋa yagɩ Wɛ cwǝŋǝ tɔgɩm tɩn, ba na lǝgi kandwɛ kamunu mʋ kʋntʋ tu ba nɩ ba dɩ-o nɩnɩʋ wʋnɩ yɩ o tɩ, kʋʋ́ ta garɩ cam dɩlʋ o na wʋ́ ba o na Wɛ tee nɩ tɩn.

eng: If your hand causes you to fall into sin, chop it off. Its better for you to enter into life crippled than to go away with two hands into the fire of hell, which cant be put out.
local: Nmʋ jɩŋa na lagɩ ka pa n kɩ lwarɩm, kʋ maŋɩ sɩ n go-ka mʋ n yagɩ. N na gɛ n jɩŋa, yɩ n daarɩ n na ŋwɩa Wɛ tee nɩ, kʋntʋ garɩ nmʋ na jɩgɩ n jɩa maama yɩ n vu min-tɩʋ, mɛ na yɩ mini dɩlʋ na bá dwe maŋa dɩ maŋa tɩn. .

eng: If your foot causes you to fall into sin, chop it off. Its better for you to enter life lame than to be thrown into hell with two feet.
local: Nmʋ naga na lagɩ ka pa n kɩ lwarɩm, kʋ maŋɩ sɩ n go-ka mʋ n yagɩ. Nmʋ na yɩ na-dʋa yɩ n na ŋwɩa Wɛ tee nɩ, kʋntʋ garɩ nmʋ na jɩgɩ n nɛ sɩm maama, yɩ ba laan dɩ-m ba yagɩ min-tɩʋ nɩ. .

eng: If your eye causes you to fall into sin, tear it out. Its better for you to enter Gods kingdom with one eye than to be thrown into hell with two.
local: Nmʋ yi nan na lagɩ dɩ pa n kɩ lwarɩm, kʋ maŋɩ sɩ n lɩ-dɩ n dʋlɩ n yagɩ mʋ. N na jɩgɩ yi dɩdʋa yɩ n zʋ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋnɩ, kʋntʋ garɩ nmʋ na jɩgɩ yiǝ yale, yɩ ba laan dɩ-m ba yagɩ min-tɩʋ nɩ.

eng: Thats a place where worms dont die and the fire never goes out .
local: Jǝgǝ kam kʋntʋ nɩ, ‹kanzwa wʋ́ ta di ba yɩra, yɩ ya ba fɔgɩ ya tʋa. Mini wʋ́ ta di-ba, yɩ dɩ ba fɔgɩ dɩ dweǝ.›.

eng: Everyone will be salted with fire.
local: Nɔɔnʋ maama maŋɩ sɩ Wɛ kwe mini mʋ DƖ ma maŋ-o DƖ nii, sɩ DƖ pa o bɩcarɩ fɔgɩ dɩ kwɛ, nɩ yɛ na kwɛ wʋdiu te tɩn.

eng: Salt is good; but if salt loses its saltiness, how will it become salty again? Maintain salt among yourselves and keep peace with each other.
local: Yɛ ywǝnǝ yɩ dɩ waɩ dɩ kwɛ wʋdiu. Yɛ ywǝǝni nan na ti, n daa n bá wanɩ n pa dɩ joori dɩ ja ywǝǝni. Pa-na á wʋrʋ taa ywǝmmǝ da-ŋwaanɩ, sɩ á jǝni dɩ yazurǝ, nɩ yɛ na paɩ wʋdiu ywǝmmǝ te tɩn.».

eng: When Jesus and his followers approached Jerusalem, they came to Bethphage and Bethany at the Mount of Olives. Jesus gave two disciples a task.
local: Zezi dɩ o karabiǝ bam dɛɛn ma vu ba twɛ Zeruzalɛm. Ba ma vu ba yi tɩ-balwa bale, ba yɩrɩ mʋ Betifaazɩ dɩ Betani. Je sɩm kʋntʋ wʋ Olivi piu kʋm saŋa nɩ mʋ. Zezi ma lɩ o karabiǝ bam bale o tʋŋɩ.

eng: saying to them, Go into the village over there. As soon as you enter it, you will find tied up there a colt that no one has ridden. Untie it and bring it here.
local: yɩ o ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Ve-na á vu á zʋ tɩʋ kʋlʋ na wʋ abam yigǝ nɩ tɩn. Á na zʋ tɩʋ kʋm wʋnɩ, á wʋ́ na bɩna-pɔla na lǝgi da, yɩ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ daa ta wʋ diini ka baŋa. Ve-na á bwǝli-ka á ja ba á pa-nɩ.

eng: If anyone says to you, Why are you doing this? say, Its master needs it, and he will send it back right away.
local: Nɔɔnʋ na bwe abam nɩ: ‹Bɛɛ mʋ yɩ á bwǝli-ka?›, sɩ á ta dɩd-o nɩ: ‹Kʋ yɩ dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm mʋ laga. Oó joori o pa ka ba lele.› ».

eng: They went and found a colt tied to a gate outside on the street, and they untied it.
local: Ba ma zaŋɩ ba vu sɩ ba nii. Ba na yi tɩʋ kʋm wʋ tɩn, ba ma sɩɩnɩ ba na bɩna-pɔla cwǝŋǝ ni nɩ. Ka lǝgi sɔŋɔ kʋdoŋ ni nɩ. Ba ma vu ba bwǝli-ka.

eng: Some people standing around said to them, What are you doing, untying the colt.
local: Nɔɔna badonnǝ maa zɩgɩ da. Ba ma bwe Zezi karabiǝ bam ba wɩ: «Bɛɛ mʋ yɩ á lagɩ á bwǝli bɩna-pɔla kam?».

eng: They told them just what Jesus said, and they left them alone.
local: Ba ma lǝri-ba nɩ Zezi na maŋɩ o ta te tɩn. Nɔɔna bam ma sɛ.

eng: They brought the colt to Jesus and threw their clothes upon it, and he sat on it.
local: Ba ma ja bɩna-pɔla kam ba ja vu ba yi Zezi te. Ba laan ma kwe ba gwaarʋ ba daŋɩ ka baŋa nɩ. Zezi ma di ka baŋa, yɩ ba ve ba maa kɛa.

eng: Many people spread out their clothes on the road while others spread branches cut from the fields.
local: Ba na maa kɛ tɩn, nɔɔna zanzan ma zul-o ba kwe ba gwaarʋ ba lwarɩ cwǝŋǝ kam yuu nɩ, sɩ Zezi ba o tɔgɩ tɩ baŋa o kɛ. Nɔɔna badaara ma vu ba go tweeru nɛ dɩ vɔɔrʋ ba dɩ cwǝŋǝ yuu nɩ, sɩ o tɔgɩ tɩ baŋa o kɛ.

eng: Those in front of him and those following were shouting, Hosanna! Blessings on the one who comes in the name of the Lord!.
local: Nɔɔna balʋ na wʋ o yigǝ nɩ dɩ o kwaga nɩ ba ve tɩn maa tɔɔlɩ ba wɩ: «Ozaana! Zulǝ taa wʋ Wɛ tee nɩ! Zuli-na wʋlʋ na tu dɩ́ Yuutu Wɛ yɩrɩ ŋwaanɩ tɩn.

eng: Blessings on the coming kingdom of our ancestor David! Hosanna in the highest.
local: Zuli-na wʋlʋ na wʋ́ ta te o nɔɔna, nɩnɛɛnɩ dɩbam ko Davidi dɛɛn na jɩgɩ paarɩ te tɩn. Ozaana! Zulǝ taa wʋ Baŋa-Wɛ tee nɩ!».

eng: Jesus entered Jerusalem and went into the temple. After he looked around at everything, because it was already late in the evening, he returned to Bethany with the Twelve.
local: Zezi ma vu o zʋ Zeruzalɛm tɩʋ kʋm wʋ. O ma vu o yi Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm o zʋ kʋ wʋ. O ma kaagɩ o nii wǝǝnu tɩm maama na yɩ te. Wɛ na lagɩ dɩ zʋ tɩn, o ma joori o nuŋi tɩʋ kʋm wʋnɩ, o tɔgɩ dɩ o karabiǝ fugǝ-bale bam o vu Betani.

eng: The next day, after leaving Bethany, Jesus was hungry.
local: Tɩga na pʋʋrɩ tɩn, ba ma zaŋɩ Betani nɩ sɩ ba joori Zeruzalɛm. Kana maa jɩgɩ Zezi.

eng: From far away, he noticed a fig tree in leaf, so he went to see if he could find anything on it. When he came to it, he found nothing except leaves, since it wasnt the season for figs.
local: Ba na maa ve tɩn, o ma tʋlɩ o na kapʋrʋ. Kʋ dɛɛn li dɩ vɔɔrʋ mʋ. O ma vu sɩ o nii mɔɔla wʋra na sɩ o di. O na yi da tɩn, o ma wʋ nɛ kʋlʋkʋlʋ, kʋ na wʋ yi kʋ lǝrim maŋa tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: So he said to it, No one will ever again eat your fruit! His disciples heard this.
local: O laan ma ta dɩ kapʋrʋ kʋm o wɩ: «Nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ daa bá na mɔɔla nmʋ yuu nɩ o di.» O karabiǝ bam ma ni o na tagɩ kʋlʋ tɩn.

eng: They came into Jerusalem. After entering the temple, he threw out those who were selling and buying there. He pushed over the tables used for currency exchange and the chairs of those who sold doves.
local: Ba ma vu ba yi Zeruzalɛm. Zezi ma vu o zʋ Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm wʋ. O ma na nɔɔna na wʋ ka kʋnkɔlɔ kʋm nɩ ba pipiǝ. O ma zǝli-ba o pa ba nuŋi. O ma vu o ja sǝbu-lǝnnǝ bam taabʋllʋ tɩm o pipiri o pu tɩga nɩ, o pa ba sǝbiǝ bam jagɩ. O ma daarɩ o yigi yitunnu tɩlʋ nɔɔna badonnǝ na je da ba yǝgi kunkwǝn-pulǝ tɩn o dɩ tɩga nɩ.

eng: He didnt allow anyone to carry anything through the temple.
local: O ma wʋ sɛ sɩ nɔɔna taa zɩŋɩ ba zɩla ba tɔgɩ kʋnkɔlɔ kʋm wʋ ba kɛa.

eng: He taught them, Hasnt it been written, My house will be called a house of prayer for all nations? But youve turned it into a hideout for crooks .
local: O ma brɩ nɔɔna bam o wɩ: «Kʋ pʋpʋnɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ nɩ, Wɛ mʋ wɩ: ‹Amʋ digǝ kantʋ maŋɩ sɩ ka taa yɩ jǝgǝ kalʋ lʋgʋ baŋa dwi tiinǝ maama na wʋ́ ta tui ba warɩ amʋ da tɩn mʋ.› Abam nan pɛ ka ji ŋwɩɩna joro jǝgǝ.».

eng: The chief priests and legal experts heard this and tried to find a way to destroy him. They regarded him as dangerous because the whole crowd was enthralled at his teaching.
local: Zwifǝ kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam dɩ ba cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ bam ma ni Zezi na tagɩ kʋlʋ tɩn. Ba laan ma beeri cwǝŋǝ sɩ ba gʋ-o. Ba ma kwarɩ fʋʋnɩ dɩd-o, o zaasɩm dɩm na sʋ nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm maama tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: When it was evening, Jesus and his disciples went outside the city.
local: Tɩga na lagɩ ka yi tɩn, Zezi dɩ o karabiǝ bam ma nuŋi tɩʋ kʋm wʋnɩ.

eng: Early in the morning, as Jesus and his disciples were walking along, they saw the fig tree withered from the root up.
local: Tɩga na pʋʋrɩ tɩn, ba ma daa zaŋɩ ba maa kɛa. Ba ma na kapʋrʋ kʋm na kʋga, dɩ kʋ bʋnnʋ maama.

eng: Peter remembered and said to Jesus, Rabbi, look how the fig tree you cursed has dried up.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ ma guli Zezi ya na tagɩ te tɩn. O ma ta dɩ Zezi o wɩ: «Karanyɩna, nii kapʋrʋ kʋm nmʋ diin na sɔɔlɩ tɩn, kʋ kʋga.».

eng: Jesus responded to them, Have faith in God!.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Fɔgɩ-na á kɩ á wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Wɛ.

eng: I assure you that whoever says to this mountain, Be lifted up and thrown into the sea—and doesnt waver but believes that what is said will really happen—it will happen.
local: A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, abam wʋlʋ na tagɩ dɩ piu kamunu kʋntʋ nɩ, kʋ gɔ kʋ na zɩgɩ mɛ tɩn kʋ vu kʋ tʋ nɩnɩʋ kʋm wʋnɩ, Wɛ wʋ́ pa kʋ kɩ. Nmʋ na jɩgɩ wʋ-dɩdʋa nɩ nmʋ na tagɩ kʋlʋ tɩn wʋ́ sɩɩnɩ kʋ kɩ, yɩ n ba jɩgɩ bʋbwɛa, Wɛ wʋ́ pa kʋ kɩ.

eng: Therefore I say to you, whatever you pray and ask for, believe that you will receive it, and it will be so for you.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ a lagɩ a ta dɩ abam nɩ, woŋo kʋlʋ maama á na wʋ́ loori Wɛ tee nɩ tɩn, sɩ á taá jɩgɩ wʋ-dɩdʋa nɩ á wʋ́ sɩɩnɩ á na-kʋ, yɩ Wɛ wʋ́ pa á na-kʋ.

eng: And whenever you stand up to pray, if you have something against anyone, forgive so that your Father in heaven may forgive you your wrongdoings.
local: Maŋa maama abam na wʋra á warɩ Wɛ, yɩ taanɩ na wʋ abam dɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ laŋa nɩ, sɩ á yagɩ á ma cɛ-o. Á Ko Wɛ laan wʋ́ yagɩ abam dɩ lwarɩm DƖ ma cɛ abam. .

eng: Jesus and his disciples entered Jerusalem again. As Jesus was walking around the temple, the chief priests, legal experts, and elders came to him.
local: Ba daa ma vu ba yi Zeruzalɛm. Zezi ma vu o zʋ Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm wʋ, o karɩ ka wʋnɩ o beerǝ. Zwifǝ kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam dɩ ba cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ bam dɩ ba nakwa bam ma vu o te.

eng: They asked, What kind of authority do you have for doing these things? Who gave you this authority to do them.
local: ba bwe-o ba wɩ: «Wɔɔ dam mʋ n ma n kɩ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ maama n na kɩ tɩn? Wɔɔ mʋ pɛ nmʋ ni sɩ n ta n kɩ kʋntʋ?».

eng: Jesus said to them, I have a question for you. Give me an answer, then Ill tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: O ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «A dɩ jɩgɩ bwiǝ a lagɩ a bwe abam, sɩ á na lǝri-ya á pa-nɩ, sɩ a laan brɩ abam wʋlʋ na pɛ amʋ ni sɩ a tʋŋɩ tɩn.

eng: Was Johns baptism of heavenly or of human origin? Answer me.
local: A lagɩ a bwe abam a nii, Zan na miisi nɔɔna na wʋnɩ tɩn, kʋ yɩ Wɛ mʋ pɛ-o cwǝŋǝ naa nabiinǝ mʋ pɛ-o?».

eng: They argued among themselves, If we say, Its of heavenly origin, hell say, Then why didnt you believe him.
local: O na bwe-ba kʋntʋ tɩn, ba maa wʋra ba bwɛ daanɩ sɩ ba nii ba na wʋ́ lǝri te tɩn. Ba maa wɩ: «Dɩbam na lǝri dɩ́ wɩ, kʋ yɩ Wɛ mʋ, o laan wʋ́ bwe dɩbam nɩ, bɛɛ mʋ yɩ dɩ́ nan wʋ sɛ Zan taanɩ dɩm?.

eng: But we cant say, Its of earthly origin. They said this because they were afraid of the crowd, because they all thought John was a prophet.
local: Kʋ daarɩ, dɩ́ bá wanɩ dɩ́ ta nɩ kʋ yɩ nabiinǝ mʋ pɛ-o cwǝŋǝ.» Ba tagɩ kʋntʋ, bɛŋwaanɩ ba kwarɩ fʋʋnɩ dɩ nɔɔna bam, dɩ ba maama na sɛ nɩ Zan sɩɩnɩ o yɩ Wɛ nijoŋnu tɩn.

eng: They answered Jesus, We dont know. Jesus replied, Neither will I tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ ba lǝri Zezi ba wɩ: «Dɩ́ yǝri wʋlʋ na pɛ Zan cwǝŋǝ tɩn.» Zezi dɩ ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Kʋ na yɩ kʋntʋ, amʋ dɩ nan bá ta wʋlʋ na pɛ amʋ ni sɩ a taa tʋŋɩ tɩn.».

eng: Jesus spoke to them in parables. A man planted a vineyard, put a fence around it, dug a pit for the winepress, and built a tower. Then he rented it to tenant farmers and took a trip.
local: Zezi ma maŋɩ mɩmaŋa o brɩ nɔɔna bam o wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ mʋ wʋra. O ma zaŋɩ o cɩ gaarɩ, yɩ o daarɩ o jǝri tweeru tɩlʋ yɩrɩ na yɩ vinyǝ tɩn dɩ wʋnɩ. O ma daarɩ o kʋ goŋo tɩ-dɩa je nɩ, sɩ o na gwǝri tweeru biǝ bam, sɩ o kɩ dáanɩ o nɔ o kan tɩ na bam. O ma daarɩ o cwi dapala sɩ nɔɔnʋ taa je da o yɩrɩ gaarɩ dɩm. O na fɔgɩ o cɩ-dɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, o laan ma kwe nɔɔna o yagɩ da sɩ ba taa tʋŋa, yɩ o daarɩ o nuŋi o vu cwǝŋǝ.

eng: When it was time, he sent a servant to collect from the tenants his share of the fruit of the vineyard.
local: Tweeru biǝ bam gwǝrim maŋa na yi tɩn, o ma tʋŋɩ o tɩntʋŋnʋ sɩ o vu nɔɔna balʋ na nii o gaarɩ dɩm baŋa nɩ tɩn te, sɩ ba cɛ o tǝri ba pa-o.

eng: But they grabbed the servant, beat him, and sent him away empty-handed.
local: Tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋm na yi ba te tɩn, ba ma ja-o ba magɩ, yɩ ba daarɩ ba pa o viiri dɩ jɩ-kʋrɩ.

eng: Again the landowner sent another servant to them, but they struck him on the head and treated him disgracefully.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋm daa ma tʋŋɩ o tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋdoŋ. O na yi ba te tɩn, ba ma magɩ o yuu ba kaarɩ yɩ ba twɩ-o.

eng: He sent another one; that one they killed. The landlord sent many other servants, but the tenants beat some and killed others.
local: O daa ta ma tʋŋɩ o tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋdoŋ o wǝli da. Wʋntʋ na yi ba te tɩn, ba ma mag-o ba gʋ. Nɔɔnʋ wʋm ta ma tʋŋɩ o tɩntʋŋna zanzan nɔɔna bam tee nɩ, yɩ ba magɩ ba badonnǝ, ba daarɩ ba gʋ ba badaara.

eng: Now the landowner had one son whom he loved dearly. He sent him last, thinking, They will respect my son.
local: Kʋ laan ma daarɩ o tɩtɩ bu wʋlʋ o na so-o zanzan tɩn yɩranɩ. O ma tʋŋ-o ba tee nɩ o ma kweeli, yɩ o bʋŋɩ sɩ, baá nɩgɩ wʋntʋ.

eng: But those tenant farmers said to each other, This is the heir. Lets kill him, and the inheritance will be ours.
local: Nɔɔna bam na nɛ-o tɩn, ba maa ŋɔɔnɩ daanɩ ba wɩ: ‹Mʋ gaarɩ dɩm tu bu, wʋntʋ mʋ lagɩ o ba o taa te o ko wʋm wǝǝnu tɩm. Nan pa-na dɩ́ gʋ-o, sɩ dɩ́ daarɩ dɩ́ taá te o wǝǝnu tɩm.›.

eng: They grabbed him, killed him, and threw him out of the vineyard.
local: Ba laan ma gʋ-o, yɩ ba daarɩ ba vaŋ-o ba ja nuŋi gaarɩ dɩm wʋnɩ ba yagɩ.

eng: So what will the owner of the vineyard do? He will come and destroy those tenants and give the vineyard to others.
local: Kʋntʋ tɩn, gaarɩ dɩm tu wʋm laan wʋ́ kɩ nɔɔna bam kʋntʋ tɩta mʋ? O maŋɩ sɩ o ba o gʋ-ba mʋ, sɩ o daarɩ gaarɩ dɩm o pa nɔɔna badonnǝ sɩ ba taa niǝ.

eng: Havent you read this scripture, The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone.
local: Kʋ pʋpʋnɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ kʋ wɩ: ‹Kandwɛ dɩlʋ lwara na vɩn tɩn laan mʋ joori dɩ ba dɩ ji kandwɛ dɩlʋ na paɩ sɔŋɔ kʋm dana tɩn. Baŋa-Wɛ na kɩ te tɩn mʋ kʋntʋ, yɩ kʋ yɩ wo-kamunu kʋ pa dɩbam.› Abam maŋɩ á wʋ karɩmɩ kʋntʋ na?».

eng: They wanted to arrest Jesus because they knew that he had told the parable against them. But they were afraid of the crowd, so they left him and went away.
local: Zwifǝ yigǝ tiinǝ bam na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma lwarɩ nɩ o maŋɩ mɩmaŋɩ dɩm bantʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ. Ba laan maa beeri cwǝŋǝ sɩ ba ja-o. Ba maa warɩ, ba na kwarɩ fʋʋnɩ dɩ nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Ba ma yag-o, yɩ ba daarɩ ba viiri.

eng: They sent some of the Pharisees and supporters of Herod to trap him in his words.
local: Zwifǝ bam dɛɛn ma lɩ Farizɩan tiinǝ badonnǝ dɩ Pɛ Erɔdɩ kwaga nɔɔna badonnǝ sɩ ba vu Zezi te. Ba maa lagɩ sɩ ba pa o ŋɔɔnɩ o tusi, sɩ ba wanɩ ba ja-o.

eng: They came to him and said, Teacher, we know that youre genuine and you dont worry about what people think. You dont show favoritism but teach Gods way as it really is. Does the Law allow people to pay taxes to Caesar or not? Should we pay taxes or not.
local: Ba ma yi o te, yɩ ba ta dɩd-o ba wɩ: «Karanyɩna, dɩ́ ye nɩ nmʋ yɩ cɩga tu mʋ. Nmʋ yigǝ tǝrǝ dɩ nɔɔna na bʋŋɩ kʋlʋ ba pa nmʋ tɩn, nmʋ nan ba kuri nɔɔna daanɩ. Nmʋ nan yǝni n brɩ nɔɔna Wɛ cwǝŋǝ dɩ cɩga mʋ. Dɩ́ lagɩ dɩ́ bwe dɩ́ nii, dɩbam Zwifǝ bam culu pɛ ni sɩ dɩ́ ŋwɩ lampoo dɩ́ pa lʋgʋ kʋm pa-farʋ wʋm naa kʋ wʋ maŋɩ? Dɩ́ maŋɩ sɩ dɩ́ ŋwɩ naa dɩ́ wʋ maŋɩ sɩ dɩ́ ŋwɩ?».

eng: Since Jesus recognized their deceit, he said to them, Why are you testing me? Bring me a coin. Show it to me.
local: Zezi nan maŋɩ o ye ba na yɩ pipiri-nyɩna te tɩn. O ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Bɛɛ nan mʋ yɩ á lagɩ sɩ á maŋɩ-nɩ á nii? Ja-na sǝbu-dalɩ dɩdʋa á ba sɩ a nii.».

eng: And they brought one. He said to them, Whose image and inscription is this? Caesars, they replied.
local: Ba ma lɩ sǝbu-dalɩ dɩm ba brɩ-o. O ma nii yɩ o bwe-ba o wɩ: «Wɔɔ nyɩnyʋgʋ dɩ o yɩrɩ mʋ pʋpʋnɩ dɩ baŋa nɩ?» Ba ma lǝr-o ba wɩ: «Kʋ yɩ pa-farʋ wʋm mʋ.».

eng: Jesus said to them, Give to Caesar what belongs to Caesar and to God what belongs to God. His reply left them overcome with wonder.
local: Zezi laan ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Kʋ lamma. Á nan taá ŋwɩ kʋlʋ na yɩ pa-farʋ wʋm nyɩm tɩn á pa-o, sɩ á daarɩ á taá paɩ Wɛ kʋlʋ dɩ na yɩ DƖ nyɩm tɩn.» O na lǝri-ba kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ ma sʋ-ba zanzan.

eng: Sadducees, who deny that there is a resurrection, came to Jesus and asked.
local: Sadusɩan tiinǝ badonnǝ dɛɛn ma zaŋɩ ba vu Zezi te. Bantʋ mʋ brɩ ba wɩ, nɔɔnʋ na tɩga, o daa bá joori o bi.

eng: Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a mans brother dies , leaving a widow but no children, the brother must marry the widow and raise up children for his brother.
local: Ba ma vu ba ta dɩ Zezi ba wɩ: «Karanyɩna, Moyisi dɛɛn na pʋpʋnɩ kʋlʋ o tiŋi o pa dɩbam tɩn, kʋ brɩ nɩ, nɔɔnʋ nu-bu na tɩga yɩ o daarɩ o kaanɩ, ba na wʋ lʋgɩ biǝ daanɩ, tʋ wʋm cʋrʋ mʋ maŋɩ sɩ o di kaanɩ wʋm sɩ ba wanɩ ba lʋ biǝ o nu-bu wʋlʋ na tɩgɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: Now there were seven brothers. The first one married a woman; when he died, he left no children.
local: Kʋntʋ, nu-biǝ barpɛ ya mʋ wʋra. Ba dɩdʋa ma di kaanɩ, yɩ o tɩ o daar-o, yɩ o wʋ lʋgɩ bu.

eng: The second married her and died without leaving any children. The third did the same.
local: Wʋlʋ na saŋɩ tɩn laan ma di kadǝm wʋm. O dɩ ma tɩ o daarɩ kaanɩ wʋm, yɩ ba dɩ wʋ lʋgɩ bu. Batɔ tu wʋm dɩ daa ma di-o.

eng: None of the seven left any children. Finally, the woman died.
local: Ba maama barpɛ di kaanɩ wʋm, yɩ ba wʋ lʋgɩ bu dɩd-o. Ba maama na tɩgɩ tɩn, kaanɩ wʋm dɩ laan ma tɩ.

eng: At the resurrection, when they all rise up, whose wife will she be? All seven were married to her.
local: Ba maama barpɛ na di kaanɩ wʋm tɩn, ba laan na tu ba bi ba yagɩ tʋʋnɩ, bantʋ wɔɔ mʋ wʋ́ ta te kaanɩ wʋm?».

eng: Jesus said to them, Isnt this the reason you are wrong, because you dont know either the scriptures or Gods power?.
local: Zezi ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Abam tiini á tusi mʋ. Abam wʋ lwarɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm zaasɩm dɩm kuri, yɩ á ta yǝri Wɛ dam dɩm na yɩ te tɩn. Á wʋ tusi cɩga na?.

eng: When people rise from the dead, they wont marry nor will they be given in marriage. Instead, they will be like Gods angels.
local: Nɔɔna na tu ba bi ba yagɩ tʋʋnɩ, ba lagɩ ba taa nyɩ dɩ Wɛ malɛsɩ sɩm na yɩ te tɩn mʋ, ba ba di kaana, kaana dɩ nan ba zʋʋrɩ banna dɩ.

eng: As for the resurrection from the dead, havent you read in the scroll from Moses, in the passage about the burning bush, how God said to Moses, I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob?.
local: Kʋ nan na yɩ twa biim dɩm dɩ, Moyisi na pʋpʋnɩ kʋlʋ tɩn brɩ nɩ nɔɔna wʋ́ sɩɩnɩ ba bi. Abam maŋɩ á wʋ karɩmɩ kʋlʋ Wɛ na tagɩ dɩ Moyisi DƖ na brɩ DƖ tɩtɩ dɩd-o sabarɩ puŋu kʋm wʋnɩ maŋa kalʋ tɩn na? Wɛ dɛɛn tagɩ DƖ wɩ: ‹Amʋ yɩ Abraham Wɛ, dɩ Yizakɩ Wɛ, dɩ Zakɔbɩ Wɛ mʋ.›.

eng: He isnt the God of the dead but of the living. You are seriously mistaken.
local: Kʋ brɩ nɩ bantʋ daa ta jɩgɩ ŋwɩa mʋ Wɛ tee nɩ, bɛŋwaanɩ Baŋa-Wɛ yɩ naŋʋna Wɛ mʋ, sɩ DƖ daɩ twa Wɛ. Mʋ kʋ kuri á tiini á tusi dɩ á na bʋŋɩ te tɩn.».

eng: One of the legal experts heard their dispute and saw how well Jesus answered them. He came over and asked him, Which commandment is the most important of all.
local: Ba na wʋra ba ŋɔɔnɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, Wɛ cullu karanyɩna tu wʋdoŋ ma yi ba te, yɩ o ni ba taanɩ dɩm. O ma lwarɩ nɩ Zezi tiini o kwaanɩ dɩ o na lǝri Sadusɩan tiinǝ bam te tɩn. O laan ma twɛ Zezi te, yɩ o bwe o wɩ: «Wɛ niǝ yam maama wʋnɩ dɔɔ mʋ yɩ kamunu dɩ dwe?».

eng: Jesus replied, The most important one is Israel, listen! Our God is the one Lord.
local: Zezi ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «Wɛ ni dɩlʋ na yɩ kamunu dɩ dwe ya maama tɩn mʋ tɩntʋ: ‹Yisɩrayɛlɩ tiinǝ-ba, cǝgi-na. Yuutu Baŋa-Wɛ mʋ yɩ dɩbam Yuutu dɩdʋa má.

eng: and you must love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your being, with all your mind, and with all your strength.
local: Ta n soe n Yuutu Baŋa-Wɛ dɩ n bɩcarɩ maama, dɩ n wʋ maama, dɩ n wʋbʋŋa maama, dɩ n dam maama.›.

eng: The second is this, You will love your neighbor as yourself . No other commandment is greater than these.
local: Wɛ ni dɩlʋ na saŋɩ tɩn mʋ tɩntʋ: ‹Ta n soe n doŋ nɩ n tɩtɩ te.› Wɛ ni dɩdoŋ daa tǝrǝ dɩ na yɩ kamunu dɩ dwe niǝ yantʋ yale.».

eng: The legal expert said to him, Well said, Teacher. You have truthfully said that God is one and there is no other besides him.
local: Wɛ cullu karanyɩna tu wʋm ma ta dɩ Zezi o wɩ: «Karanyɩna, n tagɩ cɩga. Nmʋ na tagɩ n wɩ, Wɛ dɩdʋa yɩranɩ mʋ wʋra yɩ DƖ doŋ daa tǝrǝ tɩn, kʋ yɩ cɩga mʋ.

eng: And to love God with all of the heart, a full understanding, and all of ones strength, and to love ones neighbor as oneself is much more important than all kinds of entirely burned offerings and sacrifices.
local: Kʋ maŋɩ sɩ dɩ́ taá soe Wɛ dɩ dɩ́ wʋ maama, dɩ dɩ́ wʋbʋŋa maama, dɩ dɩ́ dam maama, sɩ dɩ́ daarɩ dɩ́ taá soe dɩ́ donnǝ nɩ dɩ́ tɩtɩ te. Dɩ́ na kɩ kʋntʋ, dɩ́ kɩ lanyɩranɩ kʋ dwe dɩ́ na mɛ vara naa woŋo kʋlʋ maama dɩ́ ma kaanɩ-DƖ.».

eng: When Jesus saw that he had answered with wisdom, he said to him, You arent far from Gods kingdom. After that, no one dared to ask him any more questions.
local: Zezi na nɛ nɩ o lǝri taanɩ dɩm lanyɩranɩ dɩ wʋbʋŋ-ŋʋna tɩn, o ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Nmʋ batwarɩ dɩ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm.» Kʋ na zɩgɩ maŋa kam kʋntʋ nɩ, nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ daa maa kwarɩ sɩ o bwe-o taanɩ.

eng: While Jesus was teaching in the temple, he said, Why do the legal experts say that the Christ is Davids son?.
local: Zezi dɛɛn maa wʋ Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm nɩ o brɩ nɔɔna, yɩ o ta o wɩ: «Wɛ cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ bam yǝni ba brɩ nɩ, Krisi wʋm Wɛ na tʋŋɩ tɩn yɩ Pɛ Davidi dwi tu mʋ. Kʋ nan wʋ́ wanɩ kʋ taa yɩ kʋntʋ na?.

eng: David himself, inspired by the Holy Spirit, said, The Lord said to my lord, Sit at my right side until I turn your enemies into your footstool.
local: Wɛ Joro kʋm dɛɛn pɛ Davidi tɩtɩ ta o wɩ: ‹Yuutu Baŋa-Wɛ tagɩ dɩ a Yuutu DƖ wɩ: Ba n jǝni amʋ jazɩm nɩ, sɩ a laan kɩ nmʋ dʋna maama n nɛ kuri nɩ.›.

eng: David himself calls him Lord, so how can he be Davids son? The large crowd listened to him with delight.
local: Davidi tɩtɩ nan na sɩɩnɩ o bǝŋi Krisi wʋm o wɩ o Yuutu tɩn, dɩbam nan daa wʋ́ kɩ ta mʋ dɩ́ bǝŋ-o nɩ Davidi naa daga?» Zezi na brɩ Wɛ cɩga kam kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ ma pa nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋlʋ na je kʋ cǝgi tɩn wʋ poli zanzan.

eng: As he was teaching, he said, Watch out for the legal experts. They like to walk around in long robes. They want to be greeted with honor in the markets.
local: O daa ta ma brɩ-ba yɩ o wɩ: «Taá yɩrɩ-na á tɩtɩ dɩ cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ bam. Bantʋ soe sɩ ba taa zʋʋrɩ gwar-dɩdwaarʋ, sɩ ba taa beerǝ yɩ nɔɔna nii-ba. Ba na ve yaga, ba lagɩ sɩ nɔɔna maama taa tui ba zuli-ba mʋ.

eng: They long for places of honor in the synagogues and at banquets.
local: Ba na yǝni ba zʋ ba Wɛ-di sɩm, ba lagɩ je-kamunnu mʋ sɩ ba taa je yigǝ nɩ. Ba na ve nɔɔnʋ sɔŋɔ sɩ ba di candiǝ, kʋ yɩ je-ŋʋŋa jǝgǝ nɩ mʋ ba lagɩ sɩ ba taa je da.

eng: They are the ones who cheat widows out of their homes, and to show off they say long prayers. They will be judged most harshly.
local: Ba maa yǝni ba ganɩ kadǝnǝ ba joŋi kʋlʋ maama ba na jɩgɩ tɩn, yɩ ba kwǝri ba loori Wɛ dɩ bɩtar-dɩdwaarʋ, sɩ kʋ taa nyɩ nɩ ba yɩ nɔn-ŋʋna mʋ te. Wɛ wʋ́ tiini DƖ pa bantʋ na cam kʋ ja gaalɩ.».

eng: Jesus sat across from the collection box for the temple treasury and observed how the crowd gave their money. Many rich people were throwing in lots of money.
local: Zezi ma vu o jǝni Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm wʋnɩ, jǝgǝ kalʋ daka na zɩgɩ da yɩ nɔɔna dɩ Wɛ pɛɛra ka wʋnɩ tɩn tɩkǝri nɩ. O maa je o nii nɔɔna bam na ve ba dɩ ba sǝbiǝ ka wʋnɩ te tɩn. Nadunǝ zanzan ma ba ba dɩ sǝbu zanzan ka wʋnɩ.

eng: One poor widow came forward and put in two small copper coins worth a penny.
local: Kadǝm wʋdoŋ na yɩ yinigǝ tu tɩn ma ja sǝbu-dal-balwa bale, o ja vu o dɩ da.

eng: Jesus called his disciples to him and said, I assure you that this poor widow has put in more than everyone whos been putting money in the treasury.
local: Zezi na nɛ kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma bǝŋi o karabiǝ bam, yɩ o ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, kadǝm wʋntʋ na yɩ yinigǝ tu tɩn, o dɩ sǝbu kʋ dwǝni nɔɔna balʋ maama na dɩ da tɩn.

eng: All of them are giving out of their spare change. But she from her hopeless poverty has given everything she had, even what she needed to live on.
local: Bantʋ maama lɩ ba daarɩ sǝbiǝ mʋ tata. Kadǝm wʋm nan yɩ yinigǝ tu mʋ, yɩ o kwe woŋo kʋlʋ maama o na jɩgɩ sɩ o ma yǝgi o ni-wʋdiu tɩn o dɩ da.».

eng: When the Sabbath was over, Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James, and Salome bought spices so that they could go and anoint Jesus dead body.
local: Ba siun dɛ dɩm ma ba dɩ kɛ. Mari wʋlʋ na nuŋi Magɩdala dɩ Mari wʋlʋ na yɩ Zakɩ nu tɩn dɩ Salomɩ ma zaŋɩ ba nuŋi ba vu ba yǝgi tralɩ nugǝ, sɩ ba ma turi Zezi yɩra yam.

eng: It was two days before Passover and the Festival of Unleavened Bread. The chief priests and legal experts through cunning tricks were searching for a way to arrest Jesus and kill him.
local: Kʋ dɛɛn daarɩ da yale mʋ sɩ Pakɩ candiǝ kam yi. Candiǝ kam kʋntʋ nɩ mʋ Zwifǝ bam yǝni ba di dɩpɛ dɩlʋ na ba jɩgɩ dabɩlɩ tɩn. Zwifǝ bam kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam dɩ ba cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ bam maa kwaanɩ ba lagɩ cwǝŋǝ, sɩ ba kɩ swan ba ma ja Zezi ba gʋ.

eng: Very early on the first day of the week, just after sunrise, they came to the tomb.
local: Dɛ dɩlʋ na saŋɩ siun dɛ dɩm tɩn, ba maa zaŋɩ ba go tɩga dɩ wɛ wʋra dɩ pulǝ, ba vu yibeeli dɩm jǝgǝ kam.

eng: But they agreed that it shouldnt happen during the festival; otherwise, there would be an uproar among the people.
local: Ba ma ta ba wɩ: «Dɩ́ nan wʋ maŋɩ sɩ dɩ́ ja-o candiǝ kam maŋa nɩ, sɩ kʋʋ́ pa vuvugǝ jǝni nɔɔna bam tɩtarɩ nɩ.».

eng: They were saying to each other, Whos going to roll the stone away from the entrance for us.
local: Ba na maa ve tɩn, ba ma bwɛ daanɩ ba wɩ: «Wɔɔ mʋ wʋ́ bibili kandwɛ dɩlʋ na pɩ yibeeli dɩm ni tɩn o yagɩ daa nɩ o pa dɩbam?».

eng: Jesus was at Bethany visiting the house of Simon, who had a skin disease. During dinner, a woman came in with a vase made of alabaster and containing very expensive perfume of pure nard. She broke open the vase and poured the perfume on his head.
local: Zezi dɛɛn maa wʋ Betani nɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ sɔŋɔ nɩ, o yɩrɩ mʋ Simɔn. Wʋntʋ dɛɛn ya yɩ nanyɔŋɔ mʋ. Zezi na wʋra o di wʋdiu maŋa kalʋ tɩn, mʋ kaanɩ wʋdoŋ tu o te. O maa jɩgɩ kunkwǝlǝ ka na su dɩ tralɩ nugǝ kalʋ yɩrɩ na yɩ naadɩ tɩn. Nugǝ kam lana yɩ ka yǝlo cana zanzan. O ma puri kunkwǝlǝ kam ni, o lo nugǝ kam Zezi yuu nɩ o ma kɩ-o zulǝ.

eng: When they looked up, they saw that the stone had been rolled away.
local: Kandwɛ dɩm na yɩ kamunu tɩn mʋ te ba ŋɔɔnɩ kʋntʋ. Ba na yi jǝgǝ kam tɩn, ba ma fɔgɩ ba nii ba na dɩdaanɩ kandwɛ dɩm bibili dɩ tigi daa nɩ.

eng: Some grew angry. They said to each other, Why waste the perfume?.
local: Balʋ na tɔgɩ ba wʋra yɩ ba na tɩn badonnǝ maa ba jɩgɩ wʋpolo, yɩ ba tɛ daanɩ ba wɩ: «Bɛɛ mʋ yɩ o cɔgɩ nugǝ kam kʋntʋ?.

eng: Going into the tomb, they saw a young man in a white robe seated on the right side; and they were startled.
local: Ba ma vu ba zʋ bɔɔnɩ dɩm wʋ. Ba na zʋ tɩn, ba ma na nɔn-dʋŋʋ, kʋ na je ba jazɩm seeni. O zʋ gwar-poŋo dɩdɔrɔ mʋ. Ba na nɛ-o tɩn, kʋ ma pa ba yɩra sɔɔrɩ.

eng: This perfume could have been sold for almost a years pay and the money given to the poor. And they scolded her.
local: Dɩ́ ya waɩ dɩ́ yǝgi-ka dɩ́ na sǝbu zanzan, kʋ dwe sǝbu-dala biǝ yatɔ, yɩ dɩ́ ma wǝli yinigǝ tiinǝ.» Ba ma tiini ba jɩgɩ kaanɩ wʋm ba ŋɔɔna dɩ o kǝm dɩm.

eng: But he said to them, Dont be alarmed! You are looking for Jesus of Nazareth, who was crucified. He has been raised. He isnt here. Look, heres the place where they laid him.
local: O ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Á yɩ taá kwarɩ fʋʋnɩ. Abam lagɩ sɩ á na Nazarɛtɩ tu Zezi wʋlʋ ba na jaanɩ ba pa tʋʋn-dagara kam baŋa nɩ ba gʋ tɩn mʋ. O nan tǝri yo. O joori o bi. Nii-na ba ya na tiŋi o yɩra yam mɛ tɩn.

eng: Jesus said, Leave her alone. Why do you make trouble for her? She has done a good thing for me.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Á yag-o. Bɛɛ mʋ yɩ á jɩg-o á yaara? O na kɩ amʋ zulǝ yalʋ tɩn yɩ kǝm-laa mʋ o kɩ-nɩ.

eng: Go, tell his disciples, especially Peter, that he is going ahead of you into Galilee. You will see him there, just as he told you.
local: Nan ve-na á ta dɩ Pɩyɛɛrɩ dɩ Zezi karabiǝ bam maama nɩ, o lagɩ o da yigǝ o vu o taa cǝgi abam Galile nɩ. Dáanɩ mʋ abam wʋ́ vu á na-o. Kʋʋ́ kɩ nɩ o na maŋɩ o ta kʋlʋ o brɩ abam tɩn.».

eng: You always have the poor with you; and whenever you want, you can do something good for them. But you wont always have me.
local: Kʋ nan na yɩ yinigǝ tiinǝ, bantʋ wʋra mʋ dɩ abam maŋa maama, yɩ á wʋ́ wanɩ á zǝni-ba maŋa kalʋ á na lagɩ tɩn. Kʋ daarɩ amʋ nan bá ta wʋra dɩ abam maŋa maama.

eng: Overcome with terror and dread, they fled from the tomb. They said nothing to anyone, because they were afraid. .
local: Kaana bam na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, fʋʋnɩ maa pa ba yɩra saɩ, yɩ ba wʋbʋŋa vugimi. Ba ma nuŋi bɔɔnɩ dɩm wʋnɩ ba duri ba kɛ. Fʋʋnɩ na jɩgɩ-ba kʋntʋ yɩ ba maa ve tɩn, ba ma wʋ tagɩ kʋlʋ na kɩ tɩn ba brɩ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ. .

eng: She has done what she could. She has anointed my body ahead of time for burial.
local: Kaanɩ wʋntʋ kɩ kʋlʋ o na waɩ tɩn mʋ. O na logi nugǝ kam a yɩra nɩ tɩn, kʋ brɩ nɩ o wuuri o turi a yɩra yam mʋ, sɩ ba laan kwe-nɩ ba kɩ.

eng: They promptly reported all of the young mans instructions to those who were with Peter. Afterward, through the work of his disciples, Jesus sent out, from the east to the west, the sacred and undying message of eternal salvation. Amen. .
local: Dɛ dɩm Zezi na bi tɩtɩɩtɩ tɩn maa yɩ dɛ dɩlʋ na saŋɩ Zwifǝ bam siun dɛ dɩm tɩn. O na bi tɩn, o ma puli o brɩ o tɩtɩ Mari wʋlʋ na nuŋi Magɩdala tɩn tee nɩ. Wʋntʋ mʋ yɩ kaanɩ wʋlʋ o ya na zǝli ciciri sɩrpɛ o yɩra nɩ tɩn.

eng: I tell you the truth that, wherever in the whole world the good news is announced, what shes done will also be told in memory of her.
local: A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, ba na wʋ́ tɔɔlɩ amʋ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam lʋgʋ baŋa je sɩm maama nɩ tɩn, baá ta kaanɩ wʋm kʋntʋ dɩ taanɩ sɩ ba taa guli o gulǝ.».

eng: She went and reported to the ones who had been with him, who were mourning and weeping.
local: Mari laan ma vu o ta o brɩ balʋ ya na tɔgɩ dɩ Zezi tɩn. O yi ba te dɩ ba wʋrʋ cɔgɩ zanzan yɩ ba keerǝ.

eng: Judas Iscariot, one of the Twelve, went to the chief priests to give Jesus up to them.
local: Zudasɩ Yiskarɩyo wʋlʋ na yɩ Zezi karabiǝ fugǝ-bale bam wʋ dɩdʋa tɩn laan ma zaŋɩ o nuŋi. O ma vu Zwifǝ kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam te, sɩ o brɩ-ba cwǝŋǝ sɩ ba wanɩ ba ja Zezi.

eng: But even after they heard the news, they didnt believe that Jesus was alive and that Mary had seen him.
local: Ba na ni nɩ Mari wɩ, Zezi joori o bi o ŋwɩ yɩ wʋm na-o tɩn, ba wʋ sɛ o taanɩ dɩm.

eng: When they heard it, they were delighted and promised to give him money. So he started looking for an opportunity to turn him in.
local: Ba na ni o na tagɩ kʋlʋ tɩn, ba wʋ ma poli zanzan. Ba ma go ni nɩ baá pa-o sǝbu. O laan maa kwaanɩ o beeri cwǝŋǝ sɩ o ja Zezi o kɩ ba jɩŋa nɩ.

eng: After that he appeared in a different form to two of them who were walking along in the countryside.
local: Kʋ kwaga seeni, Zezi ma lǝni o yibiyǝ yɩ o brɩ o tɩtɩ o karabiǝ bale. Ba ya nuŋi Zeruzalɛm mʋ ba maa ve tɩ-balaŋa kadoŋ.

eng: On the first day of the Festival of Unleavened Bread, when the Passover lamb was sacrificed, the disciples said to Jesus, Where do you want us to prepare for you to eat the Passover meal.
local: Kʋ ma ba kʋ yi dɛ dɩlʋ ba na puli sɩ ba di dɩpɛ dɩlʋ na ba jɩgɩ dabɩlɩ dɩ wʋnɩ tɩn. Dɛ dɩm kʋntʋ nɩ Zwifǝ bam yǝni ba gʋɩ pibalɩ mʋ, ba ma di Pakɩ candiǝ kam. Zezi karabiǝ bam ma vu ba bwe-o ba wɩ: «Nmʋ lagɩ sɩ dɩ́ di Pakɩ candiǝ kam jǝgǝ kɔɔ nɩ mʋ, sɩ dɩ́ vu dɩ́ ti woŋo maama yigǝ dɩ́ pa-m?».

eng: When they returned, they reported it to the others, but they didnt believe them.
local: Ba na nɛ-o tɩn, ba ma joori ba vu Zeruzalɛm ba ta ba brɩ ba donnǝ karabiǝ bam. Ba maa daa ta wʋ sɛ nɩ kʋ yɩ cɩga.

eng: He sent two of his disciples and said to them, Go into the city. A man carrying a water jar will meet you. Follow him.
local: O ma tʋŋɩ o karabiǝ bam bale, yɩ o ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Zaŋɩ-na á vu tɩʋ kʋm wʋ. Á na yi da, nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ wʋ́ jeeri abam o na zɩŋɩ kɔɔrʋ dɩ na. Sɩ á da-na o kwaga á vu.

eng: Finally he appeared to the eleven while they were eating. Jesus criticized their unbelief and stubbornness because they didnt believe those who saw him after he was raised up.
local: Kʋntʋ kwaga nɩ, Zezi laan ma ba o brɩ o tɩtɩ o karabiǝ fugǝ-dɩdʋa kam, ba na wʋra ba di wʋdiu maŋa kalʋ tɩn. O ma kaanɩ-ba dɩ ba wʋbʋŋa na muri yɩ ba wʋ sɛ Wɛ cɩga kam tɩn, bɛŋwaanɩ ba vɩn sɩ ba sɛ balʋ na tagɩ ba brɩ-ba nɩ wʋntʋ joori o bi yɩ ba na-o tɩn.

eng: Wherever he enters, say to the owner of the house, The teacher asks,Where is my guest room where I can eat the Passover meal with my disciples.
local: O na zʋ sɔŋɔ kʋlʋ tɩn, sɩ á dɩ zʋ á ta dɩ kʋ tu nɩ: ‹Dɩ́ Karanyɩna mʋ wɩ, dɩ́ ba dɩ́ bwe dɩ́ nii, digǝ kalʋ wʋm na wʋ́ jǝni da dɩ o karabiǝ bam sɩ ba di Pakɩ candiǝ kam wʋdiu tɩn wʋ yǝn mʋ?›.

eng: He said to them, Go into the whole world and proclaim the good news to every creature.
local: O laan ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Zaŋɩ-na á vu lʋgʋ baŋa je maama, sɩ á tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam á brɩ nabiinǝ maama.

eng: He will show you a large room upstairs already furnished. Prepare for us there.
local: Á na bwe-o kʋntʋ, o laan wʋ́ brɩ abam wɛyuu digǝ. Kaá ta daga lanyɩranɩ, yɩ ka jɩgɩ woŋo kʋlʋ maama dɩ́ na lagɩ tɩn ka wʋnɩ. Ve-na á kwɛ candiǝ kam wǝǝnu tɩm á ti, sɩ dɩ́ laan ba.».

eng: Whoever believes and is baptized will be saved, but whoever doesnt believe will be condemned.
local: Wʋlʋ maama na kɩ o wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ amʋ yɩ o daarɩ o pa ba miis-o na wʋnɩ Wɛ ŋwaanɩ tɩn, Wɛ wʋ́ vrɩ kʋntʋ tu ŋwɩa. Kʋ daarɩ balʋ maama na wʋ sɛ tɩn wʋ́ ga bʋra yɩ ba na cam Wɛ tee nɩ.

eng: The disciples left, came into the city, found everything just as he had told them, and they prepared the Passover meal.
local: O karabiǝ bale bam ma zaŋɩ ba vu tɩʋ kʋm wʋ. Ba ma sɩɩnɩ ba na woŋo maama nɩ Zezi na maŋɩ o ta te tɩn. Ba ma kwɛ Pakɩ candiǝ kam wʋdiu kʋm ba ti.

eng: These signs will be associated with those who believe: they will throw out demons in my name. They will speak in new languages.
local: Balʋ na sɛ amʋ tɩn wʋ́ wanɩ ba taa kɩ wo-kɩnkagɩla yalʋ na brɩ Wɛ dam tɩn. Baá da amʋ yɩrɩ ŋwaanɩ ba zǝli ciciri nɔɔna yɩra nɩ. Baá ta ŋɔɔnɩ ta-gɛ.

eng: That evening, Jesus arrived with the Twelve.
local: Tɩga ma ba ka yi. Zezi ma ba o zʋ digǝ kam dɩ o karabiǝ fugǝ-bale bam.

eng: They will pick up snakes with their hands. If they drink anything poisonous, it will not hurt them. They will place their hands on the sick, and they will get well.
local: Ba na jaanɩ bɩsankwɩ dɩ ba jɩa, sɩ bá kɩ-ba kʋlʋkʋlʋ. Ba na nyɔgɩ coŋo, kʋ bá kɩ-ba kʋlʋkʋlʋ. Ba na daŋɩ ba jɩa yawɩɩna baŋa nɩ, baá na yazurǝ.».

eng: During the meal, Jesus said, I assure you that one of you will betray me—someone eating with me.
local: Ba ma jǝni sɩ ba di wʋdiu kʋm. Ba na wʋra ba di tɩn, Zezi laan ma ta o wɩ: «A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, abam balʋ na je á tɔgɩ á di dɩ amʋ tɩn wʋ nɔɔnʋ dɩdʋa mʋ lagɩ o yǝgi amʋ o pa nɔɔna.».

eng: After the Lord Jesus spoke to them, he was lifted up into heaven and sat down on the right side of God.
local: Dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi na ŋɔɔnɩ o brɩ-ba kʋntʋ tɩn, Wɛ laan ma pa o zaŋɩ o di DƖ sɔŋɔ. O ma vu o jǝni Baŋa-Wɛ jazɩm nɩ.

eng: Deeply saddened, they asked him, one by one, Its not me, is it.
local: Ba na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ ma pa ba wʋ cɔgɩ zanzan. Ba maa bwe-o dɩdʋa dɩdʋa ba wɩ: «Kʋ yɩ amʋ mʋ na?».

eng: But they went out and proclaimed the message everywhere. The Lord worked with them, confirming the word by the signs associated with them.
local: O na diini Wɛ-sɔŋɔ kʋntʋ tɩn, o karabiǝ bam ma nuŋi ba vu ba beeri je maama nɩ ba tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝrǝ kam ba brɩ nɔɔna. Dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm maa wʋ ba tee nɩ o tʋŋa. O ma pa-ba dam sɩ ba taa kɩ wo-kɩnkagɩla, sɩ kʋ brɩ nɩ o taanɩ dɩlʋ ba na brɩ tɩn sɩɩnɩ dɩ yɩ cɩga.

eng: Jesus answered, Its one of the Twelve, one who is dipping bread with me into this bowl.
local: O ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Kʋ yɩ abam fugǝ-bale bam wʋ dɩdʋa mʋ. Kʋ yɩ wʋlʋ na tɔgɩ dɩ amʋ o lu zʋŋa dɩdʋa wʋnɩ tɩn mʋ.

eng: The Human One goes to his death just as it is written about him. But how terrible it is for that person who betrays the Human One! It would have been better for him if he had never been born.
local: Kʋ nan maŋɩ sɩ amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm vu a tɩ, nɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm na maŋɩ kʋ brɩ a taanɩ te tɩn mʋ. Kʋ nan za wʋ́ ta yɩ lɛɛrʋ mʋ kʋ pa nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na wʋ́ yǝgi amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm o pa tɩn. Ba ya na maŋɩ ba wʋ lʋgɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋm kʋntʋ, kʋ ya wʋ́ ta gara.».

eng: While they were eating, Jesus took bread, blessed it, broke it, and gave it to them, and said, Take; this is my body.
local: Ba na wʋra ba di maŋa kalʋ tɩn, Zezi ma kwe dɩpɛ yɩ o kɩ Wɛ le dɩ ŋwaanɩ. O ma daarɩ o fɔ-dɩ o pa o karabiǝ bam yɩ o wɩ: «Joŋi-na, sɩ dɩntʋ yɩ a yɩra mʋ.».

eng: He took a cup, gave thanks, and gave it to them, and they all drank from it.
local: O daa ma kwe tweeru biǝ sana dɩ zʋŋa, yɩ o kɩ Wɛ le tɩ ŋwaanɩ. O ma kwe o pa-ba, yɩ ba maama nyɔ.

eng: He said to them, This is my blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many.
local: O maa wɩ: «Wo-nyɔɔrʋ tɩntʋ mʋ yɩ a jana kalʋ na lagɩ ka nuŋi ka duri sɩ ka ma vrɩ nɔɔna zanzan ba lwarɩm wʋnɩ tɩn.

eng: I assure you that I wont drink wine again until that day when I drink it in a new way in Gods kingdom.
local: A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, a daa bá nyɔ wo-nyɔɔrʋ tɩntʋ, sɩ kʋ taa ve maŋa kam a na wʋ́ jǝni Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋnɩ a daa nyɔ tɩn.».

eng: After singing songs of praise, they went out to the Mount of Olives.
local: Ba laan ma leeni ba zuli Wɛ, yɩ ba daarɩ ba nuŋi ba vu Olivi piu kʋm yuu.

eng: Jesus said to them, You will all falter in your faithfulness to me. It is written, I will hit the shepherd, and the sheep will go off in all directions .
local: Zezi ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Abam maama wʋ́ ba á joori amʋ kwaga nɩ, nɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm na maŋɩ kʋ brɩ te tɩn. Wɛ dɛɛn tagɩ DƖ wɩ: ‹Aá pa ba gʋ nayɩrʋ wʋm, yɩ o peeni sɩm maama jagɩ.›.

eng: But after Im raised up, I will go before you to Galilee.
local: Kʋ daarɩ, Wɛ na joori DƖ bi-nɩ, aá da yigǝ a vu a taa cǝgi abam Galile nɩ.».

eng: Peter said to him, Even if everyone else stumbles, I wont.
local: Zezi na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, Pɩyɛɛrɩ ma ta o wɩ: «Nɔɔna bam maama na maŋɩ ba joori nmʋ kwaga nɩ, amʋ bá fɔgɩ a joori nmʋ kwaga nɩ.».

eng: But Jesus said to him, I assure you that on this very night, before the rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times.
local: Zezi ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ nmʋ sɩ, zɩm tɩtɩɩ dɩntʋ kʋ na wʋ́ loori sɩ cɩbwɩʋ keeri kuni bɩle tɩn, nmʋ wʋ́ fʋ kuni bɩtɔ nɩ n yǝri amʋ.».

eng: But Peter insisted, If I must die alongside you, I wont deny you. And they all said the same thing.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ laan ma fɔgɩ o ta dɩ baarɩ o wɩ: «A brɩ a bá fʋ nɩ a yǝri nmʋ, kʋ na maŋɩ kʋ yɩ ba na lagɩ ba gʋ amʋ dɩ nmʋ maama mʋ dɩ.» Zezi karabiǝ bam maama dɩ ma ta kʋntʋ doŋ.

eng: Jesus and his disciples came to a place called Gethsemane. Jesus said to them, Sit here while I pray.
local: Ba laan ma zaŋɩ ba vu jǝgǝ kadoŋ gaarɩ na wʋra yɩ ba bǝ-ka nɩ Jesemanɩ tɩn. Zezi ma ta dɩ o karabiǝ bam o wɩ: «Á taá je yo, sɩ a vu a warɩ Wɛ sɩ a ba.».

eng: He took Peter, James, and John along with him. He began to feel despair and was anxious.
local: O ma pa Pɩyɛɛrɩ dɩ Zakɩ dɩ Zan tɔgɩ dɩd-o ba vu. O wʋ ma tiini kʋ cɔgɩ zanzan, yɩ o bɩcarɩ di.

eng: He said to them, Im very sad. Its as if Im dying. Stay here and keep alert.
local: O ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «A wʋ mʋ tiini kʋ cɔgɩ sɩ kʋ yi a tʋʋnɩ. Á nan taá zɩgɩ yo, sɩ á warɩ Wɛ sɩ á yɩ zaŋɩ á dɔ.».

eng: Then he went a short distance farther and fell to the ground. He prayed that, if possible, he might be spared the time of suffering.
local: O laan ma fʋfɔ o vu daa. O ma vi o yigǝ tɩga nɩ o loori Wɛ o wɩ, kʋ na waɩ sɩ cam dɩm kɛ dɩ daar-o.

eng: He said, Abba, Father, for you all things are possible. Take this cup of suffering away from me. However—not what I want but what you want.
local: O ma loori Wɛ o wɩ: «Amʋ Ko Baba, nmʋ waɩ woŋo maama n kɩa. Nan pa cam dɩlʋ na lagɩ dɩ ba amʋ baŋa tɩn kɛ. Nan kɩ te na yi nmʋ wʋbʋŋa tɩn, sɩ n yɩ kɩ amʋ wʋbʋŋa na lagɩ kʋlʋ tɩn.».

eng: He came and found them sleeping. He said to Peter, Simon, are you asleep? Couldnt you stay alert for one hour?.
local: O na loori Wɛ o ti tɩn, o ma joori o vu o karabiǝ batɔ bam te. O piini ba tigi ba dɔa. O ma ta dɩ Pɩyɛɛrɩ o wɩ: «Simɔn, n tigi n dɔ mʋ na? Bɛɛ mʋ yɩ n warɩ n ta n niǝ, sɩ n yɩ zaŋɩ n dɔ, sɩ kʋ yi maŋa fɩnfɩɩn dɩ?.

eng: Stay alert and pray so that you wont give in to temptation. The spirit is eager, but the flesh is weak.
local: Fɔgɩ-na á yɩrɩ á tɩtɩ sɩ á taá loori Wɛ, sɩ á yɩ tʋ maŋɩm dɩm maŋa nɩ. Bɛŋwaanɩ nanzwǝnǝ wʋbʋŋa lagɩ sɩ ya sɛ Baŋa-Wɛ, yɩ o yɩra laan bwǝnǝ.».

eng: Again, he left them and prayed, repeating the same words.
local: O ma yagɩ-ba, yɩ o joori o vu o loori Wɛ, nɩ o ya na maŋɩ o loori te tɩn.

eng: And, again, when he came back, he found them sleeping, for they couldnt keep their eyes open, and they didnt know how to respond to him.
local: O daa ta ma joori o vu o piini ba tigi ba dɔa, dɔɔm na tiini dɩ waɩ-ba tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Ba maa yǝri ba daa na wʋ́ ta kʋlʋ dɩd-o tɩn.

eng: He came a third time and said to them, Will you sleep and rest all night? Thats enough! The time has come for the Human One to be betrayed into the hands of sinners.
local: O daa ma vu o loori Wɛ o kɩ kuni bɩtɔ, yɩ o joori o ba ba te. O laan ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Á ta tigi á dɔa mʋ na? Kʋ maŋɩ. Nii-na! Maŋa kam yiǝ, sɩ ba ja amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm ba kɩ nɔn-balwaarʋ jɩa nɩ.

eng: Get up! Lets go! Look, here comes my betrayer.
local: Zaŋɩ-na sɩ dɩ́ vu. Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na lagɩ o yǝgi-nɩ o pa tɩn maa bɩɩna.».

eng: Suddenly, while Jesus was still speaking, Judas, one of the Twelve, came with a mob carrying swords and clubs. They had been sent by the chief priests, legal experts, and elders.
local: Zezi ta na wʋra o ŋɔɔnɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, mʋ Zudasɩ wʋlʋ na yɩ o karabiǝ fugǝ-bale bam wʋ dɩdʋa tɩn tu o yi o te, yɩ nɔn-kɔgɔ zanzan tɔgɩ dɩd-o kʋ ba, yɩ ba ze sʋ-lwaanʋ dɩ da-lwaanʋ. Kʋ yɩ Zwifǝ bam kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam dɩ ba cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ bam dɩ ba nakwa bam mʋ tʋŋɩ-ba.

eng: His betrayer had given them a sign: Arrest the man I kiss, and take him away under guard.
local: Zudasɩ ya maŋɩ o pa kɔgɔ kʋm lwarɩ nɩ, o na ve o kukwǝri nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ tɩn, sɩ ba vu ba ja kʋntʋ tu, sɩ ba fɔgɩ ba ja-o lanyɩranɩ ba ja viiri.

eng: As soon as he got there, Judas said to Jesus, Rabbi! Then he kissed him.
local: O laan ma yɔɔrɩ o vu o yi Zezi te yɩ o wɩ: «Karanyɩna.» O ma daarɩ o kukwǝr-o.

eng: Then they came and grabbed Jesus and arrested him.
local: Nɔɔna bam laan ma yi ba ja Zezi.

eng: One of the bystanders drew a sword and struck the high priests slave and cut off his ear.
local: Nɔɔna balʋ na tɔgɩ ba wʋ Zezi tee nɩ tɩn dɩdʋa ma lɩ o sʋgʋ o ma zagɩ kaanɩm yuutu wʋm tɩntʋŋnʋ zwɛ o go.

eng: Jesus responded, Have you come with swords and clubs to arrest me, like an outlaw?.
local: Zezi laan ma bwe kɔgɔ kʋm o wɩ: «Abam na kwe sʋ-lwaanʋ dɩ da-lwaanʋ á ba sɩ á ja amʋ tɩn, a yɩ ŋwɩɩnʋ mʋ na?.

eng: Day after day, I was with you, teaching in the temple, but you didnt arrest me. But let the scriptures be fulfilled.
local: Dɛ maama a yǝni a wʋ abam tee nɩ a brɩ nɔɔna Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm wʋnɩ, yɩ á ba jaanɩ-nɩ. Kʋ nan maŋɩ sɩ kʋ kɩ kʋntʋ mʋ, sɩ kʋ pa kʋlʋ na maŋɩ kʋ pʋpʋnɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ tɩn sɩɩnɩ kʋ kɩ.».

eng: And all his disciples left him and ran away.
local: O karabiǝ bam maama na nɛ kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma duri ba viiri ba daar-o.

eng: One young man, a disciple, was wearing nothing but a linen cloth. They grabbed him.
local: Nɔn-dʋŋʋ kʋdoŋ ya wʋra, o na tɔgɩ Zezi. O ya kukwǝri gar-jalɩ yɩranɩ mʋ. Ba ma kwaanɩ sɩ ba ja-o.

eng: but he left the linen cloth behind and ran away naked.
local: O ma vrɩ o tɩtɩ o daarɩ o gɔrɔ kʋm o yagɩ o pa-ba, yɩ o duri kalambolo o viiri.

eng: They led Jesus away to the high priest, and all the chief priests, elders, and legal experts gathered.
local: Ba ma ja Zezi ba vu kaanɩm yuutu wʋm sɔŋɔ. Zwifǝ kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam dɩ ba cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ dɩ ba tɩʋ kʋm nakwa bam maama ma gilimi daanɩ jǝgǝ kam kʋntʋ nɩ.

eng: Peter followed him from a distance, right into the high priests courtyard. He was sitting with the guards, warming himself by the fire.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ ma dalɩ o tɔgɩ ba kwaga o da o vu o yi kaanɩm yuutu wʋm sɔŋɔ. O ma zʋ o jǝni dɩ sɔŋɔ kʋm tɩntʋŋna bam o tɔgɩ o weeri mini.

eng: The chief priests and the whole Sanhedrin were looking for testimony against Jesus in order to put him to death, but they couldnt find any.
local: Zwifǝ bam kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam dɩ ba sarɩya-dirǝ nakwa kɔgɔ kʋm maama maa kwaanɩ sɩ ba na cwǝŋǝ kalʋ ba na wʋ́ da da ba ma saŋɩ Zezi sɩ ba laan pa ba gʋ-o tɩn. Ba ma kwaanɩ taan ba ga.

eng: Many brought false testimony against him, but they contradicted each other.
local: Nɔɔna zanzan ma zaŋɩ ba fɔ vwan ba pa-o, yɩ ba na tɛ ba wɩ o kɩ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ tɩn ba tɔgɩ daanɩ.

eng: Some stood to offer false witness against him, saying.
local: Nɔɔna badonnǝ dɩ daa ma zaŋɩ ba fɔ vwan ba pa Zezi ba wɩ:.

eng: We heard him saying, I will destroy this temple, constructed by humans, and within three days I will build another, one not made by humans.
local: «Dɩbam ni nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ na tagɩ o wɩ, oó magɩ Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋntʋ nabiinǝ na lɔgɩ tɩn o dɩ tɩga nɩ, yɩ o joori o ma da yatɔ o lɔ kadoŋ, yɩ kʋ daɩ nabiinǝ mʋ wʋ́ lɔ-ka.».

eng: But their testimonies didnt agree even on this point.
local: Nan dɩ nɔɔna bam na maŋɩ ba tɛ kʋntʋ dɩ tɩn, ba niǝ daa ta ba tɔgɩ daanɩ.

eng: Then the high priest stood up in the middle of the gathering and examined Jesus. Arent you going to respond to the testimony these people have brought against you.
local: Kaanɩm yuutu wʋm laan ma zaŋɩ ba maama tɩtarɩ nɩ, yɩ o bwe Zezi o wɩ: «Nɔɔna bantʋ na tagɩ kʋlʋ ba pa nmʋ tɩn, nmʋ ba jɩgɩ taanɩ sɩ n lǝri na?».

eng: But Jesus was silent and didnt answer. Again, the high priest asked, Are you the Christ, the Son of the blessed one.
local: Zezi maa cɩm yɩ o wʋ lǝri kʋlʋkʋlʋ. Kaanɩm yuutu wʋm ma joori o bwe-o o wɩ: «Nmʋ mʋ yɩ Krisi wʋm Wɛ na tʋŋɩ tɩn na? Nmʋ mʋ yɩ Baŋa-Wɛ dɩlʋ dɩbam na zuli tɩn Bu wʋm na?».

eng: Jesus said, I am. And you will see the Human One sitting on the right side of the Almighty and coming on the heavenly clouds.
local: Zezi ma lǝri o wɩ: «Ɛɛn, amʋ mʋ kʋntʋ. Nan taá ye-na nɩ, á wʋ́ ba á na Nabiin-bu wʋm na je Dam-fɔrɔ Tu Baŋa-Wɛ jazɩm nɩ, yɩ á ta wʋ́ na-o o na nuŋi wɛyuu nɩ o maa bɩɩnɩ kunkwǝǝnu wʋnɩ.».

eng: Then the high priest tore his clothes and said, Why do we need any more witnesses?.
local: Kaanɩm yuutu wʋm na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma zaŋɩ o kaarɩ o tɩtɩ gɔrɔ, sɩ kʋ brɩ nɩ o wʋ tiini kʋ cɔgɩ dɩ Zezi taanɩ dɩm. O ma ta o wɩ: «Dɩ́ daa ba lagɩ maana tiinǝ sɩ ba ŋɔɔnɩ o taanɩ dɩm.

eng: Youve heard his insult against God. What do you think? They all condemned him. He deserves to die.
local: Abam maama maŋɩ á ni o na ŋɔɔnɩ o twɩ Wɛ, o paɩ o dɩ Wɛ ma daanɩ mʋ. Kʋntʋ tɩn, abam wɩ bɛɛ mʋ?» Ba maama ma lǝri ba wɩ, o maŋɩ sɩ o tɩ mʋ.

eng: Some began to spit on him. Some covered his face and hit him, saying, Prophesy! Then the guards took him and beat him.
local: Ba badaara ma twɩ lileeru ba yagɩ o yɩra nɩ. Ba ma kwe gɔrɔ ba ma pu o yiǝ, yɩ ba daarɩ ba mag-o dɩ jagulǝ yɩ ba wɩ: «Lwarɩ n brɩ dɩbam wʋlʋ na magɩ-m tɩn.» Pamaŋna bam ma jɩg-o ba maga.

eng: Meanwhile, Peter was below in the courtyard. A woman, one of the high priests servants, approached.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ ya daa ta tɔgɩ o je kʋnkɔlɔ kʋm nɩ. Kaanɩm yuutu wʋm sɔŋɔ tɩntʋŋ-kana ma ba ka tɔgɩ da ka maa kɛa.

eng: and saw Peter warming himself by the fire. She stared at him and said, You were also with the Nazarene, Jesus.
local: O ma na Pɩyɛɛrɩ na je o weeri mini. O ma fɔgɩ o nii-o lanyɩranɩ, yɩ o wɩ: «Nmʋ dɩ tɔgɩ dɩ Nazarɛtɩ tu Zezi mʋ.».

eng: But he denied it, saying, I dont know what youre talking about. I dont understand what youre saying. And he went outside into the outer courtyard. A rooster crowed.
local: O ma fʋ o wɩ: «A yǝri woŋo kʋlʋ n na tɛ tɩn. A ba ni kʋ kuri.» O ma zaŋɩ o nuŋi o vu mancoŋo ni. Cɩbwɩʋ ma da kʋ keeri.

eng: The female servant saw him and began a second time to say to those standing around, This man is one of them.
local: Tɩntʋŋ-kana kam daa ma na-o. O daa ta ma ta dɩ nɔɔna balʋ na zɩgɩ da tɩn o wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ tɔgɩ o yɩ Zezi kɔgɔ kʋm wʋ dɩdʋa mʋ.».

eng: But he denied it again. A short time later, those standing around again said to Peter, You must be one of them, because you are also a Galilean.
local: O daa ma fʋ o wɩ, o daɩ ba wʋ nɔɔnʋ. Kʋ na kɩ fɩɩn tɩn, nɔɔna balʋ na zɩgɩ da tɩn ma ta dɩ Pɩyɛɛrɩ ba wɩ: «Nmʋ sɩɩnɩ n yɩ ba wʋ dɩdʋa mʋ. Nmʋ dɩ yɩ Galile tu mʋ.».

eng: But he cursed and swore, I dont know this man youre talking about.
local: O laan ma sɔɔlɩ o pa o tɩtɩ, sɩ kʋ taa yɩ nɩ cɩga mʋ o taga, yɩ o wɩ: «A brɩ a yǝri nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ á na tɛ o taanɩ tɩn.».

eng: At that very moment, a rooster crowed a second time. Peter remembered what Jesus told him, Before a rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times. And he broke down, sobbing.
local: O na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, cɩbwɩʋ laan ma da kʋ keeri kʋ kɩ kuni bɩle. Pɩyɛɛrɩ laan ma guli Zezi ya na tagɩ kʋlʋ dɩd-o o wɩ: «Kʋ na wʋ́ loori sɩ cɩbwɩʋ keeri kuni bɩle tɩn, nmʋ wʋ́ fʋ kuni bɩtɔ nɩ n yǝri amʋ.» O na guli kʋntʋ tɩn, o wʋ ma tiini kʋ cɔgɩ zanzan, o ma zaŋɩ o nuŋi yɩ o keerǝ.

eng: Many people have already applied themselves to the task of compiling an account of the events that have been fulfilled among us.
local: A ciloŋ Teofili, a bʋŋɩ sɩ kʋ lana sɩ a pʋpʋnɩ tɔnɔ kʋntʋ a pa nmʋ, sɩ n wanɩ n lwarɩ kʋlʋ na yɩ cɩga tɩn. Nmʋ maŋɩ n ye wo-kamunnu tɩlʋ na kɩ dɩbam tee nɩ tɩn ni nɩ. Nɔɔna badonnǝ dɛɛn mʋ wʋra wo-kamunnu tɩntʋ pulim nɩ. Ba nɛ kʋlʋ maama na kɩ tɩn, yɩ ba brɩ Wɛ kwǝrǝ kam. Ba ma daarɩ ba pa dɩbam lwarɩ kʋlʋ na kɩ tɩn. Nɔɔna zanzan ma kwaanɩ sɩ ba pʋpʋnɩ wǝǝnu tɩntʋ ba tiŋi. Mʋ kʋ kuri a bʋŋɩ sɩ kʋʋ́ ta lamma sɩ a dɩ pʋpʋnɩ-tɩ lanyɩranɩ a pa nmʋ. A kwaanɩ a zaasɩ wǝǝnu tɩm tɩ pulim nɩ mʋ, yɩ a lwarɩ tɩ kuri cɩga cɩga.

eng: I want you to have confidence in the soundness of the instruction you have received.
local: Kʋntʋ tɩn, aá wanɩ a pʋpʋnɩ wǝǝnu tɩntʋ na kɩ te tɩn, sɩ n wanɩ n lwarɩ lanyɩranɩ nɩ, wǝǝnu tɩlʋ ba na tagɩ ba brɩ nmʋ tɩn sɩɩnɩ tɩ yɩ cɩga.

eng: During the rule of King Herod of Judea there was a priest named Zechariah who belonged to the priestly division of Abijah. His wife Elizabeth was a descendant of Aaron.
local: Maŋa kam Erɔdɩ dɛɛn na yɩ Zude pɛ tɩn, nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ mʋ wʋra, o yɩrɩ mʋ Zakari. Wʋntʋ mʋ yɩ Wɛ kaanɩm tu. O nuŋi Abɩa dwi tiinǝ wʋnɩ mʋ. O kaanɩ yɩrɩ mʋ Elizabɛtɩ. Wʋntʋ dɩ dɛɛn nuŋi kaanɩm tu Aarɔn dwi dɩm wʋnɩ mʋ.

eng: They were both righteous before God, blameless in their observance of all the Lords commandments and regulations.
local: Bantʋ maama kɩ lanyɩranɩ Wɛ yigǝ nɩ, yɩ ba sɛ Wɛ niǝ yam dɩ DƖ cullu tɩm maama.

eng: They had no children because Elizabeth was unable to become pregnant and they both were very old.
local: Elizabɛtɩ dɛɛn yɩ ka-dʋgʋ mʋ yɩ o ba jɩgɩ bu. Ba maama dɛɛn kwɩn mʋ.

eng: One day Zechariah was serving as a priest before God because his priestly division was on duty.
local: Maŋa ma ba yi sɩ Abɩa dwi tiinǝ bam zʋ Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm ba taa tʋŋɩ ba pa Wɛ. Zakari maa tɔgɩ o wʋra o tʋŋa.

eng: Following the customs of priestly service, he was chosen by lottery to go into the Lords sanctuary and burn incense.
local: Wɛ kaanɩm tiinǝ bam yǝni ba ta jɔrɔ mʋ sɩ ba nii, ba wʋnɩ wɔɔ mʋ wʋ́ zʋ Wɛ-di-laa kʋm wʋnɩ o zwɛ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ lwǝm na ywǝmmǝ tɩn o pa Wɛ. Ba ma ta jɔrɔ yɩ Zakari di.

eng: All the people who gathered to worship were praying outside during this hour of incense offering.
local: O ma sɩɩnɩ o zʋ sɩ o zwɛ wǝǝnu tɩm o pa Wɛ. Maŋa kam o na wʋ digǝ kam wʋnɩ tɩn, nɔn-kɔgɔ zanzan dɛɛn ma wʋ kʋnkɔlɔ kʋm nɩ ba zɩgɩ ba warɩ Wɛ.

eng: An angel from the Lord appeared to him, standing to the right of the altar of incense.
local: Baŋa-Wɛ malɛka laan ma da ka ba o te, ka zɩgɩ wǝǝnu zwɛɛm bimbim dɩm jazɩm nɩ.

eng: When Zechariah saw the angel, he was startled and overcome with fear.
local: Zakari na nɛ-ka tɩn, o maa yɩ yǝǝu yɩ fʋʋnɩ zʋ-o lanyɩranɩ.

eng: The angel said, Dont be afraid, Zechariah. Your prayers have been heard. Your wife Elizabeth will give birth to your son and you must name him John.
local: Malɛka kam ma ta dɩd-o ka wɩ: «Zakari, yɩ ta n kwarɩ fʋʋnɩ, sɩ Wɛ ni nmʋ na loori-DƖ kʋlʋ tɩn. Nmʋ kaanɩ wʋm laan wʋ́ ja pugǝ o lʋ bu o pa-m, sɩ n pa o yɩrɩ nɩ Zan.

eng: He will be a joy and delight to you, and many people will rejoice at his birth.
local: O lʋra kam wʋ́ pa n na wʋpolo zanzan. Nɔɔna zanzan dɩ wʋ́ tiini ba taa jɩgɩ wʋpolo dɩ o lʋra kam.

eng: for he will be great in the Lords eyes. He must not drink wine and liquor. He will be filled with the Holy Spirit even before his birth.
local: Oó ta yɩ nɔn-kamunu Baŋa-Wɛ tee nɩ. O nan yɩ zaŋɩ o nyɔ sana naa woŋo kʋlʋ na kɩkarɩ yiǝ tɩn. Wɛ Joro kʋm wʋ́ ta wʋ o tee nɩ maŋa maama, kʋ na wʋ́ sɩŋɩ o lʋra kam maŋa nɩ tɩn.

eng: He will bring many Israelites back to the Lord their God.
local: O nan wʋ́ pa Yisɩrayɛlɩ dwi tiinǝ zanzan wʋbʋŋa joori ya taa tɔgɩ ba Yuutu Baŋa-Wɛ.

eng: He will go forth before the Lord, equipped with the spirit and power of Elijah. He will turn the hearts of fathers back to their children, and he will turn the disobedient to righteous patterns of thinking. He will make ready a people prepared for the Lord.
local: Oó tɔgɩ dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm yigǝ, yɩ Wɛ Joro kʋm dam wʋ́ ta wʋ o tee nɩ, nɩnɛɛnɩ Wɛ nijoŋnu Eli dɛɛn na yɩ te tɩn. Oó pa kwǝ joori ba taa soe ba biǝ, yɩ o kwǝri o pa vɩnvɩɩna joori ba taa jɩgɩ nɔn-ŋʋna wʋbʋŋa, sɩ o fɔgɩ o kwɛ dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm nɔɔna bam yigǝ o pa-o.».

eng: Zechariah said to the angel, How can I be sure of this? My wife and I are very old.
local: Zakari laan ma bwe malɛka kam o wɩ: «A yɩ nankwɩan mʋ, yɩ a kaanɩ dɩ kwɩn. Bɛɛ mʋ wʋ́ pa a lwarɩ nɩ nmʋ taanɩ dɩm yɩ cɩga?».

eng: The angel replied, I am Gabriel. I stand in Gods presence. I was sent to speak to you and to bring this good news to you.
local: Malɛka kam ma lǝr-o ka wɩ: «Amʋ nan yɩ Gabrɩyɛlɩ mʋ, wʋlʋ na zɩgɩ Wɛ tee nɩ tɩn. DƖ tʋŋɩ-nɩ sɩ a ba a ŋɔɔnɩ dɩ nmʋ, sɩ a ta ta-ŋʋm dɩm a brɩ-m.

eng: Know this: What I have spoken will come true at the proper time. But because you didnt believe, you will remain silent, unable to speak until the day when these things happen.
local: Nmʋ nan na wʋ sɛ a taanɩ dɩm tɩn, Wɛ wʋ́ pa n ni mǝǝri. Maŋa kam na yiǝ, wǝǝnu tɩlʋ a na tagɩ a brɩ-m tɩn wʋ́ sɩɩnɩ tɩ kɩ. Nan ta n ye sɩ nmʋ bá wanɩ n ŋɔɔnɩ, sɩ kʋ taa ve maŋa kam wǝǝnu tɩm na wʋ́ kɩ tɩn.».

eng: Meanwhile, the people were waiting for Zechariah, and they wondered why he was in the sanctuary for such a long time.
local: Kʋntʋ maama na wʋra kʋ kɩ tɩn, nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm maa zɩgɩ ba cǝgi Zakari sɩ o nuŋi Wɛ-di-laa kʋm wʋnɩ. Ba ma cǝg-o taan ba ga. Kʋ ma kɩ-ba yǝǝu dɩ o na daanɩ te tɩn.

eng: When he came out, he was unable to speak to them. They realized he had seen a vision in the temple, for he gestured to them and couldnt speak.
local: O laan ma ba nuŋi, yɩ o warɩ o ŋɔɔnɩ dɩ ba. O ni na mǝǝri tɩn, o maa maɩ o jɩa o maŋɩ o brɩ-ba. Ba ma maanɩ sɩ o nɛ wo-kɩnkagɩlɩ mʋ Wɛ-di-laa kʋm wʋnɩ.

eng: When he completed the days of his priestly service, he returned home.
local: Da yalʋ o na maŋɩ sɩ o taa tʋŋɩ Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm wʋnɩ tɩn ma ba ya ti. O ma joori o vu sɔŋɔ.

eng: Afterward, his wife Elizabeth became pregnant. She kept to herself for five months, saying.
local: Da yantʋ na kɛ tɩn, o kaanɩ wʋm laan ma ja pugǝ. O maa wʋ sɔŋɔ nɩ taan, canɩ sɩnu.

eng: This is the Lords doing. He has shown his favor to me by removing my disgrace among other people.
local: O na wʋra kʋntʋ tɩn, o maa wɩ: «Mʋ woŋo kʋlʋ Yuutu Baŋa-Wɛ na kɩ DƖ pa-nɩ tɩn. DƖ lɩ woŋo kʋlʋ ya na yɩ a cavɩɩra nɔɔna tɩtarɩ nɩ tɩn.».

eng: When Elizabeth was six months pregnant, God sent the angel Gabriel to Nazareth, a city in Galilee.
local: Bɩsankana kadoŋ dɛɛn mʋ wʋra, o yɩrɩ mʋ Mari. O ta yǝri baarʋ. O sɔŋɔ tiinǝ wʋbʋŋa dɛɛn ya yɩ sɩ o zʋ nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ mʋ, yɩ o daa ta wʋ zʋ-o. Nɔɔnʋ wʋm yɩrɩ mʋ Zʋzɛfʋ. O yɩ faŋa faŋa Pɛ Davidi dwi tu mʋ. Mari dɛɛn zʋʋrɩ Galile tɩʋ kʋdoŋ nɩ mʋ, kʋ yɩrɩ mʋ Nazarɛtɩ. Maŋa kam Zakari kaanɩ Elizabɛtɩ pugǝ kam na yi nɩnɛɛnɩ canɩ sɩrdʋ tɩn, Baŋa-Wɛ daa ma tʋŋɩ malɛka Gabrɩyɛlɩ sɩ ka vu Mari te.

eng: When the angel came to her, he said, Rejoice, favored one! The Lord is with you.
local: Ka ma zʋ o te ka jɔɔn-o yɩ ka wɩ: «Kɩ wʋpolo, n Yuutu Baŋa-Wɛ wʋ nmʋ tee nɩ sɩ DƖ zǝni-m lanyɩranɩ.».

eng: She was confused by these words and wondered what kind of greeting this might be.
local: Mari na ni ka na tagɩ kʋlʋ tɩn, o bɩcarɩ ma di. O ma bʋŋɩ o wɩ: «Taanɩ dɩntʋ kuri mʋ bɛɛ?».

eng: The angel said, Dont be afraid, Mary. God is honoring you.
local: Malɛka kam laan ma ta ka wɩ: «Mari, yɩ ta n kwarɩ fʋʋnɩ, sɩ Baŋa-Wɛ kɩ nmʋ yu-yoŋo.

eng: Look! You will conceive and give birth to a son, and you will name him Jesus.
local: Nmʋ wʋ́ ba n ja pugǝ n lʋ bǝkǝrǝ, sɩ n pa o yɩrɩ nɩ Zezi.

eng: He will be great and he will be called the Son of the Most High. The Lord God will give him the throne of David his father.
local: Wʋntʋ wʋ́ ta yɩ nɔn-kamunu. Baá ta bǝ-o ba wɩ, Baŋa-Wɛ Bu. Dɩ́ Yuutu Wɛ wʋ́ pa o di paarɩ nɩ o ko-nakwɩ Davidi dɛɛn na di paarɩ faŋa faŋa te tɩn.

eng: At daybreak, the chief priests—with the elders, legal experts, and the whole Sanhedrin—formed a plan. They bound Jesus, led him away, and turned him over to Pilate.
local: Tɩga na tu ka pʋʋrɩ tɩtɩɩtɩ tɩn, Zwifǝ kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam dɩ ba tɩʋ kʋm nakwa bam, dɩ ba karanyɩna tiinǝ bam dɩ ba sarɩya-dirǝ nakwa kɔgɔ kʋm maama ma ba ba kɩ ni daanɩ. Ba ma pa ba ja Zezi ba vɔ, yɩ ba ja-o ba vu ba kɩ gʋvɩrma tu Pilatɩ jɩŋa nɩ.

eng: He will rule over Jacobs house forever, and there will be no end to his kingdom.
local: O ma wʋ́ ta yɩ Zakɔbɩ dwi tiinǝ bam pɛ maŋa maama. O paarɩ dɩm daa bá ti maŋa dɩ maŋa.».

eng: Pilate questioned him, Are you the king of the Jews? Jesus replied, Thats what you say.
local: Pilatɩ ma bwe-o o wɩ: «Nmʋ mʋ yɩ Zwifǝ bam pɛ wʋm na?» Zezi ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «Mʋ ka da n taga.».

eng: Then Mary said to the angel, How will this happen since I havent had sexual relations with a man.
local: Mari ma lǝri malɛka kam o wɩ: «Kʋntʋ wʋ́ kɩ tɩta mʋ kʋ kɩ, yɩ a ta wʋ zʋ barʋ?».

eng: The chief priests were accusing him of many things.
local: Zwifǝ kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam ma tiini ba maŋɩ wǝǝnu zanzan ba pa-o.

eng: The angel replied, The Holy Spirit will come over you and the power of the Most High will overshadow you. Therefore, the one who is to be born will be holy. He will be called Gods Son.
local: Malɛka kam ma lǝri ka wɩ: «Kʋ na wʋ́ kɩ te tɩn, Wɛ Joro kʋm wʋ́ ba nmʋ te, yɩ Baŋa-Wɛ tɩtɩ dam-fɔrɔ kʋm wʋ́ ba kʋ kwǝli nmʋ. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ bu wʋlʋ n na wʋ́ lʋ tɩn wʋ́ ta yɩ Wɛ tɩtɩ nyɩm, yɩ ba taa bǝ-o nɩ Baŋa-Wɛ Bu.

eng: Pilate asked him again, Arent you going to answer? What about all these accusations.
local: Pilatɩ ta ma joori o bwe-o o wɩ: «Nmʋ ba lagɩ n lǝri ba taanɩ dɩm na? Nmʋ wʋ ni ba na ŋɔɔnɩ kʋlʋ maama ba pa nmʋ tɩn na?».

eng: Look, even in her old age, your relative Elizabeth has conceived a son. This woman who was labeled unable to conceive is now six months pregnant.
local: Maanɩ n nii nmʋ cʋrʋ Elizabɛtɩ na yɩ te. Wʋntʋ jaanɩ pugǝ mʋ sɩ o lʋ bu, yɩ o maŋɩ o kwɩn. Nɔɔna ya bʋŋɩ sɩ o yɩ ka-dʋgʋ mʋ, yɩ o laan jɩgɩ pugǝ ka yi nɩnɛɛnɩ canɩ sɩrdʋ.

eng: But Jesus gave no more answers, so that Pilate marveled.
local: Zezi daa ta ma wʋ lǝri ba taanɩ dɩm dɩlʋdɩlʋ. Kʋ ma pa Pilatɩ tiini o yɩ yǝǝu dɩd-o.

eng: Nothing is impossible for God.
local: Kʋlʋkʋlʋ tǝrǝ Baŋa-Wɛ na bá wanɩ DƖ kɩ.».

eng: During the festival, Pilate released one prisoner to them, whomever they requested.
local: Bɩnɩ maama Pakɩ candiǝ kam maŋa na yǝni ka yi, gʋvɩrma tu yǝni o lɩ pɩɩnʋ dɩdʋa mʋ o pa nɔɔna bam, nɩnɛɛnɩ bantʋ na lagɩ wʋlʋ tɩn.

eng: Then Mary said, I am the Lords servant. Let it be with me just as you have said. Then the angel left her.
local: Mari ma ta o wɩ: «Amʋ yɩ dɩ́ Yuutu Wɛ tɩntʋŋ-kana mʋ. Wɛ nan pa kʋ kɩ nɩ n na tagɩ te dɩ amʋ tɩn.» Malɛka kam laan ma viiri.

eng: A man named Barabbas was locked up with the rebels who had committed murder during an uprising.
local: Pɩɩnʋ wʋdoŋ dɛɛn maa wʋra, o yɩrɩ mʋ Barabasɩ. Ba dɛɛn ya pɩ-o dɩ balʋ na wǝli daanɩ ba kɩ jara dɩ ba tɩʋ kʋm yigǝ tiinǝ yɩ ba gʋ nɔɔnʋ tɩn mʋ.

eng: Mary got up and hurried to a city in the Judean highlands.
local: Da yam kʋntʋ ni nɩ Mari ma zaŋɩ o kɩ lɩla o maa diini Zude tɩʋ kʋdoŋ na wʋ pweeru tɩtarɩ nɩ.

eng: The crowd pushed forward and asked Pilate to release someone, as he regularly did.
local: Nɔɔna bam ma ba ba la kɔgɔ, yɩ ba ta dɩ Pilatɩ sɩ o lɩ pɩɩnʋ dɩdʋa o pa-ba, nɩ o na yǝni o kɩ te tɩn.

eng: She entered Zechariahs home and greeted Elizabeth.
local: O ma vu o yi Zakari sɔŋɔ. O ma zʋ o na o kaanɩ Elizabɛtɩ. O ma kɩ o pwǝgǝ.

eng: Pilate answered them, Do you want me to release to you the king of the Jews.
local: Pilatɩ ma bwe-ba o wɩ: «Á lagɩ sɩ a lɩ Zwifǝ bam pɛ wʋm mʋ a pa abam na?».

eng: When Elizabeth heard Marys greeting, the child leaped in her womb, and Elizabeth was filled with the Holy Spirit.
local: Maŋa kam Elizabɛtɩ na ni Mari na jɔɔn-o tɩn, o bu wʋm na tigi o wʋnɩ tɩn ma pipiri. Wɛ Joro kʋm ma ba Elizabɛtɩ te.

eng: He knew that the chief priests had handed him over because of jealousy.
local: O dɛɛn bwe-ba kʋntʋ, bɛŋwaanɩ o ye nɩ kʋ yɩ wʋ-gʋrʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam ya jaanɩ Zezi ba kɩ o jɩŋa nɩ.

eng: With a loud voice she blurted out, God has blessed you above all women, and he has blessed the child you carry.
local: yɩ o ŋɔɔnɩ baŋa baŋa dɩ kwǝr-dɩa o wɩ: «Wɛ tiini DƖ kɩ nmʋ yu-yoŋo DƖ dwǝni kaana maama. Kʋ na yɩ bu wʋlʋ na wʋ n wʋnɩ tɩn, Wɛ kɩ-o yu-yoŋo lanyɩranɩ.

eng: But the chief priests stirred up the crowd to have him release Barabbas to them instead.
local: Kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam ma sʋgɩ nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm, ba pa ba ta dɩ Pilatɩ nɩ ba lagɩ sɩ o yagɩ Barabasɩ mʋ o pa-ba.

eng: Why do I have this honor, that the mother of my Lord should come to me?.
local: Amʋ yɩ wɔɔ mʋ sɩ a Yuutu nu ba amʋ te?.

eng: Pilate replied, Then what do you want me to do with the one you call king of the Jews.
local: Pilatɩ laan ma bwe-ba o wɩ: «Sɩ wʋlʋ á na bǝ-o nɩ Zwifǝ bam pɛ tɩn, abam lagɩ sɩ a kɩ-o ta mʋ?».

eng: As soon as I heard your greeting, the baby in my womb jumped for joy.
local: N jɔɔnɩm dɩm na tu dɩ tʋ a zwɛ nɩ tɩn, bu wʋm na wʋ a wʋnɩ tɩn tigi o pipiri mʋ, o na jɩgɩ wʋpolo tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: They shouted back, Crucify him.
local: Ba ma kaasɩ baŋa baŋa ba wɩ: «Pa-o tʋʋn-dagara baŋa nɩ n gʋ.».

eng: Happy is she who believed that the Lord would fulfill the promises he made to her.
local: Nmʋ na sɛ nɩ Baŋa-Wɛ jɩgɩ cɩga tɩn, nmʋ yɩ yu-yoŋo tu. Kʋlʋ maama Wɛ na tagɩ DƖ brɩ-m tɩn wʋ́ sɩɩnɩ kʋ kɩ.».

eng: Pilate said to them, Why? What wrong has he done? They shouted even louder, Crucify him.
local: O ma joori o bwe-ba o wɩ: «Wʋntʋ cɔgɩ bɛɛ mʋ?» Ba laan ma tiini ba kaasɩ baŋa baŋa ba wɩ: «Pa-o tʋʋn-dagara baŋa nɩ n gʋ.».

eng: Mary said, With all my heart I glorify the Lord!.
local: Mari laan ma ta o wɩ: «Aá pa a Yuutu Baŋa-Wɛ zulǝ dɩ a bɩcarɩ maama.

eng: Pilate wanted to satisfy the crowd, so he released Barabbas to them. He had Jesus whipped, then handed him over to be crucified.
local: Pilatɩ maa lagɩ sɩ o pa kɔgɔ kʋm wʋ poli. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ o ma pa ba pʋrɩ Barabasɩ ba yagɩ. O daarɩ o pa ba magɩ Zezi dɩ balaara, yɩ o pa-ba cwǝŋǝ sɩ ba ja-o ba vu ba pa tʋʋn-dagara baŋa nɩ ba gʋ.

eng: In the depths of who I am I rejoice in God my savior.
local: Wʋntʋ na yɩ a Vɩrnʋ tɩn mʋ pɛ a na wʋpolo dɩ a wʋ maama.

eng: The soldiers led Jesus away into the courtyard of the palace known as the governors headquarters, and they called together the whole company of soldiers.
local: Pamaŋna bam laan ma ja Zezi ba vu ba zʋ gʋvɩrma tu Pilatɩ sɔŋɔ kʋm kʋnkɔlɔ. Ba ma pa pamaŋna bam kɔgɔ kʋm maama gilimi da.

eng: He has looked with favor on the low status of his servant. Look! From now on, everyone will consider me highly favored.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ amʋ yɩ Wɛ tɩntʋŋ-kana balaŋa mʋ, yɩ DƖ nii amʋ baŋa nɩ. Kʋ na zɩgɩ zɩm kʋ ta maa ve, nɔɔna maama wʋ́ ta wɩ, a yɩ yu-yoŋo tu.

eng: They dressed him up in a purple robe and twisted together a crown of thorns and put it on him.
local: yɩ ba pa o zʋ gwar-sʋŋʋ. Ba ma daarɩ ba kwe sabarɩ ba ma sɔ yipugǝ ba pu o yuu nɩ.

eng: because the mighty one has done great things for me. Holy is his name.
local: dɩdaanɩ Dam-fɔrɔ Tu Baŋa-Wɛ na kɩ kʋlʋ DƖ pa-nɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Dɩntʋ mʋ yɩ wʋ-poŋo tu cɩga.

eng: They saluted him, Hey! King of the Jews.
local: Ba laan maa kɩ nɩ ba zul-o mʋ te yɩ ba wɩ: «Zwifǝ bam pɛ, dɩnle.».

eng: He shows mercy to everyone, from one generation to the next, who honors him as God.
local: Balʋ maama na kwarɩ-DƖ tɩn, DƖ jɩgɩ ba ŋwaŋa lanyɩranɩ maŋa maama.

eng: Again and again, they struck his head with a stick. They spit on him and knelt before him to honor him.
local: Ba maa jɩgɩ miu ba lɔɛ o yuu nɩ, yɩ ba twɩ lileeru ba yagɩ o yɩra nɩ. Ba ma kuni doonǝ o yigǝ nɩ, yɩ ba kɩ nɩ ba zul-o mʋ te.

eng: He has shown strength with his arm. He has scattered those with arrogant thoughts and proud inclinations.
local: Wɛ kwe DƖ jɩŋa mʋ DƖ ma kɩ wo-kɩnkagɩla. DƖ ma pa banɩ-nyɩna jagɩ da-yigǝ nɩ dɩ ba wʋbʋŋ-yɔɔrʋ tɩm.

eng: When they finished mocking him, they stripped him of the purple robe and put his own clothes back on him. Then they led him out to crucify him.
local: Ba na yaalɩ yaal-o ba ti tɩn, ba ma lɩ gwar-sʋŋʋ kʋm, yɩ ba joori ba zʋ-o o tɩtɩ gɔrɔ kʋm. Ba laan ma ja-o ba nuŋi ba vu sɩ ba pa tʋʋn-dagara baŋa nɩ ba gʋ.

eng: He has pulled the powerful down from their thrones and lifted up the lowly.
local: DƖ joŋi balʋ na yɩ pwa tɩn paarɩ, yɩ DƖ paɩ balʋ na yɩ nabwǝnǝ tɩn na zulǝ.

eng: Simon, a man from Cyrene, Alexander and Rufus father, was coming in from the countryside. They forced him to carry his cross.
local: Ba na maa kɛ tɩn, ba ma na nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ, o yɩrɩ mʋ Simɔn. O nuŋi Sirɛɛnɩ mʋ. Wʋntʋ mʋ yɩ Alɛsandrɩ dɩ Rufusi ko. O dɛɛn ya nuŋi kara mʋ o ba o yi tɩʋ wʋ o maa kɛa. Pamaŋna bam na nɛ-o tɩn, ba ma fɩ-o sɩ o kwe tʋʋn-dagara kam o zɩŋɩ sɩ ba vu.

eng: He has filled the hungry with good things and sent the rich away empty-handed.
local: DƖ maa paɩ balʋ yinigǝ na jɩgɩ-ba tɩn wo-laarʋ zanzan, yɩ DƖ daarɩ DƖ pa nadunǝ viiri dɩ jɩ-kʋrɩ.

eng: They brought Jesus to the place called Golgotha, which means Skull Place.
local: Ba ma ja Zezi ba vu ba yi jǝgǝ kalʋ ba na bǝ nɩ Goligota tɩn. Yɩrɩ dɩm kʋntʋ kuri mʋ Yukol-pʋa piu.

eng: He has come to the aid of his servant Israel, remembering his mercy.
local: Yisɩrayɛlɩ dwi tiinǝ yɩ DƖ tɩtɩ nɔɔna mʋ. DƖ guli ba gulǝ, DƖ duri ba ŋwaŋa yɩ DƖ zǝni-ba.

eng: They tried to give him wine mixed with myrrh, but he didnt take it.
local: Ba na yi da tɩn, ba ma kɩ sana kalʋ na gwaanɩ dɩ liri sɩlʋ yɩrɩ na yɩ Miiri tɩn, ba pa Zezi sɩ o nyɔ. O ma wʋ sɛ o nyɔ.

eng: just as he promised to our ancestors, to Abraham and to Abrahams descendants forever.
local: nɩ DƖ na goni ni dɩdaanɩ dɩbam kwǝ-nakwa tɩn, dɩdaanɩ Abraham dɩ o dwi tiinǝ bam maama, sɩ kʋ taa ve maŋa kalʋ na ba ti tɩn.».

eng: They crucified him. They divided up his clothes, drawing lots for them to determine who would take what.
local: Ba laan ma ja-o ba pa dagara kam baŋa nɩ. Ba ma kwe o gwaarʋ tɩm sɩ ba maŋɩ ba pa daanɩ. Ba ma ta jɔrɔ sɩ ba nii, wɔɔ mʋ wʋ́ twǝri kʋlʋ o na lagɩ tɩn.

eng: Mary stayed with Elizabeth about three months, and then returned to her home.
local: Mari dɛɛn ma maŋɩ Elizabɛtɩ tee nɩ nɩnɛɛnɩ canɩ sɩtɔ. O laan ma joori sɔŋɔ.

eng: It was nine in the morning when they crucified him.
local: Ba na pag-o maŋa kalʋ tɩn yɩ tɩtɩɩtɩ luu nʋgʋ maŋa nɩ mʋ.

eng: When the time came for Elizabeth to have her child, she gave birth to a boy.
local: Maŋa kam dɛɛn ma yi sɩ Elizabɛtɩ lʋ. O ma sɩɩnɩ o lʋ bǝkǝrǝ.

eng: The notice of the formal charge against him was written, The king of the Jews.
local: Ba ma pʋpʋnɩ tɔnɔ, sɩ kʋ brɩ kʋlʋ o na cɔgɩ yɩ ba gʋ-o tɩn, ba tanɩ tʋʋn-dagara kam yuu nɩ. Ba pʋpʋnɩ ba wɩ: «Wʋntʋ yɩ Zwifǝ bam pɛ mʋ.».

eng: Her neighbors and relatives celebrated with her because they had heard that the Lord had shown her great mercy.
local: O tɩʋ kʋm tiinǝ dɩ o cʋrrʋ dɩ ma ni, Baŋa-Wɛ na tiini DƖ duri o ŋwaŋa yɩ DƖ kɩ kʋlʋ DƖ pa-o tɩn. Ba maama dɩ ma jɩgɩ wʋpolo zanzan.

eng: They crucified two outlaws with him, one on his right and one on his left.
local: Ba ma ja ŋwɩɩna bale dɩ ba pa dagarɩ sɩdonnǝ baŋa nɩ. Ba ma cwi dɩdʋa Zezi jazɩm nɩ. Wʋwʋm maa wʋ o jagwiǝ nɩ. .

eng: On the eighth day, it came time to circumcise the child. They wanted to name him Zechariah because that was his fathers name.
local: Bu wʋm da na yi nana tɩn, ba ma pa ba go-o, nɩ Moyisi cullu tɩm na brɩ sɩ ba taa goni ba bǝkǝri te tɩn. Ba ma lagɩ sɩ ba pa o yɩrɩ nɩ Zakari, nɩ o ko yɩrɩ na yɩ te tɩn.

eng: People walking by insulted him, shaking their heads and saying, Ha! So you were going to destroy the temple and rebuild it in three days, were you?.
local: Nɔɔna balʋ na tɔgɩ da ba kɛ tɩn maa niǝ, yɩ ba gɔgɩsɩ ba yuni ba twɩ-o. Ba ma yáal-o yɩ ba wɩ: «Nii. Nmʋ ya na bʋŋɩ sɩ n cɩcʋgɩ Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm mʋ n dɩ tɩga nɩ, sɩ n daarɩ n ma da yatɔ n joori n fɔgɩ n lɔ-ka tɩn.

eng: But his mother replied, No, his name will be John.
local: O nu wʋm ma wʋ sɛ yɩ o wɩ: «Awo, á pa o yɩrɩ nɩ Zan.».

eng: Save yourself and come down from that cross.
local: laan nan vrɩ n tɩtɩ n tu dagara kam yuu nɩ sɩ dɩ́ nii.».

eng: They said to her, None of your relatives have that name.
local: Ba ma lǝr-o ba wɩ: «Bɛŋwaanɩ mʋ yɩ n ta kʋntʋ? Nɔɔnʋ maŋɩ o tǝri á sɔŋɔ nɩ o yɩrɩ na yɩ Zan.».

eng: In the same way, the chief priests were making fun of him among themselves, together with the legal experts. He saved others, they said, but he cant save himself.
local: Zwifǝ kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam dɩ ba cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ bam dɩ maa twɩ-o ba brɩ daanɩ ba mwana. Ba maa tɛ daanɩ ba wɩ: «O yǝni o vrɩ nɔɔna badonnǝ ŋwɩa, yɩ o laan warɩ o tɩtɩ ŋwɩa o vrɩ.

eng: Then they began gesturing to his father to see what he wanted to call him.
local: Ba ma daarɩ ba ma ba jɩa ba maŋɩ ba brɩ o ko wʋm sɩ ba nii, o na lagɩ sɩ ba pa o bu wʋm yɩrɩ te tɩn.

eng: Let the Christ, the king of Israel, come down from the cross. Then well see and believe. Even those who had been crucified with Jesus insulted him.
local: O brɩ o tɩtɩ nɩ o yɩ Krisi wʋm Wɛ na tʋŋɩ tɩn, dɩ Yisɩrayɛlɩ dwi tiinǝ pɛ. O nan tu dagara kam yuu nɩ lele sɩ dɩ́ nii. Dɩ́ na nɛ kʋntʋ, dɩ́ laan wʋ́ sɛ-o.» Nɔɔna balʋ ba na pagɩ dagarɩ sɩm baŋa nɩ ba wǝli da tɩn dɩ maa jɩg-o ba twɩa.

eng: After asking for a tablet, he surprised everyone by writing, His name is John.
local: O ma pa ba kwe tɔnɔ ba pa-o, sɩ o pʋpʋnɩ o brɩ-ba. O ma pʋpʋnɩ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ o wɩ, o yɩrɩ mʋ Zan. Kʋ ma sʋ-ba dɩ o na pʋpʋnɩ te tɩn.

eng: From noon until three in the afternoon the whole earth was dark.
local: Kʋ ma vu kʋ yi maŋa kam wɛ na tu dɩ yi yuparɩ baŋa nɩ tɩn. Lʋgʋ kʋm maama ma ba kʋ ji lim kʋ vu kʋ yi wɛ-guni maŋa nɩ.

eng: At that moment, Zechariah was able to speak again, and he began praising God.
local: O na pʋpʋnɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, o ni ma puri bɩdwɩ baŋa nɩ, yɩ o dindǝlimi joori dɩ pipiri. O maa ŋɔɔnɩ o zuli Wɛ.
