eng: At three, Jesus cried out with a loud shout, Eloi, eloi, lama sabachthani, which means, My God, my God, why have you left me.
local: Kʋ na kɩ nɩnɛɛnɩ luu batɔ te tɩn, Zezi laan ma kaasɩ baŋa baŋa dɩ kwǝr-dɩa o wɩ: «Eli, Eli, lɛma sabakɩtani?» Taanɩ dɩntʋ kuri mʋ: «Amʋ Wɛ, amʋ Wɛ, bɛɛ mʋ kɩ yɩ n yagɩ amʋ?».

eng: All their neighbors were filled with awe, and everyone throughout the Judean highlands talked about what had happened.
local: Fʋʋnɩ dɛɛn ma ja ba tɩʋ kʋm nɔɔna bam maama. Balʋ maama dɛɛn na zʋʋrɩ Zude pweeru tɩm je sɩm nɩ tɩn dɩ dɛɛn ma ni wǝǝnu tɩlʋ na kɩ tɩn, yɩ ba jɩgɩ tɩ taanɩ ba ŋɔɔna.

eng: After hearing him, some standing there said, Look! Hes calling Elijah.
local: Nɔɔna balʋ na zɩgɩ da yɩ ba ni taanɩ dɩm tɩn badaara maa wɩ: «Cǝgi-na. O lagɩ o bǝŋi Eli mʋ sɩ o ba.».

eng: All who heard about this considered it carefully. They said, What then will this child be? Indeed, the Lords power was with him.
local: Ba maa jɩgɩ kʋlʋ ba na ni tɩn ba wʋrʋ nɩ, yɩ ba tɛ ba wɩ: «Bu wʋntʋ nan lagɩ o ji wɔɔ mʋ?» Bɛŋwaanɩ Baŋa-Wɛ dam dɛɛn wʋ bu wʋm tee nɩ.

eng: Someone ran, filled a sponge with sour wine, and put it on a pole. He offered it to Jesus to drink, saying, Lets see if Elijah will come to take him down.
local: Ba dɩdʋa laan ma duri o vu o kwe sɩpɔɔ o dɩ sa-nywana wʋnɩ, o kɩ-kʋ miu ni nɩ o vu o te o pa Zezi sɩ o nyɔ. O daarɩ o wɩ: «Yagɩ sɩ dɩ́ cǝgi dɩ́ nii, Eli wʋ́ ba o pa o tu dagara kam yuu nɩ na?».

eng: Johns father Zechariah was filled with the Holy Spirit and prophesied.
local: Wɛ Joro kʋm dɛɛn ma ba bu wʋm ko Zakari te, yɩ kʋ pa o ŋɔɔnɩ Wɛ yiyiu-ŋwɛ o wɩ:.

eng: But Jesus let out a loud cry and died.
local: Zezi laan ma kaasɩ baŋa baŋa, yɩ o daarɩ o pa o siun ti.

eng: Bless the Lord God of Israel because he has come to help and has delivered his people.
local: «Pa-na dɩ́ zuli Baŋa-Wɛ dɩlʋ na te Yisɩrayɛlɩ tiinǝ tɩn, bɛŋwaanɩ DƖ tu DƖ nɔɔna bam te, sɩ DƖ vrɩ-ba lwarɩm wʋnɩ DƖ yagɩ.

eng: The curtain of the sanctuary was torn in two from top to bottom.
local: Zezi na tɩgɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, gɔrɔ kʋlʋ ba na lǝgi ba ma cɩ Wɛ-di-laa kʋm tɩn ma kaarɩ bɩle, kʋ zɩgɩ baŋa nɩ kʋ tu kʋ kwaarɩ tɩga.

eng: He has raised up a mighty savior for us in his servant Davids house.
local: DƖ ma pa wʋlʋ na yɩ faŋa faŋa Wɛ tɩntʋŋnʋ Davidi dwi tu tɩn zaŋɩ dɩ́ wʋnɩ, sɩ o ba o ji dɩbam vɩrnʋ.

eng: When the centurion, who stood facing Jesus, saw how he died, he said, This man was certainly Gods Son.
local: Pamaŋna bam yigǝ tu wʋlʋ na zɩgɩ Zezi yigǝ nɩ tɩn ma na o na kaasɩ yɩ o daarɩ o tɩ te tɩn. O na nɛ kʋntʋ tɩn, o maa wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ ya sɩɩnɩ o yɩ Baŋa-Wɛ Bu mʋ.».

eng: just as he said through the mouths of his holy prophets long ago.
local: Kʋ ma sɩɩnɩ kʋ kɩ nɩ Baŋa-Wɛ na pɛ DƖ nijoŋnǝ bam maŋɩ ba ta kʋlʋ faŋa faŋa tɩn.

eng: Some women were watching from a distance, including Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of James and Joses, and Salome.
local: Kaana badonnǝ maa tɔgɩ ba wʋra, ba ma zɩgɩ yigǝ yigǝ ba niǝ. Mari wʋlʋ na nuŋi Magɩdala tɩn maa wʋ ba wʋnɩ, dɩdaanɩ Mari wʋlʋ na yɩ Zakɩ kukulǝ dɩ Zʋzɛsɩ nu tɩn. Salomɩ dɩ maa tɔgɩ o wʋra.

eng: He has brought salvation from our enemies and from the power of all those who hate us.
local: Ba dɛɛn tagɩ ba wɩ, Wɛ wʋ́ vrɩ dɩbam dɩ́ dʋna jɩa nɩ, dɩ balʋ maama na culi dɩbam tɩn jɩa nɩ.

eng: When Jesus was in Galilee, these women had followed and supported him, along with many other women who had come to Jerusalem with him.
local: Kaana bantʋ dɛɛn ya tɔgɩ dɩ Zezi Galile nɩ mʋ, yɩ ba yǝni ba tʋŋɩ ba zǝn-o. Kaana badaara zanzan dɩ maa wʋra, ba ya na tɔgɩ dɩd-o ba ba Zeruzalɛm.

eng: He has shown the mercy promised to our ancestors, and remembered his holy covenant.
local: DƖ daa ta duri dɩbam kwǝ-nakwa yibwǝnǝ.

eng: Since it was late in the afternoon on Preparation Day, just before the Sabbath.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ dɛɛn mʋ wʋra, o yɩrɩ mʋ Zʋzɛfʋ, o na nuŋi Arimatɩ. O yɩ Zwifǝ bam nakwa kɔgɔ kʋm yigǝ tu dɩdʋa, yɩ nɔɔna pa-o zulǝ. O maa jɩgɩ tɩɩna nɩ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋ́ ba nɔɔna tɩtarɩ nɩ. Dɛ dɩm ba na gʋ Zezi tɩn maa yɩ dɛ dɩlʋ Zwifǝ bam na maŋɩ sɩ ba ti ba yigǝ ba siun dɛ dɩm ŋwaanɩ tɩn. Tɩga na yi, mʋ ba siun dɛ dɩm pulim. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ Zʋzɛfʋ ma kɩ pu-dɩa, yɩ o zaŋɩ o vu Pilatɩ te o loor-o cwǝŋǝ, sɩ o kwe Zezi yɩra yam o kɩ.

eng: the solemn pledge he made to our ancestor Abraham. He has granted.
local: yɩ DƖ guli DƖ ni-ŋʋm dɩlʋ DƖ dɛɛn na goni dɩ dɩbam ko-nakwɩ Abraham tɩn woŋo.

eng: Pilate wondered if Jesus was already dead. He called the centurion and asked him whether Jesus had already died.
local: Pilatɩ na lwarɩ nɩ Zezi maŋɩ o tɩ tɩn, mʋ kʋ sʋ-o lanyɩranɩ. O ma tʋŋɩ nɔɔna sɩ ba vu ba bǝŋi pamaŋna bam yigǝ tu wʋm sɩ o ba. O na tu tɩn, o ma bwe-o sɩ o nii, Zezi sɩɩnɩ o tɩ mʋ na.

eng: that we would be rescued from the power of our enemies so that we could serve him without fear.
local: Wɛ dɛɛn wɩ, DƖ wʋ́ joŋi dɩbam dɩ́ dʋna jɩa nɩ DƖ yagɩ, yɩ dɩ́ daa bá kwarɩ fʋʋnɩ, sɩ dɩ́ taá tʋŋa nɩ DƖ wʋbʋŋa na lagɩ te tɩn.

eng: When he learned from the centurion that Jesus was dead, Pilate gave the dead body to Joseph.
local: O na lwarɩ nɩ o tɩgɩ tɩn, o ma sɛ sɩ Zʋzɛfʋ vu o kwe o yɩra yam o kɩ.

eng: in holiness and righteousness in Gods eyes, for as long as we live.
local: DƖ maa wɩ, dɩ́ wʋ́ ta tɔgɩ-DƖ dɩ wʋ-poŋo, sɩ dɩ́ taá kɩ lanyɩranɩ DƖ yigǝ nɩ dɩbam ŋwɩa maama wʋnɩ.

eng: He bought a linen cloth, took Jesus down from the cross, wrapped him in the cloth, and laid him in a tomb that had been carved out of rock. He rolled a stone against the entrance to the tomb.
local: Zʋzɛfʋ ma nuŋi o vu o yǝgi gar-jalɩ dɩlʋ na lana tɩn. O ma vu o parɩ Zezi yɩra yam dagara kam yuu nɩ. O ma kwe gɔrɔ kʋm o ma pipir-o, yɩ o daarɩ o ja vu o tiŋi piu bɔɔnɩ wʋnɩ. Dɩntʋ yɩ yibeeli dɩlʋ ba dɛɛn na lugi-dɩ piu yɩra nɩ tɩn mʋ. O laan ma bibili kandwɛ-pulɔgɔ o ma pɩ bɔɔnɩ dɩm ni.

eng: You, child, will be called a prophet of the Most High, for you will go before the Lord to prepare his way.
local: A bu, kʋ na yɩ nmʋ, baá bǝŋi nmʋ nɩ Baŋa-Wɛ nijoŋnu, bɛŋwaanɩ nmʋ wʋ́ da dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm yigǝ, sɩ n fɔgɩ n kwɛ o cwǝŋǝ kam, o na lagɩ o ba o da da tɩn.

eng: Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of Joses saw where he was buried.
local: Mari wʋlʋ na nuŋi Magɩdala dɩ Mari wʋlʋ na yɩ Zʋzɛsɩ nu tɩn maa zɩgɩ ba nii o na tiŋi Zezi yɩra yam mɛ tɩn.

eng: You will tell his people how to be saved through the forgiveness of their sins.
local: Nmʋ ta wʋ́ pa Wɛ nɔɔna bam lwarɩ sɩ DƖ wʋ́ vrɩ-ba, dɩ DƖ na wʋ́ yagɩ ba lwarɩm DƖ ma cɛ-ba tɩn.

eng: Because of our Gods deep compassion, the dawn from heaven will break upon us.
local: Wɛ maa jɩgɩ dɩbam ŋwaŋa lanyɩranɩ. DƖ ma wʋ́ pa pooni nuŋi wɛyuu dɩ ba dɩbam te dɩ nyɩɩna, nɩ wɩa na puli tɩtɩɩtɩ te tɩn.

eng: to give light to those who are sitting in darkness and in the shadow of death, to guide us on the path of peace.
local: sɩ dɩ pa nɔɔna balʋ na wʋ lim wʋnɩ tɩn dɩ balʋ na kwarɩ tʋʋnɩ tɩn na pooni, sɩ dɩ pa dɩ́ da yazurǝ cwǝŋǝ.».

eng: The child grew up, becoming strong in character. He was in the wilderness until he began his public ministry to Israel.
local: Zakari bu Zan dɛɛn ma kɩ nɔɔnʋ yɩ o wʋbʋŋa bɩ lanyɩranɩ. O dɛɛn ma zʋʋrɩ kagʋa nɩ taan, sɩ kʋ vu kʋ yi dɛ dɩm o na wʋ́ nuŋi Yisɩrayɛlɩ tiinǝ bam tɩtarɩ nɩ o brɩ-ba Wɛ kwǝrǝ kam tɩn.

eng: In those days Caesar Augustus declared that everyone throughout the empire should be enrolled in the tax lists.
local: Maŋa kam kʋntʋ nɩ lʋgʋ kʋm pa-farʋ wʋlʋ yɩrɩ na yɩ Ogusi tɩn dɛɛn ma pa ni, sɩ ba ga o tɩʋ kʋm maama nɔn-biǝ bam sɩ o lwarɩ ba ni.

eng: This first enrollment occurred when Quirinius governed Syria.
local: Garɩm dɩntʋ maa yɩ pulim garɩm, yɩ dɩ kɩ maŋa kalʋ Kwirini dɩ dɛɛn na yɩ Siiri tɩɩnɩ dɩm yuutu tɩn.

eng: Everyone went to their own cities to be enrolled.
local: Garɩm dɩm maŋa na yi tɩn, nɔɔnʋ maama ma vu o tɩtɩ tɩʋ sɩ ba ga-o.

eng: Since Joseph belonged to Davids house and family line, he went up from the city of Nazareth in Galilee to Davids city, called Bethlehem, in Judea.
local: Zʋzɛfʋ dɩ dɛɛn ma zaŋɩ o nuŋi Nazarɛtɩ kʋ na yɩ Galile tɩʋ tɩn. O ma vu Zude tɩʋ kʋdoŋ, kʋ yɩrɩ mʋ Betelɩhɛm, ba na lʋgɩ faŋa faŋa Pɛ Davidi mɛ tɩn. O dɛɛn ve daanɩ, o na yɩ Davidi dwi tu tɩn ŋwaanɩ mʋ.

eng: He went to be enrolled together with Mary, who was promised to him in marriage and who was pregnant.
local: Mari wʋlʋ ba na sɛ sɩ o taa yɩ Zʋzɛfʋ kaanɩ tɩn dɩ ma tɔgɩ dɩd-o o vu sɩ ba ga-ba. O dɛɛn jɩgɩ pugǝ mʋ.

eng: While they were there, the time came for Mary to have her baby.
local: Ba na wʋ Betelɩhɛm nɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ ma yi sɩ o lʋ bu wʋm.

eng: She gave birth to her firstborn child, a son, wrapped him snugly, and laid him in a manger, because there was no place for them in the guestroom.
local: O ma lʋ bǝkǝrǝ. Mʋ o dayigǝ bu. O ma kwe bu wʋm o ma gwaarʋ o kukwǝr-o o tiŋi vara wʋdiu dim jǝgǝ nɩ. Ba dɛɛn tigi vara digǝ nɩ mʋ, bɛŋwaanɩ vǝrǝ mʋ su tɩʋ kʋm maama, yɩ ba wʋ nɛ digǝ sɩ ba zʋ.

eng: Nearby shepherds were living in the fields, guarding their sheep at night.
local: Nayɩra dɩ dɛɛn mʋ wʋ je sɩm kʋntʋ nɩ, gaa kʋm wʋnɩ, ba yɩrɩ ba vara tɩtɩɩ nɩ.

eng: The Lords angel stood before them, the Lords glory shone around them, and they were terrified.
local: Baŋa-Wɛ malɛka ma nuŋi ba yigǝ nɩ, yɩ Wɛ zǝŋi DƖ paarɩ-zulǝ yam pooni ba baŋa nɩ, yɩ dɩ nyɩɩnɩ dɩ gilimi-ba. Fʋʋnɩ ma tiini dɩ zʋ-ba zanzan.

eng: The angel said, Dont be afraid! Look! I bring good news to you—wonderful, joyous news for all people.
local: Malɛka kam laan ma ta dɩ ba ka wɩ: «Á yɩ taá kwarɩ fʋʋnɩ. Kʋ yɩ wʋpolo taanɩ mʋ a tu sɩ a ta a brɩ abam, yɩ dɩ lagɩ dɩ pa nɔɔna maama wʋ mʋ poli zanzan.

eng: Your savior is born today in Davids city. He is Christ the Lord.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ zɩm dɛ dɩm nɩ ba lʋgɩ abam Vɩrnʋ wʋm Davidi tɩʋ Betelɩhɛm nɩ. Wʋntʋ yɩ Krisi wʋm Wɛ na tʋŋɩ tɩn mʋ. Wʋm mʋ yɩ abam Yuutu.

eng: This is a sign for you: you will find a newborn baby wrapped snugly and lying in a manger.
local: Á na ve á nii, á wʋ́ na bu-sɩsɩŋa ba na kukwǝri dɩ gwaarʋ ba tiŋi vara wʋdiu dim jǝgǝ nɩ. Mʋ kʋlʋ na wʋ́ brɩ abam nɩ kʋ yɩ cɩga mʋ a taga.».

eng: Suddenly a great assembly of the heavenly forces was with the angel praising God. They said.
local: Malɛka kam na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, Wɛ malɛsɩ kɔgɔ zanzan ma da sɩ nuŋi sɩ wǝli dɩ ka. Sɩ maama ma tee Wɛ sɩ wɩ:.

eng: Glory to God in heaven, and on earth peace among those whom he favors.
local: «Wɛ maŋɩ dɩ zulǝ wɛyuu nɩ. Kʋ daarɩ tɩga baŋa nɩ, balʋ na tigi Wɛ bɩcarɩ nɩ tɩn wʋ́ ta zʋʋrɩ lanyɩranɩ dɩ wʋ-zuru.».

eng: When the angels returned to heaven, the shepherds said to each other, Lets go right now to Bethlehem and see whats happened. Lets confirm what the Lord has revealed to us.
local: Malɛsɩ sɩm laan ma joori Wɛ-sɔŋɔ. Sɩ na kɛ tɩn, nayɩra bam ma ta daanɩ ba wɩ: «Ba-na dɩ́ vu Betelɩhɛm dɩ́ nii kʋlʋ na kɩ da yɩ Wɛ pa dɩ́ lwarɩ tɩn.».

eng: They went quickly and found Mary and Joseph, and the baby lying in the manger.
local: Ba ma zaŋɩ lɩla ba kɛ tɩʋ kʋm wʋ. Ba ma vu ba na Mari dɩdaanɩ Zʋzɛfʋ. Ba ma na bu wʋm na tigi vara wʋdiu dim jǝgǝ nɩ.

eng: When they saw this, they reported what they had been told about this child.
local: Ba na nɛ-o kʋntʋ tɩn, ba laan ma ta malɛka kam na maŋɩ ka ta bu wʋm taanɩ te tɩn.

eng: Everyone who heard it was amazed at what the shepherds told them.
local: Balʋ maama na ni nayɩra bam na tagɩ kʋlʋ tɩn, kʋ ma sʋ-ba.

eng: Mary committed these things to memory and considered them carefully.
local: Mari dɩ na ni wǝǝnu tɩlʋ na kɩ tɩn, o maa bʋŋɩ-tɩ o wʋ nɩ.

eng: The shepherds returned home, glorifying and praising God for all they had heard and seen. Everything happened just as they had been told.
local: Nayɩra bam ma joori ba viiri yɩ ba zuli Wɛ, yɩ ba tee-DƖ dɩ kʋlʋ ba na ni yɩ ba ta kwǝri ba na tɩn. Kʋ dɛɛn sɩɩnɩ kʋ kɩ nɩnɛɛnɩ malɛka kam na tagɩ ka brɩ-ba te tɩn.

eng: When eight days had passed, Jesus parents circumcised him and gave him the name Jesus. This was the name given to him by the angel before he was conceived.
local: Bu wʋm na yi da nana tɩn, ba ma pa sɩ ba go-o, nɩ Moyisi cullu tɩm na brɩ sɩ ba taa goni ba bǝkǝri te tɩn. Ba ma pa o yɩrɩ ba wɩ Zezi. Mʋ yɩrɩ dɩlʋ malɛka kam na maŋɩ ka pa-o, yɩ ba ta wʋ jaanɩ o pugǝ tɩn.

eng: When the time came for their ritual cleansing, in accordance with the Law from Moses, they brought Jesus up to Jerusalem to present him to the Lord. .
local: Maŋa kam laan ma yi sɩ Mari zarɩ o digiru tɩm sɩ o kwɛ o tɩtɩ dɩ Wɛ, sɩ kʋ tɔgɩ Wɛ cullu tɩlʋ DƖ na kɩ Moyisi jɩŋa nɩ na brɩ te tɩn. Zʋzɛfʋ dɩ Mari ma zaŋɩ ba ja bu wʋm ba vu Zeruzalɛm, sɩ ba vu ba zʋ Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm wʋ, sɩ ba pa-o ba Yuutu Baŋa-Wɛ.

eng: Its written in the Law of the Lord, Every firstborn male will be dedicated to the Lord.
local: Ba kɩ kʋntʋ, bɛŋwaanɩ Wɛ ni dɩm na brɩ te tɩn mʋ kʋntʋ: «Bǝkǝrǝ kalʋ maama na yɩ o nu dayigǝ bu tɩn maŋɩ sɩ o taa yɩ Baŋa-Wɛ nyɩm mʋ.».

eng: They offered a sacrifice in keeping with whats stated in the Law of the Lord, A pair of turtledoves or two young pigeons .
local: Ba ma vu sɩ ba kaanɩ Baŋa-Wɛ, nɩ DƖ ni dɩm na brɩ te tɩn. Ba maŋɩ sɩ ba kwe kunkwǝnǝ bale mʋ ba ma kaanɩ-DƖ, naa kunkwǝn-puli sɩle ba ma kaanɩ-DƖ.

eng: A man named Simeon was in Jerusalem. He was righteous and devout. He eagerly anticipated the restoration of Israel, and the Holy Spirit rested on him.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ dɛɛn maa zʋʋrɩ Zeruzalɛm tɩʋ kʋm wʋnɩ, o yɩrɩ mʋ Simeyon. O yɩ nɔn-ŋʋm mʋ, yɩ o kwarɩ Wɛ lanyɩranɩ. O maa yɔɔrɩ o cǝgi dɛ dɩm Wɛ na wʋ́ ba DƖ joŋi Yisɩrayɛlɩ dwi tiinǝ bam tɩn. Wɛ Joro kʋm maa wʋ o tee nɩ.

eng: The Holy Spirit revealed to him that he wouldnt die before he had seen the Lords Christ.
local: Kʋ dɛɛn ya pɛ o lwarɩ sɩ o bá tɩ, yɩ oó na Krisi wʋm dɩ́ Yuutu Baŋa-Wɛ na lagɩ DƖ tʋŋɩ tɩn.

eng: Led by the Spirit, he went into the temple area. Meanwhile, Jesus parents brought the child to the temple so that they could do what was customary under the Law.
local: Wɛ Joro kʋm dɛɛn ma pa Simeyon zaŋɩ o vu o zʋ Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm wʋ. O na wʋra kʋntʋ tɩn, Zezi nu dɩ o ko ma ja-o ba vu ba zʋ, sɩ ba kɩ kʋlʋ Wɛ ni dɩm na brɩ sɩ ba kɩ o ŋwaanɩ tɩn.

eng: Simeon took Jesus in his arms and praised God. He said.
local: Ba na jaanɩ bu wʋm ba vu ba zʋ kʋntʋ tɩn, Simeyon ma vu o kwe-o o ziǝ, yɩ o daarɩ o zuli Wɛ o wɩ:.

eng: Now, master, let your servant go in peace according to your word.
local: «A Yuutu Baŋa-Wɛ, lele kʋntʋ a nɛ kʋlʋ nmʋ dɛɛn na tagɩ nɩ aá na tɩn. A laan jɩgɩ wʋ-zuru. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ nmʋ laan waɩ n paɩ amʋ na yɩ nmʋ tɩntʋŋnʋ tɩn na cwǝŋǝ, sɩ a yagɩ lʋgʋ baŋa sɩ a ba nmʋ te.

eng: because my eyes have seen your salvation.
local: Amʋ yi nɛ Vɩrnʋ wʋm nmʋ na tʋŋɩ sɩ o vrɩ nɔɔna lwarɩm wʋnɩ tɩn.

eng: You prepared this salvation in the presence of all peoples.
local: Nabiinǝ maama lʋgʋ baŋa nɩ wʋ́ lwarɩ nmʋ vrɩm dɩm na yɩ te tɩn.

eng: Its a light for revelation to the Gentiles and a glory for your people Israel.
local: Kʋʋ́ ta nyɩ nɩ wɩa pooni mʋ te, sɩ kʋ pa dwi maama lwarɩ nmʋ cwǝŋǝ kam. Kʋʋ́ pa nmʋ tɩtɩ nɔɔna Yisɩrayɛlɩ dwi dɩm na zulǝ.».

eng: His father and mother were amazed by what was said about him.
local: Simeyon na tagɩ bu wʋm taanɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, o nu dɩ o ko maa yɩ yǝǝu dɩ o taanɩ dɩm.

eng: Simeon blessed them and said to Mary his mother, This boy is assigned to be the cause of the falling and rising of many in Israel and to be a sign that generates opposition.
local: Simeyon daa ta ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ, Wɛ wʋ́ kɩ-ba lanyɩranɩ. O ma daarɩ o ta dɩ bu wʋm nu Mari o wɩ: «Nii, Wɛ sɩɩnɩ DƖ tʋŋɩ bu wʋm sɩ o ba o pa Yisɩrayɛlɩ tiinǝ zanzan mʋ tʋ tɩga nɩ, ba na tusi tɩn ŋwaanɩ. O nan daa ta wʋ́ pa ba zanzan zaŋɩ wɛɛnɩ. Wɛ wʋ́ pa o brɩ nɔɔna cɩga, yɩ ba zanzan ta wʋ́ vɩ-o.

eng: so that the inner thoughts of many will be revealed. And a sword will pierce your innermost being too.
local: Mʋ kʋ kuri nɔɔna zanzan wʋbʋŋa ya na sǝgi tɩn wʋ́ ba ya lwarɩ jaja. Kʋ daarɩ, kʋ na yɩ nmʋ tɩtɩ, n wʋ wʋ́ di nɩ sʋ-zɔɔ na zɔgɩ nɔɔnʋ bɩcɔŋɔ nɩ te tɩn.».

eng: There was also a prophet, Anna the daughter of Phanuel, who belonged to the tribe of Asher. She was very old. After she married, she lived with her husband for seven years.
local: Kaanɩ wʋdoŋ dɩ dɛɛn mʋ wʋra, o yɩrɩ mʋ Annɩ. O ko yɩrɩ mʋ Fanuyɛlɩ, o yɩ Asɛɛrɩ dwi tu mʋ. Annɩ dɛɛn ya zʋ barʋ. Ba maa wʋra daanɩ bɩna yarpɛ, yɩ o barʋ wʋm tɩ o daar-o. O daa ma wʋ zʋ barʋ. O kwɩn zanzan yɩ o jɩgɩ bɩna funɔnɔ-yana . O ma yɩ Wɛ nijoŋnu. O maa zʋʋrɩ Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm wʋnɩ maŋa maama. Wɩa dɩ tɩtɩɩ maama o ma zuli Wɛ dɩ ni-vɔɔm dɩ Wɛ-loro.

eng: She approached at that very moment and began to praise God and to speak about Jesus to everyone who was looking forward to the redemption of Jerusalem.
local: Maŋa kam kʋntʋ nɩ nɔ́ɔ, mʋ o ve o yi bu wʋm na wʋ mɛ tɩn. O ma kɩ Wɛ le, yɩ o wʋra o ŋɔɔnɩ bu wʋm woŋo o brɩ balʋ maama na wʋra yɩ ba cǝgi maŋa kalʋ Wɛ na wʋ́ ba DƖ vrɩ Yisɩrayɛlɩ tiinǝ bam lwarɩm wʋnɩ DƖ yagɩ tɩn.

eng: When Mary and Joseph had completed everything required by the Law of the Lord, they returned to their hometown, Nazareth in Galilee.
local: Zezi nu dɩ o ko dɛɛn ma kɩ kʋlʋ maama Baŋa-Wɛ cullu tɩm na brɩ sɩ ba kɩ tɩn. Ba na kɩ ba ti tɩn, ba ma joori ba vu Nazarɛtɩ na yɩ ba tɩtɩ tɩʋ yɩ kʋ wʋ Galile nɩ tɩn.

eng: The child grew up and became strong. He was filled with wisdom, and Gods favor was on him.
local: Ba na joori ba vu ba wʋra tɩn, bu wʋm ma kɩ nɔɔnʋ dɩ ya-dɛɛra. O yiǝ ma pʋʋrɩ, Wɛ na kɩ-o yu-yoŋo tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: Each year his parents went to Jerusalem for the Passover Festival.
local: Zwifǝ bam candiǝ kalʋ yɩrɩ na yɩ Pakɩ tɩn ma yi. Bɩnɩ maama wuu, Zezi nu dɩ o ko dɛɛn yǝni ba vu Zeruzalɛm mʋ, sɩ ba di candiǝ kam kʋntʋ.

eng: When he was years old, they went up to Jerusalem according to their custom.
local: Zezi dɛɛn na yi bɩna fugǝ-yale tɩn, o ma da dɩ ba o vu candiǝ kam, nɩnɛɛnɩ ba na yǝni ba ve ba di-ka te tɩn.

eng: After the festival was over, they were returning home, but the boy Jesus stayed behind in Jerusalem. His parents didnt know it.
local: Ba na di candiǝ kam ba ti tɩn, ba ma zaŋɩ ba maa joori sɔŋɔ. Zezi ma maŋɩ Zeruzalɛm nɩ, yɩ o nu dɩ o ko yǝri kʋ ni nɩ.

eng: Supposing that he was among their band of travelers, they journeyed on for a full day while looking for him among their family and friends.
local: Ba ma vu taan dɛ dɩdwɩ, yɩ ba bʋŋɩ sɩ oó ta wʋ ba kɔgɔ kʋm wʋnɩ mʋ. Ba na wʋ nɛ-o ba tee nɩ tɩn, ba laan ma puli sɩ ba beeri o je ba nu-biǝ bam dɩ ba badonnǝ bam tee nɩ.

eng: When they didnt find Jesus, they returned to Jerusalem to look for him.
local: Ba ma wʋ nɛ-o. Ba ma joori ba vu Zeruzalɛm tɩʋ kʋm wʋ sɩ ba beer-o.

eng: After three days they found him in the temple. He was sitting among the teachers, listening to them and putting questions to them.
local: Ba ma beer-o taan da yatɔ, yɩ ba laan vu ba na-o Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm wʋnɩ, o je karanyɩna tiinǝ bam tɩtarɩ nɩ. O na je da kʋntʋ tɩn, o maa cǝgi ba na brɩ te tɩn, yɩ o bwe-ba wǝǝnu zanzan.

eng: Everyone who heard him was amazed by his understanding and his answers.
local: Balʋ maama na je ba cǝgi o taanɩ dɩm tɩn maa yɩ yǝǝu, dɩ o na jɩgɩ yǝno dɩ taanɩ dɩm lǝrim te tɩn.

eng: When his parents saw him, they were shocked. His mother said, Child, why have you treated us like this? Listen! Your father and I have been worried. Weve been looking for you.
local: Zezi nu dɩ o ko na nɛ-o kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ ma sɔɔrɩ-ba. O nu wʋm ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «A bu, bɛɛ mʋ yɩ n kɩ kʋntʋ dɩdaanɩ dɩbam? Ta n nii, amʋ dɩ nmʋ ko na tiini dɩ́ beeri nmʋ taan, yɩ liǝ dɩ wʋ-cɔgɔ jɩgɩ dɩbam.».

eng: Jesus replied, Why were you looking for me? Didnt you know that it was necessary for me to be in my Fathers house.
local: Zezi ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Bɛɛ mʋ yɩ abam karɩ á beeri amʋ je? Amʋ maŋɩ sɩ a taa wʋ a Ko sɔŋɔ kʋm nɩ mʋ. Abam yǝri kʋntʋ na?».

eng: But they didnt understand what he said to them.
local: O na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma wʋ ni o taanɩ dɩm kuri.

eng: Jesus went down to Nazareth with them and was obedient to them. His mother cherished every word in her heart.
local: Zezi dɛɛn ma tɔgɩ dɩ ba o joori Nazarɛtɩ, yɩ o sɛ ba ni. O nu wʋm maa jɩgɩ wǝǝnu tɩm kʋntʋ maama o wʋ nɩ.

eng: Jesus matured in wisdom and years, and in favor with God and with people.
local: Zezi dɛɛn bɩra o yɩra wʋnɩ, yɩ o yǝno dɩ bɩra kʋ wǝli da, yɩ Baŋa-Wɛ dɩ nabiinǝ dɛɛn so-o.

eng: In the fifteenth year of the rule of the emperor Tiberius—when Pontius Pilate was governor over Judea and Herod was ruler over Galilee, his brother Philip was ruler over Ituraea and Trachonitis, and Lysanias was ruler over Abilene.
local: Kʋ dɛɛn na kɛ yɩ Tibɛɛrɩ yɩ lʋgʋ kʋm pa-farʋ kʋ maŋɩ dɩ bɩna fugǝ-yanu tɩn, mʋ Wɛ kwǝrǝ dɛɛn tu Zakari bu Zan te. Kantʋ maŋa kam nɩ Pɔnsɩ Pilatɩ mʋ yɩ Zude tɩɩnɩ dɩm gʋvɩrma tu. Erɔdɩ maa yɩ Galile tɩʋ kʋm yuutu, yɩ o zɩmbaarʋ Filipi dɩ yɩ Yituri dɩ Tɩrakoni tɩɩnɩ dɩm yuutu. Lizanɩa dɩ maa yɩ Abɩlɛnɩ tɩʋ yuutu. Kʋ daarɩ Annɩ dɩ Kayifu dɛɛn maa yɩ Wɛ kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ. Maŋa kam Wɛ kwǝrǝ kam na tu Zan te kʋntʋ tɩn, o wʋ kagʋa wʋnɩ mʋ.

eng: John went throughout the region of the Jordan River, calling for people to be baptized to show that they were changing their hearts and lives and wanted God to forgive their sins.
local: O ma zaŋɩ o tʋlɩ Zʋrdɛn bugǝ ni tɩɩnɩ dɩm maama nɩ, yɩ o tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝrǝ kam o brɩ nɔɔna o wɩ, ba maŋɩ sɩ ba lǝni ba wʋrʋ ba yagɩ kǝm-balwaarʋ, sɩ ba daarɩ ba pa o miisi-ba na wʋnɩ, sɩ Wɛ laan wʋ́ yagɩ ba lwarɩm DƖ ma cɛ-ba.

eng: This is just as it was written in the scroll of the words of Isaiah the prophet, A voice crying out in the wilderness: Prepare the way for the Lord; make his paths straight.
local: Zan na tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝrǝ kam kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ sɩɩnɩ kʋ kɩ nɩnɛɛnɩ Wɛ nijoŋnu Ezayi dɛɛn na pʋpʋnɩ o tɔnɔ kʋm nɩ o ta te tɩn. Faŋa faŋa tɩn, o dɛɛn tagɩ o wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ mʋ wʋ kagʋa wʋnɩ o tɔɔlɩ kwǝrǝ o wɩ: ‹Zaŋɩ-na á fɔgɩ á kwɛ dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm cwǝŋǝ kam, o na lagɩ o ba o tɔgɩ da tɩn. Pa-na cwe sɩlʋ o na lagɩ o da tɩn fɔgɩ sɩ yɔɔrɩ lanyɩranɩ.

eng: Every valley will be filled, and every mountain and hill will be leveled. The crooked will be made straight and the rough places made smooth.
local: Je sɩlʋ maama na jɩgɩ gwǝǝnu tɩn maŋɩ sɩ tɩ suuri mʋ, dɩ je sɩlʋ maama na jɩgɩ zwǝǝnu dɩ pweeru tɩn maŋɩ sɩ tɩ guri mʋ tɩ daarɩ cwǝŋǝ kam. Cwe sɩlʋ maama na gugwǝli tɩn maŋɩ sɩ sɩ fɔgɩ sɩ yɔɔrɩ mʋ. Cwe sɩlʋ maama na ba lana tɩn maŋɩ sɩ sɩ fɔgɩ sɩ kwɛ mʋ.

eng: All humanity will see Gods salvation. .
local: Nabiinǝ maama laan wʋ́ na Wɛ na wʋ́ vrɩ nɔɔna lwarɩm wʋnɩ te tɩn.› » Mʋ Ezayi na ŋɔɔnɩ Zan taanɩ faŋa faŋa te tɩn.

eng: Then John said to the crowds who came to be baptized by him, You children of snakes! Who warned you to escape from the angry judgment that is coming soon?.
local: Nɔɔna zanzan dɛɛn ma zaŋɩ ba vu Zan te ba pa o miisi-ba na wʋnɩ Wɛ ŋwaanɩ. Ba na tu o te kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Abam nyɩ dɩ bɩsankwɩ-dwǝ. Wɔɔ mʋ brɩ abam sɩ á kwaanɩ á lu cam dɩlʋ Wɛ na lagɩ DƖ pa nabiinǝ na tɩn wʋnɩ?.

eng: Produce fruit that shows you have changed your hearts and lives. And dont even think about saying to yourselves, Abraham is our father. I tell you that God is able to raise up Abrahams children from these stones.
local: Abam nan maŋɩ sɩ á kɩ kǝnǝ yalʋ na wʋ́ brɩ nɩ á lǝni á wʋrʋ á yagɩ kǝm-balwaarʋ tɩn mʋ. Abam nan yɩ garɩ á bʋŋɩ á pa á tɩtɩ nɩ, abam na yɩ Abraham dwi tiinǝ tɩn, á daa wʋ maŋɩ sɩ á lǝni á wʋrʋ. Wɛ na laga, DƖ wʋ́ wanɩ DƖ pa kandwa yantʋ lǝni ya ji Abraham dwi tiinǝ.

eng: The ax is already at the root of the trees. Therefore, every tree that doesnt produce good fruit will be chopped down and tossed into the fire.
local: Nɔɔnʋ nan maŋɩ o jɩgɩ dooru o jɩŋa nɩ, sɩ o ta maa goni tweeru tɩm o dɩ tɩga nɩ. Tiu kʋlʋ maama na ba lǝri bu-ŋʋna tɩn, o maŋɩ sɩ o go-kʋ mʋ o dɩ mini nɩ sɩ kʋ di. Mʋ Wɛ dɩ na lagɩ DƖ kɩ nɔn-balwaarʋ te tɩn.».

eng: The crowds asked him, What then should we do.
local: Zan na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm ma bwe-o ba wɩ: «Dɩ́ nan wʋ́ kɩ bɛɛ mʋ?».

eng: He answered, Whoever has two shirts must share with the one who has none, and whoever has food must do the same.
local: O ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Abam balʋ na jɩgɩ gwaarʋ tɩle tɩle tɩn, á maŋɩ sɩ á kwe dɩdʋa mʋ á pa balʋ na ba jɩgɩ kʋlʋkʋlʋ tɩn. Kʋ daarɩ, abam balʋ na jɩgɩ wʋdiiru tɩn, á dɩ maŋɩ sɩ á kwe-tɩ á maŋɩ á pa nɔɔna balʋ na ba jɩgɩ ba di tɩn.».

eng: Even tax collectors came to be baptized. They said to him, Teacher, what should we do.
local: Lampo-joŋnǝ dɩ dɛɛn ma ba sɩ Zan miisi-ba na wʋnɩ. Ba ma bwe-o ba wɩ: «Karanyɩna, bɛɛ mʋ dɩbam nan wʋ́ kɩ?».

eng: He replied, Collect no more than you are authorized to collect.
local: O ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Á wʋ maŋɩ sɩ á la nɔɔnʋ yuu, sɩ kʋ dwǝni á na wʋ́ joŋ-o te tɩn.».

eng: Soldiers asked, What about us? What should we do? He answered, Dont cheat or harass anyone, and be satisfied with your pay.
local: Pamaŋna dɩ daa ma ba ba bwe-o ba wɩ: «Dɩbam nan wʋ́ kɩ bɛɛ mʋ?» O ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Abam wʋ maŋɩ sɩ á vrɩ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ sǝbu dɩ dam naa á ganɩ-ba á joŋi. Kʋ daarɩ, á taá jɩgɩ wʋpolo dɩ á ŋwɩɩrʋ tɩm na maŋɩ tɩ yɩ te dɩ.».

eng: The people were filled with expectation, and everyone wondered whether John might be the Christ.
local: Nɔɔna dɛɛn maa jɩgɩ tɩɩna dɩ Wɛ lanyɩranɩ, yɩ ba maama ma bwɛ ba wʋnɩ sɩ ba lwarɩ Zan na yɩ wʋlʋ tɩn. Ba maa wɩ: «Wʋntʋ mʋ wʋ́ ta yɩ Krisi wʋm Wɛ na lagɩ DƖ tʋŋ-o sɩ o ba tɩn na?».

eng: John replied to them all, I baptize you with water, but the one who is more powerful than me is coming. Im not worthy to loosen the strap of his sandals. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and fire.
local: Ba maama na bʋŋɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, Zan ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Kʋ na yɩ amʋ, a miisi abam na wʋnɩ mʋ. Kʋ daarɩ wʋlʋ na tiini o dwe amʋ tɩn lagɩ o saŋɩ amʋ kwaga o ba. A wʋ maŋɩ sɩ a bwǝli o nɛ natra a pa-o. Wʋntʋ nan wʋ́ miisi abam Wɛ Joro dɩ mini wʋnɩ.

eng: The shovel he uses to sift the wheat from the husks is in his hands. He will clean out his threshing area and bring the wheat into his barn. But he will burn the husks with a fire that cant be put out.
local: Wʋntʋ nan ze zʋŋ-kɔgɔ mʋ sɩ o ma caarɩ o mɩna o pɛ o kɩ o tulǝ nɩ, sɩ o daarɩ mɩn-swaanʋ tɩm o zwɛ dɩ mini dɩlʋ na ba di dɩ dwe tɩn. Mʋ o na lagɩ o pɔɔrɩ nɔɔna daanɩ o kɩ-ba te tɩn.».

eng: With many other words John appealed to them, proclaiming good news to the people.
local: Mʋ tɩntʋ wǝǝnu tɩm dɩ wǝǝnu dwi tǝri tǝri mʋ Zan dɛɛn ma o brɩ nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm Wɛ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam.

eng: But Herod the ruler had been criticized harshly by John because of Herodias, Herods brothers wife, and because of all the evil he had done.
local: O dɛɛn ma ta dɩ Erɔdɩ wʋlʋ na yɩ Galile yuutu tɩn, nɩ o kɩ kʋlʋ na wʋ maŋɩ tɩn dɩ o zɩmbaarʋ kaanɩ Erɔdɩadɩ. O daa ta ma kaan-o dɩ wo-balwaarʋ tɩlʋ maama o na maŋɩ o kɩ tɩn.

eng: He added this to the list of his evil deeds: he locked John up in prison.
local: Erɔdɩ ma ja Zan o kɩ pɩɩna digǝ nɩ. Kʋntʋ tɩn, o daa ta kɩ wo-balɔrɔ mʋ o wǝli da.

eng: When everyone was being baptized, Jesus also was baptized. While he was praying, heaven was opened.
local: Nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm dɛɛn na tu Zan te sɩ o miisi-ba na wʋnɩ tɩn, Zezi dɩ dɛɛn ma ba o te, sɩ o pa Zan miis-o dɩ. Zezi na miisi na kʋntʋ tɩn, o maa wʋra o warɩ Wɛ.

eng: and the Holy Spirit came down on him in bodily form like a dove. And there was a voice from heaven: You are my Son, whom I dearly love; in you I find happiness.
local: Wɛyuu laan ma puri, yɩ Wɛ Joro kʋm tɔgɩ da kʋ tu o baŋa, yɩ kʋ nyɩ dɩ kunkwǝŋǝ te. Kwǝrǝ laan ma ŋɔɔnɩ wɛyuu nɩ ka wɩ: «Nmʋ mʋ yɩ amʋ bu-dʋa a na soe dɩ a wʋ maama. Amʋ tiini a jɩgɩ wʋpolo dɩ nmʋ.».

eng: Jesus was about years old when he began his ministry. People supposed that he was the son of Joseph son of Heli.
local: Maŋa kam Zezi na puli sɩ o brɩ nɔɔna Wɛ cwǝŋǝ kam tɩn, o dɛɛn yɩ nɩnɛɛnɩ bɩna fiintɔ mʋ te. Nɔɔna dɛɛn bʋŋɩ ba wɩ, o yɩ Zʋzɛfʋ bu mʋ. Zʋzɛfʋ ko dɛɛn mʋ yɩ Eli.

eng: son of Matthat son of Levi son of Melchi son of Jannai son of Joseph.
local: Eli ko dɛɛn mʋ yɩ Matatɩ. Matatɩ ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Leevi. Leevi ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Meliki. Meliki ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Zanayi. Zanayi ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Zʋzɛfʋ.

eng: son of Mattathias son of Amos son of Nahum son of Esli son of Naggai.
local: Zʋzɛfʋ ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Matatɩa. Matatɩa ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Amɔsɩ. Amɔsɩ ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Nawum. Nawum ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Esili. Esili ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Nagayi.

eng: son of Maath son of Mattathias son of Semein son of Josech son of Joda.
local: Nagayi ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Maatɩ. Maatɩ ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Matatɩa. Matatɩa ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Semeyi. Semeyi ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Zozeki. Zozeki ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Zoda.

eng: son of Joanan son of Rhesa son of Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel son of Neri.
local: Zoda ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Zʋanan. Zʋanan ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Risa. Risa ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Zorobabɛlɩ. Zorobabɛlɩ ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Salatɩyɛlɩ. Salatɩyɛlɩ ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Neri.

eng: son of Melchi son of Addi son of Cosam son of Elmadam son of Er.
local: Neri ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Meliki. Meliki ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Adi. Adi ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Kosam. Kosam ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Elimadam. Elimadam ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Eri.

eng: son of Joshua son of Eliezer son of Jorim son of Matthat son of Levi.
local: Eri ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Zozwe. Zozwe ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Elizɛɛrɩ. Elizɛɛrɩ ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Zorim. Zorim ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Matatɩ. Matatɩ ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Leevi.

eng: son of Simeon son of Judah son of Joseph son of Jonam son of Eliakim.
local: Leevi ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Simeyon. Simeyon ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Zuda. Zuda ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Zʋzɛfʋ. Zʋzɛfʋ ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Zʋnam. Zʋnam ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Eliyakɩm.

eng: son of Melea son of Menna son of Mattatha son of Nathan son of David.
local: Eliyakɩm ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Mɩlɩa. Mɩlɩa ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Mɩnna. Mɩnna ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Matata. Matata ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Natan. Natan ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Davidi.

eng: son of Jesse son of Obed son of Boaz son of Sala son of Nahshon.
local: Davidi ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Zese. Zese ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Zʋbɛdɩ. Zʋbɛdɩ ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Bʋazɩ. Bʋazɩ ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Sala. Sala ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Naasɔn.

eng: son of Amminadab son of Admin son of Arni son of Hezron son of Perez son of Judah.
local: Naasɔn ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Amɩnadabɩ. Amɩnadabɩ ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Adɩmɩnɩ. Adɩmɩnɩ ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Arnɩ. Arnɩ ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Esrɔm. Esrɔm ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Farɛsɩ. Farɛsɩ ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Zuda.

eng: son of Jacob son of Isaac son of Abraham son of Terah son of Nahor.
local: Zuda ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Zakɔbɩ. Zakɔbɩ ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Yizakɩ. Yizakɩ ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Abraham. Abraham ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Tara. Tara ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Nakɔɔrɩ.

eng: son of Serug son of Reu son of Peleg son of Eber son of Shelah.
local: Nakɔɔrɩ ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Seruki. Seruki ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Rago. Rago ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Falɛkɩ. Falɛkɩ ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Ebɛɛrɩ. Ebɛɛrɩ ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Sala.

eng: son of Cainan son of Arphaxad son of Shem son of Noah son of Lamech.
local: Sala ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Kayɩnam. Kayɩnam ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Arɩfasadɩ. Arɩfasadɩ ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Sɛm. Sɛm ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Nowe. Nowe ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Lamɛkɩ.

eng: son of Methuselah son of Enoch son of Jared son of Mahalalel son of Cainan.
local: Lamɛkɩ ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Matusala. Matusala ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Enɔkɩ. Enɔkɩ ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Zarɛdɩ. Zarɛdɩ ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Malɩlɩyɛlɩ. Malɩlɩyɛlɩ ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Kayɩnan.

eng: son of Enos son of Seth son of Adam son of God.
local: Kayɩnan ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Enɔsɩ. Enɔsɩ ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Sɛtɩ. Sɛtɩ ko dɛɛn maa yɩ Adam. Adam maa yɩ wʋlʋ Wɛ na mɔɔnɩ tɩn.

eng: Jesus returned from the Jordan River full of the Holy Spirit, and was led by the Spirit into the wilderness.
local: Zezi dɛɛn ma nuŋi Zʋrdɛn bugǝ kam ni nɩ o maa kɛa. Wɛ Joro kʋm maa tɔg-o dɩ dam. Kʋ ma ja-o kʋ vu kagʋa wʋnɩ.

eng: There he was tempted for forty days by the devil. He ate nothing during those days and afterward Jesus was starving.
local: O maa wʋra taan da fiinna. O na wʋra tɩn mʋ sʋtaanɩ tu o te, sɩ dɩ sʋg-o sɩ o ya kɩ o cɔgɩ. Maŋa kam kʋntʋ nɩ o ya ba di kʋlʋkʋlʋ. Da fiinna yam na kɛ tɩn, kana maa jɩg-o lanyɩranɩ.

eng: The devil said to him, Since you are Gods Son, command this stone to become a loaf of bread.
local: Sʋtaanɩ laan ma ba dɩ ta dɩd-o dɩ wɩ: «Nmʋ na sɩɩnɩ n yɩ Baŋa-Wɛ Bu, sɩ n ta dɩ kandwɛ dɩntʋ sɩ dɩ ji wʋdiu sɩ n di.».

eng: Jesus replied, Its written, People wont live only by bread .
local: Zezi ma lǝri-dɩ o wɩ: «Kʋ nan pʋpʋnɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ kʋ wɩ: ‹Kʋ daɩ wʋdiu yɩranɩ mʋ paɩ nabiinu ŋwɩ.› ».

eng: Next the devil led him to a high place and showed him in a single instant all the kingdoms of the world.
local: Sʋtaanɩ daa ma ja-o dɩ ja vu dɩ di piu kʋlʋ na dwara tɩn yuu. Dɩ laan ma brɩ-o lʋgʋ baŋa tɩɩnɩ dɩm maama yibʋgɩsa wʋnɩ.

eng: The devil said, I will give you this whole domain and the glory of all these kingdoms. Its been entrusted to me and I can give it to anyone I want.
local: Dɩ daa ma ta dɩ wɩ: «Wo-laarʋ tɩntʋ maama yɩ amʋ nyɩm mʋ. A nan wʋ́ wanɩ a kwe-tɩ a pa wʋlʋ maama a na lagɩ tɩn. Amʋ wʋ́ pa n di dam tɩ maama baŋa nɩ, sɩ n ta n te zulǝ nɔɔna maama tee nɩ.

eng: Therefore, if you will worship me, it will all be yours.
local: Nmʋ na sɛ n kuni doonǝ n zuli-nɩ, wǝǝnu tɩntʋ maama wʋ́ ta yɩ nmʋ nyɩm.».

eng: Jesus answered, Its written, You will worship the Lord your God and serve only him. .
local: Zezi ma lǝri-dɩ o wɩ: «Kʋ pʋpʋnɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ kʋ wɩ: ‹Kʋ yɩ n Yuutu Baŋa-Wɛ yɩranɩ mʋ n maŋɩ sɩ n kuni doonǝ n zuli, sɩ n kɩ DƖ wʋbʋŋa na lagɩ te tɩn.› ».

eng: The devil brought him into Jerusalem and stood him at the highest point of the temple. He said to him, Since you are Gods Son, throw yourself down from here.
local: Sʋtaanɩ daa ma kwe-o dɩ ja vu Zeruzalɛm. Dɩ ma ja-o dɩ ja vu dɩ di Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm yuu mɛ na dwara tɩn dɩ zɩg-o da, yɩ dɩ wɩ: «Nmʋ na yɩ Baŋa-Wɛ Bu, sɩ n faŋɩ n cu tɩga nɩ sɩ dɩ́ nii.

eng: for its written: He will command his angels concerning you, to protect you.
local: sɩ kʋ pʋpʋnɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ kʋ wɩ: ‹Wɛ wʋ́ ta DƖ malɛsɩ, sɩ sɩ taa nii nmʋ baŋa nɩ.

eng: and they will take you up in their hands so that you wont hit your foot on a stone. .
local: DƖ wʋ́ pa sɩ ba sɩ ma sɩ jɩa sɩ lwǝri-m, sɩ n yɩ tʋ tɩga nɩ n magɩ n naga dɩ kandwɛ.› ».

eng: Jesus answered, Its been said, Dont test the Lord your God. .
local: Zezi laan ma lǝri o wɩ: «Kʋ ta pʋpʋnɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ kʋ wɩ: ‹Yɩ zaŋɩ n maŋɩ n Yuutu Baŋa-Wɛ n nii.› ».

eng: After finishing every temptation, the devil departed from him until the next opportunity.
local: Sʋtaanɩ laan na maŋɩ Zezi wǝǝnu maama wʋnɩ dɩ ga tɩn, dɩ ma yag-o sɩ dɩ daa beeri maŋa kadoŋ.

eng: Jesus returned in the power of the Spirit to Galilee, and news about him spread throughout the whole countryside.
local: Zezi laan ma joori o vu Galile, yɩ Wɛ Joro kʋm dam wʋ o tee nɩ. Nɔɔna balʋ maama na zʋʋrɩ je sɩm kʋntʋ nɩ tɩn ma ni o ŋwa.

eng: He taught in their synagogues and was praised by everyone.
local: O maa yǝni o zʋ Zwifǝ bam Wɛ-di sɩm o brɩ-ba Wɛ taanɩ. Nɔɔna maama ma zul-o lanyɩranɩ.

eng: Jesus went to Nazareth, where he had been raised. On the Sabbath he went to the synagogue as he normally did and stood up to read.
local: Zezi dɛɛn ma vu o yi Nazarɛtɩ, mʋ tɩʋ kʋlʋ o na kɩ nɔɔnʋ da tɩn. Zwifǝ bam siun dɛ maama, o dɛɛn maa zʋʋrɩ ba Wɛ-digǝ kam. Ba siun dɛ dɩdʋa na yi tɩn, o ma vu da. O na zʋ da tɩn, o ma zaŋɩ wɛɛnɩ sɩ o karɩmɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ o brɩ-ba.

eng: The synagogue assistant gave him the scroll from the prophet Isaiah. He unrolled the scroll and found the place where it was written.
local: Ba ma kwe tɔnɔ kʋlʋ Wɛ nijoŋnu Ezayi na pʋpʋnɩ faŋa faŋa tɩn ba pa-o. O ma puri-kʋ o karɩma, yɩ o na mɛ seeni kʋ na pʋpʋnɩ kʋ wɩ:.

eng: The Spirit of the Lord is upon me, because the Lord has anointed me . He has sent me to preach good news to the poor, to proclaim release to the prisoners and recovery of sight to the blind, to liberate the oppressed.
local: «Yuutu Baŋa-Wɛ Joro kʋm wʋ amʋ tee nɩ. DƖ ma kuri-nɩ yɩ DƖ pa-nɩ dam, sɩ a tɔɔlɩ DƖ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam a brɩ yinigǝ tiinǝ. DƖ tʋŋɩ amʋ sɩ a ganɩ nɔɔna balʋ wʋrʋ na cɔgɩ tɩn. Sɩ a tɔɔlɩ DƖ kwǝrǝ kam dɩ balʋ na wʋ gambɛɛm wʋnɩ tɩn, sɩ ba na vrɩm, sɩ a daarɩ a brɩ lilwǝ nɩ ba yiǝ wʋ́ ba ya puri. DƖ daa ta ma tʋŋɩ-nɩ sɩ a vrɩ nɔɔna balʋ ba na bɛɛsɩ tɩn, sɩ ba na siun.

eng: and to proclaim the year of the Lords favor.
local: sɩ a daarɩ a pa nɔɔna lwarɩ sɩ maŋa kam yiǝ, sɩ Baŋa-Wɛ laan ba DƖ vrɩ DƖ nɔɔna bam lwarɩm wʋnɩ.».

eng: He rolled up the scroll, gave it back to the synagogue assistant, and sat down. Every eye in the synagogue was fixed on him.
local: Zezi na karɩmɩ o ti tɩn, o laan ma pu tɔnɔ kʋm, yɩ o kwe o pa ba Wɛ-digǝ kam tɩntʋŋnʋ. O ma jǝni sɩ o brɩ-ba. Nɔɔna bam maama ma lo yiǝ o baŋa nɩ yɩ ba cǝg-o.

eng: He began to explain to them, Today, this scripture has been fulfilled just as you heard it.
local: O ma ta o wɩ: «Zɩm dɛ dɩm nɩ abam na ni Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm na brɩ kʋlʋ tɩn kɩ á yibiyǝ nɩ.».

eng: Everyone was raving about Jesus, so impressed were they by the gracious words flowing from his lips. They said, This is Josephs son, isnt it.
local: Ba maama ma zul-o yɩ ba wɩ: «O taanɩ dɩm lamma.» O kwǝrǝ kam na ywǝmmǝ tɩn, kʋ kɩ-ba yǝǝu. Ba daa ta ma wɩ: «Kʋ daɩ Zʋzɛfʋ bu mʋ tɩntʋ na?».

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Undoubtedly, you will quote this saying to me: Doctor, heal yourself. Do here in your hometown what weve heard you did in Capernaum.
local: O ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «A nan maŋɩ a ye nɩ abam wʋ́ ŋɔɔnɩ sɩsɩɩna dɩ amʋ nɩnɛɛnɩ: ‹Nmʋ wʋlʋ na pɛ nɔɔna na yazurǝ tɩn, sɩ n zǝni n tɩtɩ!› Abam daa ta wʋ́ ta á wɩ: ‹Dɩ́ ni wǝǝnu tɩlʋ nmʋ na kɩ Kapɛrnawum nɩ tɩn ŋwa. Nan daa n kɩ kʋntʋ doŋ n tɩtɩ tɩʋ kʋm nɩ.› ».

eng: He said, I assure you that no prophet is welcome in the prophets hometown.
local: O daa ta ma ta o wɩ: «A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, Baŋa-Wɛ nijoŋnu bá na zulǝ o tɩtɩ tɩʋ nɩ.

eng: And I can assure you that there were many widows in Israel during Elijahs time, when it didnt rain for three and a half years and there was a great food shortage in the land.
local: Kʋ sɩɩnɩ kʋ yɩ cɩga mʋ, sɩ maŋa kalʋ Wɛ nijoŋnu Eli dɛɛn na wʋra tɩn, dʋa dɛɛn tu ka zɩgɩ ka wʋ nɩgɩ taan bɩna yatɔ dɩ canɩ sɩrdʋ mʋ. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ kana dɛɛn maa jɩgɩ lʋgʋ kʋm maama. Kadǝnǝ maa daga zanzan Yisɩrayɛlɩ tiinǝ bam wʋnɩ.

eng: Yet Elijah was sent to none of them but only to a widow in the city of Zarephath in the region of Sidon.
local: yɩ Wɛ nan wʋ tʋŋɩ Eli sɩ o vu ba wʋlʋwʋlʋ te. Wɛ dɛɛn tʋŋ-o kadǝm wʋdoŋ tee nɩ mʋ, o na zʋʋrɩ Sarɛpɩta tɩʋ wʋnɩ, kʋ na yɩ Sidɔn tɩʋ nɩ.

eng: There were also many persons with skin diseases in Israel during the time of the prophet Elisha, but none of them were cleansed. Instead, Naaman the Syrian was cleansed.
local: Woŋo kʋlʋ ta na wǝli da tɩn, maŋa kalʋ Wɛ nijoŋnu Elize dɛɛn na wʋra tɩn, nanywaanʋ zanzan dɛɛn wʋ Yisɩrayɛlɩ tɩʋ kʋm nɩ. Ba wʋlʋwʋlʋ dɩ dɛɛn nan wʋ nɛ yazurǝ ba wʋnɩ. Elize dɛɛn pɛ Naaman yɩranɩ mʋ na yazurǝ, yɩ wʋntʋ nuŋi Siiri tɩʋ mʋ.».

eng: When they heard this, everyone in the synagogue was filled with anger.
local: Nɔɔna balʋ maama na wʋ Wɛ-digǝ kam nɩ tɩn na ni Zezi na tagɩ-ba te tɩn, ba bana ma zaŋɩ zanzan.

eng: They rose up and ran him out of town. They led him to the crest of the hill on which their town had been built so that they could throw him off the cliff.
local: Ba ma zaŋɩ ba zǝl-o ba pa o nuŋi tɩʋ kʋm wʋnɩ. Tɩʋ kʋm wʋ piu yuu nɩ mʋ. Ba ma ja-o ba vu jǝgǝ kalʋ na luunǝ dɩ tɩga tɩn, sɩ ba yig-o ba dɩ tɩga nɩ ba gʋ.

eng: But he passed through the crowd and went on his way.
local: Ba maa warɩ. Zezi ma pipiri o vu o tɔgɩ ba tɩtarɩ o nuŋi o maa kɛa.

eng: Jesus went down to the city of Capernaum in Galilee and taught the people each Sabbath.
local: Zezi dɛɛn ma vu Galile tɩʋ kʋdoŋ, kʋ yɩrɩ mʋ Kapɛrnawum. Zwifǝ bam siun dɛ ma yi. O ma vu o zʋ ba Wɛ-digǝ kam sɩ o brɩ nɔɔna bam Wɛ taanɩ da.

eng: They were amazed by his teaching because he delivered his message with authority.
local: O na brɩ-ba tɩn, ba maa tiini ba yɩ yǝǝu dɩ o zaasɩm dɩm, o ni-taanɩ dɩm na jɩgɩ dam tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: A man in the synagogue had the spirit of an unclean demon. He screamed.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ dɛɛn maa wʋ ba Wɛ-digǝ kam wʋnɩ yɩ cicirǝ jɩg-o.

eng: Hey! What have you to do with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are. You are the holy one from God.
local: O ma kaasɩ dɩ kwǝr-dɩa yɩ o wɩ: «Aa, Zezi, Nazarɛtɩ tu, bɛɛ mʋ n lagɩ dɩbam tee nɩ? Nmʋ tu sɩ n cɔgɩ dɩbam dwi mʋ na? A ye nmʋ na yɩ wʋlʋ. Nmʋ yɩ Wʋ-poŋo Tu wʋlʋ Wɛ na tʋŋɩ tɩn mʋ.».

eng: Silence! Jesus said, speaking harshly to the demon. Come out of him! The demon threw the man down before them, then came out of him without harming him.
local: Zezi ma kaanɩ cicirǝ kam o wɩ: «Cǝgi sɔɔ, sɩ n nuŋi n daarɩ baarʋ wʋm.» Cicirǝ kam na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, ka ma yigi nɔɔnʋ wʋm ka dɩ tɩga nɩ. Ka laan ma yag-o yɩ ka wʋ kɩ-o kʋlʋkʋlʋ. Kʋntʋ kɩ nɔɔna bam maama yiǝ nɩ mʋ.

eng: They were all shaken and said to each other, What kind of word is this, that he can command unclean spirits with authority and power, and they leave.
local: Kʋ ma sʋ-ba lanyɩranɩ. Ba maama ma ŋɔɔnɩ daanɩ ba wɩ: «Bɩtara kantʋ yɩ bɩtarɩ dwi dɔɔ mʋ? Nii o na maɩ o dam o tɛ dɩ ciciri sɩm, yɩ sɩ sɛ o ni yɩ sɩ viirǝ.».

eng: Reports about him spread everywhere in the surrounding region.
local: Nɔɔna balʋ maama na zʋʋrɩ je sɩm kʋntʋ nɩ tɩn laan ma ni Zezi ŋwa.

eng: After leaving the synagogue, Jesus went home with Simon. Simons mother-in-law was sick with a high fever, and the family asked Jesus to help her.
local: Zezi dɛɛn ma nuŋi Wɛ-digǝ kam wʋnɩ. O ma vu Simɔn-ba sɔŋɔ. O na yi da tɩn dɩ paa magɩ Simɔn tɩnkaanɩ kʋ cana. Ba ma loori Zezi sɩ o zǝni kaanɩ wʋm sɩ o na yazurǝ.

eng: He bent over her and spoke harshly to the fever, and it left her. She got up at once and served them.
local: O ma vu o tiiri kaanɩ wʋm baŋa nɩ. O ma ta dɩ paa kam o wɩ, ka yag-o. Ka ma sɩɩnɩ ka yag-o. O laan ma zaŋɩ lɩla, yɩ o kɩ wʋdiu o pa-ba.

eng: When the sun was setting, everyone brought to Jesus relatives and acquaintances with all kinds of diseases. Placing his hands on each of them, he healed them.
local: Kʋ na tu kʋ yi wa-zʋʋrɩ maŋa nɩ tɩn, ba ma ja nɔɔna dɩ ba yawɩɩrʋ dwi tǝri tǝri ba ba Zezi te, sɩ o pa ba na yazurǝ. O ma daŋɩ o jɩa ba dɩdʋa dɩdʋa baŋa nɩ o pa ba maama na yazurǝ.

eng: Demons also came out of many people. They screamed, You are Gods Son. But he spoke harshly to them and wouldnt allow them to speak because they recognized that he was the Christ.
local: Kʋlʋ na wǝli da tɩn, ciciri dɩ ma nuŋi sɩ yagɩ nɔɔna zanzan sɩ ya na jɩgɩ sɩ yaara tɩn yɩra. Sɩ maa yǝni sɩ kaasa sɩ wɩ: «Nmʋ yɩ Baŋa-Wɛ Bu mʋ.» Sɩ na ye nɩ o sɩɩnɩ o yɩ Krisi wʋm Wɛ na tʋŋɩ tɩn, o ma kaanɩ-sɩ, sɩ sɩ yɩ taa ŋɔɔnɩ o taanɩ.

eng: When daybreak arrived, Jesus went to a deserted place. The crowds were looking for him. When they found him, they tried to keep him from leaving them.
local: Tɩga na pʋʋrɩ tɩtɩɩtɩ tɩn, Zezi daa ma nuŋi o vu je sɩlʋ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ na tǝrǝ tɩn. Tɩʋ kʋm nɔɔna bam ma zaŋɩ ba nuŋi sɩ ba beeri o je. Ba laan ma na-o yɩ ba vu o te. Ba ma kwaanɩ sɩ ba ja-o, sɩ o yɩ viiri o yagɩ-ba.

eng: But he said to them, I must preach the good news of Gods kingdom in other cities too, for this is why I was sent.
local: O ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «A nan maŋɩ sɩ a vu a tʋlɩ tɩɩnɩ dɩdɩm dɩm mʋ, a tɔɔlɩ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam a brɩ-ba Wɛ na lagɩ DƖ di paarɩ tɩte nɔɔna tɩtarɩ nɩ tɩn. Kʋ yɩ kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ Wɛ tʋŋɩ-nɩ nabiinǝ tee nɩ.».

eng: So he continued preaching in the Judean synagogues.
local: O laan ma tʋlɩ Zwifǝ bam tɩɩnɩ dɩm nɩ. O maa yǝni o zʋ ba Wɛ-di sɩm sɩ o taa tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝrǝ kam.

eng: After Jesus finished presenting all his words among the people, he entered Capernaum.
local: Zezi dɛɛn na brɩ nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm o zaasɩm dɩm o ti tɩn, o ma vu Kapɛrnawum.

eng: A centurion had a servant who was very important to him, but the servant was ill and about to die.
local: Pamaŋna yigǝ tu wʋdoŋ dɛɛn mʋ wʋra, o na jɩgɩ tɩntʋŋnʋ. O maa so-o lanyɩranɩ. O tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋm dɛɛn ba jɩgɩ yazurǝ, kʋ gɛ fɩɩn sɩ o tɩ.

eng: When the centurion heard about Jesus, he sent some Jewish elders to Jesus to ask him to come and heal his servant.
local: Pamaŋnʋ wʋm ma ba o ni Zezi ŋwa. O ma tʋŋɩ Zwifǝ bam nakwa o tee nɩ sɩ ba vu ba loor-o, sɩ o ba o pa o tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋm na yazurǝ.

eng: When they came to Jesus, they earnestly pleaded with Jesus. He deserves to have you do this for him, they said.
local: Ba ma vu Zezi te ba tiini ba loor-o, sɩ o ba o pa ba dɩdɛɛrʋ wʋm tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋm na yazurǝ. Ba ma ta dɩd-o ba wɩ: «O yɩ nɔn-ŋʋm.

eng: He loves our people and he built our synagogue for us.
local: O soe dɩbam balʋ na yɩ Zwifǝ tɩn zanzan, yɩ o lɔ Wɛ-digǝ o pa dɩbam. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ, kwaanɩ sɩ n duri o yibwǝnǝ.».

eng: Jesus went with them. He had almost reached the house when the centurion sent friends to say to Jesus, Lord, dont be bothered. I dont deserve to have you come under my roof.
local: Zezi ma sɛ. O ma tɔgɩ dɩdaanɩ ba o maa ve pamaŋnʋ wʋm sɔŋɔ. O na ma twɛ sɔŋɔ kʋm tɩn, dɩdaanɩ pamaŋnʋ wʋm daa tʋŋɩ o cilonnǝ o tee nɩ o wɩ: «Amʋ tu, yɩ yaarɩ n tɩtɩ n ba. A wʋ maŋɩ sɩ n ba n zʋ amʋ sɔŋɔ.

eng: In fact, I didnt even consider myself worthy to come to you. Just say the word and my servant will be healed.
local: Kʋntʋ mʋ pɛ a bʋŋɩ sɩ kʋ wʋ maŋɩ sɩ a tɩtɩ ba nmʋ te. Nan weeni n ta dɩ n ni nɩ a tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋm zaŋɩ. N na tagɩ kʋntʋ, oó na yazurǝ.

eng: Im also a man appointed under authority, with soldiers under me. I say to one, Go, and he goes, and to another, Come, and he comes. I say to my servant, Do this, and the servant does it.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ, amʋ dɩ yɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ nɔɔna badonnǝ na jɩgɩ dam a baŋa nɩ tɩn, yɩ amʋ dɩ ta jɩgɩ dam pamaŋna badaara baŋa nɩ. A na tagɩ a wɩ, nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ ve, o maŋɩ sɩ o vu mʋ. A nan daa na wɩ, nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ ba, o dɩ wʋ́ ba mʋ. Kʋ daa zɩ yɩ a tɩntʋŋnʋ, a na wɩ o kɩ woŋo, oó kɩ mʋ.».

eng: When Jesus heard these words, he was impressed with the centurion. He turned to the crowd following him and said, I tell you, even in Israel I havent found faith like this.
local: Zezi na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ ma sʋ-o. O ma pipiri o nii kɔgɔ kʋlʋ na tɔg-o tɩn, yɩ o ta o wɩ: «A lagɩ a ta abam sɩ, amʋ wʋ nɛ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ o na jɩgɩ wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ amʋ nɩnɛɛnɩ wʋntʋ na jɩgɩ wʋ-dɩdʋa te tɩn, kʋ na maŋɩ kʋ yɩ Yisɩrayɛlɩ dwi tiinǝ bam wʋnɩ dɩ.».

eng: When the centurions friends returned to his house, they found the servant restored to health.
local: Pamaŋnʋ wʋm cilonnǝ bam o na tʋŋɩ tɩn laan ma joori sɔŋɔ. Ba na yi da tɩn, ba nɛ dɩ o tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋm jɩgɩ yazurǝ.

eng: A little later Jesus went to a city called Nain. His disciples and a great crowd traveled with him.
local: Kʋntʋ na kɛ tɩn, Zezi daa ma zaŋɩ o vu tɩʋ kʋdoŋ, kʋ yɩrɩ mʋ Nayim. O na maa ve tɩn, o karabiǝ bam dɩdaanɩ kɔ-fɔrɔ mʋ tɔg-o ba veǝ.

eng: As he approached the city gate, a dead man was being carried out. He was his mothers only son, and she was a widow. A large crowd from the city was with her.
local: O na yi tɩʋ kʋm ni tɩn, o ma jeeri nɔɔna na zɩŋɩ tʋ dɩ gandwa ba ma nuŋǝ. Tʋ wʋm nu yɩ kadǝm mʋ. Mʋ o bu dɩdʋa. Tɩʋ kʋm kɔ-fɔrɔ dɩ maa tɔgɩ tʋ wʋm kwaga.

eng: When he saw her, the Lord had compassion for her and said, Dont cry.
local: Dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi na nɛ kadǝm wʋm tɩn, o ŋwaŋa ma zʋ-o. O ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Yɩ ta n keerǝ.».

eng: He stepped forward and touched the stretcher on which the dead man was being carried. Those carrying him stood still. Jesus said, Young man, I say to you, get up.
local: O ma vu o dwe gandwa yam. O na dwe-ya tɩn, balʋ na zɩŋɩ tʋ wʋm tɩn ma zɩgɩ. Zezi ma ta dɩ tʋ wʋm o wɩ: «Bu-dʋŋʋ, a tɛ dɩ nmʋ: Zaŋɩ wɛɛnɩ!».

eng: The dead man sat up and began to speak, and Jesus gave him to his mother.
local: O na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, tʋ wʋm ma sɩɩnɩ o zaŋɩ yɩ o sɩŋɩ o ŋɔɔna. Zezi ma joŋ-o o pa o nu wʋm.

eng: Awestruck, everyone praised God. A great prophet has appeared among us, they said. God has come to help his people.
local: Nɔɔna bam maama na nɛ kʋntʋ tɩn, fʋʋnɩ jaanɩ-ba lanyɩranɩ. Ba ma tee Wɛ ba wɩ: «Wɛ nijoŋnu kamunu mʋ tu dɩbam tɩtarɩ nɩ. Wɛ tu sɩ DƖ zǝni DƖ nɔɔna bam.».

eng: This news about Jesus spread throughout Judea and the surrounding region.
local: Ba laan ma ni Zezi ŋwa Zude tɩɩnɩ dɩm maama wʋnɩ dɩ je sɩm maama nɩ.

eng: Johns disciples informed him about all these things. John called two of his disciples.
local: Zan dɩ dɛɛn ma ni Zezi ŋwa. O karabiǝ bam ma ta ba brɩ-o Zezi na kɩ kʋlʋ maama tɩn. O laan ma lɩ o karabiǝ bam bale.

eng: and sent them to the Lord. They were to ask him, Are you the one who is coming, or should we look for someone else.
local: yɩ o tʋŋɩ-ba dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi tee nɩ, sɩ ba vu ba bwe-o ba nii, wʋntʋ mʋ yɩ wʋlʋ Wɛ na brɩ-ba sɩ oó ba tɩn naa ba ta wʋ́ ta cǝgi nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ mʋ?.

eng: When they reached Jesus, they said, John the Baptist sent us to you. He asks, Are you the one who is coming, or should we look for someone else.
local: Nɔɔna bam ma vu Zezi te ba ta dɩd-o ba wɩ: «Zan wʋlʋ na miisi nɔɔna na wʋnɩ tɩn mʋ tʋŋɩ dɩbam nmʋ tee nɩ, sɩ dɩ́ ba dɩ́ bwe dɩ́ nii, nmʋ mʋ yɩ wʋlʋ Wɛ na brɩ dɩbam sɩ n ba tɩn naa dɩ́ ta wʋ́ ta cǝgi nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ mʋ?».

eng: Right then, Jesus healed many of their diseases, illnesses, and evil spirits, and he gave sight to a number of blind people.
local: Kantʋ maŋa kam nɩ nɔ́ɔ, mʋ Zezi dɛɛn pɛ nɔɔna zanzan na yazurǝ dɩ ba yawɩɩrʋ dwi dwi. O ma zǝli ciciri sɩlʋ na yaarɩ nɔɔna tɩn, yɩ o daarɩ o pa lilwǝ zanzan yiǝ puri.

eng: Then he replied to Johns disciples, Go, report to John what you have seen and heard. Those who were blind are able to see. Those who were crippled now walk. People with skin diseases are cleansed. Those who were deaf now hear. Those who were dead are raised up. And good news is preached to the poor.
local: O laan ma lǝri Zan karabiǝ bam o wɩ: «Ve-na á ta dɩ Zan kʋlʋ maama á na nɛ tɩn yɩ á kwǝri á ni tɩn. Á ta á brɩ-o nɩ lilwǝ yiǝ puri, kwaarʋ dɩ waɩ tɩ veǝ, nanywaanʋ yɩra dɩ kwɛ lanyɩranɩ, zwa-kwarʋ dɩ zwa ni, twa dɩ joori ba bi. Kʋlʋ na wǝli da tɩn, yinigǝ tiinǝ dɩ maa ni Wɛ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam.

eng: Happy is anyone who doesnt stumble along the way because of me.
local: Wʋlʋ maama na ba joori amʋ kwaga nɩ tɩn mʋ yɩ yu-yoŋo tu.».

eng: After Johns messengers were gone, Jesus spoke to the crowds about John. What did you go out into the wilderness to see? A stalk blowing in the wind?.
local: Zan karabiǝ bam ma joori ba viiri. Zezi laan ma ta Zan taanɩ o brɩ nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm o wɩ: «Abam dɛɛn na ve Zan te kagʋa kam wʋnɩ tɩn, á bʋŋɩ sɩ á wʋ́ na bɛɛ mʋ? Á ve sɩ á nii miu kʋlʋ viu na jɩgɩ kʋ zuuli tɩn mʋ na? Aye, kʋ daɩ kʋntʋ.

eng: What did you go out to see? A man dressed up in refined clothes? Look, those who dress in fashionable clothes and live in luxury are in royal palaces.
local: Sɩ bɛɛ mʋ? Á ve sɩ á nii nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na zʋ gwar-ŋʋnnʋ tɩn mʋ na? Aye, kʋ daɩ kʋntʋ. Nɔɔna balʋ na zʋ gwar-ŋʋnnʋ yɩ ba ŋwɩa ywǝnǝ tɩn zʋʋrɩ sɔ-ŋʋnnʋ wʋnɩ mʋ, sɩ kʋ daɩ kagʋa wʋnɩ.

eng: What did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.
local: Bɛɛ nan mʋ á ve sɩ á nii? Á ve sɩ á na Wɛ nijoŋnu mʋ na? Ɛɛn, kʋ yɩ kʋntʋ mʋ. Á nan na nɛ wʋlʋ tɩn tiini o dwe faŋa faŋa Wɛ nijoŋnǝ bam na yɩ te tɩn.

eng: He is the one of whom its written: Look, Im sending my messenger before you, who will prepare your way before you .
local: Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm nan maŋɩ kʋ ta wʋntʋ taanɩ kʋ wɩ: ‹Nii, amʋ Wɛ lagɩ a tʋŋɩ a tɩntʋŋnʋ mʋ, sɩ o da nmʋ yigǝ o vu o kwɛ cwǝŋǝ kalʋ nmʋ na wʋ́ ba n tɔgɩ da tɩn.›.

eng: I tell you that no greater human being has ever been born than John. Yet whoever is least in Gods kingdom is greater than he.
local: A lagɩ a ta abam sɩ, nabiinǝ balʋ maama ba na maŋɩ ba lʋ tɩn wʋnɩ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ tǝrǝ o na dwe Zan na yɩ te tɩn. Kʋ daarɩ lele kʋntʋ, nɔn-nabwǝm wʋlʋ maama na zʋ Baŋa-Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋnɩ tɩn laan tu o dwe Zan.

eng: Everyone who heard this, including the tax collectors, acknowledged Gods justice because they had been baptized by John.
local: Nɔɔna bam maama dɩdaanɩ lampo-joŋnǝ bam dɛɛn dɩ na ni Zan taanɩ tɩn, ba ma sɛ sɩ Wɛ jɩgɩ cɩga dɩ ba na maŋɩ ba pa Zan miisi-ba na wʋnɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: But the Pharisees and legal experts rejected Gods will for themselves because they hadnt been baptized by John.
local: Kʋ daarɩ Farizɩan tiinǝ bam dɩ cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ bam dɛɛn wʋ sɛ sɩ Zan miisi-ba na wʋnɩ, yɩ ba vɩn Wɛ na bʋŋɩ kʋlʋ sɩ DƖ kɩ DƖ pa-ba tɩn.».

eng: To what will I compare the people of this generation? Jesus asked. What are they like?.
local: Zezi daa ma ta o wɩ: «Amʋ nan wʋ́ kɩ ta mʋ a maŋɩ zɩm nɔɔna bam kǝm na yɩ te tɩn? Ba nyɩ dɩ bɛɛ mʋ?.

eng: They are like children sitting in the marketplace calling out to each other, We played the flute for you and you didnt dance. We sang a funeral song and you didnt cry.
local: Zɩm nɔɔna bam nyɩ nɩnɛɛnɩ biǝ na je yaga kaporo nɩ ba kwɛɛrɩ te tɩn mʋ. Ba maa tɛ daanɩ ba wɩ: ‹Dɩ́ magɩ gullu mʋ sɩ á sa, yɩ á wʋ sagɩ. Dɩ́ ma daarɩ dɩ́ leeni luǝ le, yɩ á yigǝ wʋ nywanɩ.›.

eng: John the Baptist came neither eating bread nor drinking wine, and you say, He has a demon.
local: Zɩm nɔɔna bam dɩ yɩ kʋntʋ mʋ. Bɛŋwaanɩ, Zan na tu tɩn, o dɛɛn pɩɩnɩ o kana mʋ yɩ o kwǝri o ba nyɔ sana. Abam na nɛ-o tɩn, á maa jɩg-o á yáala sɩ kʋ yɩ cicirǝ mʋ tɔg-o.

eng: Yet the Human Onecame eating and drinking, and you say, Look, a glutton and a drunk, a friend of tax collectors and sinners.
local: Kʋ daarɩ, amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm nan na tu tɩn, a wʋ vɔgɩ ni. Abam daa ta ma yáalɩ amʋ dɩ á wɩ, a yɩ nɔn-kolo mʋ, yɩ a yɩ sa-nyɔrʋ dɩ. Á daa ta maa wɩ, a yɩ lampo-joŋnǝ dɩ nɔn-balwaarʋ ciloŋ mʋ.

eng: But wisdom is proved to be right by all her descendants.
local: Balʋ maama nan na tɔgɩ Wɛ cwǝŋǝ tɩn mʋ lwarɩ nɩ Wɛ jɩgɩ cɩga.».

eng: One of the Pharisees invited Jesus to eat with him. After he entered the Pharisees home, he took his place at the table.
local: Farizɩan tu dɩdʋa dɛɛn mʋ wʋra, o yɩrɩ mʋ Simɔn. O ma bǝŋi Zezi sɩ o ba o vu o sɔŋɔ o di wʋdiu. Zezi ma vu o zʋ o sɔŋɔ kʋm o jǝni sɩ o di.

eng: Meanwhile, a woman from the city, a sinner, discovered that Jesus was dining in the Pharisees house. She brought perfumed oil in a vase made of alabaster.
local: Ka-cɩcwaka dɛɛn mʋ wʋ tɩʋ kʋm kʋntʋ nɩ. O ma lwarɩ sɩ Zezi wʋ Farizɩan tu wʋm sɔŋɔ nɩ o di wʋdiu. O ma kwe kunkwǝlǝ na su dɩ tralɩ nugǝ tɩn o ja vu Zezi te.

eng: Standing behind him at his feet and crying, she began to wet his feet with her tears. She wiped them with her hair, kissed them, and poured the oil on them.
local: O ma vu o zɩgɩ Zezi nɛ ni nɩ, yɩ o keerǝ. O yi-na maa lurǝ ba bugi Zezi nɛ. O ma tiiri o ma o yuuywe o guguni o yi-na bam Zezi nɛ nɩ, yɩ o kukwǝri Zezi nɛ sɩm. O ma daarɩ o pwǝri tralɩ nugǝ kam o ma turi o nɛ sɩm.

eng: When the Pharisee who had invited Jesus saw what was happening, he said to himself, If this man were a prophet, he would know what kind of woman is touching him. He would know that she is a sinner.
local: Farizɩan tu wʋlʋ na bǝŋi Zezi o sɔŋɔ nɩ tɩn na nɛ kʋntʋ tɩn, o laan ma bʋŋɩ o wʋnɩ o wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ ya na yɩ Wɛ nijoŋnu, o ya wʋ́ lwarɩ kaanɩ wʋntʋ kǝm na yɩ te tɩn, yɩ o ya wʋ́ lwarɩ nɩ kaanɩ wʋlʋ na dwe-o tɩn yɩ ka-cɩcwaka mʋ.».

eng: Jesus replied, Simon, I have something to say to you. Teacher, speak, he said.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Simɔn a lagɩ a bwe nmʋ woŋo.» O maa wɩ: «Karanyɩna, nan bwe.».

eng: A certain lender had two debtors. One owed enough money to pay five hundred people for a days work.The other owed enough money for fifty.
local: Zezi laan ma maŋɩ mɩmaŋa o wɩ: «Nɔɔna bale mʋ jɩnɩ sǝbu nɔɔnʋ tee nɩ, dɩdʋa jɩnɩ mʋrr-fiinnu, wʋdoŋ wʋm ma jɩnɩ mʋrr-tɩnu.

eng: When they couldnt pay, the lender forgave the debts of them both. Which of them will love him more.
local: Ba maa warɩ ba ŋwɩ jɩnɩ dɩm, yɩ o yagɩ o ma cɛ ba maama. Kʋntʋ tɩn, bantʋ wʋnɩ wɔɔ mʋ wʋ́ ta so-o o dwe?».

eng: Simon replied, I suppose the one who had the largest debt canceled. Jesus said, You have judged correctly.
local: Simɔn ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «A bʋŋɩ sɩ wʋlʋ jɩnɩ na daga yɩ o yagɩ o ma cɛ-o tɩn mʋ wʋ́ taa so-o o dwe.» Zezi maa wɩ: «N sɩɩnɩ n ta cɩga.».

eng: Jesus turned to the woman and said to Simon, Do you see this woman? When I entered your home, you didnt give me water for my feet, but she wet my feet with tears and wiped them with her hair.
local: O laan ma pipiri kaanɩ wʋm seeni, yɩ o ta dɩ Simɔn o wɩ: «Nmʋ nɛ kaanɩ wʋntʋ na kɩ te tɩn na? A na tu a zʋ n sɔŋɔ kʋm tɩn, nmʋ wʋ pɛ-nɩ na sɩ a ma sɩn a nɛ. Kaanɩ wʋntʋ nan bugi a nɛ dɩdaanɩ o yi-na mʋ, yɩ o daarɩ o guguni-sɩ dɩdaanɩ o yuuywe.

eng: You didnt greet me with a kiss, but she hasnt stopped kissing my feet since I came in.
local: A na tu tɩn, nmʋ wʋ kukwǝri-nɩ sɩ kʋ brɩ nɩ n jeeri-nɩ lanyɩranɩ. Kaanɩ wʋntʋ nan kukwǝri a nɛ mʋ taan, kʋ na puli dɩ maŋa kam a na tu nmʋ sɔŋɔ tɩn.

eng: You didnt anoint my head with oil, but she has poured perfumed oil on my feet.
local: A na tu nmʋ te tɩn, nmʋ wʋ kɩ nugǝ a yuu nɩ sɩ kʋ pa-nɩ zulǝ. Kaanɩ wʋntʋ nan kwe tralɩ nugǝ mʋ o lo o ma turi a nɛ.

eng: This is why I tell you that her many sins have been forgiven; so she has shown great love. The one who is forgiven little loves little.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ a lagɩ a ta nmʋ sɩ, o sono kamunu kʋm mʋ brɩ nɩ Wɛ yagɩ o lwarma yam zanzan DƖ ma cɛ-o. Kʋ daarɩ wʋlʋ Wɛ na yagɩ o lwarɩm fɩnfɩɩn DƖ ma cɛ-o tɩn jɩgɩ sono fɩnfɩɩn yɩranɩ mʋ.».

eng: Then Jesus said to her, Your sins are forgiven.
local: Zezi laan ma daarɩ o ta dɩ kaanɩ wʋm o wɩ: «N lwarɩm dɩm ti.».

eng: The other table guests began to say among themselves, Who is this person that even forgives sins.
local: O na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, nɔɔna balʋ dɩ na tɔgɩ ba wʋra tɩn ma ŋɔɔnɩ daanɩ ba wɩ: «Wʋntʋ nan brɩ o tɩtɩ nɩ o yɩ wɔɔ mʋ, yɩ o yagɩ nɔɔnʋ lwarɩm o ma cɛ-o?».

eng: Jesus said to the woman, Your faith has saved you. Go in peace.
local: O laan ma ta dɩ kaanɩ wʋm o wɩ: «Nmʋ na kɩ n wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ amʋ tɩn mʋ pɛ n na vrɩm. Ve dɩ yazurǝ.».

eng: One day Jesus was standing beside Lake Gennesaret when the crowd pressed in around him to hear Gods word.
local: Dɛ dɩdwɩ mʋ Zezi dɛɛn ve o zɩgɩ nɩnɩʋ kʋdoŋ ni nɩ, kʋ yɩrɩ mʋ Zenezarɛtɩ. O na zɩgɩ da kʋntʋ tɩn, nɔɔna ma ba o te sɩ ba taa cǝgi Wɛ kwǝrǝ kam. Ba ma yigi daanɩ sɩ ba twɛ Zezi.

eng: Jesus saw two boats sitting by the lake. The fishermen had gone ashore and were washing their nets.
local: O ma na nabwǝǝru tɩle tɩ tigi nɩnɩʋ kʋm ni nɩ, kalǝŋ-jara na yagɩ-tɩ yɩ ba wʋra ba zarɩ ba bʋrɩ.

eng: Jesus boarded one of the boats, the one that belonged to Simon, then asked him to row out a little distance from the shore. Jesus sat down and taught the crowds from the boat.
local: Naboro dɩdʋ kʋm yɩ Simɔn nyɩm mʋ. Zezi ma zʋ kʋ wʋnɩ. O ma ta dɩdaanɩ Simɔn o wɩ: «Co boro kʋm n ja n vu yigǝ fɩnfɩɩn n pa-nɩ.» O na cogǝ o ja o yi yigǝ tɩn, Zezi laan ma je naboro kʋm wʋnɩ o tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝrǝ o brɩ nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm.

eng: When he finished speaking to the crowds, he said to Simon, Row out farther, into the deep water, and drop your nets for a catch.
local: O na brɩ-ba o ti tɩn, o ma ta dɩ Simɔn o wɩ: «Co boro kʋm n ja n vu na-luŋǝ kam je, sɩ abam dɩ á bʋrɩ sɩm na bam kuri nɩ á ma ja kale.».

eng: Simon replied, Master, weve worked hard all night and caught nothing. But because you say so, Ill drop the nets.
local: O ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «A Yuutu, dɩbam maŋɩ dɩ́ dɩ bʋrɩ sɩm taan dɩ́ pʋʋrɩ tɩga, yɩ dɩ́ wʋ nɛ kʋlʋkʋlʋ. Nmʋ nan na wɩ, dɩ́ daa joori dɩ́ dɩ tɩn, dɩ́ wʋ́ dɩ-sɩ sɩ dɩ́ nii.».

eng: So they dropped the nets and their catch was so huge that their nets were splitting.
local: Ba ma vu ba yi na bam tɩtarɩ, yɩ ba dɩ bʋrɩ sɩm da ba ja kale zanzan. Kale sɩm maa tiini sɩ daga zanzan, yɩ kʋ pa bʋrɩ sɩm lagɩ sɩ kaarɩ.

eng: They signaled for their partners in the other boat to come and help them. They filled both boats so full that they were about to sink.
local: Ba ma yɩgɩsɩ jɩa ba bǝŋi ba donnǝ bam naboro kʋkʋm kʋm wʋnɩ, sɩ ba ba ba wǝli-ba. Bantʋ dɩ ma ja ba boro kʋm ba vu ba zǝni-ba. Bwǝǝru tɩm tɩle maama ma su dɩ kale. Tɩ ma ga fɩnfɩɩn sɩ tɩ miisi na bam wʋnɩ.

eng: When Simon Peter saw the catch, he fell at Jesus knees and said, Leave me, Lord, for Im a sinner.
local: Simɔn wʋlʋ yɩrɩ dɩdoŋ na yɩ Pɩyɛɛrɩ tɩn na nɛ kʋlʋ na kɩ tɩn, o ma vu o kuni doonǝ Zezi yigǝ nɩ yɩ o wɩ: «A Yuutu, fɔɔrɩ a tee nɩ, sɩ a yɩ lwarɩm tu mʋ.».

eng: Peter and those with him were overcome with amazement because of the number of fish they caught.
local: Ba na jaanɩ kale zanzan tɩn ŋwaanɩ mʋ fʋʋnɩ zʋ Simɔn dɩdaanɩ nɔɔna balʋ na wʋ o tee nɩ tɩn. Kʋntʋ mʋ te Simɔn tagɩ kʋntʋ.

eng: James and John, Zebedees sons, were Simons partners and they were amazed too. Jesus said to Simon, Dont be afraid. From now on, you will be fishing for people.
local: Fʋʋnɩ dɩm dɩ dɛɛn zʋ Zebede biǝ balʋ na tɔgɩ ba wʋra tɩn. Ba dɩdʋa yɩrɩ mʋ Zakɩ, o nyaanɩ yɩrɩ mʋ Zan. Bantʋ dɛɛn yǝni ba tɔgɩ dɩ Simɔn mʋ ba tʋŋa. Zezi daa ma ta dɩ Simɔn o wɩ: «Yɩ ta n kwarɩ fʋʋnɩ. Kʋ na zɩgɩ zɩm dɛ dɩm nɩ sɩ kʋ taa ve tɩn, nmʋ laan wʋ́ ta n beeri nɔɔna mʋ n pa-nɩ sɩ ba da amʋ kwaga, nɩ nmʋ ya na yǝni n jaanɩ kale te tɩn.».

eng: As soon as they brought the boats to the shore, they left everything and followed Jesus.
local: Ba ma joori ba ja nabwǝǝru tɩm ba nuŋi ba tiŋi buburu kʋm ni nɩ. Ba na kɩ kʋntʋ ba ti tɩn, ba yagɩ ba wǝǝnu maama, yɩ ba laan daarɩ ba tɔgɩ Zezi.

eng: Jesus was in one of the towns where there was also a man covered with a skin disease. When he saw Jesus, he fell on his face and begged, Lord, if you want, you can make me clean.
local: Dɛ dɩdwɩ mʋ Zezi ve tɩʋ kʋdoŋ. Baarʋ wʋdoŋ mʋ wʋra o na yɩ nanyɔŋɔ. O yawɩʋ kʋm dɛɛn zʋ o yɩra zanzan. O na nɛ Zezi tɩn, o ma vu o te o tʋ o yigǝ nɩ, yɩ o loor-o o wɩ: «Amʋ tu, nmʋ na laga, nmʋ wʋ́ wanɩ n pa a na yazurǝ a ji lanyɩranɩ.».

eng: Jesus reached out his hand, touched him, and said, I do want to. Be clean. Instantly, the skin disease left him.
local: Zezi ma sɛ. O ma twɩ o jɩŋa o dwe-o o wɩ: «Ɛɛn, a laga. Zaŋɩ dɩ yazurǝ sɩ n ji lanyɩranɩ.» O na tagɩ kʋntʋ bɩdwɩ baŋa nɩ tɩn, mʋ yawɩʋ kʋm je nɔɔnʋ wʋm yɩra nɩ.

eng: Jesus ordered him not to tell anyone. Instead, Jesus said, go and show yourself to the priest and make an offering for your cleansing, as Moses instructed. This will be a testimony to them.
local: Zezi ma kaan-o o wɩ: «Yɩ zaŋɩ n ta kʋlʋ na kɩ tɩn n brɩ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ. Nan yɔɔrɩ n vu kaanɩm tu wʋm te, sɩ n pa o nii n yɩra yam, sɩ n daarɩ n kwe wǝǝnu tɩlʋ Moyisi cullu tɩm na brɩ nɩ tɩ maŋɩ tɩn n pa-o, sɩ o ma kaanɩ Baŋa-Wɛ. Kʋntʋ mʋ wʋ́ brɩ nɔɔna nɩ n yawɩʋ kʋm sɩɩnɩ kʋ je.».

eng: News of him spread even more and huge crowds gathered to listen and to be healed from their illnesses.
local: Nan dɩ kʋ dɩ, nɔɔna zanzan dɛɛn ni Zezi ŋwa je maama. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ nɔn-kɔgɔ zanzan maa ve o te sɩ ba taa cǝgi o ni-taanɩ dɩm, sɩ ba daarɩ ba na yazurǝ.

eng: But Jesus would withdraw to deserted places for prayer.
local: O dɛɛn yǝni o ve mɛ seeni nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ na tǝrǝ tɩn mʋ o warɩ Wɛ.

eng: One day when Jesus was teaching, Pharisees and legal experts were sitting nearby. They had come from every village in Galilee and Judea, and from Jerusalem. Now the power of the Lord was with Jesus to heal.
local: Dɛ dɩdʋa mʋ Zezi dɛɛn wʋra o brɩ nɔɔna Wɛ taanɩ. Ba badaara yɩ Farizɩan tiinǝ mʋ dɩdaanɩ Wɛ cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ. Ba dɛɛn nuŋi tɩɩnɩ dwi tǝri tǝri mʋ ba ba. Kʋ na yɩ Zeruzalɛm dɩ tɩɩnɩ dɩlʋ maama na wʋ Galile nɩ dɩ Zude nɩ tɩn. Ba jǝni ba kaagɩ Zezi, yɩ o jɩgɩ Wɛ dam o ma pa yawɩɩna na yazurǝ.

eng: Some men were bringing a man who was paralyzed, lying on a cot. They wanted to carry him in and place him before Jesus.
local: O na wʋra o brɩ-ba kʋntʋ tɩn, nɔɔna badonnǝ ya zɩŋɩ kɔrɔ dɩ gungolo ba jɩgɩ ba bɩɩna. Ba na yi da tɩn, mʋ ba kwaanɩ sɩ ba ja-o ba ja zʋ digǝ kam wʋ ba zʋʋrɩ ba tiŋi Zezi yigǝ nɩ.

eng: but they couldnt reach him because of the crowd. So they took him up on the roof and lowered him—cot and all—through the roof tiles into the crowded room in front of Jesus.
local: Ba maa warɩ, kɔgɔ kʋm na daga tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Ba ma wʋ nɛ cwǝŋǝ sɩ ba zʋ. Ba ma ja-o ba di nayuu. Ba ma puri bɔɔnɩ yɩ ba daarɩ ba pa gungolo kʋm tɔgɩ da kʋ tu digǝ kam wʋnɩ. Kʋ pǝni nɔɔna bam tɩtarɩ nɩ Zezi yigǝ nɩ.

eng: When Jesus saw their faith, he said, Friend, your sins are forgiven.
local: Zezi ma lwarɩ nɩ ba kɩ ba wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩd-o. O ma ta o wɩ: «A badoŋ, nmʋ lwarɩm dɩm ti.».

eng: The legal experts and Pharisees began to mutter among themselves, Who is this who insults God? Only God can forgive sins.
local: O na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, Wɛ cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ bam dɩ Farizɩan tiinǝ bam ma bwɛ ba wʋnɩ ba wɩ: «O brɩ o tɩtɩ nɩ o yɩ wɔɔ mʋ, yɩ o paɩ o dɩ Wɛ yɩ bɩdwɩ? O ŋɔɔnɩ o twɩ Wɛ mʋ. Nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ nan ba jɩgɩ cwǝŋǝ sɩ o yagɩ nɔɔna lwarɩm o ma cɛ-ba, kʋ na daɩ Wɛ DƖ yɩranɩ.».

eng: Jesus recognized what they were discussing and responded, Why do you fill your minds with these questions?.
local: Zezi ma lwarɩ ba na bwɛ te tɩn, yɩ o ta-ba o wɩ: «Bɛŋwaanɩ mʋ á bʋŋɩ kʋntʋ á wʋnɩ?.

eng: Which is easier—to say, Your sins are forgiven, or to say, Get up and walk?.
local: Kɔɔ mʋ yɩ mwalɩ kʋ dwe, amʋ na tagɩ dɩ kɔrɔ kʋm a wɩ: ‹Nmʋ lwarɩm dɩm ti› mʋ yɩ mwalɩ, naa a na tagɩ a wɩ: ‹Zaŋɩ n ta n veǝ› mʋ yɩ mwalɩ?.

eng: But so that you will know that the Human Onehas authority on the earth to forgive sins—Jesus now spoke to the man who was paralyzed,I say to you, get up, take your cot, and go home.
local: Amʋ nan wʋ́ pa abam lwarɩ sɩ a na yɩ Nabiin-bu wʋm tɩn jɩgɩ dam lʋgʋ baŋa nɩ, sɩ a yagɩ nɔɔna lwarɩm a ma cɛ-ba.» O laan ma ta dɩ kɔrɔ kʋm o wɩ: «A lagɩ a ta nmʋ, zaŋɩ wɛɛnɩ sɩ n kwe n gungolo kʋm n ja n vu sɔŋɔ.».

eng: Right away, the man stood before them, picked up his cot, and went home, praising God.
local: Zezi na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, bɩdwɩ baŋa nɩ mʋ nɔɔnʋ wʋm zaŋɩ ba maama yigǝ nɩ, yɩ o kwe o gungolo kʋm o ya na tigi kʋ baŋa nɩ tɩn o zɩŋɩ o maa ve sɔŋɔ, yɩ o zuli Wɛ.

eng: All the people were beside themselves with wonder. Filled with awe, they glorified God, saying, Weve seen unimaginable things today.
local: Nɔɔna bam maama na nɛ kʋlʋ na kɩ tɩn, mʋ kʋ kɩ-ba yǝǝu. Fʋʋnɩ ma ja-ba. Ba ma zuli Wɛ, yɩ ba tɛ ba wɩ: «Dɩ́ zɩm nɛ wo-kɩnkagɩla!».

eng: Afterward, Jesus went out and saw a tax collector named Levi sitting at a kiosk for collecting taxes. Jesus said to him, Follow me.
local: Kʋntʋ maama na kɛ tɩn, Zezi ma zaŋɩ o nuŋi o maa kɛa. O na maa kɛ tɩn mʋ o nɛ lampo-joŋnu wʋdoŋ, o yɩrɩ mʋ Leevi. O dɛɛn je mɛ seeni o na yǝni o jǝni o joŋi lampoo tɩn. Zezi ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Zaŋɩ n ta n tɔgɩ amʋ.».

eng: Levi got up, left everything behind, and followed him.
local: Leevi ma zaŋɩ o yagɩ o woŋo maama yɩ o tɔgɩ o kwaga.

eng: Then Levi threw a great banquet for Jesus in his home. A large number of tax collectors and others sat down to eat with them.
local: O ma vu sɔŋɔ o kɩ wʋdiiru zanzan o ma kɩ candiǝ o pa Zezi. Lampo-joŋnǝ zanzan dɩ nɔɔna badonnǝ ma ba ba tɔgɩ ba di.

eng: The Pharisees and their legal experts grumbled against his disciples. They said, Why do you eat and drink with tax collectors and sinners.
local: Farizɩan tiinǝ dɩ ba cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ bam na nɛ kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma vu Zezi karabiǝ bam te ba ŋɔɔna yɩ ba pʋʋna ba wɩ: «Bɛɛ mʋ kɩ yɩ abam di wʋdiu dɩdaanɩ lampo-joŋnǝ dɩ nɔn-cɩcɔgɩrʋ tɩm kʋntʋ?».

eng: Jesus answered, Healthy people dont need a doctor, but sick people do.
local: Zezi ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Kʋ na yɩ balʋ na jɩgɩ yazurǝ tɩn, bantʋ wʋ maŋɩ sɩ ba vu dɔgɩta tu te sɩ o sooni-ba. Kʋ daarɩ balʋ na ba jɩgɩ yazurǝ tɩn mʋ maŋɩ sɩ ba vu ba na soonim.

eng: I didnt come to call righteous people but sinners to change their hearts and lives.
local: Amʋ na tu lʋgʋ baŋa tɩn, kʋ daɩ sɩ a bǝŋi balʋ na maŋɩ ba yɩ nɔn-ŋʋna tɩn, a nan tu sɩ a bǝŋi nɔn-balwaarʋ mʋ, sɩ ba lǝni ba wʋrʋ ba yagɩ kǝm-balwaarʋ.».

eng: Some people said to Jesus, The disciples of John fast often and pray frequently. The disciples of the Pharisees do the same, but your disciples are always eating and drinking.
local: Ba daa ta ma ta dɩ Zezi ba wɩ: «Zan karabiǝ bam yǝni ba vɔ ni mʋ maŋa zanzan yɩ ba daarɩ ba warɩ Wɛ. Farizɩan karabiǝ bam dɩ yǝni ba kɩ kʋntʋ doŋ mʋ. Bɛɛ mʋ yɩ nmʋ karabiǝ bam ba vɔ ni kʋntʋ?».

eng: Jesus replied, You cant make the wedding guests fast while the groom is with them, can you?.
local: O ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ na di kaanɩ yɩ o kɩ candiǝ, yɩ o cilonnǝ na wʋra sɩ ba tɔgɩ ba di, n sɛ n pa ba vɔ ni yɩ kan-barʋ wʋm ta wʋ ba tee nɩ na?.

eng: The days will come when the groom will be taken from them, and then they will fast.
local: Maŋa nan wʋ́ ba yi, yɩ ba ja kan-barʋ wʋm ba viiri. Maŋa kam kʋntʋ nɩ yɩ o daa na ba ta wʋ ba tee nɩ tɩn, mʋ ba laan wʋ́ vɔ ni.».

eng: Then he told them a parable. No one tears a patch from a new garment to patch an old garment. Otherwise, the new garment would be ruined, and the new patch wouldnt match the old garment.
local: Zezi daa ma maŋɩ mɩmaŋɩ dɩdoŋ o brɩ-ba o wɩ: «Nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ bá sɛ o kwe gwar-dʋŋʋ o kaarɩ sɩ o ma fǝri gwar-doŋo. O nan na kɩ kʋntʋ oó kaarɩ gwar-dʋŋʋ kʋm o cɔgɩ mʋ, yɩ kʋ bá wanɩ kʋ maŋɩ dɩ gwar-doŋo kʋm.

eng: Nobody pours new wine into old wineskins. If they did, the new wine would burst the wineskins, the wine would spill, and the wineskins would be ruined.
local: Nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ dɩ nan bá sɛ o kwe sa-dʋŋa o lo o kɩ lɔ-doŋo wʋnɩ. O na kɩ kʋntʋ, yɩ sana kam na keeri, kaá pa lɔɔ kʋm bagɩ mʋ. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ sana kam wʋ́ lo, yɩ lɔɔ kʋm dɩ cɔgɩ.

eng: Instead, new wine must be put into new wineskins.
local: Sa-dʋŋa nan maŋɩ sɩ n lo n kɩ lɔ-dʋŋʋ wʋnɩ mʋ.

eng: No one who drinks a well-aged wine wants new wine, but says, The well-aged wine is better.
local: Nɔɔnʋ nan na nyɔgɩ sa-doŋǝ o mɩ, o daa bá lagɩ sɩ o nyɔ sa-dʋŋa. Oó ta wɩ: ‹Sa-doŋǝ kam tiini ka lamma›.».

eng: One Sabbath, as Jesus was going through the wheat fields, his disciples were picking the heads of wheat, rubbing them in their hands, and eating them.
local: Zwifǝ bam siun dɛ dɩdoŋ daa ma yi. Zezi dɩ o karabiǝ bam dɛɛn ma tɔgɩ daanɩ ba da karɩ wʋ ba maa kɛa. Ba na maa kɛ tɩn, o karabiǝ bam maa bwǝri mɩna ba yu ba dʋna.

eng: Some Pharisees said, Why are you breaking the Sabbath law.
local: Farizɩan tiinǝ badonnǝ na nɛ kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma bwe ba wɩ: «Bɛɛ mʋ yɩ abam tʋŋɩ tɩtʋŋɩ dɩlʋ na cɔgɩ dɩbam siun dɛ cullu tɩm tɩn?».

eng: Jesus replied, Havent you read what David and his companions did when they were hungry?.
local: Zezi ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Abam ta wʋ fɔgɩ á karɩmɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm na brɩ Pɛ Davidi taanɩ te tɩn na? Kʋ brɩ sɩ kana dɛɛn jaan-o dɩ o nɔɔna bam.

eng: He broke the Law by going into Gods house and eating the bread of the presence, which only the priests can eat. He also gave some of the bread to his companions.
local: O ma vu o zʋ Wɛ digǝ, yɩ o kwe culu dɩpɛ dɩlʋ na tigi da Wɛ ŋwaanɩ tɩn. Dɩpɛ dɩntʋ ya culǝ sɩ nɔn-gaa di-dɩ, kʋ na daɩ Wɛ kaanɩm tiinǝ bam yɩranɩ. Yɩ Davidi dɛɛn di dɩpɛ dɩm, yɩ o daarɩ o pa o nɔɔna bam dɩ tɔgɩ ba di».

eng: Then he said to them, The Human Oneis Lord of the Sabbath.
local: Zezi daa ta ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Kʋntʋ tɩn, amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm jɩgɩ ni siun dɛ cullu tɩm baŋa nɩ.».

eng: On another Sabbath, Jesus entered a synagogue to teach. A man was there whose right hand was withered.
local: Kʋntʋ na kɛ tɩn, siun dɛ dɩdɩm daa ma yi. Zezi ma vu o zʋ Zwifǝ bam Wɛ-digǝ. O maa wʋra o brɩ-ba Wɛ taanɩ. Nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ maa tɔgɩ o wʋra, o jazɩm jɩŋa na tɩga.

eng: The legal experts and the Pharisees were watching him closely to see if he would heal on the Sabbath. They were looking for a reason to bring charges against him.
local: Wɛ cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ dɩ Farizɩan tiinǝ dɩ maa wʋra. Bantʋ maa kwaanɩ ba lagɩ cwǝŋǝ sɩ ba brɩ nɩ Zezi kɩ o tusi. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ ba dɛɛn je ba yɩr-o sɩ ba nii, oó sɛ o pa nɔɔnʋ na yazurǝ siun dɛ dɩm nɩ naa o bá sɛ.

eng: Jesus knew their thoughts, so he said to the man with the withered hand, Get up and stand in front of everyone. He got up and stood there.
local: Zezi ma lwarɩ ba na bʋŋɩ te tɩn. O ma ta dɩ jɩ-tɩga kam tu o wɩ: «Zaŋɩ wɛɛnɩ n ba n zɩgɩ nɔɔna bam maama tɩtarɩ nɩ.» O ma zaŋɩ o nuŋi o zɩgɩ da.

eng: Jesus said to the legal experts and Pharisees, Heres a question for you: Is it legal on the Sabbath to do good or to do evil, to save life or to destroy it.
local: Zezi ma bwe-ba o wɩ: «Cullu brɩ sɩ bɛɛ mʋ? Kɔɔ mʋ maŋɩ dɩ culu kʋm, nɔɔnʋ na kɩ o doŋ lanyɩranɩ siun dɛ nɩ mʋ maŋɩ naa o na kɩ-o lwarɩm mʋ gara? Nɔɔnʋ na vrɩ o doŋ ŋwɩa siun dɛ nɩ mʋ maŋɩ naa o na gʋ-o mʋ gara?».

eng: Looking around at them all, he said to the man, Stretch out your hand. So he did and his hand was made healthy.
local: O na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma nii ba maama, yɩ o daarɩ o ŋɔɔnɩ dɩ jɩ-tɩga kam tu o wɩ: «Twɩ n jɩŋa kam!» O na twɩ o jɩŋa kam tɩn, ka ma joori ka ji lanyɩranɩ.

eng: They were furious and began talking with each other about what to do to Jesus.
local: Ba na nɛ kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma fʋlɩ dɩdaanɩ Zezi, yɩ ba banɩ daanɩ sɩ ba nii ba na wʋ́ wanɩ ba kɩ-o te tɩn.

eng: Soon afterward, Jesus traveled through the cities and villages, preaching and proclaiming the good news of Gods kingdom. The Twelve were with him.
local: Kʋntʋ kwaga nɩ, Zezi dɛɛn ma beeri o tʋlɩ tɩ-kamunnu dɩ tɩ-balwa bam maama, yɩ o tɔɔlɩ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam o brɩ Wɛ na lagɩ DƖ di paarɩ tɩte nɔɔna tɩtarɩ nɩ tɩn. O karabiǝ fugǝ-bale bam dɩ dɛɛn maa tɔgɩ dɩd-o ba beerǝ.

eng: During that time, Jesus went out to the mountain to pray, and he prayed to God all night long.
local: Da yantʋ ni nɩ, Zezi ma vu o di piu kʋdoŋ yuu sɩ o warɩ Wɛ. Tɩtɩɩ dɩm kʋntʋ maama o warɩ Wɛ taan, sɩ kʋ yi tɩ-pʋra.

eng: along with some women who had been healed of evil spirits and sicknesses. Among them were Mary Magdalene.
local: Kaana badonnǝ dɩ tɔgɩ ba wʋ o tee nɩ. Bantʋ yɩ balʋ ciciri dɩ yawɩɩrʋ dwi dwi dɛɛn na yaarɩ-ba yɩ Zezi pa ba na yazurǝ tɩn. Ba dɩdʋa yɩrɩ mʋ Mari wʋlʋ na nuŋi Magɩdala tɩn. Wʋntʋ mʋ Zezi ya zǝli ciciri sɩrpɛ yɩ sɩ nuŋi sɩ daar-o.

eng: At daybreak, he called together his disciples. He chose twelve of them whom he called apostles.
local: Tɩga na pʋʋrɩ ka ti tɩn, o ma bǝŋi o karabiǝ bam sɩ ba ba o te. O laan ma lɩ nɔɔna fugǝ-bale ba wʋnɩ, sɩ ba taa yɩ nɔɔna balʋ o na wʋ́ tʋŋɩ tɩn.

eng: Joanna , Susanna, and many others who provided for them out of their resources.
local: Wʋdoŋ yɩrɩ mʋ Zannɩ. Wʋntʋ mʋ yɩ Zuuza wʋlʋ na nii Erɔdɩ sɔŋɔ baŋa nɩ tɩn kaanɩ. Wʋdoŋ dɩ yɩrɩ mʋ Suzaanɩ. Kaana zanzan dɩ tɔgɩ ba wʋra. Kaana bantʋ maama dɛɛn yǝni ba lɩ ba jɩjɩgɩrʋ ba ma zǝni Zezi dɩ o karabiǝ bam.

eng: Simon, whom he named Peter; his brother Andrew; James; John; Philip; Bartholomew.
local: Ba yɩra mʋ tɩntʋ: Simɔn wʋlʋ Zezi na pɛ o yɩrɩ o wɩ Pɩyɛɛrɩ tɩn, Andre wʋlʋ na yɩ Simɔn nyaanɩ tɩn, Zakɩ, Zan, Filipi, Batelemi.

eng: When a great crowd was gathering and people were coming to Jesus from one city after another, he spoke to them in a parable.
local: Nɔɔna zanzan dɛɛn nuŋi tɩɩnɩ tǝri tǝri ba ba Zezi te. Kɔ-fɔrɔ na gilim-o kʋntʋ tɩn, o laan ma maŋɩ mɩmaŋa o brɩ-ba o wɩ:.

eng: Matthew; Thomas; James the son of Alphaeus; Simon, who was called a zealot.
local: Matiyu, Toma, Zakɩ wʋlʋ na yɩ Alɩfɩ bu tɩn, Simɔn wʋlʋ na yɩ Zelɔtɩ nɔɔna bam wʋ nɔɔnʋ tɩn.

eng: A farmer went out to scatter his seed. As he was scattering it, some fell on the path where it was crushed, and the birds in the sky came and ate it.
local: «Valʋ dɛɛn mʋ wʋra. O ma zaŋɩ o vu o kara duum maŋa nɩ. O ma karɩ o dʋlɩ o wo-duuru tɩm kara kam wʋnɩ. Wo-duuru tɩdonnǝ ma tʋ cwǝŋǝ ni nɩ. Nɔɔna ma nɔna tɩ baŋa nɩ ba kɛa. Zunǝ ma na-tɩ ba twɛ ba di.

eng: Judas the son of James; and Judas Iscariot, who became a traitor.
local: Zudasɩ wʋlʋ na yɩ Zakɩ bu tɩn, dɩdaanɩ Zudasɩ Yiskarɩyo wʋlʋ na wʋ́ ba o yǝgi Zezi o pa nɔɔna tɩn.

eng: Other seed fell on rock. As it grew, it dried up because it had no moisture.
local: Tɩdaara ma tʋ kandwa jǝgǝ nɩ. Tɩ ma nuŋi yɩ tɩ kwaarɩ tɩ tɩ lɩla, tɩga kam na ba zurǝ tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: Jesus came down from the mountain with them and stood on a large area of level ground. A great company of his disciples and a huge crowd of people from all around Judea and Jerusalem and the area around Tyre and Sidon joined him there.
local: Zezi dɛ dɩ ba o zɩgɩ piu kʋm yuu nɩ o tu pɔgɔ-tɩga. O ma zɩgɩ da dɩdaanɩ o karabiǝ zanzan, dɩ nɔn-kɔgɔ zanzan maŋɩ kʋ gilimi kʋ su jǝgǝ kam maama. Bantʋ dɛɛn nuŋi je dwi tǝri tǝri mʋ Zwifǝ bam tɩɩnɩ dɩm maama wʋnɩ. Badonnǝ nuŋi Zeruzalɛm. Badonnǝ dɩ ma nuŋi Tiiri dɩ Sidɔn je sɩlʋ na wʋ nɩnɩʋ kʋm ni nɩ tɩn.

eng: Other seed fell among thorny plants. The thorns grew with the plants and choked them.
local: Tɩdaara ma tʋ sabarɩ jǝgǝ nɩ. Tɩ na puli tɩ zaŋɩ tɩn, sabarɩ sɩm ma tɔgɩ sɩ zaŋɩ sɩ li-tɩ sɩ gʋ.

eng: They came to hear him and to be healed from their diseases, and those bothered by unclean spirits were healed.
local: Ba dɛɛn tu Zezi te sɩ ba cǝgi o taanɩ dɩm, sɩ ba kwǝri ba na yazurǝ dɩ ba yawɩɩrʋ. Nɔɔna badaara wʋra ciciri na jɩgɩ-ba sɩ yaara, yɩ ba na yazurǝ.

eng: Still other seed landed on good soil. When it grew, it produced one hundred times more grain than was scattered. As he said this, he called out, Everyone who has ears should pay attention.
local: Kʋ daarɩ tɩdaara ma tʋ tɩ-ywǝŋǝ jǝgǝ nɩ. Tɩ ma nuŋi tɩ kɩ biǝ lanyɩranɩ nɩnɛɛnɩ biǝ bi bi te.» Zezi na tagɩ kʋntʋ o ti tɩn, o laan ma ta dɩ kwǝr-dɩa o wɩ: «Abam nan fɔgɩ á cǝgi a taanɩ dɩm lanyɩranɩ.».

eng: The whole crowd wanted to touch him, because power was going out from him and he was healing everyone.
local: Nɔɔna bam maama maa kwaanɩ sɩ ba dwe-o, bɛŋwaanɩ dam nuŋi o yɩra nɩ dɩ pa ba na yazurǝ.

eng: His disciples asked him what this parable meant.
local: Zezi karabiǝ bam laan ma bwe-o ba wɩ: «Mɩmaŋɩ dɩntʋ kuri mʋ bɛɛ?».

eng: Jesus raised his eyes to his disciples and said: Happy are you who are poor, because Gods kingdom is yours.
local: Zezi ma kwǝni o yiǝ o nii o karabiǝ bam seeni yɩ o wɩ: «Yinigǝ na jɩgɩ abam balʋ tɩn mʋ jɩgɩ yu-yoŋo, á na wʋ́ da á di Wɛ paarɩ dɩm tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: He said, You have been given the mysteries of Gods kingdom, but these mysteries come to everyone else in parables so that when they see, they cant see, and when they hear, they cant understand .
local: O ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Wɛ paarɩ dɩm kuri ya sǝgi mʋ. Kʋ nan na yɩ abam, Wɛ maŋɩ DƖ pa á lwarɩ DƖ paarɩ dɩm kuri na yɩ te tɩn. Kʋ na yɩ nɔɔna badaara, a yǝni a maŋɩ mɩmaŋa mʋ a brɩ-ba, sɩ ‹ba na niǝ, sɩ ba yɩ taa naɩ, ba na cǝgǝ, sɩ ba yɩ taa ni kʋ kuri.›.

eng: Happy are you who hunger now, because you will be satisfied. Happy are you who weep now, because you will laugh.
local: Kana na jɩgɩ abam balʋ lele tɩn mʋ jɩgɩ yu-yoŋo, Wɛ na wʋ́ pa abam kʋ daga tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Abam balʋ wʋrʋ na cɔgɩ lele tɩn mʋ jɩgɩ yu-yoŋo, Wɛ na wʋ́ pa abam wʋpolo tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: The parable means this: The seed is Gods word.
local: Mɩmaŋɩ dɩm kuri mʋ tɩntʋ: Wo-duuru tɩm brɩ Wɛ taanɩ dɩm na yɩ te tɩn mʋ.

eng: Happy are you when people hate you, reject you, insult you, and condemn your name as evil because of the Human One.
local: Nɔɔna na culi abam, yɩ ba na vaɩ abam, naa ba na twɩ abam, naa ba na jɩgɩ abam ba goonǝ, á na tɔgɩ amʋ na yɩ Nabiin-bu wʋm tɩn ŋwaanɩ, á jɩgɩ yu-yoŋo mʋ.

eng: The seed on the path are those who hear, but then the devil comes and steals the word from their hearts so that they wont believe and be saved.
local: Cwǝŋǝ kam wo-duuru tɩm na tʋ da tɩn maa maŋɩ nɔɔna balʋ na ni Wɛ taanɩ dɩm yɩ sʋtaanɩ laan ba dɩ lɩ-dɩ yɩ dɩ wʋ zʋ ba wʋbʋŋa tɩn mʋ. Dɩ kɩ kʋntʋ sɩ ba yɩ kɩ ba wʋ-dɩdʋa mʋ dɩ Wɛ, sɩ Wɛ yɩ vrɩ-ba lwarɩm wʋnɩ.

eng: Rejoice when that happens! Leap for joy because you have a great reward in heaven. Their ancestors did the same things to the prophets.
local: Maŋa kalʋ ba na kɩ abam kʋntʋ tɩn, taá faŋɩ-na á caka dɩ wʋpolo, á ŋwɩɩrʋ na yɩ zanzan Wɛ tee nɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Taá ye-na sɩ ba kwǝ-nakwa dɩ dɛɛn kɩ faŋa faŋa Wɛ nijoŋnǝ bam kʋntʋ mʋ.

eng: The seed on the rock are those who receive the word joyfully when they hear it, but they have no root. They believe for a while but fall away when they are tempted.
local: Kandwa yam jǝgǝ kam maa maŋɩ balʋ na ni Wɛ taanɩ dɩm yɩ ba joŋi-dɩ dɩ wʋpolo tɩn mʋ. Taanɩ dɩm ma wʋ tiini dɩ zʋ ba wʋbʋŋa, yɩ kʋ na kɩ fɩnfɩɩn, ba daa ba sɛa. Cam maŋa na yiǝ, baá da kwe ba yagɩ mʋ.

eng: But how terrible for you who are rich, because you have already received your comfort.
local: Kʋ daarɩ abam balʋ na yɩ nadunǝ tɩn, kʋ yɩ lɛɛrʋ dɩ abam, á na maŋɩ á na wo-laarʋ tɩm yɩ á daa bá na-tɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: As for the seed that fell among thorny plants, these are the ones who, as they go about their lives, are choked by the concerns, riches, and pleasures of life, and their fruit never matures.
local: Sabarɩ sɩm jǝgǝ kam wo-duuru tɩm na tʋ da tɩn maa maŋɩ balʋ na ni Wɛ taanɩ dɩm yɩ ba zaŋɩ ba ta jɩgɩ liǝ dɩ ba ŋwɩa wǝǝnu, yɩ sǝbu-laga dɩ lʋgʋ baŋa ywǝǝni cɔgɩ ba wʋbʋŋa nɩ sabarɩ sɩm na ligi wo-duuru tɩm te tɩn. Bantʋ wʋbʋŋa daa bá bɩ dɩ Wɛ cwǝŋǝ kam tɔgɩm.

eng: How terrible for you who have plenty now, because you will be hungry. How terrible for you who laugh now, because you will mourn and weep.
