local: Abam balʋ na jɩgɩ wʋ-fwaarʋ á di lele tɩn, kʋ yɩ lɛɛrʋ dɩ abam, á na wʋ́ ba á yaarɩ dɩ kana tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Abam balʋ na jɩgɩ wʋpolo lele tɩn, kʋ yɩ lɛɛrʋ dɩ abam, á na wʋ́ ba á keeri yɩ á yi-na lu tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: The seed that fell on good soil are those who hear the word and commit themselves to it with a good and upright heart. Through their resolve, they bear fruit.
local: Tɩ-ywǝŋǝ jǝgǝ kam wo-duuru tɩm na tʋ da tɩn maa maŋɩ balʋ na ni Wɛ taanɩ dɩm yɩ ba joŋi-dɩ lanyɩranɩ ba kɩ ba bɩcarɩ nɩ tɩn mʋ. Bantʋ mʋ jɩgɩ wʋbʋŋ-ŋʋna, yɩ ba vɔ ba wʋ dɩ Wɛ cwǝŋǝ kam tɔgɩm. Wɛ taanɩ dɩm ma ja kuri dɩ pa-ba nɩ wo-duuru tɩlʋ na kɩ biǝ zanzan tɩn.».

eng: How terrible for you when all speak well of you. Their ancestors did the same things to the false prophets.
local: Nɔɔna maama na jɩgɩ abam ba zulǝ, kʋ yɩ lɛɛrʋ dɩ abam. Á taá ye-na sɩ ba kwǝ-nakwa dɩ dɛɛn zuli balʋ na paɩ ba yɩ Wɛ nijoŋnǝ yɩ ba yagɩ ba daɩ tɩn.».

eng: No one lights a lamp and then covers it with a bowl or puts it under a bed. Instead, they put it on top of a lampstand so that those who enter can see the light.
local: Zezi daa ma ta o wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ na jɩgɩ kanɩa, o bá sɛ sɩ o tarɩgɩ-ka o pu tɩtɔgɔ kuri nɩ, naa o zɩgɩ-ka gadogo kuri nɩ. Oó kwe-ka o palɩ wɛɛnɩ mʋ, sɩ ka taa jɩgɩ pooni ka paɩ balʋ na lagɩ ba zʋ digǝ kam wʋnɩ tɩn.

eng: But I say to you who are willing to hear: Love your enemies. Do good to those who hate you.
local: Zezi daa ma ta o wɩ: «Abam balʋ na cǝgi a taanɩ tɩn nan cǝgi-na lanyɩranɩ. Taá soe á dʋna. Taá kɩ balʋ na culi abam tɩn lanyɩranɩ.

eng: Nothing is hidden that wont be exposed. Nor is anything concealed that wont be made known and brought to the light.
local: Kʋlʋ maama zɩm na sǝgi nɔɔna tee nɩ tɩn wʋ́ ba kwɩn. Kʋlʋ maama na pugi tɩn wʋ́ ba kʋ puri mʋ, sɩ nɔɔna lwarɩ kʋ kuri na yɩ te.

eng: Bless those who curse you. Pray for those who mistreat you.
local: Kʋ na yɩ balʋ na sɔɔlɩ sɩ kʋ cɔgɩ abam tɩn, sɩ á loori-na Wɛ sɩ DƖ kɩ-ba lanyɩranɩ. Loori-na Wɛ á pa balʋ na jɩgɩ abam ba bɛɛsɩ tɩn.

eng: Therefore, listen carefully. Those who have will receive more, but as for those who dont have, even what they seem to have will be taken away from them.
local: Kwaanɩ-na á pa á na ni kʋlʋ tɩn zʋ á wʋbʋŋa. Bɛŋwaanɩ, nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na maŋɩ o jɩgɩ woŋo tɩn, o ta wʋ́ joŋi kʋdoŋ o wǝli da, yɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na maŋɩ o ba jɩgɩ tɩn wʋ́ ba ga kʋlʋ fɩnfɩɩn o na bʋŋɩ sɩ o jɩgɩ tɩn.».

eng: If someone slaps you on the cheek, offer the other one as well. If someone takes your coat, dont withhold your shirt either.
local: Nɔɔnʋ na lɔgɩ n pʋpɔŋɔ, sɩ n guni kʋdoŋ kʋm sɩ o lɔ o wǝli da. Nɔɔnʋ na vrɩ n gwar-bwǝrǝ, yɩ cʋ-o sɩ o vrɩ n gwar-bu dɩ.

eng: Jesus mother and brothers came to him but were unable to reach him because of the crowd.
local: Zezi nu dɩ o nyaana ma ba o te sɩ ba na-o. Ba ma warɩ ba yi o te, kɔgɔ kʋm na daga tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: Give to everyone who asks and dont demand your things back from those who take them.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ maama na loori woŋo nmʋ tee nɩ tɩn, sɩ n pa-o. Nɔɔnʋ na kwe nmʋ woŋo o ja viiri, yɩ bwe kʋ bwiǝ sɩ n joŋi.

eng: Someone told him, Your mother and brothers are standing outside, wanting to see you.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ ma ta dɩ Zezi o wɩ: «N nu dɩ n nyaana mʋ tu ba zɩgɩ cicwǝŋǝ nɩ, yɩ ba lagɩ sɩ ba na-m.».

eng: Treat people in the same way that you want them to treat you.
local: Nan taá kɩ-na á donnǝ lanyɩranɩ, sɩ kʋ maŋɩ dɩ abam na lagɩ sɩ bantʋ taa kɩ abam te tɩn.

eng: He replied, My mother and brothers are those who listen to Gods word and do it.
local: O ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Balʋ maama na ni Wɛ taanɩ dɩm yɩ ba sɛ-dɩ tɩn mʋ yɩ a nu dɩ a nyaana.».

eng: If you love those who love you, why should you be commended? Even sinners love those who love them.
local: Abam nan na soe balʋ dɩ na soe abam tɩn yɩranɩ, á wʋ́ na pɛɛrɩ na? Nɔɔna balʋ na ba tɔgɩ Wɛ tɩn dɩ soe balʋ na soe-ba tɩn mʋ.

eng: One day Jesus and his disciples boarded a boat. He said to them, Lets cross over to the other side of the lake. So they set sail.
local: Dɛ dɩdwɩ Zezi dɩ o karabiǝ bam ma zʋ naboro. O ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Pa-na dɩ́ bɛ nɩnɩʋ kʋm ni dɩdoŋ dɩm.» Ba ma sɩŋɩ ba yɛ ba maa kɛa.

eng: If you do good to those who do good to you, why should you be commended? Even sinners do that.
local: Abam na kɩ lanyɩranɩ dɩ balʋ na kɩ abam lanyɩranɩ tɩn yɩranɩ, á wʋ́ na pɛɛrɩ na? Nɔɔna balʋ na ba tɔgɩ Wɛ tɩn dɩ maŋɩ ba kɩ kʋntʋ doŋ mʋ.

eng: While they were sailing, he fell asleep. Gale-force winds swept down on the lake. The boat was filling up with water and they were in danger.
local: Ba na maa kɛ tɩn, Zezi ma tʋ dɔɔm nɩ. Vu-fɔrɔ ma da kʋ zaŋɩ nɩnɩʋ kʋm baŋa nɩ, kʋ magɩ boro kʋm kʋ pa kʋ wʋra kʋ miisǝ na bam kuri nɩ. Ba dɛɛn maa wʋ cam wʋnɩ ba lagɩ ba tɩ.

eng: If you lend to those from whom you expect repayment, why should you be commended? Even sinners lend to sinners expecting to be paid back in full.
local: Abam na jɩnɩ balʋ abam na bʋŋɩ sɩ baá wanɩ ba joori ba ŋwɩ abam tɩn yɩranɩ, á wʋ́ na pɛɛrɩ na? Nɔɔna balʋ na ba tɔgɩ Wɛ tɩn dɩ yǝni ba paɩ ba donnǝ jɩna, sɩ ba joori ba joŋi kʋ ni nɩ mʋ.

eng: So they went and woke Jesus, shouting, Master, Master, were going to drown! But he got up and gave orders to the wind and the violent waves. The storm died down and it was calm.
local: Ba ma vu ba zaŋɩ Zezi dɔɔm nɩ, yɩ ba wɩ: «Dɩ́ Yuutu, zaŋɩ sɩ dɩ́ lagɩ dɩ́ li na dɩ́ tɩ.» O laan ma zaŋɩ wɛɛnɩ. O ma bagɩ viu kʋm dɩ na bam sɩ tɩ yagɩ. Viu kʋm laan ma yagɩ, yɩ na bam dɩ maŋɩ da cɩmm.

eng: Instead, love your enemies, do good, and lend expecting nothing in return. If you do, you will have a great reward. You will be acting the way children of the Most High act, for he is kind to ungrateful and wicked people.
local: Abam nan maŋɩ sɩ á taá soe á dʋna sɩ á taá kɩ-ba lanyɩranɩ. Pa-na jɩna, nan yɩ taá tɩɩna sɩ á joori á joŋi. Á na kɩ kʋntʋ doŋ, á wʋ́ na pɛɛrɩ zanzan Wɛ tee nɩ, yɩ kʋʋ́ brɩ sɩ á yɩ Yuutu Baŋa-Wɛ biǝ mʋ, bɛŋwaanɩ Wɛ yǝni DƖ zǝni banɩ-nyɩna dɩ nɔn-balwaarʋ dɩ.

eng: He said to his disciples, Where is your faith? Filled with awe and wonder, they said to each other, Who is this? He commands even the winds and the water, and they obey him.
local: O ma bwe o karabiǝ bam o wɩ: «Bɛɛ mʋ yɩ á ba jɩgɩ wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ amʋ?» Fʋʋnɩ ma ja-ba, yɩ kʋ kɩ-ba yǝǝu. Ba ma bwe daanɩ ba wɩ: «Wʋntʋ yɩ wɔɔ mʋ, yɩ o ta wanɩ o bagɩ viu dɩdaanɩ na, yɩ tɩ sɛ o ni?».

eng: Be compassionate just as your Father is compassionate.
local: Á taá jɩgɩ nɔɔna ŋwaŋa, nɩ á Ko Wɛ na jɩgɩ nɔɔna ŋwaŋa te tɩn.».

eng: Jesus and his disciples sailed to the Gerasenes land, which is across the lake from Galilee.
local: Ba ma yɛ ba vu ba bɛ nɩnɩʋ kʋm. Ba ma yi Zeraaza tiinǝ na zʋʋrɩ mɛ tɩn. Nɩnɩʋ kʋm tigi je sɩm kʋntʋ dɩ Galile laŋa nɩ mʋ.

eng: Dont judge, and you wont be judged. Dont condemn, and you wont be condemned. Forgive, and you will be forgiven.
local: «Abam yɩ taá ŋɔɔna á donnǝ wǝǝnu, sɩ Wɛ yɩ ŋɔɔnɩ abam dɩ wǝǝnu. Yɩ taá lwɛ-na nɔɔna, sɩ Wɛ yɩ zaŋɩ DƖ ba DƖ lwɛ abam dɩ. Nan yagɩ-na á donnǝ lwarɩm á ma cɛ-ba, sɩ Wɛ wʋ́ yagɩ abam lwarɩm DƖ ma cɛ abam dɩ.

eng: As soon as Jesus got out of the boat, a certain man met him. The man was from the city and was possessed by demons. For a long time, he had lived among the tombs, naked and homeless.
local: Zezi ma nuŋi naboro kʋm wʋnɩ. Tɩʋ kʋm nɔɔnʋ dɩdʋa ma ba o jeer-o. Ciciri mʋ jɩg-o sɩ yaara. Kʋ tiini kʋ daanɩ o na wʋ zʋ kʋlʋkʋlʋ yɩ o ba zʋʋrɩ sɔŋɔ nɩ. O yǝni o tigi yibeelǝ je nɩ mʋ.

eng: Give, and it will be given to you. A good portion—packed down, firmly shaken, and overflowing—will fall into your lap. The portion you give will determine the portion you receive in return.
local: Pa-na á donnǝ woŋo kʋlʋ na maŋɩ sɩ á pa-ba tɩn, sɩ Wɛ dɩ wʋ́ pa abam woŋo kʋlʋ na maŋɩ sɩ DƖ pa abam tɩn. Wɛ wʋ́ pa abam lanyɩranɩ nɩnɛɛnɩ ba na kwe bwaŋ-kamunu ba maŋɩ ba vuuri ka ni yɩ kʋ siiri te tɩn. Bwaŋa kalʋ abam na ma á maŋɩ á pa nɔɔna tɩn, Wɛ dɩ wʋ́ ma kantʋ mʋ DƖ maŋɩ DƖ pa abam dɩ.».

eng: When he saw Jesus, he shrieked and fell down before him. Then he shouted, What have you to do with me, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? I beg you, dont torture me.
local: O na nɛ Zezi tɩn, o ma sɩŋɩ o kaasɩ wɛwɛ. O ma vu o tʋ Zezi yigǝ nɩ, yɩ o kaasɩ dɩ kwǝr-dɩa o wɩ: «Zezi, Yuutu Baŋa-Wɛ Bu, nmʋ tu amʋ tee nɩ sɩ n kɩ bɛɛ mʋ? Popo, yɩ zaŋɩ n yaarɩ-nɩ.».

eng: Jesus also told them a riddle. A blind person cant lead another blind person, right? Wont they both fall into a ditch?.
local: Zezi laan ma maŋɩ mɩmaŋa o brɩ-ba o wɩ: «Lilwe wʋ́ wanɩ o vaŋɩ o doŋ lilwe na? Ba na vaŋɩ daanɩ, ba maama bá tʋ goŋo wʋnɩ na?.

eng: He said this because Jesus had already commanded the unclean spirit to come out of the man. Many times it had taken possession of him, so he would be bound with leg irons and chains and placed under guard. But he would break his restraints, and the demon would force him into the wilderness.
local: O tagɩ kʋntʋ, bɛŋwaanɩ Zezi maŋɩ o pa cicirǝ kam ni sɩ ka nuŋi ka yagɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋm. Kʋ daanɩ cicirǝ kam na maŋɩ ka jɩg-o. Kʋntʋ mʋ paɩ ba yǝni ba kwe capʋnnʋ ba ma vɔ o jɩa dɩ o nɛ, sɩ ba taa yɩr-o. Ba na yǝni ba vɔ-o kʋntʋ, o yǝni o kɔ-tɩ mʋ. Cicirǝ kam laan ma zǝl-o ka pa o duri o vu kagʋa wʋ.

eng: Disciples arent greater than their teacher, but whoever is fully prepared will be like their teacher.
local: Karabu bá wanɩ o dwǝni o karanyɩna. Karabu nan na zaasɩ o ti maama, o dɩ o karanyɩna kam maɩ daanɩ mʋ.

eng: Jesus asked him, What is your name? Legion, he replied, because many demons had entered him.
local: Zezi laan ma bwe-o o wɩ: «N yɩrɩ mʋ bɛɛ?» O ma lǝri o wɩ: «A yɩrɩ mʋ Kɔgɔ», bɛŋwaanɩ ciciri zanzan mʋ tɔg-o.

eng: Why do you see the splinter in your brothers or sisters eye but dont notice the log in your own eye?.
local: Abam nyɩ dɩ nɔɔnʋ na nii o doŋ yi nɩ o na gaa balaŋa da, yɩ o ba lagɩ sɩ o lwarɩ nɩ o tɩtɩ yi jɩgɩ daa tɩn. Kʋ maŋɩ sɩ kʋ taa yɩ kʋntʋ doŋ na?.

eng: They pleaded with him not to order them to go back into the abyss.
local: Ciciri sɩm ma loori Zezi, sɩ o yɩ zǝli-sɩ o pa sɩ zʋ curu goŋ-luŋu kʋm wʋ.

eng: How can you say to your brother or sister, Brother, Sister, let me take the splinter out of your eye, when you dont see the log in your own eye? You deceive yourselves! First take the log out of your eye, and then you will see clearly to take the splinter out of your brothers or sisters eye.
local: Nmʋ na wʋ nɛ daa na wʋ nmʋ yi nɩ tɩn, nmʋ wʋ́ kɩ ta n ta n doŋ nɩ o ba sɩ n lɩ gaa o yi nɩ? Nmʋ yɩ pipiri-nyɩm dɩ nmʋ na kɩ n doŋ te tɩn. Nmʋ nan maŋɩ sɩ n lɩ n tɩtɩ yi daa kam mʋ, sɩ n laan wanɩ n nii lanyɩranɩ sɩ n lɩ n doŋ yi gaa kam.».

eng: A large herd of pigs was feeding on the hillside. The demons begged Jesus to let them go into the pigs. Jesus gave them permission.
local: Tǝrǝ kɔgɔ zanzan dɛɛn ma wʋ je sɩm nɩ ba beeri piu yuu nɩ ba di. Ciciri sɩm ma loori Zezi, sɩ o pa-sɩ cwǝŋǝ sɩ sɩ vu sɩ zʋ tǝrǝ bam wʋ. O ma sɛ.

eng: A good tree doesnt produce bad fruit, nor does a bad tree produce good fruit.
local: «Tu-ŋʋŋʋ bá wanɩ kʋ lǝ bu-balwaarʋ. Tu-balɔrɔ dɩ nan bá wanɩ kʋ lǝ bu-ŋʋna.

eng: and the demons left the man and entered the pigs. The herd rushed down the cliff into the lake and drowned.
local: Ciciri sɩm laan ma yagɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋm yɩra, yɩ sɩ vu sɩ zʋ tǝrǝ bam. Tǝrǝ bam maama ma duri ba tu ba tʋ nɩnɩʋ kʋm wʋnɩ ba li na ba tɩ.

eng: Each tree is known by its own fruit. People dont gather figs from thorny plants, nor do they pick grapes from prickly bushes.
local: Abam na lagɩ sɩ á lwarɩ tiu maama na yɩ kʋlʋ tɩn, á wʋ́ ma kʋ biǝ mʋ á lwarɩ. Nmʋ bá na mɔɔla sabara yuu nɩ, asawɛ n na swan kaligoŋo kuri nɩ n twɛ.

eng: When those who tended the pigs saw what happened, they ran away and told the story in the city and in the countryside.
local: Nɔɔna balʋ ya na nii tǝrǝ bam baŋa nɩ tɩn na nɛ kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma duri ba vu ba ta kʋlʋ na kɩ tɩn tɩʋ wʋnɩ dɩdaanɩ dɛ sɩm maama ba brɩ nɔɔna bam.

eng: A good person produces good from the good treasury of the inner self, while an evil person produces evil from the evil treasury of the inner self. The inner self overflows with words that are spoken.
local: Nɔn-ŋʋm ŋɔɔnɩ wo-ŋʋnnʋ tɩlʋ na wʋ o wʋbʋŋa nɩ tɩn mʋ. Nɔn-balɔrɔ dɩ ma ŋɔɔnɩ wo-balwaarʋ tɩlʋ na wʋ o wʋbʋŋa nɩ tɩn. Woŋo kʋlʋ maama na wʋ nɔɔnʋ wʋbʋŋa nɩ tɩn mʋ tɔgɩ o ni kʋ nuŋǝ.».

eng: People came to see what had happened. They came to Jesus and found the man from whom the demons had gone. He was sitting at Jesus feet, fully dressed and completely sane. They were filled with awe.
local: Nɔɔna bam ma nuŋi ba vu sɩ ba nii kʋ na kɩ te tɩn. Ba ma vu ba yi Zezi te. Ba ma na nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ cicirǝ kam na yag-o tɩn. O maa je Zezi tee nɩ dɩdaanɩ o swan joori ya ba, yɩ o zʋ gwaarʋ. Ba na nɛ-o kʋntʋ tɩn, fʋʋnɩ ma zʋ-ba.

eng: Why do you call me Lord, Lord and dont do what I say?.
local: «Bɛŋwaanɩ mʋ yɩ á bǝ amʋ sɩ ‹Yuutu, Yuutu›, yɩ á ba kɩ a na wɩ á kɩ te tɩn?.

eng: Those people who had actually seen what had happened told them how the demon-possessed man had been delivered.
local: Balʋ yiǝ na nɛ kʋ maama tɩn ma ta ba brɩ nɔɔna bam kʋ na kɩ te yɩ ciciri sɩm nuŋi sɩ yagɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋm tɩn.

eng: Ill show what its like when someone comes to me, hears my words, and puts them into practice.
local: Wʋlʋ maama na tu amʋ te o cǝgi a taanɩ dɩm yɩ o kɩ a na wɩ te tɩn, amʋ wʋ́ brɩ abam kʋntʋ tu na nyɩ te tɩn.

eng: Then everyone gathered from the region of the Gerasenes asked Jesus to leave their area because they were overcome with fear. So he got into the boat and returned across the lake.
local: Zeraaza tɩʋ kʋm dɩ kʋ nawurǝ yam maama nɔɔna bam ma loori Zezi sɩ o viiri, bɛŋwaanɩ fʋʋnɩ tiini dɩ jɩgɩ-ba. Zezi laan ma joori o vu o zʋ naboro kʋm sɩ o viiri.

eng: Its like a person building a house by digging deep and laying the foundation on bedrock. When the flood came, the rising water smashed against that house, but the water couldnt shake the house because it was well built.
local: Wʋntʋ nyɩ dɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na lagɩ o lɔ o sɔŋɔ yɩ o kʋ goŋo o yi pulɔrɔ yɩ o laan cwi sɔŋɔ kʋm kuri pulɔrɔ kʋm baŋa nɩ tɩn mʋ. Dʋ-na na daga yɩ ba magɩ sɔŋɔ kʋm, ba bá wanɩ ba coori-kʋ, o na lɔgɩ-kʋ lanyɩranɩ yɩ kʋ dana tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: The man from whom the demons had gone begged to come along with Jesus as one of his disciples. Jesus sent him away, saying.
local: O na maa viiri tɩn, nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ ciciri sɩm na yag-o tɩn ma loor-o sɩ o pa o da o vu. Zezi ma wʋ sɛ.

eng: But those who dont put into practice what they hear are like a person who built a house without a foundation. The floodwater smashed against it and it collapsed instantly. It was completely destroyed.
local: Kʋ daarɩ wʋlʋ maama nan na cǝgi amʋ taanɩ dɩm yɩ o ba kɩ a na wɩ te tɩn, kʋntʋ tu nyɩ dɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na lɔgɩ o sɔŋɔ kasʋlʋ baŋa nɩ yɩ o wʋ kʋgɩ goŋo sɩ o ma cwi-kʋ tɩn mʋ. Dʋ-na na daga, baá magɩ sɔŋɔ kʋm ba guri yɩ ba coori kʋ maama.».

eng: Return home and tell the story of what God has done for you. So he went throughout the city proclaiming what Jesus had done for him.
local: O ma ta dɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋm o wɩ: «Joori n vu n sɔŋɔ tiinǝ te, sɩ n ta kʋlʋ Wɛ na kɩ DƖ pa nmʋ tɩn n brɩ-ba.» Nɔɔnʋ wʋm ma vu o tʋlɩ tɩʋ kʋm maama wʋnɩ o tɔɔlɩ kʋlʋ maama Zezi na kɩ o pa-o tɩn.

eng: When Jesus returned, the crowd welcomed him, for they had been waiting for him.
local: Zezi dɛɛn ma joori o bɛ nɩnɩʋ kʋm ni dɩdoŋ dɩm dɩdaanɩ kɔgɔ zanzan mʋ maŋɩ kʋ gilimi daanɩ kʋ cǝg-o. Ba na nɛ-o tɩn, ba ma jeer-o lanyɩranɩ.

eng: A man named Jairus, who was a synagogue leader, came and fell at Jesus feet. He pleaded with Jesus to come to his house.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ maa tɔgɩ o wʋra, o yɩrɩ mʋ Zayirusi. O yɩ Zwifǝ Wɛ-digǝ kam yigǝ tu. O ma vu Zezi te, o kuni doonǝ o yigǝ nɩ o loor-o o wɩ: «Popo, ba dɩbam sɔŋɔ n joŋi a bu n yagɩ, sɩ o wɔɛ o lagɩ o tɩ.» Mʋ o bu dɩdʋa yɩranɩ, o yɩ bɩsankana mʋ, o yi nɩ bɩna fugǝ-yale te. Zezi ma sɛ sɩ o vu. O na maa kɛ tɩn, kɔgɔ kʋm tiini kʋ pɩɩn-o.

eng: A woman was there who had been bleeding for twelve years. She had spent her entire livelihood on doctors, but no one could heal her.
local: Kaanɩ wʋdoŋ dɩ maa wʋra o na wɔɛ ka-wʋ taan bɩna fugǝ-yale. O ya kwe o jɩjɩgɩrʋ maama o pa liri tiinǝ sɩ ba soon-o, yɩ nɔɔnʋ dɩ dɩdʋa wʋ wanɩ o soon-o.

eng: She came up behind him and touched the hem of his clothes, and at once her bleeding stopped.
local: O ma kɛ Zezi kwaga o vu o laarɩ o dwe o gɔrɔ kʋm ni. O na dwe-o kʋntʋ tɩn, jana bam ma kɔ bɩdwɩ baŋa nɩ.

eng: Who touched me? Jesus asked. When everyone denied it, Peter said, Master, the crowds are surrounding you and pressing in on you.
local: Zezi ma bwe o wɩ: «Wɔɔ mʋ dwe amʋ?» Ba maama ma fʋ ba wɩ, ba wʋ dwe-o. Pɩyɛɛrɩ dɩ balʋ na wʋ o tee nɩ tɩn ma ta ba wɩ: «Dɩ́ Yuutu, nmʋ wʋ nɛ nɔɔna bam na tiini ba daga yɩ ba pɩɩnɩ nmʋ te tɩn na?».

eng: But Jesus said, Someone touched me. I know that power has gone out from me.
local: Zezi ma lǝri o wɩ: «Kʋ yɩ nɔɔnʋ mʋ dwe-nɩ. Amʋ ye sɩ dam nuŋi a yɩra nɩ.».

eng: When the woman saw that she couldnt escape notice, she came trembling and fell before Jesus. In front of everyone, she explained why she had touched him and how she had been immediately healed.
local: Kaanɩ wʋm ma lwarɩ sɩ o bá wanɩ o sǝgi dɩ kʋlʋ na kɩ tɩn. Fʋʋnɩ maa jɩg-o yɩ o yɩra saɩ. O ma vu o tʋ Zezi yigǝ nɩ. O ma ta dɩd-o kʋlʋ maama na pɛ o dwe-o kʋntʋ tɩn, dɩ o na nɛ yazurǝ bɩdwɩ baŋa nɩ te tɩn. Kɔgɔ kʋm maama ma ni o na tagɩ kʋlʋ tɩn.

eng: Daughter, your faith has healed you, Jesus said. Go in peace.
local: Zezi laan ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «A bukɔ, nmʋ na kɩ n wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ amʋ te tɩn mʋ pɛ n na yazurǝ. Ve sɩ Wɛ wʋ́ wǝli-m.».

eng: While Jesus was still speaking, someone came from the synagogue leaders house, saying to Jairus, Your daughter has died. Dont bother the teacher any longer.
local: Zezi ta na wʋra o ŋɔɔnɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ ma nuŋi Zayirusi sɔŋɔ o ba o ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «N bukɔ wʋm tɩga. Daa yɩ ta n yaarɩ karanyɩna kam.».

eng: When Jesus heard this, he responded, Dont be afraid; just keep trusting, and she will be healed.
local: Zezi na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma ta dɩ Zayirusi o wɩ: «Yɩ ta n fʋna. Za n weeni n kɩ n wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ amʋ, sɩ oó na yazurǝ.».

eng: When he came to the house, he didnt allow anyone to enter with him except Peter, John, and James, and the childs father and mother.
local: Ba ma vu ba yi sɔŋɔ kʋm. Zezi ma zʋ. O ma wʋ sɛ sɩ nɔɔna da-o ba zʋ, kʋ na daɩ Pɩyɛɛrɩ dɩ Zan dɩ Zakɩ, dɩ bu wʋm nu dɩ o ko yɩranɩ.

eng: They were all crying and mourning for her, but Jesus said, Dont cry. She isnt dead. Shes only sleeping.
local: Balʋ maama na maŋɩ ba wʋ sɔŋɔ kʋm wʋnɩ tɩn maa keerǝ ba coosǝ bɩsankana kam ŋwaanɩ. Zezi ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Á yɩ taá keerǝ, sɩ o wʋ tɩgɩ, o dɔ mʋ.».

eng: They laughed at him because they knew she was dead.
local: O na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, ba maama ma mwan-o, bɛŋwaanɩ ba ye nɩ bu wʋm sɩɩnɩ o tɩ mʋ.

eng: Taking her hand, Jesus called out, Child, get up.
local: Zezi laan ma vu o ja bɩsankana kam jɩŋa, yɩ o ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «A bu, zaŋɩ wɛɛnɩ.».

eng: Her life returned and she got up at once. He directed them to give her something to eat.
local: O na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, o ŋwɩa ma joori. O ma zaŋɩ wɛɛnɩ bɩdwɩ baŋa nɩ. Zezi ma ta dɩ ba sɩ ba kɩ wʋdiu ba pa bu wʋm sɩ o di.

eng: Her parents were beside themselves with joy, but he ordered them to tell no one what had happened.
local: Kʋ ma sʋ bu wʋm nu dɩ o ko lanyɩranɩ. Zezi ma kaanɩ-ba o wɩ, ba yɩ zaŋɩ ba ta ba brɩ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ woŋo kʋlʋ na kɩ tɩn.

eng: After these things, the Lord commissioned seventy-two others and sent them on ahead in pairs to every city and place he was about to go.
local: Kʋntʋ kwaga nɩ dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi daa ta ma lɩ nɔɔna fusɩrpɛ , sɩ o tʋŋɩ-ba bale bale, sɩ ba da yigǝ ba vu mɛ maama o na lagɩ o vu tɩn.

eng: He said to them, The harvest is bigger than you can imagine, but there are few workers. Therefore, plead with the Lord of the harvest to send out workers for his harvest.
local: O na lagɩ o tʋŋɩ-ba kʋntʋ tɩn, mʋ o tagɩ dɩ ba o wɩ: «Kʋ nyɩ dɩ faa na kɩ lanyɩranɩ, yɩ tɩntʋŋna fɩnfɩɩn yɩranɩ mʋ wʋra sɩ ba kɩ-kʋ te tɩn. Nan loori-na faa kʋm tu, sɩ o tʋŋɩ nɔɔna zanzan, sɩ ba vu ba kɩ o faa kʋm ba pa-o.».

eng: Go! Be warned, though, that Im sending you out as lambs among wolves.
local: O ma daarɩ o wɩ: «Ve-na! A na tʋŋɩ abam je sɩm kʋntʋ nɩ tɩn, abam nyɩ dɩ pǝlbiǝ na maa ve sɩ ba gwaanɩ nywǝnkuri wʋnɩ te tɩn mʋ.

eng: Carry no wallet, no bag, and no sandals. Dont even greet anyone along the way.
local: Á na maa ve, yɩ kwe-na sǝbiǝ gan-lɔgɔdɩ, nan yɩ kwe-na tampɔgɔ, naa á taá jɩgɩ natra á wǝli da. Yɩ zɩgɩ-na cwǝŋǝ nɩ á daanɩ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ pwǝgǝ.

eng: Whenever you enter a house, first say, May peace be on this house.
local: Á nan na yi sɔŋɔ kʋlʋ maama á na lagɩ á zʋ tɩn, sɩ á ta dɩ sɔŋɔ kʋm tiinǝ nɩ: ‹Wɛ wʋ́ pa abam yazurǝ.›.

eng: If anyone there shares Gods peace, then your peace will rest on that person. If not, your blessing will return to you.
local: Nɔɔnʋ na wʋra yɩ o soe wʋ-zuru, á yazurǝ yam wʋ́ ta wʋra dɩd-o. Wʋ-zuru-nyɩm nan na tǝrǝ, á yazurǝ yam wʋ́ joori ya ba á tɩtɩ te mʋ.

eng: Remain in this house, eating and drinking whatever they set before you, for workers deserve their pay. Dont move from house to house.
local: Taá zʋʋrɩ-na sɔŋɔ kʋm kʋntʋ wʋnɩ. Wʋdiu kʋlʋ maama ba na kɩ ba pa abam tɩn, sɩ á taá di kʋntʋ. Tɩntʋŋnʋ maŋɩ sɩ o taa joŋi o ŋwɩɩrʋ mʋ. Á yɩ zaŋɩ á tʋlɩ sam dɩdonnǝ. Á taá zʋʋrɩ dáanɩ, sɩ kʋ taa ve maŋa kalʋ á na wʋ́ ba á viiri tɩn.

eng: Whenever you enter a city and its people welcome you, eat what they set before you.
local: Á na zʋ tɩʋ kʋlʋ maama, yɩ ba na jeeri abam lanyɩranɩ, sɩ á taá di wʋdiu kʋlʋ maama ba na kɩ ba pa abam tɩn.

eng: Heal the sick who are there, and say to them, Gods kingdom has come upon you.
local: sɩ á pa yawɩɩna na yazurǝ, á daarɩ á ta dɩ ba nɩ: ‹Maŋa twɛ sɩ Baŋa-Wɛ di paarɩ á tɩtarɩ nɩ.›.

eng: Whenever you enter a city and the people dont welcome you, go out into the streets and say.
local: Kʋ daarɩ, á na zʋ tɩʋ kʋlʋ maama yɩ ba na wʋ sɛ sɩ ba jeeri abam, sɩ á nuŋi tɩʋ kʋm cwe wʋnɩ á ta dɩ ba nɩ:.

eng: As a complaint against you, we brush off the dust of your city that has collected on our feet. But know this: Gods kingdom has come to you.
local: ‹Abam tɩʋ kʋm fogo na taagɩ dɩbam nɛ sɩm tɩn, nii-na dɩ́ pɩɩsɩ-kʋ dɩ́ joori dɩ́ yagɩ da, sɩ kʋ brɩ nɩ abam wʋ kɩ lanyɩranɩ. Nan taá ye-na sɩ maŋa twɛ sɩ Baŋa-Wɛ di paarɩ nɔɔna tɩtarɩ nɩ.›.

eng: I assure you that Sodom will be better off on Judgment Day than that city.
local: A nan lagɩ a ta abam sɩ, Wɛ sarɩya dɛ dɩm na yiǝ, Sodɔm tiinǝ bá tiini ba na lɛɛrʋ nɩ tɩʋ kʋntʋ tiinǝ na wʋ́ na lɛɛrʋ te tɩn.

eng: How terrible it will be for you, Chorazin. How terrible it will be for you, Bethsaida. If the miracles done among you had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have changed their hearts and lives long ago. They would have sat around in funeral clothes and ashes.
local: Abam Korazin tiinǝ, á wʋ́ na lɛɛrʋ! Kʋ wǝli dɩ abam Betisayida tiinǝ dɩ wʋ́ na lɛɛrʋ! Bɛŋwaanɩ, wo-kɩnkagɩla yalʋ maama a na kɩ abam tee nɩ tɩn, yadonnǝ ya na kɩ Tiiri dɩ Sidɔn tiinǝ tee nɩ, bantʋ ya wʋ́ kɩ lɩla ba lǝni ba wʋrʋ ba yagɩ kǝm-balwaarʋ, yɩ ba ya wʋ́ zʋ gwar-zɩnzwara ba lo puru ba yuu nɩ, sɩ kʋ brɩ nɩ ba wʋrʋ tiini tɩ cɔgɩ zanzan.

eng: But Tyre and Sidon will be better off at the judgment than you.
local: Wɛ sarɩya dɛ dɩm na yiǝ, Tiiri dɩ Sidɔn tiinǝ bá tiini ba na lɛɛrʋ nɩ abam na wʋ́ na lɛɛrʋ te tɩn.

eng: And you, Capernaum, will you be honored by being raised up to heaven? No, you will be cast down to the place of the dead.
local: Abam Kapɛrnawum tiinǝ, á ga bʋŋɩ nɩ Wɛ lagɩ DƖ zǝŋi abam wɛyuu nɩ mʋ na? Aye. Abam nan lagɩ á tʋ tɩga nɩ mʋ á tu á kwaarɩ curu.».

eng: Whoever listens to you listens to me. Whoever rejects you rejects me. Whoever rejects me rejects the one who sent me.
local: Zezi daa ma ta dɩ o karabiǝ bam o wɩ: «Wʋlʋ maama na joŋi abam taanɩ tɩn, kʋntʋ tu joŋi amʋ taanɩ mʋ. Wʋlʋ maama nan na wʋ joŋi abam taanɩ tɩn, kʋ tu wʋ joŋi amʋ taanɩ mʋ. Wʋlʋ maama na wʋ joŋi amʋ taanɩ tɩn wʋ joŋi Baŋa-Wɛ dɩlʋ na tʋŋɩ-nɩ tɩn taanɩ mʋ.».

eng: The seventy-two returned joyously, saying, Lord, even the demons submit themselves to us in your name.
local: Kʋ na kɩ fɩɩn tɩn nɔɔna fusɩrpɛ bam ma joori ba ba Zezi te dɩ wʋpolo. Ba ma ta dɩd-o ba wɩ: «Dɩ́ Yuutu, ciciri tɩtɩ dɩ dɛ nmʋ yɩrɩ kɔrɔ ŋwaanɩ sɩ sɛ dɩbam ni.».

eng: Jesus replied, I saw Satan fall from heaven like lightning.
local: O ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «A nɛ sʋtaanɩ na zɩgɩ wɛyuu nɩ dɩ tʋ tɩga nɩ, nɩ dʋa na pɩpɩla ka baga ka tu ka kwaarɩ tɩga te tɩn.

eng: Look, I have given you authority to crush snakes and scorpions underfoot. I have given you authority over all the power of the enemy. Nothing will harm you.
local: Nii-na, a pɛ abam dam sɩ á nɔ bɩsankwɩ dɩ nwaanʋ baŋa nɩ, sɩ á wanɩ sʋtaanɩ dɩ dɩ dam maama, yɩ tɩ tɩlʋtɩlʋ bá wanɩ abam woŋo tɩ kɩ.

eng: Nevertheless, dont rejoice because the spirits submit to you. Rejoice instead that your names are written in heaven.
local: Á nan yɩ kɩ wʋpolo dɩ ciciri na sɛ abam ni tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Nan za á kɩ-na wʋpolo dɩ á yɩra na pʋpʋnɩ Wɛ-sɔŋɔ ŋwɩa tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ.».

eng: At that very moment, Jesus overflowed with joy from the Holy Spirit and said, I praise you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because youve hidden these things from the wise and intelligent and shown them to babies. Indeed, Father, this brings you happiness.
local: Maŋa kam kʋntʋ nɩ, Wɛ Joro kʋm dɛɛn pɛ Zezi na wʋpolo lanyɩranɩ. O laan ma kɩ o Ko Baŋa-Wɛ le o wɩ: «A Ko, nmʋ yɩ wʋlʋ na te wɛyuu dɩ tɩga baŋa maama tɩn. Nmʋ nan mʋ pɛ n cɩga kam sǝgi swan tiinǝ dɩ yiyiu tiinǝ tee nɩ, yɩ n daarɩ n pa balʋ na nyɩ dɩ bu-balɛ te tɩn lwarɩ ka kuri. A Ko, a kɩ nmʋ le. Nmʋ na kɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ yɩ n wʋbʋŋa na lagɩ kʋlʋ tɩn ŋwaanɩ mʋ.».

eng: My Father has handed all things over to me. No one knows who the Son is except the Father, or who the Father is except the Son and anyone to whom the Son wants to reveal him.
local: «A Ko nan pɛ-nɩ dam wǝǝnu maama baŋa nɩ. Nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ bá wanɩ o lwarɩ Wɛ Bu wʋm na yɩ wʋlʋ, kʋ na daɩ o Ko yɩranɩ. Nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ dɩ nan bá wanɩ o lwarɩ a Ko Wɛ na yɩ wʋlʋ, kʋ na daɩ amʋ wʋlʋ na yɩ DƖ Bu wʋm tɩn yɩranɩ, kʋ wǝli dɩ amʋ na lagɩ sɩ a pa nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ maama lwarɩ DƖ na yɩ wʋlʋ tɩn.».

eng: Turning to the disciples, he said privately, Happy are the eyes that see what you see.
local: O laan ma pipiri o nii o karabiǝ bam seeni, yɩ o ŋɔɔnɩ dɩ bantʋ ba yɩranɩ o wɩ: «Á jɩgɩ yu-yoŋo dɩ á yiǝ na naɩ wo-kamunnu tɩlʋ tɩn.

eng: I assure you that many prophets and kings wanted to see what you see and hear what you hear, but they didnt.
local: Lwarɩ-na sɩ, faŋa faŋa Wɛ nijoŋnǝ zanzan dɩ pwa zanzan dɛɛn jɩgɩ tɩɩna sɩ ba ya na kʋlʋ maama abam na naɩ lele tɩn, yɩ ba wʋ wanɩ ba na. Ba dɛɛn jɩgɩ tɩɩna sɩ ba ya ni Wɛ taanɩ dɩlʋ abam na ni tɩn, yɩ ba wʋ wanɩ ba ni-dɩ.».

eng: A legal expert stood up to test Jesus. Teacher, he said, what must I do to gain eternal life.
local: Wɛ cullu karanyɩna dɩdʋa dɛɛn tu Zezi te, sɩ o maŋ-o o nii o swan na yɩ te tɩn. O ma zaŋɩ o bwe-o o wɩ: «Karanyɩna, amʋ nan wʋ́ kɩ ta mʋ sɩ a joŋi ŋwɩa kalʋ na ba ti tɩn Wɛ tee nɩ?».

eng: Jesus replied, What is written in the Law? How do you interpret it.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Bɛɛ mʋ pʋpʋnɩ Wɛ cullu tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ? Bɛɛ mʋ n yǝni n karɩmɩ da?».

eng: He responded, You must love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your being, with all your strength, and with all your mind, and love your neighbor as yourself .
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋm ma lǝri o wɩ: «Kʋ pʋpʋnɩ kʋ wɩ, ta n soe n Yuutu Baŋa-Wɛ dɩ n bɩcarɩ maama, dɩ n wʋ maama, dɩ n dam maama, dɩ n wʋbʋŋa maama, sɩ n kwǝri n ta n soe n doŋ nɩ n tɩtɩ te.».

eng: Jesus said to him, You have answered correctly. Do this and you will live.
local: Zezi ma lǝri o wɩ: «N tagɩ cɩga. Nan ta n kɩ kʋntʋ, sɩ nń na ŋwɩa Wɛ tee nɩ.».

eng: But the legal expert wanted to prove that he was right, so he said to Jesus, And who is my neighbor.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋm ma lagɩ sɩ o pa o tɩtɩ bʋra. O ma daa bwe Zezi o wɩ: «Amʋ doŋ mʋ wɔɔ?».

eng: Jesus replied, A man went down from Jerusalem to Jericho. He encountered thieves, who stripped him naked, beat him up, and left him near death.
local: Zezi dɛɛn ma maŋɩ mɩmaŋa o ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ mʋ zɩgɩ Zeruzalɛm nɩ o nuŋi o maa ve tɩʋ kʋdoŋ, kʋ yɩrɩ mʋ Zeriko. O ma vu o maŋɩ dɩ nɔn-vɩrna. Nɔn-vɩrna bam ma zi-o ba vrɩ o gwaarʋ dɩ o wǝǝnu maama. Ba ma mag-o ba bǝri, yɩ ba daarɩ ba duri ba viiri.

eng: Now it just so happened that a priest was also going down the same road. When he saw the injured man, he crossed over to the other side of the road and went on his way.
local: O na tigi da kʋntʋ tɩn, Wɛ kaanɩm tu dɩdʋa dɩ ya wʋ cwǝŋǝ kam kʋntʋ nɩ o maa ve. O na maa ve tɩn, o ma na nɔɔnʋ wʋm na tigi o yigǝ nɩ. O ma ywǝri o tɔgɩ daa o kɛ.

eng: Likewise, a Levite came by that spot, saw the injured man, and crossed over to the other side of the road and went on his way.
local: Wɛ-digǝ tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋlʋ na yɩ Leviti tu tɩn dɩ maa tɔgɩ da o maa kɛa. O na yi jǝgǝ kam tɩn, o ma na nɔɔnʋ wʋm na tigǝ. O dɩ daa ma ywǝri o tɔgɩ daa o kɛ.

eng: A Samaritan, who was on a journey, came to where the man was. But when he saw him, he was moved with compassion.
local: Samari tu wʋdoŋ dɩ dɛɛn maa wʋ bǝrǝ nɩ. O ma vu o yi nɔɔnʋ wʋm na tigi mɛ tɩn. O na nɛ-o tɩn, o ŋwaŋa ma zʋ-o.

eng: The Samaritan went to him and bandaged his wounds, tending them with oil and wine. Then he placed the wounded man on his own donkey, took him to an inn, and took care of him.
local: O ma vu o te. O ma kwe nugǝ dɩ sana o ma zarɩ o fufwǝlǝ yam, yɩ o daarɩ o vɔ-ya. O laan ma kwe-o o daŋɩ o tɩtɩ bɩnaga baŋa nɩ o ja vu nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ vǝrǝ-digǝ. Ba na yi da tɩn, o maa jɩg-o o nii lanyɩranɩ.

eng: The next day, he took two full days worth of wages and gave them to the innkeeper. He said, Take care of him, and when I return, I will pay you back for any additional costs.
local: Tɩga na pʋʋrɩ tɩn, Samari tu wʋm ma zaŋɩ sɩ o kɛ. O ma lɩ sǝbu-dala yale o pa wʋlʋ na te vǝrǝ-digǝ kam tɩn. O ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: ‹Ta n nii nɔɔnʋ wʋm baŋa nɩ n pa-nɩ. Sǝbu kʋm na wʋ yi, maŋa kalʋ a na wʋ́ joori a ba tɩn, aá ŋwɩ kʋlʋ maama n na wʋ́ kɩ n wǝli da tɩn.› ».

eng: What do you think? Which one of these three was a neighbor to the man who encountered thieves.
local: Zezi laan ma bwe-o o wɩ: «Kʋntʋ tɩn, bantʋ batɔ wʋnɩ, wɔɔ mʋ yɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ nɔn-vɩrna bam na vrɩ tɩn doŋ cɩga cɩga?».

eng: Then the legal expert said, The one who demonstrated mercy toward him. Jesus told him, Go and do likewise.
local: Karanyɩna kam ma lǝri o wɩ: «Wʋlʋ wʋm na jaanɩ o ŋwaŋa tɩn.» Zezi laan ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Nan ve sɩ n dɩ ta n kɩ kʋntʋ.».

eng: While Jesus and his disciples were traveling, Jesus entered a village where a woman named Martha welcomed him as a guest.
local: Zezi dɩ o karabiǝ bam daa maa wʋ cwǝŋǝ nɩ ba veǝ. O ma vu o yi tɩʋ kʋdoŋ. Kaanɩ wʋdoŋ maa wʋra, o yɩrɩ mʋ Marɩtɩ. O ma jeeri Zezi lanyɩranɩ ba sɔŋɔ nɩ.

eng: She had a sister named Mary, who sat at the Lords feet and listened to his message.
local: Marɩtɩ nyaanɩ dɩ maa wʋra, o yɩrɩ mʋ Mari. O ma jǝni tɩga nɩ Zezi tee nɩ, yɩ o cǝgi o taanɩ dɩm maama.

eng: By contrast, Martha was preoccupied with getting everything ready for their meal. So Martha came to him and said, Lord, dont you care that my sister has left me to prepare the table all by myself? Tell her to help me.
local: Mari na je kʋntʋ tɩn, Marɩtɩ ma tiini o cɛ tɩtʋŋa nɩ yɩ o liǝ. O ma vu Zezi te o wɩ: «A Yuutu, nii amʋ na zɩgɩ a tʋŋa a yɩranɩ, yɩ a nyaanɩ wʋm cɩm. Nmʋ yigǝ tǝrǝ na? Ta dɩd-o sɩ o zaŋɩ o wǝli-nɩ.».

eng: The Lord answered, Martha, Martha, you are worried and distracted by many things.
local: Dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «Marɩtɩ, Marɩtɩ, nmʋ na li zanzan tɩn mʋ jɩgɩ-m kʋ yaara.

eng: One thing is necessary. Mary has chosen the better part. It wont be taken away from her.
local: Woŋo kʋdoŋ yɩranɩ mʋ daarɩ. Mari mʋ lɩ kʋlʋ na lana tɩn sɩ o kɩ, yɩ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ bá wanɩ o cɩ-kʋ.».

eng: Jesus was praying in a certain place. When he finished, one of his disciples said, Lord, teach us to pray, just as John taught his disciples.
local: Dɛ dɩdwɩ mʋ Zezi dɛɛn ve jǝgǝ kadoŋ sɩ o warɩ Wɛ. O na warɩ Wɛ o ti tɩn, o karabiǝ bam dɩdʋa ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Dɩ́ Yuutu, brɩ dɩbam dɩ́ na wʋ́ ta warɩ Wɛ te tɩn, nɩ Zan dɩ na brɩ o karabiǝ bam sɩ ba taa warɩ Wɛ te tɩn.».

eng: Jesus told them, When you pray, say: Father, uphold the holiness of your name. Bring in your kingdom.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Abam na lagɩ á warɩ Wɛ, sɩ á taá warɩ tɩntʋ doŋ: ‹Dɩbam Ko, sɩ nmʋ yɩrɩ na tiǝ dɩ zulǝ, sɩ n paarɩ ba.

eng: Give us the bread we need for today.
local: Ta n pa dɩbam wʋdiu dɛ maama sɩ kʋ maŋɩ dɩbam.

eng: Forgive us our sins, for we also forgive everyone who has wronged us. And dont lead us into temptation.
local: Yagɩ dɩ́ lwarɩm n ma n cɛ dɩbam, nɩ dɩbam dɩ na yagɩ dɩ́ cɛ balʋ na kɩ dɩbam lwarɩm tɩn. Yɩ pa dɩ́ na maŋɩm dɩlʋ na wʋ́ pa dɩ́ tʋ tɩn.› ».

eng: He also said to them, Imagine that one of you has a friend and you go to that friend in the middle of the night. Imagine saying, Friend, loan me three loaves of bread.
local: Zezi daa ma ta dɩ o karabiǝ bam o wɩ: «Wɛ-loro nyɩ dɩ nmʋ na jɩgɩ n ciloŋ yɩ n zaŋɩ n vu o te tɩtɩɩ kʋrʋ nɩ n ta dɩd-o n wɩ: ‹A ciloŋ, popo a loori-m sɩ n pa-nɩ dɩpwa yatɔ.

eng: because a friend of mine on a journey has arrived and I have nothing to set before him.
local: sɩ vǝru mʋ nuŋi yigǝ yigǝ o ba a te, yɩ a ba jɩgɩ kʋlʋkʋlʋ sɩ a pa-o sɩ o di.›.

eng: Imagine further that he answers from within the house, Dont bother me. The door is already locked, and my children and I are in bed. I cant get up to give you anything.
local: Nmʋ ciloŋ wʋm maa zɩgɩ digǝ kam wʋnɩ o ta dɩ nmʋ o wɩ: ‹Yɩ ta n jɩgɩ-nɩ n yaara, sɩ mancoŋo pɩ yɩ amʋ dɩ a biǝ maama nan tigi dɩ́ dɔ mʋ. A bá wanɩ a zaŋɩ a wǝli-m.›.

eng: I assure you, even if he wouldnt get up and help because of his friendship, he will get up and give his friend whatever he needs because of his friends brashness.
local: N ciloŋ wʋm wʋ́ wanɩ o ta kʋntʋ doŋ na? A nan lagɩ a ta abam sɩ, o ya zɩ wʋ zaŋɩ o pa nmʋ wʋdiu kʋm á ciloŋo kʋm ŋwaanɩ dɩ, nmʋ na zɩgɩ da taan n loor-o dɩ baarɩ tɩn, o laan wʋ́ sɛ o zaŋɩ o pa-m kʋlʋ maama n na lagɩ tɩn.

eng: And I tell you: Ask and you will receive. Seek and you will find. Knock and the door will be opened to you.
local: A nan lagɩ a ta abam sɩ á loori-na Wɛ, sɩ DƖ wʋ́ pa abam kʋlʋ á na lagɩ tɩn. Beeri-na kʋlʋ á na lagɩ tɩn, sɩ á wʋ́ na. Magɩ-na boro, sɩ baá pʋrɩ ba pa abam.

eng: Everyone who asks, receives. Whoever seeks, finds. To everyone who knocks, the door is opened.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ wʋlʋ maama na loori Wɛ sɩ DƖ pa-o woŋo tɩn wʋ́ joŋi. Wʋlʋ maama na beeri woŋo Wɛ tee nɩ tɩn wʋ́ na-kʋ. Baá pʋrɩ ba pa wʋlʋ maama na magɩ boro tɩn.

eng: Which father among you would give a snake to your child if the child asked for a fish?.
local: Abam wʋlʋ na jɩgɩ bu yɩ o na loori-m kalǝŋǝ, nmʋ wʋ́ sɛ n pa-o bɩsankwɩa na?.

eng: If a child asked for an egg, what father would give the child a scorpion?.
local: O na loori cɩcarɩ, nń sɛ n kwe nɔŋɔ n pa-o na?.

eng: If you who are evil know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will the heavenly Father give the Holy Spirit to those who ask him.
local: Aye. Abam wʋrʋ na ba lana yɩ á ta ye sɩ á pa á biǝ wo-laarʋ tɩlʋ ba na lagɩ tɩn, dɩ́ Ko Wɛ dɩlʋ na wʋ wɛyuu nɩ yɩ DƖ lana DƖ dwe abam tɩn bá pa nɔɔna balʋ na loori-DƖ DƖ Joro kʋm tɩn na?».

eng: Jesus was throwing out a demon that causes muteness. When the demon was gone, the man who couldnt speak began to talk. The crowds were amazed.
local: Dɛ dɩdʋa mʋ Zezi dɛɛn zǝli cicirǝ nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ yɩra nɩ. Cicirǝ kam ya pɛ nɔɔnʋ wʋm ji mɩmaa mʋ. Ka na yagɩ o yɩra tɩn, o laan maa ŋɔɔna. Kʋ ma sʋ nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm lanyɩranɩ.

eng: But some of them said, He throws out demons with the authority of Beelzebul, the ruler of demons.
local: Nɔɔna badaara na nɛ tɩn ba maa wɩ: «O na waɩ o zǝli sʋtaana tɩn, kʋ yɩ sʋtaana-pɛ Belizebuli dam ŋwaanɩ mʋ.».

eng: Others were testing him, seeking a sign from heaven.
local: Ba badaara maa kwaanɩ sɩ ba maŋɩ o dam dɩm ba nii, yɩ ba wɩ, o maŋɩ sɩ o kɩ wo-kɩnkagɩlɩ dɩlʋ na wʋ́ brɩ-ba nɩ o dam dɩm sɩɩnɩ dɩ nuŋi Wɛ te tɩn mʋ.

eng: Because Jesus knew what they were thinking, he said to them, Every kingdom involved in civil war becomes a wasteland, and a house torn apart by divisions will collapse.
local: Zezi ma lwarɩ ba wʋbʋŋa na yɩ te tɩn. O ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Tɩʋ kʋlʋ paarɩ na pɔɔrɩ kuni bɩle yɩ ba jɩgɩ daanɩ tɩn, kʋ bá daanɩ kʋ cɔgɩ. Kampwara na zʋ sɔŋɔ kʋlʋ wʋ tɩn, kʋʋ́ tʋ mʋ.

eng: If Satan is at war with himself, how will his kingdom endure? I ask this because you say that I throw out demons by the authority of Beelzebul.
local: Sʋtaana-pɛ dam dɩm na sɩɩnɩ dɩ pɔɔrɩ bɩle, dɩ paarɩ dɩm bá daanɩ dɩ cɔgɩ. Abam na tɛ á wɩ, kʋ yɩ Belizebuli dam ŋwaanɩ mʋ a waɩ a zǝli sʋtaana tɩn, mʋ pɛ a ta kʋntʋ doŋ.

eng: If I throw out demons by the authority of Beelzebul, then by whose authority do your followers throw them out? Therefore, they will be your judges.
local: Kʋ na sɩɩnɩ kʋ yɩ amʋ jɩgɩ Belizebuli dam mʋ a maa zǝli sʋtaana, abam nɔɔna bam dɩ nan maɩ dam dɩm kʋntʋ mʋ ba maa zǝli-ya na? Abam tɩtɩ nɔɔna bam na kɩ te tɩn mʋ brɩ sɩ cɩga tǝri á tee nɩ.

eng: But if I throw out demons by the powerof God, then Gods kingdom has already overtaken you.
local: Kʋ nan yɩ Wɛ tɩtɩ dam mʋ a maɩ a zǝli sʋtaana. Kʋ maa brɩ nɩ Wɛ maŋɩ DƖ ba abam te DƖ di paarɩ nɔɔna tɩtarɩ nɩ.

eng: When a strong man, fully armed, guards his own palace, his possessions are secure.
local: Sʋtaana-pɛ nan nyɩ dɩ nɔn-dɩdɛɛrʋ na jɩgɩ taŋa dɩ cɩna o maa yɩrɩ o sɔŋɔ te tɩn. O na yɩrɩ-kʋ tɩn, o jɩjɩgɩrʋ tɩm bá je.

eng: But as soon as a stronger one attacks and overpowers him, the stronger one takes away the armor he had trusted and divides the stolen goods.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ nan na tuǝ, yɩ o dam na dwe nɔn-dɩdɛɛrʋ wʋm, kʋntʋ tu wʋ́ wanɩ nɔn-dɩdɛɛrʋ wʋm. O laan wʋ́ vrɩ o taŋa dɩ o cɩna yalʋ o na tɩɩgɩ tɩn maama. O daarɩ o kwalɩmɩ o jɩjɩgɩrʋ tɩm maama o maŋɩ o pa nɔɔna.

eng: Whoever isnt with me is against me, and whoever doesnt gather with me, scatters.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ maama nan na daɩ a nɔɔnʋ tɩn yɩ a dʋm mʋ. Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ maama nan na ba tɔgɩ o laɩ a nɔɔna o kɩ jǝgǝ dɩdʋa tɩn, o jagɩ-ba mʋ.».

eng: When an unclean spirit leaves a person, it wanders through dry places looking for a place to rest. But it doesnt find any. Then it says, Ill go back to the house I left.
local: «Cicirǝ na nuŋi nɔɔnʋ yɩra nɩ, ka laan wʋ́ vu ka beeri kapwǝrǝ yigǝ mʋ, sɩ ka lagɩ je ka sin da. Ka nan na beeri ka ga, ka laan wʋ́ ta ka wɩ: ‹Aá joori a vu a na nuŋi mɛ tɩn mʋ, a taa wʋra.›.

eng: When it arrives, it finds the house cleaned up and decorated.
local: Ka na joori ka vu nɔɔnʋ wʋm te yɩ ka na sɩ jǝgǝ kam fɔgɩ ka kwɛ lanyɩranɩ.

eng: Then it goes and brings with it seven other spirits more evil than itself. They go in and make their home there. That person is worse off at the end than at the beginning.
local: ka laan wʋ́ joori ka vu ka lagɩ ciciri sɩrpɛ sɩlʋ na tiini sɩ yɩ balwaarʋ sɩ dwe ka tɩtɩ tɩn. Sɩ ma tɔgɩ daanɩ sɩ vu sɩ zʋʋrɩ da. Kʋntʋ, nɔɔnʋ wʋm laan wʋ́ tiini o na cam kʋ dwǝni faŋa.».

eng: While Jesus was saying these things, a certain woman in the crowd spoke up: Happy is the mother who gave birth to you and who nursed you.
local: Zezi na zɩgɩ o ŋɔɔnɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, kaanɩ wʋdoŋ tɔgɩ o wʋ kɔgɔ kʋm wʋnɩ, o ma ta dɩ kwǝr-dɩa o wɩ: «Kaanɩ wʋlʋ na lʋgɩ-m yɩ n ŋɔgɩ o yɩlɩ tɩn mʋ yɩ yu-yoŋo tu.».

eng: But he said, Happy rather are those who hear Gods word and put it into practice.
local: Zezi ma lǝri o wɩ: «Balʋ na ni Wɛ kwǝrǝ yɩ ba sɛ tɩn mʋ jɩgɩ yu-yoŋo ba dwe dɩdɩ.».

eng: When the crowds grew, Jesus said, This generation is an evil generation. It looks for a sign, but no sign will be given to it except Jonahs sign.
local: Nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm ma tiini kʋ pɩɩnɩ daanɩ. Zezi daa ma ta o wɩ: «Zɩm nɔɔna yɩ nɔn-balwaarʋ mʋ. Ba lagɩ sɩ a kɩ wo-kɩnkagɩlɩ mʋ a brɩ-ba, sɩ kʋ taa yɩ maana kʋ pa-ba. Ba nan bá na, kʋ na daɩ wo-kɩnkagɩlɩ dɩlʋ na maŋɩ dɩ kɩ Wɛ nijoŋnu Zonasɩ tee nɩ faŋa faŋa tɩn.

eng: Just as Jonah became a sign to the people of Nineveh, so the Human Onewill be a sign to this generation.
local: Wʋntʋ dɛɛn brɩ Wɛ dam Niniivi tiinǝ tee nɩ, sɩ kʋ taa yɩ maana kʋ pa-ba. Amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm dɩ wʋ́ ji maana zɩm nɔɔna bam ŋwaanɩ, nɩ kʋ dɛɛn na kɩ Zonasɩ te tɩn.

eng: The queen of the South will rise up at the judgment with the people of this generation and condemn them, because she came from a distant land to hear Solomons wisdom. And look, someone greater than Solomon is here.
local: Wɛ sarɩya dɛ dɩm na yiǝ, pa-kana kalʋ dɛɛn na te Saba tɩʋ faŋa faŋa tɩn wʋ́ zaŋɩ o di zɩm nɔɔna bantʋ sarɩya, yɩ o vɩ-ba bʋra. Wʋntʋ dɛɛn zaŋɩ o tɩtɩ tɩʋ kʋlʋ na tiini kʋ yɩ yigǝ yigǝ nɩ tɩn o ba Pɛ Salɔmɔn te, sɩ o cǝgi o swan taanɩ dɩm. Wʋlʋ nan na dwe Salɔmɔn tɩn wʋ abam tee nɩ, yɩ á ba lagɩ sɩ á cǝg-o.

eng: The people of Nineveh will rise up at the judgment with this generation and condemn it, because they changed their hearts and lives in response to Jonahs preaching—and one greater than Jonah is here.
local: Niniivi tiinǝ dɩ wʋ́ zaŋɩ dɛ dɩm kʋntʋ nɩ ba di zɩm nɔɔna bantʋ sarɩya, bɛŋwaanɩ Zonasɩ na ve ba te o tɔɔlɩ-ba Wɛ kwǝrǝ kam tɩn, ba lǝni ba wʋrʋ ba yagɩ kǝm-balwaarʋ. Wʋlʋ na dwe Zonasɩ tɩn nan wʋ abam tee nɩ, yɩ á wʋ sɛ-o.».

eng: People dont light a lamp and then put it in a closet or under a basket. Rather, they place the lamp on a lampstand so that those who enter the house can see the light.
local: «Nɔɔnʋ na jɩgɩ kanɩa, o bá sɛ o tarɩgɩ-ka o zɩgɩ gugoro nɩ, naa o pu-ka tɩtɔgɔ kuri nɩ. O nan wʋ́ kwe-ka o palɩ wɛɛnɩ mʋ, sɩ balʋ na zʋ digǝ kam wʋ tɩn taa naɩ pooni dɩm.

eng: Your eye is the lamp of your body. When your eye is healthy, your whole body is full of light. But when your eye is bad, your whole body is full of darkness.
local: Nmʋ yiǝ mʋ nyɩ nɩnɛɛnɩ kanɩa kalʋ na paɩ n yɩra jɩgɩ pooni te tɩn. Kʋntʋ tɩn, n yiǝ na lana, kʋ paɩ n yɩra maama wʋ pooni wʋnɩ mʋ. N yiǝ nan na ba lana, kʋ paɩ n yɩra maama wʋ lim wʋnɩ mʋ.

eng: Therefore, see to it that the light in you isnt darkness.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ, ta n yɩrɩ n tɩtɩ sɩ pooni dɩlʋ na wʋ nmʋ wʋnɩ tɩn daa yɩ zaŋɩ dɩ ji lim.

eng: If your whole body is full of light—with no part darkened—then it will be as full of light as when a lamp shines brightly on you.
local: Nmʋ yɩra maama na jɩgɩ pooni yɩ lim dɩ fɩnfɩɩn tǝrǝ, kʋʋ́ pa n yɩra maama wʋ pooni wʋnɩ mʋ, nɩnɛɛnɩ kanɩa na paɩ pooni wʋra te tɩn.».

eng: While Jesus was speaking, a Pharisee invited him to share a meal with him, so Jesus went and took his place at the table.
local: Zezi dɛɛn na ŋɔɔnɩ wǝǝnu tɩm kʋntʋ o ti tɩn, Farizɩan tu dɩdʋa ma bǝŋ-o, sɩ o da o vu o sɔŋɔ o di wʋdiu. O ma vu o zʋ sɔŋɔ kʋm o jǝni sɩ o di.

eng: When the Pharisee saw that Jesus didnt ritually purify his hands by washing before the meal, he was astonished.
local: Farizɩan tu wʋm ma na sɩ Zezi wʋ sanɩ o jɩa sɩ kʋ tɔgɩ ba cullu tɩm cwǝŋǝ. Kʋ ma sʋ-o.

eng: The Lord said to him, Now, you Pharisees clean the outside of the cup and platter, but your insides are stuffed with greed and wickedness.
local: Dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Ei! Abam Farizɩan tiinǝ na lagɩ á di wʋdiu, á yǝni á fɔgɩ á sɩn á zwɩ dɩ á kabǝli kwɛ lanyɩranɩ. Kʋ daarɩ abam tɩtɩ nan su dɩ digiru mʋ. Á wʋrʋ su dɩ ŋwɩɩnɩ dɩ wo-balwaarʋ kǝm.

eng: Foolish people! Didnt the one who made the outside also make the inside?.
local: Abam yɩ jwǝǝru mʋ. Wɛ nan mʋ wʋ kɩ wǝǝnu maama wʋrʋ dɩ tɩ kwɛ na?.

eng: Therefore, give to those in need from the core of who you are and you will be clean all over.
local: Á nan maŋɩ sɩ á kwe á na jɩgɩ kʋlʋ tɩn á pa yinigǝ tiinǝ dɩ á wʋrʋ maama. Á na kɩ kʋntʋ, á kʋlʋkʋlʋ daa bá ta jɩgɩ digiru.

eng: How terrible for you Pharisees! You give a tenth of your mint, rue, and garden herbs of all kinds, while neglecting justice and love for God. These you ought to have done without neglecting the others.
local: Abam Farizɩan tiinǝ-ba, lɛɛrʋ wʋ á yuu nɩ. Bɛŋwaanɩ, kʋ na maŋɩ kʋ yɩ dwǝ dwi maama ba zɩ ba daga dɩ, á yǝni á lɩ kʋlʋ na yɩ Wɛ nyɩm tɩn á pa-DƖ. Kʋ lana. Kʋ daarɩ abam yigǝ tǝrǝ dɩ cɩga tɔgɩm dɩ Wɛ sono. Kʋ ya maŋɩ sɩ á taá kɩ tɩntʋ wǝǝnu tɩm mʋ, sɩ á yɩ yagɩ Wɛ niǝ yadaara yam.

eng: How terrible for you Pharisees! You love the most prominent seats in the synagogues and respectful greetings in the marketplaces.
local: Abam Farizɩan tiinǝ-ba, lɛɛrʋ wʋ á yuu nɩ. Bɛŋwaanɩ, á na zʋ á Wɛ-di sɩm, á lagɩ je-kamunnu mʋ sɩ á taá je yigǝ nɩ. Á na ve yaga, á lagɩ sɩ nɔɔna maama taa tui ba zuli abam.

eng: How terrible for you! You are like unmarked graves, and people walk on them without recognizing it.
local: Lɛɛrʋ wʋ á yuu nɩ. Bɛŋwaanɩ á nyɩ dɩ yibeelǝ yalʋ na saarɩ ya daarɩ tɩga yɩ nɔɔna tɔgɩ ya baŋa ba kɛa yɩ ba yǝri ya ni nɩ tɩn mʋ.».

eng: One of the legal experts responded, Teacher, when you say these things, you are insulting us too.
local: Wɛ cullu karanyɩna dɩdʋa ma lǝri Zezi taanɩ dɩm o wɩ: «Karanyɩna, nmʋ na jɩgɩ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ n ŋɔɔnɩ tɩn, n twɩ dɩbam dɩ mʋ.».

eng: Jesus said, How terrible for you legal experts too! You load people down with impossible burdens and you refuse to lift a single finger to help them.
local: Zezi ma lǝri o wɩ: «Abam balʋ na yɩ Wɛ cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ tɩn, lɛɛrʋ wʋ á dɩ yuu nɩ. Bɛŋwaanɩ abam yǝni á yaarɩ nɔɔna dɩ wo-dɛɛrʋ sɩ ba taa tɔga. Kʋ maa nyɩ dɩ á na pa ba zɩŋɩ zɩla yalʋ na dunǝ ya dwe ba zɩŋɩm tɩn, yɩ abam tɩtɩ ba sɛ sɩ á twǝri zɩla yam fɩnfɩɩn dɩ á zǝŋi.

eng: How terrible for you! You built memorials to the prophets, whom your ancestors killed.
local: Lɛɛrʋ wʋ á yuu nɩ. Bɛŋwaanɩ abam yǝni á fɔgɩ á kɩ faŋa faŋa Wɛ nijoŋnǝ bam yibeelǝ. Kʋ nan yɩ á kwǝ-nakwa mʋ gʋ-ba.

eng: In this way, you testify that you approve of your ancestors deeds. They killed the prophets, and you build memorials!.
local: Á kǝm dɩm brɩ nɩ á sɛ kǝm-balwaarʋ tɩlʋ á kwǝ-nakwa bam na kɩ tɩn. Bantʋ dɛɛn gʋ Wɛ nijoŋnǝ bam, yɩ abam tɩtɩ laan fɔgɩ á kwɛ ba yibeelǝ yam.

eng: Therefore, Gods wisdom has said, I will send prophets and apostles to them and they will harass and kill some of them.
local: Abam na tɔgɩ á kwǝ kǝnǝ kʋntʋ tɩn, Wɛ dɩlʋ na maŋɩ DƖ ye wǝǝnu maama tɩn tagɩ abam ŋwaanɩ DƖ wɩ: ‹Aá tʋŋɩ a nijoŋnǝ dɩ a tɩntʋŋna ba tee nɩ. Ba ma wʋ́ ja ba badaara ba gʋ, yɩ baá yaarɩ badaara.›.

eng: Some who were present on that occasion told Jesus about the Galileans whom Pilate had killed while they were offering sacrifices.
local: Kantʋ maŋa kam nɩ nɔɔna badonnǝ maa ba Zezi te, yɩ ba ta dɩd-o Galile tiinǝ badonnǝ taanɩ. Gʋvɩrma tu Pilatɩ dɛɛn jaanɩ bantʋ o gʋ maŋa kalʋ ba na wʋra ba kaanɩ Baŋa-Wɛ tɩn, o pa ba jana nuŋi ka gwaanɩ dɩ vara jana.

eng: As a result, this generation will be charged with the murder of all the prophets since the beginning of time.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ Wɛ wʋ́ pa á na cam zanzan, nɩ kʋ yɩ abam tɩtɩ mʋ gʋ Wɛ nijoŋnǝ balʋ maama ba na gʋ lʋgʋ pulim nɩ sɩ kʋ ba kʋ yi zɩm tɩn.

eng: He replied, Do you think the suffering of these Galileans proves that they were more sinful than all the other Galileans?.
local: Zezi ma lǝri nɔɔna bam o wɩ: «Galile tiinǝ bantʋ na tiini ba na cam kʋntʋ doŋ tɩn, á bʋŋɩ sɩ kʋ yɩ ba na tiini ba yɩ nɔn-balwaarʋ ba dwe ba donnǝ bam tɩn ŋwaanɩ mʋ na?.

eng: This includes the murder of every prophet—from Abel to Zechariah—who was killed between the altar and the holy place. Yes, Im telling you, this generation will be charged with it.
local: kʋ na puli Abɛlɩ nɩ sɩ kʋ ba kʋ kweeli dɩ Zakari wʋlʋ ba na gʋ Wɛ kaanɩm bimbim dɩm dɩ DƖ di-laa kʋm tɩtarɩ nɩ tɩn. A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, kǝm-balwaarʋ tɩm kʋntʋ maama caa wʋ́ ta wʋ zɩm nɔɔna bam yuu nɩ.

eng: No, I tell you, but unless you change your hearts and lives, you will die just as they did.
local: Aye, kʋ daɩ kʋntʋ. Abam tɩtɩ dɩ na wʋ lǝni á wʋrʋ á yagɩ kǝm-balwaarʋ tɩm, abam maama dɩ wʋ́ cɔgɩ kʋntʋ doŋ mʋ.

eng: How terrible for you legal experts! You snatched away the key of knowledge. You didnt enter yourselves, and you stood in the way of those who were entering.
local: Wɛ cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ-ba, lɛɛrʋ wʋ á yuu nɩ. Bɛŋwaanɩ á yǝni á cɩ nɔɔna sɩ ba yɩ lwarɩ Wɛ cɩga kam. Abam tɩtɩ wʋ sɛ sɩ á joŋi-ka, yɩ á daarɩ á wʋ pɛ balʋ na lagɩ ba joŋi-ka tɩn cwǝŋǝ sɩ ba joŋi.».

eng: What about those eighteen people who were killed when the tower of Siloam fell on them? Do you think that they were more guilty of wrongdoing than everyone else who lives in Jerusalem?.
local: Kʋ ta na yɩ nɔɔna fugǝ-nana balʋ ya na tɩgɩ Silʋa nɩ maŋa kam wɛyuu digǝ na tʋ ba baŋa nɩ tɩn, á bʋŋɩ nɩ kʋ yɩ ba na maŋɩ sɩ ba na cɔgɩm kʋ dwǝni Zeruzalɛm tiinǝ badonnǝ bam tɩn ŋwaanɩ mʋ na?.

eng: As he left there, the legal experts and Pharisees began to resent him deeply and to ask him pointed questions about many things.
local: Zezi laan ma nuŋi jǝgǝ kam kʋntʋ nɩ o viiri. Wɛ cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ bam dɩ Farizɩan tiinǝ bam maa ba lagɩ o taanɩ dɩ fɩnfɩɩn dɩ. Ba ma yaar-o dɩ bwiǝ zanzan.

eng: No, I tell you, but unless you change your hearts and lives, you will die just as they did.
local: Aye, kʋ daɩ kʋntʋ. Abam tɩtɩ na wʋ lǝni á wʋrʋ á yagɩ kǝm-balwaarʋ tɩm, abam maama dɩ wʋ́ cɔgɩ kʋntʋ doŋ mʋ.».

eng: They plotted against him, trying to trap him in his words.
local: Ba maa lagɩ sɩ ba pa o ŋɔɔnɩ o tusi, sɩ ba wanɩ ba ja-o.

eng: Jesus told this parable: A man owned a fig tree planted in his vineyard. He came looking for fruit on it and found none.
local: Zezi laan ma maŋɩ mɩmaŋa o brɩ-ba o wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ mʋ wʋra. O ma jǝri kapʋrʋ o kara wʋnɩ. O ma vu sɩ o nii, oó na kʋ biǝ o gwǝri na. O ma wʋ nɛ.

eng: He said to his gardener, Look, Ive come looking for fruit on this fig tree for the past three years, and Ive never found any. Cut it down! Why should it continue depleting the soils nutrients.
local: Kʋ na ba lǝri biǝ tɩn, o ma ta dɩ o tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋm o wɩ: ‹Nii, dɔɔn jɩgɩ bɩna yatɔ mʋ a na tui a laga kʋ biǝ yɩ a ba naɩ kʋlʋkʋlʋ. Nan goni-kʋ n dɩ tɩga nɩ, sɩ kʋ zɩgɩ da kafɛ mʋ›.

eng: The gardener responded, Lord, give it one more year, and I will dig around it and give it fertilizer.
local: Tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋm ma lǝr-o o wɩ: ‹A yuutu, yagɩ sɩ kʋ daa kɩ bɩnɩ dɩdʋa kʋ wǝli da. Aá kʋ goŋo a kaagɩ kʋ maama yɩ a kɩ pʋpɔnɔ da.

eng: Maybe it will produce fruit next year; if not, then you can cut it down.
local: Bɩnɩ daa na yiǝ yɩ kʋ na lǝgi biǝ, kʋ lana. Kʋ nan daa ta na wʋ lǝgi, dɩ́ laan wʋ́ go-kʋ dɩ́ dɩ tɩga nɩ.› ».

eng: Jesus was teaching in one of the synagogues on the Sabbath.
local: Zwifǝ bam siun dɛ dɩdoŋ dɛɛn ma yi. Zezi maa wʋ ba Wɛ-digǝ kam nɩ o brɩ-ba Wɛ taanɩ.

eng: A woman was there who had been disabled by a spirit for eighteen years. She was bent over and couldnt stand up straight.
local: Kaanɩ wʋdoŋ maa tɔgɩ o wʋra, cicirǝ na jɩg-o ka yaara dɩ yawɩʋ taan bɩna fugǝ-nana, ka pa o tii mʋ, o warɩ o kwǝni wɛɛnɩ.

eng: When he saw her, Jesus called her to him and said, Woman, you are set free from your sickness.
local: Zezi na nɛ-o tɩn, o ma ta dɩd-o sɩ o ba o te. O laan ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Kaanɩ, n nɛ bwǝlim, n sin dɩ n yaara yam.».

eng: He placed his hands on her and she straightened up at once and praised God.
local: O na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma zi o jɩa o baŋa nɩ. Bɩdwɩ baŋa nɩ mʋ kaanɩ wʋm kwǝni wɛɛnɩ. O na kwǝni wɛɛnɩ tɩn, o ma tee Baŋa-Wɛ.

eng: The synagogue leader, incensed that Jesus had healed on the Sabbath, responded, There are six days during which work is permitted. Come and be healed on those days, not on the Sabbath day.
local: Zwifǝ Wɛ-digǝ kam yuutu wʋm na nɛ Zezi na kɩ te ba siun dɛ dɩm nɩ yɩ o pa kaanɩ wʋm na yazurǝ tɩn, o bam ma zaŋɩ. O ma ta dɩ kɔgɔ kʋm o wɩ: «Wɛ pɛ dɩbam da yardʋ mʋ sɩ dɩ́ ta maa tʋŋɩ dɩ́ tɩtʋŋa. Kʋntʋ tɩn, á maŋɩ sɩ á taá tui da yam kʋntʋ nɩ mʋ sɩ á na yazurǝ, sɩ kʋ daɩ siun dɛ dɩm nɩ.».

eng: The Lord replied, Hypocrites! Dont each of you on the Sabbath untie your ox or donkey from its stall and lead it out to get a drink?.
local: Dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «Abam yɩ pipiri-nyɩna mʋ. Abam wɔɔ mʋ wʋ́ ta jɩgɩ o nabɩa naa o bɩnaga yɩ o ba bwǝli-ka o ja vu sɩ ka nyɔ na siun dɛ dɩm nɩ?.

eng: Then isnt it necessary that this woman, a daughter of Abraham, bound by Satan for eighteen long years, be set free from her bondage on the Sabbath day.
local: Kʋntʋ tɩn, kaanɩ wʋntʋ na yɩ Abraham dwi tu yɩ sʋtaanɩ ya vɔ-o dɩ yawɩʋ bɩna fugǝ-nana tɩn, kʋ culi sɩ a bwǝl-o a yagɩ siun dɛ dɩm nɩ sɩ o sin dɩ o yawɩʋ kʋm na?».

eng: When he said these things, all his opponents were put to shame, but all those in the crowd rejoiced at all the extraordinary things he was doing.
local: O na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ ma pa balʋ na vɩ-o tɩn na cavɩɩra. Kʋ daarɩ nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm maama maa jɩgɩ wʋpolo zanzan dɩ wo-kɩnkagɩla yalʋ maama o na kɩ tɩn.

eng: Jesus asked, What is Gods kingdom like? To what can I compare it?.
local: Zezi daa ma ta o wɩ: «Wɛ paarɩ dɩm nyɩ dɩ bɛɛ? A ma bɛɛ mʋ a maŋɩ-dɩ a brɩ abam?.

eng: Its like a mustard seed that someone took and planted in a garden. It grew and developed into a tree and the birds in the sky nested in its branches.
local: Wɛ paarɩ dɩm nyɩ dɩ nɔɔnʋ na kwe wo-duu na yɩ mɩmɩɩnʋ o vu o du o kadugǝ nɩ. Wo-duu kʋm kʋntʋ ma nuŋi, yɩ kʋ tiini kʋ ji tu-kamunu. Zunǝ laan ma ba ba sɔ ba pwǝru tiu kʋm yuu nɩ.».

eng: Again he said, To what can I compare Gods kingdom?.
local: Zezi daa ma ta o wɩ: «A ta wʋ́ ma bɛɛ mʋ a maŋɩ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm na nyɩ te tɩn a brɩ abam?.

eng: Its like yeast, which a woman took and hid in a bushel of wheat flour until the yeast had worked its way through the whole.
local: Dɩ nyɩ dɩ kaanɩ na kwe dabɩlɩ o gwaanɩ dɩ dɩpɛ muni zwɩ-zɛ sɩtɔ te tɩn. Dɩ laan ma pa dɩpɛ muni dɩm maama fʋlɩ.».

eng: Jesus traveled through cities and villages, teaching and making his way to Jerusalem.
local: Zezi dɛɛn wʋ cwǝŋǝ nɩ o maa ve Zeruzalɛm. O na maa ve tɩn, o tʋlɩ tɩɩnɩ dɩm maama yɩ o brɩ-ba Wɛ taanɩ.

eng: Someone said to him, Lord, will only a few be saved? Jesus said to them.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ ma bwe-o o wɩ: «A Yuutu, nɔɔna fɩnfɩɩn yɩranɩ mʋ wʋ́ na vrɩm na?» Zezi ma ta dɩ nɔɔna bam o wɩ:.

eng: Make every effort to enter through the narrow gate. Many, I tell you, will try to enter and wont be able to.
local: «Á taá cʋ á tɩtɩ sɩ á kwaanɩ á da ni dɩlʋ na pɩɩnɩ tɩn á zʋ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋ. A lagɩ a ta abam sɩ nɔɔna zanzan wʋ́ ba ba beeri sɩ ba zʋ, yɩ ba bá wanɩ ba na cwǝŋǝ.

eng: Once the owner of the house gets up and shuts the door, then you will stand outside and knock on the door, saying, Lord, open the door for us. He will reply, I dont know you or where you are from.
local: Maŋa kam na yiǝ, yɩ sɔŋɔ tu zaŋɩ o pɩ o mancoŋo, abam laan wʋ́ ba á zɩgɩ pooni nɩ á magɩ boro, yɩ á loori á wɩ: ‹Dɩ́ Yuutu, popo, pʋrɩ sɩ dɩ́ zʋ.› O laan wʋ́ lǝri o wɩ: ‹A yǝri abam na nuŋi mɛ.›.

eng: Then you will begin to say, We ate and drank in your presence, and you taught in our streets.
local: Abam laan wʋ́ ta wɩ: ‹Dɩbam ya tɔgɩ dɩ nmʋ dɩ́ jǝni dɩ́ di wʋdiu. Kʋ yɩ dɩbam tɩʋ kʋm cwe sɩm wʋnɩ mʋ n zɩgɩ da n brɩ Wɛ taanɩ.›.

eng: He will respond, I dont know you or where you are from. Go away from me, all you evildoers.
local: O nan ta wʋ́ lǝri abam o wɩ: ‹A yǝri abam. Joori-na daa nɩ, abam kǝm-balwaarʋ tiinǝ bam.›.

eng: There will be weeping and grinding of teeth when you see Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and all the prophets in Gods kingdom, but you yourselves will be thrown out.
local: Kʋ na kɩ abam kʋntʋ, á wʋ́ na Abraham dɩ Yizakɩ dɩ Zakɔbɩ na je Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋnɩ, kʋ wǝli dɩ Wɛ nijoŋnǝ bam maama. Abam tɩtɩ nan bá na cwǝŋǝ sɩ á zʋ. Abam wʋ́ maŋɩ mɛ yaara na wʋra yɩ nɔɔna keerǝ yɩ ba dʋnɩ ba nwa tɩn.

eng: People will come from east and west, north and south, and sit down to eat in Gods kingdom.
local: Nɔɔna wʋ́ nuŋi lʋgʋ baŋa je maama nɩ ba ba ba zʋ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋ, yɩ ba jǝni ba di ywǝǝni daanɩ.

eng: Look! Those who are last will be first and those who are first will be last.
local: Á taá ye nɩ, nɔɔna badaara zɩm na yɩ kwaga tiinǝ tɩn wʋ́ ba ba ji yigǝ tiinǝ. Badaara dɩ na maŋɩ ba yɩ yigǝ tiinǝ tɩn wʋ́ joori ba ji kwaga tiinǝ.».

eng: At that time, some Pharisees approached Jesus and said, Go! Get away from here, because Herod wants to kill you.
local: Maŋa kam kʋntʋ nɩ mʋ Farizɩan badonnǝ tu Zezi te, yɩ ba ta dɩd-o ba wɩ: «Yagɩ yo seeni sɩ n vu jǝgǝ kadoŋ. Bɛŋwaanɩ Pɛ Erɔdɩ lagɩ sɩ o pa ba gʋ nmʋ.».

eng: Jesus said to them, Go, tell that fox, Look, Im throwing out demons and healing people today and tomorrow, and on the third day I will complete my work.
local: O ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Ve-na á ta á brɩ nasɩa kam nɩ, zɩm da yantʋ nɩ a maŋɩ sɩ a taa zǝli sʋtaana, sɩ a kwǝri a pa nɔɔna na yazurǝ. Da yatɔ dɛ nɩ, a laan wʋ́ yi a kweelim jǝgǝ.

eng: However, its necessary for me to travel today, tomorrow, and the next day because its impossible for a prophet to be killed outside of Jerusalem.
local: A nan maŋɩ sɩ a daa kwe a cwǝŋǝ a taa kɛa zɩm dɩ jwaanɩ dɩ jwa yigǝ nɩ, bɛŋwaanɩ kʋ bá wanɩ sɩ wʋlʋ na yɩ Wɛ nijoŋnu tɩn ga o ŋwɩa yɩ kʋ daɩ Zeruzalɛm nɩ.

eng: Jerusalem, Jerusalem, you who kill the prophets and stone those who were sent to you! How often I have wanted to gather your people just as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you didnt want that.
local: Hǝii! Zeruzalɛm tiinǝ-ba, á yǝni á gʋ Wɛ nijoŋnǝ yɩ á dʋlɩ nɔɔna balʋ Wɛ na tʋŋɩ abam tee nɩ tɩn dɩ kandwa. Kuni zanzan mʋ a ya lagɩ sɩ a la abam nɔn-biǝ a kɩ daanɩ sɩ a taa nii á baŋa nɩ, nɩnɛɛnɩ coro na kukwǝri kʋ biǝ kʋ kɩ kʋ vwana kuri nɩ te tɩn, yɩ abam wʋ sɛ.

eng: Look, your house is abandoned. I tell you, you wont see me until the time comes when you say, Blessings on the one who comes in the Lords name .
local: Kʋntʋ tɩn, Wɛ laan wʋ́ dwanɩ DƖ jɩa abam tɩʋ kʋm baŋa nɩ sɩ kʋ ji di-dwǝǝnu. Á taá ye nɩ, á daa bá na-nɩ sɩ kʋ taa ve maŋa kalʋ á na wʋ́ ta wɩ: ‹Zuli-na wʋlʋ na tu dɩ́ Yuutu Wɛ yɩrɩ ŋwaanɩ tɩn.› ».

eng: Jesus called the Twelve together and he gave them power and authority over all demons and to heal sicknesses.
local: Zezi dɛɛn ma bǝŋi o karabiǝ fugǝ-bale bam sɩ ba la daanɩ jǝgǝ dɩdʋa. O ma pa-ba dam sɩ ba wanɩ ciciri maama ba zǝli nɔɔna yɩra nɩ, sɩ ba daarɩ ba pa nɔɔna na yazurǝ ba yawɩɩrʋ wʋnɩ.

eng: He sent them out to proclaim Gods kingdom and to heal the sick.
local: O ma tʋŋɩ-ba sɩ ba vu ba tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝrǝ kam ba brɩ DƖ na lagɩ DƖ di paarɩ tɩte nɔɔna tɩtarɩ nɩ tɩn, sɩ ba daarɩ ba pa yawɩɩna na yazurǝ.

eng: He told them, Take nothing for the journey—no walking stick, no bag, no bread, no money, not even an extra shirt.
local: O ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Á na maa ve, á yɩ zaŋɩ á kwe kʋlʋkʋlʋ á wǝli da á ja vu. Á yɩ kwe nacǝgǝ naa tampɔgɔ naa wʋdiu naa sǝbu. Á yɩ kwe gwaarʋ tɩle tɩle á wǝli da.

eng: Whatever house you enter, remain there until you leave that place.
local: Á na ve á zʋ sɔŋɔ kʋlʋ maama á pǝni da, sɩ á taá zʋʋrɩ dáanɩ sɩ kʋ ba kʋ yi á viirim mɩmaŋa.

eng: Wherever they dont welcome you, as you leave that city, shake the dust off your feet as a witness against them.
local: Ba nan daa na wʋ sɛ sɩ ba jeeri abam lanyɩranɩ, sɩ á nuŋi tɩʋ kʋm kʋntʋ nɩ. Á na maa viirǝ, sɩ á pɩɩsɩ á nɛ fogo kʋm á yagɩ da, sɩ kʋ pa nɔɔna bam lwarɩ nɩ ba wʋ kɩ lanyɩranɩ.».

eng: They departed and went through the villages proclaiming the good news and healing people everywhere.
local: Zezi na tagɩ kʋntʋ dɩ ba o ti tɩn, ba laan ma nuŋi ba tʋlɩ tɩ-balɛ sɩm maama. Je sɩlʋ maama ba na ve tɩn, ba ma tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam, yɩ ba pa nɔɔna yawɩɩrʋ je.

eng: Herod the ruler heard about everything that was happening. He was confused because some people were saying that John had been raised from the dead.
local: Galile Pɛ Erɔdɩ dɛɛn ni kʋlʋ maama Zezi dɩ o karabiǝ bam na wʋra ba kɩ tɩn. Kʋ ma pa o wʋbʋŋa vugimi daanɩ, bɛŋwaanɩ nɔɔna badaara tɛ Zezi woŋo ba wɩ, Zan mʋ joori o bi o yagɩ tʋʋnɩ.

eng: others that Elijah had appeared, and still others that one of the ancient prophets had come back to life.
local: Babam maa tɛ ba wɩ, Wɛ nijoŋnu Eli mʋ joori o ba lʋgʋ baŋa. Badonnǝ dɩ maa tɛ ba wɩ, kʋ yɩ faŋa faŋa Wɛ nijoŋnǝ bam dɩdʋa mʋ joori o bi.

eng: Herod said, I beheaded John, so now who am I hearing about? Herod wanted to see him.
local: Erɔdɩ ma ta o wɩ: «Kʋ nan na yɩ Zan, a maŋɩ a pa ba go o yuu. Nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ nan yɩ nɔɔnʋ wɔɔ mʋ, yɩ o kɩ wo-kɩnkagɩla yalʋ maama a na ni ya ŋwa tɩn?» O maa tiini o kwaana, sɩ o na wʋlʋ na kɩ wǝǝnu tɩntʋ maama tɩn.

eng: When the apostles returned, they described for Jesus what they had done. Taking them with him, Jesus withdrew privately to a city called Bethsaida.
local: Zezi karabiǝ bam dɛɛn ma joori ba vu o te. Ba ma ta ba brɩ-o ba na kɩ kʋlʋ maama tɩn. O ma pa ba tɔgɩ dɩd-o ba vu jǝgǝ kadoŋ ba yɩranɩ, yɩ ba daarɩ nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm. Jǝgǝ kam ba na ve tɩn batwarɩ dɩ tɩʋ kʋdoŋ, kʋ yɩrɩ mʋ Betisayida.

eng: When the crowds figured it out, they followed him. He welcomed them, spoke to them about Gods kingdom, and healed those who were sick.
local: Nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm ma lwarɩ Zezi dɩ o karabiǝ bam na ve mɛ tɩn. Ba laan ma tɔgɩ ba kwaga. Ba ma vu ba yi Zezi te. O ma jeeri-ba lanyɩranɩ. O ma ŋɔɔnɩ o brɩ-ba Wɛ paarɩ dɩm na yɩ te tɩn, yɩ o daarɩ o pa balʋ maama na lagɩ yazurǝ tɩn na yazurǝ.

eng: When the day was almost over, the Twelve came to him and said, Send the crowd away so that they can go to the nearby villages and countryside and find lodging and food, because we are in a deserted place.
local: Maŋa kalʋ wɛ na wʋra dɩ zʋʋrɩ tɩn, Zezi karabiǝ fugǝ-bale bam ma ba o te ba wɩ: «Pa nɔɔna bam vu sam wʋnɩ ba lagɩ wʋdiu ba di, dɩ pwǝgǝ je ba pǝni, bɛŋwaanɩ jǝgǝ kalʋ dɩbam na wʋra tɩn yɩ gaa wʋ mʋ. Wʋdiu tǝri yo.».

eng: He replied, You give them something to eat. But they said, We have no more than five loaves of bread and two fish—unless we go and buy food for all these people.
local: O ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Abam tɩtɩ mʋ wʋ́ pa-ba wʋdiu sɩ ba di.» Ba ma lǝr-o ba wɩ: «Dɩ́ jɩgɩ dɩpwa yanu dɩ kale sɩle má má mʋ, kʋ na daɩ sɩ dɩ́ vu dɩ́ yǝgi wʋdiu dɩ́ ja ba dɩ́ pa nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋntʋ maama.».

eng: Jesus said to his disciples, Seat them in groups of about fifty.
local: Kɔgɔ kʋm dɛɛn yɩ nɩnɛɛnɩ baara mʋrr-tɩnu mʋ , kʋ wǝli dɩ kaana dɩ biǝ dɩ. Zezi ma ta dɩ o karabiǝ bam o wɩ, ba pa nɔɔna bam pɔɔrɩ fiinnu fiinnu ba jǝni tɩga nɩ.

eng: They did so, and everyone was seated.
local: Ba ma kɩ kʋntʋ. Nɔɔna bam maama ma jǝni tɩga nɩ.

eng: He took the five loaves and the two fish, looked up to heaven, blessed them, and broke them and gave them to the disciples to set before the crowd.
local: Zezi laan ma kwe dɩpwa yanu yam dɩ kale sɩle sɩm, yɩ o kwǝni o yuu wɛɛnɩ o kɩ Wɛ le tɩ ŋwaanɩ. O ma fɔ fɔ wʋdiiru tɩm o maŋɩ o pa o karabiǝ bam, sɩ ba ja vu ba pa nɔɔna bam.

eng: Everyone ate until they were full, and the disciples filled twelve baskets with the leftovers.
local: Nɔɔna bam maama ma di ba su ba daarɩ. Ba na di ba daarɩ kʋlʋ tɩn, ba ma pɛ cicwǝǝru tɩlʋ na daarɩ tɩn ba su tɩtwarʋ fugǝ-tɩle.

eng: Once when Jesus was praying by himself, the disciples joined him, and he asked them, Who do the crowds say that I am.
local: Dɛ dɩdwɩ mʋ Zezi dɛɛn wʋra o warɩ Wɛ o yɩranɩ. O karabiǝ bam dɩ maa wʋ jǝgǝ kam kʋntʋ nɩ. O ma bwe-ba o wɩ: «Nɔɔna yǝni ba tɛ ba wɩ, amʋ yɩ wɔɔ mʋ?».

eng: They answered, John the Baptist, others Elijah, and still others that one of the ancient prophets has come back to life.
local: Ba ma lǝr-o ba wɩ: «Badaara tɛ ba wɩ, nmʋ yɩ Zan wʋlʋ dɛɛn na miisi nɔɔna na wʋnɩ Wɛ ŋwaanɩ tɩn mʋ. Babam dɩ maa tɛ ba wɩ, nmʋ yɩ Wɛ nijoŋnu Eli mʋ. Badonnǝ dɩ maa tɛ ba wɩ, nmʋ yɩ faŋa faŋa Wɛ nijoŋnǝ bam dɩdʋa mʋ n joori n bi n yagɩ tʋʋnɩ.».

eng: He asked them, And what about you? Who do you say that I am? Peter answered, The Christ sent from God.
local: O laan ma bwe-ba o wɩ: «Kʋ nan na yɩ abam, á bʋŋɩ sɩ amʋ yɩ wɔɔ mʋ?» Pɩyɛɛrɩ ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «Nmʋ yɩ Krisi wʋm Wɛ na tʋŋɩ tɩn mʋ.».

eng: Jesus gave them strict orders not to tell this to anyone.
local: Zezi laan ma tiini o kaanɩ-ba, sɩ ba yɩ ta woŋo kʋntʋ ba brɩ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ.

eng: He said, The Human Onemust suffer many things and be rejected—by the elders, chief priests, and the legal experts—and be killed and be raised on the third day.
local: O daa ma ta o wɩ: «Kʋ fɩn mʋ sɩ amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm na yaara zanzan. Zwifǝ nakwa bam dɩ ba kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam dɩ ba cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ bam mʋ wʋ́ culi amʋ. Baá gʋ-nɩ, yɩ a laan wʋ́ joori a bi da yatɔ dɛ nɩ.».

eng: Jesus said to everyone, All who want to come after me must say no to themselves, take up their cross daily, and follow me.
local: Zezi laan ma ta dɩ nɔɔna bam maama o wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ maama na lagɩ sɩ o taa tɔgɩ amʋ kwaga tɩn, kʋntʋ tu maŋɩ sɩ o vɩn o tɩtɩ wʋbʋŋa, sɩ o sɛ yaara dɛ maama, nɩ wʋlʋ na zɩŋɩ dagara o maa ve o tʋʋnɩ jǝgǝ te tɩn, sɩ o laan taa tɔgɩ-nɩ.

eng: All who want to save their lives will lose them. But all who lose their lives because of me will save them.
local: Wʋlʋ maama nan na lagɩ sɩ o yɩrɩ o ŋwɩa tɩn, kʋntʋ tu wʋ́ ga-ka mʋ. Kʋ daarɩ, wʋlʋ maama na sɛ sɩ o ga o ŋwɩa amʋ ŋwaanɩ tɩn, kʋntʋ tu wʋ́ na ŋwɩa Wɛ tee nɩ.

eng: What advantage do people have if they gain the whole world for themselves yet perish or lose their lives?.
local: Nɔɔnʋ nan na nɛ lʋgʋ baŋa wǝǝnu tɩm maama, yɩ o laan na gɛ ŋwɩa Wɛ tee nɩ, kʋ jɩgɩ bɛɛ nyɔɔrɩ mʋ kʋ pa-o?.

eng: Whoever is ashamed of me and my words, the Human Onewill be ashamed of that person when he comes in his glory and in the glory of the Father and of the holy angels.
local: Wʋlʋ maama na kwarɩ cavɩɩra amʋ ŋwaanɩ dɩ a taanɩ dɩm ŋwaanɩ tɩn, cavɩɩra wʋ́ ta jɩgɩ amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm kʋntʋ tu ŋwaanɩ, maŋa kam a na wʋ́ joori a ba lʋgʋ baŋa dɩ paarɩ-zulǝ, nɩ a Ko Wɛ dɩ DƖ malɛsɩ sɩm na jɩgɩ paarɩ-zulǝ te tɩn.

eng: I assure you that some standing here wont die before they see Gods kingdom.
local: A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, nɔɔna balʋ zɩm na wʋ yo seeni tɩn badaara bá tɩ, yɩ baá na nɩ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm tu nɔɔna tɩtarɩ nɩ.».

eng: About eight days after Jesus said these things, he took Peter, John, and James, and went up on a mountain to pray.
local: Zezi na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ maŋɩ dɩ da nana na kɛ tɩn, o laan ma ja Pɩyɛɛrɩ dɩ Zan dɩ Zakɩ o vu o di piu yuu sɩ o warɩ Wɛ.

eng: As he was praying, the appearance of his face changed and his clothes flashed white like lightning.
local: Maŋa kam o na wʋra o warɩ Wɛ tɩn, o yibiyǝ ma lǝni. O gwaarʋ tɩm dɩ maa pɩɩrɩ parɩ parɩ, yɩ tɩ nyɩɩna.

eng: Two men, Moses and Elijah, were talking with him.
local: Nɔɔna bale ma da ba nuŋi ba yigǝ nɩ, ba na tiini ba jɩgɩ Wɛ paarɩ-zulǝ pooni lanyɩranɩ. Bantʋ yɩ Moyisi dɩ Eli mʋ. Ba ma zɩgɩ ba ŋɔɔnɩ dɩ Zezi. Ba maa wʋra ba tɛ o na wʋ́ vu o tɩ Zeruzalɛm nɩ, sɩ o ma ti kʋlʋ o na tu sɩ o tʋŋɩ tɩn.

eng: Peter and those with him were almost overcome by sleep, but they managed to stay awake and saw his glory as well as the two men with him.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ dɩ o donnǝ bam ma cɛ dɩ dɔɔnɩ. Ba laan na dɔ ba zaŋɩ tɩn, mʋ ba nɛ Zezi na jɩgɩ Wɛ paarɩ-zulǝ pooni kʋ gaalɩ te. Ba ma daarɩ ba na nɔɔna bale bam na zɩgɩ o tee nɩ.

eng: As the two men were about to leave Jesus, Peter said to him, Master, its good that were here. We should construct three shrines: one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah—but he didnt know what he was saying.
local: Maŋa kam nɔɔna bale bam na lagɩ ba viiri tɩn, Pɩyɛɛrɩ ma ta dɩ Zezi o wɩ: «Dɩ́ Yuutu, dɩ́ na wʋ yo seeni tɩn, kʋ tiini kʋ lana kʋ pa dɩbam. Nan pa dɩ́ pu vwǝ yatɔ yo seeni, sɩ nmʋ dɩ Moyisi dɩ Eli taa zʋʋrɩ da.» O dɛɛn garɩ o ŋɔɔnɩ mʋ, sɩ o yǝri kʋlʋ o na jɩgɩ o tɛ tɩn.

eng: Peter was still speaking when a cloud overshadowed them. As they entered the cloud, they were overcome with awe.
local: O na wʋra o ŋɔɔnɩ kʋntʋ tɩn mʋ kunkwǝǝnu tu tɩ ba tɩ kwǝli-ba. Tɩ na kwǝli-ba tɩn, fʋʋnɩ ma ja Zezi karabiǝ bam lanyɩranɩ.

eng: Then a voice from the cloud said, This is my Son, my chosen one. Listen to him.
local: Ba ma ni kwǝrǝ na ŋɔɔnɩ kunkwǝǝnu tɩm wʋnɩ ka wɩ: «Wʋntʋ mʋ yɩ amʋ Bu wʋm a na kuri tɩn. Á nan taá cǝgi o taanɩ lanyɩranɩ.».

eng: Even as the voice spoke, Jesus was found alone. They were speechless and at the time told no one what they had seen.
local: Kwǝrǝ kam na ŋɔɔnɩ ka ti tɩn, ba ma daa wʋ nɛ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ ba wǝli Zezi nɩ. Da yam kʋntʋ ni nɩ ba ma cɩm, yɩ ba wʋ tagɩ kʋlʋ ba na nɛ tɩn ba brɩ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ.

eng: The next day, when Jesus, Peter, John, and James had come down from the mountain, a large crowd met Jesus.
local: Tɩga na pʋʋrɩ tɩn, Zezi ma tu piu kʋm yuu nɩ dɩ o karabiǝ bam. Nɔn-kɔgɔ zanzan ma jeer-o.

eng: A man from the crowd shouted, Teacher, I beg you to take a look at my son, my only child.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ maa tɔgɩ o wʋ kɔgɔ kʋm wʋnɩ. O ma ta dɩ kwǝr-dɩa o wɩ: «Karanyɩna, popo, ba n nii a bu baŋa nɩ. Mʋ amʋ bu dɩdʋa, yɩ cicirǝ laan jɩg-o.

eng: Look, a spirit seizes him and, without any warning, he screams. It shakes him and causes him to foam at the mouth. It tortures him and rarely leaves him alone.
local: Maŋa kam cicirǝ kam na yǝni ka zaŋ-o tɩn, o da o kaasɩ mʋ, yɩ o yɩra saɩ pɔgɩ pɔgɩ, yɩ lile-puŋǝ tɔgɩ o ni ka nuŋǝ. Ka ma tiini ka yaar-o maŋa maama, yɩ ka na yǝni ka zaŋ-o, ka ba nuŋi o yɩra nɩ mwalɩ mwalɩ.

eng: I begged your disciples to throw it out, but they couldnt.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ a loori nmʋ karabiǝ bam sɩ ba zǝli-ka o yɩra nɩ, yɩ ba wʋ wanɩ.».

eng: Jesus answered, You faithless and crooked generation, how long will I be with you and put up with you? Bring your son here.
local: Zezi ma lǝri o wɩ: «Abam zɩm nɔɔna bam brɩ á ba jɩgɩ wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ amʋ, yɩ á ba kɩ lanyɩranɩ. Amʋ wʋ́ daanɩ abam tɩtarɩ nɩ a kɩ wʋ-zuru dɩ abam sɩ kʋ taa ve maŋa kɔɔ?» O laan ma ta dɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋm o wɩ: «Ja n bu wʋm n ba.».

eng: While he was coming, the demon threw him down and shook him violently. Jesus spoke harshly to the unclean spirit, healed the child, and gave him back to his father.
local: Bu wʋm na maa ve o te kʋntʋ tɩn, cicirǝ kam daa ta ma dʋl-o ka dɩ tɩga nɩ, yɩ ka paɩ o yɩra saɩ pɔgɩ pɔgɩ. Zezi ma bagɩ cicirǝ kam o pa ka yag-o. O ma pa bu wʋm na yazurǝ. O laan ma joori o kɩ-o o ko wʋm jɩŋa nɩ.

eng: Everyone was overwhelmed by Gods greatness. While everyone was marveling at everything he was doing, Jesus said to his disciples.
local: Nɔɔna bam maama ma na Wɛ dam dɩm na gaalɩ te, yɩ kʋ sʋ-ba zanzan. Nɔɔna maama dɛɛn yɩ yǝǝu dɩ kʋlʋ maama Zezi na kɩ tɩn. O laan ma ta dɩ o karabiǝ bam o wɩ:.

eng: Take these words to heart: the Human Oneis about to be delivered into human hands.
local: «Cǝgi-na taanɩ dɩlʋ a na lagɩ a ta dɩ abam tɩn, sɩ á joŋi-dɩ lanyɩranɩ. Baá ja amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm ba kɩ nɔɔna jɩŋa nɩ.».

eng: They didnt understand this statement. Its meaning was hidden from them so they couldnt grasp it. And they were afraid to ask him about it.
local: Ba ma wʋ ni o taanɩ dɩm kuri. Dɩ kuri dɩm sǝgi mʋ dɩ ba, yɩ ba wʋ wanɩ ba joŋi-dɩ. Ba ma kwarɩ sɩ ba bwe-o ba nii.

eng: An argument arose among the disciples about which of them was the greatest.
local: Kantɔgɔ dɛɛn ma ba Zezi karabiǝ bam wʋ. Ba dɛɛn lagɩ sɩ ba lwarɩ, bantʋ wʋnɩ wɔɔ mʋ wʋ́ ta yɩ nɔn-kamunu o dwe ba maama.

eng: Aware of their deepest thoughts, Jesus took a little child and had the child stand beside him.
local: Zezi ma lwarɩ ba na jɩgɩ kʋlʋ ba wʋbʋŋa nɩ tɩn. O ma ja bu balaŋa o pa o zɩgɩ o tee nɩ.

eng: Jesus said to his disciples, Whoever welcomes this child in my name welcomes me. Whoever welcomes me, welcomes the one who sent me. Whoever is least among you all is the greatest.
local: O laan ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Wʋlʋ maama na nɩgɩ bu wʋntʋ doŋ amʋ yɩrɩ ŋwaanɩ tɩn, kʋntʋ tu nɩgɩ amʋ mʋ. Wʋlʋ maama nan na nɩgɩ amʋ tɩn, kʋ yɩ o nɩgɩ Baŋa-Wɛ dɩlʋ na tʋŋɩ-nɩ tɩn dɩ mʋ. Wʋlʋ nan na yɩ balaŋa abam maama wʋnɩ tɩn mʋ yɩ nɔn-kamunu cɩga cɩga.».

eng: John replied, Master, we saw someone throwing demons out in your name, and we tried to stop him because he isnt in our group of followers.
local: Zan ma ta o wɩ: «Dɩ́ Yuutu, dɩ́ ya nɛ nɔɔnʋ na zǝli ciciri nɔɔna yɩra nɩ dɩ nmʋ yɩrɩ. O nan na daɩ dɩbam kɔgɔ kʋm wʋ nɔɔnʋ tɩn, dɩ́ ma kwaanɩ sɩ dɩ́ cɩ-o, sɩ o daa yɩ taa kɩ kʋntʋ.».

eng: But Jesus replied, Dont stop him, because whoever isnt against you is for you.
local: Zezi ma lǝri o wɩ: «Á ya wʋ maŋɩ sɩ á cɩ-o. Wʋlʋ maama na ba culi abam tɩn yɩ abam nɔɔnʋ mʋ.».

eng: As the time approached when Jesus was to be taken up into heaven, he determined to go to Jerusalem.
local: Maŋa kam dɛɛn ma ba ka twɛ sɩ Wɛ kwe Zezi DƖ ja di DƖ sɔŋɔ. Zezi ma lɩ bʋŋa sɩ o yɔɔrɩ o kwe cwǝŋǝ o vu Zeruzalɛm.

eng: He sent messengers on ahead of him. Along the way, they entered a Samaritan village to prepare for his arrival.
local: O ma tʋŋɩ nɔɔna sɩ ba da o yigǝ ba vu. Ba ma vu ba yi Samari tiinǝ tɩʋ kʋdoŋ, sɩ ba ti wǝǝnu maama yigǝ sɩ o laan ba.

eng: but the Samaritan villagers refused to welcome him because he was determined to go to Jerusalem.
local: Tɩʋ kʋm nɔɔna bam ma wʋ sɛ sɩ ba jeer-o, ba na lwarɩ dɩ o maa ve Zeruzalɛm tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: When the disciples James and John saw this, they said, Lord, do you want us to call fire down from heaven to consume them.
local: O karabiǝ bam bale Zakɩ dɩ Zan na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma ta ba wɩ: «Dɩ́ Yuutu, n lagɩ sɩ dɩ́ pa mini nuŋi wɛyuu nɩ dɩ ba dɩ cɔgɩ-ba na?».

eng: But Jesus turned and spoke sternly to them.
local: Zezi ma pipiri o nii-ba, yɩ o bagɩ-ba o wɩ: «Abam yǝri joro kʋlʋ na jɩgɩ abam tɩn.

eng: and they went on to another village.
local: Amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm wʋ tu sɩ a cɔgɩ nɔɔna ŋwɩa, a tu sɩ a vrɩ-ba lwarɩm wʋnɩ mʋ.» Ba daa ma yɔɔrɩ tɩʋ kʋdoŋ ba vu.

eng: As Jesus and his disciples traveled along the road, someone said to him, I will follow you wherever you go.
local: Dɛ dɩdwɩ ba na wʋ cwǝŋǝ nɩ ba maa kɛ tɩn, nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ ma ta dɩ Zezi o wɩ: «Nmʋ na lagɩ n vu je sɩlʋ maama tɩn, aá da a vu.».

eng: Jesus replied, Foxes have dens and the birds in the sky have nests, but the Human Onehas no place to lay his head.
local: Zezi ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «Kʋ na yɩ yirǝ, bantʋ jɩgɩ bɔɔna ba tigi da. Zunǝ dɩ jɩgɩ pwǝru ba tigi da. Kʋ daarɩ amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm ba jɩgɩ jǝgǝ sɩ a pǝni da a sin.».

eng: Then Jesus said to someone else, Follow me. He replied, Lord, first let me go and bury my father.
local: Zezi daa ma ta dɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ o wɩ: «Zaŋɩ n ta n tɔgɩ-nɩ.» Nɔɔnʋ wʋm ma ta o wɩ: «A Yuutu, yagɩ sɩ a vu a kɩ a ko luǝ, sɩ a laan ba a tɔgɩ nmʋ.».

eng: Jesus said to him, Let the dead bury their own dead. But you go and spread the news of Gods kingdom.
local: Zezi ma lǝri o wɩ: «Nan yagɩ, sɩ balʋ na ba jɩgɩ ŋwɩa Wɛ tee nɩ tɩn taa kɩ ba lwi, sɩ nmʋ daarɩ n vu n tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝrǝ kam n brɩ DƖ na lagɩ DƖ di paarɩ tɩte nɔɔna tɩtarɩ nɩ tɩn.».

eng: Someone else said to Jesus, I will follow you, Lord, but first let me say good-bye to those in my house.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ dɩ ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «A Yuutu, aá ba a taa tɔgɩ nmʋ. Nan pa-nɩ cwǝŋǝ sɩ a vu a banɩ a sɔŋɔ tiinǝ, sɩ a laan joori a ba.».

eng: Jesus said to him, No one who puts a hand on the plow and looks back is fit for Gods kingdom.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na jɩgɩ naan-vɔrɔ o maa vara yɩ o pipiri o nii o kwaga nɩ tɩn, kʋ brɩ sɩ kʋntʋ tu wʋbʋŋa pɔɔrɩ bɩle mʋ. Nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ doŋ bá wanɩ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm tɩtʋŋa o tʋŋɩ.».

eng: All the tax collectors and sinners were gathering around Jesus to listen to him.
local: Dɛ dɩdwɩ lampo-joŋnǝ dɩ nɔn-cɩcɔgɩrʋ zanzan ma fɔɔrɩ ba yi Zezi te, sɩ ba taa cǝgi o taanɩ dɩm.

eng: The Pharisees and legal experts were grumbling, saying, This man welcomes sinners and eats with them.
local: Farizɩan tiinǝ dɩ Wɛ cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ na nɛ kʋntʋ tɩn, ba maa pʋʋna ba wɩ: «Taá nii nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ na je dɩ nɔn-cɩcɔgɩrʋ, yɩ o ta kwǝri o di wʋdiu dɩ ba.».

eng: Jesus told them this parable.
local: Zezi laan ma maŋɩ mɩmaŋa o brɩ-ba o wɩ:.

eng: Suppose someone among you had one hundred sheep and lost one of them. Wouldnt he leave the other ninety-nine in the pasture and search for the lost one until he finds it?.
local: «Abam wʋlʋ na jɩgɩ peeni bi , yɩ sɩ dɩdʋa na je, o bá yagɩ peeni funʋgʋ-nʋgʋ kʋm gaa nɩ, yɩ o daarɩ o vu o beeri pi-jiǝ kam na?.

eng: And when he finds it, he is thrilled and places it on his shoulders.
local: O nan na beeri o na-ka, o laan wʋ́ kwe-ka o daŋɩ o bakala baŋa nɩ dɩ wʋpolo o ja vu sɔŋɔ.

eng: When he arrives home, he calls together his friends and neighbors, saying to them, Celebrate with me because Ive found my lost sheep.
local: O na tu o yi sɔŋɔ, oó bǝŋi o cilonnǝ dɩ o sɔŋɔ tiinǝ maama, yɩ o ta dɩ ba o wɩ: ‹Á kɩ wʋpolo dɩ amʋ, a na nɛ piǝ kalʋ ya na je tɩn ŋwaanɩ.›.

eng: In the same way, I tell you, there will be more joy in heaven over one sinner who changes both heart and life than over ninety-nine righteous people who have no need to change their hearts and lives.
local: Kʋntʋ tɩn, a lagɩ a ta abam sɩ, kʋ yɩ bɩdwɩ mʋ dɩ nɔn-cɩcɔgɔ dɩdʋa na lǝni o wʋ o yagɩ kǝm-balwaarʋ, kʋʋ́ pa wʋpolo taa daga Wɛ-sɔŋɔ nɩ. Baá ta jɩgɩ wʋpolo zanzan dɩd-o kʋ dwe nɔn-ŋʋna funʋgʋ-nʋgʋ na ba bʋŋɩ sɩ ba maŋɩ sɩ ba lǝni ba wʋrʋ Wɛ ŋwaanɩ.

eng: Or what woman, if she owns ten silver coins and loses one of them, wont light a lamp and sweep the house, searching her home carefully until she finds it?.
local: Asawɛ, kaanɩ na jɩgɩ sǝbu-dala fugǝ, yɩ dɩdʋa na je, o bá tarɩgɩ kanɩa o zuri o yɩra o zwarɩ digǝ kam wʋ maama, sɩ o nii oó na-dɩ na?.

eng: When she finds it, she calls together her friends and neighbors, saying, Celebrate with me because Ive found my lost coin.
local: O nan na nɛ-dɩ, o laan wʋ́ bǝŋi o cilonnǝ dɩ o sɔŋɔ tiinǝ maama, yɩ o ta dɩ ba o wɩ: ‹Á kɩ wʋpolo dɩ amʋ, a na nɛ sǝbu-dalɩ dɩlʋ ya na je tɩn ŋwaanɩ.›.

eng: In the same way, I tell you, joy breaks out in the presence of Gods angels over one sinner who changes both heart and life.
local: Kʋntʋ tɩn, a lagɩ a ta abam sɩ, kʋ yɩ bɩdwɩ mʋ dɩ nɔn-cɩcɔgɔ dɩdʋa na lǝni o wʋ o yagɩ kǝm-balwaarʋ, kʋʋ́ pa wʋpolo kʋntʋ doŋ taa wʋ Wɛ malɛsɩ sɩm tee nɩ.».

eng: Jesus said, A certain man had two sons.
local: Zezi daa ta ma ta o wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ mʋ wʋra o na jɩgɩ bǝkǝri sɩle.

eng: The younger son said to his father, Father, give me my share of the inheritance. Then the father divided his estate between them.
local: Dɛ dɩdwɩ nyaanɩ wʋm ma zaŋɩ o ta dɩ o ko o wɩ: ‹A ko, a loori-m sɩ n maŋɩ a tǝri nmʋ wǝǝnu tɩm wʋnɩ n pa-nɩ›. O ko ma sɛ. O ma maŋɩ o jɩjɩgɩrʋ tɩm o pa-ba.

eng: Soon afterward, the younger son gathered everything together and took a trip to a land far away. There, he wasted his wealth through extravagant living.
local: Da fɩnfɩɩn na kɛ tɩn, nyaanɩ wʋm ma zaŋɩ o kwalɩmɩ o wǝǝnu maama o ja nuŋi o vu tɩʋ kʋdoŋ yigǝ yigǝ. O maa zʋʋrɩ da, yɩ o kɩ paweeni yɩranɩ. O maa jɩgɩ o wǝǝnu tɩm o cɔgɩ yɔɔ yɔɔ.

eng: When he had used up his resources, a severe food shortage arose in that country and he began to be in need.
local: O laan ma ba o cɔgɩ o wǝǝnu tɩm o ti maama. Kana ma tiini ka zʋ tɩʋ kʋm, yɩ o daa ba jɩgɩ kʋlʋkʋlʋ.

eng: He hired himself out to one of the citizens of that country, who sent him into his fields to feed pigs.
local: O ma vu tɩʋ kʋm nɔɔnʋ dɩdʋa te, o loor-o sɩ o wǝl-o. Nɔɔnʋ wʋm ma pa o vu o tɔgɩ o tǝrǝ gaa nɩ.

eng: He longed to eat his fill from what the pigs ate, but no one gave him anything.
local: Kana na tiini ka cɛ o yɩra nɩ tɩn, o ma bʋŋɩ sɩ o tɔgɩ o di tǝrǝ yam wʋdiu kʋm, yɩ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ ba pa-o-kʋ.

eng: When he came to his senses, he said, How many of my fathers hired hands have more than enough food, but Im starving to death!.
local: O wʋbʋŋa laan ma joori. O ma bwɛ o tɩtɩ nɩ o wɩ: A ko yǝni o paɩ o tɩntʋŋna wʋdiu zanzan yɩ ba ba di ba tiǝ, yɩ a laan wʋ yo seeni kana lagɩ ka gʋ-nɩ.

eng: I will get up and go to my father, and say to him,Father, I have sinned against heaven and against you.
local: Aá zaŋɩ a joori a vu a ko wʋm te mʋ, sɩ a ta dɩd-o nɩ: A ko, a kɩ a cɔgɩ Wɛ yigǝ, yɩ a kɩ a cɔgɩ nmʋ dɩ wʋ.

eng: I no longer deserve to be called your son. Take me on as one of your hired hands.
local: Kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ daa wʋ maŋɩ sɩ n kɩ-nɩ nɩ nmʋ bu te. Nan pa a taa tɔgɩ a yɩ nmʋ tɩntʋŋna bam dɩdʋa.

eng: So he got up and went to his father. While he was still a long way off, his father saw him and was moved with compassion. His father ran to him, hugged him, and kissed him.
local: O na bʋŋɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, o laan ma zaŋɩ o maa ve o ko wʋm te. O ta na wʋ yigǝ yigǝ tɩn, mʋ o ko wʋm tʋlɩ o na-o. O na nɛ-o tɩn, o ŋwaŋa ma zʋ-o. O ma zaŋɩ o duri o vu o jeeri o bu wʋm, o kukwǝr-o dɩ wʋpolo zanzan.

eng: Then his son said, Father, I have sinned against heaven and against you. I no longer deserve to be called your son.
local: Bu wʋm laan ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: A ko, a kɩ a cɔgɩ Wɛ yigǝ, yɩ a kɩ a cɔgɩ nmʋ dɩ wʋ. Kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ daa wʋ maŋɩ sɩ n kɩ-nɩ nɩ n bu te.
