eng: But the father said to his servants, Quickly, bring out the best robe and put it on him! Put a ring on his finger and sandals on his feet!.
local: O ko wʋm ma ta dɩ o tɩntʋŋna bam o wɩ: Á kɩ lɩla á zʋ á kwe gɔrɔ kʋlʋ na lana kʋ dwe tɩ maama tɩn, á ja ba á zʋ-o. Kwe-na jafʋlɩ á kɩ o jɩŋa nɩ, sɩ á kwe natra á pa o zʋ.

eng: Fetch the fattened calf and slaughter it. We must celebrate with feasting.
local: Ve-na á ja na-paalɩ kamunu kʋm á ja ba á gʋ, sɩ dɩ́ ma kɩ wʋdiu dɩ́ di candiǝ dɩ wʋpolo.

eng: because this son of mine was dead and has come back to life! He was lost and is found! And they began to celebrate.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ a bu wʋntʋ ya tɩgɩ mʋ, yɩ o joori o bi. O ya je mʋ, yɩ a joori a na-o. Ba ma puli wʋdiiru tɩm dim yɩ ba kwɛɛra dɩ wʋpolo.

eng: Now his older son was in the field. Coming in from the field, he approached the house and heard music and dancing.
local: Maŋa kalʋ nyaanɩ wʋm na tu o yi sɔŋɔ tɩn, o zɩmbaarʋ wʋm ya wʋ kara nɩ mʋ o vara. O na nuŋi kara kam nɩ o maa bɩɩnɩ sɔŋɔ tɩn, mʋ o ni ba na magɩ wǝǝnu yɩ ba saɩ.

eng: He called one of the servants and asked what was going on.
local: O ma bǝŋi o ko tɩntʋŋna bam dɩdʋa o bwe-o woŋo kʋlʋ na kɩ yɩ ba kwɛɛrɩ tɩn.

eng: The servant replied, Your brother has arrived, and your father has slaughtered the fattened calf because he received his son back safe and sound.
local: Tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋm ma lǝr-o o wɩ: ‹Nmʋ nyaanɩ wʋm mʋ tuǝ. Nmʋ ko ma ja na-paalɩ kamunu kʋm o gʋ sɩ ba di candiǝ, o na nɛ-o yɩ o jɩgɩ yazurǝ tɩn ŋwaanɩ.›.

eng: Then the older son was furious and didnt want to enter in, but his father came out and begged him.
local: Bu-kwɩan kam na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, o bam ma zaŋɩ. O ma ba lagɩ sɩ o zʋ digǝ. O ko wʋm ma nuŋi o vu o koor-o sɩ o zʋ.

eng: He answered his father, Look, Ive served you all these years, and I never disobeyed your instruction. Yet youve never given me as much as a young goat so I could celebrate with my friends.
local: O ma ta dɩ o ko wʋm o wɩ: ‹Ta n niǝ, bɩna yantʋ maama amʋ tiini a tʋŋɩ zanzan a pa nmʋ. A yǝni a sɛ nmʋ ni maŋa maama. Nmʋ nan wʋ pɛ amʋ dɩ bʋbala dɩ sɩ a gʋ, sɩ amʋ dɩ a cilonnǝ ma kɩ candiǝ.

eng: But when this son of yours returned, after gobbling up your estate on prostitutes, you slaughtered the fattened calf for him.
local: Kʋ daarɩ nmʋ bu wʋntʋ na tu tɩn, n jaanɩ na-paalɩ kamunu kʋm n gʋ n pa-o, yɩ o yɩ wʋlʋ na kwe nmʋ jɩjɩgɩrʋ o vu o cɔgɩ ka-bwǝǝru kwaga nɩ yɩ o laan joori o ba tɩn.›.

eng: Then his father said, Son, you are always with me, and everything I have is yours.
local: O ko wʋm ma lǝr-o o wɩ: ‹A bu, nmʋ wʋ amʋ tee nɩ mʋ maŋa maama. Wǝǝnu tɩlʋ maama amʋ na jɩgɩ tɩn yɩ nmʋ nyɩm mʋ.

eng: But we had to celebrate and be glad because this brother of yours was dead and is alive. He was lost and is found.
local: Dɩ́ nan maŋɩ sɩ dɩ́ kɩ wʋpolo candiǝ mʋ zɩm, bɛŋwaanɩ nmʋ nyaanɩ wʋm ya tɩgɩ mʋ, yɩ o joori o bi. O ya je mʋ, yɩ dɩ́ joori dɩ́ na-o.› ».

eng: Jesus also said to the disciples, A certain rich man heard that his household manager was wasting his estate.
local: Zezi daa ta ma ta dɩ o karabiǝ bam o wɩ: «Nadum mʋ wʋra o na jɩgɩ tɩntʋŋnʋ. O tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋm mʋ nii o wǝǝnu maama baŋa nɩ. Nɔɔna badonnǝ ma vu ba ta dɩ nadum wʋm ba wɩ, o tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋm jɩgɩ o wǝǝnu tɩm o cɔga mʋ.

eng: He called the manager in and said to him, What is this I hear about you? Give me a report of your administration because you can no longer serve as my manager.
local: O ma bǝŋi o tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋm, yɩ o ta dɩd-o o wɩ: ‹A ni nmʋ na kɩ te tɩn. Kɔɔ mʋ n nan wʋ́ ta dɩ amʋ? Nan ve n jeeli nmʋ na kɩ te dɩ a wǝǝnu tɩm tɩn, sɩ n ba n ta n brɩ-nɩ. Nmʋ daa n bá wanɩ n ta n yɩ a tɩntʋŋnʋ.›.

eng: The household manager said to himself, What will I do now that my master is firing me as his manager? Im not strong enough to dig and too proud to beg.
local: Tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋm ma bwɛ o tɩtɩ nɩ o wɩ: ‹Amʋ nan wʋ́ kɩ ta mʋ? A tu wʋm lagɩ o lɩ-nɩ o tɩtʋŋa yam nɩ. Amʋ ba dana sɩ a wanɩ a va. Cavɩɩra maa jɩgɩ-nɩ sɩ a taa tʋlɩ a loorǝ.

eng: I know what Ill do so that, when I am removed from my management position, people will welcome me into their houses.
local: A nan ye a na wʋ́ kɩ te, sɩ o na lɩ-nɩ o tɩtʋŋɩ dɩm nɩ, sɩ nɔɔna taa joŋi-nɩ lanyɩranɩ ba sam nɩ tɩn.›.

eng: One by one, the manager sent for each person who owed his master money. He said to the first, How much do you owe my master.
local: O na bʋŋɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma bǝŋi balʋ na jɩgɩ o tu wʋm jɩna tɩn dɩdʋa dɩdʋa o pa ba ba o te. O ma bwe dayigǝ tu wʋm o wɩ: ‹Nmʋ jɩgɩ a tu wʋm jɩnɩ kʋ maɩ tɩta mʋ?›.

eng: He said, Nine hundred gallons of olive oil.The manager said to him, Take your contract, sit down quickly, and write four hundred fifty gallons.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋm ma lǝri o wɩ: ‹Kʋ yɩ nugǝ kʋnkwaanʋ bi mʋ.› Tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋm ma ta-o o wɩ: ‹Nii tɔnɔ kʋlʋ nmʋ jɩnɩ dɩm na pʋpʋnɩ da tɩn. Nan kɩ lɩla n kwe n ja n vu n jǝni n pʋpʋnɩ nɩ kʋnkwaanʋ fiinnu .›.

eng: Then the manager said to another, How much do you owe? He said, One thousand bushels of wheat.He said, Take your contract and write eight hundred.
local: O daa ta ma bwe nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ o wɩ: ‹Nmʋ jɩgɩ a tu wʋm jɩnɩ kʋ maɩ tɩta mʋ?› O ma lǝri o wɩ: ‹Kʋ yɩ mɩna ywǝǝlu bi mʋ.› Tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋm ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: ‹Kwe tɔnɔ kʋm n pʋpʋnɩ nɩ ywǝǝlu funɔnɔ .›.

eng: The master commended the dishonest manager because he acted cleverly. People who belong to this world are more clever in dealing with their peers than are people who belong to the light.
local: Nadum wʋm na lwarɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, o ta tee tɩtʋŋ-balɔrɔ kʋm dɩ o na kɩ te tɩn, bɛŋwaanɩ lʋgʋ baŋa tiinǝ jɩgɩ swan ba ye ba na wʋ́ kɩ te sɩ ba ma wǝli daanɩ ba dwe balʋ na lwarɩ Wɛ pooni dɩm tɩn.

eng: I tell you, use worldly wealth to make friends for yourselves so that when its gone, you will be welcomed into the eternal homes.
local: A lagɩ a ta abam sɩ, á maŋɩ sɩ á kwe lʋgʋ baŋa sǝbu á ma wǝli nɔɔna sɩ kʋ pa á di ciloŋo daanɩ. Á na kɩ kʋntʋ, yɩ sǝbu kʋm na ti, ba jwa wʋ́ jeeri abam lanyɩranɩ Wɛ-sɔŋɔ nɩ, sɩ á taá wʋra maŋa kalʋ na bá ti tɩn.».

eng: Whoever is faithful with little is also faithful with much, and the one who is dishonest with little is also dishonest with much.
local: «Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na tɔgɩ cɩga dɩ wo-balaŋa tɩn wʋ́ ta tɔgɩ cɩga dɩ wo-kamunu dɩ. Kʋ daarɩ wʋlʋ na ba tɔgɩ cɩga dɩ wo-balaŋa tɩn bá wanɩ o tɔgɩ cɩga dɩ wo-kamunu dɩ.

eng: If you havent been faithful with worldly wealth, who will trust you with true riches?.
local: Kʋntʋ tɩn, abam na warɩ á tɔgɩ cɩga dɩ lʋgʋ baŋa sǝbu, Wɛ bá sɛ sɩ DƖ kwe jɩjɩgɩrʋ cɩga cɩga DƖ kɩ abam jɩŋa nɩ.

eng: If you havent been faithful with someone elses property, who will give you your own?.
local: Abam na warɩ á tɔgɩ cɩga dɩ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ ba na kwe ba kɩ abam jɩŋa nɩ sɩ á nii tɩ baŋa nɩ tɩn, Wɛ bá sɛ sɩ DƖ pa abam wǝǝnu tɩlʋ na wʋ́ ta yɩ abam tɩtɩ nyɩm tɩn.

eng: No household servant can serve two masters. Either you will hate the one and love the other, or you will be loyal to the one and have contempt for the other. You cannot serve God and wealth.
local: Tɩntʋŋnʋ bá wanɩ o kwe wʋbʋŋ-dɩdwɩ sɩ o ma tʋŋɩ o pa o yuutiinǝ bale. Oó ta culi dɩdʋa kam yɩ o daarɩ o soe wʋdoŋ wʋm, naa oó ta nɩgɩ dɩdʋa kam yɩ o daarɩ o gooni wʋdoŋ wʋm. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ abam bá wanɩ á pa sɩ Wɛ taa yɩ á yuutu, sɩ á daarɩ á pa sǝbu dɩ taa yɩ á yuutu.».

eng: The Pharisees, who were money-lovers, heard all this and sneered at Jesus.
local: Farizɩan tiinǝ bam na tɔgɩ ba wʋra tɩn ma ni Zezi na tagɩ kʋlʋ maama tɩn. Ba na yɩ sǝbu-swǝn-nyɩna tɩn, ba maa jɩg-o ba mwana.

eng: He said to them, You are the ones who justify yourselves before other people, but God knows your hearts. What is highly valued by people is deeply offensive to God.
local: O ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Abam mʋ lagɩ sɩ á brɩ á tɩtɩ nɔɔna yigǝ nɩ, nɩ á yɩ nɔn-ŋʋna. Wɛ nan ye á wʋbʋŋa na yɩ te tɩn. Woŋo kʋlʋ nɔɔna na paɩ kʋ yɩ wo-kamunu tɩn, kʋ yɩ wo-zɔɔnɩ mʋ Wɛ yigǝ nɩ.

eng: Until John, there was only the Law and the Prophets. Since then, the good news of Gods kingdom is preached, and everyone is urged to enter it.
local: Nɔɔna dɛɛn jɩgɩ Moyisi cullu tɩm dɩ faŋa faŋa Wɛ nijoŋnǝ bam na brɩ kʋlʋ tɩn mʋ ba tɔga, yɩ kʋ vu kʋ yi maŋa kalʋ Zan wʋlʋ na miisi nɔɔna na wʋnɩ tɩn laan ba. O na tu tɩn, kʋ laan yɩ Wɛ paarɩ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam mʋ wʋra, yɩ nɔɔna maama tiini ba kwaanɩ sɩ ba tɔgɩ ba zʋ dɩ wʋ.

eng: Its easier for heaven and earth to pass away than for the smallest stroke of a pen in the Law to drop out.
local: Dɩ kʋ dɩ, kʋ yɩ mwalɩ sɩ wɛyuu dɩ tɩga je kʋ dwe woŋo fɩnfɩɩn na lagɩ kʋ lɩ Wɛ cullu tɩm wʋnɩ.

eng: Any man who divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery, and a man who marries a woman divorced from her husband commits adultery.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na yagɩ o kaanɩ yɩ o daarɩ o di kaanɩ wʋdoŋ, kʋntʋ tu kǝm dɩm yɩ balɔrɔ nɩnɛɛnɩ nɔɔnʋ na pǝni dɩ o doŋ kaanɩ te tɩn mʋ. Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na zaŋɩ o di kaanɩ wʋlʋ o tɩtɩ barʋ na yag-o tɩn, kʋntʋ tu kǝm dɩm dɩ yɩ balɔrɔ nɩnɛɛnɩ nɔɔnʋ na pǝni dɩ o doŋ kaanɩ te tɩn mʋ.».

eng: There was a certain rich man who clothed himself in purple and fine linen, and who feasted luxuriously every day.
local: Zezi ta ma ta o wɩ: «Nadum mʋ wʋra. O maa yǝni o zʋ gwar-ŋʋnnʋ tɩlʋ na tiini tɩ lana tɩn. O jɩgɩ woŋo maama kʋ yi-o, yɩ o ŋwɩa lana dɛ maama.

eng: At his gate lay a certain poor man named Lazarus who was covered with sores.
local: Yinigǝ tu dɩ mʋ wʋra, o yɩrɩ mʋ Lazaarɩ. O yǝni o tigi nadum wʋm sɔŋɔ mancoŋo nɩ. O yɩra maama yɩ ŋwana.

eng: Lazarus longed to eat the crumbs that fell from the rich mans table. Instead, dogs would come and lick his sores.
local: O maa lagɩ sɩ o taa di wʋdiu kʋlʋ nadum wʋm na di yɩ kʋ siiri tɩga nɩ tɩn, yɩ o ba na-kʋ o di. Kakuri dɩ ma tui sɩ dǝlimi o ŋwana yam.

eng: The poor man died and was carried by angels to Abrahams side. The rich man also died and was buried.
local: Yinigǝ tu wʋm laan ma ba o tɩ. Wɛ malɛsɩ ma ba sɩ kwe-o sɩ ja vu Wɛ-sɔŋɔ sɩ pa o jǝni Abraham tɩkǝri nɩ. Nadum wʋm dɩ ma tɩ. Ba ma kwe-o ba kɩ.

eng: While being tormented in the place of the dead, he looked up and saw Abraham at a distance with Lazarus at his side.
local: O ma vu curu. O maa wʋra o yaara zanzan. O ma kwǝni o yuu wɛɛnɩ o na Abraham na wʋ yigǝ yigǝ, yɩ Lazaarɩ je o tɩkǝri nɩ.

eng: He shouted, Father Abraham, have mercy on me. Send Lazarus to dip the tip of his finger in water and cool my tongue, because Im suffering in this flame.
local: O ma kaasɩ o ta dɩd-o o wɩ: ‹A ko Abraham, popo, duri a ŋwaŋa. A loori-m sɩ n pa Lazaarɩ vu o ma o nʋa o laŋɩ na o ba o ma zuri a dindǝlimi, sɩ a tiini a yaarɩ zanzan mini dɩm wʋnɩ.›.

eng: But Abraham said, Child, remember that during your lifetime you received good things, whereas Lazarus received terrible things. Now Lazarus is being comforted and you are in great pain.
local: Abraham ma lǝr-o o wɩ: ‹A bu, nan guli nmʋ dɛɛn na ŋwɩ lʋgʋ baŋa nɩ yɩ n na wo-laarʋ tɩlʋ maama n na lagɩ tɩn. Lazaarɩ dɛɛn nan jɩgɩ yaara yɩranɩ mʋ. Kʋ daarɩ lele kʋntʋ, wʋm bɩcarɩ laan zuri, yɩ nmʋ dɩ daa wʋ yaara wʋnɩ.

eng: Moreover, a great crevasse has been fixed between us and you. Those who wish to cross over from here to you cannot. Neither can anyone cross from there to us.
local: Woŋo kʋlʋ na wǝli da tɩn, goŋ-canluŋu mʋ cɩgɩ dɩbam dɩ abam laŋa nɩ, yɩ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ bá wanɩ o zɩgɩ yo o vu o bɛ o yi abam te. Nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ dɩ bá wanɩ o zɩgɩ abam tee nɩ o bɛ o ba dɩbam te.›.

eng: The rich man said, Then I beg you, Father, send Lazarus to my fathers house.
local: Nadum wʋm ma ta o wɩ: ‹A ko, kʋ nan na yɩ kʋntʋ, a loori-m sɩ n tʋŋɩ Lazaarɩ sɩ o vu a ko sɔŋɔ.

eng: I have five brothers. He needs to warn them so that they dont come to this place of agony.
local: sɩ a nyaana banu wʋra, sɩ o kaanɩ-ba, sɩ ba dɩ daa yɩ tɔgɩ ba ba yaara jǝgǝ kantʋ.›.

eng: Abraham replied, They have Moses and the Prophets. They must listen to them.
local: Abraham ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: ‹Ba maŋɩ ba jɩgɩ twaanʋ tɩlʋ Moyisi dɩ Wɛ nijoŋnǝ bam na pʋpʋnɩ tɩn. Ba nan maŋɩ sɩ ba sɛ twaanʋ tɩntʋ taanɩ dɩm mʋ.›.

eng: The rich man said, No, Father Abraham! But if someone from the dead goes to them, they will change their hearts and lives.
local: O ma lǝri o wɩ: ‹A ko Abraham, ba bá sɛ kʋntʋ. Nɔɔnʋ nan na bi o yagɩ tʋʋnɩ yɩ o vu ba te o ta dɩ ba, ba laan wʋ́ lǝni ba wʋrʋ ba yagɩ kǝm-balwaarʋ tɩm.›.

eng: Abraham said, If they dont listen to Moses and the Prophets, then neither will they be persuaded if someone rises from the dead.
local: Abraham ma lǝri nadum wʋm o wɩ: ‹Ba na wʋ sɛ Moyisi dɩ Wɛ nijoŋnǝ bam maama taanɩ dɩm, nɔɔnʋ na maŋɩ o bi o yagɩ tʋʋnɩ dɩ, ba ta bá sɛ sɩ o taanɩ dɩm yɩ cɩga.› ».

eng: When a crowd of thousands upon thousands had gathered so that they were crushing each other, Jesus began to speak first to his disciples: Watch out for the yeast of the Pharisees—I mean, the mismatch between their hearts and lives.
local: Maŋa kam kʋntʋ nɩ kɔ-fɔrɔ zanzan ya lagɩ daanɩ Zezi tee nɩ. Ba dɛɛn yi nɔɔna mʋrrʋ mʋrrʋ mʋ. Ba tiini ba pɩɩnɩ daanɩ yɩ ba nɔnɩ daanɩ. Zezi ma puli o ta dɩ o karabiǝ bam o wɩ: «Á fɔgɩ á cɩ á tɩtɩ, sɩ á yɩ pa Farizɩan tiinǝ bam dɩ abam ba dabɩlɩ dɩm, dɩ ba na yɩ pipiri-nyɩna yɩ ba sǝgi ba kɩ wo-balwaarʋ te tɩn.

eng: Nothing is hidden that wont be revealed, and nothing is secret that wont be brought out into the open.
local: Kʋlʋ maama na sǝgi nɔɔna tee nɩ tɩn, kʋ jwa wʋ́ ba kʋ lwarɩ jaja.

eng: Therefore, whatever you have said in the darkness will be heard in the light, and whatever you have whispered in the rooms deep inside the house will be announced from the rooftops.
local: Kʋntʋ tɩn, abam na sǝgi á ta kʋlʋ maama tɩn wʋ́ nuŋi pooni mʋ sɩ nɔɔna maama ni. Abam na waasɩ woŋo kʋlʋ digǝ wʋnɩ tɩn, nɔɔna wʋ́ di nayuu ba tɔɔlɩ-kʋ.

eng: I tell you, my friends, dont be terrified by those who can kill the body but after that can do nothing more.
local: A cilonnǝ-ba, a lagɩ a ta abam sɩ á yɩ taá kwarɩ fʋʋnɩ dɩ balʋ na waɩ ba gʋɩ á yɩra yam yɩ ba daa warɩ ba kɩ abam kʋlʋkʋlʋ ba wǝli da tɩn.

eng: Ill show you whom you should fear: fear the one who, after you have been killed, has the authority to throw you into hell. Indeed, I tell you, thats the one you should fear.
local: A nan wʋ́ brɩ abam á na maŋɩ sɩ á taá kwarɩ wʋlʋ tɩn. Á maŋɩ sɩ á taá kwarɩ Wɛ mʋ, bɛŋwaanɩ dɩntʋ jɩgɩ dam sɩ DƖ gʋ abam sɩ DƖ daarɩ DƖ dɩ abam min-tɩʋ nɩ. A lagɩ a ta abam sɩ dɩntʋ mʋ á maŋɩ sɩ á taá kwarɩ.

eng: Arent five sparrows sold for two small coins?Yet not one of them is overlooked by God.
local: Kʋ na yɩ kabǝmbǝli, ba yǝni ba yǝgi sɩntʋ mwalɩ mwalɩ mʋ. Wɛ nan ba swe sɩ dɩdʋa dɩdʋa ni nɩ.

eng: Even the hairs on your head are all counted. Dont be afraid. You are worth more than many sparrows.
local: Wɛ maŋɩ DƖ ye abam yuuywe sɩm ni na maɩ te tɩn. Á yɩ taá kwarɩ fʋʋnɩ, sɩ abam dɩdʋa dɩdʋa jɩgɩ kuri á dwe kabǝmbǝli zanzan.

eng: I tell you, everyone who acknowledges me before humans, the Human Onewill acknowledge before Gods angels.
local: A lagɩ a ta abam sɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ maama na zɩgɩ nabiinǝ yigǝ nɩ o brɩ jaja sɩ o yɩ a nɔɔnʋ tɩn, amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm dɩ wʋ́ zɩgɩ kʋntʋ tu kwaga nɩ a brɩ nɩ o yɩ a nɔɔnʋ Wɛ malɛsɩ sɩm yigǝ nɩ.

eng: But the one who rejects me before others will be rejected before Gods angels.
local: Kʋ daarɩ, wʋlʋ maama na vɩn amʋ nabiinǝ yigǝ nɩ tɩn, amʋ dɩ wʋ́ vɩn kʋntʋ tu Wɛ malɛsɩ sɩm yigǝ nɩ.

eng: Anyone who speaks a word against the Human Onewill be forgiven, but whoever insults the Holy Spirit wont be forgiven.
local: Wʋlʋ maama na ŋɔɔnɩ wo-balwaarʋ o pa amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm tɩn, Wɛ wʋ́ sɛ sɩ DƖ ma cɛ kʋntʋ tu. Wʋlʋ nan na ŋɔɔnɩ o twɩ Wɛ Joro kʋm tɩn, Wɛ bá sɛ sɩ DƖ ma cɛ-o.

eng: When they bring you before the synagogues, rulers, and authorities, dont worry about how to defend yourself or what you should say.
local: Nɔɔna wʋ́ ja abam ba vu ba saŋɩ Zwifǝ nakwa tee nɩ dɩ tɩʋ kʋm yigǝ tiinǝ tee nɩ dɩ dɩdɛɛra tee nɩ. Ba na kɩ abam kʋntʋ, sɩ á yɩ taá liǝ dɩ á na wʋ́ ŋɔɔnɩ kʋlʋ sɩ á ma vrɩ á tɩtɩ, naa á na wʋ́ tʋlɩ taanɩ dɩm te tɩn.

eng: The Holy Spirit will tell you at that very moment what you must say.
local: Kantʋ maŋa kam nɩ Wɛ Joro kʋm wʋ́ pa á lwarɩ á na maŋɩ sɩ á ta kʋlʋ tɩn.».

eng: Someone from the crowd said to him, Teacher, tell my brother to divide the inheritance with me.
local: Nɔɔnʋ dɩdʋa mʋ tɔgɩ o wʋ kɔgɔ kʋm wʋnɩ, o ma ta dɩ Zezi o wɩ: «Karanyɩna, a loori-m sɩ n ta a zɩmbaarʋ wʋm, sɩ o kwe wǝǝnu tɩlʋ dɩbam ko na tɩgɩ o daarɩ tɩn o maŋɩ amʋ nyɩm o pa-nɩ.».

eng: Jesus said to him, Man, who appointed me as judge or referee between you and your brother.
local: Zezi ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «A ciloŋ. Wɔɔ mʋ tiŋi amʋ sɩ a di abam taanɩ naa sɩ a maŋɩ á jɩjɩgɩrʋ a pa abam?».

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Watch out! Guard yourself against all kinds of greed. After all, ones life isnt determined by ones possessions, even when someone is very wealthy.
local: O laan ma ta dɩ ba maama o wɩ: «Á taá yɩrɩ á tɩtɩ dɩ wo-swǝnǝ zanzan. Nɔɔnʋ jɩjɩgɩrʋ na daga te, tɩ bá wanɩ o ŋwɩa tɩ yɩrɩ, o na maŋɩ o yɩ nadum te dɩ.».

eng: Then he told them a parable: A certain rich mans land produced a bountiful crop.
local: Zezi laan ma maŋɩ mɩmaŋa o brɩ-ba o wɩ: «Nadum wʋdoŋ mʋ wʋra. O maa jɩgɩ karɩ sɩ na kɩ wʋdiiru zanzan sɩ pa-o.

eng: He said to himself, What will I do? I have no place to store my harvest!.
local: O ma bʋŋɩ o wɩ: A nan wʋ́ kɩ ta mʋ? Je daa tǝrǝ sɩ a kɩ a wʋdiiru tɩm maama da.».

eng: Then he thought, Heres what Ill do. Ill tear down my barns and build bigger ones. Thats where Ill store all my grain and goods.
local: O laan ma bʋŋɩ o wɩ: «A na wʋ́ kɩ te tɩn, aá magɩ a tuli sɩm maama mʋ a dɩ tɩga nɩ, sɩ a daarɩ a lɔ tuli-zɛ, sɩ a wanɩ a pɛ a wo-vaalʋ tɩm dɩ a wǝǝnu tɩm maama a kɩ da.».

eng: Ill say to myself, You have stored up plenty of goods, enough for several years. Take it easy! Eat, drink, and enjoy yourself.
local: A laan wʋ́ ta a wʋnɩ a wɩ: «A jɩgɩ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ maama a na lagɩ tɩn sɩ kʋ taa ve bɩna kɔgɔ kɔgɔ. Liǝ daa bá ja-nɩ. Aá ta kɩ a wʋbʋŋa na lagɩ te tɩn, a di yɩ a nyɔa yɩ a kɩ wʋpolo.».

eng: But God said to him, Fool, tonight you will die. Now who will get the things you have prepared for yourself.
local: Wɛ ma ta dɩd-o DƖ wɩ: «Nmʋ yɩ joro mʋ. Tɩtɩɩ dɩntʋ wʋnɩ aá joŋi n ŋwɩa. Kʋntʋ tɩn, wɔɔ mʋ wʋ́ ta te wǝǝnu tɩlʋ maama n ya na jɩgɩ tɩn?».

eng: This is the way it will be for those who hoard things for themselves and arent rich toward God.
local: «Kʋ yɩ kʋntʋ doŋ mʋ dɩ wʋlʋ maama na tiŋi jɩjɩgɩrʋ zanzan o pa o tɩtɩ yɩ o ba jɩgɩ kʋlʋkʋlʋ Wɛ tee nɩ tɩn.».

eng: Then Jesus said to his disciples, Therefore, I say to you, dont worry about your life, what you will eat, or about your body, what you will wear.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩ o karabiǝ bam o wɩ: «Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ a lagɩ a ta abam sɩ á yɩ taá jɩgɩ liǝ dɩ lʋgʋ baŋa kʋlʋkʋlʋ, kʋ na yɩ á ni-wʋdiu, naa á na wʋ́ zʋ gwaarʋ tɩlʋ tɩn.

eng: There is more to life than food and more to the body than clothing.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ á ŋwɩa garɩ wʋdiiru. Yɩra yalʋ Wɛ na pɛ abam tɩn dɩ maa garɩ gwaarʋ.

eng: Consider the ravens: they neither plant nor harvest, they have no silo or barn, yet God feeds them. You are worth so much more than birds!.
local: Nan maanɩ-na á nii bacangaarʋ na yɩ te tɩn. Tɩ ba duǝ, yɩ tɩ ba zagɩ mɩna. Tɩ ba jɩgɩ tuli dɩ tikǝri sɩ tɩ kɩ wʋdiiru da. Kʋ nan yɩ Wɛ mʋ nii tɩ baŋa nɩ dɩ tɩ ni-wʋdiu. Abam nan tiini á jɩgɩ kuri á dwe zunǝ bam.

eng: Who among you by worrying can add a single moment to your life?.
local: Abam wʋlʋwʋlʋ bá wanɩ o toŋi o ŋwɩa dɩ fɩnfɩɩn dɩ, o na maŋɩ o li te dɩ.

eng: If you cant do such a small thing, why worry about the rest?.
local: Á bá wanɩ kʋ fɩnfɩɩn dɩ á kɩ, bɛɛ nan mʋ yɩ á jɩgɩ liǝ dɩ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ maama na daarɩ tɩn?.

eng: Notice how the lilies grow. They dont wear themselves out with work, and they dont spin cloth. But I say to you that even Solomon in all his splendor wasnt dressed like one of these.
local: Maanɩ-na á nii ga-punnu tɩm lam na yɩ te tɩn. Tɩntʋ nan warɩ tɩ tʋŋɩ, yɩ tɩ warɩ gwaarʋ tɩ sɔ. Nan taá ye-na nɩ, tɩntʋ jɩgɩ lam tɩ dwe Pɛ Salɔmɔn dɛɛn na maŋɩ o zʋʋrɩ gwar-ŋʋnnʋ tɩlʋ tɩn.

eng: If God dresses grass in the field so beautifully, even though its alive today and tomorrow its thrown into the furnace, how much more will God do for you, you people of weak faith!.
local: Kʋ nan na yɩ gaarʋ tɩm kʋntʋ, tɩntʋ ba jɩgɩ dɛ dɩ nɔɔna na zwɛ-tɩ. Dɩ kʋntʋ dɩ, Wɛ ta mʋ paɩ tɩ jɩgɩ lam kʋntʋ doŋ. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ, kʋ na yɩ abam, Wɛ tiini DƖ nii abam baŋa nɩ. Bɛɛ mʋ yɩ á ba kɩ á wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Wɛ?.

eng: Dont chase after what you will eat and what you will drink. Stop worrying.
local: Á yɩ taá jɩgɩ wʋbʋŋ-fara dɩ á na wʋ́ kɩ te sɩ á na wʋdiu á di tɩn. Á yɩ taá kɩ liǝ dɩ kʋntʋ woŋo.

eng: All the nations of the world long for these things. Your Father knows that you need them.
local: Lʋgʋ baŋa nɔɔna balʋ na yǝri Wɛ tɩn mʋ tiini ba bʋŋɩ wǝǝnu tɩm kʋntʋ maama wʋbʋŋa. Abam Ko Wɛ nan maŋɩ DƖ ye nɩ abam maŋɩ sɩ á taá jɩgɩ wǝǝnu tɩm kʋntʋ.

eng: Instead, desire his kingdom and these things will be given to you as well.
local: Abam maŋɩ sɩ á pa á wʋbʋŋa taa tɔgɩ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm mʋ. Á na kɩ kʋntʋ, Wɛ laan wʋ́ wǝli abam woŋo kʋlʋ na daarɩ tɩn.

eng: Dont be afraid, little flock, because your Father delights in giving you the kingdom.
local: A nɔɔna bam á na ba daga tɩn, yɩ pa-na fʋʋnɩ taa jɩgɩ abam. Abam Ko Wɛ wʋbʋŋa mʋ lagɩ sɩ DƖ pa abam tɔgɩ á di DƖ paarɩ dɩm.

eng: Sell your possessions and give to those in need. Make for yourselves wallets that dont wear out—a treasure in heaven that never runs out. No thief comes near there, and no moth destroys.
local: Kwe-na á jɩjɩgɩrʋ tɩm á yǝgi, sɩ á daarɩ á maŋɩ tɩ sǝbu kʋm á ma wǝli yinigǝ tiinǝ. Kʋntʋ mʋ á kwe á jɩjɩgɩrʋ á tiŋi Wɛ-sɔŋɔ nɩ, sǝbiǝ gan-lɔgɔdɩ na bá cɔgɩ mɛ tɩn. Á na tiŋi jɩjɩgɩrʋ Wɛ tee nɩ, tɩ daa bá je. Ŋwɩɩna daa bá wanɩ ba ŋɔ-tɩ, yɩ tʋa bá wanɩ ka mu-tɩ ka cɔgɩ.

eng: Where your treasure is, there your heart will be too.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ, abam jɩjɩgɩrʋ na tigi mɛ tɩn, á bɩcara dɩ tigi dáanɩ mʋ.».

eng: Be dressed for service and keep your lamps lit.
local: Zezi ta ma ta o wɩ: «Á taá fɔgɩ á cʋ á tɩtɩ, nɩ nɔɔna balʋ yuutu na nuŋi o vu sɩ o di kadiri candiǝ yɩ ba jɩgɩ tɩɩna sɩ o joori o ba tɩn. Ba ma fɔgɩ ba vɔ ba gwaarʋ dɩ vwɩ, yɩ ba tarɩgɩ ba min-zwǝǝnu ba zɩgɩ ba tee nɩ. Ba yuutu wʋm na tu o yi manco-pɩɩna o magɩ boro, ba laan wʋ́ wanɩ ba zaŋɩ lɩla ba pʋrɩ ba pa-o.

eng: Happy are those servants whom the master finds waiting up when he arrives. I assure you that, when he arrives, he will dress himself to serve, seat them at the table as honored guests, and wait on them.
local: Ba yuutu wʋm na joori o ba, yɩ tɩntʋŋna balʋ na zɩgɩ ba niǝ yɩ ba ba dɔ tɩn, kʋʋ́ ji wʋpolo dɩ ba. A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, ba yuutu wʋm laan wʋ́ pa ba jǝni ba di wʋdiu. O ma wʋ́ vɔ o gɔrɔ dɩ vʋa, sɩ o kwe wʋdiu kʋm o vu o zɩgɩ sɩ ba di.

eng: Happy are those whom he finds alert, even if he comes at midnight or just before dawn.
local: O nan na joori o ba tɩtɩɩ kʋrʋ nɩ dɩ, yɩ o tɩntʋŋna bam ta na zɩgɩ ba niǝ yɩ ba ba dɔa, kʋʋ́ ji wʋpolo dɩ ba.

eng: But know this, if the homeowner had known what time the thief was coming, he wouldnt have allowed his home to be broken into.
local: Á taá ye sɩ, sɔŋɔ tu ya na lwarɩ maŋa kalʋ ŋwɩɩnʋ na lagɩ o ba tɩn, o ya bá pa ŋwɩɩnʋ wʋm na cwǝŋǝ o zʋ o ŋɔ.

eng: You also must be ready, because the Human Oneis coming at a time when you dont expect him.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ abam maŋɩ sɩ á cɩ á tɩtɩ mʋ, sɩ amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm lagɩ a ba maŋa kalʋ abam na ba tɩɩna nɩ aá ba tɩn mʋ.».

eng: Peter said, Lord, are you telling this parable for us or for everyone.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ ma bwe Zezi o wɩ: «Dɩ́ Yuutu, nmʋ na maŋɩ mɩmaŋa yantʋ tɩn, kʋ yɩ dɩbam yɩranɩ ŋwaanɩ mʋ naa nɔɔna maama ŋwaanɩ mʋ?».

eng: The Lord replied, Who are the faithful and wise managers whom the master will put in charge of his household servants, to give them their food at the proper time?.
local: Dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi ma lǝri o wɩ: «Wɔɔ mʋ yɩ tɩntʋŋ-ŋʋm wʋlʋ na jɩgɩ wʋbʋŋa tɩn? O nyɩ nɩnɛɛnɩ tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋlʋ o yuutu na yagɩ o tɩtʋŋa o pa-o yɩ o daarɩ o vu cwǝŋǝ tɩn. O yuutu wʋm ma lɩ-o sɩ o taa nii o donnǝ tɩntʋŋna bam baŋa nɩ, sɩ o daarɩ o taa pa-ba ba ni-wʋdiu dɛ maama.

eng: Happy are the servants whom the master finds fulfilling their responsibilities when he comes.
local: O sɔŋɔ tu wʋm na joori o ba, yɩ o na piini dɩ o kɩ kʋlʋ maama o na pɛ-o sɩ o kɩ tɩn, tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋm kʋntʋ laan wʋ́ na wʋpolo.

eng: I assure you that the master will put them in charge of all his possessions.
local: A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, o yuutu wʋm laan wʋ́ pa o taa nii o jɩjɩgɩrʋ maama baŋa nɩ mʋ.

eng: But suppose that these servants should say to themselves, My master is taking his time about coming. And suppose they began to beat the servants, both men and women, and to eat, drink, and get drunk.
local: Tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋm nan na bʋŋɩ o wɩ, o yuutu wʋm daanɩ zanzan yɩ o ba tui, yɩ o na jɩgɩ o donnǝ tɩntʋŋ-baara dɩ tɩntʋŋ-kaana bam o maga, o di o gaala, yɩ o nyɔ sana o bugǝ.

eng: The master of those servants would come on a day when they werent expecting him, at a time they couldnt predict. The master will cut them into pieces and assign them a place with the unfaithful.
local: tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋm kʋntʋ yuutu wʋm laan wʋ́ joori o ba, dɛ dɩm tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋm na ba jɩgɩ tɩɩna dɩ maŋa kalʋ o na wʋ́ ba tɩn. O yuutu wʋm na tu o pɩɩr-o kʋntʋ, o laan wʋ́ pa o na cam, yɩ o daarɩ o pa o da dɩ balʋ na vɩn Wɛ cɩga kam tɩn o na yaara zanzan.

eng: That servant who knew his masters will but didnt prepare for it or act on it will be beaten severely.
local: Tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋlʋ maama na lwarɩ o yuutu wʋbʋŋa na lagɩ sɩ o kɩ kʋlʋ tɩn, yɩ o na wʋ sɛ sɩ o ja o tɩtɩ o kɩ-kʋ, kʋntʋ tu wʋ́ na magɩrʋ zanzan.

eng: The one who didnt know the masters will but who did things deserving punishment will be beaten only a little. Much will be demanded from everyone who has been given much, and from the one who has been entrusted with much, even more will be asked.
local: Kʋ daarɩ, wʋlʋ na yǝri o yuutu na lagɩ sɩ o kɩ kʋlʋ tɩn, yɩ o na kɩ kʋlʋ na maŋɩ sɩ ba mag-o tɩn, kʋntʋ tu wʋ́ na magɩrʋ fɩnfɩɩn mʋ. Wʋlʋ maama ba na pɛ wǝǝnu zanzan tɩn, baá bwe-o wǝǝnu zanzan bwiǝ. Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na joŋi wǝǝnu zanzan sɩ o taa nii-tɩ tɩn, Wɛ wʋ́ bwe-o wǝǝnu zanzan kʋ ja gaalɩ.».

eng: I came to cast fire upon the earth. How I wish that it was already ablaze!.
local: Zezi daa ta ma ta o wɩ: «Amʋ tu lʋgʋ baŋa sɩ a tarɩgɩ mini mʋ a ma zwɛ-kʋ. A nan ya lagɩ dɩ a wʋ maama sɩ mini dɩm na maŋɩ dɩ ya tarɩgɩ mʋ.

eng: I have a baptism I must experience. How I am distressed until its completed!.
local: Amʋ nan maŋɩ sɩ a zʋ yaara wʋ, nɩnɛɛnɩ a na wʋ́ miisi na wʋnɩ te tɩn mʋ. A nan wʋ wʋ-cɔgɔ wʋnɩ mʋ, sɩ kʋ taa ve maŋa kam kʋ na wʋ́ kɩ kʋ ti tɩn.

eng: Do you think that I have come to bring peace to the earth? No, I tell you, I have come instead to bring division.
local: Amʋ na tu lʋgʋ baŋa tɩn, á bʋŋɩ sɩ kʋ yɩ sɩ a pa nɔɔna fɔgɩ daanɩ mʋ na? Aye. A tu sɩ a pa nɔɔna pɔɔrɩ daanɩ mʋ.

eng: From now on, a household of five will be divided—three against two and two against three.
local: Kʋ na puli zɩm kʋ maa ve, sɔŋɔ dɩdʋa wʋnɩ nɔɔna banu na wʋra, baá pɔɔrɩ daanɩ batɔ dɩ bale.

eng: Father will square off against son and son against father; mother against daughter and daughter against mother; and mother-in-law against daughter-in-law and daughter-in-law against mother-in-law.
local: Ko dɩ o bu wʋ́ pɔɔrɩ daanɩ. Nu dɩ o bukɔ dɩ wʋ́ pɔɔrɩ daanɩ. Kaanɩ dɩ o barʋ nu dɩ wʋ́ pɔɔrɩ daanɩ.».

eng: Jesus also said to the crowds, When you see a cloud forming in the west, you immediately say, Its going to rain. And indeed it does.
local: Zezi laan ma ta dɩ nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm o wɩ: «Á na nɛ kunkwǝǝnu na wʋra tɩ zaŋa mɛ seeni dʋa na zɩgɩ da ka nɩ tɩn, á yǝni á ta lɩla sɩ dʋa lagɩ ka nɩ, yɩ kaá sɩɩnɩ ka nɩ mʋ.

eng: And when a south wind blows, you say, A heat wave is coming. And it does.
local: Kʋ daarɩ gungun-kʋrrʋ viu na wʋra kʋ fulǝ, á yǝni á wɩ lʋn-wɩa maa bɩɩna, yɩ kʋʋ́ sɩɩnɩ kʋ kɩ kʋntʋ mʋ.

eng: Hypocrites! You know how to interpret conditions on earth and in the sky. How is it that you dont know how to interpret the present time?.
local: Abam yɩ pipiri-nyɩna mʋ. Abam waɩ á nii wɛyuu dɩ tɩga baŋa á ma lwarɩ tɩga kam yibiyǝ na wʋ́ ta yɩ te tɩn. Bɛɛ nan mʋ yɩ á warɩ á lwarɩ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ na wʋra tɩ kɩ lele tɩn kuri?.

eng: And why dont you judge for yourselves what is right?.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ mʋ yɩ abam ba bʋŋa, sɩ á lwarɩ kʋlʋ na lana tɩn sɩ á taá kɩa?.

eng: As you are going to court with your accuser, make your best effort to reach a settlement along the way. Otherwise, your accuser may bring you before the judge, and the judge hand you over to the officer, and the officer throw you into prison.
local: Kʋ nyɩ dɩ nɔɔnʋ na jaanɩ-m o vu o saŋɩ sarɩya jǝgǝ nɩ te tɩn. Abam ta na wʋ cwǝŋǝ nɩ á maa ve tɩn, sɩ n kwaanɩ n pa á fɔgɩ daanɩ. Kʋ na daɩ kʋntʋ, o laan wʋ́ ja nmʋ o kɩ sarɩya-diru wʋm jɩŋa nɩ. Wʋntʋ dɩ laan ma wʋ́ kɩ nmʋ o tɩntʋŋnʋ jɩŋa nɩ, sɩ o kɩ nmʋ pɩɩna digǝ nɩ.

eng: I tell you, you wont get out of there until you have paid the very last cent.
local: A lagɩ a ta nmʋ sɩ nń ta n wʋra taan. N na wʋ ŋwɩ jɩnɩ dɩm maama n ti, ba bá yagɩ-m.».

eng: Jesus said to his disciples, Things that cause people to trip and fall into sin must happen, but how terrible it is for the person through whom they happen.
local: Zezi dɛɛn ma ta dɩ o karabiǝ bam o wɩ: «Kʋ bá wanɩ sɩ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ na ganɩ nɔɔna sɩ ba yagɩ Wɛ cwǝŋǝ tɔgɩm tɩn taa tǝrǝ. Kʋ nan na yɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na ganɩ nɔɔna sɩ ba yagɩ Wɛ cwǝŋǝ tɔgɩm tɩn, lɛɛrʋ wʋ o yuu nɩ.

eng: It would be better for them to be thrown into a lake with a large stone hung around their neck than to cause one of these little ones to trip and fall into sin.
local: Kʋʋ́ ta lana kʋ pa kʋntʋ tu sɩ ba lǝ kandwɛ kamunu o ba nɩ ba dɩ-o nɩnɩʋ wʋnɩ, kʋ garɩ cam dɩlʋ o na wʋ́ na dɩ o na pɛ Wɛ bu-balaŋa dɩdʋa yagɩ Wɛ cwǝŋǝ tɔgɩm tɩn.

eng: Watch yourselves! If your brother or sister sins, warn them to stop. If they change their hearts and lives, forgive them.
local: Kʋntʋ tɩn, á taá cʋ á tɩtɩ! N nu-bu na kɩ lwarɩm, sɩ n kaan-o dɩ o na kɩ kʋlʋ tɩn. O nan na sɛ yɩ o lǝni o wʋ dɩ o kǝm-balɔrɔ kʋm, sɩ n ma n cɛ-o.

eng: Even if someone sins against you seven times in one day and returns to you seven times and says, I am changing my ways, you must forgive that person.
local: O na maŋɩ o kɩ nmʋ lwarɩm kuni bɩrpɛ dɛ wʋnɩ yɩ o sɛ nɩ o kɩ o cɔgɩ, nmʋ ta maŋɩ sɩ n ma n cɛ-o mʋ.».

eng: The apostles said to the Lord, Increase our faith.
local: Dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi tɩntʋŋna bam ma ta dɩd-o ba wɩ: «Wǝli dɩbam wʋbʋŋa, sɩ dɩ́ taá jɩgɩ wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ nmʋ.».

eng: The Lord replied, If you had faith the size of a mustard seed, you could say to this mulberry tree, Be uprooted and planted in the sea, and it would obey you.
local: Zezi ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Á na kɩ á wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ amʋ, yɩ kʋ na maŋɩ kʋ yɩ fɩnfɩɩn yɩranɩ nɩ nyasaŋ-bu te dɩ, á wʋ́ wanɩ á ta dɩ tu-kamunu kʋntʋ nɩ kʋ gɔ kʋ vu kʋ jǝni nɩnɩʋ kʋm wʋnɩ, yɩ kʋʋ́ sɛ á ni.».

eng: Would any of you say to your servant, who had just come in from the field after plowing or tending sheep, Come! Sit down for dinner?.
local: Zezi daa ta ma ta o wɩ: «Abam wʋlʋ na jɩgɩ tɩntʋŋnʋ, yɩ o na wʋra o varɩ o pa-m, naa o na tɔgɩ n peeni, yɩ wʋntʋ na tʋŋɩ o ti yɩ o ba o yi sɔŋɔ, n ta dɩd-o nɩ o ba o jǝni o di wʋdiu dɩ nmʋ na?.

eng: Wouldnt you say instead, Fix my dinner. Put on the clothes of a table servant and wait on me while I eat and drink. After that, you can eat and drink?.
local: Aye. Nmʋ nan wʋ́ ta dɩd-o nɩ: ‹Ve n kɩ wʋdiu, sɩ n laan vɔ n gɔrɔ dɩ vʋa, n ja wʋdiu kʋm n ba n pa-nɩ sɩ a di. Amʋ na di a ti, sɩ n laan vu n di n tɩtɩ nyɩm.›.

eng: You wont thank the servant because the servant did what you asked, will you?.
local: Kʋ daa daɩ sɩ n kɩ n tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋm le, dɩ o na kɩ kʋlʋ nmʋ na pɛ-o ni sɩ o kɩ tɩn.

eng: In the same way, when you have done everything required of you, you should say, We servants deserve no special praise. We have only done our duty.
local: Kʋ yɩ bɩdwɩ mʋ dɩ abam dɩ. Abam na kɩ kʋlʋ maama Wɛ na pɛ abam ni sɩ á kɩ tɩn, á maŋɩ sɩ á taá wɩ: ‹Dɩbam yɩ tɩntʋŋna má mʋ. Dɩ́ kɩ kʋlʋ na maŋɩ sɩ dɩ́ kɩ tɩn yɩranɩ mʋ.› ».

eng: On the way to Jerusalem, Jesus traveled along the border between Samaria and Galilee.
local: Zezi dɛɛn ma wʋ cwǝŋǝ nɩ o maa ve Zeruzalɛm. O ma da Samari lʋgʋ dɩ Galile lʋgʋ sisǝm o maa kɛa.

eng: As he entered a village, ten men with skin diseases approached him. Keeping their distance from him.
local: O ma vu sɩ o zʋ tɩʋ kʋdoŋ. Mʋ nanywaanʋ fugǝ jeer-o cwǝŋǝ nɩ. Ba ma zɩgɩ yigǝ yigǝ.

eng: they raised their voices and said, Jesus, Master, show us mercy.
local: yɩ ba kaasɩ dɩ kwǝr-dɛ ba wɩ: «Dɩbam tu Zezi, popo duri dɩbam ŋwaŋa.».

eng: When Jesus saw them, he said, Go, show yourselves to the priests. As they left, they were cleansed.
local: Zezi na nɛ-ba tɩn, o ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Ve-na Wɛ kaanɩm tu wʋm te sɩ á pa o nii á yɩra yam.» O na tagɩ kʋntʋ yɩ ba maa ve tɩn, mʋ ba maama nɛ yazurǝ yɩ ba yɩra ji lanyɩranɩ.

eng: One of them, when he saw that he had been healed, returned and praised God with a loud voice.
local: Ba wʋ dɩdʋa na nɛ nɩ o yawɩʋ kʋm je tɩn, o ma pipiri o joori o vu Zezi te, yɩ o zuli Wɛ dɩ kwǝr-dɩa.

eng: He fell on his face at Jesus feet and thanked him. He was a Samaritan.
local: O ma vu o tʋ Zezi yigǝ nɩ, yɩ o kɩ o le. Wʋlʋ na kɩ kʋntʋ tɩn yɩ Samari tu mʋ.

eng: Jesus replied, Werent ten cleansed? Where are the other nine?.
local: Zezi ma bwe o wɩ: «Kʋ daɩ nɔɔna fugǝ kam maama mʋ nɛ yazurǝ na? Nʋgʋ kʋm nan bɛ?.

eng: No one returned to praise God except this foreigner.
local: Ba wʋlʋwʋlʋ wʋ joori o ba sɩ o kɩ Wɛ le, kʋ na daɩ dwi-gɛ tu wʋntʋ yɩranɩ.».

eng: Then Jesus said to him, Get up and go. Your faith has healed you.
local: O laan ma ta dɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋm o wɩ: «Nmʋ na kɩ n wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ amʋ tɩn mʋ pɛ n na vrɩm. Nan zaŋɩ n viiri.».

eng: Pharisees asked Jesus when Gods kingdom was coming. He replied, Gods kingdom isnt coming with signs that are easily noticed.
local: Farizɩan tiinǝ bam dɛɛn ma bwe Zezi, sɩ ba lwarɩ maŋa kalʋ Wɛ na wʋ́ di paarɩ nɔɔna tɩtarɩ nɩ tɩn. O ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Wɛ paarɩ dɩm maŋa na yiǝ, á bá wanɩ á ma á yiǝ á na-dɩ.

eng: Nor will people say, Look, here it is! or There it is! Dont you see? Gods kingdom is already among you.
local: Nɔɔna bá ta wɩ: ‹Nii-na yo seeni›, asawɛ ba wɩ: ‹Nii jǝgǝ kantʋ nɩ›, bɛŋwaanɩ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm maŋɩ dɩ wʋ abam tɩtarɩ nɩ.».

eng: Then Jesus said to the disciples, The time will come when you will long to see one of the days of the Human One,and you wont see it.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩ o karabiǝ bam o wɩ: «Maŋa wʋ́ ba, yɩ á wʋ́ ta lagɩ dɩ á wʋ maama sɩ á ya na tɔgɩ á wʋra, kʋ na maŋɩ kʋ yɩ dɛ dɩdwɩ yɩranɩ dɩ, maŋa kalʋ amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm na joori a ba tɩn. Á nan bá na.

eng: People will say to you, Look there! or Look here! Dont leave or go chasing after them.
local: Kantʋ maŋa kam nɩ nɔɔna wʋ́ ta dɩ abam ba wɩ: ‹Nii-na, o wʋ yo seeni›, asawɛ: ‹O wʋ jǝgǝ kantʋ nɩ›. Á nan yɩ sɛ ba taanɩ dɩm sɩ á vu á nii.

eng: The Human Onewill appear on his day in the same way that a flash of lightning lights up the sky from one end to the other.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ, dɛ dɩm amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm na lagɩ a ba tɩn na yiǝ, kʋʋ́ ta nyɩ dɩ dʋa na pɩpɩlɩ wɛyuu nɩ ka lɛɛnɩ ka paɩ pooni lʋgʋ baŋa je maama nɩ te tɩn mʋ.

eng: However, first he must suffer many things and be rejected by this generation.
local: Kʋ na wʋ́ loori kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ fɩn mʋ sɩ amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm na yaara zanzan sɩ zɩm nɔɔna bam culi-nɩ.

eng: As it was in the days of Noah, so it will be during the days of the Human One.
local: Amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm na wʋ́ ba maŋa kalʋ tɩn wʋ́ ta nyɩ nɩnɛɛnɩ kʋ dɛɛn na kɩ Nowe maŋa kam nɩ te tɩn mʋ.

eng: People were eating, drinking, marrying, and being given in marriage until the day Noah entered the ark and the flood came and destroyed them all.
local: Kantʋ maŋa kam nɩ, nɔɔna dɛɛn di ba wʋdiiru yɩ ba nyɔa, yɩ ba daarɩ ba di kaana, yɩ kaana dɩ zʋʋrɩ banna, sɩ kʋ vu kʋ yi dɛ dɩm Nowe na zʋ naboro kamunu kʋm wʋ tɩn. Na laan ma ba, ba li lʋgʋ baŋa ba cɔgɩ nɔɔna bam maama.

eng: Likewise in the days of Lot, people were eating, drinking, buying, selling, planting, and building.
local: Maŋa kalʋ Lɔtɩ dɛɛn na wʋra tɩn, kʋ dɩ maŋɩ kʋ yɩ kʋntʋ mʋ. Nɔɔna dɛɛn wʋra ba di ba wʋdiiru yɩ ba nyɔa, yɩ ba daarɩ ba kɩ pipiu, yɩ ba vara yɩ ba lɔ sam.

eng: But on the day Lot left Sodom, fire and sulfur rained down from heaven and destroyed them all.
local: Kʋ ma ba kʋ yi dɛ dɩm Lɔtɩ na duri o nuŋi Sodɔm nɩ tɩn. Dɛ dɩm kʋntʋ nɩ, mini dɩ min-kandwa ma nuŋi wɛyuu nɩ ya tʋ tɩga nɩ ya cɔgɩ tɩʋ kʋm nɔɔna bam maama.

eng: Thats the way it will be on the day the Human Oneis revealed.
local: Dɛ dɩm amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm na wʋ́ joori a ba lʋgʋ baŋa a brɩ a tɩtɩ tɩn, kʋʋ́ darɩ nɔɔna kʋntʋ doŋ mʋ.

eng: On that day, those on the roof, whose possessions are in the house, shouldnt come down to grab them. Likewise, those in the field shouldnt turn back.
local: Dɛ dɩm kʋntʋ nɩ, nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na wʋ nayuu nɩ yɩ o wǝǝnu na wʋ digǝ wʋnɩ, kʋntʋ tu daa yɩ tu o zʋ digǝ wʋ sɩ o kwe o wǝǝnu tɩm o ja duri. Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ nan na wʋ kara nɩ, o dɩ daa yɩ joori sɔŋɔ.

eng: Remember Lots wife!.
local: Guli-na kʋ dɛɛn na kɩ Lɔtɩ kaanɩ wʋm te tɩn.

eng: Whoever tries to preserve their life will lose it, but whoever loses their life will preserve it.
local: Wʋlʋ maama na lagɩ sɩ o taa jɩgɩ o tɩtɩ ŋwɩa tɩn, kʋntʋ tu wʋ́ ga-ka mʋ. Wʋlʋ maama nan na sɛ sɩ o ga o ŋwɩa tɩn, kʋntʋ tu wʋ́ ta jɩgɩ ŋwɩa Wɛ tee nɩ.

eng: I tell you, on that night two people will be in the same bed: one will be taken and the other left.
local: A lagɩ a ta abam sɩ, nɔɔna bale na pǝni tɩtɩɩ nɩ sara dɩdʋa wʋnɩ, Wɛ wʋ́ lɩ ba dɩdʋa DƖ daarɩ dɩdʋa.

eng: Two women will be grinding grain together: one will be taken and the other left.
local: Kaana bale na wʋra ba cʋɩ muni, Wɛ wʋ́ lɩ ba dɩdʋa DƖ daarɩ dɩdʋa. .

eng: The disciples asked, Where, Lord? Jesus said, The vultures gather wherever theres a dead body.
local: Zezi na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma bwe-o ba wɩ: «Dɩbam Yuutu, kʋntʋ woŋo wʋ́ kɩ yǝn mʋ?» O ma lǝri-ba dɩ bɩtar-dɩndɩa o wɩ: «Wo-tʋʋ na wʋ mɛ tɩn, duurǝ dɩ gilimi da mʋ.».

eng: Jesus entered Jericho and was passing through town.
local: Zezi ma vu o tɔgɩ Zeriko tɩʋ kʋm wʋ o maa kɛa.

eng: A man there named Zacchaeus, a ruler among tax collectors, was rich.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ mʋ wʋ tɩʋ kʋm wʋnɩ, o yɩrɩ mʋ Zase. O maa yɩ lampo-joŋnǝ yigǝ tu, yɩ o yɩ nadum.

eng: He was trying to see who Jesus was, but, being a short man, he couldnt because of the crowd.
local: O dɛɛn ma lagɩ sɩ o lwarɩ Zezi na yɩ wʋlʋ tɩn. O ma kwaanɩ o ga, o na yɩ nɔn-kukulǝ yɩ kɔgɔ kʋm daga tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: So he ran ahead and climbed up a sycamore tree so he could see Jesus, who was about to pass that way.
local: O ma duri o kɛ kɔgɔ kʋm yigǝ, o vu o di kapʋrʋ yuu sɩ o kwaanɩ o na Zezi, o na lagɩ o ba o da dáanɩ o kɛ tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: When Jesus came to that spot, he looked up and said, Zacchaeus, come down at once. I must stay in your home today.
local: Zezi ma vu o yi jǝgǝ kam. O ma kwǝni o yuu wɛɛnɩ o nii, yɩ o wɩ: «Zase, kɩ lɩla n tu tɩga, sɩ zɩm a maŋɩ sɩ a ba a pǝni nmʋ sɔŋɔ nɩ mʋ.».

eng: So Zacchaeus came down at once, happy to welcome Jesus.
local: Zase na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma kɩ lɩla o tu tɩga, yɩ o vu o jeeri Zezi lanyɩranɩ o sɔŋɔ nɩ dɩ wʋpolo.

eng: Everyone who saw this grumbled, saying, He has gone to be the guest of a sinner.
local: Nɔɔna bam na nɛ Zezi na kɩ te tɩn, ba maama maa pʋʋnɩ ba wɩ: «Taá nii o na ve o zʋ nɔn-cɩcɔgɔ sɔŋɔ sɩ o taa yɩ o vǝru.».

eng: Zacchaeus stopped and said to the Lord, Look, Lord, I give half of my possessions to the poor. And if I have cheated anyone, I repay them four times as much.
local: Zase laan ma zaŋɩ wɛɛnɩ o ta dɩ dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi o wɩ: «A tu, nii amʋ na lagɩ a kɩ te tɩn. Aá pɔɔrɩ a jɩjɩgɩrʋ tɩm cicoro mʋ a maŋɩ a pa yinigǝ tiinǝ. A ya na pini wʋlʋ maama a joŋi lampoo zanzan o tee nɩ tɩn, aá joori a ŋwɩ-kʋ kuni bɩna mʋ a pa kʋ tu.».

eng: One Sabbath, when Jesus went to share a meal in the home of one of the leaders of the Pharisees, they were watching him closely.
local: Siun dɛ dɩdoŋ dɛɛn ma yi. Zezi ma vu Farizɩan yigǝ tu dɩdʋa sɔŋɔ o zʋ sɩ o di wʋdiu. O na wʋra tɩn, ba maa kwaanɩ ba yɩr-o, sɩ ba nii o na wʋ́ kɩ te tɩn.

eng: Jesus said to him, Today, salvation has come to this household because he too is a son of Abraham.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Zɩm dɛ dɩm nɩ sɔŋɔ kʋntʋ tiinǝ nɛ vrɩm, bɛŋwaanɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ brɩ sɩ o dɩ tɔgɩ o yɩ Abraham dwi tu cɩga cɩga.

eng: A man suffering from an abnormal swelling of the body was there.
local: Ya-fʋla tu ma ba o zɩgɩ Zezi yigǝ nɩ.

eng: The Human Onecame to seek and save the lost.
local: Amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm na tu lʋgʋ baŋa tɩn, kʋ yɩ sɩ a beeri balʋ na je tɩn mʋ, sɩ a daarɩ a vrɩ-ba a kɩ Wɛ jɩŋa nɩ.».

eng: Jesus asked the lawyers and Pharisees, Does the Law allow healing on the Sabbath or not.
local: Zezi na nɛ-o tɩn, o ma bwe Wɛ cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ dɩ Farizɩan tiinǝ bam o wɩ: «Dɩbam cullu paɩ cwǝŋǝ sɩ dɩ́ pa nɔɔnʋ na yazurǝ siun dɛ dɩm nɩ naa kʋ culǝ?».

eng: As they listened to this, Jesus told them another parable because he was near Jerusalem and they thought Gods kingdom would appear right away.
local: Nɔɔna bam ta na wʋra ba cǝgi o taanɩ dɩm kʋntʋ tɩn, Zezi daa ma maŋɩ mɩmaŋa o brɩ-ba. O na tu o twɛ Zeruzalɛm tɩn, nɔɔna bam ga bʋŋɩ sɩ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋ́ ba lɩla mʋ ba yibiyǝ nɩ kantʋ maŋa kam nɩ.

eng: But they said nothing. Jesus took hold of the sick man, cured him, and then let him go.
local: Ba maa cɩm. O ma dwe nɔɔnʋ wʋm o pa o na yazurǝ, yɩ o yag-o o wɩ, o ve.

eng: He said, A certain man who was born into royalty went to a distant land to receive his kingdom and then return.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ o ma maŋɩ o brɩ-ba o wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ mʋ wʋra o na yɩ pabu. Dɛ dɩdwɩ o ma zaŋɩ sɩ o vu tɩʋ kʋdoŋ yigǝ yigǝ sɩ ba pa-o paarɩ, sɩ o laan joori o ba.

eng: He said to them, Suppose your child or ox fell into a ditch on the Sabbath day. Wouldnt you immediately pull it out.
local: O laan ma bwe-ba o wɩ: «Abam wʋlʋ bu na tʋ vʋlɩ wʋnɩ siun dɛ nɩ, n bá kɩ lɩla n vu n vaŋ-o n lɩ na? Kʋ na yɩ n nabɩa dɩ, n bá vu n lɩ-ka na?».

eng: He called together ten servants and gave each of them money worth four months wages.He said, Do business with this until I return.
local: O ma bǝŋi o tɩntʋŋna fugǝ, yɩ o kwe sǝbu-sɩŋa dala fugǝ o pa-ba, yɩ o wɩ: ‹Ma-na sǝbu kʋntʋ á taá maa pipiǝ, sɩ kʋ taa ve maŋa kalʋ a na wʋ́ joori a ba tɩn.›.

eng: But they had no response.
local: Ba maa warɩ ba lǝr-o kʋlʋkʋlʋ.

eng: His citizens hated him, so they sent a representative after him who said, We dont want this man to be our king.
local: O tɩʋ kʋm tiinǝ nan cul-o zanzan. Ba ma tʋŋɩ nɔɔna sɩ ba da o kwaga ba vu ba ta wɩ, ba ba lagɩ sɩ o taa yɩ ba pɛ.

eng: When Jesus noticed how the guests sought out the best seats at the table, he told them a parable.
local: Nɔɔna balʋ na tu sɩ ba tɔgɩ ba di wʋdiu Farizɩan tu wʋm sɔŋɔ nɩ tɩn maa lagɩ jǝŋǝ je sɩ ba jǝni. Zezi ma na nɩ ba lagɩ je-laarʋ je mʋ sɩ ba jǝni da. O na nɛ kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma maŋɩ mɩmaŋa o brɩ-ba o wɩ:.

eng: After receiving his kingdom, he returned and called the servants to whom he had given the money to find out how much they had earned.
local: Dɩ kʋ dɩ, ba ta pɛ-o paarɩ. O ma joori o tɩtɩ tɩʋ kʋm. O laan ma pa ba bǝŋi o tɩntʋŋna balʋ o ya na pɛ-ba sǝbu kʋm tɩn, sɩ ba ba o te ba brɩ-o nyɔɔrɩ dɩlʋ ba na nɛ kʋ baŋa nɩ tɩn.

eng: When someone invites you to a wedding celebration, dont take your seat in the place of honor. Someone more highly regarded than you could have been invited by your host.
local: «Nɔɔnʋ na kɩ kadiri candiǝ yɩ o bǝŋi-m sɩ n ba n di, yɩ kwaanɩ sɩ n jǝni nɔn-kamunu jǝŋǝ je nɩ. Nmʋ na kɩ kʋntʋ, yɩ nɔn-kɔrɔ na tuǝ.

eng: The first servant came forward and said, Your money has earned a return of one thousand percent.
local: Dayigǝ nɔɔnʋ wʋm ma ba o ta dɩd-o o wɩ: ‹A yuutu, a kwe sǝbu-sɩŋa dalɩ dɩm nmʋ na pɛ-nɩ tɩn, a ma na dala fugǝ nyɔɔrɩ mʋ a wǝli da.›.

eng: The host who invited both of you will come and say to you, Give your seat to this other person. Embarrassed, you will take your seat in the least important place.
local: sɔŋɔ tu wʋlʋ na bǝŋi abam tɩn wʋ́ ba o ta dɩ nmʋ o wɩ: ‹Zaŋɩ sɩ n daarɩ jǝgǝ kam n pa nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ.› Nmʋ laan wʋ́ zaŋɩ n vu n jǝni kwaga kwaga mʋ dɩ cavɩɩra.

eng: The king replied, Excellent! You are a good servant. Because you have been faithful in a small matter, you will have authority over ten cities.
local: O yuutu wʋm ma lǝr-o o wɩ: ‹N kɩ cɩga n jɩgɩ yawala, n yɩ tɩntʋŋ-ŋʋm. Nmʋ na kɩ cɩga dɩ kʋlʋ fɩnfɩɩn a na kɩ n jɩŋa nɩ tɩn, a laan wʋ́ pa n ta n te tɩɩnɩ fugǝ.›.

eng: Instead, when you receive an invitation, go and sit in the least important place. When your host approaches you, he will say, Friend, move up here to a better seat. Then you will be honored in the presence of all your fellow guests.
local: Kʋ daarɩ, ba na bǝŋi-m sɩ n vu n tɔgɩ n di candiǝ, ve n jǝni kwaga kwaga. Nmʋ na kɩ kʋntʋ, yɩ sɔŋɔ tu wʋm na tu o na-m, o laan wʋ́ ta o wɩ: ‹A ciloŋ, zaŋɩ n ba yigǝ n jǝni.› Kʋntʋ wʋ́ pa n na zulǝ nɔɔna bam maama yigǝ nɩ.

eng: The second servant came and said, Master, your money has made a return of five hundred percent.
local: Bale-tu wʋm ma ba o ta dɩ o yuutu wʋm o wɩ: ‹A yuutu, a kwe sǝbu-sɩŋa dalɩ dɩm a ma na dala yanu nyɔɔrɩ mʋ a wǝli da.›.

eng: All who lift themselves up will be brought low, and those who make themselves low will be lifted up.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ, wʋlʋ na zǝŋi o tɩtɩ tɩn, Wɛ wʋ́ tu kʋntʋ tu. Wʋlʋ nan na tu o tɩtɩ tɩn, Wɛ wʋ́ zǝŋi kʋntʋ tu.».

eng: To this one, the king said, You will have authority over five cities.
local: O yuutu wʋm ma lǝr-o o dɩ o wɩ: ‹Aá pa n ta n te tɩɩnɩ dɩnu.›.

eng: Then Jesus said to the person who had invited him, When you host a lunch or dinner, dont invite your friends, your brothers and sisters, your relatives, or rich neighbors. If you do, they will invite you in return and that will be your reward.
local: Zezi laan ma ta dɩ sɔŋɔ tu wʋm o wɩ: «Nmʋ na lagɩ n kɩ wʋdi-fɔrɔ n pa nɔɔna, kʋ na yɩ wɩa nɩ naa dɩdaanɩ, sɩ n yɩ bǝŋi n cilonnǝ dɩ n cʋrrʋ dɩ n donnǝ nadunǝ. Nmʋ na kɩ kʋntʋ, bantʋ wʋ́ wanɩ ba joori ba dɩ kɩ-m kʋntʋ doŋ ba ma ŋwɩ jɩnɩ dɩm.

eng: Another servant came and said, Master, here is your money. I wrapped it up in a scarf for safekeeping.
local: Wʋlʋ na yɩ batɔ-tu wʋm tɩn dɩ ma daa ba o ta dɩd-o o wɩ: ‹A yuutu, nan joŋi nmʋ sǝbu-sɩŋa dalɩ dɩm. A kwe-dɩ mʋ a kukuli dɩ yu-kwala a tiŋi.

eng: Instead, when you give a banquet, invite the poor, crippled, lame, and blind.
local: Nmʋ nan na lagɩ n kɩ candiǝ, sɩ n bǝŋi balʋ na yɩ yinigǝ tiinǝ dɩ kwaarʋ dɩ gwanɩ dɩ lilwǝ tɩn, sɩ ba ba ba di.

eng: I was afraid of you because you are a stern man. You withdraw what you havent deposited and you harvest what you havent planted.
local: a na ye nɩ nmʋ laŋa damma, yɩ a kwarɩ fʋʋnɩ dɩ nmʋ tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Nmʋ yǝni n joŋi nyɔɔrɩ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ nmʋ yɩra na wʋ cɛ tɩ baŋa nɩ tɩn, yɩ n zagɩ mɩna yalʋ n tɩtɩ na wʋ dugi tɩn.›.

eng: And you will be blessed because they cant repay you. Instead, you will be repaid when the just are resurrected.
local: Nmʋ na kɩ kʋntʋ, nmʋ wʋ́ ta n jɩgɩ yu-yoŋo. Nɔɔna bantʋ doŋ bá wanɩ ba joori ba ŋwɩ jɩnɩ dɩm. Maŋa kam Wɛ na wʋ́ pa nɔn-ŋʋna bi ba yagɩ tʋʋnɩ tɩn, nmʋ laan wʋ́ joŋi n kǝm dɩm kwǝrǝ.».

eng: The king replied, I will judge you by the words of your own mouth, you worthless servant! You knew, did you, that Im a stern man, withdrawing what I didnt deposit, and harvesting what I didnt plant?.
local: O yuutu wʋm laan ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: ‹Nmʋ yɩ tɩntʋŋ-balɔrɔ mʋ. N tɩtɩ ni-taanɩ dɩm mʋ brɩ nɩ n ba jɩgɩ bʋra. Nmʋ ya na ye nɩ a laŋa damma, yɩ a joŋi nyɔɔrɩ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ a yɩra na wʋ cɛ tɩ baŋa nɩ tɩn, yɩ a zagɩ mɩna yalʋ a tɩtɩ na wʋ dugi tɩn.

eng: When one of the dinner guests heard Jesus remarks, he said to Jesus, Happy are those who will feast in Gods kingdom.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ wʋlʋ na tɔgɩ o je Zezi tee nɩ o di wʋdiu tɩn ma ni o na tagɩ kʋlʋ tɩn. O ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ maama na zʋ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋ o jǝni o di ywǝǝni dáanɩ tɩn mʋ yɩ yu-yoŋo tu.».

eng: Why then didnt you put my money in the bank? Then when I arrived, at least I could have gotten it back with interest.
local: bɛɛ nan yɩ nmʋ wʋ kwe a sǝbu kʋm n tiŋi bankɩ nɩ. Nmʋ ya na tiŋi-kʋ bankɩ nɩ, a ya wʋ́ ba a joŋi-kʋ dɩ nyɔɔrɩ.›.

eng: Jesus replied, A certain man hosted a large dinner and invited many people.
local: Zezi ma maŋɩ mɩmaŋa o ma lǝri o wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ mʋ ya wʋra o ma lagɩ o kɩ wʋdi-fɔrɔ sɩ o pa nɔɔna. O ma bǝŋi nɔɔna zanzan sɩ ba ba ba di.

eng: He said to his attendants, Take his money and give it to the one who has ten times as much.
local: O laan ma ta dɩ balʋ na zɩgɩ da tɩn o wɩ: ‹Joŋi-na sǝbu-sɩŋa dalɩ dɩm o tee nɩ á pa wʋlʋ na nɛ dala fugǝ kam tɩn tu.›.

eng: When it was time for the dinner to begin, he sent his servant to tell the invited guests, Come! The dinner is now ready.
local: Candiǝ kam maŋa na yi tɩn, o ma tʋŋɩ o tɩntʋŋnʋ sɩ o vu o ta dɩ balʋ o na bǝŋi tɩn nɩ, ba ba sɩ woŋo maama ti kʋ yigǝ.

eng: But Master, they said, he already has ten times as much.
local: Ba ma ta dɩd-o ba wɩ: ‹Dɩ́ yuutu, wʋntʋ maŋɩ o jɩgɩ dala fugǝ mʋ.›.

eng: One by one, they all began to make excuses. The first one told him, I bought a farm and must go and see it. Please excuse me.
local: Nɔɔna bam na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, ba maama ma wʋra ba loori cwǝŋǝ o tee nɩ. Ba dɩdʋa ma wɩ: ‹Popo, pa-nɩ cwǝŋǝ, sɩ a joŋi kar-dʋŋa mʋ, kʋ yɩ fɩfɩʋn sɩ a vu a nii ka baŋa nɩ.›.

eng: He replied, I say to you that everyone who has will be given more, but from those who have nothing, even what they have will be taken away.
local: O ma lǝri o wɩ: ‹A lagɩ a ta abam sɩ wʋlʋ maama na maŋɩ o jɩgɩ woŋo tɩn, o ta wʋ́ joŋi kʋdoŋ o wǝli da. Wʋlʋ nan na maŋɩ o ba jɩgɩ kʋlʋkʋlʋ tɩn laan wʋ́ ba o ga kʋlʋ fɩnfɩɩn o ya na jɩgɩ tɩn dɩ.

eng: Another said, I bought five teams of oxen, and Im going to check on them. Please excuse me.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ dɩ ma wɩ: ‹Popo, pa-nɩ cwǝŋǝ, sɩ a yǝgi nabɛ fugǝ mʋ lele kʋntʋ sɩ a ma va. A maa ve sɩ a kɩ a nii, sɩ wʋ́ sɛ sɩ va na?›.

eng: As for my enemies who dont want me as their king, bring them here and slaughter them before me.
local: Kʋ daarɩ, kʋ na yɩ a dʋna balʋ na ba lagɩ sɩ a taa yɩ ba pɛ tɩn, ve-na á ja-ba á ba yo seeni sɩ á gʋ-ba a yigǝ nɩ.› ».

eng: Another said, I just got married, so I cant come.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ dɩ ma ta o wɩ: ‹A di kaanɩ mʋ lele kʋntʋ. Amʋ daa bá wanɩ a ba.›.

eng: After Jesus said this, he continued on ahead, going up to Jerusalem.
local: Zezi dɛɛn na tagɩ wǝǝnu tɩm kʋntʋ maama o ti tɩn, o ma zaŋɩ o kwe cwǝŋǝ o tɔgɩ nɔɔna bam yigǝ o maa ve Zeruzalɛm.

eng: When he returned, the servant reported these excuses to his master. The master of the house became angry and said to his servant, Go quickly to the citys streets, the busy ones and the side streets, and bring the poor, crippled, blind, and lame.
local: Tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋm laan ma joori sɔŋɔ o vu o ta dɩ o tu wʋm ba na lǝri te tɩn. Sɔŋɔ tu wʋm na ni tɩn, o bam ma zaŋɩ. O ma ta dɩ tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋm o wɩ: ‹Nuŋi lɩla n vu tɩʋ kʋm cwe sɩm yum maama n la yinigǝ tiinǝ dɩ kwaarʋ dɩ lilwǝ dɩ gwanɩ n pa ba ba ba di.›.

eng: As Jesus came to Bethphage and Bethany on the Mount of Olives, he gave two disciples a task.
local: Ba ma vu ba yi tɩ-balwa bale, ba yɩra mʋ Betifaazɩ dɩ Betani. Je sɩm kʋntʋ wʋ piu kʋdoŋ saŋa nɩ mʋ, kʋ yɩrɩ mʋ Olivi piu. Zezi ma tʋŋɩ nɔɔna bale o karabiǝ bam wʋnɩ.

eng: The servant said, Master, your instructions have been followed and there is still room.
local: Tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋm ma kɩ kʋntʋ, yɩ o daa ta o wɩ: ‹Amʋ tu, a kɩ tɩte maama nmʋ na tagɩ tɩn, yɩ jǝgǝ daa ta daarɩ sɩ nɔɔna jǝni da.›.

eng: He said, Go into the village over there. When you enter it, you will find tied up there a colt that no one has ever ridden. Untie it and bring it here.
local: yɩ o ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Ve-na á zʋ tɩʋ kʋlʋ na wʋ abam yigǝ nɩ tɩn. Á na zʋ kʋ wʋ, á wʋ́ na bɩna-pɔla na lǝgi da, yɩ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ daa ta wʋ garɩ ka baŋa. Ve-na á bwǝli-ka á ja ba á pa-nɩ.

eng: The master said to the servant, Go to the highways and back alleys and urge people to come in so that my house will be filled.
local: Sɔŋɔ tu wʋm daa ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: ‹Nuŋi tɩʋ kʋm wʋ n vu n beeri cwǝ-bim sɩm maama niǝ nɩ, sɩ n pa balʋ na wʋra tɩn ba ba zʋ a sɔŋɔ kʋm sɩ kʋ su.›.

eng: If anyone asks, Why are you untying it? just say, Its master needs it.
local: Nɔɔnʋ nan na bwe abam o wɩ: ‹Bɛɛ mʋ yɩ á bwǝli-ka?›, sɩ á ta dɩd-o nɩ: ‹Kʋ yɩ dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm mʋ lagɩ-ka.› ».

eng: I tell you, not one of those who were invited will taste my dinner.
local: A lagɩ a ta abam sɩ, kʋ na yɩ nɔɔna balʋ a ya na bǝŋi yɩ ba vɩn tɩn, bantʋ wʋlʋwʋlʋ daa ba jɩgɩ ni sɩ ba tɔgɩ ba di a wʋdiu kʋm.».

eng: Those who had been sent found it exactly as he had said.
local: Nɔɔna bale balʋ o na tʋŋɩ tɩn ma zaŋɩ ba vu sɩ ba nii. Ba ma sɩɩnɩ ba na nɩ kʋ yɩ o na tagɩ te tɩn.

eng: Large crowds were traveling with Jesus. Turning to them, he said.
local: Zezi na wʋ cwǝŋǝ nɩ o maa ve tɩn, nɔn-kɔgɔ zanzan maa tɔgɩ o kwaga. O ma pipiri o nii ba seeni yɩ o wɩ:.

eng: As they were untying the colt, its owners said to them, Why are you untying the colt.
local: Ba na wʋra ba bwǝli bɩna-pɔla kam tɩn, ka tiinǝ ma bwe-ba ba wɩ: «Bɛŋwaanɩ mʋ yɩ á wʋra á bwǝli bɩnaga kam?».

eng: Whoever comes to me and doesnt hate father and mother, spouse and children, and brothers and sisters—yes, even ones own life—cannot be my disciple.
local: «Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na tu amʋ te yɩ o wʋ vɩn o ko dɩ o nu, dɩ o kaanɩ dɩ o biǝ, dɩ o zɩmbaara dɩ o nyaana dɩ o nakwa, yɩ o na wʋ vɩn o tɩtɩ ŋwɩa dɩ, kʋntʋ tu bá wanɩ o ji amʋ karabu.

eng: They replied, Its master needs it.
local: Ba ma lǝri ba wɩ: «Kʋ yɩ dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm mʋ lagɩ-ka.».

eng: Whoever doesnt carry their own cross and follow me cannot be my disciple.
local: Wʋlʋ maama na wʋ sɛ sɩ o tɔgɩ amʋ kwaga, sɩ o na yaara nɩ wʋlʋ na zɩŋɩ dagara o maa ve o tʋʋnɩ je te tɩn, kʋntʋ tu bá wanɩ o ji a karabu.

eng: They brought it to Jesus, threw their clothes on the colt, and lifted Jesus onto it.
local: Ba ma ja-ka ba ja vu Zezi te. Ba laan ma kwe ba gwaarʋ ba daŋɩ ka baŋa nɩ, yɩ ba pa Zezi di ka baŋa.

eng: If one of you wanted to build a tower, wouldnt you first sit down and calculate the cost, to determine whether you have enough money to complete it?.
local: Abam wʋlʋ na bʋŋɩ sɩ o lɔ wɛyuu di-kamunu, n maŋɩ sɩ n da yigǝ n jǝni n jeeli n nii, sǝbu kʋm wʋ́ wanɩ digǝ kam kʋ lɔ kʋ ti naa kʋ bá wanɩ. Kʋ daɩ kʋntʋ na?.

eng: As Jesus rode along, they spread their clothes on the road.
local: O na maa kɛ tɩn, nɔɔna bam ma zul-o, yɩ ba kwe ba gwaarʋ ba lwarɩ cwǝŋǝ kam yuu nɩ, sɩ o taa ve tɩ baŋa nɩ o kɛa.

eng: Otherwise, when you have laid the foundation but couldnt finish the tower, all who see it will begin to belittle you.
local: N na wʋ kɩ kʋntʋ, yɩ n na cwi digǝ kam kuri yɩ ka daa warɩ ka guri, balʋ maama na lwarɩ tɩn wʋ́ ta mwanɩ nmʋ.

eng: As Jesus approached the road leading down from the Mount of Olives, the whole throng of his disciples began rejoicing. They praised God with a loud voice because of all the mighty things they had seen.
local: O na gɛ fɩnfɩɩn sɩ o yi Zeruzalɛm tɩn, o ma da cwǝŋǝ kalʋ na kwe ka tu Olivi piu kʋm seeni tɩn. O karabiǝ kɔgɔ kʋm maama laan ma puli sɩ ba taa zuli Baŋa-Wɛ dɩ kwǝr-dɛ. Ba maa tee Wɛ dɩ wʋpolo, dɩ wo-kamunnu tɩlʋ zanzan DƖ na kɩ yɩ ba yiǝ na tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: They will say, Heres the person who began construction and couldnt complete it.
local: yɩ ba wɩ: ‹Nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ puli digǝ lɔɔm, yɩ o daa warɩ-ka o guri.›.

eng: They said, Blessings on the king who comes in the name of the Lord. Peace in heaven and glory in the highest heavens.
local: yɩ ba wɩ: «Zuli-na pɛ wʋlʋ na tu dɩ́ Yuutu Wɛ yɩrɩ ŋwaanɩ tɩn. Ywǝǝni taa wʋ Wɛ-sɔŋɔ nɩ. Zulǝ dɩ taa wʋ Baŋa-Wɛ tee nɩ.».

eng: Or what king would go to war against another king without first sitting down to consider whether his ten thousand soldiers could go up against the twenty thousand coming against him?.
local: Asawɛ, pɛ na lagɩ o kɩ jara dɩ pɛ wʋdoŋ, o maŋɩ sɩ o da yigǝ o jǝni o bwɛ o nii, nɔɔna mʋrr-fugǝ kalʋ na wʋ o kwaga nɩ tɩn wʋ́ wanɩ ba jeeri nɔɔna mʋrr-fiinle kalʋ na wʋ pɛ wʋdoŋ wʋm kwaga nɩ tɩn naa ba bá wanɩ. Kʋ daɩ cɩga na?.

eng: Some of the Pharisees from the crowd said to Jesus, Teacher, scold your disciples! Tell them to stop.
local: Farizɩan tiinǝ badonnǝ na tɔgɩ ba wʋ kɔgɔ kʋm wʋnɩ tɩn ma ta dɩ Zezi ba wɩ: «Karanyɩna, bagɩ n karabiǝ bam sɩ ba yɩ taa tɛ kʋntʋ doŋ.».

eng: And if he didnt think he could win, he would send a representative to discuss terms of peace while his enemy was still a long way off.
local: O nan na bá wanɩ, pɛ wʋdoŋ wʋm ta na wʋ yigǝ yigǝ o bɩɩnɩ tɩn, oó da yigǝ o tʋŋɩ tɩntʋŋna mʋ o tee nɩ, sɩ ba koor-o sɩ o yagɩ jara yam sɩ ya ti.

eng: He answered, I tell you, if they were silent, the stones would shout.
local: O ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «A lagɩ a ta abam sɩ, bantʋ na cɩm, kandwa yam tɩtɩ wʋ́ kaasɩ dɩ kwǝr-dɛ.».

eng: In the same way, none of you who are unwilling to give up all of your possessions can be my disciple.
local: Kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ yɩ bɩdwɩ mʋ dɩ abam wʋlʋ maama na wʋ kwe o woŋo maama o yagɩ daa nɩ, o bá wanɩ o ji amʋ karabu.

eng: As Jesus came to the city and observed it, he wept over it.
local: Zezi ma vu o twɛ Zeruzalɛm. O na nɛ tɩʋ kʋm tɩn, o ma keeri kʋ nɔɔna bam ŋwaanɩ.

eng: Salt is good. But if salt loses its flavor, how will it become salty again?.
local: Yɛ ywǝnǝ yɩ dɩ waɩ dɩ kwɛ wʋdiu. Yɛ ywǝǝni nan na ti, n daa n bá wanɩ n pa dɩ joori dɩ ja ywǝǝni.

eng: He said, If only you knew on this of all days the things that lead to peace. But now they are hidden from your eyes.
local: yɩ o wɩ: «Zeruzalɛm tiinǝ-ba, á ya na lwarɩ zɩm dɛ dɩm nɩ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ ya na wʋ́ pa sɩ á taá zʋʋrɩ dɩ ywǝǝni tɩn, kʋ ya wʋ́ ta lana. Wo-laarʋ tɩm kʋntʋ nan sǝgi abam tee nɩ mʋ.

eng: It has no value, neither for the soil nor for the manure pile. People throw it away. Whoever has ears to hear should pay attention.
local: Dɩntʋ daa ba jɩgɩ kuri. N bá wanɩ n kɩ-dɩ n kadugǝ nɩ, naa n ma n kɩ pʋpɔnɔ. N maŋɩ sɩ n lo-dɩ n yagɩ mʋ. Abam nan fɔgɩ á cǝgi amʋ taanɩ dɩm.».

eng: The time will come when your enemies will build fortifications around you, encircle you, and attack you from all sides.
local: Taá ye-na sɩ maŋa wʋ́ ba, yɩ á dʋna wʋ́ ba ba gilimi á tɩʋ kʋm maama. Baá cɩ kʋ niǝ yam maama sɩ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ daa yɩ wanɩ o nuŋi kʋ wʋnɩ.

eng: They will crush you completely, you and the people within you. They wont leave one stone on top of another within you, because you didnt recognize the time of your gracious visit from God.
local: yɩ baá tiini ba cɔgɩ tɩʋ kʋm dɩdaanɩ abam balʋ na zʋʋrɩ kʋ wʋnɩ tɩn. Ba na cɔgɩ-kʋ ba ti, tɩʋ kʋm kandwɛ daa bá daŋɩ dɩdoŋ baŋa nɩ. Kʋ yɩ abam na wʋ lwarɩ nɩ maŋa kam yiǝ, sɩ Wɛ ba DƖ vrɩ abam tɩn ŋwaanɩ mʋ wǝǝnu tɩm kʋntʋ maama wʋ́ kɩ abam baŋa nɩ.».

eng: When Jesus entered the temple, he threw out those who were selling things there.
local: Zezi ma vu o zʋ Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm wʋ. O ma na balʋ na je da ba pipi tɩn. O ma puli sɩ o zǝli-ba sɩ ba nuŋi.

eng: He said to them, Its written, My house will be a house of prayer, but you have made it a hideout for crooks. .
local: O ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Kʋ pʋpʋnɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ, nɩ Wɛ mʋ wɩ: ‹Amʋ digǝ kantʋ maŋɩ sɩ ka taa yɩ jǝgǝ kalʋ nɔɔna na wʋ́ ta tui ba warɩ amʋ da tɩn mʋ.› Abam nan pɛ ka ji ŋwɩɩna joro jǝgǝ.».

eng: Jesus was teaching daily in the temple. The chief priests, the legal experts, and the foremost leaders among the people were seeking to kill him.
local: Dɛ maama Zezi maa wʋ Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm wʋnɩ o brɩ nɔɔna Wɛ taanɩ dɩm. Kʋ daarɩ Zwifǝ kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ dɩ ba cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ bam dɩ tɩʋ kʋm dɩdɛɛra bam maa kwaana, sɩ ba na cwǝŋǝ ba gʋ-o.

eng: However, they couldnt find a way to do it because all the people were enthralled with what they heard.
local: Ba maa yǝri ba na wʋ́ kɩ te, bɛŋwaanɩ nɔɔna bam maama cǝgi o taanɩ dɩm lanyɩranɩ.

eng: Jesus was telling them a parable about their need to pray continuously and not to be discouraged.
local: Zezi dɛɛn ma maŋɩ mɩmaŋa o brɩ o karabiǝ bam nɩ ba maŋɩ sɩ ba taa loori Wɛ taan mʋ, sɩ ba yɩ yagɩ.

eng: He said, In a certain city there was a judge who neither feared God nor respected people.
local: O ma maŋɩ o brɩ-ba o wɩ: «Tɩʋ kʋdoŋ nɩ mʋ sarɩya-diru wʋdoŋ dɛɛn ya wʋra, yɩ o ba kwarɩ Wɛ. O maa ba nɩgɩ nɔɔna dɩ.

eng: In that city there was a widow who kept coming to him, asking, Give me justice in this case against my adversary.
local: Kadǝm wʋdoŋ dɩ maa wʋ tɩʋ kʋm kʋntʋ nɩ. O maa yǝni o ve sarɩya-diru wʋm te maŋa maama, o loor-o zǝnǝ o wɩ: ‹Nan kwaanɩ n wǝli-nɩ, sɩ n pa a na bʋra a dʋm wʋm tee nɩ.›.

eng: For a while he refused but finally said to himself, I dont fear God or respect people.
local: Sarɩya-diru wʋm maa ba sɛ o taanɩ dɩm. Kʋ maa wʋra kʋ wʋra, o laan ma ta o tɩtɩ wʋnɩ o wɩ: ‹A ba kwarɩ Wɛ. A ba nɩgɩ nɔɔna dɩ.

eng: but I will give this widow justice because she keeps bothering me. Otherwise, there will be no end to her coming here and embarrassing me.
local: Dɩ kʋ dɩ, kadǝm wʋntʋ nan ta jɩgɩ-nɩ o yaara maŋa maama dɩ o taanɩ dɩm. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ a maŋɩ sɩ a pa-o bʋra. Kʋ na daɩ kʋntʋ, oó ta tui a te o yaarɩ-nɩ maŋa maama.› ».

eng: The Lord said, Listen to what the unjust judge says.
local: Dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi laan ma ta o wɩ: «Cǝgi-na sarɩya-diru balɔrɔ kʋm na tagɩ te tɩn.

eng: Wont God provide justice to his chosen people who cry out to him day and night? Will he be slow to help them?.
local: Sɩ kʋ laan nan na yɩ Wɛ, DƖ bá sɛ sɩ DƖ wǝli DƖ tɩtɩ nɔɔna balʋ DƖ na kuri tɩn sɩ ba na cɩga na? DƖ bá kɩ lɩla DƖ wǝli balʋ na tiini ba loori-DƖ maŋa maama wɩa dɩ tɩtɩɩ tɩn na?.

eng: I tell you, he will give them justice quickly. But when the Human Onecomes, will he find faithfulness on earth.
local: A lagɩ a ta abam sɩ, DƖ wʋ́ pa-ba cɩga lɩla. Kʋ daarɩ, amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm na joori a ba lʋgʋ baŋa, nɔɔna ta wʋ́ ta jɩgɩ wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ amʋ na?».

eng: Jesus told this parable to certain people who had convinced themselves that they were righteous and who looked on everyone else with disgust.
local: Zezi daa ma maŋɩ mɩmaŋɩ dɩdoŋ, sɩ o ma brɩ balʋ na bʋŋɩ ba paɩ ba yɩ nɔn-ŋʋna Wɛ tee nɩ yɩ ba gooni ba donnǝ tɩn.

eng: Two people went up to the temple to pray. One was a Pharisee and the other a tax collector.
local: O ma maŋɩ o brɩ-ba o wɩ: «Nɔɔna bale mʋ wʋra. Ba dɩdʋa yɩ Farizɩan tu mʋ. Wʋdoŋ wʋm maa yɩ lampo-joŋnu. Ba ma zaŋɩ ba vu Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm sɩ ba warɩ Wɛ.

eng: The Pharisee stood and prayed about himself with these words, God, I thank you that Im not like everyone else—crooks, evildoers, adulterers—or even like this tax collector.
local: Ba na zʋ tɩn, Farizɩan tu wʋm ma zɩgɩ o warɩ Wɛ o wʋnɩ o wɩ: ‹Wɛ, a kɩ nmʋ le dɩ amʋ na daɩ nɩnɛɛnɩ nɔɔna badaara balʋ na yɩ ŋwɩɩna dɩ wo-lwaan-kǝrǝ dɩ ka-lagɩna te tɩn. A kɩ nmʋ le dɩ a na daɩ nɩnɛɛnɩ lampo-joŋnu wʋntʋ dɩ te tɩn.

eng: I fast twice a week. I give a tenth of everything I receive.
local: A yǝni a vɔ ni kuni bɩle mʋ da yarpɛ maama wʋnɩ. Wǝǝnu tɩlʋ maama a na nɛ tɩn, a yǝni a lɩ nmʋ tǝri tɩ wʋnɩ a pa nmʋ.›.

eng: But the tax collector stood at a distance. He wouldnt even lift his eyes to look toward heaven. Rather, he struck his chest and said, God, show mercy to me, a sinner.
local: Kʋ daarɩ lampo-joŋnu wʋm maa zɩgɩ daa nɩ o warɩ Wɛ. O maa ba lagɩ sɩ o kwǝni o yuu wɛɛnɩ, yɩ o magɩ o japoorǝ Wɛ yigǝ nɩ o wɩ: ‹Wɛ, amʋ yɩ lwarɩm tu mʋ. Nan duri a yibwǝnǝ.›.

eng: I tell you, this person went down to his home justified rather than the Pharisee. All who lift themselves up will be brought low, and those who make themselves low will be lifted up.
local: Kʋntʋ tɩn, a nan lagɩ a ta dɩ abam sɩ, nɔɔna bantʋ bale na warɩ Wɛ ba ti yɩ ba laan joori sɔŋɔ tɩn, kʋ yɩ lampo-joŋnu wʋm mʋ nɛ cɩga Wɛ tee nɩ, sɩ kʋ daɩ Farizɩan tu wʋm. Bɛŋwaanɩ, wʋlʋ na zǝŋi o tɩtɩ tɩn, Wɛ wʋ́ tu kʋntʋ tu. Wʋlʋ nan na tu o tɩtɩ tɩn, Wɛ wʋ́ zǝŋi kʋntʋ tu.».

eng: People were bringing babies to Jesus so that he would bless them. When the disciples saw this, they scolded them.
local: Nɔɔna badonnǝ maa jaanɩ ba bu-balwa ba tui Zezi te, sɩ o daŋɩ o jɩa ba baŋa nɩ o warɩ Wɛ o pa-ba. O karabiǝ bam na nɛ kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma zaŋɩ ba kaanɩ-ba sɩ ba yɩ taa kɩ kʋntʋ.

eng: Then Jesus called them to him and said, Allow the children to come to me. Dont forbid them, because Gods kingdom belongs to people like these children.
local: Zezi laan ma bǝŋi biǝ bam sɩ ba ba o te, yɩ o wɩ: «Yagɩ-na sɩ biǝ bam taa tui a te, sɩ á yɩ zaŋɩ á cɩ-ba. Bɛŋwaanɩ balʋ na nyɩ dɩ bu-balwa te tɩn mʋ maŋɩ sɩ ba zʋ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋ.

eng: I assure you that whoever doesnt welcome Gods kingdom like a child will never enter it.
local: A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, nɔɔnʋ na wʋ sɛ sɩ Wɛ taa te-o nɩ bu-balaŋa te, o bá na cwǝŋǝ sɩ o zʋ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋ.».

eng: A certain ruler asked Jesus, Good Teacher, what must I do to obtain eternal life.
local: Zwifǝ yigǝ tu wʋdoŋ dɛɛn ma bwe Zezi o wɩ: «Karanyɩna, n yɩ nɔn-ŋʋm. Brɩ-nɩ kʋlʋ a na wʋ́ kɩ, sɩ a ma a na ŋwɩa kalʋ na ba ti tɩn Wɛ tee nɩ.».

eng: Jesus replied, Why do you call me good? No one is good except the one God.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Bɛŋwaanɩ mʋ n bǝŋi-nɩ nɩ nɔn-ŋʋm? Nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ tǝrǝ o na yɩ nɔn-ŋʋm, kʋ na daɩ Wɛ DƖ yɩranɩ.

eng: You know the commandments: Dont commit adultery. Dont murder. Dont steal. Dont give false testimony. Honor your father and mother. .
local: Nmʋ maŋɩ n ye Wɛ cullu tɩm na brɩ kʋlʋ tɩn. Yɩ zaŋɩ n pǝni dɩ n doŋ kaanɩ. Yɩ zaŋɩ n gʋ nɔɔnʋ. Yɩ zaŋɩ n ŋɔ. Yɩ fɔ vwan n pa nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ. Ta n zuli n nu dɩ n ko.».

eng: Then the ruler said, Ive kept all of these things since I was a boy.
local: Zwifǝ yigǝ tu wʋm ma ta o wɩ: «Kʋ zɩgɩ amʋ biini nɩ mʋ a maŋɩ a tɔgɩ cullu tɩntʋ maama.».

eng: When Jesus heard this, he said, Theres one more thing. Sell everything you own and distribute the money to the poor. Then you will have treasure in heaven. And come, follow me.
local: Zezi na ni o na tagɩ kʋlʋ tɩn, o ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Kʋ nan ta gɛ woŋo dɩdʋa mʋ sɩ n kɩ. Kwe wǝǝnu tɩlʋ maama n na jɩgɩ tɩn n yǝgi, sɩ n laan maŋɩ tɩ sǝbu kʋm n pa yinigǝ tiinǝ. Kʋntʋ wʋ́ pa n ta n jɩgɩ jɩjɩgɩrʋ Wɛ-sɔŋɔ nɩ. Nmʋ na kɩ kʋntʋ, sɩ n laan ba n ta n tɔgɩ-nɩ.».

eng: When he heard these words, the man became sad because he was extremely rich.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋm na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, o wʋ ma cɔgɩ zanzan, o na yɩ jɩjɩgɩrʋ zanzan tu tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: When Jesus saw this, he said, Its very hard for the wealthy to enter Gods kingdom!.
local: Zezi ma lwarɩ o wʋ na cɔgɩ te tɩn. O ma ta o wɩ: «Nadunǝ na lagɩ sɩ ba zʋ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋ, kʋ sɩɩnɩ kʋ cana kʋ pa-ba.

eng: Its easier for a camel to squeeze through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter Gods kingdom.
local: Kʋʋ́ ta yɩ mwalɩ sɩ yogondi da gar-lwe bɔɔnɩ dɩ nuŋi kʋ dwe nadum na lagɩ sɩ o zʋ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋ.».

eng: Those who heard this said, Then who can be saved.
local: Nɔɔna balʋ na ni kʋlʋ Zezi na tagɩ tɩn ma bwe-o ba wɩ: «Kʋ nan na yɩ kʋntʋ, wɔɔ mʋ wʋ́ wanɩ o na vrɩm?».

eng: Jesus replied, What is impossible for humans is possible for God.
local: O ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Kʋlʋ nabiinǝ na warɩ ba kɩ tɩn, Wɛ wa-kʋ DƖ kɩa.».

eng: Peter said, Look, we left everything we own and followed you.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ laan ma ta o wɩ: «Nii. Dɩbam yagɩ dɩ́ wǝǝnu mʋ yɩ dɩ́ daarɩ dɩ́ tɔgɩ nmʋ.».

eng: Jesus said to them, I assure you that anyone who has left house, husband, wife, brothers, sisters, parents, or children because of Gods kingdom.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ maama na tagɩ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm ŋwaanɩ yɩ o yagɩ o sɔŋɔ naa o kaanɩ naa o nu-biǝ naa o nu dɩ o ko naa o biǝ tɩn.

eng: will receive many times more in this age and eternal life in the coming age.
local: kʋntʋ tu wʋ́ sɩɩnɩ o joŋi kʋ dwǝni kʋlʋ maama o na yagɩ tɩn maŋa kantʋ nɩ. Kʋ daarɩ maŋa kalʋ na wʋ́ ba tɩn, kʋntʋ tu wʋ́ na ŋwɩa kalʋ na ba ti tɩn Wɛ tee nɩ.».

eng: Jesus took the Twelve aside and said, Look, were going up to Jerusalem, and everything written about the Human Oneby the prophets will be accomplished.
local: Zezi dɛɛn ma pɔɔrɩ o karabiǝ fugǝ-bale bam kɔgɔ kʋm wʋnɩ, yɩ o ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Taá ye-na sɩ, dɩ́ maa ve Zeruzalɛm, yɩ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ maama faŋa faŋa Wɛ nijoŋnǝ bam na pʋpʋnɩ amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm ŋwaanɩ tɩn wʋ́ kɩ.

eng: He will be handed over to the Gentiles. He will be ridiculed, mistreated, and spit on.
local: Baá ja amʋ ba kɩ dwi-gɛ tiinǝ jɩŋa nɩ. Baá mwanɩ amʋ, baá bɛɛsɩ amʋ, baá twɩ lileeru ba yagɩ a yɩra nɩ.

eng: After torturing him, they will kill him. On the third day, he will rise up.
local: baá kwe balaarɩ ba ma magɩ-nɩ, yɩ ba laan daarɩ ba gʋ-nɩ. Da yatɔ dɛ nɩ, a laan wʋ́ joori a bi a yagɩ tʋʋnɩ.».

eng: But the Twelve understood none of these words. The meaning of this message was hidden from them and they didnt grasp what he was saying.
local: Zezi na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, ba wʋ ni o taanɩ dɩm kuri. Ba maa yǝri o na jɩgɩ kʋlʋ o ŋɔɔnɩ tɩn, kʋ kuri na sǝgi dɩ ba tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: As Jesus came to Jericho, a certain blind man was sitting beside the road begging.
local: Zezi dɛɛn ma vu o twɛ tɩʋ kʋdoŋ, kʋ yɩrɩ mʋ Zeriko. Lilwe maa je cwǝŋǝ kam ni nɩ o loorǝ.

eng: When the man heard the crowd passing by, he asked what was happening.
local: O ma ni nɔn-kɔgɔ na tɔgɩ da kʋ maa kɛa. O ma bwe sɩ o lwarɩ kʋ na yɩ kʋlʋ tɩn.

eng: They told him, Jesus the Nazarene is passing by.
local: Ba ma ta ba brɩ-o sɩ kʋ yɩ Nazarɛtɩ tu Zezi mʋ tɔgɩ dáanɩ o maa kɛa.

eng: The blind man shouted, Jesus, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: O ma tɔɔlɩ o bǝŋi o wɩ: «Zezi, Pɛ Davidi dwi tu, duri a yibwǝnǝ!».

eng: Those leading the procession scolded him, telling him to be quiet, but he shouted even louder, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: Nɔɔna balʋ na wʋ kɔgɔ kʋm yigǝ nɩ ba maa ve tɩn ma kaan-o sɩ o pu o ni. O laan ma tiini o tɔɔlɩ baŋa baŋa o wɩ: «Pɛ Davidi dwi tu, duri a yibwǝnǝ!».

eng: Jesus stopped and called for the man to be brought to him. When he was present Jesus asked.
local: Zezi ma zɩgɩ, yɩ o pa ni sɩ ba vaŋɩ lilwe wʋm ba ja ba o te. O na yi o te tɩn, Zezi ma bwe-o o wɩ:.

eng: What do you want me to do for you? He said, Lord, I want to see.
local: «Nmʋ lagɩ sɩ a kɩ bɛɛ mʋ a pa-m?» O ma lǝri o wɩ: «A tu, a lagɩ sɩ a joori a taa na mʋ.».

eng: Jesus said to him, Receive your sight! Your faith has healed you.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Ta n naɩ. Nmʋ na kɩ n wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ amʋ te tɩn mʋ pɛ n na vrɩm.».

eng: At once he was able to see, and he began to follow Jesus, praising God. When all the people saw it, they praised God too.
local: Zezi na tagɩ kʋntʋ bɩdwɩ baŋa nɩ tɩn, mʋ lilwe wʋm yiǝ joori ya naɩ. O ma zaŋɩ o tɔgɩ Zezi, yɩ o zuli Wɛ lanyɩranɩ. Nɔɔna bam maama na nɛ kʋlʋ na kɩ tɩn, ba dɩ maa tee Wɛ.

eng: Looking up, Jesus saw rich people throwing their gifts into the collection box for the temple treasury.
local: Zezi ta na wʋ Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm wʋnɩ o brɩ nɔɔna bam kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma kwǝni o yuu wɛɛnɩ o nii je maama. O ma na nadunǝ na ve ba dɩ ba sǝbiǝ daka kalʋ na zɩgɩ da Wɛ pɛɛra ŋwaanɩ tɩn wʋnɩ.

eng: He also saw a poor widow throw in two small copper coins worth a penny.
local: O daa ta ma na kadǝm wʋdoŋ na tɔgɩ o wʋra, o yɩ yinigǝ tu mʋ. Wʋntʋ ma ja sǝbu dalɩ-balwa bale yɩranɩ mʋ o ja vu o dɩ daka kam wʋnɩ.

eng: He said, I assure you that this poor widow has put in more than them all.
local: Zezi na nɛ kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma ta o wɩ: «A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, kadǝm wʋntʋ na yɩ yinigǝ tu tɩn, o dɩ sǝbu kʋ dwǝni balʋ maama na dɩ da tɩn.

eng: All of them are giving out of their spare change. But she from her hopeless poverty has given everything she had to live on.
local: Bantʋ maama lɩ ba daarɩ mʋ. Kadǝm wʋm nan yɩ yinigǝ tu mʋ, yɩ o kwe kʋlʋ maama o na jɩgɩ sɩ o ma yǝgi o ni-wʋdiu tɩn o dɩ da.».

eng: Some people were talking about the temple, how it was decorated with beautiful stones and ornaments dedicated to God. Jesus said.
local: Nɔɔna badonnǝ dɛɛn ma tɛ Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm taanɩ yɩ ba wɩ: «Nii-na kandwa yam ba na mɛ ba lɔ-ka tɩn na tiini ya lana te, yɩ ba ma pɛɛra yalʋ ba na pɛ Wɛ tɩn ba ma kɩ-ka lanyɩranɩ.» Zezi ma lǝri-ba o wɩ:.

eng: As for the things you are admiring, the time is coming when not even one stone will be left upon another. All will be demolished.
local: «Kʋ na yɩ wǝǝnu tɩntʋ á na naɩ tɩn, maŋa wʋ́ ba ba, sɩ ba dɩ tɩ maama tɩga nɩ. Kandwɛ daa bá daŋɩ dɩdoŋ baŋa nɩ.».

eng: They asked him, Teacher, when will these things happen? What sign will show that these things are about to happen.
local: Ba ma bwe-o ba wɩ: «Karanyɩna, maŋa kɔɔ mʋ kʋntʋ woŋo wʋ́ kɩ? Bɛɛ mʋ lagɩ kʋ kɩ maana kʋ brɩ dɩbam, sɩ dɩ́ lwarɩ nɩ wǝǝnu tɩntʋ lagɩ tɩ kɩ?».

eng: Jesus said, Watch out that you arent deceived. Many will come in my name, saying, Im the one! and Its time! Dont follow them.
local: Zezi ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Á fɔgɩ á cɩ á tɩtɩ, sɩ á yɩ pa nɔɔna ganɩ abam. Nɔɔna zanzan wʋ́ ba abam te dɩ amʋ yɩrɩ, ba ta dɩ abam nɩ ba dɩdʋa dɩdʋa mʋ yɩ Krisi wʋm Wɛ na tʋŋɩ tɩn, yɩ baá daarɩ ba ganɩ abam ba wɩ, maŋa kam yiǝ. Á nan yɩ sɛ á tɔgɩ bantʋ kwaga.

eng: When you hear of wars and rebellions, dont be alarmed. These things must happen first, but the end wont happen immediately.
local: Á wʋ́ ba á ni tigurǝ dɩ jara ŋwa. Á nan yɩ pa fʋʋnɩ zʋ abam. Wǝǝnu tɩm kʋntʋ maama maŋɩ sɩ tɩ da yigǝ tɩ kɩ mʋ, yɩ kʋ ta daɩ lʋgʋ tiim mɩmaŋa.».

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Nations and kingdoms will fight against each other.
local: O daa ta ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Dwi wʋ́ zaŋɩ dɩ kɩ jara dɩ dwi dɩdoŋ, yɩ tɩʋ dɩ kʋ doŋ tɩʋ wʋ́ zaŋɩ tɩ magɩ jara.

eng: There will be great earthquakes and wide-scale food shortages and epidemics. There will also be terrifying sights and great signs in the sky.
local: Tɩga ma wʋ́ sisiŋi zanzan. Kana dɩ wʋ́ ba ka taa wʋ je sɩdonnǝ nɩ, yɩ yawɩ-loŋnu dɩ wʋ́ cɔgɩ nɔɔna zanzan. Wɛ wʋ́ pa wo-kɩnkagɩla yalʋ na brɩ DƖ dam tɩn taa kɩ wɛyuu nɩ, yɩ fʋʋnɩ wʋ́ ja nɔɔna ya ŋwaanɩ.

eng: But before all this occurs, they will take you into custody and harass you because of your faith. They will hand you over to synagogues and prisons, and you will be brought before kings and governors because of my name.
local: Kʋ na wʋ́ loori wǝǝnu tɩntʋ kǝm tɩn, nɔɔna wʋ́ ja abam dam ba pa á na yaara. Baá ja abam ba vu ba saŋɩ ba Wɛ-di sɩm nakwa tee nɩ, yɩ ba kɩ abam pɩɩna digǝ nɩ. Baá ja abam ba pa á zɩgɩ pwa dɩ dɩdɛɛra yigǝ nɩ, abam na tɔgɩ amʋ tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: This will provide you with an opportunity to testify.
local: Kʋ nan wʋ́ pa á na pwǝlǝ sɩ á brɩ-ba Wɛ cɩga kam.

eng: Make up your minds not to prepare your defense in advance.
local: Taá ye-na kʋntʋ ni nɩ, sɩ á pa á bɩcara kɩ dam. Á yɩ taá kɩ liǝ, dɩ á na wʋ́ lǝri-ba tɩte sɩ á ma vrɩ á tɩtɩ tɩn.

eng: Ill give you words and wisdom that none of your opponents will be able to counter or contradict.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ amʋ wʋ́ pa abam wʋbʋŋ-ŋʋna dɩ á ni-taanɩ, kʋʋ́ pa á dʋna bá wanɩ ba twanɩ dɩ á na wʋ́ ta kʋlʋ tɩn.

eng: You will be betrayed by your parents, brothers and sisters, relatives, and friends. They will execute some of you.
local: Abam tɩtɩ kwǝ dɩ á niinǝ, dɩ á cʋrrʋ dɩ á cilonnǝ wʋ́ ja abam ba kɩ dɩdɛɛra jɩŋa nɩ, yɩ baá gʋ abam badonnǝ.

eng: Everyone will hate you because of my name.
local: Nɔɔna maama wʋ́ ta culi abam á na yɩ a nɔɔna tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: Still, not a hair on your heads will be lost.
local: Baá kɩ abam kʋntʋ, yɩ Wɛ ta wʋ́ ta nii á baŋa nɩ, yɩ á bá lagɩ á yuuywe sɩm dɩdʋa tɩtɩ dɩ á ga.

eng: By holding fast, you will gain your lives.
local: Abam nan na kwaanɩ á kɩ pu-dɩa á zɩgɩ kǝŋkǝŋ, á wʋ́ na ŋwɩa Wɛ tee nɩ.

eng: When you see Jerusalem surrounded by armies, then you will know that its destruction is close at hand.
local: Abam nan wʋ́ ba á na pamaŋna na gilimi Zeruzalɛm tɩʋ kʋm maama ba pu. Á na nɛ kʋntʋ, á laan wʋ́ lwarɩ nɩ maŋa kam yiǝ sɩ ba cɔgɩ-kʋ.

eng: At that time, those in Judea must flee to the mountains, those in the city must escape, and those in the countryside must not enter the city.
local: Maŋa kam kʋntʋ nɩ, abam balʋ na zʋʋrɩ Zude nɩ tɩn maŋɩ sɩ á kɩ lɩla á nuŋi á duri á beeri pweeru je á sǝgi da. Balʋ na wʋ tɩʋ kʋm wʋnɩ tɩn maŋɩ sɩ á nuŋi lɩla. Balʋ dɩ na wʋ ba karɩ nɩ tɩn daa yɩ ve á zʋ tɩʋ kʋm wʋ.

eng: These are the days of punishment, when everything written will find its fulfillment.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ maŋa kantʋ nɩ mʋ yaara maŋɩ sɩ ya ba, Wɛ na wʋ́ vaŋɩ nɔɔna zwa tɩn ŋwaanɩ, sɩ kʋlʋ maama na pʋpʋnɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ tɩn laan kɩ.

eng: How terrible it will be at that time for women who are pregnant or for women who are nursing their children. There will be great agony on the earth and angry judgment on this people.
local: Kantʋ maŋa kam nɩ kʋʋ́ tiini kʋ taa yɩ lɛɛrʋ dɩ kaana balʋ na jɩgɩ pwi tɩn, dɩ balʋ na jɩgɩ biǝ yɩ ba ŋɔgɩ yɩlɩ tɩn. Yaara zanzan wʋ́ ba tɩʋ kʋntʋ dɩ kʋ nɔɔna maama baŋa nɩ, yɩ Baŋa-Wɛ wʋ́ kwe DƖ bam ba yɩra nɩ.

eng: They will fall by the edge of the sword and be taken away as captives among all nations. Jerusalem will be plundered by Gentiles until the times of the Gentiles are concluded.
local: Ba ma wʋ́ gʋ ba badonnǝ dɩ sʋ-zɔɔrʋ, yɩ ba daarɩ ba ja ba badonnǝ ba vɔ ba ja vu gambɛɛm dwi tiinǝ maama tɩtarɩ nɩ. Dwi-gɛ tiinǝ laan wʋ́ ba zʋ Zeruzalɛm tɩʋ kʋm wʋ ba karɩ ba nɔnɩ kʋ wǝǝnu ba cɔga taan, sɩ kʋ vu kʋ yi maŋa kalʋ Wɛ na pɛ-ba tɩn tiim.».

eng: There will be signs in the sun, moon, and stars. On the earth, there will be dismay among nations in their confusion over the roaring of the sea and surging waves.
local: «Maŋa wʋ́ ba sɩ Wɛ pa wo-kɩnkagɩla yalʋ na brɩ DƖ dam tɩn taa kɩ wɩa wʋnɩ dɩ cana dɩ calɩcwɩ sɩm wʋnɩ. Liǝ wʋ́ ja lʋgʋ baŋa dwi tiinǝ maama, yɩ ba wʋbʋŋa vugimi dɩ nɩnɩʋ kʋm na bam na pɔgɩla dɩ sɔɔ zanzan tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: The planets and other heavenly bodies will be shaken, causing people to faint from fear and foreboding of what is coming upon the world.
local: Fʋʋnɩ zanzan wʋ́ pa nɔɔna wʋbʋŋa je ba na bʋŋɩ woŋo kʋlʋ na bɩɩnɩ lʋgʋ baŋa tɩn, bɛŋwaanɩ wɛyuu wʋ́ sisiŋi kʋ pa wǝǝnu tɩlʋ na wʋ wɛyuu nɩ tɩn maama vugimi daanɩ.

eng: Then they will see the Human Onecoming on a cloud with power and great splendor.
local: Maŋa kam kʋntʋ nɩ nɔɔna wʋ́ na Nabiin-bu wʋm na maa bɩɩnɩ lʋgʋ baŋa. Oó ta wʋ kunkwǝǝnu wʋnɩ o bɩɩnɩ dɩ dam, yɩ Wɛ paarɩ-zulǝ wʋ o tee nɩ zanzan.

eng: Now when these things begin to happen, stand up straight and raise your heads, because your redemption is near.
local: Á na nɛ sɩ wǝǝnu tɩm kʋntʋ puli tɩ kɩa, sɩ á zaŋɩ á zɩgɩ lanyɩranɩ á kwǝni á yum wɛɛnɩ, bɛŋwaanɩ maŋa kam twɛ sɩ Wɛ vrɩ abam DƖ yagɩ.».

eng: Jesus told them a parable: Look at the fig tree and all the trees.
local: Zezi laan ma maŋɩ mɩmaŋa o brɩ-ba o wɩ: «Maanɩ-na á nii kapʋrʋ dɩ tweeru maama na yɩ te tɩn.

eng: When they sprout leaves, you can see for yourselves and know that summer is near.
local: Á na nɛ nɩ tweeru wʋra tɩ kwi vɔɔrʋ, abam waɩ á lwarɩ nɩ kʋ twɛ yadɛ.

eng: In the same way, when you see these things happening, you know that Gods kingdom is near.
local: Kʋ nan yɩ bɩdwɩ mʋ dɩ á na nɛ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ a na tagɩ a brɩ abam tɩn na wʋra tɩ kɩa, sɩ á lwarɩ nɩ maŋa kam laan twɛ sɩ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm lagɩ dɩ ba nɔɔna tɩtarɩ nɩ.

eng: On one of the days when Jesus was teaching the people in the temple and proclaiming the good news, the chief priests, legal experts, and elders approached him.
local: Dɛ dɩdwɩ mʋ Zezi dɛɛn wʋ Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm wʋnɩ, o tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam o brɩ nɔɔna. Zwifǝ bam kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ dɩ cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ bam dɩ ba tɩʋ kʋm nakwa bam ma vu ba yi o te.

eng: I assure you that this generation wont pass away until everything has happened.
local: A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, wǝǝnu tɩntʋ maama wʋ́ ba wʋra tɩ kɩa, yɩ zɩm nɔɔna bam ta wʋ tɩgɩ ba ti.

eng: They said, Tell us: What kind of authority do you have for doing these things? Who gave you this authority.
local: Ba ma ta dɩd-o ba wɩ: «Ta n brɩ dɩbam, wɔɔ dam mʋ n ma n kɩ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ maama n na kɩ tɩn? Wɔɔ mʋ pɛ nmʋ ni, sɩ n ta n kɩ kʋntʋ?».

eng: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will certainly not pass away.
local: Wɛyuu dɩ tɩga wʋ́ ba tɩ kɛ, yɩ amʋ taanɩ dɩm bá kɛ maŋa dɩ maŋa.

eng: He replied, I have a question for you. Tell me.
local: O ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «A dɩ jɩgɩ bwiǝ a lagɩ a bwe abam, sɩ á lǝri-ya á pa-nɩ.

eng: Take care that your hearts arent dulled by drinking parties, drunkenness, and the anxieties of day-to-day life. Dont let that day fall upon you unexpectedly.
