local: Á taá fɔgɩ á cʋ á tɩtɩ, sɩ á yɩ taá bʋŋɩ wʋdi-fwaarʋ dim naa sa-nyɔgɔ wʋbʋŋa, naa á taá jɩgɩ liǝ dɩ á ŋwɩa wǝǝnu. Á na jɩgɩ wʋbʋŋa yantʋ doŋ, dɛ dɩlʋ amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm na wʋ́ ba tɩn waɩ dɩ tui bɩdwɩ baŋa nɩ dɩ darɩ abam.

eng: Was Johns baptism of heavenly or of human origin.
local: Zan na miisi nɔɔna na wʋnɩ tɩn, kʋ yɩ Wɛ mʋ pɛ-o cwǝŋǝ naa nabiinǝ mʋ?».

eng: like a trap. It will come upon everyone who lives on the face of the whole earth.
local: nɩ cɩkʋ na jaanɩ varɩm te tɩn. Bɛŋwaanɩ dɛ dɩm kʋntʋ wʋ́ ba li nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ maama na wʋ lʋgʋ baŋa nɩ tɩn.

eng: They discussed among themselves, If we say, Its of heavenly origin, hell say, Why didnt you believe him.
local: O na bwe-ba kʋntʋ tɩn, ba maa wʋra ba bwɛ daanɩ sɩ ba nii, ba na wʋ́ lǝr-o te tɩn. Ba laan ma wɩ: «Dɩbam na lǝri dɩ́ wɩ, kʋ yɩ Wɛ mʋ, o laan wʋ́ bwe dɩbam nɩ, bɛɛ mʋ yɩ dɩ́ nan wʋ sɛ Zan taanɩ dɩm?.

eng: Stay alert at all times, praying that you are strong enough to escape everything that is about to happen and to stand before the Human One.
local: Kʋntʋ tɩn, á taá yɩrɩ á tɩtɩ maŋa maama, sɩ á taá loori Wɛ sɩ DƖ pa abam dam, sɩ á wanɩ á nuŋi yaara yalʋ maama na lagɩ ya ba tɩn wʋnɩ, sɩ á daarɩ á vu á zɩgɩ amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm yigǝ nɩ.».

eng: But if we say, Its of human origin, all the people will stone us to death because they are convinced that John was a prophet.
local: Dɩ́ nan na wɩ, kʋ yɩ nabiinǝ mʋ pɛ-o cwǝŋǝ, nɔɔna bam maama wʋ́ dʋlɩ dɩbam dɩ kandwa mʋ ba gʋ, bɛŋwaanɩ ba maama paɩ sɩ Zan yɩ Wɛ nijoŋnu mʋ.».

eng: Every day Jesus was teaching in the temple, but he spent each night on the Mount of Olives.
local: Zezi dɛɛn yǝni o wʋ Wɛ di-kamunu kʋm wʋnɩ dɛ maama o brɩ nɔɔna Wɛ taanɩ dɩm. Tɩga na yǝni ka yi, o yǝni o nuŋi tɩʋ kʋm wʋ o vu Olivi piu kʋm seeni o pǝni da.

eng: They answered that they didnt know where it came from.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ ba ma lǝri Zezi ba wɩ, ba yǝri wʋlʋ na pɛ Zan cwǝŋǝ tɩn.

eng: All the people rose early in the morning to hear him in the temple area.
local: Kʋ daarɩ tɩga na yǝni ka pʋʋrɩ, nɔɔna bam maama yǝni ba zaŋɩ lɩla ba ve Wɛ di-kamunu kʋm, sɩ ba taa cǝgi o taanɩ dɩm.

eng: Then Jesus replied, Neither will I tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Zezi laan ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Kʋ na yɩ kʋntʋ, amʋ dɩ bá sɛ sɩ a ta wʋlʋ na pɛ amʋ ni sɩ a taa tʋŋɩ tɩn.».

eng: Jesus told the people this parable: A certain man planted a vineyard, rented it to tenant farmers, and went on a trip for a long time.
local: Zezi ma maŋɩ mɩmaŋa o brɩ nɔɔna bam o wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ mʋ wʋra. O ma zaŋɩ o cɩ gaarɩ, yɩ o daarɩ o jǝri tweeru tɩlʋ yɩrɩ na yɩ vinyǝ tɩn dɩ wʋnɩ. O laan ma kwe nɔɔna o yagɩ da sɩ ba taa tʋŋa, yɩ o daarɩ o beeri o vu tɩʋ kʋdoŋ o daanɩ da.

eng: When it was time, he sent a servant to collect from the tenants his share of the fruit of the vineyard. But the tenants sent him away, beaten and empty-handed.
local: Tweeru tɩm biǝ gwǝrim maŋa ma yi. O ma tʋŋɩ o tɩntʋŋnʋ sɩ o vu nɔɔna balʋ na nii o gaarɩ dɩm baŋa nɩ tɩn te, sɩ ba cɛ o tǝri ba pa-o. Tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋm na yi ba te tɩn, ba ma mag-o. Ba daarɩ ba pa o viiri dɩ jɩ-kʋrɩ.

eng: The man sent another servant. But they beat him, treated him disgracefully, and sent him away empty-handed as well.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋm daa ma tʋŋɩ o tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋdoŋ. Ba ma magɩ wʋntʋ dɩ, yɩ ba twɩ-o zanzan. Ba ma pa o dɩ viiri dɩ jɩ-kʋrɩ.

eng: He sent a third servant. They wounded this servant and threw him out.
local: O daa ma tʋŋɩ tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋdoŋ o kɩ kuni bɩtɔ. Ba ma ja wʋntʋ dɩ ba magɩ o yɩra ba kaarɩ, yɩ ba zǝl-o gaarɩ dɩm wʋnɩ.

eng: The owner of the vineyard said, What should I do? Ill send my son, whom I love dearly. Perhaps they will respect him.
local: Gaarɩ dɩm tu wʋm laan ma bʋŋɩ o wɩ: ‹A nan wʋ́ kɩ ta mʋ? Amʋ na wʋ́ kɩ te tɩn, aá tʋŋɩ a tɩtɩ bu wʋlʋ a na so-o tɩn mʋ. Kʋ ba dwe, baá ta kwarɩ wʋntʋ.›.

eng: But when they saw him, they said to each other, This is the heir. Lets kill him so the inheritance will be ours.
local: Nɔɔna bam na nɛ-o tɩn, ba ma bwɛ daanɩ ba wɩ: ‹Mʋ gaarɩ dɩm tu bu. Wʋntʋ mʋ lagɩ o ba o taa te o ko wʋm wǝǝnu tɩm. Pa-na dɩ́ gʋ-o, sɩ dɩ́ daarɩ dɩ́ taá te o wǝǝnu tɩm.›.

eng: They threw him out of the vineyard and killed him. What will the owner of the vineyard do to them?.
local: Ba laan ma vaŋ-o ba ja nuŋi gaarɩ dɩm wʋnɩ, yɩ ba mag-o ba gʋ.» Zezi laan ma bwe-ba o wɩ: «Gaarɩ dɩm tu laan wʋ́ kɩ nɔɔna bam kʋntʋ ta mʋ?.

eng: He will come and destroy those tenants and give the vineyard to others. When the people heard this, they said, May this never happen.
local: O maŋɩ sɩ o ba o gʋ-ba mʋ, sɩ o daarɩ gaarɩ dɩm o pa nɔɔna badonnǝ sɩ ba taa niǝ.» Nɔɔna bam na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma ta ba wɩ: «Wɛ pa kʋ yɩ kɩ kʋntʋ!».

eng: Staring at them, Jesus said, Then what is the meaning of this text of scripture: The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone?.
local: Zezi ma nii ba seeni yɩ o wɩ: «Kʋ na daɩ kʋntʋ, bɛɛ mʋ yɩ kʋ pʋpʋnɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ kʋ wɩ: ‹Kandwɛ dɩlʋ lwara na vɩn tɩn laan mʋ joori dɩ ba dɩ ji kandwɛ dɩlʋ na paɩ sɔŋɔ kʋm dana tɩn›?.

eng: Everyone who falls on that stone will be crushed. And the stone will crush the person it falls on.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ maama na tʋ kandwɛ dɩntʋ baŋa nɩ tɩn wʋ́ cɩcʋgɩ o tɩtɩ mʋ. Kʋ daarɩ, wʋlʋ maama kandwɛ dɩm na tʋ o baŋa nɩ tɩn, dɩ wʋ́ mɩɩrɩ kʋntʋ tu mʋ.».

eng: The legal experts and chief priests wanted to arrest him right then because they knew he had told this parable against them. But they feared the people.
local: Zezi na tagɩ wǝǝnu tɩm kʋntʋ tɩn, Zwifǝ kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ dɩ cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ ma lwarɩ sɩ o maŋɩ mɩmaŋɩ dɩm bam ŋwaanɩ mʋ. Kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma kwaanɩ ba lagɩ cwǝŋǝ sɩ ba ja-o lɩla. Ba maa warɩ, ba na kwarɩ fʋʋnɩ dɩ nɔɔna bam tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: The legal experts and chief priests were watching Jesus closely and sent spies who pretended to be sincere. They wanted to trap him in his words so they could hand him over to the jurisdiction and authority of the governor.
local: Zwifǝ yigǝ tiinǝ bam dɛɛn ma yɩrɩ Zezi, sɩ ba daa ta nii o na wʋ́ ta kʋlʋ tɩn. Ba ma lagɩ cwǝŋǝ sɩ ba brɩ nɩ o dwǝgǝ, sɩ ba wanɩ ba ja-o, sɩ ba daarɩ ba kɩ-o gʋvɩrma tu wʋm jɩŋa nɩ sɩ o di o sarɩya. Ba ma lɩ nɔɔna sɩ ba vu Zezi te nɩnɛɛnɩ ba yɩ nɔn-ŋʋna mʋ te, sɩ ba laan kwaanɩ ba ja-o dɩ o taanɩ dɩm.

eng: They asked him, Teacher, we know that you are correct in what you say and teach. You dont show favoritism but teach Gods way as it really is.
local: Bantʋ ma vu Zezi te ba bwe-o ba wɩ: «Karanyɩna, dɩ́ ye nɩ taanɩ dɩlʋ nmʋ na yǝni n tɛ n brɩ nɔɔna tɩn yɩ cɩga mʋ. Nmʋ ba pɔɔrɩ nɔɔna daanɩ dɩ n na kɩ-ba te tɩn. Nmʋ nan yǝni n brɩ nɔɔna Wɛ cwǝŋǝ cɩga mʋ.

eng: Does the Law allow people to pay taxes to Caesar or not.
local: Dɩ́ lagɩ dɩ́ bwe dɩ́ nii, dɩbam cullu pɛ ni sɩ dɩ́ ŋwɩ lampoo dɩ́ pa lʋgʋ kʋm pa-farʋ wʋm na?».

eng: Since Jesus recognized their deception, he said to them.
local: Zezi ma lwarɩ ba na kɩ ba swan yam dɩd-o te tɩn, o ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ:.

eng: Show me a coin.Whose image and inscription does it have on it? Caesars, they replied.
local: «Kwe-na sǝbu dalɩ á brɩ-nɩ. Dɩ jɩgɩ wɔɔ nyɩnyʋgʋ dɩ o yɩrɩ mʋ dɩ baŋa nɩ?» Ba ma lǝr-o ba wɩ: «Kʋ yɩ pa-farʋ wʋm mʋ.».

eng: He said to them, Give to Caesar what belongs to Caesar and to God what belongs to God.
local: O laan ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Kʋ lamma. Á nan taá ŋwɩ kʋlʋ na yɩ pa-farʋ wʋm nyɩm tɩn á pa-o, sɩ á daarɩ á taá paɩ Wɛ kʋlʋ dɩ na yɩ DƖ nyɩm tɩn.».

eng: They couldnt trap him in his words in front of the people. Astonished by his answer, they were speechless.
local: Zezi na tagɩ kʋntʋ dɩ ba tɩn, ba maa warɩ ba ja-o nɔɔna bam maama yigǝ nɩ dɩ o na tagɩ kʋlʋ tɩn. O na lǝri-ba te tɩn sʋ-ba zanzan yɩ ba cɩm.

eng: Some Sadducees, who deny that theres a resurrection, came to Jesus and asked.
local: Sadusɩan tiinǝ badonnǝ dɛɛn ma zaŋɩ ba vu Zezi te. Bantʋ zaasɩm mʋ brɩ sɩ, nɔɔnʋ na tɩga, o daa bá joori o bi.

eng: Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a mans brother dies leaving a widow but no children, the brother must marry the widow and raise up children for his brother .
local: Ba ma vu ba bwe-o ba wɩ: «Karanyɩna, Moyisi dɛɛn na pʋpʋnɩ kʋlʋ o tiŋi o pa dɩbam tɩn, o brɩ sɩ, nɔɔnʋ nu-bu na tɩga yɩ o daarɩ kaanɩ, yɩ ba na wʋ lʋgɩ biǝ, tʋ wʋm cʋrʋ mʋ maŋɩ sɩ o di kaanɩ wʋm sɩ ba wanɩ ba lʋ biǝ o nu-bu wʋlʋ na tɩgɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: Now there were seven brothers. The first man married a woman and then died childless.
local: Kʋntʋ tɩn, nu-biǝ barpɛ mʋ ya wʋra. Ba dɩdʋa ma di kaanɩ yɩ o tɩ o daar-o, yɩ ba ba jɩgɩ biǝ daanɩ.

eng: The second.
local: Wʋlʋ na saŋɩ tɩn ma di kadǝm wʋm.

eng: and then the third brother married her. Eventually all seven married her, and they all died without leaving any children.
local: O dɩ ma tɩ o daar-o. Batɔ-tu wʋm dɩ daa ma di-o. Kʋ ma kɩ ba maama kʋntʋ doŋ. Ba maama barpɛ ma tɩ, yɩ ba wʋ lʋgɩ bu dɩ kaanɩ wʋm.

eng: Finally, the woman died too.
local: Ba maama na tɩgɩ tɩn, kaanɩ wʋm dɩ laan ma tɩ.

eng: In the resurrection, whose wife will she be? All seven were married to her.
local: Kʋntʋ tɩn, ba maama barpɛ na di kaanɩ wʋm tɩn, ba laan na tu ba bi ba yagɩ tʋʋnɩ, bantʋ wɔɔ mʋ wʋ́ ta te kaanɩ wʋm?».

eng: Jesus said to them, People who belong to this age marry and are given in marriage.
local: Zezi ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Kʋ na yɩ zɩm lʋgʋ baŋa nɔn-biǝ mʋ, baara di kaana yɩ kaana dɩ zʋʋrɩ banna.

eng: But those who are considered worthy to participate in that age, that is, in the age of the resurrection from the dead, wont marry nor will they be given in marriage.
local: Kʋ daarɩ, nɔɔna balʋ Wɛ na nii nɩ ba maŋɩ sɩ ba bi ba yagɩ tʋʋnɩ sɩ ba laan taa zʋʋrɩ DƖ lʋ-dʋŋʋ kʋm nɩ tɩn, bantʋ bá di kaana, yɩ kaana dɩ daa bá zʋ banna.

eng: They can no longer die, because they are like angels and are Gods children since they share in the resurrection.
local: Ba daa bá wanɩ ba tɩ, ba na wʋ́ ta nyɩ dɩ Wɛ malɛsɩ sɩm tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Ba yɩ Wɛ tɩtɩ biǝ mʋ, dɩ ba na bi ba yagɩ tʋʋnɩ tɩn.

eng: Even Moses demonstrated that the dead are raised—in the passage about the burning bush, when he speaks of the Lord as the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob .
local: Kʋ nan na yɩ twa biim, Moyisi maŋɩ o brɩ nɩ nɔɔna wʋ́ sɩɩnɩ ba bi. O dɛɛn pʋpʋnɩ maŋa kalʋ Wɛ na brɩ DƖ tɩtɩ dɩd-o sabarɩ puŋu kʋm wʋnɩ yɩ DƖ ta kʋlʋ tɩn. Moyisi ma brɩ sɩ Yuutu Baŋa-Wɛ yɩ Abraham Wɛ, dɩ Yizakɩ Wɛ, dɩ Zakɔbɩ Wɛ mʋ.

eng: He isnt the God of the dead but of the living. To him they are all alive.
local: Kʋ brɩ sɩ bantʋ daa ta jɩgɩ ŋwɩa mʋ Wɛ tee nɩ, bɛŋwaanɩ Baŋa-Wɛ yɩ naŋʋna Wɛ mʋ, sɩ DƖ daɩ twa Wɛ. Wɛ yibiyǝ nɩ, nɔɔna maama jɩgɩ ŋwɩa, dɩ balʋ na tɩgɩ tɩn dɩ.».

eng: Some of the legal experts responded, Teacher, you have answered well.
local: Wɛ cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ badonnǝ na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma lǝri ba wɩ: «Karanyɩna, n tagɩ cɩga.».

eng: No one dared to ask him anything else.
local: Ba dɛɛn ma kwarɩ sɩ ba daa bwe-o taanɩ.

eng: Jesus said to them, Why do they say that the Christ is Davids son?.
local: Zezi laan ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Nɔɔna yǝni ba wɩ, sɩ Krisi wʋm Wɛ na tʋŋɩ tɩn yɩ Pɛ Davidi dwi tu mʋ. Kʋ nan wʋ́ wanɩ kʋ taa yɩ kʋntʋ na?.

eng: David himself says in the scroll of Psalms, The Lord said to my lord, Sit at my right side.
local: Davidi tɩtɩ nan pʋpʋnɩ Wɛ lǝŋ-ŋwɩ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ o wɩ: ‹Yuutu Baŋa-Wɛ tagɩ dɩ a Yuutu DƖ wɩ: Ba n jǝni a jazɩm nɩ.

eng: until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet.
local: sɩ aá kɩ nmʋ dʋna maama n nɛ kuri nɩ sɩ n nɔ-ba nɩ tɩga te.›.

eng: Since David calls him Lord, how can he be Davids son.
local: Kʋntʋ tɩn, Davidi tɩtɩ na sɩɩnɩ o bǝŋi Krisi wʋm Wɛ na tʋŋɩ tɩn nɩ o Yuutu tɩn, kʋʋ́ kɩ ta mʋ sɩ o daa joori o taa yɩ Davidi naa?».

eng: In the presence of all the people, Jesus said to his disciples.
local: Nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm maama ta na wʋra kʋ cǝgi o taanɩ dɩm kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma ta dɩ o karabiǝ bam o wɩ:.

eng: Watch out for the legal experts. They like to walk around in long robes. They love being greeted with honor in the markets. They long for the places of honor in the synagogues and at banquets.
local: «Taá yɩrɩ-na á tɩtɩ dɩ cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ bam. Bantʋ soe sɩ ba taa zʋʋrɩ gwar-dɩdwaarʋ ba beerǝ, sɩ nɔɔna taa nii-ba. Ba na ve yaga, ba soe sɩ nɔɔna maama taa tui ba zuli-ba. Ba na yǝni ba zʋ ba Wɛ-di sɩm, ba ta lagɩ je-kamunnu mʋ sɩ ba taa je yigǝ nɩ. Ba na ve nɔɔnʋ sɔŋɔ sɩ ba di candiǝ, kʋ yɩ je-ŋʋŋa je mʋ ba lagɩ sɩ ba taa je da.

eng: They are the ones who cheat widows out of their homes, and to show off they say long prayers. They will be judged most harshly.
local: Ba nan yǝni ba ganɩ kadǝnǝ ba joŋi kʋlʋ maama ba na jɩgɩ tɩn. Ba kwǝri ba kɩ Wɛ-lor-dɩdwaarʋ, sɩ kʋ taa nyɩ nɩ ba yɩ nɔn-ŋʋna mʋ te. Wɛ wʋ́ tiini DƖ pa bantʋ na cam kʋ ja gaalɩ.».

eng: Very early in the morning on the first day of the week, the women went to the tomb, bringing the fragrant spices they had prepared.
local: Dɛ dɩlʋ na saŋɩ siun dɛ dɩm tɩn, kaana bam ma zaŋɩ ba go tɩga ba vu yibeeli dɩm jǝgǝ kam. Ba ma jɩgɩ tralɩa nwi sɩlʋ ba na lagɩ ba ma turi Zezi yɩra yam tɩn.

eng: They found the stone rolled away from the tomb.
local: Ba na yi jǝgǝ kam tɩn, ba ma na kandwɛ dɩlʋ ya na pɩ bɔɔnɩ dɩm ni tɩn bibili dɩ tigi daa nɩ.

eng: but when they went in, they didnt find the body of the Lord Jesus.
local: Ba ma vu ba zʋ bɔɔnɩ dɩm wʋ. Ba wʋ nɛ dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi da.

eng: They didnt know what to make of this. Suddenly, two men were standing beside them in gleaming bright clothing.
local: Ba wʋbʋŋa ma vugimi, yɩ ba yǝri ba na wʋ́ bʋŋɩ kʋlʋ. Ba na zɩgɩ da kʋntʋ tɩn, nɔɔna bale ma da ba nuŋi ba zɩgɩ ba tee nɩ, yɩ ba zʋ gwaarʋ tɩ nyɩɩnɩ pʋgʋ pʋgʋ.

eng: The women were frightened and bowed their faces toward the ground, but the men said to them, Why do you look for the living among the dead?.
local: Fʋʋnɩ ma ja kaana bam. Ba ma cɔɔlɩ ba yum tɩga nɩ. Nɔɔna bam laan ma ta dɩ ba ba wɩ: «Bɛŋwaanɩ mʋ á karɩ á beeri wʋlʋ na ŋwɩ tɩn twa wʋnɩ?.

eng: He isnt here, but has been raised. Remember what he told you while he was still in Galilee.
local: O nan tǝri yo. O joori o bi. Guli-na kʋlʋ o dɛɛn na tagɩ dɩ abam maŋa kalʋ o ta na wʋ Galile nɩ tɩn.

eng: that the Human One must be handed over to sinners, be crucified, and on the third day rise again.
local: yɩ o ta o wɩ, ba maŋɩ sɩ ba ja wʋm Nabiin-bu ba kɩ nɔn-balwaarʋ jɩŋa nɩ, sɩ ba pa-o tʋʋn-dagara baŋa nɩ ba gʋ, sɩ o laan joori o bi da yatɔ dɛ nɩ.».

eng: Then they remembered his words.
local: Nɔɔna bam na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, kaana bam laan ma guli Zezi ya na tagɩ kʋlʋ dɩ ba tɩn.

eng: When they returned from the tomb, they reported all these things to the eleven and all the others.
local: Ba ma zaŋɩ yibeeli dɩm jǝgǝ kam nɩ, ba joori ba vu ba ta dɩ Zezi karabiǝ fugǝ-dɩdʋa kam dɩ balʋ maama na tɔgɩ ba wʋra tɩn.

eng: It was Mary Magdalene, Joanna, Mary the mother of James, and the other women with them who told these things to the apostles.
local: Kaana bam kʋntʋ mʋ yɩ Mari wʋlʋ na nuŋi Magɩdala, dɩ Zannɩ, dɩ Mari wʋlʋ na yɩ Zakɩ nu tɩn, kʋ wǝli dɩ kaana balʋ na tɔgɩ dɩ ba tɩn. Bantʋ maama maa wʋra ba tɛ kʋlʋ na kɩ tɩn ba brɩ Zezi karabiǝ fugǝ-dɩdʋa kam.

eng: Their words struck the apostles as nonsense, and they didnt believe the women.
local: Nɔɔna bam na ni tɩn, ba ma wʋ pɛ kaana bam taanɩ dɩm ja kuri, yɩ ba wʋ sɛ ba ni.

eng: But Peter ran to the tomb. When he bent over to look inside, he saw only the linen cloth. Then he returned home, wondering what had happened.
local: Kʋ daarɩ Pɩyɛɛrɩ ma zaŋɩ lɩla o duri o vu yibeeli dɩm jǝgǝ kam sɩ o nii. O na yi da tɩn, o ma tiiri o nii bɔɔnɩ dɩm wʋnɩ, yɩ o na garyiǝ yam yɩranɩ na tigi da. O ma joori o viiri, yɩ kʋ kɩ-o yǝǝu dɩ kʋlʋ na kɩ tɩn.

eng: On that same day, two disciples were traveling to a village called Emmaus, about seven miles from Jerusalem.
local: Dɩntʋ dɛ dɩm nɩ mʋ Zezi karabiǝ bam bale wʋ cwǝŋǝ nɩ ba maa ve tɩʋ kʋdoŋ, kʋ yɩrɩ mʋ Emayusi. Tɩʋ kʋm kʋntʋ yɩ nɩnɛɛnɩ kilomɛtra fugǝ-dɩdʋa mʋ dɩ Zeruzalɛm.

eng: They were talking to each other about everything that had happened.
local: Ba na maa ve tɩn, ba maa ŋɔɔna kʋlʋ maama na kɩ tɩn ba veǝ.

eng: While they were discussing these things, Jesus himself arrived and joined them on their journey.
local: Ba na ŋɔɔnɩ yɩ ba bwɛ daanɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, Zezi tɩtɩ ma tʋ ba wʋnɩ o tɔgɩ dɩ ba o veǝ.

eng: They were prevented from recognizing him.
local: Ba na tɔgɩ daanɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, ba yiǝ ma pugimi yɩ ba wʋ lwar-o.

eng: He said to them, What are you talking about as you walk along? They stopped, their faces downcast.
local: Zezi ma bwe-ba o wɩ: «Bɛɛ woŋo mʋ abam jɩgɩ á ŋɔɔnɩ á veǝ?» Ba na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma zɩgɩ yɩ ba yigǝ nywanɩ.

eng: The one named Cleopas replied, Are you the only visitor to Jerusalem who is unaware of the things that have taken place there over the last few days.
local: Ba dɩdʋa yɩrɩ mʋ Klɩʋpasɩ. O ma lǝri Zezi o wɩ: «Nɔɔna balʋ maama na zʋʋrɩ Zeruzalɛm nɩ tɩn maŋɩ ba lwarɩ wo-kamunnu tɩlʋ na kɩ da lele kʋntʋ tɩn. Bɛɛ nan mʋ kɩa, yɩ nmʋ yɩranɩ wʋ ni?».

eng: He said to them, What things? They said to him, The things about Jesus of Nazareth. Because of his powerful deeds and words, he was recognized by God and all the people as a prophet.
local: O ma bwe-ba o wɩ: «Bɛɛ wǝǝnu mʋ tɩntʋ?» Ba ma lǝri ba wɩ: «Kʋ yɩ kʋlʋ na kɩ Nazarɛtɩ tu Zezi tɩn mʋ. O yɩ Wɛ nijoŋnu mʋ, yɩ o kikiǝ dɩ o ni-taanɩ maama jɩgɩ dam. Wɛ tɩtɩ brɩ sɩ kʋ yɩ cɩga mʋ, yɩ nɔɔna maama dɩ na kʋntʋ.

eng: But our chief priests and our leaders handed him over to be sentenced to death, and they crucified him.
local: Dɩbam kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam dɩ dɩ́ tɩʋ kʋm nakwa bam ma ja-o, ba vu ba pa ba di o taanɩ sɩ kʋ ji tʋʋnɩ. Ba laan ma pa pamaŋna bam ja Zezi ba vu ba pa tʋʋn-dagara yuu nɩ ba gʋ.

eng: We had hoped he was the one who would redeem Israel. All these things happened three days ago.
local: Dɩbam nan ya jɩgɩ tɩɩna, sɩ wʋntʋ mʋ yɩ wʋlʋ na lagɩ o vrɩ Yisɩrayɛlɩ dwi tiinǝ bam o yagɩ tɩn. Dɩ kʋntʋ maama, zɩm mʋ jɩgɩ da yatɔ woŋo kʋntʋ na kɩ tɩn.

eng: But theres more: Some women from our group have left us stunned. They went to the tomb early this morning.
local: Kʋlʋ na wǝli da tɩn, dɩbam kɔgɔ kʋm kaana badonnǝ ve o yibeeli dɩm jǝgǝ kam ba na kʋlʋ na sɔɔrɩ dɩbam yɩra. Ba ya ve tɩtɩɩtɩ mʋ.

eng: and didnt find his body. They came to us saying that they had even seen a vision of angels who told them he is alive.
local: yɩ ba wʋ nɛ o yɩra yam da. Ba ma joori ba ba, ba ta dɩ dɩbam nɩ ba nɛ wo-kɩnkagɩlɩ mʋ. Ba ma na Wɛ malɛsɩ, yɩ sɩ ta dɩ ba sɩ wɩ, Zezi daa ta ŋwɩ mʋ.

eng: Some of those who were with us went to the tomb and found things just as the women said. They didnt see him.
local: Dɩbam nɔɔna bam badonnǝ dɩ ma zaŋɩ ba vu yibeeli dɩm nɩ. Ba ma na nɩ kʋ sɩɩnɩ kʋ yɩ nɩnɛɛnɩ kaana bam na tagɩ te tɩn mʋ, kʋ daarɩ Zezi tɩtɩ mʋ ba wʋ nɛ.».

eng: Then Jesus said to them, You foolish people! Your dull minds keep you from believing all that the prophets talked about.
local: Zezi laan ma ta dɩ nɔɔna bam o wɩ: «Á ba jɩgɩ wʋbʋŋa na? Bɛɛ mʋ yɩ á warɩ á sɛ Wɛ kwǝrǝ kalʋ DƖ nijoŋnǝ bam dɛɛn na tɔɔlɩ faŋa faŋa tɩn.

eng: Wasnt it necessary for the Christ to suffer these things and then enter into his glory.
local: Kʋ daɩ mʋ Krisi wʋm na maŋɩ sɩ o na yaara yalʋ sɩ o daarɩ o di Wɛ-sɔŋɔ o joŋi o paarɩ tɩn na?».

eng: Then he interpreted for them the things written about himself in all the scriptures, starting with Moses and going through all the Prophets.
local: O laan ma maŋɩ kʋlʋ maama na pʋpʋnɩ o ŋwaanɩ kʋ tiŋi Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ tɩn o brɩ-ba. O ma puli o pa ba lwarɩ Moyisi zaasɩm dɩm kuri, yɩ o daarɩ o brɩ-ba faŋa faŋa Wɛ nijoŋnǝ bam maama zaasɩm dɩm kuri na yɩ te tɩn.

eng: When they came to Emmaus, he acted as if he was going on ahead.
local: Ba na maa kɛ tɩn, ba ma vu ba twɛ tɩʋ kʋlʋ ba na maa ve tɩn. Zezi ma kɩ nɩ o maa kɛ yigǝ mʋ te.

eng: But they urged him, saying, Stay with us. Its nearly evening, and the day is almost over. So he went in to stay with them.
local: Nɔɔna bam ma wʋ sɛ, yɩ ba loor-o ba wɩ: «Tɩga lagɩ ka yi. Nan zʋ n pǝni dɩbam tee nɩ.» O ma sɛ yɩ o da o zʋ.

eng: After he took his seat at the table with them, he took the bread, blessed and broke it, and gave it to them.
local: O ma jǝni sɩ o di wʋdiu dɩ ba. O ma kwe dɩpɛ yɩ o kɩ Wɛ le dɩ ŋwaanɩ. O laan ma fɔ fɔ dɩpɛ dɩm o maŋɩ o pa-ba.

eng: Their eyes were opened and they recognized him, but he disappeared from their sight.
local: O na kɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, mʋ ba yiǝ pʋʋrɩ yɩ ba laan lwar-o. Ba na lwar-o tɩn, o ma da o je ba yigǝ ni, yɩ ba daa wʋ nɛ-o.

eng: They said to each other, Werent our hearts on fire when he spoke to us along the road and when he explained the scriptures for us.
local: Ba laan ma ta daanɩ ba wɩ: «Nmʋ wʋ nɛ na? Dɩ́ ya na tɔgɩ daanɩ cwǝŋǝ nɩ yɩ o ŋɔɔnɩ o brɩ dɩbam Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm wǝǝnu tɩm kuri na yɩ te tɩn, dɩ́ ya ba tiini dɩ́ jɩgɩ wʋpolo na?».

eng: They got up right then and returned to Jerusalem. They found the eleven and their companions gathered together.
local: Ba ma zaŋɩ lɩla ba joori ba vu Zeruzalɛm tɩʋ kʋm wʋ. Ba na yi da tɩn, ba ma na Zezi karabiǝ fugǝ-dɩdʋa kam dɩ nɔɔna badonnǝ bam na lagɩ daanɩ jǝgǝ dɩdʋa.

eng: They were saying to each other, The Lord really has risen! He appeared to Simon.
local: Bantʋ ma tɛ ba wɩ: «Kʋ yɩ cɩga mʋ. Dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi sɩɩnɩ o bi o yagɩ tʋʋnɩ. O tu o brɩ o tɩtɩ dɩ Simɔn mʋ.».

eng: Then the two disciples described what had happened along the road and how Jesus was made known to them as he broke the bread.
local: Nɔɔna bale bam dɩ ma maŋɩ kʋlʋ na kɩ cwǝŋǝ nɩ tɩn ba brɩ-ba. Ba daarɩ ba maŋɩ Zezi na fɔgɩ dɩpɛ dɩm yɩ ba laan lwar-o te tɩn.

eng: While they were saying these things, Jesus himself stood among them and said, Peace be with you.
local: Zezi karabiǝ bam ta na wʋra ba ŋɔɔnɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma da o ba o zɩgɩ ba tɩtarɩ nɩ. O ma jɔɔnɩ-ba yɩ o wɩ: «Yazurǝ wʋ́ jǝni dɩ abam.».

eng: They were terrified and afraid. They thought they were seeing a ghost.
local: Ba bɩcara ma di yɩ fʋʋnɩ ja-ba zanzan. Ba ma bʋŋɩ nɩ ba nɛ ciru mʋ.

eng: He said to them, Why are you startled? Why are doubts arising in your hearts?.
local: O ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Bɛŋwaanɩ mʋ kʋ sʋ abam kʋntʋ? Bɛɛ mʋ yɩ á jɩgɩ bʋbwɛa dɩ amʋ?.

eng: Look at my hands and my feet. Its really me! Touch me and see, for a ghost doesnt have flesh and bones like you see I have.
local: Fɔgɩ-na á nii amʋ jɩa dɩ a nɛ na yɩ te tɩn. Kʋ sɩɩnɩ kʋ yɩ amʋ. Taanɩ-na amʋ á nii. Ciru ba jɩgɩ ya-nwana dɩ kwi, nɩ á na naɩ amʋ na yɩ te tɩn.».

eng: As he said this, he showed them his hands and feet.
local: O na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma brɩ-ba o jataala dɩ o nɛ.

eng: Because they were wondering and questioning in the midst of their happiness, he said to them, Do you have anything to eat.
local: Kʋ na tiini kʋ jɩgɩ wʋpolo kʋ pa-ba tɩn, ba ta warɩ ba sɛ nɩ kʋ yɩ cɩga, yɩ kʋ sʋ-ba zanzan. O ma bwe-ba o wɩ: «Á jɩgɩ wʋdiu yo nɩ sɩ dɩ́ di na?».

eng: They gave him a piece of baked fish.
local: Ba ma twǝri kalǝŋ-saŋa ba pa-o.

eng: Taking it, he ate it in front of them.
local: O ma joŋi-ka o di ba yibiyǝ nɩ.

eng: Jesus said to them, These are my words that I spoke to you while I was still with you—that everything written about me in the Law from Moses, the Prophets, and the Psalms must be fulfilled.
local: O laan ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Wǝǝnu tɩlʋ na kɩ amʋ tɩn maŋɩ dɩ taanɩ dɩlʋ a na maŋɩ a ta a brɩ abam, maŋa kalʋ a ta na wʋ abam wʋnɩ tɩn. A dɛɛn tagɩ a wɩ, kʋlʋ maama na pʋpʋnɩ amʋ ŋwaanɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ tɩn maŋɩ sɩ kʋ kɩ mʋ. Kʋ na yɩ kʋlʋ Moyisi dɩ faŋa faŋa Wɛ nijoŋnǝ bam na pʋpʋnɩ tɩn, dɩ kʋlʋ na pʋpʋnɩ Wɛ lǝŋ-ŋwɩ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ tɩn maama.».

eng: Then he opened their minds to understand the scriptures.
local: O ma daarɩ o pa ba wʋ pʋʋrɩ, sɩ ba wanɩ ba lwarɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm na brɩ kʋlʋ tɩn kuri.

eng: He said to them, This is what is written: the Christ will suffer and rise from the dead on the third day.
local: O ma ta o brɩ-ba o wɩ: «Ba pʋpʋnɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ ba wɩ: ‹Krisi wʋm lagɩ o na yaara zanzan mʋ sɩ o tɩ, yɩ o laan wʋ́ joori o bi o yagɩ tʋʋnɩ da yatɔ dɛ nɩ.›.

eng: and a change of heart and life for the forgiveness of sins must be preached in his name to all nations, beginning from Jerusalem.
local: Ba daa ta pʋpʋnɩ ba wɩ: ‹O karabiǝ wʋ́ vu lʋgʋ baŋa dwi tiinǝ maama te, yɩ wʋm yɩrɩ dɩm ŋwaanɩ baá brɩ nɔɔna nɩ ba maŋɩ sɩ ba lǝni ba wʋrʋ mʋ, sɩ Wɛ laan yagɩ ba lwarɩm DƖ ma cɛ-ba.› Á nan maŋɩ sɩ á puli Zeruzalɛm nɩ mʋ á taá tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝrǝ kam kʋntʋ.

eng: You are witnesses of these things.
local: Abam nɛ kʋlʋ maama na kɩ tɩn, á nan maŋɩ sɩ á brɩ nɔɔna kʋntʋ mʋ.

eng: Look, Im sending to you what my Father promised, but you are to stay in the city until you have been furnished with heavenly power.
local: A nan wʋ́ tʋŋɩ wʋlʋ wʋm a Ko na goni ni sɩ o ba abam te tɩn, yɩ o na tuǝ, oó pa abam na dam dɩlʋ na nuŋi Wɛ te tɩn. Maŋɩ-na Zeruzalɛm nɩ, sɩ á taá cǝgǝ dam dɩlʋ a na tɛ tɩn.».

eng: He led them out as far as Bethany, where he lifted his hands and blessed them.
local: Zezi laan ma ja-ba o nuŋi tɩʋ kʋm wʋnɩ, o ja vu o yi Betani seeni. O ma zǝŋi o jɩa wɛyuu nɩ, yɩ o ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Amʋ pɛ abam yu-yoŋo.».

eng: As he blessed them, he left them and was taken up to heaven.
local: O na kɩ-ba yu-yoŋo kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma yagɩ-ba yɩ Wɛ kwe-o DƖ ja di DƖ sɔŋɔ.

eng: They worshipped him and returned to Jerusalem overwhelmed with joy.
local: Ba ma kuni doonǝ ba zul-o, yɩ ba laan daarɩ ba joori Zeruzalɛm dɩ wʋpolo zanzan.

eng: And they were continuously in the temple praising God.
local: Ba na wʋra tɩn, ba wʋ fɔgɩ ba kwɛ Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm vǝŋǝ, yɩ ba zuli Wɛ maŋa maama.

eng: In the beginning was the Word and the Word was with God and the Word was God.
local: Pulim nɩ tɩn, Bɩtara kam maŋɩ ka wʋra mʋ, yɩ lʋgʋ baŋa daa ta tǝrǝ. Ka maŋɩ ka wʋ Wɛ tee nɩ mʋ. Wʋlʋ na yɩ Bɩtara kam tɩn dɩ Wɛ maama yɩ bɩdwɩ mʋ.

eng: The Word was with God in the beginning.
local: O maŋɩ o wʋ Wɛ tee nɩ mʋ pulim nɩ.

eng: Everything came into being through the Word, and without the Word nothing came into being. What came into being.
local: Kʋ dɛ dɩ wʋntʋ mʋ yɩ Baŋa-Wɛ dɛɛn kɩ woŋo maama. Kʋlʋkʋlʋ tǝrǝ Wɛ na kɩa, yɩ kʋ wʋ dɛ dɩ wʋntʋ.

eng: through the Word was life, and the life was the light for all people.
local: Wʋm mʋ paɩ nabiinǝ na ŋwɩa. Ŋwɩa kam kʋntʋ nan mʋ paɩ nabiinǝ na pooni.

eng: The light shines in the darkness, and the darkness doesnt extinguish the light.
local: Pooni dɩm kʋntʋ na tu tɩn, dɩ zǝli lim, yɩ lim daa warɩ pooni dɩm dɩ zǝli.

eng: A man named John was sent from God.
local: Wɛ dɛɛn tʋŋɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ, o yɩrɩ mʋ Zan.

eng: He came as a witness to testify concerning the light, so that through him everyone would believe in the light.
local: O tu sɩ o ta wʋlʋ na yɩ pooni dɩm tɩn taanɩ mʋ o brɩ nɔɔna, sɩ nabiinǝ maama wanɩ ba lwar-o sɩ ba daarɩ ba sɛ-o.

eng: He himself wasnt the light, but his mission was to testify concerning the light.
local: Zan tɩtɩ daɩ pooni dɩm. O nan tu sɩ o ta wʋlʋ na yɩ pooni dɩm tɩn taanɩ mʋ o brɩ nɔɔna.

eng: The true light that shines on all people was coming into the world.
local: Wʋlʋ na yɩ pooni dɩm cɩga cɩga tɩn dɛɛn nuŋi Wɛ te mʋ o ba lʋgʋ baŋa. Wʋntʋ mʋ paɩ nabiinǝ maama na pooni sɩ ba wanɩ ba lwarɩ Wɛ.

eng: The light was in the world, and the world came into being through the light, but the world didnt recognize the light.
local: O dɛɛn ma ba lʋgʋ baŋa o wʋra. Kʋ ya dɛ dɩ wʋntʋ mʋ yɩ Wɛ kɩ lʋgʋ. Dɩ kʋntʋ dɩ, nabiinǝ daa ta wʋ lwar-o.

eng: The light came to his own people, and his own people didnt welcome him.
local: O ma ba o tɩtɩ tɩʋ, yɩ o dwi tiinǝ wʋ jeer-o lanyɩranɩ.

eng: But those who did welcome him, those who believed in his name, he authorized to become Gods children.
local: Kʋ daarɩ balʋ maama na sɛ-o yɩ ba kɩ ba wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩd-o tɩn, o ma pa-ba ni sɩ ba ji Baŋa-Wɛ biǝ.

eng: born not from blood nor from human desire or passion, but born from God.
local: Ba na jigi Wɛ biǝ kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ daɩ nabiinǝ na lʋrɩ te tɩn, naa nɔɔna yɩra lagɩm, naa nɔɔna wʋbʋŋa na lagɩ te tɩn. Kʋ nan yɩ Baŋa-Wɛ tɩtɩ mʋ pɛ ba ji DƖ biǝ.

eng: The Word became flesh and made his home among us. We have seen his glory, glory like that of a fathers only son, full of grace and truth.
local: Bɩtara kam dɛɛn ma ji nabiinu, yɩ o zʋʋrɩ dɩbam tɩtarɩ nɩ. Wɛ zaanɩ dɩ DƖ cɩga maama tiini ka wʋ o tee nɩ. Dɩ́ ma na o paarɩ-zulǝ na dagɩ te, o na yɩ o Ko Wɛ Bu-dʋa tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: John testified about him, crying out, This is the one of whom I said, He who comes after me is greater than me because he existed before me.
local: Zan dɛɛn ŋɔɔnɩ wʋntʋ taanɩ, yɩ o ta dɩ kwǝr-dɩa o wɩ: «Mʋ wʋlʋ wʋm a na maŋɩ a ta o taanɩ a wɩ, nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ wʋ́ saŋɩ amʋ kwaga o ba. Wʋntʋ yɩ kamunu o dwe amʋ, bɛŋwaanɩ o maŋɩ o wʋra yɩ amʋ yaa ta tǝrǝ.».

eng: From his fullness we have all received grace upon grace.
local: Wʋntʋ mʋ yǝni o pa dɩbam maama na zaanɩ zanzan, o na tiini o jɩgɩ Wɛ zaanɩ lanyɩranɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: as the Law was given through Moses, so grace and truth came into being through Jesus Christ.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ Wɛ pɛ DƖ cullu tɩm Moyisi mʋ yɩ o pa dɩbam. Kʋ daarɩ, kʋ na yɩ Wɛ zaanɩ dɩ DƖ cɩga kam, DƖ dɛ DƖ Bu Zezi Krisi ŋwaanɩ mʋ DƖ pa dɩbam ka.

eng: No one has ever seen God. God the only Son, who is at the Fathers side, has made God known.
local: Nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ ta maŋɩ o wʋ nɛ Baŋa-Wɛ. Kʋ daarɩ DƖ Bu-dʋa kam mʋ pɛ dɩ́ lwarɩ-DƖ. Wʋntʋ dɩ Wɛ yɩ bɩdwɩ mʋ. O maa wʋ o Ko Baŋa-Wɛ tɩkǝri nɩ.

eng: This is Johns testimony when the Jewish leaders in Jerusalem sent priests and Levites to ask him, Who are you.
local: Zan dɛɛn na pɛ nɔɔna lwarɩ Zezi te tɩn mʋ tɩntʋ: Zwifǝ yigǝ tiinǝ balʋ na zʋʋrɩ Zeruzalɛm nɩ tɩn dɛɛn ma tʋŋɩ ba kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ dɩ Leviti tiinǝ sɩ ba vu Zan te. Ba ma vu ba bwe-o ba wɩ: «Nmʋ yɩ wɔɔ mʋ?».

eng: John confessed , Im not the Christ.
local: Zan ma vɩɩrɩ o ta-ba o wɩ: «Kʋ daɩ amʋ mʋ yɩ Krisi wʋm Wɛ na tʋŋɩ tɩn.».

eng: They asked him, Then who are you? Are you Elijah? John said, Im not. Are you the prophet? John answered, No.
local: Ba ta ma joori ba bwe-o ba wɩ: «Nmʋ nan yɩ wɔɔ mʋ za? Nmʋ yɩ Eli mʋ na?» O ma lǝri-ba o wɩ, wʋm daɩ Eli. Ba daa ma bwe-o ba wɩ: «Nmʋ yɩ Wɛ nijoŋnu wʋlʋ na maŋɩ sɩ o ba tɩn mʋ na?» O maa wɩ: «Awo.».

eng: They asked, Who are you? We need to give an answer to those who sent us. What do you say about yourself.
local: Ba laan ma ta ba wɩ: «Nmʋ nan yɩ wɔɔ? Dɩ́ maŋɩ sɩ dɩ́ ja nmʋ na lǝri te tɩn mʋ, dɩ́ vu dɩ́ ta dɩ́ brɩ balʋ na tʋŋɩ dɩbam tɩn. Ta n brɩ dɩbam nmʋ na yɩ wʋlʋ tɩn.».

eng: John replied, I am a voice crying out in the wilderness, Make the Lords path straight, just as the prophet Isaiah said.
local: Ba na bwe-o kʋntʋ tɩn, Zan ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Amʋ yɩ wʋlʋ na tɔɔlɩ kwǝrǝ kagʋa wʋnɩ a wɩ: ‹Zaŋɩ-na á kwɛ cwǝŋǝ kalʋ dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm na lagɩ o ba o tɔgɩ da tɩn, á pa ka fɔgɩ ka yɔɔrɩ lanyɩranɩ.› Mʋ Wɛ nijoŋnu Ezayi na maŋɩ o ta amʋ taanɩ te tɩn.».

eng: Those sent by the Pharisees.
local: Nɔɔna badonnǝ dɩ dɛɛn tɔgɩ ba wʋ ba wʋnɩ, ba na nuŋi Farizɩan tiinǝ kɔgɔ kʋm wʋnɩ.

eng: asked, Why do you baptize if you arent the Christ, nor Elijah, nor the prophet.
local: Ba ma bwe Zan ba wɩ: «Nmʋ nan na sɩɩnɩ n daɩ Krisi wʋm Wɛ na tʋŋɩ tɩn, yɩ n daɩ Eli naa Wɛ nijoŋnu wʋlʋ na maŋɩ sɩ o ba tɩn, wɔɔ mʋ pɛ nmʋ ni sɩ n miisi nɔɔna na wʋnɩ?».

eng: John answered, I baptize with water. Someone greater stands among you, whom you dont recognize.
local: Zan ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Kʋ na yɩ amʋ, a yǝni a miisi nɔɔna na wʋnɩ mʋ. Abam nan na zɩgɩ tɩn, nɔɔnʋ wʋ á tɩtarɩ nɩ yɩ á yǝr-o.

eng: He comes after me, but Im not worthy to untie his sandal straps.
local: Wʋntʋ mʋ lagɩ o saŋɩ amʋ kwaga o ba, yɩ a wʋ maŋɩ sɩ a lɩ o nɛ natra dɩ.».

eng: This encounter took place across the Jordan in Bethany where John was baptizing.
local: Kʋntʋ maama dɛɛn kɩ Betani nɩ mʋ, Zʋrdɛn bugǝ kam bubɛ dɩm nɩ, mɛ Zan na miisi nɔɔna na wʋnɩ Wɛ ŋwaanɩ tɩn.

eng: The next day John saw Jesus coming toward him and said, Look! The Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world!.
local: Tɩga na pʋʋrɩ tɩn, Zan ma na Zezi na ve o maa bɩɩnɩ o te. O ma ta dɩ balʋ na wʋra tɩn o wɩ: «Nii-na Wɛ Pǝlbu wʋlʋ na lɩ nabiinǝ lwarɩm tɩn.

eng: This is the one about whom I said, He who comes after me is really greater than me because he existed before me.
local: A ya tagɩ a wɩ: ‹Nɔɔnʋ wʋ́ saŋɩ amʋ kwaga o ba, yɩ o yɩ kamunu o dwe amʋ, bɛŋwaanɩ o maŋɩ o wʋra yɩ amʋ yaa ta tǝrǝ.› Kʋ nan yɩ wʋntʋ taanɩ mʋ a tɛa.

eng: Even I didnt recognize him, but I came baptizing with water so that he might be made known to Israel.
local: Amʋ tɩtɩ dɛɛn maŋɩ a yǝri o na yɩ wʋlʋ tɩn, yɩ a laan ba a lwar-o. A nan tu sɩ a pa Yisɩrayɛlɩ dwi tiinǝ mʋ lwar-o. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ a yǝni a miisi nɔɔna na wʋnɩ.».

eng: John testified, I saw the Spirit coming down from heaven like a dove, and it rested on him.
local: Zan daa ta ma ŋɔɔnɩ Zezi taanɩ o brɩ-ba o wɩ: «A ya yǝri Wɛ Bu wʋm na yɩ wʋlʋ tɩn. Wʋlʋ nan na tʋŋɩ-nɩ sɩ a ba a miisi nɔɔna na wʋnɩ tɩn tagɩ o wɩ, a na nɛ Wɛ Joro kʋm tu kʋ wʋ nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ baŋa nɩ tɩn, wʋntʋ mʋ yɩ wʋlʋ na wʋ́ miisi nɔɔna Wɛ Joro kʋm wʋnɩ tɩn. A laan nan nɛ Wɛ Joro kʋm na nuŋi wɛyuu nɩ kʋ tu kʋ ba kʋ wʋ nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ baŋa nɩ, yɩ kʋ nyɩ dɩ kunkwǝŋǝ te.

eng: I have seen and testified that this one is Gods Son.
local: Amʋ sɩɩnɩ a na kʋlʋ Wɛ na tagɩ tɩn, yɩ a lagɩ a ta cɩga dɩ abam sɩ wʋntʋ mʋ yɩ Wɛ Bu wʋm.».

eng: The next day John was standing again with two of his disciples.
local: Tɩga na pʋʋrɩ tɩn, Zan dɩ o karabiǝ bale daa ta maa wʋra.

eng: When he saw Jesus walking along he said, Look! The Lamb of God.
local: Zan ma na Zezi na ve o maa kɛa. O ma ta o wɩ: «Nii-na Wɛ Pǝlbu wʋm.».

eng: The two disciples heard what he said, and they followed Jesus.
local: Zan karabiǝ bale bam ma ni o na tagɩ kʋlʋ tɩn. Ba ma pɛ Zezi kwaga ba pu.

eng: When Jesus turned and saw them following, he asked, What are you looking for? They said, Rabbi , where are you staying.
local: Zezi ma pipiri o nii o na ba na tɔg-o tɩn. O ma bwe-ba o wɩ: «Abam beeri bɛɛ mʋ?» Ba ma lǝri ba wɩ: «Rabi, nmʋ zʋʋrɩ yǝn mʋ?».

eng: He replied, Come and see. So they went and saw where he was staying, and they remained with him that day. It was about four oclock in the afternoon.
local: O ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Ba-na sɩ á wʋ́ na.» Ba ma tɔgɩ dɩd-o ba vu ba na o na zʋʋrɩ mɛ tɩn. Kʋ yɩ dɩdaan-ni nɩ mʋ. Ba ma maŋɩ o tee nɩ ba tʋ wɛ.

eng: One of the two disciples who heard what John said and followed Jesus was Andrew, the brother of Simon Peter.
local: Nɔɔna bale balʋ na ni Zan na tagɩ Zezi taanɩ dɩm te yɩ ba daarɩ ba tɔg-o tɩn dɩdʋa yɩrɩ mʋ Andre. O yɩ Simɔn Pɩyɛɛrɩ nyaanɩ mʋ.

eng: He first found his own brother Simon and said to him, We have found the Messiah .
local: Ba na joori tɩn, Andre ma kɩ lɩla o vu o zɩmbaarʋ Simɔn te, o ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Dɩbam nɛ Mesi wʋm.».

eng: He led him to Jesus. Jesus looked at him and said, You are Simon, son of John. You will be called Cephas .
local: O laan ma ja-o o vu Zezi te. Zezi ma nii o yibiyǝ nɩ yɩ o wɩ: «Nmʋ yɩ Simɔn wʋlʋ na yɩ Zan bu tɩn mʋ. Nmʋ yɩrɩ laan wʋ́ ta yɩ Sɩfasɩ mʋ.».

eng: The next day Jesus wanted to go into Galilee, and he found Philip. Jesus said to him, Follow me.
local: Tɩga dɛɛn na pʋʋrɩ tɩn, Zezi ma bʋŋɩ sɩ o vu Galile. O ma vu o jeeri Filipi, yɩ o ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Ba n ta n tɔgɩ amʋ.».

eng: Philip was from Bethsaida, the hometown of Andrew and Peter.
local: Filipi nuŋi Betisayida mʋ, tɩʋ kʋlʋ Andre dɩ Pɩyɛɛrɩ dɩ na nuŋi da tɩn.

eng: Philip found Nathanael and said to him, We have found the one Moses wrote about in the Law and the Prophets: Jesus, Josephs son, from Nazareth.
local: Filipi laan ma zaŋɩ o vu o jeeri nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ, o yɩrɩ mʋ Natanayɛlɩ. O ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Dɩbam nɛ wʋlʋ wʋm Moyisi na pʋpʋnɩ o taanɩ faŋa faŋa Wɛ cullu tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ tɩn, yɩ Wɛ nijoŋnǝ dɩ dɛɛn pʋpʋnɩ ba ta o taanɩ tɩn. Wʋntʋ mʋ yɩ Zezi, o yɩ Zʋzɛfʋ bu mʋ. O nuŋi Nazarɛtɩ.».

eng: Nathanael responded, Can anything from Nazareth be good? Philip said, Come and see.
local: Natanayɛlɩ ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Wo-laa kʋntʋ wʋ́ wanɩ kʋ nuŋi Nazarɛtɩ na?» Filipi ma lǝri o wɩ: «Ba sɩ n na.».

eng: Jesus saw Nathanael coming toward him and said about him, Here is a genuine Israelite in whom there is no deceit.
local: Ba na maa ve tɩn, Zezi ma na Natanayɛlɩ na maa bɩɩnɩ o te. O ma ta o taanɩ o wɩ: «Wʋntʋ sɩɩnɩ o yɩ Yisɩrayɛlɩ dwi tu mʋ cɩga cɩga, o wʋ lana.».

eng: Nathanael asked him, How do you know me? Jesus answered, Before Philip called you, I saw you under the fig tree.
local: Natanayɛlɩ ma bwe-o o wɩ: «Nmʋ kɩ ta mʋ n ye amʋ?» O ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «A ya nɛ nmʋ maŋa kam n na wʋ kapʋrʋ kʋm kuri nɩ kʋ loori Filipi na bǝŋi nmʋ tɩn.».

eng: Nathanael replied, Rabbi, you are Gods Son. You are the king of Israel.
local: Natanayɛlɩ ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «Karanyɩna, nmʋ yɩ Baŋa-Wɛ Bu mʋ, yɩ n ta yɩ Yisɩrayɛlɩ tiinǝ pɛ.».

eng: Jesus answered, Do you believe because I told you that I saw you under the fig tree? You will see greater things than these!.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Nmʋ na sɛ amʋ tɩn, kʋ yɩ a na tagɩ a wɩ, a ya nɛ nmʋ kapʋrʋ kʋm kuri nɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ mʋ. Nmʋ nan wʋ́ na wo-kɩnkagɩla yalʋ na tiini ya dana ya dwe kʋntʋ tɩn.».

eng: I assure you that you will see heaven open and Gods angels going up to heaven and down to earth on the Human One.
local: O laan ma ta o wɩ: «A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, abam wʋ́ ba á na wɛyuu na pʋrɩ, yɩ Wɛ malɛsɩ tɔgɩ da sɩ diinǝ yɩ sɩ tuǝ Nabiin-bu wʋm baŋa nɩ.».

eng: The Festival of Unleavened Bread, which is called Passover, was approaching.
local: Kʋ dɛɛn gɛ fɩɩn sɩ Zwifǝ bam di ba candiǝ kalʋ yɩrɩ na yɩ Pakɩ tɩn. Candiǝ kam kʋntʋ nɩ mʋ ba yǝni ba di dɩpɛ dɩlʋ na ba jɩgɩ dabɩlɩ dɩ wʋnɩ tɩn.

eng: The chief priests and the legal experts were looking for a way to kill Jesus, because they were afraid of the people.
local: Kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ dɩ cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ bam ma kwaanɩ ba lagɩ cwǝŋǝ, sɩ ba ja Zezi ba gʋ. Ba na kwarɩ fʋʋnɩ dɩ nɔɔna bam tɩn ŋwaanɩ, ba yǝri ba na wʋ́ kɩ te sɩ ba ja-o.

eng: Then Satan entered Judas, called Iscariot, who was one of the Twelve.
local: Sʋtaanɩ laan ma zʋ Zudasɩ Yiskarɩyo bɩcarɩ. Wʋntʋ yɩ Zezi karabiǝ fugǝ-bale bam wʋ nɔɔnʋ dɩdʋa mʋ.

eng: He went out and discussed with the chief priests and the officers of the temple guard how he could hand Jesus over to them.
local: O ma vu kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ dɩ Wɛ di-kamunu kʋm yɩrɩna yigǝ tiinǝ bam te sɩ ba banɩ ba nii, o na wʋ́ kɩ te o brɩ-ba cwǝŋǝ sɩ ba wanɩ ba ja Zezi.

eng: They were delighted and arranged payment for him.
local: Ba na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, ba wʋ ma poli zanzan. Ba ma di yǝlo ba maŋɩ daanɩ dɩ sǝbu ni.

eng: He agreed and began looking for an opportunity to hand Jesus over to them—a time when the crowds would be absent.
local: O ma sɛ. O laan ma beeri cwǝŋǝ sɩ o ja Zezi o kɩ ba jɩŋa nɩ, sɩ kɔgɔ kʋm yɩ zaŋɩ kʋ lwarɩ.

eng: The Day of Unleavened Bread arrived, when the Passover had to be sacrificed.
local: Kʋ ma ba kʋ yi dɛ dɩlʋ ba na puli sɩ ba di dɩpɛ dɩlʋ na ba jɩgɩ dabɩlɩ dɩ wʋnɩ tɩn. Dɛ dɩm kʋntʋ nɩ Zwifǝ bam yǝni ba gʋɩ pibalɩ mʋ ba ma di Pakɩ candiǝ kam.

eng: Jesus sent Peter and John with this task: Go and prepare for us to eat the Passover meal.
local: Zezi ma lɩ Pɩyɛɛrɩ dɩ Zan yɩ o ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Ve-na á kwɛ woŋo maama á pa dɩbam, sɩ dɩ́ ba dɩ́ di Pakɩ candiǝ kam.».

eng: They said to him, Where do you want us to prepare it.
local: Ba ma bwe-o ba wɩ: «Nmʋ lagɩ sɩ dɩ́ vu jǝgǝ kɔɔ mʋ dɩ́ kwɛ jǝgǝ kam?».

eng: Jesus replied, When you go into the city, a man carrying a water jar will meet you. Follow him to the house he enters.
local: O ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Cǝgi-na, á na ve á maa zʋʋrɩ tɩʋ kʋm wʋ, nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ wʋ́ jeeri abam o na zɩŋɩ kɔɔrʋ dɩ na. Da-na o kwaga á vu á zʋ sɔŋɔ kʋlʋ o na wʋ́ zʋ tɩn.

eng: Say to the owner of the house, The teacher says to you,Where is the guestroom where I can eat the Passover meal with my disciples.
local: Á na zʋa, sɩ á ta dɩ sɔŋɔ kʋm tu nɩ: ‹Dɩ́ karanyɩna wɩ, dɩ́ ba dɩ́ bwe dɩ́ nii, digǝ kalʋ o na wʋ́ jǝni da dɩ o karabiǝ bam sɩ ba di Pakɩ candiǝ kam wʋdiu kʋm tɩn wʋ yǝn mʋ?›.

eng: He will show you a large upstairs room, already furnished. Make preparations there.
local: Oó brɩ abam wɛyuu digǝ, ka daga lanyɩranɩ, yɩ ka jɩgɩ woŋo kʋlʋ maama dɩ́ na lagɩ tɩn ka wʋnɩ. Ve-na á kwɛ candiǝ kam wǝǝnu tɩm á ti, sɩ dɩ́ laan ba.».

eng: They went and found everything just as he had told them, and they prepared the Passover meal.
local: Ba ma zaŋɩ ba vu, yɩ ba sɩɩnɩ ba na woŋo maama nɩ Zezi na maŋɩ o ta te tɩn. Ba ma kwɛ Pakɩ candiǝ kam wʋdiu kʋm ba ti.

eng: When the time came, Jesus took his place at the table, and the apostles joined him.
local: Wʋdiu dim maŋa na yi tɩn, Zezi jǝni dɩ o karabiǝ fugǝ-bale bam sɩ ba di wʋdiu kʋm.

eng: He said to them, I have earnestly desired to eat this Passover with you before I suffer.
local: O laan ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Amʋ ya tiini a lagɩ sɩ a di candiǝ kantʋ dɩ abam dɩ a wʋ maama, sɩ a laan daarɩ a yaarɩ.

eng: I tell you, I wont eat it until it is fulfilled in Gods kingdom.
local: A nan lagɩ a ta abam sɩ, a daa bá di candiǝ kantʋ, sɩ kʋ taa ve maŋa kalʋ candiǝ kam kʋntʋ kuri na wʋ́ vu kʋ kweeli Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋnɩ tɩn.».

eng: After taking a cup and giving thanks, he said, Take this and share it among yourselves.
local: Zezi na tagɩ kʋntʋ o ti tɩn, o laan ma joŋi tweeru biǝ sana dɩ zʋŋa, yɩ o kɩ Wɛ le tɩ ŋwaanɩ. O ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Joŋi-na tɩntʋ á nyɔ á daarɩ á pa daanɩ.

eng: I tell you that from now on I wont drink from the fruit of the vine until Gods kingdom has come.
local: A lagɩ a ta abam sɩ, kʋ na puli zɩm, a daa bá nyɔ wo-nyɔɔrʋ tɩntʋ, sɩ kʋ taa ve maŋa kalʋ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm na wʋ́ ba nɔɔna tɩtarɩ nɩ tɩn.».

eng: After taking the bread and giving thanks, he broke it and gave it to them, saying, This is my body, which is given for you. Do this in remembrance of me.
local: Zezi laan ma kwe dɩpɛ, yɩ o kɩ Wɛ le dɩ ŋwaanɩ. O ma daarɩ o fɔ-dɩ o pa o karabiǝ bam yɩ o wɩ: «Amʋ yɩra yam mʋ tɩntʋ, a na lagɩ a kwe a pa abam ŋwaanɩ tɩn. Taá kɩ-na kʋntʋ doŋ, sɩ á taá ma guli a gulǝ.».

eng: In the same way, he took the cup after the meal and said, This cup is the new covenant by my blood, which is poured out for you.
local: Ba na di ba ti tɩn, o daa ma kwe zʋŋa dɩ ka wo-nyɔɔrʋ o kɩ Wɛ le tɩ ŋwaanɩ o pa-ba, yɩ o wɩ: «Wo-nyɔɔrʋ tɩntʋ brɩ sɩ Wɛ goni ni-dʋʋrɩ dɩdaanɩ nabiinǝ amʋ jana kam ŋwaanɩ. A jana kam lagɩ ka nuŋi ka duri abam ŋwaanɩ mʋ.

eng: But look! My betrayer is with me; his hand is on this table.
local: Nan nii-na, nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na lagɩ o yǝgi-nɩ o pa nɔɔna tɩn tɔgɩ o je dɩ amʋ o di yo seeni.

eng: The Human Onegoes just as it has been determined. But how terrible it is for that person who betrays him.
local: Kʋ nan fɩn mʋ sɩ amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm tɩ, nɩ Wɛ na maŋɩ DƖ lagɩ te tɩn. Kʋ nan za yɩ lɛɛrʋ mʋ kʋ pa nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na wʋ́ yǝgi-nɩ o pa tɩn.».

eng: They began to argue among themselves about which of them it could possibly be who would do this.
local: Ba na ni o na tagɩ kʋlʋ tɩn, ba ma sɩŋɩ ba bwe daanɩ ba nii, bantʋ wʋnɩ wɔɔ mʋ wʋ́ wanɩ o kɩ kʋntʋ.

eng: An argument broke out among the disciples over which one of them should be regarded as the greatest.
local: Zezi karabiǝ bam dɛɛn magɩ kantɔgɔ kʋ cɛ, sɩ ba nii bantʋ wʋnɩ wɔɔ mʋ yɩ kamunu o dwe ba maama.

eng: But Jesus said to them, The kings of the Gentiles rule over their subjects, and those in authority over them are called friends of the people.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Kʋ na yɩ lʋgʋ baŋa pwa, bantʋ yǝni ba te ba nɔɔna dɩ dam mʋ. Balʋ na di dam nɔɔna baŋa nɩ tɩn, nɔɔna bam daa ta yǝni ba bǝ-ba ba wɩ ‹Lanyɩran-kǝrǝ› mʋ.

eng: But thats not the way it will be with you. Instead, the greatest among you must become like a person of lower status and the leader like a servant.
local: Abam nan wʋ maŋɩ sɩ á taá yɩ kʋntʋ doŋ. Wʋlʋ na yɩ nɔn-kamunu abam wʋnɩ tɩn maŋɩ sɩ o ji nɩ á nyaanɩ mʋ te. Wʋlʋ dɩ na yɩ abam yuutu tɩn maŋɩ sɩ o ji nɩ á tɩntʋŋnʋ mʋ te.

eng: So which one is greater, the one who is seated at the table or the one who serves at the table? Isnt it the one who is seated at the table? But I am among you as one who serves.
local: Wʋlʋ na jǝni sɩ o di wʋdiu tɩn, dɩ wʋlʋ na kwe wʋdiu kʋm o ja ba o pa-o tɩn, bantʋ wɔɔ mʋ yɩ kamunu? Kʋ daɩ wʋlʋ na jǝni sɩ o di tɩn mʋ na? Kʋ nan na yɩ amʋ, a wʋ abam wʋnɩ yɩ a kɩ nɩnɛɛnɩ wʋlʋ na yɩ á tɩntʋŋnʋ tɩn mʋ te.

eng: You are the ones who have continued with me in my trials.
local: Abam nan yɩ balʋ na tɔgɩ dɩ amʋ a cam maama wʋnɩ, yɩ á wʋ yagɩ a tɔgɩm.

eng: And I confer royal power on you just as my Father granted royal power to me.
local: Aá pa abam tɔgɩ á di paarɩ, nɩ a Ko Wɛ na pɛ sɩ a di paarɩ te tɩn.

eng: Thus you will eat and drink at my table in my kingdom, and you will sit on thrones overseeing the twelve tribes of Israel.
local: Kʋntʋ tɩn, á wʋ́ ba á zʋ a paarɩ dɩm wʋ, yɩ á tɔgɩ dɩ amʋ á jǝni á di ywǝǝni. Á wʋ́ ba á jǝni paarɩ jangwaanʋ tɩm baŋa nɩ, sɩ á di Yisɩrayɛlɩ dwi tiinǝ kuri fugǝ-bale bam taanɩ.».

eng: Simon, Simon, look! Satan has asserted the right to sift you all like wheat.
local: Zezi laan ma ta dɩ Simɔn Pɩyɛɛrɩ o wɩ: «Simɔn, Simɔn, sʋtaanɩ sɩɩnɩ dɩ na cwǝŋǝ sɩ dɩ maŋɩ abam dɩ nii, nɩ nɔɔnʋ na caarɩ mɩna te tɩn.

eng: However, I have prayed for you that your faith wont fail. When you have returned, strengthen your brothers and sisters.
local: Amʋ nan loori Wɛ a pa nmʋ, sɩ n kɩ n wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ amʋ sɩ n yɩ yagɩ-nɩ. Nmʋ nan na tusi, yɩ n wʋbʋŋa na joori ya tɔgɩ amʋ, sɩ n pa n ko-biǝ fɔgɩ ba zɩgɩ kǝŋkǝŋ.».

eng: Peter responded, Lord, Im ready to go with you, both to prison and to death.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ laan ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «A Yuutu, ba na lagɩ ba kɩ amʋ dɩ nmʋ pɩɩna digǝ nɩ, naa ba na lagɩ ba gʋ dɩbam, a ta bá sɛ sɩ a joori nmʋ kwaga nɩ.».

eng: Jesus replied, I tell you, Peter, the rooster wont crow today before you have denied three times that you know me.
local: Zezi ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «Pɩyɛɛrɩ, a lagɩ a ta nmʋ sɩ, kʋ na wʋ́ loori sɩ cɩbwɩʋ keeri zɩm tɩ-bra-pʋra nɩ tɩn, nmʋ wʋ́ fʋ kuni bɩtɔ nɩ n yǝri amʋ.».

eng: Jesus said to them, When I sent you out without a wallet, bag, or sandals, you didnt lack anything, did you? They said, Nothing.
local: O laan ma ta dɩ ba maama o wɩ: «A daama na tʋŋɩ abam sɩ á tʋlɩ á tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝrǝ kam yɩ á ba jɩgɩ sǝbiǝ gan-lɔgɔdɩ dɩ tampɔgɔ dɩ natra tɩn, woŋo muri abam na?» Ba ma lǝri ba wɩ: «Awo, dɩ́ wʋ gɛ kʋlʋkʋlʋ.».

eng: Then he said to them, But now, whoever has a wallet must take it, and likewise a bag. And those who dont own a sword must sell their clothes and buy one.
local: O ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Kʋ daarɩ lele kʋntʋ, wʋlʋ na jɩgɩ sǝbiǝ gan-lɔgɔdɩ tɩn, kʋntʋ tu maŋɩ sɩ o kwe-dɩ mʋ. Wʋlʋ dɩ na jɩgɩ tampɔgɔ tɩn maŋɩ sɩ o kwe-kʋ mʋ. Wʋlʋ maama na ba jɩgɩ sʋ-lɔŋɔ tɩn, kʋntʋ tu maŋɩ sɩ o kwe o gɔrɔ mʋ o yǝgi o ma yǝgi sʋ-lɔŋɔ.

eng: I tell you that this scripture must be fulfilled in relation to me: And he was counted among criminals .Indeed, whats written about me is nearing completion.
local: Amʋ nan lagɩ a ta abam sɩ, kʋlʋ na pʋpʋnɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ amʋ ŋwaanɩ tɩn maŋɩ sɩ kʋ kɩ, dɩ kʋ na wɩ: ‹Ba wǝl-o dɩ nɔn-balwaarʋ mʋ›. Kʋntʋ na pʋpʋnɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ amʋ ŋwaanɩ tɩn laan wʋ́ kɩ lele.».

eng: They said to him, Lord, look, here are two swords. He replied, Enough of that.
local: Zezi karabiǝ bam ma ta dɩd-o ba wɩ: «Dɩ́ Yuutu, nii sʋ-lwaanʋ tɩle mʋ dɩ́ jɩga.» O ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Yagɩ-na sɩ kʋ maŋɩ.».

eng: Jesus left and made his way to the Mount of Olives, as was his custom, and the disciples followed him.
local: Zezi dɛɛn ma nuŋi tɩʋ kʋm wʋnɩ o vu Olivi piu kʋm seeni, nɩ o na yǝni o kɩ te tɩn. O karabiǝ bam dɩ ma tɔg-o ba vu.

eng: When he arrived, he said to them, Pray that you wont give in to temptation.
local: Ba na yi jǝgǝ kalʋ ba na maa ve tɩn, Zezi ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Á taá loori Wɛ, sɩ á yɩ tʋ maŋɩm dɩm maŋa nɩ.».

eng: He withdrew from them about a stones throw, knelt down, and prayed.
local: O laan ma fɔɔrɩ ba tee nɩ o vu o zɩgɩ daa nɩ, kʋ maŋɩ nɔɔnʋ na wʋ́ dʋlɩ kandwɛ yɩ dɩ yi mɛ tɩn. O ma kuni doonǝ o loori Wɛ o wɩ:.

eng: He said, Father, if its your will, take this cup of suffering away from me. However, not my will but your will must be done.
local: «A Ko, nmʋ na sɛ, sɩ n pa cam dɩlʋ na lagɩ dɩ ba amʋ baŋa nɩ tɩn kɛ. Nan kɩ kʋlʋ na tɔgɩ nmʋ wʋbʋŋa tɩn, sɩ yɩ kɩ amʋ wʋbʋŋa na lagɩ kʋlʋ tɩn.».

eng: Then a heavenly angel appeared to him and strengthened him.
local: O na loori Wɛ kʋntʋ tɩn, Wɛ malɛka ma nuŋi wɛyuu ka ba o te, yɩ ka wǝl-o dɩ dam.

eng: He was in anguish and prayed even more earnestly. His sweat became like drops of blood falling on the ground.
local: O wʋ na tiini kʋ cɔgɩ zanzan tɩn ma pa o tiini o loori Wɛ dɩ o wʋ maama. O luluŋu ma ji nɩnɛɛnɩ jana te, yɩ kʋ luri tɩga nɩ.

eng: When he got up from praying, he went to the disciples. He found them asleep, overcome by grief.
local: O na loori Wɛ o ti tɩn, o ma zaŋɩ o joori o vu o karabiǝ bam te. O ma na sɩ ba tigi ba dɔa wʋ-cɔgɔ kʋm ŋwaanɩ.

eng: He said to them, Why are you sleeping? Get up and pray so that you wont give in to temptation.
local: O ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Bɛɛ mʋ yɩ á dɔa? Zaŋɩ-na sɩ á taá loori Wɛ, sɩ á yɩ tʋ maŋɩm dɩm maŋa nɩ.».

eng: While Jesus was still speaking, a crowd appeared, and the one called Judas, one of the Twelve, was leading them. He approached Jesus to kiss him.
local: Zezi ta na wʋra o ŋɔɔnɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, nɔn-kɔgɔ laan ma ba kʋ yi o te. Zudasɩ wʋlʋ na yɩ o karabiǝ fugǝ-bale bam wʋ dɩdʋa tɩn ma tɔgɩ ba yigǝ. O ma fɔɔrɩ o yi Zezi te sɩ o kukwǝr-o.

eng: Jesus said to him, Judas, would you betray the Human Onewith a kiss.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Zudasɩ, nmʋ tu sɩ n pa ba ja amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm, yɩ n ta lagɩ n kukwǝri-nɩ mʋ na?».

eng: When those around him recognized what was about to happen, they said, Lord, should we fight with our swords.
local: Zezi karabiǝ balʋ na wʋ o tee nɩ tɩn ma na kʋ na lagɩ kʋ kɩ-o te tɩn. Ba ma bwe-o ba wɩ: «Dɩ́ Yuutu, dɩ́ lɩ dɩ́ sʋ-lwaanʋ tɩm dɩ́ zagɩ-ba na?».

eng: One of them struck the high priests servant, cutting off his right ear.
local: Ba wʋ dɩdʋa ma lɩ o sʋgʋ kʋm o ma zagɩ kaanɩm yuutu wʋm tɩntʋŋnʋ jazɩm zwɛ o go.

eng: Jesus responded, Stop! No more of this! He touched the slaves ear and healed him.
local: Zezi na nɛ kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Yagɩ-na». O ma vu o dwe nɔɔnʋ wʋm zwɛ dɩm, yɩ dɩ je, dɩ joori dɩ yɩ dɩ ya na yɩ te tɩn.

eng: Then Jesus said to the chief priests, the officers of the temple guard, and the elders who had come to get him, Have you come with swords and clubs to arrest me, as though I were a thief?.
local: O ma daarɩ o ta dɩ kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam dɩ Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm yɩrɩna bam yigǝ tiinǝ dɩ nakwa bam, ba na tu sɩ ba ja-o tɩn, o wɩ: «Abam na kwe sʋ-lwaanʋ dɩ da-lwaanʋ á ba sɩ á ja amʋ tɩn, a yɩ ŋwɩɩnʋ mʋ na?.

eng: Day after day I was with you in the temple, but you didnt arrest me. But this is your time, when darkness rules.
local: Dɛ maama a yǝni a wʋ abam tee nɩ Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm wʋnɩ, yɩ á wʋ jaanɩ amʋ. Kʋ nan na yɩ te tɩn, maŋa kam laan yiǝ sɩ á kɩ á wʋbʋŋa na lagɩ kʋlʋ tɩn, bɛŋwaanɩ maŋa mʋ yiǝ sɩ lim pɛ joŋi dam.».

eng: After they arrested Jesus, they led him away and brought him to the high priests house. Peter followed from a distance.
local: Ba laan ma ja Zezi, yɩ ba ja-o ba kɛ. Pɩyɛɛrɩ ma dalɩ o tɔgɩ ba kwaga o da o vu. Ba ma vu ba yi kaanɩm yuutu wʋm sɔŋɔ.

eng: When they lit a fire in the middle of the courtyard and sat down together, Peter sat among them.
local: Ba ma dwe mini ba tiŋi sɔŋɔ kʋm tɩtarɩ nɩ. Nɔɔna bam ma vu ba je ba weeri mini dɩm. Pɩyɛɛrɩ dɩ ma tɔgɩ o vu o jǝni ba wʋnɩ.

eng: Then a servant woman saw him sitting in the firelight. She stared at him and said, This man was with him too.
local: O na tɔgɩ o je mini dɩm yigǝ nɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, sɔŋɔ kʋm tɩntʋŋ-kana kadoŋ ma yɔɔrɩ o nii o yibiyǝ nɩ, yɩ o wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ dɩ tɔgɩ o wʋ Zezi tee nɩ mʋ.».

eng: But Peter denied it, saying, Woman, I dont know him.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ ma fʋ yɩ o wɩ: «Kaanɩ, a yǝr-o.».

eng: The whole assembly got up and led Jesus to Pilate and.
local: Zwifǝ bam nakwa kɔgɔ kʋm maama dɛɛn ma zaŋɩ ba ja Zezi ba ja vu gʋvɩrma tu Pilatɩ te.

eng: A little while later, someone else saw him and said, You are one of them too. But Peter said, Man, Im not.
local: Kʋ na kɩ fɩɩn tɩn, nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ dɩ daa ma na-o, yɩ o wɩ: «Nmʋ tɔgɩ n yɩ Zezi kɔgɔ kʋm nɔɔnʋ dɩdʋa mʋ.» Pɩyɛɛrɩ ma lǝri nɔɔnʋ wʋm o wɩ: «A tɔgɩ a daɩ ba wʋ nɔɔnʋ.».

eng: began to accuse him. They said, We have found this man misleading our people, opposing the payment of taxes to Caesar, and claiming that he is the Christ, a king.
local: Ba ma sɩŋɩ sɩ ba fɔ vwan ba pa-o, yɩ ba wɩ: «Dɩbam nɛ nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ mʋ o jɩgɩ dɩ́ tɩʋ kʋm nɔɔna bam o gana sɩ ba tusi. O tɛ-ba o wɩ, ba yɩ taa laɩ lampoo ba paɩ gʋvɩrma. O maa kwǝri o brɩ o tɩtɩ nɩ wʋm mʋ yɩ Krisi, kʋ na lagɩ kʋ ta nɩ pɛ tɩn.».

eng: An hour or so later, someone else insisted, This man must have been with him, because he is a Galilean too.
local: Kʋ daa na kɩ fɩɩn nɩnɛɛnɩ luu dɩdʋa tɩn, nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ dɩ daa ma ta o wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ sɩɩnɩ o tɔgɩ dɩd-o mʋ. O dɩ yɩ Galile tu mʋ.».

eng: Pilate asked him, Are you the king of the Jews? Jesus replied, Thats what you say.
local: Pilatɩ na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma bwe-o o wɩ: «Nmʋ mʋ yɩ Zwifǝ bam pɛ wʋm na?» Zezi ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «Mʋ ka da n taga.».

eng: Peter responded, Man, I dont know what you are talking about! At that very moment, while he was still speaking, a rooster crowed.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ ma lǝri o wɩ: «Baarʋ, a yǝri n taanɩ dɩm kuri.» O ta na wʋra o ŋɔɔnɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, cɩbwɩʋ ma da kʋ keeri.

eng: Then Pilate said to the chief priests and the crowds, I find no legal basis for action against this man.
local: Pilatɩ ma ta dɩ kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam dɩ nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm maama o wɩ: «Amʋ wʋ nɛ nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ na kɩ kʋlʋ o cɔgɩ tɩn, sɩ dɩ́ di o taanɩ.».

eng: The Lord turned and looked straight at Peter, and Peter remembered the Lords words: Before a rooster crows today, you will deny me three times.
local: Dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi laan ma pipiri o yɔɔrɩ Pɩyɛɛrɩ o nii. Pɩyɛɛrɩ ma guli Zezi ya na tagɩ kʋlʋ dɩd-o o wɩ: «Kʋ na wʋ́ loori sɩ cɩbwɩʋ keeri zɩm tɩ-bra-pʋra nɩ tɩn, nmʋ wʋ́ fʋ kuni bɩtɔ nɩ n yǝri amʋ.».

eng: But they objected strenuously, saying, He agitates the people with his teaching throughout Judea—starting from Galilee all the way here.
local: Ba ma tiini ba ŋɔɔnɩ taanɩ dɩm, yɩ ba kwaana ba tɛ ba wɩ: «O nan yǝni o tʋlɩ Zude tɩɩnɩ dɩm maama, o brɩ nɔɔna bam sɩ ba yɩ taa sɛ ba tɩʋ kʋm dɩdɛɛra ni. O dɛɛn puli o kɩ kʋntʋ doŋ Galile nɩ mʋ, yɩ o laan yi yo seeni.».

eng: And Peter went out and cried uncontrollably.
local: O na guli kʋntʋ tɩn, o laan ma nuŋi, yɩ o tiini o keeri zanzan.

eng: Hearing this, Pilate asked if the man was a Galilean.
local: Pilatɩ na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma bwe-ba sɩ o nii, nɔɔnʋ wʋm yɩ Galile tu mʋ na?.

eng: The men who were holding Jesus in custody taunted him while they beat him.
local: Nɔɔna balʋ dɛɛn na yɩrɩ Zezi tɩn maa yáal-o ba mwan-o, yɩ ba kwǝri ba mag-o.

eng: When he learned that Jesus was from Herods district, Pilate sent him to Herod, who was also in Jerusalem at that time.
local: O na tu o lwarɩ nɩ Zezi nuŋi tɩʋ kʋlʋ Erɔdɩ na te tɩn, o ma pa ba ja-o ba vu wʋntʋ te sɩ o di taanɩ dɩm, bɛŋwaanɩ Erɔdɩ dɩ tɔgɩ o wʋ Zeruzalɛm nɩ maŋa kam kʋntʋ nɩ.

eng: They blindfolded him and asked him repeatedly, Prophesy! Who hit you.
local: Ba ma kwe gɔrɔ ba ma kwǝli o yiǝ, yɩ ba daarɩ ba mag-o. Ba maa wɩ: «Lwarɩ n brɩ dɩbam wʋlʋ na magɩ-m tɩn.».

eng: Herod was very glad to see Jesus, for he had heard about Jesus and had wanted to see him for quite some time. He was hoping to see Jesus perform some sign.
local: Erɔdɩ na nɛ Zezi tɩn, o maa jɩgɩ wʋpolo zanzan, bɛŋwaanɩ o ya maŋɩ o ni o ŋwa, yɩ o tiini o lagɩ sɩ o na-o. O maa lagɩ sɩ o nii Zezi wʋ́ kɩ wo-kɩnkagɩlɩ o brɩ-o na.

eng: Insulting him, they said many other horrible things against him.
local: Ba ta maa ŋɔɔnɩ wǝǝnu dwi dwi ba ma cɔgɩ o yuu, yɩ ba twɩ-o zanzan.

eng: Herod questioned Jesus at length, but Jesus didnt respond to him.
local: O ma kwaanɩ o bwe-o wǝǝnu zanzan. Zezi ma wʋ sɛ o lǝr-o kʋlʋkʋlʋ.

eng: As morning came, the elders of the people, both chief priests and legal experts, came together, and Jesus was brought before their council.
local: Tɩga na pʋʋrɩ tɩn, Zwifǝ bam nakwa kɔgɔ kʋm maama ma la daanɩ, dɩ ba kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam dɩ ba cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ maama. Ba laan ma pa Zezi nuŋi o vu taanɩ dɩm dim jǝgǝ o zɩgɩ ba yigǝ nɩ.

eng: The chief priests and the legal experts were there, fiercely accusing Jesus.
local: Kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ dɩ cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ bam maa tɔgɩ ba zɩgɩ da, yɩ ba tiini ba ŋɔɔnɩ taanɩ dɩm dɩ baarɩ sɩ ba ma dɩ-o.

eng: They said, If you are the Christ, tell us! He answered, If I tell you, you wont believe.
local: Ba ma ta dɩd-o ba wɩ: «Nmʋ na sɩɩnɩ n yɩ Krisi wʋm Wɛ na maŋɩ sɩ DƖ tʋŋɩ DƖ pa dɩbam tɩn, sɩ n ta.» Zezi ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «A na tagɩ kʋntʋ a brɩ abam, á ta bá sɛ a ni.

eng: Herod and his soldiers treated Jesus with contempt. Herod mocked him by dressing Jesus in elegant clothes and sent him back to Pilate.
local: Erɔdɩ dɩ o pamaŋna bam laan ma gooni Zezi zanzan. Ba pa o zʋ paarɩ gɔrɔ sɩ ba ma mwan-o, yɩ Erɔdɩ laan pa o joori o vu Pilatɩ te.

eng: And if I ask you a question, you wont answer.
local: A nan daa na bwe abam taanɩ dɩ, abam bá sɛ sɩ á lǝri á pa-nɩ.

eng: Pilate and Herod became friends with each other that day. Before this, they had been enemies.
local: Dɩntʋ dɛ dɩm nɩ Erɔdɩ dɩ Pilatɩ laan di ciloŋo mʋ daanɩ, yɩ ba dɛɛn ya culi daanɩ mʋ.

eng: But from now on, the Human One will be seated on the right side of the power of God. .
local: Kʋ na puli lele kʋntʋ, amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm wʋ́ vu a jǝni Dam-Paŋwa Tu Baŋa-Wɛ jazɩm nɩ a da a di paarɩ.».

eng: Then Pilate called together the chief priests, the rulers, and the people.
local: Pilatɩ ma pa kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam dɩ nakwa bam dɩ nɔɔna bam maama la daanɩ o tee nɩ.

eng: They all said, Are you Gods Son, then? He replied, You say that I am.
local: Ba maama laan ma bwe-o ba wɩ: «Kʋntʋ tɩn, nmʋ nan sɩɩnɩ n yɩ Wɛ Bu wʋm mʋ na?» Zezi ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Mʋ abam na wɩ te tɩn.».

eng: He said to them, You brought this man before me as one who was misleading the people. I have questioned him in your presence and found nothing in this mans conduct that provides a legal basis for the charges you have brought against him.
local: O ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Abam na saŋɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ amʋ tee nɩ tɩn, yɩ á tɛ á wɩ, o yɩ wʋlʋ na ganɩ á tɩʋ kʋm nɔɔna bam o pa ba tusi tɩn, kʋntʋ mʋ a nan bwe-o abam yibiyǝ nɩ sɩ a nii, yɩ a wʋ nɛ kʋlʋ o na kɩ o cɔgɩ yɩ abam ja-o kʋ ŋwaanɩ tɩn.

eng: Then they said, Why do we need further testimony? Weve heard it from his own lips.
local: Ba laan ma ŋɔɔnɩ daanɩ ba wɩ: «Dɩbam tɩtɩ laan mʋ ni o kwǝrǝ kam na brɩ o tɩtɩ cɔgɩm. Dɩ́ daa ba lagɩ maana tiinǝ sɩ ba ŋɔɔnɩ o taanɩ dɩm.».

eng: Neither did Herod, because Herod returned him to us. Hes done nothing that deserves death.
local: Erɔdɩ dɩ wʋ nɛ kʋlʋ o na cɔgɩ tɩn. Mʋ kʋ kuri o pɛ o joori o ba dɩbam te. Á taá ye sɩ kʋlʋkʋlʋ tǝrǝ kʋ na wʋ́ pa ba ja-o ba gʋ tɩn.

eng: Therefore, Ill have him whipped, then let him go.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ aá pa ba mag-o dɩ balaarɩ mʋ, yɩ ba daar-o ba yagɩ.» .

eng: But with one voice they shouted, Away with this man! Release Barabbas to us. .
local: Nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm maama ma kɩ daanɩ ba kaasɩ dɩ kwǝr-dɛ ba wɩ: «Kʋ na yɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ, ja-o n gʋ, sɩ n daarɩ n lɩ Barabasɩ n pa dɩbam.».

eng: Barabbas had been thrown into prison because of a riot that had occurred in the city, and for murder.
local: Barabasɩ yɩ wʋlʋ ba dɛɛn na kɩ pɩɩna digǝ nɩ, o na kɩ jara dɩ ba tɩʋ kʋm dɩdɛɛra yɩ o gʋ nɔɔna tɩn ŋwaanɩ mʋ.

eng: Pilate addressed them again because he wanted to release Jesus.
local: Pilatɩ na lagɩ sɩ o lɩ Zezi o yagɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ, o daa ma ta dɩ nɔɔna bam sɩ ba sɛ sɩ o yagɩ Zezi.

eng: They kept shouting out, Crucify him! Crucify him.
local: Ba ma daa ta vɩn, yɩ ba tiini ba kaasɩ baŋa baŋa ba wɩ: «Pa-o tʋʋn-dagara baŋa nɩ n gʋ. Pa-o tʋʋn-dagara baŋa nɩ n gʋ.».

eng: For the third time, Pilate said to them, Why? What wrong has he done? Ive found no legal basis for the death penalty in his case. Therefore, I will have him whipped, then let him go.
local: O daa ta ma ta dɩ ba o kɩ bɩtɔ o wɩ: «Bɛɛ wo-lɔŋɔ mʋ o kɩa? A wʋ nɛ kʋlʋ o na kɩ o cɔgɩ pa kʋ maŋɩ sɩ dɩ́ gʋ-o tɩn. Kʋntʋ tɩn, aá pa ba mag-o, yɩ a daarɩ a yag-o.».

eng: But they were adamant, shouting their demand that Jesus be crucified. Their voices won out.
local: Ba daa ta maa tiini ba kwaana ba kaasɩ dɩ kwǝr-dɛ, yɩ ba tɛ dɩ baarɩ ba wɩ: «N maŋɩ sɩ n pa-o dagara baŋa nɩ mʋ n gʋ.» Ba na tiini ba ŋɔɔnɩ dɩ kwǝr-dɛ tɩn.

eng: Pilate issued his decision to grant their request.
local: kʋ ma pa Pilatɩ sɛ sɩ o kɩ kʋlʋ ba na lagɩ tɩn.

eng: He released the one they asked for, who had been thrown into prison because of a riot and murder. But he handed Jesus over to their will.
local: O ma pa ni sɩ ba lɩ Barabasɩ wʋlʋ ba na lagɩ sɩ o yagɩ o pa-ba tɩn. Wʋntʋ mʋ yɩ wʋlʋ ba ya na jaanɩ ba kɩ pɩɩna digǝ nɩ, o na kɩ jara dɩ ba tɩʋ kʋm dɩdɛɛra yɩ o gʋ nɔɔnʋ tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Pilatɩ ma daarɩ o kɩ Zezi pamaŋna jɩŋa nɩ, sɩ ba kɩ-o nɩ kɔgɔ kʋm wʋbʋŋa na lagɩ te tɩn.

eng: As they led Jesus away, they grabbed Simon, a man from Cyrene, who was coming in from the countryside. They put the cross on his back and made him carry it behind Jesus.
local: Pamaŋna bam laan ma ja Zezi ba maa kɛa. Nɔɔna bam maama maa tɔga. Ba na maa ve tɩn, ba ma jeeri nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ, o yɩrɩ mʋ Simɔn. O nuŋi Sirɛɛnɩ mʋ. O dɛɛn ya nuŋi gaa mʋ o ba o yi tɩʋ wʋnɩ o maa kɛa. Pamaŋna bam ma ja-o, yɩ ba pa o zɩŋɩ dagara kam o bakalɩ nɩ o tɔgɩ Zezi kwaga.

eng: A huge crowd of people followed Jesus, including women, who were mourning and wailing for him.
local: Zeruzalɛm kɔ-fɔrɔ dɛɛn maa tɔgɩ Zezi kwaga. Kaana dɩ maa tɔgɩ ba wʋra, yɩ ba keerǝ ba frɩ ba tɩtɩ o ŋwaanɩ.

eng: Jesus turned to the women and said, Daughters of Jerusalem, dont cry for me. Rather, cry for yourselves and your children.
local: Zezi ma pipiri o nii-ba yɩ o wɩ: «Zeruzalɛm kaana-ba, yɩ taá keeri-na amʋ ŋwaanɩ. Á za taá keeri á tɩtɩ dɩ á biǝ ŋwaanɩ.

eng: The time will come when they will say, Happy are those who are unable to become pregnant, the wombs that never gave birth, and the breasts that never nursed a child.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ maŋa wʋ́ ba ka yi, yɩ baá ta wɩ: ‹Kaanɩ wʋlʋ maama na yɩ ka-dʋgʋ tɩn yɩ yu-yoŋo tu mʋ. Wʋlʋ na maŋɩ o wʋ lʋgɩ bu sɩ o pa o ŋɔgɩ yɩlɩ tɩn jɩgɩ yu-yoŋo zanzan.›.

eng: Then they will say to the mountains, Fall on us, and to the hills, Cover us.
local: Yaara yam kʋntʋ maŋa na yiǝ, nɔɔna wʋ́ loori pweeru sɩ tɩ tʋ ba baŋa nɩ, yɩ ba loori pu-kum sɩ tɩ kwǝli-ba.

eng: If they do these things when the tree is green, what will happen when it is dry.
local: Taá nii-na, da-lɩa na jɩgɩ mini ka di tɩntʋ tɩn, kʋ laan na yɩ da-kʋra, kaá kɩ ta?».

eng: They also led two other criminals to be executed with Jesus.
local: Ba dɛɛn ma ja nɔn-balwaarʋ tɩle ba vu sɩ ba wǝli dɩ Zezi ba gʋ.

eng: When they arrived at the place called The Skull, they crucified him, along with the criminals, one on his right and the other on his left.
local: Ba ma vu ba yi jǝgǝ kalʋ yɩrɩ na yɩ Yukol-pʋa piu tɩn. Ba laan ma ja Zezi ba pa dagara kam baŋa nɩ. Ba ma ja nɔn-balwaarʋ tɩm dɩ, ba pa dagarɩ sɩdonnǝ baŋa nɩ. Ba ma cwi dɩdʋa Zezi jazɩm nɩ. Wʋwʋm maa wʋ o jagwiǝ nɩ.

eng: Jesus said, Father, forgive them, for they dont know what theyre doing. They drew lots as a way of dividing up his clothing.
local: Ba na pagɩ Zezi dagara kam baŋa nɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma loori Wɛ o wɩ: «A Ko, yagɩ n ma n cɛ-ba, sɩ ba yǝri woŋo kʋlʋ ba na kɩ tɩn.» Pamaŋna bam na lagɩ ba maŋɩ o gwaarʋ tɩm tɩn, ba tagɩ jɔrɔ mʋ ba ma maŋɩ-tɩ.

eng: The people were standing around watching, but the leaders sneered at him, saying, He saved others. Let him save himself if he really is the Christ sent from God, the chosen one.
local: Nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm maa zɩgɩ da ba niǝ. Zwifǝ bam nakwa bam ma kɩ-o cavɩɩra, yɩ ba mwana ba wɩ: «O yǝni o vrɩ nɔɔna ŋwɩa. O na sɩɩnɩ o yɩ Krisi wʋm Wɛ na kuri tɩn, sɩ o vrɩ o tɩtɩ ŋwɩa sɩ dɩ́ nii.».

eng: The soldiers also mocked him. They came up to him, offering him sour wine.
local: Pamaŋna bam dɩ maa mwan-o, yɩ ba jɩgɩ sa-nywana ba suŋi o yigǝ nɩ.

eng: and saying, If you really are the king of the Jews, save yourself.
local: Ba maa wɩ: «Nmʋ na sɩɩnɩ n yɩ Zwifǝ bam pɛ, sɩ n vrɩ n tɩtɩ ŋwɩa sɩ dɩ́ nii.».

eng: Above his head was a notice of the formal charge against him. It read This is the king of the Jews.
local: Ba dɛɛn ya pʋpʋnɩ tɔnɔ ba tanɩ-kʋ daa kam yɩra nɩ o yuu baŋa nɩ, yɩ kʋ wɩ: «Wʋntʋ yɩ Zwifǝ bam pɛ mʋ.».

eng: One of the criminals hanging next to Jesus insulted him: Arent you the Christ? Save yourself and us.
local: Nɔn-balwaarʋ tɩlʋ ba na pagɩ dɩ Zezi tɩn dɩdʋa maa jɩgɩ Zezi o twɩa yɩ o wɩ: «Kʋ daɩ nmʋ mʋ yɩ Krisi wʋm Wɛ na tʋŋɩ tɩn na? Nan vrɩ n tɩtɩ ŋwɩa, sɩ n daarɩ n vrɩ dɩbam dɩ n wǝli da.».

eng: Responding, the other criminal spoke harshly to him, Dont you fear God, seeing that youve also been sentenced to die?.
local: Nɔn-balɔrɔ kʋdoŋ kʋm ma kaanɩ kʋkʋm kʋm kʋ wɩ: «Nmʋ ba kwarɩ Wɛ fʋʋnɩ na? Ba lagɩ ba gʋ dɩbam maama mʋ.

eng: We are rightly condemned, for we are receiving the appropriate sentence for what we did. But this man has done nothing wrong.
local: Kʋ na yɩ dɩbam, dɩbam tʋʋnɩ dɩm maŋɩ, bɛŋwaanɩ kǝm-balwaarʋ tɩlʋ dɩ́ na kɩ tɩn, kʋ maŋɩ sɩ dɩ́ tɩ mʋ. Kʋ nan na yɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ, o wʋ kɩ kʋlʋkʋlʋ o cɔgɩ.».

eng: Then he said, Jesus, remember me when you come into your kingdom.
local: O ma daarɩ o ta dɩ Zezi o wɩ: «Nmʋ na tu n ba n joŋi n paarɩ dɩm, sɩ n guli a gulǝ.».

eng: Jesus replied, I assure you that today you will be with me in paradise.
local: Zezi ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «A lagɩ a ta cɩga dɩ nmʋ sɩ, nmʋ wʋ́ jǝni dɩ amʋ Wɛ-sɔŋɔ kʋm nɩ zɩm dɛ dɩm nɩ.».

eng: It was now about noon, and darkness covered the whole earth until about three oclock.
local: Kʋ ma vu kʋ yi maŋa kam wɛ na tu dɩ yi yuparɩ baŋa tɩn. Lʋgʋ kʋm maama ma ji lim taan, sɩ kʋ vu kʋ yi luu batɔ.

eng: while the sun stopped shining. Then the curtain in the sanctuary tore down the middle.
local: Wɩa kam daa ba jɩgɩ pooni. Gɔrɔ kʋlʋ ba na lǝgǝ ba ma cɩ Wɛ-di-laa kam tɩn ma kaarɩ bɩle.

eng: There was a Pharisee named Nicodemus, a Jewish leader.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ dɛɛn mʋ wʋra, o yɩrɩ mʋ Nikodɛm. O nuŋi Farizɩan tiinǝ kɔgɔ kʋm wʋnɩ mʋ. O maa tɔgɩ o yɩ Zwifǝ bam yigǝ tu dɩdʋa.

eng: Crying out in a loud voice, Jesus said, Father, into your hands I entrust my life . After he said this, he breathed for the last time.
local: Zezi laan ma kaasɩ dɩ kwǝr-dɩa o wɩ: «Amʋ Ko, a kwe a joro a kɩ nmʋ jɩŋa nɩ.» O na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma daarɩ o tɩ.

eng: He came to Jesus at night and said to him, Rabbi, we know that you are a teacher who has come from God, for no one could do these miraculous signs that you do unless God is with him.
local: Dɛ dɩdwɩ o ma zaŋɩ o vu Zezi te tɩtɩɩ nɩ, yɩ o ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Karanyɩna, dɩ́ ye nɩ Wɛ mʋ tʋŋɩ nmʋ sɩ n ba n brɩ nɔɔna DƖ cɩga kam. Dɩ́ nɛ wo-kɩnkagɩla yalʋ nmʋ na kɩ tɩn. Kʋ pɛ dɩ́ lwarɩ nɩ Wɛ mʋ sɩɩnɩ DƖ wʋ nmʋ tee nɩ, DƖ paɩ n waɩ wo-kɩnkagɩla yam n kɩa.».

eng: When the centurion saw what happened, he praised God, saying, Its really true: this man was righteous.
local: Pamaŋna bam yigǝ tu wʋm na nɛ kʋlʋ na kɩ tɩn, o ma tee Wɛ yɩ o wɩ: «Kʋ yɩ cɩga pwɛɛn. Nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ sɩɩnɩ o yɩ nɔn-ŋʋm mʋ.».

eng: Jesus answered, I assure you, unless someone is born anew, its not possible to see Gods kingdom.
local: Zezi ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «A lagɩ a ta cɩga dɩ nmʋ sɩ, ba na wʋ joori ba lʋ nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ tɩn, kʋntʋ tu bá wanɩ o na Wɛ paarɩ dɩm na yɩ te tɩn.».

eng: All the crowds who had come together to see this event returned to their homes beating their chests after seeing what had happened.
local: Kɔgɔ kʋlʋ maama na tu sɩ kʋ nii kǝm dɩm tɩn ma sɩɩnɩ ba na kʋ na kɩ te tɩn. Ba na ma joori sɔŋɔ tɩn, ba maa frɩ ba tɩtɩ ba veǝ, ba wʋ na cɔgɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: Nicodemus asked, How is it possible for an adult to be born? Its impossible to enter the mothers womb for a second time and be born, isnt it.
local: Nikodɛm ma bwe-o o wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ na yɩ nɔn-kwɩʋn, baá kɩ ta mʋ ba lʋ-o daga? O bá wanɩ o joori o zʋ o nu wʋ sɩ ba daa lʋ-o.».

eng: And everyone who knew him, including the women who had followed him from Galilee, stood at a distance observing these things.
local: Kʋ daarɩ balʋ maama na maŋɩ ba ye-o tɩn dɩ kaana balʋ ya na tɔgɩ dɩd-o ba nuŋi Galile ba ba tɩn maa zɩgɩ daa nɩ ba niǝ.

eng: Jesus answered, I assure you, unless someone is born of water and the Spirit, its not possible to enter Gods kingdom.
local: Zezi ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «A lagɩ a ta cɩga dɩ nmʋ sɩ, nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ Wɛ na wʋ mɛ na dɩ DƖ Joro kʋm DƖ pa o na lʋr-dʋŋa tɩn, kʋntʋ tu bá wanɩ o zʋ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋnɩ.

eng: Now there was a man named Joseph who was a member of the council. He was a good and righteous man.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ dɛɛn mʋ wʋra, o yɩrɩ mʋ Zʋzɛfʋ. O nuŋi Zwifǝ tɩʋ kʋdoŋ mʋ, kʋ yɩrɩ mʋ Arimatɩ. O maa yɩ Zwifǝ bam nakwa kɔgɔ kʋm nɔɔnʋ dɩdʋa mʋ. O ma kwǝri o yɩ nɔn-ŋʋm, yɩ o tɔgɩ Wɛ dɩ cɩga. Ba kɔgɔ kʋm ya na kɩ ni daanɩ sɩ ba ja Zezi tɩn, o wʋ sɛ ba na bʋŋɩ sɩ ba kɩ-o kʋlʋ tɩn. O maa jɩgɩ tɩɩna sɩ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋ́ ba nɔɔna tɩtarɩ nɩ.

eng: Whatever is born of the flesh is flesh, and whatever is born of the Spirit is spirit.
local: Nabiinǝ na lʋgɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ tɩn, kʋ yɩ lʋgʋ baŋa ŋwɩa mʋ o jɩga. Kʋ daarɩ, Wɛ Joro kʋm na lʋgɩ wʋlʋ tɩn, kʋ yɩ Wɛ Joro kʋm ŋwɩa mʋ o jɩga.

eng: This man went to Pilate and asked for Jesus body.
local: O ma zaŋɩ o vu Pilatɩ te, o loori cwǝŋǝ sɩ o vu parɩ Zezi yɩra yam o kɩ.

eng: Dont be surprised that I said to you, You must be born anew.
local: Yɩ pa kʋ sʋ-m dɩ a na tagɩ kʋlʋ nɩ kʋ maŋɩ sɩ ba joori ba lʋ abam tɩn.

eng: Taking it down, he wrapped it in a linen cloth and laid it in a tomb carved out of the rock, in which no one had ever been buried.
local: Pilatɩ na sɛ tɩn, Zʋzɛfʋ ma vu o parɩ Zezi dagara kam yuu nɩ. Ba ma kwe gar-jalɩ ba ma pipir-o, yɩ ba daarɩ ba ja-o ba vu ba kɩ piu bɔɔnɩ wʋnɩ. Dɩntʋ yɩ bɔɔnɩ dɩlʋ ba dɛɛn na lugi-dɩ piu yɩra nɩ yɩ ba maŋɩ ba wʋ kɩ nɔɔnʋ da tɩn.

eng: Gods Spirit blows wherever it wishes. You hear its sound, but you dont know where it comes from or where it is going. Its the same with everyone who is born of the Spirit.
local: Kʋ na yɩ viu, kʋ yǝni kʋ dʋlɩ mɛ dɩ mɛ kʋ na lagɩ tɩn mʋ. N yǝni n ni kʋ sɔɔ kʋm, yɩ n yǝri kʋ na nuŋi mɛ dɩ kʋ na maa ve mɛ tɩn. Kʋ yɩ bɩdwɩ mʋ dɩ Wɛ Joro kʋm na pɛ wʋlʋ maama ŋwɩ-dʋŋa tɩn.».

eng: It was the Preparation Day for the Sabbath, and the Sabbath was quickly approaching.
local: Dɛ dɩm Zʋzɛfʋ na kɩ kʋntʋ tɩn maa yɩ dɛ dɩlʋ Zwifǝ bam na maŋɩ sɩ ba ti ba yigǝ ba siun dɛ dɩm ŋwaanɩ tɩn. Tɩga na yi, ba siun dɛ dɩm puli mʋ.

eng: Nicodemus said, How are these things possible.
local: Nikodɛm ma bwe o wɩ: «Kʋʋ́ kɩ ta mʋ kʋ kɩ kʋntʋ?».

eng: The women who had come with Jesus from Galilee followed Joseph. They saw the tomb and how Jesus body was laid in it.
local: Kaana balʋ ya na tɔgɩ dɩ Zezi ba nuŋi Galile tɩn dɛɛn ma da Zʋzɛfʋ kwaga, ba vu ba na bɔɔnɩ dɩm dɩ ba na tiŋi Zezi te tɩn.

eng: Jesus answered, You are a teacher of Israel and you dont know these things?.
local: Zezi ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «Nmʋ yɩ Zwifǝ bam karanyɩna mʋ, yɩ n daa n ta yǝri wǝǝnu tɩntʋ?.

eng: then they went away and prepared fragrant spices and perfumed oils. They rested on the Sabbath, in keeping with the commandment.
local: Ba na nɛ kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma joori ba vu sɔŋɔ. Ba ma vu ba lagɩ tralɩa nwi dwi dwi, sɩ ba ma turi Zezi yɩra yam. Ba siun dɛ dɩm nɩ ba ma sin dɩ ba tɩtʋŋa yam nɩ Wɛ cullu na brɩ te tɩn.

eng: I assure you that we speak about what we know and testify about what we have seen, but you dont receive our testimony.
local: A lagɩ a ta cɩga dɩ nmʋ sɩ, dɩ́ yǝni dɩ́ tɛ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ dɩ́ na ye tɩn mʋ, yɩ dɩ́ kwǝri dɩ́ ŋɔɔnɩ kʋlʋ dɩ́ na nɛ tɩn dɩ́ brɩ nɔɔna. Dɩ kʋntʋ dɩ, á ta ba sɛ dɩbam taanɩ dɩm.

eng: If I have told you about earthly things and you dont believe, how will you believe if I tell you about heavenly things?.
local: A na tɛ lʋgʋ kʋntʋ baŋa wǝǝnu taanɩ a brɩ abam yɩ á ba sɛ a ni tɩn, a laan na ŋɔɔnɩ Wɛ-sɔŋɔ wǝǝnu a brɩ abam, á wʋ́ kɩ ta mʋ á sɛ a ni?.

eng: No one has gone up to heaven except the one who came down from heaven, the Human One.
local: Nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ tǝrǝ o na diini Wɛ-sɔŋɔ, kʋ na daɩ Nabiin-bu wʋm na zɩgɩ da o tu tɩn yɩranɩ.

eng: Just as Moses lifted up the snake in the wilderness, so must the Human One be lifted up.
local: Nɩ Moyisi dɛɛn na zǝŋi can-na bɩsankwɩa kam o palɩ daa yuu nɩ kagʋa kam wʋnɩ te tɩn, ba nan maŋɩ sɩ ba kwe Nabiin-bu wʋm dɩ ba zǝŋi wɛyuu nɩ kʋntʋ mʋ.

eng: so that everyone who believes in him will have eternal life.
local: sɩ kʋ pa nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ maama na kɩ o wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩd-o tɩn na ŋwɩ-dʋŋa kalʋ na ba ti tɩn.».

eng: God so loved the world that he gave his only Son, so that everyone who believes in him wont perish but will have eternal life.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ Wɛ na tiini DƖ soe nabiinǝ tɩn mʋ pɛ DƖ tʋŋɩ DƖ Bu-dʋa sɩ o ba lʋgʋ baŋa, sɩ wʋlʋ maama na kɩ o wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩd-o tɩn daa yɩ cɔgɩ, sɩ o na ŋwɩ-dʋŋa kalʋ na ba ti tɩn.

eng: God didnt send his Son into the world to judge the world, but that the world might be saved through him.
local: Wɛ na tʋŋɩ DƖ Bu wʋm sɩ o ba lʋgʋ baŋa kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ daɩ sɩ o di nabiinǝ taanɩ sɩ o cɔgɩ-ba, kʋ nan yɩ sɩ o vrɩ-ba lwarɩm wʋnɩ mʋ o yagɩ.

eng: Whoever believes in him isnt judged; whoever doesnt believe in him is already judged, because they dont believe in the name of Gods only Son.
local: Wʋlʋ maama na kɩ o wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Wɛ Bu wʋm tɩn, ba daa bá di o taanɩ. Kʋ daarɩ wʋlʋ maama na wʋ sɛ-o tɩn maŋɩ o ga bʋra mʋ, o na vɩn sɩ o sɛ Wɛ Bu-dʋa kam tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: This is the basis for judgment: The light came into the world, and people loved darkness more than the light, for their actions are evil.
local: Wɛ na di nabiinǝ taanɩ te tɩn mʋ tɩntʋ: Wɛ pɛ wʋlʋ na yɩ pooni tɩn mʋ ba lʋgʋ baŋa. Nabiinǝ nan soe lim ba dwe pooni dɩm ba tɩtʋŋ-balwaarʋ tɩm ŋwaanɩ.

eng: All who do wicked things hate the light and dont come to the light for fear that their actions will be exposed to the light.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ maama na kɩ kǝm-balwaarʋ tɩn culi pooni dɩm mʋ, o bá sɛ sɩ o nuŋi dɩ yigǝ nɩ, sɩ o wo-balwaarʋ tɩm wʋ́ jalɩ jaja.

eng: Whoever does the truth comes to the light so that it can be seen that their actions were done in God.
local: Wʋlʋ maama nan na tɔgɩ cɩga cwǝŋǝ tɩn wʋ́ nuŋi pooni dɩm yigǝ mʋ, sɩ kʋ brɩ nɩ o tɩtʋŋa maama tɔgɩ Wɛ wʋbʋŋa mʋ.

eng: After this Jesus and his disciples went into Judea, where he spent some time with them and was baptizing.
local: Kʋntʋ kwaga nɩ Zezi dɩ o karabiǝ bam ma vu ba beeri Zude tɩ-balwa bam wʋnɩ. Ba ma daanɩ jǝgǝ kam kʋntʋ nɩ, yɩ o miisi nɔɔna na wʋnɩ Wɛ ŋwaanɩ.

eng: John was baptizing at Aenon near Salem because there was a lot of water there, and people were coming to him and being baptized. .
local: Zan dɛɛn maa wʋ Ɛnɔn nɩ. Tɩʋ kʋm kʋntʋ batwarɩ dɩdaanɩ Salim. O dɩ maa wʋra o miisi nɔɔna na wʋnɩ, na na daga je sɩm kʋntʋ nɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Nɔɔna zanzan dɛɛn yǝni ba tui o te maŋa maama, sɩ ba pa o miisi-ba na wʋnɩ.

eng: John hadnt yet been thrown into prison.
local: Maŋa kam kʋntʋ nɩ ba ta wʋ jaanɩ Zan ba pɩ.

eng: A debate started between Johns disciples and a certain Jew about cleansing rituals.
local: Zan karabiǝ badonnǝ dɛɛn ma magɩ kantɔgɔ dɩ Zwifu wʋdoŋ, dɩ nɔɔna na maŋɩ sɩ ba zarɩ ba tɩtɩ te sɩ kʋ tɔgɩ Zwifǝ bam cullu tɩm cwǝŋǝ tɩn.

eng: They came to John and said, Rabbi, look! The man who was with you across the Jordan, the one about whom you testified, is baptizing and everyone is flocking to him.
local: Ba ma vu Zan te ba ta dɩd-o ba wɩ: «Karanyɩna, dɩ́ nɛ nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ dɛɛn na wʋ nmʋ tee nɩ yɩ n wʋ Zʋrdɛn bugǝ kam bubɛ dɩdoŋ dɩm nɩ tɩn. Nmʋ tagɩ o taanɩ n brɩ dɩbam. Lele kʋntʋ, o laan wʋra o miisi nɔɔna na wʋnɩ, yɩ nɔɔna zanzan tiini ba ve o te ba daarɩ dɩbam.».

eng: John replied, No one can receive anything unless it is given from heaven.
local: Zan ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Kʋ ba cɔga. Nɔɔnʋ bá wanɩ o na woŋo, yɩ kʋ na daɩ Wɛ mʋ pɛ-o-kʋ.

eng: You yourselves can testify that I said that Im not the Christ but that Im the one sent before him.
local: Abam tɩtɩ maŋɩ á yɩ amʋ maana tiinǝ dɩ a na tagɩ a wɩ, amʋ daɩ Krisi wʋm, sɩ kʋ yɩ Wɛ mʋ tʋŋɩ-nɩ sɩ a tɔgɩ Krisi wʋm yigǝ a ba.

eng: The groom is the one who is getting married. The friend of the groom stands close by and, when he hears him, is overjoyed at the grooms voice. Therefore, my joy is now complete.
local: Kʋ nyɩ dɩ nɔɔnʋ na di kaanɩ te tɩn mʋ, kʋ yɩ kan-barʋ wʋm tɩtɩ mʋ te kaanɩ wʋm. Ka-yiginu wʋm maa zɩgɩ daa nɩ yɩ o cǝgǝ. O na nɛ nɩ kan-barʋ wʋm jɩgɩ wʋpolo dɩ o kaanɩ wʋm, o dɩ wʋ́ ta jɩgɩ wʋpolo. Kʋntʋ, a dɩ jɩgɩ wʋpolo kʋ ja gaalɩ.

eng: He must increase and I must decrease.
local: Wʋntʋ nan maŋɩ sɩ o ji nɔn-kamunu mʋ, sɩ amʋ joori a ji nɔn-balaŋa.

eng: The one who comes from above is above all things. The one who is from the earth belongs to the earth and speaks as one from the earth. The one who comes from heaven is above all things.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na nuŋi Wɛ te tɩn mʋ yɩ nɔn-kamunu o dwe nɔɔnʋ maama. Kʋ daarɩ wʋlʋ na nuŋi lʋgʋ baŋa tɩn yɩ lʋgʋ nyɩm mʋ. O ni-taanɩ dɩ maa yɩ lʋgʋ baŋa nyɩm. Wʋlʋ nan na nuŋi Wɛ-sɔŋɔ tɩn mʋ dwe maama.

eng: He testifies to what he has seen and heard, but no one accepts his testimony.
local: O yǝni o tɛ kʋlʋ o na niǝ yɩ o kwǝri o na tɩn mʋ o brɩ nɔɔna. Dɩ kʋntʋ dɩ, nɔɔna ta ba sɛ o zaasɩm dɩm.

eng: Whoever accepts his testimony confirms that God is true.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ nan na sɛ o zaasɩm dɩm tɩn, kʋntʋ tu sɩɩnɩ o brɩ nɩ Wɛ yɩ cɩga mʋ.

eng: The one whom God sent speaks Gods words because God gives the Spirit generously.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ, kʋ na yɩ wʋlʋ wʋm Wɛ na tʋŋɩ tɩn, Wɛ pɛ-o DƖ Joro kʋm kʋ tiini kʋ ja gaalɩ, yɩ kʋ paɩ o tɛ Wɛ tɩtɩ ni-taanɩ.

eng: The Father loves the Son and gives everything into his hands.
local: Ko Baŋa-Wɛ soe DƖ Bu wʋm yɩ DƖ pa-o dam woŋo maama baŋa nɩ.

eng: Whoever believes in the Son has eternal life. Whoever doesnt believe in the Son wont see life, but the angry judgment of God remains on them.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na kɩ o wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Wɛ Bu wʋm tɩn jɩgɩ ŋwɩ-dʋŋa kalʋ na ba ti tɩn. Kʋ daarɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na vɩn Wɛ Bu wʋm tɩn bá fɔgɩ o na ŋwɩa Wɛ tee nɩ. Wɛ ban-zɔŋɔ nan wʋ́ ta wʋ o yuu nɩ.».

eng: After this there was a Jewish festival, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
local: Kʋntʋ kwaga nɩ Zezi ma zaŋɩ o vu Zeruzalɛm, sɩ o tɔgɩ o di Zwifǝ bam candiǝ kadoŋ.

eng: In Jerusalem near the Sheep Gate in the north city wall is a pool with the Aramaic name Bethsaida. It had five covered porches.
local: Bugǝ kadoŋ mʋ wʋra, ka na batwarɩ dɩ tɩʋ kʋm ni dɩlʋ peeni na tɔgɩ da tɩn. Ka yɩrɩ dɩ Zwifǝ bam taanɩ mʋ yɩ Betizata. Ba dɛɛn te pwe sɩnu mʋ ka ni nɩ.

eng: and a crowd of people who were sick, blind, lame, and paralyzed sat there.
local: Yawɩɩna zanzan dɛɛn yǝni ba tigi pwe sɩm kuri nɩ. Badaara yɩ lilwǝ dɩ gwanɩ dɩ kwaarʋ. Ba tigi da ba cǝgǝ, sɩ Wɛ malɛka mʋ ba ka pɔgɩlɩ na bam.

eng: A certain man was there who had been sick for thirty-eight years.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ dɛɛn ma wʋra, o na ba jɩgɩ yazurǝ kʋ yi nɩ bɩna fiintɔ nana te.

eng: When Jesus saw him lying there, knowing that he had already been there a long time, he asked him, Do you want to get well.
local: Zezi ma na o na tigi da tɩn, yɩ o lwarɩ nɩ o ya maŋɩ o ba jɩgɩ yazurǝ kʋ daanɩ. O ma ta dɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋm o wɩ: «Nmʋ lagɩ sɩ n na yazurǝ na?».

eng: The sick man answered him, Sir, I dont have anyone who can put me in the water when it is stirred up. When Im trying to get to it, someone else has gotten in ahead of me.
local: Yawɩɩnʋ wʋm ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «A tu, bugǝ kam na bam na yǝni ba pɔgɩlɩ, a ba jɩgɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na wʋ́ kwe-nɩ o ja tu na bam wʋnɩ tɩn. A na yǝni a kwaanɩ sɩ a tu, nɔɔnʋ mʋ wʋ́ tu o loori amʋ.».

eng: Jesus said to him, Get up! Pick up your mat and walk.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Zaŋɩ wɛɛnɩ, sɩ n kwe n sara kam n ta n veǝ.».

eng: Immediately the man was well, and he picked up his mat and walked. Now that day was the Sabbath.
local: O na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, nɔɔnʋ wʋm ma sɩɩnɩ o na yazurǝ bɩdwɩ baŋa nɩ, yɩ o zaŋɩ o kwe o sara kam o veǝ. Kʋ yɩ Zwifǝ bam siun dɛ nɩ mʋ kʋ kɩ kʋntʋ.

eng: The Jewish leaders said to the man who had been healed, Its the Sabbath; you arent allowed to carry your mat.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ Zwifǝ yigǝ tiinǝ bam ma ta dɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋm ba wɩ: «Zɩm yɩ dɩbam siun dɛ mʋ. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ kʋ culǝ sɩ n ta n ze n sara kam.».

eng: He answered, The man who made me well said to me, Pick up your mat and walk.
local: O ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na pɛ amʋ na yazurǝ tɩn mʋ tagɩ o wɩ, a kwe a sara kam a taa veǝ.».

eng: They inquired, Who is this man who said to you, Pick it up and walk.
