local: Ba ma bwe-o ba wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ wʋm kʋntʋ yɩ wɔɔ mʋ, yɩ o wɩ n kwe n sara kam n ta n veǝ?».

eng: The man who had been cured didnt know who it was, because Jesus had slipped away from the crowd gathered there.
local: O maa daa yǝri wʋlʋ na pɛ o na yazurǝ yam tɩn, bɛŋwaanɩ Zezi zʋ nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm wʋ yɩ o daa wʋ nɛ-o.

eng: Later Jesus found him in the temple and said, See! You have been made well. Dont sin anymore in case something worse happens to you.
local: Kʋ na kɩ fɩɩn tɩn, Zezi ma vu o jeer-o Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm wʋnɩ. O ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Nii, nmʋ laan nɛ yazurǝ. Nan daa n yɩ kɩ lwarɩm, sɩ wo-lɔŋɔ kʋlʋ na dwe kʋntʋ tɩn daa yɩ kɩ-m.».

eng: The man went and proclaimed to the Jewish leaders that Jesus was the man who had made him well.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋm laan ma vu o ta dɩ Zwifǝ yigǝ tiinǝ bam o wɩ: «Kʋ yɩ Zezi ya mʋ pɛ a na yazurǝ.».

eng: As a result, the Jewish leaders were harassing Jesus, since he had done these things on the Sabbath.
local: Zwifǝ yigǝ tiinǝ bam dɛɛn maa wʋra ba yaarɩ Zezi, dɩ o na pɛ yawɩɩnʋ wʋm na yazurǝ ba siun dɛ dɩm nɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: Jesus replied, My Father is still working, and I am working too.
local: Zezi ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «A Ko Wɛ yǝni DƖ kɩ kʋlʋ DƖ wʋbʋŋa na lagɩ tɩn mʋ maŋa maama. Amʋ dɩ nan kɩ kʋntʋ mʋ.».

eng: For this reason the Jewish leaders wanted even more to kill him—not only because he was doing away with the Sabbath but also because he called God his own Father, thereby making himself equal with God.
local: O na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn ŋwaanɩ mʋ pɛ Zwifǝ yigǝ tiinǝ bam laan tiini ba bʋŋɩ o gʋm. Kʋ nan daɩ nɩ ba na wɩ o cɔgɩ ba siun dɛ cullu tɩm tɩn má ŋwaanɩ, kʋ yɩ o na tagɩ o wɩ Wɛ yɩ o tɩtɩ Ko tɩn mʋ. O taanɩ dɩm kʋntʋ brɩ nɩ o dɩ Wɛ yɩ bɩdwɩ mʋ.

eng: Jesus responded to the Jewish leaders, I assure you that the Son cant do anything by himself except what he sees the Father doing. Whatever the Father does, the Son does likewise.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, amʋ na yɩ Wɛ Bu wʋm tɩn, a bá wanɩ a kɩ kʋlʋkʋlʋ a tɩtɩ nɩ, kʋ na daɩ kʋlʋ a na nɛ a Ko Wɛ na kɩ tɩn. A Ko na kɩ kʋlʋ tɩn mʋ amʋ dɩ wʋ́ kɩ.

eng: The Father loves the Son and shows him everything that he does. He will show him greater works than these so that you will marvel.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ Baŋa-Wɛ soe DƖ Bu wʋm yɩ DƖ maa brɩ-o kʋlʋ maama DƖ tɩtɩ na kɩ tɩn. DƖ nan wʋ́ brɩ-o wo-kamunnu tɩlʋ na dwe tɩntʋ tɩn sɩ o kɩ, yɩ kʋʋ́ sʋ abam.

eng: As the Father raises the dead and gives life, so too does the Son give life to whomever he wishes.
local: Kʋntʋ, Baŋa-Wɛ na bi nɔɔna DƖ pa ba yagɩ tʋʋnɩ yɩ DƖ pa-ba ŋwɩ-dʋŋa te tɩn, DƖ Bu wʋm dɩ wʋ́ pa balʋ o na lagɩ tɩn na ŋwɩ-dʋŋa.

eng: The Father doesnt judge anyone, but he has given all judgment to the Son.
local: Kʋlʋ na wǝli da tɩn, Baŋa-Wɛ tɩtɩ bá di nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ taanɩ. DƖ nan pɛ DƖ Bu wʋm ni sɩ o di nabiinǝ maama taanɩ.

eng: so that everyone will honor the Son just as they honor the Father. Whoever doesnt honor the Son doesnt honor the Father who sent him.
local: Kʋntʋ wʋ́ pa nɔɔna maama zuli Wɛ Bu wʋm, nɩ ba na zuli o Ko Wɛ te tɩn. Wʋlʋ maama na ba zuli Wɛ Bu wʋm tɩn, o ba zuli o Ko Wɛ dɩlʋ na tʋŋ-o tɩn dɩ mʋ.».

eng: I assure you that whoever hears my word and believes in the one who sent me has eternal life and wont come under judgment but has passed from death into life.
local: Zezi daa ma ta o wɩ: «A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, wʋlʋ maama na ni a taanɩ dɩm yɩ o daarɩ o kɩ o wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ wʋlʋ na tʋŋɩ-nɩ tɩn, kʋntʋ tu jɩgɩ ŋwɩ-dʋŋa kalʋ na ba ti tɩn. Ba daa bá di o taanɩ. Kʋntʋ tu nuŋi tʋʋnɩ wʋnɩ mʋ, o zʋ ŋwɩa kalʋ na ba ti tɩn wʋnɩ.

eng: I assure you that the time is coming—and is here!—when the dead will hear the voice of Gods Son, and those who hear it will live.
local: A lagɩ a ta cɩga dɩ abam sɩ, maŋa maa bɩɩna lele kʋntʋ, yɩ balʋ na ba jɩgɩ ŋwɩa Wɛ tee nɩ tɩn wʋ́ ni amʋ Wɛ Bu wʋm kwǝrǝ. Balʋ na sɛ-ka tɩn wʋ́ na Wɛ ŋwɩ-dʋŋa kam.

eng: Just as the Father has life in himself, so he has granted the Son to have life in himself.
local: Kʋntʋ, Wɛ na yɩ ŋwɩa tu tɩn, mʋ DƖ pɛ wʋlʋ na yɩ DƖ Bu wʋm tɩn dɩ ji ŋwɩa tu.

eng: He gives the Son authority to judge, because he is the Human One.
local: DƖ ma kwǝri DƖ pa-o ni sɩ o di nabiinǝ taanɩ, o na yɩ Nabiin-bu wʋm tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: Dont be surprised by this, because the time is coming when all who are in their graves will hear his voice.
local: Yɩ pa-na kʋ sʋ abam dɩ a na tagɩ woŋo kʋlʋ tɩn. Maŋa maa bɩɩna, yɩ balʋ maama na tɩgɩ tɩn wʋ́ ni amʋ kwǝrǝ.

eng: Those who did good things will come out into the resurrection of life, and those who did wicked things into the resurrection of judgment.
local: yɩ ba nuŋi ba yibeelǝ wʋnɩ. Balʋ maama na kɩ lanyɩranɩ tɩn wʋ́ bi ba taa ŋwɩ Wɛ tee nɩ. Kʋ daarɩ balʋ maama na kɩ wo-balwaarʋ tɩn wʋ́ bi, yɩ ba di ba taanɩ, yɩ ba na cam.

eng: I cant do anything by myself. Whatever I hear, I judge, and my judgment is just. I dont seek my own will but the will of the one who sent me.
local: A bá wanɩ a kɩ kʋlʋkʋlʋ amʋ tɩtɩ nɩ. Amʋ tɔgɩ a Ko ni mʋ a maa di nabiinǝ taanɩ. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ amʋ sarɩya dim dɩm yɔɔrɩ dɩ yɩ cɩga mʋ, bɛŋwaanɩ amʋ ba kwaanɩ sɩ a kɩ kʋlʋ amʋ tɩtɩ na lagɩ tɩn, kʋ yɩ Wɛ na tʋŋɩ-nɩ sɩ a kɩ kʋlʋ DƖ na lagɩ tɩn mʋ.».

eng: If I testify about myself, my testimony isnt true.
local: Zezi ta ma ta dɩ Zwifǝ yigǝ tiinǝ bam o wɩ: «A tɩtɩ yɩranɩ mʋ ya na brɩ a cɩga kam, kʋ daɩ fɩfɩʋn sɩ á sɛ nɩ a taanɩ dɩm yɩ cɩga.

eng: There is someone else who testifies about me, and I know his testimony about me is true.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ nan mʋ zɩgɩ amʋ cɩga kam kwaga nɩ, kʋ yɩ amʋ Ko Wɛ mʋ, yɩ a ye nɩ wʋntʋ jɩgɩ cɩga dɩ o na tɛ amʋ taanɩ te tɩn.

eng: You sent a delegation to John, and he testified to the truth.
local: Abam ya tʋŋɩ nɔɔna Zan tee nɩ yɩ o ta amʋ cɩga o brɩ-ba.

eng: Although I dont accept human testimony, I say these things so that you can be saved.
local: Kʋ na yɩ amʋ, a ya ba lagɩ sɩ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ pa amʋ cɩga kam maana. A nan tagɩ Zan taanɩ abam ŋwaanɩ mʋ, sɩ kʋ wanɩ kʋ wǝli abam sɩ á na vrɩm lwarɩm wʋnɩ.

eng: John was a burning and shining lamp, and, at least for a while, you were willing to celebrate in his light.
local: Zan dɛɛn nyɩ dɩ kanɩa mini na tiini dɩ jɩgɩ pooni te tɩn mʋ. Abam ma sɛ sɩ á tɔgɩ o taanɩ dɩm dɩ wʋpolo maŋa fɩnfɩɩn yɩranɩ, yɩ á laan kwe á yagɩ.

eng: I have a witness greater than Johns testimony. The Father has given me works to do so that I might complete them. These works I do testify about me that the Father sent me.
local: A nan jɩgɩ maana yalʋ na daga ya dwe Zan na pɛ yalʋ tɩn. Tɩtʋŋa yam a Ko Wɛ na pɛ-nɩ sɩ a tʋŋɩ tɩn, yantʋ mʋ a jɩgɩ a tʋŋa, yɩ yantʋ mʋ pa-nɩ cɩga nɩ Wɛ mʋ sɩɩnɩ DƖ tʋŋɩ-nɩ.

eng: And the Father who sent me testifies about me. You have never even heard his voice or seen his form.
local: A Ko Wɛ dɩlʋ na tʋŋɩ-nɩ tɩn zɩgɩ a cɩga kam kwaga nɩ. Abam nan maŋɩ á wʋ cǝgi DƖ kwǝrǝ, naa á na DƖ na nyɩ te tɩn.

eng: and you dont have his word dwelling with you because you dont believe the one whom he has sent.
local: Abam wʋ joŋi DƖ taanɩ dɩm á kɩ á wʋ nɩ. Á na wʋ sɛ wʋlʋ wʋm DƖ na tʋŋɩ tɩn mʋ brɩ kʋntʋ.

eng: Jesus learned that the Pharisees had heard that he was making more disciples and baptizing more than John .
local: Farizɩan tiinǝ bam dɛɛn ma ni nɩ Zezi wʋra o paɩ nɔɔna zanzan tɔgɩ o kwaga kʋ dwe Zan, yɩ o ta kwǝri o miisi-ba na wʋnɩ Wɛ ŋwaanɩ. Kʋ nan daɩ Zezi tɩtɩ mʋ miisi-ba na wʋnɩ, kʋ yɩ o karabiǝ bam mʋ miisi-ba. Zezi na maanɩ nɩ Farizɩan tiinǝ bam lwarɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma zaŋɩ Zude nɩ o joori o maa ve Galile.

eng: Examine the scriptures, since you think that in them you have eternal life. They also testify about me.
local: Abam yǝni á kwaanɩ á zaasɩ Wɛ twaanʋ tɩm, bɛŋwaanɩ á bʋŋɩ nɩ tɩntʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ á wʋ́ na ŋwɩa kalʋ na ba ti tɩn. Kʋ nan yɩ amʋ taanɩ mʋ twaanʋ tɩm kʋntʋ tɛa.

eng: Jesus had to go through Samaria.
local: O na maa ve kʋntʋ tɩn, o maŋɩ sɩ o tɔgɩ Samari wʋnɩ mʋ o kɛ.

eng: yet you dont want to come to me so that you can have life.
local: Abam nan ta ba sɛ sɩ á ba amʋ te, sɩ kʋ pa á na ŋwɩ-dʋŋa kalʋ na ba ti tɩn.

eng: He came to a Samaritan city called Sychar, which was near the land Jacob had given to his son Joseph.
local: O ma vu o yi Samari tɩʋ kʋdoŋ, kʋ yɩrɩ mʋ Siswaarɩ. Kʋ batwarɩ dɩ tɩga kalʋ Zakɔbɩ dɛɛn na pɛ o bu Zʋzɛfʋ faŋa faŋa tɩn.

eng: I dont accept praise from people.
local: A nan ba bʋŋɩ sɩ nabiinǝ ta pa amʋ zulǝ.

eng: Jacobs well was there. Jesus was tired from his journey, so he sat down at the well. It was about noon.
local: Zakɔbɩ dɛɛn jɩgɩ vʋlɩ je sɩm kʋntʋ nɩ, dɩ ta na wʋra dɩ zɩm maama. Zezi na ve taan kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma bwǝni. O ma vu o jǝni vʋlɩ dɩm ni nɩ. Kʋ dɛɛn yɩ wɩa tɩtarɩ nɩ mʋ.

eng: but I know you, that you dont have Gods love in you.
local: Kʋ na yɩ abam, amʋ ye á wʋrʋ. A ye nɩ Wɛ sono tǝri á bɩcara nɩ.

eng: A Samaritan woman came to the well to draw water. Jesus said to her, Give me some water to drink.
local: O karabiǝ bam maa kɛ tɩʋ kʋm wʋ sɩ ba yǝgi wʋdiu. Zezi na je da kʋntʋ tɩn, Samari tiinǝ kaanɩ wʋdoŋ laan ma ba sɩ o mɔ na. Zezi ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ, o kɩ na o pa-o sɩ o nyɔ.

eng: I have come in my Fathers name, and you dont receive me. If others come in their own name, you receive them.
local: Amʋ nan jaanɩ a Ko yɩrɩ mʋ a ba, yɩ abam vɩn sɩ á jeeri-nɩ. Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ nan na jaanɩ o tɩtɩ yɩrɩ o ba, á wʋ́ jeeri kʋntʋ tu.

eng: The Samaritan woman asked, Why do you, a Jewish man, ask for something to drink from me, a Samaritan woman.
local: Kaanɩ wʋm ma ta dɩ Zezi o wɩ: «Nmʋ yɩ Zwifu mʋ, yɩ amʋ yɩ Samari tu. Bɛɛ mʋ yɩ n ta n wɩ, a kɩ na a pa-m?» Kaanɩ wʋm tagɩ kʋntʋ, bɛŋwaanɩ Zwifǝ bam dɩ Samari tiinǝ dɛɛn warɩ daanɩ.

eng: How can you believe when you receive praise from each other but dont seek the praise that comes from the only God?.
local: Abam nan lagɩ sɩ á taá joŋi zulǝ mʋ da-tee nɩ, yɩ á ba lagɩ sɩ á kɩ kʋlʋ na wʋ́ pa á na zulǝ Wɛ dɩlʋ na ba jɩgɩ doŋ tɩn tee nɩ. Kʋntʋ mʋ á warɩ á kɩ á wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ amʋ.

eng: Jesus responded, If you recognized Gods gift and who is saying to you, Give me some water to drink, you would be asking him and he would give you living water.
local: Zezi ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «Nmʋ ya na ye pɛɛrɩ dɩlʋ Wɛ na lagɩ DƖ pa-m tɩn, yɩ nmʋ ya na ye wʋlʋ na loori nmʋ na tɩn, nmʋ tɩtɩ ya mʋ wʋ́ loori amʋ, yɩ a pa nmʋ na balʋ na paɩ nabiinǝ ŋwɩ-dʋŋa tɩn.».

eng: Dont think that I will accuse you before the Father. Your accuser is Moses, the one in whom your hope rests.
local: Á yɩ taá bʋŋɩ nɩ, amʋ mʋ wʋ́ vɩn abam bʋra a Ko Wɛ tee nɩ. Kʋ yɩ Moyisi wʋm abam ya na bʋŋɩ sɩ á wʋ́ na ŋwɩa Wɛ tee nɩ o ŋwaanɩ tɩn mʋ wʋ́ vɩn abam bʋra.

eng: The woman said to him, Sir, you dont have a bucket and the well is deep. Where would you get this living water?.
local: Kaanɩ wʋm ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «A tu, nmʋ ba jɩgɩ goŋo yɩ vʋlɩ dɩm luunǝ. Nmʋ nan wʋ́ kɩ ta mʋ n mɔ na balʋ na paɩ nabiinǝ ŋwɩ-dʋŋa tɩn?.

eng: If you believed Moses, you would believe me, because Moses wrote about me.
local: Abam ya na sɩɩnɩ á sɛ Moyisi na tagɩ kʋlʋ tɩn, á ya wʋ́ sɛ amʋ dɩ, bɛŋwaanɩ wʋntʋ pʋpʋnɩ amʋ taanɩ mʋ.

eng: You arent greater than our father Jacob, are you? He gave this well to us, and he drank from it himself, as did his sons and his livestock.
local: Dɩbam nabaarʋ Zakɔbɩ mʋ kʋgɩ vʋlɩ dɩntʋ o pa dɩbam. Wʋntʋ tɩtɩ dɩ o biǝ dɩ o vara maama dɛɛn mɔɔnɩ na yo mʋ faŋa faŋa ba nyɔa. Nmʋ nan bʋŋɩ n paɩ n dwe Zakɔbɩ mʋ naa?».

eng: If you dont believe the writings of Moses, how will you believe my words.
local: Abam nan maŋɩ á wʋ sɛ o na pʋpʋnɩ kʋlʋ tɩn. Kʋntʋ tɩn, á nan wʋ́ kɩ ta mʋ á sɛ amʋ na tagɩ kʋlʋ tɩn?».

eng: Jesus answered, Everyone who drinks this water will be thirsty again.
local: O ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «Kʋ na yɩ vʋlɩ dɩntʋ na bam, nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ maama na nyɔgɩ-ba tɩn, na-nyɔm ta wʋ́ ja kʋntʋ tu.

eng: but whoever drinks from the water that I will give will never be thirsty again. The water that I give will become in those who drink it a spring of water that bubbles up into eternal life.
local: Kʋ nan na yɩ na balʋ amʋ na wʋ́ pa tɩn, na-nyɔm daa bá ja wʋlʋ na nyɔgɩ-ba tɩn. Na balʋ a na wʋ́ pa tɩn wʋ́ ji nɩnɛɛnɩ buli-yi mʋ te o wʋnɩ, yɩ dɩ wʋ́ ta burǝ na balʋ na paɩ ŋwɩ-dʋŋa kalʋ na ba ti tɩn.».

eng: The woman said to him, Sir, give me this water, so that I will never be thirsty and will never need to come here to draw water.
local: Kaanɩ wʋm daa ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «A tu, nan pa-nɩ na bam kʋntʋ, sɩ na-nyɔm daa yɩ ja-nɩ sɩ a taa tui yo a mɔɔnɩ na.».

eng: Jesus said to her, Go, get your husband, and come back here.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Ve n bǝŋi n barʋ sɩ á da daanɩ á ba.».

eng: The woman replied, I dont have a husband. You are right to say, I dont have a husband, Jesus answered.
local: Kaanɩ wʋm ma lǝri o wɩ, wʋm ba jɩgɩ barʋ. Zezi ma ta-o o wɩ: «Nmʋ na tagɩ nɩ n ba jɩgɩ barʋ tɩn yɩ cɩga mʋ.

eng: Youve had five husbands, and the man you are with now isnt your husband. Youve spoken the truth.
local: Nmʋ ya n zʋ banna banu mʋ, yɩ nmʋ na wʋ nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ tee nɩ lele kʋntʋ tɩn daɩ n barʋ. Kʋ yɩ cɩga mʋ n tagɩ n brɩ-nɩ.».

eng: The woman said, Sir, I see that you are a prophet.
local: Kaanɩ wʋm ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «A tu, amʋ lwarɩ nɩ n yɩ Wɛ nijoŋnu mʋ.

eng: Our ancestors worshipped on this mountain, but you and your people say that it is necessary to worship in Jerusalem.
local: Dɩbam Samari tiinǝ nabaara bam dɛɛn tui piu kʋntʋ yuu mʋ ba zuli Wɛ. Kʋ daarɩ abam Zwifǝ bam brɩ nɩ, kʋ maŋɩ sɩ nɔɔna taa zuli Wɛ Zeruzalɛm nɩ mʋ.».

eng: Jesus said to her, Believe me, woman, the time is coming when you and your people will worship the Father neither on this mountain nor in Jerusalem.
local: Zezi laan ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Kaanɩ, sɛ a taanɩ dɩm. Maŋa wʋ́ ba, yɩ abam daa bá ta ve piu kʋntʋ yuu naa Zeruzalɛm sɩ á zuli dɩ́ Ko Wɛ da.

eng: You and your people worship what you dont know; we worship what we know because salvation is from the Jews.
local: Abam Samari tiinǝ yǝri wʋlʋ á na zuli tɩn. Dɩbam Zwifǝ bam nan ye wʋlʋ dɩ́ na zuli tɩn, bɛŋwaanɩ Wɛ tɔgɩ Zwifǝ bam jɩŋa mʋ DƖ vrɩ nabiinǝ ba lwarɩm wʋnɩ.

eng: But the time is coming—and is here!—when true worshippers will worship in spirit and truth. The Father looks for those who worship him this way.
local: Kʋ daarɩ maŋa maa bɩɩnɩ lele kʋntʋ, yɩ balʋ na sɩɩnɩ ba zuli dɩ́ Ko Wɛ cɩga tɩn, baá zuli-DƖ dɩ Wɛ Joro dam dɩ cɩga. Mʋ dɩ́ Ko Wɛ na lagɩ sɩ nabiinǝ taa zuli-DƖ te tɩn.

eng: God is spirit, and it is necessary to worship God in spirit and truth.
local: Wɛ yɩ joro mʋ. Balʋ maama na lagɩ ba zuli-DƖ tɩn maŋɩ sɩ ba zuli-DƖ dɩ Wɛ Joro dam dɩ cɩga mʋ.».

eng: The woman said, I know that the Messiah is coming, the one who is called the Christ. When he comes, he will teach everything to us.
local: Kaanɩ wʋm ma ta o wɩ: «Amʋ ye sɩ Mesi wʋlʋ ba na bǝ nɩ Krisi tɩn wʋ́ ba. O nan na tuǝ, oó ta Wɛ cɩga kam maama o brɩ dɩbam.».

eng: Jesus said to her, I Am—the one who speaks with you.
local: Zezi ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «Kʋ yɩ amʋ mʋ kʋntʋ, a zɩgɩ a ŋɔɔnɩ dɩ nmʋ.».

eng: Just then, Jesus disciples arrived and were shocked that he was talking with a woman. But no one asked, What do you want? or Why are you talking with her.
local: Zezi karabiǝ bam laan ma joori ba ba. Ba na nɛ Zezi na zɩgɩ o ŋɔɔnɩ dɩ kaanɩ wʋm kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ ma sʋ-ba. Dɩ kʋntʋ dɩ, ba wʋlʋwʋlʋ nan wʋ bwe o nii, «bɛɛ mʋ o laga?» naa, «bɛɛ mʋ yɩ Zezi ŋɔɔnɩ dɩ kaanɩ wʋm?».

eng: The woman put down her water jar and went into the city. She said to the people.
local: Kaanɩ wʋm laan ma yagɩ o kɔɔrʋ wʋm, yɩ o daarɩ o vu tɩʋ kʋm wʋ. O na yi da tɩn, o ma ta dɩ nɔɔna bam o wɩ:.

eng: Come and see a man who has told me everything Ive done! Could this man be the Christ.
local: «Ba-na á nii nɔɔnʋ, o na tagɩ amʋ na kɩ kʋlʋ maama tɩn o brɩ-nɩ. A yǝri wʋntʋ mʋ wʋ́ ta yɩ Krisi wʋm Wɛ na tʋŋɩ tɩn naa?».

eng: They left the city and were on their way to see Jesus.
local: O na tagɩ o brɩ-ba kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma nuŋi tɩʋ kʋm wʋnɩ ba maa ve Zezi te.

eng: In the meantime the disciples spoke to Jesus, saying, Rabbi, eat.
local: Kantʋ maŋa kam nɩ Zezi karabiǝ bam ma ta dɩd-o sɩ o di wʋdiu.

eng: Jesus said to them, I have food to eat that you dont know about.
local: O ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «A maŋɩ a jɩgɩ wʋdiu. Abam nan yǝri kʋ na yɩ wʋdiu kʋlʋ tɩn.».

eng: The disciples asked each other, Has someone brought him food.
local: O karabiǝ bam laan ma bwe daanɩ ba wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ mʋ nan ga jaanɩ wʋdiu o ba o pa-o naa?».

eng: Jesus said to them, I am fed by doing the will of the one who sent me and by completing his work.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Amʋ wʋdiu kʋm yɩ sɩ a tɔgɩ wʋlʋ wʋm na tʋŋɩ-nɩ tɩn wʋbʋŋa mʋ, sɩ a tʋŋɩ o tɩtʋŋa yam a ti.

eng: Dont you have a saying, Four more months and then its time for harvest? Look, I tell you: open your eyes and notice that the fields are already ripe for the harvest.
local: Abam yǝni á tɛ nɩ: ‹Kʋ daarɩ canɩ sɩna mʋ sɩ dɩ́ kɩ faa.› Amʋ nan lagɩ a ta abam sɩ á fɔgɩ á nii karɩ sɩm na yɩ te tɩn. Faa kʋm bɩga, kʋ laan daarɩ zagɩm mʋ.

eng: Those who harvest are receiving their pay and gathering fruit for eternal life so that those who sow and those who harvest can celebrate together.
local: Maŋa kam yiǝ, sɩ wʋlʋ na kɩ faa kʋm tɩn joŋi o ŋwɩɩrʋ. Faa kʋm nyɩ dɩ nɔɔna balʋ Wɛ na vrɩ DƖ pa ba na ŋwɩ-dʋŋa kalʋ na ba ti tɩn mʋ, sɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na dugi tɩn dɩ wʋlʋ na zagɩ tɩn maama wǝli daanɩ ba na wʋpolo.

eng: This is a true saying, that one sows and another harvests.
local: Taanɩ dɩntʋ yɩ cɩga mʋ: ‹Nɔɔnʋ mʋ dugǝ, yɩ wʋdoŋ zagɩ.›.

eng: I have sent you to harvest what you didnt work hard for; others worked hard, and you will share in their hard work.
local: Kʋntʋ tɩn, a tʋŋɩ abam sɩ á vu á beeri nɔɔna mʋ á pa Wɛ. Kʋ nyɩ nɩnɛɛnɩ abam wʋ vagɩ mɩna, yɩ á laan wʋ́ vu á zagɩ-ya. Nɔɔna badonnǝ mʋ vagɩ-ya, yɩ abam di nyɔɔrɩ ba tɩtʋŋa yam baŋa nɩ.».

eng: Many Samaritans in that city believed in Jesus because of the womans word when she testified, He told me everything Ive ever done.
local: Samari tiinǝ kaanɩ wʋm dɛɛn na tagɩ o brɩ o tɩʋ kʋm tiinǝ nɩ Zezi tagɩ wʋm na kɩ kʋlʋ maama o brɩ-o tɩn, kʋ ma pa Samari tiinǝ zanzan kɩ ba wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Zezi.

eng: So when the Samaritans came to Jesus, they asked him to stay with them, and he stayed there two days.
local: Samari tiinǝ bam ma vu Zezi te, ba loor-o sɩ o maŋɩ ba tee nɩ. O ma kɩ da yale dɩ ba.

eng: Many more believed because of his word.
local: Nɔɔna zanzan daa ta ma kɩ ba wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩd-o o ni-taanɩ dɩm ŋwaanɩ.

eng: and they said to the woman, We no longer believe because of what you said, for we have heard for ourselves and know that this one is truly the savior of the world.
local: Ba ma ta dɩ kaanɩ wʋm ba wɩ: «Dɩbam laan kɩ dɩ́ wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩd-o, yɩ kʋ daa daɩ nmʋ na tagɩ kʋlʋ n brɩ dɩbam tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Dɩbam tɩtɩ ni o taanɩ dɩm, yɩ dɩ́ kwǝri dɩ́ lwarɩ nɩ wʋntʋ mʋ sɩɩnɩ o yɩ wʋlʋ na vrɩ nabiinǝ lwarɩm wʋnɩ tɩn.».

eng: After two days Jesus left for Galilee. .
local: Da yale yam dɛɛn na kɛ tɩn, Zezi ma zaŋɩ o maa ve o tɩtɩ tɩʋ Galile.

eng: Jesus himself had testified that prophets have no honor in their own country.
local: Zezi tɩtɩ dɛɛn mʋ tagɩ o wɩ, ba ba nɩgɩ Wɛ nijoŋnu o tɩtɩ tɩʋ nɩ.

eng: When he came to Galilee, the Galileans welcomed him because they had seen all the things he had done in Jerusalem during the festival, for they also had been at the festival.
local: Zezi nan na ve o yi Galile tɩn, nɔɔna ma jeer-o lanyɩranɩ, bɛŋwaanɩ ba ya na ve Zeruzalɛm sɩ ba di Pakɩ candiǝ kam tɩn, ba nɛ wo-kɩnkagɩla yalʋ maama o na kɩ da tɩn.

eng: He returned to Cana in Galilee where he had turned the water into wine. In Capernaum there was a certain royal official whose son was sick.
local: Zezi laan ma joori o vu Kana, Galile tɩʋ nɩ. Kʋ yɩ jǝgǝ kalʋ o ya na pɛ na bam ji tweeru biǝ sana tɩn mʋ. Dɩdɛɛrʋ wʋdoŋ maa wʋ Kapɛrnawum nɩ, yɩ o bu ba jɩgɩ yazurǝ.

eng: When he heard that Jesus was coming from Judea to Galilee, he went out to meet him and asked Jesus if he would come and heal his son, for his son was about to die.
local: O na lwarɩ nɩ Zezi nuŋi Zude nɩ o ba Galile tɩn, o ma zaŋɩ o vu o te, o loor-o sɩ o vu Kapɛrnawum o pa o bu wʋm na yazurǝ, o na lagɩ o tɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: Jesus said to him, Unless you see miraculous signs and wonders, you wont believe.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Abam na wʋ nɛ wo-kɩnkagɩla yalʋ na brɩ nɩ Wɛ tʋŋɩ-nɩ tɩn, abam bá fɔgɩ á sɛ amʋ.».

eng: The royal official said to him, Lord, come before my son dies.
local: Dɩdɛɛrʋ wʋm daa ma ta o wɩ: «A tu, pa dɩ́ vu lɩla sɩ a bu wʋm wʋ́ zɩgɩ o tɩ.».

eng: Jesus replied, Go home. Your son lives. The man believed the word that Jesus spoke to him and set out for his home.
local: Zezi laan ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Ve sɔŋɔ, sɩ n bu wʋm daa bá tɩ.» Nɔɔnʋ wʋm ma kɩ o wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Zezi na tagɩ te tɩn, yɩ o zaŋɩ o viiri.

eng: While he was on his way, his servants were already coming to meet him. They said, Your son lives.
local: O ta na wʋ cwǝŋǝ nɩ o maa ve sɔŋɔ tɩn, mʋ o tɩntʋŋna tu ba jeer-o, yɩ ba ta ba wɩ: «N bu wʋm nɛ yazurǝ.».

eng: So he asked them at what time his son had started to get better. And they said, The fever left him yesterday at about one oclock in the afternoon.
local: O ma bwe-ba o wɩ: «Maŋa kɔɔ mʋ kʋ garɩ bu wʋm yɩra nɩ?» Ba ma ta ba wɩ: «Kʋ yɩ diin, wɩa tɩtarɩ nɩ mʋ, o paa kam yag-o.».

eng: Then the father realized that this was the hour when Jesus had said to him, Your son lives. And he and his entire household believed in Jesus.
local: Ba na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, nɔɔnʋ wʋm ma guli nɩ kʋ yɩ maŋa kam kʋntʋ nɩ nɔ́ɔ mʋ Zezi tagɩ o wɩ, o bu wʋm daa bá tɩ. O dɩ o sɔŋɔ tiinǝ maama laan ma kɩ ba wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Zezi.

eng: This was the second miraculous sign Jesus did while going from Judea to Galilee.
local: Mʋ Zezi na kɩ wo-kɩnkagɩlɩ dɩlʋ, o kɩ kuni bɩle Galile nɩ, o na nuŋi Zude o joori o ba tɩn.

eng: After this Jesus went across the Galilee Sea .
local: Dɛ dɩdwɩ Zezi dɩ o karabiǝ bam ma zaŋɩ ba vu ba bɛ Galile nɩnɩʋ kʋm bubɛ dɩdoŋ dɩm. Nɔɔna badaara bǝ-ka nɩ Tiberiyadɩ nɩnɩʋ mʋ.

eng: A large crowd followed him, because they had seen the miraculous signs he had done among the sick.
local: Nɔn-kɔgɔ zanzan maa tɔgɩ o kwaga, bɛŋwaanɩ ba nɛ wo-kɩnkagɩla yalʋ o na kɩ o pa yawɩɩna na yazurǝ tɩn.

eng: Jesus went up a mountain and sat there with his disciples.
local: Kʋ dɛɛn twɛ sɩ Zwifǝ bam di ba candiǝ kalʋ yɩrɩ na yɩ Pakɩ tɩn. Zezi ma vu o di piu yuu o jǝni da dɩ o karabiǝ bam.

eng: Jesus looked up and saw the large crowd coming toward him. He asked Philip, Where will we buy food to feed these people.
local: O ma kwǝni o yuu o nii, yɩ o na nɩ kɔgɔ zanzan mʋ maa bɩɩnɩ o te. O ma ta dɩ Filipi o wɩ: «Dɩ́ wʋ́ kɩ ta mʋ dɩ́ na wʋdiu, sɩ dɩ́ yǝgi dɩ́ pa nɔɔna bantʋ maama sɩ ba di?».

eng: Jesus said this to test him, for he already knew what he was going to do.
local: O na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ yɩ sɩ o ma nii Filipi wʋbʋŋa na yɩ te tɩn mʋ, sɩ o maŋɩ o ye o na wʋ́ kɩ te.

eng: Philip replied, More than a half years salary worth of food wouldnt be enough for each person to have even a little bit.
local: Filipi ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «Dɩ́ na lagɩ dɩ́ yǝgi dɩpɛ sɩ nɔɔnʋ maama na fɩnfɩɩn o di, kʋ sǝbu kʋm wʋ́ dwǝni sǝbu-dala biǝ-yale .».

eng: One of his disciples, Andrew, Simon Peters brother, said.
local: O karabiǝ bam dɩdʋa, Andre wʋlʋ na yɩ Simɔn Pɩyɛɛrɩ nyaanɩ tɩn, ma ta o wɩ:.

eng: A youth here has five barley loaves and two fish. But what good is that for a crowd like this.
local: «Nii, bu wʋdoŋ mʋ wʋ yo seeni, o jɩgɩ dɩpwa yanu dɩ kalǝŋ-balɛ sɩle. Kʋ nan bá wanɩ kʋ yi nɔɔna bantʋ maama dim.».

eng: Jesus said, Have the people sit down. There was plenty of grass there. They sat down, about five thousand of them.
local: Zezi laan ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Á nan pa nɔɔna bam jǝni tɩga nɩ.» Gaarʋ tiini tɩ wʋra jǝgǝ kam kʋntʋ nɩ. Nɔɔna bam maama ma jǝni tɩga nɩ. Ba yi nɩnɛɛnɩ baara mʋrr-tɩnu , kʋ wǝli dɩ kaana dɩ biǝ.

eng: Then Jesus took the bread. When he had given thanks, he distributed it to those who were sitting there. He did the same with the fish, each getting as much as they wanted.
local: Zezi ma kwe dɩpwa yam yɩ o kɩ Wɛ le ya ŋwaanɩ. O ma daarɩ o maŋɩ-ya o pa nɔɔna bam na je tɩga nɩ tɩn. O ma kɩ Wɛ le kale sɩm dɩ ŋwaanɩ yɩ o pa-ba. Nɔɔna bam maama ma di nɩ ba na lagɩ te tɩn.

eng: When they had plenty to eat, he said to his disciples, Gather up the leftover pieces, so that nothing will be wasted.
local: Ba ma di ba su ba daarɩ. Zezi ma ta dɩ o karabiǝ bam o wɩ: «Ve-na á twɛ cicwǝǝru tɩlʋ na daarɩ tɩn, sɩ á yɩ yagɩ sɩ tɩ cɔgɩ.».

eng: So they gathered them and filled twelve baskets with the pieces of the five barley loaves that had been left over by those who had eaten.
local: Ba ma twɛ tɩ maama ba su tɩtwarʋ fugǝ-tɩle. Dɩpwa yanu yam ba na di ba daarɩ tɩn cicwǝǝru yɩranɩ mʋ kʋntʋ.

eng: When the people saw that he had done a miraculous sign, they said, This is truly the prophet who is coming into the world.
local: Nɔɔna bam na nɛ wo-kɩnkagɩlɩ dɩm Zezi na kɩ tɩn, ba ma ta ba wɩ: «Kʋ sɩɩnɩ kʋ yɩ cɩga mʋ, Wɛ nijoŋnu wʋlʋ ya na maŋɩ sɩ o ba lʋgʋ baŋa tɩn mʋ tɩntʋ.».

eng: Jesus understood that they were about to come and force him to be their king, so he took refuge again, alone on a mountain.
local: Zezi laan ma lwarɩ nɩ ba lagɩ ba pa o ji ba pɛ dɩ dam mʋ. O na lwarɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma zaŋɩ o joori o vu o di piu yuu o wʋra o yɩranɩ.

eng: When evening came, Jesus disciples went down to the lake.
local: Dɩdaanɩ na yi tɩn, Zezi karabiǝ bam ma vu ba yi nɩnɩʋ kʋm ni.

eng: They got into a boat and were crossing the lake to Capernaum. It was already getting dark and Jesus hadnt come to them yet.
local: Ba ma zʋ naboro ba tɔgɩ nɩnɩʋ kʋm wʋ ba maa ve Kapɛrnawum. Tɩga maa tiini ka yi, yɩ Zezi daa ta wʋ tu ba te.

eng: The water was getting rough because a strong wind was blowing.
local: Vu-dɩʋ laan ma zaŋɩ kʋ fufugǝ kʋ pa na bam pɔgɩla.

eng: When the wind had driven them out for about three or four miles, they saw Jesus walking on the water. He was approaching the boat and they were afraid.
local: Zezi karabiǝ bam ma co naboro kʋm ba vu ba yi nɩnɛɛnɩ kilomɛtra yanu naa yardʋ te. Ba laan ma na Zezi na ve na bam baŋa nɩ o maa bɩɩnɩ ba te. Fʋʋnɩ ma zʋ-ba.

eng: He said to them, I Am. Dont be afraid.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Á yɩ taá kwarɩ fʋʋnɩ, sɩ kʋ yɩ amʋ.».

eng: Then they wanted to take him into the boat, and just then the boat reached the land where they had been heading.
local: Ba ma bʋŋɩ sɩ ba pa Zezi zʋ naboro kʋm, dɩ ba dɛ ba yi buburu ni, jǝgǝ kalʋ ba ya na maa ve tɩn.

eng: The next day the crowd that remained on the other side of the lake realized that only one boat had been there. They knew Jesus hadnt gone with his disciples, but that the disciples had gone alone.
local: Tɩga na pʋʋrɩ tɩn, nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋlʋ na maŋɩ nɩnɩʋ kʋm bubɛ dɩdoŋ dɩm nɩ tɩn ma maanɩ nɩ, naboro dɩdʋa yɩranɩ ya mʋ wʋra. Ba maa ye nɩ Zezi karabiǝ bam kɛ ba daar-o, yɩ o wʋ dɛ o zʋ ba naboro kʋm o vu.

eng: Some boats came from Tiberias, near the place where they had eaten the bread over which the Lord had given thanks.
local: Nabwǝǝru tɩdonnǝ ma nuŋi Tiberiyadɩ tɩ ba, tɩ yi bubɛ dɩlʋ nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm na wʋra tɩn. Kʋ yɩ jǝgǝ kam kʋntʋ nɩ mʋ dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi ya kɩ Wɛ le wʋdiiru tɩm ŋwaanɩ, yɩ o daarɩ o pa nɔɔna bam di tɩn.

eng: When the crowd saw that neither Jesus nor his disciples were there, they got into the boats and came to Capernaum looking for Jesus.
local: Nɔɔna bam na lwarɩ nɩ Zezi dɩ o karabiǝ bam daa tǝrǝ tɩn, ba ma zʋ nabwǝǝru tɩm wʋ ba maa ve Kapɛrnawum sɩ ba beer-o.

eng: When they found him on the other side of the lake, they asked him, Rabbi, when did you get here.
local: Nɔɔna bam na bɛ nɩnɩʋ kʋm tɩn, ba ma na Zezi. Ba ma bwe-o ba wɩ: «Karanyɩna, maŋa kɔɔ mʋ n tu yoba?».

eng: Jesus replied, I assure you that you are looking for me not because you saw miraculous signs but because you ate all the food you wanted.
local: Zezi ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, abam na karɩ á beeri amʋ tɩn, kʋ daɩ á na nɛ wo-kɩnkagɩla yalʋ na brɩ nɩ Wɛ tʋŋɩ-nɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Kʋ yɩ á na di wʋdiiru tɩm á su tɩn ŋwaanɩ mʋ á beeri amʋ.

eng: Dont work for the food that doesnt last but for the food that endures for eternal life, which the Human One will give you. God the Father has confirmed him as his agent to give life.
local: Á nan yɩ taá tʋŋɩ-na wʋdiu kʋlʋ na wʋ́ ba kʋ cɔgɩ tɩn má ŋwaanɩ. Á taá tʋŋɩ wʋdiu kʋlʋ na wʋ́ pa á na ŋwɩ-dʋŋa kalʋ na ba ti tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm mʋ wʋ́ pa abam kʋntʋ wʋdiu kʋm, bɛŋwaanɩ a Ko Wɛ mʋ pɛ-nɩ ni sɩ a taa tʋŋa a pa-DƖ.».

eng: They asked, What must we do in order to accomplish what God requires.
local: Ba laan ma bwe-o ba wɩ: «Dɩbam nan wʋ́ kɩ ta mʋ, sɩ dɩ́ taá tʋŋɩ tɩtʋŋa yalʋ Wɛ na lagɩ tɩn?».

eng: Jesus replied, This is what God requires, that you believe in him whom God sent.
local: O ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Tɩtʋŋɩ dɩlʋ Wɛ na lagɩ sɩ abam tʋŋɩ tɩn, kʋ yɩ sɩ á sɛ wʋlʋ DƖ na tʋŋɩ tɩn mʋ.».

eng: They asked, What miraculous sign will you do, that we can see and believe you? What will you do?.
local: Ba ma ta bwe-o ba wɩ: «Wo-kɩnkagɩlɩ dɔɔ mʋ nmʋ wʋ́ kɩ, sɩ dɩ́ wanɩ dɩ́ lwarɩ nɩ Wɛ mʋ sɩɩnɩ DƖ tʋŋɩ nmʋ, sɩ dɩ́ laan sɛ-m? Nmʋ wʋ́ kɩ bɛɛ mʋ n brɩ dɩbam?.

eng: Our ancestors ate manna in the wilderness, just as it is written, He gave them bread from heaven to eat .
local: Faŋa faŋa tɩn, dɩbam nabaara dɛɛn na wʋ kagʋa wʋnɩ maŋa kalʋ nɩ tɩn, ba di wʋdiu kʋlʋ yɩrɩ na yɩ ‹Maanɩ› tɩn mʋ, nɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm na tagɩ te kʋ wɩ: ‹O pɛ-ba wʋdiu kʋlʋ na nuŋi Wɛ-sɔŋɔ nɩ tɩn yɩ ba di.› ».

eng: Jesus told them, I assure you, it wasnt Moses who gave the bread from heaven to you, but my Father gives you the true bread from heaven.
local: Zezi ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, kʋ daɩ Moyisi mʋ pɛ abam wʋdiu kʋlʋ na nuŋi Wɛ-sɔŋɔ nɩ tɩn. Kʋ nan yɩ a Ko Wɛ mʋ paɩ abam wʋdiu kʋlʋ na nuŋi DƖ sɔŋɔ nɩ yɩ kʋ yɩ wʋdiu cɩga cɩga tɩn.

eng: The bread of God is the one who comes down from heaven and gives life to the world.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ wʋdiu kʋlʋ Wɛ na paɩ tɩn mʋ yɩ wʋlʋ na nuŋi DƖ sɔŋɔ nɩ o tu o ba lʋgʋ baŋa o paɩ nabiinǝ ŋwɩ-dʋŋa tɩn.».

eng: They said, Sir, give us this bread all the time.
local: Ba ma ta dɩd-o ba wɩ: «Dɩbam tu, ta n paɩ dɩbam wʋdiu kʋm kʋntʋ maŋa maama.».

eng: Jesus replied, I am the bread of life. Whoever comes to me will never go hungry, and whoever believes in me will never be thirsty.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Amʋ yɩ wʋdiu kʋlʋ na paɩ nabiinǝ ŋwɩ-dʋŋa tɩn. Wʋlʋ maama na tu amʋ te tɩn, kana daa bá ja kʋntʋ tu. Wʋlʋ maama na kɩ o wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ amʋ tɩn, na-nyɔm daa bá ja kʋntʋ tu.

eng: But I told you that you have seen me and still dont believe.
local: A nan maŋɩ a ta a brɩ abam nɩ, á nɛ amʋ yɩ á ta ba sɛ-nɩ.

eng: Everyone whom the Father gives to me will come to me, and I wont send away anyone who comes to me.
local: Kʋ daarɩ wʋlʋ maama a Ko Wɛ na pɛ amʋ tɩn wʋ́ ba amʋ te. Wʋlʋ maama nan na tu amʋ te tɩn, a bá fɔgɩ a vɩn kʋntʋ tu.

eng: I have come down from heaven not to do my will, but the will of him who sent me.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ amʋ nuŋi Wɛ-sɔŋɔ a ba sɩ a kɩ wʋlʋ na tʋŋɩ-nɩ tɩn wʋbʋŋa mʋ, sɩ kʋ daɩ a tɩtɩ wʋbʋŋa.

eng: This is the will of the one who sent me, that I wont lose anything he has given me, but I will raise it up at the last day.
local: Wʋlʋ na tʋŋɩ-nɩ tɩn wʋbʋŋa na lagɩ kʋlʋ tɩn mʋ yɩ sɩ a yɩ yagɩ a pa balʋ maama DƖ na pɛ-nɩ tɩn wʋlʋwʋlʋ je, sɩ a daarɩ a pa ba maama joori ba bi lʋgʋ tiim dɛ nɩ.

eng: This is my Fathers will: that all who see the Son and believe in him will have eternal life, and I will raise them up at the last day.
local: A Ko Wɛ wʋbʋŋa mʋ yɩ sɩ, wʋlʋ maama na lwarɩ DƖ Bu wʋm yɩ o kɩ o wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩd-o tɩn, sɩ kʋntʋ tu na ŋwɩ-dʋŋa kalʋ na ba ti tɩn. Aá pa kʋntʋ tu joori o bi lʋgʋ tiim dɛ dɩm nɩ.».

eng: The Jewish opposition grumbled about him because he said, I am the bread that came down from heaven.
local: Zezi na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, Zwifǝ bam maa pʋʋna dɩ o na tagɩ o wɩ, wʋm mʋ yɩ wʋdiu kʋlʋ na nuŋi Wɛ-sɔŋɔ nɩ tɩn.

eng: They asked, Isnt this Jesus, Josephs son, whose mother and father we know? How can he now say, I have come down from heaven.
local: Ba maa tɛ ba wɩ: «Wʋntʋ mʋ daɩ Zʋzɛfʋ bu Zezi wʋm na? Dɩ́ ye o nu dɩ o ko. Bɛɛ mʋ yɩ o laan nan ta wɩ, o nuŋi Wɛ-sɔŋɔ nɩ mʋ o ba?».

eng: Jesus responded, Dont grumble among yourselves.
local: Zezi ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Yagɩ-na, á yɩ taá pʋʋna.

eng: No one can come to me unless they are drawn to me by the Father who sent me, and I will raise them up at the last day.
local: Nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ bá wanɩ o ba amʋ te, kʋ na daɩ a Ko Wɛ dɩlʋ na tʋŋɩ-nɩ tɩn mʋ vaŋɩ kʋntʋ tu jɩŋa sɩ o ba amʋ te. Amʋ wʋ́ pa kʋntʋ tu joori o bi lʋgʋ tiim dɛ dɩm nɩ.

eng: It is written in the Prophets, And they will all be taught by God. Everyone who has listened to the Father and learned from him comes to me.
local: Kʋ pʋpʋnɩ faŋa faŋa Wɛ nijoŋnǝ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ kʋ wɩ: ‹Ba maama wʋ́ zaasɩ Wɛ tee nɩ.› Wʋlʋ maama nan na cǝgi a Ko Wɛ taanɩ dɩm yɩ o sɛ DƖ zaasɩm dɩm tɩn wʋ́ ba amʋ te.

eng: No one has seen the Father except the one who is from God. He has seen the Father.
local: Kʋ nan daɩ nɩ nɔɔnʋ mʋ nɛ a Ko Wɛ, kʋ yɩ amʋ na nuŋi DƖ te tɩn yɩranɩ mʋ nɛ-DƖ.

eng: I assure you, whoever believes has eternal life.
local: A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, wʋlʋ na kɩ o wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ amʋ tɩn mʋ jɩgɩ ŋwɩ-dʋŋa kalʋ na ba ti tɩn.

eng: I am the bread of life.
local: Amʋ yɩ wʋdiu kʋlʋ na paɩ nabiinǝ ŋwɩ-dʋŋa tɩn.

eng: Your ancestors ate manna in the wilderness and they died.
local: Faŋa faŋa tɩn, abam nabaara dɛɛn na wʋ kagʋa kam wʋnɩ tɩn, ba di wʋdiu kʋlʋ yɩrɩ na yɩ ‹Maanɩ› tɩn, yɩ ba ta wʋ lugi tʋʋnɩ.

eng: This is the bread that comes down from heaven so that whoever eats from it will never die.
local: Kʋ daarɩ kʋntʋ yɩ wʋdiu kʋlʋ na nuŋi Wɛ-sɔŋɔ, yɩ wʋlʋ na di-kʋ tɩn, tʋʋnɩ daa bá ja kʋntʋ tu.

eng: I am the living bread that came down from heaven. Whoever eats this bread will live forever, and the bread that I will give for the life of the world is my flesh.
local: Amʋ yɩ wʋdiu kʋlʋ na nuŋi Wɛ-sɔŋɔ nɩ yɩ kʋ jɩgɩ ŋwɩa kʋ paɩ nabiinǝ tɩn. Wʋlʋ maama na di wʋdiu kʋm kʋntʋ tɩn wʋ́ ta ŋwɩ mʋ maŋa maama. Wʋdiu kʋlʋ a na wʋ́ pa nabiinǝ tɩn yɩ a yɩra yam mʋ, sɩ kʋ pa nabiinǝ taa jɩgɩ ŋwɩ-dʋŋa.».

eng: Then the Jews debated among themselves, asking, How can this man give us his flesh to eat.
local: Zezi na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, Zwifǝ bam laan ma tiini ba magɩ kantɔgɔ daanɩ ba wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ wʋ́ kɩ ta mʋ o kwe o yɩra o pa dɩbam sɩ dɩ́ di?».

eng: Jesus said to them, I assure you, unless you eat the flesh of the Human One and drink his blood, you have no life in you.
local: Zezi laan ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, abam na wʋ di amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm yɩra yam, yɩ á daarɩ á wʋ nyɔgɩ a jana kam, á ba jɩgɩ Wɛ ŋwɩ-dʋŋa kam.

eng: Whoever eats my flesh and drinks my blood has eternal life, and I will raise them up at the last day.
local: Wʋlʋ nan na di a yɩra yam yɩ o daarɩ o nyɔ a jana kam tɩn, kʋntʋ tu jɩgɩ ŋwɩa kalʋ na ba ti tɩn. Amʋ wʋ́ joori a bi kʋntʋ tu lʋgʋ tiim dɛ dɩm nɩ.

eng: My flesh is true food and my blood is true drink.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ a yɩra yam mʋ yɩ wʋdiu cɩga, yɩ a jana kam yɩ wo-nyɔɔrʋ cɩga cɩga.

eng: Whoever eats my flesh and drinks my blood remains in me and I in them.
local: Wʋlʋ na di a yɩra yam yɩ o daarɩ o nyɔ a jana kam tɩn wʋ́ ta ŋwɩ dɩ amʋ, yɩ amʋ ŋwɩ kʋntʋ tu wʋnɩ.

eng: As the living Father sent me, and I live because of the Father, so whoever eats me lives because of me.
local: A Ko Wɛ dɩlʋ na yɩ ŋwɩa tu tɩn mʋ tʋŋɩ-nɩ. Kʋ nan yɩ dɩntʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ amʋ dɩ jɩgɩ ŋwɩa. Kʋntʋ, wʋlʋ na di a yɩra yam tɩn, kʋntʋ tu dɩ wʋ́ ta ŋwɩ amʋ ŋwaanɩ.

eng: This is the bread that came down from heaven. It isnt like the bread your ancestors ate, and then they died. Whoever eats this bread will live forever.
local: Mʋ wʋdiu kʋlʋ na nuŋi Wɛ-sɔŋɔ nɩ tɩn. Kʋntʋ nan ba nyɩ dɩ wʋdiu kʋlʋ abam nabaara bam dɛɛn na di yɩ ba daa ta tɩ tɩn. Wʋlʋ maama na di wʋdiu kʋntʋ tɩn wʋ́ ta ŋwɩ mʋ maŋa maama.».

eng: Jesus said these things while he was teaching in the synagogue in Capernaum.
local: Mʋ Zezi dɛɛn na brɩ nɔɔna bam tɩte Kapɛrnawum Wɛ-digǝ kam wʋnɩ tɩn.

eng: Many of his disciples who heard this said, This message is harsh. Who can hear it.
local: Zezi karabiǝ bam zanzan na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma ta ba wɩ: «O taanɩ dɩm tiini dɩ cana. Nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ bá wanɩ o joŋi-dɩ.».

eng: Jesus knew that the disciples were grumbling about this and he said to them, Does this offend you?.
local: Zezi ma lwarɩ o tɩtɩ nɩ, nɩ o karabiǝ bam pʋʋna dɩ o taanɩ dɩm. O laan ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «A taanɩ dɩm kʋntʋ pɛ á wʋbʋŋa vugimi mʋ na?.

eng: What if you were to see the Human One going up where he was before?.
local: Kʋ nan na yɩ kʋntʋ, á na nɛ Nabiin-bu wʋm na maa joori o diini o ya na maŋɩ o wʋ mɛ tɩn, á laan wʋ́ kɩ tɩta mʋ?.

eng: The Spirit is the one who gives life and the flesh doesnt help at all. The words I have spoken to you are spirit and life.
local: Kʋ yɩ Wɛ Joro kʋm mʋ paɩ nɔɔna ŋwɩa. Kʋ daarɩ nabiinǝ dam bá wanɩ kʋlʋkʋlʋ dɩ kɩ. A na tagɩ taanɩ dɩlʋ dɩ abam tɩn mʋ pɛ abam Wɛ Joro kʋlʋ na paɩ nabiinǝ ŋwɩ-dʋŋa tɩn.

eng: Yet some of you dont believe.Jesus knew from the beginning who wouldnt believe and the one who would betray him.
local: Dɩ kʋntʋ dɩ, abam badaara ta wʋ sɛ.» Zezi tagɩ kʋntʋ, bɛŋwaanɩ kʋ pulim nɩ mʋ o maŋɩ o ye balʋ na bá sɛ-o tɩn, dɩ wʋlʋ na wʋ́ ba o yǝg-o o pa nɔɔna tɩn.

eng: He said,For this reason I said to you that none can come to me unless the Father enables them to do so.
local: O daa ma ta o wɩ: «Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ a tagɩ a brɩ abam nɩ, nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ bá wanɩ o ba amʋ te, yɩ kʋ na daɩ a Ko Wɛ mʋ pɛ-o cwǝŋǝ.».

eng: At this, many of his disciples turned away and no longer accompanied him.
local: Kʋntʋ na kɩ tɩn, o karabiǝ zanzan ma joori o kwaga nɩ, yɩ ba daa ba tɔg-o.

eng: Jesus asked the Twelve, Do you also want to leave.
local: Zezi laan ma ta dɩ o karabiǝ fugǝ-bale bam o wɩ: «Abam dɩ ba lagɩ sɩ á yagɩ amʋ na?».

eng: Simon Peter answered, Lord, where would we go? You have the words of eternal life.
local: Simɔn Pɩyɛɛrɩ ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «A yuutu, dɩbam wʋ́ yagɩ nmʋ, sɩ dɩ́ daa vu wɔɔ te mʋ? Nmʋ jɩgɩ taanɩ dɩlʋ na paɩ nɔɔna ŋwɩa kalʋ na ba ti tɩn.

eng: We believe and know that you are Gods holy one.
local: Dɩbam sɛ nmʋ, yɩ dɩ́ kwǝri dɩ́ lwarɩ nɩ nmʋ yɩ Wʋ-poŋo Tu wʋlʋ Wɛ na tʋŋɩ tɩn mʋ.».

eng: Jesus replied, Didnt I choose you twelve? Yet one of you is a devil.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Amʋ kuri abam fugǝ-bale bam, abam dɩdʋa nan yɩ sʋtaanɩ mʋ.».

eng: He was speaking of Judas, Simon Iscariots son, for he, one of the Twelve, was going to betray him.
local: O na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ yɩ Zudasɩ wʋlʋ na yɩ Simɔn Yiskarɩyo bu tɩn taanɩ mʋ o tɛa. Wʋntʋ tɔgɩ o wʋ karabiǝ fugǝ-bale bam wʋnɩ, yɩ wʋm mʋ lagɩ o ba o yǝgi Zezi o pa nɔɔna.

eng: After this Jesus traveled throughout Galilee. He didnt want to travel in Judea, because the Jewish authorities wanted to kill him.
local: Zezi dɛɛn maa wʋ Galile nɩ o beerǝ. O dɛɛn ba lagɩ sɩ o vu Zude o beeri da, bɛŋwaanɩ Zwifǝ yigǝ tiinǝ balʋ na wʋ dáanɩ tɩn lagɩ cwǝŋǝ mʋ sɩ ba gʋ-o.

eng: When it was almost time for the Jewish Festival of Booths.
local: Kʋ dɛɛn ma daarɩ fɩɩn sɩ Zwifǝ bam di ba candiǝ kalʋ yɩrɩ na yɩ vwǝ candiǝ tɩn.

eng: Jesus brothers said to him, Leave Galilee. Go to Judea so that your disciples can see the amazing works that you do.
local: Zezi nyaana ma ta dɩd-o ba wɩ, o yagɩ Galile je sɩm sɩ o daarɩ o vu Zude, sɩ o karabiǝ bam wanɩ ba na tɩtʋŋa yalʋ o na jɩgɩ o tʋŋɩ tɩn.

eng: Those who want to be known publicly dont do things secretly. Since you can do these things, show yourself to the world.
local: Ba ta maa ta ba wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na lagɩ sɩ ba taa ye-o lanyɩranɩ tɩn ba sǝgi kʋlʋ o na kɩ tɩn. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ, nmʋ na kɩ wǝǝnu tɩntʋ tɩn, n maŋɩ sɩ n ta n kɩ-tɩ nabiinǝ maama yigǝ nɩ mʋ.».

eng: His brothers said this because even they didnt believe in him.
local: Ba na tagɩ te tɩn brɩ nɩ ba tɩtɩ ta wʋ kɩ ba wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩd-o.

eng: Jesus replied, For you, anytime is fine. But my time hasnt come yet.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «A maŋa kam ta wʋ yi sɩ a vu candiǝ kam. Kʋ nan na yɩ abam, maŋa maama lana dɩ abam.

eng: The world cant hate you. It hates me, though, because I testify that its works are evil.
local: Nabiinǝ bá wanɩ ba taa culi abam. Ba nan culi amʋ, bɛŋwaanɩ amʋ yɔɔrɩ a tɛ-ba jaja mʋ, nɩ ba tɩtʋŋa ba tɔgɩ cwǝŋǝ.

eng: You go up to the festival. Im not going to this one because my time hasnt yet come.
local: Abam nan ve-na á di candiǝ kam, sɩ amʋ ta bá vu lele, bɛŋwaanɩ a maŋa kam ta wʋ yi ka ti sɩ a vu.».

eng: Having said this, he stayed in Galilee.
local: O na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma maŋɩ Galile nɩ.

eng: However, after his brothers left for the festival, he went too—not openly but in secret.
local: Zezi nyaana bam na kɛ ba daar-o tɩn, o dɩ laan ma zaŋɩ o vu candiǝ kam. O na ve kʋntʋ tɩn, o wʋ ve jaja sɩ nɔɔna lwar-o.

eng: The Jewish leaders were looking for Jesus at the festival. They kept asking, Where is he.
local: Zwifǝ yigǝ tiinǝ balʋ na wʋ candiǝ kam nɩ tɩn maa karɩ ba beer-o, yɩ ba bwe ba wɩ: «O wʋ yǝn mʋ?».

eng: The crowds were murmuring about him. Hes a good man, some said, but others were saying, No, he tricks the people.
local: Nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋlʋ na wʋra tɩn maa tiini ba jɩgɩ o taanɩ ba waasa. Nɔɔna badonnǝ maa tɛ ba wɩ: «O yɩ nɔn-ŋʋm mʋ.» Badaara maa tɛ ba wɩ: «Aye, o daɩ nɔn-ŋʋm, sɩ o jɩgɩ nɔɔna bam o ganɩ mʋ.».

eng: No one spoke about him publicly, though, for fear of the Jewish authorities.
local: Nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ nan wʋ tagɩ o taanɩ jaja, bɛŋwaanɩ ba kwarɩ Zwifǝ yigǝ tiinǝ bam.

eng: Halfway through the festival, Jesus went up to the temple and started to teach.
local: Ba na di candiǝ kam ba yi ka da yam tɩtarɩ tɩn, Zezi laan ma vu o zʋ Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm o wʋra o brɩ nɔɔna.

eng: Astonished, the Jewish leaders asked, Hes never been taught! How has he mastered the Law.
local: Zwifǝ yigǝ tiinǝ bam maa tiini ba yɩ yǝǝu dɩ o na brɩ nɔɔna bam te tɩn. Ba maa tɛ ba wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ nan wʋ zaasɩ tɔnɔ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ tee nɩ, yɩ o yi kɩ ta mʋ dɩ pʋʋrɩ yɩ o ye Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm kʋntʋ doŋ?».

eng: Jesus responded, My teaching isnt mine but comes from the one who sent me.
local: Zezi ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «A na brɩ kʋlʋ tɩn daɩ amʋ tɩtɩ nyɩm, kʋ yɩ wʋlʋ na tʋŋɩ-nɩ tɩn nyɩm mʋ.

eng: Whoever wants to do Gods will can tell whether my teaching is from God or whether I speak on my own.
local: Wʋlʋ nan na lagɩ sɩ o kɩ Wɛ wʋbʋŋa na lagɩ kʋlʋ tɩn, kʋntʋ tu wʋ́ lwarɩ amʋ zaasɩm dɩm kuri na nuŋi mɛ tɩn, dɩ na yɩ Wɛ nyɩm mʋ, naa dɩ na yɩ a tɩtɩ dam mʋ a maa bra, oó lwarɩ.

eng: Those who speak on their own seek glory for themselves. Those who seek the glory of him who sent me are people of truth; theres no falsehood in them.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na ŋɔɔnɩ dɩ o tɩtɩ dam tɩn, kʋntʋ tu kwaanɩ sɩ o na yɩrɩ mʋ o pa o tɩtɩ. Kʋ daarɩ wʋlʋ nan na kwaanɩ sɩ o lagɩ yɩrɩ o pa wʋlʋ na tʋŋ-o tɩn, kʋntʋ tu mʋ yɩ wʋlʋ na jɩgɩ cɩga tɩn, yɩ o ba tusi kʋlʋkʋlʋ baŋa nɩ.

eng: Didnt Moses give you the Law? Yet none of you keep the Law. Why do you want to kill me.
local: Moyisi nan maŋɩ o pa abam lwarɩ Wɛ cullu tɩm. Abam wʋlʋwʋlʋ nan ba tɔgɩ-tɩ. Bɛɛ mʋ yɩ abam kwaanɩ á lagɩ cwǝŋǝ sɩ á gʋ amʋ?».

eng: The crowd answered, You have a demon. Who wants to kill you.
local: Ba ma lǝr-o ba wɩ: «N yirisi mʋ. Wɔɔn beeri nmʋ sɩ o gʋ?».

eng: Jesus replied, I did one work, and you were all astonished.
local: Zezi ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «A tʋŋɩ tɩtʋŋ-kamunu dɩdʋ mʋ siun dɛ nɩ, yɩ kʋ sʋ abam.

eng: Because Moses gave you the commandment about circumcision , you circumcise a man on the Sabbath.
local: Moyisi dɛɛn pɛ abam ni sɩ á taá goni á bǝkǝri. Kʋ nan daɩ nɩ Moyisi mʋ puli-kʋ. Kʋ puli dɩ abam nabaara mʋ. Moyisi na pɛ abam ni dɩm kʋntʋ tɩn, abam maa goni á bǝkǝri dɩ siun dɛ nɩ dɩ, sɩ kʋ yɩ pa á cɔgɩ ni dɩm kʋntʋ.

eng: If a man can be circumcised on the Sabbath without breaking Moses Law, why are you angry with me because I made an entire man well on the Sabbath?.
local: Abam na waɩ á goni bǝkǝri siun dɛ dɩm nɩ sɩ á yɩ cɔgɩ Moyisi culu kʋm, bɛɛ mʋ yɩ á bana zaŋɩ dɩ amʋ, dɩ a na pɛ yawɩɩnʋ na yazurǝ fasɩ siun dɛ nɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ?.

eng: Dont judge according to appearances. Judge with right judgment.
local: Abam yɩ taá garɩ á yáalɩ nɔɔnʋ kǝm dɩ á yiǝ na nɛ kʋlʋ tɩn. Á nan maŋɩ sɩ á bʋrɩ dɩ cɩga mʋ.».

eng: Some people from Jerusalem said, Isnt he the one they want to kill?.
local: Nɔɔna balʋ na zʋʋrɩ Zeruzalɛm nɩ tɩn badaara laan maa ŋɔɔnɩ Zezi taanɩ ba wɩ: «Kʋ daɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ mʋ ba kwaanɩ sɩ ba gʋ tɩn na?.

eng: Here he is, speaking in public, yet they arent saying anything to him. Could it be that our leaders actually think he is the Christ?.
local: Nii-na o na zɩgɩ o ŋɔɔnɩ jaja, yɩ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ ba tɛ kʋlʋkʋlʋ sɩ ba cɩ-o. Dɩbam yigǝ tiinǝ bam laan bʋŋɩ nɩ wʋntʋ mʋ sɩɩnɩ o yɩ Krisi wʋm Wɛ na tʋŋɩ tɩn naa?.

eng: We know where he is from, but when the Christ comes, no one will know where he is from.
local: Kʋ nan na yɩ Krisi wʋm mʋ na tuǝ, nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ bá wanɩ o lwarɩ o na nuŋi mɛ tɩn. Kʋ daarɩ dɩbam maama ye wʋntʋ na nuŋi mɛ tɩn.».

eng: While Jesus was teaching in the temple, he exclaimed, You know me and where I am from. I havent come on my own. The one who sent me is true, and you dont know him.
local: Zezi dɛɛn na wʋ Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm wʋnɩ o brɩ nɔɔna tɩn, o ma ŋɔɔnɩ dɩ kwǝr-dɩa o wɩ: «Abam bʋŋɩ nɩ á sɩɩnɩ á ye amʋ, yɩ á kwǝri á ye a na nuŋi mɛ tɩn. A nan wʋ tu a tɩtɩ nɩ, wʋlʋ na tʋŋɩ-nɩ tɩn yɩ cɩga tu mʋ. Abam nan yǝr-o.

eng: I know him because I am from him and he sent me.
local: Amʋ mʋ ye-o, bɛŋwaanɩ a nuŋi wʋntʋ te mʋ, yɩ wʋm mʋ tʋŋɩ-nɩ.».

eng: So they wanted to seize Jesus, but they couldnt because his time hadnt yet come.
local: Zezi na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, ba laan ma lagɩ sɩ ba ja-o. Nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ nan ba jɩgɩ cwǝŋǝ sɩ o kɩ-o kʋlʋkʋlʋ, o maŋa kam ta na wʋ yi tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: Many from that crowd believed in Jesus. They said, When the Christ comes, will he do more miraculous signs than this man does.
local: Kʋ daarɩ nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm wʋ nɔɔna zanzan ma kɩ ba wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩd-o, yɩ ba tɛ ba wɩ: «Krisi wʋm na tuǝ, o bá wanɩ o kɩ wo-kɩnkagɩla zanzan sɩ kʋ dwǝni wʋntʋ na maŋɩ o kɩ te tɩn.».

eng: The Pharisees heard the crowd whispering such things about Jesus, and the chief priests and Pharisees sent guards to arrest him.
local: Farizɩan tiinǝ bam ma ni nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm na jɩgɩ Zezi taanɩ ba waasa te tɩn. Ba dɩ Wɛ kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam laan ma tʋŋɩ nɔɔna sɩ ba vu ba ja Zezi.

eng: Therefore, Jesus said, Im still with you for a little while before I go to the one who sent me.
local: Zezi laan ma ta o wɩ: «Amʋ bá tiini a daanɩ abam tee nɩ. Kʋ na kɩ fɩɩn, aá vu wʋlʋ na tʋŋɩ-nɩ tɩn te.

eng: You will look for me, but you wont find me, and where I am you cant come.
local: Abam nan wʋ́ ba á beeri amʋ je á ga, bɛŋwaanɩ amʋ na wʋ mɛ tɩn, abam bá wanɩ da á vu.».

eng: The Jewish opposition asked each other, Where does he intend to go that we cant find him? Surely he doesnt intend to go where our people have been scattered and are living among the Greeks! He isnt going to teach the Greeks, is he?.
local: O na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, Zwifǝ yigǝ tiinǝ bam maa ŋɔɔnɩ daanɩ ba wɩ: «O lagɩ o vu yǝn mʋ yɩ dɩbam bá na-o? O ga lagɩ o vu Zwifǝ balʋ na zʋʋrɩ Grɛkɩ tiinǝ tɩɩnɩ dɩm nɩ tɩn te mʋ, sɩ o taa brɩ-ba na?.

eng: What does he mean when he says, You will look for me, but you wont find me, and where I am you cant come.
local: O taanɩ dɩm kuri mʋ bɛɛ? O na tagɩ o wɩ, dɩbam wʋ́ ba dɩ́ beeri wʋm jǝgǝ dɩ́ ga, yɩ o ta o wɩ, wʋm na wʋ mɛ tɩn, dɩbam bá wanɩ da dɩ́ vu tɩn, kʋ kuri mʋ bɛɛ?».

eng: On the last and most important day of the festival, Jesus stood up and shouted, All who are thirsty should come to me!.
local: Kʋ dɛɛn ma ba kʋ yi candiǝ kam tiim dɛ. Dɛ dɩm kʋntʋ mʋ yɩ candiǝ kam dɛ kamunu. Zezi maa zaŋɩ wɛɛnɩ o ŋɔɔnɩ dɩ kwǝr-dɩa o wɩ: «Na-nyɔm na jɩgɩ wʋlʋ maama tɩn, sɩ o ba amʋ te o nyɔ.

eng: All who believe in me should drink! As the scriptures said concerning me, Rivers of living water will flow out from within him. .
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ wʋlʋ maama na kɩ o wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ amʋ tɩn wʋ́ na ŋwɩ-dʋŋa Wɛ tee nɩ, nɩnɛɛnɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm na maŋɩ kʋ brɩ te kʋ wɩ: ‹Na balʋ na paɩ nabiinǝ ŋwɩa tɩn wʋ́ ta buri kʋntʋ tu bɩcarɩ nɩ.› ».

eng: Jesus said this concerning the Spirit. Those who believed in him would soon receive the Spirit, but they hadnt experienced the Spirit yet since Jesus hadnt yet been glorified.
local: Zezi na tagɩ na bam taanɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ yɩ o brɩ Wɛ na wʋ́ kɩ DƖ Joro kʋm balʋ na kɩ ba wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩd-o tɩn bɩcara nɩ te tɩn mʋ. Kantʋ maŋa nɩ Wɛ Joro kʋm daa ta wʋ tu ba te, bɛŋwaanɩ Zezi daa ta wʋ tɩgɩ o joori o bi o na paarɩ-zulǝ Wɛ tee nɩ.

eng: When some in the crowd heard these words, they said, This man is truly the prophet.
local: Nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm dɛɛn na ni Zezi na tagɩ te tɩn, badaara maa tɛ ba wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ sɩɩnɩ o yɩ Wɛ nijoŋnu wʋlʋ ya na maŋɩ sɩ o ba tɩn mʋ.».

eng: Others said, Hes the Christ. But others said, The Christ cant come from Galilee, can he?.
local: Badaara dɩ maa tɛ ba wɩ: «O yɩ Krisi wʋm Wɛ na tʋŋɩ tɩn mʋ.» Kʋ daarɩ badonnǝ maa tɛ ba wɩ: «Kʋ da-o. Krisi wʋm na tuǝ, o bá nuŋi Galile.

eng: Didnt the scripture say that the Christ comes from Davids family and from Bethlehem, Davids village.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm brɩ nɩ Krisi wʋm wʋ́ ta yɩ Pɛ Davidi dwi tu mʋ, yɩ o nuŋi Betelɩhɛm, tɩʋ kʋlʋ Davidi dɛɛn na zʋʋrɩ da tɩn.».

eng: So the crowd was divided over Jesus.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ, kampwara dɛɛn ma ba nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm wʋ Zezi ŋwaanɩ.

eng: Some wanted to arrest him, but no one grabbed him.
local: Badaara lagɩ sɩ ba ja-o, yɩ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ nan wʋ nɛ cwǝŋǝ sɩ o kɩ-o kʋlʋkʋlʋ.

eng: The guards returned to the chief priests and Pharisees, who asked, Why didnt you bring him.
local: Wɛ kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam dɩ Farizɩan tiinǝ bam na tʋŋɩ nɔɔna balʋ tɩn ma joori ba ba ba te. Ba ma bwe-ba ba wɩ: «Bɛɛ mʋ yɩ á wʋ jaan-o á ba?».

eng: The guards answered, No one has ever spoken the way he does.
local: Nɔɔna bam ma lǝri-ba ba wɩ: «Nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ maŋɩ o wʋ ŋɔɔnɩ nɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋm kʋntʋ na ŋɔɔnɩ te tɩn.».

eng: The Pharisees replied, Have you too been deceived?.
local: Farizɩan tiinǝ bam ma ta-ba ba wɩ: «Abam pɛ o ganɩ abam dɩ mʋ na?.

eng: Have any of the leaders believed in him? Has any Pharisee?.
local: Abam yǝri nɩ dɩbam yigǝ tiinǝ dɩ Farizɩan tiinǝ bam wʋlʋwʋlʋ wʋ sɛ-o na?.

eng: No, only this crowd, which doesnt know the Law. And they are under Gods curse.
local: Kʋ nan na yɩ nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm, bantʋ yǝri Wɛ cullu tɩm Moyisi na tiŋi o pa dɩbam tɩn. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ kʋʋ́ cɔgɩ-ba.».

eng: Nicodemus, who was one of them and had come to Jesus earlier, said.
local: Farizɩan tiinǝ bam wʋ nɔɔnʋ dɩdʋa dɛɛn maa tɔgɩ o wʋra, o yɩrɩ mʋ Nikodɛm. Wʋntʋ mʋ yɩ wʋlʋ ya na maŋɩ o vu Zezi te tɩn. O ma zaŋɩ o ta dɩ o donnǝ bam o wɩ:.

eng: Our Law doesnt judge someone without first hearing him and learning what he is doing, does it.
local: «Kʋ na yɩ Wɛ cullu tɩm Moyisi na brɩ tɩn, kʋ culǝ sɩ dɩ́ cɔgɩ nɔɔnʋ, yɩ dɩ́ daa ta wʋ bwe-o sɩ dɩ́ ni o na kɩ kʋlʋ tɩn.».

eng: They answered him, You are not from Galilee too, are you? Look it up and you will see that the prophet doesnt come from Galilee.
local: Ba laan ma lǝri ba wɩ: «Nmʋ dɩ yɩ Galile tu mʋ na? Nan karɩmɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ wʋnɩ n nii, sɩ n na nɩ Wɛ nijoŋnu tǝrǝ o na wʋ́ nuŋi Galile nɩ.».

eng: They each went to their own homes.
local: Ba maama laan ma jagɩ ba vu sam.

eng: As Jesus walked along, he saw a man who was blind from birth.
local: Zezi dɛɛn ma vu o maa kɛa. O ma na nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ ba na maŋɩ ba lʋ-o yɩ o yɩ lilwe.

eng: Jesus disciples asked, Rabbi, who sinned so that he was born blind, this man or his parents.
local: O karabiǝ bam ma bwe-o ba wɩ: «Karanyɩna, wɔɔ lwarɩm mʋ pɛ ba lʋ nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ dɩ lilweem? Kʋ yɩ o tɩtɩ lwarɩm mʋ naa, o nu dɩ o ko lwarɩm mʋ pɛ ba lʋ-o dɩ lilweem?».

eng: Jesus answered, Neither he nor his parents. This happened so that Gods mighty works might be displayed in him.
local: Zezi ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «O na yɩ lilwe tɩn, kʋ daɩ o tɩtɩ mʋ kɩ lwarɩm naa o nu dɩ o ko mʋ kɩ lwarɩm. Kʋ nan yɩ sɩ kʋ pa nɔɔna mʋ wanɩ ba lwarɩ Wɛ tɩtʋŋa na yɩ te tɩn wʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ.

eng: While its daytime, we must do the works of him who sent me. Night is coming when no one can work.
local: Dɩbam nan maŋɩ sɩ dɩ́ tʋŋɩ wʋlʋ na tʋŋɩ-nɩ tɩn tɩtʋŋa yam yɩ wɩa ta wʋra mʋ. Kʋ daarɩ fɩɩn sɩ tɩga yi, yɩ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ daa bá wanɩ o tʋŋɩ Wɛ tɩtʋŋa yam.

eng: While I am in the world, I am the light of the world.
local: Maŋa kam a ta na wʋ lʋgʋ baŋa nɩ tɩn, amʋ yɩ pooni mʋ a paɩ nabiinǝ.».

eng: After he said this, he spit on the ground, made mud with the saliva, and smeared the mud on the mans eyes.
local: O na tagɩ kʋntʋ o ti tɩn, o laan ma twɩ lileeru o dɩ tɩga nɩ. O ma viiri tɩɩrʋ, yɩ o kɩ-tɩ o bǝ lilwe wʋm yiǝ baŋa nɩ.

eng: Jesus said to him, Go, wash in the pool of Siloam . So the man went away and washed. When he returned, he could see.
local: O ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Ve n su n yiǝ yam Silʋa bugǝ kam nɩ.» Nɔɔnʋ wʋm ma vu o su o yiǝ yam. O ma joori o ba, yɩ o laan naɩ.

eng: The mans neighbors and those who used to see him when he was a beggar said, Isnt this the man who used to sit and beg.
local: Balʋ na zʋʋrɩ o tɩkǝri nɩ dɩ balʋ na maŋɩ ba ye nɩ o yɩ looru tɩn ma na-o, yɩ ba bwɛ ba wɩ: «Kʋ daɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ mʋ yǝni o je o loori tɩn na?».

eng: Some said, It is, and others said, No, its someone who looks like him. But the man said, Yes, its me.
local: Badaara maa tɛ ba wɩ: «Kʋ yɩ wʋntʋ mʋ.» Badonnǝ maa wɩ: «Kʋ daɩ wʋntʋ, sɩ ba nyɩ daanɩ mʋ.» Nɔɔnʋ wʋm tɩtɩ ma ta o wɩ: «Kʋ yɩ amʋ kʋntʋ.».

eng: So they asked him, How are you now able to see.
local: Ba ma bwe-o ba wɩ: «Nmʋ yiǝ yam kɩ ta mʋ ya puri?».

eng: He answered, The man they call Jesus made mud, smeared it on my eyes, and said, Go to the pool of Siloam and wash. So I went and washed, and then I could see.
local: O ma lǝri o wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ yɩrɩ na yɩ Zezi tɩn mʋ kɩ tɩɩrʋ o ma bǝ a yiǝ yam baŋa, yɩ o ta-nɩ o wɩ, a ve Silʋa bugǝ kam a su-ya. A ma vu a su, yɩ a laan naɩ.».

eng: They asked, Where is this man? He replied, I dont know.
local: Ba ma bwe-o ba wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ wʋm kʋntʋ bɛ?» O ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «A yǝri o je.».

eng: Then they led the man who had been born blind to the Pharisees.
local: Ba laan ma ja-o ba vu Farizɩan tiinǝ bam te.

eng: Now Jesus made the mud and smeared it on the mans eyes on a Sabbath day.
local: Dɛ dɩm Zezi na kɩ tɩɩrʋ tɩm o ma puri nɔɔnʋ wʋm yiǝ yam tɩn ya yɩ Zwifǝ bam siun dɛ mʋ.

eng: So Pharisees also asked him how he was able to see. The man told them, He put mud on my eyes, I washed, and now I see.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ Farizɩan tiinǝ bam dɩ daa bwe nɔɔnʋ wʋm ba wɩ: «N yiǝ yam kɩ ta mʋ ya puri?» O ma ta-ba o wɩ: «O kɩ tɩɩrʋ mʋ o ma bǝ a yiǝ baŋa. A ma su-ya, yɩ a laan naɩ.».

eng: Some Pharisees said, This man isnt from God, because he breaks the Sabbath law. Others said, How can a sinner do miraculous signs like these? So they were divided.
local: Farizɩan tiinǝ bam badaara ma ta ba wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ wʋm na kɩ te tɩn, o wʋ nuŋi Wɛ te, bɛŋwaanɩ o cɔgɩ siun dɛ cullu tɩm.» Badonnǝ dɩ maa tɛ ba wɩ: «O na yɩ nɔn-balɔrɔ, o bá wanɩ o kɩ wo-kɩnkagɩla yantʋ doŋ.» Kʋntʋ ma pa kampwara ba ba tɩtarɩ.

eng: Some of the Pharisees questioned the man who had been born blind again: What do you have to say about him, since he healed your eyes? He replied, Hes a prophet.
local: Ba laan ma joori ba bwe nɔɔnʋ wʋm ba wɩ: «O nan na pɛ n yiǝ yam puri tɩn, nmʋ tɛ n wɩ, o yɩ wɔɔ mʋ?» O ma lǝri o wɩ: «O yɩ Wɛ nijoŋnu mʋ.».

eng: The Jewish leaders didnt believe the man had been blind and received his sight until they called for his parents.
local: Zwifǝ yigǝ tiinǝ bam maa ba lagɩ sɩ ba sɛ, nɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋm ya maŋɩ o yɩ lilwe mʋ yɩ o laan ba o naɩ. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ, ba ma bǝŋi nɔɔnʋ wʋm nu dɩ o ko sɩ ba bwe ba nii.

eng: The Jewish leaders asked them, Is this your son? Are you saying he was born blind? How can he now see.
local: Ba ma bwe-ba ba wɩ: «Wʋntʋ yɩ abam bu mʋ na? Á lʋg-o dɩ o maŋɩ o yɩ lilwe mʋ na? O nan kɩ ta mʋ o naɩ lele kʋntʋ?».

eng: His parents answered, We know he is our son. We know he was born blind.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋm tiinǝ bam ma lǝri ba wɩ: «Dɩbam ye nɩ o yɩ dɩbam bu mʋ. Dɩ́ ye nɩ dɩ́ lʋg-o dɩ o yɩ lilwe mʋ.

eng: But we dont know how he now sees, and we dont know who healed his eyes. Ask him. Hes old enough to speak for himself.
local: Dɩ́ nan yǝri o laan na kɩ te o naɩ, yɩ dɩ́ yǝri wʋlʋ na puri o yiǝ yam dɩ. Abam nan bwe-na o tɩtɩ, sɩ o yɩ nɔn-kwɩʋn mʋ, o tɩtɩ wʋ́ wanɩ o lǝri.».

eng: His parents said this because they feared the Jewish authorities. This is because the Jewish authorities had already decided that whoever confessed Jesus to be the Christ would be expelled from the synagogue.
local: O tiinǝ bam na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, ba dɛɛn kwarɩ Zwifǝ yigǝ tiinǝ bam mʋ. Bantʋ ya maŋɩ ba kɩ ni daanɩ sɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ maama na sɛ nɩ Zezi mʋ yɩ Krisi wʋm Wɛ na tʋŋɩ tɩn, sɩ ba lɩ kʋntʋ tu ba Wɛ-digǝ kam nɩ.

eng: Thats why his parents said, Hes old enough. Ask him.
local: Mʋ kʋ kuri o tiinǝ bam cɩm, yɩ ba ta ba wɩ, ba bwe o tɩtɩ sɩ o yɩ nɔn-kwɩʋn mʋ tɩn.

eng: Therefore, they called a second time for the man who had been born blind and said to him, Give glory to God. We know this man is a sinner.
local: Ba ma joori ba bǝŋi nɔɔnʋ wʋm ba na lʋg-o dɩ lilweem tɩn, yɩ ba ta dɩd-o ba wɩ: «Nan kwarɩ Wɛ, sɩ n ta cɩga, sɩ dɩbam ye nɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋm kʋntʋ yɩ nɔn-balɔrɔ mʋ.».

eng: The man answered, I dont know whether hes a sinner. Heres what I do know: I was blind and now I see.
local: O ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Amʋ yǝri nɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋm kʋntʋ yɩ nɔn-balɔrɔ naa o daɩ. Kʋlʋ yɩranɩ a na ye tɩn mʋ yɩ sɩ, a dɛɛn yɩ lilwe mʋ, yɩ a laan naɩ lele kʋntʋ.».

eng: They questioned him: What did he do to you? How did he heal your eyes.
local: Ba ma bwe-o ba wɩ: «O kɩ ta mʋ o puri nmʋ yiǝ yam?».

eng: He replied, I already told you, and you didnt listen. Why do you want to hear it again? Do you want to become his disciples too.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋm ma lǝri o wɩ: «A maŋɩ a ta a brɩ abam, yɩ á wʋ sɛ sɩ á cǝgi. Bɛɛ mʋ yɩ abam daa lagɩ sɩ á bwe á nii? Abam dɩ ga lagɩ sɩ á taá tɔgɩ á yɩ o karabiǝ mʋ na?».

eng: They insulted him: You are his disciple, but we are Moses disciples.
local: O na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma twɩ-o yɩ ba wɩ: «Kʋ yɩ nmʋ mʋ tɔgɩ o kwaga, sɩ kʋ daɩ dɩbam mʋ tɔg-o. Dɩbam tɔgɩ Moyisi cullu tɩm mʋ.

eng: We know that God spoke to Moses, but we dont know where this man is from.
local: Dɩbam ye nɩ Wɛ dɛɛn ŋɔɔnɩ dɩ Moyisi mʋ. Kʋ daarɩ, kʋ na yɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ, dɩ́ brɩ dɩ́ yǝri o na nuŋi mɛ tɩn.».

eng: The man answered, This is incredible! You dont know where he is from, yet he healed my eyes!.
local: O ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Aa. Kʋ yɩ kʋlʋ na tiini kʋ sʋ amʋ tɩn. Abam yǝri o na nuŋi mɛ, yɩ wʋntʋ nan mʋ puri a yiǝ yam.

eng: We know that God doesnt listen to sinners. God listens to anyone who is devout and does Gods will.
local: Dɩbam ye nɩ Wɛ ba cǝgi nɔn-balwaarʋ taanɩ. Kʋ yɩ nɔɔna balʋ na nɩgɩ-DƖ yɩ ba kɩ DƖ wʋbʋŋa na lagɩ te tɩn mʋ DƖ cǝgǝ.

eng: No one has ever heard of a healing of the eyes of someone born blind.
local: Kʋ na zɩgɩ lʋgʋ baŋa pulim nɩ sɩ kʋ ba kʋ yi zɩm tɩn, dɩ́ daa ta wʋ ni nɩ nɔɔnʋ puri wʋlʋ ba na lʋg-o dɩ lilweem tɩn yiǝ.

eng: If this man wasnt from God, he couldnt do this.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ na sɩɩnɩ o wʋ nuŋi Wɛ te, o ya bá wanɩ o kɩ wo-kɩnkagɩlɩ dɩntʋ doŋ.».

eng: They responded, You were born completely in sin! How is it that you dare to teach us? Then they expelled him.
local: Ba ma lǝr-o ba wɩ: «Ba lʋgɩ nmʋ yɩ n maama maŋɩ n yɩ lwarɩm punǝ mʋ. Bɛɛ mʋ yɩ n bʋŋɩ sɩ n brɩ dɩbam?» Ba laan ma zǝl-o, sɩ o daa yɩ tɔgɩ o zʋ ba Wɛ-digǝ kam.

eng: Jesus heard they had expelled the man born blind. Finding him, Jesus said, Do you believe in the Human One.
local: Zezi dɛɛn ma ni nɩ ba lɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋm ba Wɛ-digǝ kam wʋnɩ. O ma vu o jeer-o, yɩ o bwe-o o wɩ: «Nmʋ sɛ Nabiin-bu wʋm dɩ n wʋ maama na?».

eng: He answered, Who is he, sir? I want to believe in him.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋm ma lǝri o wɩ: «A yuutu, ta n brɩ-nɩ o na yɩ wʋlʋ tɩn, sɩ a wanɩ a sɛ-o.».

eng: Jesus said, You have seen him. In fact, he is the one speaking with you.
local: O ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Nmʋ maŋɩ n na-o. Amʋ mʋ yɩ Nabiin-bu wʋm, a zɩgɩ a ŋɔɔnɩ dɩ nmʋ.».

eng: The man said, Lord, I believe. And he worshipped Jesus.
local: O laan ma ta o wɩ: «A Yuutu, a sɛ-m.» O ma kuni doonǝ Zezi yigǝ nɩ o zul-o.

eng: Jesus said, I have come into the world to exercise judgment so that those who dont see can see and those who see will become blind.
local: Zezi ma ta o wɩ: «Amʋ tu lʋgʋ kʋntʋ baŋa sɩ a pɔɔrɩ nɔɔna balʋ na jɩgɩ bʋra dɩ balʋ na ba jɩgɩ bʋra tɩn mʋ daanɩ, sɩ balʋ na yɩ lilwǝ tɩn yiǝ puri, sɩ balʋ yiǝ na naɩ tɩn joori ya dwe.».

eng: Some Pharisees who were with him heard what he said and asked, Surely we arent blind, are we.
local: Farizɩan tiinǝ badaara balʋ na wʋra dɩd-o tɩn ma ni o na tagɩ te tɩn. Ba ma bwe-o ba wɩ: «Nmʋ lagɩ n brɩ nɩ dɩbam nyɩ dɩ lilwǝ mʋ na?».

eng: Jesus said to them, If you were blind, you wouldnt have any sin, but now that you say, We see, your sin remains.
local: Zezi ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Abam ya na nyɩ dɩ lilwǝ, kʋ lagɩ kʋ ta nɩ á yǝri Wɛ taanɩ dɩm mʋ, á lwarɩm ya bá ta wʋ á yuu nɩ. Kʋ daarɩ, á nan na brɩ nɩ á yiǝ pʋʋrɩ tɩn, á lwarɩm taa wʋ á yuu nɩ mʋ.».

eng: On the third day there was a wedding in Cana of Galilee. Jesus mother was there, and.
local: Da yale dɛɛn na kɛ tɩn, ba maa wʋra ba kɩ kadiri candiǝ Galile tɩʋ kʋdoŋ nɩ, kʋ yɩrɩ mʋ Kana. Zezi nu maa tɔgɩ o wʋra.

eng: Jesus and his disciples were also invited to the celebration.
local: Ba dɛɛn bǝŋi Zezi dɩ o karabiǝ bam kadiri candiǝ kam kʋntʋ nɩ.

eng: When the wine ran out, Jesus mother said to him, They dont have any wine.
local: Ba na wʋra tɩn, tweeru biǝ sana kalʋ yɩrɩ na yɩ vɛn ba ya na jɩgɩ ba nyɔ tɩn ma ti. Zezi nu ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Ba daa ba jɩgɩ sana sɩ ba nyɔ.».

eng: Jesus replied, Woman, what does that have to do with me? My time hasnt come yet.
local: Zezi ma lǝri o wɩ: «A nu, nmʋ lagɩ n brɩ-nɩ a na wʋ́ kɩ kʋlʋ tɩn na? A maŋa kam daa ta wʋ yi.».

eng: His mother told the servants, Do whatever he tells you.
local: O nu wʋm laan ma ta dɩ wʋdi-maŋna bam o wɩ: «O na tagɩ kʋlʋ maama dɩ abam, sɩ á kɩ.».

eng: Nearby were six stone water jars used for the Jewish cleansing ritual, each able to hold about twenty or thirty gallons.
local: Kandwa kambi-zɛ sɩrdʋ dɛɛn maa zɩgɩ da. Sɩ dɩdʋa maama jɩgɩ nɩnɛɛnɩ litra fusɩrdʋ naa bi mʋ. Sɩ dɛɛn zɩgɩ da sɩ nɔɔna ta maa sɩn ba jɩa dɩ ba zwɩ mʋ, nɩ Zwifǝ bam cullu tɩm na brɩ te tɩn.

eng: Jesus said to the servants, Fill the jars with water, and they filled them to the brim.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩ wʋdi-maŋna bam sɩ ba mɔ na ba su kambi sɩm ni. Ba ma mɔ na ba su sɩ ni ba pɛɛlɩ.

eng: Then he told them, Now draw some from them and take it to the headwaiter, and they did.
local: O daa ma ta-ba o wɩ, ba laan mɔ na bam ba vu ba pa wʋlʋ na nii candiǝ kam wǝǝnu tɩm baŋa nɩ tɩn. Ba ma mɔ ba pa-o.

eng: The headwaiter tasted the water that had become wine. He didnt know where it came from, though the servants who had drawn the water knew. The headwaiter called the groom.
local: O ma nyɔ na bam, dɩdaanɩ ba maŋɩ ba ji tweeru biǝ sana. O ma wʋ lwarɩ sana kam kʋntʋ na nuŋi mɛ, yɩ wʋdi-maŋna balʋ na mɔɔnɩ na bam tɩn nan ye. Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na nii candiǝ kam baŋa nɩ tɩn laan ma bǝŋi kan-barʋ wʋm o ta dɩd-o o wɩ:.

eng: and said, Everyone serves the good wine first. They bring out the second-rate wine only when the guests are drinking freely. You kept the good wine until now.
local: «Nɔɔnʋ maama yǝni o pa sa-ŋʋŋa mʋ yigǝ yɩ ba nyɔ. Ba laan na yǝni ba nyɔ ba su, o ma pa-ba sana kalʋ na ba tiini ka ywǝnǝ tɩn yɩ ba nyɔ. Nmʋ nan kwe sa-ŋʋŋa kam mʋ n zɩgɩ, yɩ n laan ba n kwe-ka n pa dɩbam kʋ kwaga nɩ.».

eng: This was the first miraculous sign that Jesus did in Cana of Galilee. He revealed his glory, and his disciples believed in him.
local: Mʋ Zezi na dɛ yigǝ o kɩ wo-kɩnkagɩlɩ dɩlʋ tɩn. O dɛɛn kɩ-dɩ Kana nɩ mʋ, Galile tɩʋ nɩ, sɩ kʋ brɩ nɔɔna nɩ o nuŋi Wɛ te mʋ. Kʋ ma pa nɔɔna lwarɩ o dam na yɩ te tɩn. O karabiǝ bam na nɛ kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma kɩ ba wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩd-o.

eng: After this, Jesus and his mother, his brothers, and his disciples went down to Capernaum and stayed there for a few days.
local: Kʋntʋ dɛɛn na kɛ tɩn, Zezi dɩ o nu dɩ Zezi nyaana dɩ o karabiǝ bam ma vu Kapɛrnawum ba kɩ da fɩnfɩɩn da.

eng: It was nearly time for the Jewish Passover, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
local: Kʋ dɛɛn ma daarɩ fɩɩn sɩ Zwifǝ bam di ba candiǝ kalʋ yɩrɩ na yɩ Pakɩ tɩn. Zezi ma zaŋɩ o vu Zeruzalɛm.

eng: He found in the temple those who were selling cattle, sheep, and doves, as well as those involved in exchanging currency sitting there.
local: O na yi da tɩn, o ma vu Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm nɩ. O ma na nɔɔna na jɩgɩ naanɩ dɩ peeni dɩ kunkwǝn-pulǝ ba yǝgǝ ka kʋnkɔlɔ kʋm nɩ. Sǝbu-lǝnnǝ dɩ maa je da ba lǝni ba sǝbiǝ.

eng: He made a whip from ropes and chased them all out of the temple, including the cattle and the sheep. He scattered the coins and overturned the tables of those who exchanged currency.
local: O ma kwe ŋʋnɩ o ma kɩ balaarɩ o zǝli peeni sɩm dɩ naanɩ dɩm maama, o pa dɩ nuŋi. O ma daarɩ o yigi sǝbu-lǝnnǝ bam taabʋllʋ tɩm o dɩ tɩga nɩ, o pa ba sǝbu kʋm jagɩ.

eng: He said to the dove sellers, Get these things out of here! Dont make my Fathers house a place of business.
local: O ma daarɩ o ta nɔɔna balʋ dɩ na jɩgɩ kunkwǝn-pulǝ bam ba yǝgi tɩn o wɩ: «Ja-na-ba á ja nuŋi yo. Á yɩ zaŋɩ á pa a Ko Wɛ digǝ kam taa yɩ pipiu jǝgǝ.».

eng: His disciples remembered that it is written, Passion for your house consumes me.
local: Zezi na kɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, o karabiǝ bam laan ma ba ba guli kʋ na pʋpʋnɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ kʋ wɩ te: «Amʋ na tiini a jɩgɩ sono kʋlʋ nmʋ Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm ŋwaanɩ tɩn nyɩ nɩnɛɛnɩ mini na di-nɩ te tɩn mʋ.».

eng: Then the Jewish leaders asked him, By what authority are you doing these things? What miraculous sign will you show us.
local: Zwifǝ yigǝ tiinǝ bam laan ma bwe Zezi ba wɩ: «Wo-kɩnkagɩlɩ dɔɔ nmʋ wʋ́ kɩ, sɩ kʋ pa dɩbam lwarɩ nɩ nmʋ jɩgɩ ni sɩ n zǝli nɔɔna bam kʋntʋ?».

eng: Jesus answered, Destroy this temple and in three days Ill raise it up.
local: Zezi ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Abam na magɩ Wɛ-digǝ kantʋ maama á dɩ tɩga nɩ, amʋ wʋ́ ma da yatɔ mʋ a joori a lɔ-ka a zɩgɩ.».

eng: The Jewish leaders replied, It took forty-six years to build this temple, and you will raise it up in three days.
local: Ba laan ma ta ba wɩ: «Ba mɛ bɩna fiinna yardʋ mʋ ba ma lɔ Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋntʋ ba ti. Nmʋ nan wʋ́ kɩ ta mʋ n ma da yatɔ yɩranɩ n joori n lɔ-ka n zɩgɩ?».

eng: But the temple Jesus was talking about was his body.
local: Zezi nan ya na ŋɔɔnɩ Wɛ-digǝ kam taanɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ yɩ o tɩtɩ yɩra yam taanɩ mʋ o taga.

eng: After he was raised from the dead, his disciples remembered what he had said, and they believed the scripture and the word that Jesus had spoken.
local: Kʋ dɛɛn na tu kʋ yi maŋa kam Zezi na tɩgɩ yɩ o joori o bi tɩn, o karabiǝ bam ma guli o dɛɛn na tagɩ te tɩn. Ba ma sɛ kʋlʋ na pʋpʋnɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ tɩn, dɩ Zezi na tagɩ kʋlʋ tɩn.

eng: While Jesus was in Jerusalem for the Passover Festival, many believed in his name because they saw the miraculous signs that he did.
local: Zezi dɛɛn na wʋ Zeruzalɛm nɩ Pakɩ candiǝ kam maŋa nɩ tɩn, nɔɔna zanzan ma na wo-kɩnkagɩla yalʋ o na kɩ tɩn, yɩ ba laan sɛ-o.

eng: But Jesus didnt trust himself to them because he knew all people.
local: Zezi dɛɛn nan wʋ pɛ-ba o wʋ maama, bɛŋwaanɩ o ye nɔɔnʋ maama na yɩ te tɩn.

eng: He didnt need anyone to tell him about human nature, for he knew what human nature was.
local: O maa ba lagɩ sɩ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ brɩ-o nabiinǝ na yɩ te tɩn, o na maŋɩ o ye kʋlʋ na wʋ nabiinu maama bɩcarɩ nɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: A certain man, Lazarus, was ill. He was from Bethany, the village of Mary and her sister Martha. .
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ dɛɛn zʋʋrɩ Betani nɩ, o yɩrɩ mʋ Lazaarɩ. O nakwa mʋ yɩ Mari dɩ o zɩmbaarʋ Marɩtɩ. Mari wʋntʋ mʋ yɩ wʋlʋ dɛɛn na kwe tralɩ nugǝ o lo dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm nɛ nɩ yɩ o daarɩ o ma o yuuywe o guguni-sɩ tɩn. Lazaarɩ ma ba o ba jɩgɩ yazurǝ.

eng: So the sisters sent word to Jesus, saying, Lord, the one whom you love is ill.
local: Mari dɩdaanɩ Marɩtɩ ma tʋŋɩ ni ba pa Zezi ba wɩ: «Dɩbam Yuutu, nmʋ ciloŋ-sono kʋm ba jɩgɩ yazurǝ.».

eng: When he heard this, Jesus said, This illness isnt fatal. Its for the glory of God so that Gods Son can be glorified through it.
local: Zezi na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, o maa wɩ: «Yawɩʋ kʋntʋ kweelim je bá ta yɩ o tʋʋnɩ. Kʋ nan kɩ sɩ kʋ pa Wɛ mʋ na zulǝ, sɩ amʋ Wɛ Bu wʋm dɩ na zulǝ kʋ ŋwaanɩ.».

eng: Jesus loved Martha, her sister, and Lazarus.
local: Zezi yagɩ o soe Marɩtɩ dɩ o nyaanɩ wʋm dɩ Lazaarɩ lanyɩranɩ.

eng: When he heard that Lazarus was ill, he stayed where he was. After two days.
local: O na ni nɩ Lazaarɩ ba jɩgɩ yazurǝ tɩn, o ta ma kɩ da yale o ya na wʋ mɛ tɩn.

eng: he said to his disciples, Lets return to Judea again.
local: yɩ o laan daarɩ o ta dɩ o karabiǝ bam o wɩ: «Pa-na dɩ́ joori dɩ́ vu Zude.».

eng: The disciples replied, Rabbi, the Jewish opposition wants to stone you, but you want to go back.
local: O karabiǝ bam ma ta dɩd-o ba wɩ: «Karanyɩna, Zwifǝ bam daama na lagɩ sɩ ba dʋlɩ nmʋ dɩ kandwa ba gʋ tɩn, n swe mʋ na? Yɩ n daa ta lagɩ sɩ n joori n vu da.».

eng: Jesus answered, Arent there twelve hours in the day? Whoever walks in the day doesnt stumble because they see the light of the world.
local: Zezi ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Kʋ nan maŋɩ sɩ a vu mʋ. Luu fugǝ-bale mʋ wʋra wɩa nɩ. Kʋ daɩ kʋntʋ na? Nɔɔnʋ na ve wɩa nɩ, o daa ba tri o tʋɩ, bɛŋwaanɩ lʋgʋ baŋa pooni dɩm paɩ o naɩ.

eng: But whoever walks in the night does stumble because the light isnt in them.
local: O nan na ve tɩtɩɩ nɩ, o tri o tʋ mʋ, bɛŋwaanɩ pooni tǝri o tee nɩ.».

eng: He continued, Our friend Lazarus is sleeping, but I am going in order to wake him up.
local: Zezi na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, o daa ma ta o wǝli da o wɩ: «Dɩbam ciloŋ Lazaarɩ dɔ mʋ. A nan maa ve sɩ a pa o zaŋɩ.».

eng: The disciples said, Lord, if hes sleeping, he will get well.
local: O karabiǝ bam ma ta dɩd-o ba wɩ: «Dɩbam yuutu, o na dɔa, kʋʋ́ garɩ o yɩra nɩ.».

eng: They thought Jesus meant that Lazarus was in a deep sleep, but Jesus had spoken about Lazarus death.
local: Zezi nan na tagɩ te tɩn brɩ nɩ Lazaarɩ tɩgɩ mʋ. Bantʋ maa bʋŋɩ nɩ Zezi ŋɔɔnɩ dɔɔm tɩtɩ woŋo mʋ.

eng: Jesus told them plainly, Lazarus has died.
local: O laan ma ta dɩ ba jaja o wɩ: «Lazaarɩ tɩgɩ mʋ.

eng: For your sakes, Im glad I wasnt there so that you can believe. Lets go to him.
local: Amʋ nan jɩgɩ wʋpolo abam ŋwaanɩ dɩ a na tǝri o tee nɩ tɩn. Kʋʋ́ pa á wanɩ á kɩ á wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ amʋ. Nan pa-na dɩ́ vu o te.».

eng: Then Thomas said to the other disciples, Let us go too so that we may die with Jesus.
local: Toma wʋlʋ ba na bǝ nɩ Yiywɛ tɩn ma ta dɩ o donnǝ bam o wɩ: «Pa-na dɩ́ maama tɔgɩ dɩ́ vu dɩ́ wǝli dɩ dɩ́ Yuutu dɩ́ tɩ.».

eng: When Jesus arrived, he found that Lazarus had already been in the tomb for four days.
local: Zezi ma vu o yi Betani dɩ ba kwe Lazaarɩ ba kɩ kʋ jɩgɩ da yana mʋ.

eng: Bethany was a little less than two miles from Jerusalem.
local: Betani batwarɩ dɩ Zeruzalɛm nɩnɛɛnɩ kilomɛtra yale mʋ te.

eng: Many Jews had come to comfort Martha and Mary after their brothers death.
local: Zwifǝ zanzan dɛɛn ya tu sɩ ba kɩ Marɩtɩ dɩ Mari le dɩ ba nabɔ wʋm tʋʋnɩ dɩm.

eng: When Martha heard that Jesus was coming, she went to meet him, while Mary remained in the house.
local: Marɩtɩ na ni nɩ Zezi maa bɩɩnɩ tɩn, o ma nuŋi o vu sɩ o jeer-o. Mari ma maŋɩ sɔŋɔ nɩ.

eng: Martha said to Jesus, Lord, if you had been here, my brother wouldnt have died.
local: Marɩtɩ na yi Zezi te tɩn, o ma ta o wɩ: «A yuutu, nmʋ ya na wʋ yo, a nabɔ wʋm ya bá tɩ.

eng: Even now I know that whatever you ask God, God will give you.
local: Dɩ kʋ dɩ, a ye nɩ nmʋ na loori kʋlʋ maama Wɛ tee nɩ tɩn, DƖ wʋ́ kɩ DƖ pa nmʋ.».

eng: Jesus told her, Your brother will rise again.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «N nabɔ wʋm wʋ́ joori o bi.».

eng: Martha replied, I know that he will rise in the resurrection on the last day.
local: Marɩtɩ ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «A ye nɩ oó joori o bi lʋgʋ tiim dɛ dɩm nɩ, Wɛ na wʋ́ pa twa joori ba bi ba yagɩ tʋʋnɩ tɩn.».

eng: Jesus said to her, I am the resurrection and the life. Whoever believes in me will live, even though they die.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Amʋ mʋ yɩ twa biinu dɩ ŋwɩa tu. Wʋlʋ na kɩ o wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ amʋ tɩn, o na maŋɩ o tɩ dɩ, kʋntʋ tu ta wʋ́ na ŋwɩa.

eng: Everyone who lives and believes in me will never die. Do you believe this.
local: Kʋlʋ na wǝli da tɩn, wʋlʋ maama na ŋwɩ yɩ o kɩ o wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ amʋ tɩn, tʋʋnɩ daa bá ja kʋntʋ tu maŋa dɩ maŋa. Nmʋ sɛ kʋntʋ na?».

eng: She replied, Yes, Lord, I believe that you are the Christ, Gods Son, the one who is coming into the world.
local: O ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «Ɛɛn. A yuutu, a sɛ nɩ nmʋ mʋ yɩ Krisi wʋm Wɛ na tʋŋɩ tɩn, yɩ n kwǝri n yɩ Wɛ Bu wʋlʋ na maŋɩ sɩ o ba lʋgʋ baŋa tɩn.».

eng: After she said this, she went and spoke privately to her sister Mary, The teacher is here and hes calling for you.
local: Marɩtɩ na tagɩ kʋntʋ o ti tɩn, o ma joori o vu o na Mari o yɩranɩ, yɩ o bǝŋ-o o wɩ: «Karanyɩna tuǝ, yɩ o lagɩ sɩ o na-m.».

eng: When Mary heard this, she got up quickly and went to Jesus.
local: Mari na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma zaŋɩ lɩla o nuŋi o vu sɩ o jeer-o.

eng: He hadnt entered the village but was still in the place where Martha had met him.
local: Zezi daa ta wʋ jǝgǝ kalʋ Marɩtɩ na jeer-o da tɩn mʋ. O ta wʋ yi tɩʋ kʋm wʋ.

eng: When the Jews who were comforting Mary in the house saw her get up quickly and leave, they followed her. They assumed she was going to mourn at the tomb.
local: Zwifǝ balʋ na wʋ sɔŋɔ kʋm nɩ ba larɩ dɩ Mari sɩ kʋ ganɩ o wʋ tɩn ma na o na zaŋɩ lɩla o nuŋi. Ba ma zaŋɩ ba tɔg-o, yɩ ba bʋŋɩ nɩ o ga lagɩ o vu yibeeli dɩm jǝgǝ mʋ o keeri.

eng: When Mary arrived where Jesus was and saw him, she fell at his feet and said, Lord, if you had been here, my brother wouldnt have died.
local: Mari na ve o yi Zezi na wʋ mɛ tɩn, o ma na-o yɩ o vu o kuni doonǝ o yigǝ nɩ o wɩ: «A yuutu, nmʋ ya na wʋ yo, a nabɔ wʋm ya bá tɩ.».

eng: When Jesus saw her crying and the Jews who had come with her crying also, he was deeply disturbed and troubled.
local: Zezi ma na o na keerǝ, yɩ Zwifǝ balʋ na tɔgɩ dɩd-o ba ba tɩn dɩ keerǝ. O wʋ ma cɔgɩ, yɩ o yɩra sɔɔrɩ.

eng: He asked, Where have you laid him? They replied, Lord, come and see.
local: O ma bwe-ba o wɩ: «Abam kɩ-o yǝn mʋ?» Ba ma lǝri ba wɩ: «Dɩbam yuutu, ba n nii o yibeeli dɩm.».

eng: Jesus began to cry.
local: Zezi ma kan yi-na.

eng: The Jews said, See how much he loved him.
local: Zwifǝ bam na nɛ kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma ta ba wɩ: «Nii o na tiini o so-o te.».

eng: But some of them said, He healed the eyes of the man born blind. Couldnt he have kept Lazarus from dying.
local: Kʋ daarɩ badaara maa tɛ ba wɩ: «Wʋntʋ mʋ puri lilwe wʋm yiǝ yam. Kʋ nan kɩ ta mʋ yɩ o wʋ cɩgɩ Lazaarɩ tʋʋnɩ dɩm?».

eng: Jesus was deeply disturbed again when he came to the tomb. It was a cave, and a stone covered the entrance.
local: Zezi na maa ve yibeeli dɩm jǝgǝ tɩn, o wʋ daa ma cɔgɩ zanzan. Yibeeli dɩm dɛɛn wʋ piu yɩra nɩ mʋ, yɩ ba ma kandwɛ ba pɩ dɩ ni.

eng: Jesus said, Remove the stone. Martha, the sister of the dead man, said, Lord, the smell will be awful! Hes been dead four days.
local: Zezi ma ta o wɩ: «Lɩ-na kandwɛ dɩm á yagɩ daa nɩ.» Tʋ wʋm nakɔ Marɩtɩ ma lǝri o wɩ: «A yuutu, ba na kwe-o ba kɩ tɩn, kʋ jɩgɩ da yana mʋ zɩm. Kʋntʋ tɩn, oó ta tiini o lwe.».

eng: Jesus replied, Didnt I tell you that if you believe, you will see Gods glory.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «A maŋɩ a ta dɩ nmʋ nɩ, nmʋ na kɩ n wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ amʋ, nmʋ wʋ́ na Wɛ na jɩgɩ dam te tɩn.».

eng: So they removed the stone. Jesus looked up and said, Father, thank you for hearing me.
local: Ba ma lɩ kandwɛ dɩm ba yagɩ daa nɩ. Zezi ma kwǝni o yuu wɛɛnɩ, yɩ o ta o wɩ: «Amʋ Ko, dɩnle dɩ nmʋ na cǝgi a taanɩ tɩn.

eng: I know you always hear me. I say this for the benefit of the crowd standing here so that they will believe that you sent me.
local: A maŋɩ a ye nɩ nmʋ yǝni n cǝgi a taanɩ maŋa maama. A nan tagɩ kʋntʋ nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋlʋ na zɩgɩ yo tɩn ŋwaanɩ mʋ, sɩ kʋ pa ba sɛ nɩ nmʋ tʋŋɩ-nɩ.».

eng: Having said this, Jesus shouted with a loud voice, Lazarus, come out.
local: O na tagɩ kʋntʋ o ti tɩn, o ma bǝŋi dɩ kwǝr-dɩa o wɩ: «Lazaarɩ, zaŋɩ n nuŋi!».

eng: The dead man came out, his feet bound and his hands tied, and his face covered with a cloth. Jesus said to them, Untie him and let him go.
local: Tʋ wʋm ma zaŋɩ o nuŋi, dɩ garyiǝ yam ba ya mɛ ba ba o jɩa dɩ o nɛ, yɩ gɔrɔ dɩ kɩkarɩ o yibiyǝ. Zezi ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Bwǝli-na garyiǝ yam á yagɩ sɩ o vu.».

eng: Therefore, many of the Jews who came with Mary and saw what Jesus did believed in him.
local: Zwifǝ balʋ ya na tu Mari te tɩn ma na Zezi na bi Lazaarɩ te tɩn. Ba zanzan ma kɩ ba wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩd-o.

eng: But some of them went to the Pharisees and told them what Jesus had done.
local: Kʋ daarɩ ba badaara ma joori ba vu Farizɩan tiinǝ bam te, yɩ ba ta dɩ ba Zezi na kɩ kʋlʋ tɩn.

eng: Then the chief priests and Pharisees called together the council and said, What are we going to do? This man is doing many miraculous signs!.
local: Wɛ kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam dɩ Farizɩan tiinǝ bam laan ma la sarɩya-dirǝ nakwa bam maama ba kɩ daanɩ, yɩ ba bwɛ ba wɩ: «Nii-na nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ na kɩ wo-kɩnkagɩla yalʋ maama tɩn. Dɩbam nan wʋ́ kɩ-o ta mʋ?.

eng: If we let him go on like this, everyone will believe in him. Then the Romans will come and take away both our temple and our people.
local: Dɩ́ na yag-o sɩ o taa kɩ kʋntʋ, nɔɔna bam maama wʋ́ ba ba sɛ wʋm mʋ. Kʋntʋ wʋ́ pa Rom dɩdɛɛra bam laan ba ba cɔgɩ dɩ́ Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm dɩ dɩ́ tɩʋ kʋm maama.».

eng: One of them, Caiaphas, who was high priest that year, told them, You dont know anything!.
local: Balʋ na bwɛ daanɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, ba wʋ nɔɔnʋ dɩdʋa yɩrɩ mʋ Kayifu. Wʋntʋ mʋ yɩ ba Wɛ kaanɩm yuutu bɩnɩ dɩm kʋntʋ wʋnɩ. O ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Abam yǝri kʋlʋkʋlʋ.

eng: You dont see that it is better for you that one man die for the people rather than the whole nation be destroyed.
local: Á fɔgɩ á bʋŋɩ á nii, nɔɔnʋ dɩdʋa na tɩga dɩbam nɔɔna bam maama ŋwaanɩ, kʋʋ́ ta lana kʋ pa dɩbam kʋ garɩ dɩ́ tɩʋ kʋm maama na cɔgɩ.».

eng: He didnt say this on his own. As high priest that year, he prophesied that Jesus would soon die for the nation.
local: Kayifu na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ daɩ o tɩtɩ wʋbʋŋa mʋ o mɛ o ta. O nan na yɩ Wɛ kaanɩm yuutu tɩn, mʋ Wɛ pɛ o wuuri o ta nɩ, Zezi lagɩ o tɩ Zwifǝ dwi dɩm maama ŋwaanɩ mʋ.

eng: and not only for the nation. Jesus would also die so that Gods children scattered everywhere would be gathered together as one.
local: Kʋ nan daɩ nɩ o lagɩ o tɩ bantʋ dwi dɩm má ŋwaanɩ mʋ. Kʋ yɩ Wɛ biǝ bam na jagɩ je maama tɩn dɩ ŋwaanɩ mʋ, sɩ o wanɩ o kɩ-ba daanɩ sɩ ba taa yɩ dwi dɩdʋa.

eng: From that day on they plotted to kill him.
local: Kʋ na zɩgɩ dɛ dɩm kʋntʋ nɩ tɩn, Zwifǝ yigǝ tiinǝ bam ma kɩ ni daanɩ sɩ ba gʋ Zezi.

eng: Therefore, Jesus was no longer active in public ministry among the Jewish leaders. Instead, he left Jerusalem and went to a place near the wilderness, to a city called Ephraim, where he stayed with his disciples.
local: Kʋntʋ ma pa Zezi daa ba beeri jaja Zude nɩ sɩ nɔɔna taa na-o. O ma yagɩ dáanɩ, yɩ o vu je sɩlʋ na batwarɩ dɩ kagʋa ni tɩn. Kʋ yɩ Efrayim nɩ mʋ o ve o zʋʋrɩ da dɩ o karabiǝ bam.

eng: It was almost time for the Jewish Passover, and many people went from the countryside up to Jerusalem to purify themselves through ritual washing before the Passover.
local: Zwifǝ bam candiǝ kalʋ yɩrɩ na yɩ Pakɩ tɩn dɛɛn ma daarɩ fɩɩn sɩ ka yi. Nɔɔna zanzan ma zaŋɩ tɩʋ kʋm je maama nɩ, ba vu Zeruzalɛm sɩ ba kwɛ ba tɩtɩ dɩ Wɛ, sɩ kʋ loori candiǝ kam.

eng: They were looking for Jesus. As they spoke to each other in the temple, they said, What do you think? He wont come to the festival, will he.
local: Nɔɔna bam ma beeri Zezi je. Ba ma la daanɩ Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm nɩ, yɩ ba bwe daanɩ ba wɩ: «Abam bʋŋɩ nɩ, o bá ba o di candiǝ kam na?».

eng: The chief priests and Pharisees had given orders that anyone who knew where he was should report it, so they could arrest him.
local: Wɛ kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam dɩ Farizɩan tiinǝ bam dɛɛn ya pɛ ni nɩ, nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na ye Zezi na wʋ mɛ tɩn, sɩ o brɩ-ba, sɩ ba ja-o.

eng: I assure you that whoever doesnt enter into the sheep pen through the gate but climbs over the wall is a thief and an outlaw.
local: Zezi dɛɛn ma maŋɩ mɩmaŋa o brɩ nɔɔna bam o wɩ: «A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na wʋ dɛ najɩrɩ ni o zʋ sɩ o pʋrɩ peeni yɩ o tɔgɩ jǝgǝ kadoŋ o cu o zʋ tɩn, kʋntʋ tu yɩ ŋwɩɩnʋ yɩ o kwǝri o yɩ vɩrnʋ mʋ.

eng: The one who enters through the gate is the shepherd of the sheep.
