local: Wɛ kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam dɩ ba nɔɔna bam na nɛ-o tɩn, ba maa tɛ dɩ kwǝr-dɩa ba wɩ: «Pa-o tʋʋn-dagara baŋa nɩ n gʋ. Pa-o tʋʋn-dagara baŋa nɩ n gʋ.» Pilatɩ ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Abam tɩtɩ nan ja-o á pa dagara baŋa nɩ á gʋ, sɩ amʋ wʋ nɛ kʋlʋ o na cɔgɩ sɩ a ma di o taanɩ tɩn.».

eng: The Jewish leaders replied, We have a Law, and according to this Law he ought to die because he made himself out to be Gods Son.
local: Zwifǝ bam ma joori ba lǝr-o ba wɩ: «Dɩbam jɩgɩ culu kʋlʋ na brɩ nɩ o maŋɩ sɩ o tɩ tɩn, bɛŋwaanɩ o brɩ o tɩtɩ nɩ o yɩ Wɛ Bu mʋ.».

eng: When Pilate heard this word, he was even more afraid.
local: Pilatɩ na ni ba na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, fʋʋnɩ ma tiini dɩ zʋ-o.

eng: He went back into the residence and spoke to Jesus, Where are you from? Jesus didnt answer.
local: O ma joori o zʋ sɔŋɔ kʋm wʋ, yɩ o bwe Zezi o wɩ: «Nmʋ nuŋi yǝn mʋ?» Zezi maa cɩm yɩ o wʋ lǝr-o kʋlʋkʋlʋ.

eng: So Pilate said, You wont speak to me? Dont you know that I have authority to release you and also to crucify you.
local: O ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Nmʋ ba lagɩ sɩ n lǝri amʋ na? Nmʋ yǝri nɩ amʋ jɩgɩ dam sɩ a pa ba yagɩ-m naa sɩ a pa ba pa-m tʋʋn-dagara baŋa nɩ ba gʋ na?».

eng: Jesus replied, You would have no authority over me if it had not been given to you from above. Thats why the one who handed me over to you has the greater sin.
local: Zezi ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «Nmʋ ya n ba jɩgɩ dam amʋ baŋa nɩ, kʋ na daɩ dam dɩlʋ Wɛ na pɛ-m tɩn. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na jaanɩ amʋ o kɩ nmʋ jɩŋa nɩ tɩn mʋ tiini o kɩ wo-lɔŋɔ o dwǝni nmʋ.».

eng: From that moment on, Pilate wanted to release Jesus. However, the Jewish leaders cried out, saying, If you release this man, you arent a friend of the emperor! Anyone who makes himself out to be a king opposes the emperor.
local: Maŋa kam Pilatɩ na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, o maa kwaanɩ o lagɩ cwǝŋǝ sɩ o pa ba yagɩ Zezi. Kʋ daarɩ Zwifǝ bam maa tiini ba tɛ dɩ kwǝr-dɩa ba wɩ: «Nmʋ na yagɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ, kʋʋ́ brɩ nɩ n culi lʋgʋ kʋm pa-farʋ wʋm mʋ. Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na pɛ o tɩtɩ paarɩ tɩn, kʋntʋ tu yɩ pa-farʋ wʋm dʋm mʋ.».

eng: When Pilate heard these words, he led Jesus out and seated him on the judges bench at the place called Stone Pavement .
local: Pilatɩ na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma pa ba ja Zezi ba nuŋi, yɩ o daarɩ o jǝni taanɩ dim jǝŋǝ kam je nɩ. Ba bǝ jǝgǝ kam kʋntʋ nɩ, Kandwa-pulwaarʋ je mʋ. Ka yɩrɩ dɩ Zwifǝ bam taanɩ mʋ yɩ Gabata.

eng: It was about noon on the Preparation Day for the Passover. Pilate said to the Jewish leaders, Heres your king.
local: Kʋ dɛɛn yɩ wɩa tɩtarɩ nɩ mʋ, dɛ dɩm tɩga na wʋ́ pʋʋrɩ sɩ Pakɩ candiǝ kam di tɩn. Pilatɩ laan ma ta dɩ Zwifǝ bam o wɩ: «Nii-na á pɛ wʋm.».

eng: The Jewish leaders cried out, Take him away! Take him away! Crucify him! Pilate responded, What? Do you want me to crucify your king? We have no king except the emperor, the chief priests answered.
local: Ba ma ta kaasɩ baŋa baŋa ba wɩ: «Ti o mʋmwɛ, ti o mʋmwɛ, pa-o tʋʋn-dagara baŋa nɩ n gʋ.» O ma bwe-ba o wɩ: «Abam lagɩ sɩ ba pa á pɛ wʋm dagara baŋa nɩ ba gʋ mʋ na?» Wɛ kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam ma lǝri ba wɩ: «Dɩbam ba jɩgɩ pɛ wʋdoŋ, kʋ na daɩ lʋgʋ kʋm pa-farʋ wʋm yɩranɩ.».

eng: Then Pilate handed Jesus over to be crucified. The soldiers took Jesus prisoner.
local: Pilatɩ laan ma kɩ Zezi pamaŋna bam jɩŋa nɩ, sɩ ba pa-o tʋʋn-dagara baŋa nɩ ba gʋ. Pamaŋna bam laan ma ja Zezi.

eng: Carrying his cross by himself, he went out to a place called Skull Place .
local: yɩ ba pa o zɩŋɩ dagara kalʋ ba na lagɩ ba pa-o ka baŋa nɩ tɩn. O ma vu o yi jǝgǝ kalʋ ba na bǝ nɩ Yukol-pʋa piu tɩn. Kʋ yɩrɩ dɩ Zwifǝ bam taanɩ mʋ yɩ Goligota.

eng: Thats where they crucified him—and two others with him, one on each side and Jesus in the middle.
local: Kʋ yɩ dáanɩ mʋ ba pag-o tʋʋn-dagara kam baŋa nɩ. Ba ma ja nɔɔna badonnǝ bale dɩ, ba pa dagarɩ baŋa nɩ. Zezi maa wʋ bantʋ bale tɩtarɩ nɩ.

eng: Pilate had a public notice written and posted on the cross. It read Jesus the Nazarene, the king of the Jews.
local: Pilatɩ ma pʋpʋnɩ tɔnɔ o pa ba tanɩ-kʋ dagara kam yuu nɩ. Kʋ pʋpʋnɩ kʋ wɩ: «Wʋntʋ mʋ yɩ Nazarɛtɩ tu Zezi wʋlʋ na yɩ Zwifǝ bam pɛ tɩn.».

eng: Many of the Jews read this sign, for the place where Jesus was crucified was near the city and it was written in Aramaic, Latin, and Greek.
local: Zwifǝ bam zanzan ma karɩmɩ tɔnɔ kʋm, bɛŋwaanɩ jǝgǝ kalʋ ba na pagɩ Zezi da tɩn batwarɩ dɩ tɩʋ kʋm ni. Kʋ ma pʋpʋnɩ dɩ Zwifǝ bam taanɩ dɩ Rom tiinǝ taanɩ dɩ Grɛkɩ tiinǝ taanɩ mʋ.

eng: Therefore, the Jewish chief priests complained to Pilate, Dont write, The king of the Jews but This man said, I am the king of the Jews.
local: Zwifǝ kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam na nɛ tɩn, ba ma vu ba ta dɩ Pilatɩ ba wɩ: «Kʋ wʋ maŋɩ sɩ n pʋpʋnɩ nɩ ‹Zwifǝ bam pɛ›. Kʋ maŋɩ sɩ n pʋpʋnɩ nɩ: ‹Nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ mʋ brɩ o tɩtɩ o wɩ, o yɩ Zwifǝ bam pɛ mʋ.› ».

eng: Pilate answered, What Ive written, Ive written.
local: O ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Amʋ na pʋpʋnɩ te tɩn maŋɩ.».

eng: When the soldiers crucified Jesus, they took his clothes and his sandals, and divided them into four shares, one for each soldier. His shirt was seamless, woven as one piece from the top to the bottom.
local: Pamaŋna bam na pagɩ Zezi dagara kam baŋa nɩ ba ti tɩn, ba laan ma kwe o gwaarʋ tɩm ba cɛ kuni bɩna, sɩ pamaŋnʋ maama kwe o nyɩm. Ba ma kwe o gwar-bu wʋm dɩ. Kʋ maama sɔgɩ punǝ mʋ, kʋ ba jɩgɩ toŋim je.

eng: They said to each other, Lets not tear it. Lets cast lots to see who will get it. This was to fulfill the scripture, They divided my clothes among themselves, and they cast lots for my clothing. Thats what the soldiers did.
local: Kʋ na yɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma ta daanɩ ba wɩ: «Yɩ pa-na dɩ́ kaarɩ-kʋ. Nan pa-na dɩ́ ta jɔrɔ sɩ dɩ́ nii wʋlʋ na wʋ́ joŋi-kʋ tɩn.» Kʋ dɛɛn kɩ kʋntʋ mʋ, sɩ kʋ pa kʋlʋ na pʋpʋnɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ tɩn sɩɩnɩ kʋ kɩ, nɩ kʋ na wɩ: «Ba cɛ amʋ gwaarʋ ba pa daanɩ, yɩ ba daarɩ ba ta jɔrɔ amʋ gwar-bu wʋm ŋwaanɩ.» Pamaŋna bam ma sɩɩnɩ ba kɩ kʋntʋ.

eng: Jesus mother and his mothers sister, Mary the wife of Clopas, and Mary Magdalene stood near the cross.
local: Balʋ dɛɛn na zɩgɩ ba batwarɩ dɩ Zezi dagara kam tɩn mʋ yɩ o nu wʋm dɩ o nu nyaanɩ, dɩ Mari wʋlʋ na yɩ Klopasɩ kaanɩ tɩn, dɩ Mari wʋlʋ na nuŋi Magɩdala tɩn.

eng: When Jesus saw his mother and the disciple whom he loved standing nearby, he said to his mother, Woman, here is your son.
local: Zezi na nɛ o nu wʋm dɩ o karabu wʋlʋ o na soe lanyɩranɩ ba zɩgɩ da tɩn, o ma ta dɩ o nu wʋm o wɩ: «A nu, nii wʋntʋ laan mʋ yɩ n bu.».

eng: Then he said to the disciple, Here is your mother. And from that time on, this disciple took her into his home.
local: O ma daarɩ o ta dɩ o karabu wʋm dɩ o wɩ: «Nii n nu da.» Kʋ na zɩgɩ maŋa kam kʋntʋ nɩ, o karabu wʋm laan ma joŋi Zezi nu wʋm o pa o ba o zʋʋrɩ o sɔŋɔ nɩ.

eng: After this, knowing that everything was already completed, in order to fulfill the scripture, Jesus said, I am thirsty.
local: Kʋntʋ kwaga nɩ Zezi maa ye nɩ woŋo maama kɩ kʋ ti. O maa wɩ, na-nyɔm jɩg-o. O tagɩ kʋntʋ, sɩ kʋlʋ na pʋpʋnɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ tɩn mʋ sɩɩnɩ kʋ kɩ.

eng: A jar full of sour wine was nearby, so the soldiers soaked a sponge in it, placed it on a hyssop branch, and held it up to his lips.
local: Zʋŋa dɛɛn mʋ zɩgɩ da, ka na su dɩ sana kalʋ na nywana tɩn. Ba ma kwe sɩpɔɔ ba dɩ sana kam wʋnɩ, yɩ ba vɔ-kʋ miu ni nɩ ba te ba pa kʋ yi Zezi ni.

eng: When he had received the sour wine, Jesus said, It is completed. Bowing his head, he gave up his life.
local: Zezi na laŋɩ sa-nywana kam tɩn, o ma ta o wɩ: «Kʋ laan kɩ kʋ ti.» O ma daarɩ o cɔɔlɩ o yuu tɩga nɩ, yɩ o pa o siun ti.

eng: It was the Preparation Day and the Jewish leaders didnt want the bodies to remain on the cross on the Sabbath, especially since that Sabbath was an important day. So they asked Pilate to have the legs of those crucified broken and the bodies taken down.
local: Kʋ dɛɛn yɩ dɛ dɩlʋ Zwifǝ bam na yǝni ba ti ba yigǝ sɩ ba siun dɛ dɩm laan yi tɩn mʋ. Tɩga na wʋ́ pʋʋrɩ sɩ kʋ taa yɩ ba siun dɛ kamunu kʋm tɩn, ba maa ba lagɩ sɩ nɔɔna bam yɩra yam taa wʋ dagarɩ sɩm baŋa nɩ dɛ dɩm kʋntʋ nɩ. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ Zwifǝ yigǝ tiinǝ bam ma vu Pilatɩ te, ba ta dɩd-o sɩ o pa cwǝŋǝ sɩ ba bwǝri nɔɔna bam nɛ sɩ ba tɩ, sɩ ba laan parɩ-ba ba kɩ.

eng: Therefore, the soldiers came and broke the legs of the two men who were crucified with Jesus.
local: Kʋntʋ, pamaŋna bam ma vu balʋ ba na pagɩ ba wǝli dɩ Zezi tɩn te, yɩ ba magɩ dayigǝ nɔɔnʋ wʋm nɛ ba bwǝri, dɩ o doŋ wʋm nɛ.

eng: When they came to Jesus, they saw that he was already dead so they didnt break his legs.
local: Ba ma vu ba yi Zezi. Ba ma na nɩ o maŋɩ o tɩ mʋ, yɩ ba daa wʋ bwǝri wʋntʋ nɛ.

eng: However, one of the soldiers pierced his side with a spear, and immediately blood and water came out.
local: Kʋ daarɩ pamaŋnʋ dɩdʋa ma kwe o cicwe o ma zɔ Zezi saŋa nɩ. Jana dɩ na ma da ba nuŋǝ.

eng: The one who saw this has testified, and his testimony is true. He knows that he speaks the truth, and he has testified so that you also can believe.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ yi na nɛ kʋntʋ tɩn mʋ ŋɔɔnɩ kʋ woŋo, sɩ kʋ pa abam dɩ wanɩ á sɛ. O nan ye nɩ o na tagɩ kʋlʋ tɩn yɩ cɩga mʋ, yɩ o zɩgɩ cɩga kam kʋntʋ kwaga nɩ.

eng: These things happened to fulfill the scripture, They wont break any of his bones .
local: Kʋntʋ nan kɩ sɩ kʋ pa kʋlʋ na pʋpʋnɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ tɩn mʋ sɩɩnɩ kʋ kɩ, nɩ kʋ na wɩ: «O kuǝ dɩ dɩdʋa dɩ bá bwǝri.».

eng: And another scripture says, They will look at him whom they have pierced.
local: Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm ta tagɩ kʋ wɩ: «Nɔɔna wʋ́ fɔgɩ ba nii wʋlʋ wʋm ba na zɔgɩ tɩn.».

eng: After this Joseph of Arimathea asked Pilate if he could take away the body of Jesus. Joseph was a disciple of Jesus, but a secret one because he feared the Jewish authorities. Pilate gave him permission, so he came and took the body away.
local: Kʋntʋ kwaga nɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ na nuŋi Arimatɩ o yɩrɩ mʋ Zʋzɛfʋ tɩn ma zaŋɩ o vu Pilatɩ te. O dɛɛn yɩ Zezi karabu mʋ, yɩ o ba brɩ o tɩtɩ jaja o na kwarɩ Zwifǝ yigǝ tiinǝ bam tɩn ŋwaanɩ. O ma vu o bwe Pilatɩ sɩ o nii, oó wanɩ o kwe Zezi yɩra yam na. Pilatɩ ma sɛ sɩ o kwe. Zʋzɛfʋ ma vu o par-o dagara kam yuu nɩ.

eng: Nicodemus, the one who at first had come to Jesus at night, was there too. He brought a mixture of myrrh and aloe, nearly seventy-five pounds in all.
local: Nikodɛm wʋlʋ dɛɛn na ve o na Zezi tɩtɩɩ nɩ tɩn dɩ maa tɔgɩ o wʋra. O maa jɩgɩ tralɩ nugǝ zanzan o tee nɩ, kʋ na maŋɩ dɩ garɩwaa yi. Nugǝ kam yɩ Miiri dɩ Alowɛsɩ nugǝ mʋ gwaanɩ daanɩ.

eng: Following Jewish burial customs, they took Jesus body and wrapped it, with the spices, in linen cloths.
local: Zʋzɛfʋ dɩ Nikodɛm ma kwe Zezi yɩra yam ba turi-ya dɩ tralɩ nugǝ kam, yɩ ba daarɩ ba pri-o dɩ gar-jalɩ, nɩ Zwifǝ bam na yǝni ba kɩ ba twa te tɩn.

eng: There was a garden in the place where Jesus was crucified, and in the garden was a new tomb in which no one had ever been laid.
local: Gaara mʋ wʋ jǝgǝ kalʋ ba na pagɩ Zezi dagara baŋa nɩ tɩn. Piu ma wʋ gaara yam wʋnɩ, bɔn-dʋʋrɩ maa wʋ piu kʋm yɩra nɩ ba ta na wʋ kɩ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ da.

eng: Because it was the Jewish Preparation Day and the tomb was nearby, they laid Jesus in it.
local: Kʋ na yɩ dɛ dɩlʋ tɩga na wʋ́ pʋʋrɩ sɩ kʋ taa yɩ ba siun dɛ dɩm tɩn, ba ma kwe Zezi ba tiŋi bɔɔnɩ dɩm wʋnɩ, dɩ je na batwarɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: Early in the morning of the first day of the week, while it was still dark, Mary Magdalene came to the tomb and saw that the stone had been taken away from the tomb.
local: Dɛ dɩlʋ na saŋɩ ba siun dɛ dɩm tɩn, Mari wʋlʋ na nuŋi Magɩdala tɩn ma zaŋɩ o go tɩga o vu Zezi yibeeli dɩm jǝgǝ. O ma na nɩ kandwɛ dɩlʋ ya na pɩ bɔɔnɩ dɩm ni tɩn tigi daa nɩ.

eng: She ran to Simon Peter and the other disciple, the one whom Jesus loved, and said, They have taken the Lord from the tomb, and we dont know where theyve put him.
local: O ma duri o joori o vu o ta dɩ Simɔn Pɩyɛɛrɩ dɩ karabu wʋlʋ Zezi na soe tɩn o wɩ: «Ba kwe dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm yɩra yam yibeeli dɩm wʋnɩ, dɩ́ nan yǝri ba na kɩ-o mɛ.».

eng: Peter and the other disciple left to go to the tomb.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ dɩ karabu wʋdoŋ wʋm ma zaŋɩ ba duri ba maa ve bɔɔnɩ dɩm jǝgǝ. Karabu wʋntʋ ma duri o loori Pɩyɛɛrɩ bɔɔnɩ dɩm ni nɩ.

eng: Bending down to take a look, he saw the linen cloths lying there, but he didnt go in.
local: O ma tiiri o nii bɔɔnɩ dɩm wʋnɩ o na gar-jalɩ dɩm na tigi da, yɩ o wʋ zʋ dɩ wʋ.

eng: Following him, Simon Peter entered the tomb and saw the linen cloths lying there.
local: Simɔn Pɩyɛɛrɩ ma da o kwaga o ba o yi. O ma kɛ o zʋ bɔɔnɩ dɩm wʋ o na gar-jalɩ dɩm na tigi da.

eng: He also saw the face cloth that had been on Jesus head. It wasnt with the other clothes but was folded up in its own place.
local: yɩ o na gɔrɔ kʋlʋ ba ya na mɛ ba ba Zezi yuu tɩn. Kʋntʋ nan pri mʋ kʋ tigi kʋ yɩra.

eng: Then the other disciple, the one who arrived at the tomb first, also went inside. He saw and believed.
local: Karabu wʋlʋ ya na yi da yigǝ tɩn dɩ laan ma zʋ bɔɔnɩ dɩm wʋ. O ma na kʋlʋ na wʋra tɩn, yɩ o daarɩ o sɛ nɩ kʋ sɩɩnɩ kʋ yɩ cɩga.

eng: They didnt yet understand the scripture that Jesus must rise from the dead.
local: Ba daa ta ma wʋ lwarɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm na tagɩ kʋlʋ kʋ wɩ o maŋɩ sɩ o bi o yagɩ tʋʋnɩ tɩn kuri.

eng: Then the disciples returned to the place where they were staying.
local: Ba laan ma joori ba vu sɔŋɔ.

eng: Mary stood outside near the tomb, crying. As she cried, she bent down to look into the tomb.
local: Mari daa ta maa zɩgɩ yibeeli dɩm ni nɩ o keerǝ. O na keerǝ kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma tiiri o nii bɔɔnɩ dɩm wʋnɩ.

eng: She saw two angels dressed in white, seated where the body of Jesus had been, one at the head and one at the foot.
local: O ma na malɛsɩ sɩle na zʋ gwar-pwǝǝnu, sɩ je mɛ seeni Zezi yɩra yam ya na tigi da tɩn. Dɩdʋa maa wʋ o yuu seeni, yɩ kadoŋ kam wʋ o nɛ seeni.

eng: The angels asked her, Woman, why are you crying? She replied, They have taken away my Lord, and I dont know where theyve put him.
local: Sɩ ma bwe-o sɩ wɩ: «A nakɔ, bɛɛ mʋ yɩ n keerǝ?» O ma ta dɩ sɩ o wɩ: «Ba kwe a Yuutu wʋm yɩra yam mʋ ba ja viiri, a nan yǝri ba na kwe-o ba kɩ mɛ.».

eng: As soon as she had said this, she turned around and saw Jesus standing there, but she didnt know it was Jesus.
local: O na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma pipiri o nii o kwaga nɩ o na Zezi na zɩgɩ da, yɩ o wʋ lwarɩ nɩ kʋ yɩ Zezi mʋ.

eng: Jesus said to her, Woman, why are you crying? Who are you looking for? Thinking he was the gardener, she replied, Sir, if you have carried him away, tell me where you have put him and I will get him.
local: Zezi ma bwe-o o wɩ: «A nakɔ, bɛɛ mʋ yɩ n keerǝ? Wɔɔ mʋ nmʋ beerǝ?» Mari maa bʋŋɩ nɩ o yɩ gaarɩ dɩm tu mʋ. O ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Amʋ tu, kʋ na yɩ nmʋ na kwe-o n ja n viiri, sɩ n brɩ-nɩ n na kɩ-o mɛ tɩn, sɩ a vu a kwe-o.».

eng: Jesus said to her, Mary. She turned and said to him in Aramaic, Rabbouni .
local: Zezi ma bǝŋi o yɩrɩ o wɩ: «Mari.» Mari ma pipiri o nii o seeni, yɩ o ta dɩ Zwifǝ bam taanɩ o wɩ: «Rabuni», kʋ kuri mʋ Karanyɩna.

eng: Jesus said to her, Dont hold on to me, for I havent yet gone up to my Father. Go to my brothers and sisters and tell them, Im going up to my Father and your Father, to my God and your God.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Yɩ ta n dwe-nɩ, sɩ amʋ daa ta wʋ joori a vu a Ko wʋm te. Nan ve n ta n brɩ a ko-biǝ bam nɩ, amʋ laan maa ve a Ko wʋm te, wʋlʋ na yɩ abam dɩ Ko tɩn. Wʋntʋ mʋ yɩ Baŋa-Wɛ, amʋ Tu dɩ abam dɩ Tu.».

eng: Mary Magdalene left and announced to the disciples, Ive seen the Lord. Then she told them what he said to her.
local: Mari wʋlʋ na nuŋi Magɩdala tɩn laan ma zaŋɩ o vu o ta dɩ Zezi karabiǝ bam o wɩ: «Amʋ nɛ dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm», yɩ o daarɩ o ta kʋlʋ Zezi na tagɩ o brɩ-o tɩn.

eng: It was still the first day of the week. That evening, while the disciples were behind closed doors because they were afraid of the Jewish authorities, Jesus came and stood among them. He said, Peace be with you.
local: Dɛ dɩm kʋntʋ nɩ dɩdaan-ni nɩ mʋ Zezi karabiǝ bam lagɩ daanɩ jǝgǝ dɩdʋa. Ba fɔgɩ ba pɩ digǝ kam ni dɩ borbu, ba na kwarɩ fʋʋnɩ dɩ Zwifǝ yigǝ tiinǝ bam tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Zezi laan ma da o ba o zɩgɩ ba tɩtarɩ nɩ. O ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Yazurǝ wʋ́ jǝni dɩ abam.».

eng: After he said this, he showed them his hands and his side. When the disciples saw the Lord, they were filled with joy.
local: O na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma brɩ-ba o jɩa dɩ o saŋa kam. O karabiǝ bam maa tiini ba jɩgɩ wʋpolo lanyɩranɩ dɩ ba na nɛ ba Yuutu wʋm tɩn.

eng: Jesus said to them again, Peace be with you. As the Father sent me, so I am sending you.
local: O daa ta ma joori o ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Yazurǝ wʋ́ jǝni dɩ abam. A Ko Wɛ na tʋŋɩ-nɩ lʋgʋ baŋa nɩ te tɩn, amʋ dɩ tʋŋɩ abam lʋgʋ kʋm wʋnɩ kʋntʋ doŋ mʋ.».

eng: Then he breathed on them and said, Receive the Holy Spirit.
local: O na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma fuli ba seeni yɩ o wɩ: «Joŋi-na Wɛ Joro kʋm.

eng: If you forgive anyones sins, they are forgiven; if you dont forgive them, they arent forgiven.
local: Abam na yagɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ lwarɩm á ma cɛ-o tɩn, Wɛ wʋ́ sɩɩnɩ DƖ yagɩ DƖ ma cɛ kʋ tu. Kʋ daarɩ, abam na wʋ yagɩ á ma cɛ wʋlʋ tɩn, Wɛ dɩ bá yagɩ DƖ ma cɛ-o.».

eng: Thomas, the one called Didymus, one of the Twelve, wasnt with the disciples when Jesus came.
local: Toma wʋlʋ ba na ma bǝ nɩ Yiywɛ yɩ o wʋ Zezi karabiǝ fugǝ-bale bam wʋnɩ tɩn ya tǝrǝ maŋa kam Zezi na tu o karabiǝ bam te tɩn.

eng: The other disciples told him, Weve seen the Lord! But he replied, Unless I see the nail marks in his hands, put my finger in the wounds left by the nails, and put my hand into his side, I wont believe.
local: Karabiǝ badonnǝ bam ma ta ba brɩ-o ba wɩ: «Dɩbam ya nɛ dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm.» O ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Amʋ yi na wʋ nɛ cɩna yam niǝ o jɩ sɩm nɩ, sɩ a kɩ a nʋa da a nii, sɩ a daarɩ a kɩ a jɩŋa o saŋa kam nɩ, amʋ bá sɛ.».

eng: After eight days his disciples were again in a house and Thomas was with them. Even though the doors were locked, Jesus entered and stood among them. He said, Peace be with you.
local: Da nana na kɛ tɩn, Zezi karabiǝ bam daa ma la daanɩ jǝgǝ dɩdʋa, yɩ Toma dɩ laan wʋra. Ba ma fɔgɩ ba pɩ digǝ kam ni dɩ borbu. Zezi ma da o ba o zɩgɩ ba tɩtarɩ nɩ. O ma ta o wɩ: «Yazurǝ wʋ́ jǝni dɩ abam.».

eng: Then he said to Thomas, Put your finger here. Look at my hands. Put your hand into my side. No more disbelief. Believe.
local: O laan ma ta dɩ Toma o wɩ: «Ma n nʋa n taanɩ a jɩ sɩm yo seeni n nii, n daarɩ n tʋrɩ n jɩŋa a saŋa kam nɩ. Yagɩ bʋbwɛa, sɩ n daarɩ n sɛ.».

eng: Thomas responded to Jesus, My Lord and my God.
local: Toma ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «Nmʋ yɩ a Yuutu dɩ amʋ Baŋa-Wɛ.».

eng: Jesus replied, Do you believe because you see me? Happy are those who dont see and yet believe.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Nmʋ na nɛ amʋ tɩn ŋwaanɩ mʋ n laan sɛ na? Balʋ nan na sɛ amʋ yɩ ba ta wʋ nɛ-nɩ tɩn mʋ tiini ba jɩgɩ yu-yoŋo.».

eng: Then Jesus did many other miraculous signs in his disciples presence, signs that arent recorded in this scroll.
local: Zezi dɛɛn kɩ wo-kɩnkagɩla yadonnǝ zanzan o karabiǝ bam tɩtarɩ nɩ, yɩ tɩ maama nan wʋ pʋpʋnɩ tɔnɔ kʋntʋ wʋnɩ.

eng: But these things are written so that you will believe that Jesus is the Christ, Gods Son, and that believing, you will have life in his name.
local: Wǝǝnu tɩlʋ na pʋpʋnɩ tɩn yɩ sɩ á wanɩ á sɛ, nɩ Zezi mʋ yɩ Krisi wʋm Wɛ na tʋŋɩ tɩn, yɩ o kwǝri o yɩ Wɛ Bu wʋm. Á nan na kɩ á wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩd-o, á wʋ́ na ŋwɩa Wɛ tee nɩ.

eng: I have said these things to you so that you wont fall away.
local: «A nan ŋɔɔnɩ wǝǝnu tɩntʋ a brɩ abam sɩ kʋ pa á yɩ zaŋɩ á yagɩ Wɛ cwǝŋǝ kam tɔgɩm mʋ.

eng: They will expel you from the synagogue. The time is coming when those who kill you will think that they are doing a service to God.
local: Ba nan wʋ́ lɩ abam ba Wɛ-di sɩm wʋnɩ. Maŋa nan wʋ́ ba ka yi, yɩ nɔɔna wʋ́ gʋ abam, yɩ ba bʋŋɩ nɩ ba tʋŋɩ tɩtʋŋ-ŋʋm mʋ ba pa Wɛ.

eng: They will do these things because they dont know the Father or me.
local: Ba nan wʋ́ kɩ abam wǝǝnu tɩm kʋntʋ, bɛŋwaanɩ ba yǝri a Ko wʋm, ba nan yǝri amʋ dɩ.

eng: But I have said these things to you so that when their time comes, you will remember that I told you about them. I didnt say these things to you from the beginning, because I was with you.
local: A tɛ wǝǝnu tɩntʋ maama a brɩ abam, sɩ maŋa kam na yiǝ ba na kɩ abam kʋntʋ, sɩ á wanɩ á guli nɩ amʋ maŋɩ a ta-tɩ a brɩ abam.» «A wʋ tagɩ wǝǝnu tɩm kʋntʋ a brɩ abam pulim nɩ, bɛŋwaanɩ amʋ ya wʋ abam tee nɩ mʋ.

eng: But now I go away to the one who sent me. None of you ask me, Where are you going.
local: Kʋ daarɩ lele kʋntʋ a lagɩ a vu wʋlʋ na tʋŋɩ-nɩ tɩn te. Abam wʋlʋwʋlʋ nan wʋ bwe-nɩ á nii, a lagɩ a vu yǝn mʋ?.

eng: Yet because I have said these things to you, you are filled with sorrow.
local: Amʋ nan na tagɩ a brɩ abam kʋlʋ na lagɩ kʋ kɩ tɩn mʋ pɛ á wʋrʋ tiini tɩ cɔgɩ.

eng: I assure you that it is better for you that I go away. If I dont go away, the Companion wont come to you. But if I go, I will send him to you.
local: Kʋ daarɩ, a lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, amʋ na viiri, kʋʋ́ ta lana kʋ pa abam. Bɛŋwaanɩ, amʋ na wʋ viiri, Zǝnnu wʋm bá ba abam te. A nan na veǝ, a laan wʋ́ tʋŋ-o yɩ o ba abam te.

eng: When he comes, he will show the world it was wrong about sin, righteousness, and judgment.
local: O nan na tuǝ, oó pa lʋgʋ baŋa nɔɔna lwarɩ nɩ ba tusi mʋ ba lwarɩm wʋnɩ, dɩ Wɛ cɩga kam laŋa nɩ, dɩ Wɛ na wʋ́ di nɔɔna taanɩ te tɩn.

eng: He will show the world it was wrong about sin because they dont believe in me.
local: Oó brɩ-ba nɩ ba na wʋ sɛ amʋ tɩn ba kɩ lwarɩm mʋ.

eng: He will show the world it was wrong about righteousness because Im going to the Father and you wont see me anymore.
local: Oó brɩ-ba nɩ ba tusi Wɛ cɩga kam wʋnɩ, bɛŋwaanɩ amʋ lagɩ a vu a Ko Wɛ te, yɩ á daa bá na-nɩ.

eng: He will show the world it was wrong about judgment because this worlds ruler stands condemned.
local: O ta wʋ́ brɩ-ba nɩ ba tusi Wɛ sarɩya dim dɩm wʋnɩ, bɛŋwaanɩ wʋlʋ na yɩ lʋgʋ baŋa pɛ wʋm tɩn maŋɩ o ga bʋra.

eng: I have much more to say to you, but you cant handle it now.
local: Amʋ ya ta jɩgɩ wǝǝnu zanzan sɩ a ta a brɩ abam, tɩ nan wʋ́ ta tiini tɩ camma dɩ abam lele.

eng: However, when the Spirit of Truth comes, he will guide you in all truth. He wont speak on his own, but will say whatever he hears and will proclaim to you what is to come.
local: Kʋ daarɩ Wɛ Joro kʋm yɩ cɩga tu mʋ. Kʋ na tuǝ, kʋ laan wʋ́ pa á lwarɩ cɩga kam maama. Kʋ nan daɩ kʋ tɩtɩ taanɩ mʋ kʋ lagɩ kʋ ŋɔɔnɩ. Kʋʋ́ ŋɔɔnɩ kʋlʋ kʋ na ni Wɛ tee nɩ tɩn mʋ, kʋ ma daarɩ kʋ ta wǝǝnu tɩlʋ na lagɩ tɩ ba tɩn kʋ brɩ abam.

eng: He will glorify me, because he will take what is mine and proclaim it to you.
local: Kʋ nan wʋ́ pa amʋ na zulǝ, bɛŋwaanɩ kʋʋ́ brɩ abam amʋ cɩga kam maama.

eng: Everything that the Father has is mine. Thats why I said that the Spirit takes what is mine and will proclaim it to you.
local: A Ko wʋm na jɩgɩ kʋlʋ maama tɩn yɩ amʋ dɩ nyɩm mʋ. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ a tagɩ a wɩ, DƖ Joro kʋm wʋ́ brɩ abam amʋ cɩga kam maama.

eng: Soon you wont be able to see me; soon after that, you will see me.
local: Kʋ na kɩ fɩɩn, abam daa bá na amʋ, yɩ maŋa fɩnfɩɩn na wǝli da, á wʋ́ joori á na-nɩ, a na maa ve a Ko te tɩn ŋwaanɩ.».

eng: Some of Jesus disciples said to each other, What does he mean: Soon you wont see me, and soon after that you will see me and Because Im going to the Father?.
local: Zezi na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, o karabiǝ badaara maa tɛ daanɩ ba wɩ: «O na tagɩ o wɩ, kʋ na kɩ fɩɩn dɩ́ daa bá na-o, yɩ maŋa fɩnfɩɩn na wǝli da dɩ́ daa wʋ́ joori dɩ́ na-o tɩn, kʋ kuri mʋ bɛɛ? O na tagɩ o wɩ, o lagɩ o vu o Ko te tɩn, dɩ́ yǝri kʋ kuri.».

eng: What does he mean by soon? We dont understand what hes talking about.
local: Ba ta maa tiini ba bwɛ daanɩ ba wɩ: «O na tagɩ o wɩ, kʋ na kɩ fɩɩn tɩn, kʋ kuri mʋ bɛɛ? Dɩ́ yǝri o na tɛ kʋlʋ tɩn kuri.».

eng: Jesus knew they wanted to ask him, so he said, Are you trying to find out from each other what I meant when I said, Soon you wont see me, and soon after that you will see me?.
local: Zezi ma lwarɩ nɩ ba lagɩ sɩ ba bwe-o taanɩ dɩm kuri. O ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «A na tagɩ a wɩ, kʋ na kɩ fɩɩn á daa bá na-nɩ, yɩ maŋa fɩnfɩɩn na wǝli da á wʋ́ joori á na-nɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ mʋ á bwɛ daanɩ na?.

eng: I assure you that you will cry and lament, and the world will be happy. You will be sorrowful, but your sorrow will turn into joy.
local: A nan lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, abam wʋ́ ba á keeri á coosi. Kʋ daarɩ lʋgʋ baŋa nɔɔna bam wʋ́ ta jɩgɩ wʋpolo. Abam wʋrʋ wʋ́ ba tɩ cɔgɩ, yɩ á wʋ-cɔgɔ kʋm laan wʋ́ lǝni kʋ ji wʋpolo.

eng: When a woman gives birth, she has pain because her time has come. But when the child is born, she no longer remembers her distress because of her joy that a child has been born into the world.
local: Maŋa kalʋ kaanɩ na lagɩ o lʋ bu tɩn, o jɩgɩ liǝ mʋ, bɛŋwaanɩ o canɛɛrɩ maŋa yiǝ. O nan na lʋgɩ bu wʋm, o laan wʋ́ swe o cam dɩm ni nɩ, yɩ o jɩgɩ wʋpolo dɩ o na lʋgɩ bu wʋm tɩn.

eng: In the same way, you have sorrow now; but I will see you again, and you will be overjoyed. No one takes away your joy.
local: Kʋ nan yɩ kʋntʋ mʋ dɩ abam dɩ. Lele kʋntʋ á jɩgɩ liǝ. Amʋ nan daa ta wʋ́ joori a na abam, yɩ kʋ pa á tiini á na wʋpolo, yɩ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ daa bá wanɩ-kʋ o cɔgɩ.

eng: In that day, you wont ask me anything. I assure you that the Father will give you whatever you ask in my name.
local: Dɛ dɩm kʋntʋ na yiǝ, abam daa bá bwe amʋ kʋlʋkʋlʋ. A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, a Ko wʋm wʋ́ pa abam kʋlʋ maama abam na wʋ́ loori amʋ yɩrɩ ŋwaanɩ tɩn.

eng: Up to now, you have asked nothing in my name. Ask and you will receive so that your joy will be complete.
local: Abam nan ta maŋɩ á wʋ loori kʋlʋkʋlʋ dɩ amʋ yɩrɩ. Nan loori-na, sɩ á wʋ́ na, sɩ kʋ pa abam wʋpolo kʋm tiini kʋ gaalɩ.

eng: Ive been using figures of speech with you. The time is coming when I will no longer speak to you in such analogies. Instead, I will tell you plainly about the Father.
local: A na tagɩ wǝǝnu tɩntʋ a brɩ abam tɩn, kʋ yɩ mɩmaŋa mʋ a mɛ a ta. Maŋa nan wʋ́ ba, yɩ a daa bá maŋɩ mɩmaŋa a ma brɩ abam. A laan wʋ́ ba a ŋɔɔnɩ a Ko Wɛ taanɩ dɩm jaja mʋ a brɩ abam.

eng: In that day you will ask in my name. Im not saying that I will ask the Father on your behalf.
local: Dɛ dɩm kʋntʋ nɩ, amʋ daa bá loori a Ko Wɛ abam ŋwaanɩ. Abam mʋ wʋ́ loori-DƖ amʋ yɩrɩ ŋwaanɩ.

eng: The Father himself loves you, because you have loved me and believed that I came from God.
local: bɛŋwaanɩ Wɛ tɩtɩ maŋɩ DƖ soe abam, abam na soe amʋ yɩ á kwǝri á sɛ nɩ a nuŋi DƖ tee nɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: I left the Father and came into the world. I tell you again: I am leaving the world and returning to the Father.
local: Amʋ sɩɩnɩ a nuŋi a Ko Baŋa-Wɛ te mʋ a ba lʋgʋ baŋa. Lele kʋntʋ a nan lagɩ a yagɩ lʋgʋ baŋa sɩ a joori a vu DƖ te.».

eng: His disciples said, See! Now you speak plainly; you arent using figures of speech.
local: Zezi na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, o karabiǝ bam laan ma ta dɩd-o ba wɩ: «Nii, nmʋ laan ŋɔɔnɩ jaja mʋ, n daa wʋ mɛ mɩmaŋa n maŋɩ.

eng: Now we know that you know everything and you dont need anyone to ask you. Because of this we believe you have come from God.
local: Lele kʋntʋ, dɩ́ laan lwarɩ nɩ nmʋ ye woŋo maama. Nmʋ daa n ba lagɩ sɩ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ bwe-m taanɩ. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ dɩbam sɛ nɩ nmʋ sɩɩnɩ n nuŋi Wɛ te.».

eng: Jesus replied, Now you believe?.
local: Zezi ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Lele kʋntʋ á laan sɛ na?.

eng: Look! A time is coming—and is here!—when each of you will be scattered to your own homes and you will leave me alone. Im not really alone, for the Father is with me.
local: Nii-na, maŋa maa bɩɩnɩ, ka nan yi ka ti, yɩ abam wʋ́ jagɩ, yɩ á dɩdʋa dɩdʋa wʋ́ joori o kwe o cwǝŋǝ o daarɩ amʋ yɩranɩ. A nan bá ta yɩ a yɩranɩ, bɛŋwaanɩ a Ko wʋm wʋ́ ta wʋ a tee nɩ.

eng: Ive said these things to you so that you will have peace in me. In the world you have distress. But be encouraged! I have conquered the world.
local: Amʋ tagɩ wǝǝnu tɩntʋ a brɩ abam sɩ kʋ pa á na wʋ-zuru mʋ amʋ tɔgɩm dɩm wʋnɩ. Lʋgʋ baŋa nɩ á wʋ́ na yaara. Nan taá jɩgɩ-na pu-dɩa, sɩ amʋ wanɩ lʋgʋ.».

eng: Theophilus, the first scroll I wrote concerned everything Jesus did and taught from the beginning.
local: A ciloŋ Teofili, a maŋɩ a da yigǝ a pʋpʋnɩ tɔnɔ a pa nmʋ. Tɔnɔ kʋm kʋntʋ wʋnɩ mʋ a pʋpʋnɩ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ maama Zezi dɛɛn na kɩ tɩn, yɩ a kwǝri a pʋpʋnɩ o na yǝni o brɩ nɔɔna te tɩn, kʋ na sɩŋɩ maŋa kalʋ o na puli o tɩtʋŋa yam.

eng: right up to the day when he was taken up into heaven. Before he was taken up, working in the power of the Holy Spirit, Jesus instructed the apostles he had chosen.
local: sɩ kʋ vu kʋ yi dɛ dɩm Wɛ na kwe-o DƖ ja di DƖ sɔŋɔ tɩn. Kʋ na wʋ́ loori sɩ o di Wɛ-sɔŋɔ kʋntʋ tɩn, o laan ma brɩ o tɩntʋŋna balʋ o na kuri tɩn dɩ Wɛ Joro dam, yɩ o brɩ-ba ba na maŋɩ sɩ ba kɩ kʋlʋ tɩn.

eng: After his suffering, he showed them that he was alive with many convincing proofs. He appeared to them over a period of forty days, speaking to them about Gods kingdom.
local: O tʋʋnɩ dɩm kwaga nɩ o ma joori o ba o brɩ o tɩtɩ o nɔɔna bam tee nɩ kuni zanzan, sɩ kʋ brɩ-ba lanyɩranɩ nɩ o sɩɩnɩ o bi o yagɩ tʋʋnɩ. O dɛɛn brɩ o tɩtɩ o tɩntʋŋna bam da fiinna mʋ, yɩ o ŋɔɔnɩ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm taanɩ o brɩ-ba.

eng: While they were eating together, he ordered them not to leave Jerusalem but to wait for what the Father had promised. He said, This is what you heard from me.
local: Dɛ dɩdwɩ Zezi na wʋra o brɩ-ba kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma kaanɩ-ba o wɩ, ba yɩ zaŋɩ ba nuŋi Zeruzalɛm wʋnɩ, sɩ ba taa cǝgi wʋlʋ wʋm o Ko na goni ni sɩ o ba ba te tɩn, nɩ o na maŋɩ o ta dɩ ba te tɩn.

eng: John baptized with water, but in only a few days you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Zan dɛɛn yǝni o miisi nɔɔna na wʋnɩ mʋ, da fɩnfɩɩn nan na wǝli da, Wɛ wʋ́ miisi abam DƖ Joro kʋm wʋnɩ.».

eng: As a result, those who had gathered together asked Jesus, Lord, are you going to restore the kingdom to Israel now.
local: Zezi dɩ o tɩntʋŋna bam dɛɛn na kikili daanɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma bwe-o ba wɩ: «Dɩbam Yuutu, kʋ yɩ lele kʋntʋ mʋ n lagɩ n pa dɩbam Yisɩrayɛlɩ tiinǝ bam joori dɩ́ di paarɩ na?».

eng: Jesus replied, It isnt for you to know the times or seasons that the Father has set by his own authority.
local: Zezi ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Kʋ daɩ sɩ abam lwarɩ maŋa kalʋ Wɛ na lɩ dɩdaanɩ DƖ tɩtɩ ni sɩ wǝǝnu tɩntʋ kɩ tɩn.

eng: Rather, you will receive power when the Holy Spirit has come upon you, and you will be my witnesses in Jerusalem, in all Judea and Samaria, and to the end of the earth.
local: Wɛ Joro kʋm nan wʋ́ ba abam yuu nɩ kʋ pa á joŋi dam dɩlʋ na nuŋi Wɛ tee nɩ tɩn. Abam laan wʋ́ ji a maana tiinǝ sɩ á brɩ nɔɔna amʋ cɩga kam je maama nɩ, kʋ na sɩŋɩ dɩ Zeruzalɛm dɩ Zude dɩ Samari tɩɩnɩ dɩm maama, dɩ lʋgʋ baŋa je maama nɩ.».

eng: After Jesus said these things, as they were watching, he was lifted up and a cloud took him out of their sight.
local: Zezi dɛɛn na ŋɔɔnɩ kʋntʋ dɩ ba o ti tɩn, o ma zaŋɩ o maa diini Wɛ-sɔŋɔ, yɩ ba zɩgɩ ba nii-o. Kunkwǝǝnu ma ba tɩ kwǝl-o kʋ pa ba daa warɩ ba na-o.

eng: While he was going away and as they were staring toward heaven, suddenly two men in white robes stood next to them.
local: O na maa diini kʋntʋ tɩn, ba daa ta ma kwǝni ba nii wɛyuu nɩ. Ba na zɩgɩ ba nii kʋntʋ tɩn, nɔɔna bale ma da ba ba ba zɩgɩ ba tee nɩ. Ba zʋ gwar-pwǝǝnu mʋ.

eng: They said, Galileans, why are you standing here, looking toward heaven? This Jesus, who was taken up from you into heaven, will come in the same way that you saw him go into heaven.
local: Ba ma ta dɩ ba ba wɩ: «Galile tiinǝ-ba, bɛɛ mʋ yɩ á zɩgɩ yoba á kwǝni á nii wɛɛnɩ? Abam na nɛ kʋlʋ tɩn, kʋ yɩ Wɛ mʋ kwe Zezi DƖ ja di DƖ sɔŋɔ. Wʋntʋ tɩtɩ nan ta wʋ́ joori o ba kʋntʋ doŋ, nɩ abam na nɛ-o o na maa diini te tɩn.».

eng: Then they returned to Jerusalem from the Mount of Olives, which is near Jerusalem—a sabbath days journey away.
local: Zezi tɩntʋŋna bam laan ma zɩgɩ piu kʋm ba ya na wʋ kʋ yuu nɩ tɩn, ba joori ba vu Zeruzalɛm tɩʋ kʋm wʋ. Piu kʋm kʋntʋ yɩrɩ mʋ Olivi piu, yɩ kʋ batwarɩ dɩdaanɩ tɩʋ kʋm nɩnɛɛnɩ kilomɛtrɩ dɩdʋa.

eng: When they entered the city, they went to the upstairs room where they were staying. Peter, John, James, and Andrew; Philip and Thomas; Bartholomew and Matthew; James, Alphaeus son; Simon the zealot; and Judas, James son.
local: Ba ma vu ba zʋ tɩʋ kʋm ba kɛ ba vu sɔŋɔ kʋlʋ ba na maŋɩ ba zʋʋrɩ da tɩn, ba di wɛyuu digǝ kam. Nɔɔna bam kʋntʋ yɩ Pɩyɛɛrɩ dɩ Zan dɩ Zakɩ dɩ Andre, kʋ wǝli dɩ Filipi dɩ Toma, dɩdaanɩ Batelemi dɩ Matiyu, kʋ wǝli dɩ Zakɩ wʋlʋ na yɩ Alɩfɩ bu tɩn, dɩ Simɔn wʋlʋ na yɩ Zelɔtɩ nɔɔna bam wʋ nɔɔnʋ tɩn, dɩdaanɩ Zudasɩ wʋlʋ na yɩ Zakɩ bu tɩn.

eng: all were united in their devotion to prayer, along with some women, including Mary the mother of Jesus, and his brothers.
local: Maŋa maama bantʋ dɛɛn yǝni ba wʋ daanɩ dɩ wʋbʋŋ-dɩdwɩ mʋ, yɩ ba warɩ Wɛ. Kaana badonnǝ dɩdaanɩ Zezi nu Mari dɩ Zezi nyaana dɩ tɔgɩ ba wʋra dɩ ba.

eng: During this time, the family of believers was a company of about one hundred twenty persons. Peter stood among them and said.
local: Dɛ dɩdwɩ balʋ maama na yɩ ko-biǝ Zezi ŋwaanɩ tɩn ma kikili daanɩ jǝgǝ dɩdʋa, ba yi nɩ nɔɔna bi dɩ fiinle . Ba na wʋra kʋntʋ tɩn, Pɩyɛɛrɩ ma zaŋɩ ba wʋnɩ o zɩgɩ wɛɛnɩ sɩ o ŋɔɔnɩ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, the scripture that the Holy Spirit announced beforehand through David had to be fulfilled. This was the scripture concerning Judas, who became a guide for those who arrested Jesus.
local: O maa wɩ: «A ko-biǝ-ba, kʋ na yɩ Zudasɩ wʋlʋ na brɩ Zezi dʋna bam cwǝŋǝ sɩ ba ja-o tɩn, Wɛ Joro kʋm dɛɛn maŋɩ kʋ pa Pɛ Davidi ŋɔɔnɩ wʋntʋ taanɩ faŋa faŋa. Kʋlʋ na pʋpʋnɩ kʋ tiŋi Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ wʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ tɩn mʋ maŋɩ sɩ kʋ ba kʋ kɩ.

eng: This happened even though he was one of us and received a share of this ministry. .
local: Zudasɩ dɛɛn tɔgɩ o wʋ dɩbam kɔgɔ kʋm wʋnɩ mʋ, yɩ o tɔgɩ o tʋŋɩ Wɛ tɩtʋŋa yam.».

eng: In fact, he bought a field with the payment he received for his injustice. Falling headfirst, he burst open in the middle and all his intestines spilled out.
local: Zudasɩ na kɩ te tɩn, o dɛɛn kwe sǝbu kʋlʋ o na joŋi o kǝm-balɔrɔ kʋm baŋa nɩ tɩn o ma yǝgi tɩga mʋ. O daarɩ o tʋ tɩga nɩ o tɩ. O pugǝ ma bagɩ yɩ o lʋrʋ pi tɩ nuŋi.

eng: This became known to everyone living in Jerusalem, so they called that field in their own language Hakeldama, or Field of Blood.
local: Nɔɔna balʋ maama na zʋʋrɩ Zeruzalɛm nɩ tɩn ma ni kʋlʋ na kɩ-o tɩn, kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ ba bǝŋi tɩga kam kʋntʋ nɩ Akɩlɩdama dɩ ba tɩtɩ taanɩ. Kʋ kuri mʋ Jana tɩga.

eng: It is written in the Psalms scroll, Let his home become deserted and let there be no one living in it; and Give his position of leadership to another. .
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ ta ma ta o wɩ: «Kʋ nan pʋpʋnɩ Wɛ lǝŋ-ŋwɩ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ kʋ wɩ: ‹Wɛ wʋ́ pa wʋntʋ sɔŋɔ kʋm ba kʋ cɔgɩ, sɩ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ daa yɩ daarɩ kʋ wʋnɩ.› Kʋ daa ta pʋpʋnɩ kʋ wɩ: ‹Wɛ wʋ́ pa nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ lǝni wʋntʋ yuu nɩ o taa tʋŋɩ o tɩtʋŋa yam.›.

eng: Therefore, we must select one of those who have accompanied us during the whole time the Lord Jesus lived among us.
local: Kʋ nan maŋɩ sɩ nɔɔnʋ wǝli dɩbam Zezi tɩntʋŋna bam wʋnɩ o ji maana tu, sɩ o taa ŋɔɔnɩ dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi cɩga kam dɩ o na bi o yagɩ tʋʋnɩ te tɩn. Kʋntʋ tu nan maŋɩ sɩ o taa yɩ wʋlʋ dɛɛn na tɔgɩ o wʋ dɩbam kɔgɔ kʋm wʋnɩ maŋa maama dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi dɛɛn na tɔgɩ dɩ dɩbam o beeri tɩn mʋ, kʋ sɩŋɩ maŋa kam Zan na miisi Zezi na wʋnɩ tɩn, sɩ kʋ vu kʋ yi maŋa kam Zezi na yagɩ dɩbam te yɩ o di Wɛ-sɔŋɔ tɩn.».

eng: So they nominated two: Joseph called Barsabbas, who was also known as Justus, and Matthias.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ na tagɩ kʋntʋ o ti tɩn, ba ma lɩ nɔɔna bale. Dɩdʋa mʋ yɩ Zʋzɛfʋ wʋlʋ ba na bǝ nɩ Basaba yɩ ba daa ta bǝ-o nɩ Zusitu tɩn. Wʋdoŋ wʋm maa yɩ Matɩasɩ.

eng: They prayed, Lord, you know everyones deepest thoughts and desires. Show us clearly which one you have chosen from among these two.
local: Ba laan ma loori Wɛ ba wɩ: «Dɩ́ Yuutu, nmʋ maŋɩ n ye nɔɔnʋ maama wʋbʋŋa na yɩ te tɩn. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ dɩ́ loori nmʋ tee nɩ sɩ n brɩ dɩbam nɔɔna bantʋ bale wʋnɩ nmʋ na kuri wʋlʋ sɩ o ji nmʋ tɩntʋŋnʋ tɩn.

eng: to take the place of this ministry and apostleship, from which Judas turned away to go to his own place.
local: sɩ o taa tʋŋɩ tɩtʋŋa yalʋ Zudasɩ na kwe o yagɩ yɩ o daarɩ o vu o na maŋɩ sɩ o vu mɛ tɩn.».

eng: When they cast lots, the lot fell on Matthias. He was added to the eleven apostles.
local: Ba na loori Wɛ ba ti tɩn, ba ma ta jɔrɔ sɩ ba kuri nɔɔna bale bam kʋntʋ wʋnɩ. Ba na tagɩ ba kuri wʋlʋ tɩn mʋ yɩ Matɩasɩ. Ba laan ma pa o tɔgɩ o yɩ Zezi tɩntʋŋnʋ o wǝli ba fugǝ-dɩdʋa kam wʋnɩ.

eng: When Pentecost Day arrived, they were all together in one place.
local: Dɛ dɩm Zwifǝ bam na yǝni ba di ba candiǝ kalʋ yɩrɩ na yɩ Pantɩkoti tɩn ma ba ka yi. Zezi karabiǝ bam maama maa wʋ daanɩ jǝgǝ dɩdʋa.

eng: Suddenly a sound from heaven like the howling of a fierce wind filled the entire house where they were sitting.
local: Sɔɔ ma da kʋ nuŋi wɛyuu nɩ nɩnɛɛnɩ vu-dɩʋ na fufugi te tɩn. Sɔŋɔ kʋm ba na je kʋ wʋnɩ tɩn maama ma su dɩ sɔɔ kʋm kʋntʋ.

eng: They saw what seemed to be individual flames of fire alighting on each one of them.
local: Ba ma na wǝǝnu nɩ min-vwɩ te, yɩ tɩ pwɛ pwɛ tɩ jǝni ba dɩdʋa dɩdʋa yuu nɩ.

eng: They were all filled with the Holy Spirit and began to speak in other languages as the Spirit enabled them to speak.
local: Wɛ Joro kʋm ma ba ba maama tee nɩ dɩ dam, yɩ ba sɩŋɩ sɩ ba taa ŋɔɔnɩ taana dwi dwi, nɩnɛɛnɩ Wɛ Joro kʋm na pɛ-ba dam sɩ ba ŋɔɔnɩ taanɩ dɩlʋ tɩn.

eng: There were pious Jews from every nation under heaven living in Jerusalem.
local: Kantʋ maŋa kam nɩ, Zwifǝ zanzan dɛɛn mʋ tu ba wʋ tɩʋ kʋm nɩ. Ba maa yɩ balʋ na kwarɩ Wɛ lanyɩranɩ tɩn, yɩ ba nuŋi je maama mʋ lʋgʋ baŋa nɩ.

eng: When they heard this sound, a crowd gathered. They were mystified because everyone heard them speaking in their native languages.
local: Balʋ na wʋra tɩn dɛɛn ma ni sɔɔ kʋm na kɩ te tɩn, yɩ kɔgɔ zanzan laan ma ba kʋ la daanɩ jǝgǝ kam nɩ. Ba ma ni Zezi karabiǝ bam na zɩgɩ ba ŋɔɔnɩ taana dwi dwi. Kʋ ma sʋ-ba, bɛŋwaanɩ ba maama dɛɛn ni Zezi karabiǝ bam na ŋɔɔnɩ ba dɩdʋa dɩdʋa dwi taanɩ.

eng: They were surprised and amazed, saying, Look, arent all the people who are speaking Galileans, every one of them?.
local: Ba yɩra ma sɔɔrɩ yɩ kʋ kɩ-ba yǝǝu. Ba ma ta ba wɩ: «Nii, bantʋ maama yɩ Galile tiinǝ mʋ, yɩ ba zɩgɩ ba ŋɔɔnɩ taana yantʋ doŋ.

eng: How then can each of us hear them speaking in our native language?.
local: Kʋ nan kɩ ta mʋ yɩ dɩbam maama ni ba na ŋɔɔnɩ dɩbam dɩdʋa dɩdʋa dwi taanɩ?.

eng: Parthians, Medes, and Elamites; as well as residents of Mesopotamia, Judea, and Cappadocia, Pontus and Asia.
local: Dɩbam nuŋi je dwi tǝri tǝri mʋ. Dɩbam badonnǝ nuŋi Parɩtɩ dɩ Meedi dɩ Elam, kʋ wǝli dɩ Mesopotami dɩ Zude dɩ Kapadɔsɩ, dɩdaanɩ Pɔn dɩ Azi tɩɩnɩ dɩm maama.

eng: Phrygia and Pamphylia, Egypt and the regions of Libya bordering Cyrene; and visitors from Rome.
local: Dɩ́ badonnǝ dɩ ma nuŋi Friizi dɩ Pamfili dɩ Ezipi dɩ Liibi tɩɩnɩ dɩlʋ na batwarɩ dɩ Sirɛɛnɩ tɩn. Dɩ́ badonnǝ maa nuŋi Rom.

eng: Cretans and Arabs—we hear them declaring the mighty works of God in our own languages.
local: Dɩbam yɩ Zwifǝ mʋ, kʋ wǝli dɩ dwi-gɛ tiinǝ balʋ na tɔgɩ cullu tɩlʋ Wɛ na pɛ dɩbam Zwifǝ bam tɩn. Dɩbam badonnǝ maa nuŋi Krɛtɩ dɩ Arabi. Dɩ kʋntʋ dɩ, dɩbam maama nan ta ni ba na maɩ dɩbam dɩdʋa dɩdʋa dwi taanɩ ba ŋɔɔnɩ wo-kamunnu tɩlʋ Wɛ na kɩ tɩn.».

eng: They were all surprised and bewildered. Some asked each other, What does this mean.
local: Nɔɔna bam na yɩ yǝǝu yɩ ba wʋbʋŋa vugimi daanɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, ba maa bwe daanɩ ba wɩ: «Kǝm dɩntʋ kuri mʋ bɛɛ?».

eng: Others jeered at them, saying, Theyre full of new wine.
local: Ba badaara laan maa jɩgɩ Zezi tɩntʋŋna bam ba mwana, yɩ ba tɛ ba wɩ: «Nɔɔna bantʋ nyɔgɩ sana mʋ ba su.».

eng: Peter stood with the other eleven apostles. He raised his voice and declared, Judeans and everyone living in Jerusalem! Know this! Listen carefully to my words!.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ laan ma zaŋɩ wɛɛnɩ dɩdaanɩ o donnǝ fugǝ-dɩdʋa kam. O ma ŋɔɔnɩ dɩ kwǝr-dɩa o ta dɩ nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm o wɩ: «A badonnǝ-ba, Zwifǝ-ba dɩ abam balʋ maama na zʋʋrɩ Zeruzalɛm nɩ tɩn, cǝgi-na sɩ a ta kǝm dɩntʋ kuri a brɩ abam.

eng: These people arent drunk, as you suspect; after all, its only nine oclock in the morning!.
local: Abam na bʋŋɩ nɩ nɔɔna bantʋ nyɔgɩ sana mʋ ba su tɩn, kʋ nan daɩ kʋntʋ. Kʋ daa ta yɩ tɩtɩɩtɩ mʋ, wɩa ta wʋ bɩgɩ sɩ ba nyɔ sana.

eng: Rather, this is what was spoken through the prophet Joel.
local: Kʋ nan yɩ kʋlʋ Wɛ nijoŋnu Zʋwɛlɩ dɛɛn na ŋɔɔnɩ kʋ taanɩ tɩn mʋ.

eng: In the last days, God says, I will pour out my Spirit on all people. Your sons and daughters will prophesy. Your young will see visions. Your elders will dream dreams. .
local: O tagɩ sɩ Baŋa-Wɛ wɩ: ‹Lʋgʋ tiim maŋa na yiǝ, amʋ wʋ́ pwǝri a Joro kʋm sɩ kʋ taa wʋ nabiinǝ maama tee nɩ. Kʋntʋ nan wʋ́ pa abam biǝ dɩ á bukwa ŋɔɔnɩ amʋ yiyiu-ŋwɛ. Aá pa abam nɔn-dʋnnʋ na vɩɩrɩm, kʋ daarɩ sɩ á nankwɩn dwɛ dɩndwɩa.

eng: Even upon my servants, men and women, I will pour out my Spirit in those days, and they will prophesy. .
local: Kʋ yɩ cɩga sɩ da yam kʋntʋ nɩ, kʋ na yɩ a tɩntʋŋ-baara dɩ kaana dɩ, aá pwǝri a Joro kʋm a pa ba maama, sɩ ba ŋɔɔnɩ amʋ yiyiu-ŋwɛ.

eng: I will cause wonders to occur in the heavens above and signs on the earth below, blood and fire and a cloud of smoke. .
local: A ta wʋ́ pa wo-kɩnkagɩla taa kɩ wɛyuu nɩ, yɩ a daarɩ a kɩ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ na brɩ a dam tɩn tɩga baŋa nɩ. Kantʋ maŋa kam nɩ jana wʋ́ ta wʋra dɩ mini dɩ nyʋa kalʋ na tiini ka zwǝrǝ tɩn.

eng: The sun will be changed into darkness, and the moon will be changed into blood, before the great and spectacular day of the Lord comes. .
local: Wɩa kam wʋ́ ji lim, yɩ cana kam dɩ wʋ́ ji nasʋŋʋ nɩ jana te, kʋ na wʋ́ loori sɩ amʋ Baŋa-Wɛ sarɩya dɛ kamunu kʋlʋ na jɩgɩ zulǝ ba dɩ yi tɩn.

eng: And everyone who calls on the name of the Lord will be saved. .
local: Dɛ dɩm kʋntʋ nɩ, wʋlʋ maama na wʋ́ bǝŋi dɩ́ Yuutu yɩrɩ o ma loori zǝnǝ tɩn, oó na vrɩm.›.

eng: Fellow Israelites, listen to these words! Jesus the Nazarene was a man whose credentials God proved to you through miracles, wonders, and signs, which God performed through him among you. You yourselves know this.
local: A badonnǝ Yisɩrayɛlɩ tiinǝ-ba, cǝgi-na a na lagɩ a ta kʋlʋ tɩn. A lagɩ a ta Zezi wʋlʋ na yɩ Nazarɛtɩ tu tɩn taanɩ mʋ a brɩ abam. Baŋa-Wɛ tagɩ wʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ DƖ kɩ wo-kamunnu dɩ wo-kɩnkagɩla dɩ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ na brɩ DƖ dam tɩn, sɩ kʋ brɩ abam nɩ o sɩɩnɩ o nuŋi wʋm Baŋa-Wɛ te mʋ. Abam tɩtɩ maŋɩ á ye kʋntʋ, bɛŋwaanɩ wǝǝnu tɩm kʋntʋ maŋɩ tɩ kɩ abam tɩtarɩ nɩ.

eng: In accordance with Gods established plan and foreknowledge, he was betrayed. You, with the help of wicked men, had Jesus killed by nailing him to a cross.
local: Wɛ maŋɩ DƖ ye kʋ na wʋ́ ta yɩ te tɩn. DƖ dɛɛn ma lɩ wʋbʋŋa sɩ abam ja Zezi á kɩ nɔn-balwaarʋ jɩŋa nɩ, sɩ ba pa-o tʋʋn-dagara baŋa nɩ. Á kɩ kʋntʋ mʋ á ma á gʋ-o.

eng: God raised him up! God freed him from deaths dreadful grip, since it was impossible for death to hang on to him.
local: Wɛ nan mʋ bi-o DƖ pa o yagɩ tʋʋnɩ. DƖ ma joŋ-o tʋʋnɩ yaara yam wʋnɩ DƖ yagɩ. Bɛŋwaanɩ tʋʋnɩ dɛɛn wʋ wanɩ dɩ cǝŋ-o dɩ yagɩ yibeeli dɩm wʋnɩ.

eng: David says about him, I foresaw that the Lord was always with me; because he is at my right hand I wont be shaken. .
local: Faŋa faŋa tɩn, Pɛ Davidi dɛɛn tagɩ wʋntʋ taanɩ o wɩ: ‹A naɩ a Yuutu Wɛ a yigǝ nɩ maŋa maama. DƖ wʋ a jazɩm nɩ, sɩ DƖ zǝni-nɩ sɩ a yɩ tʋ.

eng: Therefore, my heart was glad and my tongue rejoiced. Moreover, my body will live in hope, .
local: Mʋ kʋ kuri a jɩgɩ wʋpolo lanyɩranɩ, yɩ a ni-taanɩ dɩ jɩgɩ ywǝǝni. Dɩ a yɩra bwǝni dɩm maama a nan ta jɩgɩ tɩɩna nmʋ Wɛ tee nɩ.

eng: because you wont abandon me to the grave, nor permit your holy one to experience decay. .
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ, a na tɩga, nmʋ Baŋa-Wɛ bá yagɩ-nɩ sɩ a maŋɩ curu nɩ. Nmʋ bá sɛ sɩ n tɩtɩ Wʋ-poŋo Tu wʋm yɩra pɔ.

eng: You have shown me the paths of life; your presence will fill me with happiness. .
local: Nmʋ pɛ a lwarɩ cwe sɩlʋ na yɩ ŋwɩa cwe tɩn sɩ a taa tɔga. Nmʋ wʋ́ pa a na ywǝǝni zanzan nmʋ tee nɩ.›.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I can speak confidently about the patriarch David. He died and was buried, and his tomb is with us to this very day.
local: A ko-biǝ-ba, mʋ kʋlʋ dɩbam nabaarʋ Davidi dɛɛn na tagɩ tɩn. A nan lagɩ a ŋɔɔnɩ wʋntʋ taanɩ mʋ jaja a brɩ abam nɩ o tɩga, yɩ ba kwe-o ba kɩ. O yibeeli ta wʋra dɩ zɩm maama.

eng: Because he was a prophet, he knew that God promised him with a solemn pledge to seat one of his descendants on his throne.
local: O dɛɛn nan yɩ Wɛ nijoŋnu mʋ, yɩ o guli Wɛ na goni ni DƖ pa-o DƖ wɩ, DƖ wʋ́ pa o dwi tiinǝ bam dɩdʋa zaŋɩ o di paarɩ o jǝŋǝ kam je nɩ.

eng: Having seen this beforehand, David spoke about the resurrection of Christ, that he wasnt abandoned to the grave, nor did his body experience decay .
local: Davidi nan maŋɩ o da yigǝ o lwarɩ kʋlʋ Wɛ na wʋ́ kɩ tɩn. Kʋ yɩ Krisi wʋm Wɛ na tʋŋɩ tɩn tʋʋnɩ biim woŋo mʋ o tagɩ o wɩ: ‹Wɛ wʋ sɛ sɩ DƖ yag-o curu nɩ. O yɩra yam dɩ nan wʋ pɔgɩ.›.

eng: This Jesus God raised up. We are all witnesses to that fact.
local: Cɩga tɩn, Baŋa-Wɛ mʋ joori DƖ pa Zezi wʋlʋ a ya na tagɩ tɩn ŋwɩa, yɩ dɩbam maama yɩ maana tiinǝ dɩ́ brɩ nɔɔna cɩga kam kʋntʋ.

eng: He was exalted to Gods right side and received from the Father the promised Holy Spirit. He poured out this Spirit, and you are seeing and hearing the results of his having done so.
local: Wɛ na bi-o tɩn, DƖ zǝŋ-o DƖ jǝni DƖ jazɩm nɩ. Zezi laan ma joŋi Wɛ Joro kʋm o Ko Wɛ tee nɩ, nɩ Wɛ na maŋɩ DƖ go ni sɩ DƖ wʋ́ pa-o te tɩn, yɩ o pwǝri-kʋ dɩbam baŋa nɩ. Mʋ kʋlʋ abam zɩm na naɩ yɩ á kwǝri á ni tɩn.

eng: David didnt ascend into heaven. Yet he says, The Lord said to my Lord, Sit at my right side, .
local: Kʋ daɩ Davidi tɩtɩ mʋ diini Wɛ-sɔŋɔ. Wʋntʋ nan tagɩ o wɩ: ‹Yuutu Baŋa-Wɛ tagɩ dɩ a Yuutu DƖ wɩ: Ba n jǝni a jazɩm nɩ.

eng: until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet. .
local: sɩ aá kɩ nmʋ dʋna maama n nɛ kuri nɩ, sɩ n nɔ-ba nɩ tɩga te.›.

eng: Therefore, let all Israel know beyond question that God has made this Jesus, whom you crucified, both Lord and Christ.
local: Kʋntʋ tɩn, Yisɩrayɛlɩ dwi maama nan maŋɩ sɩ ba lwarɩ lanyɩranɩ nɩ, Zezi wʋlʋ abam na pagɩ tʋʋn-dagara baŋa nɩ á gʋ tɩn, kʋ yɩ wʋntʋ tɩtɩ nɔ́ɔ mʋ Wɛ laan pɛ sɩ o taa yɩ Krisi wʋm, yɩ o kwǝri o yɩ dɩbam Yuutu.».

eng: When the crowd heard this, they were deeply troubled. They said to Peter and the other apostles, Brothers, what should we do.
local: Nɔɔna bam na ni Pɩyɛɛrɩ na tagɩ kʋlʋ kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ ma tiini kʋ cɔgɩ ba wʋrʋ zanzan. Ba ma bwe Pɩyɛɛrɩ dɩ Zezi tɩntʋŋna badonnǝ bam ba wɩ: «Dɩ́ ko-biǝ-ba, dɩ́ nan wʋ́ kɩ ta mʋ?».

eng: Peter replied, Change your hearts and lives. Each of you must be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins. Then you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Abam maama maŋɩ sɩ á lǝni á wʋrʋ á yagɩ kǝm-balwaarʋ tɩm, sɩ á daarɩ á pa ba miisi abam na wʋnɩ Zezi Krisi yɩrɩ ŋwaanɩ, sɩ Wɛ wʋ́ yagɩ á lwarɩm DƖ ma cɛ abam. Wɛ laan wʋ́ pa DƖ Joro kʋm taa wʋ abam tee nɩ.

eng: This promise is for you, your children, and for all who are far away—as many as the Lord our God invites.
local: Mʋ Wɛ na goni ni sɩ DƖ kɩ te tɩn. DƖ goni ni dɩntʋ DƖ pa abam dɩ á biǝ, kʋ wǝli dɩ balʋ maama na baŋwɛ tɩn. Kʋ yɩ balʋ maama dɩbam Yuutu Baŋa-Wɛ na wʋ́ bǝŋi sɩ ba taa tɔg-o tɩn mʋ.».

eng: With many other words he testified to them and encouraged them, saying, Be saved from this perverse generation.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ ma joori o fɔgɩ o ma bɩtarɩ dwi dwi o brɩ-ba Wɛ cɩga kam, yɩ o kaanɩ-ba o wɩ: «Sɛ-na sɩ Baŋa-Wɛ vrɩ abam zɩm nɔn-balwaarʋ tɩm tɩtarɩ nɩ.».

eng: Those who accepted Peters message were baptized. God brought about three thousand people into the community on that day.
local: Nɔɔna bam zanzan ma sɛ o taanɩ dɩm, yɩ ba daarɩ ba pa ba miisi-ba na wʋnɩ Wɛ ŋwaanɩ. Dɛ dɩm kʋntʋ nɩ nɔɔna zanzan mʋ tu ba zʋ ba wǝli Zezi karabiǝ kɔgɔ kʋm wʋnɩ, ba yi nɩnɛɛnɩ nɔɔna mʋrr-tɩtɔ te.

eng: The believers devoted themselves to the apostles teaching, to the community, to their shared meals, and to their prayers.
local: Ba ma tiini ba kwaanɩ ba pa Zezi tɩntʋŋna bam brɩ-ba Wɛ cwǝŋǝ kam. Ba maama maa wǝli daanɩ dɩ ni dɩdʋa, yɩ ba kwǝri ba tɔgɩ daanɩ ba di wʋdiu nɩ Zezi dɛɛn na fɔgɩ dɩpɛ dɩm o pa o karabiǝ bam yɩ ba di te tɩn. Ba ma kwǝri ba tɔgɩ daanɩ ba warɩ Wɛ maŋa maama.

eng: A sense of awe came over everyone. God performed many wonders and signs through the apostles.
local: Fʋʋnɩ ma zʋ nɔɔna bam maama, bɛŋwaanɩ Wɛ dɛɛn pɛ Zezi tɩntʋŋna bam kɩ wo-kɩnkagɩla dɩ wǝǝnu zanzan na brɩ Baŋa-Wɛ dam tɩn.

eng: All the believers were united and shared everything.
local: Zezi karabiǝ bam maama dɛɛn yǝni ba wʋ jǝgǝ dɩdʋa mʋ, yɩ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ ba na jɩgɩ tɩn yɩ ba maama nyɩm.

eng: They would sell pieces of property and possessions and distribute the proceeds to everyone who needed them.
local: Ba maa yǝni ba kwe ba jɩjɩgɩrʋ dɩ ba zɩla ba yǝgǝ, yɩ ba maŋɩ tɩ sǝbu kʋm ba pa daanɩ, kʋ maŋɩ nɩ nɔɔnʋ maama na lagɩ zǝnǝ te tɩn.

eng: Every day, they met together in the temple and ate in their homes. They shared food with gladness and simplicity.
local: Dɛ maama ba yǝni ba la daanɩ Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm wʋnɩ. Ba ma tɔgɩ daanɩ ba di wʋdiu da sam nɩ, kʋ nyɩ dɩ Zezi dɛɛn na fɔgɩ dɩpɛ o pa karabiǝ bam te tɩn. Ba maa maŋɩ ba wʋdiiru ba pa daanɩ, yɩ ba jɩgɩ wʋpolo lanyɩranɩ dɩdaanɩ wʋbʋŋ-dɩdwɩ.

eng: They praised God and demonstrated Gods goodness to everyone. The Lord added daily to the community those who were being saved.
local: Ba maa zuli Wɛ maŋa maama, yɩ ba jɩgɩ zulǝ nɔɔna maama yibiyǝ nɩ. Dɛ maama dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm maa paɩ balʋ na nɛ vrɩm tɩn tui ba wǝli ba kɔgɔ kʋm wʋnɩ.

eng: Later, Jesus himself appeared again to his disciples at the Sea of Tiberias. This is how it happened.
local: Kʋntʋ kwaga nɩ Zezi daa ma joori o brɩ o tɩtɩ dɩ o karabiǝ bam Tiberiyadɩ nɩnɩʋ kʋm ni nɩ. O na brɩ o tɩtɩ te tɩn mʋ tɩntʋ:.

eng: Simon Peter, Thomas , Nathanael from Cana in Galilee, Zebedees sons, and two other disciples were together.
local: Simɔn Pɩyɛɛrɩ dɛɛn wʋra, dɩ Toma wʋlʋ ba na bǝ nɩ Yiywɛ tɩn. Natanayɛlɩ dɩ wʋra, wʋlʋ na nuŋi Galile tɩʋ Kana tɩn, dɩdaanɩ Zebede biǝ bam dɩ Zezi karabiǝ badonnǝ bale. Bantʋ bam maama mʋ wʋ daanɩ.

eng: Simon Peter told them, Im going fishing. They said, Well go with you. They set out in a boat, but throughout the night they caught nothing.
local: Simɔn Pɩyɛɛrɩ ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «A maa ve a ja kale mʋ.» Ba ma lǝr-o ba wɩ: «Dɩ́ wʋ́ tɔgɩ dɩ nmʋ dɩ́ vu.» Ba ma zaŋɩ ba vu ba zʋ naboro kʋm. Tɩtɩɩ dɩm kʋntʋ nɩ ba ma kwaanɩ sɩ ba ja kale, yɩ ba wʋ nɛ kʋlʋkʋlʋ ba ja.

eng: Early in the morning, Jesus stood on the shore, but the disciples didnt realize it was Jesus.
local: Tɩga na wʋra ka pʋʋrɩ tɩn, Zezi maa zɩgɩ nɩnɩʋ kʋm ni nɩ, yɩ ba wʋ lwar-o.

eng: Jesus called to them, Children, have you caught anything to eat? They answered him, No.
local: O ma bǝŋi-ba o wɩ: «A badonnǝ-ba, abam wʋ jaanɩ kale na?» Ba ma lǝr-o ba wɩ: «Dɩ́ wʋ nɛ kʋlʋkʋlʋ.».

eng: He said, Cast your net on the right side of the boat and you will find some. So they did, and there were so many fish that they couldnt haul in the net.
local: O laan ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Nan dɩ-na á bʋra kam naboro kʋm jazɩm seeni, sɩ á wʋ́ ja.» Ba ma sɩɩnɩ ba dɩ bʋra kam, yɩ ba daa warɩ ba vaŋɩ-ka ba lɩ ba dɩ boro kʋm wʋnɩ, kale sɩm na tiini sɩ dagɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: Then the disciple whom Jesus loved said to Peter, Its the Lord! When Simon Peter heard it was the Lord, he wrapped his coat around himself and jumped into the water.
local: Zezi karabu wʋlʋ o na soe tɩn ma ta dɩ Pɩyɛɛrɩ o wɩ: «Dɩbam Yuutu wʋm mʋ.» Simɔn Pɩyɛɛrɩ na lwarɩ nɩ kʋ yɩ ba Yuutu wʋm kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma kwe o gɔrɔ kʋlʋ o ya na lɩ tɩn o zʋ, yɩ o laan daarɩ o faŋɩ o cu na bam wʋnɩ.

eng: The other disciples followed in the boat, dragging the net full of fish, for they werent far from shore, only about one hundred yards.
local: Karabiǝ badaara bam ma ja naboro kʋm ba ba, yɩ ba vaŋɩ bʋra kam dɩ kale sɩm ba wǝli da ba yi na bam ni, ba na daɩ yigǝ yigǝ dɩ buburu kʋm tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Kʋ dɛɛn yɩ nɩnɛɛnɩ mɛtra bi mʋ te.

eng: When they landed, they saw a fire there, with fish on it, and some bread.
local: Ba na bɛ bubɛ dɩm tɩn, ba ma na min-cala na wʋra, yɩ kale wʋ ya yuu nɩ. Dɩpɛ dɩ maa tigi da.

eng: Jesus said to them, Bring some of the fish that youve just caught.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Twǝri-na kale sɩlʋ á na jaanɩ lele tɩn sɩdaara á ja á ba.».

eng: Simon Peter got up and pulled the net to shore. It was full of large fish, one hundred fifty-three of them. Yet the net hadnt torn, even with so many fish.
local: Simɔn Pɩyɛɛrɩ laan ma vu o zʋ naboro kʋm wʋ o vaŋɩ bʋra kam o dɩ tɩga nɩ. Ka su dɩ kalǝŋ-kamunǝ mʋ, sɩ maama yɩ bi dɩ fiinnu sɩtɔ mʋ . Dɩ kale sɩm na daga kʋntʋ tɩn dɩ, bʋra kam wʋ kaarɩ.

eng: Jesus said to them, Come and have breakfast. None of the disciples could bring themselves to ask him, Who are you? They knew it was the Lord.
local: Zezi ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Ba-na á gʋ á ni-cɛɛrɩ.» O karabiǝ bam maama ma kwarɩ sɩ ba bwe-o ba nii nɩ o yɩ wɔɔ mʋ, bɛŋwaanɩ ba maŋɩ ba lwarɩ nɩ kʋ yɩ ba Yuutu wʋm mʋ.

eng: Jesus came, took the bread, and gave it to them. He did the same with the fish.
local: Zezi ma zaŋɩ o vu o kwe dɩpɛ dɩm o pa-ba, yɩ o daarɩ o kwe kale sɩm dɩ o pa-ba.

eng: This was now the third time Jesus appeared to his disciples after he was raised from the dead.
local: Zezi na bi o yagɩ tʋʋnɩ tɩn, kʋntʋ mʋ kɩ kuni bɩtɔ o na brɩ o tɩtɩ dɩ o karabiǝ bam.

eng: When they finished eating, Jesus asked Simon Peter, Simon son of John, do you love me more than these? Simon replied, Yes, Lord, you know I love you. Jesus said to him, Feed my lambs.
local: Ba na di ba ti tɩn, Zezi laan ma bwe Simɔn Pɩyɛɛrɩ o wɩ: «Zan bu Simɔn, nmʋ soe amʋ n dwe bantʋ na?» O ma lǝri o wɩ: «A Yuutu, ɛɛn, nmʋ ye nɩ amʋ soe nmʋ.» Zezi ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Nan ta n nii a pǝlbiǝ bam baŋa nɩ.».

eng: Jesus asked a second time, Simon son of John, do you love me? Simon replied, Yes, Lord, you know I love you. Jesus said to him, Take care of my sheep.
local: O ma joori o bwe Pɩyɛɛrɩ o kɩ bɩle o wɩ: «Zan bu Simɔn, nmʋ soe amʋ na?» O ma lǝri o wɩ: «A Yuutu, ɛɛn, nmʋ ye nɩ amʋ soe nmʋ.» Zezi ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Nan ta n nii amʋ peeni sɩm baŋa nɩ.».

eng: He asked a third time, Simon son of John, do you love me? Peter was sad that Jesus asked him a third time, Do you love me? He replied, Lord, you know everything; you know I love you. Jesus said to him, Feed my sheep.
local: O daa ma bwe Pɩyɛɛrɩ o kɩ kuni bɩtɔ o wɩ: «Zan bu Simɔn, nmʋ soe amʋ na?» Pɩyɛɛrɩ wʋ ma cɔgɩ dɩ Zezi na bwe-o kuni bɩtɔ o wɩ o so-o na tɩn. O ma ta dɩ Zezi o wɩ: «Amʋ Yuutu, nmʋ ye woŋo maama. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ nmʋ ye nɩ amʋ soe nmʋ.» Zezi laan ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Nan ta n nii amʋ peeni sɩm baŋa nɩ.

eng: I assure you that when you were younger you tied your own belt and walked around wherever you wanted. When you grow old, you will stretch out your hands and another will tie your belt and lead you where you dont want to go.
local: A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ nmʋ sɩ, nmʋ dɛɛn na yɩ nɔn-dʋŋʋ tɩn, nmʋ yǝni n vɔ n kilǝ n tɩtɩ nɩ mʋ lanyɩranɩ, yɩ n daarɩ n ve nmʋ na lagɩ mɛ tɩn. Kʋ daarɩ nmʋ nan na kwɩn, n laan wʋ́ zǝŋi n jɩa mʋ wɛɛnɩ, yɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ vɔ-ka o pa-m, yɩ o daarɩ o ja-m o vu nmʋ na ba lagɩ mɛ sɩ n vu tɩn.».

eng: He said this to show the kind of death by which Peter would glorify God. After saying this, Jesus said to Peter, Follow me.
local: Zezi dɛɛn tagɩ kʋntʋ sɩ kʋ brɩ Pɩyɛɛrɩ na wʋ́ tɩ tʋʋnɩ dɩlʋ dwi sɩ kʋ pa Wɛ na zulǝ tɩn mʋ. O laan ma daarɩ o ta dɩ Pɩyɛɛrɩ o wɩ: «Ta n tɔgɩ-nɩ.».

eng: Peter turned around and saw the disciple whom Jesus loved following them. This was the one who had leaned against Jesus at the meal and asked him, Lord, who is going to betray you.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ ma pipiri o nii o kwaga nɩ, yɩ o na Zezi karabu wʋlʋ o na soe tɩn na tɔgɩ-ba. Wʋntʋ mʋ yɩ wʋlʋ ya na jǝni o salɩ Zezi wʋdiu kʋm dim maŋa nɩ yɩ o bwe-o o wɩ: «A Yuutu, wɔɔ mʋ lagɩ o yǝgi nmʋ o pa?» tɩn.

eng: When Peter saw this disciple, he said to Jesus, Lord, what about him.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ na nɛ-o tɩn, o ma bwe Zezi o wɩ: «A Yuutu, sɩ wʋntʋ, bɛɛ mʋ lagɩ kʋ kɩ-o?».

eng: Jesus replied, If I want him to remain until I come, what difference does that make to you? You must follow me.
local: Zezi ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «Amʋ na lagɩ sɩ o taa ŋwɩ sɩ kʋ taa ve maŋa kam amʋ na wʋ́ joori a ba tɩn, kʋntʋ daɩ nmʋ yigǝ. Nmʋ za n weeni n ta n tɔgɩ-nɩ.».

eng: Therefore, the word spread among the brothers and sisters that this disciple wouldnt die. However, Jesus didnt say he wouldnt die, but only, If I want him to remain until I come, what difference does that make to you.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ pɛ Zezi karabiǝ bam dɛɛn jɩgɩ-ka ba ŋɔɔnɩ ba wɩ, Zezi karabu wʋm kʋntʋ bá tɩ maŋa dɩ maŋa. Zezi nan wʋ tagɩ o wɩ, o bá tɩ. O dɛɛn tagɩ o wɩ: «Amʋ na lagɩ sɩ o taa ŋwɩ sɩ kʋ taa ve maŋa kam a na wʋ́ joori a ba tɩn, kʋntʋ daɩ nmʋ yigǝ.».

eng: This is the disciple who testifies concerning these things and who wrote them down. We know that his testimony is true.
local: Zezi karabu wʋlʋ o na tagɩ o taanɩ kʋntʋ tɩn mʋ yɩ amʋ wʋlʋ na brɩ Wɛ cɩga kam yɩ a daarɩ a pʋpʋnɩ-ka a tiŋi tɩn. Dɩbam maama nan ye nɩ a na brɩ te tɩn sɩɩnɩ kʋ yɩ cɩga mʋ.

eng: Jesus did many other things as well. If all of them were recorded, I imagine the world itself wouldnt have enough room for the scrolls that would be written.
local: Zezi ta kɩ wǝǝnu zanzan o wǝli da. Tɩ maama dɩdʋa dɩdʋa nan ya na lagɩ tɩ pʋpʋnɩ tɩ tiŋi, a bʋŋɩ nɩ lʋgʋ baŋa ya bá na je sɩ twaanʋ tɩm kʋntʋ pǝni da.

eng: While Peter and John were speaking to the people, the priests, the captain of the temple guard, and the Sadducees confronted them.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ dɩ Zan dɛɛn na wʋra ba ŋɔɔnɩ kʋntʋ doŋ ba brɩ nɔɔna bam tɩn, Zwifǝ bam kaanɩm tiinǝ dɩdaanɩ Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm yɩrɩna bam yigǝ tu wʋm dɩ Sadusɩan tiinǝ badonnǝ ma zaŋɩ ba ba ba te.

eng: They were incensed that the apostles were teaching the people and announcing that the resurrection of the dead was happening because of Jesus.
local: Ba bana tiini ya zaŋɩ dɩ Pɩyɛɛrɩ dɩ Zan dɩ ba na ŋɔɔnɩ te ba wɩ, Zezi bi o yagɩ tʋʋnɩ, yɩ kʋntʋ brɩ nɩ twa dɩ wʋ́ wanɩ ba bi ba yagɩ tʋʋnɩ tɩn.

eng: They seized Peter and John and put them in prison until the next day.
local: Ba ma ja Pɩyɛɛrɩ dɩ Zan ba kɩ pɩɩna digǝ nɩ. Tɩga na yi tɩn ŋwaanɩ, ba ma yagɩ-ba da sɩ tɩga pʋʋrɩ.

eng: Many who heard the word became believers, and their number grew to about five thousand.
local: Kʋ daarɩ kɔgɔ kʋm nɔɔna zanzan ma sɛ taanɩ dɩlʋ ba na ni tɩn, yɩ ba kɩ ba wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Zezi. Balʋ na sɛ tɩn kɔgɔ kʋm dɛɛn yɩ nɩnɛɛnɩ nɔɔna mʋrr-tɩnu .

eng: The next day the leaders, elders, and legal experts gathered in Jerusalem.
local: Tɩga na pʋʋrɩ tɩn, Zwifǝ yigǝ tiinǝ bam dɩ ba nakwa bam dɩ ba cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ bam ma la daanɩ Zeruzalɛm nɩ.

eng: along with Annas the high priest, Caiaphas, John, Alexander, and others from the high priests family.
local: Ba ma jeeri dɩdaanɩ Annɩ wʋlʋ na yɩ ba kaanɩm yuutu tɩn, dɩ Kayifu dɩ Zan dɩ Alɛsandrɩ, dɩ balʋ na tɔgɩ ba yɩ kaanɩm yuutu wʋm sɔŋɔ tiinǝ tɩn.

eng: They had Peter and John brought before them and asked, By what power or in what name did you do this.
local: Ba ma pa Pɩyɛɛrɩ dɩ Zan ba ba zɩgɩ ba yigǝ nɩ. Ba ma bwe-ba ba wɩ: «Kʋ yɩ dɩ bɛɛ dam mʋ, dɩ wɔɔ yɩrɩ ŋwaanɩ mʋ, abam mɛ á kɩ kʋntʋ?».

eng: Then Peter, inspired by the Holy Spirit, answered, Leaders of the people and elders.
local: Wɛ Joro kʋm laan ma ba Pɩyɛɛrɩ te dɩ dam. O ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Tɩʋ kʋm yigǝ tiinǝ dɩ nakwa-ba.

eng: are we being examined today because something good was done for a sick person, a good deed that healed him?.
local: abam zɩm na bwe dɩbam kʋlʋ taanɩ tɩn, kʋ na yɩ dɩbam na zǝni kɔrɔ kʋm yɩ kʋ na yazurǝ tɩn.

eng: If so, then you and all the people of Israel need to know that this man stands healthy before you because of the name of Jesus Christ the Nazarene—whom you crucified but whom God raised from the dead.
local: dɩ́ wʋ́ pa abam dɩ Yisɩrayɛlɩ dwi tiinǝ bam maama lwarɩ kʋ na kɩ te. Á taá ye nɩ Nazarɛtɩ tu Zezi Krisi yɩrɩ dam ŋwaanɩ mʋ pɛ nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ na yazurǝ, yɩ o laan ba o zɩgɩ abam yigǝ nɩ. Abam nan pagɩ Zezi Krisi tʋʋn-dagara baŋa nɩ á gʋ, yɩ Wɛ joori DƖ bi-o DƖ pa o yagɩ tʋʋnɩ.

eng: This Jesus is the stone you builders rejected; he has become the cornerstone!.
local: Wʋntʋ mʋ yɩ wʋlʋ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm na maŋɩ kʋ ta o taanɩ nɩ: ‹Kandwɛ dɩlʋ abam lwara bam na vɩn tɩn laan mʋ joori dɩ ba dɩ ji kandwɛ dɩlʋ na paɩ sɔŋɔ kʋm dana tɩn.›.

eng: Salvation can be found in no one else. Throughout the whole world, no other name has been given among humans through which we must be saved.
local: Yɩrɩ dɩdoŋ daa tǝri lʋgʋ baŋa nɩ Wɛ na pɛ nabiinǝ sɩ dɩ wanɩ dɩ pa ba na vrɩm. Kʋ na daɩ Zezi Krisi yɩranɩ mʋ waɩ o vrɩ nɔɔna o yaga.».

eng: The council was caught by surprise by the confidence with which Peter and John spoke. After all, they understood that these apostles were uneducated and inexperienced. They also recognized that they had been followers of Jesus.
local: Zwifǝ nakwa bam na nɛ Pɩyɛɛrɩ dɩ Zan na jɩgɩ baarɩ dɩ ba na ŋɔɔnɩ te tɩn, kʋ ma kɩ-ba yǝǝu dɩ ba na yɩ nɔɔna balʋ na yǝri kʋlʋkʋlʋ yɩ ba daɩ yi-pʋrɩ-nyɩna tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Ba ma lwarɩ nɩ bantʋ dɛɛn tɔgɩ ba wʋ dɩ Zezi mʋ.

eng: However, since the healed man was standing with Peter and John before their own eyes, they had no rebuttal.
local: Ba na nɛ kɔrɔ kʋm na zɩgɩ Pɩyɛɛrɩ dɩ Zan tee nɩ yɩ kʋ jɩgɩ yazurǝ tɩn, ba maa warɩ kʋlʋkʋlʋ sɩ ba daa ta.

eng: After ordering them to wait outside, the council members began to confer with each other.
local: Ba ma ta dɩ ba ba wɩ, ba nuŋi da ba taanɩ dim jǝgǝ kam nɩ sɩ ba daarɩ. Ba laan maa wʋra ba bwɛ daanɩ, sɩ ba nii ba na wʋ́ kɩ te tɩn.

eng: What should we do with these men? Everyone living in Jerusalem is aware of the sign performed through them. Its obvious to everyone and we cant deny it.
local: Ba ma bwe daanɩ ba wɩ: «Dɩ́ nan wʋ́ kɩ nɔɔna bantʋ ta mʋ? Nɔɔna balʋ maama na zʋʋrɩ Zeruzalɛm nɩ tɩn lwarɩ nɩ bantʋ mʋ kɩ wo-kɩnkagɩlɩ dɩm kʋntʋ. Dɩ́ daa bá wanɩ dɩ́ ta nɩ vwan.

eng: To keep it from spreading further among the people, we need to warn them not to speak to anyone in this name.
local: Dɩ́ nan na lagɩ sɩ kǝm dɩntʋ yɩ jagɩ dɩ vu yigǝ tɩn, dɩ́ maŋɩ sɩ dɩ́ kaanɩ nɔɔna bam mʋ, sɩ ba daa yɩ ŋɔɔnɩ Zezi woŋo dɩ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ.».

eng: When they called Peter and John back, they demanded that they stop all speaking and teaching in the name of Jesus.
local: Ba laan ma joori ba bǝŋi-ba, yɩ ba zʋ. Ba ma ta dɩ ba ba wɩ, ba ba jɩgɩ cwǝŋǝ sɩ ba daa ŋɔɔnɩ Zezi woŋo, naa sɩ ba taa brɩ nɔɔna dɩ wʋntʋ yɩrɩ.

eng: Peter and John responded, Its up to you to determine whether its right before God to obey you rather than God.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ dɩ Zan ma lǝri-ba ba wɩ: «Abam tɩtɩ bʋŋɩ-na á nii. Kɔɔ mʋ lana Wɛ yigǝ nɩ? Kʋ maŋɩ sɩ dɩ́ sɛ abam dɩ́ dwǝni Baŋa-Wɛ na?.

eng: As for us, we cant stop speaking about what we have seen and heard.
local: Dɩbam nan bá wanɩ dɩ́ yagɩ kʋlʋ dɩ́ na nɛ yɩ dɩ́ kwǝri dɩ́ ni tɩn ŋwaŋa.».

eng: They threatened them further, then released them. Because of public support for Peter and John, they couldnt find a way to punish them. Everyone was praising God for what had happened.
local: Zwifǝ nakwa bam laan ma fɔgɩ ba kaanɩ-ba zanzan, yɩ ba daarɩ ba yagɩ-ba ba pa ba viiri. Ba dɛɛn wʋ nɛ kʋlʋ kʋm ba na wʋ́ vaŋɩ ba zwa kʋ baŋa nɩ tɩn, bɛŋwaanɩ tɩʋ kʋm nɔɔna maama tiini ba zuli Wɛ dɩ woŋo kʋlʋ na kɩ tɩn.

eng: because the man who had experienced this sign of healing was over years old.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ wo-kɩnkagɩlɩ dɩm na kɩ o baŋa nɩ yɩ o na yazurǝ tɩn dwe bɩna fiinna o ya na yɩ kɔrɔ.

eng: After their release, Peter and John returned to the brothers and sisters and reported everything the chief priests and elders had said.
local: Ba na yagɩ Pɩyɛɛrɩ dɩ Zan kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma joori ba vu ba kɔgɔ kʋm te. Ba ma ta dɩ ba Zwifǝ kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam dɩ ba tɩʋ kʋm nakwa bam na tagɩ kʋlʋ tɩn.

eng: They listened, then lifted their voices in unison to God, Master, you are the one who created the heaven, the earth, the sea, and everything in them.
local: Ba na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, ba maama ma wǝli daanɩ ba warɩ Wɛ yɩ ba wɩ: «Yuutu Baŋa-Wɛ, nmʋ mʋ kɩ wɛyuu dɩ tɩga baŋa dɩ na-fara dɩ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ maama na zʋʋrɩ tɩ wʋnɩ tɩn.

eng: You are the one who spoke by the Holy Spirit through our ancestor David, your servant: Why did the Gentiles rage , and the peoples plot in vain? .
local: Dɩ́ nabaarʋ Davidi dɛɛn mʋ yɩ nmʋ tɩntʋŋnʋ. Nmʋ dɛɛn ma pa n Joro kʋm pa o ta nmʋ kwǝrǝ kam o wɩ: ‹Bɛɛ mʋ yɩ lʋgʋ baŋa tiinǝ dwi tǝri tǝri bana zaŋɩ yɩ ba bʋŋɩ wʋbʋŋ-yɔɔrʋ?.

eng: The kings of the earth took their stand and the rulers gathered together as one against the Lord and against his Christ. .
local: Ba pwa bam ti ba yigǝ sɩ ba kɩ jara. Ba yum tiinǝ dɩ ma la daanɩ sɩ ba kɩ jara dɩdaanɩ Baŋa-Wɛ dɩ Krisi wʋm DƖ na tʋŋɩ tɩn.›.

eng: Indeed, both Herod and Pontius Pilate, with Gentiles and Israelites, did gather in this city against your holy servant Jesus, whom you anointed.
local: Kʋ sɩɩnɩ kʋ kɩ kʋntʋ mʋ. Tɩʋ kʋntʋ nɩ mʋ Erɔdɩ dɩ Pɔnsɩ Pilatɩ jǝni daanɩ dɩ Yisɩrayɛlɩ tiinǝ dɩ dwi-gɛ tiinǝ, sɩ ba ja dɩ nmʋ tɩntʋŋ-ŋʋm Zezi wʋlʋ nmʋ na kuri tɩn.

eng: They did what your power and plan had already determined would happen.
local: Ba na bwɛ daanɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, ba kɩ kʋ maŋɩ dɩ nmʋ na maŋɩ n lɩ wʋbʋŋa dɩ n dam dɩm sɩ kʋlʋ wʋ́ kɩ tɩn mʋ.

eng: Now, Lord, take note of their threats and enable your servants to speak your word with complete confidence.
local: Dɩ́ Yuutu Wɛ, nii ba na lagɩ ba yaarɩ dɩbam te tɩn! Dɩbam nan yɩ nmʋ tɩntʋŋna mʋ. Nan pa dɩbam dam, sɩ dɩ́ wanɩ dɩ́ taá tɔɔlɩ nmʋ kwǝrǝ kam dɩ baarɩ.

eng: Stretch out your hand to bring healing and enable signs and wonders to be performed through the name of Jesus, your holy servant.
local: Nan twɩ n jɩ-dɩa kam n pa nɔɔna taa naɩ yazurǝ, sɩ n pa wo-kɩnkagɩla dɩ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ na brɩ n dam tɩn taa kɩ nmʋ tɩntʋŋ-ŋʋm Zezi yɩrɩ dɩm ŋwaanɩ.».

eng: After they prayed, the place where they were gathered was shaken. They were all filled with the Holy Spirit and began speaking Gods word with confidence.
local: Ba na warɩ Wɛ ba ti tɩn, jǝgǝ kam ba na lagɩ daanɩ mɛ tɩn ma sisiŋǝ. Wɛ Joro kʋm laan ma ba ba maama te dɩ dam, yɩ ba ŋɔɔnɩ Wɛ kwǝrǝ kam dɩ baarɩ.

eng: The community of believers was one in heart and mind. None of them would say, This is mine! about any of their possessions, but held everything in common.
local: Zezi karabiǝ bam kɔgɔ kʋm maama wʋbʋŋa dɩ ba ŋwɩa maama dɛɛn yɩ bɩdwɩ mʋ. Ba wʋlʋwʋlʋ wʋ tagɩ nɩ wʋm yɩranɩ mʋ te o wǝǝnu. Wǝǝnu tɩlʋ ba na jɩgɩ tɩn yɩ ba maama nyɩm mʋ.

eng: The apostles continued to bear powerful witness to the resurrection of the Lord Jesus, and an abundance of grace was at work among them all.
local: Zezi tɩntʋŋna bam maa jɩgɩ dam kamunu, yɩ ba fɔgɩ ba brɩ dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi cɩga kam nɩ o sɩɩnɩ o bi o yagɩ tʋʋnɩ. Wɛ ma zǝni ba maama dɩ DƖ zaanɩ dɩm lanyɩranɩ.

eng: There were no needy persons among them. Those who owned properties or houses would sell them, bring the proceeds from the sales.
local: Nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ tǝri ba kɔgɔ kʋm wʋnɩ o na yɩ yinigǝ tu. Balʋ na jɩgɩ karɩ naa sam tɩn yǝni ba yǝgi-tɩ mʋ.

eng: and place them in the care and under the authority of the apostles. Then it was distributed to anyone who was in need.
local: yɩ ba daarɩ ba kwe sǝbu kʋm ba ja ba ba pa Zezi tɩntʋŋna bam. Ba laan ma maŋɩ sǝbu kʋm ba pa daanɩ, kʋ maŋɩ dɩ wʋlʋ maama na lagɩ zǝnǝ te tɩn.

eng: Joseph, whom the apostles nicknamed Barnabas , was a Levite from Cyprus.
local: Kʋntʋ, Zʋzɛfʋ wʋlʋ ba na maa bǝ nɩ Banabasɩ, kʋ kuri mʋ kwiǝ tu, yɩ o yɩ Leviti tu wʋlʋ na nuŋi Sipri tɩn.

eng: He owned a field, sold it, brought the money, and placed it in the care and under the authority of the apostles.
local: o dɛɛn yǝgi o kara yɩ o kwe sǝbu kʋm o pa Zezi tɩntʋŋna bam.

eng: However, a man named Ananias, along with his wife Sapphira, sold a piece of property.
local: Kʋ daarɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ dɛɛn mʋ wʋra, o yɩrɩ mʋ Ananiya, o kaanɩ yɩrɩ mʋ Safiira. Nɔɔnʋ wʋm ma kwe ba kara o yǝgi.

eng: With his wifes knowledge, he withheld some of the proceeds from the sale. He brought the rest and placed it in the care and under the authority of the apostles.
local: O ma twǝri sǝbu kʋm wʋnɩ sɩ o ta jɩga, yɩ o kaanɩ dɩ ye kʋ ni nɩ. O laan ma kwe kʋlʋ na daarɩ tɩn o ja vu o pa Zezi tɩntʋŋna bam.

eng: Peter asked, Ananias, how is it that Satan has influenced you to lie to the Holy Spirit by withholding some of the proceeds from the sale of your land?.
local: O na kɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, Pɩyɛɛrɩ ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Ananiya, bɛɛ mʋ kɩ yɩ nmʋ pa sʋtaanɩ wanɩ nmʋ kʋntʋ doŋ, yɩ n fɔ vwan dɩ Wɛ Joro kʋm dɩ nmʋ na twǝri kara kam sǝbu kʋm wʋnɩ sɩ n ta n jɩgɩ tɩn?.

eng: Wasnt that property yours to keep? After you sold it, wasnt the money yours to do with whatever you wanted? What made you think of such a thing? You havent lied to other people but to God.
local: Maŋa kam nmʋ ta na wʋ yǝgi kara kam tɩn, ka ya yɩ nmʋ nyɩm mʋ. Nmʋ nan na kwe n yǝgi tɩn, sǝbu kʋm ta yɩ nmʋ nyɩm mʋ. Bɛŋwaanɩ mʋ n bʋŋɩ n wʋ nɩ, yɩ nmʋ kɩ kǝm dɩm kʋntʋ? Nmʋ na fɔgɩ vwan kʋntʋ tɩn, n fɔgɩ n pa Wɛ mʋ, sɩ kʋ daɩ nabiinǝ.».

eng: When Ananias heard these words, he dropped dead. Everyone who heard this conversation was terrified.
local: Ananiya na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma tʋ tɩga nɩ o tɩ. Kʋ dɛɛn ma pa fʋʋnɩ ja balʋ maama na lwarɩ kʋ ni nɩ tɩn.

eng: Some young men stood up, wrapped up his body, carried him out, and buried him.
local: Nɔn-dʋnnʋ ma zaŋɩ ba vu ba pri tʋ wʋm ba ja nuŋi ba kɩ.

eng: About three hours later, his wife entered, but she didnt know what had happened to her husband.
local: Kʋ na kɛ nɩ luu tɩtɔ te tɩn, o kaanɩ wʋm dɩ daa ma ba o zʋ Pɩyɛɛrɩ-ba te. O ma daa ta wʋ lwarɩ kʋlʋ na kɩ tɩn.

eng: Peter asked her, Tell me, did you and your husband receive this price for the field? She responded, Yes, thats the amount.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Ta n brɩ-nɩ, mʋ sǝbu kʋlʋ maama nmʋ dɩ n barʋ wʋm na joŋi á kara kam ŋwaanɩ tɩn na?» O ma sɛ o wɩ: «Ɛɛn, kʋ maama mʋ kʋntʋ.».

eng: He replied, How could you scheme with each other to challenge the Lords Spirit? Look! The feet of those who buried your husband are at the door. They will carry you out too.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ laan ma bwe-o o wɩ: «Bɛŋwaanɩ mʋ nmʋ dɩ n barʋ wʋm kɩ ni daanɩ sɩ á maŋɩ Yuutu Baŋa-Wɛ Joro kʋm á nii? Nɔɔna balʋ na kwe n barʋ wʋm ba kɩ tɩn ta zɩgɩ digǝ kam ni nɩ lele kʋntʋ. Ba nan wʋ́ zɩŋɩ nmʋ dɩ ba ja nuŋi.».

eng: At that very moment, she dropped dead at his feet. When the young men entered and found her dead, they carried her out and buried her with her husband.
local: O na tagɩ kʋntʋ o ti tɩn, bɩdwɩ baŋa nɩ mʋ kaanɩ wʋm tʋ tɩga nɩ Pɩyɛɛrɩ yigǝ nɩ o tɩ. Nɔn-dʋnnʋ tɩm ma ba tɩ yi dɩ o tɩga. Ba ma kwe-o ba ja nuŋi ba kɩ o barʋ wʋm tɩkǝri nɩ.

eng: Trepidation and dread seized the whole church and all who heard what had happened.
local: Fʋʋnɩ dɛɛn ma ja Zezi kɔgɔ kʋm dɩ nɔɔna balʋ maama dɩ na ni kʋlʋ na kɩ tɩn.

eng: The apostles performed many signs and wonders among the people. They would come together regularly at Solomons Porch.
local: Wɛ dɛɛn pɛ Zezi tɩntʋŋna bam kɩ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ na brɩ DƖ dam tɩn dɩ wo-kɩnkagɩla zanzan nɔɔna tɩtarɩ nɩ. Zezi nɔɔna bam maama dɛɛn yǝni ba jeeri daanɩ dɩ wʋbʋŋ-dɩdwɩ mʋ faŋa faŋa Pɛ Salɔmɔn kʋnkɔlɔ kʋm nɩ.

eng: No one from outside the church dared to join them, even though the people spoke highly of them.
local: Balʋ na daɩ ba kɔgɔ kʋm wʋ nɔɔna tɩn, ba wʋlʋwʋlʋ wʋ dɛ o wǝli ba wʋnɩ. Kʋ daarɩ tɩʋ kʋm nɔɔna bam maama ma tee Zezi nɔɔna bam.

eng: Indeed, more and more believers in the Lord, large numbers of both men and women, were added to the church.
local: Baara dɩ kaana zanzan dɛɛn tu ba kɩ ba wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi. Ba ma tɔgɩ ba wǝli o kɔgɔ kʋm wʋnɩ.

eng: As a result, they would even bring the sick out into the main streets and lay them on cots and mats so that at least Peters shadow could fall on some of them as he passed by.
local: Zezi nɔɔna bam na yɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, nɔɔna maa zɩŋɩ yawɩɩna ba nuŋi ba tiŋi cwe niǝ nɩ. Ba dɛɛn tiŋi-ba sarɩ dɩ gungwǝǝlu baŋa nɩ mʋ, sɩ Pɩyɛɛrɩ na maa tɔgɩ da o kɛa, sɩ o luluŋu kʋm má wanɩ kʋ tɔgɩ ba badonnǝ baŋa.

eng: Even large numbers of persons from towns around Jerusalem would gather, bringing the sick and those harassed by unclean spirits. Everyone was healed.
local: Nɔɔna zanzan maa yǝni ba nuŋi Zeruzalɛm sa-tɩɩnɩ dɩm maama wʋnɩ ba jaanɩ yawɩɩna dɩ balʋ ciciri na jɩgɩ-ba sɩ yaarɩ tɩn ba tui, yɩ ba maama nɛ yazurǝ.

eng: The high priest, together with his allies, the Sadducees, was overcome with jealousy.
local: Zwifǝ bam kaanɩm yuutu wʋm dɩ o nɔɔna bam maama na tɔgɩ ba yɩ Sadusɩan tiinǝ kɔgɔ kʋm wʋ nɔɔna tɩn dɛɛn ma na kʋlʋ na kɩ tɩn. Ba maa tiini ba jɩgɩ wʋ-gʋrʋ dɩ Zezi tɩntʋŋna bam.

eng: They seized the apostles and made a public show of putting them in prison.
local: Ba laan ma zaŋɩ ba ja-ba ba ja vu ba kɩ pɩɩna digǝ nɩ.

eng: An angel from the Lord opened the prison doors during the night and led them out. The angel told them.
local: Tɩga na yi tɩn, Baŋa-Wɛ malɛka ma ba ka pʋrɩ pɩɩna digǝ kam niǝ yam, yɩ ka pa Zezi tɩntʋŋna bam nuŋi. Ka ma ta dɩ ba ka wɩ:.

eng: Go, take your place in the temple, and tell the people everything about this new life.
local: «Ve-na á zʋ Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm, sɩ á taá brɩ nɔɔna bam Wɛ ŋwɩ-dʋŋa kam na yɩ te tɩn maama.».

eng: Early in the morning, they went into the temple as they had been told and began to teach. When the high priest and his colleagues gathered, they convened the Jerusalem Council, that is, the full assembly of Israels elders. They sent word to the prison to have the apostles brought before them.
local: Ba ma sɛ Wɛ malɛka kam ni. Tɩga na pʋʋrɩ tɩtɩɩtɩ tɩn, ba ma vu ba zʋ Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm ba wʋra ba brɩ nɔɔna bam. Kaanɩm yuutu wʋm dɩ o nɔɔna bam laan ma bǝŋi Zwifǝ sarɩya-dirǝ nakwa dɩ Zwifǝ nakwa bam kɔgɔ kʋm maama sɩ ba kikili daanɩ. Ba ma daarɩ ba tʋŋɩ nɔɔna sɩ ba vu ba lɩ Zezi tɩntʋŋna bam pɩɩna digǝ kam nɩ ba ja ba ba pa-ba.

eng: However, the guards didnt find them in the prison. They returned and reported.
local: Ba nɔɔna bam na yi da tɩn, mʋ ba wʋ nɛ Zezi tɩntʋŋna bam pɩɩna digǝ kam nɩ. Ba ma joori ba vu Zwifǝ nakwa bam te yɩ ba wɩ:.

eng: We found the prison locked and well-secured, with guards standing at the doors, but when we opened the doors we found no one inside.
local: «Dɩ́ na yi da tɩn, pɩɩna digǝ kam niǝ yam pɩ mʋ lanyɩranɩ. Nɔɔna balʋ na wʋra ba yɩra sɩ pɩɩna bam yɩ lu tɩn maa zɩgɩ da. Dɩ́ nan na pʋrɩ digǝ kam tɩn, dɩ́ wʋ nɛ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ ka wʋnɩ.».

eng: When they received this news, the captain of the temple guard and the chief priests were baffled and wondered what might be happening.
local: Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm yɩrɩna bam yigǝ tu wʋm dɩdaanɩ kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, ba wʋbʋŋa vugimi daanɩ yɩ ba yǝri kʋlʋ na kɩ Zezi tɩntʋŋna bam tɩn.

eng: Just then, someone arrived and announced, Look! The people you put in prison are standing in the temple and teaching the people.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ laan ma ba o zʋ ba na wʋ mɛ tɩn o ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Nii-na, nɔɔna balʋ abam ya na jaanɩ á kɩ pɩɩna digǝ kam nɩ tɩn, bam mʋ wʋ Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm wʋnɩ ba zɩgɩ da ba brɩ nɔɔna.».

eng: Then the captain left with his guards and brought the apostles back. They didnt use force because they were afraid the people would stone them.
local: Yɩrɩna bam yigǝ tu wʋm na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, o dɩ o nɔɔna bam ma joori ba vu ba ja Zezi tɩntʋŋna bam ba ba. Ba daa wʋ jaanɩ-ba dɩ dam, bɛŋwaanɩ ba kwarɩ fʋʋnɩ sɩ nɔɔna bam wʋ́ dʋlɩ-ba dɩ kandwa.

eng: The apostles were brought before the council where the high priest confronted them.
local: Ba laan ma pa ba zʋ ba zɩgɩ sarɩya-dirǝ nakwa bam yigǝ nɩ. Kaanɩm yuutu wʋm laan ma bwe-ba o wɩ:.

eng: In no uncertain terms, we demanded that you not teach in this name. And look at you! You have filled Jerusalem with your teaching. And you are determined to hold us responsible for this mans death.
local: «Bɛŋwaanɩ mʋ á kɩ kʋntʋ? Dɩbam maŋɩ dɩ́ kaanɩ abam sɩ á daa yɩ taá brɩ nɔɔna dɩ yɩrɩ dɩntʋ. Nii abam na kɩ te. Á pɛ abam zaasɩm dɩm yɩranɩ mʋ jagɩ Zeruzalɛm maama wʋnɩ, yɩ á ta lagɩ sɩ wʋntʋ caa kʋm tɔgɩ dɩbam.».

eng: Peter and the apostles replied, We must obey God rather than humans!.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ dɩ o donnǝ tɩntʋŋna bam ma lǝri ba wɩ: «Dɩbam maŋɩ sɩ dɩ́ sɛ Wɛ ni mʋ kʋ dwǝni nabiinǝ ni.

eng: The God of our ancestors raised Jesus from the dead—whom you killed by hanging him on a tree.
local: Zezi wʋlʋ abam na jaanɩ á pa tʋʋn-dagara baŋa nɩ á gʋ tɩn, Baŋa-Wɛ dɩlʋ dɩbam nabaara bam na tɔgɩ tɩn mʋ pɛ o bi o yagɩ tʋʋnɩ.

eng: God has exalted Jesus to his right side as leader and savior so that he could enable Israel to change its heart and life and to find forgiveness for sins.
local: Kʋ yɩ wʋntʋ mʋ Baŋa-Wɛ zǝŋ-o DƖ jǝni DƖ jazɩm nɩ, sɩ o taa yɩ dɩbam Pɛ dɩ dɩ́ Vɩrnʋ, sɩ o pa Yisɩrayɛlɩ tiinǝ na cwǝŋǝ ba lǝni ba wʋrʋ, sɩ Wɛ laan yagɩ ba lwarɩm DƖ ma cɛ-ba.

eng: We are witnesses of such things, as is the Holy Spirit, whom God has given to those who obey him.
local: Dɩbam dɩ Wɛ Joro kʋlʋ Wɛ na pɛ balʋ maama na sɛ DƖ ni tɩn yɩ cɩga kantʋ maana tiinǝ.».

eng: When the council members heard this, they became furious and wanted to kill the apostles.
local: Zwifǝ nakwa bam na ni Zezi tɩntʋŋna bam na tɛ kʋlʋ tɩn, ba bana ma tiini ya zaŋɩ dɩ ba, yɩ ba lagɩ sɩ ba gʋ-ba.

eng: One council member, a Pharisee and teacher of the Law named Gamaliel, well-respected by all the people, stood up and ordered that the men be taken outside for a few moments.
local: Sarɩya-dirǝ nakwa bam dɩdʋ ma zaŋɩ ba tɩtarɩ nɩ, o yɩrɩ mʋ Gamalɩyɛlɩ. O yɩ Farizɩan tu yɩ o ta yɩ Wɛ cullu tɩm karanyɩn-kamunu. Nɔɔna maama maa pa-o zulǝ. O na zaŋɩ ba kɔgɔ kʋm wʋnɩ tɩn, o maa wɩ, ba ja Zezi tɩntʋŋna bam ba nuŋi pooni sɩ ba cǝgi fɩɩn.

eng: He said, Fellow Israelites, consider carefully what you intend to do to these people.
local: O ma daarɩ o ta dɩ sarɩya-dirǝ nakwa bam o wɩ: «Yisɩrayɛlɩ tiinǝ-ba, á cʋ-na á tɩtɩ dɩ á na bʋŋɩ sɩ á kɩ nɔɔna bantʋ te tɩn.

eng: Some time ago, Theudas appeared, claiming to be somebody, and some four hundred men joined him. After he was killed, all of his followers scattered, and nothing came of that.
local: Kʋ ta wʋ daanɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ mʋ zaŋɩ dɩbam lʋgʋ nɩ, o yɩrɩ mʋ Tɩʋdasɩ. O maa paɩ o yɩ nɔn-kamunu yɩ nɔɔna zanzan dɛɛn tɔgɩ o kwaga, ba yɩ nɩnɛɛnɩ nɔɔna biǝ-yana te. Ba dɛɛn gʋ-o mʋ yɩ o karabiǝ bam jagɩ da yigǝ nɩ. Nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ daa wʋ daarɩ.

eng: Afterward, at the time of the census, Judas the Galilean appeared and got some people to follow him in a revolt. He was killed too, and all his followers scattered far and wide.
local: Kʋntʋ kwaga nɩ Galile tu wʋdoŋ dɩ daa ma zaŋɩ o yɩrɩ mʋ Zudasɩ. Maŋa kam ba dɛɛn na wʋra ba garɩ dɩ́ lʋgʋ kʋm nɔn-biǝ tɩn, mʋ o zaŋɩ o la kɔgɔ o kɩ o kwaga nɩ. Ba ma gʋ wʋntʋ dɩ. O dɩ kwaga nɔɔna bam maama ma jagɩ da yigǝ nɩ.

eng: Heres my recommendation in this case: Distance yourselves from these men. Let them go! If their plan or activity is of human origin, it will end in ruin.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ a lagɩ a ta abam sɩ á yɩ zaŋɩ á kɩ nɔɔna bantʋ kʋlʋkʋlʋ. Yagɩ-na-ba, bɛŋwaanɩ ba wʋbʋŋa yam dɩ ba tɩtʋŋa yam maama na yɩ nabiinǝ nyɩm mʋ, yaá ba ya ji kafɛ mʋ.

eng: If it originates with God, you wont be able to stop them. Instead, you would actually find yourselves fighting God! The council was convinced by his reasoning.
local: Kʋ nan na yɩ Wɛ nyɩm mʋ, abam bá wanɩ á cɩ-ba. Yɩrɩ-na á tɩtɩ sɩ á yɩ ba á ja dɩ Baŋa-Wɛ.» Gamalɩyɛlɩ na tagɩ kʋntʋ o ti tɩn, ba ma sɛ o taanɩ dɩm.

eng: After calling the apostles back, they had them beaten. They ordered them not to speak in the name of Jesus, then let them go.
local: Ba ma bǝŋi Zezi tɩntʋŋna bam sɩ ba joori ba zʋ ba te. Ba ma magɩ-ba dɩ balaara, yɩ ba daarɩ ba kaanɩ-ba sɩ ba daa yɩ ŋɔɔnɩ kʋlʋkʋlʋ dɩ Zezi yɩrɩ. Ba laan ma yagɩ-ba yɩ ba viiri.

eng: The apostles left the council rejoicing because they had been regarded as worthy to suffer disgrace for the sake of the name.
local: Zezi tɩntʋŋna bam ma nuŋi sarɩya-dirǝ nakwa bam tee nɩ, yɩ ba jɩgɩ wʋpolo lanyɩranɩ dɩ ba na maŋɩ sɩ ba na yaara Zezi yɩrɩ ŋwaanɩ tɩn.

eng: Every day they continued to teach and proclaim the good news that Jesus is the Christ, both in the temple and in houses.
local: Dɛ maama ba maa yǝni ba jeeri daanɩ Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm wʋnɩ, yɩ ba daarɩ ba tʋlɩ sam ba brɩ nɔɔna Wɛ cɩga kam. Ba ma tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam, nɩ Zezi mʋ yɩ Krisi wʋm Wɛ na tʋŋɩ tɩn.

eng: Peter and John were going up to the temple at three oclock in the afternoon, the established prayer time.
local: Dɛ dɩdwɩ mʋ Pɩyɛɛrɩ dɩ Zan dɛɛn zaŋɩ ba vu Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm, maŋa kalʋ nɔɔna na yǝni ba ve ba warɩ Wɛ tɩn. Kʋ yɩ wɛ-gunim maŋa nɩ mʋ.
