eng: Meanwhile, a man crippled since birth was being carried in. Every day, people would place him at the temple gate known as the Beautiful Gate so he could ask for money from those entering the temple.
local: Ba ma vu ba yi digǝ kam ni dɩlʋ ba na bǝ nɩ Ni-laa tɩn. Nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ dɛɛn mʋ wʋra, o yɩ kɔrɔ mʋ kʋ na puli o lʋra kam maŋa nɩ. O nɔɔna maa yǝni ba zɩŋ-o ba vu ba jǝni da dɛ maama, sɩ o taa loori sǝbiǝ nɔɔna balʋ na tui ba tɔgɩ da ba zʋ Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm wʋ tɩn tee nɩ.

eng: When he saw Peter and John about to enter, he began to ask them for a gift.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋm ma na Pɩyɛɛrɩ dɩ Zan na maa zʋʋrɩ Wɛ-digǝ kam. O ma loori-ba sɩ ba pa-o woŋo.

eng: Peter and John stared at him. Peter said, Look at us.
local: Ba ma nii-o ziin. Pɩyɛɛrɩ laan ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Nii dɩbam seeni.».

eng: So the man gazed at them, expecting to receive something from them.
local: O ma sɩɩnɩ o nii-ba yɩ o jɩgɩ tɩɩna sɩ baá pa-o woŋo.

eng: Peter said, I dont have any money, but I will give you what I do have. In the name of Jesus Christ the Nazarene, rise up and walk.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «A ba jɩgɩ sǝbu sɩ a pa-m. A nan wʋ́ pa nmʋ a na jɩgɩ kʋlʋ tɩn. Dɩ Nazarɛtɩ tu Zezi Krisi yɩrɩ ŋwaanɩ, zaŋɩ n ta n veǝ.».

eng: Then he grasped the mans right hand and raised him up. At once his feet and ankles became strong.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, o laan ma ja o jazɩm o zǝn-o o pa o zaŋɩ wɛɛnɩ. O na zaŋɩ kʋntʋ bɩdwɩ baŋa nɩ tɩn, mʋ o nɛ sɩm dɩ o nakwǝǝlu tɩm maama kɩ dam lanyɩranɩ.

eng: Jumping up, he began to walk around. He entered the temple with them, walking, leaping, and praising God.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋm ma faŋɩ wɛɛnɩ o joori o ba o cwi o nɛ baŋa nɩ yɩ o sɩŋɩ vǝŋǝ. O ma tɔgɩ-ba o zʋ Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm wʋ. O maa veǝ yɩ o faŋa wɛɛnɩ yɩ o zuli Wɛ.

eng: All the people saw him walking and praising God.
local: Nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm ma na o na veǝ yɩ o zuli Wɛ.

eng: They recognized him as the same one who used to sit at the temples Beautiful Gate asking for money. They were filled with amazement and surprise at what had happened to him.
local: Ba ma lwarɩ nɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋm kʋntʋ mʋ yɩ looru wʋlʋ ya na je Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm Ni-laa kʋm nɩ tɩn. Kʋ ma tiini kʋ sɔɔrɩ-ba, yɩ kʋ kɩ-ba yǝǝu dɩ kʋlʋ na kɩ tɩn.

eng: While the healed man clung to Peter and John, all the people rushed toward them at Solomons Porch, completely amazed.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋm ma yɔɔrɩ o mǝǝli Pɩyɛɛrɩ dɩ Zan. Nɔɔna bam maama maa yɩ yǝǝu. Ba ma zaŋɩ ba duri ba vu ba te, kʋnkɔlɔ kʋlʋ na jɩgɩ pwǝŋǝ yɩ ba maa bǝ nɩ Salɔmɔn kʋnkɔlɔ tɩn nɩ.

eng: Seeing this, Peter addressed the people: You Israelites, why are you amazed at this? Why are you staring at us as if we made him walk by our own power or piety?.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ na nɛ nɔɔna bam kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «A badonnǝ Yisɩrayɛlɩ tiinǝ-ba, bɛŋwaanɩ mʋ yɩ abam yɩ yǝǝu dɩ kʋlʋ na kɩ tɩn? Bɛɛ mʋ yɩ abam yɔɔrɩ á nii dɩbam kʋntʋ? Abam bʋŋɩ sɩ kʋ yɩ dɩbam tɩtɩ dam mʋ pɛ o zaŋɩ o ve na? Á bʋŋɩ nɩ kʋ yɩ dɩbam na kwarɩ Wɛ lanyɩranɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ mʋ pɛ o zaŋɩ o ve na?.

eng: The God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob—the God of our ancestors—has glorified his servant Jesus. This is the one you handed over and denied in Pilates presence, even though he had already decided to release him.
local: Kʋ nan na yɩ te tɩn, dɩbam nabaara Abraham dɩ Yizakɩ dɩ Zakɔbɩ Baŋa-Wɛ laan mʋ pɛ DƖ tɩntʋŋnʋ Zezi joŋi paarɩ dɩ zulǝ. Abam jaanɩ wʋntʋ mʋ á pa ba gʋ-o. Pilatɩ dɛɛn bʋŋɩ sɩ o yag-o yɩ abam wʋ sɛ.

eng: You rejected the holy and righteous one, and asked that a murderer be released to you instead.
local: Zezi Krisi yɩ wʋ-poŋo tu mʋ dɩ cɩga tu. Abam nan vɩ-o, yɩ á daarɩ á loori Pilatɩ sɩ o yagɩ nɔn-gʋrʋ mʋ o pa abam.

eng: You killed the author of life, the very one whom God raised from the dead. We are witnesses of this.
local: Abam na kɩ te tɩn, á gʋ wʋlʋ na yɩ ŋwɩa tu tɩn mʋ, yɩ Wɛ joori DƖ bi-o DƖ pa o yagɩ tʋʋnɩ. Dɩbam yɩ maana tiinǝ dɩ́ brɩ nɔɔna cɩga kam kʋntʋ.

eng: His name itself has made this man strong. That is, because of faith in Jesus name, God has strengthened this man whom you see and know. The faith that comes through Jesus gave him complete health right before your eyes.
local: Kʋ yɩ Zezi Krisi yɩrɩ dam dɩm ŋwaanɩ, dɩ dɩ́ na kɩ dɩ́ wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩd-o tɩn mʋ pɛ nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ abam na nɛ yɩ á ye tɩn na dam. Kʋ yɩ o na kɩ o wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Zezi tɩn mʋ pɛ o na yazurǝ abam maama yigǝ nɩ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I know you acted in ignorance. So did your rulers.
local: A ko-biǝ-ba, abam dɩ á yigǝ tiinǝ bam na kɩ Zezi te tɩn, a ye nɩ abam ya yǝri woŋo kʋlʋ á na kɩ tɩn mʋ.

eng: But this is how God fulfilled what he foretold through all the prophets: that his Christ would suffer.
local: Faŋa faŋa tɩn, Wɛ dɛɛn pɛ DƖ nijoŋnǝ bam maama tɔɔlɩ DƖ kwǝrǝ ba brɩ nɔɔna nɩ Krisi wʋm DƖ na tʋŋɩ tɩn maŋɩ sɩ o yaarɩ mʋ. Abam na kɩ-o te tɩn mʋ pɛ Wɛ taanɩ dɩm sɩɩnɩ dɩ kɩ.

eng: Change your hearts and lives! Turn back to God so that your sins may be wiped away.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ, nan lǝni-na á wʋrʋ á yagɩ kǝm-balwaarʋ, sɩ á daarɩ á pipiri á taá tɔgɩ Wɛ, sɩ Wɛ wʋ́ yagɩ á lwarɩm DƖ ma cɛ abam.

eng: Then the Lord will provide a season of relief from the distress of this age and he will send Jesus, whom he handpicked to be your Christ.
local: Kʋntʋ tɩn, dɩ́ Yuutu Baŋa-Wɛ wʋ́ pa abam wʋrʋ taa zurǝ maŋa dɩ maŋa, yɩ DƖ daarɩ DƖ tʋŋɩ Krisi wʋm DƖ na maŋɩ DƖ kuri sɩ DƖ pa abam tɩn. Wʋntʋ mʋ yɩ Zezi.

eng: Jesus must remain in heaven until the restoration of all things, about which God spoke long ago through his holy prophets.
local: O maŋɩ sɩ o taa wʋ Wɛ-sɔŋɔ nɩ mʋ, sɩ kʋ taa ve maŋa kalʋ Wɛ na wʋ́ fɔgɩ DƖ kwɛ wǝǝnu maama tɩn, nɩ Wɛ na maŋɩ DƖ pa DƖ nijoŋnǝ bam tɔɔlɩ DƖ kwǝrǝ faŋa faŋa te tɩn.

eng: Moses said, The Lord your God will raise up from your own people a prophet like me. Listen to whatever he tells you. .
local: Kʋ yɩ nɩnɛɛnɩ Moyisi dɛɛn na tagɩ o wɩ: ‹Abam Yuutu Baŋa-Wɛ wʋ́ pa abam dwi tu dɩdʋa mʋ zaŋɩ o taa yɩ Wɛ nijoŋnu abam tɩtarɩ nɩ, nɩ DƖ na tʋŋɩ amʋ Moyisi te tɩn. Wʋntʋ nan na tuǝ, abam maŋɩ sɩ á sɛ taanɩ dɩlʋ maama o na wʋ́ ta o brɩ abam tɩn.

eng: About that time, while the number of disciples continued to increase, a complaint arose. Greek-speaking disciples accused the Aramaic-speaking disciples because their widows were being overlooked in the daily food service.
local: Maŋa kam kʋntʋ nɩ Zezi karabiǝ bam kɔgɔ kʋm maa wʋra kʋ pulǝ zanzan. Ba kɔgɔ kʋm wʋnɩ Zwifǝ balʋ na yɩ vǝrǝ yɩ ba ŋɔɔnɩ Grɛkɩ tiinǝ taanɩ dɩm tɩn maa jɩgɩ balʋ na ŋɔɔnɩ ba tɩtɩ Zwifǝ taanɩ dɩm tɩn ba pʋʋna, dɩ ba na ba paɩ ba kadǝnǝ kʋlʋ na maŋɩ sɩ ba pa-ba ba ni-wʋdiu ŋwaanɩ dɛ maama tɩn.

eng: Whoever doesnt listen to that prophet will be totally cut off from the people. .
local: Wʋlʋ maama nan na wʋ sɛ kʋlʋ Wɛ nijoŋnu wʋm na wʋ́ brɩ tɩn, ba maŋɩ sɩ ba lɩ kʋ tu mʋ Wɛ nɔɔna bam wʋnɩ ba gʋ.› Mʋ kʋlʋ Moyisi dɛɛn na tagɩ tɩn.

eng: The Twelve called a meeting of all the disciples and said, It isnt right for us to set aside proclamation of Gods word in order to serve tables.
local: Zezi tɩntʋŋna fugǝ-bale bam ma bǝŋi ba kɔgɔ kʋm maama ba kɩ daanɩ, yɩ ba ta ba wɩ: «Kʋ wʋ maŋɩ sɩ dɩbam yagɩ Wɛ taanɩ dɩm zaasɩm, sɩ dɩ́ daarɩ dɩ́ taá maŋɩ wʋdiiru.

eng: All the prophets who spoke—from Samuel forward—announced these days.
local: Kʋ na puli dɩ Samʋwɛlɩ dɩ balʋ maama na saŋɩ o kwaga tɩn, Wɛ nijoŋnǝ balʋ maama na tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝrǝ tɩn dɛɛn brɩ kʋlʋ na wʋ́ kɩ da yantʋ wʋnɩ tɩn mʋ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, carefully choose seven well-respected men from among you. They must be well-respected and endowed by the Spirit with exceptional wisdom. We will put them in charge of this concern.
local: Dɩ́ ko-biǝ-ba, á maŋɩ sɩ á lɩ nɔɔna barpɛ mʋ á wʋnɩ, sɩ dɩ́ pa ba taa nii wǝǝnu tɩntʋ baŋa nɩ. Ba nan maŋɩ sɩ ba taa yɩ nɔɔna balʋ nɔɔna maama na ye nɩ ba yɩ nɔn-ŋʋna yɩ Wɛ Joro kʋm wʋ ba tee nɩ lanyɩranɩ yɩ ba jɩgɩ wʋbʋŋ-ŋʋna tɩn.

eng: You are the heirs of the prophets and the covenant that God made with your ancestors when he told Abraham, Through your descendants, all the families on earth will be blessed. .
local: Wɛ kwǝrǝ kam DƖ dɛɛn na pɛ DƖ nijoŋnǝ bam sɩ ba tɔɔlɩ tɩn yɩ abam ŋwaanɩ mʋ. Wɛ ni dɩm DƖ dɛɛn na goni DƖ pa dɩbam nabaara bam tɩn dɩ daa ta yɩ abam ŋwaanɩ mʋ. DƖ dɛɛn goni ni dɩ Abraham DƖ wɩ: ‹Nmʋ dwi dɩm ŋwaanɩ amʋ wʋ́ kɩ lʋgʋ baŋa dwi tiinǝ maama lanyɩranɩ.›.

eng: As for us, we will devote ourselves to prayer and the service of proclaiming the word.
local: Sɩ dɩbam dɩ́ daarɩ dɩ́ kwaanɩ dɩ́ nii Wɛ warɩm dɩ DƖ taanɩ dɩm zaasɩm baŋa nɩ.».

eng: After God raised his servant, he sent him to you first—to bless you by enabling each of you to turn from your evil ways.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ Baŋa-Wɛ dɛ yigǝ DƖ tʋŋɩ DƖ tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋm abam tee nɩ mʋ, sɩ o kɩ abam lanyɩranɩ dɩ o na pa abam dɩdʋa dɩdʋa yagɩ á kǝm-balwaarʋ tɩm.».

eng: This proposal pleased the entire community. They selected Stephen, a man endowed by the Holy Spirit with exceptional faith, Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and Nicolaus from Antioch, a convert to Judaism.
local: Ba na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ ma poli ba kɔgɔ kʋm maama wʋ. Ba ma kuri nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ, o yɩrɩ mʋ Etiyɛnɩ. Wʋntʋ jɩgɩ wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Zezi yɩ Wɛ Joro kʋm wʋ o tee nɩ. Ba ma wǝli Filipi dɩ Prokori dɩ Nikano dɩ Timɔn dɩ Pamɩna dɩ Nikola wʋlʋ na nuŋi Antɩɔsɩ, o yɩ dwi-gɛ tu yɩ o ba o tɔgɩ cullu tɩlʋ Wɛ na pɛ dɩbam Zwifǝ bam tɩn.

eng: The community presented these seven to the apostles, who prayed and laid their hands on them.
local: Ba ma lɩ-ba ba ja vu ba zɩgɩ Zezi tɩntʋŋna bam yigǝ nɩ. Ba ma warɩ Wɛ ba pa-ba yɩ ba daŋɩ ba jɩa ba baŋa nɩ.

eng: Gods word continued to grow. The number of disciples in Jerusalem increased significantly. Even a large group of priests embraced the faith.
local: Wɛ kwǝrǝ kam dɛɛn ma fɔgɩ ka jagɩ ka ve yigǝ yɩ nɔɔna sɛ-ka. Zezi karabiǝ bam kɔgɔ kʋm maa pulǝ zanzan Zeruzalɛm nɩ. Zwifǝ kaanɩm tiinǝ zanzan dɩ dɛɛn ma ba ba sɛ Zezi cɩga kam.

eng: Stephen, who stood out among the believers for the way Gods grace was at work in his life and for his exceptional endowment with divine power, was doing great wonders and signs among the people.
local: Etiyɛnɩ dɛɛn yɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ Wɛ na pɛ-o zaanɩ dɩ dam zanzan tɩn. O maa tiini o kɩ wo-kɩnkagɩla dɩ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ na brɩ Wɛ dam tɩn nɔɔna tɩtarɩ nɩ.

eng: Opposition arose from some who belonged to the so-called Synagogue of Former Slaves. Members from Cyrene, Alexandria, Cilicia, and Asia entered into debate with Stephen.
local: Nɔɔna badonnǝ maa zaŋɩ ba magɩ kantɔgɔ dɩd-o. Bantʋ nuŋi Zwifǝ kɔgɔ kʋlʋ ya na yɩ gambɛ yɩ ba laan ba ba te ba tɩtɩ tɩn wʋnɩ mʋ. Ba dɛɛn nuŋi Sirɛɛnɩ dɩ Alɛsandrɩ mʋ. Ba ma tɔgɩ ba wǝli dɩ Zwifǝ badaara na nuŋi Silisi dɩ Azi tɩn.

eng: However, they couldnt resist the wisdom the Spirit gave him as he spoke.
local: Ba na magɩ kantɔgɔ dɩd-o tɩn, o ma ŋɔɔnɩ dɩ bʋŋɩm dam nɩ Wɛ Joro kʋm na pɛ-o te tɩn, kʋ pa ba daa warɩ o taanɩ ba lǝri.

eng: Then they secretly enticed some people to claim, We heard him insult Moses and God.
local: Ba laan ma nuŋi ba kɩ ni dɩdaanɩ nɔɔna badonnǝ, sɩ ba fɔ vwan ba ta wɩ: «Dɩbam ni nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ na ŋɔɔnɩ o twɩ Moyisi dɩ Wɛ.».

eng: They stirred up the people, the elders, and the legal experts. They caught Stephen, dragged him away, and brought him before the Jerusalem Council.
local: Ba ma pa tɩʋ kʋm nɔɔna dɩ ba nakwa bam dɩ ba cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ bam bana zaŋɩ, yɩ ba ja Etiyɛnɩ ba ja vu ba sarɩya-dirǝ nakwa bam yigǝ sɩ ba di o taanɩ.

eng: Before the council, they presented false witnesses who testified, This man never stops speaking against this holy place and the Law.
local: Ba ma pa nɔɔna badonnǝ zʋ ba fɔ vwan ba pa-o, yɩ ba ta ba wɩ: «Maŋa maama nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ sɩɩnɩ o ŋɔɔnɩ ta-balwaarʋ mʋ o paɩ dɩbam Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm dɩ Wɛ cullu tɩlʋ DƖ na kɩ Moyisi jɩŋa nɩ dɩbam ŋwaanɩ tɩn.

eng: In fact, we heard him say that this man Jesus of Nazareth will destroy this place and alter the customary practices Moses gave us.
local: Dɩ́ ma ni o na tagɩ o wɩ, Nazarɛtɩ tu Zezi mʋ wʋ́ ba o cɔgɩ Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm, yɩ o pipiri mɩʋ kʋlʋ maama Moyisi dɛɛn na brɩ dɩ́ nabaara sɩ dɩ́ taá tɔgɩ tɩn.».

eng: Everyone seated in the council stared at Stephen, and they saw that his face was radiant, just like an angels.
local: Zwifǝ nakwa balʋ maama na je da tɩn laan maa yɔɔrɩ ba nii Etiyɛnɩ cɔɔn. Ba ma nii ba na o yibiyǝ na lǝni nɩ malɛka yibiyǝ te.

eng: The high priest asked, Are these accusations true.
local: Zwifǝ kaanɩm yuutu wʋm ma zaŋɩ o bwe Etiyɛnɩ o wɩ: «Wǝǝnu tɩlʋ ba na tagɩ ba pa nmʋ tɩn yɩ cɩga mʋ naa vwan mʋ?».

eng: Stephen responded, Brothers and fathers, listen to me. Our glorious God appeared to our ancestor Abraham while he was still in Mesopotamia, before he settled in Haran.
local: Etiyɛnɩ laan ma lǝri o wɩ: «A kwǝ-ba dɩ a ko-biǝ-ba, cǝgi-na amʋ na lagɩ a ta woŋo kʋlʋ tɩn. Faŋa faŋa tɩn Paarɩ-Zulǝ Tu Baŋa-Wɛ mʋ tu dɩbam nabaarʋ Abraham te. Kantʋ maŋa kam nɩ, Abraham dɛɛn ta zʋʋrɩ Mesopotami nɩ mʋ. O daa ta wʋ ve sɩ o taa zʋʋrɩ Aaran nɩ.

eng: God told him, Leave your homeland and kin, and go to the land that I will show you.
local: Wɛ dɛɛn ma ta dɩd-o DƖ wɩ: ‹Yagɩ n tɩtɩ tɩʋ kʋm dɩ n sɔŋɔ tiinǝ, sɩ n daarɩ n vu tɩʋ kʋlʋ amʋ na wʋ́ brɩ nmʋ tɩn.›.

eng: So Abraham left the land of the Chaldeans and settled in Haran. After Abrahams father died, God had him resettle in this land where you now live.
local: O ma sɩɩnɩ o zaŋɩ o nuŋi Kalɩdɩan tiinǝ tɩʋ kʋm nɩ o vu o yi Aaran o zʋʋrɩ da. O na wʋ dáanɩ tɩn, o ko ma tɩ. Wɛ ma pa o daa nuŋi o fɔɔrɩ o vu o yi tɩʋ kʋntʋ abam na zʋʋrɩ zɩm tɩn.

eng: God didnt give him an inheritance here, not even a square foot of land. However, God did promise to give the land as his possession to him and to his descendants, even though Abraham had no child.
local: Wɛ nan wʋ pɛ Abraham tɩga yo seeni sɩ o taa te, dɩ fɩnfɩɩn dɩ. Wɛ dɛɛn goni ni mʋ dɩd-o DƖ wɩ, wʋm wʋ́ pa-o tɩga kantʋ sɩ o taa te, yɩ o na tɩgɩ o daarɩ, sɩ o dwi tiinǝ ta ta te-ka. Wɛ na goni DƖ ni dɩm tɩn, Abraham ta ba jɩgɩ bu.

eng: God put it this way: His descendants will be strangers in a land that belongs to others, who will enslave them and abuse them for four hundred years. .
local: Mʋ Wɛ na tagɩ kʋlʋ dɩd-o tɩn mʋ tɩntʋ: ‹Abraham, nmʋ dwi tiinǝ wʋ́ vu ba taa zʋʋrɩ tʋ-gaa nɩ. Baá ta yɩ gambɛ mʋ tɩʋ kʋm kʋntʋ nɩ, yɩ nɔɔna wʋ́ bɛɛsɩ-ba taan bɩna biǝ-yana .

eng: And I will condemn the nation they serve as slaves, God said, and afterward they will leave that land and serve me in this place.
local: Amʋ Wɛ nan wʋ́ di balʋ na fɩ-ba sɩ ba tʋŋɩ gambɛɛm tɩtʋŋa tɩn taanɩ sɩ ba na cam. Kʋntʋ kwaga nɩ, ba laan wʋ́ nuŋi tɩʋ kʋm kʋntʋ nɩ ba ba, ba taa zuli amʋ jǝgǝ kantʋ nɩ.›.

eng: God gave him the covenant confirmed through circumcision. Accordingly, eight days after Isaacs birth, Abraham circumcised him. Isaac did the same with Jacob, and Jacob with the twelve patriarchs.
local: Baŋa-Wɛ ma daarɩ DƖ go ni DƖ pa Abraham DƖ wɩ, Abraham dwi tiinǝ taa goni ba bǝkǝri sɩ kʋ brɩ nɩ ba sɛ DƖ ni. Kʋntʋ, Abraham na lʋgɩ Yizakɩ tɩn, da nana dɛ nɩ o ma go-o. Yizakɩ dɩ dɛɛn ma ba o lʋ Zakɔbɩ, o dɩ ma go-o. Zakɔbɩ ma lʋ dɩ́ nabaara fugǝ-bale bam yɩ o go-ba dɩ.

eng: Because the patriarchs were jealous of Joseph, they sold him into slavery in Egypt. God was with him, however.
local: Nabaara bam dɩdʋa yɩrɩ mʋ Zʋzɛfʋ. O cʋrrʋ tɩm dɛɛn maa jɩgɩ wʋ-gʋrʋ dɩd-o. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ ba jaan-o ba yǝgi ba pa o vu Ezipi sɩ o taa yɩ gambaa. Wɛ ta maa wʋra dɩd-o.

eng: and rescued him from all his troubles. The grace and wisdom he gave Joseph were recognized by Pharaoh, king of Egypt, who appointed him ruler over Egypt and over his whole palace.
local: DƖ ma pa o nuŋi cam maama wʋnɩ dɩ yazurǝ. Zʋzɛfʋ na tu o zɩgɩ Ezipi pa-farʋ wʋm yigǝ nɩ tɩn, Wɛ pɛ-o wʋbʋŋ-ŋʋna yɩ pɛ wʋm yi su Zʋzɛfʋ lanyɩranɩ. Pɛ wʋm ma pa o ji o sɔŋɔ kʋm tu dɩ Ezipi tɩʋ kʋm maama yuutu.

eng: A famine came upon all Egypt and Canaan, and great hardship came with it. Our ancestors had nothing to eat.
local: Kana dɛɛn ma ba ka wʋ Ezipi dɩ Kanaan tɩɩnɩ dɩm maama nɩ. Kʋ ma tiini kʋ cɛ ba yɩra nɩ. Dɩ́ nabaara bam maa warɩ sɩ ba na wʋdiu ba di.

eng: When Jacob heard there was grain in Egypt, he sent our ancestors there for the first time.
local: Zakɔbɩ dɛɛn ma ni nɩ wʋdiu wʋ Ezipi nɩ. O ma tʋŋɩ dɩ́ nabaara bam sɩ ba vu ba yǝgi.

eng: During their second visit, Joseph told his brothers who he was, and Pharaoh learned about Josephs family.
local: Kʋntʋ kwaga nɩ ba daa ma joori ba vu ba kɩ bɩle. Zʋzɛfʋ laan ma pa o cʋrrʋ tɩm lwar-o. Ezipi pa-farʋ wʋm dɩ laan ma ba o lwarɩ Zʋzɛfʋ dwi dɩm na nuŋi mɛ tɩn.

eng: Joseph sent for his father Jacob and all his relatives—seventy-five in all—and invited them to live with him.
local: Zʋzɛfʋ ma daarɩ o tʋŋɩ ni o pa o ko Zakɔbɩ, sɩ o dɩ o sɔŋɔ tiinǝ bam maama ba o te Ezipi nɩ. Ba maama dɛɛn yɩ nɔɔna fusɩrpɛ-banu mʋ .

eng: So Jacob went down to Egypt, where he and our ancestors died.
local: Zakɔbɩ ma sɩɩnɩ o zaŋɩ o vu Ezipi. Dáanɩ mʋ wʋntʋ tɩga. Dɩbam nabaara bam dɩ ma ba ba tɩ.

eng: Their bodies were brought back to Shechem and placed in the tomb that Abraham had purchased for a certain sum of money from Hamors children, who lived in Shechem.
local: Ba ma ja ba yɩra yam ba joori ba vu Sisɩm ba kɩ-ba yibeeli dɩlʋ Abraham na yǝgi dɩ sǝbu Emori biǝ bam tee nɩ tɩn nɩ.

eng: When it was time for God to keep the promise he made to Abraham, the number of our people in Egypt had greatly expanded.
local: Maŋa kam ma twɛ sɩ Baŋa-Wɛ sɩɩnɩ DƖ kɩ kʋlʋ DƖ dɛɛn na goni ni dɩ Abraham tɩn. Maŋa kam kʋntʋ nɩ dɩbam nɔɔna balʋ na wʋ Ezipi nɩ tɩn maa tiini ba wʋra ba puli zanzan.

eng: But then another king rose to power over Egypt who didnt know anything about Joseph. .
local: Pa-farʋ wʋdoŋ dɩ daa ma zaŋɩ sɩ o taa te Ezipi. O maa yǝri Zʋzɛfʋ ni nɩ.

eng: He exploited our people and abused our ancestors. He even forced them to abandon their newly born babies so they would die.
local: O dɛɛn maa jɩgɩ dɩbam nabaara bam o bɛɛsa. O dɛɛn kɩ swan mʋ o fɩ-ba sɩ ba kwe ba bu-sɩsɩn ba jaanɩ ba ve ba yaga, sɩ ba taa tʋa.

eng: Thats when Moses was born. He was highly favored by God, and for three months his parents cared for him in their home.
local: Kantʋ maŋa kam nɩ mʋ ba lʋgɩ Moyisi. O dɛɛn maa yɩ bu-ŋʋm Wɛ yibiyǝ nɩ. Ba maa jɩg-o ba kɔna o ko sɔŋɔ nɩ canɩ sɩtɔ.

eng: After he was abandoned, Pharaohs daughter adopted and cared for him as though he were her own son.
local: Ba laan ma ja-o ba nuŋi ba vu ba yagɩ. Pa-farʋ wʋm bukɔ ma kwe-o o jɩgɩ o niǝ, nɩ o yɩ o tɩtɩ bu mʋ te.

eng: Moses learned everything Egyptian wisdom had to offer, and he was a man of powerful words and deeds.
local: Ba ma brɩ Moyisi Ezipi tiinǝ yi-pʋrʋ kʋm maama. O ma ji nɔn-babɩa o ni-taanɩ dɩ o kǝm maama baŋa nɩ.

eng: When Moses was years old, he decided to visit his family, the Israelites.
local: Moyisi dɛɛn na yi bɩna fiinna tɩn, o ma lɩ wʋbʋŋa sɩ o vu o nii o ko-biǝ Yisɩrayɛlɩ tiinǝ bam na yɩ te.

eng: He saw one of them being wronged so he came to his rescue and evened the score by killing the Egyptian.
local: O na ve tɩn, o ma na Ezipi tu wʋdoŋ na jɩgɩ Yisɩrayɛlɩ tu o maga. Moyisi ma vu sɩ o wǝli o tɩtɩ dwi tu wʋm jara yam wʋnɩ. O ma magɩ Ezipi tu wʋm o gʋ.

eng: He expected his own kin to understand that God was using him to rescue them, but they didnt.
local: O dɛɛn ya bʋŋɩ nɩ o tɩtɩ dwi tiinǝ bam wʋ́ lwarɩ nɩ Wɛ lagɩ DƖ pa wʋntʋ mʋ joŋi-ba Ezipi tiinǝ bam jɩŋa nɩ o yagɩ. Ba nan wʋ lwarɩ.

eng: The next day he came upon some Israelites who were caught up in an argument. He tried to make peace between them by saying, You are brothers! Why are you harming each other.
local: Tɩga na pʋʋrɩ tɩn, o daa ma na Yisɩrayɛlɩ tiinǝ bale na jɩgɩ daanɩ. O ma kwaanɩ sɩ o lo-ba, yɩ o ta dɩ ba o wɩ: ‹A ko-biǝ-ba, abam yɩ cʋrrʋ mʋ daanɩ. Bɛɛ mʋ kɩ yɩ abam daa jɩgɩ daanɩ?›.

eng: The one who started the fight against his neighbor pushed Moses aside and said, Who appointed you as our leader and judge? .
local: O na tagɩ dɩ ba kʋntʋ tɩn, mʋ wʋlʋ wʋm na dana tɩn ma yigi Moyisi o yagɩ daa nɩ, yɩ o daarɩ o bwe Moyisi o wɩ: ‹Wɔɔ mʋ pɛ nmʋ ni sɩ n ta n yɩ dɩbam yuutu sɩ n daarɩ n ŋɔɔnɩ dɩbam taanɩ?.

eng: Are you planning to kill me like you killed that Egyptian yesterday? .
local: Nmʋ lagɩ sɩ n gʋ amʋ dɩ mʋ, nɩ n diin na gʋ Ezipi tu wʋm te tɩn na?›.

eng: When Moses heard this, he fled to Midian, where he lived as an immigrant and had two sons.
local: Moyisi na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, o laan ma duri o nuŋi Ezipi nɩ o vu o zʋʋrɩ Madɩan tɩʋ nɩ. Dáanɩ mʋ o di kaanɩ yɩ ba lʋ biǝ bale.

eng: Forty years later, an angel appeared to Moses in the flame of a burning bush in the wilderness near Mount Sinai.
local: Bɩna fiinna dɛɛn na kɛ tɩn, Wɛ malɛka ma ba Moyisi te kagʋa wʋnɩ Sinayi piu kʋm tɩkǝri nɩ. O ma na min-vʋgʋ na jɩgɩ puŋu kʋ di, yɩ malɛka kam wʋ mini dɩm wʋnɩ.

eng: Enthralled by the sight, Moses approached to get a closer look and he heard the Lords voice.
local: Moyisi yɩra ma sɔɔrɩ dɩ o na nɛ kʋlʋ tɩn. O ma fɔɔrɩ o yi mini dɩm te sɩ o nii. O laan ma ni Baŋa-Wɛ kwǝrǝ na ŋɔɔnɩ dɩd-o ka wɩ:.

eng: I am the God of your ancestors, the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. Trembling with fear, Moses didnt dare to investigate any further.
local: ‹Amʋ yɩ nmʋ nabaara bam Baŋa-Wɛ. Amʋ mʋ yɩ Abraham Baŋa-Wɛ, dɩ Yizakɩ dɩ Zakɔbɩ Baŋa-Wɛ.› Moyisi yɩra maa saɩ, yɩ o daa warɩ o nii.

eng: The Lord continued, Remove the sandals from your feet, for the place where you are standing is holy ground. .
local: Baŋa-Wɛ ma ta dɩd-o DƖ wɩ: ‹Lɩ n natra yam, sɩ jǝgǝ kalʋ nmʋ na zɩgɩ da tɩn yɩ amʋ tɩtɩ jǝgǝ mʋ.›.

eng: I have clearly seen the oppression my people have experienced in Egypt, and I have heard their groaning. I have come down to rescue them. Come! I am sending you to Egypt .
local: Wɛ daa ma ta DƖ wɩ: ‹A nɛ a nɔɔna bam na wʋ Ezipi nɩ ba yaara te tɩn, yɩ a kwǝri a ni ba na kʋnɩ te tɩn. Amʋ nan tu sɩ a joŋi-ba mʋ a yagɩ. Kʋntʋ tɩn, a lagɩ a tʋŋɩ nmʋ sɩ n joori n vu Ezipi.› ».

eng: This is the same Moses whom they rejected when they asked, Who appointed you as our leader and judge? This is the Moses whom God sent as leader and deliverer. God did this with the help of the angel who appeared before him in the bush.
local: Etiyɛnɩ ta ma ta o wɩ: «Moyisi dɛɛn yɩ wʋlʋ Yisɩrayɛlɩ tiinǝ bam na vɩ-o tɩn. Ba dɛɛn bwe-o ba wɩ: ‹Wɔɔ mʋ pɛ nmʋ ni, sɩ n ta n yɩ dɩbam yuutu sɩ n daarɩ n ŋɔɔnɩ dɩbam taanɩ?› Moyisi wʋntʋ mʋ Wɛ tʋŋɩ sɩ o vu o taa te DƖ nɔɔna bam, sɩ o daarɩ o joŋi-ba o yagɩ. Wɛ ma tʋŋɩ DƖ malɛka, yɩ ka nuŋi o yigǝ nɩ puŋu wʋnɩ ka brɩ-o kʋntʋ maama.

eng: This man led them out after he performed wonders and signs in Egypt at the Red Sea and for forty years in the wilderness.
local: Moyisi dɛɛn ma kɩ wo-kɩnkagɩla dɩ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ na brɩ Wɛ dam tɩn Ezipi nɩ, yɩ o wanɩ o ja nɔɔna bam o nuŋi. Ba ma tɔgɩ nɩnɩʋ-sʋŋʋ kʋm ba bɛ, yɩ ba daarɩ ba beeri kagʋa kam wʋnɩ taan bɩna fiinna, yɩ Moyisi dɛɛn ta kɩ wo-kɩnkagɩla zanzan ba tee nɩ.

eng: This is the Moses who told the Israelites, God will raise up for you a prophet like me from your own people. .
local: Moyisi daa ta mʋ yɩ wʋlʋ na tagɩ dɩ Yisɩrayɛlɩ tiinǝ bam o wɩ: ‹Wɛ wʋ́ pa abam dwi tu dɩdʋa zaŋɩ o taa yɩ Wɛ nijoŋnu abam tɩtarɩ nɩ, nɩ DƖ na tʋŋɩ amʋ Moyisi te tɩn.›.

eng: This is the one who was in the assembly in the wilderness with our ancestors and with the angel who spoke to him on Mount Sinai. He is the one who received life-giving words to give to us.
local: Moyisi mʋ yɩ wʋlʋ dɛɛn na wʋra dɩ Yisɩrayɛlɩ tiinǝ kɔgɔ kʋm kagʋa kam wʋnɩ tɩn, yɩ o wʋra dɩdaanɩ dɩbam nabaara bam, dɩ malɛka kalʋ na ŋɔɔnɩ dɩd-o Sinayi piu kʋm yuu nɩ tɩn. Wʋntʋ mʋ joŋi Wɛ bɩtarɩ sɩlʋ na paɩ ŋwɩa tɩn o pa dɩbam.

eng: Hes also the one whom our ancestors refused to obey. Instead, they pushed him aside and, in their thoughts and desires, returned to Egypt.
local: Dɩ kʋntʋ maama, dɩ́ nabaara bam dɛɛn ba lagɩ sɩ ba sɛ Moyisi ni. Ba ma vɩ-o, yɩ ba daarɩ ba bʋŋɩ sɩ ba joori ba vu Ezipi.

eng: They told Aaron, Make us gods that will lead us. As for this Moses who led us out of Egypt, we dont know whats happened to him! .
local: Ba ma ta dɩ Aarɔn ba wɩ: ‹Mɔ jwǝnǝ n pa dɩbam sɩ ya taa tɔgɩ dɩ́ yigǝ, sɩ Moyisi wʋlʋ na jaanɩ dɩbam o nuŋi Ezipi nɩ tɩn, dɩ́ yǝri woŋo kʋlʋ na kɩ-o tɩn.›.

eng: Thats when they made an idol in the shape of a calf, offered a sacrifice to it, and began to celebrate what they had made with their own hands.
local: Maŋa kam kʋntʋ nɩ ba ma sɩɩnɩ ba mɔ kamɔgɔ kʋ nyɩ dɩ nʋa, yɩ ba pa kʋ yɩ ba jwǝni. Ba maa jɩgɩ-dɩ ba kaana. Ba ma daarɩ ba kɩ wʋpolo dɩ ba tɩtɩ na mɛ ba jɩa ba ma mɔ kʋlʋ tɩn.

eng: So God turned away from them and handed them over to worship the stars in the sky, just as it is written in the scroll of the Prophets: Did you bring sacrifices and offerings to me for forty years in the wilderness, house of Israel? .
local: Ba na kaanɩ jwǝni dɩm kʋntʋ tɩn, Wɛ ma pipiri DƖ kwaga DƖ ya-ba. DƖ ma yagɩ-ba sɩ ba taa zuli calɩcwɩ sɩlʋ na wʋ wɛyuu nɩ tɩn. Kʋ kɩ nɩnɛɛnɩ kʋ na maŋɩ kʋ pʋpʋnɩ Wɛ nijoŋnǝ bam tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ kʋ wɩ: ‹Abam Yisɩrayɛlɩ dwi tiinǝ-ba, á dɛɛn na gʋ vara á ma kaanɩ jwǝnǝ kagʋa kam wʋnɩ bɩna fiinna tɩn, kʋ daɩ amʋ mʋ á kaanɩ á pa kʋntʋ.

eng: No! Instead, you took the tent of Moloch with you, and the star of your god Rephan, the images that you made in order to worship them. Therefore, I will send you far away, farther than Babylon. .
local: Kʋ nan yɩ Moloki jwǝni dɩm vwe mʋ abam dɛɛn zɩŋɩ, dɩdaanɩ calɩcʋa kalʋ na yɩ abam jwǝni Rifanɩ tɩn. Tɩntʋ maama nan yɩ kamwarʋ tɩlʋ á na kɩ sɩ á taá zuli-tɩ mʋ. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ amʋ wʋ́ pa nɔɔna zǝli abam ba ja vu ba gaalɩ Babilɔnɩ na wʋ mɛ tɩn.›.

eng: The tent of testimony was with our ancestors in the wilderness. Moses built it just as he had been instructed by the one who spoke to him and according to the pattern he had seen.
local: Dɩbam nabaara bam na beeri kagʋa kam wʋnɩ tɩn, ba dɛɛn jɩgɩ Wɛ vwe dɩlʋ na brɩ nɩ Wɛ wʋ ba tee nɩ tɩn mʋ. Wɛ dɛɛn brɩ Moyisi o na wʋ́ kɩ te sɩ o ma pu vwe dɩm tɩn. Ba dɛɛn sɩɩnɩ ba kɩ-dɩ nɩ Wɛ na brɩ sɩ ba kɩ te tɩn mʋ.

eng: In time, when they had received the tent, our ancestors carried it with them when, under Joshuas leadership, they took possession of the land from the nations whom God expelled. This tent remained in the land until the time of David.
local: Kʋ kwaga seeni tɩn, dɩbam nabaara bam mʋ joŋi vwe dɩm ba kwǝ tee nɩ ba zɩŋɩ-dɩ ba ja tɔgɩ Zozwe kwaga. Ba ma vu ba wanɩ tɩɩnɩ dɩlʋ Wɛ na lagɩ DƖ pa-ba sɩ ba taa zʋʋrɩ da tɩn. Wɛ dɛɛn zǝli je sɩm nɔɔna bam DƖ yagɩ ba yigǝ nɩ. Vwe dɩm maa wʋra taan, kʋ ba kʋ yi Davidi na zaŋɩ o ji pɛ maŋa kalʋ tɩn.

eng: God approved of David, who asked that he might provide a dwelling place for the God of Jacob.
local: Wɛ wʋ ma poli dɩ Davidi, yɩ o loori Wɛ sɩ DƖ pa-o cwǝŋǝ sɩ o lɔ digǝ o ma lǝni vwe dɩm yuu nɩ, sɩ Wɛ taa zʋʋrɩ da sɩ DƖ taa wʋ Zakɔbɩ dwi tiinǝ bam tee nɩ.

eng: But it was Solomon who actually built a house for God.
local: Kʋ nan yɩ Davidi bu Salɔmɔn mʋ lɔgɩ digǝ kam o pa Wɛ.

eng: However, the Most High doesnt live in houses built by human hands. As the prophet says.
local: Baŋa-Wɛ dɩlʋ na yɩ kamunu tɩn nan ba zʋʋra di sɩlʋ nabiinǝ na lɔgɩ tɩn wʋnɩ. Wɛ nijoŋnu dɩdʋa dɛɛn mʋ tagɩ o wɩ:.

eng: Heaven is my throne, and the earth is my footstool. What kind of house will you build for me, says the Lord, or where is my resting place? .
local: ‹Yuutu Baŋa-Wɛ wɩ: Wɛyuu mʋ yɩ amʋ jǝŋǝ je, kʋ daarɩ tɩga baŋa maa yɩ amʋ nɛ cwiim je. Digǝ kɔɔ dwi mʋ abam wʋ́ wanɩ á lɔ á pa amʋ? Jǝgǝ kɔɔ mʋ amʋ wʋ́ vu a pǝni da a sin?.

eng: Didnt I make all these things with my own hand? .
local: Kʋ daɩ amʋ tɩtɩ jɩŋa mʋ kɩ wǝǝnu tɩntʋ maama na?› ».

eng: You stubborn people! In your thoughts and hearing, you are like those who have had no part in Gods covenant! You continuously set yourself against the Holy Spirit, just like your ancestors did.
local: Etiyɛnɩ daa ta ma ta dɩ Zwifǝ nakwa bam o wɩ: «Abam yɩ vɩɩna zanzan. Á wʋbʋŋa dɩ nyɩ dɩ balʋ na ba tɔgɩ Wɛ tɩn wʋbʋŋa. Abam zwa mʋ kwarɩmɩ yɩ á ba ni Wɛ kwǝrǝ kam. Abam ba sɛ Wɛ Joro kʋm na brɩ cɩga kalʋ tɩn. Abam dɩ á nabaara bam kikiǝ maama yɩ bɩdwɩ mʋ.

eng: Was there a single prophet your ancestors didnt harass? They even killed those who predicted the coming of the righteous one, and youve betrayed and murdered him!.
local: Wɛ nijoŋnǝ bam wʋnɩ wʋlʋwʋlʋ tǝrǝ abam nabaara bam na wʋ bɛɛs-o. Ba dɛɛn gʋ balʋ na brɩ nɩ Wɛ wʋ́ tʋŋɩ Cɩga Tu wʋm sɩ o ba tɩn. Lele kʋntʋ abam nan mʋ jaanɩ wʋntʋ á kɩ nɔɔna jɩŋa nɩ, yɩ á kɩ daanɩ á gʋ-o.

eng: You received the Law given by angels, but you havent kept it.
local: Abam yɩ balʋ na joŋi Wɛ cullu tɩlʋ DƖ na tɔgɩ malɛsɩ jɩŋa DƖ pa abam tɩn mʋ. Dɩ kʋntʋ dɩ á ta wʋ sɛ-tɩ.».

eng: Once the council members heard these words, they were enraged and began to grind their teeth at Stephen.
local: Etiyɛnɩ na tagɩ kʋntʋ o ti tɩn, kʋ ma pa Zwifǝ nakwa bam bana zaŋɩ dɩd-o zanzan, yɩ ba dʋnɩ ba yǝlǝ.

eng: But Stephen, enabled by the Holy Spirit, stared into heaven and saw Gods majesty and Jesus standing at Gods right side.
local: Wɛ Joro kʋm maa wʋ Etiyɛnɩ tee nɩ lanyɩranɩ. O ma kwǝni o yuu wɛɛnɩ o nii Wɛ-sɔŋɔ seeni yɔɔrɔ-tɔtɔ, yɩ o na Wɛ paarɩ-zulǝ na dagɩ te. O ma daarɩ o nii o na Zezi na zɩgɩ Baŋa-Wɛ jazɩm nɩ.

eng: He exclaimed, Look! I can see heaven on display and the Human One standing at Gods right side.
local: O laan ma ta o wɩ: «Nii-na, amʋ nɛ Wɛ-sɔŋɔ ni na pʋrɩ yɩ Nabiin-bu wʋm zɩgɩ Baŋa-Wɛ jazɩm nɩ.».

eng: At this, they shrieked and covered their ears. Together, they charged at him.
local: Zwifǝ nakwa bam kɔgɔ kʋm maama na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma kaasɩ dɩ kwǝr-dɩa, yɩ ba pu ba zwa sɩ ba daa yɩ taa ni o taanɩ dɩm. Ba maama ma duri bɩdwɩ baŋa nɩ ba vu ba ja-o.

eng: threw him out of the city, and began to stone him. The witnesses placed their coats in the care of a young man named Saul.
local: Ba ma vaŋ-o ba pa o nuŋi tɩʋ kʋm daa. Ba laan ma daarɩ ba jɩg-o ba dʋlɩ dɩ kandwa sɩ ba gʋ. Balʋ na dʋl-o dɩ kandwa tɩn ma lɩ ba gwaarʋ ba pa nɔn-dʋŋʋ kʋdoŋ sɩ o ta niǝ. Nɔɔnʋ wʋm yɩrɩ mʋ Sooli.

eng: As they battered him with stones, Stephen prayed, Lord Jesus, accept my life.
local: Ba na jɩgɩ Etiyɛnɩ ba dʋlɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma loori Wɛ o wɩ: «A Yuutu Zezi, joŋi a joro.».

eng: Falling to his knees, he shouted, Lord, dont hold this sin against them! Then he died.
local: O laan ma kuni doonǝ tɩga nɩ o kaasɩ dɩ kwǝr-dɩa o wɩ: «A Yuutu, yɩ kwe ba taalɩ ba kǝm-balɔrɔ kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ.» O na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, o laan ma tɩ.

eng: Saul was in full agreement with Stephens murder. At that time, the church in Jerusalem began to be subjected to vicious harassment. Everyone except the apostles was scattered throughout the regions of Judea and Samaria.
local: Sooli na nɛ ba na kɩ te ba gʋ Etiyɛnɩ tɩn, o ma sɛ nɩ kʋ maŋɩ. Dɛ dɩm kʋntʋ nɩ nɔɔna puli sɩ ba yaarɩ Zezi kɔgɔ kʋlʋ na wʋ Zeruzalɛm nɩ tɩn zanzan. Ba maama ma jagɩ ba duri ba zʋ tɩɩnɩ dɩlʋ maama na wʋ Zude dɩ Samari nɩ tɩn wʋnɩ. Kʋ daarɩ Zezi tɩntʋŋna fugǝ-bale bam yɩranɩ mʋ wʋ tɔgɩ ba duri.

eng: Some pious men buried Stephen and deeply grieved over him.
local: Nɔɔna balʋ na kwarɩ Wɛ tɩn ma kwe Etiyɛnɩ yɩra yam ba kɩ, yɩ ba daarɩ ba keeri zanzan o tʋʋnɩ dɩm ŋwaanɩ.

eng: Saul began to wreak havoc against the church. Entering one house after another, he would drag off both men and women and throw them into prison.
local: Sooli maa kwaanɩ sɩ o cɔgɩ Zezi kɔgɔ kʋm maama. O maa yǝni o tʋlɩ sam dɩm wʋnɩ o jaanɩ baara dɩ kaana balʋ na tɔgɩ Zezi tɩn o ve o kɩ pɩɩna digǝ nɩ.

eng: Those who had been scattered moved on, preaching the good news along the way.
local: Zezi karabiǝ balʋ na duri ba jagɩ tɩn maa yǝni ba ve je maama ba tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam.

eng: Philip went down to a city in Samaria and began to preach Christ to them.
local: Filipi ma zaŋɩ o vu Samari tɩʋ kʋdoŋ o tɔɔlɩ Krisi wʋm Wɛ na tʋŋɩ tɩn kwǝrǝ o brɩ nɔɔna bam.

eng: The crowds were united by what they heard Philip say and the signs they saw him perform, and they gave him their undivided attention.
local: Nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm maama maa kwaanɩ ba cǝgi Filipi na ŋɔɔnɩ kʋlʋ tɩn. Ba maama cǝgi o taanɩ dɩm, yɩ ba kwǝri ba na wo-kɩnkagɩla yalʋ o na kɩ tɩn.

eng: With loud shrieks, unclean spirits came out of many people, and many who were paralyzed or crippled were healed.
local: Ba ma na ciciri na nuŋi sɩ yagɩ nɔɔna zanzan yɩ sɩ kaasa dɩ kwǝr-dɛ. Kwaarʋ zanzan dɩ gwanɩ dɩ ma na yazurǝ.

eng: There was great rejoicing in that city.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ wʋpolo dɛɛn maa tiini kʋ wʋ tɩʋ kʋntʋ wʋnɩ.

eng: Before Philips arrival, a certain man named Simon had practiced sorcery in that city and baffled the people of Samaria. He claimed to be a great person.
local: Tɩʋ kʋm kʋntʋ nɩ nɔɔnʋ dɛɛn ya wʋra, o yɩrɩ mʋ Simɔn. O dɛɛn jɩgɩ liri mwaanʋ mʋ o kɩa. Kʋ maa paɩ Samari tiinǝ bam yɩ yǝǝu dɩd-o. O maa brɩ o tɩtɩ nɩ o yɩ nɔn-kamunu mʋ.

eng: Everyone, from the least to the greatest, gave him their undivided attention and referred to him as the power of God called Great.
local: Tɩʋ kʋm nɔɔna maama maa kwaanɩ ba cǝgi o taanɩ lanyɩranɩ, dɩdɛɛra dɩ nabwǝnǝ maama. Ba maa paɩ Wɛ dam mʋ wʋ o tee nɩ, yɩ ba jɩg-o ba bǝ ba wɩ Dam-fɔrɔ tu.

eng: He had their attention because he had baffled them with sorcery for a long time.
local: O dɛɛn ya paɩ ba yɩ yǝǝu dɩ o liri mwaanʋ tɩm taan yɩ kʋ daanɩ zanzan. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ ba kwaanɩ ba cǝg-o lanyɩranɩ.

eng: After they came to believe Philip, who preached the good news about Gods kingdom and the name of Jesus Christ, both men and women were baptized.
local: Filipi laan ma ba o tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝrǝ o brɩ-ba Wɛ paarɩ dɩm taanɩ dɩ Zezi Krisi yɩrɩ dɩm. O na brɩ-ba kʋntʋ tɩn, nɔɔna bam ma sɛ o taanɩ dɩm. Ba pa ba miisi-ba na wʋnɩ Zezi yɩrɩ ŋwaanɩ, baara dɩ kaana maama.

eng: Even Simon himself came to believe and was baptized. Afterward, he became one of Philips supporters. As he saw firsthand the signs and great miracles that were happening, he was astonished.
local: Simɔn tɩtɩ dɩ ma sɛ, yɩ o daarɩ o pa ba miis-o na wʋnɩ. O ma tɔgɩ dɩdaanɩ Filipi yɩ o naɩ wo-kɩnkagɩla dɩ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ na brɩ Wɛ dam tɩn. O na nɛ kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ ma sʋ-o.

eng: When word reached the apostles in Jerusalem that Samaria had accepted Gods word, they commissioned Peter and John to go to Samaria.
local: Zezi tɩntʋŋna balʋ na wʋ Zeruzalɛm nɩ tɩn ma ni nɩ Samari tiinǝ bam sɛ Wɛ taanɩ dɩm. Ba ma tʋŋɩ Pɩyɛɛrɩ dɩ Zan ba tee nɩ.

eng: Peter and John went down to Samaria where they prayed that the new believers would receive the Holy Spirit. .
local: Ba na ve ba yi da tɩn, ba ma loori Wɛ ba pa balʋ na sɛ Zezi tɩn sɩ ba wanɩ ba joŋi Wɛ Joro kʋm.

eng: This was because the Holy Spirit had not yet fallen on any of them; they had only been baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ Wɛ Joro kʋm daa ta wʋ tu bantʋ wʋlʋwʋlʋ te. Ba dɛɛn pɛ ba miisi-ba na wʋnɩ dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi yɩrɩ ŋwaanɩ má mʋ.

eng: So Peter and John laid their hands on them, and they received the Holy Spirit.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ dɩ Zan laan ma daŋɩ ba jɩa ba baŋa nɩ, yɩ ba joŋi Wɛ Joro kʋm ba bɩcara nɩ.

eng: When Simon perceived that the Spirit was given through the laying on of the apostles hands, he offered them money.
local: Zezi tɩntʋŋna bam na daŋɩ ba jɩa nɔɔna bam baŋa nɩ yɩ ba joŋi Wɛ Joro kʋm kʋntʋ tɩn, yɩ Simɔn na nɛ kʋ na kɩ te tɩn, o ma kwe sǝbu o ma loori Pɩyɛɛrɩ dɩ Zan o wɩ:.

eng: He said, Give me this authority too so that anyone on whom I lay my hands will receive the Holy Spirit.
local: «A lagɩ sɩ á pa amʋ dɩ taa jɩgɩ dam dɩntʋ doŋ, sɩ a na kwe a jɩa a daŋɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ baŋa nɩ, sɩ kʋntʋ tu dɩ joŋi Wɛ Joro kʋm.».

eng: Peter responded, May your money be condemned to hell along with you because you believed you could buy Gods gift with money!.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «Wɛ wʋ́ cɔgɩ nmʋ dɩ n sǝbu kʋm maama, dɩ nmʋ na bʋŋɩ sɩ nń wanɩ n ma n sǝbu n yǝgi Wɛ pɛɛrɩ dɩm tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: You can have no part or share in Gods word because your heart isnt right with God.
local: Nmʋ wʋbʋŋa ba tɔgɩ cɩga Wɛ yigǝ nɩ. Kʋntʋ tɩn, n bá na cwǝŋǝ sɩ n tɔgɩ dɩ dɩbam n tʋŋɩ Wɛ tɩtʋŋa yam.

eng: Therefore, change your heart and life! Turn from your wickedness! Plead with the Lord in the hope that your wicked intent can be forgiven.
local: Nan lǝni n wʋ sɩ n yagɩ wo-balwaarʋ tɩlʋ nmʋ na bʋŋɩ sɩ n kɩ tɩn, sɩ n daarɩ n loori dɩ́ Yuutu Wɛ sɩ, DƖ na sɛ, sɩ DƖ yagɩ n lwarɩm DƖ ma cɛ-m.

eng: for I see that your bitterness has poisoned you and evil has you in chains.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ a nɛ sɩ n jɩgɩ pu-sɩŋa zanzan, yɩ n wʋ lwarɩm gambɛɛm wʋnɩ.».

eng: Simon replied, All of you, please, plead to the Lord for me so that nothing of what you have said will happen to me.
local: Simɔn ma lǝri o wɩ: «Nan loori-na Wɛ á pa-nɩ, sɩ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ maama á na tagɩ amʋ baŋa nɩ tɩn yɩ ba tɩ cɔgɩ-nɩ.».

eng: After the apostles had testified and proclaimed the Lords word, they returned to Jerusalem, preaching the good news to many Samaritan villages along the way.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ dɩ Zan dɛɛn ma ŋɔɔnɩ dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi taanɩ dɩm ba brɩ nɔɔna bam. Ba laan ma joori ba maa ve Zeruzalɛm. Ba na maa joori tɩn, ba ma tʋlɩ Samari tɩ-niǝ yam wʋnɩ ba tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam.

eng: An angel from the Lord spoke to Philip, At noon, take the road that leads from Jerusalem to Gaza.
local: Baŋa-Wɛ malɛka ma ba Filipi te ka ta dɩd-o ka wɩ: «Zaŋɩ n da jagwiǝ seeni n vu n da cwǝŋǝ kalʋ na zɩgɩ Zeruzalɛm nɩ ka ve Gaaza tɩn.» Cwǝŋǝ kam kʋntʋ tɔgɩ kagʋa wʋ mʋ ka veǝ.

eng: So he did. Meanwhile, an Ethiopian man was on his way home from Jerusalem, where he had come to worship. He was a eunuch and an official responsible for the entire treasury of Candace.
local: Filipi ma zaŋɩ o maa kɛa. Etiyopi tu dɩ dɛɛn maa wʋ cwǝŋǝ kam nɩ o maa ve sɔŋɔ. Wʋntʋ mʋ tʋŋɩ o paɩ ba tɩʋ kʋm pa-kana kalʋ ba na bǝ ba wɩ Kandaasɩ tɩn. O yɩ dɩdɛɛrʋ wʋlʋ na nii o jɩjɩgɩrʋ tɩm maama baŋa nɩ. O dɛɛn ya ve Zeruzalɛm mʋ sɩ o zuli Wɛ.

eng: He was reading the prophet Isaiah while sitting in his carriage.
local: yɩ o laan maa joori o ve sɔŋɔ dɩ tǝriko kʋlʋ sise na vaɩ tɩn. O na maa kɛ dɩ o tǝriko kʋm kʋntʋ tɩn, o maa je o karɩmɩ tɔnɔ kʋlʋ Wɛ nijoŋnu Ezayi na pʋpʋnɩ faŋa faŋa tɩn.

eng: The Spirit told Philip, Approach this carriage and stay with it.
local: Wɛ Joro kʋm ma brɩ Filipi kʋ wɩ: «Ve n ta n tɔgɩ dɩ tǝriko kʋm.».

eng: Running up to the carriage, Philip heard the man reading the prophet Isaiah. He asked, Do you really understand what you are reading.
local: O ma duri o vu o yi tǝriko kʋm yɩ kʋ veǝ. O ma ni nɔɔnʋ wʋm na jɩgɩ Wɛ nijoŋnu Ezayi tɔnɔ kʋm o karɩma. O ma bwe-o o wɩ: «Nmʋ ni n na karɩmɩ kʋlʋ tɩn kuri na?».

eng: The man replied, Without someone to guide me, how could I? Then he invited Philip to climb up and sit with him.
local: O ma lǝri o wɩ: «A nan wʋ́ kɩ ta mʋ a ni kʋ kuri, yɩ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ wʋ maŋɩ-kʋ o brɩ-nɩ?» O laan ma loori Filipi sɩ o di tǝriko kʋm baŋa o jǝni o tee nɩ.

eng: This was the passage of scripture he was reading: Like a sheep he was led to the slaughter and like a lamb before its shearer is silent so he didnt open his mouth. .
local: O na wʋra o karɩmɩ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ tɩn mʋ tɩntʋ: «Wʋntʋ dɛɛn mʋ nyɩ dɩ piǝ kalʋ nɔɔnʋ na vaŋɩ o ja o vu sɩ ba gʋ tɩn. O ta nyɩ dɩ pǝlbu wʋlʋ ba na fanɩ o kʋrʋ yɩ o wʋ kɩ sɔɔ tɩn. O nan wʋ puri o ni o ta kʋlʋkʋlʋ.

eng: In his humiliation justice was taken away from him. Who can tell the story of his descendants because his life was taken from the earth? .
local: Nɔɔna maa goon-o, yɩ ba wʋ di o taanɩ dɩ cɩga. Wɔɔn wʋ́ wanɩ o ŋɔɔnɩ wʋntʋ dwi dɩm ŋwaŋa? Bɛŋwaanɩ ba lɩ o ŋwɩa lʋgʋ baŋa nɩ.».

eng: The eunuch asked Philip, Tell me, about whom does the prophet say this? Is he talking about himself or someone else.
local: Dɩdɛɛrʋ wʋm ma ta dɩ Filipi o wɩ: «A lagɩ a bwe a nii, wɔɔ taanɩ mʋ Wɛ nijoŋnu wʋm tɛa, o tɩtɩ mʋ o tɛ naa nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ mʋ?».

eng: Starting with that passage, Philip proclaimed the good news about Jesus to him.
local: Filipi ma joori o puli nɔɔnʋ wʋm na karɩmɩ mɛ tɩn o brɩ-o kʋ kuri, yɩ o daarɩ o ta Zezi kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam maama o brɩ-o.

eng: As they went down the road, they came to some water. The eunuch said, Look! Water! What would keep me from being baptized.
local: Ba na tɔgɩ cwǝŋǝ kam ba maa kɛ kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma vu ba yi bugǝ. Dɩdɛɛrʋ wʋm ma ta o wɩ: «Nii, na wʋ yo seeni. N bá wanɩ n miisi-nɩ na wʋnɩ na?» .

eng: He ordered that the carriage halt. Both Philip and the eunuch went down to the water, where Philip baptized him.
local: Dɩdɛɛrʋ wʋm laan ma pa tǝriko kʋm zɩgɩ. O dɩ Filipi ma tu tɩga ba vu ba zʋ na bam wʋ, yɩ Filipi miis-o na bam wʋnɩ.

eng: When they came up out of the water, the Lords Spirit suddenly took Philip away. The eunuch never saw him again but went on his way rejoicing.
local: Ba ma nuŋi na bam wʋnɩ. Ba na nuŋi tɩn, dɩ́ Yuutu Wɛ Joro kʋm ma kwe Filipi kʋ ja viiri. Dɩdɛɛrʋ wʋm ma daa wʋ nɛ-o. O ma joori o kwe cwǝŋǝ o maa kɛa, yɩ o jɩgɩ wʋpolo zanzan.

eng: Philip found himself in Azotus. He traveled through that area, preaching the good news in all the cities until he reached Caesarea.
local: Filipi ma na nɩ o wʋ Azoti nɩ mʋ. O ma tʋlɩ tɩɩnɩ dɩm maama o tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam taan, o vu o yi Sezaarɩ.

eng: There was a man in Caesarea named Cornelius, a centurion in the Italian Company.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ dɛɛn mʋ wʋra, o yɩrɩ mʋ Kɔrɩnɛɛlɩ. O maa zʋʋrɩ Sezaarɩ nɩ. O dɛɛn yɩ Rom pamaŋna dɩdɛɛrʋ mʋ. Pamaŋna kɔgɔ kʋlʋ o na tɔgɩ o wʋ kʋ wʋnɩ tɩn yɩrɩ mʋ Yitali tiinǝ kɔgɔ.

eng: He and his whole household were pious, Gentile God-worshippers. He gave generously to those in need among the Jewish people and prayed to God constantly.
local: O dɛɛn kwarɩ Wɛ, yɩ o dɩ o sɔŋɔ tiinǝ maama zuli Wɛ lanyɩranɩ. O maa kɩ wǝǝnu zanzan o wǝli Zwifǝ yinigǝ tiinǝ bam. O maa yǝni o warɩ Wɛ maŋa maama.

eng: One day at nearly three oclock in the afternoon, he clearly saw an angel from God in a vision. The angel came to him and said, Cornelius.
local: Dɛ dɩdwɩ wɛ na guni maŋa kalʋ tɩn, o nɛ vɩɩrɩm. O ma na Wɛ malɛka na tu ka bǝŋi o yɩrɩ ka wɩ: «Kɔrɩnɛɛlɩ!».

eng: Startled, he stared at the angel and replied, What is it, Lord? The angel said, Your prayers and your compassionate acts are like a memorial offering to God.
local: O maa nii malɛka kam yɩ fʋʋnɩ jɩg-o. O ma ta o wɩ: «Kʋ yɩ ta, a tu.» Malɛka kam ma lǝr-o ka wɩ: «Nmʋ na yǝni n warɩ Wɛ yɩ n kwǝri n wǝli yinigǝ tiinǝ tɩn, kʋ yi Baŋa-Wɛ wʋbʋŋa. DƖ nan guli nmʋ gulǝ.

eng: Send messengers to Joppa at once and summon a certain Simon, the one known as Peter.
local: Laan nan tʋŋɩ nɔɔna sɩ ba vu Zope ba bǝŋi nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ, o yɩrɩ mʋ Simɔn Pɩyɛɛrɩ.

eng: He is a guest of Simon the tanner, whose house is near the seacoast.
local: O yɩ vǝru mʋ o wʋ Simɔn wʋlʋ na yɩ tɔn-gɔgɔ tɩn sɔŋɔ nɩ, nɩnɩʋ kʋm ni nɩ.».

eng: When the angel who was speaking to him had gone, Cornelius summoned two of his household servants along with a pious soldier from his personal staff.
local: Malɛka kam na tagɩ dɩd-o kʋntʋ ka ti tɩn, ka ma viiri. Kɔrɩnɛɛlɩ ma bǝŋi o sɔŋɔ kʋm tɩntʋŋna bale dɩ pamaŋnʋ dɩdʋa wʋlʋ na tʋŋɩ o pa-o yɩ o dɩ kwarɩ Wɛ tɩn.

eng: He explained everything to them, then sent them to Joppa.
local: O ma ta kʋlʋ maama na kɩ tɩn o brɩ-ba, yɩ o daarɩ o tʋŋɩ-ba sɩ ba vu Zope.

eng: At noon on the following day, as their journey brought them close to the city, Peter went up on the roof to pray.
local: Tɩga na pʋʋrɩ yɩ nɔɔna bam wʋ cwǝŋǝ nɩ ba ma twɛ Zope tɩn, kʋ maŋɩ dɩ Pɩyɛɛrɩ dɩ diini sɔŋɔ kʋm nayuu sɩ o warɩ Wɛ wɩa tɩtarɩ nɩ.

eng: He became hungry and wanted to eat. While others were preparing the meal, he had a visionary experience.
local: Kana laan maa jɩg-o yɩ o lagɩ wʋdiu sɩ o di. Ba na wʋra ba kɩ wʋdiu kʋm tɩn, Wɛ ma pa o na wo-kɩnkagɩlɩ nɩ dɩndwɩa mʋ te.

eng: He saw heaven opened up and something like a large linen sheet being lowered to the earth by its four corners.
local: O nɛ wɛyuu na pʋrɩ o baŋa nɩ, yɩ woŋo nuŋi da kʋ maa tu tɩga. Woŋo kʋm nyɩ dɩ gɔrɔ kamunu mʋ, yɩ ba jɩgɩ kʋ niǝ yana, yɩ kʋ maa tu tɩga.

eng: Inside the sheet were all kinds of four-legged animals, reptiles, and wild birds.
local: Vara dwi maama mʋ wʋ kʋ wʋnɩ, dɩdaanɩ tɩga wo-vǝǝlu dɩ zunǝ.

eng: A voice told him, Get up, Peter! Kill and eat.
local: O na nɛ tɩn, o ma ni kwǝrǝ na ŋɔɔnɩ dɩd-o ka wɩ: «Pɩyɛɛrɩ, zaŋɩ n gʋ wǝǝnu tɩntʋ n di.».

eng: Peter exclaimed, Absolutely not, Lord! I have never eaten anything impure or unclean.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ ma lǝri o wɩ: «Amʋ tu, a bá sɛ. A ta wʋ fɔgɩ a di dɩbam cullu woŋo naa kʋlʋ na wʋ maŋɩ dɩ dim tɩn.».

eng: The voice spoke a second time, Never consider unclean what God has made pure.
local: O daa ta ma joori o ni kwǝrǝ kam na wɩ: «Yɩ zaŋɩ n culi woŋo kʋlʋ Wɛ na wɩ kʋ yɩ lanyɩranɩ tɩn.».

eng: This happened three times, then the object was suddenly pulled back into heaven.
local: Kʋ kɩ kʋntʋ mʋ kuni bɩtɔ. Wǝǝnu tɩm laan ma joori tɩ vaŋɩ tɩ di wɛyuu.

eng: Peter was bewildered about the meaning of the vision. Just then, the messengers sent by Cornelius discovered the whereabouts of Simons house and arrived at the gate.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ maa bwɛ wo-kɩnkagɩlɩ dɩm o na nɛ tɩn kuri. Ka maŋa kam nɩ mʋ nɔɔna balʋ Kɔrɩnɛɛlɩ na tʋŋɩ o tee nɩ tɩn bwe ba lwarɩ Simɔn sɔŋɔ kʋm je. Ba laan ma vu ba yi mancoŋo kʋm ba zɩga.

eng: Calling out, they inquired whether the Simon known as Peter was a guest there.
local: Ba ma bǝŋi nɔɔnʋ ba bwe ba wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ zʋʋrɩ yo o yɩrɩ mʋ Simɔn Pɩyɛɛrɩ na?».

eng: While Peter was brooding over the vision, the Spirit interrupted him, Look! Three people are looking for you.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ daa ta na wʋra o bwɛ sɩ o lwarɩ wo-kɩnkagɩlɩ dɩm kuri tɩn, Wɛ Joro kʋm ma ta dɩd-o kʋ wɩ: «Nii, nɔɔna batɔ mʋ tu ba lagɩ nmʋ.

eng: Go downstairs. Dont ask questions; just go with them because I have sent them.
local: Zaŋɩ n tu tɩga. Yɩ ta n bwɛa, sɩ n tɔgɩ dɩ ba n vu, bɛŋwaanɩ amʋ mʋ tʋŋɩ-ba.».

eng: So Peter went downstairs and told them, Im the one you are looking for. Why have you come.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ ma tu tɩga, yɩ o ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Amʋ mʋ yɩ wʋlʋ abam na lagɩ tɩn. Bɛɛ mʋ jaanɩ abam kʋ ba?».

eng: They replied, Weve come on behalf of Cornelius, a centurion and righteous man, a God-worshipper who is well-respected by all Jewish people. A holy angel directed him to summon you to his house and to hear what you have to say.
local: Ba ma lǝri ba wɩ: «Pamaŋna dɩdɛɛrʋ Kɔrɩnɛɛlɩ mʋ tʋŋɩ dɩbam. O yɩ nɔn-ŋʋm mʋ yɩ o kwarɩ Wɛ lanyɩranɩ. Zwifǝ bam maama maa zul-o. Wɛ malɛka mʋ tagɩ dɩd-o sɩ o bǝŋi nmʋ o sɔŋɔ nɩ, sɩ o wanɩ o ni kʋlʋ nmʋ na lagɩ n ta tɩn.».

eng: Peter invited them into the house as his guests. The next day he got up and went with them, together with some of the believers from Joppa.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ ma pa ba zʋ sɔŋɔ kʋm ba pǝni. Tɩga na pʋʋrɩ tɩn o ma zaŋɩ o tɔgɩ dɩ ba o vu. Zezi karabiǝ badaara dɩ ma zaŋɩ Zope nɩ ba tɔgɩ dɩd-o ba vu.

eng: They arrived in Caesarea the following day. Anticipating their arrival, Cornelius had gathered his relatives and close friends.
local: Tɩga daa na pʋʋrɩ tɩn mʋ o yi Sezaarɩ. O yi da dɩdaanɩ Kɔrɩnɛɛlɩ dɩ o sɔŋɔ tiinǝ dɩ o badonnǝ balʋ o na bǝŋi tɩn maŋɩ ba je ba cǝg-o.

eng: As Peter entered the house, Cornelius met him and fell at his feet in order to honor him.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ na yi sɔŋɔ kʋm yɩ o maa zʋʋrɩ tɩn, Kɔrɩnɛɛlɩ ma zaŋɩ o nuŋi o jeer-o, yɩ o kuni doonǝ o yigǝ nɩ o jɔɔn-o.

eng: But Peter lifted him up, saying, Get up! Like you, Im just a human.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ ma pa o zaŋɩ wɛɛnɩ o zɩgɩ, yɩ o ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Amʋ dɩ yɩ nabiinu mʋ.».

eng: As they continued to talk, Peter went inside and found a large gathering of people.
local: O ta maa ŋɔɔnɩ dɩd-o yɩ ba maa zʋʋrɩ sɔŋɔ kʋm. O ma na nɔn-kɔgɔ na kikili da ba je.

eng: He said to them, You all realize that it is forbidden for a Jew to associate or visit with outsiders. However, God has shown me that I should never call a person impure or unclean.
local: O ma ta dɩ nɔɔna bam o wɩ: «Abam maŋɩ á ye nɩ kʋ culǝ sɩ Zwifu taa zʋʋrɩ dɩdaanɩ dwi-gɛ tiinǝ, naa o taa tɔgɩ dɩ ba. Wɛ nan mʋ brɩ amʋ nɩ kʋ wʋ maŋɩ sɩ a taa culi nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ naa a paɩ o taa yɩ nɔn-diku.

eng: For this reason, when you sent for me, I came without objection. I want to know, then, why you sent for me.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ, abam na tʋŋɩ á bǝŋi-nɩ tɩn, a wʋ vɩn yɩ a ba. A nan lagɩ a bwe a nii, bɛŋwaanɩ mʋ á tʋŋɩ á bǝŋi-nɩ?».

eng: Cornelius answered, Four days ago at this same time, three oclock in the afternoon, I was praying at home. Suddenly a man in radiant clothing stood before me.
local: Kɔrɩnɛɛlɩ ma ta o wɩ: «Zɩm mʋ jɩgɩ da yana, kʋ maŋɩ dɩ maŋa kantʋ nɩ wɛ na guni tɩn, dɩ a wʋra a warɩ Wɛ a sɔŋɔ nɩ, nɔɔnʋ ma da o ba o zɩgɩ a yigǝ nɩ. O zʋ gwaarʋ tɩ na nyɩɩna.

eng: He said, Cornelius, God has heard your prayers, and your compassionate acts are like a memorial offering to him.
local: O ma ta dɩ amʋ o wɩ: ‹Kɔrɩnɛɛlɩ, Wɛ mʋ joŋi n loro kʋm. Nmʋ na wǝli yinigǝ tiinǝ tɩn mʋ DƖ guli nmʋ gulǝ.

eng: Therefore, send someone to Joppa and summon Simon, who is known as Peter. He is a guest in the home of Simon the tanner, located near the seacoast.
local: Nan tʋŋɩ nɔɔna sɩ ba vu Zope ba bǝŋi nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ, o yɩrɩ mʋ Simɔn Pɩyɛɛrɩ. O yɩ vǝru mʋ, o wʋ Simɔn wʋlʋ na yɩ tɔn-gɔgɔ tɩn sɔŋɔ nɩ. O zʋʋrɩ nɩnɩʋ kʋm ni nɩ mʋ.›.

eng: I sent for you right away, and you were kind enough to come. Now, here we are, gathered in the presence of God to listen to everything the Lord has directed you to say.
local: O na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, a ma tʋŋɩ nɔɔna lɩla sɩ ba vu ba bǝŋi nmʋ sɩ n ba. Nmʋ na tu tɩn, n kɩ cɩga. Dɩbam maama nan zɩgɩ Wɛ yigǝ nɩ mʋ dɩ́ cǝgi, sɩ dɩ́ ni kʋlʋ maama dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm na pɛ-m sɩ n ta tɩn.».

eng: Peter said, I really am learning that God doesnt show partiality to one group of people over another.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ laan ma zaŋɩ o ta o wɩ: «A zɩm lwarɩ nɩ kʋ yɩ cɩga mʋ sɩ Wɛ ba kuri nɔɔna daanɩ.

eng: Rather, in every nation, whoever worships him and does what is right is acceptable to him.
local: Wɛ joŋi nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ maama na kwarɩ-DƖ yɩ o kɩ kʋlʋ na lana tɩn mʋ, kʋ na maŋɩ kʋ yɩ dwi dɩlʋ nɔɔnʋ.

eng: This is the message of peace he sent to the Israelites by proclaiming the good news through Jesus Christ: He is Lord of all!.
local: Abam maŋɩ á ye Wɛ na tagɩ DƖ taanɩ dɩm DƖ brɩ Yisɩrayɛlɩ tiinǝ bam, nɩ Zezi Krisi mʋ waɩ o joŋi nɔɔna o fɔgɩ ba dɩ Wɛ daanɩ. Wʋntʋ mʋ yɩ nabiinǝ maama Yuutu.

eng: You know what happened throughout Judea, beginning in Galilee after the baptism John preached.
local: Abam ta ye wo-kamunnu tɩlʋ maama na kɩ Zude nɩ tɩn. Kʋ dɛɛn puli Galile nɩ mʋ, maŋa kam Zan na tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝrǝ dɩ nɔɔna sɩ ba miisi na wʋnɩ tɩn kwaga nɩ.

eng: You know about Jesus of Nazareth, whom God anointed with the Holy Spirit and endowed with power. Jesus traveled around doing good and healing everyone oppressed by the devil because God was with him.
local: Abam maŋɩ á ye Wɛ na pɛ DƖ Joro kʋm Nazarɛtɩ tu Zezi yɩ DƖ kwǝri DƖ pa-o dam te tɩn. Wɛ na wʋ o tee nɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, o yǝni o ve je maama o kɩ nɔɔna lanyɩranɩ, yɩ o kwǝri o vrɩ balʋ na wʋ sʋtaanɩ jɩŋa nɩ tɩn o yaga.

eng: We are witnesses of everything he did, both in Judea and in Jerusalem. They killed him by hanging him on a tree.
local: Dɩbam nan mʋ nɛ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ maama o na kɩ Zeruzalɛm nɩ dɩ Zwifǝ tɩɩnɩ dɩm maama nɩ tɩn, yɩ dɩ́ yɩ cɩga kantʋ maana tiinǝ. Ba dɛɛn jaan-o mʋ ba pa tʋʋn-dagara baŋa nɩ ba gʋ.

eng: but God raised him up on the third day and allowed him to be seen.
local: Wɛ ma pa o joori o bi o yagɩ tʋʋnɩ da yatɔ dɛ nɩ. DƖ ma pa o brɩ o tɩtɩ nɔɔna yigǝ nɩ sɩ ba na-o.

eng: not by everyone but by us. We are witnesses whom God chose beforehand, who ate and drank with him after God raised him from the dead.
local: Kʋ nan daɩ nɩ nɔɔna maama mʋ nɛ-o, kʋ yɩ dɩbam balʋ Wɛ na maŋɩ DƖ kuri sɩ dɩ́ taá yɩ o maana tiinǝ tɩn mʋ nɛ-o. Zezi na bi o yagɩ tʋʋnɩ tɩn, dɩbam tɔgɩ dɩd-o dɩ́ di wʋdiu yɩ dɩ́ nyɔ na.

eng: He commanded us to preach to the people and to testify that he is the one whom God appointed as judge of the living and the dead.
local: O ma pa dɩbam ni sɩ dɩ́ tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝrǝ kam dɩ́ brɩ nɔɔna, dɩ́ pa ba lwarɩ nɩ wʋm mʋ yɩ wʋlʋ Wɛ na tiŋi sɩ o di naŋʋna dɩ balʋ na tɩgɩ tɩn taanɩ.

eng: All the prophets testify about him that everyone who believes in him receives forgiveness of sins through his name.
local: Faŋa faŋa Wɛ nijoŋnǝ bam maama dɛɛn tagɩ wʋntʋ taanɩ ba wɩ, nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ maama na kɩ o wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩd-o tɩn, Wɛ wʋ́ yagɩ kʋntʋ tu lwarɩm DƖ ma cɛ-o wʋntʋ yɩrɩ ŋwaanɩ.».

eng: While Peter was still speaking, the Holy Spirit fell on everyone who heard the word.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ ta na wʋra o ŋɔɔnɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, Wɛ Joro kʋm ma tu balʋ maama na je ba cǝgi o taanɩ dɩm tɩn yuu nɩ.

eng: The circumcised believers who had come with Peter were astonished that the gift of the Holy Spirit had been poured out even on the Gentiles.
local: Wɛ na pɛ DƖ Joro kʋm dwi-gɛ tiinǝ dɩ tɩn, kʋ darɩ Zwifǝ balʋ na maŋɩ ba tɔgɩ Zezi yɩ ba da dɩ Pɩyɛɛrɩ ba ba tɩn.

eng: They heard them speaking in other languages and praising God. Peter asked.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ ba ni ba na ŋɔɔnɩ ta-gɛ yɩ ba kwǝri ba zuli Wɛ.

eng: These people have received the Holy Spirit just as we have. Surely no one can stop them from being baptized with water, can they.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ laan ma ta o wɩ: «Nɔɔna bantʋ sɩɩnɩ ba joŋi Wɛ Joro kʋm nɩ dɩbam dɩ na joŋi te tɩn mʋ. Kʋntʋ tɩn, dɩ́ wʋ́ wanɩ dɩ́ cɩ ba yigǝ sɩ ba yɩ miisi na wʋnɩ dɩ Zezi yɩrɩ na?».

eng: He directed that they be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ. Then they invited Peter to stay for several days.
local: O ma daarɩ o pa ni sɩ ba miisi-ba na wʋnɩ Zezi Krisi yɩrɩ ŋwaanɩ. Ba laan ma loori Pɩyɛɛrɩ sɩ o maŋɩ ba tee nɩ da fɩnfɩɩn.

eng: About that time King Herod began to harass some who belonged to the church.
local: Maŋa kam kʋntʋ nɩ Pɛ Erɔdɩ ma zaŋɩ o ja Zezi kɔgɔ kʋm wʋ nɔɔna badonnǝ sɩ o yaarɩ-ba.

eng: He had James, Johns brother, killed with a sword.
local: O ma ja Zakɩ wʋlʋ na yɩ Zan zɩmbaarʋ tɩn o pa ba gʋ-o dɩ sʋ-zɔɔ.

eng: When he saw that this pleased the Jews, he arrested Peter as well. This happened during the Festival of Unleavened Bread.
local: O na kɩ kʋntʋ yɩ o lwarɩ nɩ kʋ poli Zwifǝ bam wʋ lanyɩranɩ tɩn, o daa ma ja Pɩyɛɛrɩ dɩ. Kʋ yɩ Zwifǝ bam candiǝ kalʋ ba na yǝni ba di dɩpɛ dɩlʋ na ba jɩgɩ dabɩlɩ tɩn maŋa nɩ mʋ.

eng: He put Peter in prison, handing him over to four squads of soldiers, sixteen in all, who guarded him. He planned to charge him publicly after the Passover.
local: O na jaanɩ Pɩyɛɛrɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma kɩ-o pɩɩna digǝ nɩ, yɩ o pa pamaŋna bana bana kuri bɩna yɩr-o ba lǝni daanɩ. Erɔdɩ dɛɛn maa lagɩ sɩ Pakɩ candiǝ kam na kɛ ka daarɩ, sɩ o laan pa ba ja Pɩyɛɛrɩ ba nuŋi, sɩ o di o taanɩ nɔɔna bam maama yigǝ nɩ.

eng: While Peter was held in prison, the church offered earnest prayer to God for him.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ Pɩyɛɛrɩ maa wʋ pɩɩna digǝ kam nɩ taan, yɩ Zezi kɔgɔ kʋm nɔɔna bam tiini ba loori Wɛ o ŋwaanɩ.

eng: The night before Herod was going to bring Peters case forward, Peter was asleep between two soldiers and bound with two chains, with soldiers guarding the prison entrance.
local: Kʋ dɛɛn yɩ tɩtɩɩ dɩlʋ tɩga na wʋ́ pʋʋrɩ sɩ Erɔdɩ pa Pɩyɛɛrɩ nuŋi pooni sɩ o di o taanɩ tɩn. Pɩyɛɛrɩ ya tigi pamaŋna bale tɩtarɩ nɩ mʋ o dɔa. Ba ya vɔg-o dɩ capʋnnʋ tɩle mʋ. Pamaŋna badonnǝ dɩ ma wʋ pɩɩna digǝ kam ni nɩ ba yɩra.

eng: Suddenly an angel from the Lord appeared and a light shone in the prison cell. After nudging Peter on his side to awaken him, the angel raised him up and said, Quick! Get up! The chains fell from his wrists.
local: Baŋa-Wɛ malɛka laan ma da ka tu ka zɩgɩ da. Pooni maa da zǝŋi digǝ kam wʋnɩ. Malɛka kam ma pɩpagɩ Pɩyɛɛrɩ tɩkǝri nɩ ka pa o zaŋɩ, yɩ ka ta-o ka wɩ: «Kɩ lɩla n zaŋɩ wɛɛnɩ!» Capʋnnʋ tɩm ma kɔ Pɩyɛɛrɩ jɩa nɩ tɩ tʋ tɩga nɩ.

eng: The angel continued, Get dressed. Put on your sandals. Peter did as he was told. The angel said, Put on your coat and follow me.
local: Malɛka kam ma ta-o ka wɩ: «Fɔgɩ n vɔ n vɔɔrʋ tɩm sɩ n zʋ n natra yam.» O ma sɩɩnɩ o kɩ kʋntʋ. Ka daa ma ta ka wɩ: «Kwe n kasaa kʋm n zʋ, sɩ n ta n tɔgɩ amʋ.».

eng: Following the angel, Peter left the prison. However, he didnt realize the angel had actually done all this. He thought he was seeing a vision.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ ma tɔgɩ ka kwaga o nuŋi pɩɩna digǝ kam wʋnɩ. O maa wʋ lwarɩ nɩ malɛka kam na kɩ kʋlʋ tɩn yɩ cɩga. O maa bʋŋɩ nɩ kʋ yɩ dɩndwɩa mʋ.

eng: They passed the first and second guards and came to the iron gate leading to the city. It opened for them by itself. After leaving the prison, they proceeded the length of one street, when abruptly the angel was gone.
local: Ba ma vu ba gaalɩ dayigǝ yɩrɩna bam dɩ kwaga yɩrɩna bam. Ba laan ma vu ba yi luu-bor-zɔŋɔ kʋlʋ na pʋrɩ kʋ yɔɔrɩ tɩʋ kʋm wʋ tɩn. Boro kʋm tɩtɩ ma pʋrɩ, yɩ ba kɛ ba nuŋi. Ba ma kwe cwǝŋǝ ba ve ba maa kɛa. Malɛka kam laan ma da ka viiri ka daarɩ Pɩyɛɛrɩ.

eng: Meanwhile, Saul was still spewing out murderous threats against the Lords disciples. He went to the high priest.
local: Sooli dɛɛn maa kwaana o yaarɩ dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi karabiǝ bam, yɩ o lagɩ sɩ o gʋ-ba. O ma zaŋɩ o vu Zwifǝ bam kaanɩm yuutu wʋm te.

eng: At that, Peter came to his senses and remarked, Now Im certain that the Lord sent his angel and rescued me from Herod and from everything the Jewish people expected.
local: Kʋ laan ma ba Pɩyɛɛrɩ wʋbʋŋa. O ma ta o wɩ: «A laan lwarɩ nɩ kʋ yɩ cɩga mʋ. Wɛ mʋ sɩɩnɩ DƖ tʋŋɩ DƖ malɛka sɩ ka ba ka joŋi-nɩ Erɔdɩ jɩŋa nɩ ka yagɩ, sɩ Zwifǝ bam daa yɩ wanɩ ba kɩ amʋ nɩ ba na bʋŋɩ te tɩn.».

eng: seeking letters to the synagogues in Damascus. If he found persons who belonged to the Way, whether men or women, these letters would authorize him to take them as prisoners to Jerusalem.
local: O ma loor-o sɩ o pʋpʋnɩ twaanʋ o pa Zwifǝ Wɛ-di sɩm na wʋ Damasɩ nɩ tɩn, nɩ wʋm na nɛ nɔɔna balʋ na tɔgɩ Zezi cwǝŋǝ kam, oó ja baara dɩ kaana o ja vu o kɩ pɩɩna digǝ nɩ Zeruzalɛm nɩ.

eng: Realizing this, he made his way to Marys house. Many believers had gathered there and were praying.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ na maanɩ kʋ na yɩ te tɩn, o ma vu Mari-ba sɔŋɔ. Wʋntʋ yɩ Zan Marɩkɩ nu mʋ. Nɔɔna zanzan dɛɛn mʋ kikili daanɩ sɔŋɔ kʋm nɩ ba wʋra ba warɩ Wɛ.

eng: During the journey, as he approached Damascus, suddenly a light from heaven encircled him.
local: O na nɛ twaanʋ tɩm tɩn, o ma zaŋɩ o maa ve Damasɩ. O na twɛ tɩʋ kʋm tɩn, pooni ma da dɩ nuŋi wɛyuu dɩ pɩpɩlɩ dɩ gilim-o.

eng: When Peter knocked at the outer gate, a female servant named Rhoda went to answer.
local: O ma vu o yi mancoŋo boro kʋm o magɩ, sɩ ba pʋrɩ sɩ o zʋ. Ka-nyaanɩ maa wʋ sɔŋɔ kʋm nɩ, o yɩrɩ mʋ Rɔdɩ. O ma vu sɩ o nii, wɔɔ mʋ maga.

eng: He fell to the ground and heard a voice asking him, Saul, Saul, why are you harassing me.
local: O ma tʋ tɩga nɩ, yɩ o ni kwǝrǝ na bǝŋi o yɩrɩ ka wɩ: «Sooli, Sooli, bɛŋwaanɩ mʋ n jɩgɩ amʋ n bɛɛsɩ kʋntʋ?».

eng: She was so overcome with joy when she recognized Peters voice that she didnt open the gate. Instead, she ran back in and announced that Peter was standing at the gate.
local: O na ve tɩn, o ma lwarɩ Pɩyɛɛrɩ kwǝrǝ. O maa tiini o jɩgɩ wʋpolo zanzan kʋ pa o duri o joori, yɩ o wʋ pʋrɩ boro kʋm. O ma vu o ta dɩ nɔɔna bam o wɩ: «Pɩyɛɛrɩ mʋ zɩgɩ cicwǝŋǝ nɩ.».

eng: Saul asked, Who are you, Lord? I am Jesus, whom you are harassing, came the reply.
local: O ma bwe o wɩ: «A tu, nmʋ yɩ wɔɔ mʋ?» Kwǝrǝ kam ma ta ka wɩ: «Amʋ yɩ Zezi wʋlʋ nmʋ na jɩgɩ n bɛɛsɩ tɩn mʋ.

eng: Youve lost your mind! they responded. She stuck by her story with such determination that they began to say, It must be his guardian angel.
local: Ba ma lǝr-o ba wɩ, o co mʋ. O maa cɩm yɩ o ta o wɩ: «Kʋ sɩɩnɩ kʋ yɩ cɩga mʋ.» Ba daa ma lǝri ba wɩ: «Kʋ ga yɩ o ciru mʋ.».

eng: Now get up and enter the city. You will be told what you must do.
local: Nan zaŋɩ n zʋ tɩʋ kʋm wʋ. Nmʋ na yi da, ba laan wʋ́ ta kʋlʋ nmʋ na maŋɩ sɩ n kɩ tɩn.».

eng: Meanwhile, Peter remained outside, knocking at the gate. They finally opened the gate and saw him there, and they were astounded.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ daa ta maa zɩgɩ o magɩ boro kʋm taan. Ba laan ma ba ba pʋrɩ. Ba na nɛ-o tɩn, kʋ ma tiini kʋ sʋ-ba.

eng: Those traveling with him stood there speechless; they heard the voice but saw no one.
local: Nɔɔna balʋ na tɔgɩ dɩ Sooli tɩn maa zɩgɩ da, yɩ ba warɩ kʋlʋkʋlʋ sɩ ba ta. Ba dɛɛn ni kwǝrǝ kam, yɩ ba nan wʋ nɛ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ.

eng: He gestured with his hand to quiet them down, then recounted how the Lord led him out of prison. He said, Tell this to James and the brothers and sisters. Then he left for another place.
local: O ma zǝŋi o jɩa o wɩ, ba cǝgi sɔɔ. O ma daarɩ o maŋɩ dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm na kɩ te o ja-o o nuŋi pɩɩna digǝ kam nɩ tɩn. O ta ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ, ba ve ba ta kʋlʋ na kɩ tɩn ba brɩ Zakɩ dɩ Zezi karabiǝ bam maama. O laan ma yagɩ-ba yɩ o vu jǝgǝ kadoŋ.

eng: After they picked Saul up from the ground, he opened his eyes but he couldnt see. So they led him by the hand into Damascus.
local: Sooli ma zaŋɩ wɛɛnɩ o puri o yiǝ yɩ o daa ba naɩ. Ba ma ja-o o jɩŋa nɩ, ba vaŋ-o ba ja zʋ Damasɩ wʋ.

eng: The next morning the soldiers were flustered about what had happened to Peter.
local: Tɩga na pʋʋrɩ tɩtɩɩtɩ tɩn, pamaŋna balʋ ya na yɩrɩ Pɩyɛɛrɩ tɩn wʋbʋŋa vugimi. Ba ma bwe daanɩ ba wɩ: «Bɛɛ mʋ kɩ yɩ Pɩyɛɛrɩ tǝrǝ?».

eng: For three days he was blind and neither ate nor drank anything.
local: Kʋ dɛɛn jɩgɩ da yatɔ mʋ o yiǝ na dwe. Maŋa kam kʋntʋ nɩ o wʋ di, o nan wʋ nyɔgɩ.

eng: Herod called for a thorough search. When Peter didnt turn up, Herod interrogated the guards and had them executed. Afterward, Herod left Judea in order to spend some time in Caesarea.
local: Erɔdɩ ma pa ni sɩ ba kwaanɩ ba beeri o jǝgǝ. Ba ma beer-o taan ba ga. Erɔdɩ ma bwe pamaŋna bam o nii, ba kɩ ta mʋ yɩ Pɩyɛɛrɩ nuŋi, yɩ o daarɩ o pa ni sɩ ba gʋ-ba. Kʋntʋ na kɩ kʋ kɛ tɩn, Erɔdɩ ma zaŋɩ Zude nɩ o vu Sezaarɩ o zʋʋrɩ da.

eng: In Damascus there was a certain disciple named Ananias. The Lord spoke to him in a vision, Ananias! He answered, Yes, Lord.
local: Zezi karabu wʋdoŋ dɛɛn maa wʋ Damasɩ nɩ, o yɩrɩ mʋ Ananiya. O ma na vɩɩrɩm wʋnɩ dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm na ŋɔɔnɩ dɩd-o. O ma bǝŋi o yɩrɩ o wɩ: «Ananiya!» O ma lǝri o wɩ: «A yuutu, nii-nɩ.».

eng: Herod had been furious with the people of Tyre and Sidon for some time. They made a pact to approach him together, since their region depended on the kings realm for its food supply. They persuaded Blastus, the kings personal attendant, to join their cause, then appealed for an end to hostilities.
local: Erɔdɩ banɩ dɛɛn tiini dɩ zaŋɩ dɩ Tiiri dɩ Sidɔn tiinǝ. Je sɩm kʋntʋ nɔɔna bam ma zaŋɩ ba la kɔgɔ sɩ ba vu ba tʋ Erɔdɩ sɩ o yagɩ. Erɔdɩ dɛɛn jɩgɩ dɩdɛɛrʋ wʋlʋ na nii o sɔŋɔ kʋm baŋa nɩ, o yɩrɩ mʋ Bɩlasɩtɩ. Nɔɔna bam ma puli ba di ciloŋo dɩdaanɩ wʋntʋ, yɩ ba daarɩ ba vu Erɔdɩ te ba ŋɔɔnɩ dɩd-o sɩ o yagɩ o ban-zɔŋɔ kʋm. Ba dɛɛn lagɩ sɩ ba ni daanɩ, bɛŋwaanɩ ba tɩtɩ tɩʋ kʋm yǝni kʋ joŋi kʋ wʋdiiru pɛ wʋntʋ tɩʋ kʋm nɩ mʋ.

eng: The Lord instructed him, Go to Judas house on Straight Street and ask for a man from Tarsus named Saul. He is praying.
local: Dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Zaŋɩ n vu Zudasɩ sɔŋɔ, kʋ na zɩgɩ cwǝŋǝ kalʋ yɩrɩ na yɩ ‹Yɔɔrɔ-tɔtɔ› tɩn ni nɩ. Nmʋ na yi da, sɩ n bwe nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ yɩrɩ na yɩ Sooli tɩn bwiǝ. O yɩ Tarɩsɩ tu mʋ. Nmʋ wʋ́ na o na wʋra o warɩ Wɛ.

eng: On the scheduled day Herod dressed himself in royal attire, seated himself on the throne, and gave a speech to the people.
local: Ba ma lɩ dɛ sɩ ba jǝni daanɩ ba ŋɔɔnɩ taanɩ dɩm. Dɛ dɩm na yi tɩn, Erɔdɩ ma kwe o paarɩ gwar-fwaarʋ o zʋ. O laan ma jǝni o paarɩ jangɔŋɔ kʋm baŋa nɩ sɩ o ŋɔɔnɩ dɩ nɔɔna bam.

eng: In a vision he has seen a man named Ananias enter and put his hands on him to restore his sight.
local: Vɩɩrɩm wʋnɩ mʋ o nɛ nɔɔnʋ, o yɩrɩ mʋ Ananiya. O ma na-o yɩ o ba o daŋɩ o jɩa o baŋa nɩ, sɩ o yiǝ yam joori ya taa naɩ.».

eng: Those assembled kept shouting, over and over, This is a gods voice, not the voice of a mere human.
local: O kwǝrǝ na zaŋɩ yɩ o ŋɔɔnɩ dɩ ba kʋntʋ tɩn, nɔɔna bam ma kaasɩ baŋa baŋa ba wɩ: «Kwǝrǝ kantʋ yɩ wɛ kwǝrǝ mʋ sɩ ka daɩ nabinzwǝnǝ nyɩm.».

eng: Ananias countered, Lord, I have heard many reports about this man. People say he has done horrible things to your holy people in Jerusalem.
local: Ananiya na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma lǝri o wɩ: «A yuutu, nɔɔna zanzan maŋɩ ba ta nɔɔnʋ wʋm kʋntʋ taanɩ ba brɩ amʋ, dɩ o na kɩ wo-balwaarʋ tɩlʋ maama o ma cɔgɩ nmʋ nɔɔna balʋ na wʋ Zeruzalɛm nɩ tɩn.

eng: Immediately an angel from the Lord struck Herod down, because he didnt give the honor to God. He was eaten by worms and died.
local: Erɔdɩ na sɛ ba zulǝ yam yɩ o wʋ pɛ Wɛ DƖ zulǝ tɩn, Baŋa-Wɛ malɛka ma pa yawɩʋ ja-o lɩla. Kanzwa ma zʋ o wʋ ya di-o ya gʋ.

eng: Hes here with authority from the chief priests to arrest everyone who calls on your name.
local: O nan tu yo dɩ ni dɩlʋ o na joŋi Zwifǝ kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam tee nɩ tɩn mʋ, sɩ o ma ja balʋ maama na bǝ nmʋ nɩ ba Yuutu tɩn.».

eng: Gods word continued to grow and increase.
local: Wɛ kwǝrǝ kam dɛɛn maa jagɩ je maama, yɩ nɔɔna zanzan sɛ-ka.

eng: The Lord replied, Go! This man is the agent I have chosen to carry my name before Gentiles, kings, and Israelites.
local: Dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Nan ve, bɛŋwaanɩ wʋntʋ mʋ yɩ wʋlʋ amʋ na kuri sɩ o taa tʋŋɩ o pa-nɩ, sɩ o daarɩ o pa amʋ yɩrɩ dɩm zaŋɩ Yisɩrayɛlɩ tiinǝ bam tee nɩ dɩdaanɩ dwi-gɛ tiinǝ dɩ ba pwa dɩ tee nɩ.

eng: Barnabas and Saul returned to Antioch from Jerusalem after completing their mission, bringing with them John, who was also known as Mark.
local: Banabasɩ dɩ Sooli ma ti tɩtʋŋɩ dɩlʋ ba na ve Zeruzalɛm sɩ ba tʋŋɩ tɩn. Ba laan ma ja Zan Marɩkɩ ba wǝli ba tɩtɩ nɩ, yɩ ba nuŋi Zeruzalɛm ba joori ba vu Antɩɔsɩ.

eng: I will show him how much he must suffer for the sake of my name.
local: Amʋ tɩtɩ nan wʋ́ brɩ-o yaara yalʋ maama o na maŋɩ sɩ o na amʋ yɩrɩ ŋwaanɩ tɩn.».

eng: Ananias went to the house. He placed his hands on Saul and said, Brother Saul, the Lord sent me—Jesus, who appeared to you on the way as you were coming here. He sent me so that you could see again and be filled with the Holy Spirit.
local: Ananiya laan ma zaŋɩ o vu o yi sɔŋɔ kʋm o zʋ. O ma na Sooli yɩ o daŋɩ o jɩa o baŋa nɩ o wɩ: «A ko-bu Sooli, dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi tɩtɩ mʋ tʋŋɩ amʋ nmʋ tee nɩ. Wʋntʋ ya mʋ brɩ o tɩtɩ nmʋ yigǝ nɩ maŋa kalʋ n ya na wʋ cwǝŋǝ nɩ n maa n bɩɩnɩ tɩn. O tʋŋɩ-nɩ sɩ a ba a pa n joori n ta n naɩ mʋ, sɩ n daarɩ n joŋi Wɛ Joro kʋm dam.».

eng: Instantly, flakes fell from Sauls eyes and he could see again. He got up and was baptized.
local: Ananiya na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, bɩdwɩ baŋa nɩ mʋ wǝǝnu dwanɩ Sooli yiǝ nɩ tɩ tʋ tɩga nɩ nɩnɛɛnɩ ŋwam-pʋrrʋ te tɩ daarɩ, yɩ o laan naɩ. O ma zaŋɩ o zɩgɩ wɛɛnɩ. O ma pa ba miis-o na wʋnɩ Zezi yɩrɩ ŋwaanɩ.

eng: After eating, he regained his strength. He stayed with the disciples in Damascus for several days.
local: O laan ma di wʋdiu yɩ o dam joori dɩ ba. O ma maŋɩ Zezi karabiǝ bam tee nɩ Damasɩ nɩ da fɩnfɩɩn.

eng: Right away, he began to preach about Jesus in the synagogues. He is Gods Son, he declared.
local: Sooli dɛɛn na wʋ Damasɩ nɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, o maa yǝni o vu Zwifǝ bam Wɛ-di sɩm o brɩ-ba Zezi taanɩ. O maa tɛ o wɩ: «Zezi yɩ Wɛ Bu mʋ.».

eng: Everyone who heard him was baffled. They questioned each other, Isnt he the one who was wreaking havoc among those in Jerusalem who called on this name? Hadnt he come here to take those same people as prisoners to the chief priests.
local: Kʋ ma sʋ nɔɔna balʋ maama na ni o zaasɩm dɩm tɩn. Ba ma bwe daanɩ ba wɩ: «Kʋ daɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ dɛɛn mʋ wʋ Zeruzalɛm nɩ o cɔgɩ balʋ na ma Zezi ba bǝ nɩ ba Yuutu tɩn na? Kʋ nan daɩ kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ o tu yo seeni, sɩ o taa jaanɩ Zezi karabiǝ o ve o paɩ Zwifǝ kaanɩm yigǝ tiinǝ bam na?».

eng: But Saul grew stronger and stronger. He confused the Jews who lived in Damascus by proving that Jesus is the Christ.
local: Sooli dɛɛn maa ve yigǝ dɩ baarɩ zanzan, yɩ o brɩ nɔɔna bam nɩ Zezi mʋ sɩɩnɩ o yɩ Krisi wʋm Wɛ na tʋŋɩ tɩn. Kʋ ma paɩ Zwifǝ balʋ na zʋʋrɩ Damasɩ nɩ tɩn daa warɩ o ni-taanɩ ba lǝri.

eng: After this had gone on for some time, the Jews hatched a plot to kill Saul.
local: Da zanzan na kɛ tɩn, Zwifǝ bam ma kɩ ni daanɩ sɩ ba gʋ Sooli.

eng: However, he found out about their scheme. They were keeping watch at the city gates around the clock so they could assassinate him.
local: O ma lwarɩ ba na bʋŋɩ sɩ ba kɩ-o kʋlʋ tɩn. Dɛ maama wɩa dɩ tɩtɩɩ ba zɩgɩ ba yɩrɩ tɩʋ kʋm niǝ yam mʋ, sɩ Sooli na tu sɩ o da da, sɩ ba laan gʋ-o.

eng: But his disciples took him by night and lowered him in a basket through an opening in the city wall.
local: Dɛ dɩdwɩ tɩtɩɩ nɩ mʋ o karabiǝ bam ma pa o zʋ tɩtɔgɔ wʋ, yɩ ba lǝ ŋʋna kʋ ni nɩ ba pa o da kǝbrǝ o tu tɩga yɩ o lu.

eng: When Saul arrived in Jerusalem, he tried to join the disciples, but they were all afraid of him. They didnt believe he was really a disciple.
local: Sooli laan ma vu Zeruzalɛm. O maa kwaanɩ sɩ o tɔgɩ o wǝli Zezi karabiǝ bam wʋnɩ. Ba maama maa fʋna dɩd-o, yɩ ba ba bʋŋɩ nɩ o sɩɩnɩ o ji Zezi karabu.

eng: Then Barnabas brought Saul to the apostles and told them the story about how Saul saw the Lord on the way and that the Lord had spoken to Saul. He also told them about the confidence with which Saul had preached in the name of Jesus in Damascus.
local: Banabasɩ ma vu o zǝn-o, yɩ o ja-o o vu Zezi tɩntʋŋna bam te. O ma ta o brɩ-ba Sooli na nɛ dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi cwǝŋǝ nɩ yɩ o ŋɔɔnɩ dɩd-o te tɩn. O ta ma ta o brɩ-ba Sooli na kwaanɩ o ŋɔɔnɩ dɩ baarɩ o pa balʋ na wʋ Damasɩ nɩ tɩn lwarɩ Wɛ cɩga kam dɩ Zezi yɩrɩ te tɩn.

eng: After this, Saul moved freely among the disciples in Jerusalem and was speaking with confidence in the name of the Lord.
local: Sooli ma maŋɩ Zezi karabiǝ bam tee nɩ, yɩ o tʋlɩ Zeruzalɛm ni maama o tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝrǝ dɩ dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi yɩrɩ o brɩ nɔɔna.

eng: He got into debates with the Greek-speaking Jews as well, but they tried to kill him.
local: O maa yǝni o ŋɔɔnɩ dɩ Zwifǝ balʋ na ŋɔɔnɩ Grɛkɩ tiinǝ taanɩ tɩn, yɩ o magɩ kantɔgɔ dɩ ba. Ba laan maa kwaanɩ sɩ ba gʋ-o.

eng: When the family of believers learned about this, they escorted him down to Caesarea and sent him off to Tarsus.
local: Zezi karabiǝ bam na lwarɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma ja-o ba ja vu Sezaarɩ, yɩ ba pa o kɛ o vu Tarɩsɩ.

eng: Then the church throughout Judea, Galilee, and Samaria enjoyed a time of peace. God strengthened the church, and its life was marked by reverence for the Lord. Encouraged by the Holy Spirit, the church continued to grow in numbers.
local: Zezi kɔgɔ kʋm nɔɔna balʋ maama na wʋ Zude dɩ Galile dɩ Samari je sɩm nɩ tɩn dɛɛn ma na siun dɩ ba yaara yam. Ba kɔgɔ kʋm ma fɔgɩ kʋ pulǝ kʋ wǝli da, Wɛ Joro kʋm na zǝni-ba tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Ba maa tɔgɩ dɩ́ Yuutu yɩ ba sɛ-o ba ŋwɩa maama wʋnɩ.

eng: As Peter toured the whole region, he went to visit Gods holy people in Lydda.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ dɛɛn yǝni o beeri o ve je maama mʋ o nii Zezi karabiǝ bam. Dɛ dɩdwɩ o daa ma zaŋɩ o vu sɩ o nii karabiǝ balʋ na zʋʋrɩ Lidi nɩ tɩn.

eng: There he found a man named Aeneas who was paralyzed and had been confined to his bed for eight years.
local: O na yi da tɩn, o ma na nɔɔnʋ o yɩrɩ mʋ Ene. O dɛɛn yɩ kɔrɔ mʋ, yɩ o tigi bɩna nana o warɩ o zaŋɩ wɛɛnɩ.

eng: Peter said to him, Aeneas, Jesus Christ heals you! Get up and make your bed. At once he got up.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ na nɛ-o tɩn, o ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Ene, Zezi Krisi wʋ́ pa-m yazurǝ. Nan zaŋɩ wɛɛnɩ sɩ n pri n sara kam n tiŋi.» O na tagɩ kʋntʋ bɩdwɩ baŋa nɩ tɩn, mʋ Ene zaŋɩ wɛɛnɩ.

eng: Everyone who lived in Lydda and Sharon saw him and turned to the Lord.
local: Nɔɔna balʋ maama na zʋʋrɩ Lidi nɩ dɩ Sarɔn je sɩm maama nɩ tɩn ma na Ene na nɛ yazurǝ. Ba laan ma ba ba tɔgɩ dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi.

eng: In Joppa there was a disciple named Tabitha . Her life overflowed with good works and compassionate acts on behalf of those in need.
local: Kaanɩ wʋdoŋ dɛɛn mʋ wʋ Zope nɩ, o yɩrɩ mʋ Tabita. O maa yɩ Zezi karabu. O maa kɩ lanyɩranɩ yɩ o wǝli yinigǝ tiinǝ zanzan.

eng: About that time, though, she became so ill that she died. After they washed her body, they laid her in an upstairs room.
local: Maŋa kam kʋntʋ nɩ, o ma ba o ba jɩgɩ yazurǝ yɩ o tɩ. Nɔɔna ma kwe-o ba swɛ, yɩ ba ja-o ba di wɛyuu digǝ ba tiŋi da.

eng: Since Lydda was near Joppa, when the disciples heard that Peter was there, they sent two people to Peter. They urged, Please come right away.
local: Zope nan batwarɩ dɩdaanɩ Lidi. Kʋntʋ tɩn, Zezi karabiǝ balʋ na wʋ Zope nɩ tɩn ma lwarɩ nɩ Pɩyɛɛrɩ tu Lidi o wʋra. Ba ma tʋŋɩ nɔɔna bale o tee nɩ. Ba ma vu ba loor-o ba wɩ: «Popo, kɩ lɩla n ba dɩbam tɩʋ kʋm.».

eng: Peter went with them. Upon his arrival, he was taken to the upstairs room. All the widows stood beside him, crying as they showed the tunics and other clothing Dorcas made when she was alive.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ ma zaŋɩ o tɔgɩ dɩ ba o kɛ. O na yi Zope tɩn, ba ma ja-o ba di wɛyuu digǝ kam. Kadǝnǝ bam maama maa zɩgɩ ba gilim-o yɩ ba keerǝ. Ba ma brɩ-o gwaarʋ tɩlʋ maama Dɔrkasɩ ya na ŋwɩ yɩ o nyaanɩ-tɩ o pa-ba tɩn.

eng: Peter sent everyone out of the room, then knelt and prayed. He turned to the body and said, Tabitha, get up! She opened her eyes, saw Peter, and sat up.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ laan ma pa ba maama nuŋi pooni ba daar-o. O ma kuni doonǝ tɩga nɩ o warɩ Wɛ. O laan ma pipiri o nii tʋ wʋm yɩ o wɩ: «Tabita, zaŋɩ wɛɛnɩ.» O ma puri o yiǝ o na Pɩyɛɛrɩ. O ma zaŋɩ o je.

eng: He gave her his hand and raised her up. Then he called Gods holy people, including the widows, and presented her alive to them.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ ma twɩ o jɩŋa o ja-o o pa o zaŋɩ wɛɛnɩ o zɩgɩ. O laan ma bǝŋi kadǝnǝ bam dɩ Zezi karabiǝ bam maama. O ma pa Tabita zɩgɩ ba yigǝ nɩ yɩ o brɩ-ba sɩ o ŋwɩ.

eng: The news spread throughout Joppa, and many put their faith in the Lord.
local: Zope je sɩm nɔɔna bam maama ma ni kǝm dɩm kʋntʋ ŋwa. Kʋ ma paɩ nɔɔna zanzan kɩ ba wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi.

eng: Peter stayed for some time in Joppa with a certain tanner named Simon.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ ma maŋɩ Zope nɩ taan da zanzan. O dɛɛn zʋʋrɩ tɔn-gɔgɔ kʋdoŋ tee nɩ mʋ, o yɩrɩ mʋ Simɔn.

eng: The church at Antioch included prophets and teachers: Barnabas, Simeon , Lucius from Cyrene, Manaen , and Saul.
local: Zezi kɔgɔ kʋlʋ na wʋ Antɩɔsɩ nɩ tɩn dɛɛn jɩgɩ Wɛ nijoŋnǝ dɩ balʋ na brɩ Wɛ taanɩ dɩm tɩn ba tɩtarɩ nɩ. Bantʋ mʋ yɩ Banabasɩ, dɩ Simeyon wʋlʋ ba na bǝ nɩ nazono tɩn, dɩ Sirɛɛnɩ tu Lukiyusi, dɩ Manayɛn wʋlʋ dɛɛn na yɩ gʋvɩrma tu Erɔdɩ yuu-doŋo ba biini nɩ tɩn, dɩdaanɩ Sooli.

eng: As they were worshipping the Lord and fasting, the Holy Spirit said, Appoint Barnabas and Saul to the work I have called them to undertake.
local: Dɛ dɩdwɩ ba maa wʋra ba zuli Baŋa-Wɛ, yɩ ba ta kwǝri ba vɔ ni. Wɛ Joro kʋm laan ma ta kʋ brɩ-ba kʋ wɩ: «Lɩ-na Banabasɩ dɩ Sooli amʋ ŋwaanɩ, sɩ ba taa tʋŋɩ tɩtʋŋɩ dɩlʋ amʋ na kuri-ba sɩ ba tʋŋɩ tɩn.».

eng: The same thing happened in Iconium. Paul and Barnabas entered the Jewish synagogue and spoke as they had before. As a result, a huge number of Jews and Greeks believed.
local: Pooli dɩ Banabasɩ na wʋ Yikon nɩ tɩn, ba daa ta ve Zwifǝ bam Wɛ-digǝ kam. Ba ŋɔɔnɩ Wɛ taanɩ dɩm ba brɩ nɔɔna bam, yɩ ba zanzan kɩ ba wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Zezi, Zwifǝ dɩ dwi-gɛ tiinǝ dɩ.

eng: After they fasted and prayed, they laid their hands on these two and sent them off.
local: Zezi karabiǝ bam na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, ba daa ma vɔ ni yɩ ba loori Wɛ ba pa-ba. Ba ma daarɩ ba daŋɩ ba jɩa ba baŋa nɩ, yɩ ba laan banɩ-ba yɩ ba viiri.

eng: However, the Jews who rejected the faith stirred up the Gentiles, poisoning their minds against the brothers.
local: Kʋ daarɩ Zwifǝ badonnǝ vɩn Wɛ taanɩ dɩm. Bantʋ ma zaŋɩ ba vugimi dwi-gɛ tiinǝ bam wʋbʋŋa ba pa ba wʋrʋ cɔgɩ dɩ Zezi karabiǝ bam.

eng: After the Holy Spirit sent them on their way, they went down to Seleucia. From there they sailed to Cyprus.
local: Wɛ Joro kʋm dɛɛn na tʋŋɩ Banabasɩ dɩ Sooli kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma vu ba yi Seliusi. Ba ma zʋ naboro ba tɔgɩ nɩnɩʋ kʋm wʋ ba vu ba bɛ ba yi Sipri.

eng: Nevertheless, Paul and Barnabas stayed there for quite some time, confidently speaking about the Lord. And the Lord confirmed the word about his grace by the signs and wonders he enabled them to perform.
local: Pooli dɩ Banabasɩ maa zʋʋrɩ da taan. Ba na wʋra kʋntʋ tɩn, ba maa ŋɔɔnɩ dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi taanɩ dɩ baarɩ yɩ ba kɩ ba wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩd-o. Ba maa tɛ dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm na kɩ nɔɔna lanyɩranɩ te tɩn, yɩ o zɩgɩ ba taanɩ dɩm kwaga nɩ dɩ o na pɛ-ba dam sɩ ba taa kɩ wo-kɩnkagɩla dɩ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ na brɩ Wɛ dam tɩn.

eng: In Salamis they proclaimed Gods word in the Jewish synagogues. John was with them as their assistant.
local: Ba ma vu ba zʋ Sipri tɩʋ kʋdoŋ, kʋ yɩrɩ mʋ Salamini. Ba na yi da tɩn, ba maa zʋʋrɩ Zwifǝ bam Wɛ-di sɩm ba tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝrǝ. Zan Marɩkɩ maa wʋ ba tee nɩ o wǝli-ba.

eng: The people of the city were divided—some siding with the Jews, others with the Lords messengers.
local: Tɩʋ kʋm nɔɔna bam dɛɛn pɔɔrɩ bɩle mʋ. Badaara tɔgɩ Zwifǝ bam mʋ, yɩ badonnǝ dɩ tɔgɩ Zezi tɩntʋŋna bam.

eng: They traveled throughout the island until they arrived at Paphos. There they found a certain man named Bar-Jesus, a Jew who was a false prophet and practiced sorcery.
local: Ba ma zaŋɩ Salamini nɩ ba tʋlɩ je sɩm maama ba kɛ. Ba ma vu ba yi Pafɔsɩ. Ba ma jeeri liri tu wʋdoŋ dáanɩ, o yɩrɩ mʋ Bar-Zezi, yɩ o yɩ Zwifu. O dɛɛn paɩ o yɩ Wɛ nijoŋnu mʋ yɩ o daɩ.

eng: Then some Gentiles and Jews, including their leaders, hatched a plot to mistreat and stone Paul and Barnabas.
local: Dwi-gɛ tiinǝ dɩ Zwifǝ badonnǝ dɛɛn ma lɩ wʋbʋŋa ba wǝli dɩdaanɩ ba yigǝ tiinǝ bam, sɩ ba yaarɩ Zezi tɩntʋŋna bam, sɩ ba dʋlɩ-ba dɩ kandwa.

eng: He kept company with the governor of that province, an intelligent man named Sergius Paulus. The governor sent for Barnabas and Saul since he wanted to hear Gods word.
local: O maa yɩ gʋvɩrma tu Sɛrɩziu Polusi kwaga nɔɔnʋ mʋ. Gʋvɩrma tu wʋm maa jɩgɩ swan lanyɩranɩ. O ma bǝŋi Banabasɩ dɩ Sooli o pa ba ba o te. O maa lagɩ sɩ o ni Wɛ kwǝrǝ kam.

eng: When they learned of it, these two messengers fled to the Lycaonian cities of Lystra and Derbe and the surrounding area.
local: Ba na lwarɩ kʋ ni nɩ tɩn, ba ma duri ba vu Likoni tɩɩnɩ dɩm, Lisitri dɩ Dɛrɩbɩ dɩ tɩ-niǝ yam maama.

eng: But Elymas the sorcerer opposed them, trying to steer the governor away from the faith.
local: Liri tu wʋm ma zaŋɩ o wʋra o culi ba brɩm dɩm. O maa kwaanɩ sɩ o cɩ gʋvɩrma tu wʋm sɩ o yɩ sɛ Wɛ cɩga kam. Liri tu wʋm yɩrɩ dɩdoŋ mʋ yɩ Elimasɩ dɩ Grɛkɩ tiinǝ taanɩ.

eng: where they continued to proclaim the good news.
local: Ba na wʋra tɩn, ba maa tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam ba brɩ nɔɔna.

eng: Empowered by the Holy Spirit, Saul, also known as Paul, glared at Bar-Jesus and.
local: Sooli wʋlʋ yɩrɩ dɩdoŋ ta na yɩ Pooli tɩn dɛɛn jɩgɩ Wɛ Joro kʋm dam lanyɩranɩ. O ma nii liri tu wʋm vɩrr, yɩ o wɩ:.

eng: In Lystra there was a certain man who lacked strength in his legs. He had been crippled since birth and had never walked. Sitting there, he.
local: Nɔɔnʋ dɛɛn mʋ wʋ Lisitri nɩ, o yɩ kɔrɔ mʋ. Kʋ na zɩgɩ o lʋra maŋa nɩ o maŋɩ o warɩ o vu.

eng: said, You are a deceiver and trickster! You devil! You attack anything that is right! Will you never stop twisting the straight ways of the Lord into crooked paths?.
local: «N wʋ su dɩ sɩsɩɩna mʋ dɩdaanɩ balɔrɔ. Nmʋ yɩ sʋtaanɩ jɩŋa nɔɔnʋ mʋ. Nmʋ culi wǝǝnu tɩlʋ maama na lana tɩn. Nmʋ tiini n kwaana sɩ n pipiri dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm cɩga kam sɩ ka ji vwan mʋ.

eng: heard Paul speaking. Paul stared at him and saw that he believed he could be healed.
local: O dɛɛn maa tɔgɩ o je o cǝgi Pooli na ŋɔɔnɩ taanɩ dɩlʋ tɩn. Pooli ma nii-o ziin, yɩ o lwarɩ nɩ o kɩ o wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Zezi sɩ o wanɩ o na yazurǝ.

eng: Listen! The Lords power is set against you. You will be blind for a while, unable even to see the daylight. At once, Bar-Jesus eyes were darkened, and he began to grope about for someone to lead him around by the hand.
local: Nii, Baŋa-Wɛ wʋ́ ba DƖ magɩ-m dɩ DƖ jɩ-dɩa lele kʋntʋ. Nń ji lilwe yɩ n daa n bá na wɩa pooni, sɩ kʋ taa ve maŋa kalʋ Wɛ na lagɩ tɩn.» O na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, bɩdwɩ baŋa nɩ mʋ Elimasɩ yiǝ dwe, yɩ o zɩgɩ lim wʋnɩ. O maa ve o kɩkara, yɩ o lagɩ vaŋɩnʋ sɩ o ja o jɩŋa.

eng: Raising his voice, Paul said, Stand up straight on your feet! He jumped up and began to walk.
local: O laan ma ta dɩ kwǝr-dɩa o wɩ: «Zaŋɩ wɛɛnɩ n zɩgɩ n nɛ sɩm baŋa nɩ ywam!» O na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, kɔrɔ kʋm ma zaŋɩ wɛɛnɩ kʋ faŋɩ kʋ cwi yɩ kʋ sɩŋɩ vǝŋǝ.

eng: When the governor saw what had taken place, he came to believe, for he was astonished by the teaching about the Lord.
local: Gʋvɩrma tu wʋm na nɛ kʋlʋ na kɩ tɩn, o laan ma kɩ o wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Zezi Krisi. Dɩ́ Yuutu cɩga kam ba na brɩ-o tɩn ma sʋ-o.

eng: Seeing what Paul had done, the crowd shouted in the Lycaonian language, The gods have taken human form and come down to visit us.
local: Nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm na nɛ kʋlʋ Pooli na kɩ tɩn, ba maa tiini ba ŋɔɔnɩ baŋa baŋa dɩ ba tɩtɩ dwi taanɩ dɩm ba wɩ: «Kʋ yɩ dɩbam wa yam mʋ jigi nabiinǝ ya tu ya ba dɩbam te.».
