eng: Paul and his companions sailed from Paphos to Perga in Pamphylia. John deserted them there and returned to Jerusalem.
local: Pooli dɩ o badonnǝ bam laan ma zaŋɩ Pafɔsɩ nɩ ba zʋ naboro ba vu ba yi Pamfili tɩʋ kʋdoŋ, kʋ yɩrɩ mʋ Pɛrɩzɩ. Zan Marɩkɩ ma yagɩ-ba dáanɩ, yɩ o daarɩ o joori Zeruzalɛm.

eng: They referred to Barnabas as Zeus and to Paul as Hermes, since Paul was the main speaker.
local: Ba ma pa Banabasɩ yɩrɩ nɩ Ziusi. Pooli dɩ yɩrɩ maa yɩ Ɛrmɛsɩ, bɛŋwaanɩ kʋ yɩ wʋntʋ ya mʋ ŋɔɔnɩ taanɩ dɩm.

eng: They went on from Perga and arrived at Antioch in Pisidia. On the Sabbath, they entered and found seats in the synagogue there.
local: Ba daa maa zɩgɩ Pɛrɩzɩ nɩ ba vu Antɩɔsɩ kʋlʋ na wʋ Pisidi nɩ tɩn. Zwifǝ bam siun dɛ dɩm na yi tɩn, ba ma vu ba zʋ ba Wɛ-digǝ kam wʋ ba je da.

eng: The priest of Zeus, whose temple was located just outside the city, brought bulls and wreaths to the city gates. Along with the crowds, he wanted to offer sacrifices to them.
local: Ba dɛɛn maa jɩgɩ digǝ ba tɩʋ kʋm daa nɩ ba na yǝni ba kaanɩ ba wɛ Ziusi da tɩn. Ba kaanɩm tu wʋm ma zaŋɩ o ja nabɛ dɩ tweeru punnu tɩlʋ ba na sɔgɩ tɩn o ba o zɩgɩ tɩʋ kʋm ni nɩ. Wʋntʋ dɩdaanɩ nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm maama dɛɛn maa lagɩ sɩ ba gʋ nabɛ sɩm ba ma kaanɩ Zezi tɩntʋŋna bam.

eng: After the reading of the Law and the Prophets, the synagogue leaders invited them, Brothers, if one of you has a sermon for the people, please speak.
local: Nɔɔna ma zaŋɩ ba karɩmɩ Moyisi cullu twaanʋ tɩm dɩ faŋa faŋa Wɛ nijoŋnǝ bam twaanʋ tɩm wʋnɩ. Ba na karɩmɩ ba ti tɩn, ba yigǝ tiinǝ bam ma tʋŋɩ ni ba pa Pooli-ba, yɩ ba wɩ: «Dɩ́ ko-biǝ-ba, abam na jɩgɩ kwiǝ yalʋ na wʋ́ zǝni nɔɔna bam tɩn, sɩ á zaŋɩ á ta.».

eng: When the Lords messengers Barnabas and Paul found out about this, they tore their clothes in protest and rushed out into the crowd. They shouted.
local: Banabasɩ dɩ Pooli ma lwarɩ ba na bʋŋɩ sɩ ba kɩ te tɩn. Ba wʋ ma cɔgɩ zanzan. Ba ma ja ba gwaarʋ ba kaarɩ, yɩ ba daarɩ ba duri lɩla ba zʋ nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm wʋ. Ba maa tɛ baŋa baŋa ba wɩ:.

eng: Standing up, Paul gestured with his hand and said, Fellow Israelites and Gentile God-worshippers, please listen to me.
local: Pooli ma zaŋɩ wɛɛnɩ o zǝŋi o jɩŋa, yɩ o laan daarɩ o ta o wɩ: «Yisɩrayɛlɩ dwi tiinǝ-ba dɩdaanɩ dwi-gɛ tiinǝ balʋ na kwarɩ Wɛ tɩn, cǝgi-na a na lagɩ a ta kʋlʋ tɩn.

eng: People, what are you doing? We are humans too, just like you! We are proclaiming the good news to you: turn to the living God and away from such worthless things. He made the heaven, the earth, the sea, and everything in them. .
local: «Nɔɔna-ba, bɛɛ mʋ yɩ abam lagɩ á kɩ kʋntʋ? Dɩbam yɩ nabiinǝ mʋ, nɩ abam dɩ te. Dɩbam nan tu yo seeni sɩ dɩ́ tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam mʋ dɩ́ brɩ abam, sɩ á yagɩ jwǝnǝ kaanɩm, sɩ á daarɩ á taá tɔgɩ Ŋwɩa Tu Baŋa-Wɛ. Dɩntʋ mʋ kɩ wɛyuu dɩ tɩga dɩ na-fara dɩ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ maama na wʋ tɩ wʋnɩ tɩn.

eng: The God of this people Israel chose our ancestors. God made them a great people while they lived as strangers in the land of Egypt. With his great power, he led them out of that country.
local: Wɛ dɩlʋ dɩbam Yisɩrayɛlɩ tiinǝ bam na tɔgɩ tɩn dɛɛn mʋ kuri dɩ́ nabaara bam, sɩ ba taa yɩ DƖ tɩtɩ nɔɔna. Maŋa kam ba dɛɛn na yɩ vǝrǝ Ezipi nɩ tɩn mʋ Wɛ pɛ ba puli zanzan. DƖ ma daarɩ DƖ ma DƖ tɩtɩ jɩ-dɩa kam DƖ lɩ-ba DƖ pa ba nuŋi tɩʋ kʋm kʋntʋ nɩ.

eng: In the past, he permitted every nation to go its own way.
local: Faŋa tɩn, Wɛ dɛɛn yagɩ dwi maama sɩ dɩ kɩ dɩ wʋbʋŋa na lagɩ te tɩn.

eng: For about forty years, God put up with them in the wilderness.
local: Ba na beeri kagʋa wʋnɩ taan bɩna fiinna tɩn, DƖ ma kɩ wʋ-zuru dɩdaanɩ ba.

eng: Nevertheless, he hasnt left himself without a witness. He has blessed you by giving you rain from above as well as seasonal harvests, and satisfying you with food and happiness.
local: Dɩ kʋntʋ dɩ, DƖ ta yǝni DƖ kɩ abam lanyɩranɩ, sɩ kʋ pa á lwarɩ nɩ DƖ yɩ Wɛ cɩga cɩga. DƖ yǝni DƖ pa dʋa nɩa yɩ wʋdiiru kɩ tɩ maŋa nɩ. DƖ paɩ abam wʋdiu yɩ á di, yɩ DƖ paɩ abam wʋpolo lanyɩranɩ.».

eng: God conquered seven nations in the land of Canaan and gave the Israelites their land as an inheritance.
local: Ba na zʋ Kanaan tɩn, DƖ ma pa tɩʋ kʋm dwi dwi tiinǝ kuni bɩrpɛ cɔgɩ, yɩ DƖ daarɩ DƖ pa DƖ tɩtɩ nɔɔna Yisɩrayɛlɩ tiinǝ bam taa te tɩʋ kʋm kʋntʋ.

eng: Even with these words, they barely kept the crowds from sacrificing to them.
local: Pooli na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ ya gɛ fɩnfɩɩn mʋ sɩ nɔɔna bam kwe wǝǝnu tɩm ba ma kaanɩ-ba, yɩ ba laan yagɩ.

eng: This happened over a period of about four hundred fifty years. After this, he gave them judges until the time of the prophet Samuel.
local: Kʋntʋ kǝm dɩm maama yɩ nɩnɛɛnɩ bɩna biǝ-yana dɩ fiinnu mʋ . Kʋntʋ kwaga nɩ, Wɛ laan ma kuri yigǝ tiinǝ DƖ pa-ba yɩ bantʋ te-ba taan, kʋ vu kʋ yi maŋa kam DƖ nijoŋnu Samʋwɛlɩ na tu o ba o te-ba tɩn.

eng: Jews from Antioch and Iconium arrived and won the crowds over. They stoned Paul and dragged him out of the city, supposing he was dead.
local: Zwifǝ badonnǝ ya nuŋi Antɩɔsɩ mʋ Pisidi tɩʋ nɩ ba ba. Badaara dɩ ma nuŋi Yikon ba ba. Bantʋ ma pa kɔgɔ kʋm maama wʋrʋ lǝni dɩ Pooli. Ba laan ma dʋl-o dɩ kandwa, yɩ ba daarɩ ba tuur-o ba ja nuŋi tɩʋ kʋm wʋnɩ. Ba ya bʋŋɩ nɩ o tɩgɩ mʋ.

eng: The Israelites requested a king, so God gave them Saul, Kishs son, from the tribe of Benjamin, and he served as their king for forty years.
local: Nɔɔna bam laan ma loori Samʋwɛlɩ sɩ o tiŋi pɛ o pa-ba. Wɛ ma tiŋi Bɛnzamɛn dwi tu dɩdʋa DƖ pa-ba, o yɩrɩ mʋ Sayuli. O yɩ Kisi bu mʋ. O maa ji ba pɛ bɩna fiinna.

eng: When the disciples surrounded him, he got up and entered the city again. The following day he left with Barnabas for Derbe.
local: Zezi karabiǝ bam laan ma vu ba gilim-o. O ma zaŋɩ ba tɩtarɩ nɩ o joori o zʋ tɩʋ kʋm wʋ. Tɩga na pʋʋrɩ tɩn, o ma tɔgɩ dɩdaanɩ Banabasɩ yɩ ba vu Dɛrɩbɩ.

eng: After God removed him, he raised up David to be their king. God testified concerning him, I have found David, Jesses son, a man who shares my desires. Whatever my will is, he will do.
local: Wɛ dɛɛn ma ba DƖ lɩ Sayuli paarɩ dɩm wʋnɩ, yɩ DƖ daarɩ DƖ pa Davidi ji ba pɛ. Wɛ dɛɛn tagɩ wʋntʋ taanɩ DƖ wɩ: ‹Zese bu Davidi yɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na su amʋ wʋ tɩn. Wʋntʋ wʋ́ kɩ kʋlʋ maama a wʋbʋŋa na lagɩ tɩn.›.

eng: Paul and Barnabas proclaimed the good news to the people in Derbe and made many disciples. Then they returned to Lystra, Iconium, and Antioch, where.
local: Pooli dɩ Banabasɩ na wʋ Dɛrɩbɩ nɩ tɩn, ba ma tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam yɩ nɔɔna zanzan ji Zezi karabiǝ. Ba daa ma joori ba vu Lisitri dɩ Yikon, yɩ ba daarɩ ba kɛ ba yi Antɩɔsɩ kʋlʋ na wʋ Pisidi wʋnɩ tɩn.

eng: From this mans descendants, God brought to Israel a savior, Jesus, just as he promised.
local: Kʋ nan yɩ wʋntʋ dwi dɩm wʋnɩ mʋ Wɛ pɛ Zezi ba. Wɛ ma tɔgɩ ni dɩlʋ DƖ na goni tɩn DƖ pa Zezi ji dɩbam Yisɩrayɛlɩ tiinǝ bam Vɩrnʋ.

eng: they strengthened the disciples and urged them to remain firm in the faith. They told them, If we are to enter Gods kingdom, we must pass through many troubles.
local: Ba na maa ve kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma pa balʋ maama na yɩ Zezi karabiǝ tɩn na baarɩ, yɩ ba kwe-ba sɩ ba fɔgɩ ba taa tɔgɩ Wɛ cwǝŋǝ kam, sɩ ba yɩ yagɩ-ka. Ba ta ma brɩ-ba ba wɩ: «Dɩbam balʋ maama na tɔgɩ Zezi tɩn, dɩ́ wʋ́ na yaara zanzan mʋ sɩ dɩ́ laan zʋ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋ.».

eng: Before Jesus appearance, John proclaimed to all the Israelites a baptism to show they were changing their hearts and lives.
local: Maŋa kam Zezi ta na wʋ puli o tɩtʋŋa yam tɩn, Zan dɛɛn mʋ brɩ Yisɩrayɛlɩ tiinǝ bam maama o wɩ, ba lǝni ba wʋrʋ ba yagɩ kǝm-balwaarʋ, sɩ ba daarɩ ba pa ba miisi-ba na wʋnɩ Wɛ ŋwaanɩ.

eng: They appointed elders for each church. With prayer and fasting, they committed these elders to the Lord, in whom they had placed their trust.
local: Je sɩlʋ maama ba na ve tɩn, ba lɩ nakwa Zezi kɔgɔ kʋm wʋnɩ sɩ ba taa nii-ba. Ba maa yǝni ba vɔ ni ba loori Wɛ ba pa nakwa bam, yɩ ba kɩ-ba dɩ́ Yuutu jɩŋa nɩ, o na yɩ wʋlʋ ba na kɩ ba wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩd-o tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: As John was completing his mission, he said, Who do you think I am? Im not the one you think I am, but he is coming after me. Im not worthy to loosen his sandals.
local: Kʋ na tu kʋ daarɩ fɩnfɩɩn sɩ Zan ti o tɩtʋŋa yam tɩn, o ma bwe nɔɔna bam o wɩ: ‹Abam bʋŋɩ sɩ amʋ yɩ wɔɔ mʋ? Amʋ daɩ wʋlʋ wʋm á na tɩɩnɩ tɩn. Wʋntʋ nan lagɩ o saŋɩ a kwaga mʋ o ba, yɩ a wʋ maŋɩ sɩ a bwǝli o nɛ natra dɩ.›.

eng: After Paul and Barnabas traveled through Pisidia, they came to Pamphylia.
local: Ba dɛɛn ma da Pisidi ba kɛ. Ba ma vu ba yi Pamfili je sɩm.

eng: Brothers, children of Abrahams family, and you Gentile God-worshippers, the message about this salvation has been sent to us.
local: A ko-biǝ-ba, Abraham dwi tiinǝ dɩ dwi-gɛ tiinǝ balʋ na kwarɩ Wɛ tɩn, kʋ yɩ dɩbam mʋ Wɛ pɛ sɩ dɩ́ ni DƖ taanɩ dɩm kʋntʋ, sɩ dɩ́ wanɩ dɩ́ lwarɩ DƖ na lagɩ DƖ vrɩ dɩbam DƖ yagɩ te tɩn.

eng: They proclaimed the word in Perga, then went down to Attalia.
local: Ba na wʋra tɩn, ba ma vu Pɛrɩzɩ ba ŋɔɔnɩ Wɛ taanɩ dɩm da, yɩ ba daarɩ ba vu Atali.

eng: The people in Jerusalem and their leaders didnt recognize Jesus. By condemning him they fulfilled the words of the prophets that are read every Sabbath.
local: Zeruzalɛm tiinǝ bam dɩ ba yigǝ tiinǝ bam nan wʋ lwarɩ Zezi na yɩ wʋlʋ tɩn. Kʋ daa zɩ yɩ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ Wɛ nijoŋnǝ bam na pʋpʋnɩ ba tiŋi yɩ nɔɔna yǝni ba karɩmɩ-tɩ ba siun da yam maama nɩ tɩn, ba ba ni wǝǝnu tɩntʋ kuri dɩ. Ba nan na pɛ Zezi na cam tɩn, kʋ pɛ Wɛ nijoŋnǝ bam na pʋpʋnɩ kʋlʋ tɩn sɩɩnɩ kʋ kɩ.

eng: From there they sailed to Antioch, where they had been entrusted by Gods grace to the work they had now completed.
local: Ba laan ma zɩgɩ dáanɩ ba zʋ naboro ba joori ba vu Antɩɔsɩ kʋlʋ na wʋ Siiri wʋnɩ tɩn. Kʋ dɛɛn yɩ kantʋ jǝgǝ kam nɩ mʋ Zezi kɔgɔ kʋm nɔɔna bam ya kwe-ba ba kɩ Wɛ jɩŋa nɩ, sɩ DƖ zǝni-ba sɩ ba wanɩ ba vu ba tʋŋɩ kʋlʋ ba laan na tu ba tʋŋɩ ba ti tɩn.

eng: Even though they didnt find a single legal basis for the death penalty, they asked Pilate to have him executed.
local: Ba maa ba jɩgɩ taanɩ dɩlʋ na wʋ́ pa ba gʋ-o tɩn. Dɩ kʋntʋ dɩ, ba ta ma loori Pilatɩ sɩ o pa ba gʋ-o.

eng: On their arrival, they gathered the church together and reported everything that God had accomplished through their activity, and how God had opened a door of faith for the Gentiles.
local: Ba na yi Antɩɔsɩ tɩn, ba ma pa Zezi kɔgɔ kʋm la daanɩ, yɩ ba laan tʋlɩ kʋlʋ maama Wɛ na kɩ dɩ ba tɩn ba brɩ-ba, dɩ DƖ na pʋrɩ cwǝŋǝ DƖ pa dwi-gɛ tiinǝ sɩ ba wanɩ ba kɩ ba wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Zezi tɩn.

eng: When they finished doing everything that had been written about him, they took him down from the cross and laid him in a tomb.
local: Ba ma kɩ kʋlʋ maama na maŋɩ kʋ pʋpʋnɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ yɩ kʋ ta o taanɩ tɩn ba ti. O na tɩgɩ tɩn, ba laan ma par-o tʋʋn-dagara kam baŋa nɩ ba ja vu ba kɩ yibeeli wʋnɩ.

eng: They stayed with the disciples a long time.
local: Ba ma zʋ Zezi karabiǝ bam tee nɩ ba daanɩ.

eng: But God raised him from the dead!.
local: Wɛ laan ma pa o bi o yagɩ tʋʋnɩ.

eng: He appeared over many days to those who had traveled with him from Galilee to Jerusalem. They are now his witnesses to the people.
local: O maa wʋra da kɔgɔ kɔgɔ. O na wʋra tɩn, kuni zanzan mʋ o brɩ o tɩtɩ o karabiǝ bam yigǝ nɩ, balʋ ya na tɔgɩ dɩd-o Galile nɩ ba beeri ba ve Zeruzalɛm tɩn. Bantʋ laan mʋ yɩ o maana tiinǝ ba brɩ o cɩga kam ba pa dɩbam Yisɩrayɛlɩ tiinǝ bam.

eng: We proclaim to you the good news. What God promised to our ancestors.
local: Dɩbam nan tu abam te mʋ sɩ dɩ́ tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam dɩ́ brɩ abam. Kʋ nan yɩ nɩ, Wɛ dɛɛn na goni ni dɩ dɩbam nabaara bam nɩ DƖ wʋ́ kɩ kʋlʋ tɩn mʋ.

eng: he has fulfilled for us, their children, by raising up Jesus. As it was written in the second psalm, You are my son; today I have become your father. .
local: DƖ laan sɩɩnɩ DƖ kɩ DƖ pa dɩbam balʋ na yɩ ba naarʋ tɩn. Mʋ kʋntʋ mʋ DƖ bi Zezi DƖ pa o yagɩ tʋʋnɩ. Kʋ yɩ nɩnɛɛnɩ kʋ na maŋɩ kʋ pʋpʋnɩ Wɛ lǝŋ-ŋwɩ tɔnɔ pɔɔrɩm bɩle tu wʋnɩ te tɩn kʋ wɩ: ‹Nmʋ mʋ yɩ amʋ Bu yɩ zɩm amʋ dɩ jigi nmʋ Ko.›.

eng: God raised Jesus from the dead, never again to be subjected to deaths decay. Therefore, God said, I will give to you the holy and firm promises I made to David. .
local: Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm daa ta brɩ Wɛ na wʋ́ bi-o DƖ pa o yagɩ tʋʋnɩ, sɩ o yɩra yɩ pɔ. Kʋ ma ta kʋ wɩ: ‹Aá pa n na wo-laarʋ cɩga cɩga, nɩ a na maŋɩ a go ni sɩ a pa Davidi na-tɩ te tɩn.›.

eng: In another place it is said, You will not let your holy one experience deaths decay. .
local: Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm jǝgǝ kadoŋ nɩ Davidi pʋpʋnɩ o wɩ: ‹Nmʋ Wɛ bá sɛ sɩ n tɩtɩ Wʋ-poŋo Tu wʋm yɩra pɔ.›.

eng: David served Gods purpose in his own generation, then he died and was buried with his ancestors. He experienced deaths decay.
local: Kʋ nan na yɩ Davidi tɩtɩ, wʋntʋ dɛɛn tʋŋɩ Wɛ tɩtʋŋa yam maŋa kalʋ o na ŋwɩ tɩn, yɩ o laan ba o tɩ. Ba ma kwe-o ba kɩ o nabaara bam tee nɩ. O yɩra yam ma sɩɩnɩ ya pɔ.

eng: but the one whom God has raised up didnt experience deaths decay.
local: Kʋ daarɩ, wʋlʋ wʋm Wɛ na pɛ o bi o yagɩ tʋʋnɩ tɩn, wʋntʋ yɩra nan wʋ pɔgɩ.

eng: Therefore, brothers and sisters, know this: Through Jesus we proclaim forgiveness of sins to you. From all those sins from which you couldnt be put in right relationship with God through Moses Law.
local: A ko-biǝ-ba, abam maŋɩ sɩ á lwarɩ nɩ kʋ yɩ Zezi ŋwaanɩ mʋ dɩ́ jɩgɩ cwǝŋǝ sɩ dɩ́ brɩ abam nɩ Wɛ wʋ́ wanɩ DƖ yagɩ á lwarɩm DƖ ma cɛ abam.

eng: through Jesus everyone who believes is put in right relationship with God.
local: Wɛ cullu tɩlʋ DƖ na kɩ Moyisi jɩŋa nɩ tɩn warɩ tɩ pa á lwarɩm dɩm ti. Kʋ daarɩ wʋlʋ maama na kɩ o wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Zezi tɩn wʋ́ na vrɩm Wɛ tee nɩ, dɩ o lwarɩm dɩm maama na ti wʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ tɩn.

eng: Take care that the prophets words dont apply to you.
local: Nan taá yɩrɩ-na á tɩtɩ, sɩ lɛɛrʋ tɩlʋ Wɛ nijoŋnǝ bam na pʋpʋnɩ tɩ taanɩ tɩn yɩ zaŋɩ tɩ yi abam.

eng: Look, you scoffers , marvel and die . Im going to do work in your day — a work you wont believe even if someone told you .
local: Ba dɛɛn tagɩ ba wɩ: ‹Nii-na, abam balʋ na yǝni á gooni amʋ Wɛ cɩga kam tɩn, abam yɩra wʋ́ sɔɔrɩ yɩ á tɩ. Bɛŋwaanɩ kʋ na yɩ kǝm dɩlʋ a na lagɩ a kɩ abam da yam kʋntʋ nɩ tɩn, abam bá sɛ nɩ dɩ yɩ cɩga, nɔɔnʋ na maŋɩ o brɩ abam tɩ kuri dɩ, á ta bá sɛ.› ».

eng: As Paul and Barnabas were leaving the synagogue, the people urged them to speak about these things again on the next Sabbath.
local: Pooli na tagɩ kʋntʋ o ti tɩn, o dɩ Banabasɩ ma zaŋɩ sɩ ba nuŋi Wɛ-digǝ kam wʋnɩ. Ba na lagɩ ba viiri tɩn, nɔɔna bam ma ta dɩ ba sɩ ba joori ba ba siun dɛ dɩdoŋ dɩm nɩ, sɩ ba daa ta ŋɔɔnɩ taanɩ dɩm kʋntʋ dɩ ba.

eng: When the people in the synagogue were dismissed, many Jews and devout converts to Judaism accompanied Paul and Barnabas, who urged them to remain faithful to the message of Gods grace.
local: Nɔɔna bam ma zaŋɩ ba jagɩ. Ba zanzan ma tɔgɩ Pooli dɩ Banabasɩ, Zwifǝ bam dɩ dwi-gɛ tiinǝ balʋ na nɩgɩ Wɛ yɩ ba tɔgɩ cullu tɩlʋ DƖ na pɛ dɩbam Zwifǝ bam tɩn dɩ maa tɔga. Pooli dɩ Banabasɩ maa ta dɩ nɔɔna bam sɩ ba ta kwaanɩ ba dalɩ Wɛ DƖ zaanɩ dɩlʋ DƖ na pɛ-ba tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: On the next Sabbath, almost everyone in the city gathered to hear the Lords word.
local: Ba siun dɛ dɩm ma joori dɩ yi. Nɔɔna zanzan ma la daanɩ sɩ ba cǝgi Baŋa-Wɛ kwǝrǝ kam, kʋ gɛ fɩɩn sɩ kʋ taa nyɩ dɩ tɩʋ kʋm nɔɔna maama mʋ lagɩ da te.

eng: When the Jews saw the crowds, they were overcome with jealousy. They argued against what Paul was saying by slandering him.
local: Zwifǝ bam na nɛ kɔgɔ kʋm na su je sɩm maama tɩn, ba maa tiini ba jɩgɩ wʋ-gʋrʋ dɩ ba. Ba ma yáalɩ Pooli na tɛ kʋlʋ tɩn, yɩ ba kwǝri ba twɩ-o.

eng: Speaking courageously, Paul and Barnabas said, We had to speak Gods word to you first. Since you reject it and show that you are unworthy to receive eternal life, we will turn to the Gentiles.
local: Pooli dɩ Banabasɩ laan ma fɔgɩ ba ŋɔɔnɩ dɩ baarɩ, yɩ ba wɩ: «Dɩ́ ya maŋɩ sɩ dɩ́ puli abam tee nɩ mʋ dɩ́ ŋɔɔnɩ Wɛ kwǝrǝ kam. Abam nan na maŋɩ á wʋ sɛ-ka tɩn, abam ba bʋŋɩ nɩ á maŋɩ dɩ Wɛ ŋwɩ-dʋŋa kam. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ dɩ́ wʋ́ vu dwi-gɛ tiinǝ bam te dɩ́ daarɩ abam.

eng: This is what the Lord commanded us: I have made you a light for the Gentiles, so that you could bring salvation to the end of the earth .
local: Dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm na pɛ dɩbam ni dɩlʋ tɩn mʋ tɩntʋ: ‹Amʋ Wɛ tiŋi nmʋ sɩ n ja a pooni dɩm n vu n pa dwi-gɛ tiinǝ bam, sɩ n wanɩ n pa nabiinǝ na vrɩm lʋgʋ baŋa je maama nɩ.› ».

eng: When the Gentiles heard this, they rejoiced and honored the Lords word. Everyone who was appointed for eternal life believed.
local: Dwi-gɛ tiinǝ balʋ na wʋra yɩ ba ni kʋntʋ tɩn, ba maa jɩgɩ wʋpolo, yɩ ba pa Baŋa-Wɛ kwǝrǝ kam jɩgɩ dam. Balʋ maama na maŋɩ sɩ ba na Wɛ ŋwɩ-dʋŋa kam tɩn mʋ sɩɩnɩ ba kɩ ba wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Zezi.

eng: and the Lords word was broadcast throughout the entire region.
local: Baŋa-Wɛ kwǝrǝ kam ma jagɩ je sɩm kʋntʋ maama wʋnɩ.

eng: However, the Jews provoked the prominent women among the Gentile God-worshippers, as well as the citys leaders. They instigated others to harass Paul and Barnabas, and threw them out of their district.
local: Kʋ daarɩ Zwifǝ bam ma kwaanɩ ba pa kaana balʋ jɩŋa na jɩgɩ yɩ ba nɩgɩ Wɛ tɩn dɩ tɩʋ kʋm yigǝ tiinǝ bam bana zaŋɩ dɩ Pooli dɩ Banabasɩ. Ba dɛɛn ma pa ba yaarɩ Pooli dɩ Banabasɩ, yɩ ba daarɩ ba zǝli-ba ba pa ba nuŋi ba tɩʋ kʋm wʋnɩ.

eng: Paul and Barnabas shook the dust from their feet and went to Iconium.
local: Ba na kɩ-ba kʋntʋ tɩn, Pooli dɩ Banabasɩ ma pɩɩsɩ ba nɛ fogo kʋm ba yagɩ da, sɩ kʋ brɩ nɩ ba wʋ kɩ lanyɩranɩ. Ba laan ma daarɩ ba vu Yikon.

eng: Because of the abundant presence of the Holy Spirit in their lives, the disciples were overflowing with happiness.
local: Zezi karabiǝ bam dɛɛn tiini ba jɩgɩ wʋpolo, yɩ Wɛ Joro kʋm wʋ ba tee nɩ lanyɩranɩ.

eng: The apostles and the brothers and sisters throughout Judea heard that even the Gentiles had welcomed Gods word.
local: Zezi tɩntʋŋna bam dɩ o karabiǝ balʋ na zʋʋrɩ Zude nɩ tɩn ma ni nɩ dwi-gɛ tiinǝ dɩ laan sɛ Wɛ kwǝrǝ kam.

eng: When Peter went up to Jerusalem, the circumcised believers criticized him.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ dɛɛn ma zaŋɩ o vu Zeruzalɛm. O na yi da tɩn, Zwifǝ balʋ na tɔgɩ Zezi tɩn ma magɩ kantɔgɔ dɩd-o.

eng: They accused him, You went into the home of the uncircumcised and ate with them.
local: Ba ma bwe-o ba wɩ: «Bɛɛ mʋ yɩ n vu n zʋ dwi-gɛ tiinǝ sɔŋɔ yɩ ba yɩ balʋ na ba goni ba bǝkǝri tɩn? Bɛɛ mʋ yɩ n di wʋdiu dɩ ba?».

eng: Step-by-step, Peter explained what had happened.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ ma zaŋɩ o tʋlɩ kʋlʋ maama na kɩ tɩn o brɩ-ba o wɩ:.

eng: I was in the city of Joppa praying when I had a visionary experience. In my vision, I saw something like a large linen sheet being lowered from heaven by its four corners. It came all the way down to me.
local: «A ya wʋ Zope nɩ mʋ a warɩ Wɛ. Wɛ ma pa a na vɩɩrɩm. A nɛ wo-kamunu na nuŋi wɛyuu kʋ maa tu tɩga. Kʋ nyɩ dɩ gɔrɔ kamunu mʋ, yɩ ba jɩgɩ kʋ niǝ yana ba pa kʋ maa tu tɩga. Kʋ ma ba kʋ zɩgɩ a yigǝ nɩ.

eng: As I stared at it, wondering what it was, I saw four-legged animals—including wild beasts—as well as reptiles and wild birds.
local: A ma nii kʋ wʋnɩ yɩ a na vara na wʋra, sɔŋɔ vara dɩ ga-vara dɩ tɩga wo-vǝǝlu dɩ zunǝ.

eng: I heard a voice say, Get up, Peter! Kill and eat.
local: A laan ma ni kwǝrǝ na ŋɔɔnɩ dɩ amʋ ka wɩ: ‹Pɩyɛɛrɩ, zaŋɩ n gʋ n di.›.

eng: I responded, Absolutely not, Lord! Nothing impure or unclean has ever entered my mouth.
local: A ma lǝri a wɩ: ‹A tu, aye a bá sɛ. A maŋɩ a wʋ di dɩbam cullu woŋo naa kʋlʋ na wʋ maŋɩ dɩ dim tɩn.›.

eng: The voice from heaven spoke a second time, Never consider unclean what God has made pure.
local: Kwǝrǝ kam daa ta ma joori ka zɩgɩ wɛyuu nɩ ka ŋɔɔnɩ ka wɩ: ‹Yɩ zaŋɩ n culi woŋo kʋlʋ Wɛ na wɩ kʋ yɩ lanyɩranɩ tɩn.›.

eng: This happened three times, then everything was pulled back into heaven.
local: Kʋ ma kɩ kʋntʋ taan kuni bɩtɔ, yɩ wǝǝnu tɩm maama joori tɩ vaŋɩ tɩ di wɛyuu.

eng: At that moment three men who had been sent to me from Caesarea arrived at the house where we were staying.
local: Maŋa kantʋ nɩ nɔɔna batɔ dɛɛn tu ba zɩgɩ sɔŋɔ kʋlʋ amʋ na zʋʋrɩ da tɩn ni nɩ. Ba ya tʋŋɩ-ba Sezaarɩ nɩ mʋ sɩ ba ba amʋ te.

eng: The Spirit told me to go with them even though they were Gentiles. These six brothers also went with me, and we entered that mans house.
local: Wɛ Joro kʋm ma ta kʋ brɩ-nɩ kʋ wɩ, a yɩ taa bwɛa, sɩ a tɔgɩ dɩ ba a vu. Dɩ́ ko-biǝ bantʋ bardʋ dɩ ma da amʋ ba vu. Dɩ́ maama ma vu dɩ́ yi Sezaarɩ, yɩ dɩ́ vu dɩ́ zʋ Kɔrɩnɛɛlɩ sɔŋɔ kʋm.

eng: He reported to us how he had seen an angel standing in his house and saying, Send to Joppa and summon Simon, who is known as Peter.
local: O ma ta o brɩ dɩbam o na nɛ Wɛ malɛka na tu o sɔŋɔ ka zɩgɩ yɩ ka ta dɩd-o ka wɩ: ‹Tʋŋɩ nɔɔna sɩ ba vu Zope ba bǝŋi nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ o yɩrɩ na yɩ Simɔn Pɩyɛɛrɩ tɩn sɩ o ba.

eng: He will tell you how you and your entire household can be saved.
local: Wʋntʋ na tuǝ, o laan wʋ́ ŋɔɔnɩ taanɩ dɩlʋ na wʋ́ pa nmʋ dɩ n sɔŋɔ tiinǝ bam maama na vrɩm tɩn.›.

eng: When I began to speak, the Holy Spirit fell on them, just as the Spirit fell on us in the beginning.
local: A na sɩŋɩ sɩ a ŋɔɔnɩ dɩ ba tɩn, mʋ Wɛ Joro kʋm tu ba te nɩnɛɛnɩ kʋ na maŋɩ kʋ tu dɩbam te pulim nɩ te tɩn.

eng: I remembered the Lords words: John baptized with water, but you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.
local: A laan ma guli dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi dɛɛn na tagɩ te o wɩ: ‹Zan dɛɛn miisi nɔɔna bam na wʋnɩ mʋ, Wɛ nan wʋ́ miisi abam DƖ Joro kʋm wʋnɩ mʋ.›.

eng: If God gave them the same gift he gave us who believed in the Lord Jesus Christ, then who am I? Could I stand in Gods way.
local: Kʋ sɩɩnɩ kʋ yɩ cɩga mʋ nɩ Wɛ pɛ dwi-gɛ tiinǝ bantʋ DƖ pɛɛrɩ dɩm, nɩ DƖ na maŋɩ DƖ pa dɩbam dɩ, maŋa kalʋ dɩ́ na kɩ dɩ́ wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi Krisi tɩn. Kʋntʋ tɩn, a nan yɩ wɔɔ mʋ sɩ a cɩ Wɛ yigǝ?».

eng: Once the apostles and other believers heard this, they calmed down. They praised God and concluded, So then God has enabled Gentiles to change their hearts and lives so that they might have new life.
local: Pɩyɛɛrɩ na tagɩ kʋntʋ yɩ ba ni tɩn, ba ma yagɩ taanɩ dɩm yɩ ba daarɩ ba zuli Wɛ. Ba maa wɩ: «Wɛ sɩɩnɩ DƖ pa dwi-gɛ tiinǝ bam dɩ cwǝŋǝ sɩ ba lǝni ba wʋrʋ, sɩ ba daarɩ ba na DƖ ŋwɩ-dʋŋa kam.».

eng: Now those who were scattered as a result of the trouble that occurred because of Stephen traveled as far as Phoenicia, Cyprus, and Antioch. They proclaimed the word only to Jews.
local: Maŋa kam ba dɛɛn na gʋ Etiyɛnɩ tɩn, nɔɔna yaarɩ Zezi karabiǝ bam kʋ pa ba jagɩ da-yigǝ nɩ. Balʋ na jagɩ tɩn badonnǝ ma vu ba yi Fenisi dɩ Sipri dɩ Antɩɔsɩ. Ba na jagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, ba yǝni ba tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝrǝ kam ba brɩ Zwifǝ bam yɩranɩ mʋ.

eng: Among them were some people from Cyprus and Cyrene. They entered Antioch and began to proclaim the good news about the Lord Jesus also to Gentiles.
local: Kʋ daarɩ Zezi karabiǝ badaara na nuŋi Sipri dɩ Sirɛɛnɩ tɩn ma zaŋɩ ba vu Antɩɔsɩ ba tɔɔlɩ dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam ba brɩ dwi-gɛ tiinǝ dɩ.

eng: The Lords power was with them, and a large number came to believe and turned to the Lord.
local: Baŋa-Wɛ dam maa wʋ ba tee nɩ. Nɔɔna zanzan ma kɩ ba wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi yɩ ba tɔg-o.

eng: When the church in Jerusalem heard about this, they sent Barnabas to Antioch.
local: Zezi kɔgɔ kʋlʋ na wʋ Zeruzalɛm nɩ tɩn ma ni kʋlʋ na kɩ tɩn. Ba ma tʋŋɩ Banabasɩ sɩ o vu Antɩɔsɩ o nii.

eng: When he arrived and saw evidence of Gods grace, he was overjoyed and encouraged everyone to remain fully committed to the Lord.
local: O na ve o yi da tɩn, o ma na Wɛ na sɩɩnɩ DƖ kɩ nɔɔna bam lanyɩranɩ te tɩn. O maa jɩgɩ wʋpolo lanyɩranɩ. O ma kwe ba maama kwiǝ yalʋ na wʋ́ pa ba taa tɔgɩ dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm dɩ ba wʋ maama sɩ ba yɩ zaŋɩ ba yagɩ tɩn.

eng: Barnabas responded in this way because he was a good man, whom the Holy Spirit had endowed with exceptional faith. A considerable number of people were added to the Lord.
local: Banabasɩ dɛɛn yɩ nɔn-ŋʋm. O maa tiini o jɩgɩ wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Zezi, yɩ Wɛ Joro kʋm wʋ o tee nɩ dɩ dam. Nɔɔna zanzan dɛɛn ma ba ba tɔgɩ dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm, yɩ ba dɩ wǝli o kɔgɔ kʋm wʋnɩ.

eng: Barnabas went to Tarsus in search of Saul.
local: Banabasɩ laan ma zaŋɩ o vu Tarɩsɩ sɩ o beeri Sooli je.

eng: When he found him, he brought him to Antioch. They were there for a whole year, meeting with the church and teaching large numbers of people. It was in Antioch where the disciples were first labeled Christians.
local: O na ve o na-o tɩn, o ma ja-o o joori Antɩɔsɩ. Ba na wʋra tɩn, ba maa yǝni ba wǝli daanɩ ba brɩ Zezi kɔgɔ kʋm Wɛ taanɩ. Ba kɩ kʋntʋ taan sɩ kʋ vu kʋ yi bɩm, yɩ nɔɔna balʋ ba na brɩ tɩn dagɩ zanzan. Kʋ yɩ Antɩɔsɩ nɩ mʋ ba puli sɩ ba bǝŋi Zezi karabiǝ bam nɩ Krisi biǝ.

eng: About that time, some prophets came down from Jerusalem to Antioch.
local: Da yam kʋntʋ nɩ Wɛ nijoŋnǝ badonnǝ ma zɩgɩ Zeruzalɛm nɩ ba vu Antɩɔsɩ.

eng: One of them, Agabus, stood up and, inspired by the Spirit, predicted that a severe famine would overtake the entire Roman world.
local: Ba dɩdʋa yɩrɩ mʋ Agabusi. Wɛ Joro kʋm ma pa o ta Wɛ kwǝrǝ, nɩ kana lagɩ ka ba ka tiini ka zʋ lʋgʋ maama.

eng: Some people came down from Judea teaching the family of believers, Unless you are circumcised according to the custom weve received from Moses, you cant be saved.
local: Nɔɔna badonnǝ ma zaŋɩ Zude nɩ ba vu Antɩɔsɩ. Ba maa wʋra ba brɩ Zezi karabiǝ bam ba wɩ: «Abam na wʋ sɛ á tɔgɩ Moyisi cullu tɩm na brɩ te sɩ á go á bǝkǝri, á bá wanɩ á na vrɩm.».

eng: The disciples decided they would send support to the brothers and sisters in Judea, with everyone contributing to this ministry according to each persons abundance.
local: Zezi karabiǝ bam na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma lɩ wʋbʋŋa sɩ ba la sǝbu ba ma wǝli ba ko-biǝ balʋ na zʋʋrɩ Zude nɩ tɩn. Ba ma sɛ sɩ ba dɩdʋa dɩdʋa wʋ́ pa nɩ ba na wʋ́ wanɩ te tɩn.

eng: Paul and Barnabas took sides against these Judeans and argued strongly against their position. The church at Antioch appointed Paul, Barnabas, and several others from Antioch to go up to Jerusalem to set this question before the apostles and the elders.
local: Pooli dɩ Banabasɩ na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma tiini ba magɩ kantɔgɔ dɩ nɔɔna bam. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ Zezi kɔgɔ kʋm ma lɩ wʋbʋŋa sɩ ba lɩ Pooli dɩ Banabasɩ dɩ nɔɔna badonnǝ ba wʋnɩ, sɩ ba ja taanɩ dɩm ba vu Zeruzalɛm ba kɩ Zezi tɩntʋŋna bam dɩ nakwa bam jɩa nɩ.

eng: They sent Barnabas and Saul to take this gift to the elders.
local: Ba ma sɩɩnɩ ba kɩ kʋntʋ. Ba ma kwe sǝbu kʋlʋ ba na lagɩ tɩn ba kɩ Banabasɩ dɩ Sooli jɩŋa nɩ, sɩ ba ja vu Zeruzalɛm ba pa Zezi kɔgɔ kʋm nakwa bam.

eng: Paul reached Derbe, and then Lystra, where there was a disciple named Timothy. He was the son of a believing Jewish woman and a Greek father.
local: Pooli daa ta ma vu o yi Dɛrɩbɩ dɩ Lisitri. Zezi karabu wʋdoŋ maa wʋra, o yɩrɩ mʋ Timoti. O nu maa yɩ Zwifu yɩ o dɩ kɩ o wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Zezi. O ko maa yɩ Grɛkɩ tu.

eng: The church sent this delegation on their way. They traveled through Phoenicia and Samaria, telling stories about the conversion of the Gentiles to everyone. Their reports thrilled the brothers and sisters.
local: Zezi kɔgɔ kʋm ma banɩ-ba yɩ ba kɛ. Ba ma vu ba da Fenisi dɩ Samari je sɩm. Ba na maa ve tɩn, ba maa tɛ dwi-gɛ tiinǝ na lǝni ba wʋrʋ ba sɛ Wɛ te tɩn ba brɩ nɔɔna bam. Zezi karabiǝ bam na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ ma pa ba maama jɩgɩ wʋpolo lanyɩranɩ.

eng: The brothers and sisters in Lystra and Iconium spoke well of him.
local: Zezi karabiǝ balʋ na zʋʋrɩ Lisitri nɩ dɩ Yikon nɩ tɩn maa tɛ ba wɩ, o yɩ nɔn-ŋʋm lanyɩranɩ.

eng: When they arrived in Jerusalem, the church, the apostles, and the elders all welcomed them. They gave a full report of what God had accomplished through their activity.
local: Ba ma vu ba yi Zeruzalɛm. Zezi tɩntʋŋna bam dɩ o kɔgɔ kʋm maama dɩ ba nakwa bam ma jeeri-ba lanyɩranɩ. Ba laan ma maŋɩ ba brɩ-ba Wɛ na kɩ kʋlʋ dɩ ba tɩn.

eng: Paul wanted to take Timothy with him, so he circumcised him. This was because of the Jews who lived in those areas, for they all knew Timothys father was Greek.
local: Pooli maa lagɩ sɩ o ja-o, sɩ ba taa tɔgɩ daanɩ ba tʋŋa. Zwifǝ balʋ maama na zʋʋrɩ je sɩm kʋntʋ nɩ tɩn maa ye nɩ Timoti ko yɩ Grɛkɩ tu mʋ. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ Pooli ma pa ba go-o, nɩ Moyisi dɛɛn na brɩ Zwifǝ bam sɩ ba taa goni ba bǝkǝri te tɩn.

eng: Some believers from among the Pharisees stood up and claimed, The Gentiles must be circumcised. They must be required to keep the Law from Moses.
local: Zwifǝ badaara balʋ na wʋ Farizɩan kɔgɔ kʋm wʋnɩ yɩ ba ba ba tɔgɩ Zezi tɩn ni taanɩ dɩm. Ba ma zaŋɩ ba ta ba wɩ: «Nɔɔna bantʋ maŋɩ sɩ ba go ba bǝkǝri mʋ, sɩ ba daarɩ ba sɛ Moyisi cullu tɩm maama na brɩ te tɩn.».

eng: As Paul and his companions traveled through the cities, they instructed Gentile believers to keep the regulations put in place by the apostles and elders in Jerusalem.
local: Ba laan ma zaŋɩ ba vu ba tʋlɩ tɩɩnɩ dɩm sɩ ba nii Zezi kɔgɔ kʋm nɔɔna bam maama. Ba maa brɩ-ba niǝ yalʋ Zezi tɩntʋŋna bam dɩ o kɔgɔ kʋm nakwa bam na lɩ Zeruzalɛm nɩ tɩn. Ba ma ta dɩ ba sɩ ba taa sɛ niǝ yam kʋntʋ.

eng: The apostles and the elders gathered to consider this matter.
local: Zezi tɩntʋŋna bam dɩ o kɔgɔ kʋm nakwa bam ma la daanɩ sɩ ba bwɛ taanɩ dɩm ba nii.

eng: So the churches were strengthened in the faith and every day their numbers flourished.
local: Kʋntʋ mʋ pɛ Zezi kɔgɔ kʋm nɔɔna bam na wʋ je sɩlʋ maama tɩn na dam dɩ ba Wɛ cwǝŋǝ kam tɔgɩm, yɩ dɛ maama ba kɔgɔ kʋm fɔgɩ kʋ pulǝ kʋ wǝli da.

eng: After much debate, Peter stood and addressed them, Fellow believers, you know that, early on, God chose me from among you as the one through whom the Gentiles would hear the word of the gospel and come to believe.
local: Ba na magɩ kantɔgɔ taan tɩn, Pɩyɛɛrɩ ma zaŋɩ ba wʋnɩ o ta o wɩ: «A ko-biǝ-ba, abam ye sɩ Wɛ dɛɛn kuri amʋ abam wʋnɩ, sɩ a vu dwi-gɛ tiinǝ bam te sɩ a brɩ-ba DƖ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam, sɩ ba daarɩ ba sɛ-ka.

eng: Paul and his companions traveled throughout the regions of Phrygia and Galatia because the Holy Spirit kept them from speaking the word in the province of Asia.
local: Pooli dɩ o tɔgɩ-donnǝ bam maa lagɩ sɩ ba kɛ ba vu Azi tɩɩnɩ dɩm ba tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝrǝ kam. Wɛ Joro kʋm ma cɩ ba yigǝ sɩ ba yɩ ve. Ba daa ma vu ba tɔgɩ Friizi dɩ Galatɩ tɩ-niǝ yam wʋnɩ ba kɛ.

eng: God, who knows peoples deepest thoughts and desires, confirmed this by giving them the Holy Spirit, just as he did to us.
local: Wɛ na ye nɔɔna wʋbʋŋa na yɩ te tɩn, DƖ ma pa-ba DƖ Joro kʋm, nɩ DƖ na maŋɩ DƖ pa dɩbam te tɩn, sɩ kʋ brɩ nɩ DƖ sɩɩnɩ DƖ vrɩ-ba ba lwarɩm wʋnɩ.

eng: When they approached the province of Mysia, they tried to enter the province of Bithynia, but the Spirit of Jesus wouldnt let them.
local: Ba ma vu ba yi Miizi sisǝm dɩm ni. Ba maa kwaanɩ sɩ ba zʋ Bitini wʋ. Zezi Joro kʋm ma wʋ pɛ-ba cwǝŋǝ sɩ ba zʋ da.

eng: He made no distinction between us and them, but purified their deepest thoughts and desires through faith.
local: DƖ ma wʋ pɔɔrɩ bantʋ dɩ dɩbam daanɩ. DƖ fɔgɩ DƖ kwɛ ba wʋbʋŋa mʋ dɩ ba na kɩ ba wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Zezi tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: Passing by Mysia, they went down to Troas instead.
local: Ba na kwaanɩ ba ga kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma tɔgɩ Miizi wʋ ba kɛ, yɩ ba vu ba yi Trʋasɩ.

eng: Why then are you now challenging God by placing a burden on the shoulders of these disciples that neither we nor our ancestors could bear?.
local: Kʋntʋ tɩn, bɛŋwaanɩ mʋ á bʋŋɩ sɩ á maŋɩ Wɛ á nii dɩ á na lagɩ á pa Zezi karabiǝ bam zɩŋɩ zɩla yalʋ na dummǝ ya dwe-ba tɩn? Kʋ na yɩ dɩbam nabaara bam naa dɩbam tɩtɩ dɩ, dɩ́ warɩ dɩ́ tɔgɩ kǝnǝ yantʋ cwǝŋǝ, yɩ á lagɩ á pa bantʋ tɔgɩ-ka.

eng: A vision of a man from Macedonia came to Paul during the night. He stood urging Paul, Come over to Macedonia and help us.
local: Ba na yi da tɩn, tɩtɩɩ nɩ Pooli ma dwɛ o na nɔɔnʋ na zɩgɩ o yigǝ nɩ. O yɩ Masɩdʋanɩ tu mʋ. O ma loori Pooli o wɩ: «Popo, zaŋɩ n tɔgɩ nɩnɩʋ kʋm wʋ n bɛ n ba Masɩdʋanɩ, n zǝni dɩbam.».

eng: On the contrary, we believe that we and they are saved in the same way, by the grace of the Lord Jesus.
local: Kʋ nan daɩ kʋntʋ. Wɛ na joŋi dɩbam DƖ yagɩ te tɩn, kʋ yɩ dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi zaanɩ dɩm ŋwaanɩ mʋ, dɩ dɩ́ na kɩ dɩ́ wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩd-o tɩn. Kʋ nan yɩ bɩdwɩ mʋ dɩ bantʋ dɩ.».

eng: Immediately after he saw the vision, we prepared to leave for the province of Macedonia, concluding that God had called us to proclaim the good news to them.
local: Pooli na dwɛ kʋntʋ tɩn, dɩbam ma kɩ lɩla dɩ́ ti dɩ́ yigǝ sɩ dɩ́ vu Masɩdʋanɩ, bɛŋwaanɩ dɩ́ maanɩ nɩ kʋ yɩ Wɛ mʋ bǝŋi dɩbam sɩ dɩ́ vu dɩ́ brɩ nɔɔna bam DƖ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam dáanɩ.

eng: The entire assembly fell quiet as they listened to Barnabas and Paul describe all the signs and wonders God did among the Gentiles through their activity.
local: Ba kɔgɔ kʋm maa je, yɩ ba cǝgi sɔɔ sɩ ba ni taanɩ dɩm. Pooli dɩ Banabasɩ ma zaŋɩ ba ta Wɛ na dɛ bantʋ jɩŋa DƖ kɩ wo-kɩnkagɩla dɩ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ na brɩ DƖ dam dwi-gɛ tiinǝ bam te tɩn.

eng: We sailed from Troas straight for Samothrace and came to Neapolis the following day.
local: Dɩ́ laan ma kwe naboro Trʋasɩ nɩ dɩ́ yɔɔrɩ Samotrasɩ. Tɩga na pʋʋrɩ tɩn, dɩ́ daa ma zaŋɩ dɩ́ tɔgɩ nɩnɩʋ kʋm dɩ́ vu Nɩapolisi.

eng: When Barnabas and Paul also fell silent, James responded, Fellow believers, listen to me.
local: Ba na ŋɔɔnɩ ba ti tɩn, Zakɩ ma zaŋɩ o ta o wɩ: «A ko-biǝ-ba, á cǝgi-na a na lagɩ a ta kʋlʋ tɩn.

eng: From there we went to Philippi, a city of Macedonias first district and a Roman colony. We stayed in that city several days.
local: Dɩ́ laan ma zɩgɩ dáanɩ dɩ́ vu tɩga nɩ dɩ́ vu dɩ́ yi Filipi. Tɩʋ kʋm kʋntʋ yɩ Masɩdʋanɩ tɩ-niǝ yam dayigǝ tɩʋ mʋ. Rom tiinǝ mʋ te-kʋ. Dɩ́ ma kɩ da kɔgɔ kɔgɔ tɩʋ kʋm kʋntʋ nɩ.

eng: Simon reported how, in his kindness, God came to the Gentiles in the first place, to raise up from them a people of God.
local: Simɔn Pɩyɛɛrɩ maŋɩ o ta o brɩ dɩbam, Wɛ na sɩŋɩ DƖ brɩ DƖ tɩtɩ dwi-gɛ tiinǝ bam tee nɩ yɩ DƖ lɩ nɔɔna ba wʋnɩ sɩ ba taa yɩ DƖ tɩtɩ nɔɔna te tɩn.

eng: On the Sabbath we went outside the city gate to the riverbank, where we thought there might be a place for prayer. We sat down and began to talk with the women who had gathered.
local: Zwifǝ bam siun dɛ dɩm na yi tɩn, dɩ́ ma nuŋi tɩʋ kʋm wʋ dɩ́ vu bugǝ ni. Dɩ́ ya bʋŋɩ nɩ jǝgǝ wʋ́ ta wʋ dáanɩ Zwifǝ bam na yǝni ba ve ba warɩ Wɛ da. Dɩ́ na nɛ jǝgǝ kam tɩn, dɩ́ ma jǝni tɩga nɩ yɩ dɩ́ ŋɔɔnɩ dɩ kaana balʋ na lagɩ daanɩ jǝgǝ kam nɩ tɩn.

eng: The prophets words agree with this; as it is written.
local: Wɛ nijoŋnǝ bam na tagɩ kʋlʋ faŋa faŋa tɩn dɩdaanɩ kǝm dɩntʋ maama yɩ bɩdwɩ mʋ. Kʋ pʋpʋnɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ kʋ wɩ:.

eng: One of those women was Lydia, a Gentile God-worshipper from the city of Thyatira, a dealer in purple cloth. As she listened, the Lord enabled her to embrace Pauls message.
local: Kaanɩ wʋdoŋ dɛɛn tɔgɩ o wʋra, o yɩrɩ mʋ Lidɩa. O nuŋi Tiyatiiri mʋ, yɩ o nɩgɩ Wɛ lanyɩranɩ. O maa jɩgɩ gwaarʋ tɩlʋ na tiini tɩ jɩgɩ nɩnwaŋa tɩn o yǝgǝ. Dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm ma pʋrɩ o wʋbʋŋa yɩ o sɛ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ Pooli na jɩgɩ o tɛ tɩn.

eng: After this I will return, and I will rebuild Davids fallen tent; I will rebuild what has been torn down. I will restore it .
local: ‹Kʋntʋ kwaga nɩ amʋ Wɛ wʋ́ joori a ba, yɩ a daa lɔ Davidi sɔŋɔ kʋlʋ ya na cɔgɩ tɩn. Amʋ wʋ́ fɔgɩ a lɔ di-doŋo kʋm, sɩ kʋ ji sɔ-dʋŋʋ.

eng: Once she and her household were baptized, she urged, Now that you have decided that I am a believer in the Lord, come and stay in my house. And she persuaded us.
local: O dɛɛn ma pa dɩ́ miis-o dɩ o sɔŋɔ tiinǝ maama na wʋnɩ Zezi yɩrɩ ŋwaanɩ. O laan ma zaŋɩ o loori dɩbam o wɩ: «Abam na sɛ nɩ a sɩɩnɩ a kɩ a wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩdaanɩ dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi, sɩ á ba á taá zʋʋrɩ dɩbam sɔŋɔ nɩ.» O ma wʋ sɛ sɩ dɩ́ viiri.

eng: so that the rest of humanity will seek the Lord, even all the Gentiles who belong to me. The Lord says this, the one who does these things .
local: Kʋntʋ tɩn, nɔɔna balʋ na daarɩ tɩn wʋ́ kwaanɩ sɩ ba lwarɩ amʋ wʋlʋ na yɩ ba Yuutu Baŋa-Wɛ tɩn. Dwi-gɛ tiinǝ balʋ a na lɩ sɩ ba taa yɩ a tɩtɩ nɔɔna tɩn wʋ́ lwarɩ amʋ. Yuutu Baŋa-Wɛ mʋ tagɩ kʋntʋ, yɩ DƖ pa wǝǝnu tɩm sɩɩnɩ tɩ kɩ.

eng: One day, when we were on the way to the place for prayer, we met a slave woman. She had a spirit that enabled her to predict the future. She made a lot of money for her owners through fortune-telling.
local: Dɛ dɩdwɩ mʋ dɩ́ zaŋɩ dɩ́ maa ve Wɛ warɩm jǝgǝ kam. Dɩ́ na maa ve tɩn, dɩ́ ma jeeri bɩsankana kalʋ na yɩ nɔɔna gambaa tɩn. Cicirǝ dɛɛn maa tɔg-o ka pa o pwǝrisǝ. O na yǝni o pwǝrisǝ o tɛ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ na lagɩ tɩ ba tɩ kɩ tɩn, mʋ o yuu tiinǝ bam dɛɛn nɛ sǝbu zanzan kʋ baŋa nɩ.

eng: known from earliest times.
local: DƖ maŋɩ DƖ pa nɔɔna lwarɩ wǝǝnu tɩntʋ ni nɩ faŋa faŋa mʋ.› ».

eng: She began following Paul and us, shouting, These people are servants of the Most High God! They are proclaiming a way of salvation to you.
local: O ma tɔgɩ Pooli dɩ dɩbam kwaga, yɩ o tɔɔlɩ dɩ kwǝr-dɩa o wɩ: «Nɔɔna bantʋ yɩ Yuutu Baŋa-Wɛ tɩntʋŋna mʋ. Ba tu sɩ ba brɩ abam cwǝŋǝ mʋ sɩ á tɔgɩ da á na vrɩm.».

eng: Therefore, I conclude that we shouldnt create problems for Gentiles who turn to God.
local: Zakɩ ta ma ta o wɩ: «Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ amʋ bʋŋɩ sɩ, dɩ́ wʋ maŋɩ sɩ dɩ́ yaarɩ dwi-gɛ tiinǝ balʋ na tu ba sɛ Wɛ tɩn.

eng: She did this for many days. This annoyed Paul so much that he finally turned and said to the spirit, In the name of Jesus Christ, I command you to leave her! It left her at that very moment.
local: O dɛɛn tɔgɩ dɩbam kwaga o kɩ kʋntʋ mʋ taan da kɔgɔ kɔgɔ. Pooli wʋ laan ma cɔgɩ. O ma pipiri o ta dɩ cicirǝ kam o wɩ: «A lagɩ a ta dɩ nmʋ Zezi Krisi yɩrɩ ŋwaanɩ, nuŋi sɩ n daar-o!» O na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, cicirǝ kam ma nuŋi ka daar-o bɩdwɩ baŋa nɩ.

eng: Instead, we should write a letter, telling them to avoid the pollution associated with idols, sexual immorality, eating meat from strangled animals, and consuming blood.
local: Dɩ́ nan na wʋ́ kɩ kʋlʋ tɩn, dɩ́ wʋ́ pʋpʋnɩ tɔnɔ mʋ dɩ́ pa-ba, sɩ dɩ́ brɩ-ba nɩ ba daa yɩ taa di wʋdiu kʋlʋ nɔɔna na mɛ ba kaanɩ jwǝnǝ yɩ kʋ ba lana tɩn, sɩ ba yɩ cɔgɩ ba tɩtɩ dɩ kaana, sɩ kaana dɩ yɩ cɔgɩ ba tɩtɩ dɩ baara. Dɩ́ wʋ́ ta dɩ ba sɩ ba yɩ di varɩm wʋlʋ ba na jujugi ba gʋ tɩn, sɩ ba yɩ di jana dɩ.

eng: Her owners realized that their hope for making money was gone. They grabbed Paul and Silas and dragged them before the officials in the city center.
local: Bɩsankana kam yuu tiinǝ bam ma maanɩ ba lwarɩ nɩ ba daa bá wanɩ ba na sǝbu o ŋwaanɩ. Ba ma ja Pooli dɩ Silasɩ ba vaŋɩ ba ja ba vu yaga, sɩ ba saŋɩ-ba dɩdɛɛra bam tee nɩ.

eng: After all, Moses has been proclaimed in every city for a long time, and is read aloud every Sabbath in every synagogue.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ, kʋ na sɩŋɩ faŋa faŋa tɩn, ba maŋɩ ba karɩmɩ Moyisi tɔnɔ kʋm Zwifǝ Wɛ-di sɩm nɩ ba siun dɛ maama wʋnɩ, yɩ nɔɔna yǝni ba brɩ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ na pʋpʋnɩ da tɩn tɩɩnɩ dɩm maama wʋnɩ.».

eng: When her owners approached the legal authorities, they said, These people are causing an uproar in our city. They are Jews.
local: Ba ma ja-ba ba vu ba pa Rom tiinǝ dɩdɛɛra sɩ ba di ba taanɩ. Ba ma ta ba wɩ: «Nɔɔna bantʋ yɩ Zwifǝ mʋ, yɩ ba jɩgɩ dɩbam tɩʋ kʋm nɔɔna wʋbʋŋa ba vugimǝ.

eng: The apostles and the elders, along with the entire church, agreed to send some delegates chosen from among themselves to Antioch, together with Paul and Barnabas. They selected Judas Barsabbas and Silas, who were leaders among the brothers and sisters.
local: Ba maama na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, Zezi tɩntʋŋna bam dɩ o kɔgɔ kʋm nakwa bam maa lɩ wʋbʋŋa sɩ ba lɩ nɔɔna ba wʋnɩ, sɩ ba tɔgɩ dɩ Pooli dɩ Banabasɩ ba vu Antɩɔsɩ. Ba dɛɛn ma lɩ nɔɔna bale balʋ na yɩ yigǝ tiinǝ ba kɔgɔ kʋm wʋnɩ tɩn. Ba dɩdʋa yɩrɩ mʋ Zudi wʋlʋ yɩrɩ dɩdoŋ na yɩ Basaba tɩn. Wʋdoŋ wʋm yɩrɩ mʋ Silasɩ.

eng: who promote customs that we Romans cant accept or practice.
local: Ba brɩ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ na cɔgɩ dɩbam cullu tɩm mʋ. Dɩbam nan yɩ Rom tiinǝ mʋ, dɩ́ bá wanɩ dɩ́ sɛ bantʋ na brɩ kʋlʋ tɩn.».

eng: They were to carry this letter: The apostles and the elders, to the Gentile brothers and sisters in Antioch, Syria, and Cilicia. Greetings!.
local: Ba ma pʋpʋnɩ tɔnɔ sɩ ba tʋŋɩ-ba dɩ kʋ, yɩ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ kʋ wɩ: «Dɩbam balʋ na yɩ á ko-biǝ tɩn, Zezi tɩntʋŋna bam dɩ o kɔgɔ kʋm nakwa bam, dɩ́ jɔɔnɩ abam, dwi-gɛ tiinǝ balʋ na tu á tɔgɩ Zezi yɩ á zʋʋrɩ Antɩɔsɩ nɩ, dɩ Siiri dɩ Silisi nɩ tɩn.

eng: The crowd joined in the attacks against Paul and Silas, so the authorities ordered that they be stripped of their clothes and beaten with a rod.
local: Kɔgɔ kʋm maama ma tɔgɩ ba jɩgɩ dɩ Pooli dɩ Silasɩ. Rom tiinǝ dɩdɛɛra bam ma ja-ba ba vaŋɩ ba gwaarʋ tɩm ba kaarɩ, yɩ ba daarɩ ba pa nɔɔna magɩ-ba dɩ balaara.

eng: Weve heard that some of our number have disturbed you with unsettling words we didnt authorize.
local: Dɩbam ni nɩ dɩ́ kɔgɔ kʋm nɔɔna badonnǝ mʋ zaŋɩ ba ba abam te, ba yaarɩ abam ba pa á wʋbʋŋa cɔgɩ dɩ kʋlʋ ba na brɩ abam tɩn. Dɩbam nan wʋ pɛ-ba cwǝŋǝ sɩ ba kɩ kʋntʋ.

eng: When Paul and Silas had been severely beaten, the authorities threw them into prison and ordered the jailer to secure them with great care.
local: Ba na tiini ba magɩ-ba kʋntʋ tɩn, ba laan ma kɩ-ba pɩɩna digǝ nɩ. Dɩdɛɛra bam ma ta dɩ wʋlʋ na nii pɩɩna digǝ kam baŋa nɩ tɩn, sɩ o fɔgɩ o pɩ nɔɔna bam lanyɩranɩ.

eng: We reached a united decision to select some delegates and send them to you along with our dear friends Barnabas and Paul.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ dɩbam maama mʋ kɩ ni daanɩ sɩ dɩ́ lɩ nɔɔna dɩ́ tʋŋɩ-ba abam tee nɩ, sɩ ba tɔgɩ dɩ dɩbam cilon-sonnu Banabasɩ dɩ Pooli ba ba.

eng: When he received these instructions, he threw them into the innermost cell and secured their feet in stocks.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋm na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma pa ba zʋ tɩtarɩ digǝ kam wʋ, yɩ o daarɩ o kwaarɩ-ba dɩ dɛ.

eng: These people have devoted their lives to the name of our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Bantʋ mʋ yɩ balʋ na kwe ba ŋwɩa maama ba pa dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi Krisi tɩtʋŋa yam ŋwaanɩ tɩn.

eng: Around midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing hymns to God, and the other prisoners were listening to them.
local: Maŋa kalʋ tɩga na kwɩɩrɩ tɩn, Pooli dɩ Silasɩ maa wʋra ba warɩ Wɛ yɩ ba leenǝ ba zuli Wɛ. Pɩɩna badonnǝ bam maa je ba cǝgǝ.

eng: Therefore, we are sending Judas and Silas. They will confirm what we have written.
local: Dɩbam nan mʋ tʋŋɩ Zudi dɩ Silasɩ abam tee nɩ, sɩ ba ba ba fɔgɩ ba ŋɔɔnɩ ba brɩ abam kʋlʋ dɩ́ na pʋpʋnɩ tɩn.

eng: All at once there was such a violent earthquake that it shook the prisons foundations. The doors flew open and everyones chains came loose.
local: Tɩga kam laan ma da ka sisiŋi, yɩ pɩɩna digǝ kam sisiŋi dɩ ka kuri maama. Digǝ kam bwǝǝru tɩm maama ma da tɩ pʋrɩ, pɩɩna bam capʋnnʋ tɩm maama ma kɔ tɩ tʋ.

eng: The Holy Spirit has led us to the decision that no burden should be placed on you other than these essentials.
local: Wɛ Joro kʋm dɩdaanɩ dɩbam maama bʋŋɩ nɩ kʋ wʋ maŋɩ sɩ dɩ́ yaarɩ abam zanzan. Woŋo kʋlʋ yɩranɩ na yɩ fɩfɩʋn sɩ á yɩ kɩ tɩn mʋ tɩntʋ:.

eng: When the jailer awoke and saw the open doors of the prison, he thought the prisoners had escaped, so he drew his sword and was about to kill himself.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na yɩrɩ digǝ kam tɩn ma zaŋɩ yɩ o na pɩɩna digǝ kam niǝ na pʋrɩ. O ma bʋŋɩ nɩ pɩɩna bam maama nuŋi ba duri mʋ. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ o ma lɩ sʋ-zɔɔ sɩ o ma gʋ o tɩtɩ.

eng: refuse food offered to idols, blood, the meat from strangled animals, and sexual immorality. You will do well to avoid such things. Farewell.
local: Á yɩ zaŋɩ á di wʋdiu kʋlʋ ba na mɛ ba kaanɩ jwǝnǝ tɩn. Á yɩ di jana. Á yɩ di varɩm wʋlʋ ba na jujugi ba gʋ tɩn. Á yɩ cɔgɩ á tɩtɩ dɩ kaana, sɩ kaana dɩ yɩ cɔgɩ ba tɩtɩ dɩ baara. Á na jɩgɩ á tɩtɩ dɩ wǝǝnu tɩntʋ maama, á wʋ́ taá kɩ lanyɩranɩ. Wɛ wʋ́ pa abam yazurǝ.».

eng: But Paul shouted loudly, Dont harm yourself! Were all here.
local: Pooli laan ma bagɩ dɩ kwǝr-dɩa o wɩ: «Yɩ zaŋɩ n gʋ n tɩtɩ, sɩ dɩbam maama wʋ yo.».

eng: When Barnabas, Paul, and the delegates were sent on their way, they went down to Antioch. They gathered the believers and delivered the letter.
local: Ba na pʋpʋnɩ kʋntʋ ba ti tɩn, ba laan ma tʋŋɩ nɔɔna bam sɩ ba vu Antɩɔsɩ. Ba na yi da tɩn, ba ma la ba kɔgɔ kʋm maama daanɩ, yɩ ba daarɩ ba pa-ba tɔnɔ kʋm sɩ ba karɩmɩ.

eng: The jailer called for some lights, rushed in, and fell trembling before Paul and Silas.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋm ma bǝŋi sɩ ba ja mini ba ba ba pa-o. O ma kɩ lɩla o zʋ digǝ kam wʋ o vu o tʋ Pooli dɩ Silasɩ yigǝ nɩ, yɩ o yɩra saɩ.

eng: The people read it, delighted with its encouraging message.
local: Nɔɔna bam na karɩmɩ tɔnɔ kʋm tɩn, ba maa jɩgɩ wʋpolo lanyɩranɩ dɩ kʋ na fɔgɩ kʋ zǝni ba wʋbʋŋa tɩn.

eng: He led them outside and asked, Honorable masters, what must I do to be rescued.
local: O ma pa ba nuŋi pooni, yɩ o daarɩ o bwe-ba o wɩ: «A yuu tiinǝ-ba, a nan wʋ́ kɩ ta mʋ sɩ a ma na vrɩm?».

eng: Judas and Silas were prophets, and they said many things that encouraged and strengthened the brothers and sisters.
local: Zudi dɩ Silasɩ na yɩ Wɛ nijoŋnǝ tɩn dɛɛn ma tiini ba ŋɔɔnɩ dɩ nɔɔna bam ba pa ba na pu-dɩa dɩ baarɩ.

eng: They replied, Believe in the Lord Jesus, and you will be saved—you and your entire household.
local: Ba ma lǝr-o ba wɩ: «Nmʋ maŋɩ sɩ n kɩ n wʋ-dɩdʋa mʋ dɩdaanɩ dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi, sɩ Wɛ wʋ́ vrɩ nmʋ dɩ n sɔŋɔ tiinǝ maama.».

eng: Judas and Silas stayed there awhile, then were sent back with a blessing of peace from the brothers and sisters to those who first sent them.
local: Ba maa daanɩ ba tee nɩ, yɩ ba ba ba lagɩ ba viiri. Zezi kɔgɔ kʋm nɔɔna bam ma banɩ-ba, yɩ ba joori ba vu balʋ na tʋŋɩ-ba tɩn te dɩ yazurǝ. .

eng: They spoke the Lords word to him and everyone else in his house.
local: Ba ma daarɩ ba ta Wɛ kwǝrǝ kam ba brɩ-o dɩ balʋ maama na wʋ o sɔŋɔ kʋm nɩ tɩn.

eng: Paul and Barnabas stayed in Antioch, where, together with many others, they taught and proclaimed the good news of the Lords word.
local: Kʋ daarɩ Pooli dɩ Banabasɩ dɩ ma maŋɩ Antɩɔsɩ nɩ. Ba dɛɛn maa tɔgɩ dɩ nɔɔna zanzan ba tɔɔlɩ dɩ́ Yuutu Wɛ kwǝrǝ kam yɩ ba brɩ DƖ cɩga kam.

eng: Right then, in the middle of the night, the jailer welcomed them and washed their wounds. He and everyone in his household were immediately baptized.
local: Tɩtɩɩ dɩm maŋa kam kʋntʋ nɩ mʋ o jaanɩ-ba o vu o zarɩ ba fufwǝlǝ yam maama. O na kɩ kʋntʋ o ti tɩn, mʋ o dɩ o sɔŋɔ tiinǝ bam maama dɛ ba pa ba miisi-ba na wʋnɩ Zezi yɩrɩ ŋwaanɩ.

eng: Some time later, Paul said to Barnabas, Lets go back and visit all the brothers and sisters in every city where we preached the Lords word. Lets see how they are doing.
local: Ba na daanɩ da da yagratʋ tɩn, Pooli ma ta dɩ Banabasɩ o wɩ: «Pa dɩ́ joori dɩ́ vu dɩ́ nii dɩ́ ko-biǝ bam tɩɩnɩ dɩlʋ maama wʋnɩ dɩ́ na maŋɩ dɩ́ tɔɔlɩ dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm kwǝrǝ kam da tɩn, sɩ dɩ́ nii kʋ na yɩ te tɩn.».

eng: He brought them into his home and gave them a meal. He was overjoyed because he and everyone in his household had come to believe in God.
local: O ma daarɩ o pa Pooli dɩ Silasɩ zʋ o sɔŋɔ kʋm wʋ. O ma kɩ wʋdiu o pa ba di. O na tu o sɛ Wɛ dɩ wʋ-dɩdʋa tɩn, o maa jɩgɩ wʋpolo lanyɩranɩ dɩ o sɔŋɔ tiinǝ bam maama.

eng: Barnabas wanted to take John Mark with them.
local: Banabasɩ ma sɛ yɩ o lagɩ sɩ Zan Marɩkɩ taa tɔgɩ dɩdaanɩ ba o tʋŋa.

eng: The next morning the legal authorities sent the police to the jailer with the order Release those people.
local: Tɩga na pʋʋrɩ tɩn, Rom tiinǝ dɩdɛɛra bam ma tʋŋɩ nɔɔna sɩ ba vu pɩɩna digǝ kam yɩrɩnʋ wʋm te, ba ta dɩd-o nɩ o yagɩ nɔɔna bam sɩ ba viiri.

eng: Paul insisted that they shouldnt take him along, since he had deserted them in Pamphylia and hadnt continued with them in their work.
local: Pooli maa bʋŋɩ nɩ kʋ wʋ maŋɩ sɩ o tɔgɩ o wǝli ba wʋnɩ, bɛŋwaanɩ maŋa kalʋ ba dɛɛn na wʋ Pamfili nɩ tɩn wʋntʋ yagɩ-ba mʋ, yɩ o daa ba tɔgɩ dɩ ba o tʋŋa.

eng: So the jailer reported this to Paul, informing him, The authorities sent word that you both are to be released. You can leave now. Go in peace.
local: Pɩɩna digǝ kam yɩrɩnʋ wʋm ma ta o brɩ Pooli dɩdɛɛra bam na tagɩ te sɩ o yagɩ-ba tɩn. O maa wɩ: «Ba pɛ abam cwǝŋǝ sɩ á viiri. Nan ve-na dɩ yazurǝ.».

eng: Their argument became so intense that they went their separate ways. Barnabas took Mark and sailed to Cyprus.
local: Ba ma tiini ba magɩ kantɔgɔ daanɩ, yɩ kʋ pa ba pwɛ da-tee nɩ. Banabasɩ ma ja Zan Marɩkɩ yɩ ba vu ba zʋ naboro sɩ ba vu Sipri.

eng: Paul told the police, Even though we are Roman citizens, they beat us publicly without first finding us guilty of a crime, and they threw us into prison. And now they want to send us away secretly? No way! They themselves will have to come and escort us out.
local: Pooli laan ma ta dɩ nɔɔna bam o wɩ: «Dɩbam nan yɩ Rom tɩʋ kʋm jɩŋa nɔɔna mʋ. Ba nan wʋ di dɩbam taanɩ sɩ ba na dɩ́ kǝm-balɔrɔ, yɩ ba garɩ ba ja dɩbam ba magɩ nɔɔna maama yigǝ nɩ, yɩ ba daarɩ ba kɩ dɩbam pɩɩna digǝ nɩ. Bɛɛ mʋ yɩ ba laan lagɩ sɩ ba sǝgi ba pʋrɩ dɩbam ba yagɩ? Dɩ́ bá sɛ. Ba tɩtɩ mʋ maŋɩ sɩ ba ba ba pʋrɩ dɩbam ba yagɩ.».

eng: Paul chose Silas and left, entrusted by the brothers and sisters to the Lords grace.
local: Pooli dɩ ma lɩ Silasɩ sɩ ba tɔgɩ daanɩ ba vu. Zezi karabiǝ bam ma kɩ-ba dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm jɩŋa nɩ sɩ o zǝni-ba. Ba laan ma zaŋɩ ba kɛ.

eng: The police reported this to the legal authorities, who were alarmed to learn that Paul and Silas were Roman citizens.
local: Nɔɔna bam ma vu ba ta Pooli na tagɩ kʋlʋ tɩn ba brɩ Rom tiinǝ dɩdɛɛra bam. Ba na lwarɩ nɩ Pooli dɩ Silasɩ yɩ Rom tɩʋ kʋm jɩŋa nɔɔna mʋ tɩn, fʋʋnɩ ma zʋ-ba dɩ ba na kɩ-ba te tɩn.

eng: He traveled through Syria and Cilicia, strengthening the churches.
local: Ba ma tɔgɩ Siiri dɩ Silisi wʋ, yɩ ba pa Zezi kɔgɔ kʋm nɔɔna bam na wʋ je sɩlʋ maama tɩn fɔgɩ ba zɩgɩ dɩ dam lanyɩranɩ.

eng: They came and consoled Paul and Silas, escorting them out of prison and begging them to leave the city.
local: Ba ma vu ba koori Pooli dɩ Silasɩ sɩ ba yagɩ taanɩ dɩm sɩ kʋ ti. Ba ma daarɩ ba pʋrɩ-ba ba yagɩ, yɩ ba loori-ba ba wɩ, ba nuŋi tɩʋ kʋm wʋnɩ ba viiri.

eng: Paul and Silas left the prison and made their way to Lydias house where they encouraged the brothers and sisters. Then they left Philippi.
local: Pooli dɩ Silasɩ ma nuŋi pɩɩna digǝ kam nɩ ba vu ba zʋ Lidɩa-ba sɔŋɔ. Dáanɩ mʋ ba jeeri Zezi karabiǝ bam, yɩ ba ŋɔɔnɩ dɩ ba ba pa-ba dam dɩ baarɩ. Ba laan ma zaŋɩ ba viiri.

eng: Paul and Silas journeyed through Amphipolis and Apollonia, then came to Thessalonica, where there was a Jewish synagogue.
local: Ba dɛɛn ma da Afipolisi dɩ Apoloni ba kɛ ba vu ba yi Tesaloniki. Tɩʋ kʋm kʋntʋ nɩ mʋ Zwifǝ bam Wɛ-digǝ wʋra.

eng: As was Pauls custom, he entered the synagogue and for three Sabbaths interacted with them on the basis of the scriptures.
local: Pooli ma vu da, nɩ o na maŋɩ o yǝni o kɩ te tɩn. O dɛɛn kɩ siun da yatɔ mʋ dɩ ba. O maa karɩmɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm, yɩ o brɩ-ba kʋ kuri sɩ ba ni.

eng: Through his interpretation of the scriptures, he demonstrated that the Christ had to suffer and rise from the dead. He declared, This Jesus whom I proclaim to you is the Christ.
local: O ma maŋɩ o brɩ-ba o wɩ, kʋ dɛɛn ya maŋɩ sɩ Krisi wʋm Wɛ na tʋŋɩ tɩn yaarɩ mʋ o tɩ, sɩ o joori o bi o yagɩ tʋʋnɩ. O ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Zezi wʋlʋ a na jɩgɩ o taanɩ a tɛa a brɩ abam tɩn mʋ sɩɩnɩ o yɩ Krisi wʋm Wɛ na tʋŋɩ tɩn.».

eng: Some were convinced and joined Paul and Silas, including a larger number of Greek God-worshippers and quite a few prominent women.
local: Zwifǝ bam na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, ba badonnǝ ma sɛ o na tagɩ kʋlʋ tɩn, yɩ ba laan tɔgɩ ba wǝli Pooli dɩ Silasɩ nɩ. Grɛkɩ tiinǝ balʋ na nɩgɩ Wɛ tɩn kɔgɔ zanzan dɩ ma sɛ Pooli taanɩ dɩm, dɩdaanɩ kaana zanzan balʋ jɩa na garɩ tɩn.

eng: But the Jews became jealous and brought along some thugs who were hanging out in the marketplace. They formed a mob and started a riot in the city. They attacked Jasons house, intending to bring Paul and Silas before the people.
local: Zwifǝ badaara maa jɩgɩ wʋ-gʋrʋ dɩ ba. Ba ma vu yaga ba la zwabani-nyɩna ba pa ba tɔgɩ daanɩ, yɩ ba kɔgɔ kʋm ma pa tɩʋ kʋm maama vugimi dɩ sɔɔ bɔgɩ bɔgɩ. Ba ma vu ba ciŋi jara Zazɔn sɔŋɔ nɩ, yɩ ba kwaanɩ ba lagɩ Pooli dɩ Silasɩ jǝgǝ, sɩ ba ja-ba ba ja nuŋi ba kɩ nɔɔna bam jɩŋa nɩ.

eng: When they didnt find them, they dragged Jason and some believers before the city officials. They were shouting, These people who have been disturbing the peace throughout the empire have also come here.
local: Ba ma wʋ nɛ-ba. Ba ma ja Zazɔn dɩ Zezi karabiǝ badonnǝ ba ja vu tɩʋ kʋm dɩdɛɛra bam te, yɩ ba tɔɔlɩ ba wɩ: «Nɔɔna bantʋ mʋ yǝni ba beeri lʋgʋ baŋa je maama ba vugimi nɔɔna wʋbʋŋa. Bantʋ nan mʋ tu yo.

eng: What is more, Jason has welcomed them into his home. Every one of them does what is contrary to Caesars decrees by naming someone else as king: Jesus.
local: Zazɔn mʋ pɛ ba zʋʋrɩ o sɔŋɔ nɩ. Bantʋ maama mʋ vɩn dɩ́ pa-farʋ wʋm cullu tɩm, yɩ ba tɛ ba wɩ, pɛ wʋdoŋ mʋ wʋra, o yɩrɩ mʋ Zezi.».

eng: This provoked the crowd and the city officials even more.
local: Tɩʋ kʋm dɩdɛɛra bam dɩ kɔgɔ kʋm maama na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, ba bana ma zaŋɩ zanzan dɩ ba.

eng: After Jason and the others posted bail, they released them.
local: Dɩdɛɛra bam ma pa Zazɔn dɩ o donnǝ bam tiŋi sǝbu kʋlʋ na wʋ́ pa-ba cwǝŋǝ sɩ ba vrɩ ba tɩtɩ tɩn. Ba laan ma yagɩ-ba ba pa ba viiri.

eng: As soon as it was dark, the brothers and sisters sent Paul and Silas on to Beroea. When they arrived, they went to the Jewish synagogue.
local: Tɩga na tu ka yi tɩn, Zezi karabiǝ bam ma pa Pooli dɩ Silasɩ nuŋi tɩʋ kʋm nɩ ba vu Beere. Ba na ve ba yi da tɩn, ba ma vu ba zʋ Zwifǝ bam Wɛ-digǝ kam wʋ.

eng: The Beroean Jews were more honorable than those in Thessalonica. This was evident in the great eagerness with which they accepted the word and examined the scriptures each day to see whether Paul and Silas teaching was true.
local: Tɩʋ kʋm kʋntʋ Zwifǝ bam maa jɩgɩ wʋbʋŋ-ŋʋna ba dwe Tesaloniki nɔɔna bam. Ba ma cǝgi Wɛ kwǝrǝ kam lanyɩranɩ dɩ ba wʋ maama. Dɛ maama wʋnɩ ba yǝni ba karɩmɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm sɩ ba nii, Pooli na brɩ kʋlʋ tɩn sɩɩnɩ kʋ yɩ cɩga na.

eng: Many came to believe, including a number of reputable Greek women and many Greek men.
local: Ba zanzan ma sɛ Pooli taanɩ dɩm, Grɛkɩ tiinǝ zanzan dɩ maa sɛ, baara dɩ kaana balʋ jɩa na garɩ tɩn zanzan dɩ maa sɛ.

eng: The Jews from Thessalonica learned that Paul also proclaimed Gods word in Beroea, so they went there too and were upsetting and disturbing the crowds.
local: Tesaloniki Zwifǝ bam ma ni nɩ Pooli wʋ Beere nɩ o tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝrǝ kam. Ba ma zaŋɩ ba vu Beere ba la kɔgɔ ba pa tɩʋ kʋm nɔɔna bam bana zaŋɩ dɩ Pooli.

eng: The brothers and sisters sent Paul away to the seacoast at once, but Silas and Timothy remained at Beroea.
local: Zezi karabiǝ bam na nɛ kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma pa Pooli zaŋɩ lɩla o nuŋi o vu nɩnɩʋ kʋm ni. Kʋ ma daarɩ Silasɩ dɩ Timoti mʋ maŋɩ Beere nɩ.

eng: Those who escorted Paul led him as far as Athens, then returned with instructions for Silas and Timothy to come to him as quickly as possible.
local: Nɔɔna balʋ na kogili Pooli tɩn ma da dɩd-o ba vu ba yi Atɛɛnɩ. Ba na maa joori tɩn, Pooli ma pa-ba ni sɩ ba vu ba ta Silasɩ dɩ Timoti nɩ ba zaŋɩ ba tɔgɩ o kwaga ba ba lɩla.

eng: While Paul waited for them in Athens, he was deeply distressed to find that the city was flooded with idols.
local: Pooli dɛɛn na wʋ Atɛɛnɩ nɩ o cǝgi Silasɩ dɩ Timoti sɩ ba ba tɩn, o wʋ ma tiini kʋ cɔgɩ dɩ o na nɛ nɩ jwǝnǝ zanzan su tɩʋ kʋm tɩn.

eng: He began to interact with the Jews and Gentile God-worshippers in the synagogue. He also addressed whoever happened to be in the marketplace each day.
local: O maa yǝni o zʋ Zwifǝ bam Wɛ-digǝ kam wʋ o ŋɔɔnɩ dɩ ba dɩdaanɩ dwi-gɛ tiinǝ balʋ na nɩgɩ Wɛ tɩn. Dɛ maama o maa yǝni o ve yaga ni o ŋɔɔnɩ dɩ balʋ na wʋra tɩn.

eng: Certain Epicurean and Stoic philosophers engaged him in discussion too. Some said, What an amateur! Whats he trying to say? Others remarked, He seems to be a proclaimer of foreign gods.
local: Yi-pʋrʋ tiinǝ badonnǝ dɩ ma ba ba maŋɩ dɩd-o. Ba badaara dɛɛn tɔgɩ Epikuri zaasɩm dɩm mʋ, babam dɩ maa tɔgɩ Sitoyisi zaasɩm dɩm. Ba badaara ma ta ba wɩ: «Ŋwaŋ-fara tu wʋntʋ bʋŋɩ sɩ o brɩ bɛɛ mʋ?» Badonnǝ dɩ ma ta ba wɩ: «Kʋ nyɩ dɩ o ŋɔɔnɩ dwi-gɛ tiinǝ jwǝnǝ taanɩ mʋ te.» Ba tagɩ kʋntʋ, bɛŋwaanɩ o tɔɔlɩ Zezi kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam dɩ twa biim taanɩ mʋ.

eng: They took him into custody and brought him to the council on Mars Hill. What is this new teaching? Can we learn what you are talking about?.
local: Ba laan ma ja-o ba vu Arɩpaazɩ, . Ba ma ta-o ba wɩ: «Dɩbam ya lagɩ sɩ dɩ́ lwarɩ zaasɩm-dʋʋrɩ dɩm nmʋ na jɩgɩ n zaasɩ tɩn kuri mʋ.

eng: Youve told us some strange things and we want to know what they mean. .
local: Wǝǝnu tɩlʋ nmʋ na brɩ tɩn, dɩ́ ta wʋ fɔgɩ dɩ́ ni tɩ dwi. Kʋntʋ mʋ dɩ́ lagɩ sɩ dɩ́ lwarɩ tɩ kuri na yɩ te tɩn.».

eng: They said this because all Athenians as well as the foreigners who live in Athens used to spend their time doing nothing but talking about or listening to the newest thing.
local: Atɛɛnɩ tiinǝ bam maama dɩdaanɩ vǝrǝ balʋ na zʋʋrɩ ba tee nɩ tɩn dɛɛn tiini ba lagɩ sɩ ba taa larɩ daanɩ mʋ maŋa maama, sɩ ba taa cǝgi wǝǝnu tɩlʋ na yɩ wo-dʋnnʋ tɩn.

eng: Paul stood up in the middle of the council on Mars Hill and said, People of Athens, I see that you are very religious in every way.
local: Pooli ma zaŋɩ o zɩgɩ Arɩpaazɩ yigǝ tiinǝ bam tɩtarɩ nɩ o wɩ: «Atɛɛnɩ tiinǝ-ba, amʋ na nii tɩn, abam ŋwɩa kam maama wʋnɩ á tiini á zuli jwǝnǝ mʋ zanzan.

eng: As I was walking through town and carefully observing your objects of worship, I even found an altar with this inscription: To an unknown God. What you worship as unknown, I now proclaim to you.
local: A na beeri abam tɩʋ kʋm wʋnɩ tɩn, a nɛ je sɩm maama á na zuli á jwǝnǝ da tɩn. A na beeri tɩn mʋ a nɛ kaanɩm bimbim dɩdʋa, yɩ kʋ pʋpʋnɩ dɩ yɩra nɩ kʋ wɩ: ‹Wɛ dɩlʋ dɩ́ na yǝri tɩn›. Kʋntʋ, Wɛ dɩlʋ abam na jɩgɩ á zuli yɩ á ta yǝri-DƖ tɩn kwǝrǝ mʋ a lagɩ a tɔɔlɩ a brɩ abam.

eng: God, who made the world and everything in it, is Lord of heaven and earth. He doesnt live in temples made with human hands.
local: Dɩntʋ mʋ yɩ Wɛ dɩlʋ na kɩ lʋgʋ baŋa dɩ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ maama na wʋ kʋ wʋnɩ tɩn. DƖ maa te wɛyuu dɩ tɩga baŋa maama, yɩ DƖ ba zʋʋrɩ di sɩlʋ nabiinǝ na lɔgɩ ba pa-DƖ tɩn wʋnɩ.

eng: Nor is God served by human hands, as though he needed something, since he is the one who gives life, breath, and everything else.
local: Wɛ nan wʋ gɛ kʋlʋkʋlʋ sɩ nabiinǝ taa tʋŋa ba pa-DƖ, kʋ na yɩ dɩntʋ tɩtɩ mʋ kwe ŋwɩa dɩ siun dɩ woŋo maama DƖ pa nabiinǝ tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: From one person God created every human nation to live on the whole earth, having determined their appointed times and the boundaries of their lands.
local: Wɛ nan kɩ nabiinu dɩdʋa mʋ, yɩ DƖ laan pa nabiinǝ dwi maama zɩgɩ wʋntʋ yɩra nɩ. DƖ ma pa ba jagɩ ba zʋʋrɩ lʋgʋ baŋa maama wʋnɩ. Kʋntʋ tɩn, dɩntʋ mʋ maŋɩ DƖ lɩ wʋbʋŋa yɩ DƖ lɩ mɩmaŋa kalʋ dɩ je sɩlʋ dwi maama na wʋ́ ta zʋʋrɩ da tɩn.

eng: God made the nations so they would seek him, perhaps even reach out to him and find him. In fact, God isnt far away from any of us.
local: Wɛ na kɩ nabiinǝ kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ yɩ sɩ ba taa lagɩ-DƖ mʋ. Ba maŋɩ sɩ ba beeri-DƖ mʋ ba na DƖ jǝgǝ, sɩ ba ma wanɩ ba lwarɩ-DƖ. Wɛ nan maŋɩ DƖ daɩ yigǝ yigǝ dɩdaanɩ dɩbam maama.

eng: In God we live, move, and exist. As some of your own poets said, We are his offspring.
local: Kʋ nyɩ nɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ na tagɩ o wɩ: ‹Dɩntʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ dɩ́ ŋwɩ, dɩ́ wʋra yɩ dɩ́ veǝ.› Abam tɩtɩ lǝŋ-leenu dɩdʋa dɩ tagɩ kʋntʋ doŋ o wɩ: ‹Dɩbam dɩ maŋɩ dɩ́ yɩ DƖ dwi tiinǝ mʋ.›.

eng: Therefore, as Gods offspring, we have no need to imagine that the divine being is like a gold, silver, or stone image made by human skill and thought.
local: Dɩbam na sɩɩnɩ dɩ́ yɩ Wɛ dwi tiinǝ tɩn, dɩ́ wʋ maŋɩ sɩ dɩ́ bʋŋɩ nɩ Wɛ wʋ́ ta nyɩ dɩ kamɔgɔ kʋlʋ nabiinu na mɛ sǝbu-sɩŋa naa luguru naa kandwa o ma o kɩ dɩ o tɩtɩ wʋbʋŋa tɩn.

eng: God overlooks ignorance of these things in times past, but now directs everyone everywhere to change their hearts and lives.
local: Faŋa faŋa yɩ nabiinǝ ta yǝri kʋlʋ na lana sɩ ba taa kɩ tɩn, Wɛ wʋ kwe-kʋ DƖ kɩ DƖ wʋbʋŋa nɩ. Kʋ daarɩ lele kʋntʋ, DƖ laan pɛ ni nɩ nɔɔna maama lʋgʋ baŋa nɩ maŋɩ sɩ ba lǝni ba wʋrʋ ba yagɩ wo-balwaarʋ kǝm.

eng: This is because God has set a day when he intends to judge the world justly by a man he has appointed. God has given proof of this to everyone by raising him from the dead.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ DƖ lɩ dɛ mʋ sɩ DƖ ma DƖ di nabiinǝ maama taanɩ. DƖ nan wʋ́ tɔgɩ cɩga mʋ DƖ ma di ba taanɩ dɩm. DƖ nan maŋɩ DƖ kuri wʋlʋ DƖ na wʋ́ tɔgɩ o ŋwaanɩ DƖ ma di ba taanɩ tɩn. DƖ pɛ nɔɔnʋ wʋm kʋntʋ mʋ bi o yagɩ tʋʋnɩ, sɩ kʋ pa nɔɔna maama lwarɩ nɩ kʋ sɩɩnɩ kʋ yɩ cɩga mʋ.».

eng: When they heard about the resurrection from the dead, some began to ridicule Paul. However, others said, Well hear from you about this again.
local: Pooli na ŋɔɔnɩ twa biim taanɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, nɔɔna bam badonnǝ maa jɩg-o ba mwana. Kʋ daarɩ badaara maa tɛ ba wɩ: «Dɩ́ lagɩ sɩ n joori n ŋɔɔnɩ taanɩ dɩm kʋntʋ doŋ mʋ sɩ dɩ́ cǝgi dɛ dɩdoŋ nɩ.».

eng: At that, Paul left the council.
local: Pooli laan ma zaŋɩ o nuŋi ba tee nɩ.

eng: Some people joined him and came to believe, including Dionysius, a member of the council on Mars Hill, a woman named Damaris, and several others.
local: Nɔɔna badonnǝ dɛɛn ma sɛ ba tɔgɩ Pooli, yɩ ba kɩ ba wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Zezi. Ba dɩdʋa yɩrɩ mʋ Denisi. O tɔgɩ o wʋ Arɩpaazɩ kɔgɔ kʋm wʋnɩ mʋ. Kaanɩ wʋdoŋ dɩ wʋra, o yɩrɩ mʋ Damarisi. Nɔɔna badonnǝ dɩ tɔgɩ ba wǝli da ba na sɛ Zezi.

eng: After this, Paul left Athens and went to Corinth.
local: Kʋntʋ kwaga nɩ, Pooli dɛɛn ma zɩgɩ Atɛɛnɩ nɩ o vu Korɛntɩ.

eng: There he found a Jew named Aquila, a native of Pontus. He had recently come from Italy with his wife Priscilla because Claudius had ordered all Jews to leave Rome. Paul visited with them.
local: O na yi da tɩn, o ma maŋɩ dɩdaanɩ Zwifu wʋdoŋ, o yɩrɩ mʋ Akwila. Ba dɛɛn lʋg-o Pɔn nɩ mʋ. Maŋa kalʋ Pooli na nɛ-o tɩn, o ya nuŋi Yitali mʋ o ba, dɩ o kaanɩ Prisili, bɛŋwaanɩ Rom pa-farʋ Kloodi dɛɛn ya zǝli Zwifǝ bam maama o pa ba nuŋi Rom nɩ ba viiri. Pooli ma zaŋɩ o vu ba te.

eng: Because they practiced the same trade, he stayed and worked with them. They all worked with leather.
local: yɩ o zʋʋrɩ dɩ ba sɩ ba taa tʋŋɩ daanɩ, ba dɛɛn na tʋŋɩ tɩtʋŋɩ dɩdwɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Ba dɛɛn nyaanɩ gwaarʋ mʋ yɩ ba yǝgi ba ma pu vwǝ.

eng: Every Sabbath he interacted with people in the synagogue, trying to convince both Jews and Greeks.
local: Zwifǝ bam siun dɛ maama o maa yǝni o zʋ ba Wɛ-digǝ kam o ŋɔɔnɩ Wɛ taanɩ dɩ ba. O maa kwaanɩ sɩ o pa Zwifǝ bam dɩ Grɛkɩ tiinǝ bam maama sɛ o taanɩ dɩm.

eng: Once Silas and Timothy arrived from Macedonia, Paul devoted himself fully to the word, testifying to the Jews that Jesus was the Christ.
local: Silasɩ dɩ Timoti dɛɛn zɩgɩ Masɩdʋanɩ nɩ ba ba Pooli te. Ba na tu tɩn, Pooli laan ma cɛ dɩ Wɛ taanɩ dɩm zaasɩm. O maa kwaanɩ o pa Zwifǝ bam lwarɩ nɩ Zezi yɩ Krisi wʋm Wɛ na tʋŋɩ tɩn.

eng: When they opposed and slandered him, he shook the dust from his clothes in protest and said to them, You are responsible for your own fates! Im innocent! From now on Ill go to the Gentiles.
local: Ba ma wʋ sɛ o taanɩ dɩm yɩ ba twɩ-o. O laan ma ja o gwaarʋ tɩm o pupugi tɩ fogo kʋm ba yigǝ nɩ, yɩ o ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Abam na nɛ lɛɛrʋ Wɛ tee nɩ, kʋ yɩ abam tɩtɩ yigǝ mʋ. Kʋ daa daɩ amʋ yigǝ. Kʋ na sɩŋɩ lele kʋntʋ tɩn, a laan wʋ́ vu dwi-gɛ tiinǝ bam te a daarɩ abam.».

eng: He left the synagogue and went next door to the home of Titius Justus, a Gentile God-worshipper.
local: O ma sɩɩnɩ o nuŋi Zwifǝ bam tee nɩ o daarɩ o vu o zʋʋrɩ dwi-gɛ tu wʋdoŋ sɔŋɔ nɩ, o yɩrɩ mʋ Titiu Zusitu. Wʋntʋ maa nɩgɩ Wɛ. O sɔŋɔ kʋm maa zɩgɩ Zwifǝ bam Wɛ-digǝ kam tɩkǝri nɩ.

eng: Crispus, the synagogue leader, and his entire household came to believe in the Lord. Many Corinthians believed and were baptized after listening to Paul.
local: Wɛ-digǝ kam yuutu wʋm yɩrɩ mʋ Krisipu. Wʋntʋ dɩ o sɔŋɔ tiinǝ bam maama dɛɛn tu ba tɔgɩ dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi. Korɛntɩ tiinǝ zanzan dɩ dɛɛn ma ni Wɛ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam yɩ ba sɛ. Ba laan ma pa ba miisi-ba na wʋnɩ Zezi yɩrɩ ŋwaanɩ.

eng: One night the Lord said to Paul in a vision, Dont be afraid. Continue speaking. Dont be silent.
local: Dɛ dɩdwɩ tɩtɩɩ nɩ mʋ Pooli dwɛ o na vɩɩrɩm wʋnɩ dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm na ŋɔɔnɩ dɩd-o o wɩ: «Yɩ ta n kwarɩ fʋʋnɩ. Ta ta n ŋɔɔna, sɩ n yɩ ta n cɩm.

eng: Im with you and no one who attacks you will harm you, for I have many people in this city.
local: sɩ amʋ wʋ nmʋ tee nɩ. Nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ bá wanɩ o kɩ nmʋ lwarɩm, bɛŋwaanɩ tɩʋ kʋntʋ wʋnɩ nɔɔna zanzan mʋ wʋra ba na yɩ amʋ tɩtɩ nɔɔna.».

eng: So he stayed there for eighteen months, teaching Gods word among them.
local: Pooli dɛɛn maa zʋʋrɩ da taan bɩnɩ dɩ canɩ sɩrdʋ. O na wʋra tɩn, o ma brɩ-ba Wɛ taanɩ dɩm.

eng: Now when Gallio was the governor of the province of Achaia, the Jews united in their opposition against Paul and brought him before the court.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ dɛɛn tu o ji Akayi tɩɩnɩ dɩm gʋvɩrma tu, o yɩrɩ mʋ Galɩʋ. O na wʋra maŋa kalʋ tɩn, Zwifǝ bam ma kɩ ni-dɩdwɩ yɩ ba ja Pooli ba vu saŋɩ o tee nɩ.

eng: This man is persuading others to worship God unlawfully, they declared.
local: Ba ma ta dɩ Galɩʋ ba wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ mʋ jɩgɩ nɔɔna o sʋga, o pa ba tɔgɩ Wɛ kʋ na wʋ maŋɩ dɩ cullu na brɩ te tɩn.».

eng: Just as Paul was about to speak, Gallio said to the Jews, If there had been some sort of injury or criminal behavior, I would have reason to accept your complaint.
local: Pooli ya maa bʋŋɩ sɩ o ŋɔɔnɩ, yɩ Galɩʋ wuuri o ta dɩ Zwifǝ bam o wɩ: «Kʋ ya na yɩ ŋwɩɩnʋ taanɩ naa kǝm-balɔrɔ kʋdoŋ taanɩ mʋ á jɩgɩ sɩ á ŋɔɔnɩ, kʋ yaá maŋɩ sɩ a zuri a yɩra a cǝgi abam.

eng: However, since these are squabbles about a message, names, and your own Law, deal with them yourselves. I have no desire to sit in judgment over such things.
local: Kʋ nan na yɩ abam na magɩ kantɔgɔ abam tɩtɩ cullu tɩm baŋa nɩ yɩ kʋ yɩ bɩtarbiǝ dɩ nɔɔna yɩra ŋwaanɩ mʋ tɩn, abam tɩtɩ mʋ maŋɩ sɩ á nii kʋ baŋa nɩ. A bá sɛ sɩ a di abam taana yantʋ donnǝ.».

eng: He expelled them from the court.
local: O na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma zǝli-ba sɩ ba nuŋi taanɩ dɩm dim jǝgǝ kam nɩ.

eng: but everyone seized Sosthenes, the synagogue leader, and gave him a beating in the presence of the governor. None of this mattered to Gallio.
local: Nɔɔna bam maama laan ma zaŋɩ ba ja Sositɛnɩ wʋlʋ na yɩ ba Wɛ-digǝ kam yuutu tɩn, ba mag-o taanɩ dɩm dim jǝgǝ kam nɩ. Galɩʋ yigǝ nan maŋɩ ka tǝrǝ dɩ ba kǝm dɩm.

eng: After Paul stayed in Corinth for some time, he said good-bye to the brothers and sisters. At the Corinthian seaport of Cenchreae he had his head shaved, since he had made a solemn promise. Then, accompanied by Priscilla and Aquila, he sailed away to Syria.
local: Pooli dɛɛn ma kɩ da kɔgɔ kɔgɔ Korɛntɩ nɩ Zezi karabiǝ bam tee nɩ. O laan ma zaŋɩ o banɩ-ba sɩ o viiri. O ma pa Prisili dɩ Akwila tɔgɩ dɩd-o ba zʋ naboro sɩ ba vu Siiri. Ba ta na wʋ zʋ naboro kʋm sɩ ba kɛ tɩn, Pooli ya zɩgɩ Sankrɩ nɩ o pa ba fanɩ o yuu, sɩ kʋ brɩ nɩ o goni o ni dɩ Wɛ.

eng: After they arrived in Ephesus, he left Priscilla and Aquila and entered the synagogue and interacted with the Jews.
local: Ba ma tɔgɩ nɩnɩʋ kʋm wʋ ba vu ba yi Efɛɛzɩ. Pooli ma yagɩ Prisili dɩ Akwila dáanɩ. O ma vu o zʋ Zwifǝ bam Wɛ-digǝ kam o ŋɔɔnɩ dɩ ba.

eng: They asked him to stay longer, but he declined.
local: Ba ma loor-o sɩ o maŋɩ ba tee nɩ maŋa fɩnfɩɩn. O ma wʋ sɛ.

eng: As he said farewell to them, though, he added, God willing, I will return. Then he sailed off from Ephesus.
local: O ma banɩ-ba, yɩ o ta o wɩ: «Wɛ na sɛ, aá joori a ba a na abam.» O daa ma zʋ naboro Efɛɛzɩ nɩ o kɛ.

eng: He arrived in Caesarea, went up to Jerusalem and greeted the church, and then went down to Antioch.
local: O dɛɛn ma vu o yi Sezaarɩ. O ma zɩgɩ dáanɩ o vu tɩga nɩ o vu o yi Zeruzalɛm. O ma jɔɔnɩ Zezi kɔgɔ kʋm, yɩ o daarɩ o kɛ o vu Antɩɔsɩ.

eng: After some time there he left and traveled from place to place in the region of Galatia and the district of Phrygia, strengthening all the disciples.
local: O ma daanɩ da da fɩnfɩɩn, yɩ o laan zaŋɩ o kɛ. O ma vu o tʋlɩ Galatɩ dɩ Friizi tɩɩnɩ dɩm wʋ, yɩ o pa Zezi karabiǝ bam maama fɔgɩ ba zɩgɩ kǝŋkǝŋ.

eng: Meanwhile, a certain Jew named Apollos arrived in Ephesus. He was a native of Alexandria and was well-educated and effective in his use of the scriptures.
local: Zwifu wʋdoŋ dɛɛn mʋ wʋra, o yɩrɩ mʋ Apolɔsɩ. Ba lʋg-o Alɛsandrɩ nɩ mʋ. Wʋntʋ ma ba Efɛɛzɩ. O dɛɛn yɩ wʋlʋ na waɩ taanɩ o ŋɔɔnɩ pʋrɩ pʋrɩ tɩn mʋ. O maa kwǝri o ye Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm wǝǝnu tɩm kuri maama lanyɩranɩ.

eng: He had been instructed in the way of the Lord and spoke as one stirred up by the Spirit. He taught accurately the things about Jesus, even though he was aware only of the baptism John proclaimed and practiced.
local: Nɔɔna dɛɛn maŋɩ ba brɩ-o dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm cɩga kam na yɩ te tɩn. O maa wʋra o brɩ nɔɔna dɩ o wʋ maama, sɩ o pa ba lwarɩ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ maama na brɩ Zezi cɩga kam tɩn. Kʋ nan na yɩ nɔɔna na pa ba miisi-ba na wʋnɩ Wɛ ŋwaanɩ te tɩn, o ye Zan na miisi nɔɔna na wʋnɩ te tɩn yɩranɩ mʋ.

eng: He began speaking with confidence in the synagogue. When Priscilla and Aquila heard him, they received him into their circle of friends and explained to him Gods way more accurately.
local: O ma puli sɩ o ta ŋɔɔnɩ Wɛ cɩga kam dɩ baarɩ Zwifǝ bam Wɛ-digǝ kam nɩ. Prisili dɩ Akwila ma ba ba lwarɩ o zaasɩm dɩm na yɩ te tɩn. Ba ma pa o tɔgɩ o zʋʋrɩ dɩ ba, yɩ ba fɔgɩ ba maŋɩ Wɛ cwǝŋǝ kam cɩga na yɩ te tɩn ba brɩ-o.

eng: When he wanted to travel to Achaia, the brothers and sisters encouraged him and wrote to the disciples so they would open their homes to him. Once he arrived, he was of great help to those who had come to believe through grace.
local: Apolɔsɩ laan ma bʋŋɩ sɩ o nuŋi Efɛɛzɩ nɩ o kɛ o vu Akayi. Zezi karabiǝ bam ma pu o wʋbʋŋa yam kwaga nɩ. Ba ma pʋpʋnɩ tɔnɔ sɩ ba pa Zezi karabiǝ balʋ na wʋ Akayi nɩ tɩn, ba ta dɩ ba sɩ ba fɔgɩ ba jeer-o lanyɩranɩ. O ma sɩɩnɩ o zaŋɩ o kɛ. O na yi Akayi tɩn, o ma tiini o wǝli balʋ na sɛ Wɛ zaanɩ dɩm tɩn sɩ ba kɩ ba wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Zezi.

eng: He would vigorously defeat Jewish arguments in public debate, using the scriptures to prove that Jesus was the Christ.
local: O ma kwaanɩ o ŋɔɔnɩ dɩ Zwifǝ bam nɔɔna maama yigǝ nɩ, yɩ o magɩ kantɔgɔ o wanɩ-ba, dɩ o na mɛ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm o brɩ-ba nɩ Zezi sɩɩnɩ o yɩ Krisi wʋm Wɛ na tʋŋɩ tɩn.

eng: When the riot was over, Paul sent for the disciples, encouraged them, said good-bye, and left for the province of Macedonia.
local: Tɩʋ kʋm vuvugǝ kam na tu ka ti tɩn, Pooli laan ma zaŋɩ sɩ o kɛ. O ma bǝŋi Zezi karabiǝ bam sɩ ba jǝni daanɩ, yɩ o ŋɔɔnɩ dɩ ba o pa ba na baarɩ. O ma daarɩ o banɩ-ba yɩ o kɛ o vu Masɩdʋanɩ.

eng: He traveled through that region with a message of encouragement. When he came to Greece.
local: O maa tʋlɩ je sɩm kʋntʋ maama o ŋɔɔnɩ wǝǝnu zanzan o brɩ nɔɔna bam o pa ba na baarɩ. O daa ma kɛ o vu o yi Grɛkɩ tɩʋ.

eng: he stayed for three months. Because the Jews hatched a plot against Paul as he was about to sail for Syria, he decided instead to return through Macedonia.
local: O ma kɩ canɩ sɩtɔ dáanɩ. O laan ma ti o yigǝ sɩ o zʋ naboro o vu Siiri. O ma ba o lwarɩ nɩ Zwifǝ bam kɩ ni daanɩ sɩ ba kwaanɩ ba cɔg-o. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ o ma lǝni o wʋbʋŋa yɩ o bʋŋɩ sɩ o vu tɩga nɩ o tɔgɩ Masɩdʋanɩ cwǝŋǝ kam o joori o vu Siiri.

eng: He was accompanied by Sopater, Pyrrhus son from Beroea, Aristarchus and Secundus from Thessalonica, Gaius from Derbe, Timothy, and Tychicus and Trophimus from the province of Asia.
local: Balʋ na zaŋɩ sɩ ba tɔgɩ dɩd-o tɩn mʋ tɩntʋ: Sopatɛɛrɩ wʋlʋ na yɩ Pirusi bu yɩ o nuŋi Beere tɩn, Arisitakɩ dɩ Sɩkɔn, bantʋ nuŋi Tesaloniki mʋ, Gayusi wʋlʋ na nuŋi Dɛrɩbɩ tɩn, Timoti, Tisiki dɩ Trofim, ba na yɩ Azi tiinǝ tɩn.

eng: They went on ahead and waited for us in Troas.
local: Bantʋ mʋ dɛ yigǝ ba loori ba vu Trʋasɩ ba cǝgi dɩbam da.

eng: We sailed from Philippi after the Festival of Unleavened Bread and met them five days later in Troas, where we stayed for a week.
local: Dɩbam tɩtɩ laan ma maŋɩ Filipi nɩ, yɩ dɩ́ tɔgɩ dɩ́ di Zwifǝ bam candiǝ kalʋ ba na yǝni ba di dɩpɛ dɩlʋ na ba jɩgɩ dabɩlɩ tɩn. Candiǝ kam na ti tɩn, dɩ́ laan ma zʋ naboro dɩ́ tɔgɩ nɩnɩʋ kʋm wʋ dɩ́ kɩ da yanu dɩ́ vu dɩ́ yi Trʋasɩ. Dɩ́ ma jeeri dɩ́ tɔgɩ-donnǝ bam da, yɩ dɩ́ kɩ da yarpɛ tɩʋ kʋm nɩ.

eng: On the first day of the week, as we gathered together for a meal, Paul was holding a discussion with them. Since he was leaving the next day, he continued talking until midnight.
local: Dɛ dɩlʋ na saŋɩ Zwifǝ bam siun dɛ dɩm tɩn, dɩbam maama ma jeeri daanɩ sɩ dɩ́ di wʋdiu nɩ Zezi dɛɛn na fɔgɩ dɩpɛ o pa o karabiǝ bam yɩ ba di te tɩn. Pooli ma tiini o ŋɔɔnɩ o brɩ nɔɔna bam taan, kʋ vu kʋ yi tɩtɩɩ kʋnkʋrʋ, bɛŋwaanɩ tɩga na pʋʋrɩ o lagɩ o viiri mʋ.

eng: There were many lamps in the upstairs room where we had gathered.
local: Dɩ́ ya lagɩ daanɩ wɛyuu digǝ kalʋ na yɩ bɩtɔ tu tɩn wʋnɩ mʋ. Ba ma tarɩgɩ min-zwǝǝnu zanzan ba zɩgɩ da.

eng: A young man named Eutychus was sitting in the window. He was sinking into a deep sleep as Paul talked on and on. When he was sound asleep, he fell from the third floor and died.
local: Nɔn-dʋŋʋ kʋdoŋ maa tɔgɩ o wʋra, o yɩrɩ mʋ Yutiki. O maa je tǝkoro ni nɩ. Pooli na tiini o ŋɔɔnɩ o daanɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, nɔn-dʋŋʋ kʋm maa je o dɔ o vɩɩla. O laan ma ba o dɔ fasɩ o kɛ. O ma tɔgɩ tǝkoro kʋm o tʋ tɩga nɩ. Ba ma tu ba kwe-o dɩ o tɩga.

eng: Paul went down, fell on him and embraced him, then said, Dont be alarmed. Hes alive.
local: Pooli tɩtɩ ma tu o vu o pǝni nɔn-dʋŋʋ kʋm baŋa nɩ o kukwǝr-o dɩ o jɩa yɩ o wɩ: «Á yɩ pa á yɩra sɔɔrɩ, sɩ o ta ŋwɩ mʋ.».

eng: Then Paul went back upstairs and ate. He talked for a long time—right up until daybreak—then he left.
local: Pooli ma joori o di wɛyuu digǝ kam. O ma fɔ dɩpɛ dɩm o di. O daa ta ma larɩ dɩ ba taan, yɩ tɩga ba ka pʋʋrɩ. O laan ma viiri.

eng: They took the young man away alive, and they were greatly comforted.
local: O na viiri tɩn, nɔɔna bam ma ja nɔn-dʋŋʋ kʋm ba ja vu sɔŋɔ dɩ yazurǝ. Ba bɩcara ma pǝni tɩga nɩ yɩ ba jɩgɩ wʋpolo.

eng: We went on to the ship and sailed for Assos, where we intended to take Paul on board. Paul had arranged this, since he intended to make his way there by land.
local: Dɩbam ma yagɩ Pooli, yɩ dɩ́ vu dɩ́ zʋ naboro kʋm sɩ dɩ́ tɔgɩ yigǝ dɩ́ da nɩnɩʋ kʋm wʋ dɩ́ vu Asɔsɩ. Dɩ́ ya kɩ ni daanɩ sɩ dɩ́ jeeri Pooli Asɔsɩ nɩ mʋ, o na lagɩ sɩ o vu tɩga nɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: When he met us at Assos, we took him aboard and went on to Mitylene.
local: Dɩ́ na nɛ daanɩ Asɔsɩ nɩ tɩn, Pooli laan ma zʋ naboro kʋm, yɩ dɩ́ maama tɔgɩ daanɩ dɩ́ vu dɩ́ yi Mitilɛɛnɩ.

eng: The next day we sailed from there and arrived opposite Chios. On the day after, we sailed to Samos, and on the following day we came to Miletus.
local: Dɩ́ ma zɩgɩ dáanɩ dɩ́ tɔgɩ nɩnɩʋ kʋm wʋ dɩ́ kɛ. Tɩga ma pʋʋrɩ, yɩ dɩ́ vu dɩ́ tɔgɩ Kiyo buburu kʋm dɩ́ kɛ. Tɩga daa ma pʋʋrɩ, dɩ́ ma vu dɩ́ bɛ dɩ́ yi Samɔsɩ. Tɩga daa ta na pʋʋrɩ tɩn, dɩ́ ma vu dɩ́ yi Mileti.

eng: Paul had decided to sail past Ephesus so that he wouldnt need to spend too much time in the province of Asia. He was hurrying to reach Jerusalem, if possible, by Pentecost Day.
local: Pooli dɛɛn ya bʋŋɩ sɩ o da Efɛɛzɩ mʋ o kɛ sɩ o yɩ zɩgɩ da. O ya ba lagɩ sɩ o daanɩ Azi nɩ, bɛŋwaanɩ o lagɩ lɩla mʋ sɩ o na waɩ, sɩ o vu o yi Zeruzalɛm dɩ Pantɩkoti candiǝ kam ta wʋ yi.

eng: From Miletus he sent a message to Ephesus calling for the churchs elders to meet him.
local: Dɩbam dɛɛn na wʋ Mileti nɩ tɩn, Pooli ma tʋŋɩ ni o pa Zezi kɔgɔ kʋm nakwa bam Efɛɛzɩ nɩ o wɩ, ba ba.

eng: When they arrived, he said to them, You know how I lived among you the whole time I was with you, beginning with the first day I arrived in the province of Asia.
local: Ba na tu tɩn, o ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Abam maŋɩ á ye a dɛɛn na zʋʋrɩ abam tee nɩ yɩ a kɩ te maŋa maama, kʋ na zɩgɩ dɛ dɩlʋ a na sɩŋɩ a ba Azi tɩn.

eng: I served the Lord with great humility and with tears in the midst of trials that came upon me because of the Jews schemes.
local: A tʋŋɩ a pa dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm dɩ tu-n-tɩtɩ mʋ, a yi-na nuŋi, yɩ a na yaara dɩ Zwifǝ bam na kɩ ni daanɩ sɩ ba cɔgɩ amʋ te tɩn.

eng: You know I held back nothing that would be helpful so that I could proclaim to you and teach you both publicly and privately in your homes.
local: Abam ta ye nɩ, a wʋ yagɩ sɩ a ta woŋo kʋlʋ maama na wʋ́ wanɩ kʋ wǝli abam tɩn a brɩ abam. A ma kwaanɩ a brɩ abam Wɛ cɩga kam nɔɔna maama yigǝ nɩ dɩ á tɩtɩ sam nɩ.

eng: You know I have testified to both Jews and Greeks that they must change their hearts and lives as they turn to God and have faith in our Lord Jesus.
local: A ma tiini a brɩ Zwifǝ bam dɩ dwi-gɛ tiinǝ maama sɩ ba lǝni ba wʋrʋ ba sɛ Wɛ, sɩ ba daarɩ ba kɩ ba wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi.

eng: Now, compelled by the Spirit, Im going to Jerusalem. I dont know what will happen to me there.
local: Lele kʋntʋ a laan maa ve Zeruzalɛm. Kʋ yɩ Wɛ Joro kʋm mʋ fɩ-nɩ sɩ a vu, yɩ a yǝri kʋlʋ na wʋ́ vu kʋ kɩ amʋ da tɩn.

eng: What I do know is that the Holy Spirit testifies to me from city to city that prisons and troubles await me.
local: A na ye kʋlʋ tɩn mʋ yɩ sɩ Wɛ Joro kʋm yǝni kʋ brɩ amʋ tɩʋ maama nɩ, nɩ nɔɔna wʋ́ ja-nɩ ba vɔ yɩ ba kwǝri ba bɛɛsɩ-nɩ.

eng: But nothing, not even my life, is more important than my completing my mission. This is nothing other than the ministry I received from the Lord Jesus: to testify about the good news of Gods grace.
local: A nan na bʋŋɩ te tɩn, a ba paɩ sɩ a ŋwɩa taa jɩgɩ nyɔɔrɩ ka pa-nɩ. Kʋlʋ na yɩ wo-ŋʋŋʋ tɩn mʋ yɩ sɩ a tʋŋɩ tɩtʋŋa yalʋ dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi na kɩ a jɩŋa nɩ sɩ a tʋŋɩ tɩn a ti. Tɩtʋŋa yam kʋntʋ mʋ yɩ sɩ a kwaanɩ a brɩ Wɛ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam dɩ DƖ na kɩ nɔɔna lanyɩranɩ te tɩn.

eng: I know that none of you will see me again—you among whom I traveled and proclaimed the kingdom.
local: A nan ye nɩ abam wʋlʋwʋlʋ daa bá na amʋ, abam nan yɩ balʋ a na beeri á wʋnɩ a brɩ abam Wɛ paarɩ dɩm cɩga kam tɩn.

eng: Therefore, today I testify to you that Im not responsible for anyones fate.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ a lagɩ a tiini a kaanɩ abam zɩm dɛ dɩm nɩ, nɩ abam wʋlʋ na gɛ ŋwɩa Wɛ tee nɩ, kʋ daa daɩ amʋ yigǝ.

eng: I havent avoided proclaiming the entire plan of God to you.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ a tɔɔlɩ cɩga kalʋ maama na tɔgɩ Wɛ wʋbʋŋa tɩn a brɩ abam, yɩ a wʋ sǝgi abam kʋlʋkʋlʋ.

eng: Watch yourselves and the whole flock, in which the Holy Spirit has placed you as supervisors, to shepherd Gods church, which he obtained with the death of his own Son.
local: Nan fɔgɩ-na á taá nii á tɩtɩ baŋa nɩ, dɩdaanɩ kɔgɔ kʋlʋ maama Wɛ Joro kʋm na kwe kʋ kɩ abam jɩŋa nɩ sɩ á taá nii tɩn. Á taá nii Wɛ kɔgɔ kʋm baŋa nɩ, nɩ nayɩra na nii ba peeni te tɩn. Wɛ mɛ DƖ tɩtɩ bu wʋm jana mʋ DƖ ma vrɩ DƖ kɔgɔ kʋm sɩ kʋ taa yɩ DƖ nyɩm.

eng: I know that, after my departure, savage wolves will come in among you and wont spare the flock.
local: A ye nɩ, a na viiri a daarɩ abam, nɔn-balwaarʋ wʋ́ zaŋɩ ba zʋ abam wʋ ba yaarɩ abam nɩnɛɛnɩ nywǝnkuri na jaanɩ peeni te tɩn. Ba bá sɛ sɩ ba yagɩ abam kɔgɔ kʋm yɩ ba wʋ cɔgɩ abam wʋ nɔɔna.

eng: Some of your own people will distort the word in order to lure followers after them.
local: Nɔɔna dɩ wʋ́ zaŋɩ abam tɩtɩ wʋnɩ, ba pipiri Wɛ cɩga kam ba ganɩ Zezi karabiǝ bam, ba pa ba ywǝri Wɛ kwaga nɩ sɩ ba daarɩ ba tɔgɩ-ba.

eng: After we tore ourselves away from them, we set sail on a straight course to Cos, reaching Rhodes the next day, and then Patara.
local: Dɩ́ laan ma banɩ-ba yɩ dɩ́ daarɩ dɩ́ viiri. Dɩ́ ma kwe dɩ́ yigǝ dɩ́ yɔɔrɩ nɩnɩʋ kʋm wʋ dɩ́ vu dɩ́ yi Kosi. Tɩga ma pʋʋrɩ, dɩ́ daa ma yi Rodi. Dɩ́ ma zɩgɩ dáanɩ dɩ́ vu Patara.

eng: Stay alert! Remember that for three years I constantly and tearfully warned each one of you. I never stopped warning you!.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ á fɔgɩ á cɩ á tɩtɩ. Á taá guli nɩ amʋ zʋ abam tee nɩ bɩna yatɔ, yɩ a kaanɩ abam maama sɩ á taá tɔgɩ Wɛ dɩ cɩga. Maŋa maama a yǝni a kɩ kʋntʋ mʋ wɩa dɩ tɩtɩɩ, yɩ a yi-na nuŋi zanzan.

eng: We found a ship crossing over to Phoenicia, boarded, and put out to sea.
local: Dɩ́ na yi dáanɩ tɩn, dɩ́ ma na naboro kʋdoŋ kʋ na lagɩ kʋ vu Fenisi. Dɩ́ ma vu dɩ́ zʋ kʋ wʋ sɩ dɩ́ tɔgɩ dɩ́ vu.

eng: Now I entrust you to God and the message of his grace, which is able to build you up and give you an inheritance among all whom God has made holy.
local: A laan lagɩ a kɩ abam Wɛ jɩŋa nɩ, sɩ á taá tɔgɩ DƖ zaanɩ kwǝrǝ kam. Kaá wanɩ ka kwɛ abam lanyɩranɩ, sɩ á laan ba á tɔgɩ dɩ balʋ maama na yɩ Wɛ tɩtɩ nɔɔna tɩn á na ywǝǝni DƖ tee nɩ.

eng: We spotted Cyprus, but passed by it on our left. We sailed on to the province of Syria and landed in Tyre, where the ship was to unload its cargo.
local: Dɩ́ na maa ve tɩn, dɩ́ ma tʋlɩ dɩ́ na Sipri. Dɩ́ ma yagɩ-kʋ dɩ́ jagwiǝ nɩ, yɩ dɩ́ daarɩ dɩ́ kɛ. Dɩ́ ma vu dɩ́ yi Siiri. Dɩ́ na yi da tɩn, dɩ́ ma zɩgɩ Tiiri nɩ. Kʋ yɩ dáanɩ mʋ ba maŋɩ sɩ ba lɩ naboro kʋm zɩla yam.

eng: I havent craved anyones silver, gold, or clothing.
local: A wʋ joŋi sǝbu naa sǝbu-sɩŋa naa gwaarʋ abam wʋlʋwʋlʋ tee nɩ.
