eng: For a long time no one had eaten. Paul stood up among them and said, Men, you should have complied with my instructions not to sail from Crete. Then we would have avoided this damage and loss.
local: Nɔɔna bam maa na ve taan yɩ ba ba di wʋdiu tɩn, Pooli ma zaŋɩ o zɩgɩ ba wʋnɩ o ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «A badonnǝ-ba, abam ya na sɛ a ni yɩ dɩ́ wʋ zaŋɩ dɩ́ yagɩ Krɛtɩ, dɩ́ ya bá na cɔgɩm dɩ yaara yantʋ doŋ.

eng: Now I urge you to be encouraged. Not one of your lives will be lost, though we will lose the ship.
local: A laan nan loori abam sɩ á taá jɩgɩ pu-dɩa. Amʋ ye sɩ abam wʋlʋwʋlʋ bá ga o ŋwɩa, kʋ yɩ naboro kʋm yɩranɩ mʋ wʋ́ cɔgɩ.

eng: Last night an angel from the God to whom I belong and whom I worship stood beside me.
local: Kʋ yɩ diin tɩtɩɩ nɩ mʋ, Wɛ dɩlʋ na te amʋ yɩ a yɩ DƖ tɩntʋŋnʋ tɩn tʋŋɩ DƖ malɛka amʋ tee nɩ.

eng: The angel said, Dont be afraid, Paul! You must stand before Caesar! Indeed, God has also graciously given you everyone sailing with you.
local: Ka ma ta ka wɩ: ‹Pooli, yɩ ta n kwarɩ fʋʋnɩ, sɩ nmʋ maŋɩ sɩ n vu n zɩgɩ pa-farʋ wʋm yigǝ nɩ mʋ. Wɛ yɩ ŋwaŋa tu, yɩ DƖ wʋ́ pa nɔɔna balʋ na tɔgɩ ba wʋ naboro kʋm wʋnɩ tɩn dɩ na ŋwɩa.›.

eng: Be encouraged, men! I have faith in God that it will be exactly as he told me.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ, a badonnǝ-ba, á taá jɩgɩ-na pu-dɩa. A jɩgɩ tɩɩna dɩ Wɛ, nɩ malɛka kam na tagɩ kʋlʋ ka brɩ-nɩ tɩn wʋ́ sɩɩnɩ kʋ kɩ.

eng: However, we must run aground on some island.
local: Dɩ kʋntʋ dɩ, viu kʋm maŋɩ sɩ kʋ magɩ dɩbam kʋ ja vu kʋ zɩgɩ bubɛ dɩdoŋ yuu nɩ mʋ.».

eng: On the fourteenth night, we were being carried across the Adriatic Sea. Around midnight the sailors began to suspect that land was near.
local: Viu kʋm dɛɛn maa magɩ dɩbam taan, kʋ jɩgɩ kʋ maa ve Adrɩa nɩnɩʋ kʋm wʋnɩ. Kʋ dɛɛn yɩ tɩtɩɩ dɩlʋ na kɩ dɩbam da fugǝ yana na bam wʋnɩ tɩn mʋ. Tɩtɩɩ kʋnkʋrʋ nɩ balʋ na co naboro kʋm tɩn maa bʋŋɩ nɩ dɩ́ wʋra dɩ́ twɛ bubɛ mʋ.

eng: They dropped a weighted line to take soundings and found the water to be about one hundred twenty feet deep. After proceeding a little farther, we took soundings again and found the water to be about ninety feet deep.
local: Ba ma kwe kandwɛ na lǝgǝ ŋʋna ni nɩ ba dɩ na bam wʋnɩ, sɩ ba lwarɩ na bam na luunǝ te tɩn. Ba ma na nɩ ba luunǝ ba maŋɩ nɩ mɛtra fiinna mʋ te. Kʋ na kɩ fɩɩn tɩn, ba daa ma dɩ ŋʋna kam sɩ ba nii, yɩ ba na nɩ na bam ta luunǝ ba maŋɩ nɩ mɛtra fiintɔ mʋ te.

eng: Afraid that we might run aground somewhere on the rocks, they hurled out four anchors from the stern and began to pray for daylight.
local: Ba maa kwarɩ fʋʋnɩ sɩ viu kʋm wʋ́ magɩ naboro kʋm, kʋ ja vu kʋ magɩ dɩ pweeru. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ ba ma lɩ naboro kʋm zɩgɩm luguru tɩna kʋ kwaga nɩ ba dɩ na bam wʋnɩ sɩ kʋ ma zɩgɩ. Ba ma daarɩ ba loori Wɛ sɩ tɩga pʋʋrɩ lɩla.

eng: The sailors tried to abandon the ship by lowering the lifeboat into the sea, pretending they were going to lower anchors from the bow.
local: Naboro kʋm coonǝ bam ma bʋŋɩ sɩ ba lu sɩ ba daarɩ naboro kʋm. Ba ma lɩ naboro balaŋa kam ba dɩ na bam wʋnɩ, yɩ ba kɩ nɩnɛɛnɩ ba lagɩ ba vu ba lɩ naboro kʋm yigǝ luguru tɩm mʋ sɩ ba ma zɩgɩ-kʋ te.

eng: Paul said to the centurion and his soldiers, Unless they stay in the ship, you cant be saved from peril.
local: Pooli na lwarɩ ba na bʋŋɩ sɩ ba kɩ te tɩn, o ma ta dɩ pamaŋna yigǝ tu wʋm dɩ o nɔɔna bam o wɩ: «Nɔɔna bantʋ na nuŋi ba daarɩ naboro kʋm, abam daa bá wanɩ á lu.».

eng: The soldiers then cut the ropes to the lifeboat and let it drift away.
local: Pooli na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, pamaŋna bam ma go ŋʋnɩ sɩlʋ ba na mɛ ba lǝ boro balaŋa kam tɩn ba dɩ-ka ba yagɩ.

eng: Just before daybreak, Paul urged everyone to eat. He said, This is the fourteenth day youve lived in suspense, and youve not had even a bite to eat.
local: Tɩga na wʋra ka pʋʋrɩ tɩn, Pooli ma loori ba maama sɩ ba zaŋɩ ba di wʋdiu. O ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Zɩm mʋ jɩgɩ da fugǝ yana liǝ zanzan na jɩgɩ abam yɩ á warɩ wʋdiu sɩ á di.

eng: I urge you to take some food. Your health depends on it. None of you will lose a single hair from his head.
local: A nan loori abam sɩ á zaŋɩ á di wʋdiu. Á na wʋ kɩ kʋntʋ, á bá wǝri. Á taá ye nɩ abam wʋlʋwʋlʋ bá lagɩ á yuuyuǝ tɩtɩ dɩ á ga.».

eng: After he said these things, he took bread, gave thanks to God in front of them all, then broke it and began to eat.
local: Pooli na tagɩ kʋntʋ o ti tɩn, o ma kwe dɩpɛ o zɩgɩ ba maama yigǝ nɩ o kɩ Wɛ le, yɩ o daarɩ o fɔ-dɩ o wʋra o di.

eng: Everyone was encouraged and took some food. .
local: Kʋ ma pa ba maama pu-dɩa yɩ ba dɩ di wʋdiu.

eng: In all, there were two hundred seventy-six of us on the ship.
local: Dɩbam maama ni dɛɛn yɩ nɔɔna biǝ-yale dɩ fusɩrpɛ bardʋ mʋ naboro kʋm wʋnɩ.

eng: When they had eaten as much as they wanted, they lightened the ship by throwing the grain into the sea.
local: Ba maama na di ba zaŋɩ tɩn, ba ma lɩ mɩna ywǝǝlu tɩm naboro kʋm wʋnɩ ba dɩ na bam wʋnɩ, sɩ kʋ daa yɩ ta dunǝ.

eng: In the morning light they saw a bay with a sandy beach. They didnt know what land it was, but they thought they might possibly be able to run the ship aground.
local: Tɩga laan na pʋʋrɩ tɩn, nɔɔna bam maa yǝri buburu kʋlʋ ni nɩ ba na wʋra tɩn. Ba ma nii ba na gugoro na na ve ba tigi da tɩn. Ba maa bʋŋɩ sɩ ba nii ba na wʋ́ wanɩ ba pa naboro kʋm tɔgɩ dáanɩ kʋ bɛ.

eng: They cut the anchors loose and left them in the sea. At the same time, they untied the ropes that ran back to the rudders. They raised the foresail to catch the wind and made for the beach.
local: Ba ma go ŋʋnɩ sɩlʋ na lǝgi naboro kʋm zɩgɩm luguru tɩm tɩn ba yagɩ na bam wʋnɩ. Ba ma daarɩ ba bwǝli ŋʋnɩ sɩlʋ na vɔgɩ naboro kʋm naywi sɩm tɩn, sɩ ba taa paɩ kʋ ve nɩ ba na lagɩ te tɩn. Ba laan ma kwe naboro gɔrɔ ba lǝ naboro kʋm yigǝ nɩ sɩ viu kʋm taa magɩ-kʋ, sɩ naboro kʋm wanɩ kʋ bɛ.

eng: But they struck a sandbar and the ship ran aground. The bow was stuck and wouldnt move, and the stern was broken into pieces by the force of the waves.
local: Naboro kʋm ma vu kʋ yi na-kʋla jǝgǝ kʋ cǝŋi da. Kʋ yigǝ kam maa mʋʋrɩ kasʋla wʋnɩ, yɩ ka daa warɩ ka lɩ. Kʋ daarɩ na bam maa tiini ba pɔgɩla, yɩ ba magɩ naboro kʋm kwaga kam ba pa ka wʋra ka cɔga.

eng: The soldiers decided to kill the prisoners to keep them from swimming to shore and escaping.
local: Pamaŋna bam dɛɛn ma kɩ ni daanɩ sɩ ba ja pɩɩna bam ba gʋ, sɩ ba wʋlʋwʋlʋ yɩ zaŋɩ o dɩn na bam o vrɩ o tɩtɩ ba jɩa nɩ.

eng: However, the centurion wanted to save Paul, so he stopped them from carrying out their plan. He ordered those who could swim to jump overboard first and head for land.
local: Ba yigǝ tu wʋm maa lagɩ sɩ o vrɩ Pooli ŋwɩa o yagɩ. O ma cɩ-ba sɩ ba yɩ kɩ kʋlʋ ba na bʋŋɩ tɩn. O ma daarɩ o pa ni, sɩ nɔɔna balʋ na wʋ́ wanɩ ba dɩn ba nuŋi tɩn da yigǝ ba faŋɩ ba cu na bam wʋnɩ sɩ ba dɩn ba bɛ.

eng: He ordered the rest to grab hold of planks or debris from the ship. In this way, everyone reached land safely.
local: sɩ balʋ na daarɩ tɩn kwe naboro kʋm da-dɩdwaarʋ tɩm naa kʋ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ na bwǝri tɩn ba ma dɩn ba bɛ. Kʋntʋ mʋ dɩbam maama kɩ dɩ́ ma joŋi dɩ́ tɩtɩ.

eng: After reaching land safely, we learned that the island was called Malta.
local: Dɩbam na lugi yɩ dɩ́ bɛ bubɛ dɩm kʋntʋ tɩn, mʋ dɩ́ lwarɩ nɩ bubɛ dɩm kʋntʋ yɩrɩ mʋ Malɩtɩ.

eng: The islanders showed us extraordinary kindness. Because it was rainy and cold, they built a fire and welcomed all of us.
local: Nɔɔna balʋ na zʋʋrɩ dáanɩ tɩn maa ja dɩbam ŋwaŋa zanzan. Dʋa maa sɩŋɩ ka wʋra ka nɩa, ka pa waarʋ wʋra. Ba ma dwe mini ba pa dɩbam maama sɩ dɩ́ weeri.

eng: Paul gathered a bunch of dry sticks and put them on the fire. As he did, a poisonous snake, driven out by the heat, latched on to his hand.
local: Pooli ma puuri dɛ o wʋra o kɩ mini dɩm wʋnɩ. Mini dɩm wulim ma pa bɩsankwɩa nuŋi dɛ sɩm wʋnɩ ka karɩ Pooli jɩŋa ka lǝgi da.

eng: When the islanders saw the snake hanging from his hand, they said to each other, This man must be a murderer! He was rescued from the sea, but the goddess Justice hasnt let him live.
local: Tɩʋ kʋm tiinǝ bam na nɛ bɩsankwɩa kam na lǝgi Pooli jɩŋa kam nɩ tɩn, ba maa tɛ daanɩ ba wɩ: «Kǝm dɩntʋ brɩ nɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ yɩ nɔn-gʋrʋ mʋ. Dɩ o na lugi nɩnɩʋ kʋm wʋnɩ tɩn dɩ, kʋ ta wʋ maŋɩ sɩ o taa ŋwɩ. Wɛ wʋ sɛ sɩ o wǝri.».

eng: Paul shook the snake into the fire and suffered no harm.
local: Pooli ma mi bɩsankwɩa kam o dɩ mini dɩm wʋnɩ, yɩ kʋlʋkʋlʋ wʋ kɩ-o.

eng: They expected him to swell up with fever or suddenly drop dead. After waiting a long time and seeing nothing unusual happen to him, they changed their minds and began to claim that he was a god.
local: Nɔɔna bam maa bʋŋɩ nɩ, o yɩra ga wʋ́ fʋlɩ mʋ naa oó da o tʋ tɩga nɩ mʋ o tɩ. Ba na cǝgi kʋntʋ taan tɩn, ba ma wʋ nɛ nɩ kʋlʋkʋlʋ kɩ-o. Ba ma lǝni ba wʋbʋŋa yɩ ba daarɩ ba wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ yɩ wɛ dɩdoŋ mʋ!».

eng: Publius, the islands most prominent person, owned a large estate in that area. He welcomed us warmly into his home as his guests for three days.
local: Jǝgǝ kam kʋntʋ seeni nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ kara batwarɩ da. Nɔɔnʋ wʋm yɩrɩ mʋ Pubilu. Wʋntʋ mʋ yɩ tɩʋ kʋm kʋntʋ dɩdɛɛrʋ. O ma joŋi dɩbam lanyɩranɩ o sɔŋɔ nɩ. Dɩ́ ma kɩ da yatɔ da.

eng: Publius father was bedridden, sick with a fever and dysentery. Paul went to see him and prayed. He placed his hand on him and healed him.
local: O ko maa tigi o ba jɩgɩ yazurǝ. Paa dɩ wʋ-zuru mʋ jɩg-o. Pooli ma zʋ o digǝ kam sɩ o na-o. O ma daŋɩ o jɩa o baŋa nɩ o loori Wɛ o pa-o, yɩ o yawɩʋ kʋm je.

eng: Once this happened, the rest of the sick on the island came to him and were healed.
local: O na kɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, tɩʋ kʋm tiinǝ balʋ maama na ba jɩgɩ yazurǝ tɩn maa tui o te, yɩ ba na yazurǝ.

eng: They honored us in many ways. When we were getting ready to sail again, they supplied us with what we needed.
local: Ba ma zuli dɩbam, yɩ ba kwe pɛɛra zanzan ba ja ba ba pa dɩbam. Maŋa kalʋ dɩ́ daa na lagɩ dɩ́ zʋ naboro sɩ dɩ́ kɛ tɩn, ba ma pa dɩbam kʋlʋ maama na maŋɩ dɩ dɩ́ vǝŋǝ kam tɩn sɩ dɩ́ ja dɩ́ viiri.

eng: After three months we put out to sea in a ship that had spent the winter at the island. It was an Alexandrian ship with carvings of the twin gods Castor and Pollux as its figurehead.
local: Dɩ́ dɛɛn kɩ canɩ sɩtɔ mʋ tɩʋ kʋm kʋntʋ nɩ, yɩ dɩ́ laan na naboro kʋdoŋ kʋlʋ ba na ma bǝ nɩ Wa-yiywa tɩn. Kʋ dɛɛn nuŋi Alɛsandrɩ mʋ kʋ ba kʋ zɩgɩ bubɛ dɩm kʋntʋ ni nɩ taan, yɩ vu-dɩʋ kʋm maŋa kɛ. Dɩ́ dɛɛn ma zʋ kʋ wʋ sɩ dɩ́ kɛ.

eng: We landed in Syracuse where we stayed three days.
local: Dɩ́ ma tɔgɩ nɩnɩʋ kʋm wʋ dɩ́ vu dɩ́ yi Sirakusi. Dɩ́ ma pǝni da da yatɔ.

eng: From there we sailed to Rhegium. After one day a south wind came up, and we arrived on the second day in Puteoli.
local: Dɩ́ ma zaŋɩ dáanɩ dɩ́ tɔgɩ nɩnɩʋ kʋm wʋ dɩ́ vu dɩ́ yi Rɩgiu. Tɩga na pʋʋrɩ tɩn, viu ma daa zaŋɩ dɩ́ kwaga nɩ kʋ sɩŋɩ kʋ maga. Dɩ́ ma kɩ da yale, yɩ dɩ́ vu dɩ́ yi Pusoli dɩ́ zɩgɩ da.

eng: There we found brothers and sisters who urged us to stay with them for a week. In this way we came to Rome.
local: Dɩ́ ma nuŋi naboro kʋm wʋnɩ dɩ́ vu dɩ́ maŋɩ dɩ Zezi karabiǝ badonnǝ. Ba ma loori dɩbam sɩ dɩ́ kɩ da yarpɛ ba tee nɩ. Dɩ́ ma kɩ da yarpɛ dáanɩ, yɩ dɩ́ laan vu dɩ́ yi Rom.

eng: When the brothers and sisters there heard about us, they came as far as the Forum of Appius and the Three Taverns to meet us. When Paul saw them, he gave thanks to God and was encouraged.
local: Zezi karabiǝ balʋ na wʋ Rom nɩ tɩn ma ni dɩbam ŋwa. Ba ma zaŋɩ Rom nɩ sɩ ba nuŋi ba jeeri dɩbam cwǝŋǝ nɩ. Ba dɛɛn tu ba yi Apiusi yaga kam na wʋ mɛ tɩn, yɩ badaara dɩ dɛɛn ba jǝgǝ kalʋ ba na bǝ nɩ Vǝrǝ Di Sɩtɔ tɩn, sɩ ba jeeri dɩbam. Pooli na nɛ-ba kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma kɩ Wɛ le yɩ kʋ pa-o baarɩ.

eng: When we entered Rome, Paul was permitted to live by himself, with a soldier guarding him.
local: Dɩ́ na ve dɩ́ yi Rom kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma pa Pooli cwǝŋǝ sɩ o taa zʋʋrɩ o na lagɩ mɛ tɩn, yɩ ba pa pamaŋnʋ tɔgɩ o yɩr-o.

eng: Three days later, Paul called the Jewish leaders together. When they gathered, he said, Brothers, although I have done nothing against our people or the customs of our ancestors, Im a prisoner from Jerusalem. They handed me over to the Romans.
local: Da yatɔ na kɛ tɩn, Pooli ma bǝŋi tɩʋ kʋm Zwifǝ bam yigǝ tiinǝ sɩ ba jeeri daanɩ. Ba na tu tɩn, Pooli ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «A ko-biǝ-ba, a wʋ kɩ kʋlʋkʋlʋ a cɔgɩ dɩbam nɔɔna bam wʋ, naa a cɔgɩ cullu tɩlʋ dɩbam nabaara na tiŋi ba pa dɩbam tɩn. Dɩ kʋ dɩ, ba jaanɩ amʋ Zeruzalɛm nɩ mʋ ba kɩ Rom tiinǝ jɩŋa nɩ sɩ ba pɩ-nɩ.

eng: who intended to release me after they examined me, because they couldnt find any reason for putting me to death.
local: Ba dɛɛn ma bwe-nɩ taanɩ sɩ ba nii a na kɩ kʋlʋ tɩn. Ba ma wʋ nɛ a na kɩ kʋlʋ a cɔgɩ sɩ kʋ pa ba gʋ-nɩ tɩn. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ ba ya maa lagɩ sɩ ba yagɩ-nɩ.

eng: When the Jews objected, I was forced to appeal to Caesar. Dont think I appealed to Caesar because I had any reason to bring charges against my nation.
local: Zwifǝ bam ma wʋ sɛ kʋntʋ. Mʋ kʋ kuri kʋ dɛɛn yɩ fɩfɩʋn sɩ a ta sɩ Rom pa-farʋ wʋm tɩtɩ di a taanɩ dɩm. Kʋ nan daɩ nɩ amʋ ga jɩgɩ taanɩ mʋ dɩ a tɩtɩ dwi tiinǝ bam.

eng: This is why I asked to see you and speak with you: its because of the hope of Israel that I am bound with this chain.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ a bǝŋi abam sɩ á ba, sɩ dɩ́ ŋɔɔnɩ daanɩ dɩ́ nii. Ba na vɔgɩ amʋ dɩ capʋnnʋ tɩm tɩn, kʋ yɩ a na sɛ wʋlʋ wʋm Yisɩrayɛlɩ tiinǝ bam na jɩgɩ o tɩɩna tɩn ŋwaanɩ mʋ.».

eng: They responded, We havent received any letters about you from Judea, nor have any of our brothers come and reported or said anything bad about you.
local: Pooli na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma ta dɩd-o ba wɩ: «Dɩbam nan wʋ joŋi tɔnɔ Zude tiinǝ bam tee nɩ ba na pʋpʋnɩ ba ta nmʋ taanɩ tɩn. Dɩbam ta wʋ nɛ dɩ́ ko-bu wʋlʋ na nuŋi da o ba o ta nmʋ taanɩ o brɩ dɩbam, naa o ta wo-balɔrɔ o pa nmʋ.

eng: But we think its important to hear what you think, for we know that people everywhere are speaking against this faction.
local: Dɩbam nan wʋ́ ta lagɩ sɩ dɩ́ lwarɩ nmʋ na jɩgɩ kʋlʋ n wʋbʋŋa nɩ tɩn. Bɛŋwaanɩ dɩ́ maŋɩ dɩ́ ye nɩ, nmʋ na tɔgɩ cwǝ-dʋŋa kalʋ tɩn, nɔɔna yǝni ba ŋɔɔna ba yáalɩ-ka je maama.».

eng: On the day scheduled for this purpose, many people came to the place where he was staying. From morning until evening, he explained and testified concerning Gods kingdom and tried to convince them about Jesus through appealing to the Law from Moses and the Prophets.
local: Ba laan ma lagɩ dɛ ba pa Pooli sɩ ba jeeri daanɩ. Dɛ dɩm na yi tɩn, nɔɔna zanzan ma ba jǝgǝ kalʋ Pooli na zʋʋrɩ da tɩn. Ba na tu tɩn, o ma tiini o ŋɔɔnɩ taan o brɩ-ba, kʋ zɩgɩ zɩzɩŋa nɩ kʋ vu kʋ yi dɩdaan-ni. O ma ta Wɛ paarɩ dɩm na yɩ te tɩn o brɩ-ba. O ma ma Wɛ cɩga kalʋ na pʋpʋnɩ Moyisi cullu tɔnɔ kʋm dɩ faŋa faŋa Wɛ nijoŋnǝ bam twaanʋ tɩm wʋnɩ tɩn o brɩ-ba, yɩ o kwaanɩ sɩ o pa ba sɛ cɩga kalʋ o na tɛ Zezi ŋwaanɩ tɩn.

eng: Some were persuaded by what he said, but others refused to believe.
local: O na brɩ-ba kʋntʋ tɩn, badaara ma sɛ o taanɩ dɩm. Badaara dɩ ma wʋ sɛ.

eng: They disagreed with each other and were starting to leave when Paul made one more statement: The Holy Spirit spoke correctly when he said to your ancestors through Isaiah the prophet.
local: Ba ma zaŋɩ sɩ ba viiri, yɩ ba magɩ kantɔgɔ daanɩ dɩ Pooli na tagɩ kʋlʋ tɩn. Ba ta na wʋ viiri tɩn, mʋ Pooli tagɩ taanɩ dɩdoŋ o wǝli da o wɩ: «Wɛ Joro kʋm dɛɛn tɔgɩ Wɛ nijoŋnu Ezayi kwǝrǝ mʋ kʋ ta cɩga dɩ abam nabaara bam kʋ wɩ:.

eng: Go to this people and say: You will hear, to be sure, but never understand; and you will certainly see but never recognize what you are seeing. .
local: Ve a nɔɔna bam te sɩ n ta dɩ ba nɩ: Abam wʋ́ fɔgɩ á cǝgi Wɛ taanɩ dɩm dɩ á zwa, yɩ á bá ni dɩ kuri. Abam wʋ́ fɔgɩ á nii dɩ á yiǝ, yɩ á bá na.

eng: This peoples senses have become calloused, and theyve become hard of hearing, and theyve shut their eyes so that they wont see with their eyes or hear with their ears or understand with their minds, and change their hearts and lives that I may heal them. .
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ nɔɔna bam kʋntʋ wʋbʋŋa tɩgɩ mʋ. Ba zwa dɩ ma kwarɩmɩ, yɩ ba daarɩ ba pu ba yiǝ, sɩ ba yiǝ yɩ zaŋɩ ya na, sɩ ba zwa yɩ zaŋɩ ya ni, sɩ ba wʋbʋŋa yɩ zaŋɩ ya lwarɩ Wɛ taanɩ dɩm kuri. Kʋ na daɩ kʋntʋ, ba ya wʋ́ pipiri ba sɛ amʋ Wɛ yɩ a pa-ba yazurǝ.».

eng: Therefore, be certain of this: Gods salvation has been sent to the Gentiles. They will listen.
local: Pooli laan ma ta o wǝli da o wɩ: «Á nan taá ye nɩ Wɛ pɛ dwi-gɛ tiinǝ mʋ lwarɩ DƖ na wʋ́ joŋi-ba DƖ yagɩ te tɩn. Bantʋ nan wʋ́ sɛ DƖ kwǝrǝ kam.» .

eng: Paul lived in his own rented quarters for two full years and welcomed everyone who came to see him.
local: Pooli dɛɛn kɩ bɩna yale mʋ jǝgǝ kam kʋntʋ nɩ, yɩ o zʋʋrɩ mɛ o na ŋwɩ sɩ o taa tigi da tɩn. O maa yǝni o jeeri nɔɔna balʋ maama na tui o te sɩ ba na-o tɩn.

eng: Unhindered and with complete confidence, he continued to preach Gods kingdom and to teach about the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: O maa yǝni o brɩ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm cɩga kam, yɩ o tɔɔlɩ dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi Krisi kwǝrǝ. O dɛɛn ŋɔɔnɩ dɩ baarɩ mʋ, yɩ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ wʋ cɩgɩ o yigǝ.

eng: So every single one of you who judge others is without any excuse. You condemn yourself when you judge another person because the one who is judging is doing the same things.
local: Nmʋ wʋlʋ na co n donnǝ tɩn, kʋ yɩ nmʋ tɩtɩ mʋ n coǝ, bɛŋwaanɩ nmʋ tɩtɩ dɩ kɩ wo-lwaanʋ tɩm kʋntʋ doŋ mʋ. Kʋntʋ tɩn, n bá wanɩ n na bʋra Wɛ tee nɩ.

eng: We know that Gods judgment agrees with the truth, and his judgment is against those who do these kinds of things.
local: Dɩ́ nan ye nɩ Wɛ wʋ́ di nɔɔna balʋ na kɩ wo-lwaanʋ tɩm kʋntʋ doŋ tɩn taanɩ dɩ cɩga mʋ.

eng: If you judge those who do these kinds of things while you do the same things yourself, think about this: Do you believe that you will escape Gods judgment?.
local: Nmʋ wʋlʋ na co n donnǝ kikiǝ yɩ nmʋ tɩtɩ dɩ kɩ-ya mʋ tɩn, kʋntʋ nmʋ bʋŋɩ nɩ nń wanɩ n lu Wɛ sarɩya yam wʋnɩ mʋ na?.

eng: Or do you have contempt for the riches of Gods generosity, tolerance, and patience? Dont you realize that Gods kindness is supposed to lead you to change your heart and life?.
local: Wɛ nan tiini DƖ jɩgɩ nɔɔna ŋwaŋa dɩ ban-pʋnʋ dɩ wʋ-zuru. Nmʋ nan paɩ wǝǝnu tɩm kʋntʋ yɩ kafɛ mʋ na? Ta n ye nɩ, Wɛ na jɩgɩ nmʋ ŋwaŋa tɩn, kʋ yɩ sɩ kʋ pa nmʋ wanɩ n lǝni n wʋ mʋ n yagɩ kǝm-balwaarʋ.

eng: You are storing up wrath for yourself because of your stubbornness and your heart that refuses to change. Gods just judgment will be revealed on the day of wrath.
local: Nmʋ nan tiini n kʋ n bɩcarɩ mʋ yɩ n vɩn sɩ n lǝni n wʋ. Kʋntʋ mʋ wʋ́ pa n cam dɩm tiini dɩ puli dɛ dɩm Wɛ na wʋ́ ba DƖ brɩ DƖ ban-zɔŋɔ kʋm yɩ DƖ daarɩ DƖ di nabiinǝ sarɩya dɩ cɩga tɩn.

eng: God will repay everyone based on their works .
local: Wɛ laan wʋ́ pa nɔɔnʋ maama na o nyɩm sɩ kʋ maŋɩ dɩ o tɩtʋŋa yam na yɩ te tɩn.

eng: On the one hand, he will give eternal life to those who look for glory, honor, and immortality based on their patient good work.
local: Nɔɔna badaara yǝni ba vɔ ba wʋrʋ mʋ yɩ ba kɩ wo-laarʋ, sɩ kʋ pa ba na paarɩ dɩ zulǝ dɩ ŋwɩa kalʋ na ba cɔgɩ tɩn Wɛ tee nɩ. Wɛ nan wʋ́ pa bantʋ ŋwɩa kalʋ na ba ti tɩn.

eng: But on the other hand, there will be wrath and anger for those who obey wickedness instead of the truth because they are acting out of selfishness and disobedience.
local: Nɔɔna badaara nan wʋra ba na lagɩ bam tɩtɩ yɩranɩ zǝnǝ mʋ. Ba maa vɩn Wɛ cɩga kam, yɩ ba daarɩ ba tɔgɩ wo-balwaarʋ kǝnǝ. Wɛ cam dɩm dɩ DƖ ban-zɔŋɔ kʋm nan wʋ́ ba bantʋ baŋa.

eng: There will be trouble and distress for every human being who does evil, for the Jew first and also for the Greek.
local: Nabiinǝ balʋ maama na kɩ wo-balwaarʋ tɩn wʋ́ na yaara dɩ cam, kʋ na puli dɩ Zwifǝ bam, sɩ kʋ vu kʋ yi dwi-gɛ tiinǝ dɩ.

eng: But there will be glory, honor, and peace for everyone who does what is good, for the Jew first and also for the Greek.
local: Kʋ daarɩ, balʋ maama na kɩ kǝm-laarʋ tɩn, Wɛ wʋ́ pa-ba paarɩ dɩ zulǝ dɩ ywǝǝni, kʋ na puli dɩ Zwifǝ bam sɩ kʋ vu kʋ yi dwi-gɛ tiinǝ dɩ.

eng: God does not have favorites.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ Wɛ tɔgɩ cɩga mʋ dɩ DƖ na kɩ nɔɔna maama te tɩn, sɩ DƖ ba kuri-ba daanɩ.

eng: Those who have sinned outside the Law will also die outside the Law, and those who have sinned under the Law will be judged by the Law.
local: Balʋ na kɩ lwarɩm yɩ ba yǝri Wɛ cullu tɩlʋ DƖ na kɩ Moyisijɩŋa nɩ tɩn, baá cɔgɩ mʋ yɩ kʋ daɩ DƖ cullu tɩm ŋwaanɩ. Kʋ daarɩ, balʋ na ye Wɛ cullu tɩm na kɩ ba cɔgɩ, Wɛ wʋ́ tɔgɩ DƖ cullu tɩm kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ DƖ ma di ba taanɩ.

eng: It isnt the ones who hear the Law who are righteous in Gods eyes. It is the ones who do what the Law says who will be treated as righteous.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ, kʋ daɩ nɔɔna balʋ na cǝgi Wɛ cullu tɩm tɩn mʋ wʋ́ na cɩga DƖ tee nɩ, kʋ yɩ balʋ na sɛ ba kɩ DƖ cullu tɩm na wɩ te tɩn mʋ wʋ́ na cɩga DƖ tee nɩ.

eng: Gentiles dont have the Law. But when they instinctively do what the Law requires they are a Law in themselves, though they dont have the Law.
local: Dwi-gɛ tiinǝ bam nan yǝri Wɛ cullu tɩm sɩ ba taa tɔgɩ-tɩ. Ba tɩtɩ wʋbʋŋa nan na paɩ ba tɔgɩ cɩga kalʋ Wɛ cullu tɩm na brɩ tɩn, kʋ brɩ nɩ ba maŋɩ ba ye kʋlʋ na maŋɩ sɩ ba taa kɩ tɩn mʋ, yɩ Wɛ cullu tɩm ta tǝri ba tee nɩ.

eng: They show the proof of the Law written on their hearts, and their consciences affirm it. Their conflicting thoughts will accuse them, or even make a defense for them.
local: Ba tɩtʋŋa yam brɩ nɩ cɩga kalʋ Wɛ cullu tɩm na brɩ tɩn tigi ba bɩcara nɩ. Ba wʋbʋŋa na paɩ ba tɔgɩ cwǝŋǝ kalʋ tɩn dɩ brɩ nɩ kʋ yɩ cɩga mʋ, bɛŋwaanɩ ba wʋbʋŋa waɩ ya brɩ-ba nɩ ba kɩ ba cɔgɩ, naa ya brɩ-ba nɩ ba jɩgɩ cɩga.

eng: on the day when, according to my gospel, God will judge the hidden truth about human beings through Christ Jesus.
local: Kʋʋ́ ta yɩ kʋntʋ mʋ Wɛ sarɩya dɛ dɩm nɩ. Baŋa-Wɛ wʋ́ da Zezi Krisi ŋwaanɩ DƖ bʋrɩ nɔɔna maama wʋbʋŋa yalʋ na sǝgi tɩn, nɩ amʋ na brɩ Wɛ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam te tɩn.

eng: But, if you call yourself a Jew; if you rely on the Law; if you brag about your relationship to God.
local: Kʋ daarɩ nmʋ wʋlʋ na brɩ n tɩtɩ nɩ n yɩ Zwifu tɩn, nmʋ yǝni n dalɩ Wɛ cullu tɩm yɩra nɩ mʋ, yɩ n kɩ tɩɩra dɩ n Wɛ tɔga kam.

eng: if you know the will of God; if you are taught by the Law so that you can figure out the things that really matter.
local: Nmʋ maŋɩ n ye Wɛ wʋbʋŋa na lagɩ sɩ n kɩ kʋlʋ tɩn. Nmʋ na zaasɩ Wɛ cullu tɩm tɩn mʋ pɛ n lwarɩ woŋo kʋlʋ na yɩ cɩga tɩn.

eng: if you have persuaded yourself that you are: a guide for the blind; a light to those who are in darkness.
local: Nmʋ bʋŋɩ nɩ n jɩgɩ Wɛ pooni mʋ n paɩ balʋ ta na wʋ lim wʋnɩ tɩn, yɩ n nyɩ dɩ lilwe vaŋɩnʋ na lagɩ sɩ o pa nɔɔna tɔgɩ cwǝŋǝ te tɩn.

eng: an educator of the foolish; a teacher of infants.
local: Nmʋ maa n bʋŋɩ nɩ n jɩgɩ cwǝŋǝ sɩ n brɩ balʋ na ba jɩgɩ yǝno tɩn, sɩ n daarɩ n zaasɩ balʋ wʋbʋŋa ta na wʋ bɩgɩ tɩn. Wɛ cullu tɩm na brɩ-m cɩga dɩ yǝno maama tɩn mʋ paɩ n bʋŋɩ kʋntʋ.

eng: then why dont you who are teaching others teach yourself? If you preach, No stealing, do you steal?.
local: Nmʋ nan na yǝni n zaasɩ n donnǝ tɩn, bɛɛ mʋ kɩ yɩ n ba zaasɩ n tɩtɩ? Nmʋ yǝni n brɩ nɔɔna nɩ ba yɩ taa ŋɔna, yɩ nmʋ tɩtɩ ŋɔnɩ mʋ.

eng: If you say, No adultery, do you commit adultery? If you hate idols, do you rob temples?.
local: N tɛ nɩ ba yɩ kɩ boorim, yɩ nmʋ tɩtɩ boori mʋ. Nmʋ culi jwǝnǝ, yɩ n laan daarɩ n di nyɔɔrɩ ya baŋa nɩ.

eng: If you brag about the Law, do you shame God by breaking the Law?.
local: Nmʋ jɩgɩ tɩɩra dɩ Wɛ na kɩ DƖ cullu tɩm n jɩŋa nɩ tɩn, yɩ n laan daarɩ n kɩ n cɔgɩ Wɛ yɩrɩ dɩ n na ba sɛ DƖ cullu tɩm na brɩ te tɩn.

eng: As it is written: The name of God is discredited by the Gentiles because of you .
local: Kʋ pʋpʋnɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ kʋ wɩ: «Kʋ yɩ abam Zwifǝ bam kikiǝ ŋwaanɩ mʋ paɩ dwi-gɛ tiinǝ ŋɔɔnɩ ba cɔgɩ Wɛ yɩrɩ.».

eng: Circumcision is an advantage if you do what the Law says. But if you are a person who breaks the Law, your status of being circumcised has changed into not being circumcised.
local: Abam yǝni á goni á bǝkǝri sɩ kʋ brɩ nɩ á yɩ Wɛ nɔɔna mʋ. Á nan na kwaanɩ á sɛ Wɛ cullu tɩm, á bǝkǝrigonim dɩm jɩgɩ kuri. Kʋ daarɩ, á na cɔgɩ Wɛ cullu tɩm, á bǝkǝri sɩm gwǝŋǝ yɩ kafɛ mʋ.

eng: So if the person who isnt circumcised keeps the Law, wont his status of not being circumcised be counted as if he were circumcised?.
local: Dwi-gɛ tiinǝ nan ba goni ba bǝkǝri. Ba wʋ nɔɔnʋ dɩdʋa nan na tɔgɩ cɩga kalʋ Wɛ cullu tɩm na brɩ tɩn, kʋntʋ tu nyɩ dɩ wʋlʋ na goni sɩ kʋ brɩ nɩ o yɩ Wɛ nɔɔnʋ tɩn mʋ.

eng: The one who isnt physically circumcised but keeps the Law will judge you. You became a lawbreaker after you had the written Law and circumcision.
local: Dwi-gɛ tiinǝ wʋ́ brɩ nɩ abam Zwifǝ bam ba jɩgɩ bʋra. Bɛŋwaanɩ á jɩgɩ Wɛ cullu tɔnɔ kʋm á jɩŋa nɩ, yɩ á goni á bǝkǝri. Nan dɩ kʋ dɩ, á ta yǝni á cɔgɩ Wɛ cullu tɩm mʋ. Bantʋ nan ba goni ba bǝkǝri, yɩ ba nan tɔgɩ Wɛ cullu tɩm.

eng: It isnt the Jew who maintains outward appearances who will receive praise from God, and it isnt people who are outwardly circumcised on their bodies.
local: Kʋ daɩ wʋlʋ na brɩ o tɩtɩ nɩ o yɩ Zwifu tɩn mʋ sɩɩnɩ o yɩ Zwifu cɩga cɩga Wɛ yigǝ nɩ. Kʋ nan ta daɩ nɩ wʋlʋ na goni tɩn mʋ sɩɩnɩ o yɩ Wɛ nɔɔnʋ.

eng: Instead, it is the person who is a Jew inside, who is circumcised in spirit, not literally. That persons praise doesnt come from people but from God.
local: Wɛ yigǝ nɩ, Zwifu cɩga cɩga yɩ wʋlʋ Wɛ na lǝni o bɩcarɩ tɩn mʋ. Wʋlʋ bɩcarɩ na fɔgɩ dɩ kwɛ tɩn mʋ nyɩ dɩ o goni mʋ cɩga cɩga. Wɛ cullu tɔnɔ kʋm bá wanɩ kʋ kwɛ nɔɔnʋ bɩcarɩ, kʋ nan yɩ Wɛ Jorokʋm mʋ waɩ kʋ kwɛ nɔɔnʋ bɩcarɩ. Nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ doŋ naɩ zulǝ Wɛ tee nɩ mʋ, sɩ kʋ daɩ nabiinǝ tee nɩ.

eng: So whats the advantage of being a Jew? Or whats the benefit of circumcision?.
local: Zwifǝ nan jɩgɩ yu-yoŋo ba dwe dwi-gɛtiinǝ na? Zwifǝ na goni ba bǝkǝritɩn, kʋ jɩgɩ zǝnǝ kʋ pa-ba na?.

eng: So what are we going to say? Are we going to find that Abraham is our ancestor on the basis of genealogy?.
local: Á pa dɩ́ nii dɩ́ nabaarʋ Abrahamdɛɛn na yɩ te yɩ o na cɩga Wɛ tee nɩ tɩn.

eng: Plenty in every way. First of all, the Jews were trusted with Gods revelations.
local: Ɛɛn, kʋ tiini kʋ jɩgɩ kuri. Bɛŋwaanɩ dayigǝ tɩn, Zwifǝ bam mʋ yɩ balʋ Wɛ na kwe DƖ ni-bɩtarɩ sɩm DƖ kɩ ba jɩŋa nɩ tɩn.

eng: Because if Abraham was made righteous because of his actions, he would have had a reason to brag, but not in front of God.
local: Wɛ ya na pɛ-o cɩga o na kɩ kǝm-laarʋ tɩn ŋwaanɩ mʋ, o ya wʋ́ ta jɩgɩ woŋo sɩ o magɩ o nyɔɔnɩ kʋ baŋa nɩ. O nan ba jɩgɩ woŋo sɩ o magɩ o nyɔɔnɩ Wɛ yigǝ nɩ.

eng: What does it matter, then, if some werent faithful? Their lack of faith wont cancel Gods faithfulness, will it?.
local: Ba badaara nan wʋ kɩ ba wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Wɛ. Kʋntʋ nan bá wanɩ kʋ pa Wɛ kwanɩ DƖ ni-taanɩ dɩm.

eng: What does the scripture say? Abraham had faith in God, and it was credited to him as righteousness .
local: Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm nan brɩ dɩbam kʋ wɩ: «Abraham kɩ o wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Wɛ, yɩ Wɛ pa o na cɩga kʋ ŋwaanɩ.».

eng: Absolutely not! God must be true, even if every human being is a liar, as it is written: So that it can show that you are right in your words; and you will triumph when you are judged. .
local: Nɔɔna maama na maŋɩ ba yɩ vwa-nyɩna, Wɛ ta yɩ cɩga tu mʋ. Kʋ pʋpʋnɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ kʋ wɩ: «Nmʋ Wɛ jɩgɩ cɩga dɩ nmʋ na ŋɔɔnɩ taanɩ dɩlʋ maama tɩn. Nɔɔna na maŋɩ ba co nmʋ dɩ, nmʋ ta yɩ cɩga tu mʋ.».

eng: Workers salaries arent credited to them on the basis of an employers grace but rather on the basis of what they deserve.
local: Kʋ nan na yɩ nɔɔnʋ na tʋŋɩ tɩtʋŋɩ, o maŋɩ sɩ o joŋi o ŋwɩɩrʋ mʋ o tɩtʋŋɩ dɩm ŋwaanɩ, kʋntʋ nan daɩ pɛɛrɩ mʋ o joŋi.

eng: But if our lack of righteousness confirms Gods justice, what will we say? That God, who brings wrath upon us, isnt just ?.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ, dɩbam na kɩ lwarɩm dɩlʋ tɩn ta na brɩ nɩ Wɛ yɩ cɩga tu mʋ, dɩ́ wʋ́ ta nɩ bɛɛ? Kʋntʋ wʋ́ pa nɔɔna taa tɛ nɩ, Wɛ na pɛ ba na cam ba lwarɩm ŋwaanɩ tɩn, DƖ wʋ kɩ cɩga na?.

eng: But faith is credited as righteousness to those who dont work, because they have faith in God who makes the ungodly righteous.
local: Kʋ daarɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na kɩ o wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Wɛ, yɩ o ba tɩɩgɩ kǝm-laarʋ tɩlʋ o na kɩ tɩn, Wɛ wʋ́ pa kʋntʋ tu na cɩga dɩ o na kɩ o wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ DƖ tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Wɛ mʋ yɩ wʋlʋ na yagɩ nɔn-balwaarʋ lwarɩm DƖ ma cɛ-ba yɩ DƖ pa ba na cɩga DƖ tee nɩ tɩn.

eng: Absolutely not! If God werent just, how could he judge the world?.
local: Kʋ nan daɩ kʋntʋ. Wɛ ya na daɩ cɩga tu, DƖ wʋ́ kɩ ta mʋ DƖ di nabiinǝ taanɩ?.

eng: In the same way, David also pronounces a blessing on the person to whom God credits righteousness apart from actions.
local: Pɛ Davididɩ maŋɩ o ta kʋntʋ doŋ mʋ. O dɛɛn tagɩ o wɩ, nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ Wɛ na pɛ o na cɩga yɩ kʋ daɩ o kǝm-laarʋ ŋwaanɩ tɩn yɩ yu-yoŋo tu mʋ.

eng: But if Gods truth is demonstrated by my lie and it increases his glory, why am I still judged as a sinner?.
local: Nɔɔna ta waɩ ba tɛ ba wɩ: «Amʋ na yǝni a fɔ vwan tɩn, kʋ pɛ nɔɔna lwarɩ jaja nɩ Wɛ tiini DƖ yɩ cɩga tu, yɩ DƖ na zulǝ kʋ ŋwaanɩ. Kʋ na yɩ kʋntʋ, bɛɛ mʋ yɩ a maŋɩ sɩ a na cam a kǝm-balwaarʋ tɩm ŋwaanɩ?».

eng: Happy are those whose actions outside the Law are forgiven, and whose sins are covered. .
local: Davidi maa wɩ: «Balʋ Wɛ na yagɩ ba wo-balwaarʋ DƖ ma cɛ-ba tɩn jɩgɩ yu-yoŋo, DƖ na saarɩ ba lwarɩm dɩm tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: Why not say, Lets do evil things so that good things will come out of it?.
local: Kʋntʋ ya na yɩ cɩga, dɩ́ ya wʋ́ wanɩ dɩ́ ta dɩ́ wɩ: «Pa-na dɩ́ taá tʋŋɩ lwarɩm, sɩ kʋʋ́ ja lanyɩranɩ kʋ ba.» Nɔɔna badonnǝ yǝni ba fɔ vwan ba pa amʋ ba wɩ, amʋ tɛ kʋntʋ doŋ mʋ. Ba nan lagɩ sɩ ba cɔgɩ amʋ yɩrɩ mʋ. Ba nan wʋ́ na cɔgɩm sɩ kʋ maŋɩ dɩ ba tɩtʋŋa na yɩ te tɩn.

eng: Happy are those whose sin isnt counted against them by the Lord .
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na kɩ lwarɩm yɩ Wɛ yagɩ DƖ ma cɛ-o yɩ DƖ daa ba kɩ-dɩ DƖ bɩcarɩ nɩ tɩn jɩgɩ yu-yoŋo.».

eng: So what are we saying? Are we better off? Not at all. We have already stated the charge: both Jews and Greeks are all under the power of sin.
local: Kʋ brɩ nɩ dɩbam Zwifǝ bam garɩ dwi-gɛtiinǝ bam mʋ na? Awo. A ya maŋɩ a ta nɩ nɔɔna maama wʋ lwarɩm gambɛɛm wʋnɩ mʋ, kʋ na yɩ Zwifǝ naa dwi-gɛ tiinǝ bam dɩ.

eng: Is this state of happiness only for the circumcised or is it also for those who arent circumcised? We say, Faith was credited to Abraham as righteousness.
local: Davidi na ŋɔɔnɩ wʋpolo taanɩ dɩm kʋntʋ tɩn, o ŋɔɔnɩ balʋ na goni ba bǝkǝritɩn yɩranɩ ŋwaanɩ mʋ na? Aye. O ŋɔɔnɩ balʋ na ba goni ba bǝkǝri tɩn dɩ ŋwaanɩ mʋ. Dɩ́ maŋɩ dɩ́ lwarɩ nɩ: «Abraham kɩ o wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Wɛ mʋ, yɩ Wɛ pa o na cɩga kʋ ŋwaanɩ.».

eng: As it is written, There is no righteous person, not even one. .
local: nɩ kʋ na pʋpʋnɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ kʋ wɩ: «Nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ tǝrǝ o na jɩgɩ cɩga Wɛ yigǝ nɩ, dɩ nɔɔnʋ dɩdʋa dɩ.

eng: So how was it credited? When he was circumcised, or when he wasnt circumcised? In fact, it was credited while he still wasnt circumcised, not after he was circumcised.
local: Wɛ na pɛ o na cɩga DƖ tee nɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ yɩ maŋa kalʋ o na goni tɩn mʋ na? Awo, kʋ yɩ o ta na wʋ goni maŋa kalʋ tɩn mʋ.

eng: There is no one who understands. There is no one who looks for God. .
local: Nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ tǝrǝ o na ye kʋlʋ na maŋɩ tɩn. Nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ tǝrǝ o na lagɩ sɩ o lwarɩ Wɛ.

eng: He received the sign of circumcision as a seal of the righteousness that comes from the faith he had while he still wasnt circumcised. It happened this way so that Abraham could be the ancestor of all those people who arent circumcised, who have faith in God, and so are counted as righteous.
local: Kʋ yɩ kʋ kwaga nɩ mʋ Wɛ pɛ o go sɩ kʋ taa yɩ maana kʋ brɩ nɩ, Wɛ maŋɩ DƖ pa o na cɩga o na kɩ o wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ DƖ tɩn ŋwaanɩ, kʋ loori o na wʋ́ go tɩn. Kʋntʋ, balʋ maama dɩ na wʋ goni yɩ ba kɩ ba wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Wɛ yɩ kʋ pa ba na cɩga DƖ tee nɩ tɩn, ba dɩ yɩ Abraham dwi tiinǝ cɩga cɩga mʋ.

eng: They all turned away. They have become worthless together. There is no one who shows kindness. There is not even one. .
local: Ba maama mɛ ba kwaga mʋ ba ya Wɛ, yɩ ba maama tusi. Nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ tǝrǝ o na kɩ lanyɩranɩ, dɩ nɔɔnʋ dɩdʋa dɩ.

eng: He could also be the ancestor of those circumcised people, who arent only circumcised but who also walk in the path of faith, like our ancestor Abraham did while he wasnt circumcised.
local: Balʋ maama dɩ na goni yɩ ba kɩ ba wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Wɛ tɩn yɩ Abraham dwi tiinǝ mʋ, yɩ kʋ nan daɩ ba na goni tɩn yɩranɩ má ŋwaanɩ, kʋ yɩ ba na kɩ ba wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Wɛ tɩn ŋwaanɩ mʋ, nɩ Abraham dɛɛn na kɩ o wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Wɛ kʋ loori o na wʋ́ go maŋa kalʋ tɩn.

eng: Their throat is a grave that has been opened. They are deceitful with their tongues, and the poison of vipers is under their lips. .
local: Ba niǝ maa ŋɔɔrɩ nɩ yibeelǝ niǝ na pʋrɩ te tɩn. Ba maa maɩ ba dindǝlimǝ ba ma ganɩ daanɩ. Ba niǝ ma cana nɩ bɩsankwɩ na jɩgɩ vɩan sɩ gʋɩ nɔɔna te tɩn.

eng: The promise to Abraham and to his descendants, that he would inherit the world, didnt come through the Law but through the righteousness that comes from faith.
local: Kʋ daɩ Abrahamna tɔgɩ Wɛ cullu tɩm tɩn ŋwaanɩ mʋ pɛ Wɛ go DƖ ni dɩ Abraham dɩ o dwi tiinǝ bam. Kʋ nan yɩ Abraham na kɩ o wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Wɛ yɩ o na cɩga DƖ tee nɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ mʋ pɛ Wɛ go ni DƖ wɩ, DƖ wʋ́ pa o ba o taa te lʋgʋ kʋm.

eng: Their mouths are full of cursing and bitterness. .
local: Ba maa sɔɔlɩ wo-balwaarʋ ba pa nɔɔna.

eng: If they inherit because of the Law, then faith has no effect and the promise has been canceled.
local: Kʋ nan ya na yɩ balʋ na tɔgɩ cullu tɩm tɩn mʋ wʋ́ ba ba taa te lʋgʋ kʋm, dɩ́ na kɩ dɩ́ wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Wɛ te tɩn ya daa ba jɩgɩ kuri, yɩ Wɛ ni-gonim dɩm dɩ ya wʋ́ ji kafɛ mʋ.

eng: Their feet are quick to shed blood; .
local: Ba ma tiini ba duri lɩla ba ve ba gʋɩ nɔɔna.

eng: The Law brings about wrath. But when there isnt any law, there isnt any violation of the law.
local: Wɛ cullu tɩm vɩʋn mʋ paɩ DƖ banɩ zaŋɩ dɩ nɔɔna, ba na cɔgɩ-tɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Cullu nan na tǝrǝ, nɔɔna bá wanɩ ba cɔgɩ-tɩ.

eng: destruction and misery are in their ways; .
local: Ba na dɛ jǝgǝ kalʋ maama tɩn, ba yǝni ba cɔgɩ wǝǝnu mʋ yɩ ba ta yaarɩ nɔɔna zanzan.

eng: Thats why the inheritance comes through faith, so that it will be on the basis of Gods grace. In that way, the promise is secure for all of Abrahams descendants, not just for those who are related by Law but also for those who are related by the faith of Abraham, who is the father of all of us.
local: Kʋntʋ, dɩ́ lwarɩ nɩ kʋ yɩ Abraham na kɩ o wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Wɛ tɩn ŋwaanɩ mʋ pɛ Wɛ go ni dɩd-o, sɩ kʋ taa yɩ zaanɩ pɛɛrɩ. Kʋntʋ tɩn, Abraham dwi tiinǝ bam maama wʋ́ sɩɩnɩ ba na wo-laarʋ tɩlʋ Wɛ na goni ni sɩ DƖ pa-ba tɩn. Kʋ daɩ balʋ na tɔgɩ Wɛ cullu tɩm tɩn yɩranɩ mʋ wʋ́ na-tɩ, kʋ yɩ balʋ dɩ na kɩ ba wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Wɛ nɩ Abraham dɩ dɛɛn na kɩ te tɩn mʋ. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ dɩbam maama wʋ́ wanɩ dɩ́ bǝŋi Abraham nɩ dɩ́ nabaarʋ.

eng: and they dont know the way of peace. .
local: Ba maa yǝri ywǝǝni cwǝŋǝ sɩ ba tɔgɩ.

eng: As it is written: I have appointed you to be the father of many nations. So Abraham is our father in the eyes of God in whom he had faith, the God who gives life to the dead and calls things that dont exist into existence.
local: Wɛ dɛɛn tagɩ dɩ Abraham DƖ wɩ: «Amʋ goni a ni sɩ a pa n ji dwi zanzan nabaarʋ mʋ.» Abraham na kɩ o wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Wɛ tɩn, o sɩɩnɩ o ji dɩ́ nabaarʋ Wɛ yigǝ nɩ. Kʋ yɩ Wɛ dɩntʋ mʋ bi nɔɔna DƖ pa ba yagɩ tʋʋnɩ, yɩ DƖ pa woŋo maama kɩ maŋa kalʋ kʋlʋkʋlʋ ya ta na tǝrǝ tɩn.

eng: There is no fear of God in their view of the world. .
local: Ba maa brɩ ba ba kwarɩ Wɛ.».

eng: When it was beyond hope, he had faith in the hope that he would become the father of many nations, in keeping with the promise God spoke to him: Thats how many descendants you will have. .
local: Wɛ dɛɛn na tagɩ dɩ Abraham DƖ wɩ o dwi wʋ́ puli tɩn, Abraham ya yǝri Wɛ na wʋ́ kɩ te DƖ kɩ kʋntʋ. Dɩ kʋntʋ dɩ, o ta jɩgɩ tɩɩna lanyɩranɩ, yɩ o kɩ o wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Wɛ. Kʋntʋ ma pa o sɩɩnɩ o ji dwi zanzan nabaarʋ, nɩnɛɛnɩ Wɛ na tagɩ nɩ o dwi dɩm wʋ́ puli te tɩn.

eng: Now we know that whatever the Law says, it speaks to those who are under the Law, in order to shut every mouth and make it so the whole world has to answer to God.
local: Dɩ́ nan ye nɩ kʋlʋ maama Wɛ cullu tɔnɔ kʋm na brɩ tɩn, kʋ pʋpʋnɩ kʋ pa balʋ cullu tɩm na te-ba tɩn mʋ. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ, nabiinǝ maama wʋ́ ba ba zɩgɩ Wɛ yigǝ nɩ sɩ DƖ di ba taanɩ, yɩ ba wʋlʋwʋlʋ bá wanɩ o ni o puri sɩ o ma ŋɔɔnɩ o joŋi o tɩtɩ.

eng: Without losing faith, Abraham, who was nearly years old, took into account his own body, which was as good as dead, and Sarahs womb, which was dead.
local: Abraham dɛɛn na gɛ fɩɩn sɩ o yi bɩna bi tɩn, o ma na nɩ o tiini o kwɩn zanzan, o daa bá wanɩ bu o lʋ. O ma na nɩ o kaanɩ Sara dɩ kwɩn zanzan o kɛ lʋra. Dɩ kʋntʋ maama o dɛɛn ta kɩ o wʋ-dɩdʋa mʋ dɩ Wɛ.

eng: It follows that no human being will be treated as righteous in his presence by doing what the Law says, because the knowledge of sin comes through the Law.
local: Nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ bá wanɩ o na cɩga Wɛ tee nɩ, o na tɔgɩ DƖ cullu tɩm tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Wɛ cullu tɩm nan paɩ nabiinǝ mʋ lwarɩ nɩ ba kɩ ba tusi.

eng: He didnt hesitate with a lack of faith in Gods promise, but he grew strong in faith and gave glory to God.
local: O maa ba jɩgɩ bʋbwɛa dɩ Wɛ na goni DƖ ni sɩ DƖ pa-o kʋlʋ tɩn. O na kɩ o wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Wɛ tɩn, kʋ paɩ o zɩgɩ kǝŋkǝŋ, yɩ kʋ pa Wɛ na zulǝ.

eng: But now Gods righteousness has been revealed apart from the Law, which is confirmed by the Law and the Prophets.
local: Wɛ laan nan pɛ dɩ́ lwarɩ DƖ na paɩ nɔɔna naɩ cɩga DƖ tee nɩ te tɩn, yɩ kʋ wʋ zɩgɩ cullu tɔgɩm baŋa nɩ. Wɛ cullu tɔnɔ kʋm dɩ Wɛ nijoŋnǝ bam dɩ nan maŋɩ ba brɩ cwǝŋǝ kam kʋntʋ.

eng: He was fully convinced that God was able to do what he promised.
local: O maa sɩɩnɩ o ye lanyɩranɩ nɩ, Wɛ wʋ́ wanɩ DƖ kɩ kʋlʋ DƖ na goni DƖ ni sɩ DƖ kɩ tɩn.

eng: Gods righteousness comes through the faithfulness of Jesus Christ for all who have faith in him. Theres no distinction.
local: Kʋ nan yɩ nɔɔna na kɩ ba wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Zezi Krisi tɩn ŋwaanɩ mʋ Wɛ paɩ ba na cɩga DƖ tee nɩ. Balʋ maama na kɩ ba wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Zezi Krisi tɩn wʋ́ na cɩga Wɛ tee nɩ, bɛŋwaanɩ nɔɔna maama yɩ bɩdwɩ mʋ Wɛ yigǝ nɩ.

eng: Therefore, it was credited to him as righteousness.
local: Kʋ yɩ kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ kʋ pʋpʋnɩ kʋ wɩ: «Wɛ ma pa o na cɩga dɩ o na kɩ o wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ DƖ tɩn ŋwaanɩ.».

eng: All have sinned and fall short of Gods glory.
local: Nabiinǝ maama kɩ lwarɩm mʋ yɩ ba daa wʋ maŋɩ dɩ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm.

eng: But the scripture that says it was credited to him wasnt written only for Abrahams sake.
local: Kʋ nan na pʋpʋnɩ kʋ wɩ: «Wɛ ma pa o na cɩga» tɩn, kʋ daɩ Abraham yɩranɩ ŋwaanɩ mʋ kʋ pʋpʋnɩ kʋntʋ.

eng: but all are treated as righteous freely by his grace because of a ransom that was paid by Christ Jesus.
local: Wɛ nan paɩ nɔɔna na cɩga DƖ tee nɩ zaanɩ mʋ, Zezi Krisi na ŋwɩ ba lwarɩm jɩnɩ o ma joŋi-ba tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Wɛ paɩ nɔɔna DƖ zaanɩ dɩm kafɛ mʋ.

eng: It was written also for our sake, because it is going to be credited to us too. It will be credited to those of us who have faith in the one who raised Jesus our Lord from the dead.
local: Kʋ pʋpʋnɩ dɩbam dɩ ŋwaanɩ mʋ. Bɛŋwaanɩ Wɛ wʋ́ pa dɩbam dɩ na cɩga DƖ tee nɩ, dɩ dɩ́ na kɩ dɩ́ wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ DƖ tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Dɩntʋ mʋ bi dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi DƖ pa o yagɩ tʋʋnɩ.

eng: Through his faithfulness, God displayed Jesus as the place of sacrifice where mercy is found by means of his blood. He did this to demonstrate his righteousness in passing over sins that happened before.
local: DƖ ma pa Zezi ba o ji kaanɩm. O jana kam nuŋi ka ma vrɩ nɔɔna ba lwarɩm wʋnɩ, dɩ ba na kɩ ba wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩd-o tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Wɛ kɩ kʋntʋ mʋ sɩ kʋ brɩ nɩ DƖ yɩ cɩga tu mʋ. Faŋa faŋa tɩn, Wɛ dɛɛn kɩ wʋ-zuru mʋ yɩ DƖ wʋ vaŋɩ balʋ na kɩ lwarɩm tɩn zwa.

eng: He was handed over because of our mistakes, and he was raised to meet the requirements of righteousness for us.
local: Wɛ nan mʋ kɩ Zezi nabiinǝ jɩŋa nɩ DƖ pa o tɩ dɩbam lwarɩm ŋwaanɩ. DƖ ma daarɩ DƖ bi-o DƖ pa o yagɩ tʋʋnɩ o joori o na ŋwɩa, sɩ kʋ pa dɩ́ na cɩga DƖ tee nɩ.

eng: during the time of Gods patient tolerance. He also did this to demonstrate that he is righteous in the present time, and to treat the one who has faith in Jesus as righteous.
local: Kʋ daarɩ lele kʋntʋ Wɛ pɛ dɩ́ lwarɩ nɩ DƖ yɩ cɩga tu, dɩ DƖ na joŋi nɔɔna ba lwarɩm wʋnɩ tɩn. Kʋntʋ maa brɩ nɩ Wɛ jɩgɩ cɩga, yɩ DƖ ta paɩ balʋ maama na kɩ ba wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Zezi tɩn dɩ ta na cɩga DƖ tee nɩ.

eng: What happens to our bragging? Its thrown out. With which law? With what we have accomplished under the Law?.
local: Kʋ na yɩ kʋntʋ, dɩ́ wʋ́ magɩ dɩ́ nyɔɔna bɛɛ baŋa nɩ mʋ? Kʋlʋkʋlʋ tǝrǝ. Bɛŋwaanɩ, kʋ daɩ dɩ́ na tɔgɩ Wɛ cullu tɩm tɩn mʋ pɛ dɩ́ na cɩga, kʋ yɩ dɩ́ na sɛ dɩ́ kɩ dɩ́ wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Zezi tɩn ŋwaanɩ mʋ.

eng: No, not at all, but through the law of faith. We consider that a person is treated as righteous by faith, apart from what is accomplished under the Law.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ dɩ́ ye nɩ nɔɔna wʋ́ na cɩga Wɛ tee nɩ dɩ ba na kɩ ba wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ DƖ tɩn mʋ, sɩ kʋ daɩ cullu tɩm tɔgɩm ŋwaanɩ.

eng: Or is God the God of Jews only? Isnt God the God of Gentiles also? Yes, God is also the God of Gentiles.
local: Baŋa-Wɛ yɩ Zwifǝ bam yɩranɩ Wɛ mʋ na? DƖ daɩ dwi-gɛ tiinǝ bam dɩ Wɛ na? Ɛɛn, DƖ yɩ nabiinǝ maama Wɛ mʋ.

eng: Since God is one, then the one who makes the circumcised righteous by faith will also make the one who isnt circumcised righteous through faith.
local: Wɛ yɩ dɩdʋa mʋ. Zwifǝ bam na kɩ ba wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ DƖ, DƖ wʋ́ pa ba na cɩga DƖ tee nɩ. Dwi-gɛ tiinǝ bam dɩ nan na kɩ ba wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ DƖ, DƖ wʋ́ pa ba dɩ na cɩga DƖ tee nɩ.

eng: Do we then cancel the Law through this faith? Absolutely not! Instead, we confirm the Law.
local: Kʋntʋ nan ba lagɩ kʋ ta nɩ dɩ́ na kɩ dɩ́ wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Wɛ tɩn, DƖ cullu tɩm daa ba jɩgɩ kuri. Dɩ́ na kɩ dɩ́ wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Wɛ te tɩn laan paɩ DƖ cullu tɩm tiini tɩ jɩgɩ kuri mʋ.

eng: Therefore, since we have been made righteous through his faithfulness, we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Dɩ́ na kɩ dɩ́ wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Zezi tɩn mʋ pɛ dɩ́ na cɩga Wɛ tee nɩ. Kʋntʋ, dɩbam dɩ Wɛ fɔgɩ daanɩ dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi Krisi ŋwaanɩ mʋ.

eng: We have access by faith into this grace in which we stand through him, and we boast in the hope of Gods glory.
local: Zezi mʋ pɛ dɩbam yi Wɛ yigǝ, yɩ Wɛ kɩ dɩbam yu-yoŋo dɩ́ na kɩ dɩ́ wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩd-o tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Kʋntʋ tɩn, dɩ́ jɩgɩ tɩɩna nɩ dɩ́ wʋ́ na paarɩ-zulǝ Wɛ tee nɩ, yɩ kʋ paɩ dɩbam jɩgɩ wʋpolo lanyɩranɩ.

eng: But not only that! We even take pride in our problems, because we know that trouble produces endurance.
local: Dɩ́ nan ta jɩgɩ wʋpolo dɩ́ yaara yam wʋnɩ, bɛŋwaanɩ dɩ́ ye nɩ yaara waɩ ya paɩ dɩ́ fɔgɩ dɩ́ zɩgɩ dɩ pu-dɩa.

eng: endurance produces character, and character produces hope.
local: Dɩ́ pu-dɩa kam yaara yam wʋnɩ tɩn laan maa pa dɩ́ zɩgɩ kǝŋkǝŋ maŋɩm wʋnɩ. Dɩ́ na zɩgɩ kǝŋkǝŋ maŋɩm wʋnɩ tɩn laan maa pa dɩ́ jɩgɩ tɩɩna Wɛ tee nɩ.

eng: This hope doesnt put us to shame, because the love of God has been poured out in our hearts through the Holy Spirit, who has been given to us.
local: Dɩ́ na jɩgɩ tɩɩna kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ bá pa dɩ́ na dɩ́ cavɩɩra, bɛŋwaanɩ Wɛ tiini DƖ kɩ DƖ sono dɩbam bɩcara nɩ, dɩ DƖ na pɛ DƖ Jorokʋm wʋ dɩbam wʋnɩ tɩn.

eng: While we were still weak, at the right moment, Christ died for ungodly people.
local: Dɩbam na bwǝnǝ yɩ dɩ́ warɩ dɩ́ joŋi dɩ́ tɩtɩ tɩn, mʋ Zezi Krisi tɩga o ma vrɩ dɩbam na yɩ nɔn-balwaarʋ tɩn, maŋa kalʋ Wɛ wʋbʋŋa na lagɩ tɩn.

eng: It isnt often that someone will die for a righteous person, though maybe someone might dare to die for a good person.
local: Kʋ cana sɩ nɔɔnʋ sɛ o tɩ o doŋ ŋwaanɩ, dɩ o na wʋ́ tɩ wʋlʋ ŋwaanɩ tɩn na maŋɩ o yɩ cɩga tu te dɩ. Kʋ nan waɩ kʋ kɩ sɩ nɔɔnʋ bá kwarɩ fʋʋnɩ sɩ o tɩ nɔn-ŋʋm ŋwaanɩ.

eng: But God shows his love for us, because while we were still sinners Christ died for us.
local: Kʋ nan na yɩ Baŋa-Wɛ, dɩntʋ brɩ dɩbam DƖ na tiini DƖ soe dɩbam te tɩn, dɩ DƖ na pɛ Zezi Krisi ba o tɩ dɩbam ŋwaanɩ maŋa kalʋ dɩ́ ya ta na yɩ lwarɩm kǝrǝ tɩn.

eng: So, now that we have been made righteous by his blood, we can be even more certain that we will be saved from Gods wrath through him.
local: Zezi na tɩga yɩ o jana kam nuŋi tɩn mʋ pɛ dɩ́ na cɩga Wɛ tee nɩ. Kʋ nan na dwe dɩdɩ tɩn, o laan wʋ́ joŋi dɩbam Wɛ ban-zɔŋɔ kʋm wʋnɩ.

eng: If we were reconciled to God through the death of his Son while we were still enemies, now that we have been reconciled, how much more certain is it that we will be saved by his life?.
local: Dɩbam ya yɩ Wɛ dʋna mʋ. DƖ Bu wʋm tʋʋnɩ dɩm nan mʋ pɛ dɩbam dɩ Wɛ fɔgɩ daanɩ. Kʋ nan na dwe dɩdɩ, dɩbam laan na jigi Wɛ nɔɔna tɩn, DƖ Bu wʋm wʋ́ vrɩ dɩbam, o na joori o bi o yagɩ tʋʋnɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: And not only that: we even take pride in God through our Lord Jesus Christ, the one through whom we now have a restored relationship with God.
local: Kʋlʋ ta na wǝli da tɩn, dɩ́ jɩgɩ wʋpolo lanyɩranɩ dɩ Wɛ, dɩ dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi Krisi na pɛ dɩbam dɩ Wɛ fɔgɩ daanɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: Just as through one human being sin came into the world, and death came through sin, so death has come to everyone, since everyone has sinned.
local: Nabiinu dɩdʋa ŋwaanɩ mʋ pɛ lwarɩm ba lʋgʋ baŋa, yɩ lwarɩm dɩ ja tʋʋnɩ dɩ wǝli da dɩ ba. Kʋntʋ tɩn, nabiinǝ maama maŋɩ sɩ ba tɩ mʋ, ba maama na kɩ lwarɩm tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: Although sin was in the world, since there was no Law, it wasnt taken into account until the Law came.
local: Kʋ na wʋ́ loori sɩ Wɛ kɩ DƖ cullu tɩm DƖ nɔɔna bam jɩŋa nɩ tɩn, dɩ lwarɩm maŋɩ dɩ wʋ lʋgʋ baŋa nɩ mʋ. Cullu tɩm nan ya ta na tǝrǝ tɩn, Wɛ ba jeeli nɔɔna lwarɩm.

eng: But death ruled from Adam until Moses, even over those who didnt sin in the same way Adam did—Adam was a type of the one who was coming.
local: Kʋ na zɩgɩ Adammaŋa kam nɩ sɩ kʋ vu kʋ yi Moyisi maŋa kam tɩn, tʋʋnɩ ya maŋɩ dɩ wʋra mʋ yɩ dɩ waɩ nabiinǝ. Dɩ maa waɩ balʋ dɩ na kɩ ba tusi, yɩ kʋ daɩ nɩ Adam dɛɛn na kɩ lwarɩm o vɩn Wɛ ni te tɩn. Adam nan brɩ dɩbam Zezi na lagɩ o ba tɩn nyɩnyʋgʋ mʋ.

eng: But the free gift of Christ isnt like Adams failure. If many people died through what one person did wrong, Gods grace is multiplied even more for many people with the gift—of the one person Jesus Christ—that comes through grace.
local: Ba nan daɩ bɩdwɩ. Bɛŋwaanɩ Adam dɛɛn na kɩ lwarɩm dɩlʋ tɩn daɩ bɩdwɩ dɩ Wɛ na pɛ dɩbam pɛɛrɩ dɩlʋ zaanɩ tɩn. Nabiinu dɩdʋa kam kʋntʋ lwarɩm dɩm ŋwaanɩ mʋ pɛ nɔɔna zanzan tɩ. Wɛ zaanɩ dɩm nan dwe kʋntʋ, yɩ DƖ tʋŋɩ nabiinu dɩdʋa, wʋntʋ mʋ yɩ Zezi Krisi, sɩ o kɩ nɔɔna zanzan yu-yoŋo kʋ ja gaalɩ.

eng: The gift isnt like the consequences of one persons sin. The judgment that came from one persons sin led to punishment, but the free gift that came out of many failures led to the verdict of acquittal.
local: Wɛ na pɛ pɛɛrɩ dɩm nɔɔna zaanɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ daɩ bɩdwɩ nɩ Adam na kɩ lwarɩm te tɩn. Wʋntʋ na kɩ lwarɩm o vɩn Wɛ ni bɩdwɩ tɩn mʋ pɛ Wɛ di nɔɔna sarɩya. Kʋ daarɩ nɔɔna zanzan na kɩ ba tusi tɩn, Wɛ pɛɛrɩ dɩm nan pɛ ba lwarɩm zanzan ti yɩ ba na cɩga DƖ tee nɩ.

eng: If death ruled because of one persons failure, those who receive the multiplied grace and the gift of righteousness will even more certainly rule in life through the one person Jesus Christ.
local: Nabiinu dɩdʋa kam lwarɩm ŋwaanɩ mʋ pɛ tʋʋnɩ tɔgɩ da dɩ jaanɩ nɔɔna. Kʋ nan na dwe dɩdɩ tɩn, balʋ maama Wɛ na kɩ-ba yu-yoŋo kʋ ja gaalɩ, yɩ DƖ kwǝri DƖ pa ba na cɩga DƖ tee nɩ tɩn, mʋ wʋ́ ta ŋwɩ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋnɩ nabiinu dɩdʋa wʋlʋ na yɩ Zezi Krisi tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: So now the righteous requirements necessary for life are met for everyone through the righteous act of one person, just as judgment fell on everyone through the failure of one person.
local: Dɩ́ nan lwarɩ nɩ Adam na kɩ o tusi bɩdwɩ tɩn mʋ pɛ nabiinǝ maama na cɔgɩm Wɛ tee nɩ. Zezi Krisi yɩranɩ kǝm-laa kʋm dɩ nan mʋ pɛ nɔɔna na cɩga yɩ ba jɩgɩ ŋwɩa Wɛ tee nɩ.

eng: Many people were made righteous through the obedience of one person, just as many people were made sinners through the disobedience of one person.
local: Nabiinu dɩdʋa kam na vɩn Wɛ ni dɩm tɩn mʋ pɛ nɔɔna zanzan yɩ lwarɩm kǝrǝ. Nɔɔnʋ dɩdʋa kam na yɩ Zezi Krisi tɩn nan na sɛ Wɛ ni tɩn dɩ wʋ́ pa nɔɔna zanzan na cɩga Wɛ tee nɩ.

eng: The Law stepped in to amplify the failure, but where sin increased, grace multiplied even more.
local: Wɛ dɛɛn kɩ DƖ cullu tɩm nɔɔna jɩŋa nɩ, sɩ kʋ pa lwarɩm fɔgɩ dɩ puli. Lwarɩm nan na puli kʋntʋ tɩn, Wɛ na kɩ nɔɔna zaanɩ te tɩn wʋ́ tiini kʋ puli zanzan kʋ dwǝni.

eng: The result is that grace will rule through Gods righteousness, leading to eternal life through Jesus Christ our Lord, just as sin ruled in death.
local: Kʋntʋ tɩn, lwarɩm tɔgɩ tʋʋnɩ ŋwaanɩ mʋ dɩ maa te nɔɔna. Wɛ zaanɩ dɩm nan tɔgɩ DƖ na paɩ nɔɔna cɩga te tɩn ŋwaanɩ mʋ dɩ maa di paarɩ nɔɔna tɩtarɩ nɩ, sɩ ba taa jɩgɩ ŋwɩa kalʋ na ba ti tɩn Wɛ tee nɩ dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi Krisi ŋwaanɩ.

eng: So what are we going to say? Should we continue sinning so grace will multiply?.
local: Dɩ́ nan wʋ́ ta nɩ bɛɛ mʋ? Dɩ́ wʋ́ ta kɩ lwarɩm mʋ, sɩ kʋ wanɩ kʋ pa Wɛ zaanɩ dɩm tiini dɩ puli dɩ́ tee nɩ na?.

eng: Absolutely not! All of us died to sin. How can we still live in it?.
local: Aye, dɩ́ bá ta kɩ kʋntʋ doŋ. Kʋ na yɩ dɩ lwarɩm jǝgǝ nɩ, dɩbam nyɩ dɩ twa mʋ. Kʋntʋ tɩn, dɩ́ daa wʋ́ kɩ ta mʋ dɩ́ taá ŋwɩ lwarɩm wʋnɩ?.

eng: Or dont you know that all who were baptized into Christ Jesus were baptized into his death?.
local: Abam yǝri nɩ, dɩbam balʋ maama ba na miisi na wʋnɩ Zezi Krisi yɩrɩ ŋwaanɩ tɩn, kʋ brɩ nɩ dɩ́ tɔgɩ dɩ́ wǝli dɩd-o o tʋʋnɩ dɩm wʋnɩ mʋ na?.

eng: Therefore, we were buried together with him through baptism into his death, so that just as Christ was raised from the dead through the glory of the Father, we too can walk in newness of life.
local: Kʋntʋ, ba na miisi dɩbam na wʋnɩ tɩn, kʋ nyɩ dɩ dɩ́ tɩgɩ dɩd-o mʋ yɩ ba kɩ dɩbam dɩd-o. Kʋntʋ ma pa dɩ́ tɔgɩ dɩ Zezi Krisi dɩ́ ŋwɩ Wɛ ŋwɩ-dʋŋa kam, nɩ dɩ́ Ko Wɛ na mɛ DƖ paarɩ dam dɩm DƖ ma bi Zezi DƖ pa o yagɩ tʋʋnɩ yɩ o na ŋwɩa te tɩn.

eng: If we were united together in a death like his, we will also be united together in a resurrection like his.
local: Kʋntʋ, dɩbam na tɔgɩ dɩ Zezi dɩ́ tɩ tɩn, dɩ́ laan jigi woŋo dɩdʋa mʋ dɩd-o, yɩ dɩ́ daa ta wʋ́ tɔgɩ dɩd-o dɩ́ bi dɩ́ yagɩ tʋʋnɩ.

eng: This is what we know: the person that we used to be was crucified with him in order to get rid of the corpse that had been controlled by sin. That way we wouldnt be slaves to sin anymore.
local: Dɩ́ ye nɩ, ba na pagɩ Zezi tʋʋn-dagarakam baŋa nɩ ba gʋ tɩn, dɩbam lwarɩm ŋwɩ-doŋǝ kam dɩ tɔgɩ ka tɩ mʋ, kʋ pa lwarɩm daa warɩ dɩbam. Kʋntʋ tɩn, dɩ́ laan te dɩ́ tɩtɩ mʋ lwarɩm jɩŋa nɩ.

eng: because a person who has died has been freed from sins power.
local: bɛŋwaanɩ lwarɩm daa bá wanɩ dɩ taa te wʋlʋ na tɔgɩ dɩ Zezi o tɩ tɩn.

eng: But if we died with Christ, we have faith that we will also live with him.
local: Dɩ́ na tɔgɩ dɩ Zezi Krisi dɩ́ tɩ tɩn, dɩ́ jɩgɩ tɩɩna nɩ dɩ́ wʋ́ tɔgɩ dɩd-o dɩ́ na ŋwɩa.

eng: We know that Christ has been raised from the dead and he will never die again. Death no longer has power over him.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ dɩ́ ye nɩ Zezi Krisi bi o yagɩ tʋʋnɩ yɩ o daa bá joori o tɩ. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ tʋʋnɩ daa ba jɩgɩ dam o baŋa nɩ.

eng: He died to sin once and for all with his death, but he lives for God with his life.
local: Zezi na tɩgɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ yɩ sɩ o cɔgɩ lwarɩm dam mʋ. O tɩgɩ bɩdwɩ yɩranɩ mʋ o ma kweeli. O laan nan na ŋwɩ tɩn, o ŋwɩa kam zuli Wɛ mʋ.

eng: In the same way, you also should consider yourselves dead to sin but alive for God in Christ Jesus.
local: Kʋ yɩ kʋntʋ mʋ dɩ abam dɩ. Kʋ na yɩ dɩ lwarɩm jǝgǝ nɩ, lwarɩ-na nɩ á nyɩ dɩ twa mʋ te. Kʋ nan na yɩ abam dɩ Wɛ laŋa nɩ, á ŋwɩ mʋ Zezi Krisi ŋwaanɩ.

eng: So then, dont let sin rule your body, so that you do what it wants.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ abam daa yɩ pa lwarɩm di dam á yɩra yalʋ na tʋ tɩn baŋa nɩ. Á nan yɩ sɛ á pa wo-balwaarʋ tɩlʋ á fra na zʋʋrɩ tɩn wanɩ abam.

eng: Dont offer parts of your body to sin, to be used as weapons to do wrong. Instead, present yourselves to God as people who have been brought back to life from the dead, and offer all the parts of your body to God to be used as weapons to do right.
local: Yɩ sɛ-na á pa lwarɩm taa te abam sɩ kʋ pa á taá kɩ kǝm-balwaarʋ. Nan kwe-na á tɩtɩ á kɩ Wɛ jɩŋa nɩ, sɩ á nyɩ nɩ balʋ na tɩgɩ yɩ Wɛ pa á joori á bi á na ŋwɩa tɩn mʋ. Kwe-na á maama á pa Wɛ, sɩ DƖ ma tʋŋɩ kʋlʋ na yɩ cɩga tɩn.

eng: Sin will have no power over you, because you arent under Law but under grace.
local: Lwarɩm daa ba te abam, bɛŋwaanɩ á na jɩgɩ ŋwɩa Wɛ tee nɩ tɩn, kʋ daɩ sɩ á taá ŋwɩ DƖ cullu tɩm tɔgɩm ŋwaanɩ, kʋ yɩ DƖ zaanɩ dɩm ŋwaanɩ mʋ.

eng: So what? Should we sin because we arent under Law but under grace? Absolutely not!.
local: Kʋ nan brɩ dɩbam nɩ bɛɛ mʋ? Dɩ́ na jɩgɩ ŋwɩa Wɛ tee nɩ DƖ zaanɩ dɩm ŋwaanɩ yɩ kʋ daɩ sɩ dɩ́ taá ŋwɩ DƖ cullu tɩm tɔgɩm ŋwaanɩ tɩn, kʋ brɩ nɩ dɩ́ jɩgɩ cwǝŋǝ sɩ dɩ́ taá kɩ lwarɩm mʋ na? Awo, kʋ daɩ kʋntʋ.

eng: Dont you know that if you offer yourselves to someone as obedient slaves, that you are slaves of the one whom you obey? Thats true whether you serve as slaves of sin, which leads to death, or as slaves of the kind of obedience that leads to righteousness.
local: Á ye lanyɩranɩ nɩ, á na kwe á tɩtɩ á kɩ nɔɔnʋ jɩŋa nɩ sɩ á taá yɩ o gambɛ yɩ á sɛ o ni, á sɩɩnɩ á yɩ á na sɛ wʋlʋ tɩn gambɛ mʋ. Kʋntʋ tɩn, á na yɩ lwarɩm gambɛ, tʋʋnɩ laan mʋ wʋ́ ba dɩ ja abam. Á nan daa na yɩ balʋ na sɛ Wɛ ni tɩn, á wʋ́ na cɩga DƖ tee nɩ.

eng: But thank God that although you used to be slaves of sin, you gave wholehearted obedience to the teaching that was handed down to you, which provides a pattern.
local: A nan kɩ Wɛ le, dɩ abam dɛɛn ya na yɩ lwarɩm gambɛ yɩ á laan kwe á tɩtɩ á pa Wɛ cɩga kam zaasɩm yɩ á sɛ-ka dɩ á wʋ maama tɩn.

eng: Now that you have been set free from sin, you have become slaves of righteousness.
local: Wɛ mʋ vrɩ abam lwarɩm jɩŋa nɩ, yɩ á laan ji DƖ cɩga kam tɩntʋŋna.

eng: Once, you offered the parts of your body to be used as slaves to impurity and to lawless behavior that leads to still more lawless behavior. Now, you should present the parts of your body as slaves to righteousness, which makes your lives holy.
local: Abam wʋbʋŋa ta na wʋ bɩgɩ tɩn mʋ pɛ a kwe nabiinǝ mɩmaŋa a ma maŋɩ a brɩ abam kʋntʋ doŋ. Abam dɛɛn kwe á maama mʋ á pa wo-digiru dɩ lwarɩm kikiǝ yɩ á yɩ tɩ gambɛ, yɩ á tʋŋɩ wo-lwaanʋ kʋ ja gaalɩ. Lele kʋntʋ, á laan nan maŋɩ sɩ á kwe á maama mʋ á pa Wɛ cɩga kam, sɩ á taá yɩ ka gambɛ sɩ á taá ŋwɩ dɩ wʋ-poŋo.

eng: When you were slaves of sin, you were free from the control of righteousness.
local: Abam dɛɛn na yɩ lwarɩm gambɛ tɩn, Wɛ cɩga kam ya ba te abam.

eng: What consequences did you get from doing things that you are now ashamed of? The outcome of those things is death.
local: Abam na kɩ wo-yɔɔrʋ tɩlʋ tɩn dɛɛn jɩgɩ bɛɛ nyɔɔrɩ mʋ tɩ pa abam? Tɩ laan nan jɩgɩ cavɩɩra mʋ dɩ abam. Wǝǝnu tɩntʋ kweelim je nan yɩ tʋʋnɩ mʋ.

eng: But now that you have been set free from sin and become slaves to God, you have the consequence of a holy life, and the outcome is eternal life.
local: Kʋ daarɩ lele kʋntʋ Wɛ vrɩ abam lwarɩm gambɛɛm wʋnɩ, yɩ á laan yɩ DƖ gambɛ-sonnu mʋ. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ á jɩgɩ nyɔɔrɩ dɩ á na ŋwɩ dɩ wʋ-poŋo tɩn, yɩ kʋ kweelim je nɩ á wʋ́ na ŋwɩa kalʋ na ba ti tɩn Wɛ tee nɩ.

eng: The wages that sin pays are death, but Gods gift is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord.
local: Lwarɩm ŋwɩɩrʋ yɩ tʋʋnɩ mʋ. Kʋ daarɩ Wɛ pɛɛrɩ dɩlʋ DƖ na pɛ dɩbam zaanɩ tɩn mʋ yɩ ŋwɩa kalʋ na ba ti tɩn Wɛ tee nɩ, dɩ́ na ŋwɩ dɩ dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi Krisi tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: From Paul, a slave of Christ Jesus, called to be an apostle and set apart for Gods good news.
local: Amʋ Pooli wʋlʋ na yɩ Zezi Krisi gamba-sono tɩn mʋ pʋpʋnɩ tɔnɔ kʋntʋ. Wɛ mʋ kuri-nɩ sɩ a taa yɩ Zezi tɩntʋŋnʋ, sɩ a taa tɔɔlɩ o kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam.

eng: God promised this good news about his Son ahead of time through his prophets in the holy scriptures. His Son was descended from David.
local: Faŋa faŋa tɩn Wɛ maŋɩ DƖ pa DƖ nijoŋnǝ bam mʋ pʋpʋnɩ DƖ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ, nɩ DƖ goni ni sɩ DƖ pa nɔɔna lwarɩ DƖ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam kʋntʋ.

eng: God promised this good news about his Son ahead of time through his prophets in the holy scriptures. His Son was descended from David.
local: DƖ kwǝrǝ kam kʋntʋ nan yɩ DƖ Bu wʋm taanɩ mʋ, wʋlʋ na yɩ dɩbam Yuutu Zezi Krisi tɩn. Wʋntʋ na jigi nabiinu tɩn, ba lʋg-o Pɛ Davididwi tiinǝ bam wʋnɩ mʋ. Wɛ Jorokʋm nan wʋ o tee nɩ dɩ dam, yɩ kʋ brɩ nɩ o yɩ Baŋa-Wɛ Bu mʋ maŋa kalʋ o na bi o yagɩ tʋʋnɩ tɩn.

eng: Through him we have received Gods grace and our appointment to be apostles. This was to bring all Gentiles to faithful obedience for his names sake.
local: Wʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ Wɛ kɩ dɩbam yu-yoŋo lanyɩranɩ, yɩ DƖ pa dɩ́ yɩ Zezi tɩntʋŋna sɩ dɩ́ vu dwi maama tiinǝ te dɩ́ pa ba sɛ o kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam, sɩ ba daarɩ ba kɩ ba wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩd-o, yɩ kʋntʋ wʋ́ pa Zezi Krisi na zulǝ.

eng: You who are called by Jesus Christ are also included among these Gentiles.
local: Abam dɩ tɔgɩ á yɩ balʋ Wɛ na kuri sɩ á taá yɩ Zezi Krisi nɔɔna tɩn mʋ.

eng: To those in Rome who are dearly loved by God and called to be Gods people. Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: A pʋpʋnɩ a pa abam balʋ na wʋ Rom nɩ yɩ Wɛ soe abam yɩ DƖ kuri abam sɩ á taá yɩ DƖ nɔɔna tɩn mʋ. Dɩ́ Ko Wɛ dɩ dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi Krisi wʋ́ pa abam yazurǝ dɩ yu-yoŋo.

eng: First of all, I thank my God through Jesus Christ for all of you, because the news about your faithfulness is being spread throughout the whole world.
local: Dayigǝ woŋo yɩ, amʋ lagɩ a tɔgɩ Zezi Krisi ŋwaanɩ mʋ a kɩ a Yuutu Wɛ le abam maama ŋwaanɩ, dɩ nɔɔna na tɛ abam taanɩ je maama nɩ á kɩ á wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Zezi tɩn.

eng: I serve God in my spirit by preaching the good news about Gods Son, and God is my witness that I continually mention you.
local: Amʋ yǝni a loori Wɛ abam ŋwaanɩ maŋa maama, yɩ Wɛ dɩlʋ a na tʋŋɩ a paɩ dɩ a wʋ maama dɩ a na tɔɔlɩ DƖ Bu Zezi kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam tɩn ye nɩ amʋ tɛ cɩga mʋ.

eng: in all my prayers. Im always asking that somehow, by Gods will, I might succeed in visiting you at last.
local: A ta yǝni a loori-DƖ sɩ DƖ pa-nɩ cwǝŋǝ, sɩ a laan wanɩ a ba abam te.

eng: I really want to see you to pass along some spiritual gift to you so that you can be strengthened.
local: Abam fra tiini ya jɩgɩ amʋ. A lagɩ sɩ a na abam, sɩ Wɛ Jorokʋm laan wanɩ kʋ tɔgɩ amʋ kʋ zǝni abam dɩ Wɛ-pɛɛrɩ dɩlʋ na wʋ́ pa á zɩgɩ lanyɩranɩ tɩn.

eng: What I mean is that we can mutually encourage each other while I am with you. We can be encouraged by the faithfulness we find in each other, both your faithfulness and mine.
local: A na lagɩ kʋlʋ tɩn mʋ yɩ sɩ dɩ́ na baarɩ da-tee nɩ, dɩbam maama na jɩgɩ wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Zezi tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: I want you to know, brothers and sisters, that I planned to visit you many times, although I have been prevented from coming until now. I want to harvest some fruit among you, just as I have done among the other Gentiles.
local: A ko-biǝ-ba, á taá ye nɩ a kwaanɩ kuni zanzan mʋ sɩ a na nɛ cwǝŋǝ sɩ a ba a na abam, yɩ a daa ta wʋ nɛ cwǝŋǝ dɩ zɩm maama. A lagɩ sɩ a ba sɩ a wanɩ a tʋŋɩ tɩtʋŋɩ dɩlʋ na wʋ́ ja kuri tɩn mʋ abam tɩtarɩ nɩ, nɩ a na maŋɩ a kɩ te dwi tiinǝ badonnǝ dɩ tɩtarɩ nɩ tɩn.

eng: I have a responsibility both to Greeks and to those who dont speak Greek, both to the wise and to the foolish.
local: Kʋ yɩ fɩfɩʋn mʋ sɩ a tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam a brɩ nɔɔna maama, yɩ kʋ nyɩ nɩ jɩm mʋ te a yuu nɩ, kʋ na yɩ balʋ yi na pʋʋrɩ, naa balʋ yi na wʋ pʋʋrɩ tɩn, dɩ swan tiinǝ dɩ balʋ na yǝri kʋlʋkʋlʋ tɩn.

eng: Thats why Im ready to preach the gospel also to you who are in Rome.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ amʋ tiini a lagɩ sɩ a ba a tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam a brɩ abam balʋ na wʋ Rom nɩ tɩn dɩ.

eng: Im not ashamed of the gospel: it is Gods own power for salvation to all who have faith in God, to the Jew first and also to the Greek.
local: Amʋ ba kwarɩ cavɩɩra sɩ a brɩ nɔɔna Wɛ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam. Taanɩ dɩm kʋntʋ mʋ jɩgɩ dam, yɩ Wɛ ma-dɩ DƖ vrɩ nɔɔna balʋ maama na kɩ ba wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ DƖ tɩn, kʋ puli dɩ Zwifǝbam mʋ, yɩ kʋ laan ba kʋ wǝli dwi-gɛtiinǝ dɩ da.

eng: Gods righteousness is being revealed in the gospel, from faithfulness for faith, as it is written, The righteous person will live by faith. .
local: Wɛ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam mʋ brɩ nɔɔna DƖ na wʋ́ pa ba na cɩga DƖ tee nɩ te tɩn. Kʋ maama nan zɩgɩ ba na jɩgɩ wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Wɛ tɩn ŋwaanɩ mʋ. Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm maŋɩ kʋ brɩ nɩ: «Wʋlʋ maama na jɩgɩ cɩga Wɛ tee nɩ tɩn mʋ yɩ wʋlʋ na kɩ o wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ DƖ tɩn, oó na ŋwɩa cɩga cɩga.».

eng: Gods wrath is being revealed from heaven against all the ungodly behavior and the injustice of human beings who silence the truth with injustice.
local: Baŋa-Wɛ nan brɩ nɔɔna DƖ banɩ na zaŋɩ dɩ ba ba lwarɩm dɩ ba kǝm-lwaanʋ tɩm maama ŋwaanɩ. Ba na kɩ wo-lwaanʋ tɩn ŋwaanɩ mʋ pa ba yǝni ba cɩ cɩga kam nɔɔna tee nɩ.

eng: This is because what is known about God should be plain to them because God made it plain to them.
local: Wɛ nan vaŋɩ ba zwa, bɛŋwaanɩ DƖ maŋɩ DƖ pa nɔɔna cwǝŋǝ sɩ ba lwarɩ DƖ cɩga kam. Wɛ tɩtɩ brɩ-ba DƖ cɩga kam jaja yɩ ba wʋ sɛ.

eng: Ever since the creation of the world, Gods invisible qualities—Gods eternal power and divine nature—have been clearly seen, because they are understood through the things God has made. So humans are without excuse.
local: Nabiinǝ warɩ ba naɩ Baŋa-Wɛ dɩ ba yiǝ. Kʋ nan na zɩgɩ DƖ na kɩ lʋgʋ baŋa tɩn, nabiinǝ sɩɩnɩ ba naɩ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ DƖ na kɩ tɩn. Kʋ ma pa ba wanɩ ba lwarɩ lanyɩranɩ nɩ DƖ yɩ Wɛ cɩga cɩga, yɩ DƖ jɩgɩ dam dɩlʋ na ba jɩgɩ tiim tɩn. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ nabiinǝ bá wanɩ ba na bʋra Wɛ tee nɩ dɩ ba na wʋ sɛ-DƖ tɩn.

eng: Although they knew God, they didnt honor God as God or thank him. Instead, their reasoning became pointless, and their foolish hearts were darkened.
local: Ba maŋɩ ba ye Wɛ na yɩ wʋlʋ tɩn, yɩ ba nan ba zuli-DƖ nɩ ba Wɛ, naa ba taa kɩ-DƖ le. Kʋ maa pa ba wʋbʋŋa tɩ, yɩ ba bɩcara yam laan yɩ lim, yɩ ba yǝri woŋo kʋlʋ na lana tɩn.

eng: While they were claiming to be wise, they made fools of themselves.
local: Ba maa paɩ ba jɩgɩ swan mʋ, yɩ ba laan yɩ jwǝǝru.

eng: They exchanged the glory of the immortal God for images that look like mortal humans: birds, animals, and reptiles.
local: Ba ba sɛ sɩ ba taa zuli Ŋwɩa Tu Baŋa-Wɛ, yɩ ba daarɩ ba zuli wǝǝnu tɩlʋ ba tɩtɩ na mɔɔnɩ dɩ ba jɩa tɩn. Wǝǝnu tɩm kʋntʋ mʋ nyɩ dɩ nabiinǝ balʋ na tʋ tɩn, dɩ zunǝ dɩ vara dɩ tɩga wo-vǝǝlu.

eng: So God abandoned them to their hearts desires, which led to the moral corruption of degrading their own bodies with each other.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ Wɛ yagɩ-ba, sɩ ba taa tɔgɩ wo-yɔɔrʋ tɩlʋ ba tɩtɩ fra na zʋʋrɩ tɩn. Ba maa yǝni ba kɩ wo-digiru, yɩ ba tɔgɩ boorim kikiǝ yalʋ na yɩ ba cavɩɩra tɩn.

eng: They traded Gods truth for a lie, and they worshipped and served the creation instead of the creator, who is blessed forever. Amen.
local: Ba maa vɩn Wɛ cɩga kam, yɩ ba daarɩ ba tɔgɩ vwan. Ba maa yǝni ba zuli wǝǝnu tɩlʋ Wɛ na kɩ tɩn, yɩ ba tɔgɩ-tɩ nɩ tɩ yɩ Wɛ mʋ te. Ba daarɩ ba yagɩ wʋlʋ tɩtɩ na yɩ tɩ maama Kǝru tɩn. Wʋntʋ nan mʋ maŋɩ sɩ o joŋi zulǝ sɩ kʋ taa ve maŋa kalʋ na ba ti tɩn. Amina.

eng: Thats why God abandoned them to degrading lust. Their females traded natural sexual relations for unnatural sexual relations.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ Wɛ yagɩ-ba, yɩ ba kɩ wo-digiru kikiǝ yalʋ ba fra na zʋʋrɩ yɩ ya jɩgɩ cavɩɩra tɩn. Kaana ma yǝni ba yagɩ ba banna, yɩ ba daarɩ ba tigi dɩ ba donnǝ kaana, nɩ baarʋ dɩ o kaanɩ na tigi daanɩ te tɩn.

eng: Also, in the same way, the males traded natural sexual relations with females, and burned with lust for each other. Males performed shameful actions with males, and they were paid back with the penalty they deserved for their mistake in their own bodies.
local: Kʋ yɩ kʋntʋ mʋ dɩ baara dɩ. Ba dɩ ba lagɩ kaana, yɩ ba daarɩ ba paɩ ba fra zʋʋrɩ ba donnǝ baara ba ma tigi dɩ ba, yɩ kʋ ya wʋ maŋɩ sɩ ba taa kɩ kʋntʋ. Baara yǝni ba kɩ cavɩɩra kǝnǝ yantʋ doŋ mʋ daanɩ, yɩ kʋ pa ba na cam dɩlʋ na maŋɩ dɩ ba lwarɩm kikiǝ yam tɩn.

eng: Since they didnt think it was worthwhile to acknowledge God, God abandoned them to a defective mind to do inappropriate things.
local: Nɔɔna bam kʋntʋ nan bʋŋɩ nɩ Wɛ cɩga kam ba jɩgɩ kuri mʋ sɩ ba taa jɩgɩ-ka ba wʋbʋŋa nɩ. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ Wɛ yagɩ-ba, sɩ ba taa tɔgɩ ba wʋbʋŋ-yɔɔrʋ tɩm kwaga, yɩ ba kɩ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ ba ya na wʋ maŋɩ sɩ ba kɩ tɩn.

eng: So they were filled with all injustice, wicked behavior, greed, and evil behavior. They are full of jealousy, murder, fighting, deception, and malice. They are gossips.
local: Ba wʋbʋŋa maa su dɩ wo-balwaarʋ tɩlʋ na ba tɔgɩ cɩga kam cwǝŋǝ tɩn, dɩ kǝm-lwaanʋ dwi maama. Ba yi maa zʋ wǝǝnu zanzan. Ba tiini ba jɩgɩ pu-sɩŋa, yɩ ba jɩgɩ wʋ-gʋʋ. Ba ma lagɩ nɔɔna gʋm, yɩ ba kwǝri ba jaanɩ daanɩ, yɩ ba daarɩ ba ganɩ ba donnǝ. Ba maa bʋŋɩ wo-balwaarʋ ba paɩ ba donnǝ, yɩ ba yɩ bɩbarɩ-nyɩna.

eng: they slander people, and they hate God. They are rude and proud, and they brag. They invent ways to be evil, and they are disobedient to their parents.
local: Ba maa ŋɔɔnɩ ba cɔgɩ ba donnǝ yum. Ba maa culi Wɛ, yɩ ba twɩ ba donnǝ. Ba ta kwǝri ba yɩ banɩ-nyɩna, yɩ ba brɩ ba tɩtɩ nɔɔna yigǝ nɩ. Ba ta ma tiini ba bʋŋɩ kǝm-balwaarʋ wʋbʋŋa, yɩ ba ba nɩgɩ ba tiinǝ balʋ na lʋgɩ-ba tɩn.

eng: They are without understanding, disloyal, without affection, and without mercy.
local: Ba yǝri kʋlʋ na wʋ maŋɩ tɩn. Ba maa jɩgɩ niǝ yale yale. Ba ma ba soe ba donnǝ, yɩ ba ba jɩgɩ ba ŋwaŋa dɩ.

eng: Though they know Gods decision that those who persist in such practices deserve death, they not only keep doing these things but also approve others who practice them.
local: Ba nan maŋɩ ba ye Wɛ kwǝrǝ kam na brɩ kʋlʋ, nɩ balʋ na kɩ kǝm-balwaarʋ tɩntʋ doŋ tɩn maŋɩ sɩ ba tɩ mʋ. Nan dɩ kʋntʋ dɩ, ba ta yǝni ba kɩ-tɩ mʋ, yɩ ba tɛ dɩ balʋ na kɩ lwarɩm tɩn dɩ, nɩ kʋ lana.

eng: Brothers and sisters, Im talking to you as people who know the Law. Dont you know that the Law has power over someone only as long as he or she lives?.
local: A ko-biǝ-ba, á maama ye cullu laŋa na yɩ te. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ á ye nɩ cullu na te nɔɔnʋ, kʋ yɩ sɩ kʋ taa ve o tʋʋnɩ mʋ. O nan na tɩga, tɩ daa ba te-o.

eng: A married woman is united with her husband under the Law while he is alive. But if her husband dies, she is released from the Law concerning her husband.
local: Kʋ nyɩ nɩ kaanɩ na zʋ barʋ te tɩn mʋ. Culu brɩ nɩ o barʋ wʋm ta na ŋwɩ maŋa kalʋ tɩn, o ba jɩgɩ cwǝŋǝ sɩ o yagɩ o barʋ wʋm. O barʋ wʋm nan na tɩga, o laan jɩgɩ ni sɩ o zʋ barʋ wʋdoŋ, culu daa wʋ vɔgɩ-ba daanɩ.

eng: So then, if she lives with another man while her husband is alive, shes committing adultery. But if her husband dies, shes free from the Law, so she wont be committing adultery if she marries someone else.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ, o barʋ wʋm ta na ŋwɩ, yɩ kaanɩ wʋm na ve o zʋ barʋ wʋdoŋ, o cɔgɩ mʋ. O barʋ wʋm nan na tɩga, culu kʋm daa wʋ vɔgɩ-ba daanɩ. O laan na zʋ barʋ wʋdoŋ, o daa wʋ cɔgɩ.

eng: Therefore, my brothers and sisters, you also died with respect to the Law through the body of Christ, so that you could be united with someone else. You are united with the one who was raised from the dead so that we can bear fruit for God.
local: A ko-biǝ-ba, kʋ yɩ kʋntʋ mʋ dɩ abam dɩ. Kʋ na yɩ cullu tɩm laŋa nɩ, abam maama nyɩ dɩ twa mʋ te, Zezi Krisi na tɩgɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Kʋntʋ ma pa dɩ́ yigǝ lɩ cullu tɩm wʋnɩ, yɩ dɩ́ jɩgɩ cwǝŋǝ sɩ dɩ́ kɩ dɩ́ tɩtɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ jɩŋa nɩ, wʋntʋ yɩ Zezi wʋlʋ na bi o yagɩ tʋʋnɩ tɩn. Kʋ maa paɩ dɩ́ tʋŋɩ tɩtʋŋ-ŋʋna dɩ́ pa Wɛ.

eng: When we were self-centered, the sinful passions aroused through the Law were at work in all the parts of our body, so that we bore fruit for death.
local: Dɩ́ dɛɛn kɩ wo-yɔɔrʋ tɩlʋ dɩ́ wʋbʋŋa na lagɩ tɩn. Wɛ cullu tɩm nan brɩ dɩbam kʋlʋ na yɩ lwarɩm tɩn, yɩ dɩ́ fra ta yǝni ya zʋʋrɩ wǝǝnu tɩm kʋntʋ. Kʋ dɛɛn ma pa dɩ́ kɩ kǝm-balwaarʋ tɩlʋ na jɩgɩ dɩbam tɩ ve tʋʋnɩ tɩn.

eng: But now we have been released from the Law. We have died with respect to the thing that controlled us, so that we can be slaves in the new life under the Spirit, not in the old life under the written Law.
local: Cullu tɩm dɛɛn tiini tɩ pɩɩnɩ dɩbam mʋ dɩ dam. Lele kʋntʋ tɩ nan daa ba te dɩbam, dɩ́ na tɔgɩ dɩ Zezi dɩ́ tɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Kʋntʋ tɩn, dɩ́ na tʋŋɩ Wɛ tɩtʋŋa yam te tɩn, kʋ yɩ DƖ Jorokʋm na wʋ dɩ́ bɩcara nɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ mʋ. Dɩ́ laan tɔgɩ cwǝ-dʋŋa kam kʋntʋ, sɩ kʋ daɩ cwǝ-doŋǝ kalʋ na yɩ cullu tɩlʋ na pʋpʋnɩ tɩ tiŋi tɔnɔ wʋnɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: So what are we going to say? That the Law is sin? Absolutely not! But I wouldnt have known sin except through the Law. I wouldnt have known the desire for what others have if the Law had not said, Dont desire to take what others have .
local: Bɛɛ mʋ dɩ́ nan wʋ́ ta cullu tɩm laŋa nɩ? Tɩ yɩ lwarɩm mʋ na? Aye, kʋ daɩ kʋntʋ. Wɛ cullu tɩm nan mʋ pɛ a lwarɩ lwarɩm na yɩ te tɩn. Tɩ ya na wʋ brɩ nɩ: «Yɩ pa n yi zʋ n donnǝ wǝǝnu», a ya bá ta ye nɩ a yi na zʋ a donnǝ wǝǝnu, kʋ yɩ lwarɩm mʋ.

eng: But sin seized the opportunity and used this commandment to produce all kinds of desires in me. Sin is dead without the Law.
local: Culu kʋm kʋntʋ nan mʋ pɛ lwarɩm na cwǝŋǝ dɩ zʋ a wʋbʋŋa kʋ pa a yi zʋ wǝǝnu zanzan. Cullu nan na tǝrǝ, lwarɩm bá ta jɩgɩ dam.

eng: I used to be alive without the Law, but when the commandment came, sin sprang to life.
local: A dɛɛn ya ta na yǝri Wɛ cullu tɩm tɩn, a ŋwɩ mʋ a ya ba liǝ. A laan nan na tu a lwarɩ Wɛ cullu tɩm na brɩ kʋlʋ tɩn mʋ pɛ lwarɩm jɩgɩ dam a baŋa nɩ.

eng: and I died. So the commandment that was intended to give life brought death.
local: yɩ a nyɩ nɩ a tɩgɩ mʋ te. Wɛ cullu tɩlʋ ya na maŋɩ sɩ tɩ pa nɔɔna ŋwɩa tɩn laan pɛ tʋʋnɩ mʋ ja-nɩ.

eng: Sin seized the opportunity through the commandment, deceived me, and killed me.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ, a na ni cullu tɩm tɩn mʋ pɛ lwarɩm laan na cwǝŋǝ dɩ ganɩ-nɩ, yɩ dɩ tɔgɩ cullu tɩm ŋwaanɩ dɩ pa tʋʋnɩ ja-nɩ.

eng: So the Law itself is holy, and the commandment is holy, righteous, and good.
local: Kʋntʋ nan brɩ nɩ Wɛ cullu tɩm ba jɩgɩ lwarɩm mʋ. Ni dɩlʋ maama Wɛ na pɛ dɩbam tɩn ba jɩgɩ lwarɩm. Dɩ yɩ lanyɩranɩ mʋ yɩ dɩ jɩgɩ cɩga.

eng: So did something good bring death to me? Absolutely not! But sin caused my death through something good so that sin would be exposed as sin. That way sin would become even more thoroughly sinful through the commandment.
local: Kʋntʋ nan brɩ nɩ Wɛ cullu tɩlʋ na lana tɩn mʋ jaanɩ a tʋʋnɩ dɩm dɩ ba na? Awo. Kʋ yɩ lwarɩm mʋ dɛ Wɛ cullu tɩm dɩ pa tʋʋnɩ ja-nɩ. Kʋntʋ kɩ sɩ nɔɔna mʋ wanɩ ba lwarɩ lwarɩm na sɩɩnɩ dɩ yɩ te tɩn. Cullu tɩm ma pa lwarɩm tiini dɩ puli dɩ brɩ dɩ na yɩ balɔrɔ te tɩn.

eng: We know that the Law is spiritual, but Im made of flesh and blood, and Im sold as a slave to sin.
local: Dɩ́ ye nɩ Wɛ cullu tɩm tɔgɩ DƖ wʋbʋŋa na lagɩ te tɩn mʋ. Amʋ nan yɩ lʋgʋ baŋa nabiinu mʋ, yɩ a yɩ lwarɩm gambaa.

eng: I dont know what Im doing, because I dont do what I want to do. Instead, I do the thing that I hate.
local: A nan yǝri woŋo kʋlʋ a na kɩ tɩn kuri. Amʋ yǝni a ba kɩ kʋlʋ a na lagɩ tɩn, yɩ a laan daarɩ a kɩ kʋlʋ a na culi tɩn.

eng: But if Im doing the thing that I dont want to do, Im agreeing that the Law is right.
local: A na yǝni a kɩ kʋlʋ a na ba lagɩ sɩ a kɩ tɩn, kʋ brɩ nɩ a sɛ nɩ Wɛ cullu tɩm yɩ cɩga mʋ.

eng: But now Im not the one doing it anymore. Instead, its sin that lives in me.
local: Kʋntʋ, kʋ daɩ nɩ a tɩtɩ mʋ paalɩ a kɩ wǝǝnu tɩm kʋntʋ, kʋ nan yɩ lwarɩm dɩlʋ na wʋ a wʋnɩ tɩn mʋ pa a kɩa.

eng: I know that good doesnt live in me—that is, in my body. The desire to do good is inside of me, but I cant do it.
local: A nan ye nɩ lanyɩranɩ tǝri a wʋnɩ, dɩ a na yɩ nabiinu yɩ a fra zʋʋrɩ lʋgʋ baŋa wo-yɔɔrʋ tɩn. Bɛŋwaanɩ, a na yǝni a jɩgɩ wʋbʋŋa sɩ a kɩ lanyɩranɩ, a ta warɩ lanyɩranɩ wʋm a kɩ.

eng: I dont do the good that I want to do, but I do the evil that I dont want to do.
local: A yǝni a ba kɩ lanyɩranɩ nɩ a wʋbʋŋa na lagɩ te tɩn, yɩ a laan daarɩ a kɩ wo-balwaarʋ tɩlʋ a na ba lagɩ sɩ a kɩ tɩn.

eng: But if I do the very thing that I dont want to do, then Im not the one doing it anymore. Instead, it is sin that lives in me that is doing it.
local: A na yǝni a kɩ kʋlʋ a wʋbʋŋa na ba lagɩ sɩ a kɩ tɩn, kʋ brɩ nɩ kʋ daɩ a tɩtɩ mʋ paalɩ a kɩ wǝǝnu tɩm kʋntʋ, kʋ yɩ lwarɩm dɩlʋ na wʋ a wʋnɩ tɩn mʋ paɩ a kɩa.

eng: So I find that, as a rule, when I want to do what is good, evil is right there with me.
local: A nan lwarɩ kʋ na yǝni kʋ kɩ amʋ te tɩn: A na yǝni a bʋŋɩ sɩ a kɩ kʋlʋ na lana tɩn, kʋ yɩ lwarɩm yɩranɩ mʋ aá kɩ.

eng: I gladly agree with the Law on the inside.
local: A tiini a jɩgɩ wʋpolo a bɩcarɩ nɩ dɩ Wɛ cullu tɩm.

eng: but I see a different law at work in my body. It wages a war against the law of my mind and takes me prisoner with the law of sin that is in my body.
local: A nan ye nɩ dam dɩdoŋ mʋ wʋ amʋ wʋnɩ, kʋ na lagɩ sɩ kʋ pa a taa tɔgɩ cwǝŋǝ kalʋ na ba lana tɩn. Dam dɩm kʋntʋ maa lagɩ sɩ dɩ cɔgɩ a wʋbʋŋa yam na lagɩ sɩ ya da cwǝ-ŋʋŋa kalʋ tɩn. Kʋntʋ maa pɩɩnɩ amʋ, yɩ lwarɩm dɩlʋ na wʋ a wʋnɩ tɩn paɩ a ta tɔgɩ lwarɩm cwǝŋǝ.

eng: Im a miserable human being. Who will deliver me from this dead corpse?.
local: Lɛɛrʋ maa jɩgɩ-nɩ, lwarɩm dɩlʋ na wʋ a wʋnɩ tɩn na lagɩ sɩ dɩ pa tʋʋnɩ ja-nɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Wɔɔ mʋ wʋ́ wanɩ o vrɩ-nɩ o yagɩ?.

eng: Thank God through Jesus Christ our Lord! So then Im a slave to Gods Law in my mind, but Im a slave to sins law in my body.
local: Amʋ kɩ Wɛ le dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi Krisi ŋwaanɩ. Wʋntʋ mʋ wʋ́ joŋi-nɩ. Kʋ na yɩ a wʋbʋŋa yam wʋnɩ, a sɛ Wɛ cullu tɩm mʋ. A nan na yɩ lʋgʋ baŋa nabiinu tɩn mʋ paɩ a tɔgɩ lwarɩm cwǝŋǝ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, my hearts desire is for Israels salvation. Thats my prayer to God for them.
local: A ko-biǝ-ba, amʋ tiini a laga sɩ Yisɩrayɛlɩ tiinǝ bam na vrɩm, yɩ a yǝni a loori Wɛ a pa-ba kʋ ŋwaanɩ.

eng: I can vouch for them: they are enthusiastic about God. However, it isnt informed by knowledge.
local: Amʋ wʋ́ wanɩ a pa cɩga nɩ ba tiini ba lagɩ sɩ ba tʋŋɩ ba pa Wɛ, ba nan yǝri cwǝŋǝ kalʋ ba na maŋɩ sɩ ba tɔgɩ tɩn mʋ.

eng: So now there isnt any condemnation for those who are in Christ Jesus.
local: Kʋntʋ tɩn, balʋ na ŋwɩ dɩ Zezi Krisi tɩn, Wɛ daa bá di ba taanɩ sɩ DƖ cɔgɩ-ba.

eng: They dont submit to Gods righteousness because they dont understand his righteousness, and they try to establish their own righteousness.
local: Ba maa yǝri cwǝŋǝ kalʋ Wɛ na pɛ sɩ nabiinǝ tɔgɩ da ba ma na cɩga DƖ tee nɩ tɩn. Ba maa tiŋi ba tɩtɩ cwǝŋǝ sɩ ba maa na cɩga Wɛ tee nɩ, yɩ ba ba sɛ sɩ ba taa tɔgɩ cwǝŋǝ kalʋ Wɛ na tiŋi tɩn.

eng: The law of the Spirit of life in Christ Jesus has set you free from the law of sin and death.
local: Wɛ Jorokʋm mʋ paɩ dɩ́ jɩgɩ ŋwɩa Wɛ tee nɩ Zezi Krisi ŋwaanɩ. Wɛ Joro kʋm dam dɩm mʋ pɛ dɩ́ na vrɩm lwarɩm dɩ tʋʋnɩ jɩŋa nɩ.

eng: Christ is the goal of the Law, which leads to righteousness for all who have faith in God.
local: Zezi Krisi mʋ pɛ Wɛ cullu tɩm tɔgɩm cwǝŋǝ kam laan ba ka kweeli, kʋ ma pa balʋ maama na kɩ ba wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩd-o tɩn laan na cɩga Wɛ tee nɩ.

eng: God has done what was impossible for the Law, since it was weak because of selfishness. God condemned sin in the body by sending his own Son to deal with sin in the same body as humans, who are controlled by sin.
local: Dɩbam na yɩ nabiinǝ yɩ dɩ́ bwǝnǝ tɩn mʋ paɩ dɩ́ warɩ Wɛ cullu tɩm dɩ́ da. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ dɩ́ daa bá wanɩ dɩ́ na cɩga Wɛ tee nɩ DƖ cullu tɩm tɔgɩm ŋwaanɩ. Woŋo kʋlʋ cullu tɩm na wʋ wanɩ tɩ kɩ tɩn, Wɛ mʋ kɩa, dɩ DƖ na tʋŋɩ DƖ tɩtɩ Bu sɩ o ba o ji nabiinu, yɩ o nyɩ dɩ nabiinǝ balʋ lwarɩm na waɩ-ba tɩn. O ma ba o tɩ nabiinǝ lwarɩm ŋwaanɩ, sɩ o ma cɔgɩ lwarɩm na jɩgɩ dam dɩlʋ nabiinǝ baŋa nɩ tɩn.

eng: Moses writes about the righteousness that comes from the Law: The person who does these things will live by them. .
local: Moyisi dɛɛn pʋpʋnɩ nɔɔnʋ na wʋ́ kɩ te sɩ o ma na cɩga Wɛ tee nɩ DƖ cullu tɩm tɔgɩm ŋwaanɩ tɩn. O pʋpʋnɩ o wɩ: «Wʋlʋ na kɩ Wɛ cullu tɩm na wɩ te tɩn wʋ́ na ŋwɩa kʋ ŋwaanɩ.».

eng: He did this so that the righteous requirement of the Law might be fulfilled in us. Now the way we live is based on the Spirit, not based on selfishness.
local: Wɛ kɩ kʋntʋ sɩ kʋ pa dɩ́ na cɩga mʋ DƖ tee nɩ, sɩ dɩ́ na cwǝŋǝ dɩ́ kɩ kʋlʋ maama Wɛ cullu tɩm na brɩ nɩ kʋ maŋɩ tɩn. Bɛŋwaanɩ dɩbam yɩ balʋ na tɔgɩ Wɛ Joro kʋm cwǝŋǝ tɩn mʋ, sɩ dɩ́ daa ba tɔgɩ wo-yɔɔrʋ tɩlʋ nabiinǝ fra na zʋʋrɩ tɩn.

eng: But the righteousness that comes from faith talks like this: Dont say in your heart , Who will go up into heaven? .
local: Kʋ nan daa na yɩ nɔɔnʋ na wʋ́ kɩ te sɩ o ma na cɩga Wɛ tee nɩ o na kɩ o wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ DƖ tɩn ŋwaanɩ, kʋ pʋpʋnɩ kʋ wɩ: «Yɩ zaŋɩ n bwe n tɩtɩ nɩnɛɛnɩ, wɔɔ mʋ wʋ́ wanɩ o di Wɛ-sɔŋɔ?» Kʋ lagɩ kʋ ta nɩ, sɩ o pa Zezi Krisi tu o ba o zǝni dɩbam.

eng: People whose lives are based on selfishness think about selfish things, but people whose lives are based on the Spirit think about things that are related to the Spirit.
local: Balʋ nan na tɔgɩ wo-yɔɔrʋ tɩm kʋntʋ doŋ tɩn tiini ba kwe ba wʋbʋŋa ba tiŋi wǝǝnu tɩlʋ nabiinǝ wʋbʋŋa na lagɩ tɩn baŋa nɩ mʋ. Kʋ daarɩ balʋ na tɔgɩ Wɛ Joro kʋm cwǝŋǝ kam tɩn kwe ba wʋbʋŋa ba tiŋi wo-laarʋ tɩlʋ Wɛ Joro kʋm wʋbʋŋa na lagɩ tɩn baŋa nɩ mʋ.

eng: or Who will go down into the region below? .
local: «Nan yɩ bwe n tɩtɩ nɩ, wɔɔ mʋ wʋ́ wanɩ o tu curu?» Kʋ lagɩ kʋ ta nɩ, sɩ o pa Zezi bi o yagɩ tʋʋnɩ sɩ o joori o ba.

eng: The attitude that comes from selfishness leads to death, but the attitude that comes from the Spirit leads to life and peace.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋbʋŋa na tɔgɩ wo-yɔɔrʋ tɩlʋ nabiinǝ fra na zʋʋrɩ tɩn, tʋʋnɩ wʋ́ ja kʋntʋ tu. Wʋlʋ wʋbʋŋa nan na tɔgɩ Wɛ Joro kʋm cwǝŋǝ kam tɩn wʋ́ na ŋwɩa Wɛ tee nɩ dɩ wʋ-zuru.

eng: But what does it say? The word is near you, in your mouth and in your heart .
local: Kʋ nan maŋɩ kʋ pʋpʋnɩ kʋ wɩ: «Wɛ taanɩ dɩm daɩ yigǝ yigǝ dɩ nmʋ, dɩ wʋ nmʋ ni nɩ dɩ n bɩcarɩ nɩ.» Kwǝrǝ kam kʋntʋ mʋ dɩbam tɔɔlɩ dɩ́ brɩ nɔɔna sɩ ba kɩ ba wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Zezi.

eng: So the attitude that comes from selfishness is hostile to God. It doesnt submit to Gods Law, because it cant.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ, nɔɔnʋ na tɔgɩ nabiinǝ wʋbʋŋ-yɔɔrʋ tɩm, o yɩ Wɛ dʋm mʋ, o ba kɩ kʋlʋ Wɛ niǝ yam na brɩ tɩn, o nan warɩ-ya dɩ o sɛ.

eng: Because if you confess with your mouth Jesus is Lord and in your heart you have faith that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved.
local: Kʋ tagɩ kʋ wɩ, nmʋ na tagɩ dɩ n ni nɩ Zezi mʋ yɩ woŋo maama Yuutu, yɩ nmʋ na sɛ n bɩcarɩ nɩ, sɩ Wɛ sɩɩnɩ DƖ bi-o DƖ pa o yagɩ tʋʋnɩ, Wɛ wʋ́ vrɩ-m.

eng: People who are self-centered arent able to please God.
local: Balʋ maama na tɔgɩ wo-yɔɔrʋ tɩlʋ nabiinǝ wʋbʋŋa na lagɩ tɩn bá wanɩ ba pa Wɛ wʋ poli.

eng: Trusting with the heart leads to righteousness, and confessing with the mouth leads to salvation.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ, kʋ yɩ nmʋ bɩcarɩ nɩ mʋ nń sɛ dɩ n wʋ-dɩdʋa yɩ n na cɩga Wɛ tee nɩ, yɩ nmʋ wʋ́ ma n ni mʋ n ta jaja nɩ Zezi mʋ yɩ Yuutu, sɩ n ma n na vrɩm.

eng: But you arent self-centered. Instead you are in the Spirit, if in fact Gods Spirit lives in you. If anyone doesnt have the Spirit of Christ, they dont belong to him.
local: Abam nan ba tɔgɩ nabiinǝ wʋbʋŋ-yɔɔrʋ na lagɩ kʋlʋ tɩn. Á tɔgɩ Wɛ Jorokʋm na lagɩ kʋlʋ tɩn mʋ, kʋ na wʋ á bɩcarɩ nɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Zezi Krisi Joro kʋm nan na tǝri nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ bɩcarɩ nɩ tɩn, o daɩ Zezi nɔɔnʋ.

eng: The scripture says, All who have faith in him wont be put to shame .
local: Kʋ pʋpʋnɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ kʋ wɩ: «Wʋlʋ maama na kɩ o wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩd-o tɩn, cavɩɩra bá ja kʋntʋ tu.».

eng: If Christ is in you, the Spirit is your life because of Gods righteousness, but the body is dead because of sin.
local: Zezi Krisi na wʋ á bɩcara nɩ tɩn, o Joro kʋm mʋ paɩ á ŋwɩ dɩ Wɛ, á na nɛ cɩga DƖ tee nɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Á yɩra yam nan maŋɩ sɩ ya tɩ mʋ lwarɩm dɩm ŋwaanɩ.

eng: There is no distinction between Jew and Greek, because the same Lord is Lord of all, who gives richly to all who call on him.
local: Kʋntʋ, kʋ yɩ bɩdwɩ mʋ dɩ nɔɔna maama, kʋ na yɩ Zwifǝ dɩ dwi-gɛ tiinǝ bam dɩ. Ba maama Yuutu yɩ dɩdʋa mʋ, yɩ wʋntʋ mʋ yǝni o paɩ balʋ maama na loor-o tɩn zǝnǝ zanzan.

eng: If the Spirit of the one who raised Jesus from the dead lives in you, the one who raised Christ from the dead will give life to your human bodies also, through his Spirit that lives in you.
local: Wɛ mʋ bi Zezi DƖ pa o yagɩ tʋʋnɩ. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ, DƖ Joro kʋm na sɩɩnɩ kʋ wʋ á bɩcara nɩ, Wɛ wʋ́ pa abam yɩra yalʋ na maŋɩ sɩ ya tɩ tɩn joori ya na ŋwɩa, DƖ Joro kʋm na wʋ á bɩcara nɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: All who call on the Lords name will be saved. .
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ, «wʋlʋ maama na loori zǝnǝ dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm yɩrɩ ŋwaanɩ tɩn wʋ́ na vrɩm.».

eng: So then, brothers and sisters, we have an obligation, but it isnt an obligation to ourselves to live our lives on the basis of selfishness.
local: Kʋntʋ, a ko-biǝ-ba, dɩ́ maŋɩ sɩ dɩ́ taá ŋwɩ nɩ Wɛ Joro kʋm na lagɩ te tɩn mʋ, sɩ kʋ daɩ sɩ dɩ́ taá tɔgɩ wo-yɔɔrʋ tɩlʋ nabiinǝ wʋbʋŋa na lagɩ tɩn.
