eng: Who has known the mind of the Lord, who will advise him? But we have the mind of Christ.
local: Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm tagɩ kʋ wɩ: «Nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ bá wanɩ o lwarɩ dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm wʋbʋŋa, nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ nan bá wanɩ o brɩ-o o na wʋ́ kɩ te tɩn.» Wɛ nan na pɛ dɩbam DƖ Joro kʋm tɩn mʋ pɛ dɩbam dɩ jɩgɩ Zezi Krisi wʋbʋŋa yam.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I couldnt talk to you like spiritual people but like unspiritual people, like babies in Christ.
local: A ko-biǝ-ba, a dɛɛn na wʋ abam tee nɩ tɩn, a warɩ a ŋɔɔnɩ Wɛ taanɩ dɩm dɩ abam nɩ Wɛ Jorokʋm na wʋ balʋ tee nɩ tɩn. A dɛɛn ŋɔɔnɩ dɩ abam nɩnɛɛnɩ á yɩ balʋ lʋgʋ baŋa wǝǝnu fra na jɩgɩ-ba tɩn, yɩ á ta nyɩ nɩ bu-balwa mʋ te Zezi Krisi cwǝŋǝ kam wʋnɩ.

eng: I gave you milk to drink instead of solid food, because you werent up to it yet.
local: A dɛɛn na brɩ abam wo-mwaalʋ tɩlʋ tɩn nyɩ dɩ a pɛ abam nayɩla mʋ sɩ á nyɔ. A dɛɛn wʋ pɛ abam wʋdiu kʋlʋ na duunǝ tɩn, á dɛɛn na wʋ yi kʋ dim tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Dɩ zɩm dɩ, á daa ta wʋ yi wʋdiu dim.

eng: Now you are still not up to it because you are still unspiritual. When jealousy and fighting exist between you, arent you unspiritual and living by human standards?.
local: bɛŋwaanɩ á ta yɩ balʋ lʋgʋ baŋa wǝǝnu fra na jɩgɩ tɩn mʋ. Abam jɩgɩ wʋ-gʋʋ yɩ á magɩ kantɔgɔ daanɩ. Kʋntʋ brɩ nɩ lʋgʋ baŋa wo-yɔɔrʋ fra mʋ taa jɩgɩ abam, yɩ á kɩ nɩ balʋ ta na yǝri Wɛ tɩn mʋ te. Kʋ yɩ vwan mʋ a fɔgɩ na?.

eng: When someone says, I belong to Paul, and someone else says, I belong to Apollos, arent you acting like people without the Spirit?.
local: Abam wʋ nɔɔna badonnǝ na tɛ ba wɩ, bam tɔgɩ Pooli mʋ, yɩ badonnǝ dɩ wɩ, ba tɔgɩ Apolɔsɩ mʋ tɩn, kʋ daɩ balʋ na yǝri Wɛ tɩn kikiǝ mʋ kʋntʋ na?.

eng: After all, what is Apollos? What is Paul? They are servants who helped you to believe. Each one had a role given to them by the Lord.
local: Wɔɔ mʋ yɩ Apolɔsɩ? Wɔɔ mʋ yɩ amʋ Pooli? Dɩbam maama yɩ Wɛ tɩntʋŋna balʋ abam na dɛ dɩbam jɩŋa á kɩ á wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Zezi Krisi tɩn mʋ. Dɩbam maama tʋŋɩ kʋlʋ dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm na pɛ dɩbam sɩ dɩ́ tʋŋɩ tɩn mʋ.

eng: I planted, Apollos watered, but God made it grow.
local: Dɩbam nyɩ nɩ vala mʋ te, amʋ Pooli mʋ jǝri wǝǝnu yɩ Apolɔsɩ yagɩ na. Kʋ daarɩ kʋ yɩ Wɛ mʋ paɩ tɩ bɩra.

eng: Because of this, neither the one who plants nor the one who waters is anything, but the only one who is anything is God who makes it grow.
local: Mʋ kʋ kuri wʋlʋ na jǝri dɩ wʋlʋ na yagɩ na da tɩn maama daɩ kʋlʋkʋlʋ. Wɛ yɩranɩ mʋ waɩ DƖ paɩ DƖ taanɩ dɩm kɩ dam nɔɔna bɩcara nɩ.

eng: The one who plants and the one who waters work together, but each one will receive their own reward for their own labor.
local: Wʋlʋ na jǝri dɩ wʋlʋ na yagɩ na tɩn maama yɩ bɩdwɩ mʋ. Wɛ wʋ́ pa ba maama joŋi ba ŋwɩɩrʋ kʋ maŋɩ dɩ ba na tʋŋɩ te tɩn mʋ.

eng: We are Gods coworkers, and you are Gods field, Gods building.
local: Dɩbam maama wǝli daanɩ mʋ dɩ́ tʋŋɩ dɩ́ pa Wɛ. Abam nyɩ dɩ Wɛ kara kalʋ dɩ́ na tʋŋɩ ka wʋnɩ tɩn mʋ. Abam daa ta nyɩ dɩ digǝ kalʋ Wɛ na wʋra DƖ lɔ tɩn mʋ.

eng: I laid a foundation like a wise master builder according to Gods grace that was given to me, but someone else is building on top of it. Each person needs to pay attention to the way they build on it.
local: Wɛ na pɛ amʋ pɛɛrɩ dɩlʋ sɩ a ma tʋŋɩ tɩn, kʋ yɩ sɩ amʋ cwi digǝ kam kuri mʋ lanyɩranɩ nɩ lwar-ŋʋm te. Nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ dɩ maa wʋra o lɔ o daŋɩ da. Wʋlʋ maama nan na lagɩ sɩ o lɔ o daŋɩ da tɩn maŋɩ sɩ o taa ye o na lɔ te tɩn mʋ.

eng: No one can lay any other foundation besides the one that is already laid, which is Jesus Christ.
local: Wɛ maŋɩ DƖ pa digǝ kam kuri cwi, nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ daa bá wanɩ o cwi ka kuri dɩdoŋ. Kuri dɩm kʋntʋ mʋ yɩ Zezi Krisi.

eng: So, whether someone builds on top of the foundation with gold, silver, precious stones, wood, grass, or hay.
local: Balʋ nan na lɔ ba daŋɩ da tɩn badaara maɩ sǝbu-sɩŋa naa can-na naa kandwa-ŋʋna, naa dɛ naa gaarʋ naa kasʋrʋ mʋ ba maa lɔa.

eng: each ones work will be clearly shown. The day will make it clear, because it will be revealed with fire—the fire will test the quality of each ones work.
local: Nɔɔnʋ maama na tʋŋɩ kʋlʋ tɩn nan wʋ́ ba kʋ lwarɩ jaja dɛ dɩm Zezi Krisi na wʋ́ joori o ba tɩn, mini mʋ wʋ́ brɩ nɔɔnʋ maama tɩtʋŋa na yɩ te.

eng: If anyones work survives, theyll get a reward.
local: Balʋ na lɔgɩ dɩ wo-laarʋ yɩ mini dɩm wʋ wanɩ dɩ di tɩn wʋ́ joŋi ba ŋwɩɩrʋ.

eng: But if anyones work goes up in flames, theyll lose it. However, they themselves will be saved as if they had gone through a fire.
local: Kʋ daarɩ, balʋ mini dɩm na wanɩ dɩ di ba lɔɔm dɩm tɩn, ba bá na kʋlʋkʋlʋ Wɛ tee nɩ. Wɛ za wʋ́ vrɩ ba tɩtɩ DƖ yagɩ. Ba nan wʋ́ ta nyɩ dɩ mini na di nɔɔnʋ yɩ o wǝri te tɩn mʋ.

eng: Dont you know that you are Gods temple and Gods Spirit lives in you?.
local: Abam yǝri nɩ, Wɛ Joro kʋm na wʋ abam bɩcara nɩ tɩn, á nyɩ dɩ Wɛ tɩtɩ digǝ mʋ DƖ na zʋʋrɩ ka wʋnɩ na?.

eng: If someone destroys Gods temple, God will destroy that person, because Gods temple is holy, which is what you are.
local: Wʋlʋ nan na cɔgɩ Wɛ digǝ kam tɩn, Wɛ wʋ́ cɔgɩ kʋntʋ tu dɩ mʋ. Bɛŋwaanɩ Wɛ digǝ kam yɩ DƖ tɩtɩ di-laa mʋ. Abam tɩtɩ nan mʋ yɩ DƖ digǝ kam kʋntʋ.

eng: Dont fool yourself. If some of you think they are worldly-wise, then they should become foolish so that they can become wise.
local: Nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ yɩ zaŋɩ o ganɩ o tɩtɩ. Abam wʋlʋ na bʋŋɩ nɩ o jɩgɩ swan nɩ zɩm lʋgʋ baŋa swan na yɩ te tɩn, kʋntʋ tu maŋɩ sɩ o joori o ji joro mʋ, sɩ o laan wanɩ o na swan cɩga cɩga.

eng: This worlds wisdom is foolishness to God. As its written, He catches the wise in their cleverness. .
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ lʋgʋ baŋa swan yɩ jwǝrim mʋ Wɛ yigǝ nɩ. Kʋ pʋpʋnɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ kʋ wɩ: «Wɛ vugimi swan tiinǝ swan DƖ pa ya joori ya ja-ba.».

eng: And also, The Lord knows that the thoughts of the wise are silly .
local: Kʋ nan daa ta pʋpʋnɩ kʋ wɩ: «Yuutu Baŋa-Wɛ ye nɩ swan tiinǝ wʋbʋŋa maama yɩ kafɛ mʋ.».

eng: So then, no one should brag about human beings. Everything belongs to you.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ, nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ yɩ zaŋɩ o magɩ o nyɔɔnɩ o ta o wɩ: «Amʋ tɔgɩ wʋntʋ mʋ.» Sɩ woŋo maama yɩ abam mʋ te.

eng: Paul, Apollos, Cephas, the world, life, death, things in the present, things in the future—everything belongs to you.
local: Kʋ na yɩ amʋ Pooli dɩ Apolɔsɩ dɩ Pɩyɛɛrɩ, dɩbam dɩ yɩ abam nɔɔna mʋ. Kʋ nan na yɩ lʋgʋ baŋa dɩ ŋwɩa dɩ tʋʋnɩ dɩ zɩm dɩ jwa dɩ, abam mʋ te tɩ maama.

eng: but you belong to Christ, and Christ belongs to God.
local: Zezi Krisi nan mʋ te abam, yɩ Wɛ dɩ te Zezi Krisi tɩtɩ dɩ.

eng: So a person should think about us this way—as servants of Christ and managers of Gods secrets.
local: Kʋntʋ tɩn, abam maŋɩ sɩ á taá ye nɩ dɩbam yɩ Zezi Krisi tɩntʋŋna mʋ. Wɛ nan kwe DƖ cɩga kalʋ ya na sǝgi nabiinǝ yigǝ nɩ tɩn DƖ kɩ dɩbam jɩŋa nɩ mʋ, sɩ dɩ́ ta-ka dɩ́ brɩ nɔɔna.

eng: In this kind of situation, what is expected of a manager is that they prove to be faithful.
local: Ba nan na kwe woŋo ba kɩ nɔɔnʋ jɩŋa nɩ, kʋ maŋɩ sɩ o taa nii kʋ baŋa nɩ dɩ cɩga mʋ.

eng: I couldnt care less if Im judged by you or by any human court; I dont even judge myself.
local: Kʋ nan na yɩ amʋ, abam naa nabiinǝ balʋ maama na di amʋ taanɩ te dɩ te tɩn, kʋ ba kɩ amʋ kʋlʋkʋlʋ. Amʋ bɩcarɩ nɩ a ye nɩ, kʋlʋkʋlʋ tǝrǝ a na kɩ a cɔgɩ. Nan dɩ kʋntʋ dɩ, a bá wanɩ a pa a tɩtɩ bʋra, a na bá wanɩ a di a tɩtɩ taanɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Wʋlʋ yɩranɩ na wʋ́ wanɩ o di amʋ taanɩ tɩn mʋ yɩ dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm.

eng: So dont judge anything before the right time—wait until the Lord comes. He will bring things that are hidden in the dark to light, and he will make peoples motivations public. Then there will be recognition for each person from God.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ, nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ yɩ zaŋɩ o wuuri o di o doŋ taanɩ, kʋ loori maŋa kalʋ Wɛ na lɩ tɩn, sɩ dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm na tuǝ, oó ja pooni o ba o pa woŋo kʋlʋ maama na sǝgi lim wʋnɩ tɩn taa naɩ jaja. Wʋntʋ wʋ́ pa nɔɔna wʋbʋŋa yalʋ na sǝgi tɩn lwarɩ jaja. Maŋa kam kʋntʋ nɩ mʋ Wɛ wʋ́ pa nɔɔnʋ maama na zulǝ yalʋ na maŋɩ dɩd-o tɩn.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I have applied these things to myself and Apollos for your benefit. Ive done this so that you can learn what it means not to go beyond what has been written and so none of you will become arrogant by supporting one of us against the other.
local: A ko-biǝ-ba, kʋ yɩ abam ŋwaanɩ mʋ a kwe a tɩtɩ dɩdaanɩ Apolɔsɩ a ma maŋɩ a brɩ abam, sɩ á wanɩ á nii dɩbam, á ma lwarɩ á na wʋ́ taá kɩ te tɩn, sɩ á yɩ zaŋɩ á gaalɩ kʋlʋ na pʋpʋnɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ tɩn, sɩ abam dɩdʋa dɩdʋa yɩ zaŋɩ o kɩ kamunni o zuli nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ o daarɩ o gooni o doŋ.

eng: Who says that you are better than anyone else? What do you have that you didnt receive? And if you received it, then why are you bragging as if you didnt receive it?.
local: Wɔɔ mʋ wɩ, nmʋ dwe n doŋ? Bɛɛ mʋ n jɩga yɩ n wʋ joŋi-kʋ Wɛ tee nɩ? Nmʋ nan na joŋi-kʋ Wɛ tee nɩ mʋ tɩn, bɛɛ mʋ kɩa yɩ n magɩ n nyɔɔnɩ kʋ baŋa nɩ, nɩ nmʋ tɩtɩ mʋ mɛ n dam dɩ n swan n ma n na-kʋ te?.

eng: Youve been filled already! Youve become rich already! You rule like kings without us! I wish you did rule so that we could be kings with you!.
local: Abam bʋŋɩ nɩ á maŋɩ á jɩgɩ kʋlʋ maama á na lagɩ tɩn, á kwǝri á yɩ nadunǝ, yɩ á ta bʋŋɩ nɩ abam di paarɩ á daarɩ dɩbam. Kʋ ya lana sɩ á sɩɩnɩ á di paarɩ, sɩ dɩbam dɩ taá tɔgɩ dɩ́ di paarɩ dɩ abam.

eng: I suppose that God has shown that we apostles are at the end of the line. We are like prisoners sentenced to death, because we have become a spectacle in the world, both to angels and to humans.
local: Amʋ na bʋŋɩ te tɩn, kʋ nyɩ dɩ Wɛ pɛ dɩbam balʋ na yɩ DƖ tɩntʋŋna tɩn ji kwaga tiinǝ mʋ nɔɔna bam maama wʋnɩ, nɩnɛɛnɩ balʋ ba na di ba taanɩ ba lagɩ ba gʋ-ba te tɩn. Dɩbam nyɩ dɩ nɔn-mwaanʋ mʋ lʋgʋ kʋm nɔɔna bam dɩ malɛsɩdɩ yibiyǝ nɩ.

eng: We are fools for Christ, but you are wise through Christ! We are weak, but you are strong! You are honored, but we are dishonored!.
local: Dɩbam jigi jwǝǝru mʋ Zezi Krisi ŋwaanɩ, abam nan pa á yɩ swan tiinǝ mʋ Zezi Krisi tɔgɩm dɩm wʋnɩ. Dɩbam yɩ nabwǝnǝ mʋ, abam maa paɩ á yɩ dɩdɛɛra. Nɔɔna zuli abam, yɩ ba daarɩ ba gooni dɩbam.

eng: Up to this very moment we are hungry, thirsty, wearing rags, abused, and homeless.
local: Dɩ zɩm maama dɩ, kana dɩ na-nyɔm jɩgɩ dɩbam, yɩ gwaarʋ muri dɩbam. Nɔɔna maa bɛɛsɩ dɩbam, yɩ dɩ́ ba jɩgɩ cirigim jǝgǝ.

eng: We work hard with our own hands. When we are insulted, we respond with a blessing; when we are harassed, we put up with it.
local: Dɩbam maɩ dɩ́ tɩtɩ jɩa mʋ dɩ́ tiini dɩ́ tʋŋa, sɩ dɩ́ ma wanɩ dɩ́ na dɩ́ ni wʋdiu. Nɔɔna na twɩ dɩbam, dɩ́ ta yǝni dɩ́ loori Wɛ mʋ sɩ DƖ kɩ-ba lanyɩranɩ. Nɔɔna na bɛɛsɩ dɩbam, dɩ́ ta yǝni dɩ́ sɛ bɛɛsa yam mʋ.

eng: when our reputation is attacked, we are encouraging. We have become the scum of the earth, the waste that runs off everything, up to the present time.
local: Nɔɔna na ŋɔɔnɩ wo-lwaanʋ ba pa dɩbam, dɩ́ yǝni dɩ́ joori dɩ́ lǝri-ba lanyɩranɩ mʋ. Dɩbam nyɩ dɩ lʋgʋ baŋa weeru mʋ. Dɩ zɩm maama, dɩ́ nyɩ dɩ wo-yɔɔrʋ mʋ nɔɔnʋ maama tee nɩ.

eng: Im not writing these things to make you ashamed but to warn you, since you are my loved children.
local: A na pʋpʋnɩ tɔnɔ kʋntʋ tɩn, a pʋpʋnɩ sɩ a ma kwe abam mʋ, á na yɩ amʋ bu-sonnu tɩn ŋwaanɩ, sɩ kʋ daɩ nɩ a lagɩ sɩ a kɩ abam cavɩɩra mʋ.

eng: You may have ten thousand mentors in Christ, but you dont have many fathers. I gave birth to you in Christ Jesus through the gospel.
local: Abam ya na maŋɩ á jɩgɩ karanyɩna tiinǝ mʋrr-fugǝ mʋ ba na brɩ abam Zezi Krisi cwǝŋǝ kam wʋnɩ, á ko yɩ dɩdʋa yɩranɩ mʋ. Amʋ yɩ á ko, dɩ a na brɩ abam Wɛ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam yɩ á na ŋwɩ-dʋŋa Zezi Krisi ŋwaanɩ tɩn.

eng: so I encourage you to follow my example.
local: Mʋ kʋ kuri a loori abam sɩ á taá lwǝni amʋ na kɩ te tɩn.

eng: This is why Ive sent Timothy to you; hes my loved and trusted child in the Lord; hell remind you about my way of life in Christ Jesus. Hell teach the same way as I teach everywhere in every church.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ a tʋŋɩ Timoti á tee nɩ. Wʋntʋ mʋ yɩ a bu-sono Wɛ cwǝŋǝ kam wʋnɩ, yɩ o tɔgɩ dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi dɩ cɩga. Wʋntʋ wʋ́ guli abam amʋ ŋwɩa kam na yɩ te Zezi Krisi cwǝŋǝ kam wʋnɩ, sɩ kʋ maŋɩ nɩ amʋ na yǝni a brɩ Zezi kɔgɔ kʋm je maama nɩ te tɩn.

eng: Some have become arrogant as if Im not coming to see you.
local: Abam badaara paɩ á tɩtɩ kamunni mʋ, bɛŋwaanɩ á daa ba bʋŋɩ nɩ amʋ wʋ́ joori a ba abam te.

eng: But, if the Lord is willing, Ill come to you soon. Then I wont focus on what these arrogant people say, but Ill find out what power they possess.
local: Wɛ nan na sɛ, aá ba abam te lele. A na tuǝ, a laan wʋ́ wanɩ a lwarɩ kamun-nyɩna bam kʋntʋ na jɩgɩ dam dɩlʋ ba ma kɩ tɩn, yɩ kʋ daa daɩ ba ni-ŋwaŋa yɩranɩ má.

eng: Gods kingdom isnt about words but about power.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm paɩ dɩbam dam mʋ, sɩ dɩ́ wanɩ dɩ́ ma kɩ kʋlʋ na maŋɩ tɩn, sɩ kʋ daɩ ni-ŋwaŋa yɩranɩ má.

eng: Which do you want? Should I come to you with a big stick to punish you, or with love and a gentle spirit?.
local: Abam lagɩ sɩ a ba abam te a vaŋɩ á zwɛ mʋ naa á lagɩ sɩ a brɩ abam sono dɩ wʋ-bono mʋ?.

eng: Everyone has heard that there is sexual immorality among you. This is a type of immorality that isnt even heard of among the Gentiles—a man is having sex with his fathers wife!.
local: Amʋ nan ni abam labaarɩ nɩ nɔɔnʋ dɩdʋa mʋ karɩ o boorǝ. Ba wɩ, abam wʋnɩ baarʋ dɩdʋa mʋ tigi dɩ o ko kaanɩ. Balʋ na ba tɔgɩ Wɛ cwǝŋǝ tɩn dɩ maŋɩ ba ba sɛ ba kɩ wo-zɔɔna yantʋ doŋ.

eng: And youre proud of yourselves instead of being so upset that the one who did this thing is expelled from your community.
local: O na kɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, bɛɛ mʋ yɩ á taá paɩ á tɩtɩ kamunni? Kʋ ya maŋɩ sɩ á pa á wʋrʋ cɔgɩ kʋ ŋwaanɩ, sɩ á daarɩ á lɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na kɩ kǝm dɩm kʋntʋ doŋ tɩn á wʋnɩ.

eng: Though Im absent physically, Im present in the spirit and Ive already judged the man who did this as if I were present.
local: Amʋ tɩtɩ mʋ tǝri abam tee nɩ, sɩ a wʋbʋŋa maama wʋra dɩ abam. Kʋntʋ, a maŋɩ a van baarʋ wʋm kʋntʋ bʋra o cɔgɩm dɩm ŋwaanɩ, nɩ a wʋ abam tee nɩ mʋ te.

eng: When you meet together in the name of our Lord Jesus, Ill be present in spirit with the power of our Lord Jesus.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ a lagɩ a ta sɩ, á na jeeri daanɩ, amʋ wʋbʋŋa wʋra dɩ abam nɩ a wʋ á tɩtarɩ mʋ te, sɩ á ma dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi dam dɩm á ma kwe nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ á kɩ sʋtaanɩ jɩŋa nɩ, sɩ dɩntʋ wanɩ dɩ cɔgɩ o yɩra dɩ wo-zɔɔna fra yalʋ na jɩg-o tɩn. Á na kɩ kʋntʋ, o joro kʋm laan wʋ́ wanɩ kʋ na vrɩm dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi tum dɛ dɩm nɩ.

eng: Your bragging isnt good! Dont you know that a tiny grain of yeast makes a whole batch of dough rise?.
local: Abam na kɩ kamunni te tɩn, kʋ wʋ maŋɩ. Á ye nɩ dabɩlɩ fɩnfɩɩn na wʋ dɩpɛ muni dɩlʋ ba na viiri tɩn wʋnɩ, kʋ laan wʋ́ pa dɩ maama fʋlɩ mʋ.

eng: Clean out the old yeast so you can be a new batch of dough, given that youre supposed to be unleavened bread. Christ our Passover lamb has been sacrificed.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ, lɩ-na kǝm-lɔŋɔ kʋm kʋntʋ kʋ na nyɩ nɩ dabɩlɩ-doori te tɩn á wʋnɩ, sɩ kʋ yɩ loŋi á maama. Kʋntʋ á laan wʋ́ taá yɩ nɩnɛɛnɩ dɩpɛ muni dɩlʋ ba na wʋ kɩ dabɩlɩ dɩ wʋnɩ tɩn. Á nan maŋɩ á yɩ nɔn-ŋʋna mʋ Wɛ yigǝ nɩ, Baŋa-Wɛ Pǝlbu Zezi Krisi na kwe o tɩtɩ o ma kaanɩ Wɛ sɩ DƖ yagɩ á lwarɩm mʋ DƖ ma cɛ abam tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: so lets celebrate the feast with the unleavened bread of honesty and truth, not with old yeast or with the yeast of evil and wickedness.
local: Pa-na dɩ́ di candiǝ nɩ kʋ na maŋɩ te tɩn, sɩ dɩ́ lɩ lwarɩm dɩdaanɩ tɩtʋŋ-balwaarʋ tɩlʋ maama na nyɩ nɩ dabɩlɩ-doori te tɩn dɩ́ wʋnɩ, sɩ dɩ́ daarɩ dɩ́ taá jɩgɩ wʋ-poŋo dɩ cɩga, nɩ dɩpɛ dɩlʋ na ba jɩgɩ dabɩlɩ dɩ wʋnɩ te tɩn.

eng: I wrote to you in my earlier letter not to associate with sexually immoral people.
local: Amʋ dɛɛn na dɛ yigǝ a pʋpʋnɩ tɔnɔ kʋlʋ a pa abam tɩn, a tagɩ dɩ abam sɩ á yɩ taá tɔgɩ dɩdaanɩ balʋ na karɩ ba boori tɩn.

eng: But I wasnt talking about the sexually immoral people in the outside world by any means—or the greedy, or the swindlers, or people who worship false gods—otherwise, you would have to leave the world entirely!.
local: Amʋ na kaanɩ abam kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ daɩ nɩ balʋ na yǝri Wɛ yɩ ba boori tɩn, naa ba yɩ wo-swǝn-nyɩna naa ŋwɩɩna naa jwǝ-kaana tɩn taanɩ mʋ a tɛa. Abam bá wanɩ á pwɛ á tɩtɩ dɩ nɔɔna bam kʋntʋ kʋ na daɩ nɩ á yagɩ lʋgʋ baŋa.

eng: But now Im writing to you not to associate with anyone who calls themselves brother or sister who is sexually immoral, greedy, someone who worships false gods, an abusive person, a drunk, or a swindler. Dont even eat with anyone like this.
local: Amʋ nan na pʋpʋnɩ kʋlʋ tɩn kuri mʋ yɩ sɩ á yɩ taá tɔgɩ dɩ wʋlʋ maama na brɩ nɩ o yɩ á ko-bu Wɛ cwǝŋǝ wʋnɩ yɩ o boorǝ, naa o yɩ wo-swǝn-nyɩm naa jwǝ-kaanʋ naa nɔn-twɩɩnʋ naa sa-nyɔrʋ, naa o yɩ ŋwɩɩnʋ tɩn. Wʋlʋ na yɩ kʋntʋ doŋ tɩn, sɩ á pwɛ á tɩtɩ dɩd-o, sɩ á brɩ á yɩ di wʋdiu dɩd-o dɩ.

eng: What do I care about judging outsiders? Isnt it your job to judge insiders?.
local: Kʋ na yɩ balʋ na daɩ dɩ́ ko-biǝ Wɛ cwǝŋǝ wʋnɩ tɩn, kʋ daɩ amʋ wʋ́ di bantʋ taanɩ. Kʋ yɩ Wɛ mʋ wʋ́ di ba taanɩ. Abam nan maŋɩ sɩ á di balʋ na wʋ á kɔgɔ kʋm wʋnɩ tɩn taanɩ. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ kʋ pʋpʋnɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ kʋ wɩ: «Zǝli-na nɔn-balɔrɔ kʋm á wʋnɩ.».

eng: When someone in your assembly has a legal case against another member, do they dare to take it to court to be judged by people who arent just, instead of by Gods people?.
local: Abam wʋlʋ na jɩgɩ taanɩ dɩ o doŋ yɩ á yɩ ko-biǝ daanɩ Zezi ŋwaanɩ tɩn, kʋ wʋ maŋɩ sɩ n ja-o n vu sarɩya-dirǝ balʋ na yǝri Wɛ tɩn te, sɩ ba di abam taanɩ dɩm. Kʋʋ́ ta yɩ cavɩɩra mʋ kʋ pa-m, bɛŋwaanɩ kʋ ya maŋɩ sɩ Wɛ nɔɔna bam mʋ di á taanɩ dɩm.

eng: Or dont you know that Gods people will judge the world? If the world is to be judged by you, are you incompetent to judge trivial cases?.
local: Abam yǝri nɩ Wɛ nɔɔna bam mʋ wʋ́ ba ba di nabiinǝ maama taanɩ na? Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ, bɛɛ mʋ kɩa yɩ á tɩtɩ warɩ á kwɛ wo-balwa balʋ na wʋ á tɩtarɩ nɩ tɩn?.

eng: Dont you know that we will judge angels? Why not ordinary things?.
local: Abam yǝri nɩ dɩbam mʋ wʋ́ di malɛsɩsɩm taanɩ na? Kʋ nan na yɩ kʋntʋ, dɩ́ bá wanɩ zɩm lʋgʋ kʋntʋ taanɩ dɩ́ di na?.

eng: So then if you have ordinary lawsuits, do you appoint people as judges who arent respected by the church?.
local: Abam nan na jɩgɩ taanɩ dɩntʋ doŋ á tɩtarɩ nɩ, á wʋ maŋɩ sɩ á ja á vu balʋ na tɔgɩ ba tǝri Zezi nɔɔna bam wʋnɩ tɩn te.

eng: Im saying this because you should be ashamed of yourselves! Isnt there one person among you who is wise enough to pass judgment between believers?.
local: A tagɩ kʋntʋ sɩ cavɩɩra mʋ ja abam. Kʋntʋ, swan tu dɩ dɩdʋa tǝri abam wʋnɩ sɩ o wanɩ o fɔgɩ abam balʋ na yɩ ko-biǝ Zezi ŋwaanɩ tɩn daanɩ na?.

eng: But instead, does a brother or sister have a lawsuit against another brother or sister, and do they do this in front of unbelievers?.
local: Abam na kɩ te tɩn wʋ maŋɩ, abam na yǝni á saŋɩ daanɩ sarɩya-dirǝ balʋ na yǝri Wɛ tɩn tee nɩ yɩ ba di á taanɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: The fact that you have lawsuits against each other means that youve already lost your case. Why not be wronged instead? Why not be cheated?.
local: Abam na saŋɩ daanɩ tɩn, kʋ maŋɩ kʋ brɩ nɩ á tusi mʋ. N ko-bu na kɩ-m lwarɩm, naa o na ŋɔgɩ-m, kʋ ya lana sɩ n yagɩ n ma n cɛ-o mʋ.

eng: But instead you are doing wrong and cheating—and youre doing it to your own brothers and sisters.
local: Abam nan ba kɩ kʋntʋ, abam tɩtɩ joori á kɩ balʋ na yɩ á ko-biǝ Wɛ cwǝŋǝ wʋnɩ tɩn wo-lwaanʋ mʋ, yɩ á kwǝri á ŋɔnɩ-ba.

eng: Dont you know that people who are unjust wont inherit Gods kingdom? Dont be deceived. Those who are sexually immoral, those who worship false gods, adulterers, both participants in same-sex intercourse.
local: Abam yǝri nɩ balʋ na ba tɔgɩ cɩga tɩn bá na jǝŋǝ je Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋnɩ na? Yɩ zaŋɩ-na á ganɩ á tɩtɩ. Balʋ na boori tɩn, naa jwǝ-kaana naa ka-lagɩna, naa baara balʋ na tigi dɩ ba donnǝ ba maa jiri kaana tɩn.

eng: thieves, the greedy, drunks, abusive people, and swindlers wont inherit Gods kingdom.
local: naa ŋwɩɩna naa wo-swǝn-nyɩna, naa sa-nyɔra naa nɔn-twɩɩna naa vɩnvɩrna bá na jǝŋǝ je Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋnɩ.

eng: That is what some of you used to be! But you were washed clean, you were made holy to God, and you were made right with God in the name of the Lord Jesus Christ and in the Spirit of our God.
local: Abam badaara dɛɛn yɩ kʋntʋ doŋ mʋ. Wɛ nan yagɩ á lwarɩm DƖ ma cɛ abam. DƖ ma pa á ji DƖ nɔɔna yɩ á jɩgɩ cɩga DƖ tee nɩ. Wɛ dɛ dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi dɩ DƖ Jorokʋm dam ŋwaanɩ mʋ DƖ kɩ kʋntʋ DƖ pa abam.

eng: I have the freedom to do anything, but not everything is helpful. I have the freedom to do anything, but I wont be controlled by anything.
local: Abam badaara tɛ á wɩ, á jɩgɩ ni sɩ á kɩ kʋlʋ maama á na lagɩ tɩn. Amʋ nan lagɩ a ta abam nɩ, kʋ daɩ woŋo maama mʋ jɩgɩ zǝnǝ kʋ pa abam. Amʋ dɩ jɩgɩ ni sɩ a kɩ kʋlʋ maama a na lagɩ tɩn, a nan bá sɛ sɩ a pa a ji kʋlʋkʋlʋ gambaa.

eng: Food is for the stomach and the stomach is for food, and yet God will do away with both. The body isnt for sexual immorality but for the Lord, and the Lord is for the body.
local: Abam yǝni á tɛ á wɩ, Wɛ kɩ wʋdiu kana ŋwaanɩ mʋ, yɩ DƖ kɩ kana dɩ wʋdiu ŋwaanɩ. Maŋa nan wʋ́ ba, yɩ Wɛ cɔgɩ wʋdiu dɩ kana maama. Wɛ nan wʋ kɩ dɩbam yɩra yam sɩ dɩ́ ta maa kɩ boorim. Wɛ mɔɔnɩ dɩ́ yɩra yam sɩ dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi mʋ taa te-ya, sɩ dɩ́ ta maa tʋŋɩ dɩ́ pa-o.

eng: God has raised the Lord and will raise us through his power.
local: Wɛ mʋ bi dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi DƖ pa-o o yagɩ tʋʋnɩ. Maŋa nan wʋ́ ba, yɩ DƖ ma DƖ dam dɩm DƖ bi dɩbam dɩ DƖ pa dɩ́ yagɩ tʋʋnɩ.

eng: Dont you know that your bodies are parts of Christ? So then, should I take parts of Christ and make them a part of someone who is sleeping around? No way!.
local: Abam yǝri nɩ á yɩra yam yɩ Zezi Krisi yɩra yam daa dɩdʋa mʋ na? Abam yɩra yam dɩ Zezi yɩra yam na yɩ bɩdwɩ tɩn, á yɩ zaŋɩ á kwe-ya á ma pǝni dɩ ka-tʋla, sɩ yaá ji yɩra dɩdʋa dɩdaanɩ ka-tʋla kam yɩra yam. Á brɩ á yɩ zaŋɩ á kɩ kʋntʋ.

eng: Dont you know that anyone who is joined to someone who is sleeping around is one body with that person? The scripture says, The two will become one flesh .
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ, wʋlʋ na pǝni dɩ ka-tʋla tɩn, o dɩ ka-tʋla kam maama laan jigi yɩra dɩdʋa mʋ. Á yǝri kʋntʋ na? Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm tagɩ kʋ wɩ: «Baarʋ dɩ kaanɩ wʋ́ wǝli daanɩ sɩ ba ji nɔɔnʋ dɩdʋa mʋ.».

eng: The one who is joined to the Lord is one spirit with him.
local: Wʋlʋ nan na ŋwɩ dɩ dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi tɩn, o joro dɩ Zezi Joro maama yɩ woŋo dɩdʋa mʋ.

eng: Avoid sexual immorality! Every sin that a person can do is committed outside the body, except those who engage in sexual immorality commit sin against their own bodies.
local: Á nan yagɩ boorim kǝm dwi maama. Kǝm-balwaarʋ tɩlʋ maama nɔɔnʋ na wʋ́ kɩ tɩn, o bá kɩ o yagɩ o yɩra wʋnɩ. Nɔɔnʋ nan na boori, kʋntʋ yɩ kǝm-balɔrɔ kʋlʋ o na kɩ o yagɩ o yɩra wʋnɩ tɩn mʋ.

eng: Or dont you know that your body is a temple of the Holy Spirit who is in you? Dont you know that you have the Holy Spirit from God, and you dont belong to yourselves?.
local: Abam yǝri nɩ á yɩra yam nyɩ dɩ Wɛ tɩtɩ digǝ mʋ DƖ Jorokʋm na zʋʋrɩ ka wʋnɩ na? Wɛ kɩ DƖ Joro kʋm á bɩcara nɩ mʋ. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ kʋ daa daɩ abam tɩtɩ mʋ te á yɩra yam.

eng: You have been bought and paid for, so honor God with your body.
local: Á yɩ Wɛ nɔɔna mʋ. DƖ yǝgi abam dɩ yǝlo kʋlʋ na cana tɩn mʋ. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ, kwe-na á yɩra yam á maa tʋŋɩ sɩ kʋ pa Wɛ na zulǝ.

eng: Now, about what you wrote: Its good for a man not to have sex with a woman.
local: A lagɩ a lǝri á na pʋpʋnɩ á bwe amʋ taana yalʋ tɩn mʋ a pa abam. Kʋ ya lana dɩ baarʋ na wʋ di kaanɩ.

eng: Each man should have his own wife, and each woman should have her own husband because of sexual immorality.
local: Kʋ nan ba lana sɩ baara dɩ kaana taa lagɩ daanɩ yɔɔ yɔɔ. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ, kʋ maŋɩ sɩ baarʋ maama taa jɩgɩ o tɩtɩ kaanɩ, sɩ kaanɩ maama dɩ taa jɩgɩ o tɩtɩ barʋ.

eng: The husband should meet his wifes sexual needs, and the wife should do the same for her husband.
local: Baarʋ yɩ zaŋɩ o vɩn sɩ o taa wʋra dɩ o kaanɩ. Kaanɩ dɩ nan yɩ zaŋɩ o vɩn sɩ o taa wʋra dɩ o barʋ.

eng: The wife doesnt have authority over her own body, but the husband does. Likewise, the husband doesnt have authority over his own body, but the wife does.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ kaanɩ ba te o tɩtɩ yɩra, kʋ yɩ o barʋ mʋ te-ya. Barʋ dɩ nan ba te o tɩtɩ yɩra, kʋ yɩ o kaanɩ dɩ mʋ te-ya.

eng: Dont refuse to meet each others needs unless you both agree for a short period of time to devote yourselves to prayer. Then come back together again so that Satan might not tempt you because of your lack of self-control.
local: Yɩ zaŋɩ-na á vɩn sɩ á taá wʋra daanɩ, kʋ na daɩ nɩ á maama mʋ sɛ sɩ á lɩ pwǝlǝ á yagɩ da-pwǝgǝ maŋa fɩnfɩɩn, sɩ á wanɩ á nywanɩ á yigǝ á loori Wɛ, sɩ kʋ taa ve maŋa kalʋ á na lagɩ tɩn. Kʋntʋ kwaga nɩ, sɩ á joori á taá wʋra daanɩ. Kʋ na daɩ kʋntʋ, sʋtaanɩ wʋ́ sʋgɩ abam dɩ pa á tusi dɩ á na warɩ á tɩtɩ á ja tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: Im saying this to give you permission; its not a command.
local: Amʋ na pʋpʋnɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, a tagɩ sɩ kʋ wǝli abam mʋ, sɩ kʋ daɩ nɩ a lagɩ a fɩn abam.

eng: I wish all people were like me, but each has a particular gift from God: one has this gift, and another has that one.
local: Amʋ ya wʋ́ ta lagɩ sɩ nɔɔnʋ maama taa yɩ nɩ amʋ na yɩ badǝm te tɩn. Wɛ-pɛɛra nan yɩ tǝri tǝri mʋ yɩ DƖ pa nɔɔnʋ maama o nyɩm.

eng: Im telling those who are single and widows that its good for them to stay single like me.
local: Kʋ daarɩ abam balʋ na yɩ badǝnǝ dɩ kadǝnǝ tɩn, a lagɩ a ta dɩ abam nɩ kʋ lana sɩ á taá yɩ á yɩranɩ kʋntʋ, nɩ amʋ na yɩ te tɩn.

eng: But if they cant control themselves, they should get married, because its better to marry than to burn with passion.
local: Á nan na warɩ á tɩtɩ á ja, kʋ maŋɩ sɩ á di kaana naa á zʋ banna mʋ. Kʋntʋ garɩ á na paɩ ka-laga fra tiini ya jɩgɩ abam.

eng: Im passing on the Lords command to those who are married: A wife shouldnt leave her husband.
local: Kʋ daarɩ kʋ na yɩ banna dɩ ba kaana, kʋ yɩ fɩfɩʋn mʋ sɩ kaanɩ yɩ yagɩ o barʋ, naa barʋ dɩ yɩ vɩn o kaanɩ. Kʋntʋ daɩ amʋ tɩtɩ ni, kʋ yɩ dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi mʋ pɛ culu kʋntʋ. Kaanɩ nan na maŋɩ o yagɩ o barʋ, o maŋɩ sɩ o taa wʋra o yɩranɩ mʋ, naa o joori o vu o barʋ wʋm te mʋ sɩ ba fɔgɩ daanɩ, o daa wʋ maŋɩ sɩ o zʋ barʋ wʋdoŋ.

eng: Im telling everyone else : If a believer has a wife who doesnt believe, and she agrees to live with him, then he shouldnt divorce her.
local: Kʋ daarɩ, baarʋ wʋlʋ na tɔgɩ Zezi tɩn na jɩgɩ kaanɩ, yɩ kaanɩ wʋm na ba tɔgɩ Zezi yɩ o sɛ sɩ ba taa zʋʋrɩ daanɩ, kʋntʋ tu yɩ zaŋɩ o vɩn o kaanɩ wʋm. Kʋ yɩ amʋ mʋ pɛ ni dɩm kʋntʋ sɩ kʋ daɩ dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi.

eng: If a woman has a husband who doesnt believe and he agrees to live with her, then she shouldnt divorce him.
local: Kaanɩ wʋlʋ dɩ nan na tɔgɩ Zezi yɩ o barʋ na ba tɔgɩ Zezi, yɩ o barʋ wʋm na sɛ sɩ ba taa zʋʋrɩ daanɩ, sɩ o yɩ zaŋɩ o yagɩ o barʋ wʋm.

eng: The husband who doesnt believe belongs to God because of his wife, and the wife who doesnt believe belongs to God because of her husband. Otherwise, your children would be contaminated by the world, but now they are spiritually set apart.
local: Barʋ wʋlʋ na wʋ kɩ o wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Zezi tɩn batwarɩ dɩ Wɛ, o kaanɩ wʋm na kɩ o wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Zezi tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Kaanɩ wʋlʋ dɩ na wʋ kɩ o wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Zezi tɩn batwarɩ dɩ Wɛ, o barʋ wʋm na kɩ o wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Zezi tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Kʋ ya na daɩ kʋntʋ, ba biǝ wʋ́ ta yɩ nɩ balʋ na yǝri Wɛ tɩn biǝ, yɩ ba laan yagɩ ba batwarɩ dɩ Wɛ.

eng: But if a spouse who doesnt believe chooses to leave, then let them leave. The brother or sister isnt tied down in these circumstances. God has called you to peace.
local: Wʋlʋ nan na ba tɔgɩ Zezi tɩn na bʋŋɩ sɩ o viiri, kʋ na yɩ kaanɩ wʋm naa barʋ wʋm dɩ, sɩ á yag-o sɩ o viiri. Wʋlʋ na daarɩ yɩ o tɔgɩ Zezi tɩn laan wʋ́ ta te o tɩtɩ. Wɛ na bǝŋi dɩbam sɩ dɩ́ taá tɔgɩ-DƖ tɩn, kʋ yɩ sɩ dɩ́ taá zʋʋrɩ daanɩ dɩ ywǝǝni mʋ.

eng: How do you know as a wife if you will save your husband? Or how do you know as a husband if you will save your wife?.
local: Kaanɩ, nmʋ na tɔgɩ Zezi tɩn, nń kɩ ta mʋ n lwarɩ nɩ nń wanɩ n pa n barʋ wʋm sɛ Zezi naa n bá wanɩ n pa o sɛ? Barʋ, nmʋ dɩ wʋ́ kɩ ta mʋ n lwarɩ nɩ nń wanɩ n pa n kaanɩ wʋm sɛ Zezi naa n bá wanɩ n pa o sɛ?.

eng: Nevertheless, each person should live the kind of life that the Lord assigned when he called each one. This is what I teach in all the churches.
local: Abam dɛɛn na yɩ te yɩ Wɛ bǝŋi abam sɩ á taá tɔgɩ-DƖ tɩn, á ta taá yɩ kʋntʋ, sɩ kʋ maŋɩ dɩ dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm na pɛ abam ŋwɩa kalʋ tɩn. Mʋ kʋlʋ a na brɩ Zezi kɔgɔ kʋm jǝgǝ maama nɩ tɩn.

eng: If someone was circumcised when called, he shouldnt try to reverse it. If someone wasnt circumcised when he was called, he shouldnt be circumcised.
local: Wɛ na bǝŋi abam tɩn, abam wʋlʋ dɛɛn na maŋɩ o go nɩ Zwifǝ bam cullu tɩm na brɩ te tɩn, kʋntʋ tu daa yɩ ta sǝgi o tɩtɩ o na goni tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Kʋ daarɩ, wʋlʋ dɛɛn nan na maŋɩ o wʋ goni tɩn, o daa yɩ ta kwaanɩ sɩ o go.

eng: Circumcision is nothing; not being circumcised is nothing. What matters is keeping Gods commandments.
local: N na gonǝ naa n zɩ wʋ goni dɩ, kʋ daɩ kʋlʋkʋlʋ. Woŋo kʋlʋ na jɩgɩ kuri tɩn mʋ yɩ sɩ nɔɔnʋ maama kwaanɩ o sɛ Wɛ niǝ yam lanyɩranɩ.

eng: Each person should stay in the situation they were in when they were called.
local: Nɔɔnʋ maama dɛɛn na yɩ te yɩ Wɛ bǝŋ-o tɩn, kʋ maŋɩ sɩ o ta taa yɩ kʋntʋ mʋ.

eng: If you were a slave when you were called, dont let it bother you. But if you are actually able to be free, take advantage of the opportunity.
local: N dɛɛn na yɩ gambaa mʋ yɩ Wɛ bǝŋi-m sɩ n ta n tɔgɩ-DƖ, yɩ ta n kɩ liǝ kʋ ŋwaanɩ. N nan na nɛ cwǝŋǝ sɩ n ta n te n tɩtɩ, sɩ n sɛ sɩ kʋ ji kʋntʋ.

eng: Anyone who was a slave when they were called by the Lord has the status of being the Lords free person. In the same way, anyone who was a free person when they were called is Christs slave.
local: Wʋlʋ maama dɛɛn na yɩ nɔɔnʋ gambaa yɩ Wɛ bǝŋ-o sɩ o taa tɔgɩ dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi tɩn, kʋntʋ tu daa daɩ sʋtaanɩgambaa, dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm na vrɩ-o tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Wʋlʋ dɩ dɛɛn na te o tɩtɩ yɩ Wɛ bǝŋ-o sɩ o taa tɔgɩ-DƖ tɩn, kʋntʋ tu laan sɛ Zezi Krisi ni, nɩ o yɩ o gambaa mʋ te.

eng: You were bought and paid for. Dont become slaves of people.
local: Á yɩ Wɛ nɔɔna mʋ. DƖ yǝgi abam dɩ yǝlo kʋlʋ na cana tɩn mʋ. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ, yɩ zaŋɩ á sɛ sɩ á ji nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ gambaa.

eng: So then, brothers and sisters, each of you should stay with God in the situation you were in when you were called.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ, a ko-biǝ-ba, abam maama dɛɛn na yɩ te yɩ Wɛ bǝŋi abam sɩ á taá tɔgɩ-DƖ tɩn, á ta taá yɩ kʋntʋ DƖ yigǝ nɩ.

eng: I dont have a command from the Lord about people who have never been married, but Ill give you my opinion as someone you can trust because of the Lords mercy.
local: Kʋ daarɩ, a laan lagɩ a ŋɔɔnɩ balʋ na wʋ zʋ banna dɩ balʋ na wʋ di kaana tɩn woŋo mʋ. Kʋ daɩ dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi mʋ pɛ-nɩ ni dɩntʋ, kʋ yɩ amʋ tɩtɩ na bʋŋɩ te tɩn mʋ, yɩ á wʋ́ taá jɩgɩ amʋ cɩga, dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm na wʋra dɩ amʋ tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: So I think this advice is good because of the present crisis: Stay as you are.
local: Amʋ bʋŋɩ nɩ, yaara yalʋ na wʋ dɩbam baŋa nɩ lele kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋʋ́ ta lamma sɩ nɔɔnʋ yɩ zaŋɩ o di kaanɩ.

eng: If you are married, dont get a divorce. If you are divorced, dont try to find a spouse.
local: Kʋntʋ tɩn, wʋlʋ na maŋɩ o jɩgɩ kaanɩ, o daa yɩ zaŋɩ o yag-o. Wʋlʋ nan na ba jɩgɩ kaanɩ, o daa yɩ zaŋɩ o taa kwaanɩ sɩ o di kaanɩ.

eng: But if you do marry, you havent sinned; and if someone who hasnt been married gets married, they havent sinned. But married people will have a hard time, and Im trying to spare you that.
local: Kʋ daɩ nɩ, n na di kaanɩ, n ga n kɩ wo-lɔŋɔ. Kʋ nan daɩ nɩ, bukɔ na zʋ barʋ, o kɩ wo-lɔŋɔ. Banna dɩ ba kaana nan bá lu lʋgʋ baŋa yaara yam wʋnɩ. Kʋntʋ mʋ a ba lagɩ sɩ abam dɩ tɔgɩ á na yaara yam kʋntʋ doŋ.

eng: This is what Im saying, brothers and sisters: The time has drawn short. From now on, those who have wives should be like people who dont have them.
local: A ko-biǝ-ba, woŋo kʋlʋ na pɛ a ta kʋntʋ tɩn mʋ yɩ sɩ maŋa kam daa mʋ ba tiini ka daga, sɩ dɩ́ maa tʋŋɩ Wɛ tɩtʋŋa yam. Kʋ na zɩgɩ lele kʋntʋ sɩ kʋ ta maa ve tɩn, kʋ maŋɩ sɩ balʋ na jɩgɩ kaana tɩn taa tʋŋɩ Wɛ tɩtʋŋa yam nɩ ba wʋ di kaana mʋ te.

eng: Those who are sad should be like people who arent crying. Those who are happy should be like people who arent happy. Those who buy something should be like people who dont have possessions.
local: Kʋ maŋɩ sɩ balʋ wʋrʋ na cɔgɩ tɩn taa tʋŋɩ Wɛ tɩtʋŋa yam nɩ ba wʋrʋ wʋ cɔgɩ mʋ te, sɩ balʋ wʋrʋ na poli tɩn taa tʋŋɩ Wɛ tɩtʋŋa yam nɩ ba wʋrʋ wʋ poli mʋ te, sɩ balʋ na yǝgi wǝǝnu tɩn taa tʋŋɩ Wɛ tɩtʋŋa yam nɩ ba ba te wǝǝnu tɩm kʋntʋ mʋ te.

eng: Those who use the world should be like people who arent preoccupied with it, because this world in its present form is passing away.
local: Kʋ daarɩ sɩ balʋ na tʋŋɩ lʋgʋ baŋa tɩtʋŋa tɩn taa tʋŋɩ Wɛ tɩtʋŋa yam nɩ ba ba jɩgɩ lʋgʋ baŋa tɩtʋŋa mʋ te. Bɛŋwaanɩ lʋgʋ kʋm lagɩ kʋ ti mʋ dɩ kʋ wǝǝnu tɩm maama.

eng: I want you to be free from concerns. A man who isnt married is concerned about the Lords concerns—how he can please the Lord.
local: Amʋ ba lagɩ sɩ kʋlʋkʋlʋ liǝ taa jɩgɩ abam. Wʋlʋ na ba jɩgɩ kaanɩ tɩn yǝni o kwe o wʋbʋŋa maama o pa dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi tɩtʋŋa yam yɩranɩ má ŋwaanɩ mʋ, yɩ o kwaanɩ sɩ o taa tʋŋɩ o poli o wʋ.

eng: But a married man is concerned about the worlds concerns—how he can please his wife.
local: Wʋlʋ nan na jɩgɩ kaanɩ tɩn yǝni o kwe o wʋbʋŋa o kɩ lʋgʋ baŋa wǝǝnu wʋnɩ mʋ, yɩ o kwaanɩ sɩ o taa tʋŋɩ o poli o kaanɩ wʋ.

eng: His attention is divided. A woman who isnt married or who is a virgin is concerned about the Lords concerns so that she can be dedicated to God in both body and spirit. But a married woman is concerned about the worlds concerns—how she can please her husband.
local: Kʋ ma brɩ nɩ o wʋbʋŋa yam cɛ kuni bɩle mʋ. Kʋ yɩ bɩdwɩ mʋ dɩ bukɔ naa kaanɩ wʋlʋ na ba jɩgɩ barʋ tɩn dɩ. Wʋntʋ kwe o wʋbʋŋa maama mʋ o pa dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi tɩtʋŋa yam ŋwaanɩ, o na lagɩ sɩ o yɩra dɩ o joro maama taa yɩ Wɛ nyɩm tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Kʋ daarɩ kaanɩ wʋlʋ na jɩgɩ barʋ tɩn kwe o wʋbʋŋa o kɩ lʋgʋ baŋa wǝǝnu wʋnɩ mʋ, yɩ o kwaanɩ sɩ o taa tʋŋɩ o poli o barʋ wʋ.

eng: Im saying this for your own advantage. Its not to restrict you but rather to promote effective and consistent service to the Lord without distraction.
local: Amʋ nan tɛ bɩtarɩ sɩntʋ sɩ kʋ wǝli abam mʋ. Kʋ daɩ nɩ a lagɩ sɩ a fɩn abam. A nan lagɩ sɩ a pa á taá tɔgɩ cwǝ-laa mʋ lanyɩranɩ, sɩ á nywanɩ á yigǝ á taá tʋŋɩ á pa dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi, sɩ á yɩ taá jɩgɩ wʋbʋŋa yale yale.

eng: If someone thinks he is acting inappropriately toward an unmarried woman whom he knows, and if he has strong feelings and it seems like the right thing to do, he should do what he wants—hes not sinning—they should get married.
local: Kʋ daarɩ nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na bʋŋɩ nɩ o wʋ kɩ o katɔgɔ lanyɩranɩ o na wʋ di-o tɩn ŋwaanɩ, yɩ o fra na tiini ya zʋ o katɔgɔ kʋm kʋ pa o daa warɩ o tɩtɩ o ja, kʋ maŋɩ sɩ o di-o mʋ nɩ o na bʋŋɩ te tɩn. O na kɩ kʋntʋ, o wʋ kɩ wo-lɔŋɔ.

eng: But if a man stands firm in his decision, and doesnt feel the pressure, but has his own will under control, he does right if he decides in his own heart not to marry the woman.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ nan na lɩ wʋbʋŋa sɩ o yɩ di o katɔgɔ kʋm yɩ o waɩ o tɩtɩ o jaana, sɩ o daa yɩ di kaanɩ tɩn, o kɩ lanyɩranɩ mʋ.

eng: Therefore, the one who marries the unmarried woman does right, and the one who doesnt get married will do even better.
local: Kʋntʋ tɩn, wʋlʋ na di kaanɩ tɩn kɩ lanyɩranɩ. Wʋlʋ nan na wʋ di kaanɩ tɩn mʋ tiini o kɩ lanyɩranɩ o dwǝni.

eng: A woman is obligated to stay in her marriage as long as her husband is alive. But if her husband dies, she is free to marry whomever she wants, only it should be a believer in the Lord.
local: Kaanɩ na jɩgɩ barʋ, yɩ o barʋ wʋm ta na ŋwɩ, o ba jɩgɩ cwǝŋǝ sɩ o yag-o. O barʋ wʋm nan na tɩga, o laan jɩgɩ cwǝŋǝ sɩ o zʋ o na lagɩ wʋlʋ tɩn. O nan maŋɩ sɩ o zʋ wʋlʋ dɩ na sɛ dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi tɩn mʋ.

eng: But in my opinion, she will be happier if she stays the way she is. And I think that I have Gods Spirit too.
local: Amʋ nan bʋŋɩ nɩ o na je o yɩranɩ, oó ta jɩgɩ wʋpolo zanzan kʋ dwe o daa na zʋ barʋ. Wɛ Jorokʋm dɩ wʋ amʋ tee nɩ, yɩ a bʋŋɩ nɩ kʋntʋ mʋ pɛ-nɩ wʋbʋŋa yam kʋntʋ.

eng: Now concerning meat that has been sacrificed to a false god: We know that we all have knowledge. Knowledge makes people arrogant, but love builds people up.
local: A laan lagɩ a ŋɔɔnɩ jwǝnǝ nwana taanɩ mʋ dɩ abam. Kʋ yɩ cɩga, dɩ́ maama jɩgɩ yǝno, nɩ abam na tɛ te tɩn. Nan taá ye-na nɩ, nɔɔnʋ na jɩgɩ yǝno, kʋ pa-o kamunni mʋ. Nɔɔnʋ nan na jɩgɩ sono, kʋ jɩgɩ zǝnǝ kʋ paɩ o ve yigǝ mʋ.

eng: If anyone thinks they know something, they dont yet know as much as they should know.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na bʋŋɩ nɩ o jɩgɩ yǝno, kʋ brɩ nɩ o ta yǝri kʋlʋ na maŋɩ sɩ o taa ye tɩn mʋ.

eng: But if someone loves God, then they are known by God.
local: Wʋlʋ nan na soe Wɛ tɩn, Wɛ ye kʋntʋ tu.

eng: So concerning the actual food involved in these sacrifices to false gods, we know that a false god isnt anything in this world, and that there is no God except for the one God.
local: Kʋ na yɩ jwǝnǝ nwana yam dim taanɩ, dɩ́ ye nɩ jwǝnǝ ba ŋwɩ, Baŋa-Wɛ dɩdʋa yɩranɩ mʋ wʋra.

eng: Granted, there are so-called gods, in heaven and on the earth, as there are many gods and many lords.
local: Balʋ na ba tɔgɩ Wɛ tɩn jɩgɩ wǝǝnu tɩdonnǝ mʋ ba maa bǝ ba wa. Wǝǝnu dwi zanzan mʋ nɔɔna bǝ-tɩ nɩ ba wa dɩ ba yuu tiinǝ, kʋ na yɩ tɩga baŋa wǝǝnu dɩ wɛyuu wǝǝnu.

eng: However, for us believers, There is one God the Father. All things come from him, and we belong to him. And there is one Lord Jesus Christ. All things exist through him, and we live through him.
local: Kʋ nan na yɩ dɩbam, dɩ́ ye nɩ Wɛ dɩdʋa yɩranɩ mʋ wʋra. Dɩntʋ mʋ yɩ dɩbam Ko wʋlʋ na kɩ woŋo maama, yɩ dɩntʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ dɩ́ ŋwɩ. Dɩ́ ta ye nɩ, dɩbam Yuutu yɩ dɩdʋa mʋ, wʋntʋ mʋ yɩ Zezi Krisi wʋlʋ Wɛ na tagɩ o ŋwaanɩ DƖ kɩ woŋo maama tɩn, yɩ wʋntʋ mʋ paɩ dɩbam ŋwɩa.

eng: But not everybody knows this. Some are eating this food as though it really is food sacrificed to a real idol, because they were used to idol worship until now. Their conscience is weak because it has been damaged.
local: Kʋ nan daɩ nɔɔnʋ maama mʋ ye nɩ jwǝnǝ ba jɩgɩ dam. Nɔɔna badaara dɛɛn ŋwɩ dɩ jwǝnǝ kaanɩm mʋ, yɩ ba laan ba ba sɛ Wɛ. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ ba kwarɩ fʋʋnɩ yɩ ba bʋŋɩ nɩ, ba na di jwǝni wʋdiu, kʋʋ́ ta nyɩ nɩnɛɛnɩ ba joori ba sɛ jwǝnǝ kaanɩm mʋ. Ba wʋbʋŋa ta na wʋ bɩgɩ Wɛ cwǝŋǝ wʋnɩ tɩn, ba bʋŋɩ nɩ ba kɩ ba cɔgɩ mʋ.

eng: Food wont bring us close to God. Were not missing out if we dont eat, and we dont have any advantage if we do eat.
local: Wʋdiu dim nan bá wanɩ kʋ pa dɩ́ twɛ Wɛ. Dɩ́ na culi wʋdiiru naa dɩ́ zɩ ba culǝ dɩ, kʋ ba zǝni dɩbam dɩ kʋlʋkʋlʋ naa dɩ́ ga kʋlʋkʋlʋ Wɛ yigǝ nɩ kʋ ŋwaanɩ.

eng: But watch out or else this freedom of yours might be a problem for those who are weak.
local: Kʋ daarɩ abam balʋ na ye nɩ á jɩgɩ ni sɩ á di kʋlʋ maama á na lagɩ tɩn, á nan cʋ-na á tɩtɩ sɩ á yɩ pa balʋ wʋbʋŋa na wʋ bɩgɩ Wɛ cwǝŋǝ kam wʋnɩ tɩn tusi.

eng: Suppose someone sees you eating in an idols temple. Wont the person with a weak conscience be encouraged to eat the meat sacrificed to false gods?.
local: Nmʋ wʋlʋ na paɩ n jɩgɩ yǝno tɩn na ve n zʋ jwǝni digǝ n tɔgɩ n di dɩ wʋdiu kʋm, yɩ n ko-bu wʋlʋ wʋbʋŋa na wʋ bɩgɩ Wɛ cwǝŋǝ wʋnɩ tɩn na nɛ-m, kʋntʋ wʋ́ wanɩ kʋ gan-o sɩ o dɩ vu o tɔgɩ o di.

eng: The weak brother or sister for whom Christ died is destroyed by your knowledge.
local: Kʋntʋ, o na dɛ o di jwǝni wʋdiu kʋm, kʋ brɩ nɩ nmʋ mʋ mɛ n yǝno kʋm n ma n cɔgɩ n ko-bu wʋlʋ wʋbʋŋa ta na wʋ bɩgɩ Wɛ cwǝŋǝ wʋnɩ yɩ Zezi Krisi ya tɩ o dɩ ŋwaanɩ tɩn.

eng: You sin against Christ if you sin against your brothers and sisters and hurt their weak consciences this way.
local: Nmʋ na kɩ kǝm-balɔrɔ kʋm kʋntʋ doŋ n yagɩ n ko-bu yuu nɩ, yɩ n cɔgɩ o wʋbʋŋa o ta na wʋ bɩgɩ Wɛ cwǝŋǝ kam wʋnɩ tɩn, kʋ brɩ nɩ n kɩ dɩ Zezi Krisi mʋ te.

eng: This is why, if food causes the downfall of my brother or sister, I wont eat meat ever again, or else I may cause my brother or sister to fall.
local: Kʋntʋ, wʋdiu dim na lagɩ kʋ pa a ko-bu tusi Wɛ cwǝŋǝ nɩ, a daa bá fɔgɩ a di wʋdiu kʋm kʋntʋ, a na ba lagɩ sɩ a pa a ko-bu tusi Wɛ cwǝŋǝ kam wʋnɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: Am I not free? Am I not an apostle? Havent I seen Jesus our Lord? Arent you my work in the Lord?.
local: Amʋ ba jɩgɩ cwǝŋǝ sɩ a kɩ a na lagɩ kʋlʋ tɩn na? Amʋ daɩ Zezi Krisi tɩntʋŋnʋ na? Abam bʋŋɩ nɩ a wʋ nɛ dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi Krisi mʋ na? Abam mʋ yɩ tɩtʋŋa yalʋ a na tʋŋɩ a pa dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi tɩn, á na dɛ amʋ jɩŋa á na vrɩm tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: If Im not an apostle to others, at least I am to you! You are the seal that shows Im an apostle.
local: Nɔɔna badaara na maŋɩ ba ba bʋŋɩ nɩ amʋ yɩ Zezi Krisi tɩntʋŋnʋ cɩga cɩga, abam bá wanɩ á vɩn nɩ amʋ daɩ Zezi Krisi tɩntʋŋnʋ. Abam na ŋwɩ dɩdaanɩ dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi te tɩn mʋ brɩ nɩ amʋ sɩɩnɩ a yɩ o tɩntʋŋnʋ mʋ.

eng: This is my defense against those who criticize me.
local: Nɔɔna na lwɛ amʋ, a na wʋ́ lǝri-ba te tɩn mʋ tɩntʋ:.

eng: Dont we have the right to eat and drink?.
local: Abam bʋŋɩ nɩ, dɩbam balʋ na yɩ Wɛ tɩntʋŋna tɩn ya ba jɩgɩ cwǝŋǝ sɩ dɩ́ joŋi dɩ́ ni-wʋdiu dɩ́ tɩtʋŋa yam ŋwaanɩ mʋ na?.

eng: Dont we have the right to travel with a wife who believes like the rest of the apostles, the Lords brothers, and Cephas?.
local: Dɩbam ya ba jɩgɩ cwǝŋǝ sɩ dɩ́ di kaana balʋ na sɛ Zezi tɩn, sɩ dɩ́ taá tɔgɩ daanɩ dɩ́ beerǝ, nɩnɛɛnɩ dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi tɩntʋŋna babam bam dɩ o nyaana dɩ Pɩyɛɛrɩ na yǝni ba kɩ te tɩn na?.

eng: Or is it only I and Barnabas who dont have the right to not work for our living?.
local: Amʋ dɩ Banabasɩ yɩranɩ nan mʋ maŋɩ sɩ dɩ́ taá tʋŋɩ dɩ́ lagɩ dɩ́ tɩtɩ ni-wʋdiu na?.

eng: Who joins the army and pays their own way? Who plants a vineyard and doesnt eat its fruit? Who shepherds a flock and doesnt drink its milk?.
local: Nɔɔnʋ na yɩ pamaŋnʋ, o bá wanɩ o joori o ŋwɩ o tɩtɩ. Nɔɔnʋ jɩgɩ kara yɩ o ba di ka wǝǝnu na? Nɔɔnʋ jɩgɩ naanɩ o tɔga yɩ o ba di dɩ nayɩla na?.

eng: Im not saying these things just based on common sense, am I? Doesnt the Law itself say these things?.
local: Kʋ daɩ lʋgʋ baŋa mɩmaŋa yantʋ yɩranɩ mʋ brɩ dɩbam cɩga kam kʋntʋ. Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm dɩ maŋɩ kʋ brɩ kʋntʋ doŋ mʋ.

eng: In Moses Law its written: You will not muzzle the ox when it is threshing . Is God worried about oxen.
local: Kʋ pʋpʋnɩ Wɛ cullu tɩlʋ DƖ na pɛ Moyisitɩn tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ kʋ wɩ: «Nmʋ na jɩgɩ nabɩa n ma n nwanɩ wo-vaalʋ, yɩ kɩ tampɔgɔ ka ni nɩ.» Wɛ na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ yɩ nabɩa kam yɩranɩ ŋwaanɩ mʋ na?.

eng: or did he say this entirely for our sake? It was written for our sake because the one who plows and the one who threshes should each do so with the hope of sharing the produce.
local: Aye, kʋ yɩ dɩbam ŋwaanɩ mʋ. Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na vagɩ dɩ wʋlʋ na wʋra o kɩ faa tɩn maama tɩɩnɩ nɩ baá na ba tǝri mʋ.

eng: If we sowed spiritual things in you, is it so much to ask to harvest some material things from you?.
local: Dɩbam na tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝrǝ kam dɩ abam tɩn, kʋ nyɩ dɩ dɩ́ dugi Wɛ cɩga kam mʋ á bɩcara nɩ. Kʋ nan wʋ maŋɩ sɩ dɩ́ joŋi dɩ́ ni-wʋdiu abam tee nɩ na?.

eng: If others have these rights over you, dont we deserve them all the more? However, we havent made use of this right, but we put up with everything so we dont put any obstacle in the way of the gospel of Christ.
local: Abam na paɩ nɔɔna badonnǝ ba ni-wʋdiu tɩn, kʋ ya wʋ maŋɩ sɩ dɩbam joŋi dɩ́ dwǝni bantʋ na? Nan dɩ kʋ dɩ, dɩbam wʋ bwe sɩ dɩ́ joŋi dɩ́ ni-wʋdiu abam tee nɩ, nɩ dɩ́ ya na jɩgɩ cwǝŋǝ sɩ dɩ́ kɩ te tɩn. Dɩ́ nan vɔgɩ dɩ́ wʋ mʋ woŋo maama baŋa nɩ, sɩ kʋ yɩ wanɩ kʋ cɩ Zezi Krisi kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam.

eng: Dont you know that those who serve in the temple get to eat food from the temple, and those who serve at the altar share part of what is sacrificed on the altar?.
local: Á tɩtɩ maŋɩ á ye nɩ balʋ na tʋŋɩ Wɛ tɩtʋŋa DƖ di-kamunu kʋm wʋnɩ tɩn yǝni ba joŋi ba ni-wʋdiu dáanɩ mʋ. Balʋ dɩ na joŋi vara ba ma kaanɩ Wɛ tɩn dɩ joŋi ba tǝri kʋ wʋnɩ mʋ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I want you to be sure of the fact that our ancestors were all under the cloud and they all went through the sea.
local: A ko-biǝ-ba, amʋ lagɩ sɩ á guli kʋlʋ dɛɛn na kɩ dɩ́ kwǝ nakwa balʋ na dɛ Moyisikwaga ba nuŋi Ezipinɩ tɩn woŋo mʋ. Wɛ dɛɛn pɛ kunkoŋo mʋ kwǝli ba maama, yɩ DƖ daarɩ DƖ pɔɔrɩ nɩnɩʋ kʋm na bam kuni bɩle DƖ daarɩ cwǝŋǝ, yɩ ba maama tɔgɩ da ba kɛ.

eng: In the same way, the Lord commanded that those who preach the gospel should get their living from the gospel.
local: Kʋ nan yɩ bɩdwɩ mʋ dɩ balʋ dɩ na tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam tɩn. Dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi pɛ ni nɩ ba dɩ maŋɩ sɩ ba joŋi ba ni-wʋdiu tɩtʋŋɩ dɩm kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ.

eng: All were baptized into Moses in the cloud and in the sea.
local: Kʋntʋ na kɩ tɩn, kʋ nyɩ dɩ bantʋ maama dɛɛn miisi na wʋnɩ mʋ, sɩ ba taa yɩ Moyisi karabiǝ, kunkoŋo kʋm na kwǝli-ba yɩ ba da nɩnɩʋ kʋm wʋ ba kɛ tɩn ŋwaanɩ.

eng: But I havent taken advantage of this. And Im not writing this so that it will be done for me. Its better for me to die than to lose my right to brag about this!.
local: Nan dɩ kʋ dɩ, amʋ wʋ bwe sɩ a joŋi kʋlʋkʋlʋ abam tee nɩ. Kʋ nan daɩ nɩ a pʋpʋnɩ tɩntʋ sɩ a ma joŋi woŋo mʋ abam tee nɩ. Aá tɩ mʋ, sɩ a bá fɔgɩ a za a na yɩ te tɩn. A bá sɛ a pa nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ cwǝŋǝ sɩ o cɔgɩ woŋo kʋlʋ na yɩ amʋ wʋpolo nyɩm tɩn.

eng: All ate the same spiritual food.
local: Wɛ dɛɛn ma pa ba maama di wʋdiu dɩdʋa.

eng: If I preach the gospel, I have no reason to brag, since Im obligated to do it. Im in trouble if I dont preach the gospel.
local: A bá wanɩ a magɩ a nyɔɔnɩ a na tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam tɩn ŋwaanɩ. Kʋntʋ yɩ fɩfɩʋn mʋ sɩ a kɩ. Amʋ ya na yagɩ Wɛ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam tɔɔlɩm, kʋʋ́ ta yɩ lɛɛrʋ mʋ dɩ amʋ.

eng: and all drank the same spiritual drink. They drank from a spiritual rock that followed them, and the rock was Christ.
local: yɩ ba daarɩ ba nyɔ na bɩdwɩ. Na balʋ ba maama dɛɛn na nyɔgɩ tɩn nuŋi piu kʋdoŋ yɩra mʋ, yɩ piu kʋm kʋntʋ mʋ yɩ wo-kɩnkagɩlɩ dɩlʋ Wɛ na kɩa, sɩ kʋ brɩ nɩ Zezi Krisi tɩtɩ mʋ tɔgɩ o wʋ ba tee nɩ.

eng: If I do this voluntarily, I get rewarded for it. But if Im forced to do it, then Ive been charged with a responsibility.
local: Amʋ tɩtɩ mʋ nan ya na lɩ wʋbʋŋa sɩ a taa tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam, a ya wʋ́ ta tɩɩnɩ sɩ a joŋi ŋwɩɩrʋ mʋ kʋ ŋwaanɩ. Kʋ nan daɩ a tɩtɩ wʋbʋŋa mʋ a kɩa, kʋ yɩ Wɛ mʋ kwe-ka DƖ kɩ amʋ jɩŋa nɩ.

eng: However, God was unhappy with most of them, and they were struck down in the wilderness.
local: Nan dɩ kʋntʋ dɩ, Wɛ wʋ dɛɛn wʋ poli dɩ nɔɔna zanzan ba wʋnɩ. DƖ dɛɛn ma pa ba zanzan tɩ ba maŋɩ kagʋa kam wʋnɩ.

eng: What reward do I get? That when I preach, I offer the good news free of charge. Thats why I dont use the rights to which Im entitled through the gospel.
local: Kʋntʋ, bɛɛ ŋwɩɩrʋ mʋ amʋ wʋ́ joŋi? Amʋ nyɔɔrɩ mʋ yɩ a na tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam zaanɩ, yɩ a ba bwe sɩ a joŋi kʋlʋkʋlʋ kʋ ŋwaanɩ, nɩ a ya na jɩgɩ cwǝŋǝ sɩ a kɩ te tɩn.

eng: These things were examples for us, so we wont crave evil things like they did.
local: Wǝǝnu tɩntʋ maama nan kɩ sɩ tɩ brɩ dɩbam mʋ, sɩ dɩ́ daa yɩ ta pa dɩ́ fra zʋʋrɩ wo-balwaarʋ nɩnɛɛnɩ bantʋ na kɩ te tɩn.

eng: Although Im free from all people, I make myself a slave to all people, to recruit more of them.
local: A te a tɩtɩ mʋ, a daɩ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ gambaa. Amʋ tɩtɩ nan mʋ pɛ a ji gambaa nɔɔna maama tee nɩ, sɩ a wanɩ a joŋi nɔɔna zanzan a kɩ Wɛ jɩŋa nɩ.

eng: Dont worship false gods like some of them did, as it is written, The people sat down to eat and drink and they got up to play .
local: Á yɩ zaŋɩ á zuli jwǝnǝ, nɩ bantʋ badaara dɛɛn na kɩ te tɩn. Kʋ pʋpʋnɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ kʋ wɩ: «Yisɩrayɛlɩ tiinǝ bam dɛɛn jǝni ba di wʋdiu ba daarɩ ba nyɔ, yɩ ba laan zaŋɩ ba sa ba kwɛɛra.».

eng: I act like a Jew to the Jews, so I can recruit Jews. I act like Im under the Law to those under the Law, so I can recruit those who are under the Law .
local: Amʋ na wʋ Zwifǝbam tee nɩ, a yǝni a tɔgɩ a kɩ nɩ Zwifǝ bam na kɩ te tɩn mʋ, sɩ a ma wanɩ a joŋi-ba a kɩ Wɛ jɩŋa nɩ. Wɛ cullu tɩlʋ DƖ na pɛ Zwifǝ bam tɩn ya ba tɔgɩ tɩ te amʋ. Amʋ nan na zʋʋrɩ dɩ Zwifǝ tɩn, a dɛ a sɛ-tɩ mʋ, sɩ a wanɩ a joŋi tɩ na te balʋ tɩn a kɩ Wɛ jɩŋa nɩ.

eng: Lets not practice sexual immorality, like some of them did, and twenty-three thousand died in one day.
local: Á yɩ zaŋɩ á pa dɩ́ taá boorǝ, nɩ bantʋ badaara dɛɛn na kɩ te yɩ ba nɔɔna mʋrr-fiinle-tɩtɔ tɩ dɛ dɩdʋa wʋnɩ tɩn.

eng: I act like Im outside the Law to those who are outside the Law, so I can recruit those outside the Law .
local: Kʋ daarɩ, a na yǝni a wʋ dwi-gɛtiinǝ balʋ na ba tɔgɩ Wɛ cullu tɩm tɩn tee nɩ, a dɩ tɔgɩ a kɩ nɩ bantʋ na kɩ te tɩn mʋ, sɩ a ma wanɩ a joŋi-ba a kɩ Wɛ jɩŋa nɩ. Kʋ nan daɩ nɩ a yigǝ tǝrǝ dɩdaanɩ Wɛ niǝ yam, kʋ yɩ Zezi Krisi niǝ yam mʋ te amʋ.

eng: Lets not test Christ, like some of them did, and were killed by the snakes.
local: Á yɩ pa-na dɩ́ maŋɩ dɩ́ Yuutu Baŋa-Wɛ dam dɩ́ nii, nɩ bantʋ badaara dɛɛn na kɩ te yɩ bɩsankwɩ dʋ-ba sɩ gʋ tɩn.

eng: I act weak to the weak, so I can recruit the weak. I have become all things to all people, so I could save some by all possible means.
local: Kʋ daarɩ, kʋ na yɩ balʋ wʋbʋŋa na wʋ bɩgɩ dɩ Wɛ cwǝŋǝ kam tɔgɩm tɩn, a yǝni a ji nɩ bantʋ dɩ mʋ te, sɩ a ma wanɩ a joŋi-ba a kɩ Wɛ jɩŋa nɩ. Amʋ na wʋ nɔɔna balʋ maama tee nɩ, a yǝni a ji nɩ bantʋ mʋ te, yɩ a kwaanɩ a kɩ a na wʋ́ kɩ te sɩ a ma joŋi ba badaara a kɩ Wɛ jɩŋa nɩ.

eng: Lets not grumble, like some of them did, and were killed by the destroyer.
local: Á yɩ taá pʋʋnɩ dɩ Wɛ, nɩ bantʋ badaara dɛɛn na kɩ te yɩ tʋʋnɩ malɛka kam gʋ-ba tɩn.

eng: All the things I do are for the sake of the gospel, so I can be a partner with it.
local: Amʋ nan kɩ wǝǝnu tɩntʋ maama sɩ Wɛ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam mʋ wanɩ ka vu yigǝ, sɩ a dɩ tɔgɩ a na a pɛɛrɩ DƖ wo-laarʋ tɩm wʋnɩ.

eng: These things happened to them as an example and were written as a warning for us to whom the end of time has come.
local: Wǝǝnu tɩntʋ maama dɛɛn nan kɩ dɩ́ kwǝ nakwa bam, sɩ tɩ taa yɩ maana mʋ tɩ pa dɩbam. Tɩ nan pʋpʋnɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ, sɩ kʋ wanɩ kʋ kaanɩ dɩbam balʋ ta na ŋwɩ yɩ lʋgʋ baŋa tiim da yam batwarɩ tɩn mʋ.

eng: Dont you know that all the runners in the stadium run, but only one gets the prize? So run to win.
local: Abam ye nɩ, nɔɔna na wʋra ba durǝ, nɔɔnʋ maama mʋ kwaanɩ sɩ o di yigǝ, yɩ ba dɩdʋa yɩranɩ mʋ wʋ́ di o joŋi pɛɛrɩ dɩm. Nan kwaanɩ-na kʋntʋ doŋ, sɩ á wanɩ á di yigǝ sɩ á joŋi pɛɛrɩ dɩm.

eng: So those who think they are standing need to watch out or else they may fall.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ, wʋlʋ na bʋŋɩ nɩ o zɩgɩ lanyɩranɩ tɩn nan fɔgɩ o yɩrɩ o tɩtɩ sɩ o yɩ tʋ.

eng: Everyone who competes practices self-discipline in everything. The runners do this to get a crown of leaves that shrivel up and die, but we do it to receive a crown that never dies.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ maama na lagɩ sɩ o duri tɩn yǝni o tiini o kwaanɩ mʋ o kɩ o tɩtɩ o kɩ daanɩ. Bantʋ nan yǝni ba kwaanɩ kʋntʋ sɩ ba joŋi pɛɛrɩ mʋ, yɩ ba pɛɛrɩ dɩm kʋntʋ bá daanɩ dɩ cɔgɩ. Kʋ daarɩ dɩbam pɛɛrɩ dɩlʋ dɩ́ na lagɩ dɩ́ joŋi Wɛ tee nɩ tɩn bá fɔgɩ dɩ cɔgɩ maŋa dɩ maŋa.

eng: No temptation has seized you that isnt common for people. But God is faithful. He wont allow you to be tempted beyond your abilities. Instead, with the temptation, God will also supply a way out so that you will be able to endure it.
local: Woŋo kʋlʋ maama na maŋɩ abam sɩ á tusi tɩn waɩ kʋ yi nɔɔnʋ maama. Wɛ nan yɩ cɩga tu mʋ, DƖ bá sɛ sɩ woŋo kʋlʋ na maŋɩ abam tɩn dwǝni abam dam. DƖ nan wʋ́ pa abam dam, sɩ á wanɩ á na cwǝŋǝ á nuŋi kʋ wʋnɩ maŋa kam kʋntʋ nɩ.

eng: So now this is how I run—not without a clear goal in sight. I fight like a boxer in the ring, not like someone who is shadowboxing.
local: Kʋntʋ, amʋ ba duri nɩ wʋlʋ na yǝri durim yɩ o duri yɔɔ yɔɔ te tɩn. A nan ba magɩ jara nɩ wʋlʋ na yǝri jara magɩm yɩ o magɩ yɔɔ yɔɔ te tɩn.

eng: So then, my dear friends, run away from the worship of false gods!.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ, a badon-sonnu-ba, lɩ-na á tɩtɩ jwǝnǝ kaanɩm wʋnɩ.

eng: Rather, Im landing punches on my own body and subduing it like a slave. I do this to be sure that I myself wont be disqualified after preaching to others.
local: Amʋ nan kwaanɩ a tiini a fɩn a tɩtɩ mʋ, sɩ a pa a yɩra yam lwarɩ nɩ amʋ mʋ te-ya. A kɩ kʋntʋ sɩ kʋ yɩ pa, a na tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam a brɩ nɔɔna badonnǝ tɩn, sɩ amʋ tɩtɩ laan ba a ga pɛɛrɩ mʋ DƖ tee nɩ.

eng: Im talking to you like you are sensible people. Think about what Im saying.
local: Amʋ nan lagɩ a ŋɔɔnɩ dɩ abam nɩ swan tiinǝ mʋ te, sɩ á bʋŋɩ á nii a taanɩ dɩm yɩ cɩga mʋ naa vwan mʋ.

eng: Isnt the cup of blessing that we bless a sharing in the blood of Christ? Isnt the loaf of bread that we break a sharing in the body of Christ?.
local: Dɩ́ na yǝni dɩ́ wʋra dɩ́ di Zezi Krisi tʋʋnɩ gulim wʋdiu kʋm, dɩ́ yǝni dɩ́ kwe zʋŋa mʋ dɩ ka wʋ wo-nyɔɔrʋ yɩ dɩ́ kɩ Wɛ le tɩ ŋwaanɩ, yɩ dɩ́ daarɩ dɩ́ nyɔ. Dɩ́ na kɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ brɩ nɩ dɩbam maama tɔgɩ dɩ́ sɛ nɩ Zezi Krisi jana kam nuŋi dɩbam ŋwaanɩ mʋ. Dɩ́ daa ta na yǝni dɩ́ kwe dɩpɛ dɩm dɩ́ fɔ fɔ dɩ́ di tɩn, kʋ brɩ nɩ dɩbam maama tɔgɩ dɩ́ sɛ nɩ o yɩra yam cɔgɩ dɩbam ŋwaanɩ mʋ. Kʋ daɩ kʋntʋ na?.

eng: Since there is one loaf of bread, we who are many are one body, because we all share the one loaf of bread.
local: Dɩbam maama na fɔgɩ dɩpɛ dɩdʋa kam dɩ́ di tɩn, kʋ brɩ nɩ dɩbam maama yɩ nɔɔnʋ dɩdʋa mʋ, dɩ́ ya zɩ maŋɩ dɩ́ daga te maama dɩ.

eng: Look at the people of Israel. Dont those who eat the sacrifices share from the altar?.
local: Nan bʋŋɩ-na Yisɩrayɛlɩ tiinǝ bam na yǝni ba ma wǝǝnu tɩlʋ ba kaanɩ Wɛ yɩ ba daarɩ ba di tɩn woŋo. Balʋ maama na dɛ ba di tɩn dɩ tɔgɩ ba sɛ kaanɩm dɩm mʋ.

eng: What am I saying then? That food sacrificed to a false god is anything, or that a false god is anything?.
local: Amʋ na tagɩ kʋlʋ tɩn brɩ bɛɛn? Kʋ brɩ nɩ wʋdiu kʋlʋ ba na mɛ ba kaanɩ jwǝnǝ tɩn jɩgɩ kuri mʋ na? Naa kʋ brɩ nɩ jwǝnǝ yam tɩtɩ mʋ jɩgɩ kuri?.

eng: No, but this kind of sacrifice is sacrificed to demons and not to God. I dont want you to be sharing in demons.
local: Awo, kʋ daɩ kʋntʋ. Amʋ lagɩ a brɩ abam nɩ, jwǝ-kaana na kaanɩ dɩ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ tɩn, ba paɩ ciciri mʋ sɩ kʋ daɩ Wɛ. Amʋ nan ba lagɩ sɩ abam na wǝl-dɔnɔ dɩ ciciri.

eng: You cant drink the cup of the Lord and the cup of demons; you cant participate in the table of the Lord and the table of demons.
local: Kʋ nan wʋ maŋɩ sɩ á nyɔ dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi Krisi wo-nyɔɔrʋ tɩm, sɩ á kwǝri á vu á nyɔ dɩ ciciri. Abam nan bá wanɩ á jǝni á di dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm tʋʋnɩ gulim wʋdiu kʋm, sɩ á kwǝri á vu á da á di wʋdiu kʋlʋ na wʋ́ pa á taá ŋwɩ dɩ ciciri tɩn.

eng: Or should we make the Lord jealous? We arent stronger than he is, are we?.
local: Á yɩ pa-na dɩ́ kɩ kʋlʋ na wʋ́ zaŋɩ dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm wʋ tɩn. Abam bʋŋɩ á paɩ dɩbam jɩgɩ dam dɩ́ dwe wʋntʋ mʋ na?.

eng: Everything is permitted, but everything isnt beneficial. Everything is permitted, but everything doesnt build others up.
local: Abam badaara na tɛ á wɩ, á jɩgɩ ni sɩ á kɩ kʋlʋ maama á na lagɩ tɩn, kʋ yɩ cɩga. Kʋ nan daɩ nɩ woŋo maama mʋ jɩgɩ kuri kʋ pa dɩbam. Kʋ ta daɩ nɩ woŋo maama mʋ jɩgɩ zǝnǝ.

eng: No one should look out for their own advantage, but they should look out for each other.
local: Nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ yɩ zaŋɩ o taa lagɩ o tɩtɩ zǝnǝ má. O nan maŋɩ sɩ o taa kɩ lanyɩranɩ o zǝni o donnǝ dɩ mʋ.

eng: Eat everything that is sold in the marketplace, without asking questions about it because of your conscience.
local: Á jɩgɩ ni sɩ á yǝgi nwana yalʋ maama ba na jɩgɩ ba yǝgi yaga nɩ tɩn á di. Nan yɩ bwe-na á nii ya yɩ jwǝnǝ nwana mʋ na, sɩ kʋʋ́ pa liǝ ja abam kʋ ŋwaanɩ.

eng: The earth and all that is in it belong to the Lord .
local: Kʋ pʋpʋnɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ kʋ wɩ: «Lʋgʋ baŋa dɩ kʋ wǝǝnu maama yɩ Baŋa-Wɛ nyɩm mʋ.».

eng: If an unbeliever invites you to eat with them and you want to go, eat whatever is served, without asking questions because of your conscience.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na ba tɔgɩ Zezi tɩn na bǝŋi abam sɩ á vu o sɔŋɔ á di wʋdiu dɩd-o, yɩ á na sɛ á vu, o na kwe wʋdiu kʋlʋ maama o pa abam tɩn, sɩ á di. Á yɩ zaŋɩ-na á bwe kʋ kuri, sɩ kʋʋ́ ta jɩgɩ liǝ dɩ abam.

eng: But if someone says to you, This meat was sacrificed in a temple, then dont eat it for the sake of the one who told you and for the sake of conscience.
local: Nɔɔnʋ nan na tagɩ dɩ abam o wɩ, wʋdiu kʋntʋ yɩ ba mɛ ba kaanɩ jwǝni mʋ, sɩ á daa yɩ zaŋɩ á di, bɛŋwaanɩ liǝ wʋ́ zʋ nɔɔnʋ wʋm kʋntʋ bɩcarɩ.

eng: Now when I say conscience I dont mean yours but the other persons. Why should my freedom be judged by someone elses conscience?.
local: Kʋ daɩ nɩ á yagɩ kʋ dim liǝ na wʋ́ zʋ abam tɩtɩ bɩcarɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ, kʋ yɩ liǝ na wʋ́ ja wʋlʋ na tagɩ o brɩ abam tɩn ŋwaanɩ mʋ. Dɛdoŋ abam waɩ á bwe amʋ á nii, bɛŋwaanɩ mʋ dɩ́ maŋɩ sɩ dɩ́ yagɩ dɩ́ na jɩgɩ ni sɩ dɩ́ kɩ kʋlʋ tɩn, kʋ yɩ liǝ na wʋ́ zʋ nɔɔna badonnǝ tɩn ŋwaanɩ mʋ?.

eng: If I participate with gratitude, why should I be blamed for food I thank God for?.
local: Abam daa ta waɩ á bwe amʋ nɩ, dɩ́ na kɩ Wɛ le wʋdiu kʋm ŋwaanɩ yɩ dɩ́ daarɩ dɩ́ di tɩn, nɔɔnʋ daa wʋ́ lwɛ dɩbam bɛɛ ŋwaanɩ mʋ?.

eng: So, whether you eat or drink or whatever you do, you should do it all for Gods glory.
local: Kʋ nan na yɩ te tɩn, woŋo kʋlʋ maama abam na wʋ́ kɩ tɩn, kʋ na yɩ á na wʋ́ di naa á nyɔ naa kǝm dɩlʋ maama á na wʋ́ kɩ tɩn, kʋ maŋɩ sɩ á taá kɩ kʋlʋ maama na wʋ́ pa Wɛ na zulǝ kʋ ŋwaanɩ tɩn mʋ.

eng: Dont offend either Jews or Greeks, or Gods church.
local: Á yɩ taá kɩ-na kʋlʋ na wʋ́ cɔgɩ nɔɔna wʋbʋŋa tɩn, kʋ ya zɩ yɩ Zwifǝ bam naa dwi-gɛ tiinǝ naa Wɛ nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm dɩ.

eng: This is the same thing that I do. I please everyone in everything I do. I dont look out for my own advantage, but I look out for many people so that they can be saved.
local: Taá kɩ-na nɩ amʋ na kɩ te tɩn. Woŋo kʋlʋ maama amʋ na kɩ tɩn, a kwaanɩ sɩ kʋ taa poli nɔɔna maama wʋ mʋ. Amʋ ba lagɩ a tɩtɩ yɩranɩ zǝnǝ. A lagɩ sɩ a zǝni nɔɔna maama mʋ, sɩ kʋ wanɩ kʋ pa Wɛ vrɩ-ba DƖ yagɩ.

eng: Follow my example, just like I follow Christs.
local: Taá lwǝni-na amʋ, nɩ amʋ na lwǝni Zezi Krisi te tɩn.

eng: I praise you because you remember all my instructions, and you hold on to the traditions exactly as I handed them on to you.
local: Abam kɩ cɩga lanyɩranɩ dɩdaanɩ á na yǝni á guli amʋ gulǝ, yɩ á kwǝri á sɛ zaasɩm dɩlʋ maama a na brɩ abam tɩn.

eng: Now I want you to know that the head of every man is Christ, and the head of the woman is the man, and the head of Christ is God.
local: Amʋ nan lagɩ sɩ abam lwarɩ nɩ, Zezi Krisi mʋ yɩ nɔɔnʋ maama yuutu. Kaanɩ dɩ yuutu maa yɩ o barʋ. Baŋa-Wɛ nan mʋ yɩ Zezi Krisi dɩ yuutu.

eng: Every man who prays or prophesies with his head covered shames his head.
local: Kʋntʋ tɩn, á na jeeri daanɩ sɩ á warɩ Wɛ, yɩ baarʋ na zaŋɩ sɩ o loori Wɛ naa o ŋɔɔnɩ Wɛ yiyiu-ŋwɛ, yɩ o na mɛ woŋo o kwǝli o yuu, kʋntʋ tu yáalɩ o yuutu wʋm mʋ.

eng: Every woman who prays or prophesies with her head uncovered disgraces her head. It is the same thing as having her head shaved.
local: Kaanɩ dɩ nan na zaŋɩ sɩ o loori Wɛ naa o ŋɔɔnɩ Wɛ yiyiu-ŋwɛ, yɩ o na wʋ mɛ woŋo o kwǝli o yuu, o dɩ yáalɩ o yuutu wʋm mʋ. O na kɩ kʋntʋ, o dɩ wʋlʋ na ceeri o yuu tɩn maama yɩ bɩdwɩ mʋ.

eng: If a woman doesnt cover her head, then she should have her hair cut off. If it is disgraceful for a woman to have short hair or to be shaved, then she should keep her head covered.
local: Kaanɩ na wʋ sɛ sɩ o kwǝli o yuu, kʋ ya maŋɩ sɩ o cɔ-kʋ mʋ naa o ceeri-kʋ. Kʋ nan na jɩgɩ cavɩɩra sɩ kaanɩ kɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ maŋɩ sɩ o taa kwǝli-kʋ mʋ.

eng: A man shouldnt have his head covered, because he is the image and glory of God; but the woman is mans glory.
local: Baara nan ba jɩgɩ sɩ ba kwǝli ba yuni, bɛŋwaanɩ bantʋ jɩgɩ Wɛ nyɩnyʋgʋ mʋ, yɩ ba brɩ DƖ zulǝ na yɩ te. Kʋ nan na yɩ kaana, bantʋ mʋ brɩ baara zulǝ na yɩ te.

eng: Man didnt have his origin from woman, but woman from man.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ Wɛ mɛ baarʋ yɩra mʋ DƖ kɩ kaanɩ, sɩ kʋ daɩ kaanɩ yɩra mʋ DƖ mɛ DƖ kɩ baarʋ.

eng: and man wasnt created for the sake of the woman, but the woman for the sake of the man.
local: Kʋntʋ, Wɛ wʋ kɩ baarʋ kaanɩ ŋwaanɩ, DƖ kɩ kaanɩ mʋ baarʋ ŋwaanɩ.

eng: Because of this a woman should have authority over her head, because of the angels.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ, dɩ Wɛ malɛsɩsɩm ŋwaanɩ, kaana maŋɩ sɩ ba taa kwǝli ba yuni, sɩ kʋ brɩ nɩ ba jɩgɩ ni sɩ ba ŋɔɔnɩ Wɛ kɔgɔ kʋm wʋnɩ.

eng: However, woman isnt independent from man, and man isnt independent from woman in the Lord.
local: Kʋ daarɩ, dɩbam balʋ maama na wʋ dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi kɔgɔ kʋm wʋnɩ tɩn, kaanɩ ba te o tɩtɩ baarʋ tee nɩ, baarʋ dɩ nan ba te o tɩtɩ kaanɩ tee nɩ.

eng: As woman came from man so also man comes from woman. But everything comes from God.
local: Wɛ sɩɩnɩ DƖ ma baarʋ yɩra mʋ DƖ kɩ kaanɩ, yɩ kaanɩ dɩ nan mʋ lʋrɩ baarʋ. Woŋo maama nan nuŋi Wɛ te mʋ.

eng: Judge for yourselves: Is it appropriate for a woman to pray to God with her head uncovered?.
local: Abam tɩtɩ nan bʋŋɩ-na á nii. Kaanɩ na zaŋɩ sɩ o loori Wɛ, yɩ o na wʋ kwe woŋo o ma kwǝli o yuu, kʋ lamma na?.

eng: Doesnt nature itself teach you that if a man has long hair, it is a disgrace to him.
local: Nɔɔna maama ye nɩ, baarʋ yuuywe na tiini sɩ dwara, kʋ yɩ cavɩɩra mʋ kʋ paɩ o tɩtɩ.

eng: but if a woman has long hair, it is her glory? This is because her long hair is given to her for a covering.
local: Kʋ nan na yɩ kaanɩ, o yuuywe na tiini sɩ nuŋi sɩ daga, kʋ zul-o mʋ. Kʋ yɩ o dɩ Wɛ pɛɛrɩ mʋ, sɩ sɩ taa daga yɩ sɩ kwǝli o yuu.

eng: But if someone wants to argue about this, we dont have such a custom, nor do Gods churches.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ nan na wʋ sɛ amʋ zaasɩm dɩm kʋntʋ yɩ o magɩ kantɔgɔ dɩ baŋa nɩ tɩn, kʋntʋ tu taa ye nɩ dɩbam maama tɔgɩ kantʋ cwǝŋǝ kam mʋ, yɩ Wɛ nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm je maama nɩ tɩn dɩ tɔgɩ kantʋ mʋ.

eng: Now I dont praise you as I give the following instruction because when you meet together, it does more harm than good.
local: Woŋo kʋlʋ amʋ na lagɩ a ta dɩ abam tɩn, a ba lagɩ a tee abam nɩ á kɩ lanyɩranɩ. Abam na yǝni á jeeri daanɩ tɩn, á cɔgɩm mʋ dwe á fɔgɩm.

eng: First of all, when you meet together as a church, I hear that there are divisions among you, and I partly believe it.
local: Dayigǝ woŋo, amʋ ni nɩ á na yǝni á jeeri daanɩ sɩ á zuli Wɛ, á jɩgɩ kampwara mʋ daanɩ. Amʋ bʋŋɩ nɩ kʋ sɩɩnɩ kʋʋ́ ta yɩ cɩga mʋ.

eng: Its necessary that there are groups among you, to make it clear who is genuine.
local: Kʋ nan fɩn mʋ sɩ kampwara taa wʋ abam tɩtarɩ nɩ, sɩ kʋ brɩ balʋ na sɩɩnɩ ba tɔgɩ Zezi dɩ cɩga tɩn.

eng: So when you get together in one place, it isnt to eat the Lords meal.
local: Abam na yǝni á jeeri daanɩ sɩ á di dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi tʋʋnɩ gulim wʋdiu kʋm tɩn, á ba kɩ nɩ á na maŋɩ sɩ á kɩ te tɩn.

eng: Each of you goes ahead and eats a private meal. One person goes hungry while another is drunk.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ nɔɔnʋ maama yǝni o lɔgɩrɩ o di o tɩtɩ wʋdiu kʋm mʋ. Abam nan na kɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ pɛ kana jɩgɩ badonnǝ, yɩ á badonnǝ nyɔ á su á ja gaalɩ.

eng: Dont you have houses to eat and drink in? Or do you look down on Gods churches and humiliate those who have nothing? What can I say to you? Will I praise you? No, I dont praise you in this.
local: Abam ya na lagɩ sɩ á di dim dɩm kʋntʋ doŋ tɩn, á ya wʋ́ di á tɩtɩ sam nɩ mʋ. Abam lagɩ sɩ á cɔgɩ Wɛ nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm yuu mʋ na? Abam lagɩ sɩ á pa cavɩɩra mʋ ja yinigǝ tiinǝ balʋ na wʋ á kɔgɔ kʋm wʋnɩ tɩn na? Abam lagɩ sɩ amʋ ta bɛɛ mʋ dɩ abam? Nɩ á kɩ lanyɩranɩ mʋ na? A bá wanɩ a ta nɩ á kɩ lanyɩranɩ dɩ kʋntʋ woŋo.

eng: I received a tradition from the Lord, which I also handed on to you: on the night on which he was betrayed, the Lord Jesus took bread.
local: Cɩga kalʋ dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi na brɩ-nɩ o tʋʋnɩ gulim wʋdiu kʋm ŋwaanɩ tɩn mʋ a zaasɩ abam, kʋ yɩ sɩ tɩtɩɩ dɩlʋ ba na jaanɩ dɩ́ Yuutu Zezi tɩn mʋ o kwe dɩpɛ.

eng: After giving thanks, he broke it and said, This is my body, which is for you; do this to remember me.
local: yɩ o kɩ Wɛ le dɩ ŋwaanɩ o daarɩ o fɔ fɔ o pa o karabiǝ bam, yɩ o wɩ: «Amʋ yɩra yam mʋ tɩntʋ, ya na lagɩ ya cɔgɩ abam ŋwaanɩ tɩn. Taá kɩ-na kʋntʋ doŋ sɩ á taá ma guli amʋ gulǝ.».

eng: He did the same thing with the cup, after they had eaten, saying, This cup is the new covenant in my blood. Every time you drink it, do this to remember me.
local: Ba na di ba ti tɩn, o daa ma kwe zʋŋa dɩ ka wʋ wo-nyɔɔrʋ yɩ o kɩ Wɛ le tɩ ŋwaanɩ, yɩ o wɩ: «Wo-nyɔɔrʋ tɩntʋ brɩ nɩ Wɛ goni ni-dʋʋrɩ mʋ dɩdaanɩ nabiinǝ amʋ jana kam ŋwaanɩ. Maŋa kalʋ maama á na lagɩ á nyɔ-tɩ tɩn, sɩ á kɩ-na kʋntʋ á taá ma guli amʋ gulǝ.».

eng: Every time you eat this bread and drink this cup, you broadcast the death of the Lord until he comes.
local: Bɛŋwaanɩ, maŋa kalʋ maama á na di dɩpɛ dɩm kʋntʋ yɩ á nyɔ wo-nyɔɔrʋ tɩm kʋntʋ tɩn, á kɩ á ma á brɩ dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm tʋʋnɩ dɩm na yɩ te tɩn mʋ, sɩ kʋ vu kʋ yi maŋa kam o na wʋ́ joori o ba tɩn.

eng: This is why those who eat the bread or drink the cup of the Lord inappropriately will be guilty of the Lords body and blood.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ, nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na di dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm tʋʋnɩ gulim dɩpɛ dɩm naa o nyɔ wo-nyɔɔrʋ tɩm, yɩ o kɩ kʋlʋ na wʋ maŋɩ dɩ dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm tɩn, kʋntʋ tu kɩ kǝm-balɔrɔ mʋ dɩ dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm yɩra yam dɩ o jana kam.

eng: Each individual should test himself or herself, and eat from the bread and drink from the cup in that way.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ, nɔɔnʋ maama maŋɩ sɩ o da yigǝ o taa ye o tɩtɩ mʋ, sɩ o laan di dɩpɛ dɩm o daarɩ o nyɔ wo-nyɔɔrʋ tɩm.

eng: Those who eat and drink without correctly understanding the body are eating and drinking their own judgment.
local: Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ nan na di dɩpɛ dɩm yɩ o kwǝri o nyɔ wo-nyɔɔrʋ tɩm dɩ goonǝ tɩn wʋ maŋɩ dɩ dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm yɩra yam mʋ, kʋntʋ tu vaŋɩ taanɩ mʋ o daŋɩ o tɩtɩ yuu nɩ.

eng: Because of this, many of you are weak and sick, and quite a few have died.
local: Kʋntʋ nan mʋ paɩ á zanzan ba jɩgɩ yazurǝ, yɩ á badaara bwǝnǝ, yɩ á badonnǝ tʋa.

eng: But if we had judged ourselves, we wouldnt be judged.
local: Dɩbam nan ya na yǝni dɩ́ da yigǝ dɩ́ bwe dɩ́ tɩtɩ dɩ́ nii yɩ dɩ́ lwarɩ dɩ́ na yɩ balʋ tɩn, Wɛ ya daa bá pa dɩ́ na cam.

eng: However, we are disciplined by the Lord when we are judged so that we wont be judged and condemned along with the whole world.
local: Dɩ́ Yuutu Wɛ nan yǝni DƖ vaŋɩ dɩbam zwɛ sɩ DƖ pa dɩ́ joori dɩ́ tʋ cwǝŋǝ nɩ mʋ, sɩ maŋa kalʋ DƖ na wʋ́ ba DƖ di nɔɔna maama taanɩ tɩn, sɩ dɩ́ yɩ zaŋɩ dɩ́ ga bʋra DƖ tee nɩ, nɩ balʋ na yǝri Wɛ tɩn te.

eng: For these reasons, my brothers and sisters, when you get together to eat, wait for each other.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ, a ko-biǝ-ba, á na jeeri daanɩ sɩ á di Zezi tʋʋnɩ gulim wʋdiu kʋm, sɩ á zuri á yɩra á cǝgi daanɩ.

eng: If some of you are hungry, they should eat at home so that getting together doesnt lead to judgment. I will give directions about the other things when I come.
local: Wʋlʋ kana na wɔɛ tɩn, kʋntʋ tu da yigǝ o di o sɔŋɔ nɩ, sɩ o laan daarɩ o ba, sɩ á yɩ vaŋɩ Wɛ sarɩya á daŋɩ á yuu nɩ maŋa kalʋ á na jeeri daanɩ tɩn. Kʋ daarɩ, kʋ na yɩ wǝǝnu tɩlʋ na daarɩ tɩn, amʋ na tuǝ, a laan wʋ́ brɩ abam á na wʋ́ kɩ te.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I dont want you to be ignorant about spiritual gifts.
local: Kʋ daarɩ, a laan lagɩ a ŋɔɔnɩ Wɛ Jorokʋm pɛɛra yam laŋa nɩ mʋ. A ko-biǝ-ba, a lagɩ sɩ a pa á lwarɩ Wɛ Joro kʋm na tʋŋɩ te dɩ te tɩn mʋ.

eng: You know that when you were Gentiles you were often misled by false gods that cant even speak.
local: Abam ye nɩ, maŋa kalʋ á dɛɛn ta na wʋ lwarɩ Wɛ tɩn, á dɛɛn tɔgɩ jwǝnǝ yalʋ na yɩ kafɛ tɩn mʋ, yɩ ya ganɩ abam sɩ á taá tɔgɩ cwe sɩlʋ maama ya na lagɩ tɩn.

eng: So I want to make it clear to you that no one says, Jesus is cursed! when speaking by Gods Spirit, and no one can say, Jesus is Lord, except by the Holy Spirit.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ a lagɩ a pa á lwarɩ nɩ, Wɛ Joro kʋm bá ganɩ abam. Kʋntʋ, nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na ŋɔɔnɩ dɩ Wɛ Joro dam tɩn bá wanɩ o twɩ Zezi. Nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ nan bá wanɩ o ta nɩ, Zezi mʋ yɩ o Yuutu, yɩ Wɛ Joro kʋm tǝri o tee nɩ.

eng: There are different spiritual gifts but the same Spirit.
local: Wɛ Joro kʋm pɛɛra yam yɩ dwi tǝri tǝri mʋ, yɩ DƖ Joro kʋm dɩdʋa yɩranɩ mʋ paɩ-ya DƖ nɔɔna.

eng: and there are different ministries and the same Lord.
local: Wɛ tɩtʋŋa yam dɩ yɩ dwi tǝri tǝri mʋ, yɩ dɩ́ Yuutu wʋlʋ dɩ́ na tʋŋɩ dɩ́ pa-o tɩn yɩ dɩdʋa yɩranɩ mʋ.

eng: and there are different activities but the same God who produces all of them in everyone.
local: Wɛ na pɛ dɩbam dam sɩ dɩ́ ma tʋŋɩ DƖ tɩtʋŋa te tɩn dɩ yɩ dwi tǝri tǝri mʋ. Wɛ nan yɩ dɩdʋa yɩranɩ mʋ, yɩ dɩntʋ mʋ pɛ dɩbam maama dam dɩm kʋntʋ maama.

eng: A demonstration of the Spirit is given to each person for the common good.
local: Wɛ Joro kʋm nan pɛ nɔɔnʋ maama pɛɛrɩ sɩ o wanɩ o maa zǝni o donnǝ.

eng: A word of wisdom is given by the Spirit to one person, a word of knowledge to another according to the same Spirit.
local: Wɛ Joro kʋm paɩ nɔɔna badonnǝ dam sɩ ba ŋɔɔnɩ swan taanɩ, yɩ kʋntʋ mʋ ta paɩ nɔɔna badonnǝ dam sɩ ba ŋɔɔnɩ yǝno taanɩ dɩ.

eng: faith to still another by the same Spirit, gifts of healing to another in the one Spirit.
local: Wɛ Joro kʋm nan ta mʋ paɩ nɔɔna badonnǝ dam sɩ ba taa tiini ba jɩgɩ wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Wɛ, yɩ kʋ ta paɩ badonnǝ dam sɩ ba ta maa paɩ yawɩɩna naɩ yazurǝ.

eng: performance of miracles to another, prophecy to another, the ability to tell spirits apart to another, different kinds of tongues to another, and the interpretation of the tongues to another.
local: Kʋntʋ nan ta mʋ paɩ badonnǝ dam sɩ ba ta maa kɩ wo-kɩnkagɩla, kʋ daarɩ sɩ badonnǝ taa waɩ ba ŋɔɔnɩ Wɛ yiyiu-ŋwɛ. Kʋ daa ta maa paɩ nɔɔna badonnǝ waɩ ba pɔɔrɩ kʋlʋ na yɩ Wɛ Joro kʋm nyɩm dɩ kʋlʋ na daɩ kʋ nyɩm tɩn daanɩ. Kʋ ta paɩ badonnǝ waɩ ba ŋɔɔnɩ ta-gɛ, yɩ badonnǝ dɩ waɩ ba lwarɩ ta-gɛ sɩm kʋntʋ kuri yɩ ba tɛ ba brɩ ba donnǝ.

eng: All these things are produced by the one and same Spirit who gives what he wants to each person.
local: Kʋ nan yɩ Wɛ Joro kʋm kʋntʋ dɩdʋa yɩranɩ mʋ jɩgɩ pɛɛra yam kʋntʋ dwi tǝri tǝri, yɩ kʋ paɩ nɔɔnʋ maama nɩ kʋ wʋbʋŋa na lagɩ te tɩn.

eng: Christ is just like the human body—a body is a unit and has many parts; and all the parts of the body are one body, even though there are many.
local: Nan bʋŋɩ-na á nii nabiinu yɩra na yɩ te tɩn. Ya yɩ woŋo dɩdʋa mʋ, yɩ ya laan pɔɔrɩ je dwi tǝri tǝri. Ya pɔɔrɩm dɩm daga zanzan, yɩ ya maama laan yɩ yɩra dɩdʋa mʋ. Zezi Krisi dɩ o kɔgɔ kʋm maama dɩ nan yɩ kʋntʋ mʋ.

eng: We were all baptized by one Spirit into one body, whether Jew or Greek, or slave or free, and we all were given one Spirit to drink.
local: Dɩbam maama na pɛ ba miisi dɩbam na wʋnɩ Wɛ Joro kʋm dɩdʋa dam ŋwaanɩ tɩn, dɩ́ laan jigi nɔɔnʋ dɩdʋa mʋ. Kʋ na yɩ Zwifǝ naa dwi-gɛ tiinǝ, naa gambɛ naa balʋ na te ba tɩtɩ tɩn maama, Wɛ Joro kʋm kʋntʋ yɩranɩ mʋ wʋ dɩbam maama tee nɩ.

eng: Certainly the body isnt one part but many.
local: Nabiinu yɩra pɔɔrɩ je tǝri tǝri mʋ, sɩ kʋ daɩ jǝgǝ dɩdʋa yɩranɩ.

eng: If the foot says, Im not part of the body because Im not a hand, does that mean its not part of the body?.
local: Naga na tagɩ ka wɩ, kantʋ daɩ jɩŋa, kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ yɩra yam ba te-ka, kʋntʋ bá sɩɩnɩ kʋ pa ka taa tɔgɩ ka tǝri yɩra yam wʋnɩ.

eng: If the ear says, Im not part of the body because Im not an eye, does that mean its not part of the body?.
local: Zwɛ dɩ nan na wɩ, dɩntʋ daɩ yi, sɩ yɩra yam ba te-dɩ, kʋntʋ bá sɩɩnɩ kʋ pa dɩ taa tɔgɩ dɩ tǝri yɩra yam wʋnɩ.

eng: If the whole body were an eye, what would happen to the hearing? And if the whole body were an ear, what would happen to the sense of smell?.
local: Yɩra yam maama nan ya na yɩ yi yɩranɩ mʋ, yaá kɩ ta mʋ ya taa ni taanɩ? Ya nan ya na yɩ zwɛ yɩranɩ mʋ, yaá kɩ ta mʋ ya taa ni lwǝm?.

eng: But as it is, God has placed each one of the parts in the body just like he wanted.
local: Kʋ nan yɩ Wɛ mʋ kɩ je sɩm dwi tǝri tǝri DƖ kɩ yɩra yam wʋnɩ, kʋ maŋɩ dɩ DƖ wʋbʋŋa na lagɩ te tɩn.

eng: If all were one and the same body part, what would happen to the body?.
local: Ya maama nan ya na yɩ yɩra yam jǝgǝ dɩdʋa mʋ, yaá kɩ ta mʋ ya ji yɩra?.

eng: But as it is, there are many parts but one body.
local: Kʋntʋ tɩn, je dwi tǝri tǝri mʋ wʋra, yɩ sɩ maama laan yɩ yɩra dɩdʋa.

eng: So the eye cant say to the hand, I dont need you, or in turn, the head cant say to the feet, I dont need you.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ, yi bá wanɩ dɩ ta dɩ jɩŋa nɩ dɩ ba lagɩ-ka, naa yuu ta dɩ naga nɩ kʋ ba lagɩ-ka.

eng: Instead, the parts of the body that people think are the weakest are the most necessary.
local: A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam nɩ, dɩbam yɩra yam je sɩlʋ dɩ́ na bʋŋɩ nɩ sɩ tiini sɩ bwǝnǝ tɩn jɩgɩ kuri zanzan.

eng: The parts of the body that we think are less honorable are the ones we honor the most. The private parts of our body that arent presentable are the ones that are given the most dignity.
local: Dɩ́ yɩra yam je sɩlʋ dɩ́ na bʋŋɩ nɩ sɩ ba tiini sɩ jɩgɩ kuri tɩn mʋ dɩ́ yǝni dɩ́ zuli lanyɩranɩ. Je sɩlʋ na jɩgɩ cavɩɩra tɩn, mʋ dɩ́ yǝni dɩ́ fɔgɩ dɩ́ kwǝli-sɩ lanyɩranɩ.

eng: The parts of our body that are presentable dont need this. But God has put the body together, giving greater honor to the part with less honor.
local: Kʋ daarɩ ya je sɩlʋ na lana tɩn, dɩ́ daa ba fɔgɩ dɩ́ nii sɩ baŋa nɩ. Wɛ mʋ kɩ nabiinu yɩra yam je sɩm maama DƖ kɩ daanɩ, yɩ DƖ paɩ je sɩlʋ na gɛ zulǝ tɩn na zulǝ lanyɩranɩ.

eng: so that there wont be division in the body and so the parts might have mutual concern for each other.
local: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ kampwara tǝri yɩra yam wʋnɩ. Ya je sɩm maama zǝni daanɩ mʋ.

eng: If one part suffers, all the parts suffer with it; if one part gets the glory, all the parts celebrate with it.
local: Kʋ na cana yɩra yam daa kalʋ nɩ, yɩra yam maama wʋ́ tɔgɩ ya na cam dɩm kʋntʋ doŋ mʋ. Kʋ daarɩ daa kalʋ na nɛ zulǝ tɩn, yɩra yam maama dɩ wʋ́ tɔgɩ ya na wʋpolo mʋ.

eng: You are the body of Christ and parts of each other.
local: Abam maama yɩ Zezi Krisi yɩra yam mʋ, yɩ á dɩdʋa dɩdʋa yɩ o je sɩm dwi tǝri tǝri mʋ.

eng: In the church, God has appointed first apostles, second prophets, third teachers, then miracles, then gifts of healing, the ability to help others, leadership skills, different kinds of tongues.
local: Kʋntʋ tɩn, Zezi kɔgɔ kʋm wʋnɩ, Wɛ kɩ nɔɔnʋ maama o jǝgǝ nɩ mʋ. Dayigǝ tɩn yɩ Zezi tɩntʋŋna bam o tɩtɩ na lɩ tɩn mʋ. Wɛ nijoŋnǝ bam maa saŋɩ. Balʋ na kɩ bɩtɔ tɩn maa yɩ balʋ na brɩ Wɛ cɩga kam tɩn. Kʋ wǝli dɩ balʋ na kɩ wo-kɩnkagɩla tɩn, dɩ balʋ Wɛ na pɛ-ba dam sɩ ba ta maa paɩ yawɩɩna naɩ yazurǝ tɩn, dɩ balʋ na zǝni nɔɔna tɩn, dɩ balʋ na nii Wɛ tɩtʋŋa yam baŋa nɩ tɩn, dɩ balʋ Wɛ Joro kʋm na paɩ ba ŋɔɔnɩ ta-gɛ tɩn.

eng: All arent apostles, are they? All arent prophets, are they? All arent teachers, are they? All dont perform miracles, do they?.
local: Kʋ nan daɩ nɩ dɩ́ maama mʋ yɩ Zezi tɩntʋŋna balʋ o tɩtɩ dɛɛn na lɩ tɩn, kʋ daɩ dɩ́ maama mʋ yɩ Wɛ nijoŋnǝ, naa dɩ́ maama mʋ brɩ Wɛ cɩga kam. Kʋ nan daɩ nɩ dɩ́ maama mʋ waɩ dɩ́ kɩ wo-kɩnkagɩla.

eng: All dont have gifts of healing, do they? All dont speak in different tongues, do they? All dont interpret, do they?.
local: Kʋ nan daɩ dɩ́ maama mʋ Wɛ pɛ dam sɩ dɩ́ taá ma paɩ yawɩɩna naɩ yazurǝ, naa dɩ́ maama mʋ Wɛ Joro kʋm pɛ dɩ́ ŋɔɔnɩ ta-gɛ, naa dɩ́ maama mʋ waɩ dɩ́ lwarɩ sɩ kuri dɩ́ brɩ nɔɔna.

eng: Use your ambition to try to get the greater gifts. And Im going to show you an even better way.
local: Nan kwaanɩ-na sɩ á joŋi Wɛ-pɛɛra yalʋ na jɩgɩ kuri kʋ ja gaalɩ tɩn. Kʋ daarɩ, a nan lagɩ a brɩ abam woŋo kʋlʋ na tiini kʋ lana kʋ dwe dɩdɩ tɩn mʋ.

eng: If I speak in tongues of human beings and of angels but I dont have love, Im a clanging gong or a clashing cymbal.
local: Amʋ na maŋɩ a waɩ a ŋɔɔnɩ nabiinǝ dwi tǝri tǝri taana, dɩ malɛsɩsɩm taanɩ dɩ, yɩ a na ba jɩgɩ sono, kʋ nyɩ dɩ sɔɔ mʋ a kɩa, nɩnɛɛnɩ ba na magɩ kwaŋa dɩ loŋini yɩ tɩ kɩ sɔɔ te tɩn.

eng: If I have the gift of prophecy and I know all the mysteries and everything else, and if I have such complete faith that I can move mountains but I dont have love, Im nothing.
local: Amʋ na maŋɩ a jɩgɩ Wɛ-pɛɛrɩ dɩlʋ na paɩ a waɩ a ŋɔɔnɩ Wɛ yiyiu-ŋwɛ tɩn, yɩ a jɩgɩ yǝno maama, yɩ a kwǝri a ye cɩga kalʋ maama na sǝgi nabiinǝ yigǝ nɩ tɩn, yɩ a na maŋɩ a jɩgɩ wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ Wɛ sɩ a taa waɩ a paɩ pweeru zaŋɩ tɩ je nɩ tɩ ve tɩ tʋ mɛ a na lagɩ tɩn, yɩ a na ba jɩgɩ sono, kʋ yɩ kafɛ mʋ.

eng: If I give away everything that I have and hand over my own body to feel good about what Ive done but I dont have love, I receive no benefit whatsoever.
local: Amʋ na maŋɩ a kwe a jɩjɩgɩrʋ maama a maŋɩ a pa yinigǝ tiinǝ, yɩ a ta kwǝri a sɛ sɩ ba kwe a yɩra yam ba dɩ mini nɩ, yɩ a na ba jɩgɩ sono, kʋ ta maŋɩ kʋ ba jɩgɩ kuri kʋ pa-nɩ.

eng: Love is patient, love is kind, it isnt jealous, it doesnt brag, it isnt arrogant.
local: Sono tu jɩgɩ wʋ-zuru mʋ, yɩ o kɩ nɔɔna lanyɩranɩ. Sono tu ba jɩgɩ pu-sɩŋa naa o magɩ o nyɔɔnɩ, o nan ba jɩgɩ kamunni dɩ.

eng: it isnt rude, it doesnt seek its own advantage, it isnt irritable, it doesnt keep a record of complaints.
local: Sono tu ba kɩ kʋlʋ na wʋ maŋɩ tɩn, o nan ba beeri o tɩtɩ yɩranɩ zǝnǝ má. O nan jɩgɩ ban-pʋnʋ. Nɔɔnʋ na kɩ-o lwarɩm, o ba kwe o kɩ o wʋnɩ.

eng: it isnt happy with injustice, but it is happy with the truth.
local: Sono tu ba kɩ wʋpolo dɩ woŋo kʋlʋ na yɩ balɔrɔ tɩn, o kɩ wʋpolo dɩ woŋo kʋlʋ na yɩ cɩga tɩn mʋ.

eng: Love puts up with all things, trusts in all things, hopes for all things, endures all things.
local: Sono tu waɩ o vɔ o wʋ woŋo maama baŋa nɩ, yɩ o jɩgɩ wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ tɩɩna dɩ wʋ-zuru woŋo maama baŋa nɩ.

eng: Love never fails. As for prophecies, they will be brought to an end. As for tongues, they will stop. As for knowledge, it will be brought to an end.
local: Sono ba tiǝ. Wɛ yiyiu-ŋwɛ sɩlʋ nɔɔna na ŋɔɔnɩ tɩn wʋ́ ba sɩ kɛ. Wɛ-pɛɛrɩ dɩlʋ nɔɔna na maa ŋɔɔnɩ ta-gɛ tɩn dɩ wʋ́ ba dɩ kɛ. Yǝno dɩ wʋ́ ba kʋ kɛ.

eng: We know in part and we prophesy in part.
local: Lele kʋntʋ yǝno kʋlʋ dɩbam na jɩgɩ tɩn yɩ cicoro mʋ. Wɛ yiyiu-ŋwɛ sɩlʋ dɩ́ na ŋɔɔnɩ tɩn dɩ yɩ cicwǝǝru mʋ.

eng: but when the perfect comes, what is partial will be brought to an end.
local: Kʋ daarɩ maŋa kalʋ Wɛ na wʋ́ ba fɔgɩ wǝǝnu maama DƖ pa tɩ maŋɩ fasɩ tɩn, wo-cicwǝǝru tɩm maama laan wʋ́ je mʋ.

eng: When I was a child, I used to speak like a child, reason like a child, think like a child. But now that I have become a man, Ive put an end to childish things.
local: Maŋa kalʋ a na yɩ bu tɩn, a dɛɛn ŋɔɔnɩ biim mʋ, yɩ a kwǝri a bʋŋɩ nɩ bu te, yɩ a jeeli nɩ bu te. Lele kʋntʋ, a na kɩ nɔɔnʋ tɩn, a kwe busɔɔnɩ wǝǝnu tɩm maama a yagɩ mʋ.

eng: Now we see a reflection in a mirror; then we will see face-to-face. Now I know partially, but then I will know completely in the same way that I have been completely known.
local: Lele kʋntʋ, dɩ́ na lwarɩ Wɛ cɩga kam te tɩn, kʋ taa nyɩ dɩ niu mʋ dɩ́ jɩgɩ dɩ́ nii kʋ wʋnɩ liirǝ liirǝ. Kʋ daarɩ maŋa kam kʋntʋ na yiǝ, dɩ́ laan wʋ́ ta naɩ jaja mʋ. Lele kʋntʋ yǝno kʋlʋ a na jɩgɩ tɩn yɩ cicoro mʋ. Maŋa kam kʋntʋ nɩ, a laan wʋ́ ta ye wǝǝnu maama kuri a tiǝ, nɩ Wɛ na ye amʋ DƖ ti te tɩn.

eng: Now faith, hope, and love remain—these three things—and the greatest of these is love.
local: Kʋ daarɩ wǝǝnu tɩtɔ mʋ wʋ́ ta wʋra maŋa maama. Wǝǝnu tɩntʋ mʋ yɩ wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ tɩɩna dɩ sono. Kʋlʋ nan na dwe tɩ maama tɩn mʋ yɩ sono.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I want to call your attention to the good news that I preached to you, which you also received and in which you stand.
local: A ko-biǝ-ba, a lagɩ sɩ a pa á guli Wɛ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam a na maŋɩ a tɔɔlɩ dɩ abam tɩn mʋ. Abam maŋɩ á sɛ-ka, yɩ á kɩ á wʋ-dɩdʋa á fɔgɩ á zɩgɩ kǝŋkǝŋ.

eng: You are being saved through it if you hold on to the message I preached to you, unless somehow you believed it for nothing.
local: Amʋ na pɛ á lwarɩ Wɛ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam te tɩn, á na wʋ joori ka kwaga nɩ, Wɛ wʋ́ vrɩ abam DƖ yagɩ kantʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ. Kʋ na daɩ kʋntʋ, á na sɛ Zezi Krisi te tɩn yɩ kafɛ mʋ.

eng: I passed on to you as most important what I also received: Christ died for our sins in line with the scriptures.
local: Wɛ cɩga kalʋ na dwe maama tɩn mʋ yɩ kalʋ amʋ na dɛ yigǝ a joŋi yɩ a laan daarɩ a brɩ abam tɩn. Kantʋ mʋ tɩntʋ: Zezi Krisi tɩgɩ dɩbam lwarɩm ŋwaanɩ mʋ, nɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm na maŋɩ kʋ ta te tɩn.

eng: he was buried, and he rose on the third day in line with the scriptures.
local: Ba ma kɩ-o bɔɔnɩ nɩ. Da yatɔ dɛ nɩ o ma joori o bi o yagɩ tʋʋnɩ, nɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm na maŋɩ kʋ ta te tɩn.

eng: He appeared to Cephas, then to the Twelve.
local: O na bi tɩn, o ma pa Pɩyɛɛrɩ na-o, yɩ o tɩntʋŋna fugǝ-bale bam maama dɩ na-o.

eng: and then he appeared to more than five hundred brothers and sisters at once—most of them are still alive to this day, though some have died.
local: Kʋntʋ kwaga nɩ, o ma brɩ o tɩtɩ o karabiǝ zanzan bɩdwɩ baŋa nɩ, bantʋ dwe nɩ nɔɔna biǝ yanu . Nɔɔna bam kʋntʋ badaara zanzan ta ŋwɩ, ba fɩnfɩɩn yɩranɩ mʋ tɩga.
